Name: Plan International France
Description:
Membre du réseau Plan International, l’ONG Plan International France agit aux côtés des filles, et plus largement des enfants et des jeunes, pour faire progresser leurs droits et atteindre l’égalité filles-garçons.

En contexte de développement et de crises humanitaires, nous mettons en place des programmes pour assurer la protection des filles, et plus largement et des enfants et des jeunes, contre toutes formes de violence. Nous agissons pour leur accès à l’éducation et à la formation professionnelle, nous défendons le respect de leurs droits à la santé sexuelle et reproductive et accompagnons leur engagement en tant que jeunesses actrices. 

Grâce à nos 40 000 donateur.trices, dont plus de 30000 marraines et parrains, au soutien des bailleurs institutionnels et des mécènes, nous mettons en place/développons dans plus de 40 pays, des solutions durables construites avec les communautés, les équipes et les partenaires locaux.

Nous assurons également, en France, un travail de plaidoyer auprès des décideur·euses politiques et sensibilisons le grand public à l’égalité de genre.

Plan International, jusqu’à l’égalité !

Name: Walton Family Foundation
Description:
We are a family-led organization, supporting multiple generations of Sam and Helen Walton’s family. 
We value trust, mutual respect, and a workplace where every associate belongs and can do their best work. We bring together talented people with different backgrounds and viewpoints, support their growth, and empower them to contribute meaningfully. 
We are an equal opportunity employer committed to a workplace that is free of discrimination and harassment of any kind. All applicants receive consideration for employment without regard to race, religion, color, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, age, veteran status, genetic information, or any other status protected by the laws or regulations in the locations where we operate.

Name: Annie E. Casey Foundation
Description: The Annie E. Casey Foundation is a private national philanthropy that creates better futures for the nation’s children by strengthening families, building economic opportunities and transforming neighborhoods into safer and healthier places to live, work and grow.

Name: Robert Bosch Stiftung
Description:
The Robert Bosch Stiftung GmbH is one of the major German foundations associated with a private company in Europe. It works in the areas of health, education, and global issues. With its charitable activities, it contributes the development of viable solutions to social challenges. For this purpose, the Foundation implements its own projects, enters into alliances with partners, and supports third-party initiatives.
 
The Global Issues support area centers around the topics of peace, inequality, climate change, democracy, migration, and immigration society. When it comes to education, the focus is on individual and organizational learning, while with its support for health, the Foundation is committed to securing a sustainable health care system that focuses more on people.

Name: Médecins du Monde Canada
Description:
Médecins du Monde est une ONG qui soigne, témoigne et accompagne le changement social à l'aide de son réseau international regroupant 15 délégations. Notre mission est d’offrir et promouvoir l’accès aux soins de santé aux personnes exclues et vulnérables, ici et ailleurs.

Nous allons au-delà des urgences et des soins médicaux. En plus d’offrir des soins aux populations marginalisées, notre objectif est d’apporter des changements structurels par un plaidoyer informé et constructif. Nous voulons inspirer une approche de la santé qui tienne compte des besoins et des droits des personnes en situation de vulnérabilité. 
Depuis sa création au Canada, il y a 20 ans, Médecins du Monde va à la rencontre de ces personnes, là où elles se trouvent, que ce soit dans la rue, dans les camps de déplacés ou encore dans des coins de pays négligés par les États.

Name: India Climate Collaborative
Description: The India Climate Collaborative (ICC), founded in 2020 by philanthropists and industry leaders, is a first-of-its-kind collaborative committed to the climate ecosystem in India. It does this by unlocking philanthropic capital, identifying catalytic climate priorities, and creating a connective infrastructure for impactful funding.

Name: The Commonwealth Fund
Description: The Commonwealth Fund is a private foundation dedicated to affordable, quality health care--for all.

Name: African Women's Development Fund (AWDF)
Description:
African Women’s Development Fund (AWDF) is a grant-making foundation which supports local, national and regional organisations in Africa working towards women’s empowerment. AWDF through institutional capacity building and programme development seeks to build a culture of learning and partnerships within the African women’s movement.

 The vision of AWDF is for African women to live in a world in which there is social justice, equality and respect for women’s human rights.  To this end, our mission is to mobilise financial resources to support local, national and regional initiatives led by women, which will lead to the achievement of this vision.  AWDF funds women's groups across Africa working in the areas of Women’s Human Rights, Political Participation, Peace Building, Health and Reproductive Rights, HIV/AIDS and Economic Empowerment.

To date AWDF has granted over $26.2 million to 1,200 women's organisations in 42 African countries. AWDF’s grants range from U$1,000- U$50,000, and multi-year grants of up to $120,000 can be made to grantees engaged in strategic work.

Name: Sawiris Foundation for Social Development
Description:
Sawiris Foundation for Social Development (SFSD) is one of the first donor foundations in Egypt. Established in 2001 by the Sawiris family, SFSD philanthropically promotes and supports innovative solutions that target comprehensive and sustainable development in Egypt.

Over the last 23 years, SFSD has addressed the most pressing issues facing marginalized Egyptians, such as poverty, unemployment, social exclusion, and limited access to quality education, by funding the delivery of programs aimed at achieving comprehensive and sustainable development in partnership with the governmental and private sectors as well as civil society. By creating thousands of job opportunities, providing quality education, and accessibility to basic services, SFSD has impacted the lives of nearly one million Egyptians in 24 Egyptian governorates, focusing on the most remote villages, with total investments of close to 2.5 billion EGP.

Name: Bodossaki Foundation
Description:
Bodossaki Foundation is a public benefit organisation established in 1972 for continuing the charitable and social solidarity offering of its founder, Prodromos Bodossakis Athanassiades, to the Greek society. Its vision is a society of equal opportunities and prospects for all.  

To promote its vision, the Foundation funds, plans and implements, in full alignment with the principles of transparency, accountability and integrity, actions and programmes of relevance to its four strategic pillars: supporting education, strengthening healthcare, protecting the environment and empowering Civil Society.

Today, Bodossaki Foundation also acts as a catalyst for fostering a broader culture of social contribution in the Greek society, managing third-party resources (re-granting) on behalf of grantors wishing to fund programmes with a strong social impact.

Name: Inside Philanthropy
Description: Inside Philanthropy helps nonprofit professionals expand their impact. We publish new articles daily about trends in philanthropy and also provide in-depth grant research on foundations and individual donors. Thousands of nonprofits, foundations, universities, consultants, and media organizations rely on IP to understand and navigate the fast-changing world of philanthropy.

Name: Global Fund for Children
Description:
Global Fund for Children partners to build a world where all children and youth enjoy equal resources and opportunities in society and live free from violence, discrimination, and exploitation. We do this by investing in  innovative, grassroots organizations that provide children and youth with the resources to reach their full potential and advance their rights worldwide.

In the fight for children’s rights, too many great ideas go unheard and unfunded. Global Fund for Children is here to change that. We discover grassroots organizations pursuing bold ideas, and provide the funding, coaching, and connections to give those ideas a chance to work. We take smart risks that others don’t, walking alongside our partners to help them develop and succeed, so that, together, we can build a future where all children and youth are safe, strong, and valued.

Name: Porticus
Description:
Porticus is a philanthropic organisation that aims to create a just and sustainable future where human dignity flourishes. 

Porticus advises and manages the philanthropic funds of the Brenninkmeijer family business owners. In close collaboration with our partners, we design initiatives around global social and environmental challenges around four areas: Earth, Education, Faith and Society. Through special entities Argidius and Community Arts Lab, we tackle poverty through small business development and seek to transform lives through community arts.

Across our priorities and special entities, our mission and approach are consistent: creating a just and sustainable future for all through systems change that is inclusive, participatory and makes a difference for the long term.

Name: Paul Hamlyn Foundation
Description:
Paul Hamlyn Foundation was established in 1987. Since then, we have used our resources to work towards a just future in which everyone, especially young people, can realise their full potential and enjoy fulfilling and creative lives.

In all of our work, including our grant-making, we are guided by six strategic priorities where we want to see change:
 
• Investing in young people
• Migration and integration
• Arts access and participation
• Education and learning through the arts
• Nurturing ideas and people
• Creating opportunities for people and communities in India

Find out more about our work and apply for funding: www.phf.org.uk

Name: The Asian American Foundation
Description: The Asian American Foundation (TAAF) is a convener, incubator, and funder committed to accelerating opportunity and prosperity for Asian American and Pacific Islander communities. We strive to be a catalyzing force for belonging for the 23 million AAPIs across the U.S.

Name: Carlsberg Foundation
Description:
The Carlsberg Foundation is one of the world's oldest industrial foundations. Here, ambitious brewing business flourishes alongside support for science, art, and culture.

One of the primary aims of the Carlsberg Foundation is to provide support for outstanding basic research within the natural sciences, humanities, and social sciences.

The Carlsberg Foundation places particular emphasis on supporting talent development and generational change in Danish research, internationalization of Danish talents, and visionary research breakthroughs.

Name: Trust-Based Philanthropy Project
Description: The Trust-Based Philanthropy Project builds tools, conducts experiments, forges alliances, and deploys narrative strategies to make trust-based philanthropy the standard of practice for all philanthropies committed to a just and equitable society.

Name: McKnight Foundation
Description: McKnight Foundation, a family foundation based in Minnesota, advances a more just, creative, and abundant future where people and planet thrive.

Name: ONG AMSODE
Description: L’Association Malienne pour la Solidarité et le Développement (AMSODE) est une organisation non gouvernementale nationale humanitaire à but non lucratif basée en Afrique de l’Ouest, précisément au Mali.AMSODE se fait entre autre objectif: la mise en place des projets de développement en étroite collaboration avec les populations locales afin d’assurer la promotion et le développement des initiatives de base ainsi que la conception et l’exécution de toute action en faveur du développement. Son a approché désormais est l’auto promotion pour faire participer largement les populations auxquelles reviennent les initiatives des projets. AMSODE participe désormais aux actions de soutiens aux populations vulnérables au Mali en général et des victimes de la sécheresse à travers les aides d’urgence et les actions de réhabilitation.

Name: Fondation AXIAN
Description:
La Fondation Axian est la fondation d’entreprise du Groupe Axian, présent à Madagascar, dans l’océan Indien et en Afrique. 

La Fondation Axian a pour mission de mutualiser les ressources humaines et financières des entreprises du Groupe Axian afin d’agir sur le terrain pour améliorer durablement les conditions de vie des populations vulnérables des pays dans lesquelles elles sont implantées. 

La Fondation Axian intervient dans 4 domaines : la santé, l’éducation, le développement des communautés /l’aide humanitaire et l’environnement/développement durable.

Fondation Axian – Let’s Act Together

Name: The Centre for Effective Altruism
Description:
Effective altruism is a philosophy and community focused on maximising the good you can do through your career, projects, and donations. The Centre for Effective Altruism (CEA) is a charity dedicated to building and nurturing this global community of people who are thinking carefully about the world’s biggest problems and taking impactful action to solve them.

CEA is an Effective Ventures project. Effective Ventures Foundation (UK) (EV UK) is a charity in England and Wales (with registered charity number 1149828, registered company number 07962181, and is also a Netherlands registered tax-deductible entity ANBI 825776867). Effective Ventures Foundation USA Inc. (EV US) is a section 501(c)(3) organization in the USA (EIN 47-1988398). EV UK and EV US are separate charities that collaborate closely under the “Effective Ventures” name.

Name: Echidna Giving
Description:
Echidna Giving is a philanthropic fund that joins with critical thinkers, innovators and practitioners, to advance girls' education in the developing world.

We’re passionate about being effective – in converting resources to real, sustainable change. That’s why we invest in the Global South, where we think our efforts can go further, and why we focus on girls’ education, an idea that we believe can change the world.

Name: Schusterman Family Philanthropies
Description:
At Charles and Lynn Schusterman Family Philanthropies, formerly Charles and Lynn Schusterman Family Foundation, we partner with our grantees in the United States and Israel to pursue more just and inclusive societies. 
 
In the U.S., we aim to advance racial, gender and economic equity through investments in criminal justice, democracy and voting rights, K-12 public education, gender and reproductive equity and the family’s hometown of Tulsa, Oklahoma. We also invest in fostering a more joyful and diverse Jewish community. In Israel, we work toward a secure homeland for the Jewish people, a thriving democracy and an inclusive society that cares for its most vulnerable. 
 
Charles and Lynn Schusterman established the Charles and Lynn Schusterman Family Foundation in 1987 in Tulsa, Oklahoma. In 2021, we updated our name to Charles and Lynn Schusterman Family Philanthropies to reflect the full range of the Schusterman family’s philanthropic and advocacy work. Under the leadership of Stacy Schusterman, we invest in leaders, organizations and U.S. public policy to drive long-term systemic change.  
 
Our team works with grantees, experts, civic institutions and governments to create pathways to greater equity and inclusion. Our work is inspired by our founders’ commitment to pursue justice, repair the world and ensure all people are treated with dignity and respect. Our team members bring a wide range of professional expertise, cultural backgrounds, lived experiences and perspectives to our work. 
 
To learn more about Schusterman Family Philanthropies, visit www.schusterman.org. To explore career opportunities at Schusterman, visit https://www.schusterman.org/who-we-are/careers.

Name: Center for Disaster Philanthropy
Description:
We mobilize philanthropy to strengthen the ability of all communities to withstand disasters and recover equitably when they occur.

CDP’s reach and impact through its educational resources, consulting services and grantmaking have continued to grow since its founding more than 13 years ago. As natural hazards and humanitarian crises persist and devastate communities worldwide, we will build on our successes, lessons learned and vital partnerships with community members to amplify our impact and move us closer to achieving our mission.

In alignment with our vision, commitment to racial and intersectional equity and organizational values, five goals will guide our work for the next five years.

Our primary goals are to:
• Advance the equitable and long-term recovery of marginalized communities before and after a disaster.
• Increase CDP's visibility, reputation and influence as a trusted expert in philanthropy.
• Be the leading expert in support of equitable recovery for marginalized communities at risk of or affected by disasters.
• Continue our transformation toward becoming an anti-racist organization.
• Strengthen CDP's organizational capacity.

Name: Fundación Minera Escondida
Description:
Somos una institución sin fines de lucro que desde su creación, en 1996, ha buscado caminos innovadores de Responsabilidad Social Empresarial que reflejen la voluntad corporativa de Minera Escondida Limitada

En nuestro trabajo con las comunidades mantenemos relaciones que se caracterizan por su permanente retroalimentación comunicacional y monitoreo respecto de los problemas y oportunidades que se producen en su entorno, particularmente en las comunas urbanas y rurales de la Región de Antofagasta.

Name: Bureaux du Coeur
Description: Les Bureaux du Cœur mobilisent les entreprises pour transformer leurs bureaux inoccupés, le soir et le week-end, en refuges temporaires pour accueillir une personne en situation de grande précarité.

Name: Charter School Growth Fund
Description: The Charter School Growth Fund identifies the country’s best charter schools, funds their expansion, and helps to increase their impact. We are driven by a conviction that all children deserve great public schools in their communities. As a national nonprofit, we make multi-year, philanthropic investments in talented education entrepreneurs building networks of great charter schools, and we provide them with support as they grow.

Name: Canada Learning Code
Description:
Canada Learning Code is Canada's leading national charity championing computer science education. Canada Learning Code is proud to work from coast-to-coast-to-coast to make sure that everyone in Canada has access to the knowledge they need to prosper in our digital world.

Originally founded as Ladies Learning Code in 2011, the organization has evolved to run programming for adults, youth and educators through our Ladies, Girls, Kids, Teens and Teachers Learning Code programs. Today, Canada Learning Code operates in over 250 communities across the country, driving impact through program design and delivery, strategic industry and public partnerships, research and advocacy. To date, the organization has had over 600,000 learners attend an in-person or live online experience.

Name: The END Fund
Description:
The END Fund is a leading collaborative philanthropic fund dedicated to ending neglected tropical diseases (NTDs) that limit the lives and livelihoods of 1.7 billion people globally. Through our partners, we support country-led programs that control and eliminate intestinal worms, schistosomiasis, lymphatic filariasis, river blindness, trachoma, and visceral leishmaniasis.

We efficiently put private capital to work in support of NTD programs that are innovative, integrated, cost-effective, and sustainable. By facilitating strong partnerships with  governments, private sector, multilateral organizations, and local program partners, we collaboratively support national disease programs that improve the well-being of  communities.

Name: SOLIDARITYNOW
Description: SolidarityNow is a non-governmental organization, committed to improve vulnerable people’s lives in order to pursue a better future, with dignity and perspectives.

Name: Radiology Across Borders
Description:
Radiology Across Borders is an Australian charity that saves lives, every single day. 

We do this by focussing on education. Sharing knowledge that developing nation doctors simply don't have access to, and the results are startling.

Lives are saved not when we visit, but every day thereafter, and as the knowledge is passed on it has an exponential affect - because when you have the ability to save a life, you have the responsibility to do so.

EDUCATION - SAVES LIVES
We teach radiology techniques to developing nation doctors, increasing survival rates from a number of easily treatable injuries, diseases & illnesses.

EQUIPMENT - ENABLES EDUCATION
We donate computers to overseas health centres, allowing doctors to learn life-saving techniques through our seminars, and we provide modern imaging equipment, when funding permits, enabling previously impossible assessment of injury & disease. 

PARTNERSHIPS - EXTEND OUR REACH
We partner with specialist organisations to extend our reach, saving more lives, in more locations, more often.

AND WE NEED HELP TO CONTINUE 
People are dying of easily preventable diseases, right now, only a few hours from us here in Sydney. 

- - - - It's like having the cure for cancer, and not being able to use it.

So please help. Please get in touch. Every dollar helps.
To donate, or find out about corporate sponsorship opportunities, click below:

More details:  www.radiologyacrossborders.org.au

Name: ACT Foundation | Aksi Cepat Tanggap
Description:
ACT Foundation | Aksi Cepat Tanggap is a humanitarian organization which focuses on natural and humanitarian integrated disaster management, covering emergency, rescue, medical, relief, reconstruction and recovery.

ACT was established on 2005 as an official and independent institution. The programs that we handled have developed beyond natural disaster, but also focus on social disaster or humanitarian disaster. These included malnutrition, famine, children, health and sanitation issues, education, economy empowerment, community development, and also social conflict.

With our vision to become a pioneer for awakening compassion souls with volunteerism basis towards society independence, ACT always brings up the values of Compassion, Volunteerism, and Society Independence in every single project that we do.

ACT is independent, objectively neutral, non-discriminative, transparent, and accountable. Therefore, ACT doesn’t distinguish ethnicity, race, religion, or party when performing its programs domestically and also internationally.

ACT’s programs funding came from public donation and company’s Corporate Social Responsibility’s (CSR) fund, which utilization will be audited by public accountant as a form of transparency to stakeholders.

To reach beneficiaries spread to the most remote areas, ACT in executing its programs developed local volunteer network under the banner of Masyarakat Relawan Indonesia (MRI – Indonesian Volunteer Society. The existence of MRI makes the execution of ACT’s programs more efficient and effective.

Name: The Wallace Foundation
Description: Our mission is to help all communities build a more vibrant and just future by fostering advances in the arts, education leadership, and youth development.

Name: 9 Seeds Of Tomorrow
Description:
Our Mission:
transform people's lives, in something better.
Plant and cultivate nine more seeds.
Be Light

Our chellenge:
We are going to sow the world

Commitment:
help the people in all ways, and pass on the knowledge learned.

Name: Near East Foundation
Description: Near East Foundation is a global leader and trusted partner, bringing international expertise, innovative programs and long-term commitment to conflict and crisis-affected communities.

Name: Grantmakers In Health
Description: Grantmakers In Health (GIH) is dedicated to helping philanthropic organizations and advisors improve the health of all people. GIH works with hundreds of organizations across size and geographic focus to support organizations in building knowledge and skills, strengthening organizational effectiveness, and connecting grantmakers with peers and potential partners. Visit our website for more information.

Name: Cleaning for a Reason
Description:
Cleaning for a Reason is a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization that partners with more than 1,400 residential cleaners throughout the United States and Canada to offer free house cleaning to any household battling cancer. Since 2006, the nonprofit and its partners have donated more than $23 million in services, helping more than 65,000 cancer patients. 

In 2017, Cleaning for a Reason was adopted by ISSA Charities, the charitable arm of ISSA, the worldwide cleaning industry association. 

To learn more about Cleaning for a Reason and to apply for free house cleaning, visit cleaningforareason.org.

Name: A.T.E. Chandra Foundation (ATECF)
Description:
A.T.E. Chandra Foundation (ATECF) is one of India’s largest non-CSR foundations, established in 2013 to channel the giving of Archana & Amit Chandra (Chandra Family) and the A.T.E. Group. We invest in scalable, sustainable and equitable solutions so that the most marginalized can thrive. 
Our work is primarily focused across two strategic domains:
1.Capacity Building - strengthening social sector leadership & organisation effectiveness. Investing in the philanthropic ecosystem to serve society more effectively.
2.Water & Agriculture - partnering with communities and governments to revive water bodies as a means towards efficient water security; and investing in scalable solutions that materially uplift farming.
3. Additional Focus Areas - driving impact across India’s cities, ecosystems, and cultural legacies — through sustainable urban projects, conservation partnerships, and heritage philanthropy.
We are not a fundraising foundation or a CSR vehicle — we invest our principal resources to catalyze long-term change, leveraging open-source our learnings and collaboration widely across ecosystem partners.

Name: The ELMA Philanthropies
Description: The ELMA Philanthropies provides philanthropic advisory services to the ELMA Group of Foundations from offices in New York, Cape Town, Johannesburg, and Kampala.

Name: Fondazione Italiana Accenture ETS
Description:
Fondazione Italiana Accenture ha come mission primaria il trasferimento di tecnologie, competenze ed esperienze dal mondo profit al mondo non profit.
La Fondazione attraverso la sua piattaforma proprietaria gestisce concorsi per idee e programmi di formazione, realizzando progetti di Digital Social Innovation sostenibili, scalabili, replicabili e in grado di generare occupazione.

Name: Mennonite Central Committee
Description:
Mennonite Central Committee (MCC) shares God's love and compassion for all in the name of Christ  by responding to basic human needs and working for peace and justice. MCC has workers or financial commitments in more than 60 countries around the world, including the U.S. and Canada. Together, MCC U.S. and MCC Canada administer this work.
 
MCC is concrete and hands-on. MCC addresses basic human needs such as water, food and shelter and works alongside churches and communities in a variety of efforts to build peace. You can take part in the work of MCC, whether by serving in your home country or abroad, by donating time or money or by canning meat, sewing blankets, assembling kits for humanitarian aid, volunteering in thrift shops or for relief sales or participating in advocacy campaigns.
 
MCC is focused on relationships. MCC works through and with local churches and community groups to enable them to better respond to the needs within their own communities. MCC builds bridges to connect people and ideas across cultural, political and economic divides.
 
MCC values peace and justice. MCC addresses injustice and oppression. MCC is committed to nonviolent peacemaking, including loving those who might be considered enemies.
 
MCC is rooted in faith in Jesus Christ and the witness of the global church. We welcome your prayers and encourage you to explore and participate in the world of MCC.

Name: Community Foundation Ireland
Description:
For change. For better. For all. With 25 years expertise and experience, Community Foundation Ireland understands that philanthropy is informed, intentional and impactful. We, as experts, with the generous support of donors and the expertise of our community partners continue to use that force into the future to achieve positive change for all.

What we promise

Leadership – we are strategic and work towards being a centre of influence in the community;
Integrity – we are accountable for what we do and transparent in how we do it;
Stewardship – we are responsible stewards of Community Foundation Ireland's assets and focus our time, resources and energy on making a measurable difference;
Capacity-building – we value supporting the community and helping organisations succeed.

How we help

Experience matters
Since 2000, we have a proven track record in empowering donors who want to make a real difference in their communities by tackling social issues effectively.

Knowledge matters
We have a dedicated and committed staff with extensive experience of philanthropy and grant making in Ireland and overseas.

Connections matter
We connect donors with dedicated non-profit organisations that can make the best use of their gift and we work with professional advisers to find the best charitable solutions for their clients.

Relationships matter
We have insights and a working relationship with over 5,000 non-profit organisations at a national, regional and local level throughout Ireland.

Results matter
We make giving fast, efficient and rewarding, as well as cost effective for our donors.

Governance matters
We have a strong commitment to ensuring that the Foundation maintains impeccable standards of governance and transparency and follows best international practice.

Name: Womanity
Description:
We are a Swiss-based organisation with a highly experienced global team specialising in investing and developing solutions to address gender-based issues. Be it innovating and delivering our own programmes or supporting social changemakers and movements, we aim to boldly disrupt the status quo and promote meaningful change to advance the rights of women across the world.

Our vision for a gender-equal and just world has directed our focus towards preventing violence against women and girls, promoting the development of grassroots organisations, supporting girls’ education, leveraging media for gender equality and, most recently, unlocking women’s access to land rights. We have found these to be critical challenges faced in low-income communities in the Global South and we work extensively to strengthen high-impact on-ground projects to catalyse change.

Since 2015, we have partnered with philanthropists and investors to channel over $16 million into high-impact programmes across Afghanistan, Brazil, Cape Verde, India, Mexico, South Africa, and the Middle East and North Africa. Our strategic support has helped these programmes reach more than 26 million people — most of them women and girls.

Name: H2Global Foundation
Description:
Purpose of the H2Global Foundation is the promotion of environmental and climate protection. This purpose is fulfilled through measures that promote the production and use of green hydrogen and other climate-neutral energy carriers.

The foundation supports activities that contribute to an improved use of these energy sources through scientific, journalistic, and other activities such as consulting.

The foundation is also dedicated to the implementation of the   H2Global funding instrument, which pursues an auction-based promotion of the timely ramp-up of the international green hydrogen and Power-to-X economy. The German government is   providing funding of 900 million euros for the instrument.

The foundation currently represents 18 companies along the entire power-to-X value chain. The H2Global Foundation is happy to welcome further donors.

Name: FRRR (Foundation for Rural & Regional Renewal)
Description:
FRRR supports remote, rural and regional communities across Australia to be more vibrant, resilient, empowered and sustainable. We work alongside communities to catalyse locally led solutions, helping them navigate disruption and unlock opportunity.

We bridge the systemic gap by connecting resources, build enduring local skills, knowledge and participation, and reshape how remote and rural Australia is seen, valued and supported.

Established in 2000 as a partnership between the Australian Government and the Sidney Myer Fund, to date we’ve awarded more than $213 million in grants to more than 16,000 projects.

It’s more than money. Our funding and capacity building support is delivered at the hyper-local level. We back people and organisations in and of their place because we know that targeted, well-informed investment stimulates ideas that lead to positive changes, both big and small – especially in smaller communities.

Name: Iran & Its Future.org
Description:
Our mission at Iran & Its Future.org is simple yet at the same time complex, much like the history and the culture of the country, itself. 

Our mission lies deep within the understanding of our culture and the world in which we live today. The world needs and seems to be eager to understand the human element of the Iranian culture; yet is frightened by what they see and sometimes baffled by how little they know despite what they thought they ‘knew’. Bridging that gap is what we are expert at in our NGO and is what we are going to share with you… 


When it comes to history, Iran does not disappoint. Our written history goes back as far as 7000 years. 

The name Persian Gulf was not given to the body of water between Iran and the Southern border countries during the past dynasty. It has been so named since way before words were written on paper anywhere, in the world. The evidence is found carved into the stones and artifacts from ancient Persia dating back thousands of years.

There is no short way to explain what Iran is; the only way to understand Iran is to see it. Iran & Its Future.org aims to become a bridge between the Iranian people and the outside world.

We aim to be a platform for Iranian artists to showcase their art through our future museum project. The current sample page found on LinkedIn and Facebook can be, an educational page to visit. 

Our NGO is a place of gathering of great minds and our aim is to make Iran & Its Future.org website a sort of Silk Road of cross cultural exchange; a cyber café if you will, where you will get to know Iran.

When it comes to collaborating with Iranians outside Iran, our goal is to tap into the massive talent pool of highly sought after medical professionals to create a program to benefit not just Iranian children but any burned child. Our first patient from our Burned Children Program is on his way to the United States to receive reconstructive surgery

Name: Alfanar Venture Philanthropy
Description:
Alfanar is the Arab region's first venture philanthropy organisation.

Our aim is to strengthen ambitious social enterprises that generate financial and social returns while transforming lives in the region's poorest communities. 

We offer tailored financing, management support and real-time monitoring & evaluation to social enterprises working to improve the quality of children's education and empower women, youth and refugees across the Arab region.

Name: Women's Peace & Humanitarian Fund (WPHF)
Description: The Women’s Peace & Humanitarian Fund (WPHF) is a partnership of the United Nations and grassroots women's organizations in 25 countries experiencing crisis or conflict. WPHF rallies behind women's organizations that are working to build peace, advance equality, and improve humanitarian and economic conditions. WPHF provides funding, capacity building support, and advocacy. Since its launch in 2016, WPHF has supported over 220 women's rights and women-led civil-society organizations around the world, directly serving over 1.2 Million people - 90% of them being women and girls.

Name: Kansfonds
Description:
Niemand kan zonder thuis. Een dak boven je hoofd, voldoende geld om van te leven en het diepe besef dat je erbij hoort. Toch zijn er in ons land meer dan 1 miljoen mensen die geen thuis hebben. Ze missen wat nodig is voor een menswaardig bestaan. Dat laten we natuurlijk niet gebeuren in een samenleving waarin we omzien naar elkaar. Daarom helpt Kansfonds al 65 jaar thuisgeven. Samen ontdekken hoe het anders kan en wat de maatschappelijke oorzaken zijn van ‘geen thuis’. Gedurfde oplossingen uitproberen. Én een vliegwiel zetten op wat werkt. Net zo lang tot iedereen een thuis heeft. 

|

Everybody needs a home. A roof above their head, enough money to live from and the profound realisation that you belong somewhere. Nevertheless, there are more than 1 million people in our country that do not have a home. They lack what is essential to have a humane life. We cannot let this happen in a caring society. That is why Kansfonds helps out. Together we explore how we can change things and find out what are the underlying social causes of being homeless. By trying out bold solutions and propelling those that work. We will continue to do so until everybody has a home.

Name: Jack Kent Cooke Foundation
Description: The Jack Kent Cooke Foundation is dedicated to advancing the education of exceptionally promising students who have financial need. Since 2000, the Foundation has awarded $332 million in scholarships to nearly 3,900 students from 8th grade through graduate school, along with comprehensive educational advising, career counseling, and other support services. The Foundation has also provided $144 million in grants to organizations that serve such students.

Name: Nightingale Awards of Pennsylvania
Description:
A statewide, non-profit organization established in 1989 to honor nurses living and employed in the Commonwealth of Pennsylvania. To achieve its mission of recognizing the exceptional nurses of today and engaging the nurses of tomorrow, the organization has established a scholarship fund and awards program.
 
NAP is governed by a volunteer Board of Trustees, consisting of individuals who are leaders in nursing, business, industry, and other health care fields.

Name: Surf Life Saving Foundation
Description:
The role of Surf Life Saving in the community is acknowledged by a vast majority of Australians - but we are much more than the red and yellow flags and volunteer beach patrols. 

Surf Life Saving delivers a wide array of community benefits including healthy lifestyle, leadership, mateship, volunteerism and a sense of belonging. Surf Clubs have become local community hubs right around Australia.

Literally millions of Australians and overseas tourists directly interact with Surf Life Saving each year by using our charitable services - some realise this and some don't. Many do not give a second thought as to who sets up the red and yellow flags - the service is simply expected and as much a part of the Australian landscape as the beaches themselves. 

It is fair to say that most people know we are a worthy organisation and they can rely on the services we provide however very few truly understand that we are charity and are very much in need of public support. 

The role of the Surf Life Saving Foundation is to ensure the financial security and viability of the Surf Life Saving movement.

There are a myriad of ways that individuals and organisations alike can support Surf Life Saving - from our Guardians of the Surf program, Workplace Giving, Legacy gifting, Corporate partnerships and Fundraising Events through to our House Lotteries and Trade promotions. To see how you can become the silent partner in every rescue, visit our website www.slsfoundation.com.au

Name: Soquity
Description:
Soquity is a cause-driven platform that enables users to amplify their social impact. 
We strive to democratize the act of social giving, allowing users to contribute in a format that suits them best - volunteering, donating, or ideating.

Name: Grant Professionals Foundation
Description: The Grant Professional Foundation strengthens non profits by advancing the profession of the grant professionals they employ.  The Foundation administers GPA membership and GPA National Conference scholarships for the 2000+ members of the Grant Professionals Association.

Name: SocialWorks
Description:
SocialWorks is a non-profit headquartered in Chicago. Our mission is dedicated to community advancement, creating the blueprint for modern social activism and engagement, and encouraging the proactive investment in the aspirations of youth everywhere.

Since inception, we've run successful national and grassroots campaigns - ranging from raising over $2,000,000 for Chicago Public Schools, creating summer school camps for Chicago youth, and creating a city-wide initiative to get out the vote and register over 7,000 citizens for the election process.

Alongside founder Chancelor Bennett (aka "Chance the Rapper") and a diverse, multi-national group of supporters, SocialWorks plans on continuing to voice the plight of the people while seeking to unify communities under the umbrella of ambitious and positive social change.

Name: GMA Foundations
Description:
GMA Foundations helps you define a clear path to transformative philanthropy, and then walks the path with you as an ally and a guide. We partner with individual and organizational donors as managers, advisors, and facilitators. With over 40 years of insight and field experience, GMA Foundations is changing the world by helping you change the world.

GMA Foundations serves as trustee headquarters, administrative and program staff and consultants for multiple private foundations and charitable  trusts.  We specialize in offering customized levels of grantmaking, administrative, financial and advisory services, providing donors with a cost-effective way to keep their overhead low while enhancing their ability to make a positive difference to the organizations they support and to the constituents they serve. Client interests vary across program categories, geographic areas and type and size of grants.

Name: Disabled Patriot Fund
Description:
The Disabled Patriot Fund was formed in 2005 by a group of business people and government officials who came together because of a common interest: to help disabled American veterans who have fought in the War on Terrorism. As our mission statement explains, our common goal is to "provide financial relief for local U.S. military families who have been adversely affected by the War on Terror", and we are asking for your support.

Men and women who are protecting our freedom are coming home with varying levels of disabilities and may receive minimal financial assistance from existing governmental agencies. We hope that you feel, as we do, that they deserve our support. What better reason to give back to than to help those within our communities who have sacrificed so much?

Name: Rainbow New Zealand Charitable Trust
Description:
The Rainbow NZ Charitable Trust is the cheerleader, advocate, and nurturing partner of Aotearoa's Rainbow communities.

The Trust raises and donates funds to LGBTTQIA+ led community organisations, Pride events and activities across Aotearoa, provides scholarships to young LGBTTQIA+ students completing tertiary education and orchestrator of GiveOUT Day, the annual peer to peer rainbow giving day.

Over 25 years, the Rainbow NZ Charitable Trust has distributed over $720,000 with grants to over 130 organisations and through giving over 80 scholarships to our rainbow community leaders of the future.

Learn more on our website: https://rainbownzcharitabletrust.org.nz/what-we-fund/

Name: API Armenia Peace Initiative
Description:
Armenia Peace Initiative est un fonds de dotation qui a pour but d’aider et promouvoir tout type d'action favorisant une paix durable et harmonieuse au Caucase du Sud, et particulièrement en Arménie. Créé en avril 2020 en France, Armenia Peace Initiative se déploie sur deux axes : renforcer la sécurité, préparer la paix.
www.armeniapeace.org

Name: ASEDER - Associação para Saúde e Desenvolvimento Rural
Winter Park Health Foundation
Description:
The Winter Park Health Foundation (WPHF) is a private, not-for-profit organization working to inspire healthy communities in Winter Park, Maitland and Eatonville, Florida. It also conducts research and provides education on health issues affecting youth, older adults and the community-at-large.

A long-trusted leader on health care issues, the Foundation serves as a catalyst for information gathering, research and collaboration on health issues impacting Central Florida.

Our mission is to make a positive difference in people’s lives by creating the healthiest community in the United States.

Name: Mija, Yes You Can.
Description:
Mija, Yes you can Mija, Yes you can is a 501(c)3 non-profit organization and a proud featured fund for The Paso Del Norte Community Foundation’s Community of Philanthropy. 

Our Vision: To unite, encourage and remind women and young girls that an equal world is possible.

Our Mission: We empower and advocate for women of all ages, races, ethnicities, social-economic status, sexual and gender identities to achieve gender equality in all aspects of life.

We aim to provide resources and education to women and girls in order to help them achieve their ambitions in life.

We achieve this empowerment by focusing on our main pillars: Education, Wellness, Mentorship, Development, Philanthropy.

Name: Operation Sacred Promise
Description: OSP started on August 16, 2021 as an ad-hoc group of volunteers – American Service Members working around the clock to help our Afghan partners escape the Taliban. Working across the globe, we have since helped these partners navigate the bureaucratic pathways to legal residence in the United States and work toward securing housing, employment, and education. OSP members represent every branch of the U.S. Armed Forces and include dedicated civilian volunteers with specialized experience in immigration law, public affairs, and humanitarian aid.

Name: Niswarth The Selfless
Description:
निःस्वार्थ ( Niswarth): The Selfless, is a registered Non-Governmental Organisation (NGO) which was founded in August 2018. Niswarth: the Selfless, is the community of people who work for the betterment of society without expecting something in return and hence got its name.
We believe that if one works for impacting someone’s life positively, then one’s existence becomes meaningful because everybody live for themselves but those who live and work for others make a difference.
Our efforts are majorly focused on various social issues which need attention and action in order to create a better society for us and our future generations. We work in various domains like Healthcare, Education and Hunger Eradication along with Environmental issues. We work to promote awareness among the masses to lead a healthy lifestyle.
Currently, our team is working on spreading awareness about Delhi’s air pollution and its perilous effects on the city. It’s high time that we step up and make a difference.


Our Campaigns  so far :

°Providing healthy food to slum children and homeless senior citizens. We have organized three food donation drive in 2018: 
August 19, at Sikenderpur metro station, Gurugram. 
September 02, at Sikenderpur metro station, Gurugram. 
October 15, at Nathupur shills slum area, Gurugram. 
°Tree Plantation Drive conducted on September 18, 2018
°Winter Clothes/ Blanket distribution to the homeless during December 2018. 
°Blood Donation Campaign also in December 2018.
°Campaign on Spreading awareness of Delhi's Air Pollution in January 2019.
°Dental awareness camp and Phullo wali Holi Celebration with Praya's shelter home Girls! on March 20, 2019.

Name: Vida Volunteer
Description:
Vida Volunteer is a 501c3 registered nonprofit humanitarian organization with offices in the US and Central America. We promote human and animal health, through Service-Learning experiences, that bringing together the efforts of communities, students, and local healthcare systems. 

Vision:
To become leaders in the Health Promotion Movement through a service-learning experience in Central America.

Mission Statement:
To promote human and animal health by bringing together the efforts of communities, students, and local healthcare systems.

Slogan:
Travel with purpose, learn through service

Name: Fundación Lukas
Description:
Fundación Lukas es una organización sin ánimo de lucro de ámbito nacional que nace en 2009 con el objetivo de mejorar la calidad de vida de las personas con discapacidad múltiple severa y la de sus familias a través de a través de modelos asistenciales innovadores como son una vivienda tutelada asistida, un centro de terapias,  ocio inclusivo y programas de respiro familiar.

FUNDACIÓN LUKAS, AYUDA A LA DISCAPACIDAD está inscrita en el Registro de Fundaciones Asistenciales del Ministerio de Educación bajo el número 28-1564 con fecha 9 de junio de 2009; y está acreditada por la Fundación Lealtad.

Name: African Potential
Description:
At African Potential, we specialize in solving today’s multifaceted and critical challenges through innovative, community-driven approaches. As a public benefit organization, we thrive in complexity, transforming crises into opportunities by leveraging technology, multidisciplinary expertise, and cross-sector collaboration.

Our beneficiaries are not just recipients of aid—they are partners in the co-creation of sustainable solutions that make a lasting impact.
Our Approach
•	Visionary Thinking: We uncover opportunities where others see obstacles and take strategic action when challenges seem insurmountable.
•	Collaborative Innovation: By engaging communities and stakeholders, we foster bottom-up solutions that address the root causes of problems, ensuring scalable and meaningful impact.

Operating Model

1.	Complex Problem Solving: We tackle high-risk, high-reward challenges with community-centric, systems-driven solutions.
2.	Systems Design Thinking: Building networks of practice around each problem to ensure sustainable and adaptive interventions.
3.	Value Chain Mapping: We engage stakeholders to co-create value propositions that drive investment in transformative solutions.
4.	Ecosystem Building: By uniting decision-makers, innovators, and institutions across civil society, government, and academia, we create pathways for scalability and impact.
5.	Holistic Focus Areas: Our interconnected solutions address health, economic development, technology, and environmental sustainability.
6.	Continuous Improvement: Clear metrics, adaptive frameworks, and collaborative feedback loops ensure the effectiveness and growth of our initiatives.

Our Commitment
We believe that with the right partnerships, resources, and mindset, no problem is too complex to solve. Join us as we redefine what’s possible, unlocking the potential of communities and ecosystems for a better, more equitable future.

Name: Endangered Species Foundation New Zealand
Description: The Endangered Species Foundation of New Zealand (ESFNZ) is a registered charitable organisation supporting high-priority conservation projects that protect New Zealand’s most vulnerable indigenous species and habitats from extinction. We aim to save the 'Rarest of the Rare'.

Name: Fund for Lake Michigan
Description: The mission of the Fund for Lake Michigan is to support efforts, and in particular those in southeastern Wisconsin, that enhance the health of Lake Michigan, its shoreline and tributary river systems for the benefit of the people, plants and animals that depend upon the system for water, recreation and commerce.

Name: Bakala Foundation
Description:
We are a Czech family foundation established in 2007 by Michaela and Zdeněk Bakala. Our goal is to help talented youth get a high-quality education so they can realize their dreams and improve society.

The Bakala Foundation is unique for the breadth of its support for education and other programs for talented young people. Through our own programs and in cooperation with independent partners in the Czech Republic and abroad, we also support the values of freedom, democracy, human rights, and independent, professional journalism.

Name: The Robin Hood Foundation
Description:
MANIFIESTO
Cada día millones de ciudadanos y de ciudadanas ven negados sus derechos como consecuencia de la pobreza y la exclusión social en la que viven. Millones de niños y niñas mueren como consecuencia del hambre y de enfermedades prevenibles, no tienen acceso al agua potable, a saneamiento básico y a un entorno medioambiental seguro, no acuden regularmente a la escuela y tienen limitado el acceso a la cultura y a sus tradiciones.

Frente a esta grave situación que afecta a más de la mitad de los niños y las niñas de todo el mundo, existe una realidad muy distinta: diariamente la economía internacional, crecientemente globalizada, genera beneficios inimaginables hace unas décadas, nuevos avances tecnológicos y científicos, que nos deslumbran a diario, mejoran la vida de unos pocos.

Como consecuencia de lo anterior, vivimos por tanto en un mundo cada vez más desigual donde los ricos son cada vez más ricos y los pobres más pobres y excluidos. Y en esta situación los más vulnerables siempre son los niños y las niñas.

Como ciudadanos comprometidos y convencidos de que Otro Mundo es Posible, tenemos que unir nuestros esfuerzos para cambiar la realidad y conseguir que la pobreza que afecta a la infancia sea un hecho del pasado. Es una situación que podemos y tenemos que erradicar lo antes posible.

Unamos nuestros ESFUERZOS para:

Reclamar que se cumplan todos los compromisos asumidos por la comunidad internacional en la lucha contra la pobreza, especialmente los que se exponen en la Declaración del Milenio y los Objetivos del Desarrollo del Milenio.

Lograr que se modifiquen las reglas del comercio internacional para que realmente sea una estrategia al servicio de la lucha contra la pobreza.

Demandar a los gobiernos y demás acreedores de los países ricos que hagan todo lo posible para que se condone la deuda externa, deuda impagable por los países pobres. Reclamar que los gobiernos de los países pobres asuman sus esponsabilidades e inviertan el canje de la deuda en servicios sociales básicos.

Los gobiernos, especialmente los países ricos, incrementen la Ayuda Oficial al Desarrollo en al menos 50.000 millones de dólares adicionales anuales; y que los países aporten cuanto antes al menos el 0,7 % del PIB a la cooperación al desarrollo. Esta cooperación debe ser de calidad.

The Robin Hood Association, como red de personas vinculadas a la cultura y el arte consideramos que avanzar en la lucha contra la pobreza significa crear sociedades ricas culturalmente y capaces de incluir a todos.

Así apostamos por la cultura como motor de desarrollo en favor de los niños y las niñas, aportando igualdad de oportunidades a los niños y las niñas de todo el mundo que ven conculcados sus derechos.

Por ello, The Robin Hood Association se une para ampliar mensajes que sensibilicen a la población española sobre las causas y las consecuencias que inciden en la dramática situación que afecta a la infancia y, nos sentimos comprometidos a promover y apoyar programas de cooperación al desarrollo en los que la promoción de la cultura sea el eje vertebrador capaz de superar situaciones de pobreza extrema.

Necesitamos que te sumes a nosotros y nosotras.

Únete a The Robin Hood Association, suma tu ESFUERZO en favor de un mundo mejor para todos los niños y las niñas del mundo.

Name: CATHOLIC WORLD MISSION
Description: Catholic World Mission is recognized in the Official Catholic Directory and affiliated with the Legionaries of Christ and Regnum Christi. Our work extends the missionary outreach of the Catholic Church by empowering local Church leaders, missionaries, and lay faithful to bring Christ’s light to communities around the world. Everything we do flows from our mission to spread the Catholic faith and strengthen the Church’s presence where it is most needed.

Name: Angel Xpress Foundation
Description:
Angel Xpress Foundation (AXF), a citizen led movement to bridge the social divide.

*What is AXF?*
A 12A & 80G certified NGO, AXF enables educated & committed citizens to tutor, mentor & guide school going-children from slums in their neighbourhoods.   Free learning centers are set up in community spaces like parks where volunteers meet students for an hour everyday. 
From 18 children on the day the movement was launched in 2012, by 2019 AXF had
- 700+ volunteers                                        
- 2200+ students 
- 20 Centres across Mumbai

*Who volunteers?*
Stay-at-home mums, retired citizens, students, & professionals who can spare 2-3 hrs/week

*What do they teach?*
- English, Math, Lifeskills for grades 2 to 8, School curriculum (SSC/HSC) for students upto Grade12
- AXF YEP (Youth Engagement) offers
Career & academic counseling, mentoring & job readiness workshops for job readiness (spoken English, Digital literacy, softskills & a scholarship program giving bright students from poor homes a real shot at success. 

*Covid response at AXF*
- Monthly Ration/Cash aid for student families & other individuals & families in distress from April to August
- Student Volunteer interaction shifted online to ensure continued learning
- Smartphone redistribution drive & sponsoring of datapacks for students, distribution of text books & notebooks. 

*How can you help?*

Sign up to volunteer - we need people who can coach students  from Hindi/Marathi medium, (8th grade & above) in Math, Science, Languages etc. Sign up 
https://tinyurl.com/ycd3nx3a

Nearly 1400 students attend online classes. Many unable to join/be regular due to lack of access to a smartphone device, ability to afford datapacks. 

To donate a device drop us a message at 9820244562. 

Donate for datapacks/phones: https://milaap.org/fundraisers/support-educating-underprivileged

List of centers & contact details: https://angelxpress.org/contact

Website: www.angelxpress.org

Name: Dogs For Our Brave
Description:
Our mission is to provide service dogs, at no cost, to veterans who have suffered catastrophic injuries.

We're looking for veterans who are suffering from traumatic physical injuries, such as loss of limbs, and whose lives would be improved by the addition of a highly trained service dog.

We provide professionally trained service dogs for each veteran's specific needs. No service member is ever charged. All costs, including training, food, equipment and veterinary care, are covered by the program.

To apply, visit our website at www.DogsForOurBrave.com and click on the APPLY TODAY button at the top of the page.

We're dedicated to making life better for our veterans, and we're proud of our mission. Our goal is to save two lives at the same time—train a rescued dog to provide assistance to a veteran in need.

NO SERVICE MEMBER IS EVER CHARGED. 100% of all costs, including rescue, training, dog food and care, medications and daily care are covered by the program.

Name: Manchester Young Professionals - NH
Description: MYP seeks to foster a community where young professionals lead, create, innovate and influence their surroundings. We are the next generation of leaders who will impact Greater Manchester's future!

Name: Alihsan Charity Association
Description: تأسست جمعية الإحسان الخيرية عام 1990م، بجهود فردية، وتم إشهارها رسمياً في مرحلتها الثانية في عام 1998م بإمارة عجمان تحت اسم "مركز الإحسان الخيري" بمكرمة سامية من صاحب السمو الشيخ حميد بن راشد النعيمي عضو المجلس الأعلى حاكم عجمان.

Name: Smile House Fondazione ETS
Description: Smile House Fondazione ETS accompagna bambini e giovani adulti nati con malformazioni cranio-maxillo-facciali verso interventi chirurgici gratuiti e cure mediche specialistiche, garantendo loro il percorso di assistenza individuale di cui hanno bisogno.

Name: The Simlai Foundation
Description: Because we believe individuals’ skills and expertise have a tremendous impact on nonprofits’ success, we grant them with the best in-kind donation: professional services! With us, talented professionals have the opportunity to solve Israeli social challenges by offering their skills, knowledge, and talent to nonprofits.

Name: Africa Renewal Ministries
Description:
ABOUT US:
Founded in 1990, ARM is a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization with the vision of developing & equipping generations of transformational Christian leaders

OUR VISION:
Generations of Transformational Christian Leaders

OUR MISSION:
Developing & Equipping Generations of Leaders through ARM Initiatives, Churches & Other Strategic Partnerships for Holistic Societal Transformation. 

WHAT WE DO:
We plant and develop churches.
We provide holistic care for  vulnerable children.
We inspire, identify and equip emerging Christian leaders for the transformation of society.


COMPANY INFORMATION:
Africa Renewal Ministries, founded in 1990, is a non-governmental organization located in Uganda, East Africa with the vision of developing and equipping Generations of Transformational Christian Leaders. ARM currently cares for 7,500+ sponsored students and 200 churches. 

In addition to being registered as an NGO in Uganda, ARM is also registered as a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization in the United States of America. Therefore, all Donations made to Africa Renewal Ministries are tax-deductible in the USA.

SPONSORSHIP INFORMATION:
Please submit sponsor-related questions to Sheryl in our USA Office. She can be reached at: armusa@africarenewal.org or by phone at: 1 (210) 979-7441.

Name: The Kern Family Foundation
Description:
The Robert D. and Patricia E. Kern Family Foundation empowers the rising generation of Americans to lead flourishing lives.

In 1959, Robert D. and Patricia E. Kern founded what became Generac Power Systems, one of the world’s largest manufacturers of complete engine-driven power generator systems. After selling a division of Generac in 1998, the Kerns established the Kern Family Foundation. The Foundation focuses on efforts that have long-term, systemic impact. 

The Kern Family Foundation does not accept unsolicited proposals.

Name: The October Club
Description:
In 1987, moved by the plight of Leukaemia sufferers, a few of us working in the City decided to hold a fundraising dinner at The Savoy. This proved so successful that we decided it should become an annual event every October - hence The October Club.

For over 30 years our committee have chosen a small UK charity for which the funds raised will prove transformational.

We have now raised just over £10 million with substantial benefits to the charities we have supported including kick-starting groundbreaking research, raising profiles and enabling brave expansion plans. It is our goal to help innovative fledgling charities reach that next level. 

The October Club Dinner, attended by approximately 400 largely City-based individuals, is the City’s Equity Business “Trade association event”.  A pre-sold out event every year, previously attended by HRH The Duke of Cambridge, to receive an invitation is something quite special.  

The dinner is also complemented by The October Club Ascot Race Day, held in July, a much welcomed warm-up to the main event.

We are enormously grateful to all those who support our efforts – whether by sponsorship, advertising in our programmes, donating prizes for the auction, attending the evening, or giving generously.

Name: Caritas-Gemeinschaftsstiftung im Bistum Limburg
Description:
Die Caritas-Gemeinschaftsstiftung im Bistum Limburg wurde im Jahr 2005 gegründet. Der Caritasverband für die Diözese Limburg e. V. hat die Caritas-Gemeinschaftsstiftung im Bistum Limburg als Dachstiftung konzipiert, was heißt: Man kann sich nicht nur finanziell in der Dachstiftung engagieren, vielmehr auch eine eigene Stiftung mit eigenem thematischen Schwerpunkt gründen, die von der Caritasstiftung treuhänderisch verwaltet wird.

Ziel der Caritasstiftung ist, caritatives und soziales Engagement im Bistum Limburg finanziell und ideell mit Hilfe der Stiftungserträge und entsprechendem Wissen zu unterstützen. Dies war auch das Anliegen unseres Schirmherrn Bischof em. Dr. Franz Kamphaus, dessen Leitmotiv schon jeher eine "Option für die Armen" war.

Als rechtsfähige, gemeinnützige, kirchliche Stiftung ist die Caritasstiftung staatlicherseits anerkannt und unterliegt zudem der Kontrolle durch die Stiftungsaufsicht. Transparenz und Kontrolle sind eines unserer Leitmotive, deshalb lassen wir jährlich freiwillig einen Jahresabschluss von einem unabhängigen Wirtschaftsprüfer erstellen und haben uns zusätzlich der Initiative Transparente Zivilgesellschaft angeschlossen.

Weiterhin ist unsere Finanzanlagepolitik charakterisiert durch ethische Werte und eine optimale Mischung von renditeorientierten und substanzerhaltenden Werten - denn wir wollen das Vermögen verantwortungsvoll vermehren, um mehr finanzielle Mittel für unsere caritative Arbeit zu erhalten.

Unsere Caritasstiftung orientiert sich überdies an den "Grundsätzen guter kirchlicher Stiftungspraxis", die Sie in der gleichnamigen Publikation nachlesen können.

Im März 2020 wurde die vormals benannte "Caritasstiftung in der Diözese Limburg" in "Caritas-Gemeinschaftsstiftung im Bistum Limburg" umbenannt. 

Herkunftsnachweis Header-Bild:
Lizenzfreie (Shutterstock) Stockfoto-Nummer: 1474278002
Fotograf: Henryk Sadura

Name: Human Act
Description: Human Act is a foundation with a vision to eliminate extreme poverty. We advocate for a global wealth tax.

Name: Turner Foundation Inc
Description:
Mission

The mission of the Turner Foundation (TFI) is to protect and restore the natural systems – air, land, and water – on which all life depends.

Guiding Principles

•Reflect the entrepreneurial spirit of our Founder and Chairman Ted Turner by our willingness to be bold and take risks towards our priorities;


•Leverage partnerships to maximize the impact of TFI, such as partnerships with other foundations and collaborations between nonprofit organizations, communities, businesses and government;


•Break down the silos between our issue areas and prioritize initiatives with cross-cutting benefits, with a particular focus on city leadership and agriculture initiatives; and


•Maintain a diverse “tool box” of approaches to philanthropy.

Name: Octobre Rose en Nord
Description:
Chaque année, en octobre, le collectif Octobre Rose en Nord organise des événements afin de lever des fonds pour soutenir un projet en lien avec la lutte contre le cancer du sein.

2022 en est la première édition !
Il ne faut pas oublier que la région Hauts-de-France est la région la plus touchée par le cancer en France et que le cancer du sein touche plus de 60 000 femmes par an, soit 1 femme sur 8.

Ce collectif d’entreprises engagées au service d’une cause solidaire a pour objectif de récolter le maximum de fonds possible et ainsi permettre à différents projets de voir le jour.

Name: Altruisme Efficace France
Description: L’altruisme efficace est une démarche qui combine l’empathie et la réflexion : il s’agit d’identifier les  moyens d’agir qui ont le plus d'impact positif et de les mettre en pratique, en se basant sur une approche rationnelle et des données empiriques.

Name: Turing Foundation
Description:
The Turing Foundation is a private charity based in Amsterdam, Holland. It was established in 2006 by Pieter and Francoise Geelen, using the wealth Pieter Geelen acquired being the co-founder of TomTom NV, a company listed at the Dutch stock exchange. 

The Turing Foundation spends its funds on behalf of four goals: to enable and facilitate education for children around the world; to let people in the Netherlands enjoy art; to protect nature and to fight leprosy and aid its victims.

Name: Paani Project
Description: Paani is a 501(c)3 nonprofit that works with community partners and institutions in the United States and Pakistan to drive sustainable, evidence-based interventions that primarily focus on alleviating clean water inaccessibility, advocating for gender equity, and addressing a variety of pressing health disparities.

Name: Night Owl Reconnaissance
Description: Night Owl Reconnaissance is a 501(c)3 non-profit organization with the purpose to contribute to the detection and prevention of causes that lead to missing individuals, assisting in investigations of missing persons, and offering victim assistance resources to those recovered.

Name: Nadace J&T
Description:
Jsme tady proto, abychom  individuálně pomáhali rodinám s dětmi, které aktivně čelí nepřízni osudu. Poskytujeme finanční, materiální pomoc a poradenství.

Na základě našich zkušeností z terénu usilujeme o systémové změny v prevenci a péči o ohrožené rodiny.

Nadace poskytuje příspěvky jak fyzickým, tak právnickým osobám - neziskovým organizacím, pokud jejich projekty souvisí s posláním a cíli nadace. Pomoc nadace směřuje především k ohroženým dětem za účelem prohloubení všestranné péče o ně a sociálně slabým rodinám, lidem nemocným a s handicapem. Jednou z priorit nadace je rozvoj náhradní rodinné péče. Podrobné informace o výši podpory v jednotlivých oblastech se dočtete ve výročních zprávách.

Name: IdeasTap
Description:
Creative inspiration and support.

IdeasTap helps creative people like you get to where you want to be. We're a charity that works with leading arts organisations to create industry opportunities for our members. Join today for free. You'll get access to funding, competitions, arts jobs, training and advice. 

www.ideastap.com

Name: Alive Foundation
Description:
We believe that real change starts with education.
This is why Alive Foundation is committed to supporting educational development through its own initiatives and partnerships with local NGOs.

Our main mission is to prevent school dropout by implementing concrete and impactful programs that:
• Promote the integration of children with developmental or behavioural challenges
• Organize educational, cultural, and artistic activities
• Support exchange programs with similar organizations
• Modernize and improve educational facilities

Through these actions, we aim to ensure equal opportunities, strengthen communities, and build a brighter future for every child.
Join us in making change happen through education.

Name: Sales Memes
Description: Bringing positivity in a tough time through Memes!

Name: GiveInternet.org
Description: At GiveInternet.org you can sponsor Internet fees and laptops for students in need from developing countries. As our donor, you'll receive monthly updates on how your money was spent, who you helped and how you changed their lives.

Name: WTS-DC Chapter
Description:
For over 40 years, WTS-DC has continued to provide opportunities for transportation professionals to connect with one another and grow in their careers. Founded in 1977, our membership includes planners, engineers, landscape architects, policy makers, government leaders, communications and outreach professionals, and students committed to promoting the WTS mission of attracting, sustaining, connecting, and advancing women's careers to strengthen the transportation industry. 

WTS-DC is one of the largest WTS chapters—is a trendsetting, vibrant chapter that provides outstanding advantages to its members through exceptional speakers who are leaders in the industry, pioneering programs, leadership training, high school and university outreach, and mentoring and professional development.

Name: US Peace Corps
Description: Revisited Peace Corp with Peace Corp Morocco

Name: Pacific Vision Foundation
Description:
Pacific Vision Foundation (PVF) is dedicated to improving health equity in eye health and vision in collaboration with a community of physicians and other partners providing care and training. In 2020, PVF opened Pacific Vision Surgery Center, a brand-new 2 Operatory Room Ambulatory Surgery Center (ASC) in our Eye Institute building, to ensure more access to cataract, glaucoma and retina surgeries for patients from Federally Qualified Health Centers (FQHCs) referred to our partner Lions Eye Foundation (LEF)/CPMC/Sutter Clinic, which in turn refers patients to PVSC who need surgery, at no cost to them.

For more than 40 years PVF has supported the physicians and researchers providing care to the under-served and has provided support services for the  Department of Ophthalmology at California Pacific Medical Center (CPMC) Residency Program. Our work ensures the next generation of eye care specialists have the expertise they need to build thriving careers, while helping some of the most vulnerable members of our community gain access to sight-saving surgeries.

PVF is proud to play a significant role in leading edge training and research, and direct line to access to care as we work towards equity in eye health.

Name: More Than Champs Foundation (MTC)
Description:
More Than Champs Foundation is an educational foundation committed to bettering lives through education. Our specific focuses and goals for educational foundations vary. Our aim to explain education from a variety of disciplinary perspectives, including philosophy, sport history, sociology, arts, science, and comparative and international studies.

More coming soon.

Name: Ad Relief of Greater Los Angeles
Description:
Ad Relief of Greater Los Angeles (formerly Advertising Industry Emergency Fund) is a volunteer organization made up of advertising and promotions people brought together to help their colleagues in times of life crisis. 

If you or an industry peer has an accident or experiences some form of life crisis, is unable to work and can't afford outstanding bills—and have exhausted all personal assets — Ad Relief is there. Our reason to exist is to lend a hand and help people get back on their feet again.

Since 1972, the Ad Relief has granted more than two million dollars in emergency money to individuals in the Greater Los Angeles advertising and promotions community. Confidential loans may also be arranged for qualified individuals who have been employed or consulted in the industry for at least one year, absent from the industry for no longer than one year. Priority is given to medical emergencies, but other emergency situations will also be considered. 

We’ve helped hundreds of people over the years, and none of them ever thought they would need Ad Relief. Don’t be afraid, and try not to worry; your dignity and confidentiality are our utmost concern. We care about our colleagues in need and want to help.

All contributions are tax deductible to the fullest extent of the law. AIEF is a 501(C)3 organization. Our Fed. ID # is 23-7236394.

Name: ANAK-Tnk
Description:
Since 1998, we have helped the most unfortunate children get off the streets and out of the slums of Manila by reaching out to them and catering to their basic needs (shelter, education, nutrition and health).
Our mission is to bring back the smiles and dignity to Manila’s most unfortunate children. 

- Education : In the Philippines, one out of ten children does not receive any education. We enable and support the education of every child, from the moment he joins the foundation until he becomes independent. 

- Nutrition : One out of ten children is malnourished and one out of three suffers from growth retardation. We provide children with balanced meals daily and we train local communities to raise awareness on nutrition issues. 

- Health 40% of the urban population lives in conditions of extreme poverty. We cater to the medical needs. 

- Protection Drugs, violence, rape, prostitution: children are always the first victims. We support children in need with psychological and legal help.

Name: Berkshire Community Foundation
Description:
Berkshire Community Foundation is Berkshire's charitable hub: connecting people who care with causes that matter. 
For almost 40 years we have worked across Berkshire to raise, then distribute money directly to vital local causes. We are part of a network of 47 accredited Community Foundations across the UK, affiliated to the UK Community Foundations membership organisation.  

Every year we award grants to a wide range of local projects and organisations. We provide advice, networking and skills support, and raise the profile of local charities and community groups addressing vital needs across Berkshire, including child poverty, homelessness, physical and mental health and isolation. 

We aim to ensure our grant-making contributes to the UN's Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs). A large part of our work goes towards tackling key SDGs at local level. 

We work alongside philanthropic individuals, businesses and organisations to raise funds and guide giving decisions, helping to maximise the positive impact of their contributions on local communities.

Name: Humanity Healing International
Description:
Mission Statement

When you look at the view of Earth from space, you are immediately in awe of what you do see: the incredible beauty of Creation. It is not until you look closer that you realize what you do not see. You do not see lines partitioning countries. You do not see separation between races. You do not see division between religions. You do not see the walls people build to isolate themselves from each other.

There is just One Planet. There is only One Humanity.

The Humanity Healing Foundation is a humanitarian, nonpolitical, nondenominational spiritual organization promoting Spiritual Activism as a means to foster Healing for communities around the world that have little or no Hope.

Spiritual Activism is expressed by Compassion in Action.

We believe that when we help others through compassionate action, we Heal ourselves and through us, Humanity as a whole.

Our Mission is to seek out and identify specific projects worldwide and to implement definable and sustainable solutions. Our initiatives are not broad mandates to eradicate poverty, end famine, or stop violence in war-torn countries. Neither are they overnight cures. They are specific projects created to restore Hope in the lives of children and communities that have lost it.

We are Children of the same Universe. We are all Seeds of Humanity.

Name: Operation Broken Silence
Description: Operation Broken Silence is dedicated to Sudanese communities, cultivating resilience and driving meaningful change through crowdfunded programs.

Name: Fondation ALIMA
Description:
La Fondation ALIMA s’engage pour le développement d’un écosystème humanitaire médical plus efficace et plus localisé en Afrique. Elle agit pour cela dans deux domaines confrontés à l’insuffisance de l’aide internationale publique et privée :
