- la formation de cadres humanitaires issus des pays d’intervention d’ALIMA
- la recherche dédiée à l’amélioration de la médecine humanitaire et permettant de trouver des solutions innovantes et des nouveaux traitements adaptés aux besoins des populations locales.

La démarche des fondateurs s’inscrit dans la philosophie et l’approche portée par l’association ALIMA, l’Alliance pour l’Action Médicale Internationale. Créée en 2009, cette ONG médicale humanitaire porte un nouveau modèle qui renouvelle la pratique humanitaire en combinant des compétences locales avec l’expertise scientifique internationale.

La Fondation ALIMA a été créée en novembre 2020  par ses sept co-fondateurs, elle est abritée par la Fondation de France.

Name: Mobile Voting
Description: In late 2017, Tusk Philanthropies launched a campaign for Mobile Voting to increase voter turnout by making voting more convenient and accessible. This nonprofit, nonpartisan initiative is working to expand voting options across the country by adding mobile voting for all voters.

Name: United Way of Racine County
Description:
United Way of Racine County mobilizes the community to action so all can thrive. By partnering with individuals and organizations, we fund local programs, special projects and our own initiatives to build an educated workforce in Racine County.

To learn how you can join us, visit
UnitedWayRacine.org or call 262-898-2240.

Name: BID Services
Description:
BID Services is a charity which works to remove barriers and deliver services which enable and empower those who are D/deaf, hard of hearing, sight impaired, deafblind or have other disabilities to live full lives

Our specialist service areas include Advocacy, Employment, British Sign Language Interpreting and Training, Rehabilitation and Mobility Training, Specialist Equipment, Residential Care, Social Work, Support for Tinnitus and Housing Related Services. 

Our sister organisation, the Deaf Cultural Centre is the UK’s first Deaf Cultural Centre supporting a range of community and art activities, as well as providing high quality conference, meeting and serviced office space, through our social enterprise brand ‘The Signing Tree Venue’.

Name: Stiftungsnetzwerk Region Stuttgart e.V.
Description:
Als Zusammenschluss von Stiftungen aus Stuttgart und der Region sowie von Freunden des Stiftungswesens gehen wir gemeinsam innovative Themen an, bearbeiten neue Ideen und fördern innovative Projekte. Jede einzelne Stiftung ist bei uns eingeladen, Themen einzubringen, sich Arbeitspartner*innen zu suchen und die dafür passende Form zu finden. 

Unsere Mitglieder des Stiftungsnetzwerks Region Stuttgart sind davon überzeugt, dass Projekte, die Veränderungen anstoßen und Wirkung erzielen, am effektivsten auf der Basis von Kooperationen durchgeführt und gefördert werden. Ein weiterer Vorteil: Neben dem Wissenstransfer können auch finanzielle Ressourcen gebündelt werden.

Name: Paint a Smile Foundation
Description:
2020 = 20 years = 200 projects = 20 countries : will you join the change ?

Since 2000, Paint a Smile creates and offers colourful wall art to ease the suffering of children in hospitals, patients in specialized institunions and résidents in nursing homes worldwide. Custom made and based on medical directives, bare white environments become worlds of therapeutic tools to help aleviate the traumas of sometimes unfriendly care facilities. Medical staff, families and all patients benefit from our permanent tangible work. Mentally escaping the often harsh medical surroundings, stimulating sense memory, dedramatizing medical apparel, soothing and accompanying, our 20 years of experience have prooved the trusting value of our work for over a million patients and residents in 19 countries worldwide.

Name: ITALIANspaceWISE + Quaderno
Description: Come with me to discover Italian architecture, precious heritage and breathtaking landscapes —

Name: Palas Athena
Description: Nas suas 4 décadas e meia de atividade permanente, como organização da sociedade civil sem fins lucrativos, a Palas Athena presenciou e tornou-se palco de grandes transformações sociais, educativas e culturais, motivadas por sua vocação de contribuir para aprimorar a convivência humana por meio de programas que visam a sustentabilidade de redes de participação cidadã, nas quais tradição, inovação e transformação articulam-se para desenvolver o potencial criativo, inclusivo e solidário da sociedade brasileira.

Name: Child Liberation Foundation
Description:
The Child Liberation Foundation was founded in February 2017 with a passion and commitment to end child trafficking around the world. In 2023 we have expanded our efforts to not only doing child rescue missions but also in focusing on the rehabilitation and healing of childhood sexual trauma by providing tools, modalities, awareness and education.

Our mission is to liberate humanity from the horrors of child sex abuse by helping victims heal and preventing the trauma from ever being passed on.

Our Vision is that every child will feel safe, valued, loved, and nurtured within a family unit. We provide the tools for children to heal as well as adults who have suffered as a child.

Our core values are centered on the complete respect of all people and include:

Freedom – We believe all people, and especially children, should be free from slavery and entrapment.
Equality – We believe all people deserve to have the help they need to live a healthy and happy life.
Integrity – Truth heals, we will not be controlled by misaligned agendas, we stand firm in our mission to heal the world.
Respect – We respect the individual trauma and healing process of each survivor.
Love – Our foundation is built on the belief that all people deserve to feel supported and loved in a healthy, empowering way.

Name: Center for Arab American Philanthropy (CAAP)
Description: The Center for Arab American Philanthropy (CAAP) is a national community foundation. A program of ACCESS, CAAP’s objective is to unite and empower the Arab American community by demonstrating the impact of giving together. We improve communities by making grants to nonprofit organizations. We serve donors by providing them with smart tools and resources to carry out their philanthropic giving. We build leaders by educating and inspiring Arab Americans, including youth, to realize their potential for change. Most of all, we shape perceptions about Arab Americans by highlighting their generous heritage and exceptional contributions to society. Visit www.centeraap.org to learn more.

Name: Patrimonio de la Benefiencia Pública
Description:
El Patrimonio de la Benecencia Pública (PBP) es una institución autónoma y única en su género en México, que fortalece e integra las acciones de promoción, prevención, educación, investigación y atención en el cuidado de la salud.

MISIÓN: 
Contribuir a mejorar las condiciones de salud de la población en situación de vulnerabilidad, especialmente aquella de escasos recursos sin
cobertura de seguridad social, mediante el otorgamiento de ayudas funcionales en especie y apoyos a proyectos de co-inversión realizados por organizaciones de la sociedad civil en materia de prevención, atención,
educación e investigación de la salud.

VISIÓN: 
Ser el patrimonio de afectación que subsidiariamente al logro de los objetivos del Sistema Nacional de Salud, de manera oportuna y eciente en benecio de la población en situación de vulnerabilidad, especialmente aquella de escasos recursos sin cobertura de
seguridad social, mejorando así su calidad de vida.

Name: Fundação Piedoso
Description:
Fundação Piedoso created on April 19, 2012 is a non-profit and general interest corporate body with legal personality.

Fundação Piedoso is an institution of social solidarity whose social object is education, health and the execution of actions to support vulnerable people and families.

The Piedoso Foundation has its vision based on the objective of becoming the biggest partner of the Angolan state in terms of community development across the entire national territory in various fields of education, training and health, with a focus on actions to combat poverty and poverty. illiteracy.

The Piedoso Foundation aims at defining, implementing, developing, promoting and supporting social actions that aim to create the necessary conditions so that the needy children and young people of the Republic of Angola have better access to Education, Health, and technical- professional.

Name: Fundacja im. Abrahama Jakuba Sterna
Description: The Stern Foundation is aimed at fostering an understanding of the common history of Poland, Polish-Jews, and the United States in the development of science, technology and culture as platform for future cooperation.

Name: San Diego Seniors Community Foundation
Description:
We are approaching one of the largest demographic shifts in U.S. history. By the year 2030, one in five Americans will be 65 or older. In San Diego the population of adults 60 and up will skyrocket to nearly 1 million over the next decade. This influx will strain the already fragile network of existing services for seniors, increasing the risk of social isolation, loneliness and many other serious health concerns. 

What’s going to happen to our seniors? Who will care for them as they age? Are we prepared to accommodate this new generation of dynamic older adults?
At the San Diego Seniors Community Foundation, we are developing solutions to these questions. We are a not-for-profit 501(c)(3) dedicated to improving the quality of life for local seniors. 

We believe all seniors deserve access to the resources to thrive, and envision a world where the older population of San Diego is nurtured into a healthy, fulfilling lifestyle; where they are socially connected, physically active, and a vital element of our community; where they are no longer vulnerable, but vibrant. 

Through philanthropy, we can make this mission a reality, and ensure everyone has someone they can turn to and trust as they age.

Name: HH JAWAHER BINT KHALIFA AL KHALIFA YOUTH EMPOWERMENT FOUNDATION & INNOVATION CENTER
Description:
Lets make the world better together!

OUR VISION
We believe in the power of youth to make a difference in the world.

Our mission is to support the youth in order to create positive change towards more inclusive, fair and sustainable communities.

To help innovative entrepreneurs solve some of the problems commonly associated with running a startup by providing workspace, seed funding.

OUR MISSION
Empowering young people to reach the world through belief in their abilities and innovations.

Nurturing the energy of these young people and transforming it into positive actions that enable them to later serve their societies.

Finding solutions for youth issues through various awareness programs.

Sponsoring youth projects in different fields. Attending international conferences and events that are youth focused, whilst also participating and exchanging experience dialogues.

Name: Grantmakers of Western Pennsylvania
Description: Grantmakers of Western Pennsylvania (GWP) is a professional membership association formed in 1985 by leaders who wanted to create a place where grantmakers could learn and work together to increase their community impact. It is a nonprofit, tax-exempt organization serving 26 counties in western Pennsylvania. Today there are more than 80 member organizations from Armstrong County in the north to Fayette County in the south.

Name: ESC Sans Frontières
Description:
ESC Sans Frontières est l'association d'aide au développement de Neoma BS qui, depuis 1993, agit en faveur de l'éducation au Sénégal, à Madagascar et au Népal. Mais de nombreuses missions ont existé que ce soit au Cambodge, aux Philippines, au Pérou ou encore en Arménie.

Au Sénégal, ESCSF répond à deux problèmes majeurs: la difficulté d'accès aux fournitures scolaires dans les régions isolées et les prix trop élevés de ces dernières. ESCSF a créé 10 boutiques dans lesquelles nous vendons les fournitures au tiers du prix coûtant de leur production. L'objectif est également de s'intégrer dans le circuit économique sénégalais en démarchant les entreprises sénégalaises pour ensuite lancer la production des fournitures et les revendre dans les régions isolées.

Au Népal, ESC travaille avec l'association SHAKTI Népal depuis 2023, afin de sensibiliser des collégiens et lycéens aux droits des femmes dans le monde, à l'égalité des genres et à la sexualité. Nos volontaires préparent les supports des cours et les remettent à jour d'année en année voire les changent complètement comme ça a été le cas en 2024. À partir de 2025, nos volontaires vont également ajouter un chapitre sur l'environnement, à la demande du ministère de l'éducation népalais, à notre partenaire.

Également depuis 2023, la mission Madagascar a vu le jour en partenariat avec l'association 2400 Sourires. Nous intervenons dans le Village des Sourires, un village en cours de construction destinée à être auto-suffisant pour prendre en charge les enfants des rues d'Antananarivo. En trois ans de missions, nous avons eu des missions variées :
- Divers travaux de construction avec notamment des plantations sur des terres initialement vides
- aide dans la comptabilité du village
- apport de matériel de communication, tournages, et planification de la communication pour l'association
- Mise en place d'un dictionnaire dématérialisée de la langue des signes malgache
- Rédaction de dossier de subvention pour le village

Name: Outset Contemporary Art Fund (UK)
Description: Established in 2003, Outset Contemporary Art Fund is an international, independent charity pooling donations from patron circles and partners to support new art for the widest possible audiences. Present in 9 countries, it has raised over £8.5m worldwide to support the creative ecosystem. This spans education, professional development, the production of new work and exhibitions, institutional collecting, and initiatives that underpin the creative infrastructure for the long term. Outset is recognised for pioneering arts philanthropy through bespoke funding solutions. These include OFT, inviting curators from around the world to join the Tate’s curatorial team in selecting 100 works of art from Frieze for the Tate Collection; Studiomakers, providing entrepreneurial solutions for the creation of artist studios in partnership with the Mayor of London and others; and encouraging partnerships around the world to support the realisation of challenging art projects at defining biennials and international art destinations. Always keeping patrons, partners and professionals at its core, Outset creates unparalleled experiences for people curious to engage in dynamic artistic discourse.

Name: Bakul Foundation
Description: Bakul is a movement for volunteerism in India starting in the state of Odisha. It has been harnessing the volunteerism of individuals towards initiatives in Education, Environment and the Arts in addition to promoting volunteerism in itself.

Name: Woodcock Foundation
Description: The Woodcock Foundation is working toward a more equitable and sustainable world through strengthening our democracy, advancing educational and economic opportunity, and protecting our environment.

Name: Otto Bremer Trust
Description: The Otto Bremer Trust is a private charitable trust based in St. Paul, Minn. Created in 1944 by Otto Bremer, it is committed to supporting a better quality of life for residents of Minnesota, Montana, North Dakota, and Wisconsin. Since its founding, OBT has invested nearly $1 billion in people, places, and opportunities in its region.

Name: Arkansas Black Philanthropy Collaborative
Description: Powered by the Derek Lewis Foundation, the Arkansas Black Philanthropy Collaborative brings together a coalition of Black professionals to co-develop a shared transformational plan for Arkansas that is rooted in the aspirations, culture, and history of the Black community.

Name: Phillips Foundation
Description:
Phillips Foundation is a private family foundation and catalytic capital platform that leverages its assets to maximize social, environmental and financial value.

Following the entrepreneurial legacy of founder Kermit G. Phillips II, the Foundation conceived a first-of-its-kind Generational Grantmaking strategy in 2013, launched two original programs in 2016, and now leverages its full endowment for good through impact investing.

Generating meaningful social, environmental and financial outcomes in unison, the Foundation uses tools beyond traditional grantmaking for sustainable impact with its annual distribution and endowment. We seek only to profit from investments that “First, do no harm” and are aligned with the Foundation’s values.

Name: Fundación Haciendas del Mundo Maya
Description:
Somos una organización sin fines de lucro que trabaja con comunidades mayas de la Península de Yucatán. Implementamos proyectos de desarrollo económico y social enmarcados en nuestro Programa de Desarrollo Integral Comunitario. Promovemos la difusión, promoción y rescate de la riqueza natural y cultural maya, estableciendo la base para la generación de polos de desarrollo micro regional, impulsando estrategias para fortalecer las capacidades y habilidades de los habitantes de estas comunidades.


Desde 2002 promovemos la mejora de las condiciones de vida de comunidades rurales mayas en la Península de Yucatán mediante el desarrollo de base. Usamos esta metodología como el eje rector para el diseño e implementación de proyectos que promuevan el desarrollo humano de las personas a través de la construcción de capacidades y el empoderamiento de la población local. 

Actualmente, en Fundación Haciendas del Mundo Maya, hemos extendido nuestra intervención a  los estados de Yucatán, Campeche y Quintana Roo a través de un modelo holístico, con un abordaje interdisciplinario, que se estructura en nuestro Programa de Desarrollo Integral Comunitario, el cual de abarca 6 áreas estratégicas: Desarrollo Humano, Recursos Naturales, Vivienda e Infraestructura, Empresas Sociales, Educación y Salud.

Name: Rotary Club of Singapore
Description:
The Rotary Club of Singapore is Singapore's oldest and largest Rotary Club. Rotary is a global organization with 1.2 members and 35,000+ clubs across the globe. Together, we see a world where people unite and take action to create lasting change – across the globe, in our communities, and in ourselves.

The Rotary Club of Singapore just celebrated its 95th Anniversary in 2025 and is a vibrant club with over 160 diverse members who are business leaders and professionals across a wide range of industries and nationalities. They volunteer their time, talent, and treasure to solve pressing problems and support the community in Singapore, and around the world.

Each day, our members pour their passion, integrity, and intelligence into completing projects that have a lasting impact. We persevere until we deliver real, lasting solutions. Rotary members volunteer 16 million hours each year.

For more than 110 years, Rotary has bridged cultures and connected continents to champion peace, fight illiteracy and poverty, promote clean water and sanitation, and fight disease.

Are you an established professional who wants to make positive changes in your community and the world? Our club members are dedicated people who share a passion for community service and friendship. Becoming a Rotarian connects you with a diverse group that shares your drive to give back.

--- Your LinkedIn Admins 2025–2026 ---

Director of Public Image:
VJ Africa

Communications Strategy:
Sarah-Tabea Sammel

Name: «fit4future» foundation
Description:
Die fit4future foundation Schweiz setzt – ohne Reibungsverluste und Umwege – dort an, wo Engagement sinnvoll und notwendig ist. Im Zentrum der Stiftungsaktivitäten stehen Kinder und deren gesunde Entwicklung im Hauptprojekt «fit4future». 

In Kooperation mit zahlreichen Fachpartnern werden Projekte entwickelt. Bei verschiedenen Programmen zu mehr Bewegung, ausgewogener Ernährung und psychischer Gesundheit nehmen schweizweit knapp 165'000 Kids teil. Ob in Schulküchen, Turnhallen, Klassenzimmern, auf Sportplätzen, Skipisten oder Pausenplätzen: In über 350 Veranstaltungen pro Jahr werden spielerisch Impulse für einen aktiven und nachhaltig gesunden Lebensstil gesetzt. Lehrpersonen erhalten zudem pfannenfertige Unterlagen und wertvolle Unterstützung zur motivierenden Umsetzung von Gesundheitsförderung im Unterricht. Und auch die Eltern werden einbezogen: Mit Tipps und Tricks für den Alltag Zuhause unterstützen wir Mütter, Väter und Bezugspersonen.

Name: Fundación Padre Semería
Description:
Somos una organización sin fines de lucro que acoge en sus 3 residencias a cerca de 90 niños, niñas y adolescentes que han sido derivados por Tribunales de Familia por graves vulneraciones a sus derechos.
Trabajamos porque: "Queremos que nuestros niños, niñas y adolescentes, sin importar lo que hayan vivido antes, logren desarrollar sus potencialidades y ocupen el rol que ellos quieran/sueñen en la sociedad"

Name: Engenheiros Sem Fronteiras - Núcleo Juiz de Fora
Description:
Engenheiros Sem Fronteiras é uma ONG que utiliza conhecimentos de Engenharia e Arquitetura para melhorar a qualidade de vida de comunidades e indivíduos em situação de vulnerabilidade social. A organização está presente em 65 países e mais de 70 cidades brasileiras.
O núcleo Juiz de Fora foi fundado em 2015. Desde então, estudantes e graduados em engenharia e arquitetura já desenvolveram e executaram mais de 70 projetos de forma totalmente voluntária. Entre esses projetos, destacam-se os de usucapião, captação de água da chuva, reformas elétricas e revitalizações, além de diversas ações sociais e campanhas de arrecadação.

Name: Pincus Family Foundation
Description: Founded in 2005 by David and Gerry Pincus, the Pincus Family Foundation is driven with the intention of improving the lives of children in Philadelphia and abroad. We focus on supporting organizations and programs that promote children’s wellness, including education, safety, nutrition, recreation and the arts.

Name: Inner Wings
Description: Our mission is to build confidence in children aged 6 to 12, especially girls. We offer free programmes to primary schools to help young children to develop a growth mindset, achieve their unique potential and improve their public speaking skills.

Name: Philanthropic Planning Group of Greater New York (PPGGNY)
Description:
The Philanthropic Planning Group of Greater New York (PPGGNY) is a professional association for advocates of charitable giving. If you are a planned giving officer, fundraising professional, attorney, accountant, trust officer, or in an allied field in the areas of charitable giving, tax law, ethics, marketing or investments, we offer a wide range of networking and educational opportunities to widen your reach and raise your awareness of current issues in the world of philanthropy.

We offer educational and training opportunities designed to strengthen professional knowledge and provide innovative tools to better serve clients, donors, and the charitable foundations and organizations that connect us.

Membership in PPGGNY allows members to stay abreast of changing tax law and regulations that affect philanthropy and provide quick access to current charitable giving resources. Our luncheons, seminars, and programs are valuable sources of inspiration, motivation, and networking. Our speakers and educators are top industry leaders from across the nation. We encourage our members to be involved by volunteering their time and talent on various committees where ideas, skills, and experience will get you noticed.

Name: Chicana Latina Foundation
Description: The Chicana Latina Foundation is a non-profit organization that promotes professional and leadership development of Latinas.

Name: Christ's Haven For Children
Description: Christ’s Haven For Children is a 501c3 that provides a trauma-informed, family model of care for displaced children, teens, and young adults. As a voluntary placement organization, we are an option outside of traditional foster care, often when a caregiver has exhausted all efforts to care for a child.

Name: Women on a Mission
Description:
Women Beyond Limits is a non-profit mentorship and empowerment platform dedicated to helping women design purposeful careers and lives - and to driving meaningful change in vulnerable communities worldwide.
Through BOLD, all-female expeditions into some of the world’s most remote and extreme environments - from polar ice caps to the highest peaks and vast deserts - we raise awareness and critical funds for women impacted by violence, including survivors of war and conflict, human trafficking, and domestic abuse. These journeys are powerful symbols of courage and determination, inspiring resilience while highlighting the urgent need to support those who have faced unimaginable challenges.
Alongside expeditions, Women Beyond Limits champions mentorship, advocacy, and community building. Through personal mentorship, strategic storytelling, and global networks, we empower women to enrich their careers, explore new opportunities, and design purposeful portfolio paths. At the same time, we partner with organisations and grassroots initiatives to fund programmes that support survivors’ recovery and advance their economic empowerment, education, and healthcare.
By challenging ourselves physically, mentally, and emotionally, we also challenge societal barriers - unlocking opportunities for women everywhere to heal, thrive, and lead. Women Beyond Limits exists to amplify women’s voices, build solidarity across borders, and shape a world where every woman has the opportunity to rebuild her life and create lasting impact.

Name: Brightli Foundation
Description:
The Brightli Foundation is the philanthropic arm of Brightli, the parent company for Burrell Behavioral Health (and subsidiary Comprehensive Mental Health Services), Preferred Family Healthcare, Southeast Missouri Behavioral Health, Firefly Supported Living & Employment Services, Adult & Child Health, and Places for People.

Our mission is to support the Brightli system to increase access to high quality community-based healthcare across the Midwest through cultivating collaborative donor relationships. We believe that by finding innovative ways to bridge the gap to care created by financial constraints, we can help more people live their brightest lives.

Name: Executive Women Of The Palm Beaches Foundation
Description:
Executive Women of the Palm Beaches Foundation, Inc. (EWPBF) was founded in 1982 by senior professional and executive women in Palm Beach County, Florida, to support and advance women in business and community leadership. Today, EWPBF membership includes leaders in arts & culture, banking, business, construction, education, finance, government, healthcare, law, marketing, non-profit management, publishing, real estate, utilities, and many other business endeavors in Palm Beach County.

EWPBF is dedicated to inspiring women personally and professionally for the betterment of business and our community.

Our Vision is “To be the voice that inspires integrity, equality and leadership in the workplace and our community.” 

EWPBF strives to further the interests of and opportunities for executive and professional women to excel by providing opportunities for networking and collaboration, educational programs that address timely issues, mentorship, and the development of leadership and advocacy skills. In addition, our membership offers professional and volunteer expertise to numerous nonprofit organizations in our area. 

A significant fundraising event for our organization is the annual Cynthia Allen Gracey Women In Leadership Awards (WILA) Luncheon, the first event of its kind in Palm Beach County to recognize the leadership accomplishments of women in the public, private, volunteer and nonprofit sectors. Each year, proceeds from the annual WILA Luncheon benefit girls and women in Palm Beach County through college scholarships as well as the Lois Kwasman Program for Community Impact which provides grant funding for critical programs that change the lives of children, teens and young adults.

For more information on Executive Women of the Palm Beaches Foundation, Inc., please call visit our website at ewpb.org or call 561.868.7070.

Name: The Collins Foundation
Description: The Collins Foundation has been serving the communities of Oregon since 1947. As a family foundation, we take pride in generations of family service, each new generation working to keep pace with a rapidly changing Oregon to serve the state’s communities in the best possible way.  Demographic changes and growing disparities in Oregon, combined with an evolving awareness around issues of diversity, equity, and inclusion, inspired us to seek a deeper understanding of ourselves and others.

Name: We Trust You(th) Initiative
Description:
We Trust You(th) is an initiative that is co-led by five youth-led organizations from around the world. Together with other partners, we are here to challenge and support youth-focused donors and iNGOs to partner, fund, and support young people more intentionally and equitably.

A joint initiative by: CHOICE for Youth and Sexuality, Copper Rose Zambia, Green Girls Platform, IYAFP and ¿Y yo, por qué no?

Kickstarter funds provided by: FP2030

Name: Northwest Health Foundation
Description: NWHF is a nonprofit foundation. By making grants, partnering with community leaders and organizations, and advocating for policy change, we are furthering our vision of giving every person in Oregon and SW Washington the opportunity to lead a healthy life.

Name: Social Venture Partners Vancouver
Description:
We’re a venture philanthropy foundation backing bold nonprofits to help B.C.’s children and youth thrive.

Think VCs—but for nonprofits. Since 2001, SVP Vancouver has done philanthropy differently. We go beyond the grant, combining funding, hands-on expertise, and deep partnership to help high-potential organizations grow, lead, and scale what works—with the shared goal of improving outcomes for B.C.’s children and youth.

We Find promising nonprofits, Fund critical gaps, Strengthen their leadership and operations, and Scale programs that deliver real results for kids and families.

Together with 140+ engaged Partners, we’ve helped transform dozens of nonprofits across B.C.—amplifying their impact and changing what’s possible for the communities they serve.

Name: Impact CEE
Description:
THBE is a membership association based in Budapest, focusing on the CEE region and involves individuals and organizations interested in or practicing impact investing or venture philanthropy. The association is pursuing it's mission to evangelize the impact focused, high-engagement investment strategy and is building a network of committed investors and supporters. Social impact investors realize their impact through investing in social enterprises that challenge societal issues in an innovative and sustainable way.  

A Társadalmi Hasznosságú Befektetők Egyesülete (THBE) azon befektetői szemléletmódot hívatott erősíteni, hogy a befektetési döntések azok társadalmi, környezeti és kulturális hatásainak figyelembevételével szülessenek. A társadalmi hasznosságú befektető a befektetett tőke eredményes megtérülésével egynértékű célként tekint a befektetés társadalmi haszon- és értékteremtésére. A befektető olyan társadalmi vállalkozásokba történő befektetésével érheti el impactját, akik a különböző társadalmi kihívásokra fenntartható és innovatív megoldást válaszolnak. Az egyesület budapesti központtal a kelet-közép-európai régióra koncentrálva végzi evangelizációs, edukációs és network építő tevékenységét, összefogva a társadalmi hasznosságú befektetési stratégia iránt érdeklődő és gyakorló egyéneket és szervezeteket.

Name: ONGB
Description: Oakland Natives Give Back is a 21st-century community-based philanthropic hub that is empowering the next generation of high achieving citizens.

Name: Protection And Help of CHildren Against Abuse & Neglect
Description: PAHCHAAN is now the country partner for International Society for Prevention of Child Abuse And Neglect (ISPCAN), through our shared, experiences, resources and researches we hope to better understand effective and sustainable child abuse and neglect prevention.

Name: Comer Family Foundation
Description:
The Comer Family Foundation is a private, family foundation dedicated to seeding ideas and supporting innovative programs that make a lasting and positive impact on the lives of individuals and their communities. 

The late Gary Comer, founder of the Lands’ End clothing company, possessed not only remarkable business savvy, but also a unique vision that inspired all who knew him. Mr. Comer’s singular curiosity and passion for helping others to build better lives led to the pursuit of numerous charitable endeavors.

The Comer Foundation was created in 1986 with two major grant-making programs focusing on the city of Chicago and on syringe exchange programs nationwide. The Comer Science & Education Foundation was incorporated in 1998 to provide funding for organizations in the fields of education, environment and health. In 2013, the two foundations came together to create the Comer Family Foundation which is dedicated to combining these efforts.

Since Mr. Comer’s death, his family continues his legacy and remarkable generosity. Each initiative receives not only financial support, but also the active involvement of the Comer family to make every goal a reality.

Name: Association LES ENFANTS DE L'OVALE
Description: Former des enfants à devenir des adultes responsables et autonomes. Encourager l’émancipation des jeunes filles. Telles sont les missions que se sont assignées Les Enfants de l’Ovale. Fondée en 2003 par Philippe Sella, l’ancien trois-quarts centre du XV de France, cette association conduit des actions dans les domaines du sport, de l’éducation et de la nutrition/santé, en s’appuyant sur les valeurs du rugby : engagement, solidarité et respect de l’autre. Des valeurs aisément transposables dans la vie de tous les jours. Près de 1 300 enfants (40% de filles) participent régulièrement aux différentes activités dans 12 centres, répartis sur 7 pays (France, Madagascar et Afrique), encadrés par 130 éducateurs.

Name: Catholic Education Foundation of Los Angeles
Description:
OUR MISSION

The Catholic Education Foundation of the Archdiocese of Los Angeles is committed primarily to providing tuition assistance to the most financially deserving students attending Catholic elementary and high schools within the Archdiocese.


OUR VISION

CEF envisions a society where every students and family can choose the right school for them and have ready access to that school. Children living in poverty especially deserve to exercise this right. Because money is often the principal barrier to school choice, CEF is committed to removing that barrier by giving tuition assistance to children who want the advantages of a Catholic education.

98% of CEF students graduate from high school.
98% of CEF graduates go on to college.

100% of donations go directly towards tuition awards.

Name: Kain Foundation
Description: The Kain Foundation is a public ancillary fund established in 2005 by South Australian business owner John Kain. We are a collective of businesses supporting a collective of projects, all aimed at creating opportunities for children and youth in need to help them fulfil their potential. We exist quite simply to enrich lives.

Name: Hand in Hand e.V.
Description: Hand in Hand organisiert jedes Jahr einen Spendenlauf zu Gunsten des Kinder und Jugendhospiz in Stuttgart. Dieses Jahr findet dieser hybrid statt. Es kann in der Filderhalle Leinfelden-Echterdingen und digital gelaufen werden.

Name: Rochas Foundation College of Africa
Description:
Rochas Foundation College of Africa (ROFOCA) is a free, comprehensive, and qualitative educational scheme established by the Rochas Foundation Inc. to make education free and accessible to less privileged children across the continent of Africa. ROFOCA like its sister (RFC) schools in Nigeria, admits smart, intelligent, talented, and brilliant less privileged orphans and indigent African children whose parents cannot afford to send them to school due to their financial situation.
Rochas Foundation College of Africa is a mordern co-educational boarding school designed to provide a condusive learning enviroment for Five (5) children from all african countries from the poorest of the poor families. The admission is annual.

Name: Soul Hub Newcastle
Description:
Soul Hub | More Than a Meal

Soul Hub is a grassroots community organisation based in the heart of Newcastle, providing practical support and genuine care to people facing homelessness, hardship, and social isolation.

Established in 2003 as Soul Café, we began by serving free meals to those in need. Today, we’ve grown into a vibrant hub of connection and support, offering not just meals but practical support and a wide range of essential services to some of the most vulnerable members of our community.

Each day, Soul Hub serves hundreds of free sit-down and takeaway meals. But our mission goes beyond meeting immediate needs. We exist to walk alongside people as they navigate complex challenges like homelessness, addiction, mental health, domestic violence, financial hardship, and social disconnection. Our goal is to help every Guest find a pathway to greater safety, health, and purpose. At Soul, everyone’s welcome, because nobody’s perfect, and anything is possible.

Soul Hub is non-government funded and relies on the generosity of individuals, businesses, and local community partners. Soul Hub truly is a compassionate community response to a deep community need.

Name: International Brain Research Foundation
Description:
IBRF is committed to excellence in the neurosciences and is dedicated to promoting brain health. Historically, cures for brain disease, injury, and dysfunction have been typically slow in development. In the face of that challenge, IBRF seeks to accelerate the development of brain cures by supporting translational, clinical research that is state-of-the-art and innovative in its approach. By gathering an international cadre of experts, and utilizing a network of worldwide, multidisciplinary venues, IBRF is able to harness the brightest talent and resources to tackle the complexities of the brain and provide hope for future cures. IBRF brings a think-tank landscape to the frontier of neuroscience research, ultimately to improve the quality of life and functioning of brain impaired individuals.

Chief Executive Officer / Chief Scientific Officer & Founder: Dr. Philip DeFina

Name: ANIMAL FARM FOUNDATION INC
Description: ANIMAL FARM FOUNDATION INC working to create inclusive communities for people and their dogs

Name: Fundación Dr. José Ignacio Rivero Cosme-Más Allá del Cáncer
Description: Philanthropic institution

Name: Friends of the House
Description: Friends of the House is a young professional group that comes together to support the children and families served by Ronald McDonald House Charities of Central Texas. Together, members fundraise, volunteer and participate in events that bring comfort to families.

Name: Alabama Civil Justice Foundation
Description:
The mission of the Alabama Civil Justice Foundation is to assist in “removing barriers to a civil and just society for Alabama children, families and seniors,” and we accomplish this through statewide grantmaking to support nonprofits assisting disadvantaged families and children.   

Participants in the Alabama Civil Justice Foundation believe that civil justice embodies the opportunity for all citizens to have adequate food and shelter, good health care, a safe environment in which to live and work, a sense of well-being, a quality education, and the chance to work and earn a living. The Foundation is supported by Alabama's legal community as a charitable philanthropy committed to the vision of civil justice. 

Founded in 1993 by the Alabama Association of Justice (formerly the Alabama Trial Lawyers Association), ACJF was authorized as an Interest on Lawyers Trust Account (IOLTA) program. 

Funds from the IOLTA program as well as from donations have allowed the Foundation to award $16 million to nonprofit organizations statewide. ACJF has awarded grants in all 67 counties of the state.

Name: The Unquiet Professional
Description: The Unquiet Professional is a registered 501(c)3 charitable organization committed to recognizing and honoring our nation’s Gold Star Families and Veterans by providing rewarding and purposeful opportunities. Founded in memory of Staff Sergeant Michael H. Simpson.

Name: Care Net Manasota Pregnancy Center
Description: We are a 501(3) non-profit pregnancy center in Bradenton, FL.  We provide free pregnancy tests and ultrasounds to women who find themselves facing an unexpected pregnancy.  We also offer for free parenting classes, we have a mens program, a teen sexual integrity teaching, post-abortion help and healing and we offer needed community referrals.  We can provide these services for free due to the financial support of individuals, business and churches in our surrounding area.  We couldn't do what we do without their financial support, prayers and passion for helping save lives and educating women.

Name: YES! Vancouver
Description:
YES! Vancouver is a philanthropic networking group of professional women looking to make powerful connections with like-minded peers.

Our mission is two-fold: to fundraise for Dress for Success Vancouver and to create innovative and meaningful events throughout the year for motivated and driven career women in Metro Vancouver and the Lower Mainland. 

100% of the money we raise goes directly to Dress for Success Vancouver, a community empowering women into the workforce by providing them with professional attire, career services, and skills development programs.

Name: Crossroads:Pathways to Success, Inc.
Description: The  mission of Crossroads: Pathways to Success, Inc. is to develop young adults into productive leaders of our community. Crossroads utilizes academic, self development and empowerment tools to create college ready students.

Name: AFCEA Educational Foundation
Description:
The AFCEA Educational Foundation is a 501 (c) 3 not-for-profit organization established to solicit, receive, administer and expend funds for scholarships in AFCEA’s areas of interest.

We provide incentives, opportunities and assistance to people engaged in the science, technology, engineering and mathematics (STEM) disciplines.

Our programs focus on information technology, cybersecurity, telecommunications and electronics supporting the defense, homeland security and intelligence communities. The foundation, in conjunction with AFCEA chapters worldwide, provides more than $2 million in scholarships a year.

Name: Gladiator Project
Description: Supporting brain cancer research and helping glioblastoma multiforme (GBM) patients and their families.

Name: Brendan Looney Foundation
Description: The mission of the Brendan Looney Foundation (BLF) is to help individuals and families—both U.S. military and non-military—who share the values of service, honor, integrity, and perseverance exemplified by LT (SEAL) Brendan Looney. As Brendan so honorably lived, BLF believes in service to community and country, a foundation of strong character and morals, and a commitment to reach one’s greatest potential in mind, body and spirit. Through educational scholarships, assistance programs, awards, grants and other activities, BLF seeks to promote the development of future leaders, create advancement opportunities and enrich the lives of those in need.

Name: Digital Naari
Description: Digital Naari is the program run by Hockeystic to equip #unemployed #underprivileged women with digital skills and make these women #employable in today's times. It's a 4-month program for training women with the goal to improve the Female #Labour Force Participation #Rate of India.

Name: Fondazione Cassamarca
Description:
La Fondazione Cassamarca è la continuazione della Cassa di Risparmio della Marca Trivigiana.

Quest’ultima fu istituita su iniziativa del Monte di Pietà di Treviso con deliberazione consiliare del 27 marzo 1913 ed eretta in ente morale con Regio Decreto n. 1348 del 30 novembre 1913.

Nel 1992, in adempimento alle previsioni della Legge Amato, dalla Cassa di Risparmio è stata scorporata l'azienda bancaria per apportarla alla società conferitaria "Cassamarca, Cassa di Risparmio della Marca Trivigiana SPA", con atto n. 74758/23191 di repertorio del notaio Arrigo Manavello di Treviso in attuazione del progetto di ristrutturazione deliberato dal Consiglio di Amministrazione della Cassa stessa e approvato con Decreto del Ministro del Tesoro n. 436062 del 29.7.92.

La Cassa di Risparmio della Marca Trivigiana a sua volta era la continuazione della prima Cassa di Risparmio aperta in Treviso il 12 febbraio 1822 su "Avviso" promulgato il 31 gennaio dello stesso anno. Questa, con Regio Decreto 21 giugno 1928 n. 1692, si era in seguito unita consensualmente con la Cassa di Risparmio di Castelfranco Veneto aperta il 12 febbraio 1822 in base al Manifesto Istitutivo del 30 gennaio precedente.

Nel 1938 aveva quindi assorbito la Banca Popolare di Asolo e ne11942, il 31 dicembre, aveva incorporato il Monte di Pietà di Treviso fondato ne11496.

La Cassa di Risparmio della Marca Trivigiana era anche la continuazione dei Monti di Pietà di Asolo, di Castelfranco Veneto e di Vittorio Veneto.

La Fondazione ha la sua sede in Treviso ed ha durata illimitata.

Name: Ardesi Foundation
Description:
Ardesi Foundation, nasce da una moderna idea filantropica del suo fondatore Alessandro Ardesi che, con il supporto dell’intera famiglia e diversi associati, conduce il progetto sostenendo attivamente associazioni No profit in tutto il mondo ed ideando progetti self-made volti alla beneficenza ed alla raccolta fondi.

Dalla sua istituzione nel 2018, la fondazione si impegna a divulgare la cultura tra i giovani e sensibilizzarli verso il mondo no profit, proteggere le categorie più fragili e sostenere la salvaguardia dell’ambiente.

Name: Zoo Conservation Outreach Group
Description: Zoo Conservation Outreach Group (ZCOG) is a non-profit coalition of North American zoological facilities dedicated to assisting Latin American zoos and aquariums in their regional wildlife and habitat conservation efforts.  The group’s main focus areas are building conservation leadership capacity in Latin American zoological institutions and promoting the development of collaborative zoo and aquarium –based wildlife and habitat conservation programs throughout the Americas.  The former is achieved by providing access to information and training opportunities through a well-coordinated International Training Scholarship Program, while the latter is accomplished by pooling technical, material, and financial support from North American zoological institutions and delivering these resources to our counterparts in Latin America and the Caribbean Basin.

Name: Chicago Angels
Description:
The Chicago Angels mission is to walk alongside children in foster care and their caretakers by offering consistent support through intentional giving, relationship building and mentorship.

INTENTIONAL GIVING:
We define intentional giving as the giving of thoughtful gifts and resources that meet the real needs of a foster child and their entire family.

RELATIONSHIP BUILDING:
We define relationship building as devoting time and energy to foster healthy relationships that promote healing through connection.

MENTORSHIP:
We define mentorship as equipping and empowering our children to be prepared for adult living and reach their fullest potential.

Our Love Box program is an opportunity to take further action by connecting with other dedicated volunteers and creating a pathway of hope for children in the foster care system. Volunteers meet practical, everyday needs, as well as build intentional relationships with the family they serve.

Our dream is to see every child in the foster care system supported by a Love Box group. Our long-term vision is to open chapters in every major metropolitan city in the nation, so every single foster child in the United States will be supported and empowered to succeed.

Name: Fundación Moisés Itzkowich
Description: Resolver problemas sociales desde su raíz, articulando aliados clave por medio de metodologías y apoyo económico que fomente la institucionalización, responsabilidad y rendición de cuentas y multiplique el impacto en la mejora de la calidad de vida en México.

Name: Grantwell
Description:
Grantwell is a graduate-student-run, faculty advised nonprofit consulting program at Brigham Young University. At Grantwell, we empower students to provide meaningful strategic planning, research, and program evaluation deliverables to our clients. Graduate students work with organizations interested in charitable works in order to gain valuable experience while making an impact. Grantwell provides students with the opportunity to be leaders in the public and social sector. In the 10 years since being founded, Grantwell student teams have helped direct over $45 million in charitible giving for over 30 organizations.

Past clients include:
The Walmart Foundation
The Forever Young Foundation
The Ray and Tye Noorda Foundation
The Tyler Robinson Foundation
The Sorenson Legacy Foundation
and many others...

Name: FriendShip Prague
Description: FriendShip Prague is an English-speaking network of Czechs and Expats which exists to provide support and friendship to people in need in Prague. It's a network of friends with a "people matter" attitude who aim to turn philanthropy into a habit.

Name: Charity Foundation Happy Paw
Description:
We solve the problem of stray dogs in Ukraine in humane manner
The creators of the Foundation used to help homeless dogs for a number of years. But this practice wasn’t systematic. So decided to create the charity foundation.
In March 2012 we prepared all documents for registration. And on 23th of June 2012 Happy Paw Charity Foundation was officially registered. In November 2012 the Foundation took a guardianship on its first shelter where 41 dogs were living. That month Happy Pa” also started to work with one more shelter. And in the next six months our Foundation worked with 15 more shelters.
In February 2013 the national serach system of lost&found dogs search started to work. Also we have opened an internet-shop of souvenir products with the trade mark of “Happy Paw”. In April 2013 the educational work in schools and universities started.
Today the activities of the Foundation are held all over Ukraine.

Name: Janivo Stichting
Description: Janivo Foundation is a grant making foundation with a focus on children and young adults. Since 1978 the Dutch-based foundation has been part of a family owned group, consisting of Janivo Holding (an investment company), Museum De Pont (a museum for the modern arts), Huis Marseille (a museum for contemporary photography) and several other foundations.

Name: Projeto Sol
Description: Project Sol aims the prevention of drug addiction and violence to children and adolescents from low-income families. The initiative is already 40 years old, in which a social technology was been developed to allow the cultural and humanistic development of thousands of children and their families. Projeto Sol offers art-education classes with excellent results, and also carries out sports and leisure activities, offering two daily nutritious meals and also psycho-pedagogical accompaniment.

Name: W. Clement & Jessie V. Stone Foundation
Description: Following the vision of its founders to "change the world and make it a better place for this and future generations," the W. Clement & Jessie V. Foundation concentrates its grantmaking in three areas: early childhood development, youth development, and education (with an emphasis on teacher quality and principal leadership). Today, the Foundation has an asset base of over $100 million and distributes almost $5 million in grants each year primarily in Chicago, Boston, New York, Philadelphia, and the San Francisco Bay Area.

Name: ONG-ACCESS-MALI
Description:
EDUCATION
- Vulgarisation des droits des enfants
- Causeries éducatives- Sensibilisation
- Offre éducative Alternative
- Alphabétisation
Renforcement des CGS, APE, AME et staff des écoles
Promotion féminine/Genre
- Leadership communautaire
- Gouvernance
- Protection de l’environnement
- Technologies innovantes
- Recherche-Action sur l’éducation, la réconciliation, Paix et stabilité. 
DEVELOPPEMENT DE L’AGROBUSNESS
- Formation techniques agricoles
- Organisation paysanne
- Formation entrepreneuriat agricole
- Appui à l’élaboration de plans d’affaires
- Mise en relation avec les  services financiers
- Information sur le marché
- Médiation pour accès des jeunes apprenants à des stages professionnels
- Appui aux centres de formation professionnelle notamment des filles
SECURITE ALIMENTAIRE
- Vulgarisation du droit à l’alimentation et à la nutrition
- Organisation paysanne
- Formation techniques production, transformation et conservation 
- Appui à la promotion des Activités Génératrices de Revenus des femmes
- Formation des pôles d’entreprises agricoles
- Suivi nutritionnel des enfants
- Education nutritionnelle
- Plaidoyer
SANTE
Formation des pairs éducateurs
Education sexuelle
Sensibilisation sur les maladies sexuellement transmissibles
Lutte contre le paludisme
Wash

Name: Twentse Noabers Fonds
Description: Het Twentse Noabers Fonds is een initiatief van de Twentse Noabers Coöperatie, Leader Zuid West Twente  en Leader Noord Oost Twente. Het Twentse Noabers Fonds wil mensen met een goed idee voor hun stukje Twente verder helpen. Dat doen wij door kennis te delen en initiatieven zo nodig ook op weg te helpen met financiering. Dat doen niet allemaal zelf maar vooral ook door samen te werken met organisaties en mensen in onze netwerken.

Name: Fundación Lifting Hands
Description:
¿Qué es Lifting Hands?
Buscamos dar Herramientas a personas en comunidades en alto riesgo social de Costa Rica para salir adelante. Creando centros de aprendizaje y motivación a través de voluntarios con un alto nivel educativo que desean transmitir lo que saben, pero más allá dar herramientas queremos trasmitir el amor de Dios y esperanza, creando una relación de amistad con nuestros estudiantes. 

Creemos que la educación puede cambiar la mente de una persona y prepararla para un futuro próspero, pero solo el amor de Dios puede transformar sus corazones. 

Trabajamos de una manera integral en 5 áreas: Apoyo académico, psicológico, nutricional, desarrollo comunal y concientización.
Para más información visitá www.liftinghands.org

Name: Fondazione Santa Rita da Cascia onlus
Description:
Il 7 marzo 2012 sboccia un nuovo fiore di carità: la Fondazione Santa Rita da Cascia onlus, un progetto reso possibile grazie alla vitalità e alla partecipazione di tante persone.

Promossa dal Monastero Santa Rita da Cascia, la Fondazione rappresenta uno strumento totalmente a servizio delle opere sociali, con l’obiettivo di sostenere in modo continuato i progetti di solidarietà che le monache portano avanti da sempre, primo fra tutti: l’Alveare.

Name: The James Jay Dudley Luce Foundation
Description: The Mission of the James Jay Dudley Luce Foundation, Inc. is to support young global leadership impacting positive social change, particularly in the fields of the Arts, Education, and Orphan Care. Support includes microgrants and ‘spotlighting’ through social media and our the Foundation's Stewardship Report on Connecting Goodness, which features original and aggregated content. The Foundation was envisioned upon the death of Jim's father, Stanford L. Luce, in 2008. It was incorporated in New York State in 2011

Name: CLAC. Centro Libre. Arte y Cultura
Description:
El CLAC  nace en febrero del 2015 en Barcelona con el fin de construir un lugar de encuentro para realizar actividades culturales y educativas con una vocación global e independiente. Fundado por Francesc de Carreras, Felix Ovejero, Ignacio Vidal-Foch, Manuel Cruz y Miriam Tey

El principal  es crear un centro capaz de promover y reforzar el carácter cosmopolita que Barcelona ha tenido siempre, estimulando su papel de referente cultural de Cataluña dentro de España.
El CLAC quiere facilitar los intercambios culturales con la fluidez propia de una sociedad democrática y abierta. Crear un espacio que refleje la realidad lingüística presente en la sociedad catalana, en el que se alienten las ideas y los debates basados en el respeto a las diferencias, la libertad y la pluralidad.

OBJETIVOS 
- Contribuir a crear un espacio cultural en Barcelona, con vocación global y con independencia de las lenguas y tradiciones.
- Reflejar la variedad y diversidad de la cultura catalana, orientada a los asuntos y retos presentes y futuros de nuestras sociedades.
 - Crear un espacio al servicio de otras iniciativas ya existentes, que actúe como amplificador de sus actividades.
- Generar estudios, debate y reflexión sobre asuntos polémicos y de actualidad y facilitar su divulgación.
- Apoyar a la industria cultural catalana con independencia de la lengua o la ideología.
- Recuperar el papel de Barcelona como ciudad de encuentro, cosmopolita y librepensadora.

- El CLAC es un centro totalmente independiente de cualquier institución o entidad.
- El CLAC fomenta valores que favorezcan la integración política y social de los catalanes en los movimientos nacionales e inter- nacionales de carácter social, científico o cultural.
- El CLAC estará financiado con las cuotas de sus miembros, la provisión de servicios y las subvenciones públicas y privadas que reciba para el desarrollo de sus actividades.
- El CLAC no tiene fines lucrativos.

Name: Global Vision International (GVI) Foundation
Description: GVI Charitable Programs is a 501 (c) (3) (EIN 84-2009644) non profit organization. We bring people, communities, local and international partner organizations together to collaborate on assessed and qualifying conservation and sustainable development projects around the world.

Name: Ape Alliance
Description:
The Ape Alliance is an international coalition of organisations and individuals, working for the conservation and welfare of apes.

The Ape Alliance exists to:

 - Provide a forum for discussion of issues relating to apes.
 - Develop position papers on key issues.
 - Lobby collectively for enactment and/or enforcement of legislation to improve the welfare and/or conservation of apes.
 - Campaign for greater public awareness of ape issues and increase respect for apes.
 - Facilitate information exchange between member groups and, where appropriate, coordinate activities to maximise their beneficial effect.

Name: Hope it up
Description:
Follow our new company page at https://www.linkedin.com/company/alayagood/ and find us at www.alayagood.com

The social enterprise Hope it up was founded in 2016, and in January 2018 merged with Share A Dream, another major employee volunteering and corporate giving actor to become Alaya.
 
Alaya is a leader in employee volunteering and corporate giving, enabling employees to choose their way of contributing and providing a powerful tool to analyse the impact created by an entire company.

Our team believes that companies and their employees have a remarkable potential to create change. So we are committed to helping non-profit organisations around the world have access to funds, volunteers, knowledge and skills.

Name: Nyamwange Foundation
Description:
- Joseph Nyamwange
Here at Nyamwange Foundation Inc, our primary focus is on improving urban cities one child at a time.  We want to be a catalyst for positive change, and since our inception, we’ve been driven by three key ideals: scholarship, empowerment through self-identify, and community service. Learn more about our mission and partner with us

Name: Ek Muskurahat Team
Description: Helping People's Happiness doesn't results from what we get, but from what we give.

Name: Team Veteran Foundation, Inc. 501(c)3
Description:
The First Cost of Freedom is Supporting Our Veterans! We have reached a crisis point with the Protectors of Liberty and Freedom our Veterans and their families. We need your help with this mission.

The VA’s focus on psychotropic drugs is one of the leading causes of the 22-30 daily Veteran suicides. Suicide ideation is one of the effects of these drugs along with heart, kidney, thyroid and liver damage. Why would anyone prescribe 5-25 drugs daily for the treatment of Post Traumatic Stress (PTS) and Traumatic Brain Injury (TBI) when there are drug-free alternatives?

Team Veteran Foundation’s (TVF) mission is to bring a permanent end to the 22-30 daily Veteran suicide epidemic (https://tvfaz.org/). One of the cornerstone services of the TVF Wellness Clinics is the Hyperbaric Oxygen Therapy (HBOT) chambers for the treatment of the physical brain injuries suffered during a Traumatic Brain Injury (TBI). There are NO double-blind studies that prove the efficacy of psychotropic drugs for the treatment of TBI (all 50 drugs are “off-label” for TBI) and only 4 are “on-label” to treat PTS.

The TVF Wellness Clinics will utilize Integrative medicine therapies focusing on the use of Hyperbaric Oxygen Therapy (HBOT) chambers to treat Traumatic Brain Injury (TBI). Once the brain injury is physically healed then other non-drug therapies to treat Post Traumatic Stress (PTS) can be utilized. The founder of Team Veteran Foundation is a survivor of a near fatal Traumatic Brain Injury (TBI) and underwent emergency brain surgery on 11/5/2002. On 09/21/2015 he started the recommended 40 1-hour “dives” in a multiplace Hyperbaric Oxygen Therapy chamber and finished the 40th dive on 11/13/2015. The TBI negatively impacted his cognition and the HBOT dives returned his cognition to normal or better.

We are partnering with Team Veteran LLC (TVet) because TVet’s mission is to create funds for legitimate Veteran nonprofits that is completely different than the common fundraising methods.

Name: Peaks4All
Description:
Peaks4All est une association née le 19 septembre 2021 à Genève, ville de paix, de fondue et de montagnes; projet tout droit sorti de l’esprit rêveur et déterminé de ses deux fondatrices, Clémence et Laëtitia. 

Notre pari: emmener une équipe de 7 réfugiés en haut d’un sommet de 4000m emblématique de Suisse, courant août 2022 et ainsi leur faire découvrir les immenses joies de la haute montagne. 

Comment? En s’entraînant de manière régulière et en constituant une cordée motivée et soudée d’alpinistes chevronnés; grandissant à travers différentes activités, entraînements, sorties et formations en / à la moyenne et haute montagne.

Name: Népal ãśã
Description: Association étudiante humanitaire lié à HumaCS. Nous soutenons une centaine d'enfants en conditions précaires au centre éducatif UDMF-Népal en assurant leur soutien scolaire en dehors de leurs horaires de cours et en évitant aux enfants de se retrouver dans la rue.

Name: UNC Panhellenic Association
Description: The UNC Panhellenic Council is the largest women’s organization at UNC–CH and the governing body of the 11 National Panhellenic Council sororities and 1 Associate Member sorority at UNC–Chapel Hill.

Name: Into the Light: A New Jersey Non-Profit Organization
Description: Imagine a society where people struggling with mental illness, regardless of their circumstances, felt comfortable reaching out for help; where in their worst moments, they were met with understanding, compassion and solutions; where as a result they experienced a greater level of safety, dignity and healing.

Name: AFP Inland Empire
Description:
AFP Inland Empire is a member of AFP International – an association of fundraising professionals with chapters in the United States, Canada, Mexico and Hong Kong. AFP has more than 30,000 members in 230 chapters. AFP serves our profession through a Code of Ethical Principles and Standards, education, advocacy and communication to our members and the general public.

Since 1960, the Association of Fundraising Professionals (AFP) has inspired global change and supported efforts that generated over $1 trillion. AFP's nearly 30,000 individual and organizational members raise over $100 billion annually, equivalent to one-third of all charitable giving in North America and millions more around the world.

The AFP Inland Empire Chapter is comprised of professional fundraisers who represent a variety of educational, health and community-based institutions and nonprofits. Our growing chapter encompasses more than 27,000 square miles, including all of San Bernardino County and most of Riverside County, excluding the Desert Region (Coachella Valley). The Inland Empire is bordered by Orange, Los Angeles and Kern counties to the west, San Diego and Imperial counties to the south, Inyo County to the north and the borders of Arizona and Nevada to the east.

Name: Thyroid Disease Awareness Kenya Foundation (TDAK)
Description:
Thyroid hormones are essential for growth, neuronal development, reproduction and regulation of energy metabolism. Hypothyroidism and hyperthyroidism are common conditions with potentially devastating health consequences that affect all populations worldwide. Iodine nutrition is a key determinant of thyroid disease risk; however, other factors, such as environmental factors, genetic susceptibility, ethnicity, determine the epidemiology of thyroid disease.

Thyroid disease is a global health problem that can substantially impact the  overall well-being of the sufferers.

In advanced economies, the prevalence of undiagnosed thyroid disease is falling owing to widespread thyroid function testing and relatively low thresholds for treatment initiation, a variety of options of different approaches of treating thyroid disease. 

 However, In Emerging Economies, and developing countries, like Africa, the concept of thyroid disease testing is still a very foreign concept, and because of this, The extent of autoimmune thyroid disorders  In Africa remains unknown because of underdiagnoses and underreporting.
Thyroid disorders are becoming common in Africa and mostly arise from environmental and nutritional factors, representing a severe public health problem in several countries of the sub-Saharan area, and East African countries such as Kenya. 
In Sub Saharan Africa, where most of the countries are  Low- Middle-Income Countries(LMIC) the privilege of having different types of treatment options to treat different forms of thyroid disease is not there, and therefore, only the use of generic antithyroid drugs, and limited types of thyroid surgeries are the most common forms of treatment of thyroid disorders.

Here at TDAK, we are passionate to work in helping people understand the concepts of thyroid disease through education and creating awareness. We also aim to ensure that the treatment options available are of optimum quality, affordable and accessible to all Kenyans.

Name: Zonta Club of Sydney Breakfast Inc.
Description:
Zonta Club of Sydney Breakfast is a division of Zonta International. We meet on the first Tuesday of every month over breakfast to liaise on current and upcoming projects, and hear from a range of inspiring women.

Zonta International is a leading global organisation of professionals empowering women worldwide through service and advocacy. With more than 30,000 members belonging to more than 1,200 Zonta Clubs in 67 countries and geographic areas, Zontians all over the world volunteer their time, talents and support to local and international service projects, as well as scholarship programs aimed at fulfilling Zonta's mission and objects. 

Zonta International envisions a world in which women’s rights are recognized as human rights and every woman is able to achieve her full potential. In such a world, women have access to all resources and are represented in decision making positions on equal basis with men. In such a world, no woman lives in fear of violence.

For more information please visit www.zonta.org

Name: Casa Valentina, Inc.
Description: Since opening in 2006, Casa Valentina has provided intensive life skills programming and affordable housing to former foster care and other youth at risk of homelessness (ages 18-23) as they transition to independent living. We support them by offering structured, realistic education support, daily living lessons, job preparedness, financial literacy, safe housing, and, most importantly, the security of a caring supportive team. We work with our young women and men to give them the individualized survival tools to become self-sufficient and productive, and then stay with them after they leave with continued support during that second period of transition.

Name: Rotary Club of Nairobi-Lang'ata
Description:
Our Club was chartered on 14th May 2002 and currently has 149 members making it the biggest Club in District 9212. Our Charter President Alex Nyaga is a past District Governor. We have 96 Paul Harris Fellows and have started 2 Rotary Clubs, 2 Rotaract Clubs and 1 Interact Club.
Our club theme this year is Unlimited Fun in Service, which was borne out of making our membership experience both worthwhile and enjoyable

Name: Fondazione Antonio Lombardi ONLUS
Description:
La Fondazione nasce con l’intento di perseguire lo spirito filantropico del fondatore Antonio Lombardi per incoraggiare e valorizzare la crescita dei giovani.
L'obiettivo è la conservazione, l’arricchimento e la valorizzazione del patrimonio storico italiano, materiale e immateriale.

Name: Juguemos en el Bosque Foundation
Description:
Juguemos in Bosque foundation works to contribute to education and emotional development in stages: early childhood, childhood, adolescence and youth, through a continuous process of strengthening in values and the development of capacities, to influence in a positive way the life of the vulnerable population. 

Currently we work with 562 children, adolescents and youth in foster care and protection homes of the Valley of Aburrá, who, through recreational activities and  ludic out goings, gives them the opportunity to interact with the outside world and generate the construction of positive referents, based on values, rights and duties, tools that lead them to the search for positive environments and protect them against risk situations that they may face when they leave. Likewise, through these activities we return to them the hope of having a quality of life and the motivation to achieve their personal fulfillment. Thats the way that we turn those smiles into gifts of life for them.

Name: Centraide Abitibi Témiscamingue et Nord-du-Québec
Description: Centraide ATNQ vise à briser le cycle de la pauvreté et de l’isolement social. Pour ce faire, nous souhaitons donner à chacun et chacune les moyens d’améliorer les conditions de vie des gens et bâtir des communautés inclusives. Notre raison d’être se matérialise grâce à nos relations étroites avec les donateurs, les bénévoles, les entreprises privées et publiques ainsi qu’un réseau d’organismes communautaires.

Name: transglobalfamilyheritage@gmail.com
Description: Building a global ownership society for all

Name: WE CARE FOR HUMANITY
Description:
We Care for Humanity (WCH) is a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization established in 2011 by HRH Maria Leonora Amor Torres, the Queen of Maharlika and Bae Mompong of the Sultanate of Magindanaw. With headquarters in California and Illinois, WCH has gained global recognition for its dedication to tackling urgent global issues, notably aligning with the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals (UNSDG). These goals encompass eradicating poverty, ending hunger, promoting good health and well-being, ensuring quality education, advocating for gender equality, addressing climate change, and fostering peace and strong institutions.

Our mission is to assume a prominent position in advocating for innovative and sustainable social progress, supporting global humanitarian initiatives, and aligning with the United Nations' 17 Sustainable Development Goals. We aim to inspire individuals to embrace humanitarian principles and collaborate towards positive change. Through nurturing a culture of empathy and ingenuity, we strive to cultivate a more equitable and compassionate world, setting an example of social responsibility and environmental conservation.

Name: Fundación Ricardo Fisas Natura Bissé
Description: Ricardo Fisas Mulleras and Gloria Vergés Lasaga created the Foundation in 2008, with the vocation to support people who are living in disadvantaged situations and return to woman everything that she had given him trusting in his company. The Foundation, whose resources come from 0.7% of the turnover of Natura Bissé, has two projects of its own: the Oncology Aesthetics Program, with a professional network of voluntary beauticians who attend individually and free of charge to alleviate unwanted effects on the skin of people in oncological treatment in hospitals and patient associations, and the Project Difference Program to support children with learning differences, especially dyslexia, dyscalculia and ADHD, within an inclusive school. In addition, it works in a network with social action and international cooperation organizations such as Cáritas, Nuevos Caminos, Fundació Roure or Asociación AcidH, whose values are in line with those of the Foundation.

Name: Ghiorsi & Sorrenti, Inc.
Description: Ghiorsi & Sorrenti, Inc. is a full-service philanthropic consulting firm that specializes in planning and executing capital campaigns and major gift efforts. Since 1989, GSI has partnered with thousands of nonprofit organizations in raising billions of dollars in philanthropic support. We marvel at each client's uniqueness. This motivates us to go to great lengths to first understand a client's situation and then tailor our services accordingly.

Name: World Health Dental Organization
Description: The Maasai Dental Clinic is the flagship clinic of the World Health Dental Organization. It was established in 2008 and today treats more than 3,000 members of the Maasai community every year. The clinic is adjacent to the world-famous Maasai Mara National Game Park in Kenya, East Africa.

Name: The  Community Foundation Martin-St. Lucie
Description:
Founded in 1988, The Community Foundation Martin-St. Lucie helps individuals, families and businesses realize their philanthropic goals in the smartest and most impactful way possible.  As the largest community Foundation grantor to nonprofit organizations in Martin and St. Lucie Counties, we invest over $5 million in our region every year, creating important, lasting changes for the enrichment and betterment of our community.  

We believe that generosity knows no limits, and that every gift at any level matters.  What’s most important is that each charitable investment does the most good possible, in the way that its donors intend.    

For Donors
We offer this simple, straightforward promise:  Charitable giving through The Community Foundation reduces taxes, simplifies giving and ensures that your gifts support proven, qualified nonprofits.  We provide personalized, concierge-level guidance and support, and an in-depth knowledge of the local charitable landscape.  

For Professional Advisors
We partner with advisors to create charitable giving strategies for their clients that actualize their philanthropic goals while protecting and growing their charitable dollars.  We offer the flexibility for wealth managers to actively manage their clients’ philanthropic assets.

Name: Fonds de Développement des Services de Santé (FDSS)
Description: Le Fonds de Développement des Services de Santé (FDSS) est une organisation non gouvernementale d’utilité publique, une ASBL dotée d’une personnalité juridique, créée depuis 2008 en République Démocratique du Congo, dans le but de gérer les financements destinés à l’amélioration du fonctionnement des services de santé et du système national de santé, et de rechercher et orienter tout bailleur de fonds disposé à financer le fonctionnement du système de santé, tant au niveau central qu’au niveau intermédiaire et périphérique. Le principal domaine d'action du FDSS est l’appui institutionnel aux provinces et aux zones de santé,  avec un accent sur le financement et la gestion de l’Action en Santé, le renforcement des capacités des prestatataires et des gestionnaires, et la gestion fiduciaire.  Il est doté d’un  conseil d’administration, d’une Direction nationale, des bureaux provinciaux, des antennes locales, et des coordinations des projets.

Name: Cherry Checkout
Description:
Give weight to your CSR  Offer 1 charity Donation = 1 VOUCHER to your customers.
+ 13% of orders
90% increase their shopping cart
88% potential customer loyalty

 By becoming a CHERRY CHECKOUT partner, make your CSR plan a reality, benefit from free visibility, reach a committed and already active target.
Be part of our 30 partners and stand out from the competition.

 How does it work? 
The customer makes a charitable donation and receives a voucher for more than the amount of his donation. We work with more than 50 charities including Amnesty International, Action contre la Faim, Enfants du Monde, Paris du Coeur, Animal Testing, Terre des Femmes and many others...

Name: National Angels
Description:
National Angels is a nonprofit organization changing the way children, youth, and families experience foster care across the United States. Since the launch of our founding chapter in Austin, Texas, our programs have scaled through 19 chapters across 14 states. Each chapter walks alongside children, youth, and families in their local foster care community by offering consistent support through intentional giving, relationship building, and mentorship. 

The radical support delivered by our programs aims to change the statistics plaguing the child welfare system, and positively impact society as a whole. National Angels represents a sustainable solution to one of the country's toughest social issues.

Name: EndUCD.org
Description:
Unexplained cardiac death (UCD) occurs as a result of a sudden cardiac arrest, which has a mortality rate of 90%, killing more than 2000 Australians under 50 each year. This makes it one of the leading causes of death, on par with death rates due to suicide for Australians under 50. It is many more than the number of deaths from road traffic accidents or cancer for this demographic. In many cases death is instantaneous, with only 25% of people making it to a hospital. 

UCD is a death due to a heart cause in which the victim was seemingly well and in which the cardiac arrest was completely unexpected. 

The causes of UCD under 50 are largely unknown and there are no accurate screening tests that can predict those at high risk of UCD. It has received almost no government or philanthropic funding to date. 

EndUCD was created in 2017, following the tragic death of Captain Ross Dennerstein in 2016 due to UCD. It was formed to provide a voice for people affected by UCD under 50 and to raise awareness and funding that will enable research to explain the unknown and end UCD.

Name: Halo House Foundation
Description:
