Halo House is a non-profit organization serving all ages with blood cancers by providing fully-furnished temporary housing, at a very low cost, to people undergoing treatment for a blood cancer (leukemia, lymphoma, myeloma) at the Texas Medical Center. 

This is done by providing apartments near the Texas Medical Center for $25 per day where a patient who lives more than 50 miles from TMC can stay for two weeks to four months while receiving active treatment.

To date, Halo House has served over 1000 families and provided over 70,000 days of temporary housing.

Name: OxFizz
Description:
We're a not-for-profit social enterprise, working in the education sector. We're powered by 150+ volunteers, and are looking to grow our volunteer base even further in 2020!

Interested in volunteering? For more details visit our website or contact james.tibbles@oxfizz.org

Name: Projeto Criança Feliz
Description:
O Projeto Criança Feliz é uma iniciativa social e educativa sem fins lucrativos, fundada em 2006 na Escola de Engenharia de Lorena por João Donizette, um membro da equipe da instituição. A proposta inicial do projeto era reunir, aos sábados, crianças de comunidades periféricas para engajá-las em atividades lúdicas e recreativas. Com o passar dos anos, graças ao apoio de estudantes voluntários, o projeto expandiu significativamente seu alcance e impacto.

Atualmente, o Projeto Criança Feliz beneficia mais de 95 famílias de áreas periféricas, fomentando o protagonismo de crianças, adolescentes e seus pais. O objetivo é proporcionar a eles acesso a novas perspectivas de vida e inspirá-los a redefinir seus papéis na sociedade.

O programa adota uma abordagem pedagógica que segmenta participantes por faixa etária e habilidades a serem desenvolvidas. Para os jovens, são oferecidas atividades que visam ao desenvolvimento de competências específicas. Com os adultos, o foco recai sobre a realização de oficinas profissionalizantes diversificadas, com o intuito de instigar o espírito empreendedor e habilitá-los a gerar renda para suas famílias.

Name: The Michigan Masonic Charitable Foundation
Description:
In 1891, the doors opened to the first Michigan Masonic Home at Reeds Lake near Grand Rapids. In order to meet the financial responsibilities associated with maintaining the Masonic Home, the Masonic Foundation of Michigan, Inc. (since renamed the Michigan Masonic Charitable Foundation) was established and supported by dedicated Masons who were determined to give life to the term “brotherhood.”

While supporting Masonic Pathways remains a priority, the Michigan Masonic Charitable Foundation is more involved than ever in improving the lives and communities of Masons, acting as a powerful philanthropic partner for Michigan’s Masonic lodges. Today, the Foundation supports several valuable and life-changing programs such as annual matching grants for community charities and student scholarships and the Masonic Model Student Assistance Program. It is this spirit of collaboration that has helped the Michigan Masonic Charitable Foundation become one of Michigan’s preeminent foundations, ensuring that Michigan Masons will have the capacity to support our local community needs well into the future.

Name: Black Cooperative Impact Fund
Description: Black Cooperative Impact Fund (BCIF) is a 501(c)3, community-based organization that provides microloans to the Black business community with a specific focus on communities in the Southern California region. Our vision is to provide a dedicated, reliable, and perpetual source of capital to create assets and build wealth for, and within, the Black community, while also serving as a catalytic force for building a critical mass movement of change based on economic imperatives.

Name: Saadho Sangha Foundation
Description:
Guided by the vision of Sri Anish, Saadho is a vehicle to enable multiple initiatives towards ‘raising the collective human consciousness’. These initiatives range from; satsanghs (spiritual teachings), retreats, corporate leadership programs, mentoring, community building, youth empowerment, value education for future leaders at higher education institutions, meditation programs, yoga courses, healing through sound, kirtans & chants, community food service, media production, Indic wisdom & content publishing etc. 
There is a very beautiful and powerful mantra at the core of all Saadho’s initiatives; ‘Lokah Samastah Sukhino Bhavantu’ meaning, ‘May all the beings, in all planes of existence, achieve their highest Sukha (well-being). And the highest Sukha for any being is freedom from illusion & limited-ness; and the realisation of eternal truth & ever-expanding sense of oneness.

Name: Fondation Salvia
Description: Fondation Salvia is a foundation based in Geneva.

Name: Greater Watertown Community Health  Foundation
Description:
The Foundation equips community change leaders with the tools they need to build strong families and thriving communities. We foster transformative change by supporting local organizations in learning, growing, collaborating and moving their missions further.

Our Mission? To inspire collaboration, mobilize resources and encourage innovation that measurably contributes to the wellbeing of our community.

Connecting the “doers” 
GWCHF is a connector – bringing together the people, organizations and resources needed to create lasting change. We believe that transforming community health requires more than just writing a check. It requires listening. Engaging. Empowering the “doers” of our community to apply their talents to create a healthier tomorrow.  

Big challenges demand big solutions
GWCHF strategically focuses our resources to “move the needle” on community health indicators. We value collaboration, maximizing community assets and improving health over the long-term. We invest in root cause solutions to create sustainable change.

Name: Cocreation Foundation
Description: The number of humans living on earth is growing rapidly. According to the statistician Hans Rosling we might reach a maximum number of 12 billion people by 2050. This prognosis leaves us with the question as to how 12 billion people will be able to peacefully coexist on and with this planet. While no one can predict if this is possible at all, the Cocreation Foundation believes that we have no other chance than to face this challenge: to create a world of mutuality and coexistence in peace, health, freedom and ecological abundance for all and everything. In order to achieve this goal we firmly believe that we must develop a new cultural technique of cocreation.

Name: CORPORACION CLEO
Description:
CLEO est la première ONG à développer le CONSEIL REDISTRIBUTIF. Cette notion, que nous avons inventée, consiste à réinvestir les produits des missions de conseil réalisées auprès du secteur social dans des projets d’innovation sociale que nous développons.
Comment ça marche? D’un côté, nous réalisons des missions de conseil auprès des acteurs du secteur social et humanitaire afin d’améliorer la qualité de leurs actions. De l’autre, grâce aux résultats générés par ces missions, nous finançons et développons des projets d’innovation sociale que nous décidons d’accompagner ou que nous mettons nous-mêmes en œuvre.
Cette démarche nous permet de créer plusieurs cercles vertueux :
•  Nous offrons aux clients qui font appel à nos services l’occasion de contribuer, par leur investissement, à d’autres projets d’innovation sociale.
•  Notre propre expérience, développée dans le cadre de nos projets, nous garantit de nous maintenir au plus près des besoins les acteurs que nous accompagnons dans le cadre de nos missions de conseil.
•  Les consultants qui sont rémunérés dans le cadre de ces missions s’engagent à consacrer un temps bénévole pour conseiller d’autres projets d’innovation sociale qui se démarquent par leurs bonnes
pratiques.

Cleo propone un acompañamiento personalizado para todo tipo de proyecto de innovación social: 
•	Creación y/o aporte de métodos o herramientas técnicas o tecnológicas permitiendo el mejoramiento del trabajo de los actores involucrados en los sectores social y humanitario; 
•	Formación de equipos para el uso de estas herramientas
•	Evaluación de los proyectos et de sus impactos
•	Fundraising (recaudación de fondos)
•	Creación de sinergias entre cada uno de los actores involucrados en un mismo territorio o en un conjunto de acciones: ONG, patrocinadores privados, socio público, responsables políticos, profesionales locales.

Name: National Foundation for Medical Research and Innovation
Description:
The National Foundation For Medical Research and Innovation is a not for profit organisation that is entirely independent. It is not affiliated with any University, Hospital, Government or State body. The Foundation provides financial support to research projects conserving and building its capital base.

The National Foundation For Medical Research and Innovation was incorporated on 3 November 1977, originally as the Sydney Hospital Foundation for Research. The name was changed on 12 January 1994.

In 2013 the Foundation also decided to increase its support nationally and to change its name to the National Foundation for Medical research & Innovation.  This will take effect from 2nd January 2014
 
Our Mission

“To advance innovations in medical research related to the nature, prevention, diagnosis, treatment and incidence of disease and other health problems that have a significant impact on the health of humans”

Name: Water Ambassadors Canada
Description: Changing lives with clean water

Name: California Girls State Alumnae Foundation (GSAF)
Description: California's Girls State Alumnae Foundation is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization that exists to support and promote the American Legion Auxiliary's California Girls State program and its alumnae. All references to "women and girls" include gender-expansive individuals.

Name: Roy A. Hunt Foundation
Description: The Roy A. Hunt Foundation's mission is to support organizations that strive to improve the quality of life. Three generations of the family gather to make grants to a variety of nonprofit organizations in the U.S. The foundation has special initiatives in community development, the environment, and youth violence intervention. Around half of its grants are in the Pittsburgh and Boston regions.

Name: Cursinho Popular Angela Davis - Rede Emancipa
Description:
A Rede Emancipa é um movimento social de educação popular que desde 2007 constrói um importante trabalho voltado à educação de jovens de escolas públicas. O principal foco de atuação da Rede Emancipa tem sido a organização de cursinhos populares pré-universitários para atender à demanda represada dos estudantes de escolas públicas pelo acesso ao ensino superior em geral, e às universidades públicas em particular.

Nos cursinhos, além de refletir sobre o conteúdo exigido pelos vestibulares de uma maneira que esteja de acordo com o contexto vivido pelos estudantes, também é priorizada a educação transformadora que ofereça o máximo de instrumentos para que estes pensem as suas realidades de maneira crítica e emancipadora.

Construímos um projeto político-pedagógico que vai além do ensino para o vestibular, que possibilite à juventude um espaço inovador de debate, criação e recriação do saber acerca do mundo e da vida. Nesse projeto se engajam estudantes universitários, secundaristas, professores do ensino básico, professores e estudantes da Rede Emancipa, família, associação de bairro, comunidade e todos que queiram construir outro futuro.

As aulas são ministradas voluntariamente por professores já formados e também por professores estudantes universitários de várias universidades públicas e privadas do país. O espaço da Rede Emancipa não se limita apenas ao cursinho, ao longo dos anos, o entendimento de que o processo educativo é ininterrupto ao longo da vida, tem servido de inspiração para que outras experiências de educação popular sejam realizadas, temos planos que incluem alfabetização de jovens e adultos, preparação para concursos, educação infantil e pré-vestibulinho.

A Rede Emancipa começará o ano presente em 19 cidades de 7 estados, em todas as 5 regiões do país. São 32 cursinhos, cerca de 5000 estudantes ao longo do ano e mais de 600 professores. Ao longo de sua história a Rede Emancipa já atendeu a mais de 20 mil estudantes, auxiliando milhares a entrar na

Name: Brackets For Good
Description:
Brackets For Good (BFG) is an Indianapolis-based nonprofit charitable organization that hosts single-elimination, bracket-style fundraising tournaments across the country. We focus on activating new donors and increasing awareness for nonprofits of all sizes. We are the only sport for nonprofits!

Brackets For Good capitalizes on the country’s obsession with the annual college basketball tournament and helps boost local philanthropy at a typically quiet time of the year (early Spring). Each nonprofit receives training, social media support, email plugs, and a personalized stat sheet to showcase their organization's purpose and gather names and emails of those interested in further supporting their missions.

Name: Red Crescent Society of Kazakhstan
Description:
Kazakh Red Crescent is part of the International Red Cross and Red Crescent Movement - the largest and most respected humanitarian community in the world, which exists in 190 countries. We train people in first aid techniques. Each year, our training sessions on first aid are visited by more than 1,000 people.
We are reviving the culture of free-willing blood donations

Name: Teia do Bem
Description: Assistência a pessoas em vulnerabilidade social e promoção da dignidade humana no bairro Jardim Canadá, Nova Lima/MG.

Name: Summit County CASA Program
Description:
CASA is a network of community-based programs that recruit, train and support citizen-volunteers to advocate for the best interests of abused and neglected children in courtrooms and communities.

Every day in this country, 1,900 children become victims of abuse or neglect, and four of them will die. Every day. Volunteer advocates—empowered directly by the courts—offer judges the critical information they need to ensure that each child’s rights and needs are being attended to while in foster care. Volunteers stay with children until they are placed in loving permanent homes. For many abused children, a CASA volunteer is the only constant adult presence in their lives.

Name: The Bernard Garrett Sr. Foundation
Description:
The Bernard Garrett Sr. Foundation was established to continue the legacy of economic freedom through advancing financial literacy and opportunity for African-Americans. Bernard Garrett Sr was the first African-American man to build a banking and real estate empire during the height of the racial divide in America. He built a legacy that continues to inspire today. There are many great African-American entrepreneurs, owners, developers, financiers, and investors waiting for their potential to be tapped.

In the United States, 44 million African-Americans account for 13% of the U.S. population and have a significant impact on the economy, with $1.2 trillion in annual purchases. With a 38% financial literacy rate, financial well-being of African-Americans lags that of the U.S. population as a whole. The reasons for these gaps are complex, but one area of importance in addressing them is increased financial literacy, as well as increased opportunity in finance. We believe that partnering with the best organizations - that bring together resources, expertise, and vision – can identify issues, find answers, and drive change. 

Our Mission:
Within a generation, lift African-American financial literacy rates from 38% to 50%. 
Within a generation, increase African-American employment in finance.
Within a generation, see African-Americans create generational wealth.

Name: Every Orphan's Hope
Description: Every Orphan's Hope shares the love of Jesus Christ with orphans in Zambia, Africa. We don't build orphanages. We build homes in AIDS-affected communities where orphans and widows build new lives together as a family. Guided by a "Mama," orphans are raised to life in Christ in the community where they are known and are surrounded by their peers. Jesus Christ invites you to join Him, together with us, in raising up a generation of children for His glory! Jesus is their hope - but He needs your heart and hands - He needs yours, too.

Name: Helping Hounds Dog Rescue
Description: The Helping Hounds Dog Rescue's mission is to take unwanted, neglected, abused, stray or homeless dogs from high-kill shelters and provide them with emotional, medical, behavioral and physical support so they can be adopted into loving permanent homes.

Name: La Maison des enfants de l'île de Montréal
Description:
La Maison des enfants de l'île de Montréal est un lieu unique créé selon les désirs des enfants il y a plus de 30 ans. Située en plein cœur du quartier Hochelaga-Maisonneuve, elle rayonne dans une quinzaine de quartiers de Montréal grâce à un partenariat avec une vingtaine d'écoles primaires. 

Appuyée par plus de 5000 heures de bénévolat chaque année, ce sont plus de 3000 enfants différents de 5 à 12 ans qui reçoivent une attention exceptionnelle d'un adulte par le biais de ses 4 programmes d'activités. Qu'il vienne s'amuser à La Maison, qu'il nous écrive une lettre ou qu'il se confie dans le « coin doux » de sa classe, l'enfant est écouté, entendu et considéré. 

1) L’Accueil des enfants: Les enfants sont accueillis dans notre belle maison centenaire par une coordonnatrice et une équipe de bénévoles formés. Ces derniers accompagnent les enfants qui ont la possibilité de s’amuser, de discuter et de faire les projets qui les intéressent.

2) L’Accueil des Groupes-Classes : Les professeurs qui souhaitent offrir un moment spécial aux élèves de leur classe réservent un avant-midi à la Maison des enfants. Accompagnée de parents volontaires, la classe passe un moment magique où ils s'amusent ensemble et se découvrent sous un autre jour.

3) L’Écoute en classe : Ce programme offre aux professeurs la possibilité de recevoir la présence d’un bénévole formé dans leur classe ou dans un local de l’école chaque semaine. Les enfants nous confirment que de se confier librement et en toute confidentialité à une adulte leur permets d’être mieux disposés pour leurs apprentissages, car ils ont « le cœur plus léger ».

4) Le Courrier des enfants : L’Objectif du Courrier aux enfants est d’offrir la parole aux enfants en les invitant à nous écrire leurs joies, leurs inquiétudes, leurs rêves, leurs peines. Les enfants nous écrivent librement, sans crainte d’être jugés.

Name: NGO AVEC (Aide Volontaire aux Enfants du Cambodge)
Description:
Since 2008, the NGO AVEC (Voluntary Aid for the Children of Cambodia) has stood as a bulwark against child trafficking and exploitation in Cambodia. In a country still scarred by poverty, corruption, and impunity, the most vulnerable children become easy targets. AVEC refuses to accept this fate.

Our mission is simple but vital: to protect, educate, and rebuild. Through our secure shelter, we welcome children who are victims or at risk, offering them a place of care, peace, and attentive listening. Here, they find a protective environment, access to education, healthy food, medical care—and most importantly, a future.

But AVEC is much more than just a roof over their heads. It’s a recreated family, psychosocial support, tailored schooling, and thoughtful reintegration into families whenever possible. We never give up on a child.

We work hand in hand with local authorities, social workers, teachers, psychologists, and volunteers from around the world to fight human trafficking sustainably and break the cycles of violence and poverty.

Name: Universal Society of Hinduism
Description:
Mission Statement - Motivating, educating and empowering people through Hinduism to make positive social, personal and environmental changes.

Purpose - By using the basic tenants of Hinduism, USOH will serve as the collaborative body to bring together people, organizations and resources to leave the world a better place for future generations.

Name: End Poverty. Make Trillions.
Description: Get a copy of “End Poverty. Make Trillions.” to learn how to make poverty a thing of the past.

Name: Building Block Foundation Fund
Description:
Building Block Foundation Fund (BBFF) is a philanthropic organization for the commercial real estate industry that brings together real estate professionals to give back in an organized and meaningful way for the long-term future of Orange County. BBFF funnels the goodwill of the local industry into a performance-based giving program that is sustainable, predictable and accountable. 

Our mission is to facilitate public / private partnerships that support programs aimed at encouraging underprivileged but deserving youth in Orange County to achieve more positive lifestyles. 

BBFF combines multiple resources, both financial and human, from the real estate community into a powerful support vehicle for effective programs targeting at-risk youth. Programs have helped to successfully mentor teens as well as move otherwise unskilled young adults into economically self-sustaining, skilled jobs and productive lives guided by fundamental values.

Name: INSTITUTO LACOS DE SOLIDARIEDADE
Description:
O Centro de Aconselhamento, Assistência Integral e Treinamento: INSTITUTO LAÇOS DE SOLIDARIEDADE é uma Instituição sem fins lucrativos, filantrópica, de direito privado, interdenominacional. Com Sede e Foro na cidade de Joinville-SC. 
Constituído em 05 de agosto de 2006, de direito privado, sem fins lucrativos, filantrópica, interdenominacional, de duração por tempo indeterminado, com sede e foro na Rua Sehnen, 85 Bairro Saguaçu - CEP 89221-015.no município de Joinville, Estado de Santa Catarina.
Missão

O Instituto Laços de Solidariedade trabalha na promoção à saúde: física-emocional, mental, social e espiritual dos assistidos pelos Programas bem como na formação e capacitação de voluntários.

Visão

Visão: Implantar um Centro de excelência, referência e consultoria, em Assistência Integral e Treinamento para formação de voluntários voltado ao terceiro setor na área de projetos sociais e de assistência.

População-Alvo

População que mora e/ou vive nas ruas
Dependentes de álcool e outras drogas
Portadores do HIV/doentes de AIDS
Pessoas com Problemas Emocionais Diversos
Projetos: Implantar um Complexo com: um Centro de Aconselhamento 24 Horas por Telefone e Atendimento Pessoal; Centro-dia de atenção à População de rua, Casa de Apoio; Casa de Passagem; República; Casas-Lares; Hospital de Desintoxicação e um Centro de Treinamento Integral.

Lema

Pergunta: NINGUÉM FAZ NADA???

Resposta: POR QUE NÃO COMEÇA POR VOCÊ???

(Agosto-1992)

Name: Casa do Produtor Rural - ESALQ/USP
Description:
Criada no dia 03 de junho de 2005, a Casa do Produtor Rural (CPRural)  é um projeto de extensão rural da ESALQ que dissemina o conhecimento desenvolvido no campus “Luiz de Queiroz”.

A proposta inicial foi criar um centro de atendimento ao produtor rural, mas o projeto foi além, atua como um elo entre a universidade e a comunidade rural, executando diversas atividades extensionistas. As informações geradas no meio acadêmico são decodificadas pela CPRural e divulgadas ao produtor em uma linguagem simples, didática e de fácil entendimento, para que possam ser aplicadas na propriedade rural.

O projeto atende produtores rurais de todo o Brasil e do exterior, por meio da elaboração gratuita de respostas técnicas; palestras e práticas no campo; publicações, visitas técnicas em propriedades rurais no município; vídeos técnicos e por meio de cultivos agrícolas que são apresentados aos produtores rurais. Todas as atividades são feitas de forma integrada com os docentes da universidade.

O público atendido é composto por produtores rurais, técnicos agropecuários, empreendedores e demais interessados no setor agropecuário.

Name: Opportunity Fund
Description:
Opportunity Fund is a Pittsburgh-based private foundation that awards grants to organizations and initiatives that advance the arts, and social and economic justice, with at least 75% of grants benefiting the greater Pittsburgh community. The Fund was established in 2015 and seeks grant partners who champion the rights of our communities’ diverse citizens, treasure the arts and support the lives of artists, pursue social and economic justice, and proactively provide safety net resources for those in need. Opportunity Fund awards grants to organizations and initiatives in the following funding areas. 

Arts
•	Performing Arts, with an emphasis on theatre, dance and music, especially classical chamber music and jazz, presented by small and mid-sized arts organizations
•	Visual Arts, with an emphasis on craft and small to mid-sized organizations

Social & Economic Justice
•	Civil Liberties
•	Civil Rights, with special interest in equality for African Americans, LGBTQ people and other minorities
•	Human Rights
•	Reproductive Freedom
•	Safety Net Resources for people in need, including social services, economic independence, housing, tangible aid, transportation, healthcare

Grant cycles take place twice a year. Letter of Inquiry deadlines are January 15 and July 15. Full information about applying for grants as well as a directory of funded programs and projects is available at www.theopportunityfund.org.

Name: The Planned Giving Council of Broward County
Description:
The Planned Giving Council of Broward County is a not-for-profit corporation created in response to a need for the dissemination of more information and the development of expertise in the field of Charitable Planned Giving. 

Our Purpose:

- Offer an educational forum in the many technical aspects, marketing strategies, and implementation procedures of an effective planned giving program.

-Provide the vehicle for networking of local professionals in the planned giving process.

-Promote increased charitable giving from the general public this providing a financial resource for bettering our community.

Name: The Good Samaritan Inn
Description:
The Good Samaritan Inn creates social and economic opportunity through nourishment, gardening, and education. Our vision is a community where all members have sustainable autonomy and lead a joyful life.

Currently, we have two main programs: The Noon Meal Program and the Mercy Gardens Program.

Noon Meal Program: Open Year-Round, 7 days a week serving a hot lunch 11:30AM-1pm. Sack lunches can be taken to-go after eating inside, or via our walk-up window during lunch service.

Mercy Gardens: We value growing fresh, nutritious food and using our gardens for job training and employment to help the community grow. We have gardens throughout Decatur including several around our building in the Old King's Orchard neighborhood.

We accept volunteers daily and love to partner with anyone and everyone wanting to serve those in need.

Name: Fondation Cédric Martin
Description:
La création de la Fondation est le fruit d’une longue et profonde réflexion de Danielle et Jacques Martin, parents de Cédric, pour perpétuer la mémoire et l’engagement de leur fils.

Constituée à Genève par acte notarié du 4 mai 2000, la Fondation Cédric Martin est une Fondation de droit privé, reconnue d’utilité publique, sans but lucratif.

Dirigée par un Conseil formé de cinq personnes, la Fondation œuvre de manière indépendante à la réalisation de projets humanitaires.

Son but, humanitaire, est de venir en aide à des communautés nécessitant une urgente amélioration de leur situation sanitaire, notamment en matière d’assainissement et de distribution des eaux.

Ces communautés peuvent se trouver tant en Suisse qu’à l’étranger. L’aide ne peut être dispensée que dans des régions sûres où toutes les conditions de sécurités sont garanties.

Le capital initial apporté par les fondateurs, ainsi que les fonds ultérieurs en provenance de généreux donateurs, ont permis de financer la totalité des projets menés par la Fondation. Depuis le début de son activité, l’aide financière s’est élevée à plusieurs millions de francs suisses.

Name: Africa New Day
Description: Africa New Day is a non-profit determined to end the cycles of violence, poverty, and inequality fostered by lack of knowledge, bad governance, and the absence of opportunities through sustainable and long-term solutions.

Name: RoundUp App
Description:
Donate your change to help create the change you want to see in the world. 

The RoundUp App makes it easy to donate the change from your credit/debit card purchases to support a nonprofit of your choice. For nonprofits, the RoundUp App is a turnkey fundraising tool that allows you to build a predictable monthly support base, so you can spend less time fundraising and more time making a difference.

Name: Heroes Supporting Heroes
Description:
The Vision of HSH is to support stable and safe housing to military members and their families, senior citizens and others in need in order to honor God!  

The mission of Heroes Supporting Heroes is to meet the basic life needs of others through: 
• Seeking to bear one another’s burdens
        • The foundation of HSH began with serving those in military uniform (past, present & families)
        • This remains a cornerstone of our organization, even as we have expanded into the civilian population

• Leveraging the gifts of our Servant Heroes
        • As our ranks grow with more gifts, we will continue to seek ways to maximize them
        • Everything from technical trades to hard-working individuals wanting to help others will shape our service offering

• Serving for the greater good
        • We seek to maximize the impact to our local communities by sponsoring the West Allegheny Workcamp and other projects to improve the lives of our neighbors

We are proud to be designated by the IRS as a 501(c)(3) organization, which allows all donations for Heroes Supporting Heroes to be tax-deductible!

Name: The Lewis Prize for Music
Description: The Lewis Prize for Music believes that music in the lives of young people is a catalytic force to drive positive change in our society. We’re committed to recognizing and investing in leaders who are advancing positive change for young people through music.

Name: Espoirs d'Enfants
Description:
Fondée en 2011, Espoirs d'Enfants est une ONG près de Rennes, en Bretagne.

L'association est créée suite à un voyage familial effectué en fin d'année 2010 au sein d'un orphelinat au Bénin, nommé « Espoir d’Enfant ».
Isabelle, Pierrick et leurs 3 enfants ont participé durant 3 semaines à la vie quotidienne de cet orphelinat, en mettant en place de nombreuses activités pour les 53 enfants âgés de 2 à 14 ans.
C’est à la suite de ce voyage qu’est née l’association Espoirs d’Enfants au pluriel, en référence à cet orphelinat. Notre ONG est aujourd'hui présente dans 4 pays : Bénin, Inde, Madagascar et Colombie. 

Nos objectifs sont de co-construire des projets économiques avec les familles et de re-scolariser et former les enfants et les jeunes afin de leur permettre de grandir et de se construire un avenir. 

Espoirs d'Enfants développe des actions à long terme selon une approche globale et spécifique et sensibilise aux droits de l'enfant. Les enfants et familles sont au cœur de nos actions.

Name: PEBA Onlus
Description:
"Affianchiamo le Pubbliche Amministrazioni nell'esecuzione dei PEBA. 
Realizziamo interventi di abbattimento delle barriere architettoniche a costo zero per la Pubblica Amministrazione patrocinante"

Name: COMUNIDADE INAMAR Educação e Assistência Social
Description:
A Comunidade Inamar Educação e Assistência Social é uma Organização Não Governamental, fundada em 1971, que tem como missão atender crianças e suas famílias em situação de vulnerabilidade social, promovendo a educação e a cidadania por meio da qualificação e preparo dos profissionais envolvidos.

Desde 1971, vem estudando formas simples, de baixo custo e de grande eficiência no campo da Educação Infantil na intenção de conscientizar a população de que todos têm direito a uma escola de qualidade.

Sua proposta pedagógica é diferenciada, inspirada nas melhores propostas do mundo. Em que todos os conceitos são aprendidos por meio de jogos e brincadeiras, propiciando o desenvolvimento completo da criança. Para tanto, a equipe pedagógica passa por treinamentos anuais, planejamentos mensais e reuniões diárias para que todas estejam alinhadas com a proposta.

A  Comunidade Inamar também possui um Núcleo de Assistência Social (NASCI) para atender as famílias, conscientizando-as e fortalecendo seus vínculos familiares. E ainda atender crianças e adolescentes no contra-turno escolar.

Name: Chicagoland Italian American Professionals
Description: Welcome to the Chicagoland Italian American Professionals.  We are a Chicago based organization dedicated to facilitating professional networking for all Chicagoland Italians, Italian Americans and Italophiles.  We also are dedicated to promoting Italian businesses, culture, language and lifestyle.  We look forward to having you join us at our next event.  Any questions, feel free to email me at salvatore@chicagolanditalians.com

Name: Tellus Mater Foundation
Description: The Tellus Mater Foundation is dedicated to exploring the potential for disruptive technologies to solve critical environmental and social challenges, and to educate key audiences about the solutions.

Name: Kapadia Education Foundation
Description: We are a 501(c)3 nonprofit organization with the mission to connect bright and dedicated students in the developing world with the mentorship and financial resources they need to pursue higher education and catalyze ongoing positive change in their home communities. Through lean and efficient operations, we cultivate dignified and mutually enriching cross-cultural relationships for students, staff, and supporters alike.

Name: Amrita-Seattle
Description:
We are a non-profit organization dedicated to providing basic education, nutrition, healthcare and clean water solutions to underprivileged communities in India and carry out food initiatives for homeless in US.

The focus is towards reaching out to communities which do not get adequate support and attention through government agencies, larger non-profits or other programs.

We identify partners who are local non-profits who have a proven track-record for working within their community.  We work with them to identify potential projects. As they implement the projects, we monitor their progress.

We fundraise in the US and India to support our ongoing projects.

All of our staff and contributors volunteer their time and effort so that we minimize administrative overhead.

Name: Club Social EHTP
Description: Le club social EHTP est un groupe des élèves ingénieurs, ayant le but de développer l’esprit d’initiative, de solidarité et de responsabilité.

Name: Rede de Educação Santos Anjos
Description: A Rede de Educação Santos Anjos é a união de instituições de ensino já consolidadas no mercado brasileiro por mais de um século. Conta com a força pedagógica dos principais sistemas de ensino do país e com a atuação de profissionais dispostos à educação transformadora, baseada na excelência acadêmica e realização humana.

Name: National Center for Missing & Exploitation Children, Pakistan. Managed by MSI
Description:
The National Center for Missing & Exploited Children Pakistan which is also known as Missing kids Pakistan or Missing Children Pakistan concept has been developed in 2001 launched in 2013 to serve as the nation’s clearing house on issues related to missing and sexually exploited children in Pakistan. Today NCMECP is authorized by independent governing body its funding organization National Trust of Pakistan to perform several programs and services to assist law enforcement, families and the professionals who serve them.

The leading organization in the Pakistan providing assistance to law enforcement and families to find missing children, reduce child sexual exploitation and prevent child victimization and saving the future generation.

NCMECP & National Trust of Pakistan role in this area will build on our existing strengths and expertise. The main areas we will focus on include:

A) Raising public awareness of the issues associated with missing children;
B) Developing strategic knowledge of missing children; providing practitioners with the knowledge, resources and skills to further their professional development; and Offering tactical support on complex cases to police forces.
C) Developing facial recognition application.

History
Conceptualised in 2001, the National Center for Missing & Exploited ChildrenPakistan NCMECP (http://misskingkids.org.pk  or http://missingchildren.org.pk or http://www.ncmecp.org ) is the only leading organization in the Pakistan working with law enforcement, families and the professionals who serve them on issues related to missing and sexually exploited children.

As part of its National Trust of Pakistan authorization, NCMECP creating a unique public and private partnership to build a coordinated, national response to the problem of missing and sexually exploited children, establish a missing children hotline and serve as the national clearinghouse for information related to this core issue.

Local and Overseas Pakistani can join through a program called Act for Pakistan too.

Name: Ubbo Emmius Fonds
Description: The philanthropic foundation for the University of Groningen

Name: Fondation des Gouverneurs de l'espoir
Description:
La Fondation des Gouverneurs de l’espoir essentially does two things: funding pediatric cancer research programs and financially supporting Quebec families whose children have cancer and who are in serious financial difficulty. Since 2002, the Foundation has collected $ 13 million by holding various fundraising activities.

This large amount has been invested in various research, support and direct support programs for families. 
• François-Karl Viau Research Chair in Pediatric Oncogenomics, the only one in Canada 
• 6 rooms for palliative care at CHU Ste-Justine 
• Highly specialized equipment 
• Funding of the Center of Excellence in pediatric palliative care 
• Recurrent donations to the families with sick children.
La Fondation des Gouverneurs de l’espoir is a major partner of the Sainte-Justine Hospital Foundation and CHU Sainte-Justine.

Name: Haiti Teen Challenge
Description: We believe that change in Haiti will occur when the male youth of Haiti are transformed by God and equipped to lead their nation. We seek this change by equipping male youth to become godly leaders, by collaborating with others to maximize resources and long-term impact, and by seeking to bring restoration to the lives of these men, their families, communities, and Haiti as a nation.

Name: Miss Amazing Inc.
Description:
Started in Omaha, Nebraska by a thirteen-year-old girl with a big dream, Miss Amazing Inc. has grown from a small community program into the nation’s largest empowerment event circuit exclusively for girls and women with disabilities. Our mission is simple: To provide opportunities for girls and women with disabilities to build confidence and self-esteem in a supportive environment.

We’ve seen proof that, if granted the opportunity, girls and women with disabilities show great potential to be leaders, role models, and valuable members of society. Our state and national programs aim to open those doors.

Since expanding nationwide for the first time in 2011, Miss Amazing programs are now in 30 states from coast to coast and have touched the lives of nearly 1500 girls and women with disabilities.

Name: Hunter Street Charity
Description:
The Hunter Street Charity is a non-profit organization and shall be operated exclusively for the support and financial assistance to children and families in need during critical junctures of their lives, for charitable purposes.

Ideal candidates are children or families in need during critical junctures of their lives. A special focus on providing emergency funds for home, transportation, and education, college funding support, addiction, health or psychiatric services or health care costs. Ideal candidates may show these attributes:

Perseverance
-Overcome a notable challenge in their life
-Proven willingness to work hard
-Demonstrated willingness to grow and learn

Belief in their future success
-Social Grace
-Inclusive
-Participates in volunteer programs

Willing to contribute to the greater good
-Financial Need
-Median to low-income families or individuals needing to overcome recent catastrophe: death, house fire, eviction, or escaping abuse
-Median to low-income families or individuals with an immediate critical need: rent, utilities, or car repair
-Median to low-income families or individuals with a financial need for drug rehabilitation, psychiatric services, or healthcare expense
-Median to low-income children and first-time family college-bound students

Name: Williamsburg Christadelphian Foundation
Description: The Williamsburg Christadelphian Foundation strives to help individuals, families, and groups (ecclesias) grow their faith.  We aim to be a catalyst for good by supplying faith-building tools.  We also seek to inspire and galvanize collective purpose by bringing together faith-seeking people in common service.

Name: Black Women for Black Girls Giving Circle
Description: Black Women for Black Girls is a giving circle that seeks to amass knowledge and financial resources that will support organizations committed to the empowerment of black girls in New York City.

Name: Cincinnati Area Chapter of the American Red Cross
Description: Local chapter of the American Red Cross.

Name: Charlotte Angels
Description:
Charlotte Angels is a non-profit organization founded in 2019 as a chapter of National Angels. The mission of Charlotte Angels is to walk alongside children, youth, and families in the foster care community by offering consistent support through intentional giving, relationship building, and mentorship.


The vision of Charlotte Angels is to create sustainable change in the foster care system here in North Carolina and beyond through the Love Box and Dare to Dream programs. This is accomplished through the deliverance of core values surrounding community, empowerment and hope. 

Connecting volunteers to children and parents in the foster care system through the Love Box and Dare to Dream programs is the vehicle through which Charlotte Angels and all the other National Angels chapters are working to change lives. 

For more information about these programs, or to learn how to sign up to volunteer, please visit CharlotteAngels.org.

Name: Catalyst Games
Description:
Team building meets charitable giving during this annual competition filled with networking, fun and philanthropy. Startup Games is a high energy event engaging startups in tournament-style games for charity. Young professionals and senior executives play side-by-side in ping pong, dodgeball, Mario Kart 64 and more. Each startup plays for the charity of their choice, and every charity walks away with a grant for their organization. The largest portion of the funds raised go to the charities of the 1st, 2nd and 3rd place teams. Founded in 2012, Startup Games has given away over $600,000 to local charities while fueling inspiring, purpose-filled team activities.

How does it work?
1. Companies and communities enter teams in our citywide office Olympics.
2. Their registration fees create a pool of charitable donations.
3. Each team selects a partner nonprofit.
4. On game day, teams battle it out in fun events like dodgeball, ping pong & Mario Kart.
5. The better each team does, the more money they win for their favorite nonprofit.
6. Every charity gets a donation, no matter what.

Name: Rotary Club for Global Action
Description:
Welcome to the LinkedIn page for the Rotary Club for Global Impact in District 5150, San Francisco Bay Area, California, USA.   The Rotary Club for Global Action is a new Rotary eClub made up of diverse members from around the world. The club focuses on international service and cooperation with digital events to build empathy across cultures.
As a common courtesy, please ONLY POST content relating to Rotary Club for Global Action.   
If you would like to be a charter member or would like to attend one of our meetings please email: rotaryglobalaction@gmail.com   

OUR MEETINGS  We meet online via zoom video-conferencing software leased by our E-Club.

Name: 100 Black Men of South Metro Atlanta, Inc.
Description: The South Metro chapter of The 100 Black Men of America, Inc. is an award-winning 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization that serves to enhance educational and economic opportunities and improve the quality of life throughout south Atlanta and beyond.

Name: America for Animals
Description:
America for Animals is a new type of animal welfare organization – one that empowers advocates to promote compassion and fight cruelty in the communities where they live.  We are building state-of-the-art web and mobile applications (“apps”) to help advocates connect, organize, participate, and, most importantly, make a difference in the lives of animals.  

These new technologies hold tremendous potential for doing good.  We see cities that are full of animal advocates who have no outlet for their passion; of groundbreaking animal welfare organizations that no one has even heard of; of horrible animal abuse that remains hidden from concerned citizens.  Through well-designed and easy-to-use web and mobile apps, we believe we can change this reality. 

America for Animals is a 501(c)3 nonprofit organization.  We are headquartered in San Diego, but work with highly skilled volunteers around the world.

Name: Venn Foundation
Description:
Venn Foundation is a nonprofit public charity on a mission to unleash the full power of Program-Related Investments (PRIs) to achieve charitable impact.  Our vision is to create a new category of flexible, below-market-rate investment capital that can be directed creatively to projects and organizations across all sectors that are helping advance charitable goals.  

By opening a special donor-advised fund called a Venn Account, any philanthropic individual or entity can recommend that their tax-advantaged, charitable donations be used by Venn to make PRIs. Venn can syndicate any one PRI among any combination of Venn Accounts. Financial returns from these PRIs go back to participating Venn Accounts for donors to recommend redeployment into new PRIs or as grants to nonprofits.

Name: OIC Cambodia
Description:
Many Cambodians have a communication or swallowing disorder, which has a huge impact on their quality of life. Yet there are few services to help them. OIC Cambodia is going to change that in three ways:
1 – by providing training in speech therapy across Cambodia
2 – by advocating at every level of community and government for speech therapy
3 – by establishing a speech therapy program at a university. 

We do not directly provide speech therapy services. Rather, we are establishing speech therapy as a profession in Cambodia, to ensure there are Cambodian speech therapists within the country that can offer comprehensive and sustainable speech therapy services.

Currently, we are focusing on a training program for teachers, health professionals and government officials. Educating teachers will better integrate students with communication and swallowing disorders into their classrooms.

Educating health professionals will help people with communication and swallowing disorders be more accurately diagnosed and treated. Educating government officials will strengthen the political will to provide speech therapy services across the country.

Get connected with OIC Cambodia:
www.oiccambodia.org
www.facebook.com/oiccambodia
www.twitter.com/oiccambodia
www.vimeo.com/oiccambodia

Newsletter signup: http://eepurl.com/8wVKX

Description:
An utmost favourable hub for the growth & mutual betterment of the novice & rising motivators.

• Spreading Happiness and supporting anyone who works as a depression counselor1.

Name: BIF MARAUDE
Description:
BIF Maraude est une organisation à but non lucratif créée en 2019 par deux jeunes issus de la banlieue nord parisienne (95) - Cheyms et Redha - avec l’aide de leurs amis. 
Aujourd’hui, plus de 300 bénévoles soutiennent notre cause. 

L’objectif de nos actions est de lutter contre la faim et l’exclusion sociale. Il ne s’agit pas seulement d’apporter une aide matérielle aux personnes dans le besoin ; nous souhaitons par-dessus tout créer des liens avec ces personnes. Au départ, l’association distribuait chaque dimanche et ce toute l’année,  des centaines de repas aux alentours des différentes gares parisiennes (Gare de Lyon, Gare du Nord, Gare de l’Est, etc.). 
De surcroît, l’association a déjà effectué des maraudes dites fixes à Porte d’Aubervilliers. Cette année, nous avons diversifié les actions en faisant des distributions mensuelles de colis solidaires à l'intention des familles et étudiants dans le besoin ainsi que des maraudes Petits Dej' les samedis. Les maraudes Petits Dej' sont l’occasion d’échanger d’autant plus avec nos bénéficiaires. 

Si vous souhaitez nous rejoindre en tant que bénévole, je vous prie de vous diriger directement sur notre site internet et de cliquer sur la rubrique « Nous Rejoindre ».

Name: Unicum Stiftung
Description:
Gemeinnützige Stiftung
Anbieter eines mehrfach ausgezeichneten Jobportals für Praktikanten, Werkstudenten und Hochschulabsolventen
Betreiber des studentischen Ratgeberportals unicum.de

Name: Yayasan SoleFamily Bali
Description:
Our mission is to help the disadvantaged in Bali, Indonesia.

Yayasan Solemen Indonesia (Solemen), an Indonesian registered non-profit Foundation, was formed in October 2010 to raise awareness and provide funds to support accredited agencies and projects for the disadvantaged in Bali.  Sole Man Robert declared his intention to be barefoot until SOLEMEN had raised one million US Dollars.  The reason for being barefoot is “to be in solidarity with those who don’t have a choice to wear or not wear shoes”.

Solemen‘s first ‘awareness’ 535km barefoot walk around Bali was not just a ‘walk’.  The Solemen held frequent health education checks and made presentations in schools, orphanages and villages together with a medical team from Anak Anak Bali (Bali Kids).  The Solemen also taught the children to be aware of and how to clean up their immediate environment.

As well as raising awareness, we are now primarily focusing our efforts on helping the disadvantaged in Bali, Indonesia.  We are constantly looking for donations not just in monetary terms but also food, clothing, mobility/disability aids, sponsors and more.

Name: Hope Funds for Cancer Research
Description:
MISSION:
The mission of the Hope Funds for Cancer Research is to encourage investigation of innovative cancer treatment and detection for the most difficult-to-treat and understudied cancers. The Hope Funds for Cancer Research supports scientific and medical research programs aimed at increasing knowledge relating to both cancer care and prevention. We support programs we believe have the highest probability of success in addressing unmet medical needs, which we will determine by rigorous scientific and economic analysis.

Name: Covid Grief Network
Description:
The COVID Grief Network, a project of Reimagine, is an international mutual aid initiative that connects young adults in their 20s and 30s grieving the death of someone to COVID-19 with free group grief support--and a community that knows what it's like. 

To learn more, and to join the network as a young adult (or to volunteer your time as a grief worker), visit our website at: www.covidgriefnetwork.org.

Name: Sayfty
Description: Sayfty works with communities to facilitate open conversations with the aim to bring a fundamental shift in how violence against women is perceived

Name: Earthlings Trust
Description:
Founded in December 2018 by Sagarika Banerjee & Ditej Garg, Earthlings Trust is a Noida-based NGO committed to giving dogs a good chance at life. From rescuing abandoned and injured dogs to providing medical care, vaccinations and sterilizations, we ensure that every animal receives the attention they deserve.

Our work extends beyond rescue - we actively facilitate adoptions, helping dogs find loving homes, and promote peaceful human-animal coexistence through education, our programs (virtual adoption, twilight sponsor program and foster parent program) and outreach. With 400+ rescues and a growing network of veterinarians and volunteers, we are creating a world where no dog is left behind.

With 400+ dogs rescued and counting, we stand by one belief - every wagging tail is a life worth saving. Join us in rewriting their stories.

Name: Analytical Ones
Description:
We use market research and data analytics to help nonprofits win new donors, lift the performance of current donors and keep active donors longer.

We focus on service directly to nonprofit organizations as well as marketing agencies that serve the nonprofit industry.

Our services include:

Data Analytics - A deep dive into your donor data to find areas of opportunity as well as predict performance in the future

Market Research - Focus groups, in-depth interviews and online surveys of your current donors and prospective donors

Data Modeling - Mid and major donor modeling services to help target donors most likely to upgrade their giving

Message Testing - Identify what messages resonate with your donors so you can speak directly to your donors interests

To learn more about how we can help you win more donors, lift the performance of your current donors and keep your active donors longer contact us today.

Name: Support Humanity
Description: We are helping people in disaster.

Name: Pokelanthropy Inc.
Description: The Original Pokémon Charity: To date succesfully donated 200,000+ cards, toys, and accessories to kid in need. Primarily Partners: Shriner's Hospitals for Children & The Ronald McDonald House Charities. Internationally. Plus dozens of other agencies. Our focus is foster homes, hospitals, and homeless shelters. We provide smiles, love, and comfort to famlies in need all over the globe. We strive to keep the hobby alive by repurposing bulk cards, toys, and other Pokémon related items to bring happiness wherever a smile is needed.

Name: Elbi Digital
Description:
Elbi brings innovation to charity and philanthropy. We remove the barriers to charitable giving by making it simple, fun and rewarding for people to make a difference, while giving great causes much needed visibility and support. By connecting digital do-gooders with charities through the Elbi platform we’ve so far helped 854 causes, run by 37 charities, in 88 countries across 5 continents.

The beauty is in the simplicity. Every day the Elbi community participate in 3 daily causes and do their bit to help. It’s as straightforward as sending a picture to help teach a class, sharing a fun fact to raise awareness, or taking a selfie to brighten someone’s day in hospital.

Every time a user creates content in support of a campaign, they earn points. If they love someone else’s creation or the campaign, that’s a $1 micro-donation to the charity. If their creation is loved, they earn points. These points equate to LoveCoins which users can spend in the LoveShop to buy products from our brand partners.

This is philanthropy for a mobile, digital age.

Name: All We Are Nonprofit
Description:
All We Are is on a mission to improve infrastructure and transform communities in Uganda through sustainable energy solutions. Since launching our solar program in 2015, we've completed 60 solar installations, impacting 140,000+ people every day and raising over $1 million to support this work. Our proven project model blends local leadership, thoughtful design, and long-term sustainability to drive lasting impact.

From brighter classrooms and safer streets to cleaner clinics and more resilient communities, we believe reliable energy is the foundation for opportunity, and a more equitable future.

Name: Robert W Deutsch Foundation
Description: The Robert W. Deutsch Foundation invests in innovative people, projects, and ideas that improve the quality of life in Baltimore and beyond.

Name: The Luv u Project
Description:
Carolyn Mattingly understood what it meant to love.

Often signing her notes with “Luv u,” she brought a special grace to even the simplest exchange. Carolyn’s warm heart and genuine concern for making a difference was also demonstrated every day—through her countless commitments to family, causes, and community leadership.

On September 30, 2014, her life was abruptly, violently, and senselessly ended. In memoriam of Carolyn and as a tribute to her goodness, The Luv u Project has been established as a lasting commitment from her husband, daughter, family, and friends to continue her legacy.

The mission of The Luv u Project is to turn an unacceptable tragedy into a quantifiable agenda and responsible actions that advance the understanding of, and treatments for, mental health issues.

Name: APAE-DF
Description: Somos uma Organização da Sociedade Civil, fundada em 1964, que promove atendimentos especializados em favor de pessoas com deficiências intelectual e múltipla a partir dos 14 anos. Nossas unidades promovem ações de educação e assistência social com focos na autonomia de vida e inclusão na sociedade, especialmente no mundo do trabalho.

Name: Hudson Valley Honor Flight
Description:
Hudson Valley Honor Flight (“HVHF”) honors American Veterans for all their sacrifices.  We bring Veterans from the Hudson Valley on an unforgettable one-day trip to Washington, D.C. to be honored at their memorials. All Veterans go on this journey of honor at absolutely no cost to them.  
 
Currently we fly four times a year; twice from Stewart Airport, and twice from Westchester Airport.  The day begins with an amazing motorcycle escort to the airport, followed by a Send Off ceremony – giving the veterans a small glimpse into the exciting day ahead of them.  While in Washington DC, the group visits the memorials built in their honor and Arlington National Cemetery to see the Changing of the Guard.  Our group is hailed with applause and admiration at every stop, giving these heroes the recognition they rightfully deserve.  We keep the veterans on their toes, throwing in many surprises throughout the day.  The experience is concluded when the veterans arrive back in NY, and are greeted by hundreds of community members cheering them on.  For many, it is the Welcome Home Reception they never received.

Top priority is given to those veterans of the Greatest Generation, that of WWII, and terminally ill veterans from other wars.  We have begun to take Korean War veterans on this memorable journey, and will eventually move on to the Vietnam War, as well.

Name: Galcom International Canada
Description: Galcom exists to give help and tools to the missionaries that have answered the call of Christ to “go into all the world and preach the gospel to every creature.” By providing solar-powered, fix-tuned radios, installing radio stations, and using audio technology, Galcom helps missionaries multiply their impact and reach more people in a larger area with the Gospel every day.

Name: Ball 4 a Cure, Inc.
Description: Ball 4 a Cure Inc. unites people and communities using sports as a vehicle to positively transform lives. Over the past six years, our organization has hosted six tournaments with hundreds of participants from all over the Northeast. The mission of these events is to spread awareness about pediatric cancer and raise money for the Oncology department at The Connecticut Children’s Medical Center in Hartford, CT. Ball 4 a Cure Inc. helps fund various programs to improve the quality of life for patients and their families in need of financial and emotional support.

Name: Bathtub 2 Boardroom
Description:
Bathtub 2 Boardroom is a not-for-profit charity that manages a coworking space, helping people create jobs for themselves and others through entrepreneurship and creativity. 

We provide any body wanting to start and grow a business with a wealth of resources, such as training, mentoring, and a vibrantly diverse start-up community. 

Our low cost work spaces are a ready made home-from-home for new entrepreneurs. We have shared workspace on City road

If you'd like to find out more about membership, partnering or events, please get in touch on hello@bathtub2boardroom.com, stalk us on Twitter and instagram @bath2board or pop in and say hello at 263-269 City Road, London EC1V 1JX and we'll pop the kettle on.

Name: Community Foundation of Tompkins County
Description:
The Community Foundation of Tompkins County is dedicated to working with all people committed to local philanthropy and civic engagement as a means to enhance the quality of life for all who live in, work in, and love Tompkins County.

The mission of our Community Foundation is to bring people and resources together to build a more equitable Tompkins County by inspiring diverse philanthropy, catalyzing collaborations, and championing inclusion and justice. 

Vision Statement: Tompkins County thrives thanks to engaged philanthropy.

Our Values: 
• Community-driven
• Engagement 
• Integrity
• Racial Equity
• Relationships 
• Social Justice

Name: Stichting Kibos
Description:
De stichting stelt zich ten doel om bij kinderen in Suriname die bedreigd zijn in hun omgeving en/of hun ontwikkeling, netwerken te stimuleren voor de acute opvang en het opstarten van projecten ter bevordering van hun ontwikkeling.

De stichting tracht haar doel onder meer te verwezenlijken door het organiseren van bijscholingen voor zorgprofessionals, het opzetten van intersectorale netwerken en opzetten van projecten ter bevordering van de ontwikkeling van deze kinderen. De stichting dient het algemeen belang. De stichting heeft geen winstoogmerk en de bestuurders ontvangen geen beloning voor hun werkzaamheden.

Name: Children's Craniofacial Association
Description:
Children's Craniofacial Association is a national, 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization, headquartered in Dallas, Texas, but serving the entire US and beyond. Nationally and internationally, CCA addresses the medical, financial, psychosocial, emotional, and educational concerns relating to craniofacial conditions.

CCA's mission is to empower and give hope to individuals and families affected by facial differences.

CCA envisions a world where all people are accepted for who they are, not how they look.

At CCA, we are making the world a kinder place for all people.

We fund three main programs:
1.	Financial Assistance for children and families traveling to receive quality medical care. CCA can help with ancillary expenses not covered by insurance, such as food, travel, and lodging when traveling for surgery or mdical procedures.
2.	We host regional gatherings for families to meet each other and develop friendships, including our Annual Family Retreat and Educational Symposium, where families gather to hear medical and mental health professionals present and conduct workshops on emerging research, self-esteem issues, self-care, parenting, and more. 
3.	Our Speakers' Bureau and educational curriculum are available to teachers and educators. Buy booking a speaker or engaging with our materials, we hope audiences will come away with a deeper understanding and appreciation of the human experience and learn that we are truly more alike than different. Our goals with this program are to decrease bullying, cultivate conversations, and inspire acceptance that combats the stigma of living with a facial difference. 
You can learn more at www.ccakids.org.

Name: GiveDirect
Description:
GiveDirect Mission: Building the best blockchain direct giving solutions to reduce global suffering.

The GiveDirect team is focused on reducing human suffering in Ghana, while operating within a highly secure transparent donation network, using blockchain technology.

Name: 100+ Women Who Care Fairfield County
Description:
We are a group of women who gather four times a year for a one-hour meeting to fundraise in order to give to those in greatest need in Fairfield County, CT. Each quarter, we select a local charity as a group and we each donate $103 ($100 donation + $3 processing fee).

This combined donation of $10,000 makes a real impact right here in Fairfield County, CT, without time-consuming fundraising events and planning. And it takes only an hour. No volunteering, no telemarketing, no event planning, no silent auction donations to solicit. Simply a great way to give back right in our own community.

You'll be amazed by the level of efficiency and the level of FUN!

For more information email: 100WWCFairfieldCounty@gmail.com

Name: Mighty Meredith Project
Description:
Mighty Meredith Project, Inc. is an IRS recognized 501(c)(3) non-profit organization established by Meredith Casey and her family to raise awareness of Traumatic Brain Injuries (TBI) and the impact of having a hidden illness/disease, with the message of kindness as the beacon of the organization.  It was from the kindness of others that got Meredith through some of her darkest days.   

Meredith hit her head in December 2015. After months of attempting to recover from an initial diagnosis of a concussion, it was also determined Meredith had a blood clot in her brain, due to the head trauma. The associated head trauma has required Meredith to no longer participate in many of the activities she came to love and has changed her outlook on life. The Mighty Meredith Project was created after Meredith settled into a new life of living with traumatic brain injury in an effort for Meredith to create her “new normal”.  

The purpose of Mighty Meredith Project) is simple: 

Mighty Kind:  The Mighty Meredith Project promotes the impact of kindness, especially to those "who are fighting a battle you know nothing about."

Mighty Giving:  The Mighty Meredith Project gives back to the medical community where Meredith has and continues to receive treatment, and to those who may be suffering in silence.

Mighty Smart:  The Mighty Meredith Project is dedicated to bringing education and awareness to traumatic brain injuries and more specifically, the impacts of having a "hidden disease". 

To learn more about the Meredith and the Mighty Meredith Project, please visit our website at www.mightymeredith.com.

Name: Salem Bin Mahfouz Foundation (SBMF)
Description:
