Name: The Vladimir Potanin Foundation
Description:
The Vladimir Potanin Foundation was established in 1999 as a private foundation by the Russian entrepreneur Vladimir Potanin to help nurture active and creative professionals and to develop philanthropy in Russia. One of the largest private philanthropic organization in Russia with a longstanding commitment to building capacity for professional development, the Vladimir Potanin foundation is a member of the European Foundation Centre. 

The Foundation mission is to foster the development of knowledge and professionalism, to encourage volunteer activity and individual creativity, and to elevate the development of philanthropy in Russia.
The core grant-making areas of the Foundations are education, philanthropy development, arts and culture.

Name: African Orphan Educational Foundation
Description:
African Orphan Educational Foundation is a tax exempt 501(c)(3) charitable organization.  We equip, educate and empower orphans and vulnerable youth in Africa.  At-risk children in the villages we serve are personally identified by our mission and aid partners; we then align children with caring individuals who will cover their primary, secondary and post-secondary educational expenses and personal development needs. 

By concentrating all resources on education, we are able to produce greater learning outcomes for more students while providing needed support to the U.S. mission and aid organizations who maintain operations on the ground.  Currently we are operating in Uganda, Kenya and Burundi.

In sub-Saharan Africa, 32 million children of primary school age are out of school.  And, in the countries we serve, less than 25% of youth will complete the equivalent of a high school education.  An education offers hope and expands opportunities in these regions largely plagued by conflict, HIV/AIDS and other diseases, poor healthcare and sanitation, job and food insecurity, and extreme poverty.

Student sponsorship fees cover a student's school tuition, school supplies, personal requirements, a school uniform, shoes and a backpack, at least one hot meal per day at school, basic healthcare (in locales where provided by the school or affiliated clinic), preferred schools, gap protection, a proprietary leadership curriculum and international placement program (in development).

Name: 1 Million Cups Seattle
Description:
This page is for the Seattle / Puget Sound area chapter of 1 Million Cups.
The official site is here: https://www.1millioncups.com/seattle

Ewing Marion Kauffman Foundation developed 1 Million Cups (1MC) in 2012. 1MC is free program designed to educate, engage and connect entrepreneurs with their communities. 

We work with entrepreneurs offering them tools and resources to break down barriers that stand in the way of starting and growing their businesses. Each week a community member will brief the 1MC Seattle Community about their business. The format is a six-minute time slot with a 20-minute Q&A.

Banner photo credit:  1MC Member Amy Corcoran

Name: SoCal Latinx in Philanthropy
Description:
Southern California Latinx in Philanthropy (SCLP) is a network made up of volunteer grantmaking professionals who are working to leverage networks and resources in order to mitigate the impact of historical and systemic inequities in the Latinx community. We convene funders in the greater Los Angeles area to connect, learn, and take action with other funders to create collaborative impact across Southern California. We work in partnership with other affinity groups to build racial solidarity while also advancing opportunities for change. SCLP leads with philanthropic practices to create an engaged philanthropic ecosystem that supports healthy and thriving Latinx communities. 


SCLP offers a place for philanthropic professionals to:
 - Network and build relationships with knowledgeable, collegial peers through learning and social events and convenings.
 - Deepen knowledge and learn about issues and innovations through curated conversations with sector leaders and other experts to help optimize grantmaking.
 - Connect and align with funders on Latinx community-related issues of shared interest.

Name: Vaincre les maladies Lysosomales
Description:
Depuis 1990, Vaincre les Maladies Lysosomales (VML) est l'association des patients, parents et tous ceux qui se battent pour vaincre les 60 maladies génétiques rares lysosomales. 

Association de patients reconnue d'Utilité Publique depuis 2006. Nos actions sont financés par la générosité de nos actions terrains, nos donateurs, mécènes et partenaires. A ce titre nous nous devons d'être transparents et efficaces dans la gestion de cette générosité publique et sommes doublement agréés par le "Don en Confiance" et  le "Label IDEAS". 

La solidarité entre patients et familles est au cœur du projet associatif.  Un projet consolidé par une équipe professionnelle au service des missions. 

Des missions au service de la communauté des malades :
- Une action d'entraide, de soutien, d'information, d'accompagnement pour mieux vivre la maladie, ses évolutions, les bouleversements familiaux, sociaux au quotidien.
- Une politique de financement et soutien à des équipes de recherches scientifiques internationales pour créer des traitements pour ces maladies. 
- Une action de représentativité des patients auprès des différentes instances nationales, européennes et internationales. 
- Un rôle d'information pour mieux faire connaitre ces maladies afin de mieux les diagnostiquer et prendre en charge.

Name: N
Description: N

Name: Rockland Community Foundation
Description: The Rockland Community Foundation was created as a smarter way for individuals, families and businesses to engage in charitable giving. We connect donors with the causes that matter most to them, through the establishment of funds that they create and we steward.  Rather than giving once to a charity, monies placed into these funds are responsibly invested, and can be added to by the fund creator or others so that the fund continues to benefit its specific cause for many generations.

Name: AMCA - Association d'aide médicale Centro América (Suisse romande)
Description: AMCA - Association pour l'aide médicale Centro América travaille en étroite collaboration avec AMCA, née au Tessin, qui porte depuis plus de 30 ans des projets humanitaires, solidaires et équitables dans plusieurs pays d’Amérique centrale. Autour d'experts médicaux issus du CHUV, AMCA - Association pour l'aide médicale Centro América met en oeuvre plusieurs projets afin de soutenir des enfants et des femmes atteints de cancer, principalement au Nicaragua. AMCA - Association pour l'aide médicale Centro América est composée d'une équipe 100% bénévole

Name: Fond du Lac Area Foundation
Description: The Fond du Lac Area Foundation is a community foundation that exists for the good of the community. The Foundation is a 501(c)3 organization with a purpose to build a permanent and lasting endowment to support the charitable needs of Fond du Lac County, now and forever. Since 1975, the Fond du Lac Area Foundation has partnered with generous individuals, families, and businesses to strengthen our community through charitable giving. The Foundation is dedicated to helping others achieve their charitable dreams, no matter how big or small.

Name: The Rosenberg Foundation
Description:
Together with our grantees and peers in philanthropy, we work to advance justice by dismantling the systemic barriers that stand in the way for far too many Californians, particularly Black communities and other communities of color.

For more than 80 years we have provided close to 3,000 grants totaling nearly $90 million to regional, statewide and national organizers and advocates who are making freedom and opportunity for all possible.

Today, through our Leading Edge Fund, we continue to support courageous and innovative leaders who are at the forefront of confronting racial and economic injustice. Through our core grantmaking, we currently focus on ending mass incarceration, transforming inhumane and destructive immigration policies, stopping the exploitation of low-wage and immigrant workers, tackling child poverty, and strengthening civil rights advocacy in California.

Name: The Saville Foundation
Description: As supporters of education, entrepreneurship and enablement of individuals, we operate across multiple sectors internationally, always from a unique place of receptivity. In all we do, we look to be humble, mindful, and flexible. We are always willing to evolve and adapt - listening deeply to what is actually required - and look to activate meaningful long-term partnerships underpinned by open feedback and appropriate strategic or other guidance and support.

Name: iguacu
Description:
For 5 years to 2018, the non-profit innovator iguacu (igwah-soo) rigorously investigated serious humanitarian crises around the world, raising awareness of great suffering and identifying exceptional charities the public could support with confidence. The mission of iguacu was to make it effortless to give effectively to where the needs are great.

No longer active, you can read about our work here: https://medium.com/@weareiguacu

The iguacu archive is maintained by www.linkedin.com/in/davieswriter

Name: Nineteen, a Texas Women's Foundation Network
Description:
Nineteen honors the 19 trailblazing founders of Texas Women's Foundation through an annual giving society made up of dynamic individuals who leverage their passion, gifts and leadership to elevate Texas women and girls and build equitable communities across our state. 

Nineteen contributions support Texas Women’s Foundation research, advocacy and grantmaking to ignite positive change in economic security and leadership opportunities for women and their families.

Name: Steam4all
Description:
Actualmente en nuestro país, el 90% de los puestos de trabajo exigen competencias digitales. 

Las necesidades de perfiles laborales polivalentes con capacidad analítica y creativa que genera la 4ª Revolución Industrial, requieren introducir en la escuela el concepto de Escuela 4.0 como un entorno pedagógico más, donde las enseñanzas transversales de “ciencias” puedan ser exploradas y consolidadas en entornos físico-virtuales, abordando el aprendizaje competencial para resolver problemas reales desarrollando el pensamiento innovador y estratégico.

Por otro lado, las escuelas rurales son el agente de cambio necesario para evitar la despoblación y cohesionar pequeñas poblaciones y territorios.

Steam4All, es una asociación sin animo de lucro,  nace con el objetivo de promover la colaboración público-privada para promocionar las STEAM (Science, Technology, Engineering, Arts and Maths) en el alumnado de las escuelas rurales incorporando una perspectiva de genero.

Name: Global Research and Consulting Group (U of T Chapter)
Description:
The Global Research and Consulting (GRC) Group is an internationally-oriented educational non-profit with an expansive network of students from universities all over the world. Our mission is to drive global social impact change and empower students to become responsible future leaders, and we have completed over 60 projects with past clients including UNESCO, World Bank, Wikipedia, Oxfam, WWF, and many more.

As a part of the GRC, you become part of a powerful and growing network of current students and alumni who have gone on to work at McKinsey, BCG, and Bain in the consulting sphere, Goldman Sachs and Morgan Stanley in the financial services industry, Silverlake, Bain Capital, and Point72 on the buy-side, as well as at the United Nations and various social impact non-profits.

At the University of Toronto's GRC Chapter, we look for candidates that are effective team players, passionate about social impact and have diverse interests. By working with like-minded students at U of T and other prestigious universities to engage in impactful pro-bono projects, the GRC will support you in your personal and professional development journey. Our team is growing so feel free to reach out to any of us to learn more about the club and how you can get involved!

Name: Microfinancing Partners in Africa
Description:
Microfinancing Partners in Africa works with organizations in Africa in five categories of microfinancing programming: Financial Loan Program, Cow Living Loan Program, Clean Water Program, Education Program, and Safe Woman Program. At the center of each of these programs is the opportunity for a hand up from poverty to possibility available to all no matter their race or religion. 

Each of our projects at the core are grassroots microfinancing organizations offer training, support, and a reliable method of saving, borrowing, and growing a business. Through strategic steps with each microfinance project individuals and communities come together to develop and advance business opportunities centered around microfinancing to help lift themselves out of extreme poverty.

Microfinancing looks different for each of our projects – while some are animal-based and the loan is structured around the repayment with a “living loan”, others are structured around a community of individuals working together to create products for sale. At the center of each of our projects is one thing: the opportunity for a hand up from poverty to possibility

Our mission is to provide grants for the strengthening and expansion of microfinancing programs to empower Africa’s most destitute individuals to lift themselves out of poverty with dignity through access to financial services and education.

Name: 5 Buckets Foundation
Description:
Our mission is to empower learners of all ages with the knowledge and tools for lifelong financial wellness.

Check out our website and follow us on IG (@5buckets) to learn more

Name: The Graeme McDaniel Foundation
Description:
We lost our little boy on May 3, 2015. He now plays with Jesus. Most people knew him as Graeme Bone or Little Brother. He had the most infectious smile and giant brown eyes you could ever see. His voice was pure innocence. He was so smart. He loved everything that his big brother had. He loved tractors of all types, colors, and sizes. He loved tractor rides, swinging, and pacis of all flavors. He loved to cook with his momma and do anything his daddy happened to be doing at the time. 
He was brought into this world in a whirlwind. He never had it easy in life due to his complex little heart and stomach. He was our little fighter. He spent the first couple of months flirting with the various nurses of the NICU. After that, he came home to spread happiness to everyone he came in contact with. So many people have benefitted from being around him. Although he had plenty of excuses not to be, he was the happiest little kid we could ask for. We are so blessed that we were able to spend two and a half years with him. He has taught us so much about family, love, and the gift of children. 
We hope you can join & support our events throughout the year to help us share Graeme and fundraise to support congenital heart defect (CHD) research.  We will always keep trying to make life better for heart warriors and their families.

Name: Data Is For Everyone
Description:
Do you know what is 

𝐏𝐡𝐢𝐥𝐚𝐧𝐭𝐡𝐫𝐨𝐩𝐲 + 𝐃𝐚𝐭𝐚 +  𝐒𝐨𝐜𝐢𝐚𝐥 𝐄𝐪𝐮𝐢𝐭𝐲 𝐚𝐧𝐝 𝐈𝐧𝐜𝐥𝐮𝐬𝐢𝐨𝐧 = ?

Healing? Justice? Well, there can be more than one right answer. Just like there can be more than one way to solve that equation of bringing equity-focused data practices to nonprofits. For me, these days that answer is this virtual school for individuals and organizations (all social sector) -  a space where all forms of unlearning, learning and evolving around data is encouraged.

Here you can find a collection of  trainings, workshops and webinars. A few exists a range of topics include - data collection with the lens of equity, building inclusive fundraising surveys and moving towards human-centric AI.

Interested in a data or analytics topic that is not covered here yet? Send me a message (www.namastedata.org) and I'll find a way to cover the topic.

Interested to chat? Now LinkedIn allows you and me to interact directly through this page. You can find the button below.

Name: Rotary Club Zürich-Bellevue
Description:
Der Rotary Club Zürich-Bellevue ist durch den Zusammenschluss der Rotary Clubs «Zürich Adlisberg», «Zürich Bellerive» und «Zürich Zürichberg» entstanden. 
Über diese Seite berichten wir über unser Clubleben, unsere Anlässe und die diversen Projekte und Aktivitäten zu Gunsten der Gesellschaft.

Der Club besteht aus rund 90 Rotarierinnen und Rotariern und pflegt Partnerschaften mit den Rotary Clubs Berlin Gedächtniskirche, Hamburg Wandsbek und Paris Concorde.

Name: The #BlackProfessionals Project
Description:
You have been asked the HARDEST question in life a million times...

What do you want to do with your life?

This is an extremely difficult question for anyone to answer, especially Black and Brown Youth. Black people, in particular, account for about 12% of the U.S. population but occupy only 3.2% of the senior leadership roles at large companies in the U.S. and just 0.8% of all Fortune 500 CEO positions. Meanwhile, Black and Brown people make up 76.2% of the NBA and 47% of Billboard's top 100 artists of 2019.

Knowledge might be power, but role models inspire, and Black and Brown youths do not see themselves represented in the professional world.

We here at the #BlackProfessionals Project share the stories of Black and Brown professionals with Black and Brown Youths across the globe. We partner with other non-profits around the world to help share these journeys with Black and Brown youths everywhere. 

Join our family and network with Black and Brown Professionals by following our LinkedIn, Facebook, Instagram, and YouTube pages (@blk_professions)!

INTERESTED IN SHARING YOUR STORY?

If you are a Black or Brown professional interested in sharing your professional journey with us, then please follow us and sign-up at the buttons above. Our video chats last between 15-20 mins so we can be flexible with you busy schedules.

Name: Healthcare Initiative Foundation
Description: The Healthcare Initiative Foundation supports organizations that offer solutions to improve the quality and delivery of healthcare for residents of Montgomery County, MD.

Name: Causa Nuestra
Description: Brindar apoyo económico e intelectual a organizaciones sin fines de lucro que compartan una visión para el desarrollo y el bienestar integral de las personas, elevando la calidad de vida de los sectores menos atendidos del país.

Name: S&P GLOBAL FOUNDATION
Description: S&P GLOBAL FOUNDATION is a charitable foundation based out of 55 WATER ST, New York, New York, United States.

Name: Casa Chama
Description:
A Casa Chama é uma ONG fundada e coordenada por pessoas transvestigêneres , cuja atuação sociopolítica e cultural visa garantir emancipação, valorização e qualidade de vida para as populações trans. Compreendendo a carência de direitos básicos e a constante marginalização de corpos trans, atuamos junto ao poder público e privado buscando garantir suporte jurídico, econômico, psicológico, médico e cultural à nossa comunidade.
A partir de uma ética antirracista e antitransfóbica, nossa missão é promover a dignidade para vidas trans a fim de prolonga-las e potencializá-las, fomentando e assegurando espaços de participação política, artística e social. Compreendemos também a aliança com pessoas cisgêneras  como um alicerce fundamental da nossa organização, sendo as cis aliades parte importante da nossa história e do fomento ao protagonismo trans.

“QUEM ACOLHE É ACOLHIDE, E QUEM É ACOLHIDE TAMBÉM ACOLHE” é o lema da Casa Chama por sermos um espaço de acolhimento mútuo. Somos uma equipe extremamente diversa, composta majoritariamente por pessoas trans, junto às cis aliades, que está sempre disposta a compartilhar outras maneiras transversais de estar no mundo. Atualmente contamos com 270 voluntáries, em diversas frentes, e é através dessa nossa rede de parcerias e ações que produzimos afeto, cuidado e troca.

Name: The Winning Edge
Description: Weekly Business Interactive Sessions with renowned industry professionals.

Name: Metropolitan Regional Arts Council
Description: Metropolitan Regional Arts Council improves arts access for Twin Cities metro communities through grants, trainings, resources, and advocacy for artists and organizations.

Name: ammado
Description:
ammado is a unique online donation and community engagement platform connecting companies, nonprofits and individuals around the world.  Supporting over 80 currencies and multiple languages, ammado caters for a global audience and works with the world’s most respected companies and nonprofits providing technology solutions to power their partnerships. With ammado, brands benefit from a complete giving solution where workplace giving, fundraising, cause marketing campaigns and disaster response can all be managed from a single digital platform.

ammado is headquartered in Switzerland and has software and business development centres all over the world.

ammado – Your Way to Make a Difference

Name: Go Go Dog Toys, LLC
Description: Sharing is caring and loving.

Name: BSOCIAL PROJETOS SOCIAIS
Description: Plataforma criada para agilizar e facilitar doações de forma segura e transparente, além de fornecer para organizações sem fins lucrativos a possibilidade de cadastrar suas causas sociais para receber contribuições de usuários da plataforma.

Name: Precious Project, Inc.
Description: With the belief that every one has the right to an education and care, the Precious Project works to help those in extreme poverty have a better future. There are four significant projects that Precious has undertaken in the rural impoverished village in Nshupu, Tanzania. They are the care and education of orphans and abandoned children, delivery of quality education, implementation of agriculture best practices to achieve self-sustainability, and empowerment of women with skills to create micro enterprises. Precious was founded in 2010 by William and Sarah Modest in Tanzania. It became an independent US 501c(3) charity organization in 2014 under the direction of Susie Rheault and Gil Williams and supported by a team of passionate volunteers dedicated to Precious' cause.

Name: Native Son
Description: NATIVE SON harnesses the collective power of Black gay men with the goal of ensuring that our voices, visibility, and lived experiences are elevated in all of the communities in which we exist and never again disregarded.

Name: Baleno Foundation
Description: Baleno is a Charitable Organization fostering holistic wellbeing on Physical, Mental and Spiritual levels by supporting research projects, activities and services which raise awareness that "dis-ease" in essence represents a conflict between Body, Mind and Soul.

Name: Donorinfo ✦ I give with confidence
Description:
✦ 240+ CHARITABLE ORGANISATIONS 100 % TRANSPARENT
Donorinfo.be is a Belgian public utility foundation set up to ensure that donors can give with complete confidence to their causes of choice. We give information on the financing and management of over 240 high-impact charitable associations, NGOs and foundations in Belgium and guarantee that those in our portfolio are financially transparent. They attest to their good management and good governance by making their accounts visible on donorinfo.be. 
Our foundation is independent, without recourse to public subsidies or sponsorship. Our services are free.

✦ IF YOU ARE AN ORGANISATIONAL OR PRIVATE DONOR 
Among the millions of NGOs, non-profit and social organisations, do you wonder which are worthy of your support? We will help you to finance the cause of your choice with confidence and optimise your giving decisions by identifying those where your donation will have the most impact: 
1. It will ensure that your donations go to beneficiary organisations whose finances and operations have been rigorously vetted through a due diligence process
2. Our expertise and financial analysis means you can give with confidence based on verifiable and measurable criteria
3. Donorinfo remains connected and engaged with the organisations in its portfolio and the causes that most matter to you

✦ IF YOU ARE A NON-PROFIT ASSOCIATION 
If you would like to join the transparent ranks of other charitable bodies, you should consider registering with Donorinfo, who will testify to the 100% probity of your organisation and strengthen the trust of private and organisational donors:
1. You will prove to be serious, financially transparent with published audited accounts, and ethical
2. It will include you on donorinfo.be, the online cause finder with multi-search criteria
3. It will help target your audience thanks to our communications aimed at increasing your visibility and communicating with your donors

Name: The HOPE Initiative Foundation
Description:
The HOPE Initiative aims to work in partnership with marginalized societal groups in a way that shows awareness and respect for their backgrounds, cultures, and beliefs. We are a peer-generated grassroots initiative and we do not work under the constraints of any existing organizations. Our philosophy resonates around the concept of mutual aid—where there is a common established goal that guides our actions so that our events will benefit all parties involved.

We work around 3 pillars. The first is student empowerment, where our members are given space and freedom to plan their own ideas and are provided with the resources and tools to tackle future projects or initiate new events out of their own ideas. Our second pillar is outreach within our schools. HOPE's wish is to challenge young people to reach deeper within their hearts and arrive at a place not just of sympathy but of true empathy for the people around them. With this mindset, we run events at high schools across Vancouver, engaging students with developmental disabilities. We also have a one-on-one mentorship program between university students and those who are prospective students at specific high schools help their transitions to post-secondary. Lastly, we reach out towards local and international community groups such as the homeless. Through our continued efforts, the story of HOPE's creation and purpose was recently introduced on the international stage at We Day Vancouver 2014. Also with a connection to Free the Children, HOPE plans to carry out an international service project and increase UBC’s international community service presence sometime in the coming years.

The vision for this organization is not only for youth to break free of their stagnant realities, but to instill passion within them to take action towards critical issues in society and to further appreciate and understand the lives of the people who have shaped and continue to shape Vancouver.

Name: The Transition Project
Description:
The Transition Project is a student-founded non-profit organization based in Montreal, QC. With a focus on reducing unemployment and poverty, our initiative provides a comprehensive program to our clients who are either currently homeless or on the verge of homelessness. 

This organization was started in 2016 as a response to the growing unemployment rate in Montreal. Through the awareness of our founding members, The Transition Project was born with one goal: helping financially-disadvantaged individuals obtain employment and attain long-term financial autonomy.

Since then, The Transition Project has transformed immensely from the help of various individuals and organizations. From conception, we have helped over 100 individuals find jobs. 

Currently, The Transition Project is converting to a completely digital platform. This will allow our program to be implemented more efficiently while allowing us to expand our outreach capabilities.  

For more information, please check out our website or direct message us on one of our social media platforms!

Kind regards,

The Transition Project Team

Name: Wild Gift
Description: Each year, Wild Gift selects a small group of environmental entrepreneurs for a fellowship that includes mentoring, fundraising support, and wilderness-based programs teaching leadership and business skills while building a lifelong support network.

Name: Sisters of Charity Foundation of Canton
Description: The Sisters of Charity Foundation of Canton supports and furthers the mission and ministry of the Sisters of Charity of St. Augustine. The principal components of their ministry are health, education, and social services.

Name: Oak Spring Garden Foundation
Description: OSGF is an operating foundation dedicated to sharing the gifts and ideas of Rachel "Bunny" Mellon.  Our mission is to support and inspire fresh thinking and bold action on the history and future of plants, including the art and culture of plants, gardens, and landscapes.

Name: Association of Fundraising Professionals NWA Chapter (AFP-NWA)
Description:
The Northwest Arkansas Chapter of the Association of Fundraising Professionals (AFP NWA) offers members the opportunity to connect with colleagues, engage in creative thinking, network with new friends and achieve great results in their work.

We focus on delivering educational programs and events to help you reach your goals. AFP NWA is your link to professional networking and programs for advanced executives, mid-career professionals, and those new to the field. We also offer opportunities for volunteering, sponsor special events like National Philanthropy Day Luncheon, and much more.

Name: SP@RK Belgium
Description:
The SP@RK program connects young talents from diverse origins with employers committed to diversity and inclusion. The aim is to increase the employability of the participants, in order to facilitate and accelerate their access to employment! The participants have the opportunity to attend thematic workshops and Job Meetups led by practitioners from partner companies. SP@RK is an initiative of ABPA (Association Belge des Professionnels Africains).
#sdg10 #sdg8 #sdg17

Name: Cedar Rapids Downtown Rotary
Description:
Our club has a proud tradition, spanning for over 100 years and including contributions from many of our community's greatest leaders. Our members have a passion for service and a willingness to lead. We are in the top 25 largest clubs in the United States with over 330 members and we are the 4th oldest club in Iowa founded on June 1, 1914.  The Rotary Club of Cedar Rapids prides itself as an opportunity for:

Quality and diversity of membership 
Opportunity to network
Quality and educational value of programs
Rich history and established traditions
Commitment to education and children
Gateway to all metro, national, and international clubs

Name: Janaseva Foundation Pune Est.1988(NGO)
Description:
Janaseva Foundation is a registered charitable trust managed by board of 14 trustees and is a non-political, non-religious, equal opportunity employer. While imparting its services foundation does not discriminate on the basis of religion, caste, nationality, locality, gender etc. Inspired by the teachings and spirit of service of Rashtrapita Mahatma Gandhi, Sane Guruji, Baba Amte and Saint Mother Teresa foundation has been serving the society with the motto ‘Service to Man is Service to God’ and ‘Service to Humanity is the Best Work of Life’. Starting it’s service from a hired rural hospital, foundation has gradually evolved into well established, widely recognized and one of leading Charitable Organizations.

It serves the society through diverse fields like health care, elderly care, disabled care, destitute care, education, vocational training and research, care and school education of street children, construction of toilets, village development etc. It’s beneficiaries are elderly, sick, disabled, destitutes, women, divorcees, poor youth etc.

Mrs. Meena Shah is the driving force behind all this and she works tirelessly for the success of the foundation along with all the trustees. All the Patrons / Trustees are the who’s who of Pune and their reputation has spread beyond Pune, Maharashtra and even India.

United Nations Organization has given Janaseva Foundation Special Consultative Status. Ministry of Social Justice & Empowerment, Government of India, has chosen Janaseva Foundation to run Regional Resource Training Centre and conferred Janaseva Foundation with Service NGO status and to run Mobile Medical Unit rural villages. Foundation is associated with WHO, and Duke University, USA also.

Name: HUDSON COMMUNITY FOUNDATION
Description: The mission of the Hudson Community Foundation is to enhance the quality of life in Hudson by increasing charitable giving, educating and connecting donors to community needs and leading on community issues.

Name: Fondation d'entreprise Libellud
Description: Pour aider ses lauréats à faire grandir leur projet, la Fondation Libellud filiale d'Asmodee Group utilise une approche philanthropique engagée. Nous avons donc conçu deux programmes : le programme Explore et le programme Eureka, afin de s'adapter au mieux à leurs besoins.

Name: GoodLife Kids Foundation
Description: GoodLife Kids Foundation is a registered Canadian charity that enables and supports kids and youth with autism and intellectual disabilities through physical activity and fitness.

Name: Northern Lights Foundation
Description:
The purpose of the Northern Lights Foundation is to provide local children facing a life-threatening illness with an unrestricted grant for their family to use during a very challenging emotional and financial time. Over the past 15 years, we have raised more than $500,000 and made grants to hundreds of Northland families.

We work to assist as many families as possible who fall under the following qualifying conditions:

 At the time of referral, the medical condition has become life-threatening
 Child is up to the age of 18
 Family lives within 100 miles of Duluth, Minnesota

Name: RAS Foundation
Description:
The RAS Foundation is the charitable arm of the Royal Agricultural Society of NSW.  The RAS Foundation was created in 2007 to help build strong, vibrant rural and regional communities in NSW. Since its inception the RAS Foundation has provided more than $2.86 million in community grants and educational scholarships to rural communities in New South Wales.  

The RAS Foundation is a company limited by guarantee with its own Board which comprises four directors from the Royal Agricultural Society of NSW, and four independent directors.

Name: UpPurpose
Description: A better way to create, share and measure content so you can connect with donors, meet fundraising goals, see what’s working and amplify your impact.

Name: Casa do Vovô - Sociedade Espírita Cinco de Setembro
Description:
Com 45 anos de experiência na proteção social de alta complexidade (abrigo institucional), a Casa do Vovô acolhe idosos com idade igual ou superior a 60 anos, de ambos os sexos, residentes na cidade de Ribeirão Preto.

O acolhimento na entidade é previsto para idosos desta faixa etária que não dispõem de condições de permanecer com suas famílias, com vivência de situações de violência e negligência, em situação de rua e de abandono, com vínculos familiares fragilizados ou rompidos.

Name: Youth In Diaspora
Description:
YID is a for-purpose, youth led, charitable organization, a Global Grassroots Leadership Movement empowering youth of African descent in the socio-economic development of their local communities.

In the Diaspora, we are empowering young people of African descent to obtain the necessary resources to be represented in various sectors as leaders for decision making and to develop their local communities where they live. We’re building up the leaders not only of tomorrow but of today who don’t have a voice in decisions that impact their everyday life.

On the continent, we're fighting against poverty and inadequate education by providing innovative, low cost, scaleable solutions in the areas of education in order to achieve the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals.

The African diaspora consists of the worldwide collection of communities descended from native sub-Saharan Africans or people from sub-Saharan Africa, predominantly in the Americas. The phrase African diaspora gradually entered common usage at the turn of the 21st century. The term diaspora originates from the Greek διασπορά (diaspora, literally "scattering") 

We are convinced that the youth of African Descent in the diaspora that have experienced the way of life in the Diaspora are extremely valuable to the development of their local communities and Africa and the Caribbean! With our knowledge, exposure, experience, and expertise we will be able to develop the diaspora and collaborate with youths living in Africa and the Caribbean to ultimately build a future of a more secure Africa and Caribbean. 
We are Global Leaders, Creating An Impact!  #NotTooYoungToLead

Name: Youth Renewal Fund
Description: Youth Renewal Fund  is the philanthropic funding partner for Darca Schools in Israel, investing in an innovative education throughout Israel's most underserved communities as a means of improving social mobility.

Name: The Miles Foundation
Description: The Miles Foundation endeavors to honor the legacy of its founder, Ellison Miles, through investments in education that promote the next generation’s success. Mr. Miles demonstrated a passion for education throughout his life, and The Miles Foundation carries on this commitment today through its five core funding areas: 1) early childhood education, 2) third grade literacy, 3) family engagement, 4) leadership development, and 5) character development. To learn more, visit www.milesfdn.org.

Name: E-ženy
Description: Sme komunita, ktorá tvorí biznis so srdcom. Od roku 2010 spájame a podporujeme ženy v podnikaní. Vzdelávame, inšpirujeme a prepájame podnikateľky z celého Slovenska. Našim poslaním je, aby žena ženou bola - autentická, sebavedomá a úspešná.

Name: The Good People Fund
Description:
The Good People Fund, inspired by the Jewish concept of tikkun olam (repairing the world), responds to significant problems such as poverty, disability, trauma and social isolation, primarily in the United States and Israel. We provide financial support, guidance and mentoring to charitable activities of modest proportions that are undertaken by Good People acting singly or in small groups. Target endeavors are those that, by their personal scale and often-unusual nature, might otherwise find it difficult to attract sufficient support. The Good People Fund operates responsively, flexibly and with a minimum of bureaucracy. 

We serve as a means for donors to reach many groups and individual needs and act as an instigator of good, inspiring people to do tikkun olam and tzedakah (to do the right thing by giving) responsibly and regularly. To expand these good works (ma’asim tovim), The Good People Fund also educates youth, adults, teachers, recipients and donors about the process and power of helping others.

Name: Nébéday
Description:
Nébéday est une association sénégalaise de protection et de valorisation des ressources naturelles.

L’objectif principal de l’association Nébéday est la gestion participative des ressources naturelles par et pour les populations locales.

Il s’agit de protéger les ressources naturelles mais aussi de les valoriser avec des activités qui s’articulent autour d’aires protégées (forêts communautaires, aires marines protégées…).

La mise en place de différentes filières de valorisation des ressources naturelles (poudre de feuilles de moringa, pain de singe, bissap, miel, poissons, coquillages…) permet de sensibiliser les populations à la nécessité d’une gestion durable des ressources et de développer l'économie locale.
L’association a également pour objectif de développer l’écotourisme.

Name: Mary's Pence
Description:
At Mary's Pence, we envision a world where empowered women and their communities flourish in solidarity and justice.

In the United States and Canada we provide grants to women’s organizations working for social change. 

In Central America and Mexico we partner with women’s organizations to form community lending pools and provide on-going mentorship to foster women’s empowerment and economic well-being. 

We believe it is our responsibility to provide resources and long-term accompaniment to the women we partner with. In addition to providing financial resources, we work in solidarity with our partners, encouraging participation, responding to changing needs, building skills, and amplifying the voices of women because we know this is the only way to change unjust systems. 

Together we are working toward a common vision of women’s empowerment and well-being. 

To learn more please visit www.maryspence.org
Like us on Facebook, Instagram or Twitter: @maryspence1987

Name: Healthier Hens
Description: Millions of egg-laying hens needlessly suffer from painful bone fractures, osteoporosis, and elevated mortality rates due to suboptimal feed standards. Hens are now producing 10 times as many eggs as they were naturally born to do, and this forced increase of production weighs heavily on their well-being by depleting their bodies of key nutrients, including calcium, phosphorous, and vitamin D. By ensuring that an optimal level of these nutrients are supplied via feed, we can cost-effectively reduce the suffering of >72 million hens (over 10 years) and allow them to express their natural behaviors via improved welfare. As recent studies have shown, this neglected issue occurs not only in battery cages but also in cage-free systems (the preferred housing system), so we must act now to achieve unprecedented counterfactual impact and more healthier hens.

Name: Kid in the Corner
Description: Kid in the Corner is a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization that is dedicated to shattering the stigma that surrounds mental illness by helping kids, teens and young adults feel connected to and supported by the community as well as find a path to hope and positivity.

Name: Round Table Southern Africa
Description:
Round Table Southern Africa is a non-political, non-denominational association of young men between the ages of 18 and 40. Embracing representatives of nearly every profession and occupation, and whose objects encompass the encouragement of high ethical standards. The promotion of social networking and fellowship among young professional men, the quickening of individual interest in everything affecting the public welfare, and the promotion of understanding amongst peoples of different cultural, language and political backgrounds.

ARTSA - the Association of Round Tables in Southern Africa - consists of all the Round Tables of Namibia and South Africa.

The main aim of Round Table is to enjoy fellowship with people from all walks of life, as well as organising fund raising events and community activities.

Not what you are looking for? Please still get involved in your community through any of the following service organisations:
1. Ladies Circle South Africa: http://ladiescircle.co.za
2. Lions Club South Africa: http://www.lionsclubs.co.za
3. Rotary: http://www.rotary.org/en

Name: O'Sénégal
Description: O'Sénégal est une association humanitaire à but non lucratif créée par des étudiants à l'EM Normandie. Elle a pour vocation d'apporter de l'aide à la population de la région Sud-Casamance, en y finançant des projets tel que la construction de puits ou la formation d'activités génératrices de revenus. Nous souhaitons ainsi améliorer leur qualité de vie sur place et leur permettre de se développer économiquement.

Name: BAPS Campus Fellowship
Description: The purpose of BAPS Campus Fellowship is to unite students on university campuses across North America and provide opportunities to learn about Hindu heritage and culture and a more Swaminarayan way of life. It will introduce the Hindu faith to the rest of the local campus communities and will foster issues affecting Hindus through youth group discussions, campus events and community service projects. Students will be able to discuss topics that affect them in college and will be able to learn methods on balancing life of as college student while practicing the Hindu faith. BCF is an organization approved by the Bochasanwasi Shri Akshar Purushottam Sanstha, Inc. (BAPS) a leading denomination of the Hindu faith, and represents itself in a way that is consistent with the guidelines set forth by BAPS.

Name: Fundació Castell del Remei
Description:
Defensa i promoció del patrimoni de la finca que acull el celler més antic de Catalunya en elaboració de vins de criança.
La Fundació Castell del Remei ha rebut el Premi al Lideratge Social atorgat pel Consell Social de la Universitat de Lleida l'any 2024.
Ha merescut el Reconeixement a l'Acció Social i Solidaritat a Lleida 2024.

Name: JustGive
Description: JustGive is a fundraising platform that has raised over $600 million for tens of thousands of nonprofits since 1999.

Name: Good Culture Consulting
Description:
Helping companies help people. Good Culture Consulting works with leaders who strive to accelerate their passion for an inspired workplace and employee engagement.

We design and manage customized Corporate Social Responsibility (CSR) programs and initiatives incorporating leadership’s vision. The company’s brand and ethos are at the center of any plan, including congruent non-profits and vetted partners.

CSR programs and initiatives include but are not limited to:
  * Adopt a Park
  * Adopt a School
  * Beach Clean Up
  * Cause-Directed Competitive & Fun Multi-Sport Events
  * Community-Based Computer & IT Support
  * Community Clean Up
  * Community Tree Plantings & Tree Care
  * Construction & Building Projects
  * Elderly Care
  * Food Service Programs
  * Fundraising
  * Harnessing the Creative Talents of Employees 
  * Mentoring
  * Participation in Community Initiatives
  * Volunteer Time Off (VTO) Global Projects = Do Good + Do Fun!

CLIENTELE
Influential and innovative leaders, including but not limited to the following sectors:
  * Technology
  * Start-ups
  * Consumer Products
  * Business to Business (B2B)
  * Business to Consumer (B2C)
  * Entertainment
  * Media
  * Advertising
  * Design/Arts 
  * Government Agencies
  * Non-Government Organizations (NGO)
  * Community-Based Organizations (CBO)

SERVICES
  * Consultation to identify objectives, budget and contribution categories.
  * Create customized Social Responsibility program(s) and initiatives that support the 
  company’s values, goals, and budget, including establishing partnerships with congruent

  * Management of program(s), including timely communication with leadership teams.
  * Event production.
  * Video and social media support.

CONTACT
Contact Michelle Bagnato in one of the following ways:
