* Email: michelle@goodcultureconsulting.com

Name: IEEE ENSIT STUDENT BRANCH  - Tunisia Section
Description: IEEE ENSIT STUDENT BRANCH

Name: SOMOSMAISQUE1
Description:
Levantar, debater e tentar encontrar soluções para questões de nossa sociedade e cultura no mundo atual é o objetivo dos EVENTOS #+Q1

O Evento somosmaisque1 acontecem em diversas cidades brasileiras em ondas bimestrais. 

 Criado na rede de contatos profissionais Linkedln, o evento #+Q1 – O Futuro do Trabalho, pretende apontar caminhos e possibilidades para sua carreira, sua profissão e seu emprego num futuro que já está bem mais próximo do que você pensa.

Através de palestras presenciais, com no máximo 20 minutos, filmadas e postadas nas redes sociais, profissionais experientes das mais diversas áreas falam sobre o assunto de uma maneira clara e direta. 

Empresários, Professores, Inovadores, Jovens Empreendedores, Pesquisadores, Representates do Poder Público, Líderes da Economia Colaborativa, Artistas e Agitadores Culturais estarão juntos nesta tarefa.

O nome do evento surgiu das publicações realizadas por Professor Fabiano Caxito na rede social LInkedin, com o objetivo de incentivar que as pessoas compartilhem seus conhecimentos e experiências. 

Os posts com a #SOMOSMAISQUE1 somam mais de 5 milhões de visualizações e são realizadas por centenas de pessoas na rede social.

Todas as palestras do #+Q1 estarão disponíveis de graça no site do evento.

Para mais informações sobre esta troca de experiências, ideias e conceitos sobre as mudanças que envolvem o mercado de trabalho entre no site 

www.somosmaisque1.com

Name: The Sanshil Foundation
Description:
The Sanshil Foundation helps those who are at the bottom of the 'economic pyramid’. We aid them with academics, self-sustainable employment and vocational opportunities, so that they no longer fall under the category of “those in need”. It is registered under Section 25 of the Companies Act (India) as a Non-Profit Organization.

We have over 300 beneficiaries in India. Over 200 students have been mainstreamed to schools that make their academic pursuits easier and  structured. The hand-holding continues until we can provide them with vocational training and support, and help them acquire  skills that contribute to their economic and social development.

Name: Williamsburg Health Foundation
Description: The Williamsburg Health Foundation (WHF) is a private legacy health foundation with the mission to collaborate, innovate, and invest to impact systems that improve the health and well-being of individuals living in Greater Williamsburg. The Foundation was established in September 1996 when the Williamsburg Community Hospital and Sentara developed an affiliation agreement and later merged. This agreement included a provision for a new, locally organized, and managed Foundation to benefit community health. Since inception, WHF has awarded over $106 million in grants to improve community health and well-being.

Name: Ciudad de los Niños de Monterrey, A.B.P.
Description:
¿Sabías que en México solo 1.8 de cada 10 niños que comienzan primero de primaria llegan a graduarse de la universidad?

En Ciudad de los Niños de Monterrey, A.B.P. trabajamos para cambiar esa realidad.

 Nuestro dato de mayor impacto: 9 de cada 10 egresados de Ciudad de los Niños concluyen una carrera universitaria.

Somos un colegio privado con más de 70 años de experiencia, que ofrece educación gratuita y de calidad a niñas, niños y jóvenes de escasos recursos, así como acompañamiento integral a sus familias. Brindamos una formación académica, técnica, humana y espiritual que empodera a cada persona para convertirse en un motor de desarrollo en su comunidad.

 Nuestro impacto

 Más de 1,200 estudiantes atendidos

 Más de 900 familias acompañadas

 Más de 2,500 egresados formados


 ¿Cómo lo hacemos?

 Compromiso familiar: Cada familia firma una carta compromiso “A favor de la educación de mis hijos” y asiste a la Escuela para Padres durante el ciclo escolar.

 Atención personalizada: Cada estudiante trabaja quincenalmente con una preceptora o preceptor para trazar su plan de vida. Los padres también reciben orientación tres veces al año para alinear el proyecto familiar con la formación de sus hijas e hijos.

 Formación integral: Robótica, orquesta, arte, deportes, coro y más, como parte del desarrollo completo del estudiante.

 Beca del 100%: Todas las familias reciben apoyo económico total para garantizar el acceso a una educación de excelencia.

Transformamos a personas y familias completas para construir una sociedad más justa, unida y con futuro.

 Ciudad de los Niños de Monterrey, un compromiso que cambia destinos.

Name: HLD pour la Méditerranée
Description: HLD pour la Méditerranée is a fund launched by the HLD group. Our objective is to take part in the development of projects and solutions responding to the threats impacting the ecosystems in the Mediterranean area. In order to do so we will try to support innovative, federative and ambitious projects with our expertise and values.

Name: Thompson Family Foundation
Description:
The mission of the Thompson Family Foundation is to enrich the lives of youth in the United States and the Bahamas through fitness and education.

Julie, Mychal, Mychel, Klay and Trayce Thompson work to support the lives of all youth no matter their background. They hope to teach children the values of teamwork and the importance of pursuing an education outside of their athletic goals.

"To whom much is given, much is required. If blessed with riches, health, or knowledge, you should be willing to pass it on." - Mychal Thompson

Name: Space Coast Health Foundation
Description: Space Coast Health Foundation is a nonprofit organization that supplies grants to other local nonprofits to support the improvement of health in Brevard County.

Name: Givio
Description:
Givio is on a mission drive impact, engagement and community activation. 

From the Givio mobile app to our Charitable Banking solutions for financial institutions and their depositors, we're making giving and fundraising simple, secure and rewarding.

Name: Maison Chance
Description:
Maison Chance is a Non Governmental Organization founded by Tim Aline Rebeaud and based in Ho Chi Minh City, Vietnam. Officially recognized by the Vietnamese authorities in 1998, it is a springboard for orphans, street children, disadvantaged people and physically handicapped people in the country. 

The mix of able-bodied and handicapped people, of adults and children living together in a family setting is a unique characteristic of Maison Chance. We provide housing, health care, education and vocational training, and a place where the under-privileged children and the disabled can study and work. It also offers them the means to facilitate their rehabilitation and their reinsertion into social life. 

At Maison Chance, the saying "Give A Man A Fish, Feed Him for A Day. Teach A Man to Fish, Feed Him for A Lifetime" is practiced every day.

Currently more than 500 people benefit from the social services of Maison Chance. Many projects are ongoing in the 3 centers of the NGO located in Ho Chi Minh City: Shelter, Take Wing Center and Village Chance.

A new social center is under construction in the province of Dak Nong, 300km away from Ho Chi Minh. Its inauguration is scheduled for 2018. Click the following link to know more about the Dak Nong Project: https://www.maison-chance.org/maison-chance/dak-nong/introduction/
Location: Ho Chi Minh City, Binh Tan District

Name: General Service Foundation
Description:
The General Service Foundation ("GSF") is a private grant making family foundation that dedicates all of its resources to bringing about a more just and sustainable world.  Founded and endowed by Clifton and Margaret Musser, the General Service Foundation has been committed to social change and social justice since 1946. 

We believe everyone should have a chance to enjoy life with dignity, and every family should have the opportunity to thrive.  GSF works with nonprofit organizations as a funder and partner. These organizations are embedded in grassroots communities, networked with one another, and connected to larger movements for a decisive impact. They’re pioneers, breaking new trails toward social justice with women, people of color and people with low incomes leading the way.

Name: Projeto Pupila
Description:
O Projeto Pupila visa promover ensino por meio do curso de Auxiliar Administrativo ministrado por voluntários de diversas áreas de atuação, porém, todos com vivência de mercado. Com o curso o jovem e/ou o adulto poderá aprender técnicas para aplicar em sua experiência profissional na área administrativa, sendo também uma oportunidade de melhorar o futuro do aluno e do nosso País. 
O curso é totalmente gratuito contando com apoio de escolas e empresas parceiras, proporcionando um ambiente agradável de ensino e materiais de apoio. 
Durante o curso o aluno será preparado para o mercado de trabalho, conhecendo rotinas administrativas e trabalhos em equipe.

Name: LaRae Infinity LLC
Description: Through our multifaceted approach encompassing business consulting, public speaking, fine visual artistry, civic leadership, youth mentorship, and media production. We amplify individuals and organizations to tell their stories authentically and effectively.

Name: Get Outside Alliance
Description:
Get Outside Alliance is a nonprofit organization We are building a community that’s willing to get outside and help our state parks.
GOA
Go Adventure. Go Admire. Go Assist.

Name: Mentor-Escale ASBL
Description:
Mentor - Escale Non-Profit Organisation which assures an educative, social and psychological environment which are indispensable to Unaccompanied Foreign Minors (UFM).
Mentore Escale operates out of 19 Rue Souveraine, Brussels, Belgium but also has operations in Namur

Name: Neya
Description:
Neya is a network of individual believers in giving back to the community. Our community ecosystem is composed of individuals, small groups, business and corporate organization, non-governmental organizations and social service agencies. The purpose of this tool is to build a platform for the development community, where tools for awareness, volunteering, donating, sponsoring and supporting a cause or initiative will be made available. Neya translated means your intrinsic value to do good, our platform is the tool that generates a more socially sustainable and aware community growing from your own individual intrinsic value to do good.
A vision driven platform, Neya builds on the awareness and drive of Millennial and Gen Zers who want to make a difference. Neya is the jumpstart to youth integration channeling the potentials of the civil society through a vision to generate active awareness and bettering the standard of living for future generations to come.

Name: Enactus FZEA USP
Description:
Somos uma comunidade de estudantes comprometidos a usar ações empreendedoras para transformar vidas e formar um mundo melhor.
Siginificado de Enactus:
ENtrepreneurial – ser capaz de ver em algo uma oportunidade e possibilidade, criando valores e construindo ideias;
ACTion – a vontade de fazer algo e se comprometer com isso, mesmo que o retorno não seja garantido;
US – (nós) um grupo de pessoas conectados por uma causa importante, somos parte de um todo.

Missão:
Empoderar e impactar positivamente pessoas por meio do empreendedorismo social.

Visão:
Ser uma organização de referência nacional por meio de projetos de empreendedorismo social que sejam impactantes, sustentáveis e replicáveis.

Valores:
- Empatia
- Comprometimento
- Pertencimento
- Proatividade
- Inovação

Name: #SmileMovement
Description:
#SmileMovement – Action no.1 : Donner chaleur et sourire aux itinérants de Montréal.
 
Pourquoi l’Action no.1?
 
Le froid de l’hiver québécois s’en vient à grands pas. Tandis que la plupart d’entre nous ont un toit pour s’abriter en hiver, plusieurs n’ont pas ce privilège.Ils sont malheureusement plus de 3000 sur l’Île de Montréal.
 
Notre action no.1 : amasser vos dons pour munir les itinérants de Montréal avec des tuques #SmileMovement.
 
Pour nous aider à y parvenir, faites-nous un des dons suivants!
 
1 – Pour chaque don de 7$, une tuque sera remise à un itinérant de Montréal
2 – Pour chaque don de 15$, 2 tuques seront remises : une à un itinérant de Montréal et une à vous.
3 – Un don du montant que vous désirez qui servira à financer le #SmileMovement
 
Merci! :)
 
À propos
La pauvreté, la colère, le stress, la maladie et la tristesse se tissent une place de choix avec comme allié le négatif, dépeignant les grandes villes avec un cynisme populaire, dans tous nos médias, sur le web, à la radio, dans les journaux, sur les chaines de télé...
 
Le virus le plus puissant du monde nous donne la maladie du sourire.
Celui que l’on perd trop souvent.
 
Qui peut faire une différence? Créer une énergie humaine positive à l’échelle planétaire?
#SmileMovement
 
Créer du positif, une action à la fois.
 
Chez #SmileMovement, nous agissons pour redonner de la beauté à notre monde qui peut paraître parfois si terne.
 
Comment? En mettant en place une série d’actions et d’événements pour créer du positif, aider des causes et faire naître un sourire dans le visage des gens.
 
Nous ne limitons pas à aucune frontière. Nos actions sont prévues partout à travers le monde, sous diverses formes, au profit de différentes causes. Tout pour voir un monde plus beau, plus heureux, plus souriant.
 
Maintenant, il ne manque plus que vous. Envie de créer du positif avec nous? :)
 
#SmileMovement
 
*You may say I'm a dreamer,but I'm not the only one...

Name: Bürgerstiftung Stuttgart
Description:
Bei der Bürgerstiftung Stuttgart engagieren sich Bürger mit Zeit, Geld, Ideen und sehr viel Herzblut für ihre Stadt.

Wir bringen alle an einen Runden Tisch und finden gute Lösungen für aktuelle Themen in Stuttgart.

Hier fördern wir vor allem Kinderprojekte, Initiativen der Flüchtlingshilfe, die Palliativ-Versorgung und unterstützen Ehrenamtliche in Ihrem Engagement.

Ziel dieser Arbeit ist es, das soziale Kapital Stuttgarts zu vermehren, niemanden auszuschließen und so die Lebensqualität in der Landeshauptstadt zu verbessern.

Wir verstehen uns als ein Akteur lebendiger Demokratie und schaffen Möglichkeiten, bei denen sich Menschen selbst als wirksam erfahren und Vertrauen in andere Menschen und Strukturen geschaffen wird.
Wir stoßen dabei neue Projekte an, bilden und unterstützen aber auch Netzwerke und Initiativen.

Die Begleitung solcher Beteiligungsprozesse erfordert mehr Zeit als eine klassische Projektförderung, führt aber am Ende zu besseren Lösungen weil das Ergebnis eines Runden Tisches von allen getragen und umgesetzt wird. Nur so kommen neue Lösungsansätze in die Stadt und die Akzeptanz demokratischer Prinzipien innerhalb Stuttgarts wird gestärkt.

Name: Green Sciences for Sustainable Development Foundation
Description:
Our Foundation promotes innovation and dissemination of chemical, physical and environmental sciences of the disciplines that foster sustainability and of their applications, through the coordination and management of activities and services as laboratories and research centres.
We support and disseminate a scientific, technological and cultural development in the Sustainable Development, according to the 17 United Nations goals, in the use of renewable resources, energy saving, clean processes and products, with low or no environmental impact, not harmful to health and green solutions against climate change.
The GSSD Foundation promotes and participates to companies, consortia, associations or foundations that share its purposes, as well as organizations dealing with research, advanced training and technology transfer, both in Italy and abroad.
The first international initiative organized by the Green Sciences for Sustainable Development Foundation was the 12th edition of the the Green Chemistry Postgraduate Summer School held on July 2020.
The Foundation is located in Venice.

Name: Tesselschade
Description:
Opgericht in 1871, is Tesselschade is de oudste, eerste vrouwenvereniging van Nederland. Wij ondersteunen vrouwen door het geven van financiële bijdrage aan vrouwen die een opleiding willen volgen en daarmee financieel onafhankelijk willen worden. 

De benodigde fondsen worden gegeneerd uit contributies, donaties en de verkoop van handwerkproducten.

Vanaf 1871 verkoopt Tesselschade naald- en breiwerkproducten. De opbrengst van de verkoop viel in het verleden toe aan de handwerksters die daardoor in hun eigen onderhoud konden voorzien. Tegenwoordig worden nog steeds  handwerksters financieel ondersteund.Het behouden van oude handwerktechnieken en het stimuleren van handwerken is nog steeds een belangrijke nevendoelstelling.

Tesselschade is opgebouwd uit regionale afdelingen en wordt volledig door vrijwilligers bestuurd.

Name: Donate.in.ua
Description:
We are a humanitarian volunteer mission based off of the IT company Crunch and IT recruitment agency StaffingPartner.

Our goal is to provide IMMEDIATE humanitarian assistance to the front lines including first aid and transportation of injured soldiers, assisting women and children at overcrowded border crossings as well as to refugees living in shelters all over the country.

We are focused on helping Ukrainians here and now, because the cost of every second is someone’s life.

Every donation counts!
https://donate.in.ua

Name: The Community Foundation of Mendocino County
Description:
The Community Foundation of Mendocino County is a county-wide nonprofit organization that administers permanent charitable funds established through gifts and bequests from individuals, families, businesses and other organizations. Our mission is to offer people effective ways to engage in advancing the well-being of our communities.  

We accomplish our mission by serving donors, making grants, and acting as a community catalyst for positive change.  We bring together people and organizations that want to make a difference in our corner of the world.

We are a local organization supported and led by thoughtful and engaged people who care about Mendocino County and have a vision for how it can thrive. Our board, staff, and volunteers include representatives from each of the seven geographically distinct regions of Mendocino County. We provide leadership, expertise and capital to build local resources and opportunities and serve as a resource for nonprofit, civic, government, and philanthropic organizations.

Name: Polikós
Description:
Richiamiamo la necessità per osservatori e decisori politici di volgere lo sguardo verso la Stella Polare del confronto e del dibattito, al fine di incoraggiare la partecipazione dei cittadini al perseguimento del Bene comune, alla tutela della Giustizia Sociale ed alla difesa della Democrazia. 

Simbolo di Polikós è la Sfera Armillare, antico strumento astronomico utilizzato per orientarsi e rielaborato fino alle soglie della modernità.

Name: The Ubuntu Creative Arts Project (The UCAP)
Description: "I am because we are."

Name: Funders for LGBTQ Issues
Description:
About Us: Working side-by-side with funders and our movement’s organizations through three decades, Funders for LGBTQ Issues remains the sole organization dedicated exclusively to increasing institutional giving to LGBTQ communities.

Mission: Funders for LGBTQ Issues seeks to mobilize philanthropic resources that enhance the well-being of lesbian, gay, bisexual, transgender and queer communities, promote equity and advance racial, economic and gender justice.

Vision: An empowered and functioning democracy with full equality under the law, equal access to services, unconditional respect for difference and the meaningful participation of all communities at tables where decisions are made.

Name: Rede La Salle Brasil
Description:
Na Rede La Salle Brasil, acreditamos que educar é um compromisso com as pessoas e com o mundo, e por isso oferecemos as melhores oportunidades para que cada estudante se torne um agente de transformação, contribuindo para um futuro melhor.

Com sede mundial em Roma, na Itália, estamos presentes em 80 países no mundo todo. No Brasil, somos 44 instituições de ensino presentes em 9 estados e no Distrito Federal.

Promovemos uma educação integral, humana e cristã, que toca corações e transforma vidas. Junte-se a nós e faça parte dessa história de ensino e transformação!

Name: Vanguard Charitable
Description:
Vanguard Charitable is a leading U.S. nonprofit organization that fulfills its mission to increase philanthropic giving by sponsoring donor-advised funds—a tax-effective way to consolidate, accrue, and grant assets to charity. Our philanthropic accounts offer donors a flexible and convenient way to give and, ultimately, help them make a greater charitable impact.

Vanguard Charitable was founded by Vanguard in 1997 as an independent 501(c)(3) organization. Although we are separate entities, Vanguard Charitable is strongly aligned with Vanguard’s principled investment philosophy and unwavering values, including its commitment to diversification, transparency, and cost-effective service. We firmly believe in the importance of a long-term, strategic charitable plan that minimizes costs and maximizes impact.

Name: Co-Develop
Description:
In the 20th century, physical infrastructure – electrical grids, highways, mass plumbing – created unimaginable public value and prosperity. In the 21st century, digital public infrastructure has the same potential and could accelerate progress towards the Sustainable Development Goals.

Co-Develop is a global non-profit fund supporting countries to build digital public infrastructure that is inclusive, safe, and equitable.

Name: Proteus Fund
Description: Proteus Fund connects philanthropy to the frontlines of social justice. We strive to advance the interconnected goals of racial, gender, queer and disability justice and an inclusive, fully representative democracy. Our work lifts up opportunities, gaps, and challenges facing movement organizations, especially at the state and local level, and then mobilizes support for these efforts. Our donor collaboratives are best-in-class models for bridging responsive philanthropy with cutting-edge social justice movements to create long-lasting impact. Through fiscal sponsorship, Proteus Fund partners with dynamic social justice initiatives which play a powerful role in the larger ecosystem for social change. To date, Proteus Fund, and its affiliated 501c4 organization, the Proteus Action League, have distributed $250 million in grants. Proteus Fund has offices in New York City and metro Boston. Our staff is based across the entire United States.

Name: Missing Children Europe
Description:
Missing Children Europe is the European federation for missing and sexually exploited children, representing 33 organisations in 27 countries. Our mission is to protect children from any kind of violence, abuse or neglect that is caused by or results from them going missing.

Every two minutes, a child is reported missing in the EU. The cause as well as the effect of the disappearance may include situations of violence and abuse, including sexual exploitation. Missing Children Europe’s vision is that all children should be able to rely on effective and holistic systems of child protection, where all measures are taken to empower and protect them from any situation of harm.

Name: Phoenix Children's Foundation
Description:
Phoenix Children’s Foundation is dedicated to providing philanthropic funding support to provide hope, healing and the best possible care to thousands of children each year. 

Social media guidelines: https://pch.foundation/socialguidelines

Name: Philanthropy New York
Description: Philanthropy New York is the principal professional community of philanthropic foundations based in the New York City region. Taken together, our more than 280 member organizations—including the leading private, corporate, family, and public grantmaking foundations in the world—each year provide support totaling more than four billion dollars to thousands of nonprofit organizations and NGOs located in New York, the U.S., and around the world, which in turn focus on an almost endless range of issues and concerns.

Name: The New York Women's Foundation
Description:
Established in 1987, The New York Women’s Foundation has advanced a robust philanthropic strategy rooted in gender, racial, and economic justice, placing us among the largest women-led grantmaking organizations in the world.

Among the first to practice trust-based philanthropy that many have now embraced, we take our role in influencing the future of philanthropy sincerely. Challenging the traditional top-down philanthropic approach, we engage on day one and invest in emerging women-led strategies. Early investment sets the stage for community leaders to flourish. Bringing them to the decision-making table builds agency, while ensuring funds find their best use. By breaking down barriers and facilitating conversations at the deepest level, The Foundation goes beyond the grant to enable continued support and sustain progress.

The Foundation's core areas include:
Economic and Environmental Justice: Championing workers’ rights, dismantling systemic economic barriers in the backdrop of a growing climate crisis.

Safety, Health Equity & Bodily Autonomy: Expanding equitable abortion and birth control access, gender-affirming healthcare, and informed consent, and supporting survivor-led strategies for healing justice.

Civic Engagement and Leadership: Strengthening democratic systems, expanding representation of women and gender-expansive people in civic and political leadership.

Name: Stichting De Verre Bergen
Description: Stichting De Verre Bergen is een filantropische organisatie die zich sinds 2011 inzet voor een sterker en beter Rotterdam. Stichting De Verre Bergen heeft kennis en kapitaal voor gedurfde ideeën. We werken samen met allerlei initiatiefnemers om programma’s te ontwikkelen, uit te voeren en op effectiviteit te onderzoeken. Deze initiatieven hebben met elkaar gemeen dat ze op een vernieuwende manier maatschappelijke vraagstukken aanpakken .

Name: Funders for LGBTQ Issues
Description:
About Us: Working side-by-side with funders and our movement’s organizations through three decades, Funders for LGBTQ Issues remains the sole organization dedicated exclusively to increasing institutional giving to LGBTQ communities.

Mission: Funders for LGBTQ Issues seeks to mobilize philanthropic resources that enhance the well-being of lesbian, gay, bisexual, transgender and queer communities, promote equity and advance racial, economic and gender justice.

Vision: An empowered and functioning democracy with full equality under the law, equal access to services, unconditional respect for difference and the meaningful participation of all communities at tables where decisions are made.

Name: Ebbefos Fonden
Description:
Vi oplever alle, at livet slår Knuder. 

Ebbefos Fonden er sat i verden for at løsne disse knuder for så mange som muligt. 

Vores projekter og samarbejder skal lokalisere konkrete behov hos mennesker, der mistrives, vi skal undersøge nye løsninger og hjælpen fra os skal kunne mærkes.

Vi skal trives.

Ebbefos Fonden er stiftet i 2021 af ægteparret Morten Ebbesen og Vibeke Nyfos og deres to døtre Lærke og Mathilde Nyfos Ebbesen.

Fonden er skabt ud fra et ønske om at bidrage til forebyggelse af den stigende mistrivsel, som udgør en af de helt store samfundsudfordringer i disse år.

Name: Tim Bergling Foundation
Description:
The Tim Bergling Foundation was created by Klas Bergling and Anki Lidén and their family after the death of their son Tim Bergling, also known as Avicii – one of the most acclaimed and beloved producers/performers/songwriters in modern music. Tim’s parents have endowed the foundation with a significant contribution from his estate and will direct proceeds from future Avicii projects and the recent release of the posthumous album ”TIM" (2019) to the foundation.
 

The Tim Bergling Foundation will advocate for the recognition of suicide as a global health emergency and promote removing the stigma attached to the discussion of mental health issues. It will support science-based organizations that engage in research into the causes and prevention of suicide, particularly for young people. 
 

Additionally, the foundation will support non-profits that address issues for which Tim had a passion such as climate change, preservation of endangered species, global hunger, and more.

Name: Schöpflin Stiftung
Description:
Die Schöpflin Stiftung ist eine unabhängige und gemeinnützige Stiftung des bürgerlichen Rechts mit Sitz in Lörrach. Wir sind operativ und fördernd tätig. Gegründet würde die Schöpflin Stiftung im Jahr 2001 von dem Unternehmer Hans Schöpflin gemeinsam mit seinen Geschwistern Heidi Junghanss und Albert Schöpflin.

Als Stiftung fördern wir deutschlandweit Projekte zur Stärkung von Demokratie, Journalismus und Zivilgesellschaft. In Lörrach betreiben wir den Kultur- und Debattenort »Werkraum Schöpflin«, die »Schöpflin Schule« sowie das soziokulturelle Begegnungsprojekt »FABRIC«. Die Schöpflin Stiftung ist Trägerin des Suchtpräventionszentrums »Villa Schöpflin« in Lörrach sowie des »Publix – Haus für Journalismus und Öffentlichkeit« in Berlin.

Impressum: https://www.schoepflin-stiftung.de/impressum/

Name: Ubuntu Pathways
Description:
Ubuntu Pathways (formerly Ubuntu Education Fund) is a nonprofit organization that provides an integrated support system of health, education, and social support in the townships of Port Elizabeth, South Africa. Ubuntu Pathways breaks the cycle of poverty by providing South Africa's most vulnerable children with what all children deserve—everything, every day.

Founded in 1999 by Jacob Lief and Malizole “Banks” Gwaxula, Ubuntu Pathways has become an internationally-recognized model for development. Our impact is made possible by trusting those closest to the problem to create the solution. Today, 95% of the team at the Ubuntu Campus in Port Elizabeth, South Africa comes from the community we serve. These staff members are leaders in their community, driving the vision of one of the most innovative organizations on the continent.

Name: Central Indiana Community Foundation
Description:
CICF is the community foundation for the region of Central Indiana. We help people give to the causes they care about, and we support nonprofits so they can do their work more effectively.

Our Mission: To build a community of opportunity no matter place, race, or identity.

Our Vision: Central Indiana is the most connected, philanthropic, and impactful region in the nation.

Name: Fundação O Pão dos Pobres
Description:
O Pão dos Pobres atende mais de 1.400 mil crianças e adolescentes em vulnerabilidade em seus projetos, e serve 57 mil refeições por mês, em seus três projetos: Acolhimento Institucional, Aprendizagem Profissional e Serviço de Convivência e Fortalecimento de Vínculos e Educação Integral.
A missão da instituição é potencializar o desenvolvimento integral dessas crianças e adolescentes numa perspectiva solidária, construída por meio de práticas socioeducativas.

O Acolhimento Institucional tem como objetivo principal  proteger as crianças e adolescentes de zero a 18 anos, que, por determinação judicial, foram afastados de suas famílias e proporcionar o desenvolvimento integral, buscando garantir os direitos fundamentais (saúde, educação, moradia, profissionalização, convivência familiar). 

A Aprendizagem da Fundação O Pão dos Pobres oferece Cursos Profissionalizantes para adolescentes e jovens em situação de vulnerabilidade e de violação de direitos, de acordo com a Lei de Aprendizagem nº 10.097/2000.  O objetivo deste programa é a formação integral dos jovens, visando sua colocação no mundo do trabalho, como cidadãos éticos e profissionais competentes. 

O Serviço de Convivência e Fortalecimento de Vínculos (SCFV) desenvolve em parceria com a Prefeitura Municipal de Porto Alegre. O atendimento é realizado no turno inverso ao escolar para crianças e adolescentes na faixa etária de seis a 15 anos. Proporciona um ambiente acolhedor e afetivo com atividades educacionais e sociais, priorizando a construção da cidadania e de um projeto de vida.

Neste contexto, a Fundação realiza um sólido trabalho, propiciando a todos um processo de mudança por meio da construção de um projeto de vida embasado no fortalecimento da autonomia e do protagonismo dos mesmos, com vistas ao retorno à família, colocação em família substituta ou a construção de uma vida mais independente.
Location: Porto Alegre, Rio Grande do Sul

Name: IPPF European Network
Description:
The International Planned Parenthood Federation European Network (IPPF EN) is a leading advocate for sexual and reproductive health and rights for all. We work in 41 countries to empower all women, men and young people, especially the most underserved and socially excluded, to live with dignity and to exercise their human right to universal access to sexual and reproductive care. Eradicating poverty and gender inequity is essential to achieving the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) in all countries, yet this cannot be achieved without universal access to sexual and reproductive health and rights (SRHR).

IPPF EN covers a hugely diverse region stretching throughout Europe and Central Asia. From Iceland to Kyrgyzstan, and Norway to Israel, our member associations are independent organisations championing the needs of their local populations, especially the most marginalised.  They all provide essential sexual and reproductive health care, from equipping young people with emotional and sexual literacy, family planning guidance for all and HIV prevention, and care, to safe and legal abortion, support for survivors of rape and sexual violence, and maternal health care.

Name: SOS Préma
Description:
Aide et soutien aux parents d'enfants prématurés et amélioration de la prise en charge globale de la prématurité en France et en Europe.
60 bénévoles, 120 000 parents aidés chaque année.
La prématurité en France représente 8% des naissances (65 000 enfants/an soit 180 bébés par jour).
Nécessité d'adapter les réglementations et les produits.
Initiateur de la Charte de la Parentalité en Entreprise.

Name: Livestrong
Description: Livestrong is dedicated to disruptive, energetic and breakthrough ideas designed to ensure that each and every cancer patient doesn’t just survive, they Livestrong. Known for its powerful brand, Livestrong has become a symbol of hope and inspiration around the world. Since its inception, the Foundation has served 11 million people affected by cancer.

Name: Fehosp
Description: A Fehosp - Federação das Santas Casas e Hospitais Beneficentes do Estado de São Paulo é uma instituição que representa mais de 310 entidades beneficentes por todo território paulista, as quais desenvolvem atividades na área de Saúde.

Name: Compassion Togo
Description:
Compassion International est la principale organisation mondiale en matière de développement holistique de l'enfant grâce au parrainage. Nous libérons les enfants de la pauvreté au nom de Jésus. C'est notre mission. Grâce à une approche holistique du développement de l'enfant, nous mélangeons soigneusement les soins physiques, sociaux, économiques et spirituels… Nous réalisons tout notre travail grâce à des partenariats avec des milliers d'églises locales dans 31 pays à travers le monde.

Au #Togo , nous venons en aide à ce jour plus de 93 000 enfants et jeunes.
 
Nos partenaires de l'église locale adaptent notre modèle holistique de développement de l'enfant aux besoins contextualisés des enfants dans leurs communautés afin de fournir au mieux, tout au long de la vie, les soins dont les enfants qu'ils servent ont le plus besoin.

Name: Ratna Nidhi Charitable Trust
Description:
Ratnanidhi Charitable Trust began not as an institution, but as a family’s heartfelt promise to serve humanity. Established in 1967 by the visionary late Mahashree Mahipat Mehta, Ratnanidhi was born out of a deep-rooted culture of trust, compassion, and humility passed down through generations of the Mehta family.
Built on strong family values, today Ratnanidhi is professionally managed with the spirit of a close-knit family and the precision of a global organization. Guided by love but driven by results, we have partnered with globally respected institutions such as Google Foundation USA, IIT Mumbai, World Bank, and EssilorLuxottica France to scale up our impact with integrity and innovation.
Every project we execute is designed to restore dignity—one life at a time.
- 2.8 lakh+ prosthetic aids provided, helping people walk again with dignity, including the advanced Ratnanidhi Leg.
- 4  lakh+ children screened for vision, with 40,000+ spectacles distributed.
- 1.3 million books donated to 5,000+ schools, nurturing a culture of reading in every corner of India.
- 2.3 crore hot meals served under our Food for Education program, ensuring no child studies on an empty stomach.
- 14 lakh garments lovingly donated to underprivileged communities, spreading warmth in more ways than one
From mobility to meals, from books to surgeries, Ratnanidhi’s reach extends across India—touching lives in slums, villages, and schools where help was once just a dream.
What sets us apart is the seamless blend of heart-led compassion and data-driven execution. Every program is carefully measured for impact and scaled with professionalism, making your support go further, faster.
We are not just an NGO. We are a living mission—powered by your belief, and our family's timeless values.
Come, explore our journey. And let’s create a kinder India together.

Name: Denis & Lenora Foretia Foundation
Description: The Denis & Lenora Foretia Foundation was established to catalyze Africa’s economic transformation by focusing on social entrepreneurship, science and technology, innovation, public health and progressive policies that create economic opportunities for all. The foundation works in partnership with local governments, policy makers, private enterprises, civil society organizations as well as development partners to expand the resources available to entrepreneurs, farmers, and small business owners in addition to improving individual livelihoods.

Name: Red Umbrella Fund
Description:
Red Umbrella Fund is the first-ever global grantmaking collaborative guided by and for sex workers. The fund was launched in 2012 to support movements and organisations fighting for sex workers’ health, human and labour rights and self-determination.

An International Steering Committee comprised of sex workers and donors oversees the strategic vision of the fund. Sex workers hold the majority of seats on the Programme Advisory Committee of the Red Umbrella Fund where the decisions on grants are made. A small secretariat oversee the day-to-day functions and supports the grantee-partners. The Red Umbrella Fund is hosted by Mama Cash, an international women’s fund.

Name: Patterson Family Foundation
Description: The Patterson Family Foundation, based in Kansas City, Missouri, is a family-led foundation that extends the legacy of Neal and Jeanne Patterson. The Foundation’s mission, “Working together to help rural communities thrive,” is carried forward through strategic grantmaking and other collaborative initiatives that serve rural counties across Kansas and western Missouri.

Name: World Family Coin
Description:
World Family Coin (WFC Coin) – is the first decentralised platform for the management of donations and fundraising, which will be managed independently by the DAO. the DAO will be made up of the entire WFC Token holding community. All the holders acquire decision-making power while maintaining full control over the management of the WFC Platform, the decisions will be carried out by the DAO through votes managed by smart contracts. All active members within the WFC ecosystem will be incentivised by receiving remuneration for each vote performed.

The DAO will have full control over future decisions and implementations that will be proposed in the future within the ecosystem.

World Family Coin is a no-profit blockchain project born to solve many problems regarding donations and fundraising that operates on the fields of:

medical research
natural disasters
innovative startups and philanthropic projects 

PROBLEMS

Lack of security that the donation reaches the cause
Lack of transparency
Poor vision on development and conclusion of projects
Centralised platforms (No Blockchain)
No incentives for donors
Bank charges and long times to supply the funds, high fees

SOLUTIONS

WFC solves all problems by differentiating itself from traditional donation methods.

Safety and transparency are guaranteed from the blockchain
Decentralized management from the DAO
Full view on monitoring through the WFC Platform
Incentives to feed the ecosystem
Reduced management costs thanks to the use of cryptocurrencies

Name: Hersenletsel-Uitleg + Brain Injury Explanation
Description:
Hersenletsel-Uitleg heeft tot doel om kennis over hersenletsel toegankelijk te maken, ook voor de leek, in  begrijpbaar Nederlands  middels een website, kennisbank + social media. Hersenletsel-Uitleg heeft tevens projecten opgezet om kansen te creëren voor hersenletsel-getroffenen zodat zij beter in staat zijn deel te nemen aan de maatschappij. Hersenletsel-Uitleg wil samenwerken met hersenletsel-organisaties professionals, mantelzorgers en hersenletsel-getroffenen en waar mogelijk bruggen slaan om de puzzel die hersenletsel heet samen verder af te maken. Hersenletsel-Uitleg is een non-profit organisatie waar medewerkers pro bono hun diensten aan verlenen.


Non Profit Organization connecting brain injury organizations and brain injury victims. Building bridges. Sharing knowledge about acquired brain injury and stimulating research for sensory overload /over stimulation after brain injury called 'flooding'. Creating possibilities for brain injured people. See http://braininjuryx.wix.com/project-flooding and http://www.braininjury-explanation.com/questionaire-on-flooding

Name: The Eli and Edythe Broad Foundation
Description: We elevate organizations, initiatives and leaders that boldly address the challenges of our time and expand opportunity for Los Angeles’ historically marginalized communities. In addition to supporting the important work of scores of non-profit organizations around the country, the foundation has created groundbreaking independent institutions in each of its three investment areas, including The Broad Center, which develops leaders to help transform America’s urban public schools, The Broad Institute, a global leader in genomics, and The Broad, a museum in downtown Los Angeles devoted to showcasing great contemporary art.

Name: Gary Community Ventures
Description: We partner with the community to deliver breakthrough solutions that transform the arc of opportunity for Colorado's kids and families.

Name: The Pollination Project Foundation
Description:
OUR IMPACT
With an 11-year legacy, we've empowered over 5,000 extraordinary humans who have left an indelible mark on local, national, and global scales. These remarkable leaders, who we call "Heartivists," have saved lives and forever changed destinies. In many cases, our Foundation provided their first check, igniting that vital spark of belief to kindle years of transformative impact. Explore our stories to discover more!

HOW WE WORK
The Pollination Project Foundation provides seed funding ranging from $1,000 to $10,000 through a network of 65 grant advisors and five country coordinators. This participatory grantmaking approach empowers individuals to drive positive change in their communities. Besides financial aid, our Foundation supports grantees with fundraising guidance, capacity-building workshops, mindfulness retreats to prevent burnout, and resources for achieving tangible goals.

WORKING WITh US FOR FUNDERS
Foundations and donors can engage with us in two ways. First, we accept pass-through funding to channel resources to the most deserving hands on the ground, recognizing the fundamental importance of strengthening leadership on the ground to catalyze change. Our strategic partners nourish the front line leaders through TPP.

Second, we serve as a top-of-the-funnel resource for larger funders, ensuring that exceptional changemakers and projects receive the attention they merit. After we collect reports from our most successful grantees, we offer connections to future funding opportunities.

Funders please reach out here: partnerships@thepollinationproject.org

Working with us if you’d like a grant: 

Supporting your good work is the reason we exist! To apply for a grant please follow this link: https://thepollinationsproject.org/apply

Name: 20/20 Levels
Description:
20/20 Levels is a social mobility organisation, empowering Black and racially underrepresented young people through opportunities to maximise their potential.

Whether you want to land your dream job, build a business, or just figure out where you’re headed – we’ll help you find success by opening doors to the best opportunities.

We’re not saying it will be easy. Nothing worthwhile is. You need a willingness to evolve, and an ability to work hard. In return, we’ll back you. Prepare you. And push you further than you thought you could go.

So you can put your future in the best hands possible. Your own.

Our programmes have transformed the lives of over a thousand young people across London. With over 60% of our candidates gaining new employment within 3 months of completing our programme.

We strive to deliver the best support and resources possible. We work with the best businesses and we ask that our young people try to show up as their best selves.

We are currently hiring. 

Chief of Staff: https://www.peridotpartners.co.uk/jobs/chief-of-staff-20-20-levels/
Social Media Manager: https://www.charityjob.co.uk/social-media-manager-jobs

Name: Fundação João Paulo II
Description:
A Fundação João Paulo II é uma entidade sem fins lucrativos, mantenedora do Sistema Canção Nova de Comunicação, do Departamento de Áudio Visuais (Davi) e da Rede de Desenvolvimento Social Canção Nova.

Tem como fonte de recursos as doações espontâneas de pessoas que acreditam neste trabalho e aderem a esta missão. Posiciona-se como instrumento de atendimento à população em situação de vulnerabilidade social, comprometendo-se em promover os valores universais e responsabilidade social. Para isso, conta com a participação de parceiros, missionários, voluntários e com a sociedade, fazendo com que homens e mulheres tenham suas vidas transformadas.

Com o objetivo de transformação é que a Fundação João Paulo II atua com foco nas áreas de saúde, assistência social, educação, cultura e meios de comunicação.

Name: Pakistan Centre for Philanthropy (PCP)
Description:
PCP is an independent not for profit government support organization, established in 2001 under Section 42 of the Companies Act, 2017 to facilitate linkages between the government, civil society and private organizations. Its mission is to promote the volume and effectiveness of philanthropy for social development in Pakistan. During the past two decades, the PCP has created a niche for itself as the lead organization for advancing the agenda of philanthropy and improving its utilization for gainful social outcomes. Besides being a partner of development networks at local level and the Government of Pakistan, PCP is also engaged in regional and global activities with organizations and individuals of common interest to share, learn, explore and strengthen the essence and value of philanthropy. 

PCP is a designated Certification Agency by the Federal Board of Revenue (FBR) to conduct performance evaluation and capacity enhancement of non-profit organizations and foundations that contribute to the development and well-being of both the nation and the communities they serve within Pakistan. In its journey of 22 years, the Centre has achieved a substantial increase in the number of certified not-for-profit organizations (NPOs) reaching a mark of about 1500, and effectively linking donors with these NPOs. 

PCP is also the sole organization generating knowledge and evidence-based research on various dimensions of philanthropy. The Centre’s research work has expanded the empirical map on indigenous giving routed through individuals, corporates, Pakistani diaspora and institutions or foundations that has mustered public policy support through effective advocacy and helping the givers better plan and organize social investments for civic good in Pakistan. Additionally, the PCP’s Philanthropy Support Services program has come a long way in facilitating both national and international foundations/donors to get linked with agile and trustworthy non-profit organizations working

Name: The Howard G. Buffett Foundation
Description:
Established in 1999, the Howard G. Buffett Foundation (HGBF) is a private family foundation working to improve the standard of living and quality of life for the world’s most impoverished and marginalized populations. We look for opportunities where our funding is the catalytic capital that seeds sustainable, transformational change.

We focus our funding in three main areas:
1.food security;
2.water security; and
3.conflict mitigation, resolution and post-conflict development.

The Foundation does not accept unsolicited proposals and we typically do not provide general operating support.

December 31, 2045, is the final dissolution of the Foundation’s assets.

Name: Veritus Group
Description: A full-service, mid-level, major gift and planned giving fundraising agency serving the non-profit community. We specialize in helping non-profits, create, build and manage their fundraising programs as they work to create authentic donor relationships, strengthen their donor pipeline, and increase net revenue. We provide unparalleled support to help executive directors and fundraising professionals be successful by keeping them focused and accountable. And, we have fun doing it.

Name: FUNDACIÓN INTERRA
Description:
La Fundación Colombiana para el Desarrollo Local Sostenible e Inclusivo INTERRA de carácter no lucrativo y no gubernamental, tiene como misión co-construir el desarrollo local mediante la generación de conocimientos, análisis y apoyos técnicos que sean factibles e innovadores para promover la transformación positiva, responsable y sostenible del país.

Los principales retos de la Fundación INTERRA ante un entorno dinámico y cambiante:

* Atender necesidades locales en co-construcción con la comunidad y autoridades en territorio.
* Generar conocimiento y capacidades en entes territoriales para fortalecer el diseño de política pública local, su implementación, seguimiento y evaluación.
* Apoyar la gestión pública territorial para una adecuada toma de decisiones basada en evidencia, criterios técnicos y ambientalmente sostenibles.
* Promover y ejecutar alianzas con entidades internacionales, nacionales y locales que favorezcan la transformación positiva de las realidades sociales de los territorios.
* Aportar a la discusión y comprensión de las necesidades locales en Colombia.
* Incrementar las capacidades económicas, ambientales, institucionales, tecnológicas para hacer frente a los retos globales impulsando las ventajas locales.

Name: perifaCode
Description:
Nossa missão é inserir pessoas de origem periférica na área de programação.

Sabemos que a área de desenvolvimento de software é grande e continua crescendo. É uma área de acesso democrático, pois o conteúdo necessário para trabalhar com isso está (quase sempre) livre e aberto na internet, mas para pessoas periféricas, com menos acesso a informação, ainda falta um certo apoio.

Como comunidade nós focamos em inserir essas pessoas o mais rápido no mercado de trabalho, pois temos a vivência de pessoas que vieram da periferia e confirmamos que depois que entramos nesta área a nossa vida mudou 100%.

Nós estamos aqui para levar a informação para a periferia e servir de grupo de apoio para que essas pessoas tenham as oportunidades que não tiveram ainda para chegar nesse universo.

Name: The Dollywood Foundation
Description:
The Dollywood Foundation is a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization founded in 1988 by Dolly Parton to inspire the children in her home county to achieve educational success. Initial efforts focused on decreasing the dropout rate in the county's high school. In the early 1990's, Dolly promised every 7th and 8th-grade student $500 if they successfully graduated from high school. This effort, called The Buddy Program, reduced the dropout rate for these two classes from 35% to 6%. More importantly, the program catalyzed the community to provide additional resources to sustain this improvement.   Currently each year five high school students in Sevier County, receive a $15,000.00 Dolly Parton Scholarship fund.

This local program's success was the impetus of what is now the flagship program of The Foundation, Dolly Parton's Imagination Library.  This monthly book gifting program for children under five focuses on inspiring a love of books and reading . What was founded as a local program grew into an international movement with over 3 million children receiving books each month in five countries and over 240 million books gifted globally since inception. 

The mission of The Dollywood Foundation is to share the life and legacy of Dolly Parton as an inspiration for children to Dream More, Learn More, Care More, and Be More.

Name: The Rufford Foundation
Description:
The Rufford Foundation is a UK registered charity that funds nature conservation projects across the developing world.

The flagship Rufford Small Grants Programme awards funding to individuals working in developing countries who are starting on the ladder of conservation research and establishing pilot programmes. Grantees who complete project reporting may apply for one of the further grants from the Foundation. 

Since the Rufford Small Grants Programme was set up over 20 years ago, it has given in excess of £30 million through more than 5100 grants to projects in over 150 countries.

Name: Acelerar España
Description: Acelerar España es una aceleradora filantrópica sin fines de lucro liderada por Leandro Sigman que tiene como objetivo acompañar la internacionalización de startups argentinas en su desembarco en España

Name: InnovAction Lab
Description:
#InnovActionLab is a non-profit organisation, founded in 2011, whose sole mission is to create talent-preneurs.

It achieves this through an emotionally high impact, no-nonsense course made up of seminars and trial pitches. The course is offered for free to students between 18 and 38 years of age. The seminars are held by entrepreneurs and startuppers and the student teams are all assigned mentors. Of about 50 teams, up to 8 finalists are selected for the finals, usually held end of June.

The finals are in front of about 800 people with about 100 investors! That's high impact!

It is all financed through the generous donations from our sponsors and supporters. Why do they donate? Because the unintended end-product are startups. Since 2011, InnovActionLab alumni have created or become part of a total of 25 startups to date (March 2013), raising over  €4,000,000 from VCs and angels.

However not everyone decides that entrepreneurship is the best outcome for them. It's horses for courses! But that's the whole point of #InnovActionLab!

Name: Fondation Jérôme Lejeune
Description:
La Fondation Jérome Lejeune a pour mission la recherche de traitements pour la trisomie 21 et les autres déficiences intellectuelles d’origine génétique, le suivi médical et la défense de la dignité humaine. 
Au service des malades et de leur famille, la Fondation Jérôme Lejeune poursuit trois objectifs : chercher, soigner, défendre .

- Chercher
1er financeur en France de la recherche sur les maladies génétiques de l’intelligence, la Fondation Jérôme Lejeune développe et coordonne les travaux de chercheurs et finance des programmes en France comme à l’étranger.
Une banque de données biologiques – le laboratoire BioJeL– a été mise en place : il permet à des chercheurs du monde entier de se procurer les matériaux génétiques nécessaires à l’avancée de leurs recherches. 

- Soigner
L’Institut Jérôme Lejeune est l’organisme de la Fondation dédié au soin des malades. Ce centre de consultations médicales spécialisées prévient et traite en particulier les sur-handicaps affectant fréquemment les trisomiques 21. Au-delà du suivi médical proprement dit, d’autres spécialistes accompagnent les malades pour faciliter leur insertion, notamment en milieu scolaire.

- Défendre
La Fondation Jérôme Lejeune défend la vie des personnes handicapées dès la conception. Elle est donc vigilante aux questions de bioéthique : dans un contexte où l’accélération des progrès scientifiques pose à la société des interrogations fondamentales.

Name: Pisces Foundation
Description: At the Pisces Foundation, we believe if we act now and boldly, we can quickly accelerate to a world where people and nature thrive together. We support innovators who know what it takes and are doing what’s necessary to have clean and abundant water, a safe climate, and kids with the environmental know-how to create a sustainable world. By mainstreaming powerful new solutions, we will spark immediate gains and lasting benefits for people and nature.

Name: Télécoms Sans Frontières (TSF)
Description:
Télécoms Sans Frontières is the leading international NGO specialised in technology and telecommunications for humanitarian crises of all kinds. Our teams are made up of telecom and network engineers who are ready to deploy to disaster zones 24/7 thanks to their global presence in Europe, Asia and the Americas.

Throughout the years, our areas of intervention have evolved according to the needs of the humanitarian field, however our principal mandate remains to connect the unconnected, be it governments, the United Nations, fellow NGOs, or families who have survived devastating crises.

Today, we are innovators in our field, and develop technologies that are applied to a wide variety of long-term humanitarian issues. We place emphasis on developing tools to respond to a plethora of needs including protection, education and health and nutritional issues. Our unique expertise often sees our teams deployed throughout the world to train national and international bodies in emergency response in the aim of galvanising the impact of global humanitarian aid thanks to technology.

Name: Fondation de Luxembourg
Description:
The Fondation de Luxembourg is an independent center of expertise in the field of philanthropy. The Foundation acts as umbrella foundation and assists donors from all over Europe in the structuring, managing and monitoring of their charitable foundations active in Europe and worldwide. 

Created by the State of Luxembourg and the Oeuvre Nationale de Secours Grande-Duchesse Charlotte, the Fondation de Luxembourg's mission is to promote and facilitate private philanthropic engagement.

For more detailed information, please visit http://www.fdlux.lu/.

Name: Michelson Found Animals Foundation
Description:
We're a non-profit with a focused mission: Saving pets, Enriching lives. We love pets, but they aren't the best arbiters for their own cause. Don't worry, they're not offended by that. They give us endless love, compassion, loyalty, and cuddles. It's our end of the bargain to give them food, shelter, protection, and endless tummy rubs. That's why it's our mission to put pets in homes and keep them there– because all pets are meant to be with good people. Currently our programs include: 

Better Neighbor Project:
www.betterneighborla.org

Pet-Inclusive Housing Initiative
www.petsandhousing.org 

Michelson Institute for Pet Professions 
www.foundanimals.org/michelson-institute-for-pet-professions/ 

Leap Venture Studio
https://www.foundanimals.org/leap/

Michelson Prize & Grants 
www.michelsonprizeandgrants.org/  

Join us in our mission. 

See career opportunities by visiting https://www.foundanimals.org/careers/

Support for Found Animals and its initiatives comes from the generous contributions of Dr. Gary Michelson and Alya Michelson.

Name: Greater Kansas City Community Foundation | Greater Horizons
Description:
The Greater Kansas City Community Foundation has ranked among the top 1 percent of community foundations in America since 1999, delivering innovative products and services that make charitable giving convenient and efficient for donors. The Community Foundation knows that every gift matters – no matter how big or small – and encourages donors to maximize their giving and support the causes they care about through charitable giving accounts like donor-advised funds. 

In 2004, the Community Foundation created Greater Horizons to serve those who wish to use the Community Foundation's products and services with the flexibility of a national entity.

View our most recent annual report: https://www.growyourgiving.org/annual-report/

Name: Fondation Sylvia Bongo Ondimba
Description:
La Fondation Sylvia Bongo Ondimba pour la Famille soutient les populations vulnérables du Gabon à travers des projets destinés aux femmes, aux jeunes et aux personnes fragilisées.

Depuis 2010, la Fondation promeut les valeurs d’honnêteté et d’intégrité, de solidarité, de complémentarité et de
professionnalisme.

Notre mission est d’encourager tous les Gabonais à devenir de véritables partenaires du développement et les premiers acteurs de leur propre réussite, afin que tous bénéficient des nouvelles opportunités du développement.

Name: MIDE | Madrid Innovation Driven Ecosystem
Description:
MADRID INNOVATION DRIVEN ECOSYSTEM

Formed by a multi-stakeholder team, MIDE aims to develop the Entrepreneurship and Innovation Ecosystem of Madrid region with a global perspective. It was born from the REAP (Regional Entrepreneurship Acceleration Program) developed by MIT. 

We seek to be a platform for the main stakeholders of the ecosystem to be connected (Startups, Corporates, Universities, Venture Capital, Government and other relevant agents). We aim to unify efforts among them in the improvement of the Global Competitive Position of Madrid and the generated IMPACT in terms of innovation and entrepreneurship

Members of the GIN (Global Innovation Network) of Massachusetts Institute of Technology, currently connecting with more than 45 cities around the world within the REAP program.

MIDE = (Madrid Innovation Driven Ecosystem)
This is not about creating. 
This is about connecting.

Name: Mercy-USA for Aid and Development
Description: A nonprofit organization committed to humanitarian relief and building self-sufficiency around the world since 1988.

Name: The Paul & Daisy Soros Fellowships for New Americans
Description: The Paul & Daisy Soros Fellowships for New Americans provides 30 Fellowships each year to US graduate students who are either immigrants or the children of immigrants. Each new class of Fellows joins a community of immigrant leaders and scholars who all are working to make an impact in their academic field and American society. For full eligibility requirements, please visit www.pdsoros.org.

Name: Proyecto Programar
Description: Programar es un proyecto de inserción laboral en tecnología mediante una capacitación integral de alto valor agregado. Es un proyecto que apunta no sólo a la formación técnica sino también al desarrollo personal, el trabajo en equipo y el desarrollo general de facultades que permitan a los jóvenes insertarse al mercado laboral de manera adecuada, potenciando sus posibilidades.

Name: TARMAC
Description: TARMAC accompagne les populations les plus précaires : Hommes ou femmes isolées et à la rue, familles en situation de grande précarité, demandeurs d’asile, personnes éloignées de l’emploi, victimes de violence, souffrant de problèmes de santé, de troubles psychiatriques et/ou addictifs…
Location: Le Mans, Pays de la Loire

Name: Hope Air
Description:
In Canada, healthcare coverage starts at the door of the doctor’s office. How you get to the doctor and any related travel costs fall to you. For people living in small and rural communities across the country, traveling long distances to their specialist medical appointments comes at a cost of time or money. Canadian families living near or below the poverty line can afford neither.   

We're the only national charity providing free flights to low-income Canadians who must travel to specialist medical care that does not exist in their communities. Hope Air believes that where you live in Canada and how much money you make should not determine your ability to access our healthcare system. A free flight lets families focus on what is truly important – their health. 

We serve people from across Canada, of every age, with any illness. Since 1986, we are proud to have arranged over 150,000 flights. 

Better access. Better health.

Name: Green Crescent Trust
Description:
Green Crescent Trust (GCT) is a non-profit organization in Sindh, Pakistan, that focuses on development through education. It was established in 1995 by a group of critically conscious and passionate citizens with one goal: making a better Pakistan by providing access to quality education. It started out with just one school and a few students in Karachi.

GCT is operated by professionals under the supervision of the Board of Trustees, which now runs a network of more than 150 schools in underserved areas of Sindh. GCT aims to shift mindsets in the communities regarding education by providing opportunities to bring enhancements to their daily lives. It has also expanded its services through other programs like the Orphan Support Program (OSP), Center for Educational Research and Development (CERD), and The Water Project.

Name: The Families and Workers Fund
Description: Today is our once-in-a-generation shot to repair and reimagine the systems that fuel economic security and opportunity in the United States: our jobs, workforce and public benefits systems. Families and Workers is a multi-donor fund helping philanthropy to meet this moment.

Name: مؤسسة سليمان بن عبدالعزيز الراجحي الخيرية
Description:
رؤيتنا: ريادة المنح الخيري لتنمية المجتمع بإيمان وإتقان


رسالتنا: تقديم المنح لتعظيم الخير وتحقيق النمو المجتمعي وتمكين المستفيدين بأفضل الممارسات


قيمنا: المسؤولية، الإتقان، الشراكة المجتمعية، الشفافية، الإبداع والابتكار


استراتيجياتنا: المنح الإستراتيجي، النفع المجتمعي، الارتقاء بالجهات الغير ربحية

Name: NoVo Foundation
Description: Established in 2006, the NoVo Foundation supports initiatives that promote a holistic, interconnected and healing vision for humanity. To learn more, please visit our website.

Name: The Pivot Fund
Description: The Pivot Fund is a new venture philanthropy organization dedicated to investing $500 million into independent BIPOC-led community news.

Name: Women's Foundation of Alabama
Description: Women’s Foundation of Alabama is the leading voice and philanthropic engine for women in the state, driving a mission of gender and economic equity. Driven by research, we identify and fund innovative solutions to address critical needs and remove barriers to create deep, lasting change for our region and state. As Alabama’s first and only philanthropic foundation focused solely on addressing the needs of women and girls, we leverage the resources of generous individuals, nonprofits, corporate partners, and community leaders to build a world where all women thrive.

Name: Samvid Ventures
Description: Samvid Ventures is committed to improving lives of underserved individuals through education, entrepreneurship, and wellness.

Name: ONG Santé Diabète
Description:
The NGO Santé Diabète was founded in 2001 to improve the prevention and management of diabetes in Africa. We provide technical assistance to support prevention policies and treatment of diabetes.

Our actions are guided by humanitarian principles of independence, impartiality and neutrality. 

Santé Diabète works closely with 40 collaborators and 200 partners, screened over 82 000 people, trained 2 000 healthcare professionals, and supported 25 000 people with type 2 diabetes and 1 500 people with type 1 diabetes.

Name: H3D Foundation
Description: The H3D Foundation was established to complement the work of the H3D Drug Discovery and Development Centre (www.H3D.uct.ac.za), specifically in relation to capacity development that enables and unlocks the potential for sustainable drug discovery and development activities across Africa.

Name: Philanthropy and Nonprofit Leadership at Carleton University
Description:
The Philanthropy and Nonprofit Leadership (Master or Diploma) program prepares graduates to be innovators and leaders in nonprofits, social enterprises, philanthropic foundations, development offices, and in the financial institutions, governments and businesses that interact with them. The MPNL or DPNL program is designed for full-time students preparing to be changemakers in this field, as well as experienced professionals studying part-time. The program, offered through the School of Public Policy and Administration, is offered in a flexible format involving intensive summer institutes at Carleton and interactive online learning. 

A shorter Graduate Diploma in Philanthropy and Nonprofit Leadership enables
working professionals to upgrade their knowledge and skills to meet the challenges of the complex and rapidly changing world of philanthropy and leadership in this sector. 

Faculty associated with the program and with the Carleton Centre for Community Innovation (3ci) are engaged in a wide range of collaborative and individual research projects, providing students with great opportunities for engagement and supervision. 

Graduates of the MPNL program will be innovators, leaders and makers of social change. Students can tailor the program for leadership positions
in nonprofits, charities and social enterprises; advocacy organizations
and international NGOs; grantmaking foundations; resource development and fundraising in universities, hospital foundations and other nonprofits; governments that develop policy or collaborate in service delivery with the nonprofit sector; and financial institutions and other corporations working on corporate social responsibility and impact investing

Name: Philanthropy Network Greater Philadelphia
Description:
Philanthropy Network Greater Philadelphia convenes and connects the funders as catalysts for positive action, innovation, and effective giving in our region, providing a unique forum for learning and collaboration. The Network brings funders together with community partners and leaders to spark solutions that foster a more prosperous, innovative and equitable region. 

We welcome all types of funders to join the Network, including foundations, corporations, grantmaking public charities, individual donors and others who give for the greater good of Greater Philadelphia. We also welcome wealth managers, family offices, advisors and other professionals who share the mission of fostering effective philanthropy and see Philanthropy Network as a unique resource to support their clients’ giving.

Please visit our website or contact us to learn more.

Name: The Russell Family Foundation
Description:
George and Jane Russell bought the small brokerage and mutual fund business of George's grandfather in 1958 and developed it into one of the world's leading investment management and advisory firms, Frank Russell Company. George pioneered the business of pension fund consulting and Jane was the company's architect of corporate culture. Together they earned Frank Russell Company national recognition as an outstanding workplace—achieving the ranking of number 11 on Fortune Magazine's 100 Best Companies to Work For in America.

In 1999, the Russells sold Frank Russell Company to Northwestern Mutual Life. The company was renamed the Russell Investment Group. Jane and George Russell created The Russell Family Foundation with part of the sale proceeds, providing a way for their extended family to make a quiet, positive impact in the community. The Russell Family Foundation values integrity, mutual trust, constructive communication, life-long learning, and courage. We seek to demonstrate these values in all of our activities.

Name: Philanthropy Ireland
Description:
Philanthropy Ireland is the network for the philanthropy community in Ireland

Philanthropy Ireland is a dynamic membership organisation dedicated to advancing philanthropy in Ireland. Our aim is to cultivate a thriving community of philanthropic entities, fostering collaboration and innovation to address the most pressing societal challenges.

Established in 1998, we now have a community of 50+ members, 40+ collaborations and 20+ partnerships. Reach out to become a member of Philanthropy Ireland and join our vibrant community of grant makers and their supporters.

PI is a company limited by guarantee. Registered Charity number 20047382. Companies Registration Office number 371896. CHY (Revenue) number 14484.

Name: ACODEV asbl
Description:
ACODEV is the professional federation of French-speaking Belgian development NGOs.  Her mission is to foster the quality of the work of her members through capacity building support and through lobbying for an enabling environment.

Fin 2010, ACODEV compte 82 ONG membres.

Name: Be The Earth Foundation
Description:
Be The Earth is a living lab experimenting with ethical ways to redistribute wealth and regenerate ecosystems.

We are part of a global movement of individuals and organisations committed to nurturing life and nature. Together, we create economies that prioritise the regeneration of all life over the pursuit of profit and power.

Name: The Actuarial Foundation
Description: Our goal is to unleash the power of math education and transform futures by leveraging the talents and resources of the actuarial community. Through an array of programs an resources, we envision a future where everyone experiences confidence and opportunities available through math proficiency. Contact us to learn more about how your time and resources support programs and activities!

Name: Wyss Foundation
Description: The Wyss Foundation is a private, charitable foundation dedicated to supporting innovative, lasting solutions that improve lives, empower communities, and strengthen connections to the land. The Foundation is headquartered in Washington, DC in a restored historic building that houses its offices and rents space to several other non-profits.

Name: WAHA International
Description:
-----------------------------------ENGLISH--------------------------------------
Women and Health Alliance International is a not-for-profit organization with the over-arching goal of giving access to quality medical care to disadvantaged communities around the world. WAHA International has become an important player in terms of implementing and funding a wide range of activities that improve healthcare services in conflict zones and in settings where the needs are greatest, and in advocating for greater action to be taken to reduce maternal deaths. Created in 2009 in Paris, the humanitarian organization is also specialized in addressing maternal care, especially post-partum complications and obstetric fistula operations. 
-----------------------------------FRANÇAIS--------------------------------------
Women and Health Alliance International est une organisation non-gouvernementale qui a pour objectif principal d'offrir des soins médicaux de qualité aux communautés défavorisées à travers le monde. WAHA International est devenu un acteur majeur dans la gestion et le financement de projets visant à améliorer les services de santé en zone de conflit et dans les communautés où les besoins sont les plus grands, en veillant à contribuer à la réduction des décès maternels. Créée en 2009 à Paris, l'organisation humanitaire est aussi spécialisée dans la santé maternelle, notamment la prise en charge des complications post-partum et des opérations de fistules obstétricales.

Name: Meyer Foundation
Description: The Meyer Foundation pursues and invests in solutions that build an equitable Greater Washington community in which economically vulnerable people thrive.

Name: Barbara Bush Foundation for Family Literacy
Description:
Over the past 30 years, the Barbara Bush Foundation for Family Literacy has raised and provided more than $110 million in support to create or expand adult and family literacy programs in all 50 states and the District of Columbia. 

Adult literacy is a sweeping, silent crisis in our country. Today, there are tens of millions of American adults who cannot fill out a job application, read to their child, understand simple medication labels, or use a ballot to vote. This crisis is largely ignored, historically underfunded, and woefully under-researched, despite being one of the great solvable problems of our time. 

Barbara Bush chose literacy as her life’s work based on a simple, but powerful belief: “If more people could read, write, and comprehend, we would be that much closer to solving so many of the problems plaguing our society.” 

The Barbara Bush Foundation for Family Literacy holds ready solutions with high impact outcomes. We are committed to fulfilling the legacy of Barbara Bush, the First Lady of Literacy, by working towards an America in which all adults have the opportunity to read, write, and comprehend in order to navigate the world with dignity.

Name: Fonselp.org
Description:
Conectamos donaciones con causas que valen la pena.

Fonselp es una plataforma digital que vincula empresas con organizaciones sociales de América Latina, maximizando el impacto positivo que las empresas generan en su comunidad.
Location: Buenos Aires, Ciudad autónoma de Buenos Aires

Name: Behring Foundation
Description:
Founded in 2020, the Behring Foundation is a philanthropic family organization focused on education and technology in Brazil.

We invest in people committed to excellence, support extraordinary initiatives that harness technology for social transformation, and focus on delivering consistent, lasting impact.

With dozens of projects supported, hundreds of scholarships awarded, and thousands of young lives reached, we continue to build pathways that empower Brazilian talent to take on the challenges of our time.

Name: Compassion Ecuador
Description: Compassion se preocupa por los niños que viven en situación de extrema pobreza. En esas circunstancias, los niños necesitan más que solo un alivio temporal; necesitan una transformación que viene de un proceso de desarrollo a largo plazo y centrado en DIOS.

Name: Linking Indy Women
Description:
We meet on the third Wednesday of the month at 7:30am at The Hatch in Broad Ripple and feature a guest speaker who shares her inspiring story. 

Text LIW to (317) 942-3064 to get our app.

Name: Linking Independent Women
Description: We meet on the third Wednesday of the month at 7:30am at The Hatch in Broad Ripple and feature a guest speaker who shares her inspiring story.

Name: Fred and Barbara Erb Family Foundation
Description:
We are a family foundation based in southeast Michigan and proud to honor the legacy of Fred and Barbara Erb, who were deeply dedicated to their community.
