Their shared values inspired the work of the Foundation, which started in 2009. Since then, we've made grants totaling over $180 million to initiatives that improve the health of the Great Lakes, strengthen the region's arts and cultural organizations, advance Alzheimer's research, promote sustainable business, and protect democracy.

In 2022, we made the bold decision to spenddown all our assets and conclude our grantmaking by 2034. In our final decade we remain committed to supporting the leaders and organizations who are shaping a more sustainable and culturally vibrant future for Southeast Michigan and the Great Lakes region.

Name: Fondation VISEO
Description:
UN DÉFI POUR MIEUX VIVRE ENSEMBLE

Reconnue d’utilité publique a été agréée par décret en conseil du gouvernement (2018/422)
Levier du développement économique et social de notre pays, elle a pour objectif d’agir dans trois domaines : l'éducation, la santé et la culture.

--------------------

NOS ENGAGEMENTS

L’engagement de l’ensemble des salariés du groupe est une clé de réussite :

Par esprit de solidarité, plus de 700 collaborateurs du groupe VISEO se sont engagés à soutenir les actions de le Fondation.

Notre objectif pour l’année 2018-2019 est de fédérer l’Etat, les entreprises, les ONG, les associations afin de mettre en place des projets pérennes et d’envergure dans un souci de réussite collective.

--------------------

REJOIGNEZ-NOUS

En participant aux projets de la Fondation, vous bénéficierez d’une fiscalité avantageuse.

Vos apports matériels ou financiers contribueront à soutenir nos actions au service de la population Malagasy la plus démunie.

Au titre de la coopération avec Madagascar, les institutions nationales et internationales pourront nous apporter leur expertise, leur réseau d’influence, et leur concours financier.

Name: Foellinger Foundation
Description: Foellinger Foundation, Inc. is a private, charitable foundation that awards grants for the benefit of people in Fort Wayne and Allen County, Indiana. The Foundation was created in 1958 by Helene R. Foellinger, The News-Sentinel’s publisher from 1936 to 1980, and Esther A. Foellinger, her mother. It was established to carry forward their family’s tradition of civic involvement and active concern for the well-being of their community. From 1958 through 1987, their gifts and bequests developed the Foundation into a permanent philanthropic endowment. Its grantmaking continues to reflect the Foellinger family’s interest in encouraging a community of self-reliant citizens.

Name: Can'd Aid
Description: We believe in the healing power of human connection. We envision a world fueled by authentic relationships that spark joy and unlock our inherent capacity to create positive change. Together, we are building a movement and model for innovative, responsive and connected community engagement.

Name: Not for Profit
Description:
Provide overall vision and leadership in growing the programs and participation within a
Faith based NGO community. Primary responsibilities include but are not limited to:

Selecting and working with new board members, Program selection and management, Development/Fundraising to include: Annual Golf Tournament(s) Super Bowl Parties, Oktoberfest Activities, St. Patrick’s Day Dinner Party, Retreats, Silent Auctions, Major Gift Solicitation, and more

-The funds raised provided support for our fallen military and their families; helped feed the hungry in our community, and provided struggling families partial scholarships for education and books
-Raised Operational Funding for Edmund Rice School/Barry University
Transferrable: Develop appropriate cultivation strategies, including working with volunteers
- Move potential donors in an appropriate and timely fashion toward solicitation and closure
- Make solicitations when appropriate
- Maintain stewardship contacts with donors; adhere to the highest ethical standards; demonstrate empathetic disposition, and perseverance; reflect optimistic and positive attitude, and convey sensitivity to needs of the donor

Name: Médecins Sans Frontières Logistique (MSF Logistique)
Description:
MSF Logistique, association de loi type 1901 (but non lucratif), est l’une des deux centrales d'approvisionnement du mouvement Médecins Sans Frontières (MSF).

MSF Logistique assure l'approvisionnement des programmes MSF, en médicaments et matériel médical et non médical, pour des programmes courants et des interventions d'urgence (populations déplacées, épidémies, catastrophes naturelles, conflits, etc.) depuis l’achat jusqu’au transport dans le pays de destination.

MSF Logistique intègre l'ensemble des métiers de la chaine d'approvisionnement, et propose également des services liés à son activité. MSF Logistique est agréé établissement pharmaceutique, entrepôt sous douane et est reconnu par l’Union Européenne comme centrale d’achat humanitaire. 

La Centrale a également des activités de prestations connexes comme l’appui des équipes de terrain MSF (domaines techniques & opérationnels) ; l’adaptation des produits existant sur le marché aux spécificités des terrains d’intervention MSF, tel que les véhicules. La centrale s’occupe également d’identifier, contractualiser et gérer des moyens aériens utilisés sur les terrains d’intervention MSF via la Cellule Avion qui travaille pour les 5 sections opérationnelles.

Chiffres clés 2016 :
- 22 M€ valeur stock
- 114 M€ chiffre d'activité
- 165 salariés
- 177 véhicules préparés par an
- 82 000m² superficie site
- 20 000m² capacité de stockage
- 8 420 kits fabriqués par an
- 15 107 références produits
- 20 000 passagers de la "cellule avion" 
- 5 142 tonnes expédiées dont 80% de médicaments et matériel médical

Name: Gates Family Foundation
Description: The Gates Family Foundation is committed to advancing long-term quality of life for all Coloradans, through support for educational equity, vibrant and sustainable communities, and stewardship of our state’s extraordinary natural resources. Gates is one of Colorado’s oldest private foundations, having invested more than $384 million in philanthropic activities since 1946.

21/64 is an independent nonprofit practice providing multigenerational advising, facilitation and training for next generation engagement, especially within family philanthropy and other family enterprises. We specialize in serving families with funds, foundations or other family enterprises as well as wealth and philanthropy advisors—so that multiple generations can work, give, and serve together more effectively.

Name: DCU Educational Trust
Description:
DCU Educational Trust is a registered charity (Charity Regulator No. 20022419 / CHY 8960) established in 1988 to advance the development of Dublin City University.

At DCU Educational Trust, we are passionate about the power of higher education to transform lives and societies, and we believe great things are possible when philanthropy combines with a young, dynamic and ambitious university like DCU. Through our fundraising work, we build meaningful partnerships with DCU's supporters to further DCU's mission to transform lives and societies through education, research, innovation and engagement.

Name: FEDORA Platform
Description:
FEDORA - The European Circle of Philanthropists of Opera and Ballet is a non-profit association committed to supporting and contributing to the future of opera and dance in Europe. The association was founded in 2013 in Paris, under the presidency of Jérôme-François Zieseniss, as a tribute to Rolf Liebermann. FEDORA’s main objective is to encourage emerging artists to renew these genres, thus making opera and dance more accessible, while fostering the emergence of new business models. In order to achieve this goal, FEDORA raises funds to stimulate innovation in the sector not only on, but also behind and beyond the stage, fostering its sustainable, inclusive and digital transformation. FEDORA aims to drive change through two funding programmes for opera houses, dance companies and festivals: The FEDORA Prizes Biennale and the Next Stage Initiative.

Since 2017, the FEDORA Platform has been selected as a beneficiary of the Creative Europe programme of the European Union, representing an accumulated support of more than 4 million euros. Thanks to this funding, FEDORA is strengthening its impact on the opera and dance sector in Europe. The interactive platform - www.fedora-platform.com - acts as a European hub: a European quality label, promoting innovative opera and dance co-productions created by emerging artists from various disciplines. This showcase of European creation encourages the engagement of audiences by enabling them to participate in the funding and promotion of projects through crowdfunding campaigns and online voting.

Name: Charity Foundation La Terra Nostra
Description:
Charity Foundation La Terra Nostra is a project that we decided to start in order to start giving back.

We hope as the time progresses that La Terra Nostra will help those young people from Bosnia and Herzegovina who are eager to study and to accomplish more in their lives in order to fulfill their dreams. 

Our dream is to help them on their journey and to not let them forget who they are and where they are coming from and hopefully with knowledge we will fight all the battles the future might bring.

On our path we also hope to encourage other people regardless of their origin to join us in our efforts to create a brighter future for Bosnia and Herzegovina.

Name: The Winston-Salem Foundation
Description: We are a nonprofit community foundation, comprised of over 1,600 funds, that assists people and organizations with their charitable giving, provides financial aid to college students, and makes grants to nonprofit organizations in Forsyth County. We are the oldest foundation in North Carolina, and we are one of the oldest community foundations in the country.

Name: Tan Chin Tuan Foundation
Description:
The Tan Chin Tuan Foundation (TCTF) was established on 10 April 1976 by the late banker and philanthropist Tan Sri (Dr) Tan Chin Tuan (1908 – 2005) to share with society the rewards of a highly illustrious career. He rendered assistance to the deserving and provided services that helped uplift the less fortunate. Today, the contributions of the foundation reflect the founder’s philosophy of how money should be channelled for social good. The foundation strives to support causes and projects that are viable, sustainable and well-managed with definable social outcomes.

Vision
To lead in the way philanthropy is practised by inspiring others through our words and deeds.

Mission
We strive to achieve optimal social outcomes that are measurable and sustainable, particularly in education and community development. We encourage collaboration amongst deserving groups, to achieve a multiplier and lasting benefit.

Name: OptionB.Org
Description: OptionB.Org is a nonprofit initiative to help people build resilience and find meaning in the face of adversity.

Name: Lubetzky Family Foundation
Croix-Rouge Malagasy
Description: Humanitarian Organization: to prevent and to alleviate human suffering,to reduce vulnerability in Madagascar

Name: The Haberdashers' Company
Description:
The Haberdashers' Company is one of the Great Twelve Livery Companies, incorporated in 1448. Closely tied with The City of London it is an organisation rich in history, yet relevant today. 

The Company's philanthropic work is focused around education and learning: empowering young people from every background to fulfil their potential.

Name: Ray C. Anderson Foundation
Description:
The Ray C. Anderson Foundation was created in honor of the late Ray C. Anderson (1934-2011), founder of Interface, Inc. During his time at Interface, Ray championed the notion of businesses doing well by doing good. It’s these noble qualities of advancing knowledge and innovation around environmental stewardship and sustainability that recognized Ray as a pioneer in industrial ecology.

The purpose of the Foundation is to perpetuate these shared values and continue the legacy that Ray left behind. Through research and funding, the Foundation aims to help create a better world for future generations—tomorrow’s child.

We seek to:

Fund innovative ideas and projects that promote visionary change in society.

Educate the public and business leaders alike in meaningful ways that propel revolutionary, sustainable change.

Inspire a new generation of leaders and consumers to be good stewards of the planet's resources, igniting action that radically impacts the way we live, work and play.

Connect thinkers, builders, innovators and idealists to a shared, ethical responsibility to the environment.

Name: The Gill Foundation
Description:
The Gill Foundation is one of the nation’s largest funders of lesbian, gay, bisexual, and transgender equal rights work.  The goal of our work is to create an America in which all people are treated equally and respectfully. For more information, please visit www.gillfoundation.org.

Over the course of our history, we have invested more than $357 million to support programs and nonprofit organizations across the country working to advance equal rights for LGBT people.

Name: Alfond Scholarship Foundation
Description: The Alfond Scholarship Foundation (ASF) is the non-profit organization founded by Mr. Harold Alfond that invests these funds until the child is ready to use them.  The funds can be used for education after high school – college or university, certificate programs and trade schools. And the funds can be used in Maine or out of state. Children awarded the Alfond Grant have until age 28 to use it.

Name: Adams Street Foundation
Description:
The Adams Street Foundation funds and implements high-quality, individualized programming and counseling for low-income, underserved high school students, to prepare them for success in college and career.

As the in-house, nonprofit partner of the Urban Assembly School for Law & Justice, ASF serves its students - the majority of whom live near or below the poverty line and will be the first in their family to go to college - by integrating pre-college enrichment, and college and career preparation into the school’s curriculum.

Name: The Counselling Foundation of Canada
Description: The Counselling Foundation of Canada champions life-long learning and career development that empowers people in Canada to develop their skills and talents.

Name: Cultural Philanthropy
Description:
Vision: Crediamo in una societa' adulta che nell'esercizio della liberta' con responsabilita' non ha piu' bisogno dello stato. Tutti hanno il dovere di tentare di farcela da soli con la coscienza che non esiste alcun risultato raggiungibile da soli che sia superiore al risultato che si puo' raggiungere con la propria comunita', il proprio pubblico e in generare con tutti coloro che ci amano. Quanto accettiamo di avere delle responsabilita' collettive, possiamo ottenere tutto, perche' questo ci rende meritevoli di fiducia. 

Mission: Noi, Cultural Philanthropy Ltd, proponiamo al mondo culturale e artistico una rivoluzione: la fine della dipendenza dai fondi pubblici. Sulla base di studi accademici ed esperienze vincenti, proponiamo un percorso per farvi raggiungere la piena liberta' finanziaria, gestionale e strategica solo secondo scienza e coscienza. 
Per fare questo, vi proponiamo una scelta di campo consapevole finalizzata a rendere irrilevanti i fondi pubblici per l’esistenza del vostro ente. A tutti coloro che si sentono imprigionati dai vincoli di bilancio dello Stato, offriamo la soluzione per non dover penare mai piu'.

Name: Le Camion Douche
Description:
Le Camion Douche est une association à but non lucratif, loi 1901 qui a pour but de lutter contre la pauvreté et l'exclusion sociale.
L’association a pour objectif de venir en aide et d'accompagner au plan individuel les personnes en situation de pauvreté, d'isolement et d'exclusion, notamment les sans-abri et les mal-logés de Toulouse.
L’association aide à l'insertion ou la réinsertion sociale et professionnelle des personnes en grande difficulté en leur donnant accès à un dispositif d’hygiène mobile.
A travers ces dispositifs, nous voulons permettre aux plus démunis d’accéder gratuitement à l’hygiène corporelle : douche, produits d'hygiène, vêtements.

Des services d’hygiène mobiles qui offrent aux  sans–abris et aux mal–logés de Toulouse et sa périphérie :
- Un accueil et un échange sur les besoins au niveau de l’hygiène.
- L’accès à l’hygiène corporelle, douche kits d’hygiène à disposition.
- Des vêtements adaptés selon les saisons.

Le Camion Douche fonctionne de mars à décembre à Toulouse :
- Lundi matin de 10 h à 12 h, Secours Populaire Français (147 Av. des Etats – Unis à Toulouse)
- Mercredi soir de 19 h à 21 h, place Héraclès à Toulouse
- Dimanche matin de 9 h 00 à 12 h 00, allée Jules Guesde, devant le quai des savoirs, à Toulouse.

Name: AJL Foundation
Description: AJL's mission is to invest in people, programs and movements that benefit Colorado's youth and families.

Name: Petrucci Family Foundation
Description: Founded in 2006, The Petrucci Family Foundation (PFF) actively responds to the needs of the communities it serves. The PFF mission is to support education and create opportunity for Americans at every stage of and station of life.

Name: Hockey Without Borders
Description:
Hockey Without Borders is a registered non-profit organization which connects hockey coaches to partner organizations in non-traditional hockey markets worldwide with the goal of affecting positive change in the lives of participant youth.

By living abroad in these non-traditional hockey communities, our coaches experience intercultural learning which develops knowledge, leadership, communication techniques, and life skills that are integral to becoming better a coach and an engaged citizen.


Hockey Sans Frontières est un organisme sans but lucratif qui a pour mission de mettre en relation les entraîneurs de hockey recrutés par HSF à nos clubs partenaires. Notre initiative a pour but d’influencer positivement la vie des jeunes hockeyeurs partout dans le monde en se concentrant principalement sur les organisations communautaires se trouvant dans un marché de hockey non-traditionnel.

L’aventure de coacher à l’étranger parmi ces communautés permet à nos entraîneurs de vivre une expérience unique d’intégration où l’apprentissage de nouvelles techniques de communication, de leadership et l’assimilation de connaissances générales aideront à développer des compétences essentielles dans le but de devenir un meilleur entraîneur, mais surtout, un citoyen engagé.

Name: Collective 365
Description:
Collective 365 is a group of individuals coming together to use their talent, time, resources, and passion to invest in their community. The voting members of the Collective pay a yearly investment of $100 (or $50). The members’ investments are used to support the community grants. Each full-paying member has a vote on how the grant money is awarded. Those who are unable to make a financial investment are encouraged to join as general members.

Your desire to be involved is needed and appreciated. If you are unable to commit financially at this time, we still invite you to become a general member. As a general member you volunteer your time and talents through our "capacity building" component of the grant application.


Collective 365 will be offering "capacity building" in addition to grant funding for applicants.  Capacity building is the Collective's way of connecting members to the work in the community.  For example, you may be a social media marketing guru and would be willing to provide "x" amount of hours to develop social media evergreen graphics to a grant applicant.

Name: Frontline Women’s Fund
Description: Frontline Women's Fund (formerly Donor Direct Action Fund for Women) supports frontline women's organizations from around the world by linking them with funding, media visibility and political power. We connect individual donors to credible frontline women's organizations from around the world. Our partners are leaders in their communities with a long history and proven track-record of safeguarding the rights of women and girls.

Name: Innovaid Advisory Services
Description:
Innovaid is an international group of consultants who advise Corporates & HNIs on their philanthropy and CSR in India. We merge international best practice with local knowledge to help our clients become leaders in Philanthropy and CSR in India - making their money go the extra mile in making an impact.

We offer a range of tailored services in CSR & Philanthropy, including:

- Strategy Development
- Assessment & Benchmarking of CSR & Philanthropy
- Foundation Establishment & Management
- Sustainability Reporting
- Cause Marketing Campaigns
- Program Identification & NGO Due Diligence
- Impact Assessment

Our clients include some of the biggest names in business, industry, entertainment and sport: Bajaj Auto, Shaadi.com, Mahindra Swaraj, Tata AIG, Birla Ultratech, Muthoot Pappachan Group, Dainik Bhaskar, Shah Rukh Kahn, Amit Patni, Jagdish Chanrai, The Prince of Udaipur and many more.

Together, we make a BIGGER difference!

Name: First Stop, Inc.
Description: First Stop encounters the homeless where they are, connects them to critical services and support, and empowers them toward independent sustainable living.

Name: Dance Marathon at Florida State University
Description: Dance Marathon at Florida State University, a Miracle Network Dance Marathon, is a year-long effort that culminates into an annual 40-hour event, where more than 1,800 students stay awake and on their feet to raise money and awareness for our local beneficiaries. Over the past 22 years, Dance Marathon at FSU has raised nearly $10 million for our local Children’s Miracle Network Hospital, Shands Children’s Hospital, and the FSU College of Medicine. In 2017, DM at FSU raised a grand total of $1,830,167.28 For The Kids. These funds make the difference in providing the medical care, treatment, and research needed at our local Children’s Miracle Network Hospital and the FSU College of Medicine!

Name: Social Venture Partners (SVP) Pittsburgh
Description: SVP Pittsburgh is a community of engaged philanthropists from the private sector (SVP Partners) who contribute their expertise, skills, networks and finances to grow the impact of nonprofits that tackle our community’s greatest challenges. SVP’s Full Circle Impact Accelerator is a new, unique model of engaged philanthropy that scales the impact of nonprofits tackling the region’s greatest social, economic and environmental challenges as well as cultivates the next generation of philanthropists committed to solving critical issues of inequality in Pittsburgh. SVP Full Circle is the only accelerator in the Pittsburgh region and one of very few accelerators in the United States exclusively dedicated to scaling nonprofit impact.

Name: Fundashon Alton Paas
Description:
Fundashon Alton Paas’ is a non-profit organization founded on 7th of September 2012. This foundation was founded by Alton Paas, a 21 year old inspiring miracle with the help of some supporters.
 
Alton is a miracle because he was involved in a car accident due to a heart problem (Wolf Parkinson White Syndrome) on November 22, 2009. He had suffered a serious spinal cord injury. After the subsequent 
medical procedures and the rehabilitation programs, he remains dependent on a wheelchair, unable to walk. Despite the limited after-care opportunities locally, Alton is committed to the efforts to seek further improvement of his condition. After his injury Alton has discovered that he is on this earth to help, inspire and motivate his fellowman. 
On his path to recovery he made himself a promise to work for a better quality of life for himself and his fellowman with a SCI or with other physical disabilities. That’s why he founded ‘Fundashon Alton Paas’ (Alton Paas Foundation).
 
At this moment Curacao doesn’t have any law to help or protect the disable community. Also, there is no center on the island where persons suffering from a spinal cord injury or other neurological problems in a wheelchair can go to receive special intense exercise based training. A normal person has to exercise to be in condition. For a person with a neurological disorder or with a spinal cord injury or in a wheelchair, it is a must to do exercises to maintain a long healthy life. With a disabling condition on-going therapeutic exercise should become a way of life. Exercise is their medicine.

Name: Minds Matter Philadelphia
Description:
Minds Matter transforms the lives of accomplished high school students from low-income families by broadening their dreams and preparing them for college success. 

Our students are hand-selected by guidance counselors from more than a dozen local high schools and go through a rigorous application process. Once admitted, students are paired with two dedicated mentors. Our mentors are Philadelphia professionals who give their time while pursuing full-time careers or graduate degrees. 

The organization’s services have developed into a comprehensive three year curriculum for high school sophomores, juniors and seniors. Each year students are guided through valuable lessons in Academic Preparedness, Writing & Critical Thinking and Test Preparation.

Today, with over 70 Philadelphia volunteers, each contributing over 50 hours of service every year, Minds Matter is one of the largest volunteer run organizations in the city dedicated to promoting higher education.

Name: The Cova Project
Description: The Cova Project aims to provide benevolent relief to girls and women of reproductive age across the world who are experiencing poverty, suffering, distress and disadvantage due to lack of financial resources and access to basic necessities. We do this by donating menstrual cups, to allow girls to participate while menstruation. Girls can miss up to a quarter of their school year because of a lack of access to safe sanitary care. It's time we change that.

Name: Life Rolls On Foundation
Description: Life Rolls On is a non-profit 501(c)3 that exists to be a grassroots resource that provides hope and is an advocate on behalf of young people whose lives have been affected by spinal cord injury (SCI). LRO utilizes action sports to push the boundary of possibility for those with SCI.

Name: Maine Justice Foundation
Description:
Access to our system of justice is a basic human right, one that should be extended to all Maine people. The Maine Justice Foundation funds programs around the state that make sure that poor and vulnerable Maine residents have a lawyer when faced with civil legal issues like divorce, eviction, protection from abuse and immigration status.

In 1983, leaders of the Maine State Bar Association and the University of Maine School of Law created the Maine Bar Foundation, which is today’s Maine Justice Foundation. They recognized that low-income Mainers involved in civil matters like disputes with landlords or serious child custody issues could not afford legal representation. The complexity of legal proceedings had tipped the scale in favor of those who could afford to hire a lawyer.In 2015, we became the Maine Justice Foundation. Our goals remain the same: we are committed to helping those for whom the courthouse doors are closed. 

We make grants to civil legal aid programs throughout Maine and the more than 19,000 clients they serve each year. These include, but are not limited to, the following: The University of Maine School of Law Cumberland Legal Aid Clinic; Immigrant Legal Advocacy Project; Legal Services for the Elderly; Maine Equal Justice Partners; Pine Tree Legal Assistance; and Volunteer Lawyers Project. The Foundation supports other law-related program initiatives, education and services.

Name: Dawood Global Foundation
Description:
The Dawood Global Foundation was launched in 2010 with three obectives - to help promote and impact Healthcare, Education and Sustainable Development through Social Enterprises across Pakistan.

OUR VISION

To build an educated, healthy and prosperous society with dignity and honor for all people.

OUR MISSION STATEMENT

Promote quality education, healthcare and social enterprise with a special focus on women, minorities, youth and the disabled through establishing and supporting institutions, including focus on all types of disaster relief, that ensure participation of vulnerable sections of society in the economy.

Name: Association MALAK D'ALGER جمعية ملاك لولاية الجزائر MALAK NGO | Ex. Cité des Anges d'Alger - C.A.A.
Description:
The MALAK NGO or جمعية ملاك لولاية الجزائر, ex. "C.A.A. - Cité des Anges d'Alger" is a Local non-profit and humanitarian NGO, leading charitable activities, solidarity and assistance to the benefit of children and their mothers through Expression Workshops and Artistic and Ludic Mediation in hospitals and specialized centers of Algiers. It tends to spread a "culture of compassion and comfort" by putting human at the center of concern, acting for the service of the most vulnerable people in society, we mean children and mothers. 

Our association introduces Artistic and Playful Expression and Mediation Workshops in hospital settings as well as in specific environments such as specialized centers, care facilities, and orphanages. These workshops aim to harness the artistic expression potential of hospitalized children for therapeutic purposes, as well as for their personal development and well-being. 

E-mail : contact-malak@citedesanges.org

Name: Benevolent
Description:
The mission of Benevolent is to bring dignity and self-determination to both sides of the giving equation, illuminating the realities of life on the edge of sustainability.

Benevolent makes it possible for everyday donors to give to help one low-income individual over one hurdle at one moment in time. We work exclusively in partnership with local nonprofits and public agencies so that every person's story presented on the Benevolent platform is validated and managed professionally and locally.

Donors contribute to help with things like uniforms for someone to accept a job, laptops for school and security deposits and furniture for people exiting homelessness or domestic violence. At Benevolent, we know that the opportunities and challenges that pop up in the lives of striving low-income families are as unique as the families themselves. We believe in helping people follow the paths and reach the goals they’ve set for themselves.

Name: Change for Children
Description:
Change for Children's mission is to build civil society capacity in the global south and Canada to promote
health, human rights and create solutions to poverty through sustainable development.

Change for Children partners with carefully chosen grassroots partners in Latin America and Africa to identify the root causes of poverty and, in the spirit of solidarity,  assist in finding long-term solutions

Our international projects focus on improving access to clean water, education, food security, healthcare, and economic development opportunities. 

Locally, Change for Children offers unique global education and community leadership opportunities.

Name: Fundação Amor Horizontal
Description:
Somos a ponte entre você e quem mais precisa!

A Fundação Amor Horizontal é uma Instituição Filantrópica que há 8 anos tem como objetivo erradicar a pobreza infantil apoiando a criança em todo o seu desenvolvimento e fomentando a solidariedade no Brasil. Atualmente impactamos 59 mil crianças em 149 projetos sociais certificados, por meio de doações de produtos de necessidades vitais.

Nossa missão é estimular doações da sociedade como forma de valorizar o amor ao próximo, contribuindo para um país melhor. Para isso, contamos com as nossas Parcerias do Bem, nos aliando a empresas para maximizar nosso impacto. A FAH facilita, planeja, executa e presta contas de todas as atividades da parceria.

Name: Women Golfers Give Back
Description:
Women Golfers Give Back supports golf programs for girls in the greater Philadelphia region that encourage personal development through unique opportunities for skill building, playing and competing. Exposure to golf and the character-building aspects of the game will help girls develop leadership and communication skills and lead to greater educational and career opportunities- all while exercising and having fun!

WGGB was founded in 2003 by a group of women golfers who wanted to “give back” to the community and promote the game of golf for girls who would not otherwise have the chance to play. Our own love of the game is a common bond that encourages us to share our passion. Since our inception, we have raised more than $900,000 for our thirteen girls’ golf programs promoting girls’ golf. With confidence in our ever-growing support base, we hope to fund even more girls’ golf programs in 2018 and beyond.

Name: Krembil Foundation
Description: The Krembil Foundation is a small family led foundation focused on supporting medical research in Canada. The foundation began in 2001 with the initial focus on neuroscience. Our current interests have expanded since then to include focused research pillars in the areas of neurodegeneration, the immune system, and arthritis. We’ve established a nursing development program and consolidated our non-medical interests into our director initiated portfolio. The Krembil Foundation is a charitable foundation with the goal to accelerate research and impact lives through scientific discoveries leading to advancements in medicine.

Name: Lilly Endowment
Description: Lilly Endowment is a private foundation that is committed to the causes of community development, education, and religion. It is based in Indianapolis, United States.

Name: Freemasons of Arizona
Description: The mission of Freemasonry is to promote a way of life that binds like minded men in a worldwide brotherhood that transcends all religious, ethnic, cultural, social and educational differences; by teaching the great principles of Brotherly Love, Relief, and Truth: and, by the outward expression of these, through its fellowship, its compassion and its concern, to find ways in which to serve God, family, country, neighbors and self.

Name: KCYP | Kootenai County Young Professionals
Description:
The Kootenai County Young Professionals (KCYP) is a group started in 2007 with the goal of connecting professionals (of all ages) with each other and within the community through education and leadership opportunities, philanthropy and networking and social events.  KCYP strives to attract and retain professionals by providing meaningful connections both professionally and personally along with opportunities to be involved in the community.

KCYP hosts a variety of events monthly, ranging from social events to networking and educational events like our monthly Tavern Talk speaker series to volunteer opportunities.

Community members of all ages are welcome to become involved in KCYP membership. Experience, knowledge and backgrounds of all varieties are what continue to help grow our members to become active and developed community leaders.

Join Today!

Name: Children's Music Fund
Description: Children's Music Fund helps children affected by chronic health conditions or life-altering illnesses on their journey towards a better life by providing fully funded Music Therapy sessions. We also conduct research on the healing benefits of Music Therapy to manage pain, fear and anxiety associated with hospitalization and medical treatments a child must undergo related to a diagnosis.

Name: Community Foundation of West Tennessee
Description: The Community Foundation of West Tennessee was established in 1985 to strengthen the health and well-being of our region by serving as a catalyst for positive changes in the arts, education, healthcare, and other causes. We enhance the quality of life for local residents by supporting programs, initiatives, and organizations serving rural West Tennessee.

Name: MAGFest, Inc
Description: A 501(c)(3) non-profit organization dedicated to furthering the appreciation of video games, video game culture, and video game music.

Name: Applebaum Family Philanthropy
Description: The work of an inspirational leader, Eugene Applebaum, forms the basis of our philanthropy: to provide unique opportunities for the next generation.

Name: CharityVision
Description:
CharityVision International is a non-profit organization focused on treating people with curable sight impairments in developing nations. We restore lives through sight!

We are a 501(c)(3) nonprofit tax exempt organization recognized by the IRS, and all donations to CharityVision International are tax-deductible in accordance with US tax regulations.

We empower highly-trained and highly-motivated physicians throughout the world, mostly in developing countries. We provide them with whatever it takes to help those without sight in their communities.

Name: Connect Fund
Description: The Connect Fund was set up as a partnership between Barrow Cadbury Trust & Access - The Foundation for Social Investment to strengthen the social investment market in England to better meet the needs of charities and social enterprises.

Name: Fresh Artists
Description:
Fresh Artists is a nonprofit organization dedicated to empowering young lives through the transformative power of art and philanthropy.

Mission:	
Creating the ability for generous children to make a real difference in their world, Fresh Artists is an innovative nonprofit that engages children as full partners in philanthropy, widely exhibits children's artwork in highly-visible places, and provides art supplies and innovative programs to severely under-resourced public schools.

Program:
Fresh Artists identifies and invites select student artists to donate the use of high-resolution images of their stunning artwork.

Then, Fresh Artists accepts corporate and individual donations and uses those funds to purchase and deliver much needed, innovative programs and art supplies directly to art teachers in public schools.

As a thank-you gift from the children, donors may choose high-resolution digital reproductions of student art to display in their facilities.

Name: Care-for-Rare Foundation
Description: Die Care-for-Rare Foundation ist eine rechtsfähige Stiftung für Kinder mit seltenen Erkrankungen

Name: Karpatská nadácia
Description:
Už 28 rokov poskytujeme finančnú podporu, vzdelávanie a poradenstvo aktívnym ľuďom a organizáciám. Vedieme ich k zodpovednosti za seba, komunitu, región aza jeho budúcnosť. 

Prečo? 

Pretože chceme vytvoriť krajšie a lepšie východné Slovensko, a to dokážeme len, keď budeme spolupracovať.  

Zároveň realizujeme vlastné projekty a výskumné aktivity, odborné podujatia, zdieľame príklady dobrej praxe a rozvíjame koncept darcovstva. Zviditeľňujeme význam a prínos mimovládneho neziskového sektora i dobrovoľníctva pre celú spoločnosť.Snažíme sa búrať negatívne stereotypné názory a dezinformácie.   

⋙ Karpatská nadácia od svojho vzniku (1994) podporila vyše tisíc projektov sumou viac ako 4 miliónov eur.

Name: Núcleo de Programadores do Amanhã (NUPA)
Description:
NUPA provides underprivileged students with the opportunity and the resources to learn computer programming. We are supported by The John Ernest Foundation (TJEF), and we collaborate with partnering schools to host programming courses that are fun and engaging. Currently, our program focuses on junior high and high school students going to public schools in Rio de Janeiro. We identify promising students and pair them with mentors to guide long-term learning and provide advice to help students prepare for their future careers. 

Our long-term goal is to expand our network of partnering schools, mentors and donors. Please reach out if you would like to know more about our project.

Name: Rocking Ur Teens CIC
Description:
Rocking Ur Teens is a community interest company  developed to motivate and empower early teens by creating a platform to provide them with aspiration and inspiration in relation to future goals and career path.

Our mission is to support young people to create personal and social change. To understand their strength in numbers and how collective energy will impact their futures and generations to come. This is done in an informative and entertaining way.  

We formed in response to research which highlights lack of self-esteem, inspiration and aspiration as an epidemic among young people, and we aim to counteract this.  

Rocking Ur Teens is open to both girls and boys.  However, in Year 1 we have specifically targeted 13 – 14 year old girls in Years 8 and 9 of school. 

We have a five-year focus for teen conferences in the UK, Europe and the rest of the world

Do donate, £50 provides a free place which can change the direction of a girls life. Any amount is appreciated https://www.paypal.me/RockingUrTeensCIC

Name: WUMEA
Description:
Founded in 1954, the Wits University Mining Engineers Association (WUMEA) is a global network of Wits Mining Engineering alumni and associates dedicated to advancing the mining profession and contributing to sustainable mineral and economic development. As a non-profit organization, WUMEA fosters collaboration, mentorship, and lifelong connections among its members while supporting student development at Wits University.

Our activities are made possible through annual memberships, sponsorships, and donations from our valued community and partners.

Name: The Community Foundation of South Alabama
Description:
The Community Foundation of South Alabama (CFSA) was founded in 1976 by a core group of civic and professional leaders to serve as a vehicle to promote charitable giving and build a permanent philanthropic resource to address the changing needs in Mobile and the surrounding areas. For more than 45 years, we have been southwest Alabama’s trusted partner to help create philanthropic funds that contribute to grant and scholarship opportunities, improving our community for years to come.

The Foundation assembles and directs philanthropic assets to make southwest Alabama a better place. We are committed to acting with integrity with all of our partners, and to be equitable, inclusive and transparent in all we do.  We are passionate about developing long-term, sustainable relationships based on mutual respect and trust in order to create measurable impact in our communities.

Name: Mon Bonheur à Moi
Description:
Pour que les enfants fragilisés* ou atteints d'une maladie réenchantent leur quotidien grâce à des outils sources de bien-être, de relaxation, de divertissement utilisables sans l'aide d'un adulte. 

Force est de constater que la confiance en soi est primordiale pour l’épanouissement d’un enfant, afin qu’il soit à l’aise avec les autres et qu’il affronte plus facilement certaines situations de la vie.
C’est encore plus vrai pour un enfant fragilisé dont l’estime de soi est bouleversée.
Il peut se sentir seul, et développer un sentiment de peur du moment présent mais aussi de l’avenir…

LES MISSIONS
DONNER AUX ENFANTS LES MOYENS D’ÊTRE BIEN AVEC EUX-MÊME
• Mise en œuvre des projets destinés à aider les enfants à prendre confiance en eux, afin d’appréhender leur situation de manière plus sereine. 
• Leur donner les clés pour adoucir leur quotidien et les rendre acteurs de leur développement.

ROMPRE LA SOLITUDE DES ENFANTS FRAGILISÉS
• Créer une bulle de bonheur en finançant des outils (des supports) leur permettant d’occuper les moments de solitude sans l’aide ou l’intervention d’une tierce personne.

COMMUNIQUER AVEC L’ENTOURAGE
• Mise à disposition d’outils pédagogiques permettant d’accompagner, et d’aider les enfants à mieux vivre leur quotidien mais aussi à croire en leurs ressources.

* Les actions sont menées en lien avec un comité scientifique constitué de médecins et de spécialistes de la santé, du bien être et de l’enfance.

Name: Motivation For Excellence (MFE)
Description:
The Motivation for Excellence Initiative (MFE) is the brainchild of two charitable foundations, the RG Manudhane Foundation for Excellence based in Mumbai, India and the Indira Foundation based in Greenwich, CT, USA.
 
We target specific subsets of 3 broad areas: Education, Health-Care, and Self-Help (especially for women). While some programs are initiated and run by MFE a large majority are run by partner organizations. In these cases we collaborate with dynamic partners, supporting them with resources in the form of grants, guidance, and networking. At MFE, we have a strong belief in value-based excellence and a passion for helping catalyze improvements in society. We focus on solutions that are innovative, yet pragmatic and effective. We support programs that share the following five characteristics:

1. Innovation
2. Effectiveness
3. Validation
4. Excellence
5. Values

We prefer long-term relationships to improve the odds of success and encourage eventual self-sufficiency for each program. Email us at info@motivationforexcellence.org.

Name: Make-A-Wish Foundation of Alaska and Washington
Description:
Since 1986, Make-A-Wish Alaska and Washington has granted wishes to more than 5,300 children with life-threatening medical conditions to enrich the human experience with hope, strength and joy. This year, the local chapter will grant about 300 wishes. 

According to a 2011 U.S. study of wish impact, most health professionals surveyed believe a wish-come-true can influence the health of children. Kids say wishes give them renewed strength to fight their illnesses, and their parents say these experiences help strengthen the entire family. 

For more information about Make-A-Wish Alaska and Washington and how you can share the power of a wish please call 800.304.9474, visit www.akwa.wish.org or connect with us at www.facebook.com/makeawishakwa or on Twitter @MakeAWishAKWA.

Name: We Raise Foundation
Description: We Raise Foundation identifies innovative ideas, people, and programs that raise consciousness, community leadership, and capital. Motivated by the belief that freedom is grace in action (Galatians 5:1a), We Raise provides grants and assistance with resource development to organizations that work at the intersection of poverty, violence, and inequality. We Raise supports Christian nonprofit organizations and emerging leaders and has a preference for funding solutions within the areas of education, workforce development, and criminal justice. To learn more, please visit weraise.org.

Name: Here to Serve, Inc.
Description:
Here to Serve identifies and mobilizes people, charities, businesses and organizations to carry each other’s burdens during times of crisis, insuring guidance and assistance does not have to be sought after, but is offered freely by people and organizations that collaborate to compassionately respond to those navigating cancer. We seek to demystify the plethora of service organizations and to make them easily accessible, thereby easing the cancer journey. 

Here to Serve provides assistance to families with children battling cancer. We:

Mobilize people and resources to meet the daily living requirements of families who do not qualify for government assistance.
Build online communities of support called Care Communities, that provide a network of services and financial assistance for families caring for their child or spouse battling cancer.
Provide hard-to-find resources at the start and throughout treatment as needs arise.

Here to Serve is a health and human services organization providing non-medical support services to patients and their caregivers who are navigating cancer.  We are a 501 (c) (3) charity that collaborates and cooperates with the community including individuals, businesses, and other nonprofit organizations to reduce the suffering and emotional stress of individuals, families and caregivers during cancer treatment. Here to Serve combines the services of other charities, corporations, and professional service organizations and mobilizes a network of friends, neighbors and loved ones to provide targeted non-medical assistance to families battling cancer. We identify resources to help with insurance and legal issues, financial, emotional and mental health support, and navigate other NPO services, as well as provide assistance with child care, meals, household chores, transportation, pet care, and general logistics.

Name: Streets For People
Description:
Streets For People was founded in Perth, Western Australia in 2018. It is an incorporated not-for-profit community organisation that advocates for the human-centric design of public space.

We assist communities and politicians in street design and policy through education on the benefits of healthy street design. We seek a seat at the table on discussions about streets and communities. We investigate creating guiding documents to assist stakeholders in decision making for street design and policies.

Name: American Red Cross Southern Arizona Chapter
Description:
Organized in 1916, the American Red Cross Southern Arizona Chapter serves more than 1.3 million residents in a five county area, including Cochise, Graham, Greenlee, Pima, and Santa Cruz counties. The chapter is headquartered in Tucson and offers a service center in Sierra Vista, Arizona.

Through the combined efforts of 500 volunteer staff and 20 paid staff, the Chapter provides services for disaster victims, health and safety instruction, and social services for members of the military and their families.

Name: Future Urban Leaders
Description:
Who is Future Urban Leaders?

Future Urban Leaders (FUL) provides afterschool and summer enrichment programming to over 150 students in the Milwaukee community (grades K4 through 12th). FUL programs break down barriers that typically disrupt equal access to activities and resources for historically marginalized communities of color.  Through exploration, community engagement, academic support, and leadership building activities, students learn comprehensive tools to create pathways to a successful future. Collaboration is key to the success of FUL's capacity to open doors of opportunity and strengthen our student's trust and drive to be leaders in their own communities and across settings that they're not typically engaged in. FUL's programming is structured around five core areas that all work together to provide a solid foundation for our scholars to learn, grown, and prosper. 

Core Programming Areas:
-Health & Wellness
-Arts & Humanities
-Academic Enrichment
-Service & Leadership
-College & Career Readiness

History:
FUL was founded through the passion and philanthropic efforts of two childhood friends: one a local business executive and the other a public-school teacher, as an athletic enrichment and mentorship program in 2015.  The first cohort of 13 students met twice per week for academic support and athletic enrichment. As the program grew, mentorship and academic support evolved into the core of all programming, along with community and health and wellness.  

Today the program meets twice per week, with other opportunities periodically mixed in on unscheduled programming days.  After school programming takes place at Milwaukee College Prep-Lloyd Street Campus for students in grades K4-8th grade. High school programming takes place at Greater Life Community Center, strengthening our connection to their greater Milwaukee community.

Name: Working Bikes
Description:
Countless bicycles are discarded or sit unused in basements or garages in the United States every year. But in a developing country, a bicycle can truly change the life of an individual, their family and their community. Bicycling is, of course, a form of transportation. But bicycles are also used for towing cargo, and even as a source of electricity through the creation of pedal-powered machines! In areas plagued by poverty, high levels of unemployment and lack of reliable, accessible transportation, a bicycle can help provide access to jobs, education medical attention, and other resources.

Global Impact:  Working Bikes ships thousands of bicycles each year to our partners in countries throughout Latin America and Africa including, El Salvador, Guatemala, Panama, Uganda, Ghana, and many others.

Local Impact:  Working Bikes partners with homeless transition, refugee resettlement, and youth empowerment programs here in Chicago to donate bicycles and put them to good use on the streets of our own community.  Hundreds of volunteers help out at Working Bikes, and in the process they learn bike mechanics and build community.  

Working Bikes donates over 6000 bicycles each year to our international and local partners.

Name: Young Leaders Fund (YLF)
Description: Young Leaders Fund (YLF) provides young professionals with an innovative way to make a difference. YLF empowers its donors to manage the grant making process. With support from the Trust, they research innovative grassroots organizations, conduct site visits, interview staff and evaluate grant requests. As a result, members become more effective volunteers and more knowledgeable civic leaders.

Name: TIZIAD Association
Description:
OPENNESS • DEVELOPMENT • COOPERATION
� Aimer le Maroc
� Goal = Développement continu
� Coopération pour atteindre la prospérité et un avenir meilleur

Name: Croix-Rouge française Val-de-Marne
Description:
La Croix-Rouge française, ce sont plus de 59 000 bénévoles et 18 000 salariés présents sur l’ensemble du territoire. Auxiliaire des pouvoirs publics dans ses missions humanitaires, la Croix-Rouge française mène un combat de tous les instants pour soulager la souffrance des hommes. 

+ La Croix-Rouge française, une double identité association - entreprise
Dans la tradition du Mouvement Croix-Rouge, la Croix-Rouge française est d’abord une association de bénévoles. Elle est aussi devenue une entreprise non profit de services dans les secteurs humanitaire, sanitaire, social, médicosocial et de la formation. Pour mener à bien ses missions, cette association-entreprise à but non lucratif, compte sur son réseau de bénévoles et de salariés, qui agissent chaque jour selon un même principe inaltérable de solidarité, en privilégiant les actions de proximité qui apportent des réponses concrètes et durables..

+ La Croix-Rouge française, 5 métiers fondamentaux : Action internationale, Action sociale, Formation, Santé, Urgence et secourisme

+ La Croix-Rouge française, présente partout en France
Au travers d’un réseau très dense de délégations et d’établissements, la Croix-Rouge française est présente sur l’ensemble du territoire français y compris dans les départements et territoires d'outre-mer (DOM-TOM). Ce maillage lui permet d’intervenir au plus vite auprès des personnes en difficulté quelque soit le point du territoire ou le type d’intervention requis..

+ La Croix-Rouge française, autonome et auxiliaire des pouvoirs publics
La Croix-Rouge française est une association indépendante qui agit aux côtés des pouvoirs publics. Association loi 1901, elle est reconnue d'utilité publique depuis 1945.
Sa mission d’auxiliaire des pouvoirs publics :
- Apporter son aide dans toutes les calamités publiques. 
-auprès des pouvoirs publics dans des missions de secours lors de situations d'exceptions nécessitant des moyens humains et logistiques importants. 
- Médiateur entre les personnes

Name: SPA de Québec
Description: Améliorer la qualité de vie des animaux domestiques en contribuant à leur santé, leur bien-être et leur sécurité et ce, tout en éduquant et en sensibilisant la population à l’importance de la vie animale.

Name: Generosity: Bay Area
Description: Inspiring a culture of transformational generosity in the Bay Area and beyond.

Name: Upkarma
Description:
UpKarma is dedicated to revolutionizing the philanthropy through a community-driven approach to volunteering and NGO support. Our mission is to create a more compassionate and connected world where technology and human passion work together to drive positive change.

Our Vision:
At UpKarma, we envision a world where every act of kindness and every effort to help others contributes to a larger tapestry of compassion and community. By harnessing the power of collective action, we aim to solve the challenges of distributive and collaborative philanthropy, ensuring that help reaches those who need it most efficiently and effectively.

Our Approach:
UpKarma is more than just a platform; it is a movement towards a more compassionate and
connected world. We believe in the transformative power of community and compassion, and we strive to create an ecosystem where these values drive positive change. By combining technology with a passion for making a difference, we empower individuals and organizations to work together towards common goals. 

Join us in our mission to make the world a better place, one collaborative effort at a time.
Together, we can build a brighter future where compassion and community are at the heart of everything we do.

Contact: upkarmaofficial@gmail.com

Name: Edinburgh University Women in Business
Description:
Network
EUWIB aims to provide a range of opportunities for its members to network with each other, with external speakers and company representatives. Successful networking is a vital skill for life after graduation and career development. EUWIB helps their members to acquire the skills to build a strong network by connecting people with similar interests and ambitions.

Inspire
EUWIB aims to inspire all students by inviting along female business leaders from a variety of backgrounds, including public sector, journalism, charities, law, financial services, communications and plenty others! Through these inspiring connections, we look to encourage students to reflect on their own aspirations, qualities and interests with the aim to discover the career path that suits them best.

Mentor
EUWIB is dedicated to the creation of a community wherein students can speak comfortably with one another about their career aspirations and concerns. Our long term goal is to create an alumni mentorship scheme, where former members could stay in contact with the society through events, and an online presence.

Name: The Butterfly Foundation
Description:
“The Butterfly Foundation is dedicated to supporting people who have suffered a severe loss, illness, or other hardship by empowering them emotionally, spiritually, physically, or financially. We focus on those that don’t have other resources or charities to turn to - by providing services to quickly stabilize their lives.”

OUR MISSION
The Butterfly Foundation is a non-profit organization that is dedicated to supporting people who have suffered a severe loss, illness, or other hardship by empowering them emotionally, spiritually, physically, or financially.”

OUR VALUES
We aim to empower people to succeed by treating those in need with dignity, integrity and compassion. We believe in helping people brought down by tragedy rebuild their lives through acts of generosity and caring.

OUR SOLUTION
We carefully screen those seeking help and award assistance to recipients who embrace our philosophy of paying it forward.  We accept nominations for support from caring family and friends. At The Butterfly Foundation we know a little help can go a long way.

Name: Operation Thermos Operatie A.S.B.L.
Description:
Au départ d’une organisation simple d’une poignée de jeunes scouts, s’est crée une ASBL de plusieurs centaines de bénévoles. Aujourd'hui, l’ASBL coordonne 30 équipes qui servent des repas tous les soirs de novembre à avril dans la gare de métro botanique.
-
Van een enkele organisatie van en paar boyscouts, heft een breed VZW van hondreds van Vrijwilligers geemergeed. Vandag, het VZW coördineert 30 teams die serveren maaltijden elke avond tussen november en april in de botanique metro station.
-
From a simple organisaion of a few boyscouts, emergeed a large NPO of hondreds of volunteers. Today, the NPO coordinates 30 teams serve meals every night from november to april in the botanique metro station..

Name: earth fund
Description:
sustain credits is a grassroots reward system (executed as a centralised digital currency) with sustain credits tokens issued to earth doctors that heal the world. Anyone can become an earth doctor: plant trees to capture carbon, clear out environmentally destructive novel chemicals (e.g. plastic) from nature, regenerative agriculture or anything that returns our planet to its true form. Our environmentalists review the proof of work and issue sustain credits in line with ecological science. With our economic system, we hope to provide a living wage to full time earth doctors in the most polluted parts of the world.

How do we fund these sustain credits? We engage in commercial activity to back sustain credits. We are not limited by our commercial activity, we use all paths to revenue, including fully measurable turnkey ESG solutions, philanthropy, grants, awards, our ecommerce platform for truly sustainable products (planezon.com), sale or sponsorship via transfer of digital ownership for work done (trees, nature clean-ups), etc.

80% of the net income derived from sustain credits commercial activity go to the earth fund, the internal cash reserve which back the sustain credits we issue. we use net income instead of profit: this is transparent with additional funds available for impact compared to similar initiatives that use profit - a figure that represent a small fraction of total income. these funds go straight the point of need: to the earth doctors when they redeem the sustain credits in their account.

Name: Helping Hands Program
Description:
The Helping Hands Program is a truly unique opportunity for organisations to get involved with a worthwhile cause at the same time as super-charging employee engagement. More than just a team building or training activity, participants in our programs build prosthetic hands that are then donated to amputee landmine victims throughout the developing world. The activity empowers every participant in just a few hours to make a real and lasting contribution and in doing so reminds people what it feels like to be engaged in a truly purposeful activity.

Check out our promotional video to learn more: https://youtu.be/kledpv_oX_E

Name: Global Listening Centre
Description: The Global Listening Centre (GLC) is a dedicated premier international non-profit organization, which promotes the noble cause of listening worldwide and encourages individuals, organizations and institutions to work together to meet the urgent challenges of society. The GLC is organized according to its constitution and led by the Global Listening Board, which is comprised of experienced and passionate advocates who are leaders and experts in their respective fields.It instigates projects amongst motivated teams in support of its listening goals. The Global Listening Centre welcomes collaboration and partnership with organizations having a convergence of interest in studying, researching and promoting the practice of listening as well as advancing innovative technology for enhancing effective listening.

Name: St. Louis Aquarium Foundation
Description: As the nonprofit partner to the St. Louis Aquarium at Union Station, the Aquarium Foundation engages individuals through access, conservation and education programs, building powerful communities for water stewardship.

Name: Association IMAG'IN
Description:
Association humanitaire française qui oeuvre pour un meilleur accès à l'éducation. Créée en 2007 par un groupe d'amis, IMAG'IN agit principalement en (re)construisant des écoles à Madagascar. Elle propose des tournées de spectacles de marionnettes pour les enfants défavorisés  et présente des expositions photos. 
Aidez-nous en rejoignant-nos bénévoles ou en mettant à notre disposition vos compétences et réseaux !

Name: Sourire à l'innocence
Description:
Sourire à l'innocence est un club associatif créé par les étudiants de l'ESI et qui vise à rendre le sourire aux enfants, malades, pauvres, orphelins...

Nous sommes une association humanitaire à but caritatif, statuée à l'école nationale supérieure d'Informatique d'Alger (ex INI) en tant que club estudiantin qui y est proprement rattaché, notre but principal est de prôner les valeurs humanitaires et humanistes, en organisant des événements de charité ou des collectes, là où les dons et les recettes sont récoltés pour assouvir les besoins des plus nécessiteux.

C'est parce qu'ils sont en majorité des enfants dont le sourire a été ôté par les conditions particulières auxquelles ils ont pu faire face, et c'est parce que la vie ne leur a pas toujours souri, que nous essayons de de leur offrir un minimum de réconfort, de compassion et surtout d'accompagnement, et ce afin qu'ils puissent regagner le sourire et croire en leur avenir !

Notre principale mission est d’apporter une aide à tous les centres d’accueil, orphelinats, ou hôpitaux dans lesquels se trouvent des enfants innocents et dans le besoin, Sachez que notre aide ne représente pas grand-chose sur le plan financier mais que nos actions véhiculent un message beaucoup plus important. Nous souhaitons que ces êtres gardent à l’esprit que même si leur vie ne fut pas facile, même si certains les ont abandonnés ils ne sont et ne seront pas seuls…

Parmi nos événements organisés :
- La collecte des vêtements d'hiver : Il s'agit d'un appel aux âmes charitables de pouvoir contribuer avec des dons de vêtements en tout genre.
- Cooking Day : Des recettes de cuisine aux recettes budgétaires à aider un nécessiteux.
- Breaking Science : Des expériences en tout genre des différents clubs et organisations réunies dans un espace d'exposition.
- Visites aux orphelinats, hôpitaux et pouponnières.
- ART Day : Réunion des œuvres artistiques faîtes par des personnes talentueuses.
- Différentes journées de sensibilisation.

Name: Gobioff Foundation
Description: Located in the Tampa Bay area, the Gobioff Foundation is a private family foundation which works to support human rights organizations and the Tampa, FL arts community. Our grants strengthen the ability of human rights and arts organizations to enrich the Tampa community and improve the state of human rights worldwide so that the world is a better place as intended by our founder. Our mission is not static. It is constantly developing as we grow and our family grows.

Name: Rescue Her
Description:
Rescue Her exists to empower survivors of sex trafficking to live in freedom. We do this through crisis response, restoration, and raising awareness.

We respond to crisis calls received through our hotline, and assist survivors with safe shelter, emergency medical assistance, legal rights, emotional support, transportation, basic needs, relocation, and more. We also assist with recoveries through collaboration with law enforcement. 

We offer restorative services through our long-term Empower Program where we focus on empowering survivors in five core areas: legal, independent living, community, safe environment, and health. Survivors are paired with a Staff Advocate for casework and resources, and Volunteer Mentor to offer emotional support.

We raise awareness through speaking events, education, and awareness campaigns. Visit our website to invite us to speak at your church, community group, or event.

Name: Association Yob A Yoo
Description:
Yob A Yoo est une association humanitaire suisse basée à Genève, engagée aux côtés des enfants défavorisés de Yaoundé, au Cameroun.
Nous œuvrons pour leur offrir nourriture, éducation, soins médicaux et un centre d’accueil où grandir avec dignité et espoir.

Name: Darbe
Description: Our platform makes philanthropy more accessible by allowing people to easily create a community of giving around causes they care about. Engage your friends and family with online challenges to volunteer or donate to charity. Keep track of your charitable activity, find volunteer opportunities in your area, and give directly to nonprofits in a transparent way.

Name: American Himalayan Foundation
Description:
The Himalaya are a famously beautiful part of the world. But many of those who live in the shadow of these magnificent mountains struggle without healthcare or education, young girls live in danger of being trafficked, and traditional ways of life are fast disappearing. The American Himalayan Foundation exists to respond to these pressing problems and bring positive, tangible change to people’s lives.

Since AHF’s first partnerships formed in 1981, with Sir Edmund Hillary and His Holiness the Dalai Lama, we have expanded to touch the lives of over 300,000 Sherpas, Tibetans, and Nepalis throughout the Himalaya each year. 

Our mission is to take care of people in the Himalaya who are in need and have no one else: by opening doors to education and health care, helping build stronger communities, and developing lasting, trusting local partnerships. By working in a way that respects both tradition and innovation, we give people real hope and opportunity. 

info@himalayan-foundation.org

Name: UMMA Center
Description: The mission of the UMMA Center is to empower the lives of underprivileged individuals in Lake County by providing educational support and community resources. The UMMA Center believes that Lake County will be strengthened by the growth that education, hope and purpose bring to those seeking to enrich their lives with opportunities.

Name: The Extra Mile Organization
Description:
We collect monetary donations & frequent flyer miles and provide them to people who desperately need them to visit terminally ill loved ones with cancer.

I’m Matt Dimmer, the Founder of The Extra Mile. My dad was diagnosed with stage 4 pancreatic cancer in November 2011. I had just moved to Los Angeles with my wife, and not knowing how long he had left, began scheduling flights back to see him in Michigan. At least once a month I would fly back and spend 3-4 days reminiscing about good times with my dad, and creating some new memories to treasure.

During this extremely stressful span, I spent a lot of time on airplanes, and when I wasn’t focused on the idea of losing him, I thought about people in similar situations who couldn’t afford to do what I was doing. I couldn’t imagine not being able to see him that one last time before he was gone. 

A few months after beginning The Extra Mile, my dad passed away, but not before seeing the early sketches of the plans. I’m creating this in his honor, to help others, because that’s what he would do. 

Go the Extra Mile by giving yours.

Name: IFCJ Canada
Description:
The International Fellowship of Christians and Jews of Canada (IFCJ Canada) was founded by Rabbi Yechiel Eckstein  to foster better relations and understanding between Christians and Jews, encourage greater cooperation between both communities on issues of shared biblical concern, and support Israel and Jews in crises or need. Our vision is that Jews and Christians will reverse their 2,000-year history of discord and replace it with a relationship marked by dialogue, respect and cooperation.

IFCJ Canada operates under the leadership of Yael Eckstein, and is governed by an independent Board of Directors representing many faiths who share our strong belief in building bridges between the Christian and Jewish communities.

Today, The Fellowship is the largest philanthropy for social welfare causes in Israel, with thanks to our global efforts from here in Canada, along with our sister organizations in the United States and South Korea. We have helped hundreds of thousands of Jews escape poverty and anti-Semitism and return to their biblical homeland, funded humanitarian assistance that has touched the lives of impoverished Israelis, and provided life-giving aid to suffering Jews around the world. 

About Rabbi Eckstein, President and Founder, IFCJ Canada; Goodwill Ambassador, State of Israel
Rabbi Yechiel Z. Eckstein, President and Founder of IFCJ Canada, has devoted his time to building bridges of understanding between Christians and Jews and broad support for the State of Israel. He is the leading advocate of religious freedom worldwide.

Name: Eloquentia Bayonne
Description:
Eloquentia Bayonne est un programme éducatif d'intérêt général créé en juillet 2019. 

Eloquentia a pour but d’aider et de former à la prise de parole en public et propose deux types de formations :

- Des ateliers-débats gratuits et ouverts à tous. Ils se déroulent les mercredis soir de 18h30 à 20h30, hors périodes de vacances scolaires.
Durant ces ateliers, différents exercices sont proposés pour aider tout à chacun à s'améliorer et à se perfectionner dans la maîtrise de l'art oratoire : jeux d'improvisation, travail de l'intonation et de la gestuelle, réduction des tics de langage, travail de la rhétorique.
- Nous organisons également des Masterclass une fois par mois. Nous avons ainsi pu recevoir Maître Saladin (gagnant du concours d’éloquence du Barreau de Bayonne) et Monsieur Jean-Pierre Massias (professeur agrégé de droit public à l’Université de Bayonne).

- Eloquentia Bayonne organise également un des plus grand concours d'éloquence national et international (présent dans 19 villes et universités partenaires, dont Paris Nanterre, Paris Seine-Saint-Denis). Ce concours est ouvert à toute personne entre 18 et 30 ans.

Name: Communities of Coastal Georgia Foundation, Inc.
Description: The Coastal Georgia Foundation is a launching point for strategic and sustained philanthropy in the Golden Isles-bringing together fundholders and our local community. By matching an individual's personal interests with the immediate needs of our community, the Foundation promotes unity and a holistic approach to community betterment. For good. For ever.

Name: Peter & Elizabeth Tower Foundation
Description:
With over $135 million in assets, the Peter & Elizabeth Tower Foundation distributes more than $6 million in grants annually. Our geographic funding areas are Erie and Niagara Counties in New York State and Barnstable, Dukes, Essex, and Nantucket Counties in Massachusetts.   

Our Mission
As grantmaker, partner, and advocate, the Tower Foundation strengthens organizations and works to change systems to improve the lives of young people with learning disabilities, mental illness, substance use disorders, and intellectual disabilities.

Our Vision
The Tower Foundation envisions equitable communities where all young people are included, accepted, and valued; and are confident in themselves.

Our Values
The Tower Foundation values:
* Diverse Voices – We listen to and learn from those with different points of view and perspectives. We seek to be proximate with the communities we serve – continually engaging in respectful conversation and incorporating what we hear into our work.

* Collaboration – We understand that in order to make progress, we must build relationships with our partners and share knowledge, resources, expertise, and strategies.

* Innovation – We embrace change and are willing to take risks, recognizing the potential for transformative effect. We look for opportunities to try new and creative strategies and encourage others to do the same.

* Perseverance – We recognize that change work can be slow and challenging. We are committed to the long-term improvement of organizations and communities.

* Equity – We pay close attention to the convergence of our focus areas with systemic issues of racism, sexism, classism, ableism, and more, because living at the intersection of multiple identities is a reality that factors importantly into our grantmaking.

Name: Ability Central
Description:
Ability Central is a nonprofit on a mission to be a resource, convener, and educator working collaboratively to ensure that people who are Deaf, disabled, and neurodivergent can communicate and access information.

We serve as an educator, convener, and resource that works collaboratively in service of individuals who are Deaf, disabled, and neurodivergent. We accomplish this by offering grants, cloud consulting services, and a comprehensive online informational portal.

Name: PeaceMaker Minnesota
Description:
The mission of PeaceMaker Minnesota is to help schools be safer places, free from bullying and harassment, and to help children learn positive relational skills like empathy, respect and how to resolve conflicts peacefully.  

Our strategy is to help schools use the best practices in youth violence prevention, to develop sustainable funding to strengthen their work and to celebrate results so that more schools are inspired to make improvements. Our objective is to help schools to improve their student results each year and to reduce bullying below state and national averages.  

Please join us!

Name: Samuel H. Kress Foundation
Description: The Samuel H. Kress Foundation devotes its resources to advancing the study, conservation, and enjoyment of the vast heritage of European art, architecture, and archaeology from antiquity to the early 19th century.

Name: Fonds de dotation Judaïsme En Mouvement
Description: Le Fonds de dotation Judaïsme en Mouvement a pour objet de soutenir un judaïsme ouvert et éclairé, et de développer des activités d’intérêt général à caractère culturel, éducatif, social, scientifique et artistique en lien avec les valeurs qu’il soutient.

Name: BellRinger
Description: BellRinger is a community movement to end cancer. Our year-round effort peaks with BellRinger weekend when we come together to celebrate our impact and ride our bikes to raise awareness and funds for cancer research at Georgetown Lombardi Comprehensive Cancer Center.

Name: Aide Humanitaire ONG Equinoxe
Description: « Un monde juste qui garantit aux enfants de vivre et de grandir dignement avec leur famille et leur environnement, pour devenir des femmes et des hommes libres, acteurs de la société »

Name: Sustainable Development For All -Kenya
Description:
S.D.F.A is a fully registered non-profit, non-partisan, social enterprise operating in Sub Saharan Africa. We aim to help poor communities attain sustainable development, and in turn live better lives, through initiatives that originate from communities themselves.Our key thematic areas are Environment and Energy, Education and Economic Empowerment.

Our main focus program is the ‘Use Solar, Save Lives program’ which incorporates the three thematic areas.

Name: Garfield Foundation
Description: Garfield Foundation supports projects that impact environmental sustainability.

Name: BUHL REGIONAL HEALTH FOUNDATION
Description: BRHF aims to make an impact in Mercer County through health-focused programming, education, grants, scholarships, and collaboration with local organizations.

Name: LexisNexis Rule of Law Foundation
Description:
The LexisNexis Rule of law Foundation is a not for profit organization (501(c)(3) US) dedicated to advancing the Rule of Law around the world.

The Rule of Law comprises 4 key elements;

* Equality under the law
* Independent Judiciary
* Publication of Laws
* Access to Remedy 

The foundation seeks to advance the Rule of Law in one or more of these areas by deploying skills in development projects and working with partners who share the vision of advancing the Rule of Law.

Name: The Younger Family Fund
Description:
