We envision a world where people are seen with dignity, justice prevails, healing and resilience take root, and thriving families and communities take care of each other.

We practice generosity by partnering with high-impact organizations demonstrating leadership in the fields of justice, climate, education, healthcare, economic opportunity, and spiritual formation.

Our giving reflects where we live, work, and have deep ties — focusing on the San Francisco Bay Area and Oxford, UK - while thoughtfully supporting global initiatives that align with our values and vision for wider impact.

The Younger Family Fund, established in 1991, is the philanthropic initiative of a multi-generational family rooted in the San Francisco Bay Area. The Fund reflects our family's enduring commitment to generosity, faith, service, and shared responsibility. The second generation now serve as active Directors of YFF, shaping its direction and evaluating partners.

Name: Real Humanitarian
Description:
Real Humanitarian is a non-religious, non-political charitable organization dedicated to assisting disadvantaged children and their families in Ethiopia, Ghana, and Guatemala.

Canadian Humanitarian helps individuals break free from the cycle of poverty by providing access to health care, education, vocational training, empowerment workshops, and the basic necessities of life. Canadian Humanitarian believes in local solutions to local struggles.  As they adopt a community based development approach, they are able to provide tools to community members to enable them to get themselves out of poverty.

Each year we run multiple expeditions to Ethiopia (travel restrictions allowing).  Volunteers work in our projects for 2 weeks rubbing shoulders with the children we benefit in their home communities. Come and join a group of passionate people that believe in the power of educating children.

Name: TAI (The Accessibility Institute)
Description:
TAI, The Accessibility Institute focuses on the design, manufacture and distribution of inclusive mobility solutions for low-resource settings. Mobility isn’t just about movement, it’s the key to an independent, fulfilling life. Mobility allows us to socialise, to access education and healthcare, to provide for our families and to make the most of life’s opportunities.

Our mission at TAI is to help low income, mobility impaired people break free from the disability-poverty cycle. Our flagship product is the SafariSeat wheelchair; an all-terrain, low-cost, and locally manufactured product, designed specifically for low-resource regions of the world - please visit the TAI website if you'd like to know more.

Name: Filana Foundation
Description:
Het doel van de Filana Foundation is het ondersteunen van mensen die onrecht werd, wordt of kan worden aangedaan in België. We kiezen bewust voor deze regio om het overzichtelijk te houden, dicht bij huis, en om een zichtbare impact te kunnen realiseren.

Er zijn - jammer genoeg - veel problematieken. Zelfs in een ‘welvarend’ land als België. We denken hierbij aan voor de hand liggende thema’s zoals armoede en mishandeling, maar dit is slechts het topje van de ijsberg.
Het zijn onderwerpen waar wel eens over gesproken wordt, maar vaak stopt het daar. Het is een latent probleem in de maatschappij. De Filana Foundation wil dit niet alleen bespreekbaar maken, maar wil de problematiek ook daadwerkelijk aanpakken. Dit kan gaan over het doorbreken van taboes, sensibilisering, empowerment en effectieve actie of een combinatie van al deze aspecten.

Belangrijk hierbij is het niet blijven hangen als slachtoffer, maar net in eigen kracht gaan staan om zowel de persoon zelf, maar ook anderen te inspireren, motiveren en empoweren.

Name: Fundação Lar de Nossa Senhora do Livramento
Description:
A Fundação Lar Nossa Senhora do Livramento (FLNSL) é uma Instituição Particular de Solidariedade Social (IPSS) sem fins lucrativos, composta por duas respostas sociais com acordos celebrados com o Centro Distrital de Segurança Social do Porto:

a Casa de Acolhimento Residencial (CAR) que recebe crianças e jovens do sexo feminino entre os 6 e os 25 anos e o Jardim de Infância (JI) que dá resposta a crianças entre os 3 anos e a idade de ingresso no 1º Ciclo.

A história da CAR confunde-se com a história da cidade do Porto remontando às invasões napoleónicas. Nesta altura, um conjunto de cidadãos organizou-se para proteger crianças e suas mães do abandono e maltrato e, cumprindo o dever cívico e moral de participação ativa na sociedade, criaram a primeira casa de acolhimento no ano de 1810. Ao longo dos anos a CAR tem investido na qualificação da sua resposta, na intencionalidade reparadora da sua intervenção.

No seguimento da missão em 1978, é aberto à comunidade o Jardim de Infância, ainda hoje em funcionamento. Nos últimos anos tem sido alvo de profundas mudanças estruturais e pedagógicas, para dar resposta às necessidades das famílias e crianças que procuram iniciar um percurso educativo de qualidade, assente na humanização e proximidade entre escola e famílias

Name: National Autism Association - New York Metro Chapter
Description:
National Autism Association - New York Metro is a chapter of the National Autism Association serving the five boroughs of New York City, Long Island, Westchester, and Rockland County.  We are an all-volunteer, grassroots organization.

Our mission is to educate and empower those in the autism community. We will never give up in the search to help affected individuals reach their full potential.  We provide educational programs, free parent mentoring, professionally moderated support groups and other events and services to families and professionals in the NY Metro area whose lives are affected by autism.  We bring information and awareness about all aspects of autism spectrum disorders to the community, including both traditional and alternative therapeutic and treatment modalities.

Name: Instituto Shobukan de Cultura e Artes Marciais
Description: Founded in 1990 to promote practice and support teaching and research initiatives aimed at developing Karate, Judo and Okoboji Kobubo. The Shobukan Institute of Culture and Martial Art was founded in 1990 as Shobukan Association of Martial Arts, with the main objective of training children and youth in the practice of Shotokan Karate and Judo in its fullness, in the cultural aspects of Japan and in the relationship between Brazil and that country. The technical direction of the evolution of teachers and students has always been under the responsibility of Sensei Naoyuki Hirakawa, one of the most respected Martial Arts Masters of Brazil recognized for his technical ability and legacy in many countries. The incorporation of new techniques derived from the vast knowledge of the Sensei Naoyuki, was fundamental for the development of all that were oriented by him. A0 Sensei Aristeu Silveira - In addition to the duties of the Institute's directors, it was the responsibility of the development of the teaching-learning methodology for children and adolescents and the training of teachers and monitors in the method. At this stage of our history, the support of Vice-President Sensei Francisco Carlos was fundamental. Due to his academic background and professional experience - in addition to acting as a manager in companies, he began to teach in preparatory courses for vestibular exams, as teacher and coordinator in Higher Education Institutions, was successful in applying the didactic-pedagogical foundations in the teaching of Martial Arts. In 1992 the Shobukan Association of Martial Arts had students training in six units (Dojos) in the City of Rio de Janeiro and throughout the years 1990-2000 it consolidated as one of the best schools of Martial Arts and diffusion of the Japanese culture for children and adolescents . Throughout the decade of 1990 and 2000 the then Shobukan Association of Martial Arts became one of the best Institutions for the practice and study of the Jap

Name: ICOSI - Institut de Coopération Sociale Internationale
Description:
L’ICOSI, Institut de Coopération Sociale Internationale, est une organisation non gouvernementale fondée en 1983 qui a comme principal objectif de promouvoir les valeurs de l’économie sociale : solidarité et respect des principes et des modes de gestion démocratiques.

L’ICOSI crée et réalise depuis plus de 30 ans des programmes de coopération avec le monde entier, et plus particulièrement avec le pourtour méditerranéen depuis quelques années.

En application de cet objet, les programmes mis en place touchent donc à l’appui à la démocratie sociale (rôle des partenaires sociaux et des corps intermédiaires), à la démocratie économique (développement des coopératives, mutuelles, associations...) et à la démocratie politique (enjeux de citoyenneté et d’engagement, droits fondamentaux).

Sur tous ces sujets, l’Institut se place résolument dans la promotion, la diffusion et la défense des normes internationales du travail.

L’ICOSI exerce également, en collaboration avec ses partenaires, un travail de « lobbying » auprès des institutions publiques (Institutions européennes, Gouvernements, Collectivités territoriales) visant à encourager la recherche de solutions solidaires et conduisant à placer la personne humaine au cœur des choix économiques.

L’ICOSI bénéficie donc du soutien logistique et financier de nombreuses structures de l’économie sociale qui constituent son Assemblée Générale et son Conseil d’Administration.

Ses adhérents et partenaires sont en effet issus des « familles » traditionnelles de l’économie sociale (coopératives, associations, mutuelles et fondations) et des institutions paritaires de prévoyance collective.

Les cinq organisations syndicales représentatives (CFDT, CFE-CGC, CFTC, CGT, CGT-FO) ainsi que l’UNSA Education sont également adhérentes et membres de notre Conseil d’Administration.

Name: Just For Kids Foundation
Description:
At Just for Kids Foundation, we're on a mission to make a difference for patients at the Montreal Children’s Hospital! By raising funds for high-priority medical equipment and essential initiatives, we work to improve the quality of care for young patients every day. 

Our vision is clear: we’re dedicated to enhancing the hospital experience and ensuring that every child receives the best possible care.

Here’s how we’re making an impact:
  
 Over $12.5 million raised to provide state-of-the-art medical equipment  
 More than 200,000 children touched by our support  
 1,500+ healthcare professionals better equipped to care for their patients  

Join us in creating brighter futures for kids and their families. Every donation helps bring smiles, comfort, and healing to children who need it most. Together, we can make a lasting impact! 

Learn more about how you can get involved and support our cause at https://www.justforkidsfoundation.org

Name: Saanjha
Description:
Saanjha is a movement(a registered NGO) to empower vulnerable communities by creating sports role models who inspire change. We focus on youth from vulnerable territories/backgrounds in India, where the dangers of arms, drug abuse, and social instability are prevalent.By nurturing athletes in these communities, we aim to instill a powerful belief that young people have the potential to achieve greatness and overcome adversity.

Through this transformative journey, we aim not only to change individual lives but also to contribute to India's success on the global sports stage, fostering a generation that not only brings home more medals but also builds brighter, more hopeful futures for their communities.

Our approach is centred on four key areas: providing access to international events, essential sports equipment, proper nutrition, and sports psychology support.

Come help us and be a part of the mission !Every type of support is welcome & can be extended by individuals or groups- financial, connecting with institutes/ sports bodies, brand representation, providing equipments and much more.

Name: El Paso Leadership Network
Description: The El Paso Leadership Network (EPLN) is a non-profit organization committed to fostering the progress of El Paso, Texas. We aspire to influence our community’s education, economy, government, and culture by providing leadership development programs and networking opportunities for El Pasoans committed to creating a positive impact.

Name: Tour Du Gambia
Description:
Het doel van dit project is het zorgvervoer en de medische apparatuur op de kinderafdeling in Serrekunda (Gambia) te verbeteren. Dit doen we door twee ambulances en een facilitair ziekenhuisvoertuig, alle in perfecte staat, te schenken aan Riders For Health en het Kanifing General Hospital in Serrekunda (Gambia). Tevens voorziet het project hetzelfde ziekenhuis van (zo goed als) nieuwe medische apparatuur zoals bloeddrukmeters, HB-meters, echoapparatuur, couveuses etc. 

Om het project onder de aandacht te brengen, leggen we de route van Hoogeveen naar Banjul (Gambia) af via de weg, ca, 6000 km. Een uitdagende tocht en niet geheel zonder risico’s. 

Er is een nauwe samenwerking met het management van het ziekenhuis, Riders for Health en het Gambiaanse ministerie van gezondheidszorg. De inzet van zowel de ambulances, het facilitaire voertuig en het gebruik van de medische apparatuur is hierdoor gegarandeerd en duurzaam, doordat het onderhoud en opleiding met deze organisaties zijn gewaarborgd.

De funding van het project realiseren we door het organiseren van diverse benefietactiviteiten, sponsoring van bedrijven, digitaal collecteren en deels door een bijdrage vanuit verschillende fondsen. De financiële verantwoording loopt via Stichting Samen voor Gambia, een Hoogeveense stichting met ANBI-status, met al ruim 10 jaar ervaring in Gambia. De verblijfkosten van de deelnemers betalen zij uit eigen middelen. 

De reis en de overhandiging van de voertuigen en apparatuur vindt plaats in november 2022.

Name: Reintegra
Description: Reintegra is an anti-human trafficking organization that supports survivors as they rebuild their lives and reintegrate into society. We offer a family structure of love and encouragement to foster and support dreams of a better future. We provide financial, emotional, and psychological resources to facilitate restoration and healing.

Name: The Philanthropic Collaborative for Education
Description:
The Collaborative strengthens, convenes, and catalyzes networks of philanthropy to advance equitable and racially just education and systemic policies and practices that create the conditions every learner needs to achieve, thrive and succeed in a globally diverse society.

Our vision is to unify philanthropy, educators, policymakers, and communities to actively support equitable and just policies and systems promoting opportunities for all learners to develop and prosper.

Name: Fulbright New Zealand, Te Tūāpapa Mātauranga o Aotearoa me Amerika
Description: Fulbright New Zealand  promotes mutual understanding through educational and cultural exchanges between New Zealand and the United States of America.  Offers a range of enriching and life-changing exchange awards for New Zealanders and Americans wanting to study, research, teach or present their work in each other’s country.

Name: Vector Stiftung
Description: Die Vector Stiftung ist eine unternehmensverbundene Stiftung. Sie fördert MINT-Forschungsvorhaben und MINT-Bildung in Baden-Württemberg. Schüler und Studierende für unternehmerisches Denken zu begeistern, ist ein weiterer Teil ihrer Förderaktivitäten. Die Stiftung setzt sich in der Region Stuttgart darüber hinaus für die Bekämpfung der Wohnungslosigkeit und die Integration sozial benachteiligter junger Erwachsener in Arbeit und Gesellschaft ein.

Name: Halifax Foundation for Northern Ireland
Description:
The Halifax Foundation for Northern Ireland is one of four independent grant-making trusts, covering England & Wales, Scotland, Northern Ireland and the Channel Islands. 

The Halifax Foundation for Northern Ireland is committed to supporting underfunded, grassroots charities that enable people, especially disabled and disadvantaged people, to play a fuller role in society. Applications are accepted from organisations with recognised charitable status, which fit within the published grant-giving criteria.

Name: Turning the Tide Marine Industry Awards
Description: Turning the Tide Marine Industry Awards is the first of its kind in North America and only 1 of 3 globally. These prestigious awards recognize and celebrate marine excellence in our province.

Name: Humanitaire Baudot
Description: HUMANITAIRE BAUDOT est une association humanitaire de santé destinée à aider les populations indigentes du monde entier.

Name: Challah for Hunger: A Project of MAZON
Description:
We are building communities of people — on college campuses and beyond — who are eager to take action to end hunger. Challah for Hunger employs an innovative philanthropic model of bringing together groups around the Jewish tradition of baking challah, and donating the proceeds of sales to the fight to end hunger locally and nationally. We are developing leaders committed to social justice, engaging them in an understanding of the realities and complexities of hunger in America, and guiding them to realize and actualize long-term solutions to end hunger.

Learn more at mazon.org/ChallahForHunger

Name: 1 Million Cups Charleston
Description: As a program of the Kauffman Foundation, 1 Million Cups works with entrepreneurs, empowering them with the tools and resources to break down barriers that stand in the way of starting and growing their businesses.

Name: Junior League of Miami
Description:
The Junior League of Miami, Inc. is an organization of women whose mission is to advance women’s leadership for meaningful community impact through volunteer action, collaboration, and training. Its purpose is exclusively educational and charitable.

Our vision is to empower Miami's women, children and families to conquer tomorrow's challenges and to build a united community.

Name: Women's Entrepreneurship Accelerator
Description: Women’s Entrepreneurship Accelerator (WEA) is a multi-partnership initiative on women’s entrepreneurship established in during UNGA74 by five UN agencies (ILO, ITC, UNDP, UNGC and UN Women) and Mary Kay Inc. to empower 5 million women entrepreneurs by 2030. The ultimate goal of the initiative is to maximize the development impact of women entrepreneurship in achieving Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) by creating an enabling ecosystem for women entrepreneurs around the world. The Accelerator exemplifies the transformational power of a multi-partnership of unique magnitude to harness the potential of women entrepreneurs.

Name: Agenda Vestlandet
Description:
Agenda Vestlandet er ei stifting oppretta av Sparebanken Norge for å akselerere det grøne skiftet på Vestlandet. 

Aldri før har Vestlandet og verda rundt oss stått ovanfor ei vanskelegare, viktigare oppgåve: Å endre vårt næringsliv, vårt samfunn og vårt eige tankesett tidsnok til å møte dei utfordringane og dei moglegheitene som følgjer av omstillinga til framtidas fornybarsamfunn.

Agenda Vestlandet står bak ei rekke prosjekt som på ulike felt har som mål å styrke regionen for framtida. Mellom anna gjennom leiarprogramma Women in Tech, Unge leiarar og Handlekraft. 

Stiftinga står også bak sentrale initiativ som:
- Vestlandskonferansen
- Vestlandsindeksen
- Vestlandsmeldinga
- Volumkonferansen

Name: Stichting Voedselbank Haaglanden
Description:
Voedselbank Haaglanden bestaat uit een distributiecentrum van Voedselbank Nederland dat goederen in ontvangst neemt en voedsel in bulk en omgepakt levert aan 14 voedselbanken in Zuid-Holland West. Voedselbank Haaglanden is op dezelfde locatie gehuisvest en biedt wekelijks voedselhulp aan onze klanten via uitgiftepunten en 6 winkels in Den Haag, Zoetermeer en Rijswijk.
Wij verstrekken gezond en gevarieerd voedsel aan circa 2000 gezinnen/5000 personen. Dit doen we uitsluitend met vrijwilligers! 

Regionaal Distributiecentrum Haaglanden: grote partijen voedsel – houdbaar, koel/vers en diepvries –  inzamelen, tijdelijk opslaan, ompakken en verzenden naar 14 Voedselbanken in de regio Zuid West Nederland.

Het distributiecentrum is gevestigd aan de De Lierseweg 2B, 2291 PD in Wateringen.

Hier worden elke week vooral op woensdagavond de voedselpakketten samengesteld. Op de andere dagen wordt het voedsel ingezameld en gesorteerd onder andere ten bate van de winkels te bevoorading. Ook pakken we grote partijen voedsel om in kleinere hoeveelheden, om ze gelijkmatig over de verschillende voedselpakketten te kunnen verdelen.

Het transport
In het distributiecentrum werken de vrijwilligers met professionele apparatuur zoals pompwagens en vorkheftrucks. Voor transport van voedsel naar en van het distributiecentrum rijden transportbussen en koelwagens. Zo rijden ongeveer 25 chauffeurs in een poule 5 dagen in de week.

Duurzaam
Het distributiecentrum van Voedselbank Haaglanden is energiezuinig dankzij onder andere zonnepanelen, warmtepompen, ledlampen en goede isolatie.
Wens is om het elektrische wagenpark uit te breiden.
De Voedselbank voorkomt voedselverspilling en zet daar nog verder op in. Onder andere door versproducten sneller te verspreiden via de Voedselbankwinkels of te verwerken in ingevroren maaltijden.

Name: THE BRITISH-UZBEK SOCIETY (London, UK)
Description:
We are pleased to present the only official British-Uzbek Society formally established in July 2002 as a Charitable organisation by a group of prominent people in the UK, including business, academic and artistic communities and with the support of the Embassy of Uzbekistan in London. 
The British Uzbek Society is funded by the subscriptions of corporate and individual members. BUS activities include lectures, as well as receptions to celebrate mutually significant festivals, such as Nauruz (Uzbek New Year) and a joint event to mark Uzbek Constitution Day (8 December) and Christmas.
We will welcome all individuals interested in economy, history and culture of Uzbekistan and Central Asia. We aim to promote friendship and business links through discussion forums, social and cultural events.
So, if you are interested in the future development of relations between the United Kingdom and Uzbekistan, please JOIN and UNITE! Here is our membership page: http://uzbek.org.uk/membership/

Name: New Hampshire Children's Trust
Description:
New Hampshire Children’s Trust is the Governor’s designated statewide community-based child abuse prevention agency, the New Hampshire chapter of Prevent Child Abuse America, and a member of the National Alliance of Children’s Trust & Prevention Funds.

Founded in 1986 as "New Hampshire Children’s Trust Fund," a quasi-governmental organization, the agency transitioned to "New Hampshire Children’s Trust, Inc.," a 501(c)(3) nonprofit corporation in 2011. We are a public charity and donations are tax deductible.

New Hampshire Children’s Trust is leading the drive to ensure safe, stable, and nurturing relationships and environments for children by educating, advocating, and collaborating.

All New Hampshire children thrive in safe, nurturing, and supportive environments.

Name: Enactus uOttawa
Description:
Enactus uOttawa is a community of student, academic and business leaders committed to using the power of entrepreneurial action to transform lives and shape a better more sustainable world.

We aim to ignite positive change in the quality of life and the standard of living of those in need through the power of entrepreneurship.

Name: MOHAMED CHERIF INITIATIVE
Description: Donner aux autres plus qu'à toi même • Mohamed Cherif Initiative (MCI) a pour mission de construire un avenir plus inclusif, juste et sain pour tous.

Name: الجمعية الخيرية للرعاية الصحية بمنطقة تبوك
Description: تتشرف الجمعية الخيرية للرعاية الصحية بمنطقة تبوك بتقديم حزمة من الخدمات الصحية متمثلة بالدعم العيني والتثقيف الصحي والحملات التثقيفية لسكان المنطقة من المواطنين والمقيمين .

Name: Revitalize Milwaukee
Description:
Mission: 
To provide free, professional-quality home repairs and comprehensive services for low-income seniors, veterans and homeowners with disabilities in Milwaukee and Waukesha Counties

Promise of Experience:
• Awarding priority to disabled, elderly and veteran homeowners
• Providing a friendly and skilled volunteer force
• Responding to strategic plans established by government agencies, including county and municipal plans
• Ensuring a one-stop process for homeowners to receive services form many agencies
• Engaging in flexible and consistent collaborations with homeowners
• Qualifying homeowners to receive services more than once

What Makes RTGM Unique:
• Completely FREE and essential home repairs
• Long-standing relationships with dozens of community agencies
• Occupational therapist assessment and modifications 
• Extensive referral services for needs other than housing
• Few qualifications

Name: Association Chiens Guides d'Aveugles - Centres Paul Corteville
Description:
L'Association Chiens Guides d'Aveugles a pour vocation d'améliorer l'autonomie et le confort de déplacement des personnes privées de la vue. Pour cela, elle remet GRATUITEMENT : 
- Des chiens guides d'aveugles (qui sont éduqués dans nos centres d'éducation)
- Des cannes blanches électroniques

L'association ne perçoit aucune subvention de l'État et fonctionne uniquement à l'aide de la générosité publique (Dons et Legs). 
L'association est Reconnue d'Utilité Publique depuis 1973 et exerce son activité sur 14 départements qui couvrent la partie nord du pays. 

Les Centres Paul Corteville c'est aussi un moyen pour votre entreprise de vous engager dans un projet associatif. Nous sommes habilités à recevoir l'aide et le soutien d'entreprises mécènes.  
En nous aidant matériellement ou financièrement, vous pourrez bénéficier d'une réduction fiscale à hauteur de 60% du don (dans la limite de 0.5% du chiffre d'affaires). 
En contrepartie de ce soutien, votre entreprise peut bénéficier d'une prestation de sensibilisation au handicap visuel, d'une mise à disposition de nos locaux ou encore d'autres contreparties qui sont définies dans un contrat liant votre entreprise et notre association.  Un excellent moyen de lier l'image de votre entreprise à un projet associatif, tout en fédérant vos collaborateurs sur un travail commun.

Name: Effective Giving
Description: Effective Giving helped UHNW philanthropists to find – and fund – the most promising solutions to the world’s most pressing problems. It laid the foundation for what has now become Ergo Impact.

Name: The Matt Ratana Rugby Foundation
Description:
Following the tragic death of Matt Ratana, the inspiring grassroots rugby coach and highly regarded police officer, we established The Matt Ratana Rugby Foundation.

The Foundation will champion Matt’s values of using the medium of rugby to create transformational change in young people.  We will deliver and design development initiatives across grassroots rugby clubs and schools, targeting vulnerable people to bring about sustainable change.

We are raising funds to support coaches and players through a variety of locally run training programmes and improved facilities.  Matt believed that rugby wasn’t just about the playing of sport, it was about bringing people together from across the community and in that endeavour creating an environment in which young people can thrive and grow as individuals and as part of a team.

Community is more important now more than ever.

Name: Grupo Punto Omega
Description:
Asociación Punto Omega es una Entidad Sin Fines Lucrativos constituida el 31 de mayo de 1984. Se dedica a combatir y prevenir la exclusión social, prestar servicios sociales, sanitarios y sociosanitarios, e impartir formación relacionada con un proceso de inserción social y laboral a colectivos en situación de vulnerabilidad social.

VALORES

Compromiso con los Derechos Humanos.

Solidaridad.

Igualdad de oportunidades.

Inclusión.

Participación Social.

Innovación.

Autonomía, tanto en la gestión como en la toma de decisiones.

Gestión responsable con el Medio Ambiente.

Transparencia en la gestión.

Excelencia en la gestión.

La Entidad nace del compromiso con los derechos humanos y descansa en los valores de la solidaridad, igualdad de oportunidades, inclusión y participación social. Son principios rectores básicos de la Entidad: la autonomía en la gestión, la participación democrática, la transparencia en la gestión, promover la igualdad de oportunidades y de trato así como la no discriminación entre todas las personas con especial atención al principio de igualdad de oportunidades entre hombres y mujeres. La Entidad impulsa el reconocimiento y el ejercicio de los derechos civiles, así como de los derechos económicos, sociales o culturales de las personas y grupos que sufren condiciones de vulnerabilidad o que se encuentran en situación o en riesgo de exclusión social.

Name: HAIR REBORN
Description:
Founded by Lina and Joe Milazzo, HAIR REBORN is a charity offering support, advice and a free hair styling service at appointed hair salons, for people suffering hair loss as a result of cancer chemotherapy treatment. 

Since we launched in 2017, we have supported almost 850 people across the UK prepare and deal with hair loss due to cancer treatment and achieve a new manageable hair style once their hair grows back, regaining their identity, confidence and self esteem.

At HAIR REBORN we are here to help you deal with hair loss through cancer chemotherapy treatment with understanding, support and practical advice from the moment you are told you may lose your hair through to styling your hair as it grows back.

Our four stage programme offers expert advice, support and up to 3 free visits at a HAIR REBORN-appointed UK salon: 

RE-ASSURE
Helping to prepare you to feel more confident whilst going through your temporary hair loss journey & provide ongoing advice and support.

RE-START
A first salon visit to trim and tidy your new hair as it begins to grow back at least 8 weeks after chemotherapy has ended to prepare for your new hairstyle.

RE-STYLE
This would be an appointment at least 8-12 weeks after your Re-Start visit to trim & shape your hair, getting your new hairstyle underway.

RE-BORN
This final salon visit is to complete your new manageable hairstyle. We will give advice on managing, colouring and looking after your new hair.

Give us a call or drop us an email at info@hairreborn.uk to start your journey with HAIR REBORN.

If you'd like to support us with a donation, please visit justgiving.com/hair-reborn

Registered charity no 1185832

Name: The Catholic Foundation (Columbus, Ohio)
Description: The Catholic Foundation transforms donor generosity into real-world impact by investing in the future of parishes, education, vocations, and ministries. The Catholic Foundation fulfills its mission by working with donors to establish charitable funds to support current and future needs and distributing earnings according to the United States Conference of Catholic Bishops' Guidelines for Socially Responsible Investing and donor intent. Since its inception in 1984, The Catholic Foundation has granted over $200 million throughout the 23-county diocese of Columbus and beyond.

Name: Border Trust
Description:
Welcome to Border Trust Community Foundation for the Albury Wodonga Region.

Border Trust is a local, independent, charitable organisation that was established in 2005 to create a strong, sustainable financial base that can be drawn on to meet the needs of our community, today and in the future.

Border Trust raises funds through donations and community events, invests that money and uses the income to fund important community initiatives. In this way, donations are preserved in perpetuity and our donors gifts continue to benefit the community year after year. 

Donations to Border Trust stay 100% local & help us to support not-for-profit organisations achieve positive community outcomes.  But you don’t just have to take our word for it – we’ve asked 4 beneficiaries to tell our story for us.

Name: Launch22
Description: Launch22 was an organisation helping to accelerate next-generation entrepreneurs from all walks of life and their startup businesses. We combined low-cost office space with world-class mentoring and events.

Name: Carlos Morgado Foundation
Description: The Foundation Carlos Morgado is a Mozambican non-profit organization that aims to strengthen the development of Mozambique. We build on programs in education, health and sports in order to cover the largest number of Mozambicans. Engaging communities, government and the private sector as partners to share responsibilities towards the common objectives.

Name: Australian Jewish Funders
Description: The Australian Jewish Funders (AJF) is a network of mindful funders who believe that greater impact can be achieved collectively. Driven by Jewish values, the AJF aims to provide a space to enhance and nurture strategic philanthropy, explore philanthropic best practice and encourage innovation amongst like-minded funders, inspiring members to have a greater impact on the Jewish community, wider Australian society and the world.

Name: CoCoon Foundation
Description: The CoCoon Foundation has been established in 2016. We envision a world in which entrepreneurs can make a difference to their community, meet the resources they need to grow and reach their full potential. To achieve this goal, we focus on connecting aspiring entrepreneurs with experiential learning and the entrepreneurship community. We work in collaboration with start-ups, universities and corporate partners to develop exposure to the skills and network they will need to succeed in the future.

Name: John M. Belk Endowment
Description: Transforming postsecondary educational opportunities to meet North Carolina’s evolving workforce needs.

Name: The Veolia Environmental Trust
Description: Since 1997 The Veolia Environmental Trust has supported community & environmental projects through the Landfill Communities Fund - #TheLCF

Name: The Big Event at Texas A&M University
Description:
Through service-oriented activities, The Big Event promotes campus and community unity as students come together for one day to express their gratitude for the support from the surrounding community. 

The Big Event at Texas A&M University is an organization which strives to uphold the ideals of unity and service. This one-day event is not based on socioeconomic need, but rather a way for the student body to express their gratitude to the entire community which supports Texas A&M. It is important to remember The Big Event is not about the number of jobs completed or the number of students who participate each year. Instead, it is the interaction between students, and residents and the unity that results throughout the community that makes The Big Event such a unique project.

Name: Aqueduct Foundation
Description:
Aqueduct Foundation is a Canadian public charitable foundation established in 2006, dedicated to facilitating personal philanthropy through individual charitable funds, including donor-advised funds, legacy funds, and charity funds. 

As one of Canada's largest grant makers, Aqueduct has granted $766 million to other registered charities since its inception and holds over $1 billion in assets. 

Aqueduct offers a range of fund mandates, from short-term immediate payouts to long-term endowments, supporting donors' diverse charitable priorities. Aqueduct also provides fundholders support with charitable programs, such as community-based scholarships art foundations, and ecologically sensitive land. 

Aqueduct's cause-neutral stance allows it to support any Canadian registered charity or qualified donee, providing flexibility and personalized service to donors nationwide. 

For more information, visit: https://aqueductfoundation.ca/mission-approach/

Name: Fonds de dotation Albert Marie
Description:
Le fonds de dotation Albert Marie, créé en 2020 par la 4ème génération de la famille Biotteau, famille fondatrice du Groupe Eram, s'inscrit pleinement dans les valeurs historiques du Groupe : simplicité, responsabilité et ouverture d'esprit.  
 
Le fonds de dotation Albert Marie s'inscrit dans l'évolution du Groupe, en accompagnant et finançant toute initiative d’intérêt général, à caractère philanthropique, portée par des enjeux sociaux, environnementaux ou territoriaux, pour agir en faveur d’un avenir durable et solidaire. 
 
Nous œuvrons aux côtés d'associations ayant pour objectifs : la solidarité, l'inclusion et l'éducation, la transition écologique et la dynamisation du territoire du Maine-et-Loire et de ses environs. Côté fonctionnement, nous lançons un à deux appel(s) à projet chaque année. En parallèle, nous développons également notre propres projets en lien avec des associations.

Name: Fund For Housing and Opportunity
Description: FHO is a cross-sector, non-partisan, nation-wide funder collaborative committed to bettering life outcomes for the 12 million households who are most precariously housed or homeless.  The collaborative seeks to ensure that housing security is an essential part of our nation’s efforts to improve family health, educational achievement, and economic mobility and thereby create stable pathways to opportunity.

Name: Lui Che Woo Prize for World Civilisation
Description:
The Lui Che Woo Prize for World Civilisation is an international award honouring individuals or organisations who contribute to world civilisation in the areas of sustainability of world resources, welfare of humankind, and positive energy. 

The Lui Che Woo Prize not only recognises laureates’ accomplishments and allows them to continue on their missions, but also inspires the global community to build a better world together. 

The vision for the Lui Che Woo Prize is to achieve a harmonious society by promoting the virtues of mutual understanding, compassion, and the exchange of knowledge.

Name: IBBY México
Description:
Somos una asociación civil mexicana que tiene como misión fomentar la lectura para contribuir al desarrollo de personas criticas y reflexivas. En IBBY México concebimos el acceso a la lectura como un derecho que debe estar garantizado a todas las personas, y con mayor énfasis para las niñas, niños y jóvenes.

Desde 1979, somos expertos en provocar encuentros gozosos con la lectura. Con base en nuestra experiencia y metodología, contribuimos a la formación de lectores autónomos. Porque la capacidad lectora de una persona, es crucial para el crecimiento personal y el cambio social.

Estamos afiliados a IBBY (International Board on Books for Young people) un colectivo fundado en 1953, al que actualmente están afiliadas más de 75 secciones nacionales, cuya misión es propiciar un mejor entendimiento entre los pueblos a través de la literatura infantil, así como garantizar el acercamiento de la infancia a los libros.

Cumplimos nuestra misión mediante la implementación de diversos programas como:
•	Abuelos Lectores y Cuentacuentos
•	Biblioteca BS-IBBY México/A leer
•	Bunkos (bibliotecas comunitarias)
•	Formación a mediadores de lectura (presencial y en línea)
•	ImaginaLee (www.imaginalee.org)
•	Programa de Inclusión (para atención a la diversidad y discapacidad)
•	Selección de acervos para niños y jóvenes

Name: Fondation de Gaspé Beaubien Foundation
Description:
Private Family Foundation

Our mandate: to help raise awareness on fresh water issues and help the development of effective solutions for water conservation

Our mission: Listening, exchanging and working together with the community to initiate sustainable actions and changes together

Name: TimePledge
Description:
Timepledge.org (TPO) is a global program helping emerging markets entrepreneurs in several domains of their businesses, from product to strategy to channels management to investors pitching.

We are building the largest free coaching and advisory platform entrepreneurs by providing mentorship opportunities based on pledged time.

Name: Refugee Hope Partners
Description: Refugee Hope Partners strives to engage, equip, and encourage our refugee neighbors so that all may thrive.

Name: The FedPROPEL Institute
Description:
1.7%.
Per Bloomberg Government Research, African American led firms receive 1.7% of federal contracting dollars. The numbers are similarly disappointing for Hispanic/Latinx community. Those firms receive 1.8% of contracts even though they represent 18% of America’s population. In summary, firms that represent nearly 33% of country’s population receive 3.5% of its contracts.
This disparity represents a significant disconnect in the system. The FedPROPEL Institute was created to change this. We provide firms from under resourced communities with the wherewithal to successfully access the world of Federal government contracting.

FedPROPEL Vision 2030
Over the next decade, the FedPROPEL Institute mission will assist 3,000 minority-led firms in becoming elite Federal Contractors securing more than $4.5 billion in contracts annually while creating thousands of jobs in their communities. By 2030, FedPROPEL projects its graduates will create 120,000 to 150,000 direct and indirect living wage jobs.

Why FedPROPEL exists
The Biden Administration has a goal of increasing federal contracting spend to small businesses by 50% by 2025 – with a focus on African American and Hispanic/Latinx-led firms. However, there isn’t a scalable solution to prepare minority entrepreneurs to access these opportunities. FedPROPEL fills that gap.

What FedPROPEL does
FedPROPEL uses a bifurcated model leveraging a web-based platform and extensive partner network, FedPROPEL prepares firms to perform at an elite level. Our interactive group training and individual consulting services deliver transformative organizational development. 

How FedPROPEL differs
The FedPROPEL Institute employs an innovative deferred cost solution. Firms will pay a nominal deposit, and FedPROPEL covers the rest of the costs until they win a contract. Our goal is to reduce the upfront investment necessary to win a federal contract by 18-months and $100,000 increasing our participants ability to invest in their companies and

Name: stiftung.ch
Description:
With many years of experience, stiftung.ch offers the following services to Non-Profits:

Consulting: legal and strategic advice in establishing and fusing foundations, tax exemption, etc.

Name: ENSEM ELKHIR-CIDH
Description:
ENSEM EL KHIR CIDH , le club d’initiative et de développement humain est créé en 2010 par des élèves ingénieurs de l'ENSEM passionnées par le monde du bénévolat .Dans le but de valoriser la formation de l’école et le parcours de ses diplômés surtout en ce qui concerne le coté  humaine et solidaire .Animées par un désir d’aide, de générosité notamment de responsabilité ce club a pu gagner la confiance des ENSEMIENNES et ENSEMIENS et les rendre toujours fières grâce à ses objectifs nobles.
Durant toutes l’année le club ENSEM EL KHIR CIDH, réalise plusieurs activités qui concernant le domaine du bénévolat. Ces évènements demandent des préparatifs intenses pour en élaborer des évènements et des activités à titres humanitaires et qui sont reliés au bénévolat qui sont sensés être  à la hauteur.

Name: Maimonides Fund
Description: Maimonides Fund is a private grantmaking organization.

Name: Punto Omega
Description:
Asociación Punto Omega es una Entidad Sin Fines Lucrativos constituida el 31 de mayo de 1984. Se dedica a combatir y prevenir la exclusión social, prestar servicios sociales, sanitarios y sociosanitarios, e impartir formación relacionada con un proceso de inserción social y laboral a colectivos en situación de vulnerabilidad social.

VALORES

Compromiso con los Derechos Humanos.

Solidaridad.

Igualdad de oportunidades.

Inclusión.

Participación Social.

Innovación.

Autonomía, tanto en la gestión como en la toma de decisiones.

Gestión responsable con el Medio Ambiente.

Transparencia en la gestión.

Excelencia en la gestión.

La Entidad nace del compromiso con los derechos humanos y descansa en los valores de la solidaridad, igualdad de oportunidades, inclusión y participación social. Son principios rectores básicos de la Entidad: la autonomía en la gestión, la participación democrática, la transparencia en la gestión, promover la igualdad de oportunidades y de trato así como la no discriminación entre todas las personas con especial atención al principio de igualdad de oportunidades entre hombres y mujeres. La Entidad impulsa el reconocimiento y el ejercicio de los derechos civiles, así como de los derechos económicos, sociales o culturales de las personas y grupos que sufren condiciones de vulnerabilidad o que se encuentran en situación o en riesgo de exclusión social.

Name: One Hundred Black Men
Description:
For over 60 years, One Hundred Black Men has worked with government, corporations, and community leaders to transform economic and social policy, as well as launch independent programs and organizations that serve low-income communities. Our members include leaders across the business, political, and non-profit sectors in New York. The organization provides scholarships, educational support, economic empowerment, mentoring, health and wellness initiatives and an overall voice for the African American community.

One Hundred Black Men began in NYC in 1963 as a non-profit organization of like-minded leaders who met to advocate for improvement in conditions in their communities. These men envisioned an organization that would capitalize on the collective power of community to address issues of inequities and to empower Black Americans to become agents of change. These visionaries were business and industry leaders such as David Dinkins, Robert Mangum, Cyril Tyson, J. Bruce Llewellyn, Livingston Wingate, Andrew Hatcher, and Jackie Robinson. They forever changed the face of social, economic and political empowerment.

One Hundred Black Men is proud to continue support for many initiatives including:

1) The school Eagle Academy for Young Men in the Bronx was founded by members of One Hundred Black Men, Inc. as a response to the dire crisis facing young boys. When the doors opened in 2004, it was the first all male public school to open in New York City in 30 years. In 2008, a second Eagle Academy opened in Brooklyn. Currently, six Eagle Academy schools are in operation serving over 3,000 students via the independent organization, Eagle Academy Foundation.

2) In 1986, members of the One Hundred Black Men helped found a national organization based on our model, 100 Black Men of America, Inc. which is based in Atlanta, Georgia, The organization has grown to a confederation of over 116 chapters located in the United States and abroad.

Name: Ian & Shirley Norman Foundation
Description:
The Foundation’s purpose is “To Encourage and Support Organisations that are Capable of Responding to Social and Ecological Opportunities and Challenges”

The Ian and Shirley Norman Foundation will provide support to projects that focus on:

•	Pillar 1 – Being Ecologically Sound

•	Pillar 2 – Protecting People from Harm

•	Pillar 3 – Walking Together with Traditional Owners

•	Pillar 4 – Empowering Communities

The following values are important to the Board and staff of the Ian and Shirley Norman Foundation:

•	Family - All families are unique and constructed differently – to belong to one is that sense of coming home and being with your people.

•	Integrity - In all our dealings we will listen, and we will be honest, principled and kind.

•	Innovation and Taking Risks - We understand that creating real change can require doing things differently – we are happy to walk out of step
•	Community Capacity Building - We want to work alongside communities in a way that is positive, empowering and grows active citizenship

The Ian and Shirley Norman Foundation will operate:

In Deep Engagement 
•	With communities
•	With individuals committed to change
•	With other Foundations and NFP
•	With government (local, state, federal)
•	With Universities and research institutes

Safely
•	Ensuring physical safety
•	Ensuring psychological safety
•	Ensuring cultural safety

•	With an Evidence Base
•	Researching beforehand
•	Measuring impact
•	Peer reviewing
•	Seeking and keeping good data

Sustainably
•	With a small environmental footprint – treading lightly on the planet
•	Ensuring community self-determination in all that we do

Name: Fundo Centenário
Description:
O Fundo Patrimonial Centenário é uma Associação sem Fins Lucrativos cujo objetivo é ser uma fonte de recursos perpétua, dedicada a apoiar projetos de ensino, pesquisa, extensão e inovação da Escola de Engenharia da Universidade Federal do Rio Grande do Sul (UFRGS). 

A Associação foi criada por ex-alunos com o objetivo de apoiar a instituição e é baseada pelas melhores práticas de governança e transparência adotadas por fundos patrimoniais no Brasil e no exterior. A iniciativa fomenta o pensamento de longo prazo no estado gaúcho e incentiva a participação da sociedade na universidade, um dos motores de desenvolvimento científico e econômico da região.

Conhecidos como endowment funds no exterior, os fundos patrimoniais são estruturas que garantem a perenidade e a sustentabilidade financeira de instituições. Esse mecanismo de financiamento capta doações para a formação de um principal e utiliza apenas seus rendimentos para custear investimentos de longo prazo, dando previsibilidade e garantindo a sustentabilidade da instituição apoiada.

Para conhecer mais, visite www.fundocentenario.com.br

Name: Real Academia Europea de Doctores
Description: La Real Academia Europea de Doctores se constituye como una corporación de Derecho Público que incorpora en su seno a eminentes científicos, quienes representan la vanguardia del conocimiento en diversos campos.

Name: Fondazione Pierfranco e Luisa Mariani
Description:
The Pierfranco and Luisa Mariani Foundation was established on 18 June 1984 by Luisa Toffoloni Mariani in memory of her husband, a well-known businessman from Milan. The Foundation is a non-profit organization with legal status, its registered offices are in Milan, and it was granted legal recognition at national level.

Its core mission is child neurology and the major areas of expertise include:
• Services
• Training
• Research

The Statute singles out the “Carlo Besta” Neurological Institute of Milan as the privileged partner of the Foundation for its activities of training and project management and implementation.

Transparency, subsidiarity, efficiency, and effectiveness are the fundamental principles underlying the whole range of actions taken by the Mariani Foundation.

Name: Johnson Family Foundation
Description:
JFF funds programs to improve the health of our environment; promote equality and social progress; and support education and youth.

Our grantmaking is place-based and, in LGBT issues, national in scope.

Name: Portland Israel
Description: At Portland Israel, we aim to expand the geographic and social boundaries of the Israeli tech industry, by challenging talent acquisition biases, creating new opportunities for employment and entrepreneurship, and developing the infrastructure that will provide access into the industry for tech professionals from Israel’s underserved communities.

Name: Connecticut Bar Foundation
Description: Connecticut Bar Foundation is a nonprofit philanthropic organization that provides funding to legal service organizations that represent Connecticut residents who cannot afford an attorney. Since 1952, the Foundation has also worked in partnership with judges, attorneys, and law schools to promote understanding and improvement of the law, legal institutions, and the legal profession.

Name: Association La Gerbe
Description:
La Gerbe est une ONG à caractère humanitaire et social qui intervient sur le plan national et international, sans distinction de race, de sexe, de religion ou d'appartenance politique.  La Gerbe est actuellement très impliquée dans l'aide en #Ukraine https://www.lagerbe.org/ukraine avec de spartenaires depuis 2009.

Elle a pour vocation d'aider les personnes en difficulté qui se retrouvent à un moment de leur vie exclues de la société.

Un des support d'action est la Ressourcerie d'Ecquevilly : http://Ressourcerie.lagerbe.org

Name: iGiftFund
Description:
The iGiftFund is an IRS-recognized independent public charity that sponsors donor-advised funds.  Unique by design, our program works the way you work, on any custodial platform, with open investment architecture – free of any conflicts of interest.

iGiftFund donor-advised funds are designed to be simple, yet flexible for sophisticated planning.

For Advisors: 

Financial advisors serve an important role in planning, investment management, and wealth transition at iGiftFund. 

At iGiftFund, you help your clients establish the investment account inside the donor-advised fund; you manage the investments on your preferred platform; donors provide the vision, succession, and the grant advice … and we take care of the rest.
 
In short, we become “joint stewards” in helping you develop and carry out your client’s charitable legacy, both now and in the future.

For Donors:

Our donors are typically individuals and multi-generational families located throughout the United States. We work with: Individuals, Families, Financial advisors, Attorneys, Accountants, Family offices, Private foundations, Trust companies, Financial institutions, Corporate foundations.

Whether you want to start with just yourself, or include your family and/or trusted advisors, you’ll work directly with our experienced founders. We’re happy to communicate in your preferred method… phone, email, chat, conference call, in person, or through some combination of these.

https://igiftfund.org/donors/independent-platform/
https://igiftfund.org/donors/investments/

Name: PHILANTHROPI
Description:
Philanthropi is leading the next generation of philanthropy through its innovative giving platform, which democratizes giving for everyone and amplifies its impact. 

By partnering with financial institutions, employers, universities, and non-profit organizations, Philanthropi fosters new levels of engagement and empowers everyday donors to support their favorite non-profit organizations with the click of a button. 

Tapping into the largest donor segment – the everyday philanthropist – Philanthropi unlocks the potential of the charitable ecosystem.

Name: Backpack Buddies BC
Description:
For over 6,000 families, Backpack Buddies provides a weekly bag of food including meals and snacks to feed children the weekend and beyond.

We tackle the crisis of child hunger in B.C. While vulnerable students may take advantage of school-based meal programs during the week, few supports exist on weekends, when many kids suffer in secret. Our community of donors, volunteers and caring partners address this crisis by discreetly providing bags of food to students in need every Friday, with enough meals and snacks to last the entire weekend. Backpack Buddies reaches out to hungry kids across B.C. with the food they need to learn and grow.

Name: SEIplus
Description:
SEIplus is a nonprofit organization that aims to close the gap between young talents, innovative startups and companies, in order to enhance and spread opportunities.  

How? By developing tailor-made projects for companies, supporting startups in all phases of development and organizing network sharing and events.

SEIplus wants to become a reference point for all those who want to bet on the spirit of youth initiative, in order to attract the best opportunities and make them available to the most deserving.

The team is always looking for passionate, enthusiastic members from all study fields, willing to grow and improve their soft skills.

Name: Black Excellence Inc
Description:
Mission Statement: 

Our mission is to raise awareness of the community resources available to minority businesses while promoting diversity and inclusion through strategic partnerships


Vision Statement: 

Black Excellence KC (BXKC) strives to sustain and retain longevity with resources in our community while contributing towards a future for Black Kansas City.


Executive Summary: Call To Action

BXKC efforts will be used to highlight areas of economic engagement, philanthropy, and awareness to individuals in their perspective fields. BXKC will provide advancement, partnerships, and accessibility towards a collective pool of resources already existing within the black ecosystem of the greater Kansas City area. BXKC will contribute towards moving forward our minority community while creating a lasting impact that will reach many by bringing together to celebrate forthcoming and upcoming initiatives in our community.

Name: TOOLS FOR LIFE Foundation
Description:
Sauberes Trinkwasser und eine funktionierende Energieversorgung sind in vielen Teilen der Erde immer noch keine Selbstverständlichkeit. Ohne funktionierende Rohre und Stromleitungen, die vergleichbar mit den Arterien im menschlichen Körper, Wasser und Energie zu den Menschen bringen, ist ein menschenwürdiges Leben nicht möglich. Sehr oft fehlen Mittel und Bildung, um die teilweise katastrophalen Zustände zu verbessern. 
Vor diesem Hintergrund hat Dr. Helmut Rothenberger 2008 die Stiftung TOOLS FOR LIFE ins Leben gerufen, die sich heute in der Tradition ihres Stifters für das nachhaltige Gemeinwohl unserer Gesellschaft engagiert. 
TOOLS FOR LIFE hat sich zum Ziel gesetzt, Menschen in allen Regionen der Erde mit den essentiellen Ressourcen zu verbinden, die für ein gesundes Leben notwendig sind. Dies bezieht die materielle Versorgung mit Energie und Wasser als auch die Erschließung immaterieller Ressourcen wie Bildung, Training und Coaching mit ein. So hat TOOLS FOR LIFE in ausgewählten Regionen ein Netzwerk aus Organisationen und lokalen Partnern geschaffen, das in den letzten Jahren mehrere Bildungsstätten und Wasserversorgungsnetze gebaut hat und bis heute betreibt und Instand hält.

Name: Energy Assistance France
Description:
HISTORY
Energy Assistance (EA) was created in Belgium in 2001 by employees of Tractebel, Electrabel and Fabricom. Today, EA brings together volunteers (both retired employees and currently active staff) from the energy business lines of the ENGIE Group and from the Belgian grid operators.

OUR OBJECTIVES
The objective of the association is to offer the skills and expertise of its members to give technical support to humanitarian projects that serve communities with little to no access to energy all over the world

MAIN FIELD OF ACTIVITY
Health, education, economic development.

CHAIN OF KNOW HOW
Study, purchasing, logistic, installation, training …

TYPICAL PROJECTS
Off-grid electricity generation • Connection to the grid of isolated communities • Upgrading electrical installation

Name: Scharbauer Foundation, Inc.
Description: Scharbauer Foundation, Inc. is a private charitable foundation that works to make Midland a better place in which to live and operate a business.  Established in 2011, the Foundation prioritizes projects that make significant impacts within its four focus areas of education, healthcare, quality of place, and human services.

Name: The Memorial Foundation
Description: The Memorial Foundation, Inc. exists to promote awareness of the Martin Luther King, Jr. Memorial located in Washington, DC and its tenets of democracy, justice, hope, and love as well as to support the general upkeep of the Memorial in future years.

Name: Forest Interactive Foundation
Description:
DEVELOPING FUTURE INNOVATORS

Part of our commitment at Forest Interactive Foundation is to establish and uphold empowered communities and sustainable environments, including our own people and the world’s future innovators in tech. Since 2006, we have embarked on an important journey as a company to create positive impacts not only to our business but to society as a whole.

OUR PILLARS

Forest Interactive joined the UN Global Compact corporate responsibility initiative and its principles in 2017 in our commitment to the following areas:

Future Innovators
Curious students today, tech pioneers tomorrow. We facilitate programs and initiatives that harness the potential of digital talents, supporting their growth into next-gen leaders and innovators.

Empowered Communities
We believe in the power of technology to transform lives. By pooling together our strengths for the greater good, we aim to empower marginalized communities and reduce inequalities in our increasingly connected world.

Sustainable Environment
We invest in the future - both our people and the planet. We are committed to raising awareness and driving action for sustainable best practices that protect the environment and its resources.
Location: Kuala Lumpur, Federal Territory of Kuala Lumpur

Name: Women in Industry Day
Description:
On April 17, 2026 the Society of Women Engineers (SWE) Lowcountry Chapter will host the eighth annual Women in Industry Day, an in-person conference designed to connect professional women from a variety of industries across the Lowcountry (including but not limited to, manufacturing, infrastructure, software, medical, and facilities fields) and aims to help participants feel brave, powerful, and heard in their personal and professional lives.

Charleston's Women in Industry Day is an annual event focused on providing professional development opportunities through networking and education. Stay tuned for more information on this year's event!

Follow this page for updates including:
- Sponsorship Opportunities
- Speaker Announcements
- Registration Information
...and more!

Please join us for this dynamic day devoted to growing and supporting women in industry throughout the Lowcountry.

Name: Foundation for Developmental Disabilities
Description: The Foundation for Developmental Disabilities provides essential funding for innovative programs that serve individuals with intellectual and developmental disabilities in San Diego and Imperial counties.

Name: Association of Fundraising Professionals of Southeastern Wisconsin (AFP SEWI)
Description:
Our Mission
AFP – Southeastern Wisconsin Chapter develops fundraising professionals, advances the profession, and inspires a culture of philanthropy in our communities.

Our Chapter Goals
Active membership in AFP-SEWI is recognized as a vital component of professional development for Southeastern Wisconsin fundraisers
     - AFP-SEWI is considered the fundraising expert in Southeastern Wisconsin.
     - Diversity exists in all aspects of the chapter.
     - Highly effective operations ensure the sustainability of the chapter
 
AFP International
The Southeastern Wisconsin Chapter is one of 230 chapters of the Association of Fundraising Professionals, the largest association of professional fundraisers in the world.  Each year, our chapter submits documentation to AFP to certify our compliance with association standards.  In fact, our chapter has received the AFP 10 Star Award for many years, for activities we run that increase professionalism within fundraising and public awareness of the importance of philanthropy.  All AFP chapters are first and foremost communities that foster the development and growth of fundraising professionals and promote high ethical standards in the fundraising profession. 

To learn more about AFP worldwide, please visit the AFP website at www.afpnet.org

Name: Graham Boeckh Foundation
Description:
GBF is a private foundation created by J. Anthony Boeckh and his family in 1990. The Graham Boeckh Foundation (GBF) is committed to improving the lives of young people across Canada. Our mission is to support initiatives that create a functional and organized mental health care framework, ensuring early 
intervention and prevention is prioritized.

We improve mental health outcomes by prioritizing three main areas:
Transforming the Youth Mental Health system
Catalyzing Community-Driven Mental Health Advancements
Fostering Collaboration and Knowledge Exchange

Name: Alaska Conservation Foundation
Description:
Founded in 1980, Alaska Conservation Foundation is the only foundation in Alaska and the country solely dedicated to the protection of Alaska’s natural environment and the diverse cultures and ways of life it sustains. We drive long-term conservation impact by leading critical initiatives, deploying focused grants and providing strategic support where it’s needed most to ultimately protect the magnificence of Alaska for today and the future.

Our leadership and investment are made possible by the generosity of individual donors, foundations and businesses from around the country who share our passion for the Great Land. We serve as a conduit between these generous people and a diverse community of nonprofit and tribal organizations working across the state on conservation and environmental justice.

Name: One for Democracy
Description: One for Democracy unlocks resources to build a healthy democracy by fostering meaningful relationships between philanthropists and the democracy leaders of our time.

Name: Northern New Jersey Community Foundation
Description: The mission of the Northern New Jersey Community Foundation (NNJCF) is to improve community life through its work in the environment, the arts/culture,education, public health, education, civic engagement, and philanthropy. Central to its success is fostering collaboration among local governments, school districts, businesses, non-profits, and citizen groups. Our partners are building stronger communities in Northern New Jersey by answering the question, “What can we do together that we can't do alone?”

Name: Anschutz Family Foundation
Description: Anschutz Family Foundation is a philanthropy company based out of 555 17Th St, Denver, CO, United States.

Name: Trøndelagsstiftelsen
Description: Stiftelsen har som formål å underbygge og synliggjøre Trøndelag som viktig base for kapitalforvaltning, eierskapsutøvelse og hovedkontor både i privat og offentlig sektor. Virkemidler for å realisere formålet er å bestille rapporter, utredninger, analyser, studentoppgaver, avhandlinger eller andre målrettete, konkrete tiltak som kan styrke Trøndelag som viktig kapital-, beslutnings- og kompetansesenter.

Name: Iraqi Children Foundation (ICF)
Description: Iraqi Children Foundation (ICF) intervenes in the lives of orphans and street children who are vulnerable to abuse, neglect and exploitation by criminals, traffickers, and extremists.

Name: Equation Charity
Description:
Equation’s goal as a charity is for everyone to have equal, healthy relationships, in a society free from domestic abuse, sexual violence and gender inequality. 

Working primarily in Nottingham and Nottinghamshire, Equation is a specialist charity with expertise to work with the whole community to reduce the impact of domestic abuse, sexual violence and gender inequality. We ensure survivors get the most effective support tocreate space for action by facilitating a co-ordinated approach across the sector and providing practical tools and guidance to support survivors.We also work to prevent domestic abuseand sexual violence by raising awareness and educating people about equal and healthy relationships.

Name: Charitable Fund "The Expendables"
Description: We close inquiries of Ukrainian Army

Name: IPAGES - Instituto Paulista de Apoio à Gestão da Saúde Pública
Description:
Instituição do terceiro setor sem fins lucrativos que tem o objetivo de apoiar a modernização da administração pública, especialmente na área da Saúde Pública.

Nossa organização conta com reconhecidos profissionais no mercado que desenvolvem auditorias, serviços de consultoria, assessoria e gestão de profissionais.

Realizamos também a oferta de suporte e ferramentas para o desenvolvimento dos instrumentos de gestão adequadas na condução da gestão da saúde pública nos diferentes níveis de complexidade da esfera municipal.

Name: Made in St Gallen Initiative
Description: Die «Made in St Gallen Initiative» ist ein Verein, welcher Macherinnen und Macher sowie produzierende Betriebe jeder Couleur, vom KMU bis zum Konzern, vereinigt und dem Kanton St. Gallen als Produktionsstandort ein Gesicht geben will. Für Produkte, die im Kanton St. Gallen hergestellt werden, schafft er mit «Made in St Gallen» ausserdem eine offizielle Herkunftsbezeichnung.

Name: Mandel Institute for Nonprofit Leadership
Description:
The Mandel Institute for Nonprofit Leadership empowers leaders to achieve visions of social, cultural, and educational change through three core programs: the Executive Leadership Program, the Educational Leadership Program, and the Cultural Leadership Program. 

Graduates of Mandel Institute programs participate in continuing educational, training and mentoring activities sponsored by the Mandel Institute for Nonprofit Leadership.

For more information, please visit our website: http://mandelinstitute.org/

The Jack, Joseph and Morton Mandel Foundation covers travel and program expenses. Sponsoring organizations provide release time for participation in the program seminars.

Name: FEEA - Federal Employee Education & Assistance Fund
Description: The Federal Employee Education and Assistance Fund (FEEA) is the only independent, nonproﬁt 501c3 organization devoted solely to providing emergency ﬁnancial assistance and scholarships to our dedicated civilian federal and postal public servants and their families. FEEA also has a for-profit subsidiary, FEEA Childcare Services, which administers childcare subsidy programs on behalf of a number of federal agencies.

Name: The Community Foundation of Frederick County
Description:
Welcome to The Community Foundation of Frederick County. We are an organization that works with those who live in Frederick County. We act on behalf of those citizens who love this amazing area, and along side those who want to ensure we leave a legacy of continued prosperity for generations to come. And this all starts by joining together to develop solutions, build hope, and create meaningful, lasting change.

Our mission is simple. The Community Foundation is dedicated to connecting people who care with causes that matter to enrich the quality of life in Frederick County now and for future generations.

Name: Fondation Philanthropique Next
Description:
Une philanthropie concrète, responsable et mesurable pour impacter positivement des vies sur le long terme.

Nous avons la volonté d’être actifs mais pas activistes, et d’avoir une approche professionnelle.

Name: Or Bleu
Description:
OR BLEU est une organisation humanitaire à but non lucratif qui agit pour l'accès à l'eau potable des populations les plus démunies partout dans le monde. 
--
OR BLEU is a non-profit humanitarian organization that improves access to safe drinking water to all.

Name: Espero Organization
Description:
Espero is a non-profit organization created in 2016 to respond to social and environmental dilemmas. Our mission is to reconcile People and Nature. 
The objective is three-fold: develop projects that favor social and professional integration in the field of sustainability (namely apiculture, and permaculture); develop an awareness of the civil society on environmental and social issues; and finally, protect and enrich the Paris region's biodiversity.

Name: bringkids2schools
Description: Together with YOU we enable education by building schools in the worlds poorest countries.

Name: Athletes for Hope Australia
Description:
Athletes for Hope Australia (AFHA) was established in 2020 by co-founders Laura Johnston and Iain Roy. AFHA is the first international chapter of Athletes for Hope, a non-profit organisation that was founded in the United States in 2007 by Andre Agassi, Muhammad Ali, Warrick Dunn, Jeff Gordon, Mia Hamm, Tony Hawk, Andrea Jaeger, Jackie Joyner-Kersee, Mario Lemieux, Alonzo Mourning and Cal Ripken Jr. As athletes who shared a deep commitment to giving back, the US founders joined forces to create an organisation that brings athletes together to educate, inspire, and empower them to channel their energy for a common goal: to make a difference in the world through sports philanthropy.

In Australia, AFHA is building a community of purpose-led athletes from all pathway stages, who want to create impact for the causes and charities they care about. We empower athletes with the knowledge and networks they need to make a meaningful contribution with impact, with ongoing learning and support through the AFHA community.

Our vision is to create an athlete-driven movement that creates social impact.

Our mission is to empower a community of purpose-led athletes to contribute with impact to causes, charities and communities.

AFHA relies on funding to deliver programs for athletes, sporting organisations and  community organisations.  Please contact us through our website www.athletesforhope.org.au to explore opportunities to support and partner with us.

Name: Women in Industry Day
Description:
On May 9, 2025 the Society of Women Engineers (SWE) Lowcountry Chapter will host the seventh annual Women in Industry Day, an in-person conference designed to connect professional women from a variety of industries across the Lowcountry (including but not limited to, manufacturing, infrastructure, software, medical, and facilities fields) and aims to help participants feel brave, powerful, and heard in their personal and professional lives.

Charleston's Women in Industry Day is an annual event focused on providing professional development opportunities through networking and education. Stay tuned for more information on this year's event!

Follow this page for updates including:
- Sponsorship Opportunities
- Speaker Announcements
- Registration Information
...and more!

Please join us for this dynamic day devoted to growing and supporting women in industry throughout the Lowcountry.

Name: Portsmouth Creates CIC
Description: Portsmouth Creates is a Community Interest Company (CIC), based in the heart of the city. We support the creative and cultural sector across Portsmouth, collaborating with a wide range of partners and using diverse approaches to strengthen both the city’s cultural landscape and its wider growth.

Name: Stand Together Trust
Description:
We believe that America’s challenges are best addressed by bottom-up solutions that revitalize the key institutions of society: business, communities, education, and government. We partner with social entrepreneurs with passion and personal knowledge in their subject area and deep belief in the dignity of all people. We seek out partners who share our vision of a society based on equal rights, mutual benefit, openness, and self-actualization. 

We have a holistic view of partnership — we come alongside social entrepreneurs and ask how we can empower them. This could be in the form of a transformational grant or a talent partnership with our educational programs. But our support doesn’t stop there.

Name: anaSudan / اناسودان
March for Science
Description: The March for Science champions robustly funded and publicly communicated science as a pillar of human freedom and prosperity. We unite as a diverse, nonpartisan group to call for science that upholds the common good and for political leaders and policy makers to enact evidence based policies in the public interest.

Name: Sofia Business Community
Description:
Sofia Business Community is a non-profit organization established in 2011! It provides business networking opportunities in Sofia, Bulgaria!

Our regular networking events and website are places where: business owners, entepreneurs, freelancers and professionals meet and share their experience and ideas, discuss business problems and find solutions for them.

Name: Epsilon Nu Tau Fraternity, Inc.
Description: Established in 2008, Epsilon Nu Tau is the Nation's First Entrepreneurship Fraternity.  ENT is a coed, professional organization, committed to create a society of entrepreneurial individuals, advance the ideals of professionalism & entrepreneurship, promote the art and science of salesmanship, and encourage ethical business practices within the community.

Name: Asociația Niciodată Singur - Prietenii Vârstnicilor
Description:
Niciodată Singur este despre „a fi împreună”: împreună în cauza singurătății vârstnicilor, împreună pentru demnitate la orice vârstă, împreună prin voluntariat și implicare, pentru un impact de durată în viața bătrânilor noștri.

Povestea a început în 2006, ca program dezvoltat de Fundația Regală Margareta a României pentru sprijinul vârstnicilor afectați de izolare socială și sărăcie. În plus, parteneriatul cu Asociația Les Petits Frères des Pauvres (Franța) ne-a echipat cu metode noi de mobilizare voluntară și instrumente de combatere a singurătății.

Astăzi, cu ajutorul voluntarilor, suntem alături de vârstnicii singuri din București, Brăila, Pitești, Ploiești, Târgoviște, Râmnicu Vâlcea, Constanța, Iași și Cluj-Napoca

Name: Huntsman Cancer Foundation
Description: Huntsman Cancer Foundation’s sole purpose is to raise funds to support the mission of Huntsman Cancer Institute (HCI):  To understand cancer from its beginnings, to use that knowledge in the creation and improvement of cancer treatments, to relieve the suffering of cancer patients, and to provide education about cancer risk, prevention, and care.  Huntsman Cancer Foundation is dedicated to ensuring excellence in these endeavors through the development and prudent stewardship of private resources.

Name: Sedarvp Ghana
Description:
SEDARVP-Ghana is a non-profit Youth Focused United Nation ECOSOC Consultative status Accredited Organization setup in 2008 with Registration number CG152072015 and also social welfare registration number D.S.W./7292. The organization is set up to create supportive platforms to challenge youth creativity in the areas of Human Rights, Environmental protection, Agriculture and Food Security, Youth and Culture, Health, Population and Drug Abuse, General Humanitarian Aid, Small-Scale Enterprise Development, Advocacy/Research/Policy, Education/Vocal Skills, Volunteering and community development
The GOAL of the organization is to Build a Sustainable Society with Equitable Representation, Vibrant Economy, and access to Quality Education, Health and Nutrition, Food Security and Sound Environment within its communities in Ghana and beyond. These would enable communities uplift their economic activities, access clean and potable water, quality education, nutrition, and healthcare, and provide the foundations for local businesses to flourish.
SEDARVP-GHANA has a VISION of achieving “A SOCIETY WHERE EVERYONE REGARDLESS OF THEIR BACKGROUND LIVES A VERY DIGNIFIED AND FULFILLING LIFE IN A SAFE AND HEALTHY ENVIRONMENT” by year 2040.
The organization seek to fulfill this vision by committing to its MISSION “TO CREATE A HEALTHY, SUSTAINABLE AND ECOFRIENDLY ENVIRONMENT BY EMPOWERING THE YOUTH, WOMEN AND CHILDREN THROUGH ACTIVE COMMUNITY PARTICIPATION AND INTERNATIONAL PARTNERSHIP”
SEDARVP-GHANA in partnership with local and international organizations has provided diverse support to many communities in Ghana, through its project implementation and volunteerism.
