Some international projects include: Community-based Youth work and Sustainable Development in EU and Africa, Inclusion on Stage Project, Innovative Youth for a Sustainable Entrepreneurship, Youth Workers United towards an Intercultural Citizenship, Youth Social Entrepreneurship Education for All with Moodle, ETC.

Name: Vincent de Paul Belgium
Description:
Vincent de Paul Belgium is a movement of 275 local associations for social inclusion in the heart of Belgian towns, districts and villages. It also stands for 6.500 volunteers who offer practical solutions to more than 150,000 people in need, all with the help of private donors.

The parent company was founded in 1833 and numbers about 800,000 members in some 150 countries worldwide. Members operate through local "conferences", helping 30 million people in the world (through emergency aid, development, education, rebuilding,serving, sharing, supporting or visiting). 

The Belgian branch relies on 275 local associations and6,000 volunteers helping destitute people in Belgium. The national council acts as supporting and strategic entity for that network.

Name: Fondazione SeSa
Description: Fondazione SeSa nasce dalla consapevolezza di chi non solo è capace di guardare avanti ma sa anche, e soprattutto, guardarsi intorno. È con questi presupposti che la Fondazione è stata creata da Paolo Castellacci e Piero Pelagotti: ideata come strumento dell’agire in favore dei dipendenti del Gruppo SeSa, del territorio, dei giovani, delle famiglie e di chi ha bisogno di un aiuto o di un sostegno. La sua missione è svolgere attività di solidarietà sociale per l'educazione, la ricerca scientifica, l'istruzione, l'assistenza sociale e sanitaria.

Name: 52 Lives
Description:
52 Lives is an award-winning charity that aims to change a life every week of the year and spread kindness, with the help of almost 100,000 supporters. We also run a School of Kindness, providing free Kindness Workshops and Assemblies in primary schools for more than 50,000 children every year and developing educational resources.

www.52-lives.org
www.schoolofkindness.org
www.facebook.com/52lives 
www.twitter.com/52lives
www.Instagram.com/52.lives

Name: Society Of Autism Families
Description: Nonprofit organization supporting lives of autistic individuals and their families

Name: Bolster Community
Description:
Bolster Community (www.bolstercommunity.org) is a practical, hands-on charity and social enterprise that works alongside individuals, families, and communities to improve health and wellbeing.

Through our charity services, we’ve had the privilege of helping countless children, parents, young people with mixed ability and seniors to reach their full potential. We support ordinary people facing extraordinary challenges from early years right through to later years. With an uncompromising, people-first approach, we go the extra mile to complement existing services. 

In our social enterprise, our products are lovingly handcrafted by young adults of mixed ability and all profits go back into supporting our vital work. Shop and support us to do more good: www.bolstercommunity.org/shop

Name: Toronto Cat Rescue
Description:
Our mission is to rescue and find homes for abandoned, sick or injured cats from situations of abuse, neglect or imminent euthanasia. We provide the highest quality of care and treatment for all the cats at TCR with experience and compassion.

Our vision is to make Toronto a city where every adoptable cat knows what it means to be loved. By acting with integrity and accountability, we hope to one day make Toronto a place where organizations like us are no longer needed.
TCR relies on volunteers to foster cats in their homes until a cat lover adopts them. Having a network of foster homes allows every cat in our care to be part of someone’s home. It gives a rescued cat a chance to adapt to living with a family, sometimes with other cats, dogs and/or children.

Name: MitoAction
Description:
MitoAction is a nonprofit organization founded by patients, parents, and Boston hospital healthcare leaders who had a vision of improving quality of life for children and adults with mitochondrial disease. The organization began in 2005 as an idea and has evolved from a small New England support group to a dynamic, active service organization helping thousands of patients and families. Despite the growth of the organization, the mission remains the same.

MitoAction’s mission is to improve the quality of life for children, adults, and families living with mitochondrial disease through support, education, outreach, advocacy, clinical research initiatives and by granting wishes for children affected by mitochondrial disease.

To do this, MitoAction’s goals include:

*To improve quality of life for adults and children affected by mitochondrial disease.
*To internationally raise awareness about mitochondrial disorders, and their relationship to other diseases.
*To provide specific and practical materials that help patients to manage their symptoms
*To aggregate and connect the international mitochondrial disease community
*To engage pharmaceutical industry working toward therapeutic approaches to mitochondrial disorders.
*To create tools which empower patients and caregivers to be advocates for themselves or their children.
*To create unique experiential opportunities for adults and children affected by mitochondrial disease.

We strive to offer comprehensive, up-to-date, expert resources, information, and support at no cost to any patient, family, clinician, educator, or member of the community.

We continue to seek multiple ways to offer information such that the resources are accessible and user-friendly for people of all abilities.

We foster relationships with the scientific, academic, research and business community in order to create sustainable support for the mission of MitoAction.

Name: PyroTalks CIC
Description: PyroTalks is a Community Interest Company that delivers knowledge to the not for profit sector.

Name: The Associates of the Asper School of Business
Description:
The Associates are senior-level executives and leaders who share the Asper School of Business’ commitment to excellence in management education, and represent a spectrum of industries and backgrounds. Over the past 37 years, the School has evolved to meet the needs of the local and national business community with the help of the Associates through their advisement, philanthropic contribution, mentoring, and many other types of involvement.

Throughout the year, The Associates participate in professional development and networking events, strengthening connections between members, the Asper School of Business, and the business community at large. The Associates host the annual International Distinguished Entrepreneur Award, celebrating the achievements of globally recognized entrepreneurs.

To learn more about our organization, please visit our website.

Name: Florence Crittenton Services of Colorado
Description:
Florence Crittenton Services pioneered a nationally recognized trauma-responsive two-generation program model that helps teen families break the cycle of poverty through education, health and wellness, and economic and social asset building.

Our campus is a full-service family resource center that includes an Early Childhood Education (ECE) Center, the Alethia E. Morgan, MD Health Center, and, through our 36-year partnership with Denver Public Schools, the on-site Florence Crittenton High School. On our new campus, we have extended our programming to offer services for teen families year round.

We are part of the Ascend Network, a prestigious group of two-generation approach leaders and organizations working to influence practice and policy change, and we were instrumental in establishing the Colorado Teen Parent Collaborative, a statewide group of organizations which raises public awareness, shares resources, and advocates for public policy to benefit teen parents and their children.

Our mission is to educate, prepare, and empower teen mothers and their children. We break the cycle of poverty for two generations–teen mother and child–through education, health and wellness, and economic and social asset building. Our trauma-informed, two-generation service model more than doubles the national high school graduation rate of teen mothers and ensures that their children are kindergarten-ready.

Name: The CASS Foundation
Description: CASS is an acronym for ‘Contributing to Australian Scholarship and Science’. Working to support and promote the advancement of education, science and medicine, and research and practice in those fields. CASS is also the Trustee of The Walter Mangold Trust Fund.

Name: Fondazione Caript
Description: Fondazione Caript è un ente privato senza scopo di lucro che opera per il benessere, la valorizzazione e la crescita del proprio territorio di riferimento. Muovendosi all'interno di 5 aree di intervento - Cultura, Educazione, Salute Pubblica, Sviluppo, Volontariato - la Fondazione non solo sostiene le attività di soggetti terzi attraverso la pubblicazione di bandi specifici, ma realizza progetti in autonomia o in partenariato con altre istituzioni.

Name: EALgreen
Description:
At EAL, our mission is to act as a catalyst of transformation. We work with corporations to transform their excess and landfill-bound inventory into opportunities for institutions of higher learning to obtain much-needed equipment. These colleges and universities then create scholarships for needy students to make it possible for them to pursue their own, personal transformation. The result: companies reduce waste, schools thrive, students prosper, society is strengthened and the environment is preserved. This is the transformation that inspires us. Together with our partners, EAL makes it possible. #fundingfutures

EAL + Corporate Donors = Transformation & Triple Impact

Name: Bicicletas sin Fronteras
Description:
Somos una pequeña gran familia de personas motivadas para hacer gestos que mejoren nuestro mundo.

Entregamos bicicletas desde 2012 a personas, entidades y colectivos con el objectivo de mejorar el acceso a la educación, a la integración y al desarrollo de sus integrantes.

Desarrollamos proyectos en los territorios donde trabajamos que sean viables, sostenibles a largo plazo y autogestionables.

Nos financiamos con donaciones de fondos procedentes de campañas lideradas por nuestros colaboradores y de donaciones de material nuevo (bicicletas) procedentes de empresas que nos apoyan.

https://linktr.ee/BicicletassinFronteras

Name: Engenheiros Sem Fronteiras - Núcleo Divinópolis
Description:
O “Engenheiros Sem Fronteiras” é uma ação mundial que busca promover projetos e intervenções, ambientais e sociais, não somente na área da engenharia, mas sim, na multidisciplinaridade das necessidades humanas. O termo “Engenheiros” tem o sentido de construtores de uma nova forma de pensar e agir em relação ao desenvolvimento sustentável da humanidade; e o termo “Sem Fronteiras” tem o sentido do rompimento das barreiras que impedem esta nova forma de percepção.

A ideia do núcleo de Divinópolis surgiu na Universidade Federal de Lavras, onde um estudante de mestrado e também professor universitário na UEMG e Pitágoras, ambas em Divinópolis, teve seu primeiro contato com o núcleo de Lavras e consequentemente sua participação no I Congresso Nacional – ESF, em agosto de 2014, onde visualizou o caminho para estruturar um núcleo em Divinópolis. Surgiu então, no dia 21 de setembro de 2014 o “Engenheiros Sem Fronteiras – Núcleo Divinópolis”.

O ano de 2014 foi finalizado com o projeto, vindo do núcleo de Viçosa, “Sabão Ecológico” e com o evento social na Praça da Igreja Matriz com o nomeado biólogo Richard Rasmussen. No ano de 2015, o núcleo realizou nacomunidade rural de Branquinhos o projeto “Comunidade Sustentável”, onde buscou melhorias tanto às pessoas quanto ao meio ambiente. Foram elaborados e executados os subprojetos de sustentabilidade específicos, transformando a localidade em uma comunidade modelo para as demais localidades do município e região. Já no ano de 2016, o núcleo disseminou a essência do projeto “Comunidade Sustentável” em escolas periféricas do município, através da ação “Projetando a Sustentabilidade”, mostrando os resultados e ensinando os alunos e pais a incorporarem a sustentabilidade nas escolas e nas comunidades periféricas. Os projetos “Comunidade Sustentável” e “Projetando a Sustentabilidade” foram premiados como “Projeto Destaque” em 2015 e 2016 consecutivamente, no Congresso Nacional – ESF.

Name: NinetyToZero
Description:
Centuries of structural racism have led to a 90% racial wealth gap between Black and white families. With collective action, we can eliminate it, so that all American families can thrive. 

NinetyToZero provides a roadmap for companies and organizations to drive change now, and acts as an engine for continued collaborative learning and growth. Partner companies and organizations - across all sectors of our national economy - commit to a set of seven expert-driven, needle-moving actions to advance racial equity by growing Black talent and investing in Black businesses.

It’s more than a zero-sum game. When Black families have the means for economic security and agency, everyone benefits: it’s estimated that closing the racial wealth gap could increase the US GDP by $1.5 Trillion over the next 10 years, according to "The Economic Impact of Closing the Racial Wealth Gap" by McKinsey & Co.

To close the gap, we need deliberate, collective action to counteract the centuries of discrimination, segregation, and financial exploitation. We need all of the players in our economy wielding all of their influence and power.

Now is a time to be bold, transparent, and reflective. 

We’re asking national organizations across all sectors to join NinetyToZero as partners and turn this once-in-a-generation moment into a generation-defining movement.

Name: Collier Community Foundation
Description: We optimize your charitable giving in Collier County and beyond. Informed giving. Powerful results.

Name: Tilbudskriger
Description:
I en travl hverdag kan vi glemme at sige tak til de mennesker der sætter os i stand til at udføre vores arbejde. Det er de tilbuds-ildsjæle, der arbejder “bag scenen”. Deres arbejde kommer aldrig i avisen: der er ingen festlig indvielse, ingen røde snore og ingen champagnepropper, når tilbudsmedarbejdere binder sløjfe på et tilbud.

Men deres arbejde er helt afgørende for at alle vi andre har et arbejde både i dag - og i fremtiden. 

Vær med og sig tak til Danmarks tilbudskrigere!

Name: Den Frie Rådgivning
Description:
Den Frie Rådgivning tilbyder gratis og uvildig rådgivning til marginaliserede mennesker i udsatte positioner.
Vores frivillige har rødder til hele verden og bruger deres faglige kompetencer og levede livserfaringer til, at hjælpe mennesker med at opbygge ressourcer og handlemuligheder i eget liv.

I løbet af et år tilbyder vi rådgivning på 28 forskellige sprog. 
I 2024 hjalp vi knap 2.000 mennesker med rødder til 62 forskellige lande.

Du kan læse mere om Den Frie Rådgivning på www.dfr.dk.

Hvis du vil høre mere er du velkommen til at sende os en mail på info@dfr.dk.

Name: Community Foundation for Loudoun and Northern Fauquier Counties
Description:
We invest in community by building local philanthropy.  Our Mission is to foster a community of grantmakers, promote strategic local leadership, and invest in partnerships for the benefit of the community of Loudoun and Northern Fauquier.

One of the most common questions the staff and Board of the Community Foundation receives is, “What is a community foundation?”

It is never easy to sum up the breadth and scope of our work.  

There is one common denominator shared by every person who supports the Community Foundation—it is love of place.  It is love of the Loudoun and Fauquier community that connects us to the people we care about . It is love of the impressive countryside that renews us. It is love of the organizations improving our lives.

By being a supporter of our core mission—building local, everlasting endowment as well as current community philanthropy—our donors find a way to tangibly express a love of place.   What they give to the Community Foundation, they give to their communities, forever.

Our everlasting vision is the great beauty of the Community Foundation.  What we collectively build today, will be here long after we all are gone.  It is the concept of perpetuity, of being “forever,” that makes a community foundation a unique asset within its footprint..  Our children—and children of our children—will continue to benefit from what has been generously given over this past year and since our founding in 1999.

We hope you’ll continue to join us by building upon our love of our home.  The places and people of Loudoun and Northern Fauquier can forever benefit from your vision of our wonderful and unique community.

Name: Sea Save Foundation
Description:
Sea Save Foundation is a 501 (c) 3 nonprofit organization whose mission is to preserve and protect the world's oceans. 

Previous Sea Save Projects Have Included: 

Documenting shark finning  at Cocos Island, Costa Rica and presenting our findings to Costa Rica President Jose Maria Figueres and the Minister of Natural Resources, Alvaro Ugalde. The images stood witness to this illegal practice and were published throughout the Costa Rica and Internernational press and influenced a positive vote several weeks later that expanded the protected areas around Cocos Island National Park.

2. Partnered with the government of Costa Rica and assisted in the development of the successful petition for Cocos Island's inclusion in the prestigious list of United Nations World Heritage Site. (The first petition submitted was rejected.) Our images illustrated the importance of the underwater and land animals resident to the island. We accompanied the United Nations delegation to Cocos Island, and the petition was accepted. Cocos Island is now a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

3. Developed a series of postage stamps with the Costa Rican Postmaster General illustrating the beauty of the Pacific and Caribbean underwater areas of Costa Rica. This series sold out faster than any other in Costa Rica's history and was lauded by the Costa Rican press, as well as international magazines such as The Pacific Rim and the Cousteau Society's publications

Name: Energy Assistance asbl-vzw
Description: Energy Assistance (EA) was created in Belgium in 2001 by employees of Tractebel, Electrabel and Fabricom. Today, EA brings together volunteers (either retired and active employees) from the ENGIE Group and from the Belgian grid operators. The association performs worldwide charitable projects targeting communities having no access to essential energy services. It levers from the skills and knowledge of their members, along with their technical resources.

Name: Porter County Community Foundation
Description:
Since 1996, the Porter County Community Foundation has been helping people just like you do more for your favorite causes by creating and managing permanent endowment funds that make it possible for your generosity to last forever. The PCCF gathers generosity from our community, grows those donations through investment, and gives grants to organizations all for the good of Porter County. Our grants are awarded through an open grant process for humanitarian, cultural, educational and environmental purposes.

Tell us what you love, discover your options, make a powerful gift that lasts.

Name: 45South Foundation
Description:
Connecting your legacy with your community... ...forever.

Giving With Purpose

Simple.
Efficient.
Flexible.

The Wakatipu Community Foundation was born from the recognition that we all benefit if we live in a caring community with strong ties and diverse opportunities.

We have deep roots in the Wakatipu. We pair our strong relationships with our decades of expertise to invest in an array of important charitable causes that improve the quality of life for the whole community.
By delivering grants, building partnerships and strengthening the capacity of non-profits, the Wakatipu Community Foundation will use your donation to make the Wakatipu the best place in the world to live.

We have developed a straightforward set-up process and a structure that you can plug into without any of the hassle of registering and running a separate charity.

We are ready-to-go with our experienced trustees and external professional partners who are very experienced in funds management for community trusts.

We can work alongside your professional advisors to offer personalised expertise and design the most effective strategy for fulfilling your philanthropic passions.

Name: Hawke's Bay Foundation
Description: Connecting local people with the local causes they love

Name: Inclusive Education Project
Description: The Inclusive Education Project (“IEP”) is a non-profit organization that provides public education of special education rights and pro bono legal aid to California families. IEP seeks to spread awareness of parent rights by providing parent workshops and seminars. IEP also fundraise through charity events to fund pro bono legal aid to California children and families that could not otherwise fund legal representation. IEP is here to make a difference, one child at a time.

Name: Information Security Leadership Foundation
Description: A 501(c)(3) not-for-profit, volunteer organization, the Information Security Leadership Foundation is a community of information security executives focused on practitioner collaboration and the education, mentorship, and development of the next generation of information security leaders. The foundation partners with educational programs to offer grants for students aspiring to be future security leaders.

Name: International Development Exchange
Description:
IDEX promotes sustainable solutions to poverty by providing long-term grants and access to resources to locally run organizations in Africa, Asia, and Latin America.

Check us out:
	
http://www.idex.org
http://www.idex.org/blog
http://www.facebook.com/idex.org
http://www.twitter.com/idex
http://www.flickr.com/photos/idex

Name: The Heart Fund
Description:
We created The Heart Fund in 2010 to participate in the global fight against cardiovascular disease (CVD), the number one killer responsible for 30% of all deaths, 75% of which take place in low- and middle- income countries.
For more than a decade, we have been developing patient-centered initiatives to democratize access to care and health education in low-resource settings, while providing local healthcare providers with the tools to advance their practice.

From last mile delivery (smart ecosystem The Heart Mobile) to corrective surgery and epidemiological research, THF has been developing innovative and culturally-relevant solutions to better prevent, assess, and manage chronic diseases in low-resource settings.

The Heart Fund was granted the United Nations - ECOSOC Special Consultative Status in 2018 and has been working with international development agencies (UN, WHO, PAHO, OCHA, PADF), governments, medical companies, as well as with local and international partners of civil society. It is a member of the World Heart Federation and has offices in Paris, Abidjan, and New York City.

Take part to the ecosystem & become a partner !

Name: BLAQUE KC
Description: BLAQUE Kansas City is a nonprofit committed to advancing educational equity to significantly improve

Name: Garden of Dreams
Description: The Garden of Dreams Foundation is a 501(c)3 non-profit charity dedicated to bringing life changing opportunities to young people in need. In partnership with MSG Entertainment – which features famed showplaces, regional sports and entertainment television networks, the Rockettes and TAO Group Hospitality – and MSG Sports – which includes the Knicks, Rangers and Westchester Knicks – Garden of Dreams provides access to educational and skills opportunities, mentoring programs and memorable experiences that enhance young peoples’ lives, help shape their futures and create lasting joy. All of the Foundation’s activities are driven by four guiding principles – Education, Inspiration, Grants and Moments of Joy – and target young people facing illness or financial challenges, as well as children of uniformed personnel who have been lost or injured while serving our communities. Since its inception in 2006, the Garden of Dreams Foundation has impacted more than 400,000 young people and their families.

Name: TEV UK
Description:
Empower One, Influence Many: Lighting the Path to Collective Progress

Launched in January 2021, TEV UK embodies an independent charitable spirit, inspired by the legacy of the Turkish Education Foundation (TEV) founded in 1967. While we share a common vision with TEV of providing educational opportunities, TEV UK operates independently in the UK, focusing on empowering Turkish students through access to world-class education.

Our journey began with a passion to help, fueled by the fast-growing Turkish community in England. Today, TEV UK stands as a secular, non-political, and liberal organization dedicated to providing equal educational opportunities. Run by 15 volunteers from diverse backgrounds, we share a common goal: 
To make a difference in the lives of deserving students.

Our Impact:

Funds Raised: Over £750,000 was generated within three years to support our scholars.

Strategic Partnerships: Collaborations with the University of Oxford, University of Cambridge, and Imperial College London, offering significant scholarship contributions.

Scholarships Provided: 
5 full scholarships annually to deserving Turkish students lacking financial means.
10 merit-based scholarships each year within Turkiye.
Additional support for students affected by February 2023 Earthquake.

 Get Involved: Your support can change destinies. Whether through direct scholarship sponsorships, participation in our annual online auction, or regular donations, your contribution paves the way for collective progress.

 Join Our Cause:

Discover More & Donate: Visit our website at www.tev.org.uk.
Stay Updated: Subscribe to our newsletter for the latest on our events and impact.  https://mailchi.mp/bcad9d1ae557/tev-uk-signup
Reach Out: Interested in collaborating? Email us at info@tev.org.uk.


Let's Illuminate the Path to Educational Excellence Together.

Name: Pride Live
Description:
Pride Live is a social advocacy and community engagement organization dedicated to accelerating LGBTQ+ awareness and support for full equality. We strive to amplify the voices of the LGBTQ+ community and create spaces that celebrate our history and inspire future generations. 

A cornerstone of Pride Live’s work is the creation and operation of the Stonewall National Monument Visitor Center (SNMVC). As the first LGBTQ+ visitor center within the National Park Service, the SNMVC is dedicated to protecting and preserving the Stonewall legacy. The SNMVC is a vibrant hub for education, reflection, and community engagement and is sustained entirely by the generous donations of individuals, community groups, and corporations. Through the SNMVC and Stonewall Day, a global day of action, Pride Live continues to advance LGBTQ+ rights.

Name: Donors Forum of Wisconsin
Description:
Donors Forum of Wisconsin is a professional membership association for grantmakers in Wisconsin. For 30 years, the Donors Forum has been Wisconsin’s premiere resource for philanthropy. 

DFW creates opportunities for the exchange of ideas and information, disseminates current nonprofit sector trends and research, fosters relationships between service providers and funders, builds partnerships within and outside the sector, and advances the growth and enhancement of stewardship of philanthropic resources for the betterment of all Wisconsin residents.

Donors Forum of Wisconsin Members represents: 
-Family Foundations 
-Donor Advised Funds 
-Community Foundations 
-Corporate Giving Programs & Company Foundations 
-Independent Foundations 
-Operational Foundations 
-Individuals with charitable distributions of $25,000 annually.

Name: Thierer Family Foundation
Description:
The Thierer Family Foundation is committed to improving life for the people of Chicago by increasing the impact of local organizations that are helping working to the cycle of poverty.

We do this by collaborating with nonprofit organizations and strategically funding innovative technology projects that make organizations and systemic processes more effective, efficient and sustainable. In addition to financial resources, we provide our time, expertise and passion to our partner organizations to help increase the impact of our grants.

Name: HUB Mustakis
Description:
Te invitamos a conocer el HUB Mustakis, un espacio creado para apoyar la implementación y el fortalecimiento de
iniciativas y promover la  sinergia de experiencias entre equipos de trabajo comprometidos con la innovación social.

Name: Community Foundation of Northern Illinois
Description:
The Community Foundation of Northern Illinois (CFNIL) was founded in 1953 as the Rockford Community Trust. Today, CFNIL serves four counties in Northern Illinois (Boone, Ogle, Stephenson, and Winnebago) with the mission: to inspire endowment and promote philanthropy for the current and future needs of the people of Northern Illinois. We work to connect the charitable goals and intentions of donors with organizations and causes that make our community a healthier, safer, and more vibrant place to live. The nature of our work is long-term; the endowments we steward are designed to support the unique intent of each donor into perpetuity. A portion of our assets were made as unrestricted gifts. These gifts are distributed for charitable activities through our Community Grants Program, a program with six focus areas: Arts & Humanities; Education; Health; Human Services; Sustainable Communities; Youth & Families.
Founded in 1953 as a public charity, CFNIL is the area’s leading provider of grants and scholarships. With more than $85 million in assets and more than 400 individual funds, CFNIL provides charitable dollars, provided by generous donors, to the Northern Illinois community.

Funds at CFNIL are prudently invested and preserved as a way to provide permanent funding for community needs. Investment income is distributed to nonprofit agencies in the form of grants and scholarships, helping our community respond to the many needs of today and tomorrow. Nearly 90% of CFNIL's assets are permanently endowed, meaning that those assets will serve Boone, Ogle, Stephenson and Winnebago Counties in perpetuity.

Name: Pionero Philanthropy
Description: Advancing Guatemala's philanthropy and nonprofit landscape through data-driven initiatives centered around credibility, trust, and best practices.

Name: The Catholic Foundation of Central Florida
Description:
The Catholic Foundation of Central Florida, Inc. is an independent nonprofit foundation committed to raising, managing and distributing charitable funds that sustain and grow our Catholic and broader community in Central Florida. 

We professionally manage socially screened endowment investments that respect Catholic/Christian values allowing our donors to enhance their charity by investing in funds that align with their values. We provide philanthropic advisory services supporting our donors and ensuring they maximize their giving, their intentions are clear and honored and they are able to achieve their philanthropic aspirations. We provide networking and educational opportunities for professional legal and financial advisors to engage in philanthropic conversations benefiting their business, their clients and our community through enhanced philanthropy.

Fully endorsed by the Diocese of Orlando and its Bishop, we also work extensively with Catholic parishes, schools and ministries to support them in stewarding their donors and providing necessary resources they need to further their long-range goals and expand their ministries.

Vision: To be the foremost philanthropic organization through which our community contributes to fund God’s work in order to impact lives and promote the Gospel, which builds the Kingdom of God.

Mission: To inspire and increase giving, to prudently invest the resources entrusted to us, and to capably steward those charitable gifts to support the spiritual and corporal needs of the community of the Diocese of Orlando.

Values:
Faith-filled Passion
Collaboration
Stewardship
Transparency
Servant Leadership
Impact

Name: Ensemble Ukraine
Description:
Nous, citoyens d’une Europe de la paix, entrepreneurs tournés vers la concorde et le progrès, nous nous mobilisons pour soutenir le peuple Ukrainien. 2022, l’histoire tragique, violente et cynique se répète.

Nous, Européens, connaissons l’engrenage, ô combien, destructeur qui pourrait ravager une fois encore notre continent, notre planète.

Ainsi nous voilà face à un choix, un tournant.

Aujourd’hui, nous pouvons peser de tout notre poids pour dévier le cours de l’histoire, pour ne pas revivre les heures sombres de la guerre totale, pour que la paix et la démocratie triomphent, sans attendre.

Pour les générations futures bien sûr, mais aussi au nom des générations qui nous ont précédées, celles qui ont souffert dans leur chair de l’indécision, du nationalisme et de l’égoïsme. Nous leur disons, nous nous souvenons. Vos tourments n’auront pas été vains. L’histoire ne se répétera pas car, chacun d’entre nous à notre échelle, nous prenons la mesure de l’urgence de notre responsabilité.

Nous nous mobilisons pour apporter un soutien matériel aux familles soudainement projetées sur les routes de l’exode et, pour ceux et celles qui, sur place, font face au déchaînement de violence. Un soutien matériel et moral pour leur dire, que nous nous tenons, à leurs côtés, fiers et solidaires de leur courage.

L'association #EnsembleUkraine

Name: Payton Alumni Community & Endowment
Description:
The Payton Alumni Community & Endowment is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization dedicated to supporting the students, faculty, and alumni of Walter Payton College Preparatory High School in Chicago, Ill. Our volunteer board manages an endowment used to supplement academic and extracurricular programming, student scholarships for study abroad and internships, and networking events, such as class reunions. 

Our board is made up of current and former students, as well as parents of current and former students. We oversee alumni relations, communications, events, and investments.

Name: Big Brothers Big Sisters of Central Mass & Metrowest, Inc.
Description:
Together, we empower youth to realize their potential. By creating professionally supported mentoring relationships, we defend that potential and build brighter futures.

Founded in 1963, Big Brothers Big Sisters of Central Mass & Metrowest has supported children in our community through individualized mentoring programs for over 50 years. We reach the children who need us most through strong partnerships with local schools and youth development programs, and with the support of community volunteers, many coming from local colleges and corporations.

Volunteers serve as mentors (Bigs) to children (Littles) who are living in poverty, come from single-parent households, or face other types of adversity. Bigs and Littles meet weekly for activities that might include working on homework, attending a sporting event, taking a hike or walk, cooking a healthy meal, doing a science experiment, and so much more. The essential activity is quality, uninterrupted time between a reliable, caring mentor and a child in need of additional adult support. Mentors are a consistent adult presence in the lives of mentees celebrating their successes, facing challenges with them and helping them reach their full potential.

Name: Games for Change Asia-Pacific
Description:
Games For Change empowers game creators and social innovators to drive impact through games and technology.

G4C APAC is doing some amazing work in the games for change space, covering the use of games for learning, health, and social change.

Name: Leksell Social Ventures
Description:
Leksell Social Ventures is the first professional impact investing company in Sweden. 

LSV seeks to evolve the practice of philanthropy through establishing social impact investing at an institutional scale in Sweden, building on inspiration from the UK and US.

We are a non-profit, private investment company incorporated in Sweden.

LSV finances scalable, economically sustainable, well governed initiatives with evidence based and measurable social impact. 

LSV invests up to 1 mUSD through equity, loans, guarantees or grants. 

LSV is currently focusing on integration, mental ill health and tech for good initiatives

Founded by life-science billionaire Laurent Leksell (Elekta AB) and Family

Name: Boys & Girls Clubs Serving Wake & Johnston Counties
Description:
To enable all young people, especially those who need us most, to reach their full potential as productive, caring, responsible citizens.

Here is what makes us special:

Dedicated youth facility - A Boys & Girls Club is a place—an actual neighborhood-based building— designed solely for youth development purposes.

Open daily - The Club is open after every school day and on Saturdays, when kids have free time and need positive, productive outlets.

Professional staff - Every Club has full-time, trained youth development professionals— providing role models and mentors. Part-time staff and volunteers provide key supplementary support.

Available and affordable to all youth - Clubs reach out to kids who cannot afford, or may lack access to, other community programs. Membership fees are low, only $7.50 per year to join.

Name: Fondazione Kon
Description:
La Fondazione KON opera come un fondo di investimento il cui obiettivo principale è quello di raccogliere Tempo dagli investitori per la realizzazione di progetti di elevato interesse sociale.
Il concetto tradizionale di Banca del Tempo, quindi, si evolve in un fondo di investimento del Tempo raccolto presso i propri investitori che, sottoscrivendo dei veri e propri soft commitment si impegnano a valutare i progetti sociali strategici.

Name: agentsC Inc. Change Starts Here...
Description:
AgentsC is an international, B-Corp Certified consulting company based in Toronto, Canada.  We offer smart strategic solutions to socially and environmentally conscious organizations around the world.

Our comprehensive, transformational change services support clients wanting to achieve sustainable, revenue growth, as well as rigorous program evaluation and impact measurement.

Drawing on the African tradition of Ubuntu (I am because we are), AgentsC is guided by our trademark principle, Equity Philanthropy – the belief that love of community and social justice form an essential alchemy for tackling the world’s biggest problems.

We start with your mission and vision, tap into your strengths, design tailored approaches - and empower your team to achieve the best social returns.

Contact us today for a complimentary consultation.

Name: Southern Colorado Impact Group
Description:
The Southern Colorado Impact Group (SCIG) is focused on serving social impact organizations with access to education and resources. SCIG also seeks to increase awareness amongst consumers about these types of entities and how their money goes further when they purchase products from a social impact business. SCIG’s mission is to “Harness Social Impact to accelerate the evolution of business.”

SCIG’s impact services include: education and training, Social Return on Investment (SROI) measurement, events and networking, consultation services, and speaking engagements.

For more information, head to our website at southerncoloradoimpactgroup.org

Name: The Grace and Mercy Foundation
Description: The Grace and Mercy Foundation supports the poor and oppressed, and helps people learn, grow and serve.

Name: Tzedek Social Justice Fund
Description:
The Tzedek Social Justice Fund (Tzedek) has been supporting the social justice work of grassroots organizations, collectives and cooperatives, nonprofits, and local leaders since the late 1980’s. We fund work that is centered on LGBTQ Justice, Racial Justice, and Combatting Anti-Semitism and is aligned with our mission.

Our Mission
The Tzedek Social Justice Fund redistributes money, resources, and power to support systems change and community healing in Asheville, North Carolina. Through adaptive, trust-based philanthropy, we resist oppressive systems and work to transform our collective home into a place where everyone flourishes.

Our Vision
We dream of a thriving Asheville, where everyone’s needs are abundantly met and where everyone is safe, respected, and celebrated. We believe that a community rooted in joy and love is possible.

Name: Centro de Voluntariado Universitário - CVU
Description:
O CVU (Centro de Voluntariado Universitário) é uma associação sem fins lucrativos, de caráter não-religioso e não-político, fundada em Abril de 2011, em Ribeirão Preto-SP, composta por estudantes e professores universitários. A finalidade do CVU é fomentar o trabalho voluntário no meio acadêmico a partir de eventos; cursos de capacitação; projetos sociais; parcerias; informação e acompanhamento para o voluntário.

Hoje temos 8 núcleos espalhados pelo país, em Bauru - SP, Limeira - SP, Maringá - PR, Ribeirão Preto - SP, São Carlos - SP, Uberaba - MG, Uberlândia - MG, Franca - SP,  e outros ainda em estruturação.

Name: Philanthropy West Virginia
Description: As West Virginia’s leading voice and premier resource for philanthropy, Philanthropy West Virginia is committed to helping its members fulfill their charitable goals.  It does so by providing opportunities for trustees and staff to network, build skills, enhance knowledge and demonstrate leadership. Philanthropy WV is the forum for funders to exchange information, discuss common interests, learn about relevant issues, hone their grantmaking skills and establish networks of important relationships across the state.  Through its programs and services, Philanthropy WV helps funders become more efficient, effective grantmakers.

Name: Orlie's Shoe Drive
Description: At Orlie’s Shoe Drive, we focus on providing school-age children with one of life’s basic necessities to carry them through the day: shoes. It is an item that is often taken for granted, yet thousands of children in the United States are forced to wear shoes that don’t properly fit, are falling apart, or go barefoot altogether. Dozens of medical studies over the years have linked a lack of proper footwear to a decrease in school attendance, health, and even self-esteem among peers. We strive to never let a single child go without.

Name: Amplifier Strategies
Description:
Amplifier Strategies is a full-service social impact agency that helps philanthropists, mission investors and social enterprises achieve positive social impact. 

We're a team of strategists, designers, and technologists working together to deliver an integrated set of capabilities across a diverse set of projects. 

Wherever you are on your philanthropic journey, we can customize a set of services to advance your work and achieve results. 

We customize our engagements to each client's unique context and stage of development, learning deeply about your mission, history and current challenges your organization is facing. By offering a comprehensive package for philanthropists, mission investors and social enterprises in each stage of the journey, we are able to be very flexible. With each case, we operate in a partnership model, and align our interests with our clients' to deliver a high-quality solution every time.

Name: Toi Foundation
Description:
Toi Foundation is a philanthropic organisation where our community’s success drives everything we do. The foundation supports in traditional, philanthropic ways, while also looking to the future with a focus on strategic and innovative granting and impact investing to achieve even greater long-term, intergenerational and transformational benefits for our communities.

In 1990 – our first year of making grants – we gave out $161,000 in funds. Now our annual grant making runs into the millions, with over $150 million granted since 1990.

Name: The Skinners' Company
Description:
The Skinners’ Company is a modern and dynamic membership organisation committed to schools, education and philanthropy. 

The Company’s mission is to ensure our communities of young, old and the vulnerable have an improved quality of life. We invest in education and unlock potential, we provide sheltered housing and enable security, we give grants to those in need and create hope.
Our strength is our people, connected by a belief to shape a more equitable, sustainable and inclusive society, ensuring a legacy of impact and fellowship for future generations to build on.

The Skinners’ Company dates from the 14th Century and is one of the City of London’s Great Twelve Livery Companies, each established to represent their respective trades. The Company is no longer aligned to the fur trade and focuses its resources on charitable impact, supported by its Members.

Name: Stichting Dapper Kind
Description:
Missie en visie
Elk acuut, langdurig of ernstig ziek kind is dapper, ongeacht zijn of haar ziektebeeld!

Daar zijn wij van Stichting Dapper Kind van overtuigd.  Wij willen met het DapperDagboek de kinderen en hun ouders helpen om positief om te gaan met hun situatie. Wij hopen dat ze hiermee hun ervaringen beter kunnen verwerken en makkelijker kunnen delen.

Dit willen we bereiken door zoveel mogelijk kinderafdelingen en zorginstellingen het DapperDagboeksysteem kosteloos aan te bieden, zodat zij het Dagboek aan de kinderen kunnen uitdelen. Dit bereiken we door nieuwe contacten te leggen in ziekenhuizen en zorginstellingen. Soms via een mail van ouders die ons gevonden hebben, soms door een contactmoment met het ziekenhuis zelf die van ons gehoord hebben en anders via een contactmoment van de stichting naar het ziekenhuis toe.

Om de DapperDagboeken en de DapperPlakkers kosteloos aan te bieden moeten we veel sponsors en donateurs aantrekken, acties houden en fondsen aanschrijven. Ook een belangrijk onderdeel in dit geheel is het zoeken van partners die in materiële zin ondersteunen. Denk hierbij bijvoorbeeld aan een drukkerij die het drukwerk tegen kostprijs aanbiedt of een bedrijf dat gratis borduringen uitvoert op T-shirts voor vrijwilligers. Zo proberen we gebruik te maken van de kennis en kunde en niet alleen van donaties of giften.

Simpel maar doeltreffend, daar staan we voor!

Onze doelen zijn:

• Het DapperDagboek uitdelen aan ieder dapper kind die het nodig heeft.

• Iedere professional een handreiking geven om op een laagdrempelige manier iets te kunnen uitdelen zonder al te veel belasting voor het team.

Name: Old Colony Habitat for Humanity
Description: Old Colony Habitat for Humanity was founded in 1989 as Attleboro Area Habitat for Humanity and is a locally run affiliate of Habitat for Humanity International, a nonprofit, ecumenical Christian housing organization. Habitat succeeds in reintroducing into neighborhoods the most basic building block of any community – the homeowner. Habitat provides resources, guidance, and hope to aspiring families. These families work from within their neighborhoods, creating the change we all seek, and repaying the investment that we have made in them.

Name: 1Million Project Foundation
Description:
Potential is everywhere. Opportunity is not. 

Our mission is to help 1 million high school students who do not have reliable Internet access at home reach their full potential by giving them mobile devices and free high-speed Internet access.

More than 113,000 high school students participated in the 1Million Project program during the 2017-18 school year. Of these students, 84% say the 1Million Project enabled them to complete more homework in comfortable, convenient and safe places, 82% say they are more confident in their ability to succeed at school and 49% say the 1Million Project has improved their school attendance.

Name: Pleiades Nonprofit Advisors, LLC
Description:
Your Planned Giving Experts

At Pleiades Nonprofit Advisors, LLC, we understand the challenges your organization faces and we work to help you solve your fundraising and strategy needs, particularly as it pertains to gift planning.

We are a national organization offering expert, award-winning talent with an outstanding record of building stellar planned giving programs, securing future gifts through bequests, charitable trusts, charitable gift annuities, and current gifts through appreciated assets and tax-saving gift strategies.  

Our team of experts is ready to help you identify and develop strategies for your various nonprofit fundraising goals.

Name: inuit Fundación
Description:
inuit Fundación aspira a contribuir al cambio social a través de la innovación en la forma de abordar la inversión social de impacto. Ofrecemos a particulares y empresas la posibilidad de canalizar su filantropía y responsabilidad social, respectivamente, a través un vehículo de inversión social colectiva, el Fondo de Impacto Social, de caracter filantrópico, gestionado profesionalmente. La Medición de Impacto es nuclear y transversal a todo lo que hacemos.

El Fondo tiene por objeto la financiación de proyectos de acción social y sostenibilidad de alto impacto, innovadores, viables y escalables, específicamente en los sectores de la inclusión, la educación, el empleo y el desarrollo sostenible. Además de financiación, inuit Fundación se compromete a ofrecer a la entidad sin ánimo de lucro que administra el proyecto, apoyo no financiero y asesoramiento dirigido al fortalecimiento de sus capacidades de gestión, de acuerdo con sus necesidades estratégicas y operativas. 
La innovación, la independencia y la integridad, valores sobre los que se asienta el proyecto gestionado por inuit Fundación, garantizan la consecución del objetivo de transformación social propuesto.

Name: Jewish Federation of St. Louis
Description:
Jewish Federation of St. Louis mobilizes the Jewish community and its human and financial resources to preserve and enhance Jewish life in St. Louis, in Israel, and around the world.

Our vision is an engaged, vibrant, and flourishing St. Louis Jewish community, in which individuals live with dignity, meaning, and purpose, and a sense of belonging to the Jewish People, as part of a life well-lived.

Dignity: We help ensure that the most vulnerable have a place to live, food to eat and services to protect them.
Meaning: We help provide points of access to anyone who wishes to make the Jewish community, learning and our tradition part of a life well lived.
Purpose: Guided by Jewish values, we inspire and mobilize our community to serve those in need.

Name: Who Is Carter Foundation
Description:
WHO WE ARE:
The Who Is Carter Foundation started out as a family who sought possibilities for one child, Carter Abbott. Now, we are a community of families, caretakers, complementary medicine practitioners, and supporters who want to make possibilities available for all children facing a pediatric neurological condition.

WHAT WE DO:
The Who Is Carter Foundation provides families with the access they need to information, cutting-edge research, and complementary treatment options for children with atypical neurological conditions.

HOW WE PROVIDE THAT ACCESS:
Through TheBrainPossible.com. The Brain Possible houses articles on pediatric neurological conditions and a variety of complementary treatments for those conditions. In the near future, The Brain Possible features a searchable Practitioner Registry so families can find treatment options in their area and a catalog of Recommended Products to promote children's health and development.  In the future, the site will feature interactive forums for families to support one another, support with insurance navigation, and a podcast of interviews and stories to supplement the informative, inspirational content of the website. 

We aim to make The Brain Possible the go-to resource for parents seeking the best treatment options for children with atypical neurological conditions.  We also aim to make The Brain Possible a thought leader in addressing the complexities of pediatric neurological disorders.

Name: Palo Alto Community Fund
Description: The Palo Alto Community Fund (PACF) focuses on the unique needs of our community and channel charitable giving of local donors to effective organizations that improve the quality of life for everyone in Palo Alto, East Palo Alto and Menlo Park.

Name: Obra Social Dona Meca
Description:
A Obra Social Dona Meca é uma instituição de caráter filantrópico e sobrevive da boa vontade e solidariedade de organizações e pessoas que colaboram com doações. Atualmente, atendemos gratuitamente mais de 250 crianças com deficiência. Nosso procedimento para ingresso na Instituição é feito através de um criterioso processo de triagem, avaliação social familiar e posterior encaminhamento à terapia. O trabalho desenvolvido é baseado no envolvimento familiar, oportunizando a criança sua inclusão social e garantindo seus direitos básicos à saúde e à alimentação. As crianças atendidas pela OSDM fazem diversas atividades: fisioterapia motora, fisioterapia respiratória, terapia ocupacional, hidroterapia, fonoaudiologia, psicologia, psicopedagogia, psicomotricidade, atividades esportivas e culturais, entre outras. As mães também são beneficiadas através de orientações e palestras, acompanhamento psicológico e tratamentos alternativos como: shiatsu, acupuntura, auriculoterapia, florais e orientações sobre direitos sociais e benefícios. 

A Casa Lar Dona Meca, hoje, acolhe até 20 crianças, com idade de ate 16 anos. Além de uma casa com todo o carinho e dedicação, estes pequenos também realizam atendimento com os terapeutas da OSDM.
Location: Rio de Janeiro, Rio de Janeiro

Name: PlanetRomeo Foundation
Description:
PlanetRomeo Foundation provides funding to grassroots emerging LGBTI projects and initiatives in countries and regions where LGBTI rights are most severely infringed. 

The Foundation funds projects which provide Shelter, as well as projects under the themes of Community Organizing and Sports & Culture.

Our support focuses on achieving societal change in order to improve the position of LGBTI people throughout the world, with an emphasis on the most disadvantaged regions and communities. We also focus on mobilizing and activating the LGBTI community in these challenging contexts. By providing grants up to € 6,000 we set out to be a stepping stone and fill funding gaps between zero funding and access to larger grants.

Over the last 16 years, the Foundation funded more than 419 projects in 97 different countries, adding up to a total amount of € 1,779,492 in funding.

PlanetRomeo Foundation is an independent, registered non-profit established in 2009 to contribute to the fight for equal rights and opportunities for LGBTI (lesbian, gay, bisexual, transgender and intersex) people. With the support of ROMEO BV, the company behind PLANETROMEO, the Foundation is uniquely placed to draw strength from one of the largest online platforms.

Please visit our website for more information.

Name: Alamo Colleges Foundation
Description: Alamo Colleges Foundation is the philanthropic arm of the Alamo Colleges. We serve the students of Northeast Lakeview College, Northwest Vista College, Palo Alto College, San Antonio College, and St. Philip's College. Our focus is scholarships — life-changing gifts that help students come in and stay in, and achieve their dreams of a college education.

Name: Lewa Wildlife Conservancy (Canada)
Description: Lewa Wildlife Conservancy is a UNESCO World Heritage Site which focuses on wildlife conservation, sustainable development and responsible tourism in northern Kenya.

Name: Tiramisù Academy
Description: We gather tiramisù supporters from all over the world, we promote and enhance the values of Made in Italy abroad, by organising and participating in cultural and social activities, by involving the Members of the Academy in the development of innovative and forward-looking projects.

Name: RebelTHON, University of Mississippi's Dance Marathon
Description:
RebelTHON is The University of Mississippi’s Dance Marathon benefitting
Children's of Mississippi, our state's only children's hospital.
RebelTHON aims to not only raise funds for the hospital, but to also advocate for the vitality of funds and specialized care, while building connections with the miracle kids, cheering them on every step of the way. 
We are one generation fighting for the next.

Name: Fondazione Ravasi Garzanti
Description:
Nata per volere di Livio Garzanti, la Fondazione opera nel territorio milanese e nelle province limitrofe per migliorare le condizioni di vita delle persone anziane, in particolare quelle più fragili e sole. 

Superando, in una visione di moderna filantropia, l’alternativa tra la gestione di specifiche attività ed il sostegno a progetti di altre realtà, la Fondazione promuove, partecipa e sostiene, spesso con il coinvolgimento di varie realtà del territorio, iniziative volte a:
- migliorare la conoscenza e consapevolezza della comunità tutta (persone, istituzioni e organizzazioni che la popolano) sulle tematiche dell’invecchiamento, creando i presupposti per ispirare, di fronte al fenomeno della longevità, comportamenti consapevoli e decisioni e azioni appropriate, evolutive e sinergiche;
- attivare diverse forme di intervento e specifici servizi in grado di migliorare in modo diffuso e generalizzato le condizioni di vita delle persone anziane, soprattutto fragili e sole e delle loro famiglie.

La longevità non va considerata un peso per la società, ma essere riconosciuta come risorsa, in cui valorizzazione e tutela delle persone sappiano integrarsi lungo linee di sviluppo plurali: scientifiche, culturali-antropologiche, educative, socio-economiche, assistenziali. Esse vanno combinate in una convergenza che porti a una visione nuova del fenomeno della longevità, tale da generare una stagione innovativa di consapevole promozione, tutela e valorizzazione delle persone anziane e del patrimonio di conoscenza, esperienza e memoria, nonché delle potenzialità di cui sono portatrici.

Name: First Community Foundation Partnership of Pennsylvania
Description: FCFP brings together people, partners and places to grow local giving and investment, strengthen area organizations and results, and take on the critical issues and efforts needed to build vibrant communities and a thriving north central Pennsylvania region. We guide the philanthropic gifts of donors by investing and administering funds to charitable programs and purposes via grants, scholarships, endowments and more. While our efforts are focused in Lycoming, Montour, Northumberland, Snyder and Union Counties, we also reach into Bradford, Clinton, Columbia, Potter, Sullivan and Tioga Counties through donors and their geographic area funds and scholarships.

Name: Be Like Brit Foundation
Description:
Be Like Brit is a 501(c)3 nonprofit organization dedicated to serving the children of Haiti. Be Like Brit will continue the compassionate life and legacy of Britney Gengel by establishing a safe, nurturing and sustainable orphanage in an environment where children can grow, learn and thrive.

In many ways, Britney Gengel from Rutland Massachusetts was a typical 19-year-old young woman, but her humor, passion and deep sense of caring for those less fortunate made her memorable to all who met her. It was her compassion for others that brought Britney to Haiti on January 11, 2010, just a day before a catastrophic earthquake claimed an estimated 230,000 lives and rocked the small, poverty-stricken country. Britney had spent the day visiting a Haitian orphanage where she and fellow students of Lynn University on a “Journey of Hope”, met the Haitian orphans they had traveled so far to help. Just a few hours later, Britney, three of her fellow students, and two Lynn University professors lost their lives in the rubble of the Hotel Montana in Port-Au-Prince. Britney was missing for 33 days before her remains were recovered. 

Fortunately, Britney’s dream did not die with her. Only hours before the earthquake, Britney sent a text message to her family that read: “They [Haitian orphans] love us so much and everyone is so happy. They love what they have and they work so hard to get nowhere, yet they are all so appreciative. I want to move here and start an orphanage myself.” 

These forty words would sustain Britney’s family through the tragedy and, ultimately, serve as the driving force behind the establishment of the Be Like Brit Foundation and the decision to build an orphanage in Grand Goâve for the children of Haiti. 

Mission: To serve the children of Haiti by establishing a safe, nurturing and sustainable orphanage in an environment where they can grow, learn and thrive.
Vision: To raise the next generation of leaders in Haiti.

Name: BYkids
Description:
Talented young storytellers from around the world are paired with seasoned filmmakers to create powerful documentaries about their lives.

In partnership with public television and education innovators, we share the films and their activation resources to help make global issues feel personal, relevant and actionable for millions of students.

Our work serves as a catalyst for change—igniting important conversations and inspiring a new generation of social activists.

Name: OutNebraska
Description: Join OutNebraska and make Nebraska the best place for LGBTQ people to Live, Work and Play.

Name: Precious Forests Foundation
Description: PRECIOUS FORESTS FOUNDATION (PFF) engages itself for the lasting preservation of natural tropical forests. On the one hand, the value of ecosystem services of tropical forests have to be demonstrated more clearly and on the other hand, the multiple use of renewable timber and non-timber products has to be promoted. PFF is committed to the development of innovations based on application-oriented research in tropical forests and sustainably used forest-areas.

Name: T.J. Martell Foundation
Description:
Our Mission: The T.J. Martell Foundation is the music industry's nonprofit foundation that funds innovative, life-saving cancer research.

Who We Are: For 50 years, we have been the bridge between the music community and a cure for cancer. Founded on a promise from music executive Tony Martell to his son T.J., who lost his battle with leukemia, we unite artists, executives, and fans to support groundbreaking medical research and oncology advancements. Our Board of Trustees includes leaders from every vertical of the music industry, all dedicated to using their influence to save lives.

What We Fund: We champion courageous, early-stage research that other institutions might deem too risky. By funding high-risk, high-reward projects at top NCI-designated cancer centers, we accelerate the bench-to-bedside process, turning promising science into clinical trials and life-saving treatments.

Our Impact: From pioneering medications to advanced technologies for tumor tracking, our support helps brilliant scientists make the breakthroughs that lead to a cure. We are inclusive, progressive, and honor the courageous spirit of our founding.

Get Involved: Join our group of supporters and help us fulfill our promise. Whether you attend an event, donate, or spread the word, you are part of the music community's fight to end cancer.

Name: Stichting Telefoon van de Wind Gouda
Description: De Telefoon van de Wind is een plek waar je denkbeeldig kunt bellen met een overledene.. waar je je gedachten kunt ordenen.. kunt rouwen.. verdriet mag hebben.. los mag laten.. kunt mijmeren.. in gesprek gaat met jezelf.. een plek om opgelucht te verlaten

Name: T.J. Martell Foundation
Description:
The T.J. Martell Foundation is the music industry's largest foundation that funds innovative medical research focused on finding cures for cancer.  The Foundation sources and supports early-stage research projects aimed at developing more effective clinical treatments for patients which otherwise might not be funded.

Over the years, we have kept the Foundation’s roots deep in the music industry and hundreds of volunteers have worked passionately with our staff to raise more than $280 million for cancer research which has been successfully leveraged into several billion dollars in additional funding from larger funding sources.

Name: Royal Humane Society
Description: The society recognises acts of bravery involved in saving life. Founded in 1774. This year we are celebrating 250 years. Follow us for exciting news and events in 2024!

Name: The Dietrich Foundation
Description: Based in Pittsburgh, The Dietrich Foundation and its predecessor entities (collectively, the Foundation) were established by William S. Dietrich II and principally funded from proceeds of the sale of Dietrich Industries in 1996.  Following Mr. Dietrich’s passing in October 2011, the Foundation was formed and ultimately funded with ~$550 million in assets in January 2013.  Its principal mission is to provide ongoing and increasing financial support to selected institutions of higher education and certain other non-profits in Western Pennsylvania. The growth engine of the Foundation assets continues to be its significant allocation to a top-tier, global private equity investment portfolio, with an emphasis on investments in emerging markets.

Name: Fondation Grace Dart Foundation
Description: The Grace Dart Foundation provides love & care to the elderly in the greater Montreal area. The Foundation supports a warm and personal environment for the well being of the elderly through it’s various donation efforts, in line with the following five areas of giving: Basic Needs, Loneliness & Isolation, Socialization & Personal Development, Cognitive Disorders, and Living Environment.

Name: 1440 Foundation
Description:
1440 Foundation supports people and programs creating hope for living well with the 1,440 minutes we have each day. Hope for thriving with cancer. Hope for new mental health therapies. Hope for creating communities that care.  

1440 Foundation aligns with leaders whose vision illuminates the future we are bringing to light.  We’re on a mission for: 

o	Cancer collaboration: Empowering people with hope, new treatments, and innovative options to live beyond pancreatic cancer
o	Inner wellbeing: Advancing the field of inner fitness to unlock the potential within each person to heal and thrive
o	Community building: Supporting nonprofit leaders who nurture compassion and trust in our communities

Name: Aripana Foundation
Description:
Aripana Foundation is a grassroots NGO based in Darbhanga, working towards the development of North Bihar, with a focus on education, primary healthcare and allied areas of development. 

Being a Darbhanga based organization, with the Mithila region as its focus, it is named after an integral aspect of the Mithila culture, the Aripana – the floor drawings, the home art adorning Maithil homes.  Aripana is an inseparable part of the Maithil people’s lives, heralding new beginnings, auspiciousness, joy and celebration.

Name: Long Island Imagine Awards
Description:
The 13th Annual Long Island Imagine Awards will be held on Tuesday, April 29, 2025 at the Crest Hollow Country Club, Woodbury, NY from 5:30-9:30pm.  This gala event was created to offer formal acknowledgement to some of Long Island’s most effective and innovative nonprofit organizations.

The Long Island Imagine Awards was created as a way to showcase the nonprofit sector and all the good work that it does, as well as provide opportunities for deeper collaboration within the sector. The event acts as an opportunity to meet new people, discover new organizations doing business on Long Island, develop new relationships, and learn from the organizations that are being honored.

Long Island based nonprofits compete against each other in 6 categories – Arts & Culture; Diversity, Equity, Inclusion, & Accessibility (DEIA); Innovation; Leadership Excellence; Rising Star; & Social Impact, with the winner in each category winning a $5,000 grant. In addition, the 24 finalists, 4 in each category, will each receive a professionally produced video, which will be showcased the night of the event, and two free tickets to the event. ADDITIONALY, THANKS TO OUR DIAMOND SPONSOR, STERLINGRISK, EACH FINALIST WILL RECEIVE A $1,000 CHECK! This is in addition to the other awards.

Name: Les Courses du Coeur
Description: La générosité en faisant ses courses: penser à soi et penser aux enfants des familles les plus défavorisées de Madagascar !

Name: AYO Association humanitaire
Description: AYO est une association d'aide au développement qui vise à améliorer les conditions de vie et d'éducation des enfants en Arménie.

Name: BEYOND Philanthropy Podcast
Description:
How do we move beyond traditional philanthropy?

Join co-hosts Monique and Valerie monthly to discuss disruption and systemic change to the philanthropy and nonprofit sectors.

Name: The Worshipful Company Of Saddlers
Description: As one of the very oldest City of London Livery companies, The Saddlers' Company is proud of its heritage and to this day remains a 'working' company. It retains a close involvement with the British saddlery trade and equestrianism, alongside its broad educational and charitable activities, and its affiliations with the armed forces. Saddlers Hall is available for venue hire.

Name: TAAF
Description: Our mission is to connect with partners around the world to improve public health, solve all women and children health problems in Africa.

Name: Centro Bem Me Quer
Description:
O Centro de Desenvolvimento Social Educacional e Cultural Bem Me Quer é uma ONG que atua como clínica-escola sem fins lucrativos, de natureza social, beneficente e filantrópica, prestando serviços a pessoas com deficiência. A instituição foi inaugurada em Agosto de 2009, por iniciativa de um grupo de profissionais que se preocupavam com a continuidade assistida do trabalho de apoio, acompanhamento terapêutico e socialização para a pessoa com deficiência. Oferecemos atendimento Pedagógico,Psicopedagógico e Terapêutico, como Fisioterapia, Fonoaudiologia, Terapia Ocupacional, Psicologia, Musicoterapia, Arte-Terapia e Esporte Adaptado à crianças, jovens, adultos e idosos com todos os tipos de deficiências; físicas, neuromotoras e síndromes raras. Oferecemos ainda oficinas de Pet-Terapia, atendimento familiar junto a uma Assistente Social,  orientação Nutricional e Jurídica.
Nós acreditamos que todas as pessoas tem direito de participar plenamente da sociedade. O Centro Bem Me Quer promove inclusão, independência e qualidade de vida da pessoa com deficiência e de seus familiares. Desenvolvemos um trabalho com amor em busca da igualdade.
Atualmente atendemos 45 pessoas com diversos tipos e graus de deficiência e com as mais variadas síndromes, e para que nosso trabalho seja realizado, dependemos exclusivamente de doações - tanto de Pessoas Físicas, quanto de Pessoas Jurídicas.

Name: UniRef - University for Refugees
Description:
UniRef, University for Refugees, is a Swiss non-governmental charitable organization recognized, founded in 2013 by Yvelyne and Bryan Wood. 

UniRef's main strength is its unique and innovative approach, which brings a lasting solution to the problems faced by refugees, such as unemployment or poverty. 

We offer them the opportunity to enroll in higher education. We make a difference by directly providing them with short-term, vocational education programs. Thanks to this vocational training, they will be able to enter the active labour market as quickly as possible, with, among other things, solid strategic skills. 

UniRef reaches the victims of armed conflict, not the other way around.

Name: Chile Psicólogos
Description:
CHILE PSICOLOGOS es un espacio terapéutico conformado por profesionales altamente calificados, entre los que cuentan psicólogos, psicopedagogos, psiquiatras y neurólogos.

El objetivo de CHILE PSICOLOGOS es ofrecer sus servicios profesionales a toda la comunidad para ayudar a construir vidas más concientes y gratificantes, respetuosas de sí mismas y de las demás…

Al poseer experiencia en una diversidad de ámbitos laborales, el equipo se retroalimenta continuamente en periódicas reuniones de supervisión, donde la intervención de los casos se ve enriquecida por una mirada interdisciplinaria.

El equipo de profesionales está conformado por 12 personas provenientes de distintas universidades y con diversos estudios de especialización de posgrado. La consulta se encuentra ubicada en Paseo Bulnes 166, a 2 cuadras del Metro Moneda.

Name: Laudes India
Description: Laudes India LLP is a service organisation, providing support services for Laudes Foundation and Porticus.

Name: Rotaract Genève International
Description:
Rotary brings together a global network of professionals and volunteers who dedicate their time and talent to tackle the world’s most pressing humanitarian challenges. Rotary connects 1.2 million members from more than 200 countries and geographical areas. Their work impacts lives at both the local and international levels, from helping families in need in their own communities to working toward a polio-free world.

With the support of the English speaking Rotary Club of Genève International of District 1990, a new Rotaract Club based at the Graduate Institute aims to promote leadership, professional development, and service among young professionals aged 18-30 in Geneva.


Email : info@rotaract-geneve-international.org

Name: The Sheckler Foundation
Description: The Sheckler Foundation was established in 2008 as a way for Ryan Sheckler, his family, friends and business associates to give back to the community and action sports industry they love. Our mission is to support great causes that directly benefit and enrich the lives of children and injured action sports athletes, inspiring them to “Be the Change”!

Name: Indigenous Medicine Conservation Fund
Description: The Indigenous Medicine Conservation Fund (IMC Fund) is ensuring a future where Indigenous Peoples, their medicines and knowledge thrive for generations to come. As an Indigenous led philanthropic vehicle we work to ensure the resilience of our Peoples in the face of cultural appropriation, environmental extraction, human rights violations, and climate change. Partnering with funders globally, informed by robust ecological and community-based assessments, we build alliances with organizations on ground, fund their efforts and do strategic engagement with leaders of each bio-culture.

Name: French Red Cross (Bagneux)
Description:
We are 18, 64, 41 or 25 years old, we are students, retired workers, nurses, teachers....no matter our profession, no matter our ages, we are all volunteer.
Thanks to our first aid, social aid and training activities, we're offering skills and hapiness to everyone who needs it.

Name: The Change Reaction
Description:
With all of the humanity, and none of the bureaucracy, The Change Reaction is breaking the traditional model of philanthropy by directly transforming the lives of individuals in need with ingenuity, efficiency and heart.
 
The model is simple. Fueled by donors who have accumulated significant wealth, and who, as private citizens, want to effect change in their communities in a way that no government or any public entity can. Empowered by community organizations who identify individuals and small businesses facing financial hardships, donors make direct grants or loans to keep them from complete collapse. Maximized impact with immediate love, hope and relief for hard-working members of our communities.

Name: Impact100 Philadelphia
Description:
Impact100 Philadelphia engages women in philanthropy and collectively funds high-impact grants to nonprofits in the Philadelphia area. Since 2008, we have awarded more than $3.5 million to support the programs and services of 55 nonprofits in five focus areas.

Founded in 2008, Impact100 was inspired by a growing trend in women’s collective giving. The simple premise is that a group of women pooling their contributions can make a significant philanthropic impact in their community.

Impact100 has grown from 111 members in 2009 to 451 in 2020, representing a mosaic of backgrounds, occupations, experiences, broad concerns, and individual interests. We are a community of professionals, entrepreneurs, teachers, mothers, artists, students, and retirees who live in the Philadelphia and surrounding counties.

Together, we learn about local needs and the nonprofits working to meet them. We research, discuss, and experience first-hand the vital work being done by local nonprofits, and provide significant funding to our grantees. 

Please join us!

Name: Old Colony Habitat for Humanity
Description: Old Colony Habitat for Humanity was founded in 1989 as Attleboro Area Habitat for Humanity and is a locally run affiliate of Habitat for Humanity International, a nonprofit, ecumenical Christian housing organization. Habitat succeeds in reintroducing into neighborhoods the most basic building block of any community – the homeowner. Habitat provides resources, guidance, and hope to aspiring families. These families work from within their neighborhoods, creating the change we all seek, and repaying the investment that we have made in them.

Name: Società Geologica Italiana
Description: La Società Geologica Italiana è stata fondata a Bologna il 29 Settembre 1881. È la più antica e rappresentativa associazione scientifica italiana nel campo delle geoscienze. La SGI si adopera per il progresso, la promozione e la diffusione delle conoscenze geologiche nei loro aspetti teorici e applicativi.

Name: NCAT Giving
Description: The Office of Development serves as the fundraising arm of the university. North Carolina Agricultural and Technical State University is continuing and building upon its historic commitment to excellence. That commitment has only succeeded because of generous giving from alumni and friends. There are a broad range of areas across the university where your support will make the difference between good and great and many ways for you to participate in the campaign.

Name: Phuket Has Been Good To Us Foundation
Description:
Phuket Has Been Good To Us Foundation works to improve the opportunities for the children of Phuket, Thailand. As a not-for-profit registered charity, our goal is to give underprivileged children, a chance to learn English so that they may have a bright future.

The Foundation fosters vocational opportunities by funding and implementing high-quality, practical English language education and running extra curricula activities in government schools. 

Of the students we currently teach at the Rachaprachanukroh School in Kamala, 150 are orphans, or their families cannot care for them. These 6 – 18 students live seven days a week at the school.  These children’s self-esteem and education have suffered due to their family circumstances and they are without many of the advantages and opportunities of their peers.
