Coconut Club, an afterschool and weekend programme, was established to help them with their English language studies, develop their self-confidence and self-worth, and enhance their life skills.

Name: ONG EKKLESIA
Description:
"ceux qui sont appelés du dehors"

ONG qui œuvre en faveur d'enfants et d'adolescents démunis 

Abidjan, Côte d'Ivoire 
 Construction EkkleTech 
 Distribution alimentaire
 Activités sociales et pédagogiques
 Aide à la prise en charge médicale

Name: I Am My Bodyguard
Description: Give all children and women a fighting chance.

Name: Lutheran Partners in Global Ministry
Description:
Lutheran Partners in Global Ministry is an independent pan-Lutheran 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization. Since 1995, LPGM has provided access to quality education for people at the margins, especially women and girls.

Alongside our partners in Central Africa, Guatemala, India, and Tanzania, we  focus on opening doors to education for those most at risk of dropping out. 
 
Our Vision: LPGM envisions a world where relationships transform boundaries and where all people have the resources and opportunities they need to thrive.

Our Mission: LPGM provides access to quality education for people at the margins–especially women and girls. We work together with global partners to listen, respond and bear witness to local needs and priorities.

How We Do It: LPGM focuses on four main ways to help those in need: Partnerships, Education, Empowerment, and Travel.

Our EIN is 41-1818525.

Name: The White House Gift Shop, Est 1946
Description:
The White House Gift Shop, Est. 1946 was established September 9, 1946 as the White House Flower Fund. In November, 1950, by permanent order of President Harry S. Truman the White House Flower Fund became the White House Security Fund which soon, thereafter, became the White House Gift Shop. Back then, the White House Gift Shop was located in the basement of the White House and in the 1960s relocated to the Executive Office Building in an area known as the old War Department. Consequent of security reasons, post-9/11, the White House Gift Shop went online only at this location for your convenience and accessibility.

You may be interested to know that here at the White House Gift Shop, our missions are funded the same way you fund your own projects at your home or business -- through creative, honest, and hard work. No government funds or resources, direct or indirect, are ever used in the operation of the White House Gift Shop. We are often asked but do not accept donations.

Many friends have asked about my experiences and visions relative to future gifts, collectibles, and philanthropic goals for the White House Gift Shop.

Our core goal is provide you and global friends of America with Presidential, White House and Diplomatic gifts and collectibles while doing our best to do good for others.

Our philosophy is gift diplomacy: The act of gift giving is an innate human trait through which friendship, affiliation, or identification whether individual, group, or across cultures is symbolically expressed, affirmed, and enhanced by the act of gift exchanges.

Thank you for your support of the White House Gift Shop on LinkedIn.  Please check back often as we will be posting original content as well as information about new products, sales, and more.

Name: Big League Impact
Description:
Founded by former Cardinals Pitcher Adam Wainwright, Big league Impact partners with professional athletes through unique, sports-related fundraising platforms to help people meet basic human needs like food, clean water, medical care, shelter, and education in an effort to save lives, restore dignity, and instill hope.

We create opportunities for professional athletes and celebrities to interact and compete with fans in ways that allow them to raise money for the causes close to the heart.

Since 2013, Big League Impact has raised more than $11.5 million, worked with over 150 MLB players and impacted thousands of people in the US and around the globe.

Name: Philanthro
Description:
Philanthro is a national 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization whose mission is to inspire the next generation of leading philanthropists. Through fun and engaging social and volunteer events and activities, Philanthro connects young professionals with charitable causes. Philanthro is a 100% volunteer-based organization that prides itself on its core values of transparency, efficiency, and creativity. Beyond financially supporting charities, Philanthro’s greatest contributions are building awareness, inciting action, and empowering change among current and future generations.

Philanthro has chapters in Los Angeles, New York,  San Diego and San Francisco.

Although financial contribution is just one of our core benchmarks, inception-to-date, we have donated over $320,000 in cash to our nonprofit beneficiaries.

Name: Jackson Chance Foundation
Description:
The Jackson Chance Foundation is an Illinois non-for-profit, tax exempt 501(c) (3) organization dedicated to providing resources to families with babies in neonatal intensive care unites (NICU). Our mission is to provide support to NICU families, allowing them to spend more time with their baby. By contributing to the Jackson Chance Foundation, you help families be with their critically ill child, thus enriching and potentially extending their life.

The Jackson Chance Foundation is a charity providing complimentary parking and transportation to families with babies in the NICU, allowing families to spend more life-improving time with their children. The Jackson Chance Foundation’s mission was inspired by co-founders Carrie and Terry Meghie after the passing of their son, Jackson Chance, who spent all but 48 hours of his ten-month life in the NICU. Carrie and Terry were fortunate to have the resources and support to spend each and every day with Jackson, a factor they firmly believe contributed to the happiness and length of his short life. 

Nationally, parking at NICUs is a major hurdle for new parents ranging from $4,200/month in New York City to $1,800/month in Chicago; the average NICU stay is 27 days, and most babies are there for many months. The Meghies witnessed families struggling to pay for parking; parents desperate to see their children, but held back with work commitments; mothers and fathers forced to leave their babies alone in the NICU despite their best efforts to visit. The Jackson Chance Foundation ensures that the financial burden of parking and transportation does not stand in the way of any parent seeing their child. In partnership with Ann & Robert H. Lurie Children’s Hospital of Chicago, JCF created the NICU Transportation Program, which provides complimentary transportation to all families, each and every day their child is in the NICU and is fully funded by the Jackson Chance Foundation.

Name: The Arc of Frederick County
Description: The Arc of Frederick County provides advocacy, access to resources, and assistance for increasing individual and family connections for people with developmental disabilities.  The organization seeks creative solutions to help people with developmental disabilities attain their goals.

Name: The Crystal Charity Ball
Description: Our mission is to offer hope to underserved children by funding projects and programs focused on health, education, social services, and the arts

Name: Women And Men Against Child Abuse
Description:
Women and Men Against Child Abuse was founded in 1997 as a Non-Government, Non-Profit Organisation. It is a Section 21 Company and is recognised by the South African Revenue Service as a Public Benefit Organisation with Section 18A tax exemption status. 

The spiralling increase in incidents of child abuse in our country necessitated the provision of specialised child-abuse facilities, as well as the introduction of preventative programmes to break the complex cycle of abuse. 

Our first Kidz Clinic opened its doors in Boksburg in June 2000, and the Kidz Clinic in Alexandra was opened in December 2003. With funding from Pick 'n Pay our third clinic officially opened in Orange Farm in May 2006. The Kidz Clinics are one-stop child-friendly centres providing free medical and psychological treatment for children who have been sexually, physically and emotionally violated.

By identifying the need to introduce effective measures to prevent the abuse of children, our organisation introduced three preventative and support programmes that deal with education, rehabilitation and counseling. 

Women and Men Against Child Abuse has been extremely outspoken in its offensive against child abuse. The organisation has also been instrumental in raising awareness, which has led to growing public support.

Name: Olivet Boys & Girls Club
Description: Olivet Boys & Girls Club was one of the original 50 organizations that banded together with like youth groups from across the country to form what became Boys & Girls Clubs of America. Today, we serve over 3,000 youth annually, and address the region’s most pressing and immediate youth issues of education support, after school programming, and mentoring. With 84% of our members living at or below the poverty line, Olivet provides the needed support for them to succeed and lead fulfilling personal and professional lives. Olivet currently operates 6 brick and mortar locations throughout Berks County, as well as two satellite sites and a campground.

Name: Chispafest
Description:
Chispa es la plataforma de inspiración y educación dedicada al desarrollo de la comunidad creativa de Nicaragua.

Creemos en el talento como motor de desarrollo: 

Nicaragua está llena de talento creativo, y con los estímulos adecuados, podemos convertirlo en uno de los principales motores de desarrollo socioeconómicos del país.

Por ello, contribuimos a legitimar, potencializar y acelerar el crecimiento de la comunidad creativa a través de encuentros, charlas y talleres prácticos.

Name: Genesis Joy House Homeless Shelter, Inc.
Description:
Genesis Joy House is making history by being the first transitional housing facility in Houston County (Middle Georgia) to house our homeless female veterans.

Our mission is to facilitate social change and empower our veterans and community by providing transitional housing, enrichment programs, and counseling services to improve quality of life.

Our vision is to end chronic homelessness among female veterans in our communities.

It's estimated that the ranks of homeless female veterans had risen more than 140 percent since 2006.  For the sacrifices they and their families have made, this is an unacceptable state for any of them to be in.  The challenges facing our veterans are mounting.  However, many more community-based service providers and workers remain overwhelmed due to a severe lack of tangible resources.

Genesis is creating a pioneering solution to help our female veterans overcome two of the greatest challenges associated with homelessness and incarceration.  Through providing residential housing and uplifting programs, Genesis will be poised to noticeably impact homelessness and incarceration among our female veterans in America.

Name: Top of the South Community Foundation
Description: Top of the South Community Foundation was founded to encourage and enable generosity in the top of the South Island for the benefit of our communities.

Name: DACS Services L3C
Description: DACS Services L3C is dedicated to advancing academic achievement and honoring empowering unsung heroes. By partnering with forward-thinking donors, we equip college students to reach their full potential while creating impactful change. Together, we are shaping the future and building a legacy of opportunity, excellence, and achievement.

Name: HEAR Scholarship Foundation
Description:
The HEAR Scholarship Foundation, founded in 2011, is dedicated to supporting high-achieving, low-income students from Chicagoland in their pursuit of higher education. Each year, HEAR enrolls up to 30 students in its College Success Program. This offers each student a renewable $20,000 scholarship, paid directly to college in increments of $5,000 annually for tuition, room, and board. Scholarships are renewable because the foundation believes this fosters greater accountability and sustained support for student success. To-date, over 230 students have enrolled in HEAR’s program.

HEAR’s support extends beyond financial aid, offering wraparound services addressing academic, career, and social needs. Scholars are paired with mentors who guide them along their college journey and who act as a cheer-team encouraging and advising them on a regular basis. They also benefit from college and career workshops on skills like interviewing, résumé writing, and networking. Additionally, HEAR connects students with paid internships through partnerships with local organizations, offering practical experience crucial to professional success.

Diversity and inclusion are central to HEAR’s mission: around 70% of scholars are first-generation college students, and nearly 90% are from diverse ethnic backgrounds. With students coming from over 80 high schools in the Chicagoland area and attending universities across the country, HEAR has committed more than $4.5 million to scholarships and achieved a remarkable 98% four-year graduation rate—significantly higher than the national average.
By supporting HEAR, donors are investing in the next generation of leaders and ensuring access to higher education for students who might otherwise face significant financial barriers. HEAR’s comprehensive approach equips scholars to thrive academically and professionally, preparing them for long-term success and empowering them to give back to their communities.

Name: Fundación Social Molano
Description:
FUNDACIÓN PARA EL DESARROLLO SOCIAL MOLANO

Somos una organización que promueve el desarrollo de programas integrales en poblaciones vulnerables de Colombia en pro de minimizar las brechas de pobreza y desnutrición.

Name: Six Feet Above
Description: Six Feet Above (SFA) was established in 2021, with a mission to connect and support veterans and service members through skateboarding, so we may shred the trend of veteran suicides. SFA was founded and is operated by veterans. Our desire is to provide veterans and service members a cost-free retreat/resource center focused around skateboarding and other positive therapies to help veterans and service members cope with mental illness and post traumatic stress (PTS).

Name: University of Tasmania Foundation USA
Description:
The University of Tasmania Foundation USA was created in 2006. The purpose of our 501(c)(3) charitable organization in the United States is to provide support and promote research and education in the USA and Australia, with a specific focus on the University of Tasmania.
 
The University of Tasmania Foundation USA Board of Directors meets quarterly to plan alumni outreach and fundraising activities. We are building the network of University of Tasmania alumni in the United States, seeking out support for international exchange, and facilitating connection points between US-based alumni, current UTAS faculty, students and friends of the University. Each year alumni and former students of the University of Tasmania gather at multiple events across the USA. Our community is growing and becoming more connected.

Name: The 100 Black Men of Middle Tennessee, Inc.
Description:
History of The 100

Established in 1991, The 100 Black Men of Middle Tennessee, Inc. (The 100) is a non-profit organization providing resources to further the academic and social development of black male students in Nashville and surrounding Middle Tennessee.  The 100 is an affiliate of The 100 Black Men of America, Inc. a service organization chartered in 1986 with a commitment to improve the academic and social proficiency of inner-city black youth. Since 1991, The 100 organization has contributed over $4 million dollars to the operation of its youth programs and provided over $790,000 in college scholarships to the young black men that have successfully completed its programs.

Overview of the 100 KINGS: The 100 KINGS is a leadership development program created and funded by the 100 Black Men of Middle Tennessee, Inc. (The 100.) The program is designed to help black male youth develop the knowledge, skills and confidence necessary to succeed in school and become significant community contributors. Beginning in middle school through high school, the 100 KINGS is a seven-year leadership development program which provides the foundation these young men will need to excel in school, pursue a college education, and succeed in life. 

Through the 100 KINGS program, we provide academic enrichment, social skills building, college preparation, community service, and career development for underserved black male youth. This group of young Americans is frequently rated the lowest in critical areas like literacy, academic achievement, self-esteem and positive lifestyle choices. Our organization is committed to this group because we believe they can maximize their talents and gifts with the right education, exposure, experience and encouragement. Serving as role models, mentors, and coaches, The 100 are committed to their mission “to nurture and enhance the growth, development and opportunities for young, black males of Middle Tennessee.”

Name: 100 Companies That Care
Description:
100 Companies That Care exists to foster a culture of philanthropy within the business community in Orange County.

We believe that the whole is greater than the sum of its parts. That is why we come together to harness our collective synergy to make a larger impact in our community.

COLLECTIVE EFFORT. EXPONENTIAL IMPACT.

https://www.100ctc.org/impact

Name: The Big Event at Virginia Tech
Description:
The Big Event at Virginia Tech is a student-run community service effort that has grown into the 2nd largest event of its kind in the nation. Every spring, thousands of students, faculty, and staff come together to complete hundreds of community service projects throughout Blacksburg, Christiansburg and the New River Valley.

Projects are completed regardless of need or socioeconomic status; instead, it is The Big Event’s goal to simply say, “Thank you.” Seeded in our university’s motto of Ut Prosim (That I may Serve) students and community members come together for a truly unparalleled experience.

The Big Event: “One Big Day. One Big Thanks.” April 6th, 2024

Name: YBConnects - HBCU Alumni Relations
Description:
YBConnects - HBCU Alumni Relations is a Social Capital Investment Firm. We Connect HBCU Alumni To Resources That Will Increase Their Visibility, Credibility, Profitability and Wealth.

The mission of YBConnects - HBCU Alumni Relations is to enhance the Visibility, Credibility, Profitability & Wealth of HBCU Alumni, while serving as a catalyst for the increase of Alumni Giving to HBCUs.

Our vision is: Empowered, Engaged and Economically Free HBCU Alumni.

Objectives: 

To engage HBCU Alumni and Cultivate Ambition within them.

To facilitate connections between HBCUs and their Alumni that lead to outstanding outcomes for both parties. 

To expose HBCU Alumni to Wealth Consciousness, Wealth Creation & Wealth Transfer Solutions which will result in Generational Impact. 

To remove all obstacles that prevent HBCU Alumni from giving their Time, Talent & Treasure to their HBCU.

Our Values:
• Engagement
• Empowerment
• Economic Prosperity 
• Entrepreneurship
• Exposure
• Excellence

We offer access to:

• HBCU Alumni Business Development
• HBCU Alumni Career Coaching 
• HBCU Alumni Financial Consulting
• HBCU Alumni Leadership Development
• HBCU Alumni Personal Development
• HBCU Alumni Philanthropy Development

Name: Morris Educational Foundation
Description: The Morris Educational Foundation is the nonprofit partner of the Morris School District providing educational opportunities for more than 5,700 students in all 10 schools. It is our mission to enrich and inspire the academic pursuits of all our students, from preschool through high school.

Name: Leeway Foundation
Description: The Leeway Foundation supports women, gender nonconforming, and trans artists and cultural producers working in communities at the intersection of art, culture, and social change.

Name: NCF Greater New York and the Northeast
Description:
For over 40 years, the National Christian Foundation (NCF) has come alongside generous givers and their advisors to mobilize more than $25 billion in grants to 90,000 charities in our community and around the world. 

NCF's mission is to mobilize resources by inspiring biblical generosity, with a vision of every person reached and restored through the love of Christ.

Name: Niels Brock Fonden
Description:
Fonden yder støtte til dygtige og flittige elever ved Niels Brocks Handelsgymnasium samt støtte til tidligere og nuværende elever ved Niels Brocks Handelsgymnasium til uddannelse i udlandet.

                                                           -o0o-

Niels Brock Fonden er stiftet 18. august 2016 og er en sammenlægning af Einar Wilslevs Fond og Foreningen Niels Brocks Fond.

Foreningen Niels Brocks Fond blev stiftet 4. november 1944 af Foreningen Niels Brock. 

Der herskede i tiden en særlig fællesskabsfølelse og indbyrdes hjælpsomhed. En inderkreds, bestående af direktør H. O. Damgaard-Nielsen, grosserer John Brünnich, generalkonsul Einar Høyvald, skolens forstander Aage Rasmussen samt direktør Kay Sonne-Hansen, og som i praksis var Foreningen Niels Brocks bestyrelse, blev enige om at etablere en fond med det formål at yde støtte til tidligere og nuværende elever, primært til videreuddannelse i udlandet.

I sine unge år var Einar Wilslev elev på Niels Brocks Handelsskole, som han huskede med så stor glæde, at han besluttede at efterlade størstedelen af sin formue til oprettelse af en fond for elever på skolen.

Einar Wilslevs Fond blev stiftet 16. juni 1986. Den første bestyrelse bestod af kontorchef Gunnar Metzlaff, der var slægtning af Einar Wilslev, som formand samt tre af Foreningen til Unge Handelsmænds Uddannelse (nu DSEB) udpegede medlemmer, nemlig grosserer Aage Rask-Pedersen, grosserer Thorvald Kortbæk og direktør Pelle Trock-Jansen.

Name: VER Voluntariado Emílio Ribas
Description: Missão: Promover a valorização da vida, a dignidade e a cidadania dos pacientes atendidos no Instituto de Infectologia Emílio Ribas.

Name: Weld Legacy Foundation
Description: The Weld Legacy Foundation promotes excellence in health and education in Weld County.

Name: Bahini
Description:
Bahini has empowered survivors of sex-trafficking with independence and freedom since 2018.

Through our partnering Fair Trade certified suppliers we employ adult survivors where they are taught how to create handicraft renown from their own culture. With an independent and dignified life they are able to reintegrate into society and live a life in freedom.

With a Swedish design and a sustainable approach we produce items that are vegan, plastic free and made of natural materials to ensure our Mother Earth loves them just as much as we do. Available for sale both online and in physical stores worldwide.

The profit from our sales goes towards educational scholarships for young survivors and girls at risk. We are currently active in Nepal and Bangladesh with a long term goal to help women and girls in need all over the world.

Name: The Alfred E. Smith Memorial Foundation
Description:
The Alfred E. Smith Memorial Foundation was founded in 1945 by the Archdiocese of New York in honor of "The Happy Warrior," Governor Alfred E. Smith. His pioneering labor reform in New York State at the turn of the century addressed critical deficiencies in a system that disproportionately affected children and young, immigrant women.

              Governor Smith spent his career as governor advancing the cause of the most vulnerable members of society. He did so with good humor and an uncanny ability to work across the aisle, earning him a reputation as a man of integrity and an exceptional legislator. In 1928, he won the Democratic Party's nomination for President of the United States, making him the first Roman Catholic nominee of a major party to run for office. While he didn't win the race, he served as governor of New York for four terms and his ground-breaking labor reform would become the blueprint for the New Deal.

              Governor Smith's legacy of helping children and families in underserved communities "regardless of race, creed, or culture" lives on in the Alfred E. Smith Memorial Foundation and its signature event, the Al Smith Dinner. 

              An illustrious list of political dignitaries and cultural leaders, including John F. Kennedy, Winston Churchill, Dwight D. Eisenhower, Ronald Reagan, Barack Obama, Tony Blair, Colin Powell, Tim Russert, Stephen Colbert, General Mattis, and Nikki Hailey, have all been speakers at the Dinner.

              Every four years, in a tradition that dates back to 1960, United States Presidential Nominees deliver the Keynote Speeches at the Al Smith Dinner, leaving politics at the door and sharing timely reflections with a healthy dose of humor. All of the proceeds of the dinner go to charities that support underserved children.

Name: Spelling Bee of Canada
Description:
Spelling Bee of Canada (SBOC) is an educational organization that encourages youth, parents, teachers and the community at large to participate in the education process and celebrate academic achievement.

Every child that participates in the spelling bee program is a winner just by participating. As a registered charity , we depend on local businesses, organizations and individuals to contribute time, materials, services and/or funds to help us reach more communities and attract more participants.

Partner with us to help the youth in your community !

Schedule a meeting with us to understand how you can engage the youth in your school and community and help them build a solid foundation of lifelong learning and academic achievement. Contact us.

Name: Fundacja Łąka
Description: Zazieleniamy miasta! Poprzez zakładanie łąk kwietnych polepszamy jakość życia, zwiększamy bioróżnorodność i integrujemy lokalne społeczności. Pracujemy z samorządami, firmami i lokalnymi społecznościami. Nasz produkt to nie tylko łąka i związane z nią usługi, ale także edukacja i wsparcie w dążeniu do zrównoważenia i trosce o planetę.

Name: Wichita Falls Area Community Foundation
Description: The Wichita Falls Area Community Foundation is a not-for-profit philanthropic institution organized and operated primarily as a collection of charitable funds that will have long term benefit for the Wichita Falls area. Theses funds are used wisely and efficiently to respond to area needs and to sustain existing organizations and institutions through grants in many areas, including arts, culture, education, environment, historic preservation, health, and human services.

Name: Fundación Carlos Hank
Description: Somos una organización sin fines de lucro, convencida de que la educación es un pilar en el desarrollo humano y clave en el progreso del país, por lo que enfocamos nuestros esfuerzos en desarrollar el potencial y mejorar la retención y permanencia escolar de niños y jóvenes de diversos estados de la República Mexicana, a través de diferentes programas que mejoren la calidad de vida en el entorno familiar y les permita continuar con éxito hacia su camino profesional.
Location: Ciudad de México, Ciudad de México

Name: The Avril Lavigne Foundation
Description: The Avril Lavigne Foundation supports people with Lyme Disease, serious illness or disabilities. Through programs and grants, we provide prevention resources, fund treatment and accelerate research in order to provide hope and transform lives.

Name: Alpha Phi Omega - Rho
Description:
Alpha Phi Omega is a national gender-inclusive service organization founded on the principles of Leadership, Friendship and Service. It provides its members the opportunity to develop leadership skills as they volunteer on their campus, in their community, to the nation, and to the organization. 

With over 100 siblings, the Rho chapter of Alpha Phi Omega provides service to the UNC campus and Chapel Hill community through running a campus-wide lost and found system, funding philanthropy grants to local nonprofits, and volunteering with various campus and community organizations.

Name: Pilotes Volontaires
Description:
Volunteer Pilots is a civic initiative whose goal is to deploy aerial means dedicated to rescuing persons in distress at sea.  Our objective is to support the NGOs in their rescue operations to people in exile attempting desperately to cross the Mediterranean to Europe.

This initiative stems from the admission that the Mediterranean has become a huge sea cemetery.  Over 3,000 men, women and children die each year in the utmost indifference.  This sea has become the biggest migrant cemetery in the world.

We refuse to accept this as fate and we want to act in order to save more lives.  We ask the general public, associations, foundations and companies to give us the means to act on a long-term basis.
To support the action of Volunteer Pilots, please access our website.

Name: GreenOvations (Redefining Lives)
Description:
GreenOvations is a one stop solution to your organic and eco-friendly lifestyle.

We truly believe in Trust, Transparency, Technology, and Tonic which leads to a hassle free Society and a Healthier India.

With a vision of making people easily adopt organic and eco-friendly products, GreenOvations aims to remove the single-use plastics and in-organic products by 2030, making it a lifestyle that gives back to Mother Nature.

Name: The Berkeley Project
Description:
The Berkeley Project aims to permanently change the relationship between the students and residents of Berkeley through hands-on community service.

The Berkeley Project hosts two large scale community service events every year:

1) Each spring semester we will be holding our annual Berkeley Project Month. We will be hosting community service events on the last 3 Saturdays before Spring Break. Each Saturday, we will be sending 100 student volunteers to different community organizations all over the Berkeley area! Mark your calendars!

2) Every fall semester we hold our annual Berkeley Project Day event, where we send over 2,000 volunteers to work on various service projects with our community social organizations throughout Berkeley and Oakland. BP Day begins at 8 am and ends ~3-4 pm with closing festivities on campus.

History

The Berkeley Project (BP) began in 2006 as a giant, one-day service event held on Saturday, November 11th. Over 1000 students gathered from all over Cal to work together with our community partners at 43 service project sites all over the City of Berkeley. Working with our community organizations, students did everything from building playgrounds and planting trees, to making meals for the homeless and helping out at local senior centers. We are setting out to permanently change the relationship between the students and residents of Berkeley. Our ultimate goal is to create a network of service activities which will

a.) serve the needs of Berkeley and its residents and
b.) forge a positive relationship between the campus and the community.

Name: Good Friends Charlotte
Description: Good Friends Charlotte is a women's organization supporting individuals and families with financial resources to improve lives and inspire hope.

Name: Juntos por la Vida
Description: Somos una organización sin fines de lucro cuyo objetivo principal es la preservación de la vida humana, animal y ambiental, a través de la acción social, la formación, la cooperación y la solidaridad ciudadana.

Name: CardioChavitos
Description:
Nuestro objetivo es lograr igualdad de oportunidades en la vida para menores con cardiopatías congénitas y sus familias.
Proveemos a la salud integral de niños y niñas que padecen cardiopatías congénitas y que proceden de familias en situación económica vulnerable. Desde el 2008 proporcionamos procedimientos médicos (cirugías y cateterismos), atención psicológica y emocional, así como diversos cuidados de soporte de la más alta calidad y de forma gratuita, a la vez que se brinda información idónea y oportuna dentro de una comunidad de apoyo altamente empática.
Location: San Pedro Garza Garcìa, Nuevo León

Name: Next Generation Nepal
Description:
NEXT GENERATION NEPAL IS NOW CLOSED.  THIS IS A LEGACY PAGE. 

Next Generation Nepal (NGN) rebuilds family connections torn apart by child trafficking into children's homes.

NGN is a non-governmental and non-profit humanitarian organization based in the United States of America. It was established in 2006 to further child protection activities in Nepal, particularly in relation to children who have been displaced or trafficked into institutions. NGN was founded by Conor Grennan, author of the bestseller, Little Princes: One Man's Promise to Bring Home the Lost Children of Nepal. 

NGN works in several ways: (i) we search for the families of displaced children and reunify them; (ii) we raise awareness of the links between orphanage voluntourism and orphanage trafficking; (iii) we provide scholarships and mentoring for a small group of ex-trafficked youth; and (iv) we advise and mentor others in our approach. NGN implements its projects through its local partner, The Himalayan Innovative Society. NGN also works closely with Government stakeholders such as the Central Child Welfare Board and District Child Welfare Boards, and with local NGOs, INGOs, embassies and tourists.

Name: Alpha Phi Omega - Alpha Delta Chapter
Description:
Alpha Phi Omega is a national coeducational service organization founded on the principles of Leadership, Friendship and Service. It provides its members the opportunity to develop leadership skills as they volunteer on their campus, in their community, to the nation, and to the organization. With more than 400,000 members on over 375 campuses, Alpha Phi Omega continues to provide more service on more campuses than any other collegiate service organization.  
  
It is the VISION of Alpha Phi Omega to be recognized as the premier service-based leadership development organization. 
  
It is the MISSION of Alpha Phi Omega to prepare campus and community leaders through service. 
  
The VALUES of Alpha Phi Omega are to develop leadership, promote friendship and provide service. 
  
The OBJECTIVES of Alpha Phi Omega are share, grow, improve and invest.

The Alpha Delta Chapter at San Diego State University is one of the oldest Chapters of Alpha Phi Omega. The 28th Chapter initiated under the cardinal principles of leadership, friendship, and service on June 14, 1932. Due to the wars occurring in the nation, Alpha Delta became inactive for numerous years. However, a group of enthusiastic students were determined to recharter Alpha Delta and successfully did so on May 10, 2008. 

Alpha Delta is apart of Region X that spans the entire state of California, as well as parts of Arizona, Hawaii, and Nevada. The restoration of Alpha Delta Chapter at San Diego State University and the exemplification of Alpha Phi Omega's cardinal principles of leadership, friendship, and service through its members are known throughout the San Diego area.

Name: Nursing Beyond Borders
Description: Nursing Beyond Borders is a 501(c)3 public charity which exists to deliver preventative healthcare and education to children and their communities on a global scale. We focus on preventing disease before it starts by sending nurses to serve in our network of partnered orphanages, shelters, underserved schools, clinics, and their surrounding communities.  Through our focused healthcare initiatives and educational presentations we empower children and families to improve not only their own lives, but also to impact the lives of future generations

Name: Entregenios
Description:
Instagram: @entregenios
YouTube: @entregenios_eg

EntreGenios aúna experiencias, networking y solidaridad a través de eventos 100% solidarios a favor de Fundaciones a los que acuden expertos en distintos ámbitos con un cóctel posterior.

La Fundación Columbus, la ONG Mamás en Acción, la Fundación Bobath, Mundo Justo y la Fundación Ana Carolina Díez Mahou han sido, hasta ahora, las beneficiarias de la recaudación obtenida en los eventos celebrados.

Name: Fondation AMSHI - Alliance Maroc Suisse handicap intégration
Description:
La Fondation AMSHI - Alliance Maroc Suisse handicap intégration est une association suisso marocaine née de la rencontre d’exception de sa Présidente Nora Ajabli et du Professeur Bruno Verdi, tous deux très actifs dans l’humanitaire et les besoins urgents des nécessiteux...

L’association AMSHI est engagée à oeuvrer afin que les facteurs environnementaux, en premier lieux desquels, l’accessibilité, les conditions socioéconomiques, le cadre juridique et législatif, regardés comme des éléments intrinsèques du handicap, qu’ils peuvent alourdir ou alléger, fassent l’objet d’une prise en compte accrue, au même titre que l’approche médicale.

AMSHI est animé par la conviction que chaque être humain peut se prévaloir de tous les droits de l’Homme et de toutes les libertés fondamentales, et que nul ne doit subir de discrimination du fait d’une déficience physique, mentale, intellectuelle, ou sensorielle. L’association poursuit résolument l’objectif de pleine participation à la vie sociale, des personnes en situation de handicaps, à travers une intégration entière, assumée par l’ensemble des acteurs, et épurée des stéréotypes, des préjugés, et des pratiques préjudiciables sur lesquelles se fondent les habitudes et les comportements.

L’association AMSHI est fermement attachée au fait que les personnes en situation de handicap
sont des membres à part entière de la société, ces deniers jouissent des mêmes droits, et des mêmes devoirs, que leurs concitoyens/concitoyennes. C’est dans ce sens qu’il est primordial de placer l’individu en situation de handicap au centre des initiatives, des décisions, et des actions, afin qu’il soit maître de son avenir, et membre actif de la vie sociale.

Name: Duffy Books in Homes
Description:
Duffy Books in Homes is dedicated to nurturing a love for reading among New Zealand's tamariki. We provide over 100,000 children in nearly 530 lower-decile schools and more than 280 Early Childhood centres with free books three times a year. Our mission is to break the cycle of booklessness by instilling a passion for reading in Duffy tamariki, fostering future generations of avid readers and leaders.

Research underscores our impact: historically schools in our program have seen reading scores improve by up to 35%. Now, tamariki in Duffy schools maintain their reading achievements over the summer holidays, a challenge faced by many others.

We take pride in our role as an integral part of school literacy programs, offering tamariki something they can cherish and pass down through generations. Witnessing the original 'Duffy Kids' now passing on their love of reading to their own children is among our greatest rewards.

Want to join us in making a lasting impact? Your support will help us to inspire young minds, enabling them to dream big and achieve even bigger. Visit our website to learn how you can make a difference today.

Name: Lippman Kanfer Foundation for Living Torah
Description: Lippman Kanfer Foundation for Living Torah promotes and supports “Living Torah” — Judaism as a powerful, evolving wellspring of accumulating wisdom and sensibilities that enriches people’s lives and helps create a better world.  We are also continuing the Lippman Kanfer family’s commitment to strengthening the ecosystem for innovation in the Jewish community and to the pursuit of justice.

Name: Asociación Talenta
Description: Talenta es la plataforma que facilita el contacto entre el talento y profesionales de diferentes sectores laborales, para ofrecer mentorships y orientación a las personas en búsqueda de empleo. Talenta surge con el objetivo de unir ideas y recursos mediante la colaboración de las personas, ante la crisis económica derivada de las consecuencias del COVID-19.

Name: Girls on the Run New Orleans
Description: At Girls on the Run we inspire girls to recognize their inner strength and celebrate what makes them one of a kind. Trained coaches lead small teams through our research-based curricula which includes dynamic discussions, activities and running games. Over the course of the ten-week program, girls in 3rd-8th grade develop essential skills to help them navigate their worlds and establish a lifetime appreciation for health and fitness. The program culminates with girls positively impacting their communities through a service project and being physically and emotionally prepared to complete a celebratory 5K event.

Name: Genitori Antismog
Project WOMN Inc
Description: Project WOMN Inc. is a not-for-profit organisation that was established in 2020 by three friends – Amanda Yong, Ning Lai and Jewel Tan in Perth, Western Australia. A surge of domestic violence reports in Western Australia during the initial nationwide Covid-19 lockdown was the primary catalyst for the Project WOMN initiative. Parallel to the trio’s desire to help those within the community affected by hardship, a shared love of fine food and wine, and a genuine appreciation of the many local producers and wineries that WA has to offer, has helped propel Project WOMN’s events to local acclaim in a relatively short space of time. The team hopes to collaborate with other local chefs, entrepreneurs, and entertainers, and expand its initiative around the state in order to continue to raise funds and awareness for important charitable work.

Name: Tomahawk Charitable Solutions
Description:
Tomahawk Charitable Solutions (TCS) is a 501(c)3 non-profit organization that supports the immediate and ongoing needs for the Special Operations Forces (SOF) and law enforcement communities and their families. Through our events and related fundraising, we generate funds that TCS allocates to a select number of organizations that TCS has carefully vetted and can personally vouch for the highly efficient and honorable utilization of charitable funds.

TCS operates with 0% overhead cost, allowing us to donate money collected to those who have sacrificed so much for our great country and their families. Our focus on the SOF and law enforcement communities is to provide financial assistance for medical, educational and other urgent needs, as well as support the affected families, as they navigate their lives during and after the fight.
 
We believe that our SOF and law enforcement communities are among our nation’s greatest assets, but also require the most support when tragedy and hardship occur. TCS will always be committed to honoring and supporting our wounded and fallen and their families, so they can survive and reset through difficult and tragic situations, and be better positioned to thrive in future life.

Name: Clúster Tecnológico Catamarca
Location: San Fernando del Valle de Catamarca, Catamarca

Name: The Neal Peirce Foundation
Description:
The Neal Peirce Foundation was established in 2020 to support journalism on city solutions, in honor of American journalist Neal Peirce. 

In his long and distinguished journalism career, Neal was known for traveling across the U.S. to interview local leaders, grassroots activists, and other urban changemakers. In his nationally syndicated column, Neal chronicled their ideas for building affordable housing, boosting transit, improving education, and all the ways they aimed to make cities and metro regions better places for all their people. Neal was the only journalist covering local government and regions from a national perspective. 

To honor and continue this legacy, the Neal Peirce Foundation offers journalists reporting grants to enable them to spread the word about promising ideas percolating in America’s metropolitan areas. 

The grants of up to $1,500 will cover travel and related expenses for journalists to report on what’s working and why, informed by thorough on-the-ground research.

Name: Cupcakes of HOPE
Description: Cupcakes of HOPE is a Non-Profit Company (NPC 2012/103028/08) and a Public Benefit Organisation (PBO930053726), and our aim is to raise awareness and funds for families in need of medical assistance, we do this through our love of baking cupcakes…

Name: RiSE - Homeless Giving
Description:
https://www.risehomeless.com

Our mission is to connect homeless people and local generous citizens.

In one hand the last report done by Homeless World Cup Foundation in 2005 estimated 100 millions homelesses worlwide. 1.6 billion people lacked adequate housing.

In the other one, the The World Bank recently informed us that 1/3 of the food produced in the world for human consumption every year — approximately 1.3 billion tons — gets lost or wasted. 

In 2012, the worlds’ cities generated 1.3 billion tonnes of solid waste per year, amounting to a footprint of 1.2 kilograms per person per day. With rapid population growth and urbanization, municipal waste generation is expected to rise to 2.2 billion tonnes by 2025.
In solid waste you can find for e.g textile, foods, e-wastes, furnitures..

Don't you think it's the moment for us to join both parts with technology ?

Name: IU Alternative Break Program
Description: Through international and domestic service learning trips, Alternative Break Program gives students the opportunity to not only learn more about social issues and connect with like-minded peers, but also to see how their service can leave a lasting impression on communities around the world. ABP works with various non-profit partner organizations that are dedicated to the specific social issue a trip focuses on. Students complete trips during Indiana University's academic breaks (Thanksgiving Break, Winter Break, Spring Break).

Name: Child Aid International
Description:
Child Aid International is a registered NGO in Iraq (NGO #2K70812) and a registered charitable foundation in Canada (811538057RR0001)

Child Aid International is a not-for-profit organization that respects and champions the rights of all orphan children - regardless of race, ethnicity, gender or faith. 

The war in Iraq has devastated thousands of lives - families have been destroyed, homes have been lost, and countless children have been orphaned. There is a serious orphan crisis in the country without thousands of recently orphaned children in dire need of aid. 

Founded in 2004, Child Aid International seeks to help rebuild the lives of these orphan children.

The organization has developed its Orphan Child Sponsorship Program through which it provides donors with the opportunity to help transform the life of an orphan child in Iraq. Sponsorship covers the healthcare, food, clothing and education cost for a child.  Child Aid International currently sponsors over 400 orphan children in Iraq. 


Child Aid International is a registered NGO in Iraq, and a registered charitable foundation in Canada.

Name: Trajectory Foundation
Description:
At Trajectory Foundation our mission is clear...we want to change the trajectory of Black students' lives through scholarships to attend HBCUs.  

As of November '21 we are funding 10 students at $5,000 per year.  After having two students graduate in the spring of '22, we will add 4 new scholarship winners and raise our impact to 12 students next academic year.  

We rely on the generosity of donors to fund our mission.  We have been so fortunate to be able to increase the student impact through the first four years of history because people see the impact and can learn about the students they fund through our team.

Name: Million Women Mentors MN
Description:
MWM-MN – Who we are
MWM-MN is a cross-corporate, governmental, and educational network - passionate about connecting students, parents, educators, non-profits, and organizations aligned to advance diversity in STEM. 

Trailblazers Model & Brand – What we do
The Trailblazers mentoring panel model was originally designed and branded by Anjie Cayot of Keyot Consulting, one of the founding board members of the MWM-MN chapter. Another founding board member, Tonya Tatro of Experis then enhanced the model to include coordinating between local schools and corporations to provide onsite STEM tours and experiences. 

MWM-MN Outreach - Strategy & impact
Trailblazers – Panels
Mentoring event for high school / college-age girls that provides them the opportunity to hear from a panel of young ladies thriving in STEM within a diverse variety of careers and organizations. 
-Audience: Female high school and college students
-Corporate hosts partners – Cargill, Securian Financial, Prime Therapeutics, Principal Financial Group, and Best Buy
-Corporate panelist partners – 3M, Bremer Bank, Keyot, Cargill, Abbott, Wells Fargo, C.H. Robinson, Mayo Clinic, UnitedHealthcare, Optum Technology, Merrill Corporation, Target, DuPont Pioneer, Kermin, Principal Financial Group, TCF Bank, Prime Therapeutics, Securian Financial, General Mills, Viking Engineering, Experis

Trailblazers – Tours
Coordinate the introduction of educational institutions to corporations for onsite STEM tours and experiences.
-Audience: Middle school girls, High school students
-Corporate host / panelist partners – Medtronic, Boston Scientific, St. Jude Medical, Optum Technology, Cargill, Experis
-Educational/Outreach partners – Girl Scouts of River Valley, American Heart Association, MN High Schools – Apple Valley, Blaine, Coon Rapids, Maple Grove, North, Saint Thomas Academy, Spring Lake, White Bear, Eagan.

Name: Samaritans - NSS Club of TMSL
Description: We are the NSS club of Techno India Salt Lake.

Name: The Shears Foundation
Description: We are a family foundation making grants in the North East of England, York, Harrogate, Greater Manchester and The City of Bradford.

Name: Attacq Foundation
Description: The Attacq Foundation promotes and supports programmes aimed at education and training.

Name: The HBCU Hub, Inc.
Description: The HBCU Hub, Inc. is a 501(c)(3) Not For Profit organization founded in 2016 to help spread awareness to NYC youth about the benefits of attending a Historically Black College or University. The HBCU Hub intends to support the recruitment of area youth to HBCUs, as well as serve as an overall HBCU advocacy organization.

Name: Fleur de Bitume HEC Paris
Description:
Créée en 1993, Fleur de Bitume HEC Paris est la plus grosse association du campus d'HEC Paris en nombre de membres actifs (plus de 120 étudiants). Elle a pour objet de proposer des cours à des jeunes issus de milieux défavorisés, et ce de l'école primaire aux classes préparatoires aux Grandes écoles. L'objectif de l'association est d'ouvrir le champ des possibles des jeunes issus de milieux défavorisés.

Fleur de Bitume HEC Paris possède 4 programmes principaux: 

- Le programme PREP'HEC organise des séances hebdomadaires de préparation aux concours des écoles de commerce pour des élèves en première année de classes préparatoires ECT. 

- Le programme PACE HEC organise des séances bi-mensuelles qui prennent la forme de débat sur des sujets d'actualité, de l'aide sur l'orientation, d'improvisation théâtrale ou d'initiation à l'art. 

- Le programme Collège organise des séances bi-mensuelles d'encadrement des devoirs pour redonner goût au travail aux collégiens des différents centres partenaires des Yvelines. 

- Le programme Accompagnement à distance permet à un élève en classes préparatoires ECT, ECS ou ECE d'être accompagné toute l'année par un tuteur lors d'appels téléphoniques réguliers.

Name: Hue Help
Description: Hue Help is an international NGO that seeks to improve the health, education and future prospects of disadvantaged children in central Vietnam.
Location: Phuong Thuan Thanh, Thua Thien Hue

Name: Benesys
Description: Benesys is an impact advisory service that provides bespoke philanthropy to our corporate, non profit and individual clients. We unlock the collaborative potential that exists between donors, businesses and charities for mutual benefit and sustainable impact.

Name: Future Generation Nepal
Description:
Future Generation Nepal (FGN) is a Nepal Government registered nonprofit organization founded in 2019 by a young and dynamic team of passionate individuals. 
Our senior staff have over 12 years’ experience in child protection, human rights and social development. We are experts in the deinstitutionalization of child care homes including rescues, managing “temporary safe houses”, family reintegration and alternative care. Poverty can be a key factor in family separation and Future Generation Nepal offers family strengthening livelihoods to families in need and are vulnerable to child trafficking.

Our staff are experienced “first responders” to unplanned situations caused by natural disasters and emergencies. We first learned these skills during the Nepal earthquake in 2015 and used them during the COVID-19 pandemic to help over 100 vulnerable families and children. We provided families and their children with relief support including food, supplies, and hygiene kits to survive the Covid-19 crisis and not become the victims of traffickers.

Our team is trained, experienced and reliable in implementing projects for our funding partners. Future Generation Nepal works closely with other local organizations, local community leaders, teachers, government officials and influencers.

Name: Fundação Paiol Grande
Description:
Paiol Grande. Desde 1946 aperfeiçoando métodos de convivência entre jovens, através do esporte, lazer, diversão e cultura, possibilitando, ao mesmo tempo, uma vivência de valores e noções fundamentais de cidadania, solidariedade, ética, moral e convívio social. O jovem aprende na prática, enquanto se diverte. E se torna um Paioleiro – um ser humano que vai lembrar dessas experiências marcantes. Que vai se orgulhar de ter participado delas. E que vai difundir seus princípios básicos para o resto de sua vida.

Ser Paioleiro é ser cidadão. É dar valor às coisas mais simples da vida. É saber transformar vivência em ensinamento. E é, sobretudo, entender esta fantástica experiência que é...viver! De uma forma alegre, divertida e positiva.

Localizado na bela Serra da Mantiqueira, no município de São Bento do Sapucaí, fomos o primeiro acampamento a se firmar no Brasil.
Constituídos juridicamente como uma Fundação sem fins lucrativos, podemos disponibilizar todos nossos recursos no cumprimento de nossa missão.
Location: São Bento do Sapucaí, São Paulo

Name: Junior League of Grand Rapids
Description:
The Junior League of Grand Rapids is an organization of women whose mission is to advance women’s leadership for meaningful community impact through volunteer action, collaboration, and training.

Above all else, our goal is to promote and perpetuate social change in the communities of Grand Rapids that will benefit most from it. By empowering women and providing valuable leadership training, we are giving local women the tools to get involved, put their talents to work, and positively impact the communities of West Michigan.

Right now, our primary focus of Improving Women’s Health and Development requires immediate action, and our multi-faceted approach is making an impact.

If you’re interested in learning more about our current focus or developing your own civic leadership skills, we welcome you to learn more or connect with us.

Name: Banco de Alimentos de Tijuana A.C.
Description:
El BANCO DE ALIMENTOS DE TIJUANA, A.C. ( BANATI ), fue creado en el año de 1996 con el fin de combatir EL HAMBRE en nuestra comunidad y trabaja como un puente entre la abundancia y la carencia, realizando el manejo de alimentos a gran escala, obtenidos a través de donativos o compra a bajo precio con el fin de abastecer a la gente carente en el área urbana.

El alimento también va encausado hacia Instituciones asistenciales tales como Orfanatorios, Asilos de ancianos, Centros de Rehabilitación, Comedores, etc., las cuales son elegidas por medio de un estudio socioeconómico, dando prioridad a las más necesitadas; BANATI trabaja día a día con el fin de hacer llegar los alimentos a aquellos que más lo requieren. 

El BANATI es una Institución que funciona con una plantilla operativa de 36 personas.

Name: Fairtree.org
Description:
Fairtree is a nonprofit that accelerates community-based tree-growing initiatives through a ‘pay-to-grow’ model that uses digital tools for remote recruitment, management and funding. In addition to supporting individual-led efforts, Fairtree partners with established environmental organizations, helping them integrate the pay-to-grow model into their operations. Ultimately, we serve to increase access to resources for everyday tree growing champions, to increase operational efficiency for established organizations, and to build a coalition towards universal application of the pay-to-grow approach. Within the greater ecosystem of tree-growing organizations Fairtree plays four critical roles:  

1. Incubator for smaller community-based initiatives and individual-led projects, providing professional and technical assistance
2. Partner/service provider for established tree-growing organizations, assisting with implementation of the pay-to-grow system
3. Fundraiser for tree-growers and partner organizations
4. Advocacy and coalition-building organization, promoting use of the pay-to-grow model as a means of equitable inclusion and economic empowerment in the tree-growing movement

Name: Beloved Communities Network
Description:
Our vision is to cultivate a pathway to transform the culture of violence, domination and extraction into a culture of collective thriving and regeneration.

The Beloved Communities Network is a continuation of the years of work and wisdom that went into building the Transitions Initiative. As we continue on this journey of transitioning to a world of love, interdependence and resilience, the Beloved Communities Network will build from the foundation that has been laid,
while also strengthening, reinforcing and designing new ways to leap into the world we imagine.

Beloved Community is not ours in conception. The concept is rooted in the legacy of the Fellowship of Reconciliation, Dr. Martin Luther King Jr., Grace Lee Boggs and others, and is carried forward through many people investing in the idea that we can live in a world of economic and social justice. With the imagination we bring to this work, we envision a holistic approach that includes leading with bold vision and values, embodied practice, radical connection and strategic navigation.
We know that now is a time of great transition and change. Around the globe we see unprecedented climate disruption and upheaval across economic, political, and cultural systems. We are all facing uncertainty and seeking paths to a future we can believe in. In this time we also feel a calling, an invitation, a possibility, beyond what we can presently see.

We have the capacity to answer this call. We have the capacity to bring forth a future that is kicking to be born. We have the capacity to be that future, to be the power and strength of our vision, our purpose, and our relationships.

Our communities are calling on us to recognize our undeniable interdependence and make a courageous commitment to love. Our mutual future depends on generating new solutions that reflect this recognition and commitment.

Name: Fondation Albatros
Description:
Fondation philanthropique familiale, de droit suisse, soutenant organisations et projets à but social et/ou humanitaire dans les domaines suivants: éducation, enfance, autonomisation de la femme, médical, sanitaire et infrastructures d’intérêt public.

La fondation Albatros est animée d’un esprit de solidarité et s’engage afin de mieux faire respecter les doits des individus, qu’ils s’agisse d’enfants, de femmes ou d’hommes. Elle a pour objectif de contribuer à la construction d’un monde plus juste, en soutenant ceux qui sont les plus exposés à la détresse, l’insécurité, l’injustice, la violence et la maladie.  Elle soutient les initiatives proposant des solutions pragmatiques et durables afin de rétablir autant que possible l’égalité de chances et permettre à chacun de se construire une vie de qualité.
Elle contribue ainsi à la mise en place de mesures et d’actions générant une amélioration sensible et durable de leurs conditions de vie et perspectives d’avenir.
La fondation croit aux cercles vertueux où celui qui a pu saisir une main secourable la tendra à son tour à une personne dans le besoin, afin qu’elle aussi puisse devenir maître de son destin.

Name: Global India Fund
Description: Global India Fund (GIF) is a U.S.-based non-profit organization aimed at inspiring philanthropy and service across the globe to support innovative programs and interventions in India. GIF was founded in 2007 by Dr. Amita Vyas, and has been raising awareness and support for health, education, and livelihood programs among individuals, corporations, and other donors. GIF has been awarded grants from federal agencies and foundations to partner with organizations in India on various projects focused on CSR, entrepreneurship, and gender equality.  Currently GIF is partnering with Samhita Social Ventures on GoodCSR, a platform that enhances opportunities for both NGOs and corporates to collaborate; and a TechCamp workshop for young entrepreneurs across South Asia.

Name: Girls on the Run New Orleans
Description: At Girls on the Run we inspire girls to recognize their inner strength and celebrate what makes them one of a kind. Trained coaches lead small teams through our research-based curricula which includes dynamic discussions, activities and running games. Over the course of the ten-week program, girls in 3rd-8th grade develop essential skills to help them navigate their worlds and establish a lifetime appreciation for health and fitness. The program culminates with girls positively impacting their communities through a service project and being physically and emotionally prepared to complete a celebratory 5K event.

Name: Walking for Water
Description:
Walking for Water is a non-profit organization based in Denmark, dedicated to providing safe water access to communities in need. Our community-driven programs work directly with locals to develop and implement tailored solutions, ensuring their effectiveness and sustainability in the long run. Our goal is to establish a consistent and recognizable brand identity, build credibility and trust with our target audience, and increase brand awareness and engagement on social media. 

As we continue to expand our reach, we strive to build partnerships with individuals and organizations who share our commitment to social and environmental causes. We work with non-profit organizations focused on water access and sanitation, environmental organizations, corporations with sustainability and social responsibility programs, philanthropic organizations and foundations, and individuals interested in social justice issues. 

As a non-profit organization, we rely on the support of individuals, organizations, and foundations to continue our work. With your support, we can reach more communities and create a lasting impact. Together, we can make a difference and ensure that clean water is available for generations to come. 

Would you like to donate? Danske BankAccount number: 11649904 Registration number: 9570 IBAN Number: DK 85300000 11649904 Mobile pay: 42 68 65 53 Read more and see how you can be a part of the Walking for Water community on www.walkingforwater.dk

Name: Indian Hill Foundation
Description:
The Indian Hill Public Schools Foundation builds financial and other community resources to support sustained excellence in The Indian Hill Public Schools so that all students have the opportunity to achieve their highest potential.  The Foundation achieves its purpose with the help of Alumni, Parents and the Community.

The Foundation is the Indian Hill School District's official Alumni organization.

We support seed funding for innovative programming, professional development, and community engagement.

Name: V3Gate Foundation
Description: As a Service-Disabled, Veteran-Owned Small Business (SDVOSB), V3Gate is determined to give back to those who serve and protect our great nation. V3Gate Foundation’s primary goal is to support organizations that promote the welfare of United States military veterans and their families.

Name: The Gateway Foundation
Description: Gateway Group has embarked upon a number of initiatives under its Corporate Social Responsibility (CSR) umbrella. The fundamental thought process behind our CSR programs is the fact that if we are drawing the best out of society, it becomes our duty to give society the best we have. Gateway Group has always been proactive in terms of contributing to the society in different ways. This act of contribution is a regular, year after year affair since the past 2 decades of our business excellence.

Name: GrandyZ, la solution de maintien à domicile intergénérationnelle!
Description:
Le manque de logements à prix raisonnable dans les grandes villes et le vieillissement de la population offre l’opportunité d’envisager la résolution de deux problématiques avec un œil solidaire :
- en aidant nos ainés à trouver facilement une aide dans les services du quotidien et la prévention dans le cadre du "Bien Vieillir"
- en proposant aux étudiants en soins (IDE, AS, Kinés..) en recherche d’une chambre une opportunité à prix discounté en échange de services
Grandy’s est une plateforme créée grâce au soutien de Korian puis de Humanis Malakoff, les leaders de l’offre de soins en Europe et la synergie volontaire d’étudiants de l’ESSEC BUSINESS SCHOOL avec l’envie de proposer une offre qui ait un impact positif sur la société. 
"Que chacun apporte sa contribution et devienne le bâtisseur d'un monde fondé sur l'entraide" @Serge Desjardins

Name: L'Association pour la Promotion de la REussite Scolaire (L'APRES)
Description:
L’APRES (L’Association pour la Promotion de la REussite Scolaire) est née en 2004. Elle résulte de la volonté d’un groupe d’amis qui veut « tordre le cou » à l’idée reçue que venant de quartiers difficiles, il n’était pas possible de s’en sortir. Ce discours défaitiste a un effet dévastateur parmi les jeunes de ces quartiers.
Devant ce gâchis d’avenir, il était important pour nous d’agir en allant à leur rencontre, afin de les rassurer quant à leurs capacités et de les motiver à faire les efforts nécessaires pour réaliser leurs rêves.

Nous nous sommes fixés comme objectifs :
- D’insister sur la valeur du diplôme (filière professionnelle ou générale) comme clé essentielle de réussite
- De démystifier les études longues qui paraissent pour certains jeunes comme inaccessibles ou réservées à d’autres
Nous voulons aussi sensibiliser les parents à encourager et soutenir leurs enfants dans les études. 

L’APRES a pour ambition, comme son nom l’indique, de promouvoir la réussite scolaire par l’exemplarité.
Pour atteindre son but, l'association propose l'intervention d'adultes diplômés, en milieux scolaire ou associatif. Ils ont en commun d'avoir suivi tout ou partie de leur scolarité en “ZEP”.
Les ingrédients de leur réussite : travail, volonté, effort et ouverture d'esprit. Ils souhaitent aujourd'hui témoigner de leur parcours à travers un discours simple et réaliste.

Name: Marine Credit Union Foundation
Description: In addition to supporting local organizations, we provide critical resources, tailored advice, and encouragement to people with financial challenges to help them build a better future. We operate two primary programs: Community Giving and Finding HOME.

Name: Tucson Values Teachers
Description: Tucson Values Teachers (TVT) is a partnership of business leaders, educators and individuals with a shared mission to help schools and districts attract, retain and support the very best teachers for every PreK-12 classroom in Southern Arizona. We recognize the critical role teachers play in student achievement and the vital influence they have on the future of Tucson and our region. Through our work, we seek to raise public awareness of the value of the teaching profession and provide a range of programs to support teachers professionally and financially. We also leverage our partnerships with the Southern Arizona Leadership Council and other business and education groups to advocate for funding and policies that positively impact teachers, students and schools.

Name: E.A. Michelson Philanthropy
Description:
Throughout our lifetimes, creativity lies waiting to be awakened and expressed, bringing joy, a sense of accomplishment and new connections with our communities. E.A. Michelson Philanthropy supports programs that bring these benefits to people of all ages. 

E.A. Michelson Philanthropy is a private foundation based in Minneapolis, Minnesota. For more information, visit eamichelsonphilanthropy.org.

Name: Urban Society for Aboriginal Youth
Description: The Urban Society for Aboriginal Youth has been an influential not-for-profit organization in Calgary since 2001. USAY strives to provide essential programming and services to Calgary’s Indigenous youth between the ages of twelve and twenty-nine.

Name: Children's Fund
Description: Founded in 1986 by the Honorable Patrick Morris, the late Jack H. Brown, and Co-founder A. Gary Anderson, Children’s Fund is a nonprofit whose mission is giving our vulnerable children support, opportunity, and hope by breaking destructive cycles through community partnerships.  Last year, Children’s Fund provided more than 69,000 services to children in need in San Bernardino County and since its inception has facilitated over 1.5 million points of service to children and families. For more information on Children’s Fund or to learn how you can give a child hope for brighter tomorrows, go to www.childrensfund.org or call 909-379.0000.

Name: Instituto Vertere
Description:
TRANSFORMAR PELA EDUCAÇÃO:  O PODER DAS OLIMPÍADAS DO CONHECIMENTO

Somos o Instituto Vertere. Acreditamos que a educação é a melhor e mais eficaz ferramenta de transformação social do país. Nosso século nos convida a viver aventuras tecnológicas e desafios educacionais, e os alunos, bombardeados por uma diversidade de mídias e conteúdos, perdem o interesse pela educação formal. Excesso e inadequação de conteúdo, e ausência de contextualização e relevância estão entre as críticas mais frequentes, e a dinâmica tradicional de ensino tampouco ajuda.

Em meio a tudo isso, uma grata surpresa: os estudantes brasileiros têm um enorme potencial, e com isso Brasil é particularmente bem-sucedido - em resultados e engajamento - em olimpíadas do conhecimento, com algumas posições de destaque mundial em diferentes modalidades.

Além de ótimas ferramentas de engajamento de alunos, as olimpíadas também contribuem com a educação em outras 3 frentes: são excelentes campos de teste e vitrines para novos projetos pedagógicos, ajudam no diagnóstico da educação no país e servem como instrumento de capacitação e reconhecimento de professores.

Buscamos, através de nosso trabalho, disseminar o exemplo de nossos “atletas olímpicos”.  Eles têm o poder de estimular a ação de muitos, e são a prova de que expandir o conhecimento é possível e está ao alcance de todos. Nossos colégios precisam de campeões olímpicos que sejam ídolos das crianças de seu entorno.

Name: Human Links Foundation
Description: The Human Links Foundation is committed to leveraging its industry resources and networks to deliver empowerment energy initiatives aimed at disenfranchised schools and communities.

Name: Pacific Hospital Preservation & Development Authority
Description:
We envision a world with equity in health for all. We champion equity in health through exemplary stewardship of PHPDA’s public resources.

The Pacific Hospital Preservation & Development Authority (PHPDA) partners with community organizations to improve access to health care services in King County. In 1981, the City of Seattle chartered PHPDA to steward the historic landmark Pacific Tower Campus. We work to advance health equity through the Health Equity Fund grant program, selective Campus programming, and upstream systemic change.

Since 2003, PHPDA has provided more than $20 million in funding for projects and programs to support health access and reduce health disparities.

Name: Arslanian Foundation
Description: La Fondation Arslanian soutient les Arméniens défavorisés à travers des initiatives dans l'éducation, la santé, le logement et l'agriculture. Elle aspire à devenir un modèle de charité ayant un impact concret sur la vie des Arméniens.

Name: CREA Goiás Jovem
Description:
Instituição restrita a participação de acadêmicos dos cursos cadastrados no CREA-GO, com exceção daqueles que ainda não possuem a primeira turma formada e que, por isso, ainda não estão cadastrados no conselho. Os membros do chamado Fórum, conjunto de membros efetivos,  são admitidos via processo seletivo e os trabalhos são exclusivamente voluntários, os trabalhos permeiam desde a gestão de pessoas (Grupo de Trabalho de Recursos Humanos), representação acadêmica (Grupo de Trabalho de Assuntos Acadêmicos) até formulação de artes e campanhas publicitárias (Grupo de Trabalho de Comunicação e Marketing).
     Nós acreditamos que podemos auxiliar na boa formação profissional dos estudantes de engenharia e cursos técnicos através de palestras técnicas, cursos, mini-cursos, visitas técnicas e uma experiência única em gestão que cada membro do CREA-GO Jovem é capaz receber.

Name: Hungry For Life International
Description:
We support leaders to cultivate spiritual vibrancy and human flourishing, resulting in thriving global communities.

Are you looking for an opportunity to make a difference? We can share this journey together, fuelled by a vision of a world transformed. Through challenge and success, we believe it’s worth it. If our work and vision is of interest to you—and you understand the importance of raising personal financial and prayer support—we want to hear from you.

Name: The Hoglund Foundation
Description: The mission of The Hoglund Foundation is to improve the lives of families and at-risk children in the City of Dallas, Texas by partnering with organizations that provide education and family support services.

Name: Empower Assistance Dogs
Description:
Empower Assistance Dogs enhance quality of life and independence for people with disabilities by providing professionally trained and government certified guide, hearing and assistance dogs.  We are the first organisation that is government certified to train all three types of service dogs – guide, hearing and assistance dogs.  The organisation achieves this through a program of selecting, raising and training dogs to assist disabled people with everyday tasks.

Guide, Hearing and Assistance dogs are trained to enhance the quality of life of vision or hearing impaired, and/or physically or mentally challenged persons whilst still maintaining as much independence for the handler. 

Emotional support and Therapy dogs undergo specific training to offer comfort and support in various settings like hospitals or schools. These dogs play crucial roles in contributing a positive impact within an individual/community's mental health and well-being.

Name: Bennelong Foundation
Description: The Bennelong Foundation and Bennelong Foundation UK work hand-in-hand with charities to support participation, empower individuals and strengthen communities. Because when we are part of something bigger - we all win.

Name: The Black Heart Foundation
Description:
The Black Heart Foundation is a UK and US registered charitable organisation dedicated to improving the educational access, quality and outcomes for children from under-privileged backgrounds and those who lack the resources to pursue their ambitions. 

The Foundation’s goal is to inspire aspiration and remove barriers to achievement for the young people it helps and serves.

Name: King Charles III Charitable Fund
Description:
The story began in 1979, when His Majesty The King, or His Royal Highness The Prince of Wales as he was then, established a Fund to support environmental projects in Wales. Over time, King Charles III Charitable Fund has grown in scale and ambition and our reach expanded across the four corners of the United Kingdom, the Commonwealth and beyond.

From the environment, countryside and conservation to social inclusion, health and education, the Fund has supported charitable causes His Majesty cares so deeply about. In the 45 years since its inception, the Fund has invested over £100m in charitable causes through grants and incubating new charitable initiatives.

Much of this good work would not have been possible were it not for the creation of the pioneering Duchy Originals brand in 1990 which was set up to sell organic food and provide a donation to the Fund. An exclusive partnership with Waitrose, established in 2009 and now known as Duchy Organic, has gone from strength to strength, generating in excess of £40m to date to support the Fund’s charitable activities.

Name: TEDxBarletta
Description:
TEDxBarletta è l'evento TED organizzato in maniera indipendente nella città di Barletta dal 2018. 
Stiamo già lavorando al prossimo evento

Name: Rotary Club of Fresno
Description: The Rotary Club of Fresno unites business professionals to take action and create lasting positive transformation in ourselves, our community and across the world.

Name: Real Care
Description:
Real￨Care er en velgørende forening, skabt for samfundsansvarlige aktører i ejendomsbranchen, der har fokus på at hjælpe udsatte børn og unge.

Formål.
Real￨Cares formål er at bidrage til udviklingen af et socialt bæredygtigt samfund med fokus på at skabe trygge rammer og sikre social retfærdighed for udsatte børn og unge.

Vision.
Vores vision er, at udsatte børn og unge skal have lige muligheder for at få et liv i trivsel, hvor de kan bryde den negative social arv og realisere deres fulde potentiale i det samfund, som de lever og befinder sig i. Et liv hvor den enkeltes muligheder ikke afhænger af social oprindelse, men hvor trygge rammer og opvækstbetingelser sikrer social mobilitet og skaber muligheden for et selvforsørgende og velfungerende voksenliv.

Mission.
Real￨Cares mission er at indsamle og forvalte økonomisk støtte fra aktører i ejendomsbranchen til velgørende formål, der understøtter foreningens formål.
Yderligere vil vi gøre det muligt for aktører i ejendomsbranchen at indgå i et engageret, dedikeret og forpligtende samarbejde, hvor de i fællesskab og på tværs af branchen, iværksætter konkrete og dokumenterbare initiativer, der skaber rammer for, at udsatte børn og unge kan få et godt og trygt liv med lige muligheder. Initiativer der rækker ud over vores partneres økonomiske bidrag.

Name: Kitchener Waterloo Community Foundation
Description: On July 1st, 2022 – Cambridge & North Dumfries Community Foundation (CNDCF) and Kitchener Waterloo Community Foundation (KWCF) unified to become Waterloo Region Community Foundation (WRCF). Visit our new page at https://www.linkedin.com/company/wrcommfdn/

Name: Jewish Community Foundation of Montreal
Description:
