Our VISION is a more philanthropic society, enabled by the JCF’s expertise in impactful giving.

Our MISSION is to inspire, enable and optimize philanthropy to meet the ideals, aspirations and growing needs of the Montreal Jewish Community and society at large.

The JCF is committed to the Jewish values of Tzedakah (Charity and Justice), Tikkun Olam (Repairing the World), and Chesed (Caring). Its work is anchored in the values of excellence, respect, trust, transparency, inclusion, education, social responsibility and intergenerational continuity.

The foundation has distributed $133 million to a wide range of charities and programs in 2022.

Name: The Maclellan Foundation
Description: For more than 60 years, a desire to serve Christ and his Kingdom lies at the very heart of the Maclellan Foundation.  As God led us to support various ministries, we have borne the privilege of seeing many come to a saving knowledge of Him, and we continue to strive toward seeing the Great Commission fulfilled: "Therefore go and make disciples of all nations, baptizing them in the name of the Father and of the Son and of the Holy Spirit” (Matthew 28:19).

Name: Ordem dos Economistas - Delegação Regional da Madeira
Description:
A Ordem dos Economistas, associação profissional de direito público representativa dos licenciados na área da ciência económica, nasceu a 2 de Julho de 1998 (DL n.° 174/98 de 27/6), a partir da transformação da Associação Portuguesa de Economistas (APEC).

Consignada no seu Estatuto está uma primeira atribuição que consiste em "Reconhecer o título profissional de Economista aos titulares de licenciaturas na área da ciência económica". Com esse intuito a Ordem organiza a sua actividade no sentido da dignificação e do prestígio da profissão, tendo em conta as vertentes de valorização técnica e das funções ética e social da sua actividade.

Em 20 de Maio de 2004 realizou-se a primeira eleição para os Órgãos da Delegação Regional da Madeira – Mesa da Assembleia e Secretariado (atualmente designado de Direção).

Name: Entrepreneurs Foundation
Description:
Entrepreneurs' Foundation is a non-profit public charity on a mission to encourage, empower and enhance corporate philanthropy and corporate community involvement.  
 
- Entrepreneurs' Foundation encourages companies to be philanthropic by providing guidance and expertise to companies that haven’t yet adopted a community or philanthropy program.
 
- Entrepreneurs' Foundation empowers companies to be philanthropic by providing open sourced resources, tools and best practices and on-line and in-person forums to make it easy for companies to learn on their own or from each other.  www.efbayarea.org/resources-tools/
 
- Entrepreneurs' Foundation enhances corporate philanthropy by providing direct services to companies who have programs in place but need strategic or tactical support to make their programs better.

Name: MaolKeki Foundation
Description: MaolKeki Foundation is a pioneering organization established to bring positive development in Manipur, India. It achieves this purpose by designing and implementing socially impactful initiatives in agriculture, education and healthcare.

Name: Santuário do Divino Pai Eterno
Description:
A Associação Filhos do Pai Eterno nasceu a partir do sonho de reunir o maior número possível de fiéis católicos e devotos, com a finalidade de proporcionar auxílio na vivência da fé e propagar a devoção ao Divino Pai Eterno. A Afipe iniciou suas ações em maio de 2004, e hoje conta com uma equipe de funcionários pronta para atender aos filhos e filhas do Pai Eterno.

Os trabalhos desenvolvidos têm colaborado para o amadurecimento da fé de milhares de famílias que se renovam no compromisso pessoal, comunitário e, principalmente, na vida prática cristã. As doações recebidas são voltadas para a evangelização por meio da TV, para obras sociais e para a construção da Nova Casa do Pai, em Trindade (GO).

Através da doação espontânea e fiel de cada devoto, foi possível montar uma estrutura televisiva, incluindo uma produtora, para transmitir, ao vivo, as celebrações do Santuário Basílica, as Novenas diárias do Divino Pai Eterno e do Perpétuo Socorro e o Santo Terço dos Filhos do Pai Eterno, além do Programa Pai Eterno.

A Associação também apoia as obras sociais que têm por objetivo acolher crianças, adolescentes, jovens e adultos em situação de vulnerabilidade social, proporcionando a essas pessoas e suas famílias maior integração com a sociedade, além de evangelizá-las.

As doações dos devotos também são direcionadas para a realização de melhorias na estrutura física do Santuário Basílica e, agora, passam a ter mais um destino: a construção do Novo Santuário.

Name: Deborah's Place
Description:
Believing that everyone has a right to safe, clean and affordable housing, Deborah's Place has been breaking the cycle of homelessness for women in Chicago since 1985.  Originally a volunteer-run emergency overnight shelter, today we are Chicago's largest provider of supportive housing for women.  

The mission of Deborah's Place is to open doors of opportunity for women who are homeless in Chicago. Supportive housing and services offer women their key to healing, achieving their goals and moving on from the experience of homelessness

Deborah's Place is a registered 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization.

Name: Unbound Philanthropy
Description:
Unbound Philanthropy: Welcoming newcomers. Strengthening communities.


Unbound Philanthropy is an independent private grantmaking foundation that invests in leaders and organizations in the US and UK working to build a vibrant, welcoming society and just immigration system, as part of a pluralist and inclusive democracy.

We focus our work in three areas, which are highly connected:

1. Activate Public Support: We seek to activate public support for an immigration system rooted in justice and a welcoming society.

2. Ensure a Just System: We seek an immigration system and policies that are rooted in justice, honor the dignity of all people, and that advance the common good.

3. Strengthen Belonging: We strive for welcoming communities where all people belong and can fully participate in social, civic, and economic life.

Name: Nuevo Amanecer A.B.P.
Description:
Nos dedicamos a mejorar la calidad de vida de niños y jóvenes con parálisis cerebral hasta su edad adulta, incluyendo a sus familias; a través de un modelo de atención integral que impulsa su máximo nivel de autonomía y la inclusión a su entorno.

En 1978 un matrimonio regiomontano buscó ayuda profesional en Monterrey para rehabilitar a su hijo con parálisis cerebral. No encontraron ningún lugar en donde se ofrecieran todas las terapias y atenciones que requiere un niño con esta discapacidad. Deciden entonces iniciar el esfuerzo de apoyar integralmente a estos niños de familias que no contaran con los recursos económicos suficientes para brindarles una atención especializada.

Así, hace más de tres décadas nace el Instituto Nuevo Amanecer brindando atención a seis pacientes con tres terapistas y dos maestras ofreciéndoles terapia física y educación, con el objetivo de mejorar su calidad de vida.

Conoce más del Instituto visitando la página www.nuevoamanecer.edu.mx
Location: San Pedro Garza García, Nuevo León

Name: Air Force Academy Foundation
Description:
The Air Force Academy Foundation and the Association of Graduates are together transforming to better serve graduates, support the Air Force Academy and preserve the proud heritage of the institution and the Long Blue Line. 

𝐌𝐢𝐬𝐬𝐢𝐨𝐧
Together, we support the Academy, serve our graduates and preserve the heritage of the institution.

𝐕𝐢𝐬𝐢𝐨𝐧
To be an Association and Foundation of influence and impact, supporting the Academy’s enduring mission of developing leaders of character for the nation, and providing a lifetime of service to the Long Blue Line.
Location: United States Air Force Academy, Colorado

Name: Respon.cat
Description:
[ca] Respon.cat, iniciativa empresarial per al desenvolupament de la responsabilitat social a Catalunya
[es] Respon.cat, iniciativa empresarial para el desarrollo de la responsabilidad social en Cataluña
[en] Respon.cat, a business initiative for the development of social responsibility in Catalonia

Name: Cáritas del Perú
Description:
Organización de la Iglesia Católica que se dedica a la ejecución de programas y proyectos de desarrollo humano integral que contribuyan a brindar condiciones de vida más dignas a los sectores pobres y excluidos del Perú.
Red Nacional conformada por 48 oficinas diocesanas que trabaja en las siguientes áreas: Salud y nutrición; Desarrollo económico productivo; Educación; Ayuda humanitaria, Gestión de riesgos y emergencias; Sistemas de agua y saneamiento básico; Desarrollo local.

Name: Latino Community Fund (LCF Georgia)
Description:
Latinos, Latinas, Latinx,
Hispanos, Latino Americans in Georgia.

WE HELP GEORGIA GROW.

Collectively, we do more.
we champion community issues;
we support our organizations;
we invest in our families and youth;
we drive change with positive narratives;
we speak our truth and we strengthen our identity.
There is power in us and there is strength in our numbers.
We have nothing to lose but everything to gain.

Latino Community Fund (LCF Georgia) is a catalyst for investment, collaborative work and positive narrative.  We work within and with the Latinx/Hispanic Community in Georgia by supporting Latino-serving nonprofits.

We Amplify Our Voice and Drive Positive Narratives, we create space for the diversity of our voices and recognize contributions, we advance knowledge of our community, we consolidate information, we fight for a seat at the table and build our own tables. 

We Build Capacity in Latino-serving nonprofits by providing low-fee or free technical assistance in the areas of development and fundraising strategies, meeting planning, contract negotiating, grants writing, meeting facilitation, communications, storytelling, coalition building and advocacy training.

We Invest in our Community through collaborative grantmaking, direct scholarships, capital investments in micro-entrepreneurs, civic engagement and disaster relief and operational funds.

Name: Community Foundation for MetroWest
Description: The Community Foundation for MetroWest improves the quality of life in 30+ MetroWest communities by providing financial and educational resources to local nonprofit organizations, sharing essential data about our region to inform decision making, and partnering with donors to help guide and align their giving with the areas of greatest need in the community.

Name: Draper Richards Kaplan Foundation Nederland
Description: De Draper Richards Kaplan Foundation werd opgericht met als basis het geloof in de kracht van innovatie en de overtuiging dat met stevige ondersteuning, gepassioneerde individuen met geweldige ideeën de wereld kunnen veranderen. De DRK Foundation is een wereldwijd opererende filantropische organisatie die sociale ondernemingen in een vroeg stadium ondersteunt. Wij geloven dat met vroegtijdige financiering en intensieve ondersteuning uitzonderlijke ondernemers die een aantal van de meest complexe problemen in de samenleving aanpakken, van de wereld een betere plek kunnen maken. De DRK Foundation opereert sinds 2020 ook in Europa.

Name: Mannifera
Description:
Mannifera is a collective of Australian funders backing civil society organisations advocating for the systems change we need to strengthen our democracy and build an economy that works for everyone. 

Our vision is of a more complete democracy and an economy with fairness at its heart, protected by a robust and vibrant civil society sector. 

Since our inception in 2019, we’ve grown to 24 members and have delivered over $3.5 million in grants to 50 civil society and First Nations-led organisations. We’ve also convened over 70 events, helping to foster shared learning and build a more connected civil society and philanthropic movement. 

As a collective we are committed to listening to and learning from First Nations perspectives on the state of our democracy and economy, and we have worked to build a First Nations-led approach into our practice by having First Nations advisors guide our granting to First Nations-led solutions.

Name: Terres d'Ophtalmo
Description: Terres d'Ophtalmo  (TO) is an association law 1901, created by the initiative of a group of ophthalmologists and non-doctors, volunteers, with experience of humanitarian action or simply the will to act and support actions in favor of beneficiaries.

Name: Capitol Impact
Description:
Capitol Impact enriches the quality of life for California's most vulnerable populations. Our firm brings an entrepreneurial approach to leadership and consulting, with a track record for catalyzing small initiatives to statewide movements.

Among the firm's current management projects are some of the California's most high-impact policy initiatives:

* Alliance of Regional Collaboratives to Heighten Education Success (ARCHES)
* California Legislative Staff Education Institute
* CORE Districts
* James Irvine Foundation Leadership Awards
* Regional K-16 Education Collaboratives Grant Program
* Talent Pipeline Management

Name: EMI-Khayr
Description:
Le comité EMIKAYR s’engage pour son unique mission qui est celle de la création d’un monde d’abondance de partage et de corrélation entre les différentes tranches de la société. Ses Élèves Ingénieurs militent pour la cause humanitaire dans une perspective visant à remettre en valeur la subsistance humaine et l’importance de son assurance, déconnectés de l’impératif des dissimilitudes
sociales. 
Notre comité lutte contre la vivisection du nécessiteux et milite pour un futur hors du territoire ploutocratique, un futur où la bienfaisance et la bonhomie représentent le vrai nerf des choses.

Name: Friendship France
Description:
Depuis le Bangladesh, un pays confronté aux défis les plus pressants de l'humanité, Friendship développe des solutions innovantes reproductibles pour renforcer les communautés marginalisées et donner à chacun les moyens de transformer sa vie et réaliser pleinement son potentiel.

  Depuis 2015, Friendship France, membre de l’ONG internationale Friendship, agit avec conviction pour promouvoir la dignité, les droits fondamentaux et la résilience des populations les plus vulnérables du Bangladesh, en partenariat étroit avec Friendship Bangladesh.

 Grâce à une approche holistique du développement, nos projets répondent aux besoins essentiels de manière intégrée, durable et profondément humaine. Nous intervenons notamment dans les zones fluviales et les camps Rohingyas de Cox’s Bazar, avec pour objectif de renforcer l’autonomie et la résilience des communautés.

 Nos axes d’action :
	• Sauver des vies
	• Réduire la pauvreté
	• S’adapter au changement climatique
	• Favoriser l’autonomisation

 Friendship France, association loi 1901, développe une contribution spécifique à travers des missions de conseil en stratégie de développement, en management et gouvernance, et par la formation des équipes du siège mais aussi des équipes opérationnelles sur le terrain. Pour ce, Friendship France mobilise son réseau d’experts, conseils, docteurs et formateurs, tout au long de l’année.
En retour, Friendship Bangladesh apporte la compréhension des enjeux locaux, dans l’élaboration de programmes répondant aux besoins des communautés et dans l’innovation, via des solutions d’adaptation et de résilience au changement climatique notamment.

Nous menons également des actions de sensibilisation de l’opinion publique, en France, sur le changement climatique et le développement humanitaire en participant à des conférences, événements, en dispensant des formations, et à travers notre communication.

 Nos valeurs fondamentales:
Intégrité – Dignité – Qualité – Justice – Espoir

Name: Good Ventures
Description:
Established in 2011, Good Ventures is a foundation whose mission is to help humanity thrive. 

For more information about our approach to philanthropy, visit www.goodventures.org. Explore our portfolio of grants at www.goodventures.org/our-portfolio.

Name: Greater Worcester Community Foundation
Description:
Greater Worcester Community Foundation is central Massachusetts’ leading community foundation, employing its expertise and resources to address the region’s most urgent needs. 

Since 1975, it has worked in partnership with donors to build a $200 million endowment composed of more than 600 named charitable funds and award over $167 million in grants that have helped transform communities and build a strong and vibrant region. 

For more information on the Foundation’s programs and how to give, please visit www.greaterworcester.org.

Name: The Jensen Project
Description:
We believe we’re stronger together.

As a rape survivor, our founder Janet Jensen discovered that organizations are doing the hard work needed, but faced similar obstacles.

This journey led her to create The Jensen Project, unifying local nonprofits in the fight against sexual violence.

Name: Statistics for Sustainable Development
Description:
Statistics for Sustainable Development (Stats4SD) is a not-for-profit, social enterprise established in May 2016. We promote better use of statistical methods for decision making to benefit society and the environment.

To achieve this, we advise on:

- The use statistical tools in the real world
- Effective and efficient use of research methods
- Data engineering, data analysis and associated information technology
- Generation of useful information products derived from data

We work as a team and with our partners throughout the world, supporting sustainable development processes. Much of our work takes place in developing countries, however our areas of work are not determined by geography but by the contribution we can make towards fulfilling the objectives for which we were set up.

Statistics for Sustainable Development (Stats4SD) has been established to: 

- Contribute towards better research designs, more efficient data and information gathering, and production of relevant information for decision making.
- Provide services to a global community of people working towards sustainable development.

We charge for these services to cover our costs but we do not make a profit. Any surpluses from our operations are invested in the work defined by our goals of promoting better use of statistics and capacity building.

Name: Ruth Mott Foundation
Description: The Ruth Mott Foundation is a private family foundation established by Ruth Mott, philanthropist and wife of automotive pioneer Charles Stewart Mott. The Ruth Mott Foundation’s mission is to connect people, ideas, and resources for a vibrant and inclusive Flint. In 2016, the Ruth Mott Foundation shifted to a place-based focus on north Flint. At the core of this strategic focus is a commitment to support community voiced and driven priorities and plans.

Name: Otis & Rosie Brown Foundation
Description: The Extraordinary Is movement is a social campaign created by the Otis and Rosie Brown Foundation, a 501(c)(3) charitable organization.

Name: SOS Ação Mulher e Família
Description:
Entidade de utilidade pública municipal e estadual, fundada em Campinas/SP em 1980, o SOS Ação Mulher e Família promove o atendimento psicológico/social/jurídico de mulheres, homens, adolescentes e crianças em situação de violência de gênero, violência doméstica e intrafamiliar.

Como Colaborar com o SOS Ação Mulher e Família:
Trabalho Voluntário nas áreas de psicologia, serviço social, eventos e captação de recursos
Associado Mantenedor
Doação em espécie ou produtos
Destinação de crédito da Nota Fiscal Paulista
Patrocínio de projetos e profissionais técnicos

Name: Unité Du Monde
Description:
L'association UNITE DU MONDE favorise l’apprentissage de valeurs responsables et humanitaires chez les jeunes, via une participation à des activités sportives et pédagogiques.

Plus d’informations sur l'association sur: 

www.UniteduMonde.com
https://www.facebook.com/unitedumonde/

Name: Michael Reese Health Trust
Description: Michael Reese Health Trust envisions a future where every community has the resources needed to be a healthy and safe place for people to live, learn, work and play. We partner with stakeholders across Chicago to help individuals and communities reach and maintain health by prioritizing some of the most urgent health challenges facing our city while remaining committed to honoring our Jewish legacy. By using our tools of advocacy, incubation and grantmaking, we go deep within an issue to create real, lasting change.

Name: Community Foundation of Utah
Description: The Community Foundation of Utah is Utah's catalyst for philanthropy that is innovative, data-driven, and serves the community in perpetuity.

Name: YesLiberia, Inc.
Description:
We provide and create educational opportunities for talent to thrive in Africa. 

YesLiberia is a 501c3 non-profit (NPO) that is constantly remodeling itself with unconventional and cost-effective ideas to provide services despite the limitless challenges we face.  We use innovative program models to address problems effecting youths in Africa. Our mission is to empower young people through meaningful service learning opportunities in education, healthcare, and technology. We do this through strategic partnerships and learning models while respecting the communities in which we work.        

To date, we’ve given over 480 scholarships in Africa; partnered with 14 schools in Africa; worked with approximately 90 volunteers and interns; provided experiential learning projects for course credits; provided school supplies for over 3000 children; sent 4000 text books and reading books to Africa; and collaborated with student organizations at 8 universities across the USA. Simply put, if you’re a good citizen, a student organization, a business, or a religious organization, we are the partner you’ve been looking for; if you’re an individual with leadership skills, we invite you to join our team of amazing interns/volunteers, and advocates.  You may also visit our website at www.YesLiberia.org

Name: Kids for Peace/Kindness Certified Companies
Description:
Our Mission: We create peace through youth leadership, community service, global friendships, and thoughtful acts of kindness.

Our History: Kids for Peace is a California-based global nonprofit that is uplifting our world through love and action. Inspired by Gandhi’s quote, “If we are to achieve lasting peace, we must begin with the children,” Kids for Peace was founded in 2006 by a mom and a high school honors student. In ten years, Kids for Peace has launched over 350 chapters with our projects reaching 121 countries. Our chapters are made up of boys & girls of all ages and socioeconomic backgrounds. Steadily, Kids for Peace has grown from a "club of caring kids" to a worldwide movement of kids creating a culture of peace, love and kindness.

Everything we do is based on our Peace Pledge. Kids meet regularly to put the words of our Peace Pledge into action in ways that are personally meaningful and culturally relevant.

Our PEACE PLEDGE
I pledge to use my words to speak in a kind way.
I pledge to help others as I go throughout my day.
I pledge to care for our earth with my healing heart and hands.
I pledge to respect people in each and every land.
I pledge to join together as we unite the big and small.
I pledge to do my part to create peace for one and all.

Featured on Good Morning America, our signature program is The Great Kindness Challenge, a school-wide bully-prevention initiative. In 2017, we engaged over 10 million students in 15,057 schools who inspired over half a billion acts of kindness. 

With optimism and tenacity, we work to fulfill our mission and vision. Our vision is a safe and peaceful world where all people respect and care for each other and our planet. Kids for Peace serves as a model and inspiration for creating this reality with children leading the way, not only for today, but for generations to come.

Name: Michael and Suzanne Borrin Foundation
Description:
The Michael and Suzanne Borrin Foundation is a New Zealand philanthropic organisation that was created by a generous bequest of $38 million from the late Judge Ian Borrin. The foundation is the Borrin family's gift to New Zealand. 

Our vision:   We believe law is essential to a flourishing society – one that is just, inclusive, tolerant and free.  Our vision is of an Aotearoa New Zealand where everyone understands the role and value of the law, and everyone enjoys the protection and opportunity that it provides. 

Our mission:   We support legal research, education and scholarship that contributes to our vision for Aotearoa New Zealand. 

We aim to fund high-performing people and organisations to do work that will have a significant and enduring impact on the lives of New Zealanders, through the law.

Name: Spectaculu - Escola de Arte e Tecnologia
Description:
A Spectaculu é uma organização sem fins lucrativos que há 25 anos atua no Cais do Porto do Rio de Janeiro. A instituição oferece capacitação profissional nas áreas de Arte e Tecnologia para jovens de 17 a 21 anos, oriundos da rede pública de ensino e moradores de regiões de vulnerabilidade social do Grande. Nossa missão é gerar oportunidades de desenvolvimento através da educação e oferecer uma possibilidade real de empregabilidade, inserindo jovens talentos no mundo dos espetáculos para suprir a grande demanda da indústria criativa na cidade.

Entre os cursos oferecidos, estão Adereços de Cena, Beleza, Contrarregragem & Camarim, Tratamento de Imagem e Fotografia Digital, Iluminação Cênica, Montagem de Cenários, Vídeo & Edição e Mídias Sociais. A Spectaculu também proporciona aos alunos formação nas disciplinas transversais de Filosofia, Olhar e Palavra, que trabalham o pensamento crítico, expressão corporal, interpretação, criatividade e contexto social e histórico.

A escola é uma iniciativa da sociedade civil com o intuito de auxiliar a região metropolitana do Rio de Janeiro e seus jovens a enfrentarem dificuldades sociais e econômicas. Os alunos recebem duas refeições diárias, material para aulas e uma bolsa-auxílio de R$400,00 para os custos com deslocamento via transporte público.

Cerca de 150 alunos em vulnerabilidade social passam pela escola anualmente, oriundos de diversos territórios periféricos do Rio de Janeiro e outros municípios da região metropolitana. A localização da instituição, situada ao lado da Rodoviária Novo Rio (Zona Portuária), é essencial para essa alta mobilidade. A escola já formou 3.000 jovens e inseriu seus jovens técnicos em quase 6.000 vagas de trabalho fixas e temporárias.

Fundada em 1999 pelo diretor artístico e arquiteto Gringo Cardia e pela atriz e cantora Marisa Orth, a Spectaculu conta com o apoio da classe artística e tem três importantes colaboradores na direção de suas atividades: o artista plástico Vik Muniz, a diretora

Name: Health Warriors
Description:
Nacemos un 16 de marzo de 2020 a consecuencia de la pandemia del Covid-19. En un principio, nos centramos en dar soporte a los sanitarios de los hospitales del Área Metropolitana de Barcelona que estaban completamente colapsados.

Gracias a la colaboración de restaurantes, empresas y particulares que nos ofrecieron su ayuda , pudimos repartir más de 30.000 raciones de comida.

Una crisis de esta magnitud dejó a un gran número de familias sin recursos para acceder a un alimentación básica y es por esto, que decidimos unir esfuerzos también en esta dirección.

El mes de mayo fue clave, el estrés en las ucis empezaba a disminuir y la necesidad en la calle cada vez era mayor. Fue entonces cuando lo tuvimos claro, no podíamos quedarnos indiferentes ante esta situación.

Son muchas las entidades a las que ofrecemos alimentos y nuestro principal objetivo es poder aumentar esta ayuda semana tras semana. En la actualidad, hemos donado cerca de 215 toneladas de alimento.

Name: Ripples of Change Foundation
Description: ‘Solve that one problem that solves all’ forms the foundation of our thinking leading us to evolve solutions for the society to thrive in a world of constant, rapid and exponential change with balanced outcomes

Name: Women's Foundation of the South
Description: The Womens Foundation of the South is a nonprofit public foundation that addresses racial and gender equity by shifting philanthropic resources to the womxn of color

Name: The Africa Philanthropy Network (Formerly The African Grantmakers Network)
Description:
The African Philanthropy Network (Formerly the African Grantmakers Network) is a continent-wide network of emerging and established African philanthropy organizations.  Our mission is to be a platform that promotes an African voice and agenda for philanthropy. 

We strive to adopt and scale up effective giving strategies for our members to enhance the standards of philanthropy on the Continent for increased impact.  We believe the origin and basis for finding the ‘voice’ of African philanthropy is an improved environment for local philanthropy across African states, and a strong influence on Pan African and country level policy improvements on giving. 

By knowing what others are doing, our work provides a basis for collaborating to map private and public giving in Africa; understanding how these resources are allocated; creating a unified knowledge platform that targets giving to high-impact development needs and promotes sustainable investments. 

We achieve our Mission by: 
- Creating and curating African inspired content that informs our members about developments in the Sector.
- Coordinating demand-driven peer learning opportunities for our members to share unique indigenous approaches on philanthropy and best practices across the Continent.  
- Creating an enabling environment for policy change by forming strategic partnerships with change actors and leaders to develop practical actions for governments that support the spirit of giving and sharing.
- Strengthening systems within philanthropy institutions for more efficient governance and management of resources.
- Convening Annual Assemblies whose outcomes influence the design and development of our programmes. The Assemblies bring together our African and non-African members to discuss emerging trends and issues in Africa's Philanthropy landscape.

Visit www.africaphilanthropy.org to find out more about our work.

Name: Cordes Foundation
Description: The Cordes Foundation, founded in 2006, connects social entrepreneurs with the resources they need, convenes events to strengthen the ecosystems of impact investing and social entrepreneurship, and catalyzes 100% of its balance sheet for impact.

Name: RETAS LEEDS - Refugee Education Training Advice Service
Description:
Established in 2002, RETAS (Refugee Education Training Advice Service) has a vision to see all refugees, asylum seekers and other vulnerable migrants to feel welcome and supported to fully integrate into society. 

We provide assistance with access to education, re-qualification, training and employment to help them better integrate in their communities. We strive to build confidence and hope in their clients, and empower them to build a safe and secure life here in the UK.

To achieve this we offer specialist employment, integration and advocacy services to refugees and asylum seekers which include:

- An initial ‘Welcome to Leeds’ orientation service
- Our 28 Day Transition Project to enable asylum seekers who have very recently been granted refugee status   to transition successfully from living as asylum seekers in HO accommodation, to living independently in alternative accommodation and integrating successfully into local communities
- An 8 weeks Steps to Settlement taught programme
- ESOL classes
- IELTS (International English Language Testing System)
- 1 to 1 information, advice and guidance to support with accessing education and employment opportunities
- Employability workshops including: managing basic skills to university applications; volunteering and work placements.

Name: Verband für gemeinnütziges Stiften
Description: Der Verband für gemeinnütziges Stiften ist die politisch unabhängige Interessensvertretung gemeinnützig aktiver Stiftungen in Österreich. Diese setzen sich in den Bereichen Bildung und Soziales, Kunst und Kultur, Wissenschaft und Forschung, Umwelt und darüber hinaus ein. Seit 2014 vertritt und vernetzt der Verband selbstständig und unabhängig gemeinnützige Akteure in und für Österreich. Er versteht sich als Sprachrohr gegenüber Politik und Gesellschaft. Ziel des Verbandes ist es, seine Mitglieder zu vernetzen und sie in ihrem engagierten Handeln bestmöglich zu unterstützen.

Name: The Edna McConnell Clark Foundation
Description:
For five decades, the Edna McConnell Clark Foundation (EMCF), established by a daughter of the founder of Avon Products and her husband, has sought to help people and communities in need. Since 1999, it has championed economically disadvantaged youth, developing a collaborative, performance-based investment strategy that increases philanthropy’s impact exponentially.

Now the Foundation is investing all its assets over the next decade in making an enduring impact on the lives of children and families mired in poverty in the U.S. Its staff and operations have transitioned to Blue Meridian Partners, a collaborative philanthropic investment vehicle that EMCF incubated and in which it is a General Partner. This independent organization is helping EMCF fulfill its commitments and complete its limited-life strategy efficiently and responsibly.

Name: Merage Foundation Israel
Description:
The Merage Foundation Israel (MFI) is a private philanthropic foundation established by David and Laura Merage. We are venture philanthropists bringing the highest standards of the business world to accelerate social impact– professionalism, innovation, boldness, dynamism, forward-thinking. Our vision is a  a prosperous and cohesive Israeli society that is committed to the wellbeing of all its citizens. We believe in the power of empowerment, leadership development and community building.  We incubate and accelerate circles of change—those who are strengthened, strengthen others, and lead the way to promoting the prosperity of Israel and the wellbeing of all its citizens. 

We work in 5 main fields:
1.	We are promoting prosperity and demographic growth in the Negev by accelerating regional economic clusters of innovation and revitalizing city centers. 

2.	We are building a renewed sense of community and solidarity among army reservists by training and supporting community leaders within the reserve units. 

3.	We are connecting hundreds of high school and university students to train senior citizens in using their smartphones—strengthening the social and family ties, while building the resilience and independence of thousands of senior citizens in Israel

4.	We are scaling Wings, Israel’s leading lone soldier program, supporting over 3,500 young Olim from the moment they arrive to Israel and years after they return to civilian life. 

5.	We are promoting the advancement of the Iranian community in Israel through women leadership programs and the exposure of the Persian culture to Israeli society

Name: FADICA - Catholic Philanthropy Network
Description:
FADICA - Catholic Philanthropy Network, is network of foundations and donors supporting Catholic activities and initiatives. United by shared values, we go farther together, multiplying impact through shared learning, engagement with Catholic leadership, and collaboration.

FADICA accomplishes this mission through ongoing education, fostering the exchange of information and experience, commissioning research, building a spirit of fellowship and shared faith, facilitating occasional joint funding ventures, and promoting interaction with Catholic leadership.

Name: HOPE (Humanitarian Organization Promoting Equity)
Description:
HOPE (Humanitarian Organization Promoting Equity) est une association humanitaire et sociale qui est présente à Lille, Paris et Sophia Antipolis au sein des campus de SKEMA Business School. 

Les différentes entités ont des missions locales différentes et développent des projets en commun, comme l'installation de 3 ruches d'abeilles, symboliquement une par campus, à Sophia Antipolis pour promouvoir la biodiversité.

HOPE - Lille : 
L'association est composée de 5 missions : trois missions internationales au Vietnam, Cambodge et Sénégal, (aide matérielle dans des orphelinats, écoles et centre pour enfants des rues); ainsi que deux missions locales, Action sociale (promotion du développement durable, aide aux sans-abris, aide aux réfugiés, don du sang...), et Tutorat (soutien scolaire pour une trentaine de collégiens, organisation de sorties culturelles et d'un voyage de fin d'année). 

HOPE - Paris : 
L'association s'organise autour de 2 missions : une mission locale d’aide aux sans-abris, d’aide aux orphelins et aux enfants issus de milieux défavorisés et de l’immigration (accès à l’enseignement, parrainage, récoltes de jouets pour Noël...), et de développement des prêts solidaires destinés à dynamiser les économies de régions pauvres; et une mission humanitaire internationale au Népal (rénovation d’une école, envoi de matériel scolaire et pédagogique).

HOPE - Sophia Antipolis : 
L'association allie l'humanitaire au niveau local avec des missions pour la croix rouge, l'organisation d'un gala de charité pour une association pour enfants hospitalisés, et au niveau international avec une mission internationale au Maroc pour une école pour enfants atteints de trisomie 21, et une au Brésil (construction d'un centre pour enfants défavorisés à Belo Horizonte avec la fondation Camille Muris). Elle a également une mission sociale avec du tutorat proposé aux collégiens de la région, et une sensibilité forte à l'environnement (distribution de paniers bios aux étudiants...).

Name: Connecticut Health Foundation
Description: Everyone deserves the opportunity to be as healthy as possible — regardless of race, background or income level. We are dedicated to improving the health of all Connecticut residents. We focus on improving health outcomes for people of color and ensuring that all Connecticut residents have access to affordable health care.

Name: HUMANA
Description: HUMANA, foundation that enables the philanthropic organizations with web technology, education and business process

Name: IgnitedThinkers
Description: A non-profit organization to spread space education to all by providing opportunities, knowledge, and breaking all stereotypes.

Name: America's Charities Engage CSR Solution
Description:
A Holistic CSR Solution from the Market’s Leading Technology and Service Provider Ready to Help
From corporate social responsibility (CSR) and philanthropy to employee giving and engagement, and from tracking, measuring, and sharing your organization’s impact, Engage is the most comprehensive and flexible solution for streamlining all your community programs and maximizing your social investments.

Engage seamlessly blends together Americas’ Charities’ 40 years of experience in helping organizations meet their giving and engagement goals, along with SmartSimple’s SaaS cloud platform – Platform3 – preferred by more than 350 leading organizations worldwide. One solution brings two experts together to optimize your experience and social impact.

Name: Fundación Alimerka
Description:
La Fundación Alimerka persigue el bienestar de los colectivos más vulnerables o desfavorecidos, procurando aliviar las carencias o necesidades originadas por la privación material, las enfermedades o cualquier otra circunstancia personal, incluida la diversidad funcional o social.

MISIÓN: Desarrollar programas relacionados, preferentemente, con la alimentación, la salud y la calidad de vida de colectivos vulnerables.

VISIÓN: Apoyar y visibilizar a los miembros de colectivos vulnerables con baja representación en la sociedad: #NuestrosVecinosInvisibles.

Name: The Australian Literacy & Numeracy Foundation
Description:
The Australian Literacy and Numeracy Foundation (ALNF), established in 1999, is a national charity assisting people in our most marginalised communities to gain vital language, literacy and communication skills. ALNF’s specialised and innovative programs address the needs of a diverse range of individuals and communities – specifically supporting First Nations, refugee and other vulnerable Australians. 

ALNF believes that literacy is freedom- literacy allows individuals to access education, participate meaningfully in our society and to have a voice. It is a basic human right. 

We see a world where everyone, wherever they come from, however they identify, can confidently express themselves, engage with society and share their stories, embracing the extraordinary opportunities life brings.

Name: Sermig Arsenale della Pace
Description: Il Sermig - Servizio Missionario Giovani - è nato nel 1964 da un'intuizione di Ernesto Olivero, sua moglie Maria e un gruppo di giovani con un sogno condiviso da molti: sconfiggere la fame con opere di giustizia e di sviluppo, vivere la solidarietà verso i più poveri e dare una speciale attenzione ai giovani cercando insieme a loro le vie della pace.

Name: AMSS - Association Malienne pour la Survie au Sahel
Description:
L’Association Malienne pour la Survie au Sahel (AMSS est une organisation non gouvernementale nationale créée en 1991 et qui promeut et soutient les solutions durables aux problèmes des personnes vulnérables au Nord du Mali à travers une approche transversale en matière de Gouvernance et de construction de la Paix.

 

Elle a son siège à Tombouctou en 6ème région. Depuis, 1992 elle est active dans le Septentrion à travers la mise en œuvre de divers projets et programmes de développement et humanitaires couvrant plusieurs domaines dont l’assistance aux populations affectées par les conflits à travers des actions incluant la sécurité alimentaire, l’éducation, la gouvernance, le relèvement économique, l’approvisionnement en eau potable et l’assainissement, l’assistance d’urgence et la cohésion sociale.

Name: Tom Coughlin Jay Fund Foundation
Description:
Our mission is to help families tackle childhood cancer by providing comprehensive financial, emotional and practical support. From diagnosis to recovery and beyond, we are part of the team, allowing parents to solely focus on their child’s well being. Our goal is to BE THERE for parents facing the unthinkable so they can be there for their families.

Families battling childhood cancer are faced with the unthinkable. And what compounds their anxiety is the financial uncertainty they face. Take heart. From helping with household bills to financial game planning to simply making a child’s life brighter, we’re here for parents so they can BE THERE for their child.

Name: Mercury One, Inc.
Description:
Mercury One is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization committed to the restoration of the human spirit. Mercury One seeks to restore Honor through Faith, Courage through Hope, and Love through Charity. 

Mercury One is committed to taking action through our humanitarian aid campaigns, disaster relief efforts, and education initiatives.

Our goal is to inspire, organize and mobilize individuals to improve the human condition physically, emotionally and spiritually with malice towards none and charity for all.

Name: Setec Foundation
Description:
Created in June 2020 and sheltered by the Fondation de France, the Setec Foundation, is dedicated to funding initiatives that directly contribute to the environmental transformation in the fight against climate change, biodiversity restoration, and marine environment conservation.

Aware of the significant social and societal problems associated with environmental change, the foundation considers fight against poverty (precariousness, energy sovereignty, food, transparency, access to education and treatment, …) to be an important corollary of its action.
 

The Foundation supports cultural activities that raise social questions about these themes. And the Foundation is particularly responsive to projects with social vocations such as integration, training, and education.

The Setec Foundation is sheltered by the Fondation de France. In this context, it has committed to an initial endowment of 200,000 euros over 5 years (2020-2024).

This sum can be supplemented by contribution from other partners, development of co-financing and individual contribution.

The Setec Foundation has awarded 188 000 euros in total to the winners of the 2020 and 2021 call for projets thanks to the additional contributions of the Setec Group’s subsidiaries to its endowment.

Name: Ask Miss A
Description:
With a presence in 21 U.S. markets, Miss A is the first and leading online volunteer community covering the intersection of charity and life. With a team of 30 editors and hundreds of writers, we write about charity meeting a part of life – fashion, beauty, sports & fitness, home, food, kids, men, entertainment, business, technology, travel, and education. We shine a spotlight on the best nonprofits, cause marketing products and services, corporate philanthropy and philanthropic people to inspire others to give as they live. Since so much of the Arts are supported by philanthropy, we love covering the arts – performing arts, music, dance, theatre and film.

Founded in 2008 by Andrea Rodgers who has a strong philanthropic track record of her own in Washington, DC, Miss A believes in the positive power of volunteering and charity — not only to benefit those less fortunate, but to improve the individual, business or brand that gives their time, talent and treasure to a cause.

Name: Carina Initiatives
Description:
Let's send more kids from more communities to the frontiers of science and technology.

Innovation in science and technology is shaping all aspects of society in the 21st century, yet too few communities are participating in this innovation. 

In order to get more people to the innovation table, we must address inequity in our math talent system. Math is fundamental to science, engineering, and computer science, fields that serve as the gateway to the frontiers of science and technology. Yet, most high-achieving kids in the U.S. do not have access to high quality math talent development. 

We want to support the creation of a better math talent system. A system that believes more kids are capable of reaching the frontiers of science and technology and a system that does more to help kids succeed at the highest levels of math and computer science.

We believe a robust math talent system should develop early problem solving identity in all kids, build deep math pathways throughout K-12 for those who develop high interest and motivation, and support top universities in recruiting and supporting students from a broader talent base.

Name: Lar Madre Regina
Description:
Inaugurado em 25 de novembro de 1997, em comemoração ao primeiro centenário da chegada das Irmãs de Santa Catarina ao Brasil, o Lar Madre Regina é administrado pela Associação Congregação de Santa Catarina, instituição privada filantrópica.

Atualmente, o Lar Madre Regina abriga 80 idosos, de ambos os sexos, com idade igual ou superior a 60 anos, moradores da região de Guarulhos e adjacências, em vulnerabilidade, risco social e/ou fragilidade familiar.

Name: Stiftung Kindergesundheit
Description:
Die Gesundheit unserer Kinder ist eine entscheidende Voraussetzung sowohl für ihre individuellen Lebens- und Entwicklungschancen als auch für die Zukunft und das Wohlergehen der Gemeinschaft insgesamt.
Die Stiftung Kindergesundheit engagiert sich aktiv für ein gesundes Aufwachsen von Kindern und Jugendlichen, auf wissenschaftlicher Grundlage. 
Sie informiert die Öffentlichkeit zu wichtigen Gesundheitsfragen und erarbeitet in Kooperation mit Kompetenzpartnern und anerkannten Spezialisten Präventionsmaßnahmen z. B. für KITAS, Kindergärten und Schulen.
Sie fördert den fachlichen Austausch von Ärzten und Wissenschaftlern und liefert Expertise auf dem Gebiet der pädiatrischen Prävention.

Impressum: https://www.kindergesundheit.de/kontakt/impressum/

Name: Fondazione Homo Ex Machina
Description:
Techno-philanthropic non-profit Foundation for the safeguard and welfare of humanity, accelerated by ideas and digital technologies.

Fondazione HExMA
Think human, act digital. 

--
We operate for citizens, through Public Administrations and Public/Private institutions, supporting the latter in the development of adequate technological appliances for the delivery of high standard services. Through its network of professionals, experts and volunteers, Homo Ex Machina acts as a connector, discloser and introducer, allowing our partners to grasp the opportunities and take advantage of the tools offered by the digital revolution.

--
The ultimate goal of the foundation is to spread internet access and digital freedoms, to promote free availability of technological instruments, to eliminate the digital divide, to foster education and information for a full and conscious use of the internet in order to facilitate the development and evolution of digital ecosystems.

Name: New Hampshire Women's Foundation
Description: We invest in opportunity and equality for women and girls in New Hampshire through research, education, philanthropy, and advocacy.

Name: The Reece Foundation
Description:
The Reece Foundation was established in 2007. Our main objective is to increase the long term and sustainable prosperity of the North East of England primarily through the promotion of engineering and manufacturing.

In particular, we focus on the improvement of education in engineering and related scientific and mathematical subjects, training in engineering skills, and the development of employment opportunities.

Other projects and causes which, in the opinion of the trustees, are beneficial to the well-being of the area or the country as a whole may also be supported from time to time.

If you have a project requiring support that fits the above criteria, please submit an application for funding detailing your organisation and project, we will aim to respond to you in a timely manner.

Name: The Japan Foundation, New York
Description:
The mission of the Japan Foundation is to promote international cultural exchange and mutual understanding between Japan and other countries.  With our headquarters in Tokyo, offices in 21 countries, and two Japanese Language Institutes in Japan, The Japan Foundation New York (JFNY) provides funding and exchange opportunities in the fields of Arts & Culture, Japanese Studies, and Japanese Language Education.  The Center for Global Partnership (CGP - www.cgp.org) provides funding and fellowship opportunities in the fields of Intellectual Exchange and Grassroots Exchange and Education.  

JFNY is dedicated to sharing Japanese culture and language through supporting U.S.-based projects that ultimately foster stronger relationships and mutual understanding between Japan and the U.S, as well as through arts and cultural programs including concerts, film screenings, exhibitions, and Japanese Language classes in New York City.  

In addition to connecting with us on LinkedIn, please subscribe to our monthly newsletter via our websites (https://ny.jpf.go.jp/) as well as follow us on social media to stay up to date on our news, events, and grant opportunities.

Name: Fundación
Description:
La Fundación Naturgy es una institución sin ánimo de lucro fundada en 1992 por Naturgy, con una vocación de formación y sensibilización de la sociedad en la mejora de la eficiencia energética y la innovación tecnológica en el ámbito de la energía. También desarrolla programas de acción social tanto en el ámbito nacional como internacional, incidiendo especialmente en actuaciones destinadas a paliar la vulnerabilidad energética

Se trata de una fundación operativa que concibe y pone en marcha proyectos propios alineados con su misión, cuyos ejes troncales se basan en la formación, divulgación y sensibilización en temas de energía y medio ambiente, con el objetivo fundamental de promover el uso racional de los recursos energéticos y fomentar un desarrollo sostenible.

Por otro lado, la Fundación, sensible a los problemas de la sociedad actual, quiere combatir una realidad social que cada día afecta a un colectivo más amplio de la sociedad: la vulnerabilidad energética. Y, para ello, emprende medidas con especial incidencia en los colectivos vulnerables como acciones sociales directas a ONG´s, rehabilitación de viviendas sociales, la puesta en marcha de una escuela de energía o la formación profesional de personas en riesgo de exclusión en temas de energía y medio ambiente.

Asimismo, promueve las actividades formativas y divulgativas orientadas a la preservación y difusión del patrimonio histórico y cultural del sector del gas y la electricidad, y dispone de un extenso Archivo Histórico, considerado como uno de los más representativos del país.

La Fundación tiene una clara vocación internacional y actúa en aquellos territorios y países en los que está presente el grupo Naturgy, especialmente en los ámbitos de la formación y la sostenibilidad, desarrollando programas adaptados a la personalidad y requerimientos de los diferentes entornos. Sus actividades internacionales se llevan a cabo en Argentina, Brasil, Chile, Marruecos, México, Marruecos y Panamá

Name: 10 Percent For the Ocean
Description: A worldwide superfund for global ocean issues. We act as the platform to enable the frontline charities and organisations to achieve their goals.

Name: Fundación Iker Casillas
Description:
La Fundación Iker Casillas se crea en abril de 2011, año en el que Iker Casillas es nombrado embajador de buena voluntad del PNUD, Programa de las Naciones Unidas para el Desarrollo.

Desde sus inicios, la Fundación Iker Casillas, trata de hacer realidad los sueños de muchas niñas y niños fomentando programas de educación, salud y desarrollo utilizando como herramienta el Deporte, medio que Iker Casillas conoce a la perfección y que es un elemento básico en la educación y como agente de cambio social, potenciando valores como el trabajo en equipo, el esfuerzo, perseverancia, respeto y confianza.

Conseguir un mundo mejor es posible, crear un lugar donde los niños sean respetados y donde prime, por encima de todo, sus derechos fundamentales: protección, desarrollo y educación.

Nuestro trabajo está alineado con los ODS con el firme propósito de no dejar a nadie atrás. Por este motivo, además del apoyo a la infancia, un pilar fundamental para su bienestar, también es importante ayudar a las familias a través de acciones que ayuden a las mismas a cubrir sus necesidades básicas, así como el acompañamiento en el tránsito del niño a la edad adulta.

Creemos en la cooperación entre entidades como motor de cambio y desarrollo, motivo por el cual creamos alianzas con entidades para la consecución de nuestros fines y los de dichas entidades y como consecuencia poder llegar a más personas.

Trabajamos todos estos fines tanto en el ámbito nacional como internacional.

Name: n3awnouk
Description: Groupe de bénévoles qui ont pour but d’agir pour la bonne cause en vendant des bouchons en plastique qui polluent l’environnement ce qui servira à réaliser des actions humanitaires.

Name: The Earth Saviours Foundation
Description:
The Earth Saviours Foundation is an Internationally recognized NGO, situated in Gurgaon, Haryana, India. The Foundation is dedicated to serve less privileged people and is making every possible effort to protect the environment. It was founded in 2008 by Ravi Kalra. He is a well-known Karma Yogi, social activist and environmentalist.
The Earth Saviours Foundation is registered under 80 G of Income Tax Exemption Act and FCRA license from The Ministry of Home Affairs, Government of India. The selfless, dedicated, committed, vigilant staff and volunteers of The Earth Saviours Foundation are working day and night for the community welfare.

Name: Autisme et Familles
Description:
Autisme & Familles est une Association loi 1901 à but non lucratif, ayant pour objectif de :
     -	Permettre la mise en place d’une éducation individualisée et spécifique.
     -	Participer à la création et à la gestion d’établissements et de services appropriés tendant à l’éducation, le soin, la rééducation l’adaptation, la préparation à l’âge adulte, l’insertion sociale, l’hébergement, l’organisation de loisirs, etc… afin d’intégrer les personnes avec autisme dans la Société et de leur permettre d’accéder à une vie digne en veillant à leur épanouissement. 
     -	Informer et former les parents, les professionnels et le grand public sur les stratégies éducatives spécifiques à l’Autisme en relation étroite avec la Recherche Scientifique dans ce domaine. 
     -	Promouvoir et soutenir des actions de Recherche Scientifique. 
     -	Collaborer avec toutes associations et organismes poursuivant des buts voisins, à tous niveaux : adhésion à des structures fédératives, montage de projets communs etc… 
Notre association est issue de la fusion depuis 2014 des associations suivantes : Sésame Autisme Nord-Pas-De-Calais, Autisme Nord, Le Chevêtre, Autisme Picardie 80 et ECLA. Ainsi notre association est une association militante de parents qui tiennent une place centrale dans la gouvernance. 

Nos Valeurs :
     - RESPECT
     - BIENVEILLANCE
     - SOLIDARITE
     - ENGAGEMENT

Name: Foundation for Appalachian Kentucky
Description:
The Foundation for Appalachian Kentucky is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization that believes in consistently and intentionally shifting control back into the hands of local leaders. By supporting communities, growing assets and strengthening leadership, we are rising where we’re rooted. 

We Support Communities
We bring together people, ideas and resources to build communities and encourage philanthropy. Our approach is to meet communities where they are instead of telling them where they should be. Because, at the end of the day, we believe that investing in the people rooted in communities is the way to make them better.

We Grow Assets
We bring together people, ideas and resources to build We connect investors with local people and programs working toward lasting, positive change. We are reshaping grantmaking strategy in the region by creating conditions for businesses and programs to succeed. We pursue relationships with startups and organizations that celebrate Appalachian culture and grow local economies. Capital meant to drive positive impact in local communities should stay in those communities.

We Strengthen Leadership
We motivate and develop community leaders, and eliminate barriers to growth. We use our network and voice to inspire support for persistent problems within communities. By sharing experience and expertise, we provide a platform for communities to speak for themselves. We celebrate the culture of Appalachia by helping people rooted in communities grow opportunities and economies.

Name: Spartanburg County Foundation
Description: The Spartanburg County Foundation is dedicated to improving the lives of Spartanburg County residents by promoting philanthropy, encouraging community engagement and responding to community needs. Established in 1943, The Spartanburg County Foundation is the oldest community foundation in South Carolina. Additional information about the Foundation is available at www.spcf.org.

Name: iSeluleko Youth Leadership Development Forum (iSeluleko NPC)
Description:
A 100% black, female and 33% disabled owned non-profit company, iSeluleko Youth Leadership Development Forum also known as iSeluleko NPC, offers a holistic approach to leadership and personnel development solutions to the youth. This is done through partnering with and engaging our client, which is the KZN Department of Education, to ensure that we offer a client-centered service.
The Nguni name “Seluleko”, meaning advice, is derived from our desire to offer our clients practical and sustainable advice and training on leadership at the different levels of development, using experienced leaders in different fields both in the private and public sector. Our main aim is to develop youth leadership through Leadership Talks, Workshops, Exhibitions and Seminars/Conferences. We also seek to cater for the specific needs of the disadvantaged youth by offering basic skills training and mentorship. We are a Zululand-based company, but seek to grow as the demand arises to the rest of KZN and countrywide. We seek to establish strategic alliances with local industries and government departments including our local municipalities.

Name: First Star, Inc.
Description:
First Star works to uplift communities and create change by building strong pathways for hundreds of high-school age foster youth to post-secondary education and successful adult lives. We partner with child welfare agencies, universities, and school districts to ensure foster youth have the academic, life skills, and adult supports needed to transition to higher education and adulthood successfully. We pursue our mission through innovative, university-based college-preparatory programs, providing technical assistance to stakeholders, and advocating for policy change.

First Star was founded in 1999 as a national 501(c)(3) public charity dedicated to improving life for child victims of abuse and neglect. Since 2011 First Star has pioneered support programs to launch foster children into productive lives and careers through higher education.

Name: BALAFRES
Description:
Balafres est le premier site destiné aux hommes atteints d'un cancer.
Nous informons, mettons en place des actions de prévention et soutenons les personnes malades.
Nous souhaitons libérer la parole des hommes sur un sujet encore tabou !

Name: Muslim Engagement and Development
Description:
MEND is a not-for-profit company that was founded to help empower and encourage British Muslims within local communities to be more actively involved in British media, politics and democracy.

British Muslims have remained on the margins of public and political debate about their religion and place in modern Britain for too long and the level of Muslim participation in media and politics remains woefully low. MEND seeks to enable British Muslims to engage more effectively with political and media institutions and play a greater role in British politics and society by instilling confidence, competence and awareness. Enhancing mainstream participation by communities that are under-represented and vulnerable is an important step towards deepening and strengthening our democracy.

MEND will encourage voter registration and political engagement by British Muslims so that through civic involvement Muslims can responsibly exercise their duty in working for the common good.

MEND aims to equip Muslims with the skills, resources and materials necessary for them to play a more active role in society.

MEND believes that all citizens have a responsibility to contribute to the positive and sustained development of a Britain in which all members of society are valued and respected whatever their religious, racial or ethnic background, gender or sexual orientation.

MEND endeavours to enable active citizenship and participation by British Muslims in furtherance of its aims to create a more inclusive and tolerant Britain.

MEND aims to provide commentary and analysis on the high volumes of news content and coverage that maligns Islam and Muslims and foments Islamophobia in the UK and across Europe.

MEND aims to work with Muslim and non Muslim organisations to ensure that anti-Muslim prejudice is regarded just as socially unacceptable as anti-Semitism and other forms of racism and xenophobia.

Name: TangaYetu
Description: A Fondation Botnar initiative that works in partnership with children and youth in Tanga city to improve their health and wellbeing.

Name: Cookies for Kids' Cancer
Description:
Cookies for Kids' Cancer, a national 501(c)3 non-profit, is committed to raising funds for research to develop new, improved treatments for pediatric cancer, the #1 disease killer of children in the U.S. We provide inspiration and support for individuals, businesses and organizations to raise funds by hosting grassroots bake sales and other fundraising events. 

Cookies for Kids’ Cancer has granted $10 million to date, funding 80 research projects, including 32 that are now treatments available for children battling cancer today. The organization inspires individuals, organizations, and businesses to join in the fight against pediatric cancer by raising the funds for new and improved and less toxic treatments. Since Cookies for Kids’ Cancer launched in 2008, there have been more than 7,000 events in all 50 states and 16 countries. Grants are peer reviewed by a scientific advisory board of esteemed pediatric cancer oncologists, with funds going directly to the leading pediatric cancer institutions in the country  including: Memorial Sloan Kettering Cancer Center, Dana Farber, Children’s Hospital of Philadelphia, St. Jude Children’s Research Hospital and Texas Children’s Cancer Center. In addition, Cookies for Kids’ Cancer is the largest single donor to the Children’s Oncology Group Phase I Consortium project with a donation twice the amount that the Federal Government allocates per child enrolled. The organization has been profiled in the Wall Street Journal, Today Show, Good Morning America, NY Daily News, Family Circle Magazine and dozens of other local and national media outlets. In addition, the organization has received several national awards for its work. Follow the organization on Facebook at facebook.com/Cookiesforkidscancer, Twitter (cookies4kids), Instagram (cookies4Kids)  and Pinterest (cookies4kids). For more information, to sign up for a bake sale or purchase cookies, please visit www.cookiesforkidscancer.org.

Name: Women and Girls Fund of Waukesha County
Description: The Women & Girls Fund of Waukesha County is dedicated to acknowledging, celebrating, and elevating all women and girls in our community. For more than two decades we have been committed to raising money to help women and girls reach their full potential. We believe this work builds strong families and communities.

Name: Choose Life
Description:
Choose Life is a nonprofit association dedicated to fighting leukemia and other blood-related illnesses. 

Our mission is to provide any kind of tangible and intangible support to patients and their families, to expand the volunteer bone marrow donor base, and to support research on new and promising treatments which could increase patients’ survival rate and improve their quality of life.

Name: Nurses Without Borders (NWB/IISF/EESF)
Description:
Nurses Without Borders (NWB) is a non-profit organization and a charity that brings together people from all health professions interested in international cooperation in Quebec, Canada and around the world. NWB is registered under the number 1163018204 at the Registraire des entreprises du Québec and under the number 84474 0944 RR 0001 with the Canada Customs and Revenue Agency as a charitable organization.

Each year, NWB supports approx. ten groups to carry out an international cooperation initiative.

OUR MISSION
- support individual or group projects
- facilitate the logistical and administrative process of the project
- advise in essential preparation for the project
- offer preparatory training

Name: The Redlich Horwitz Foundation
Description:
The Redlich Horwitz Foundation (RHF) invests in transformative strategies that ensure children and families can thrive together in well-resourced communities across New York. We support partners and movements dedicated to opposing oppressive surveillance tactics that system-involved youth and parents say fracture and harm their families. We call for the expansion of community-driven supports that keep New York families intact and ensure that the small percentage of children at risk of physical harm remain safe and supported. 

GRANTMAKING STRATEGY: 
RHF’s funding portfolio reflects the priorities voiced by people and communities most affected by the child welfare system. We seek to open access to philanthropic support for grassroots organizations and coalitions working to build the family justice movement –– particularly in communities disproportionately impacted by child welfare investigations and especially in regions outside of NYC, where private funding for this work is scarce. 

• MOBILIZE:  Movement and Power Building 
• INFLUENCE:  Policy and Advocacy Action for Change 
• REIMAGINE:  Community Response for Child and Family Well-being
• SUPPORT:  Youth Who Experience the Foster System 

Visit https://www.rhfdn.org/strategy to learn more about our Strategic Funding Vision.

Name: Saul Zaentz Charitable Foundation
Description: The Saul Zaentz Charitable Foundation builds on the legacy of three-time Best Picture winner Saul Zaentz’s celebrated career as an independent media entrepreneur. True to Zaentz’s vision, the Foundation is dedicated to improving outcomes for poor and vulnerable minority children in the US.

Name: New York Foundation
Description: The New York Foundation is a steadfast supporter of community organizing and advocacy. We believe that the resilience and vitality of New Yorkers is the city's greatest resource; and we value the courage and experience of community groups and their leaders in the struggle against systemic inequity. The Foundation supports grassroots initiatives that confront structural barriers and inspire people to work toward a more just and inclusive city.

Name: JH Philanthropy
Description: JH Philanthropy (JHP) is a team of philanthropy advisors who specialize in helping high-capacity and generous individuals and families bring their charitable visions to life and move more money into communities. We partner with families and foundations on a long-term basis and serve as partners, coaches, and advisors to bring joy, structure, and clarity to their social impact goals.

Name: Argentina Cibersegura
Description: Somos una Asociación Civil cuya misión es concientizar y educar a la comunidad sobre el buen uso de Internet y las tecnologías.

Name: Lehigh Valley Community Foundation
Description:
Mission

The Lehigh Valley Community Foundation promotes philanthropy in order to improve the quality of life in our region.

Vision

The Lehigh Valley Community Foundation will be a major force in the development of philanthropy in our region. The Foundation will promote and encourage collaboration among area philanthropists, and will be a source of information and expertise regarding charitable giving. The Foundation will be recognized and respected for its trustworthiness, its permanence, and the quality of its Board of Governors.

Name: WAPA International
Description:
War-Affected People's Association
fights 
against the recruitment and use of child soldiers
for their reintegration into stronger communities

Name: Empower Foundation
Description: Empower Foundation is a non-profit organization setup by 10Pearls. We are committed to building a better future by empowering communities in geographies where 10Pearls operates out of. Our focus is: helping needy children (food and education), empowering women an working with the disadvantaged population at large. 10Pearls was founded on the principles of not just corporate growth, but to contribute positively and responsibly to society... doing good while doing well.

Name: Sigma Sigma Sigma Foundation
Description: Sigma Sigma Sigma Foundation provides transformational philanthropy:  funding innovative leadership training for women, awarding scholarships, and serving children through grants to March of Dimes and play therapy.  As a non-profit organization, we are the conduit to help donors create a legacy.

Name: MaLisa Stiftung
Description:
Die MaLisa Stiftung wurde 2016 von Maria und Elisabeth Furtwängler gegründet. Ihr Ziel ist eine freie, gleichberechtigte Gesellschaft. Sie engagiert sich auf internationaler Ebene für die Beendigung von Gewalt gegen Frauen und Mädchen. In Deutschland setzt sie sich zudem für gesellschaftliche Vielfalt und die Überwindung einschränkender Rollenbilder ein.
Ihre Grundlage hat die MaLisa Stiftung in vielen Jahren internationaler Erfahrungen: Seit 1998 hat Maria Furtwängler als ehrenamtliche Ärztin mit German Doctors in den Slums von Nairobi, Kalkutta und Gujarat sowie auf den Philippinen erlebt, wie allgegenwärtig Gewalt gegen Frauen ist und als Normalität beschrieben wird. Elisabeth hat während ihrer Reisen durch Kambodscha und die Philippinen begonnen, sich mit den Auswirkungen sexueller Ausbeutung von Mädchen und Frauen auseinanderzusetzen. Um aktiv Hilfe zu leisten, initiierten Maria und Elisabeth Furtwängler 2011 MaLisa Home, einen Zufluchtsort für Mädchen, die auf den Philippinen Opfer von Menschenhandel und Zwangsprostitution wurden.
 
Mit der MaLisa Stiftung erweitern Maria und Elisabeth Furtwängler ihr Engagement für die Stärkung von Frauen und Mädchen und widmen sich der Überwindung von geschlechtsspezifischen Formen der Diskriminierung in Deutschland.  Schwerpunkt der ersten Initiativen der MaLisa Stiftung ist die Darstellung von Frauen und Männern in den Medien.
 
Die Stiftung arbeitet ausschließlich operativ.

Name: Omaha Central High School Foundation
Description: Since 1859, Central High School has offered educational excellence and personal growth to all of its students. The Central High School Foundation was established in 1996 to provide support to ensure the tradition of excellence continues.

Name: Kiwanis Wavre
Description: "Serving the children of the World"

Name: Somerset Capital Partners Foundation
Paws 4 Autism
Description:
Helping Families Help Their Kids to Connect to the World.....4 Paws at a Time

Our mission is to help the families of children with autism connect to the world through the use of an Autism Service Dog.

Service dogs provide a physical and emotional anchor for children with autism. With their child tethered to a service dog, families feel they are newly freed to engage in activities as simple as shopping at the mall. On the streets, parents are relieved of the worry their child might run away. In many cases, the service dog accompanies the child to school, where its calming presence can minimize and often eliminate emotional outbursts, enabling the child to more fully participate. Transitioning among school day activities is eased and the service dog provides a focus through which the child can interact with other children. This helps increase the opportunity for the child to develop social and language skills.

Not every child with autism will benefit from a service dog. Autism Service Dog Agencies and Trainers screen and evaluate each situation carefully. Their goal is to ensure a successful match for the child, the family and the dog. Our goal at Paws 4 Autism is to help fund the costs associated with acquiring and Autism Service Dog through our matching grant program.

Name: Young and Empowered Malawi
Description: Our mission is to provide high quality supplementary academic support, leadership training, mentorship, and economic opportunities to children, girls, and youth living in vulnerable communities.

Name: The Sunday Breakfast Club
Description:
The Sunday Breakfast Club is an invitation-only organization comprised of a diverse group of current and emerging leaders from the Greater Philadelphia Region’s business, non-profit, and government sectors who come together regularly to learn from each other as well as other thought leaders and engage in conversation about the issues of our time.


Board of Directors:
Oscar Wang, Chair
Siobhan Lyons, Vice-Chair
Eli Reilly, Treasurer
Catherine Cahill
Naomi Johnson-Booker
Kelsey DeMerlis
Emmy Miller
Anthony Robinson
Chris Satullo
Russ Starke
Mark Travis
Tom Tritton

Name: Community Action
Description: Community Action is a private, non-profit 501(c)3 human service organization founded in 1966 and that is dedicated to helping people achieve and maintain independence. To this end, the agency provides basic needs assistance to elderly, disabled and low- to moderate-income families in the Michigan counties of Barry, Branch, Calhoun, and St. Joseph. Services and programs we provided include Head Start, food assistance, Meals on Wheels, housing assistance, utility assistance and senior transportation.

Name: Modzi
Description: Modzi is a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization that works directly with orphaned and vulnerable youth in Zambia. Through an innovative model rooted in local partnerships and sustainability, Modzi provides the means and the resources necessary for at-risk youth to receive a formal, quality education. Modzi’s students benefit from individual mentorship programs paired with a holistic support system that empowers each child to succeed. Beyond the classroom, Modzi provides study, financial, and other life skills that encourage children to unfold their potential and create positive change in their communities. Join the #modzimovement today at www.modzi.org!

Name: Résistons Ensemble
Description:
La campagne RESISTONS ENSEMBLE est une Initiative citoyenne visant à changer la perception de la population sur la situation sécuritaire, à susciter la mobilisation des Burkinabè autour des actions de l’État ainsi qu'aux sacrifices des Forces de Défense, de Sécurité et des VDP. 

Cette campagne trouve son fondement dans la gravité de la situation difficile que notre pays le Burkina Faso vit ses heures les plus dures depuis son accession à l’indépendance.
Ces énormes pertes en vies humaines, ces situations de détresse que nous vivons en plus de susciter de la tristesse, du chagrin et de la désolation, suscite également de la colère et de la frustration.

Mais l’heure est à la résilience et à l’unité nationale devant la barbarie et la lâcheté. 

Le concept « Résistons Ensemble » se présente comme un concept fort autour duquel, nous filles et fils du Burkina Faso devront nous reconnaître.
 
Il tire sa substance dans la conception originelle du peuple Burkinabè, un peuple intègre qui au cours de son histoire à travers aussi bien des leaders charismatiques que sa population en général, s’est toujours illustré par sa bravoure et son attachement profond à la justice. 

Justice, bravoure, sacrifice, union, voici ce que symbolise l’image qui accompagne le slogan, à savoir le poing fermé, cette force de l’union pour faire barrage à l’adversité. 

L’objectif de la campagne « Résistons Ensemble » est clairement de changer la perception de la population sur la situation sécuritaire, de susciter la mobilisation des populations auprès de l’État dans cette guerre, et obtenir l’adhésion de la population aux sacrifices des FDS et VDP.

Notre Vision, c'est d'Unir les Burkinabè pour l’honneur de la patrie.

Et une telle vision ne peut que se nourrir de Valeurs fortes que sont: l'Amour, la tolérance, la solidarité, le pardon, la citoyenneté, la justice, l'intégrité dans l'unité qui nous conduiront à la PAIX.

Notre Slogan: Unis, nous triompherons de cette guerre injuste !

Name: Ubbo Emmius Foundation
Description: The philanthropic foundation for the University of Groningen

Name: Sinergi Foundation
Description: Lembaga filantropi yang mengelola dana Wakaf, Zakat, dan Infak-Sedekah yang di produktifkan melalui berbagai program kebaikan.
