Van een enkele organisatie van en paar boyscouts, heft een breed VZW van hondreds van Vrijwilligers geemergeed. Vandag, het VZW coördineert 30 teams die serveren maaltijden elke avond tussen november en april in de botanique metro station.
-
From a simple organisaion of a few boyscouts, emergeed a large NPO of hondreds of volunteers. Today, the NPO coordinates 30 teams serve meals every night from november to april in the botanique metro station..

Name: Chuckanut Health Foundation
Description: For 40 years, we have invested our experience and resources to strengthen and advance individual and community health.

Name: Whatcom Community Foundation
Description:
Whatcom Community Foundation connects people, resources and ideas so that communities flourish.  
Our vision is everyone who lives here thrives.

Name: Techbikers
Description:
Techbikers is a non-profit bringing together entrepreneurs, venture capitalists and techies around long distance cycling challenges to raise funding for children literacy in the developing world, in partnership with Room to Read. 

Founded in 2012 by Eze Vidra, Techbikers raised over £305,000 which has sponsored 6 schools, 12 libraries, 300 scholarships for girls, teacher education programmes and books in the local languages in Asia and Africa (Nepal, Cambodia, Bangladesh, India, Vietnam and Tanzania). 

The Techbikers community now has over 400 members from the European startup ecosystem.

Name: Cascade Community Foundation
Description:
Cascade Community Foundation serves the businesses, nonprofits and neighborhoods in the Ada, Cascade and Forest Hills communities by bridging the gap between philanthropy and impact.  

If your business or family is interested in creating impact in this area, please contact us at (616) 499-8122.

Name: The Yeh Family Philanthropy
Description:
The Yeh Family Philanthropy (YFP) is a Hong Kong based family foundation with the mission of “Collaborating to reimagine learning systems for the development of youth potential." YFP's program portfolio supports education development focusing on social emotional learning (SEL) in Hong Kong and early childhood in China. 

YFP adds value beyond funding, having patiently scaled programs through grantmaking, and in developing our own programs. We seek to create systems-level impact - through deeper understanding of the systems in which we operate, forging alliances across sectors toward shared system views, and nurturing promising, evidence-based solutions.

We believe SEL to be a critical tool to address the current and future needs for all learners in Hong Kong, to help them achieve personal and professional fulfilment. We also believe that engaging parents and providing quality interventions during the crucial early days of a child’s life can have tremendous impact on their subsequent development, contributing to educational equality between rural and urban China.

Name: NOVA Foundation
Description: The NOVA Foundation is the non-profit organization dedicated to securing private resources to support NOVA students, faculty and staff.

Name: Vreds Philanthropy
Description:
Vreds Philanthropy works with individuals, families and foundations to understand your donor intent and connect your philanthropic vision to the real-world strategies and insights you need to achieve it. In creating change strategies with philanthropists, we draw from broad network of experts from science, medicine, social issues, and policy, as well as people who have experienced first-hand the inadequate responses of current systems when they and their loved ones seek help.

Our areas of focus revolve around the following areas:

Strategy
We help you navigate the policy levers, philanthropic partners, and social entrepreneurs required to achieve mission-driven results.

Partnerships
We build impactful funder relationships and coalitions based on meaningful partnerships around shared goals, achieving results through value-driven connections, informed messaging, and well-tailored convenings.

Grantee Performance
We develop outcome-driven agreements with grantees and establish relationships and accountability that brings your mission and goals to life. Our team will support you in your grantee partnerships every step along the way.

Visit our website to learn more.

Name: CureGRIN Foundation
Description:
CureGRIN Foundation is a parent-run charity organization dedicated to improving the lives of people around the world with GRI Disorders (GRIA, GRID, GRIK, and GRIN) and their families through research, education, and support. We work closely with scientists and the medical community to drive patient-centered research that will lead to treatments and cures. 

GRI Disorders may be rare, but hope should not be!

Our goal is simple and bold: To find cures and therapies for people living with GRI disorders caused by variants in GRIA, GRID, GRIK, and GRIN genes.

GRI Disorders are part of a larger family of genetic diseases related to ionotropic glutamate receptors. These receptors play an important role in learning and memory as well as other critical biological functions.

GRI Disorders are rare genetic conditions with severe life-limiting consequences. Many GRI patients are non-verbal, unable to walk, and many cannot feed or toilet themselves. They often suffer severe and frequent seizures that are not responsive to medical therapies.

Name: Scalabrini International Migration Network
Description:
As a leading international network for migration, SIMN’s strategic focus is centered on five main goals:

1. Research on migration to raise awareness for a better understanding of migration issues, policies and programs.
2. Training and capacity building programs for people and entities working with migrants.
3. Services to migrants, refugees, internally displaced people, seafarers and itinerant people to protect and promote their human, economic, social and cultural rights.
4. Advocacy programs to promote policies and programs that recognize the rights and participative citizenship of migrants and their families.
5. Sustainability of programs and services to migrants promoted by local Scalabrinian entities.

Name: The Confectionery Foundation
Description:
The mission of The Confectionery Foundation is to support and strengthen the American confectionery industry through education, research and philanthropy.

NCA's The Confectionery Foundation is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization bringing together many volunteers who are working on many outstanding initiatives in our industry, among them linking our communities with STEM opportunities provided in and by the American confectionery industry.

Name: Villages du Monde Pour Enfants
Description:
Villages du Monde pour Enfants est une association d’intérêt général,
à but non-lucratif et à vocation humanitaire, créée en 1999.

Elle œuvre, aux côtés de la Congrégation des Sœurs de Marie, pour aider les enfants à sortir de la pauvreté grâce à l'éducation.

Grâce à nos donateurs et à l’action des Sœurs de Marie, plus de 20 000 enfants ayant connu la pauvreté la plus extrême sont actuellement accueillis dans treize Villages, répartis dans six pays (Philippines, Brésil, Guatemala, Mexique, Honduras et Tanzanie). Ils y reçoivent un accompagnement complet et entièrement gratuit, de leur 11 à 17 ans, en moyenne. Au sein des Villages, ils sont nourris, soignés et hébergés et surtout ils bénéficient d'une éducation ainsi que d'une formation professionnelle. Grâce à cette aide, les enfants peuvent développer tout leur potentiel et construire une vie loin de la pauvreté.

Nos actions comprennent notamment la récolte des fonds en France et l’information ainsi que la sensibilisation de nos donateurs et futurs donateurs à cette cause, afin d’assurer la conduite et le développement des actions des Sœurs de Marie. Jusqu'à présent, ces programmes ont permis à plus de 170 000 enfants d'échapper à une vie de pauvreté et de construire un avenir meilleur. Notre œuvre ne poursuit pas un but religieux mais bien caritatif et humanitaire.

Name: RightGift
Description:
RIghtGift is a technology company that builds free giving software for the charitable ecosystem. Our technology solves the specific issues faced by non-profits, charities, schools, foundations, businesses, and donors.

Organizations of every size successfully launch gift-in-kind wish list campaigns for needed items, increasing the impact of their charitable missions and delighting donors with transparency in giving.

We are experts in the nonprofit supply chain and provide free procurement services for thousands of charitable organizations. The RightGift Team increases savings and reduces time spent on purchasing the items nonprofits need most.

Name: Instituto Joel Malucelli
Description:
Desde 1966, as empresas JMalucelli apoiam e realizam atividades em benefício da comunidade.

Campanhas de doações, incentivo a programas estruturados na área social, esporte sempre estiveram presentes na história as empresas e no DNA do seu fundador Joel Malucelli.



A decisão de ausentar-se da presidência do grupo em 2012, o empresário deixa seu legado e seu sonho concretizado no Instituto Joel Malucelli.



O Instituto Joel Malucelli nasceu para centralizar, integrar e gerenciar as verbas de incentivo fiscal das empresas JMalucelli.  

Este é o proposito do Instituto Joel Malucelli.

Name: Beyond the Bake Sale
Description: At Beyond the Bake Sale, our goal is to help individuals and organizations raise more time, talent and treasure for the causes they care about. We believe in telling the stories of those in the sector, whether professional or voluntary who have achieved success and how we can harness that success to support others. We do so through talking with leaders and innovators in the sector, and curating and creating tools and training to build capacity within the sector.

Name: FONDATION SCELLES
Description:
La Fondation Scelles a d’abord axé son travail sur la constitution d’un centre de documentation unique en Europe, le CRIDES (Centre de Recherches Internationales et de documentation sur l’Exploitation Sexuelle). Il permet de suivre l’évolution des formes que revêt l’exploitation sexuelle partout dans le monde.

Dans le même temps, outre les auditions par les instances officielles françaises, les actions de lobbying ont amené la Fondation Scelles à participer à la création de plusieurs collectifs regroupant des ONG, opérationnelles au niveau français ( Abolition 2012 ) et mondial (CAP). Depuis 3 ans, la Fondation Scelles développe des actions de sensibilisation et d’information auprès du grand public et des jeunes, futurs acteurs de la société civile : Prix Fondation Scelles, Rapport Mondial.

La Fondation Scelles réunit aujourd’hui un panel élargi de compétences et d’actions qui en font un partenaire de premier plan des institutions et de l’univers associatif. L’originalité de la Fondation Scelles lui permet de faire le lien entre tous les acteurs de la lutte contre l’exploitation sexuelle et les générations en devenir qu’elle associe à ses réalisations.

Name: Zamani Foundation
Description: The Zamani Foundation’s mission is to provide people a life of opportunity that improves communities around the world.

Name: We Are Ready Togo
Description:
We Are Ready Togo est un des projets de l'association Solid'Earth de Kedge Business School. 

Notre projet vise au financement d'une cantine collaborative au sein du village de Wonougba

Name: explore.org
Description: A multimedia division of the Annenberg Foundation, explore is a multimedia organization that champions the selfless acts of others, documenting leaders around the world who have devoted their lives to extraordinary causes. Both educational and inspirational, explore films cover topics from animal rights, health and human services, and poverty to the environment, education, and spirituality. explore.org also hosts one of the largest online networks of live HD nature and animal cameras.

Name: Les Domestiques Cycling Club
Description:
Since Les Domestiques inception in 2009, our club motto, “Cyclists Who Serve,” has always reflected the important ethos of club membership: to serve others through support, leadership and advocacy. Whether it’s a group ride where no one is left behind or collectively raising millions of dollars to support important initiatives, we pitch in and help. 

Our love of cycling, the camaraderie and teamwork of a group ride, and our interest in teaming up to raise funds and awareness for worthy causes are what bonds us. Our club values are just as relevant today: our mandate as like-minded cyclists having fun promoting philanthropy, the value of cycling, and an active, healthy lifestyle, and our vision of becoming an influential, unified voice for cycling philanthropy and sustainable advocacy in Canada.

Name: Kids of Ukraine
Description: The Kids of Ukraine is a Ukraine, UK and USA non-profit organization dedicated to assisting initiatives that aid children and their families in overcoming war trauma, adjusting to new circumstances, and uncovering their inherent strengths.

Name: Startups Give Back
Description:
Startups Give Back is the volunteering event where startups get out of their 
offices and work together to help nonprofits in their communities.

The mission is simple: make philanthropy a top priority for startups around the world instead of an afterthought. 

Since its founding in 2014, Startups Give Back has blossomed into a thriving ecosystem of impact with the launch of Changemaker Forum, a live event series and digital publication for social entrepreneurs to share the problems they believe are worth solving; Social Impact Chat, an invite-only Slack community for social impact professionals; a series of well attended social impact meetups; and much more.

Some of the companies that have attended Startups Give Back's volunteering events include BuzzFeed, Uber, LinkedIn, and Salesforce. 

"Startups Give Back made my life so much easier and made getting our office out and volunteering easy and enjoyable! If you want to get your company out into your community but aren’t sure where to start or how to do it, I would highly recommend using Startups Give Back!” – Farryn Lewis, BuzzFeed

“Startups Give Back is at the forefront of a movement encouraging the tech community to give back in a meaningful way. Any tech company looking for a fun way to engage their employees in social impact should check them out. I personally have enjoyed the beach cleanups and giving back to local low-income seniors!” – Rachel Katz, Airbnb

Name: Los18.org
Description:
Los18 somos una plataforma social que promueve los 17 Objetivos de Desarrollo Sostenible (ODS) a través de eventos y acciones épicas y disruptivas. Pretendemos ser el mayor punto de encuentro e inspiración de personas, instituciones y empresas que apuesten por darle la vuelta al mundo a través del emprendimiento social y sostenible.

Los valores que nos definen son: 
- Optimismo radical
- Responsabilidad
- Transparencia
- Trabajo en equipo con pasión
- Impulso y desarrollo del talento humano

Te puedes unir desde https://www.los18.org/

Name: Club des affaires sociales de l'INPT
Description: Ceci est un club qui organise plusieurs activités afin de s'occuper des gens qui ont besoin d'aide et ceci en planifiant plusieurs activités diversifiés organisés par les membres.

Name: 101 Genomes Foundation
Description: La F101G a pour objectif de promouvoir la recherche génomique dans le domaine des maladies rares, notamment en mettant à la disposition des scientifiques une base de données croisées contenant les données génomiques et phénotypiques de patients atteints de maladies rares. Avec l’espoir que cet outil, accessible par le biais d’une plateforme informatique sécurisée, permette aux chercheurs d’identifier l’existence d’éventuels gènes modificateurs qui protégeraient certaines personnes contre les principales atteintes généralement causées par la maladie rare qui les affecte. L’identification d’éventuels gènes modificateurs pourrait permettre d’envisager la mise au point de traitements qui répliquent les effets de ces gènes modificateurs protecteurs chez les malades chez qui ces gènes ne sont pas activés de la même manière.

Name: Poze
Description: Poze is an online dating auction app designed to build strong, lasting relationships grounded in shared values and common interest.

Name: Lost Our Home Pet Rescue
Description:
Lost Our Home Pet Rescue (LOH) in Phoenix, Arizona, provides comprehensive solutions to permanent and temporary displacement, pet abandonment, and hunger by helping both pets and their families, especially during times of domestic violence, eviction, homelessness, illness, etc. As a no-kill rescue with programs linked to preserving the human-animal bond whenever possible, Lost Our Home Pet Rescue takes a proactive approach to keep pets out of overcrowded shelters, where they are at risk of being euthanized, and instead, LOH works hard to keep them with the families that love them. The organization's primary goal is to provide safety net services to pet parents during challenging circumstances, so they don't have to choose between staying in an unhealthy or dangerous situation or losing their pets forever.  Our mission is to provide compassionate services to pets and people in crisis.

For over 15 years Lost Our Home has focused on finding homes for abandoned or orphaned pets and helping pet parents in need during times of crisis.   We have helped over 34,250 pets, fed over 1 million meals to hungry pets, adopted over 10 thousand pets into loving forever homes, and have helped over 20,000 pet families in crisis through our Rescue & Adoption, Temporary Care/Domestic Violence, Pet Food Bank, and Tempe Police Department Partnership Programs. We have provided pets with the care and love they deserve and offered pet parents hope during the most difficult times in their lives and through our partnership with the Tempe Police Department we have provided 24/7 access to our shelter so no matter what time of the day or night a pet is in need, there is a safe place for those pets to land.  
Our rescue is able to do this work because of donors, volunteers and adopters.  If you are able to help us save more lives or keep more families together by donating, volunteering or adopting, please visit our website for more info.

Name: جمعية لأجلهم لخدمة الأشخاص ذوي الإعاقة
Description: الحساب الرسمي لــ #جمعية_لأجلهم تحت إشراف وزارة الموارد البشرية والتنمية الاجتماعية "رقم الترخيص 1053"

Name: Mi Fundación Alex
Description:
En Fundación Mi Álex, como fundación corporativa de Clínicas Mi, creemos que el amor es la capacidad de las personas para mover montañas. Para cambiar el mundo.

Por ello, trabajamos a favor de la inclusión. Ponemos todo nuestro conocimiento y amor para que las personas, independientemente de su condición, tengan la oportunidad de mejorar su salud, de mejorar su vida.

Name: Lewisham Local
Description:
OUR VISION
A connected Lewisham community that gives, shares and works together to build happier, healthier communities.

OUR MISSION
*Connect and Collaborate with individuals, community groups, businesses and statutory organisations
*Encourage all to give and share what they can; time, skills, expertise, knowledge, resources and money
*Respond to needs and opportunities through events, projects and communications
*Raise awareness of existing and emerging community needs
*Promote and strengthen Lewisham’s voluntary and community groups
*Promote asset-based community development, making the most of what we already have in Lewisham
Increase the impact and sustainability of Lewisham Local


OUR VALUES
*Everyone in the community is an asset and able to contribute, give and share
*We are inclusive, welcoming and open to all
*We are collaborative and committed to working together with a positive attitude
* We are dynamic and responsive to Lewisham’s communities’ needs and willing to innovate
*We value the diversity of Lewisham’s communities and are committed to proactively representing Lewisham’s diversity in our staff, trustees and projects

Name: Sociedade Artística Brasileira
Description:
A Sociedade Artística Brasileira é uma associação civil, sem fins lucrativos.
Desenvolve e apoia ações de natureza artística, cultural e social.
Mantem a Orquestra Sinfônica de Betim, a Oficina Musical em Betim, o Coro e Orquestra de Câmara Lobo de Mesquita e Corais Infantis e Adultos em Betim e Belo Horizonte. Edita partituras de música brasileira dos séculos XVIII e XIX.

Name: Fundacion para el desarrollo interdisciplinario de la ciencia, la tecnología y las artes
Description:
Generamos instancias y proyectos que buscan enriquecer la vida a partir del trabajo colaborativo. 

El carácter Interdisciplinario  es la respuesta a la necesidad de romper con el esquema reduccionista y anti-integrativo promovido en la sociedad actual.

En DICTA buscamos conectar personas que compartan las características necesarias para el trabajo colaborativo, sin importar sus conocimientos o condición social. Algunas de éstas características son honestidad, compromiso, responsabilidad, repudio a la corrupción, preferencia por el enriquecimiento interno por sobre el enriquecimiento económico, curiosidad temática, desinterés por la fama, y en general ideas sobre el buen vivir, el crecimiento sustentable y la colaboración.

Name: Hunters Helping Hunters USA
Description:
Hunters Helping Hunters USA, Inc. (HHH USA) is a 501 (c) (3) charitable corporation established in 2001, that assists hunting families that have had an interruption in the family structure or support. Founded by fellow hunters from across the United States, our mission is to help ease the unexpected financial burden caused by such interruptions so that our recipients can concentrate on the most important things in their lives…their families.

Proceeds of fundraising events and donations are awarded applicants who demonstrate financial need. HHH USA pays the creditors of applicants directly and may provide other assistance to applicants as applicable. HHH USA does not make direct grants of cash directly to applicants. Grants are awarded at the discretion of HHH USA. 

HHH USA is nearing $35,000 in total grants paid.

How does HHH USA raise money?

HHH USA relies primarily on private donations from individuals and corporations and online fundraisers.  Donations are tax deductible in accordance with IRS regulations. To get involved or donate to the organization, please visit our website: www.huntershelpinghuntersusa.org

How does HHH USA use donations?

HHH USA awards financial assistance to hunters and their families who demonstrate financial need.  HHH USA pays the creditors of applicants directly or may participate in larger fund-raising events for hunters in need.  Examples include paying medical service providers, funeral expenses, utilities, or purchasing items for live auctions or raffles for local fund-raising events.   

HHH USA is run by 100% unpaid volunteers.  A small portion of donations go toward overhead expenses such as website maintenance, but the vast majority of donations are directly passed through to applicants.

Donors and Philanthropists are welcome and much needed. All donations go to helping those hunters and their families in helping them through rough times.

Name: Hirschfeld-Eddy-Stiftung
Description: Hirschfeld-Eddy-Stiftung is a foundation for the human rights of lesbians, gays, bisexuals, trans and intersex people (LGBTI)

Name: Couch Family Foundation
Description:
Healthy Children, Strong Families, Vibrant Communities

Working to improve the lives of children & families across New England.

The mission of the Couch Family Foundation is to be a catalyst for change in the lives of children and their families by creating quality and equitable opportunities to help them learn, thrive, and lead healthy, fulfilling lives.

Name: Oliver Patch Project
Description:
The Oliver Patch Project (OPP) is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit corporation whose mission is to create a fun, engaging platform where children with childhood cancers and their families can connect through a series of unique patches created by artists and designers just for them.  
**Please follow us on Instagram and Facebook for more up to the minute news!**

Empowering kids to find their inner warriors, as they endure life changing milestone events, by providing a bit of sunshine throughout their entire cancer journey from discovery through completion.  

The project is supported by donations and all the fun is provided to our Warriors 100% free of charge.  

We believe in the power of positivity to promote healing.

Name: Collectif OLYMPE
Rocky Mountain Gator Club
Description:
As part of the Rocky Mountain Gator Club, your Alumni Association membership directly supports the RMGC, as well as providing you with your official membership into the Gator Nation. Whether you are an alumnus, parent, or a friend, you can join the UF Alumni Association.

Network socially and professionally with the hundreds of Gators in the Rocky Mountain region, support scholarships, and enjoy the best Gator game day experience outside of Gainesville.

The Mile High Swamp at Jackson’s Lodo (1520 20th Street) broadcasts all our football games on multiple big screen HDTVs and the sound system pumps out our fight song with each touchdown. We sway to ’We are the Boys’ and cheer on those boys from ole Florida just as rowdy Gator fans should!

Help RMGC support the local community and Gator Nation causes through local community projects and our annual participation in International Gator Day. We offer other club activities including Rocky Mountain Gator Ski Day, SEC Picnic, Football Kickoff Parties, Happy Hours, and SEC/ACC Mixers.

Hope you can join us soon! Go Gators!

Name: The Spinoza Foundation
Description: We are passionate about the capability and strength of Ugandan (African) communities. Through a multi-dimensional approach, we believe that human, social, political, and economic development is feasible. This year's focus is youth leadership development.

Name: Gradyoumate Foundation
Description: Gradyoumate Foundation is a social venture working with a motive to help, support and enable college going students achieve their dream careers through dedicated programs

Name: NY Funders Alliance
Description:
NY Funders Alliance is a statewide membership association of grantmakers and philanthropic advisors working across New York State. We create opportunities for funders to connect with peers, learn together, and engage in timely conversations that strengthen philanthropy and support thriving communities.

Through conferences, peer communities, workshops, and shared resources, NY Funders Alliance fosters learning, collaboration, and reflection on the issues shaping philanthropy today and the road ahead.

Name: Charity Bridge Fund
Description:
Charity Bridge Fund was created to respond to a growing crisis in the nonprofit sector: sudden and unexpected cuts to government funding.  

Programs that once had steady support are now facing urgent gaps, leaving communities without essential services. 

Formerly JustGive—a trusted giving platform that has connected donors to causes since 1999 and raised over $600 million—Charity Bridge Fund provides a centralized, nonpartisan resource to identify and support nonprofits most impacted by these funding losses.

We maintain the first consolidated database of defunded charities, highlighting:
• Which programs lost government support
• The amount of funding withdrawn
• The urgent needs going forward

Through real-time giving opportunities, transparent impact stories, and trusted research, we make it simple for donors to connect with nonprofits where their support is needed most. Whether it’s a small gift or a major contribution, every donation helps bridge the gap and sustain critical services.

Our mission is to give nonprofits a voice, connect donors to their impact, and make giving our shared purpose.

Together, we can close the gap and strengthen the social sector.

Name: Mandel Institute for Nonprofit Leadership
Description:
The Mandel Institute for Nonprofit Leadership empowers leaders to achieve visions of social, cultural, and educational change through three core programs: the Executive Leadership Program, the Educational Leadership Program, and the Cultural Leadership Program. 

Graduates of Mandel Institute programs participate in continuing educational, training and mentoring activities sponsored by the Mandel Institute for Nonprofit Leadership.

For more information, please visit our website: http://mandelinstitute.org/

The Jack, Joseph and Morton Mandel Foundation covers travel and program expenses. Sponsoring organizations provide release time for participation in the program seminars.

Name: Semaine Belge de Kinshasa
Description: Une initiative des Belges et Congolais actifs en RDC qui ont souhaité mettre en avant les liens durables et variés qui existent entre la RDC et la Belgique au niveau économique mais aussi au niveau culturel et au niveau de la coopération au développement entre les deux pays,  &. .

Name: Barnabas Foundation
Description:
Since 1976, Barnabas Foundation has helped thousands of generous Christians give more effectively to the churches and ministries close to their hearts. We help people accomplish the greatest good -- for their families and for God's Kingdom -- through efficient, tax-wise giving. 

Barnabas Foundation also supports the important, Christ-honoring work of more than 800 churches and 200 member ministries. Members benefit from expert planned giving resources and services in four key areas:  

+ Distinctly Christian marketing content
+ Administration of complex gifts
+ Planning expertise for supporters
+ Ongoing training for staff

Name: CharityCAN
Description:
CharityCAN is Canada's premier prospect research, donor identification and relationship mapping platform.  

At CharityCAN, we are guided by the idea that prospect research can be made more efficient through data. The datasets in CharityCAN are carefully selected to give our subscribers the ability to find useful, meaningful, and accurate information about the people, companies, and charities they are researching.  

Most importantly, CharityCAN connects the data points with our Prospect Profiles, Relationship Maps, and Relationship Path tools. After all, individual data points are important to quality prospect research; mapping relationships and inferring connections across multiple datasets is invaluable.

Name: ONG Education Sans Frontière
Description: Fondée à Genève, Education Sans Frontière est un organisme à but non lucratif dont la vocation consiste à venir en aide aux jeunes des milieux défavorisés dans les pays en voie de développement. Cette aide est orientée dans le domaine éducatif à travers la réalisation de structures scolaires, des micro-projets et du soutien didactique et financier aux élèves démunis.

Name: Project Optimism
Description:
Spreading Optimism one Optimist at a time!
Optimism can be learnt and it can help in both business and personal pursuits.
Please look at our first free course 'The habits of an Optimist' and contributing to our Wall of Optimism.
www.projectoptimism.com.au

Name: Mertz Gilmore Foundation
Description: A private grantmaking institution in New York City.

Name: EuApoio
Description:
Todas as empresas podem e devem se engajar na construção de uma sociedade mais justa contribuindo para diminuir as desigualdades e essa é a missão da Eu Apoio. 

Nós te ajudamos a fazer o bem de forma correta e com suporte de especialistas, para conectar sua empresa a projetos sociais e de impacto. Aqui você garante consultoria com foco em comunicação e tributos. 

A prática da cidadania, empatia e amor não deve ser um processo burocrático. Vamos começar a transformar vidas juntos?

Name: The Cincinnati Eye Institute Foundation
Description: To preserve and improve eye sight by providing access to eye care for those who are under-served, furthering the education of ophthalmology, and funding research.

Name: Fondation Richard Lokossa
Description:
Intervenir en milieu éducatif dans le but d’encourager l’excellence sous toutes ses formes.
Nous œuvrons pour le bien-être et l'épanouissement des filles en milieu scolaire et universitaire.

Name: Angels of America's Fallen
Description:
Angels of America's Fallen is a (501(c)(3) nonprofit organization that exists to support the children of our fallen military and first responder heroes. Over 16,000 children have lost their hero since 9-11-2001 and the average age at the child’s time of loss is just seven years old. 

Our Mission: To help children of our fallen military and first responders develop into strong and successful adults by providing them positive mentoring and developmental activities at their most crucial developmental phase of life. 

Our Vision: To walk alongside the children of our fallen when they need it most, while they are developing, regardless of the cause of their parent's death. Many children fail to qualify for benefits and fall through the cracks because of their parent's cause of death or unit of assignment. Each child suffers regardless if mom or dad died in a combat zone, training accident, on the job illness, or from suicide as a result of the stress of their jobs. From the child's perspective, the impact is the same, mom or dad is no longer there. No one can ever replace their lost parent, but we can be there for them. 

We help when the need is greatest - the time between the loss of a parent and adulthood. We grant the funds for educational and developmental activities the children choose as an outlet to help them stay on track as they develop into adults. Activities supported are under the watchful eye of a coach, teacher, or instructor who can provide mentoring and guidance.

Name: Milford Foundation
Description: The Milford Foundation's mission is to strengthen New Zealand communities through the lenses of youth, education and environment. Through a combination of investment expertise, our generous donor community, a commitment to efficiency, effectiveness and transparency, and strong partnerships, we can – and will – make a meaningful impact for New Zealand’s future generations.

Name: Actions de Solidarité Internationale
Description:
ASI intervient en République du Congo, depuis 2006, dans le cadre d’un programme de « Prise en charge de jeunes filles en situation de rue et de vulnérabilité » à Brazzaville, et à Pointe Noire depuis 2012.
L’objectif principal d’ASI au Congo est de contribuer à l’amélioration du niveau socio-économique des jeunes filles en situation de rue. Ce programme a su montrer son efficacité au travers d’une méthodologie innovante, s’appuyant sur un cycle de prise en charge complet, allant de la prévention dans la rue à l’insertion sociale et professionnelle de ces jeunes filles.

Depuis 2006, un autre programme a vu le jour, celui de la « Prévention du phénomène des enfants des
rues » à Brazzaville.
Ce projet cible les jeunes filles et garçons, de 6 à 15 ans, en situation de grande vulnérabilité (sociale et économique). Ce programme assure la prise en charge et le développement de l’enfant par un processus global regroupant des dimensions affective, physique, sociale et cognitive. 
Les activités menées sont :
•	La prise en charge scolaire : équipement scolaire, inscription à l’école, frais de transport
•	La prise en charge sanitaire et sociale : un repas complet distribué trois fois par semaine, suivi social / médiation familiale
•	Le soutien scolaire : organisation de cours de soutien scolaire en petits groupes, orientation scolaire en fonction des capacités de chacun, visite dans les écoles, rencontre avec les enseignants, séances de découverte de la lecture, séances de loisir, culturelle ou artistique, excursions
•	Le soutien aux familles : organisation de séances de sensibilisation, orientation vers des activités génératrices de revenu ou de l'alphabétisation

Name: Churchill Fellowship
Description:
Churchill Fellowships assist New Zealanders to travel overseas, to visit other communities, to learn from others and study topics that will help them to increase their contribution to the community, and advance their trade, industry, profession or business. Fellows return to New Zealand with inspiration, new networks, and knowledge that supports and develops skills within their field of interest. 

The Winston Churchill Memorial Trust (WCMT) was established in 1965 to honour the memory of Sir Winston Churchill by awarding Churchill Fellowships that provide ordinary New Zealanders with extraordinary opportunities. Churchill believed that world peace and greater international understanding could be promoted through people travelling to other countries and experiencing other cultures.

To find out more, apply for a fellowship, or to donate to the Trust, visit our website.

Name: Open Space STL
Description:
The Open Space STL is a nonprofit organization that works independently and collaboratively to conserve and sustain land, water and other natural resources throughout the St. Louis region.

Throughout our history, Open Space STL has played an integral role in:

    * Restoring and supporting a clean and healthy Meramec River
    * Helping establish Castlewood State Park, Forest 44 Conservation Area, Beetree County Park, the Glassberg Conservation Area, Forest River Trail Park and an addition to the Young Conservation Area. 
    * Restoring land and water along almost 500 miles of river in the Meramec Watershed
    * Helping to thwart efforts to dam the Meramec River

In addition to restoring and maintaining the region's already dedicated open spaces, the organization's ongoing work includes identifying areas that can be acquired and set aside for public use, and discouraging improper land use and detrimental development.  Acting as a Land Trust, we raise and provide funds for new open space projects, offering land owners an alternative to development.

Through environmental and community building events such as Operation Clean Stream and Operation Wild Lands, Open Space STL seeks to raise awareness about the importance of land and water conservation, and the value of clean water.

Name: visite
Description:
Der Verein visite organisiert Praktika für Berufslernende in der Schweiz.
Dies fördert die Mobilität und stärkt ihre beruflichen, sprachlichen und interkulturellen Kompetenzen. Gleichzeitig überbrücken wir Sprach- und Kulturgrenzen, wirken dem Fachkräftemangel entgegen und stärken die Wirtschaft.
Eine Initiative der Rotary Clubs Schweiz / Liechtenstein.

Name: One Mother to Another NZ
Description:
We at 'One Mother To Another' provide care packs to parents and caregivers who find themselves going through a difficult time in hospital with a sick child or newborn and Mothers going their own mental health journey. 
We currently provide over 500 care packs a month to 20 hospital wards and four community groups in New Zealand's South Island.

Our aim is to comfort and support parents and caregivers who are in hospital in what can be a very vulnerable and distressing time. We try to bridge the gap between two strangers and offer understanding and acknowledgement of their difficulty but mostly we want to put a smile on their face and let them know we care about what they are going through. We hope in some way to brighten their day.

Name: Associazione Fede Cristiana Fidenza Odv
Description:
La nostra missione è combattere la povertà e l'emarginazione in due aree ben definite.

In Africa è nostra volontà realizzare in Costa d'Avorio progetti di sviluppo sostenibile, in particolare aiutando giovani e donne nella lotta contro la disoccupazione, assicurando loro un lavoro dignitoso e l'inclusione sociale, migliorando le condizioni sanitarie.

In Italia la nostra missione è quella di aiutare le famiglie in difficoltà economiche ed emarginate nelle provincie di Reggio Emilia e Parma, tramite fornitura di generi di prima necessità.

Name: Colorado Pet Pantry
Description:
The Colorado Pet Pantry temporarily feed Colorado pets, allowing families to increase their ability to care for pets with the goal of keeping them out of shelters and with their families.

Every dollar donated to the Colorado Pet Pantry is worth $5 in pet food. Combine that with the fact that your donation helps a pet stay with its family – keeping the dog or cat out of the shelter where it would need to be housed, fed, walked, cared for, and REHOMED – and your donation makes a huge difference!

In 2017, we fed 13,333 pets for a month. We grew to 15 food bank partners, serving each neighborhood once per month with the goal of ensuring that all families in a neighborhood have the resources to consistently feed their pets, keeping them out of shelters. 

In 2017, in addition to our monthly pet food banks, we launched an Emergency Pet Food Program (info at http://www.coloradopetpantry.org/about-us/how-to-get-pet-food/emergency-pet-food-program/) and the Animal Welfare Share Program, where we share pet food and supplies with ~50 animal welfare organizations. More Info:  http://www.coloradopetpantry.org/about-us/animal-welfare-share-program/. 

Volunteers contributed 11,375 hours in 2017. 

In 2018, we plan to distribute one-month supply of pet food and accessories to 20,000 dogs and cats, increasing our direct impact from 2017 by 67%.

Name: EHLsmile - Association
Description:
EHLsmile is a non-profit organization founded in 1996 by a group of EHL students. We co-create and revamp hospitality vocational training centers to fight against poverty and encourage sustainable tourism development together with other NGOs. 

At EHLsmile, we believe in the power of collaboration and diverse perspectives to drive positive change. Our organization is led by a dynamic and dedicated team comprising a board and a student committee. This unique governance structure allows us to harness the wisdom of experienced leaders and the fresh insights of passionate young minds.

Name: AFP Golden Horseshoe Chapter
Description: If you are a development professional, fundraising volunteer, not-for-profit board member, allied professional or thinking about philanthropy as a career choice, AFP Golden Horseshoe welcomes you!

Name: "4090 Charity" Foundation
Description: Test

Name: The Florence Institute Trust
Description:
The Florrie is a stunning Grade II Victorian community heritage venue located in south Liverpool.
Open Monday to Friday, we offer a wide variety of participatory activities for people of all ages, multi-purpose event spaces available for hire and fully inclusive workspaces for businesses.

Name: Hopalong Animal Rescue
Description:
Each year, thousands of animals are abandoned and must be euthanized throughout Northern California due to lack of shelter space from pet overpopulation. Since 1993, Hopalong and Second Chance Animal Rescue has rescued, fostered, spayed/neutered, and placed thousands of companion animals.

In the last 18 years, we have found homes for over 18,000 homeless and ill animals. Each story is different and many are heart-breaking, but in every case there is a common thread: an animal that deserves a second chance at life and a permanent, loving home.

Name: dr. Hendrik Muller's Vaderlands Fonds
Description: Het Hendrik Mullerfonds is een middelgroot particulier vermogensfonds dat beurzen geeft aan studenten voor een studie of stage in het buitenland en bijdragen schenkt t.b.v. restauraties van rijksmonumenten, wetenschappelijke prijzen, cultuurhistorische publicaties en diverse culturele projecten in Nederland. Het fonds is in 1941 in het leven geroepen door dr. Hendrik P.N. Muller en sinds 1945 actief.

Name: Organismo de Nutrición Infantil A.C.
Description:
Nuestra misión es prevenir la malnutrición infantil y recuperar a los niños que la padecen, buscando impedir a largo plazo sus consecuencias biológicas, psicológicas y sociales.

Es la Asociación Civil legalmente constituida más antigua de Jalisco (1954).

Está afiliado al IJAS Instituto Jaliscience de Asistencia Social. 

Recibió el Reconocimiento al Compromiso con los demás otorgado por el Centro Mexicano para la Filantropía (CEMEFI) y cuenta desde el 2005 con el Reconocimiento de Institucionalidad y Transparencia otorgado por CEMEFI.

Name: ONG COEUR D'OR CI
Description:
ONG CŒUR D'OR S'engage à réhabiliter et insérer les orphelins, enfants de la rue, veuve et prostituées sur tout l’étendu du territoire Ivoirien. Elle lutte pour leur bien être. Créer en 2004, ONG CŒUR D'OR est une organisation à caractère social et humanitaire, apolitique et à but non lucratif. 
Elle a pour mission d'apporter une aide aux populations les plus défavorisés dans les domaines de l'alimentation, la santé, l’éducation et la santé.
www.facebook.com/Solidarityspirit987/
www.instagram.com/ongcoeurdorci/
www.twitter.com/CoeurOng
ongcoeurdorci2004@gmail.com

Name: Clothed in Hope
Description:
Clothed in Hope is empowering vulnerable women in Zambia through education and economic opportunity. We are a 501(c)(3) tax-deductible charitable organization based in NC and operating in Lusaka, Zambia. 

We are dedicated to breaking the cycle of poverty, one stitch at a time.

Name: Walking Life
Description:
Nous avons réalisé au travers de différentes missions d’évangélisation, la chance et les opportunités que donnent une bonne éducation, plus encore quand celle-ci est dispensée gratuitement et est de qualité.
Axés sur les principes bibliques, les pédagogies dites alternatives, combinées aux pédagogies traditionnelles ; nous travaillons à soutenir matériellement, pédagogiquement mais également financièrement des structures existantes ou en construction à l’international.
Nombre de nos projets sont au stade embryonnaire mais nous œuvrons pour leur réalisation avec une équipe motivée et dévouée.

Name: Foreningen BogSpiloppen
Description: Foreningens formål er at styrke børns faglige og sociale kompetencer, således at de vil være bedre rustet ved skolestart. Dette skal ske via højtlæsning i daginstitutioner og andre tiltag. Særligt fokus er på udsatte børn.

Name: Stephanie's Lifeline
Description:
Stephanie's Lifeline is a community benefit organization, founded in 2011 in Seattle, WA. The programs and services by Stephanie's Lifeline have provided support to foster, homeless, low-income, and other underserved/at-risk individuals and families in need for the past eleven years.

REVIVE
With the Revive program, Stephanie's Lifeline collects the clothing that is in the lost-and-found at elementary, middle, and high schools, wash and sanitize, and refurbish the clothing to a like-new state. The clothing is then donated back to schools in King County to children and families in need. Stephanie's Lifeline takes on the responsibility of picking up the items, sanitizing and washing them, refurbishing them, and then transporting them back to the the students in need.

BackPACKS bring HOPE
In June each year, Backpacks are filled with school supplies and given to students in need in the King County area prior to the beginning of the school year. We also collect and fill Backpacks with hygiene, food, and clothing items that are needed most by people experiencing homelessness. The backpacks are then giving to people on the streets or within homeless encampments in King County. If you would like to make a donation toward our backpack program please visit our GoFundMe page https://www.gofundme.com/backpacksbringhope in order to help us raise the funds to purchase more items to fill backpacks for people in need.

Holiday Gifts of HOPE
Stephanie’s Lifeline Homeless Outreach, Prevention and Education Program is hosting our 10th year of our Holiday Gifts of HOPE gift giving program. This year our Dear Santa two-day gift giving and photo event for foster, homeless, low-income, and other underserved/at-risk youth and families in King County. A total of 40 referrals from local social workers for our two-day event at The Blu Grouse in White Center. Each child referred to the program will receive gifts and a photo with Santa Claus.

Name: Causeway CARes
Description: Causeway CARes’ mission is to make a direct and profound impact in and around Ocean County in the areas of education, food security and quality of life. Founded in 2004 and funded by The Wintrode Family Foundation, Inc., Causeway CARes strives to make the community a better place to live, grow and thrive.

Name: Rotary Club Paris-Nord
Description:
Réunions hebdomadaires le mardi de 12 h 30 à 13 h 45 au Cercle National des Armées, 8 place Saint-Augustin 75008 PARIS.

Présentation du Rotary-club Paris-Nord.
La devise : « Servir d'abord »
Nos valeurs : convivialité, éthique, service.
Nos actions caritatives et amicales

Santé :
• éradication de la poliomyélite : participation au financement du programme mondial « PolioPlus » du Rotary International
• financement de la recherche sur le cerveau : vente de places pour l'opération nationale « Espoir en tête »
• « Handivoile » : une journée sur l'eau et autour de l'eau offerte aux personnes en situation de handicap mental
 
Développement économique :
• week-end annuel de collecte en faveur de la Banque alimentaire
• aide au retour à l'emploi de chômeurs de longue durée
• financement d'adduction d'eau et de sanitaires dans les pays en voie de développement
 
Jeunes générations :
• soutien à l'Association « Jeunesse de Saint Vincent », maison des jeunes du 12 rue Bossuet à Paris 10e
• attribution de bourses et soutien à des lycéens en BTS du 10e arrondissement de Paris
• parrainage d'échanges de lycéens et d'étudiants boursiers entre pays
• participation aux séminaires de préparation des 20-30 ans à la vie professionnelle
 
Amitié internationale et paix mondiale :
• relations suivies avec nos clubs-contacts à Anvers, Berlin et Turin
• accueil de visiteurs Rotariens du monde entier
• visites aux clubs Rotary du monde entier
 
Un réseau de 45 membres, femmes et hommes, âgés de 30 à plus de 80 ans, tous responsables ou anciens responsables professionnels ou civiques, de nationalités et origines diverses.
 
Des réunions hebdomadaires le mardi de 12 h 30 à 13 h 45 au Cercle National des Armées, 8 place Saint-Augustin 75008 PARIS.
 
Sans oublier nos conférences professionnelles et culturelles, sorties et voyages amicaux.

Pour en savoir plus sur le Rotary en général :
Rotary International
Rotary francophone

Name: Indiana State Police Alliance
Description:
The Indiana State Police Alliance (ISPA) was established in 1974 as an association of Indiana State Police troopers with a focus toward promoting better law enforcement to the citizens of Indiana and gaining state-wide support for Indiana troopers. Since our humble beginning in 1974 our scope has broadened significantly. Today, the Alliance has expanded its responsibilities to include increased member benefits such as legal assistance, legislative lobbying, funeral support and survivor benefits, college scholarships, and numerous community support activities throughout the state not to mention benevolent funds geared directly toward the membership.

The ISPA works directly with the Indiana State Police department, the governor's office, and members of the state legislature on issues of state-wide law enforcement and public safety. Additionally, the ISPA is the only association of Indiana state police officers recognized by the National Troopers Coalition (NTC). Ever growing, and always looking for ways to meet the needs of our members and the citizens of our great Hoosier state, the ISPA has incorporated Alliance Security, a company that offers off-duty employment opportunities for troopers across the state. Likewise, a non-profit organization called Cops for Kids was established in order to build priceless relationships with our state's children through a variety of programs.

Name: Kids Against Hunger
Description:
KAH is a program whose purpose is to feed starving and malnourished children and adults, both in the US and internationally.
A typical event has 200 volunteers, who pack a total of 50,000 meals within 4 hours!  Ages 8 to 88 can help pack meals--a wonderful experience for families to work together to help the community.

Name: Mirante Cultural
Description: O Mirante Cultural acredita que transformar o mundo é preciso. O mundo, em todas as eras, precisou de pequenos ou grandes ajustes que tornassem a vida diferente, melhor. Essa transformação acontece através de um encontro verdadeiro com o amor de Deus, expresso em Jesus Cristo. Nós entendemos que esse encontro é possível através de experiências educacionais inspiradoras, que promovam emancipação na forma de pensar e viver. Hoje nós temos projetos nas áreas de arte, educação e cultura que garantem que um aluno consiga levar a transformação para o seu mundo.

Name: Hack The Crisis NL
Description:
COVID-19 has turned our world upside down. Humanity is facing significant health and economic challenges, and we need to find ways to tackle them. That’s why – inspired by the #hackthecrisis global movement – TNW and Innofest Nederland initiated a volunteer-led online innovation marathon in the Netherlands co-organized with StartupAmsterdam, Startupbootcamp Amsterdam, Brain Fuel, Women in AI, GoodUp, HOAX Amsterdam, Smart Camels event design, coronacommunitycare.nl, and EY.
 
The coronavirus hackathon took place on April 3-5, and focused on finding solutions in the fields of patient care, protection for medical and sanitary staff at the hospitals, virus containment, and digital solutions for people and businesses during social distancing, among others.

Name: Minds Matter Portland
Description: Minds Matter Portland is a not-for-profit organization whose mission is to transform the lives of accomplished high school students from low-income families by broadening their dreams and...

Name: Footprints of Fight
Description: Footprints of Fight is a non-profit 501(c)(3) organization that is dedicated to providing services for the family and patient of pediatric cancer in Washington while going through the treatment process. The purpose of these services is to reduce the family's stress of everyday life through which increased levels of courage, optimism, strength, and resiliency will form to help the family conquer cancer in present and future.

Name: Pró-Menor Dom Bosco
Description: O Pró-Menor Dom Bosco, é uma instituição de caráter filantrópico de Assistência Social básica, educacional e religiosa, sem fins lucrativos, que há 39 anos atua na cidade de Manaus, na garantia de direitos e proteção socioassistenciais de adolescentes e jovens, oriundos de famílias em vulnerabilidade social, utilizando como ferramenta a capacitação e qualificação profissional como meio de inclusão e transformação de vidas.

Name: Rotary Club Of Long Beach
Description:
The mission of Long Beach Rotary is to provide service to others, promote integrity, and advance world understanding, goodwill, and peace through its friendships of business, professional, and community leaders.

What would it take to change the world? Our members believe it starts with a commitment to Service Above Self.

Core Values:
Ethical Standards
Fun and Friendship
Inclusion and Diversity
Personal Growth
Leadership

The Four-Way Test
This test, which has been translated into more than 100 languages, asks the following questions:
Is it the TRUTH?
Is it FAIR to all concerned?
Will it bring GOODWILL and BETTER FRIENDSHIPS?
Will it be BENEFICIAL to all concerned?

Name: Associação dos Celiacos - Seção Brasilia - ACELBRA-DF
Description:
A Associação estimulará a criação de Associações congêneres, em todas as unidades da Federação.

A Associação tem por objetivo colaborar, com todos os meios possíveis, quer sejam econômicos, acadêmicos, sociais ou de índoles, institucionais como universitários, hospitalares, investigatórios, os quais tenham por objetivo a fiscalização, o diagnóstico e o tratamento da doença celíaca.

A Associação buscará a solução e a melhoria da qualidade de vida em todos os seus aspectos comunitários, ao celíaco, inclusive enquanto consumidor e cidadão.

Para tanto exercerá seus direitos em nível nacional, buscará apoio inclusive, internacionalmente, podendo num e noutro caso firmar convênios com entidades privada, públicas e filantrópicas.

 A Associação divulgará, a doença celíaca e suas conseqüências, por intermédio de palestras, conferências, reuniões, bem como, outros meios possíveis, desde que, sob orientação do Conselho Técnico de Profissionais da Saúde.

A Associação trará junto aos órgãos governamentais dos níveis Estaduais e Federais, o fomento, pesquisa e análise de produtos alimentícios, comercializados, para que possam ser usados por celíacos, bem como, exercerá fiscalização, visando o cumprimento da legislação, pelas empresas fornecedoras, assim definidas por intermédio das leis vigentes.

A Associação tem como objetivo, ainda, efetivar esclarecimentos aos parlamentares, no sentido de implementar ações legais que possam favorecer a melhoria da qualidade de vida do celíaco, enquanto cidadão e consumidor.

 A Associação promoverá eventos no sentido de angariar recursos em beneficio dos objetivos a que se propõe, cumprindo as formalidades legais.

Name: The Marion I. & Henry J. Knott Foundation
Description:
The Marion I. & Henry J. Knott Foundation is a Catholic family foundation committed to honoring our founders’ legacy of generosity to strengthen our community.

Henry and Marion Knott were incredibly dedicated to two things: their family and their surrounding community of Baltimore. In 1977, they started the Foundation as a way of joining their two passions.

Since our founding, we have awarded more than $70 million in grants to nonprofit organizations throughout central and western Maryland. Today, we award approximately $2.5 million in grants each year across five program areas arts & humanities, Catholic activities, education, health care and, human services.

Our work in these areas includes three distinct funding opportunities General Fund Grants, Cash Flow Loans and, Discretionary Grants

Name: Highline Schools Foundation
Description:
The Highline Schools Foundation for Excellence was established in 1999 with the purpose of providing community resources to help Highline Public Schools succeed. We believe that by helping to enrich the classroom and school experience, we can engage students to reach a higher level of learning, and the entire community will benefit. 

The Foundation is governed by a dedicated group of volunteer Trustees and is the only 501(c)(3) organization supporting all Highline Public Schools. The Foundation strives to be the single point of contact for community members, local and national business, service organizations and other foundations to connect their philanthropic efforts with a single school or the entire district.

The mission of the Highline Schools Foundation is “providing community resources to help Highline Public Schools succeed”. The Foundation’s purpose is to secure resources that will enhance education for all students in the district. The Foundation shall insure that revenue, goods, services and other contributions, will to every extent possible have broad utilization, and substantially equal distribution throughout the schools in Highline Public Schools.

Our long-term goals are to improve student success by supporting Academic achievement, College & career readiness & Enriched learning experiences.

We support educators through classroom grants, recognition, & professional development.

Our short-term goal is to raise funds for Academic Achievement, Classroom Enrichment; The Arts; Athletics; College & Career Readiness; Recognition; & Community Programs

Highline Schools Foundation for Excellence values:
*  An environment that helps all students reach their highest potential
*  Educators and community members that are empowered to bridge educational gaps in the district
*  The diversity and ethnicity of the district of our community
*  Giving back in ways that make a real difference to students and the district

Name: The Pauls Charitable Fundation
Description: The Pauls Charitable Fund promotes the livelihood of targeted communities through education-sponsorship and education-related projects in Kenya, Canada, and Ethiopia.

Name: Gotham Cheer
Description:
Gotham Cheer is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit that was created out of our personal drive to give back to those who are in need, particularly homeless or disadvantaged youth. We aim to be leaders in the community through volunteer work and community service events.

Who are our members? 

Inclusivity is the heart of our organization. Gotham Cheer welcomes diverse people from all types of backgrounds and talents. The Gotham Cheer family is made up of unique service-oriented LGBTQIA+ members dedicated to making an impact on our neighbors, Community Partners, the U.S. and beyond.

What is our motto?

“Community, Diversity and Spirit!“

Name: Ally Squared
Description: Through its work to navigate the positive and negative consequences of speaking up in real-life situations, Ally Squared envisions a world where people are empowered to be their own ally as well as be there for others.

Name: Rotary Club of Portsmouth
Description:
Join us for lunch!
We meet Thursdays (with the exception of the last Thursday of each month) at Roger Brown's Restaurant and Sports Bar, 316 High Street, Portsmouth from 12:30 - 1:30 pm. 

Rotary is a global network of 1.4 million neighbors, friends, leaders, and problem-solvers who see a world where people unite and take action to create lasting change – across the globe, in our communities, and in ourselves.

Since 1918, the Rotary Club of Portsmouth, VA has helped the citizens of Portsmouth and Hampton Roads build better communities through productive fellowship and charitable endeavors.

Today, our Club is comprised of 45 members who have experience working in a variety of sectors throughout Hampton Roads. This diversity in skills and networks, along with an intense commitment to community, allows our Club to successfully fund projects and initiatives that create true impact in Portsmouth and the surrounding Hampton Roads communities.

Name: American Lung Association
Description: Since 1904, the American Lung Association is the leading organization working to save lives by improving lung health and preventing lung disease through research, education and advocacy.

Name: Fondazione Carlo Enrico Giulini
Description:
La Fondazione Carlo Giulini, sorta nel 2014, crede in una società moderna, solidale, aperta, equa e tollerante, che consideri la diversità una ricchezza, che sappia valorizzare il capitale “umano” prima di ogni altra cosa, che guardi alle fasce più deboli e in difficoltà con l’intento di reintegrarle, offrendo prospettive di futuro.

Attraverso i propri interventi diretti e indiretti, la Fondazione mira a sostenere lo sviluppo sociale, culturale ed economico del proprio territorio di riferimento, a rafforzare il senso di appartenenza e di coesione sociale della comunità, contribuendo alla risoluzione autonoma delle criticità esistenti. 

Sono quattro le tematiche principali mediante le quali si articolano i progetti sociali promossi: cura della salute e dell’infanzia, sviluppo di comunità, educazione e sport, integrazione sociale.

La complessità delle sfide affrontate richiede che tutte le forze sociali e le Istituzioni cooperino nello sforzo di favorire il miglioramento e il rafforzamento della società: è per questo che la Fondazione Giulini si pone come catalizzatore delle risorse della comunità locale capaci di collaborare nella produzione di “beni pubblici”, prevalentemente nel territorio sardo.

Name: Open Hands Initiative
Description:
The Open Hands Initiative (OHI) is a US-based 501(c)3 non-profit organization dedicated to improving people-to-people understanding and international friendship through cultural and educational exchanges and other projects that focus on our shared values and common humanity.

Our mission is to promote greater understanding and goodwill between the United States and its global neighbors. We aim to build bridges among people of all nations and dispel misconceptions and misunderstandings.

Name: Mitzvah Opportunity
Description: Created in 2017 to help those with an immediate financial need in Israel  without the red tape often needed to cut through for the necessary aid  As of November 2021 we have raised over 1,600,000 shekels. When possible a US or Israel tax receipt will be obtained but sometimes it will just be a cash transaction where you get all the credit in shamayim.

Name: League Of Michigan Bicyclists
Description:
The mission of the League of Michigan Bicyclists (LMB) is to promote bicycling and the safety of bicyclists on the roadways of Michigan.
LMB is a non-profit, tax-exempt statewide membership organization working to improve conditions for bicycling in Michigan. LMB supports many programs and projects and cooperates with state and local agencies and other like-minded groups toward this goal.

Name: Laird Norton Family Foundation
Description: The Laird Norton Family Foundation (LNFF) is a private family foundation in Seattle, Washington, with a mission to honor and reflect the family’s shared values through giving and engage the Laird Norton family in philanthropy as a platform for strengthening family connections.

Name: UNASA University of Pretoria
Description:
Capturing the magical moments of young people who are dedicated to promoting the United Nations (UN) Values and keeping them updated on global issues! #UNASAUP

United Nations Association of South Africa (UNASA) University of Pretoria (UP) Chapter is a student-run organization/society associated with UNASA and the World Federation of United Nations Associations (WFUNA). It is dedicated to building awareness of the values and work of the UN as well as upholding those values and contributing to that work in the student community of UP and the communities of Tshwane as a whole.

Name: Sigfox Foundation

Antarctica project was about to provide GPS signals to help scientists to be secured 
The rhinos project, still running in Africa, is a pionniering solution providing vital data to the rangers, who protect the area.

Name: The Women's Fund of Winston-Salem
Description: The Women’s Fund of Winston-Salem brings together women who care about the economic and social well-being of all women and girls in Forsyth County. Members of The Women’s Fund help identify the needs in our community, then vote with their hearts and dollars to support projects that meet those needs. Through the power of collective giving, members of The Women’s Fund seek to address the root causes of problems by supporting projects that bring about social change rather than simply provide social services.

Name: Simcoe County Young Professionals Association
Description: The Simcoe County Young Professionals Association (SCYPA) is a group of professionals in the early years of their careers. Our members live in all parts of Simcoe County, from Bradford to Midland and from Orillia to Collingwood, with a large portion of our membership residing in Barrie. SCYPA was formed to address the lack of opportunities specifically for people starting a new career or business to network with each other and support and promote each others’ businesses. We run monthly social and business networking events that allow our members to meet and connect with other young professionals, as well as maintain a directory of young professionals in Simcoe County. Our organization is built around the following four pillars: community involvement, professional development, business networking and social events.

Name: The Net Fort Worth
Description: The Net is a non-profit based in Fort Worth, Texas serving folks experiencing homelessness, women survivors of sexual exploitation, and low-income youth through empowering programs fueled by relationships.

Name: SHOE4AFRICA
Description:
Shoe4Africa is a non-profit organization dedicated to improving the lives of children in Africa through various initiatives, primarily focusing on healthcare, education, and sports. 

Founded in 1995 by Toby Tanser, a former professional athlete, Shoe4Africa has made significant strides in addressing the unique challenges faced by children in the African continent.

History
Shoe4Africa originated from Toby Tanser’s vision to leverage the power of sports to make a positive impact on the lives of African children. The organization’s initial focus was on collecting and distributing athletic shoes to aspiring young runners in Kenya. Over the years, Shoe4Africa expanded its scope to address broader issues affecting children, including healthcare and education.

Key Initiatives
Children’s Hospitals: Shoe4Africa has played a pivotal role in the establishment of children’s hospitals in Africa. Notably, the Shoe4Africa Public Children’s Hospital, inaugurated in 2015, serves as a dedicated facility for pediatric care, treating hundreds of children daily. Currently Shoe4Africa is constructing a specialized children’s cancer hospital.

Education: The organization has been actively involved in providing educational opportunities to children across Africa. This includes the construction of free-to-attend public schools, contributing to the education of thousands of children. Six schools have been constructed and the institutions are linked to renowned Kenyan athletes. 

Sports Development: Shoe4Africa supports sports development programs, including organizing races and events to promote physical activity and healthy living among African youth. The organization believes in the transformative power of sports to instill discipline, teamwork, and resilience. the organization recently opened a sports related boys and girls running club which is thought to be the first running club for kids aged five to fifteen in Kenya.

Name: Rotaract Club of Lake Victoria
Description:
Rotaract Club of Lake Victoria was chartered on 25th November 2011 and its located in the district 9213 sponsored by Rotary Club of Port Bell

We fellowship every Tuesday starting from 7:00pm up to 8:00pm EAT at Terranga Hotel, Bugolobi-Kampala, Uganda. Your free to join us every Tuesday. Your most welcome!!

Name: Fondazione Valsesia Ente Filantropico del Terzo Settore
Description:
Fondazione Valsesia Ente Filantropico del Terzo Settore è una fondazione di comunità, un ente non profit parte del Terzo Settore, sostenuto da Compagnia San Paolo e Fondazione Cassa di Risparmio di Vercelli.
#ilvaloredellepersone
Siamo una fondazione di comunità nata ufficialmente nel 2018, diventata Onlus ad ottobre 2019, per valorizzare il territorio valsesiano e sostenerlo attraverso il potenziamento delle risorse e delle reti locali per progettare insieme un futuro più inclusivo e sostenibile. La nostra “visione” è diffondere la “cultura” della filantropia, la cultura e l’economia del Dono e la fiducia quali valori imprescindibili per il benessere diffuso e quali concrete opportunità per le comunità.
#unitifacciamograndicose
La nostra missione è facilitare le reti collaborative, la progettazione sociale e la cittadinanza attiva per ampliare il sistema integrato di solidarietà in un’ottica di dialogo e di idee condivise, per rispondere prontamente ai bisogni e a desideri delle nostre comunità, con una visione di lungo periodo che sappia implementare le risorse e le energie a beneficio di tutti.
Siamo un’efficace “strumento di sussidiarietà per le comunità” che sostiene i fondi e i progetti impegnandosi per far crescere nel territorio opportunità e filantropia negli ambiti del socio-sanitario-assistenziale, economia sociale, educazione, formazione, sport, cultura, territorio ed ambiente.
“#facciamoloinsieme”
La nostra Fondazione collabora e si confronta con tutti gli attori locali in una logica strategica di rete progettuale, perché insieme possiamo davvero raggiungere traguardi impensabili, per un domani migliore. La nostra fondazione opera in 3 province (Bi, No, Vc) e comprende 35 comuni,

Name: Waukesha Rotary Club
Description:
The Waukesha Rotary Club as a whole belongs to Rotary International - 31,000 clubs strong, with a total membership of over 1,243,000 Rotarians, in 164 countries world wide.  

If you are an established professional wanting to make positive changes in your community and around the world, then Rotary may be for you.  Becoming a Rotarian connects you with a diverse group of professionals who share your drive to give back.  We meet weekly to:

    Discuss our community's needs and discover creative ways to meet them
    Expand our leadership and professional skills
    Catch up with good friends and meet new ones

The Waukesha Rotary Club donates services and over $20k annually in support of local charities through our various events and fund raisers.

As a part of Rotary International, along with our 1,243,000 fellow Rotarians, our primary goal right now is the eradication of polio in the world.  The good news is that we are 99% polio free.  But if we don't finish the fight right now, more than 10 million children under the age of five could be paralyzed by polio in the next 40 years.

Name: AAPACN Education Foundation
Description:
The AAPACN Education Foundation positively impacts the lives of 1.4 million people living in nursing homes by providing educational scholarships and research grants for nurses committed to improving the quality of life and care for residents living in long-term care.  

Our mission is to advance long-term and post-acute care nursing by providing nursing scholarships for higher education; and promote evidence-based healthcare nursing practice through academic research grants.

Overview:

Founded in 2014, the Foundation is the philanthropic arm of the American Association of Post-Acute Care Nursing (AAPACN). The Foundation is a 501(c)(3) charitable organization that funds and promotes the continued growth and development of nurses and of academic evidence-based research to advance the work of the nursing profession.

Thousands of dedicated women and men working in long-term and post-acute care dream of the chance to learn more, do more, be more. By funding nursing scholarships and research grants, the Foundation helps to make some of those dreams a reality for these committed healthcare professionals. 

How You Can Help:

•	Mail your tax deductible donation to: AAPACN Education Foundation, 400 S. Colorado Blvd., Suite 600, Denver, CO 80246
•	Online donations - Click the Donate Now button below to get started!
•	Establish a named scholarship - Individuals, companies, and organizations may establish an annual named academic or professional development scholarship. For more information, email Foundation@AAPACN.org. 
•	Planned giving - Planned giving supports the Foundation through financial and estate plans. For more information, email Foundation@AAPACN.org.
•	Volunteer for the Foundation – Learn how you can get involved with the Foundation by emailing Foundation@AAPACN.org.

Name: Teapot Mountain Advisory
Description: Teapot Mountain Advisory, LLC helps boards of directors, CEOs and CDOs develop strategies to establish or accelerate outreach and philanthropy programs.  Our focus is to help universities and charities develop positioning, harness data, and develop implementation strategies.
