Name: Stichting Centraal Sint Nicolaas Comité Tilburg
Description:
Het Centraal Sint Nicolaas Comité Tilburg is opgericht in 1901  en is hiermee de oudste organisatie in Nederland die zich richt op het organiseren van allerlei openbare activiteiten, zoals o.a. de intocht van de Sint passend bij het traditionele Sint Nicolaas feest in Tilburg. 

DOELSTELLING 
Daarnaast wil het Comité met haar doelstelling:  "Anoniem geven zonder er iets voor terug te krijgen" het algemeen belang dienen met de speciale aandacht voor kwetsbare groepen in de Tilburgse samenleving waar de Sint soms "het huisje voorbij gaat". Het betreft dan met name kinderen uit gezinnen die door bijzondere omstandigheden niet in staat zijn zelf een Sinterklaasfeest met kadootjes te vieren, maar ook de ouderen onder ons die door verminderde mobiliteit of door het wegvallen van familieleden en vrienden steeds meer in een isolement komen. 

GEEN WINSTOOGMERK 
Het Centraal Sint Nicolaas Comité Tilburg heeft uitdrukkelijk geen winstoogmerk. Alle met de activiteiten behaalde opbrengsten komen ten goede aan de hierboven genoemde doelstelling. 

150 VRIJWILLIGERS 
Het Comité heeft ongeveer 150 vrijwilligers die volledig belangeloos hun medewerking verlenen aan de activiteiten van het Comité. 

MEER INFORMATIE 
Voor aanvullende informatie kunt u de website van Sinterklaas bezoeken: www.sintintilburg.nl

Name: Solano Community Foundation
Description:
Solano Community Foundation is dedicated to building a stronger community and enhancing the quality of life in Solano County through the support of philanthropic activities that make a deep and lasting positive impact.

- As a grantmaker, we award grants and scholarships to improve the lives of Solano County residents.
- As a vehicle for philanthropy, we encourage private giving for public good.
- As a community leader, we inspire, educate, and cultivate a spirit of philanthropy.
- To respond to changing needs, we promote community involvement and collaboration.

Name: The SOCH, RGIT
Description:
Everything 'Great' was a mere 'Thought' once. What we do is what we think. This is the power that exceeds all material forces. This is the power of your thoughts, your SOCH!
Established in 2014. The social service wing of MCT's Rajiv Gandhi Institute of Technology, Mumbai.
Prime objective: Building a better society.
Our name stands for Social Organization for Conserving Humanity.

Name: AMAH
Description:
AMAH est une fondation d’aide et assistance aux personnes atteintes des maladies virales comme L’hépatite A,B et C 
Elle réalise ses actions au travers les sensibilisations, les campagnes de dépistages, la distribution des vaccins, les campagnes

Nos objectifs 
  Participer à des séances de sensibilisation contre l’hépatique.
  Réduire l’incidence des hépatites A, B et C
  Diminuer la morbidité et la mortalité attribuables aux hépatites chroniques B et C
  Maintenir en dessous de 2% la prévalence de l’infection par le virus de l’Hépatite C 
 dans la population générale

Nos domaines d’intervention
  Dépistage
  Prévention de la transmission par la vaccination
  Sensibilisation
  Information/Education/Communication
  Campagne recouvrement des fonds à l’endroit des personnes n’ayant pas 
  suffisamment d’argent pour suivre équitablement leur traitement

La Direction Générale est l’instance de décision et de contrôle de la FONDATION. Elle est composée des personnes ressources de la FONDATION qui se réunissent en session ordinaire deux fois par an. Elle peut cependant siéger en session extraordinaire chaque fois que la nécessité s’impose.

La Direction Générale  prend ses décisions à la majorité des membres présents.
 
Elle, examine et approuve les rapports d’activités et les rapports financiers ; Adopte les programmes et les budgets ; Délibère sur toutes les questions soumises à son ordre du jour.

Name: UN Association of Lund / Lunds FN-förening
Description:
Lunds FN-förening är en del av den svenska FN-rörelsen och är knuten till Svenska FN-förbundet. Föreningen är partipolitiskt och religiöst obunden. All verksamhet bygger på lokala och nära krafter – vi tänker globalt och agerar lokalt.

Föreningen bildades 1977 och hade då 15 medlemmar. Under årens lopp har medlemsantalet stadigt ökat och idag har vi ett drygt hundratal medlemmar.

Lunds FN-förening har ett nära samarbete med Svenska FN-förbundet, men också med lokala FN-föreningar samt andra partipolitiskt och religiöst obundna föreningar främst i lundaområdet. Vi arrangerar ofta, eller står som medarrangörer till, aktiviteter och föreläsningar som berör olika FN-relaterade frågor.

_________________________________________________________________________

The UN Association of Lund is part of the Swedish UN movement and is attached to the UN Association of Sweden. The association is party politically and religiously unrelated. All activities are based on local and close forces - we think globally and act locally.

The association was formed in 1977 and then had 15 members. Over the years, membership has steadily increased and today we have more than a hundred members.

The Lund UN Association works closely with the Swedish UN Association, but also with local UN associations and other party politically and religiously united associations, mainly in the field of play. We often arrange or stand as co-organizers for activities and lectures that concern various UN related issues.

les donateurs pourront participer par mobile money et par Carte bancaire.

Name: Association Bambi Aide globale à la famille
Description:

L’association souhaite financer des projets spécifiques pour des enfants de 0 à 6 ans à en Colombie ou ailleurs, ainsi que des programmes de formations professionnelles destinés aux parents.

Name: The Catholic Community Foundation
Description:
The Catholic Community Foundation develops, acquires and distributes funds that provide support to parish, educational, religious, charitable and other organizations.

Follow us on Facebook at www.facebook.com/TheCatholicCommunityFoundation

#catholicphilanthropy #donoradvisedfunds #endowments

Name: LAZNAS Sahabat Yatim
Description:
How does it feel to live a comfortable life while some people out there are struggling to live from hand to mouth? There must be a lot of pain and pity deep inside the heart. That was how our founders perceived in Sahabat Yatim Indonesia (SAYATI).
We strongly encouraged the establishment of this social foundation in September 2009. We hoped that orphans and needy children in Indonesia are able to find a shelter to live safely and peacefully, away from the dread poverty.
All praises to God, we successfully made it. We currently have 26 shelters throughout Indonesia, each handles thousands of non-reside children and hundreds of foster children. Of course, your cooperation plays a big role.
However, we are aware that there are still many orphans and needy children out there who need your help. For this reason, SAYATI encourages you to become their partner in this world and hereafter. Together we spread our wings of virtue and hopefully it becomes our continuous charity that brings us to His jannah.

Name: Stichting Utrechts eigenDom
Description: Stichting Utrechts eigenDom wil de beleving van de toren versterken. Wij houden ons bezig met fondsenwerving, waarmee we een steentje willen bijdragen aan de restauratie van de Domtoren. Op 14 december organiseren we ons 'Domdiner': een fundraising Diner waarmee we geld inzamelen voor onze geliefde Dom.

Name: High-Tech High Heels Southeast Texas
Description:
Our mission is to close the gender gap in Science, Technology, Engineering and Math (STEM). We provide targeted support to girls and young women in the Southeast Texas region to support them and inspire them to pursue careers in STEM that align with their passions.

For inquiries, please reach out to setx_info@hightechhighheels.org.

Name: New Canaan Community Foundation
Description: New Canaan Community Foundation is your local charitable partner and offers a range of philanthropic and educational services to all residents.  We collaborate with donors to help each individual achieve their personal giving goals.  Volunteer leaders guide our work and help us maximize our impact.  Building on New Canaan's generosity of spirit, we make careful investments in area nonprofit organizations and award scholarships to college students.

Name: Association of Social Workers in Malawi - ASWiM
Description: The Association Social Workers in Malawi is a duly registered non-profit organization aimed at promoting the advancement and protection of social work profession by focusing on both social workers, social service providers and users.

Name: RETSER (Réseau Etudiant Toulousain en Soutien aux Exilé.e.s et Réfugié.e.s)
Description:
L’association Réseau des Étudiants Toulousains en Soutien aux Réfugiés et aux Exilés (RETSER) a pour but l’aide et le soutien aux réfugiés et demandeurs d’asile en région toulousaine et au-delà, et leur mise en relation avec les étudiants toulousains afin de développer des échanges riches permettant de construire le vivre ensemble au sein de la ville.  

Notre objectif est de permettre la rencontre entre étudiants concernés par ces problématiques pour mettre en place ensemble diverses actions régulières et projets communs. 

NOS ACTIONS 

NOS ACTIVITÉS
Les actions du RETSER sont régulières et forment le pôle d'activité principal de l'association. 
- Cours de français
- Collecte de dons (tickets de métro, vêtements, nourriture, produits hygiéniques)
- Activités culturelles (sortie cinéma, musée, visite de la ville, Cinéma avec Kinomady, ateliers théâtre)
- Santé
- Activités sportives (organisation de tournoi de foot, match de cricket, ateliers swing)
- Information et sensibilisation (café-débat, rencontre-débat, cinéma-débat)
- Soutien administratif & juridique

NOS ÉVÉNEMENTS 
Les projets du RETSER sont d'une durée limitée et changent au cours de l'année. 
*Projets passés : 
- Déplacement à Calais
- Saint Pierre Solidaire
- Exposition et projection-débat avec Médecins sans frontières
- Let's Colour walls of connection
- Participation à la Journée Mondiale des Réfugié.e.s (éditions 2017 et 2018)
- Soirée/concert de soutien au RETSER à la Maison Blanche (Toulouse), avec la participation du groupe The Perrines
- Sportis
- Vélorution

- Saveurs d'exil
- Jardins partagés
- Café des langues


AVEC QUI? 
- CAES de la Vache
- CAES des Pradettes
- PRAHDA de Roques / Mirail
- Lieu de vie de Purpan
- Appartement diffus de France Horizon

Soutenir le RETSER via Lilo :  https://www.lilo.org/fr/soutien-aux-refugies/
Faire un don : https://www.helloasso.com/associations/retser31/formulaires/2

Name: Singapore Fashion Runway
Description:
Singapore Fashion Runway (SFR) is a social enterprise that nurtures people with special needs/ disadvantaged groups through its Fashion for a Social Cause movement. Through our enterprise, the disadvantaged communities learn about Creative arts (design, art, crafts-making and photography), Performing arts (modelling, dance, singing and public speaking) and/ or Entrepreneurship (retail management, events management and online business), so that their talents are presented in our Singapore Fashion Runway shows/ platforms.

SFR operates an inclusive space at MARINA SOUTH PIER MRT STATION, where the children/ youths with special needs/ families visit the space weekly to receive training and development. We also have a retail front at People's Park (32 New Market Road #02-1128 S'pore 050032) to provide an authentic learning environment to the People with Disabilities (PwDs) and caregivers,  so that they can learn to serve real customers and showcase their talents/ passion.

We are seeking outreach partners/ organisations, so that the funds we raise continue to sustain our social enterprise and provide support to the people with special needs/ disadvantaged groups.

Name: Les Dames d'Escoffier - Atlanta
Description:
Les Dames d’Escoffier International (LDEI) is a worldwide society of women dedicated to creating a culture in the community that fosters excellence and promotes the achievement of women in culinary professions through educational and charitable activities. 

LDEI was founded in New York in 1976 by Carol Brock, a food reporter for the New York Daily News.  At the time there were few opportunities for women in the culinary arts and there were no prominent women chefs or sommeliers. Brock saw a need to create an organization of professional leading women in the industry who would serve as role models and mentors to others. From its inception, the organization has been a membership-by-invitation, philanthropic organization that has provided education, networking opportunities and scholarship support.

Overall, the organization has grown to more than 1,400 members in 28 chapters, with the newest chapter being chartered in London, England in 2010.

We remain dedicated in our support of women through scholarships, educational programs, externships and networking.

Name: Peace and Development Foundation
Description: Our Mission is to promote peace and development, support humanitarian efforts in the world, focusing on Ukraine now. Our partners on the ground in Ukraine and Europe include local governmental, non-governmental (for-profit, non-for-profit) as well as religious organizations.

Name: The Generations Foundation
Description: Founded by Liverpool Partners and Inspire Impact, and supported by our employees and portfolio companies, TGF partners with charitable organisations across the world, working with local communities to give disadvantaged children ongoing access to quality education.

Name: Destiny | With | Purpose
Description:
Destiny With Purpose - is about helping you define your purpose, whether it is in business, career or your personal life. 

We are in the business of adding value to other women and seeing them become better versions of themselves. We believe that women need to take up challenging roles in business to become economically independent and self sufficient. We do this through Motivational workshops, Life coaching, Business workshops, Entrepreneurial programmes and Conferences.

We also bring you a series dedicated to showcasing everyday Africans people don't normally "see" or hear of, through our weekly podcast called “The Purpose Cafe”, where we talk to a varied cross-section of amazing Africans, (male and female), in the hopes of inspiring our tribe of women.

Name: Summer in the City
Description: Twelve years ago, Summer in the City was born from an idea: three college students from Metro Detroit wanted to create an engaging volunteer experience in the city that was both accessible and rewarding. An experience described with three terms that have become, in a way, the organization’s creed: Fun, Flexible, and Fulfilling. Over time, what began as one carful of volunteers has grown often over 200 a day. By bringing a diverse group of young people together to invest their energy in Detroit, Summer in the City programs address the immediate needs of city neighborhoods while fostering a regional mindset. Through the creation and maintaining of asset-based urban revitalization projects throughout the city of Detroit, SITC aims to incorporate area youth in the city and its future.

Name: George4 Foundation
Description:
The George4 Foundation is primarily focused on the education and welfare of our youth, pediatric medical research and uplifting underprivileged and minority families.

Funds raised by the foundation will support important and critical missions for youth in communities around the United States and across the world.

George Steinbrenner IV, Founder
Julia Steinbrenner, Co-Founder & Executive Director

E-mail us at: info@George4.org

Name: SJ5K Foundation
Description:
The SJ5K Foundation is a benefit 5K benefit that started in 2011 after P-CEP student Jesse Lindlbauer experienced a life-altering brain injury.

2023 will mark the 13th year of the Super Jess 5K. This event is spearheaded by local high school students in the Plymouth-Canton community. 

Since the first race, the SJ5K has raised over $1 Million to support 60 Plymouth-Canton families facing a medical crisis. Each year, the cause receives immense support from the community, with 2,500+ runners and walkers, and 50+ sponsors.

Join the movement in 2023 and help us continue to make positive change in our world. Thank you for your support!

Name: Just Beginnings Collaborative
Description:
Just Beginnings Collaborative (JBC) is a national survivor-centered intermediary working to end child sexual abuse and transform how communities respond to harm. We believe what is not transformed gets transferred — and that includes the trauma and violence carried across generations.

Founded in 2015, JBC bridges bold grassroots ideas and vital funding to build the conditions where true safety can take hold. We resource and connect the community leaders who are creating care-centered, non-punitive approaches to healing, accountability, and prevention.

Through our three-part framework — Source, Strategy, and Story — we:
 Source: Mobilize and redistribute resources to fund community-led safety strategies while cultivating funders ready to move beyond punitive models.
 Strategy: Invest in grassroots organizers and survivor-leaders developing transformative, community-based approaches to accountability and healing.
 Story: Shift narratives about safety and harm through storytelling, advocacy, and thought leadership that center survivor wisdom and collective care.

Our network of grantee partners anchors JBC’s vision in practice. Together, we are building a new safety ecosystem — one where survivors are believed and supported, those who have caused harm are guided toward transformation, and communities have the tools to prevent harm before it happens.

Leadership
Nicole Pittman, Executive Director, is a survivor and national leader whose landmark Human Rights Watch report, Raised on the Registry, exposed the harms of placing children on sex-offender registries and helped catalyze reforms nationwide. Nicole’s work advances a vision of prevention that includes — not excludes — those who have caused harm, centering collective care, nonviolence, and transformation.

 Our belief is simple yet radical: Safety is built by us. Not through control or punishment, but through care, trust, and shared responsibility.

Name: Cardborigami
Description: The mission of Cardborigami is to provide instant space to protect people from the elements through innovation and design. Our centerpiece product provides temporary, portable shelter to homeless and/or disaster-stricken populations, allowing us to support the work of established partner organizations that offer transitional pathways into permanent housing and other human services. We also create temporary, local US jobs in the production of these shelters in an effort to help support the livelihoods of others in transition or rehabilitation.

Name: Tennessee Valley Fair
Description: The Tennessee Valley Fair has been dedicated to showcasing East Tennessee heritage, agriculture and the arts. The Tennessee Valley Fair is a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization that exists to celebrate and promote agricultural heritage, family values, and individual life skills in our community with an emphasis on youth development activities through exhibits, tours and the funding of scholarships. The Tennessee Valley Fair showcases traditions of the past, emphasizes ingenuity in Tennessee through the products of the present and supports the vision of the future in the areas of agriculture, trade, and industry.

Name: EAH - Efrei Aides Humanitaires
Description:
Crée en 1991, l’association EFREI Aides Humanitaires rassemble actuellement plus de 60 étudiants ingénieurs.

L’objectif de notre association est de réaliser des actions sociales et humanitaires afin de réduire la fracture du numérique mais également de favoriser l’égalité des chances face à l’accès à l’éducation.

Nos actions prennent donc place aussi bien en France auprès de collèges, que dans des établissements ou orphelinats situés dans des pays en voie de développement. 

Pôles principaux : 
• Tutorat : aide aux devoirs dans trois collèges à Villejuif (jeudi après-midi de 15h15 – 17h30)
• Convois : partir à l’étranger pour équiper les écoles d’ordinateurs et distribuer des fournitures scolaires
• Evénements : participation aux journées humanitaires -> Téléthon, Sidaction, Journée internationale de la Femme, mise en place d'un projet caritatif avec des récoltes de dons pour aider les personnes dans le besoin et mise en place de maraudes dans Paris
• Partenariat : Prenez dès maintenant contact avec notre pôle partenariats pour faire don de matériel ou faire un don financier

Name: Mission d'Action
Description: Association de promotion et de défense des droit Humains à travers des actions dans les domaines de l’éducation, la Protection de l’enfance, les violences basées sur le Genre et la cohésion sociale

Name: SoRewarding
Description:
THE SOCIAL NETWORK AND CAUSE MARKETING PLATFORM FOR PHILANTHROPY
SoRewarding’s Platform for Philanthropy engages the entire community in supporting causes through everyday 
activities.  Non-profits gain increased donations and new connections, businesses receive positive social recognition, and individuals connect with other cause-minded members while supporting their favorite causes.

Name: IDEES Madagascar
Description: L’association IDÉES a été créée en 1994 par des étudiants de l’école d’ingénieurs ESME Sudria. Depuis 25 ans, elle réalise de nombreux projets de développement à Madagascar en collaboration directe avec ses habitants, dans le but d’améliorer leurs conditions de vie.

Name: The Kindness Foundation
Description:
The Kindness Foundation is an NGO & kindness movement, created with a vision to expand the perception of kindness, and create compassionate communities worldwide. 

We believe that kindness is the innate nature of every human being, and even the simple act of talking about it can inspire people to become the best version of themselves. 

WHAT WE DO
Kindness Week- Annual week-long sharing of experiences with those who cannot afford them.

Kind Campus- Empathy education Program for schools and colleges 

Kindness All-In- Fundraising workshops that add value and meaning to  life by experts from various fields.

Kindness Brigade- Identifying and celebrating young impact-makers and kindness ambassadors

Kindness Vouchers-Gift vouchers for the under-privileged

Kindness Speaks-  A Platform for youth to voice their perception and ideas for a kinder world.

Kindness Gallery- Showcasing meaningful and kindness-inspiring artworks from around the world

Kindness Canvas- KIndness as seen through the eyes of artists young and old.

Kindness Direct Impact - Fundraising and direct outreach in times of need like during the Covid Lockdown; Regular fundraising and donation drives for clothes, food, toys, books etc.

You do not need a magic wand to change the world. It just requires one person to start with one small step and watch that ripple out in all directions. Indeed, what we do now .... echoes forever in humanity.

Name: Chimie Solidarité
Description:
Chimie So est l'association solidaire de l'ENSCM. Nous organisons différents évènements tout au long de l'année pour récolter des fonds que nous reversons à des associations. Nous organisons des goûters, un loto, une chasse aux trésors dans les rues de Montpellier, des vide-dressings et bien d'autres. N'hésitez pas à faire un tour sur notre page ! 

Chimie So is a NGO handled by the students of the National Graduate School of Chemistry of Montpellier. We organize different events during the year in order to raise funds for several associations. For example, we organize a lotto, a treasure hunt, breakfasts, clothes exchange etc. Please, feel free to look at our LinkedIn page!

Name: Happy Child International
Description:
Happy Child International is an organisation that champions the rights of the most at risk children and teenagers in the deprived areas of Belo Horizonte and Recife in Brazil and Lubango, Angola.

Since its inception in 1993, 11,500 children’s lives have been transformed because of Happy Child and their mission to support and protect. The charity strives to counter the devastating effects of child poverty and abuse by rescuing children from the streets, where possible reintegrating them with their families and communities, working in the favelas to prevent child exploitation and rehabilitating abused children through work in its project homes.

3 main areas of the work of Happy Child:-
- Advocacy & Global Campaigning (UK)
- Child & Family Care (Brazil & Angola, Africa)
- Research & Advisory

Happy Child's Advocacy and Global campaigning services, in parliament in London on behalf of neglected and exploited children and adolescents. In February 2014 it launched the global IT'S A PENALTY Campaign against child exploitation during the World Cup 2014 with Jubilee Campaign, the A21 Campaign and supported by international footballers David Luiz, Frank Lampard, Gary Lineker and Alan Shearer, as well as the National Crime Agency, Europol, the European Government, the Foreign & Commonwealth Office, the Football Association, British Airways, the British Prime Minister and the Brazilian Government.
For more information see our website
www.happychild.org
and
our Campaign

www.itsapenalty.com - Taking a stand against the exploitation of children.

Name: Cofradía del Dulce Nombre de Jesús Nazareno
Description:
Desde finales del siglo pasado, nuestra Cofradía colabora habitualmente con distintas asociaciones sin ánimo de lucro y un marcado carácter social: Fundación Aladina, Cáritas, Grupo Amigos de Thillene, Amidown, Teléfono de la esperanza, Fundaspe, Autismo León, Asociación Leonesa de Caridad, Cruz Roja Española… a través de la recaudación en distintos actos benéficos, en los que colaboran, fundamentalmente, las secciones musicales de la Cofradía.

Queriendo dar un paso más, desde el año 2010 ha puesto en marcha una bolsa de acción social y laboral, buscando ayudar a los hermanos que peor lo están pasando, en estos difíciles tiempos.

Name: Chicago Teachers Union Foundation
Description: The Chicago Teachers Union Foundation is dedicated to developing networks that advance education for students, advocate for teachers and resource underserved communities through grantmaking.

Name: Fondation Mapon
Description:
La fondation Mapon soutient des actions ciblées et concrètes qui s’inscrivent dans la durée et bénéficient en priorité à la jeunesse et aux familles. Son action se concentre sur trois enjeux principaux : l’éducation, la santé et l’agriculture.
La fondation Mapon a développé plusieurs projets d’ordres sociaux pour aider au développement de la communauté rurale de Lumbu-Lumbu, et au bien-être de sa population. Pour cela, elle a créé un centre scolaire primaire et secondaire, un centre hospitalier performant, une ferme pilote eco-responsable et en cours, une université axée sur les hautes technologies.

Véritable bouffée d’oxygène dans le quartier de Lumbu-Lumbu, la présence de la Fondation Mapon redonne de l’espoir à la population, et du respect pour sa dignité humaine

Name: Nonlinear
Description: We incubate longtermist nonprofits by connecting founders with ideas, funding, and mentorship.

Name: SPEAK! FOR THE UNSPOKEN
Description:
SPEAK! for the Unspoken is a registered 501(c)(3) pet rescue located in the Columbus, Ohio area. We are a foster based rescue, devoted to both animal rescue and education. We focus our rescue efforts on special needs animals, most often those with hearing and vision impairments. Many of the animals we save are double merle Australian Shepherds born with vision and/or hearing deficits due to inappropriate merle to merle breeding practices. We believe special needs dogs are capable of living happy and healthy lives, and we work to educate the public about these amazing animals. 

Our Mission statement is “Special needs, good deeds. We see possibilities, not disabilities.” Our mission reflects the fact that we also will assist any animal in need whether it be an exceptionally difficult medical case---like cataract surgery to return vision to an otherwise normal Aussie, or a litter of puppies at risk of parvo in a shelter environment. We also work with a puppy mill rescue group to save those animals discarded from the mills as "no longer useful".

These special dogs are truly amazing creatures that overcome their disabilities with courage and grace. Until the careless breeding stops, we will continue to find these special dogs the homes they deserve.

Name: Texas de Peru
Description: Texas de Peru is a medical mission organization which enables individuals and organizations to deliver healthcare, grow leaders and make a difference in the delivery of healthcare to the undeserved, globally and locally.    Texas de Peru facilitates the sourcing and delivery of much needed medical supplies equipment and professional expertise.  We conduct multiple medical missions throughout the year and in our short 3 year history, have treated nearly 700 patients and performed nearly 100 surgeries.   Here in Texas, we are currently connected to 208 North Texas healthcare organizations representing over 40,000 medical professionals.  With the help of our sponsors and donors, we are working to expand our reach throughout the Texas medical communities and across the high-Andes of Peru.

Name: JustMap Foundation
Description:
JustMap Foundation (JMF) uses rigorous analytics to give charitable donors new tools for identifying strategically important — yet underfunded and under-recognized — nonprofits and innovative initiatives working to achieve social justice goals.  On crucial issues ranging from keeping kids in school and out of the prison pipeline to mitigating climate change, we conduct research, share what we learn, and support advocacy to change long-term systemic inequities. We incorporate and value the input of community members affected by these issues.

A 501(c)(3) private foundation, JMF maps the complex interrelated elements of social justice challenges, enabling donors to visualize how individual nonprofit organizations fit into the broader nonprofit landscape.  We complement the work of other organizations that deeply profile individual nonprofits. These maps can reveal that related nonprofits are counterproductively competing with one another, collaborating on a common goal without realizing it, or collectively struggling to achieve their goal due to a common obstacle.  We believe these innovative insights empower donors to focus and maximize the social justice impacts of their charitable contributions.

Name: StoryCause
Description:
We empower our nonprofit partners with the resources to engage donors at-scale. By capturing your donors’ stories, we will connect you to their intent, passion, and potential and can create hyper-personalized appeals and stewardship that align with each donors' passion.  As a full-service fundraising services provider we can create annual fund programs that utilize mail, digital and telephone to meet donors where they are.  We have developed programs that leverage our extensive expertise and focus on some key development areas:

Grateful Patient Acquisition
Donor Story Capture and Survey
Sustaining Donor Programs
Donor Stewardship Programs
Mid-Level Donor Portfolio Management
Planned Giving Lead Generation

Name: Emmaus Inc
Description:
We affirm the value of human dignity and self-respect for every person. We are called by our collective spiritual and social responsibility to serve all people in need—the homeless, disadvantaged, disabled, unemployed, and oppressed. Our goal is to enhance societal and personal advancement through provision of housing and support services that empower individuals and families to reach their fullest potential.

Emmaus helps people rebuild their lives by creating and renewing a sense of community.

On any given night 300 different people of all ages call Emmaus “home.” More than 80,000 meals will be served to shelter residents, and many housing residents and program participants receive emergency food from Emmaus’ food pantry at 101 Winter Street.

Emmaus’ housing and shelter programs include emergency shelter, transitional housing, permanent affordable housing, housing search and stabilization, job training and adult education, financial and life skills training, substance abuse education, support groups, and case management. Emmaus also connects people with other community-based organizations, treatment programs, medical and mental health services, and mainstream resources to help them rebuild their lives and reach their fullest potential.

Name: horsesmouth.co.uk
Description:
horsesmouth is where social networking meets social enterprise; where personal contributions create public value; where human capital is unlocked to create social capital. It's social networking with a purpose. It's social networking for grown-ups. We call it the wisdomocracy.

horsesmouth.co.uk aims to provide an informal mentoring service for anyone who needs advice for running a business, accessing education or to get through life's general challenges.

We believe truly that no matter how old you are, or what experiences you've had, everyone needs mentoring every now and then. But it's often difficult to access the right advice on difficult or obscure issues from among your circle of friends. And there are times when you want to access advice without going through all the hassle and expense of formal advisory services; you just want an answer and you want it from someone who knows.

This is where horsesmouth can help. Join horsesmouth as a user and search for the issue for which you are seeking advice. Among our many thousands of mentors, you're bound to find someone who can help.

Best of all, becoming a member and accessing the mentoring community at horsemouth is absolutely FREE. And we will NEVER charge you for use of the service!

Name: The Global Volunteer Partnership
Description:
The mission of The Global Volunteer Partnership is to meet the resource needs of America’s skilled volunteers, nonprofits and companies seeking to have measurable impact on global challenges such as poverty, illiteracy, and disease.  

Our vision is to become the leading catalyst for efforts by these skilled volunteers, nonprofits and companies to improve lives around the world.

The Global Volunteer Partnership is a 501 (c)(3) organization that is developing resources to enable skilled American volunteers and US nonprofits and companies to contribute to global development through projects targeting economic growth, education, health, the environment, information technology and agriculture. These resources will:

- Help skilled Americans find and afford relevant volunteer service opportunities abroad 
- Help nonprofits to recruit and deploy more skilled volunteers 
- Inform the public on the vital roles of skilled volunteers in addressing global problems 
- Assist companies in efforts to involve or strengthen the participation of their employees in volunteer service abroad 
- Encourage Americans and US organizations unable to participate directly in volunteer service abroad to support the mission of The Global Volunteer Partnership with financial and in-kind contributions

Contact Information:

+1 703 519 0804

The Global Volunteer Partnership
2308 Mount Vernon Avenue, Suite 704
Alexandria, VA 22301

info@globalvolunteerpartnership.org

www.globalvolunteerpartnership.org

Name: The Josh Paul Foundation
Description: The Josh Paul Foundation (TJPF) is a 501(c)3 non-profit organization founded to honor the legacy of the late educator, Joshua Paul Schwarz. TJPF raises money annually, through special events that coincide with Josh Paul's passions: music, health and fitness, and education. These events benefit a scholarship fund dedicated to students that have faced adversity, and/or financial barriers, but wish to continue their education beyond high school graduation.

Name: American Red Cross Desert to the Sea Region
Description:
The American Red Cross Desert to the Sea Region serves Orange, Riverside and San Bernardino Counties. As one of Southern California’s leading non-profit agencies, the Desert to the Sea Region serves a total of 128 cities and unincorporated areas and over 7,900,000 people.

The American Red Cross exists to provide compassionate care to those in need. Our generous donors, volunteers and employees are part of a nationwide network committed to preventing and relieving suffering here at home, across the country, and around the world.

The Red Cross empowers ordinary people to perform extraordinary acts in emergency situations. We train. We mobilize. We connect donors and volunteers to those in urgent need of a helping hand. Whether it is a wildfire or a heart attack, a call for blood, a call for help after a devastating home fire, or a call from a service member or military family in need, the Red Cross is there.

All Red Cross assistance is free, made possible by voluntary donations of time and money from the American people.

We are dedicated to providing help and hope to those in need. We provide food and shelter in emergencies, assist members of our armed forces, veterans and their families, teach lifesaving skills to ensure our communities are prepared to respond to emergencies, and so much more.

Name: YVR for Kids
Description:
The annual YVR for Kids Gala Dinner and Golf Tournament now in it’s 29th year, has become the preeminent and much loved airport celebration of the year.

Through our partnerships with the Vancouver International Airport, Canuck Place Children’s Hospice and many other key sponsors, YVR for Kids brings together over 350 guests for a Gala Dinner and Auction followed by an afternoon of golf.

Name: The Higher Foundation
Description: The Higher Foundation promotes post-secondary education by providing financial assistance to Georgia college students. We help Collegiate Georgians secure their basic needs with our need-based grants and merit-based scholarships. Since May 2018, we have served over 1,000 Georgia college students and close to $40,000 in grants & scholarships. With our services, we aim to help Georgia college students overcome basic needs insecurity, so they can go higher in their education and career.

Name: GenZ Girls
Description: Uplifting young females from marginalized communities with various undiscovered stories

Name: The Shawn Thornton Foundation
Description: The Shawn Thornton Foundation is dedicated to helping find cures for diseases close to Shawn's heart, specifically, Parkinson's and cancer. The Foundation will work towards this mission by raising awareness, funding innovative research and helping to relieve the burden and improve the quality of life for patients and their families.

Name: Rotary Club of Grand Cayman
Description:
Women and men from all professions coming together for a common purpose to give back to their communities locally and worldwide. Using their professional affiliations, networking and passion for specific causes, WE, align as a powerful collective to make positive change. 

Meeting weekly for lunch socials to hear about community project updates and be informed with key speaker presentations on topics affecting our world. We welcome guests and prospective members who have a passion for community service, philanthropy and building new friendships.

Name: Follow Your Dreams Charity
Description:
Follow Your Dreams is a national charity operating in England and Wales that inspires children and young people with learning disabilities to follow their dreams. 

Our vision is for children and young people with learning disabilities to recognise their potential, be ambitious and be provided with the opportunities to follow their dreams. The charity focuses on children’s abilities, growing and developing skills through the delivery of creative and interactive events, workshops, and help to fulfil "dreams".

Name: Pedal the Pacific
Description: Pedal the Pacific's mission is to empower young women in the fight against sex trafficking through a leadership development program and 1,700 mile bike ride down the Pacific Coast.

Name: THE SAHEL INITIATIVE
Description:
Water scarcity is the root cause of many conflicts in the Sahel and puts women and the girl child at a particular disadvantage. A lot of their time is taken up fetching water for their families consequently missing out on education and many life opportunities. 

We are focussed on providing clean drinking water mainly by drilling and maintaining boreholes, providing storage facilities and solar powered pumps. 

We believe strongly that deforestation plays a significant role in water shortage and we integrate tree planting to our water provision projects. 

The next level of our programme is our Community Upliftment Programme (CUP) where we partner with other charities particularly those involved with education, healthcare and orphan support to wrap around our host communities and help lift them out of poverty.

Name: Solidar-IT
Description:
Initiative collective, solidaire et bénévole ayant pour challenge de contribuer à ce que toutes les personnes dans le besoin puissent bénéficier d'une aie ! 
Vous pouvez nous aider et supporter ces actions à travers des dons, matériels ou financiers !
Merci pour votre générosité

Name: The Giving Project
Description: Our mission is to help more people feel good about giving. We do this by supporting and scaling collective impact through a statewide network of giving circles. Instagram: @givingprojectvt

Name: Spiral Giving
Description:
Spiral Giving is an extra-curricular, non-credit program, designed for teens, that discusses the needs of our neighbors. The students fundraise, evaluate grant applications, and award needed funds to local charities.

Our mission is to introduce the next generation to philanthropy and the importance of giving back.  We accomplish this by having teens read, evaluate, and vote on grant requests from local nonprofits.

Name: The Community Table
Description: The Community Table’s Mission is to serve balanced, nutritious meals in a safe, welcoming environment and to connect those in need with existing resources. We do this by engaging diverse volunteers in service and by fostering partnerships with local organizations. TCT also enlightens the public to issues of hunger in our community; and supports efforts of community agencies to increase the self-reliance of individuals and families.

Name: Stratford Town Trust
Description:
We award almost £2million each year…
…to support charities, organisations, projects, events, groups, clubs, schools and individuals across Stratford-upon-Avon.
Our mission is to enhance the quality of life of the people living in Stratford-upon-Avon.

Name: Schuler Education Foundation
Integrated Capital Strategies, LLC
Description: Integrated Capital Strategies, LLC assists founders and family offices to set up and manage hybrid impact organizations that use a range of tools to accomplish their social mission, from grants and investments to advocacy and operating entities.

Name: Bridge Builders Community Foundations
Description:
History: The Venango Area Community Foundation was founded on April 30, 1975. The first discretionary fund was created within the Venango Area Community Foundation portfolio on  June 1976 by Galena Rew Harrington and Mary E. Rew, both of Franklin, who were two local community volunteers who wanted to make a difference.  They started that initial endowment with a gift and trust transfer totaling almost $80,000.

Today that endowment still exists as the oldest continued endowment in Bridge Builders Community Foundations’ portfolio, and distributes approximately $5,000 annually to local causes every year.

The Venango Area Community Foundation changed its name to Bridge Builders Community Foundations in 2006 and created affiliates to serve the Venango, Clarion, Forest and most recently Punxsutawney area to leverage assets, fund management, and giving.

It took a long time to evolve as a community foundation, and to become the umbrella foundation serving our four area affiliates.

Through economic hardships, staff changes, and social impact over the years we have been able to uphold our values and stay true to what we were founded on 40 years ago.

The hope of a better, stronger community.

Our Vision:  By unifying the community foundations serving Clarion, Forest, Venango, Jefferson and other adjacent counties the Bridge Builders Foundations will provide an effective way of serving donors and communities by creating and growing endowments that serve the needs in our area.

Name: The PeaceBoat Project
Description:
The PeaceBoat Project aims at:

• Gathering students from countries involved in a conflict, where no solution is foreseen, on the same boat for the EDHEC Sailing Cup - the leading European student sport event, of which the historical heart is a 230-boats regatta.

• Building a crew with students from both nations and show the world that despite their differences, they can unite: the project enables to put sport and more specifically the values of sailing, such as respect and excelling oneself, at the benefit of fraternity, solidarity and peace.

Name: Action Against AMD (Age-Related Macular Degeneration)
Description:
Action Against Age-related Macular Degeneration (Action Against AMD) is a charity driving a fundamental change in the way we tackle Age-related Macular Degeneration - the leading cause of sight loss in the UK and the developed world. 

We were founded as a collaboration of four leading sight loss charities o find and enable safe, convenient, equitable, affordable, and accessible solutions to stop AMD in its early stages, before it causes sight loss.

Name: StoryCon
Description:
StoryCon is the ultimate experience for writers, educators, screenwriters, gamers, and creatives of all ages, combining the fun of a national book festival with the education and guidance of a serious writing conference. 
Keynotes and workshops are presented by NY Times Bestsellers and the most popular authors and creatives in the country.

Name: WE CARE FOR EUROPE
Description:
Wir wollen nicht dabei zusehen, wie diese Krise auch noch zur Re-Nationalisierung der Europäischen Staaten führt. Im Gegenteil: Gerade in der Krise brauchen wir eine starkes Europa!

IN VIELFALT VEREINT UND SOLIDARISCH VERBUNDEN – IN GUTEN WIE ERST RECHT IN SCHLECHTEN ZEITEN.
 
Noch im vergangenen Jahr war es eine Energie der Hoffnung, als sich Millionen von Europäern*innen versammelten, um für ein solidarisches Europa der Einheit und Vielfalt zu streiten. Seit Beginn der Krise wird nun unentwegt an den Gemeinschaftssinn und die Solidarität der Bürger appelliert. Aber gilt das auch für Europa?
 
In der Krise wird sich entscheiden, ob unsere Europäische Union eine reine Wirtschaftsunion oder eine tatsächliche Solidarunion ist. 
 
Wir entscheiden uns ganz klar für europäische Solidarität. Deshalb ist es uns so wichtig, jetzt ein deutliches Zeichen zu setzen. Mit den Masken geben wir Europa ganz viele Gesichter – und mit den Spenden zeigen wir nicht nur symbolisch, dass die Solidarität zählen muss, wenn unsere Partner in großen Schwierigkeiten sind.

Zusammen geben wir der Hoffnung wieder Kraft: Für ein starkes und vereintes Europa!

Name: West Marin Fund
Description:
West Marin Fund is a community foundation that inspires giving and mobilizes resources to enhance the long-term wellbeing and quality of life for all in coastal West Marin. West Marin Fund provides grants and training for local nonprofits, and convenes key stakeholders to help identify and fund equitable solutions to challenges facing people who live, work and visit in West Marin.

The Fund serves the West Marin coastal area, from Muir Beach in the south to Dillon Beach in the north, and the contiguous ranch lands. The area includes the 11 unincorporated villages of Muir Beach, Stinson Beach, Bolinas, Olema, Inverness, Inverness Park, Point Reyes Station, Marshall, Dillon Beach, Nicasio, and Tomales.

Name: The Racial Equity Asset Lab
Description:
The REAL works at the intersection of racial equity and impact investing to shift capital to improve the material conditions of Black people and communities for the betterment of all. 

The REAL is a network of people who are dedicated to advancing policy & practice, changing the narrative and implementing strategies for equitable outcomes in Black and other communities of color.

The REAL leverages data (disaggregated) and research to understand how different groups of people fare within current structures and institutions, systems and policies and to inform systemic changes that make for an inclusive economy to improve material conditions for Black and other communities of color.

The REAL shares knowledge and insight to help decision makers, investors, advocates, program managers get on the same page for advancing equity.

The REAL is a lab where ideas become strategies and change happens.

Description:
FUNDAMIND es una organización de desarrollo sin fines de lucro, declarada de interés social y educativo, que desde 1990 es referente en la Argentina y resto de Latinoamérica y el Caribe, en la atención integral de chicos vulnerables ante la pobreza y el VIH.
Sus programas abarcan el ámbito local, nacional e internacional y cuentan con el apoyo de diferentes agencias internacionales, organismos gubernamentales y un grupo importante de empresas con responsabilidad social, artistas y deportistas de renombre, profesionales con amplio liderazgo, más la solidaridad de la comunidad en general.
En la sede central de la fundación, ubicada en el barrio de Balvanera, funciona un Centro Materno Infantil en el que se atienden diariamente en forma integral, 60 niños de 2 y 3 años, además de más de 300 familias vulnerables socialmente. Allí también funcionan talleres para niños de 6 a 13 años, una Escuela de Música para adolescentes, un taller de Costura y diferentes talleres socioeducativos que apuntan a fortalecer las potencialidades de salud y capacidades de más de 100 mujeres en riesgo.

Proximamente tendremos un Hostels Universitario, en el piso 2 del edificio central.
Location: Buenos Aires, Ciudad Autónoma de Buenos Aires

Name: HiveSTRONG
Description:
The mission of HiveSTRONG is to break the cycle of trauma and unleash human potential. We help survivors of abuse and human trafficking achieve safety, stability, and independence through a unique whole-person model that combines community, care, and career.

Our survivor-centered programs create measurable, lasting impact — proving that healing and empowerment are possible with the right support system.

Who We Help
Survivors of all forms of abuse — including domestic, relational, sexual, narcissistic, workplace, and human trafficking.

Our Programs

Safe Space Community – A trusted environment for healing, belonging, and connection.

Wraparound Services – Coordinated support addressing housing, legal, emotional, and financial needs.

Career Pathways – Job readiness, education, and employment support leading to sustained economic empowerment.

Our 2025 Results

100% safety rate — every survivor who chose to leave an abuser did so safely.

88.9% overall success rate — the highest in HiveSTRONG history.

75% retention — sustained engagement beyond six months.

These outcomes set a new benchmark for trauma recovery, demonstrating that when survivors are supported with compassion, structure, and opportunity, they don’t just survive — they thrive.

HiveSTRONG for Life.

Name: The Harris Foundation
Description: Founded in 1998 by Dr. Bernard A. Harris, Jr., The Harris Foundation, Inc. (THF) is a 501 (c) (3), non-profit organization based in Houston , Texas , whose mission is to invest in community-based initiatives to support education, health and wealth. THF supports programs that empower individuals, in particular minorities and other economically and/or socially disadvantaged, to recognize their potential and pursue their dreams. Through our interactive STEM education, health and wealth programs, we prepare students and communities for a world of infinite possibilities.

Name: Thomas R. Brown Foundations
Description:
Our guiding principles and strategies are inspired by the life of Thomas R. Brown and the company he created, Burr-Brown Corporation. Its corporate purpose was "To provide something of value to mankind"; we continue this mission through The Thomas R. Brown Foundations.

Burr-Brown was an economic driver for Tucson and Arizona and employed thousands of southern Arizonans in interesting jobs that paid high wages relative to many other jobs in the community. The workforce was diverse, and customers spanned the globe. There was an employee stock-sharing program that allowed employees to participate in ownership of the company, and many of them gained significantly when the company was sold. We would like to re-create opportunities like this for future Arizonans. Accordingly, we support education, training and responsible civics. A healthy community and healthy business environment depend on an educated citizenry and a well-functioning public sector. We have an unfailing commitment to doing this in a strictly nonpartisan manner based on data, rigorous analysis, and inclusiveness.

As we look forward to our next 20 years, the board of trustees have chosen the following four principal focus areas:
-Research and Education
-Workforce Development
-Civic Leadership
-Economics Education

Through investing in programs that advance these focal areas we expect to create opportunities for Arizonans to become more self-determined, more productive, and able to enjoy a higher overall quality of life.

Name: Creative Cares
Description:
CREATIVECARES MISSION

To match creative professionals with organizations in need—locally, regionally and globally.

NON-PROFIT + CREATIVE = GREATER GOOD

There are hundreds of organizations worldwide that need the expertise of “creatives” — photographers, writers, graphic designers and production artists — to tell their story. In fact, without a powerful message, they cannot effectively reach the donors and volunteers they so desperately need to effect real change.


The problem is that organizations for social change are strapped for time, resources and funding. Many of these groups rely solely on charitable donations. Hiring a creative team is usually not a financial possibility: there are lots of worthy causes whose message is not being heard because of this.   

That’s where CreativeCares comes in. Think of us as a matchmaking service for non-profits and the creative community.  We link an organization’s needs—social media strategy, photojournalism, web design, event planning, you name it—to creative professionals with the right skills and a passion for causes to work on a pro-bono basis.

With CreativeCares, they have the potential to find creative individuals in their own neighborhood, state, or even across the globe!

Non-profits and creative professionals teaming up for the greater good—that’s what CreativeCares is all about.

Name: Instituto Social e Educacional Bem Querer
Description:
O Instituto Social e Educacional Bem Querer para a Sustentabilidade Comunitária (IBQ) iniciou suas atividades no município de Sumaré/SP em 2009. Um grupo de pessoas, conhecedoras das necessidades da comunidade dos bairros do entorno da região de São Domingos, em Sumaré, uniu-se com o objetivo de oportunizar a educação cultural e complementar, prestar assistência social e propiciar conhecimento por meio de vivências que levassem à melhoria da qualidade de vida das famílias dessa região.
Hoje, o Instituto conta com equipe multidisciplinar bem mais diversificada, que desenvolve seus projetos de forma descentralizada no município, principalmente nas três unidades de atendimento do IBQ e atende cerca de 500 pessoas por ano. Tem a finalidade de promover, com agilidade e em níveis exemplares, a melhoria das condições de vida das famílias,  promovendo o bem social de maneira sustentável.

Name: 4-H Foundation Kenya
Description:
The Four H Foundation (Kenya) [4-H Kenya] was founded with inspiration from the Global family of 4-H and the Global Clover Network. Its main purpose is to empower Kenya’s youth with the necessary skills and supportive environment they need in order to realize their potential to address Kenya’s and Africa’s food security and livelihood challenges.

The foundation is spearheading a Green Revolution in Kenya by the youth, in the hopes that this shall spread across the continent. Our broad objectives in Agriculture and food security intervention include:
1) To transform the image of Agriculture in the eyes of the young generation, Exposing the youth to more than 1,000 diverse careers along the value chain through vibrant 4-H clubs in schools and within communities.
2) To focus youth innovations in technology to better catalyze the growth of a vibrant agribusiness sector, through business incubation, and strategic partnerships with research, academic and corporate partnerships.
3) To Promote smarter organization of the youth actors in the sector to enable youth enterprises to benefit from economies of scale and improved productivity
4) To develop youth to be responsible leaders who can champion solutions for various problems the Nation faces (e.g. Corruption, Youth Unemployment, Health e.t.c)
5) Address Policy gaps that form barriers to Youth in Agriculture, e.g. access to Land and Capital for Agribusiness.
6) To establish Agribusiness Mentorship Centres of excellence which transform the way communities understand and engage in Agribusiness

Name: COVID-19 Early Treatment Fund
Description:
The COVID-19 Early Treatment Fund was created to ensure the rapid and successful completion of outpatient clinical trials that lead to effective early treatments for COVID-19, using existing drugs. This bold new approach offers the shortest path to saving lives, by bringing new treatments online within a matter of months.

CETF was founded by entrepreneur and philanthropist Steve Kirsch, as a way to expedite the fight against COVID-19.  Early treatment with existing drugs is the fastest, most effective, and lowest cost way to safely reopen the world.  CETF aims to reduce COVID-19 hospitalization and death by at least 75%.  Once funded, outpatient trials could validate an effective early treatment within just a few months.  Help us expedite the fight against COVID-19 - donate now.

Name: Associação São Joaquim de Apoio à Maturidade
Campanha USP do Agasalho
Description:
A Campanha USP do Agasalho foi criada em 2007 com o objetivo de estreitar os laços entre a comunidade da cidade de São Carlos e a universidade, promovendo uma atividade relevante para ambos. Desde sua criação, o projeto cresceu muito e, em 2019, arrecadamos quase 60 mil peças de roupa que foram distribuídas entre mais de 30 entidades de São Carlos e região, chegando até mesmo em comunidades africanas.
.
No contexto da pandemia as atividades tradicionais foram paralisadas priorizando a saúde de todos os envolvidos. Novos projetos foram criados com o objetivo de amparar as pessoas em situação de vulnerabilidade da cidade de São Carlos.
.
Cestou na Quarentena: projeto criado com o objetivo de arrecadar cestas básicas por meio de patrocínio de Organizações Estudantis de São Carlos. Em união com mais quatro extras curriculares chegamos em uma marca de mais de 1000kg de alimentos, cerca de 1700 produtos de higiene, mais 800 produtos de limpeza e quase 1000 fraldas em 8 semanas de projeto.
.
Agasalhando as Ruas: ação criada para a arrecadação de cobertores para pessoas em situação de rua de São Carlos. Através de uma campanha de crowdfunding virtual com uso do marketing das mídias sociais foram arrecadados 800 cobertores corta frio distribuídos para 10 entidades.
.
Missão: “Promover o empoderamento de pessoas em situação de vulnerabilidade e conscientizar a sociedade através da coleta e doação de roupas e eventos sociais.”
.
Visão: “Ser inspiração em trabalho social, colaborar para a redução da desigualdade social de São Carlos e Região e aproximar universidade e comunidade.”
.
Valores: "Empatia; Responsabilidade Social; Conexão; Coletividade; Respeito"

Name: Philanthropists Association International X (PAIX)
Description: Philanthropists Association International X (PAIX) is founded in Geneva, Switzerland in 2012. It is a platform for social entrepreneurs, social workers, NGOs, NPOs, and social advocators to collaborate from online to offline projects. The mission of PAIX is to make everyone the X influencer who makes changes to better our world. PAIX offers open and sharing resources for impact investors and philanthropic funds to find sustainable projects, also for social workers and initiators to work together and brainstorm effective and creative ways of giving and changing.

Name: Casamasanté
Description:
Créée en 2013, Casamasanté est une association française loi 1901 reconnue d’intérêt générale et dont la mission est de faciliter l'accès à la santé et promouvoir l'aide à l'enfance en Afrique.
Son action en Casamance s'appuie sur l'équipe locale, soudée, motivée et dynamique de Casamasanté Sénégal.
Avec le soutien des autorités administratives et sanitaires régionales, l'association a mis en place depuis 2014 un programme de médecine scolaire pour les 1500 enfants scolarisés en maternelle et primaire à Cap Skirring.
L'association a doté chacun des enfants scolarisés d'un carnet de santé individuel permettant leur suivi, notamment en y consignant les informations des consultations de médecine scolaire annuelles, des campagnes de vaccinations, des consultations spécialisées.
Casamasanté poursuit sa mission avec la construction et la mise en service en janvier 2107 d'un centre de soin pilote qui permet un élargissement de l'action de médecine préventive et des soins de première nécessité au bénéfice des familles adhérentes.

Name: NBIA Disorders Association
Description:
NBIA Disorders Association is a non-profit organization dedicated to providing emotional support to families affected by Neurodegeneration with Brain Iron Accumulation (NBIA), educating  the public about these rare disorders, and monitoring and supporting research. 
NBIA is a group of rare, inherited, neurological disorders. The common feature among all individuals with NBIA is iron accumulation in the brain, along with a progressive movement disorder. 
All NBIA disorders are considered a rare and orphan disease by the National Institutes of Health.
We are a Patient Advocacy Group.
