Description: Eagan's community foundation - The Eagan Foundation has been working since 1991 to make Eagan the best place to live, learn, work, and play.

Name: Ensemble Pour le Développement Humain
Description:
Bienvenue sur le compte LinkedIn de notre association EPDH, Ensemble Pour le Développement humain !

Notre association est une association socio-éducative et culturelle s’impliquant dans de nombreux domaines et notamment dans l’éducation.
D’après le CNESCO (Conseil national d’évaluation du système scolaire), le taux de réussite aux épreuves écrites du diplôme national du brevet dans les territoires cumulant le plus de difficulté socio-économique est de 24,3%. Un chiffre alarmant mais qui nous pousse à agir et à combattre ces inégalités. Nous visons l’excellence et nous voulons offrir le meilleur pour les jeunes indépendamment de leur genre et de leur origine. L’excellence académique est certes notre priorité mais la culture l’est également. Nous organisons donc des sorties pédagogiques où nous permettons aux jeunes d’en apprendre plus sur les valeurs françaises et républicaines.

Notre association a également pour objectifs d’aider et d’accompagner des personnes rencontrant des difficultés d’insertion en leur enseignant notamment des cours de français mais également les valeurs françaises et républicaines.

L’association milite pour de nombreuses autres causes en travaillant par exemple contre les violences conjugales. L'association œuvre pour une dynamique de développement local pour créer et maintenir le lien social entre les habitants, et faire en sorte que la solidarité et l'entraide soient au cœur de l'action.

Enfin, nous sommes à la recherche de bénévoles s’identifiant et partageant nos valeurs.


Nos valeurs : Discipline, savoir et solidarité.

Notre devise : Toujours plus haut, plus fort.

Association EPDH, Ensemble pour le développement humain.

Name: The Hearing Foundation of Canada
Description:
The Hearing Foundation of Canada (THFC) was incorporated as a charitable, not-for-profit organization in 1979.  In the years that followed, THFC has remained focused on hearing health issues and continues to work hard to advance hearing health in Canada.

Mission:  The Hearing Foundation of Canada is a national nonprofit organization dedicated to eliminating the devastating effects of hearing loss on Canadians by promoting prevention, early diagnosis, leading edge medical research and successful intervention.

Position:  We believe that: hearing loss can have a devastating impact on communication, and in turn, hinder language development, education, employment and personal safety; sharing information on how to prevent hearing loss will help Canadians make informed decisions about protection and treatment; medical research will lead to new or improved diagnostic and intervention techniques and treatments and eventually to restoration or reversal of some forms of hearing loss. 

Mandate: We are committed to improving hearing health among Canadians by fostering safe listening habits and hearing loss detection, from an early age, and supporting medical research that improves diagnosis and treatment.  We make a difference to Canadians by: 

•	Providing grants to enable Canadian researchers to conduct clinical and biological medical research in hearing-related fields. 
•	Educating the public, particularly young people, on how to detect 
and prevent hearing loss. 
•	Advocating awareness of hearing protection and related issues 
to governments, media and the public.

Name: Neurofinanzas A. C.
Description:
- En México y Latinoamérica más del 62% de la población no tiene una cuenta bancaria (México, CNBV PIC2019). 
- 47% de las mujeres han sido víctimas de algún incidente de violencia por parte de su pareja (México, ENDIREH 20112).
- El círculo de la violencia es difícil de romper por el temor de la mujer a no contar con una solvencia económica para ella y sus hijos. 

En Neurofinanzas AC nuestra misión es construir prosperidad económica y social a través del empoderamiento con la Educación Financiera. 

Nuestro objetivo es enseñar habilidades financieras y conceptos elementales de emprendimiento para que mujeres y niñas puedan empezar a construir un patrimonio propio que les permita alcanzar independencia financiera y así evitar o evadir relaciones violentas por dependencia económica.

Name: Friends of Miami Animals Foundation
Description: Our mission is to save and improve the lives of homeless pets in Miami Dade County through education, advocacy, increased awareness, financial support and increased awareness

Name: Teen Author Boot Camp
Description:
Teen Author Boot Camp is a DBA of Operation Literacy, a 501c Nonprofit Organization that creates educational content and publishing opportunities for kids. We create webinars, podcasts and events that promote literacy. Our  annual teen writers conference held every spring in Provo, Utah is the largest of it's kind and attracts dozens of NYT Bestsellers.
Past keynote speakers include Marissa Meyer, Ally Carter, Jay Asher, Marie Lu, Brandon Sanderson, Jessica Day George, James Dashner, Ally Condie, Shannon Hale, Kiersten White, Brandon Mull, Dan Wells, Leigh Bardugo and Margaret Peterson Haddix.
Teen Author Boot Camp also has a program, TABC Book Drop, that delivers free books and author visits to Title One schools.

Name: Project Care Volunteering | Volunteer Abroad
Description:
Project Care Volunteering organises group volunteering trips for university students to schools and colleges for disadvantaged young people in India and Uganda. 

It was founded in 2012 and has since coordinated 15 group volunteering trips, raising over £100,000 towards transformational projects, including sanitation projects and the building of new school infrastructure (such as classrooms, playgrounds, toilets and kitchens) at a number of sponsored schools in India and Africa. 

What makes us different? 
As a charity:
- 100% of donations received by us reach our partner schools 
 - No one within the charity receives a salary or other reimbursement 
 - Each of our Trustees has previously volunteered at our partner schools
- We do not charge any administration or other fees for the trips

For volunteers:
- A low and achievable fundraising target is set for volunteers
- Affordable accommodation, meals and transfers costs
- Support and training before and during the trips, including a full-day in person workshop
 - No local language skills or prior teaching experience required
 - Immersion in the local community
 - Simple application and enrolment process

Currently organise trips to:
- Navchetan Andhjan Mandal School, a school for children with a variety of physical and mental impairments in Bhuj, Gujarat in India
-  Himalayan Buddhist Cultural Association School, the only school providing both education and residential facilities in a remote location in the foothills of the Himalayan mountains in Manali, Himachal Pradesh in India
- Auro Technical School, a vocational college providing courses in hairdressing, tailoring, bricklaying and carpentry in the rural town of Pajwenda near Tororo in Uganda

For any enquiries, please contact projectcareuk1@gmail.com

Name: Peace Partners
Description:
Peace Partners is a registered UK charity committed to meeting the fundamental human needs of clean air and water, nutritious food and individual peace.  Peace Partners aims to raise public awareness, improve knowledge and expertise, and develop sustainable solutions to these global challenges.

To fulfil our aims, Peace Partners works with individuals, groups and organisations who share this vision.

In particular we partner with and support the unique, innovative programmes of The Prem Rawat Foundation (TPRF), who are helping to assist people most in need, worldwide.

We support the Peace Education Programme in community and prison contexts throughout the UK.

Name: SkillChai
Description: SkillChai is a network where everyone shares his or her skill to make others life more easy and successful.  Join our team and make a change. www.skillchai.com

Name: The HALO Initiative
Description:
The Halo Initiative supports Catholic Schools in the Diocese of Dallas in getting students to college and heaven. We believe that Catholic education changes lives and improves communities, and that a family’s ability to pay should not limit their access to a better life through one of our Catholic Schools.

Through coordinated partnership with Diocesan funds and scholarship organizations, The Halo Initiative coordinates over $1.9 million in direct, need-based tuition assistance each year to students across Catholic schools in the Diocese of Dallas.

Name: Letters Charity
Description:
The Mission
To use Art as a conduit to transform passive compassion to immediate assistance through the distribution of money given, without expectation or judgment, directly to families suffering poverty. Our budget is paid for through private funding which allows us to give 100% of our proceeds to the people we serve.

The Vision
To combat poverty with money by producing events, driven by our artistic philosophies and respecting the artists to make it possible. These events raise money from those who are inspired by our message that true philanthropy requires you to behave righteously and trust the process. 

25 YEARS
250+ HOUSEHOLDS
2500+ INDIVIDUALS
$3 MILLION+ DISTRIBUTED
0 STRINGS ATTACHED

Name: Top League Alumni Club
Description:
Der Absolventenverein der Top League-Studierenden (ATL) ist die offizielle Vereinigung der Absolventen des Top League High-Potential Programms der Wirtschaftsuniversität Wien.

Die WU Top League wurde 2005 gegründet mit dem Ziel ambitionierte und engagierte Studierende zu fördern. Inzwischen haben bereits über 1000+ Studierende dieses Förderprogramm erfolgreich abgeschlossen und sind nun durch den Absolventenverein auch über das Studium hinaus miteinander vernetzt. Jedes Jahr kommen in etwa 100 erfolgreiche Alumnis hinzu.

Informationen zum WU Top League Programm
https://www.wu.ac.at/studierende/bachelorguide/foerderprogramme/wutopleague/

Name: HUMAN ISC
Description:
Human ISC est une association humanitaire de proximité de loi 1901 créée en 1993 qui a pour mission d’apporter un soutien à la fois moral et matériel à des enfants de foyer, des enfants malades, des jeunes issus des quartiers défavorisés ainsi que des adultes et des enfants en situation de handicap. Nous sommes 80 bénévoles et 12 membres au bureau, et nous aidons plus de 300 personnes par an à se construire un avenir, à vivre des moments magiques leur permettant de sortir de leur routine et d’exploiter au maximum leur potentiel afin qu’ils ressentent le moins possibles les inégalités et qu’ils se sentent accompagnés.

Nos principaux moyens d'actions: 

- Des permanences à caractère ludique
- Des voyages culturels et divertissants
- Des journées pour les grandes fêtes (Noël et Pâques)
- Des cours de culture générale
- De l’aide aux devoirs

Nos 6 pôles sont : 

- Human Comedy Club 
- Human Heart 
- Human Charity 
- Human Academy 
- Communication 
- Part & Collecte 

" Le professionnalisme au service du cœur "

Name: Beat Cancer Today, Inc.
Description:
Formed by Iowa families whose children have been diagnosed with childhood cancer, the mission of Beat Cancer Today (www.beatcancertoday.org) is to create awareness of childhood cancer and provide financial support for continued childhood cancer research and patient and family support programs. Funds raised by Beat Cancer Today t-shirt sales and special events have been distributed to Band of Parents (www.bandofparents.org), Children’s Cancer Connection (www.childrenscancerconnection.org), Children's Oncology Group (www.childrensoncologygroup.org), Pascale's Pals (www.pascalespals.org) and University of Iowa Dance Marathon (dancemarathon.uiowa.edu).

While not all of our committee members are parents of children with cancer, we have all been touched by childhood cancer in some way and are committed to working hard to raise awareness of it and provide financial support for continued research and support programs.  Beat Cancer Today is completely run by volunteers, there is no paid staff.

Cancer is the #1 cause of death by disease in children, more than the next four childhood diseases combined.

Facts provided by www.CureSearch/Childhood-Cancer-Statistics/:

Nearly 13,500 children and adolescents are diagnosed with cancer each year, the equivalent of two school classrooms a day.
Only 4% of Federal cancer research funding is directed to childhood cancers, which is the lowest in history.
1 out of 5 children diagnosed with cancer will not survive.
3 out of 5 children who do survive will suffer devastating, long-term side effects.
More than 40,000 children undergo treatment for cancer each year in our country.

http://www.twitter.com/beatcancer
http://www.facebook.com/beatcancertoday

Name: Actions pour une Résilience aux Crises Humanitaires
Description:
La mission essentielle de l'ONG ARCH est d'apporter une assistance aux personnes en état de vulnérabilité et de créer une résilience des populations face aux crises humanitaires en apportant:
- Venir en aide d'urgence aux populations vulnérables et sinistrées
- Lutter contre l'insécurité alimentaire
- Faciliter l'accès à l'eau potable 
- Défendre les droits des femmes et des enfants
- Apporter une assistance psychologique 
- Favoriser l'épanouissement des populations et leur bien-être

Name: Ukraine Charity
Description: Ukraine Charity was established in the UK by volunteers in 2007 when the team began funding projects for disadvantaged children and young adults in Ukraine. We are still managed predominantly by professionals from various backgrounds who are volunteering their time in addition to full-time daily jobs. Our experience and accumulated knowledge of administering help for more than 17 years helped to develop a substantial network of well-trusted partners in Ukraine and internationally, which enables us to operate in an efficient and transparent manner. Since full-scale Russia`s invasion in February 2022, we have secured more than £2 million to provide people with medical supplies and other humanitarian relief. Ukraine Charity is registered with the Charity Commission for England and Wales (Registration No. 1163036).

Name: Action Humanitaire pour le Développement
Description: Action Humanitaire pour le Développement  est une organisation de bienfaisance sans but lucratif qui apporte une aide financière à ses membres et à leur famille. Ils offrent aussi de l’aide aux malades, aux personnes handicapées et à celles moins fortunées ainsi qu’à leur famille, en plus de procurer de l’assistance sociale et intellectuelle par le biais d’œuvres de soutien de nature éducative, charitable, religieuse, sociale, d’aide en temps de guerre et d’aide communautaire.

Name: Foundation House
Description:
Foundation House is a new, intentional shared workspace located in Toronto.

Drawing on the many excellent examples of shared work environments, Foundation House is both a work space and convening hub for Canada’s philanthropic and not-for-profit community. The residents of Foundation House represents 10 engaged and passionate organizations that want to change the way the Canadian philanthropic and not-for-profit sectors work together.

Name: Emerie Agunwah Foundation
Description: we are passionate about leadership conversations

Name: The Fundraiser's Planner
Description:
The Fundraiser's Planner puts everything you need to do in one, easy-to-use, chronological system. You start with setting personal and professional goals and the plan you'll follow to reach them.Then you'll follow, day-by-day, how to track those goals yearly, quarterly, monthly - and, yes, every day and week as well. 

 

Now you have a system that will help you steward that major donor or submit that game-changing grant proposal - and also remember to pick up milk on your way home from work. It's your life and your work - focused, organized, successful.

Name: Instituto Inkiri
Description: Inkiri is a place where dreams come true and putting them into practice has everything to do with our socio-environmental impact. All of our actions take place in more than 25 projects that make up a community non-profit organization called the Inkiri Institute. Thus, the resources moved here are reinvested in our transformation activities.

Name: Latvian Foundation Inc
Description:
The Latvian Foundation is an association of donors who wish to cultivate and support Latvian education and culture for the future. Our mission is to preserve Latvian heritage outside of Latvia and support Latvian culture and education within Latvia by financially assisting projects relating to Latvian literature, language, music, theater, arts, and sciences, and educational pursuits to maintain and enhance Latvian heritage.
The Latvian Foundation is managed by a board of six trustees elected by LF members.

Name: Música nos Hospitais
Description:
Humanization through music. A new space for musicians. 

Founded in April 2006, The Portuguese Association for Musicians in Hospitals and Institutions of Social Solidarity is a non-profit organization, whose mission is to bring music into paediatric hospitals, retirement homes, and institutions who give support to children.

Name: Rock Chalk Revue Advisory Board
Description: Rock Chalk Revue Advisory Board is a student-led organization responsible for running the University of Kansas annual variety show that has raised more charitable funds since inception than any other philanthropic organization on campus. The board is comprised of twenty members who are elected into positions to assist in the coordination of the production. The actual Rock Chalk Revue production is a variety show that invites Greek organizations, residence halls, scholarship halls, and other student organizations to try out and create an original musical. Student groups work all year and compete to be chosen for five slots in the show, from which one winner is selected by a panel of judges. The show is staged at the Lied Center each spring, with proceeds going to support a new local charity every three years. Since the first show in 1949, over $2.5 million has been donated to local charities.

Name: Horizon Health Foundation
Description:
Health care isn’t a job. It’s our calling—to help all those in need in rural areas and hometowns. The Horizon Health Foundation distributes 100% of donated resources to support patients with transportation, food insecurity, cancer screenings, children’s oral health, and more.

For more information, contact us at:

Office Phone: 605-772-4553

Address:
Horizon Health Foundation
125 South Main St
PO Box 728
Howard, SD 57349

Name: Arlington EATS
Description: Arlington EATS envisions an an engaged and active community in which everyone has access to sufficient and nutritious food and understands the causes and effects of food insecurity.

Name: DonorUP
Description:
DonorUP strives to be the most innovative and cost effective fundraising solution on the market. 

We offer premium fundraising tech for FREE to nonprofits. 

Nonprofits come to us for custom fundraising campaigns, event ticketing, and peer-to-peer where we showcase the latest in design and frictionless payment flow.

Join the hundreds of nonprofits using DonorUP to up their fundraising!

Name: The Loving Rose Foundation
Description: The Loving Rose Foundation is a 501c3 non-profit organization dedicated to supporting cancer and Alzheimer's patients and their caregivers.  The organization dedicated in memory of the Founder & President's beloved mother, Mrs. Rose Catherine G. Bates, who lost her battle with Non-Hodgkin's Lymphoma on November 3, 2007.  The organization began as a walking team supporting the Leukemia & Lymphoma Society’s "Light the Night Walk" in 2012.  The organization raise funds and awareness for various cancer treatment and research; and also support local cancer patients and survivors financially and spiritually.

Name: Secret Happiness
Description: Cursos, Talleres, Conferencias, Workshops de Espiritualidad, Amor Propio y Salud Holística

Name: Diseña el Cambio
Description:
Diseña el Cambio es el movimiento de emprendimiento social más grande del mundo.
Cuenta con una metodología de cuatro sencillas palabras: Siente, Imagina, Haz y Comparte; con las cual niños, jóvenes y maestros realicen proyectos a favor de su comunidad cada ciclo escolar.
Al día de hoy se han realizado más de 23,000 proyectos de cambio, involucrando directamente a más de 23,000 profesores y 115,000 alumnos, impactando positivamente a más de 5.7 millones de mexicanos.
Te invitamos a visitar www.disenaelcambio.com y formar parte de este gran movimiento.

Name: Electus Life Education Foundation, Inc.
Description: Targeted Approach to Combating Opportunity Equality by Advancing Youth Financial Literacy & Career Education.

Name: World Startup! NGO
Description:
World Startup! NGO helps people in developing countries grow existing startups that benefit their communities, their countries, and the world.

We believe that startups are a key indicator of the health of a population.
We believe that startups are worth helping in any way possible.

World Startup! NGO helps startups by:
- listening: working with startups to figure out how we can help
- connecting: connect startups with the resources that they need
- empowering: building startups into successful enterprises that encourage others to form startups

Current areas of expertise:
- Connection to resources
- Conceptual consulting
- Tech infrastructure
-- Web and social media presence
-- Software development consulting
- English

Name: Pierce Family Charitable Foundation
Description: The Pierce Family Foundation’s primary focus is on nonprofits working in housing and homelessness, and helping those nonprofits to build the capacity they need to operate effectively. The Foundation understands the real costs nonprofits face, and so supports “full mission funding,” providing unrestricted general operating dollars for the essentials that keep organizations strong and programs thriving—things like staffing, technology, and fundraising capacity.

Name: SAMSA - South African Medical Students Association
Description:
The South African Medical Students Association (SAMSA) is a nationally run nonprofit organization comprising of medical students from various medical schools across the country. Essentially, SAMSA is an organisation run by medical students for medical students and for the greater communities of our beautiful nation. 

Our members have a passion for both medicine and creating positive change in the South African and global communities at large. SAMSA has taken shape to create a network of medical students who support and encourage each other to make this positive change. By recruiting members from all over the country, SAMSA allows members to create a national movement and a scope of influence far outreaching that of a single university. We aim to create opportunities for professional development and acts as a representative voice for all South African medical students. 

Our projects are chosen, coordinated and funded on a national level. They are then carried out by our corresponding divisions on a local level. They aim to encompass our 4 Pillars or Portfolios: Student Representation, Outreach, Collaboration and Professional Development.

Name: GoodWork Army
Description: GoodWork is a grassroots organization designed to make volunteering for good-work projects easy and rewarding. GoodWork creates a work-from-home volunteer opportunity for people who want to work on humanitarian projects.

Name: VoedselLoket Almere
Description:
Soms verkeren mensen in zo’n financiële nood, dat ze nauwelijks geld hebben voor de dagelijkse boodschappen. Deze mensen kunnen bij het VoedselLoket Almere terecht.

Samen met een professionele hulpverlener wordt er aan een structurele verbetering van de persoonlijke situatie gewerkt. In de tussentijd worden via VLA levensmiddelen ter beschikking gesteld.

Met een speciale betaalpas waarop maandelijks een tegoed wordt gestort, vullen onze klanten hun boodschappenmandje met brood, groente, zuivel en houdbare goederen.

Zo kunnen zij zelf kiezen welke voedingsmiddelen bij hun gezinssamenstelling passen. Bovendien winkelen zij gratis bij het VLA Kleding en het VLA Speelgoed.

Name: Industrial Engineering Foundation UGM
Description: Industrial Engineering Foundation (IEF) adalah organisasi non-profit dari alumni teknik industri UGM. IEF merupakan sebuah gerakan untuk menjalin tali silaturahim yang leih erat dan bentuk solitdaritas alumni teknik industri UGM terhadap adik-adik mahasiswa aktif TI UGM yang menempuh perkuliahan di DTMI-UGM.

Name: Real World Foundation, Inc.
Description: Targeted Approach to Combating Opportunity Equality by Advancing Youth Financial Literacy & Career Education.

Name: Association Prométhée Humanitaire
Description:
Notre mission : sortir les enfants des rues

Depuis plus de 30 ans, Prométhée Humanitaire accompagne des enfants vivant dans la rue en Haïti, à Madagascar et au Sénégal. Nous leur offrons un toit, une éducation, un accompagnement affectif et un avenir.

 Ce que nous faisons :

Accueillir les enfants dans des centres sécurisés

Les soigner, les nourrir, les scolariser

Les accompagner jusqu’à l’âge adulte, avec un vrai projet de vie

 +2 000 enfants déjà sortis de la rue, dont plusieurs diplômés universitaires

 Une association 100% indépendante, portée par plus de 250 bénévoles engagés

 Nous organisons 2 fois par an une braderie solidaire de créateurs pour financer nos actions. Grâce au soutien de +250 marques, les ventes permettent de transformer du shopping en solidarité.

 Pour en savoir plus : https://www.prometheehumanitaire.org

Name: Make The Circle Bigger
Description:
Make the circle bigger (MTCB) is a non-profit company that specialises in supporting Africa’s small and medium enterprises with access to information and support that is key to the growth and sustainability of their businesses.
SMEs are the cornerstone of all economies and represent 90% of businesses, contribute about 50% to the global domestic product and 60 to 70% to global employment. In addition, they contribute 40% of national income in emerging economies.
It is estimated that 600 million jobs will be needed by 2030 to absorb the growing global workforce which makes SME development a big priority.
However, despite the significance of their contribution to building, growing, and sustaining economies, SMEs, remain the most vulnerable and unsupported contributors to economic growth.
Historically SMEs struggle to survive beyond the three years of existing impacting job creation, stability and retention considering that 7out of 10 jobs are created by SMEs.
Make the Circle bigger aims to provide much needed knowledge sharing and support to SMEs these include and are not limited to business knowledge sharing and encouraging and enabling a culture of continuous learning amongst SMEs. 
As well as providing speed mentoring with global business leaders, facilitating contributions by corporates to SMEs and sharing access to professional services.

Name: Melior Mundi Charity Fund
Description:
Vår visjon er å maksimere bidraget til samfunnet over tid. Vi er priviligerte som er født i en posisjon hvor vi har mer enn vi trenger, og ved å gi til veldedighet bidrar vi til muligheter, redistribusjon, og en verden som i større grad tar vare på hverandre.

Ved å begynne tidlig kan vi bygge opp en forståelse og posisjon for hvordan man best mulig kan gi tilbake. Samtidig håper vi at fondet bidrar til å øke den kollektive interessen og forståelsen av å gjøre verden til et litt bedre sted.

Name: AmeriGives, Inc.
Description:
For two generations, AmeriGives has served as a trusted advisor and service partner to Fortune 500 and other organizations eager to mobilize their resources on behalf of favorite charities, important causes, and socially relevant programs. No other company has done so much for so long – and we’re just getting started.

We are a full-service provider for Corporate Social Responsibility, workplace giving, and philanthropic programs. From up-front strategic planning and implementation to ongoing management and staffing to the technology powering those programs, AmeriGives does it all.

Whether you are looking to re-engage a stagnant initiative, design and implement new programs, or just better understand where you stand among your peers, AmeriGives Consulting delivers answers you need. Staffed with experienced consultants who have worked with both large and small companies, as well as members of the Philanthropy 400, we would be happy to talk to you about your strategic vision and path to implementation.

AmeriGives delivers industry-leading service quality in support of our clients’ workplace giving.  Corporate Social Responsibility, and philanthropy programs. Ultimately, our job is to represent your brand to your employees, charity partners, and communities. Program services include, but are not limited to: Annual Campaigns, Disaster Relief, Dollars for Doers, Event Management, Gift Matching, Grants Management, and Volunteerism.

Successful service delivery depends on reliable, stable technology platforms. And that’s just what we deliver. At AmeriGives, we understand that technology helps to deliver the services you need, but it should not define the limits of the services you receive. AmeriGives also licenses our GivingPrograms Platform to clients who have the internal staffing to do it themselves. We are flexible, and ready to serve. Just let us know what you need.

www.AmeriGives.com

Name: Harris and Eliza Kempner Fund
Description: HEKF's mission is to invest in the long-term success, sustainability, and well-being of Galveston and its residents.

Name: Feed the Hungry Foundation, Inc. (Retired)
Description:
Feed the Hungry Foundation, Inc. was established in 1977.  Food boxes are delivered into the homes of those who call.  Needy families can call the Foundation once every three months.  Currently the Foundation provides over 1,000,000 meals to families in North Georgia each year.  The Foundation is volunteer driven.  Volunteers fill our food boxes and do the deliveries.  Volunteers are always welcome.

In November of 2012 Feed the Hungry Foundation sold its assets with the understanding the new company would carry out the mission of delivering food boxes into the homes of the needy.  It is with sadness that I tell you by July of 2013 they had discontinued that program and changed the program.  They now SELL the food boxes, food for the most part that came from Publix customers who donated this food, in my opinion not something the customers would approve or support.

Name: The Girl Foundation
Description: “The Girl Foundation” aims to work consistently to create awareness and empower girl students from Social Welfare Schools/Colleges on Life Skills. It’s an effort to make them understand and apply bio-pyscho-social-spiritual aspects of normal growth and development. We intend to enhance their professional and personal attitudes, values and ethics in practice by conducting regular training programs and workshops. We inspire students to use their knowledge as an instrument of social change thus contributing to the personality development of the girl students.

Name: Womenade Boston
Description:
Womenade Boston is a giving circle, a collective giving organization that enables members to pool individual contributions in order to make significant grants to Boston-area organizations dedicated to empowering teen girls and women. We fund organizations serving people and communities from Boston out to the I-495 corridor. Each year, Womenade Boston undertakes a six month long, competitive grant-making process during which a committee of members reviews grant applications, makes site visits to applicants, and narrows the pool down to a set of finalists to present to the full giving circle membership. At the June Voting Meeting, members hear presentations from the finalists and then cast their votes to award the year’s grants.

We invite EVERYONE to attend our events, enjoy our education programs, learn about our grantees, and support our mission by joining or by making a donation. We strive to be a welcoming community for women from all walks of life with a passion to give, a hunger to learn, and a commitment to the exponential power of collective giving.

Name: KidiTekk Foundation
Description: KidiTekk Foundation is a private foundation with a key focus on positively impacting the lives of vulnerable people through supporting various causes in the following thematic areas; Women and Girls Education; Financial Inclusion and Entrepreneurship; Renewable Energy; Climate Change.

Name: The Recyclery Collective
Description:
The Recyclery Collective is an educational bike shop that promotes sustainability by giving access to tools, skills and opportunities for collaboration.

We envision a diverse, resilient neighborhood filled with knowledgeable, self-reliant cyclists.

Our Values

Collaboration: We create opportunities for people of all backgrounds to work, share, and learn together to support a practical form of transportation.

Social Justice: We provide tools and resources to empower underserved populations and nurture community leaders.

Ecological Sustainability: We encourage the mindful use of resources and promote cycling as a part of building more resilient communities.

Celebrating Everything We Do: We like to have fun while working hard to make bicycles and mechanics accessible.

Our Structure

The Recyclery is a 501c3 non-profit. All major decisions are made by a quorum of collective members at a twice-a-month meeting. Decisions are made by consensus and the collective is open to anyone willing to put in the time and effort to fulfill collective entry requirements. A few staff members are paid for administrative tasks, fixing bikes for sale, and teaching classes. Regular volunteers fix bikes that we give away and help during Open Shop.

Name: Recycled Baseball Items, Inc.
Description:
The Recycled Baseball Items Foundation (RBI) is a non-profit 501 (c) (3) organization committed to keeping the Greater Houston Area's underprivileged youth off the streets, away from gangs, crime, and drugs, while offering them a positive and viable alternative in the form of participation in baseball/softball.  RBI accomplishes this by providing baseball equipment, instructional clinics and college scholarships through individual and corporate donations received during various fundraising events held throughout the year. 

In addition to individual and corporate donations, the RBI Foundation receives used baseball and softball equipment donated from the community, which we clean and refurbish, and then distribute to local youth groups.  Since its inception in 1992, RBI has provided equipment to over 22,000 children in over 220 organizations, including The Salvation Army, Houston Housing Authority, Police Activities League, as well as to numerous youth leagues, community centers, and churches.

Name: Ramadan Aman
Description: توزيع وجبات افطار على السائقين في الشوارع و التقاطعات المرورية وقت اذان المغرب بالشراكة مع وزارة الداخلية و التعاون مع قيادات الشرطة في جميع امارات الدولة و بمشاركة اكثر من 4000 متطوع او متطوعة طيلة ايام الشهر الكريم للمساهمة في الحد من الحوادث المرورية و السرعة الزاءدة الناتجة عن محاولة بعض سائقي المركبات اللحاق بموعد الافطار

Name: GerontoVida
Description: Gerontovida aspira a la integración de los adultos mayores en forma activa en todas las instancias de la vida social, reconceptualizando el envejecimiento, potenciando cualitativamente su integración social, garantizando la calidad de vida, reinsertándolos en los circuitos de atención y en los nuevos conceptos de Mercado, vinculados con la producción, el bienestar y predictores de una vida saludable.

Name: Himpunan Mahasiswa Teknik Elektro - Universitas Jenderal Soedirman
Compassion's Way, Inc.
Description:
Compassion's Way was founded by the Rice family. It is a 501(c)(3) humanitarian aid organization with the mission to serve and inspire hope to the homeless and underprivileged.
 
Their inspiration for starting the organization can be traced back to the many people who touched their lives during domestic and international missions.  

Compassion’s Way works to empower and inspire hope to the homeless and underpriviledged of our communities through outreach and resource discovery to end their term on the streets.

Today, hundreds of people are being served throughout the Baltimore-DC area, Central Ohio and Detroit Michigan by Compassion's Way. We are proud that we work through local farmers, many businesses and other nonprofits to provide resources and hope to the homeless and those who are underprivileged, hungry and in need.

Learn more at www.compassionsway.org.

Name: FSU St. Louis Seminole Club
Description:
A Seminole Club unites Florida State University alumni and friends in an organized effort. Our mission is to promote FSU and facilitate networking among FSU graduates, Boosters and future Seminoles in St. Louis, MO. We plan to provide social events and networking opportunities as well as a conduit for members to connect with all aspects of FSU. This also presents incredible corporate opportunities for companies looking to introduce their services to an untapped resource. 

If you'd like to get involved, share your contact info and level of interest here: tinyurl.com/STLnoles

Connect with us:
Facebook: www.facebook.com/STLSeminoleClub
Instagram: www.instagram.com/fsustl
LinkedIn: www.linkedin.com/groups/14084024/
Email: stlnoles@gmail.com

Name: Fundacion ValuArtes
Description:
La Fundación ValuArtes tiene como propósito contribuir a una vida más plena mediante la promoción del arte, la cultura, la educación creativa, el reciclaje y la protección del medio ambiente.
Desarrollamos experiencias culturales, organizamos exposiciones y apoyamos a artistas emergentes, impulsando proyectos que fortalecen creatividad, identidad y bienestar en personas y organizaciones.

Name: Ekipi
Description:
Ekipi facilitates physical activity and sporting opportunities for underprivileged youth worldwide.

Our world is home to an estimated 1.8 billion young people. A large percentage of them come from poor, underprivileged and war-torn environments. Research shows that children who play sports have fewer emotional and behavioral problems, and develop stronger mental health. Sports also reduce the risk of chronic diseases and mortality rates. Psychologically, exercise helps young minds fight mental depression and anxiety.

We at Ekipi engage with schools, clubs, professional sports training, health, and other nonprofit organizations to implement sporting curricula, and provide underprivileged children with the right equipment and training for their favorite sporting activities.

Ekipi collects used sports gear & apparel from donors and distribute them to less fortunate kids. This helps us achieve a sustainable supply chain and, combined with monetary donations, equip more kids. We coordinate pick-up or drop-off with donors. We take care of the cleaning and sanitization of donated items. As you can understand, we expect that donated items are in good condition.

Ekipi projects are currently executed in Belize, India, and Kenya. We would like to build worldwide operations with focus on Africa, Asia, Central, and Latin America. While doing that, we are actively looking for opportunities to bring the joy of sports to underprivileged youth in the US, home country of Ekipi.

We really do not exist without you. Your monetary and in-kind donations are our only source of funds. We welcome suggestions from you about who we can potentially help, who we should partner with, and how we can grow as an organization. You are our promoters and marketers. Tell your friends and families about Ekipi. Join us as a core team member. You and we together - we are Ekipi!

Name: Empower One Woman
Description:
To stop the exploitation of women we must provide safe, dignified income opportunities & empower women to empower their communities in ethical, peaceful, healthy, low-waste, eco-friendly, balanced, joyful ways;

• Giving a woman a first job in which she is respected, safe & paid a dignified salary increases her employability & sets the base salary for her future jobs; Empowering her to live comfortably, peacefully & healthily (alone) in a safe neighbourhood, protecting her from numerous risks of exploitation, crime & abuse (even from within her own family).

• Empowering one woman to run her own ethical, eco-friendly, low-waste (non-profit) company, empowers her to create more dignified income opportunities, empower others & address local, environmental, societal &/or global injustices without exploiting people, animals or the planet.

• By being empowered to live a healthy, eco-friendly, low-waste life, a woman can also protect her body, community & our planet from destruction.

• An empowered woman can share her knowledge & resources to empower other women around her.

It all starts with the empowerment of one woman.

Here are some of many ways you can empower one woman. We are here to share exactly how you can:

1. Give an inexperienced woman/new graduate her first job & testimonial so she can find further safe, dignified income opportunities.

2. Teach a woman how to run her own ethical, eco-friendly, low-waste business so she can create safe, dignified income opportunities for other women.

3. Buy a woman internet access & ask her to share it with other women in her community to learn skills, find safe dignified income opportunities, work & start their own businesses.

4. Share any knowledge and resources you have that can empower a woman to live a safe, healthy, empowered, ethical, low-waste, eco-friendly life; Tutorials, recipes, courses.

5. Give a woman your unused, unwanted items, such as phones, laptops, clothing,..

& more..

All details in our content

Name: Fundación Ressa
Description: La fundación Ressa pretende sensibilizar y crear una nueva cultura social. Involucrar, integrar e implicar a todos los agentes sociales para desarrollar una nueva y mejor forma de vivir.
Location: Pozuelo de Alarcón, Comunidad de Madrid

Name: Act ChatBot
Description: Bienvenue sur la page de Act, le Chatbot citoyen qui vous permet de financer par le biais de micro-don des projets associatifs qui vous tiennent à cœur en moins de deux minutes !

Name: Noel Buxton Trust
Description:
The Noel Buxton Trust is a small grant-making charity.  We were established in 1919 to achieve social and economic progress in Britain and overseas.  Today we have three grants programmes. Two are in the UK where we support organisations that work with families affected by domestic abuse and organisations that support prisoners and former offenders.  We also have an African grants programme supporting community-based organisations working to promote sustainable livelihoods for low-income families.
The Trust is run by a Board of seven trustees who meet twice a year to decide on grant applications and governance issues. We have Trustees with special expertise in our three  programme areas.  
Our funding comes solely from investments.
 
We are a UK-registered charity.  Our Charity Number is 220881

Name: Hope For Hope
Description:
Hope For Hope is a nonprofit organization with the goal of providing access to clean water, proper nutrition, safe shelter and elementary education to children worldwide.We started this organization with a simple idea: improve the lives of children. 

We believe that every child has the right to these bare essentials and it is our goal to provide them to children, one child at a time. The numbers are large but when you try to take on the world it can seem like an incredibly daunting task but that is why we have become a project specific organization. By concentrating on a few small scale projects at a time it allows us to help the individual child. They become more than just a number on a website. They become a friend and we become theirs. We believe in creating a bond with those we help in hopes to 
pass on our idea that everyone has the right to have hope. 

Our organization is young. We are new to the field and but what we lack in age and wisdom we more than make up for in our passion, drive, and willingness to learn and educate ourselves as to how we can help. All members of our organization are involved because they just want to help. We have no other reason other than the fact that we feel it is only fair to try to give back. 

We believe that no matter where you are born you should always have access to the four hopes. We aim to deliver the four hopes, even if it is one child at a time. So come join us and help give hope!

Name: The Justice Conference NL
Description:
The Justice Conference is een spraakmakend evenement over gerechtigheid dat jouw invloed op onrecht vergroot. Sprekers en artiesten uit binnen- en buitenland nemen je mee in hun zoektocht om hun gewoontes liefdevoller en rechtvaardiger te maken. Bovendien is The Justice Conference een ideaal platform om ander rechtbrengers te ontmoeten. We vieren veranderingen die we al zien, leren van goede voorbeelden uit binnen- en buitenland en moedigen elkaar aan om in ons eigen huis en onze eigen omgeving aan de slag te gaan. 

De conferentie bestaat uit een hoofdprogramma op 27 oktober in de Midden Nederland Hallen in Barneveld en een pre-conference op 26 oktober op de CHE in Ede. 

The Justice Conference is een initiatief van ontwikkelingsorganisatie Tear en wordt mede mogelijk gemaakt door Compassion, IJM, CHE, ChristenUnie, Civic Engagement Alliance, EO, Hogeschool Viaa, Light for the World, Micha Nederland, Stichting Present en 24-7 Prayer NL.

Name: The Kobo Trust Foundation
Description:
The Kobo Trust is a humanitarian foundation which operates in various countries of sub equatorial Africa whose objective is to provide support to different projects in the areas of education, food, healthcare and disadvantaged communities. The Kobo Trust is a humanitarian foundation which operates in various countries of sub equatorial Africa whose objective is to provide support to different projects in the areas of education, food, healthcare and disadvantaged communities.

Our Mission:
The Kobo Trust offers collaborators and donors an opportunity to contribute to whichever project they are interested in a manner that is easy and transparent. The Kobo trust ensures the whole of your contribution actually reaches the project that you’re supporting.

Name: Community Builders International Network
Description: Community Builders International Network is a group of humanitarian organizations operating in Canada, Haiti, Burundi, DR Congo, Kenya, Tanzania and Uganda. Currently, the network consists of 34 partnered organizations using four different primary languages. Community Builders was founded in 1983 with a mission to support neighbourhoods experiencing acute social challenges in their quest to find natural pathways to wellness and self-sufficiency. Community Builders values respect for all persons, sustainability, open learning and emergence, and has a vision to increase its efforts globally, keeping its distinctive decentralized structure

Name: Continental Societies, Inc.
Description:
Continental Societies, Inc., a non-profit, tax-exempt, international public service organization founded as a national organization in 1956 - and now has 43 chapters in 21 states in the USA, District of Columbia and Bermuda and is continuing to grow.

Through our Five-Point Programmatic Thrust: Health, Education, Employment, Recreation (HEER), plus Arts & Humanities, Continental women adhere to providing humanitarian service through our HEER programs to children in need. Nationally, regionally and locally, Continentals have created innovative activities while volunteering through outreach programs.

Name: Barbara and Steve Wilson Educational Foundation
Description: Founded in 2017, the Barbara and Steve Wilson Educational Foundation facilitates education and training opportunities for post-secondary education, particularly within the field of prosthetic urology and andrology.

Name: Street Child Norge
Description:
Foreningen tar sikte på å eliminere fattigdom og blant barn som bor på gaten og unge mennesker ved å tilby ly, mat, helsetjenester, utdanning og andre veldedige tjenester som styret fra tid til annen anser passende. Foreningen har også som mål å fremme barns rettigheter som beskrevet i FNs barnekonvensjon 1989. Til slutt har foreningen som mål å utvikle kapasiteten og ferdighetene til personer i sosialt og økonomisk vanskeligstilte samfunn på en slik måte at de er bedre i stand til å identifisere og dekke deres behov og delta mer i samfunnet. 
Hensikten er å oppnås gjennom følgende aktiviteter:

Lindre fattigdom hos gatebarn og andre vanskeligstilte barn og deres verger og familier.  Gjenforening av barna med deres omsorgspersoner i trygge omgivelser, samt gjennom psykologisk og psykososial støtte. Forbedre tilgangen til utdanning og øke bevisstheten om viktigheten av utdanning for barnas fremtid ved å jobbe direkte med lokalsamfunn så vel som med omsorgspersonene og familiene.  Å bygge skoler og forbedre eksisterende utdanningsinstitusjoner, samt videreutdanning og opplæring for lærere. Heving av økonomiske midler gjennom innsamling av alle slag, tjenester og bidrag fra tredjeparter og offentlig sektor, medlemskontingent og sponsing. Overføring av midler til utenlandske organer som tjener formålet med foreningen med sitt arbeid.

Name: FairPlay Libraries
Description:
FairPlay strives to make play accessible and affordable for all children, including kids with disabilities who may not be able to play with toys as they come off-the-shelf. 

Though it is simple and inexpensive to modify toys for children with disabilities, the market prices of adapted toys can be 3-5x that of normal toys. For families already navigating inordinate care-related costs, this financial burden is significant. Given children’s quickly changing interests and these high costs, children with disabilities tend not to have adequate access to play.

Our solution to this inequity is a lending library system. We run workshops focused on toy adaptation and accessibility, create adapted toy libraries in community spaces, and provide educational resources for the communities we serve.

Name: Secret Sisterhood
Description:
Secret Sisterhood is a women's movement and social enterprise that aims to empower, uplift and inspire women globally. We wish to make a difference to women's equality through our Secret Sisterhood symbol. We believe that women need to come together so we can create change throughout the world. 

Our jewellery, features our Secret Sisterhood symbol of women's equality. 90% of our profits are given to women in need through our charity partners. When you wear our symbol you are not only representing your support for women around the world but you are tangibly making a difference.

www.secretsisterhood.com


Secret Sisterhood Philanthropic Journeys empower women to connect more deeply to their philanthropic giving and allow networking with successful women. The journeys are structured as insight trips, allowing women to see first hand the injustices that gender inequality brings to women and girls in developing countries. 

Each journey is created with a five star experience in mind. At the end of each trip there is an exclusive three day retreat where you can reflect on the experiences that you have had and also allows you rejuvenate your mind and body before heading back home. 

Each trip requires a minimum donation amount, so as a team we can achieve great measurable impact outcomes. We believe we can make a bigger impact together, and by the end you will have created a significant impact to women and girls who need it most. 

Spots fill up extremely quickly, and we look for the right select group of influential women. If you are interested please head to our website and fill out our expression of interest form.

www.secretsisterhood.com/shop

“In the course of your lives, without any plan on your part, you’ll come to see suffering that will break your heart. When it happens, and it will, don’t turn away from it, turn toward it. That is the moment when change is born.” - Melinda Gates

Name: Oxford Business Alumni (OBA) Los Angeles Chapter
Description:
OBA Los Angeles Chapter

The OBA Los Angeles Chapter constitutes a friendly professional organisation where Oxonians have the opportunity to network, reunite, cooperate and exchange ideas about the development of their personal and professional life to mutual benefit. Recreating the living and studying experience at Oxford University, where academic, personal and professional stimuli have positively influenced us all, the OBA Los Angeles Chapter aspires to continue the fundamental core of this experience: knowledge, interaction and growth.

The OBA Los Angeles Chapter would like to bring Oxonians together through high caliber events and regular educational and social gatherings. At the same time we welcome new and prospective students of Oxford University to support the development and maintenance of world-class business and management education. Our Chapter's support encompasses all Oxonians with an interest in business living in Los Angeles and surrounding areas.

Name: Fundação Gerações
Description:
A Fundação Gerações foi criada em 12 de novembro de 1993, por iniciativa do Dr Oliveiros Valim, na época vereador em Campinas, e contou com o apoio do rotariano Antonio Carlos Micolli. Sua criação teve como objetivo realizar um trabalho voltado para a criança e ao adolescente em situação de vulnerabilidade social e pessoal.
Em 1995, a Fundação recebeu como doação da família Zornig, uma área de 20.000m2, no bairro Pq. Valença II – região do Campo Grande – onde já havia sido construído pelo Rotary Clube de Campinas um prédio de 750 m2, para abrigar uma creche: “Creche Adélia Correa Hoog Zornig”, que iniciou seu atendimento com 60 crianças em 1996.
Através do convênio com a Secretaria Municipal da Educação recebemos a Merenda escolar desde o início de seu funcionamento, proporcionando às crianças atendidas, alimentação supervisionada por nutricionista, garantindo qualidade necessária ao seu crescimento. Esta iniciativa foi um acordo com a diretoria vigente da época e a Prefeitura que, apoiou-nos para que pudéssemos atender a demanda do bairro, já que não havia outra unidade de educação infantil no local.

Name: Initiative-V
Description: We really believe A Perfect Initiative can change a lot. This is why our goal is to incorporate some interesting ideas to make impactful changes in Bangladesh.

Name: PAWS AND NOSE, INC
Description:
Paws and Nose, Inc, is dedicated to charitable and educational purposes in preventing cruelty to animals in Iran and elsewhere by
• Working with veterinary clinics, shelters, rescue groups and individuals to humanely control the over-population of street and homeless animals
• Improving the health of such animals by providing medical care, spaying/neutering, vaccination, food and shelter
• Promoting and supporting adoption of homeless animals
• Educating the public on the humane treatment of and prevention of cruelty to animals.

Name: Charity Foundation "Zhinki poruch"
Description: Our Charity Foundation “Zhinky poruch” is a noncommercial, nongovernmental organization which was established by a team of concerned women who cannot stay indifferent to other’s people grief and pain.

Name: Associação Transportando Esperança
Description: A Transportando Esperança nasceu com a perspectiva de promover vida digna às famílias que vivem em situação de vulnerabilidade social das comunidades da Zona Leste de São Paulo, gerando empoderamento no processo de superação do ciclo da pobreza.

Name: Strength In Our Voices (SiOV)
Description:
SiOV understands that mental health affects many. Our mission is to create an environment of trust and support for those impacted by mental health through open dialogue, education, empowerment, and positive change. We hope to enact that mission everyday through our work in the community.

SiOV is pleased to offer the following services

Sources of Strength – Peer Leader Training
Sources of Strength – Adult Advisor Training
Sources of Strength – Mini-Trainings
Sources of Strength – Year 1, Year 2+ consultations
Sources of Strength – Program Health Assessments

Contact us to learn more about how we can work with your school, youth group, organization, or community: info@strengthinouroices.org

“Working in collaboration with Strength in our Voices has resulted in nearly 100 of our McLean students being trained as Sources of Strength peer-leaders. This year, these leaders have advocated for the addition of a mindfulness period that has been built into our school day. They are also working hard to plan a anti-bullying community Town Hall discussion and a McLean’s Got Talent assembly!”
-McLean High School

See NBC4’s Changing Minds news segment covering the work McLean High School has done with Sources of Strength featuring SiOV’s President, Bobby Donohoe, conducting the training

Name: Mission to Seafarers Scotland
Description:
The Scottish operation of the international Mission to Seafarers entirely funded by voluntary donations offering emergency assistance, practical support and a friendly welcome to crews visiting 230 ports around the world.

For details see the main website at www.missiontoseafarers.org and our site at www.missiontoseafarersscotland.org

Name: War Toys
Description:
War Toys is a California-based nonprofit organization. Its mission is to advocate for children who have been affected by war, educate and train caregivers on the ground in expressive therapy-based interventions for children, and provide toys to children that comfort, promote resilience, and encourage new ways of thinking about war. 

War Toys was founded by toy photographer Brian McCarty in response to his experiences producing a series by the same title. Working in the field since 2011, Brian has met and collaborated with hundreds of children from Iraq, Syria, Lebanon, West Bank, Israel, and Gaza under the care of local and international NGOs and UN agencies. Seeing firsthand the severe effects of displacement and trauma on these children, Brian assembled a team of directors, advisors, and officers that includes experts in expressive therapy, childhood development, human rights, advocacy, and toy design & manufacturing. Together, they are continuing the advocacy work of the photo series while developing new programs and forging alliances that will positively impact the lives of children around the world.
