Name: Climate Answers
Description:
'Continuing the search for answers to climate change'

Climate Answers is a charity for the advancement of education for the public benefit in environment and climate change to assist training which further develops the work Stephen Tindale began. We are dedicated to advancing pragmatic solutions for major environmental and climactic challenges.  Stephen never believed he had all the answers but he always believed there were answers to be found. 

Climate Answers aims to help build a more sustainable future through promoting projects and individuals who will develop local solutions to global issues through the following routes;

Climate Answers Future Leaders programme
 - aims to equip committed environmentalists to adapt to the wide, constantly changing agenda climate change presents and help produce innovative solutions through fully supported work placements in industry, environmental, research and policy.

PLAN(e)T TREES
- Climate Answers greening the environment initiative to plant 1000 trees in every local authority by 2025

Name: Fundación de Bienestar Social CER
Description:
¿Qué es?
Es una iniciativa personal de una Dra. que busca ayudar a todos quienes lo necesiten entregando consultas medicas gratuitas presenciales a cambio de donaciones para personas de escasos recursos.

¿De qué se trata la consulta médica?
Es una consulta médica “particular” de medicina general adultos.

¿Cuál es el valor de la consulta?
Es gratuita, el “pago” es con donaciones que irán en ayuda de familias de escasos recursos y personas en situación de calle.

¿Se atiende por Fonasa o Isapre?
No importa la previsión que tengas, todos quienes necesiten pueden recibir atención medica.

Name: CVU Uberlândia
Description:
O CVU Uberlândia (Centro de Voluntariado Universitário) é uma associação sem fins lucrativos, de caráter não-religioso e não-político, composta por estudantes universitários.

Buscamos fomentar o trabalho voluntário no meio acadêmico a partir de eventos; cursos de capacitação; projetos sociais; parcerias; informação e acompanhamento para o voluntário.

Além de fazer o vínculo entre as ONG’s e voluntários, o CVU de Uberlândia também presta assistência a essas organizações através de projetos e ações.

Nossos valores:
1. Abnegação
2. Organização
3. Proatividade
4. Respeito
5. Comprometimento
6. Amor
7. Colaboração

Name: Action for Reaction Charity
Description: Increasing Mental Health Awareness. By young people for young people.

Name: The Green Foundation
Description:
The Green Foundation is a private foundation in Los Angeles, CA founded in 2004 seeking to preserve and enhance Leonard Green’s philanthropic vision by focusing on Community Services, Education and The Arts.

Our mission is to uncover new opportunities and encourage growth to effect positive change within institutions that best reflect our focus areas and the communities they serve.

Name: Sustainable Development Fund
Description:
SDF believes that sustainable communities are possible if zero waste is designed into the community for its inception.  The community can learn to function in a collaborative, holistic fashion and apply zero waste principles that are designed into the community infrastructure.
SDF's mission is to utilize available / proven technology to create decentralized, circular economy communities that can provide services and living comforts comparable to those that we are accustomed to in our centralized, linear economy communities.  This can be accomplished by utilizing several strategies, that when combined, will yield our goal to achieve zero waste and environmental impacts

Name: Maylands Rotary
Description:
"POSITIVE CHANGE THROUGH COLLECTIVE ACTION"

What started as a couple of people meeting up to discuss a vision has become a group of like-minded people with  shared  values and a desire to give back to their local and global community.

Today, we are welcoming more members each week, which means we can have an ever greater impact.

Our strategy and vision are clear. Every member is given the chance to contribute in the way they want to, whether it be through professional skills, financially or simply lending a hand.

We've created a diverse, progressive and inclusive club which gives everyone a chance to pitch in and feel rewarded.

Name: GAPAC - Grupo de Apoio e Prevenção à AIDS de Criciúma
Description: O GAPAC é uma instituição que trabalha efetivamente na prevenção de DST/ HIV/ AIDS; apoiando e realizando ações que promovam a vida e a igualdade de direitos, excluindo preconceitos e discriminação. Propondo atividade fundamentais afim de auxiliar nesse processo de construção da cidadania,

Name: THE DAVID AND LURA LOVELL FOUNDATION
Description: DAVID AND LURA LOVELL FOUNDATION is a private foundation based out of TUCSON, Arizona.

Name: INSTITUTO ICRIM
Description: Instituição filantrópica destinada a dar apoio às famílias carentes com crianças e adolescentes com deficiências renais.

Name: AidArena
Description:
AidArena is a not for profit organisation that facilitates the sale and auction of items with all, or a portion of the proceeds going to various charities.  Mark and Katie Wilson, Don MacMillan and Bill Paige established this online selling and buying platform in 2009, and have been working to help charities nationwide.  AidArena is a unique website, providing an exceptional fundraising platform for charities in a cost effective and efficient way.   

We give people the opportunity to buy items on the AidArena website and by doing so, their purchase will benefit the sellers preferred charity.   Our website makes it easy for you to support a particular cause or charity by offering you the ability to search by charity rather than item type.   If you are dedicated to a particular cause, you now have the option of purchasing items to help support your charitable interests. 

We also allow you to help in other ways that do not involve spending money.  If you're unable to purchase an item, we welcome you to list goods and services for sale.    Since AidArena is an auction and sales platform, you can help raise funds by listing items for sale and selecting your preferred charity.  When your item is purchased, a portion (which you nominate) of the proceeds will be donated to your charity and the balance transferred to you via the AidArena website.   If you own a business this is a very effective way of giving to charity and moving stock simultaneously.   Once a sale occurs, a tax receipt is issued to the seller with the donation portion of the sale and the charity is issued with regular reports of all transactions and earnings. 

The mission of AidArena is clear: to provide a way for people and businesses to donate in a way which maximises benefits and enables charities to receive a seamless and additional income stream.

Name: Leschly Tennis Foundation
Description: Stiftet af familien Leschly med ønsket om at give tilbage til dansk tennis og løfte sporten i Danmark via økonomisk støtte, talentudvikling og internationalt netværk.

Name: Global Good Networks
Description:
Investing in social entrepreneurs with financial and human capital, leadership and marketing, and more.

Our network includes several web properties with varying business models from news sites to marketing services that are geared towards delivering positive news or helping market social entrepreneurs. 

Doing good in the world while remaining profitable and scalable is key to long term growth and a rise in web market share.

From Fortune 500 sponsors to celebrities, not-for-profits, and start up entrepreneurs around the world we have helped spread good and provide business services in many ways.

Name: United Way of Madison County (Indiana)
Description:
United Way of Madison County is working to create the kind of community we all want to live in. Where every child graduates and achieves their dreams, where every person has a good job that allows them to fully provide for their family, and where every family is stronger because they are healthy. 
 
We work to improve the quality of life in our community by engaging people and organizations to advance education, income and health outcomes for all.
 
We invite you to join with us. Together, united, we can inspire hope and create a better future for everyone that calls Madison County home.
 
That’s what it means to LIVE UNITED. 
Our Mission

To improve the quality of life in our community by engaging people and organizations to advance education, income and health outcomes for all.

Our Vision

United Way envisions a community where all individuals and families achieve their full potential through education, income stability and healthy lives.

Community Impact Statement

United Way of Madison County will work with all available partners to improve the capacity of our citizens to achieve and sustain self sufficiency.

Name: Freedom Golf Association
Description:
FGA is a 501(c)(3) company dedicated to bringing a sense of freedom and joy to those with special needs through  the game of golf.

FGA’s primary goal is to advance the delivery of grass roots golf instruction through the collaboration and coordination of qualified golf professionals, skilled amateurs, health care professionals, and local organizations. Together, we can provide resources to increase the effective contribution to the special needs community.

There are more than 55 million* physically disabled individuals in the United States. According to the Professional Golfers’ Association of America (PGA), 17 million would like to play golf but only 5 million play today. Many who want to learn stated that golf facilities  didn’t know how or want to serve them. Our intention is to include golfers with physical and mental challenges regardless of source or origin, assisting them in focusing on the positive aspects of their lives and increasing their sense of enjoyment.     * 2010 U.S. Census

Name: National Association of Adventurous Black Women
Description: NAABW is a 501(c)(3) dedicated to empowering black women to expand their world views through sisterhood, cultural exposure, and global community service. NAABW is in the business of creating opportunities for women to empower one another and learn more about the world together, while collectively participating in global community service. NAABW seeks to promote the importance of sisterhood and cultural education, while simultaneously diversifying the face of global community service.

Name: YSD Great Lakes
Description: Realization of noble human aspirations for a flourishing life for sustainable development

Name: Gulf Coast State College Alumni Association
Description: The Gulf Coast State College Alumni Association is organized to promote the welfare, development and advancement of Gulf Coast State College by encouraging community awareness and support for educational programs, enhancements and opportunities. The Alumni Association serves Alumni in pursuit of their careers, recognizes their accomplishments, sets the stage for volunteerism and active participation and perpetuates a sentiment of affection and responsibility for the betterment of Gulf Coast State College.

Name: Fondation SEPT ANS
Description:
La Fondation Sept Ans finance et accompagne des projets et initiatives ayant pour objectif de réduire notre dépendance et notre addiction aux écrans et au monde numérique.  

La Fondation Sept Ans est sous égide de la Fondation de France. Chaque don à la Fondation bénéficie des mêmes garanties qu'un don à la Fondation de France.

Name: Horizon Cameroun
Description:
Horizon Cameroun est une association humanitaire à but non lucratif de loi 1901, créée à Angers en 2000. Composée d'une cinquantaine de membres, elle récolte des fonds et organise chaque année une mission humanitaire au Cameroun afin de mettre en œuvre des initiatives destinées au développement. 
Cette mission se déroule au sud du Cameroun, dans un village rural de 5 000 habitants du nom de N’Goma, situé dans le département de Mvila. 
Nous avons déjà réussi à y amener l'éducation en construisant une école et à y développer une coopérative agricole. Cependant, le chemin est encore long pour que le village puisse subvenir à tout ses besoins. 
L’objectif de cette année 2017-2018 est d’aider à la commercialisation des produits agricoles de la coopérative agricole de N’Goma. Cette aide à la commercialisation permettra aux planteurs de vendre le cacao à un prix raisonnable par rapport au prix extrêmement bas qui leur est imposé.
Afin de récolter les fonds nécessaires à l’accomplissement de cet objectif, nous organisons, à coté des dons, des petits évènements comme de la vente de gâteaux ou de chocolats. Notre évènement principal est une course nature autour du lac de Maine que nous organisons chaque année.

Name: Shoe Aid Project
Description:
The Shoe Aid Project is a shoe recycling and shoe donation program where shoes are collected in well developed countries, and freely distributed to children and families in Cameroon and other African countries to enhance development standards.

HOW TO TAKE ACTION:

Donate Shoes:
Companies, schools, fitness studios & gyms, sports clubs and events, churches and private persons can ALL take part in the project by donating gently worn shoes. This could be new or second handed.

Donate Money:
Your financial support and donations will assist us to cover rents of the warehouses and transportation fee both at the local and international level.
Companies and private persons recieve a donation tax-exempt certificate upon every donation.

Donate Time:
We constantly need volunteers to asssit us with picking, cleaning, and packing shoes. You can as well donate you time to assist us during events, campaigns or in spreading the word about our project.

check on our website: www.shoeaid.org for more information or send us an email anytime : info@shoeaid.org

This is a project initiated by the non-governmental organisation Hope Foundation which is a registered charity in Cameroon and Germany. The organisation has as objective to promote development and alleviate poverty in rural areas of Cameroon and promote intercultural relationships and dialogues in Europe!

Name: Performing Stars Of Marin
Description: Performing Stars Of Marin is an enrichment program for children and young people based out of 271 Drake Ave, Sausalito, CA, United States.  More than 3000 children have been through the program and moved forward into productive, thriving lives. Celebrating 32 years of success!

Name: Respectacles
Description:
Our Mission:
Our mission is to promote sustainability and reduce waste by recycling and repurposing spectacle frames to give them a new life, while supporting those in need of visual aid.

Our Values:
Sustainability: Prioritizing eco-friendliness and reducing waste.
Compassion: Addressing vision-related needs in disadvantaged communities.
Innovation: Encouraging creative repurposing and upcycling.

Name: Colégio Dom Elizeu
Description: O Colégio Dom Elizeu foi fundado em 1966, pertence a Congregação das Irmãs Carmelitas Missionárias de Santa Teresa do Menino Jesus. Faz parte de uma rede de escolas em todo o Brasil e em alguns países do mundo.

Name: Future For Humanity
Description: Future For Humanity brings together people from all walks of life with a goal of raising awareness and to create the opportunities, resources, action and the impact for what’s needed to solve the United Nations Global Goals to ensure the brightest Future of Humanity possible.

Name: HAYCAR Association of Architects and Engineers
Description:
Getting together in the light of the idea of gathering all the architects and engineers who belong to the Armenian society in Turkey, the HAYCAR group officially became an association in 2007.
The main goals of foundation of our association is to support the rehabilitation and revitalization of cultural entities, to contribute to the public awareness about the importance and value of these entities and, to strengthen the cooperation among professionals who can actually contribute to this context. In this outline the objectives of HAYCAR.
Association of Architects and Engineers are:
- To organize panels, seminars, conferences, courses and presentation meetings to enhance the technical knowledge and experience of people who are still attending or already attended to engineering or architectural programs in universities.
- To create effective platforms where, technical and social interactions among members are possible.
- To give technical support and counselling by providing the required communication for our institutions.
- To keep up with developments and innovations in architecture, engineering and related fields, to share these with members.

Name: Swim With Mike
Description: Swim With Mike provides financial resources for the advanced education of physically challenged athletes to overcome their tragedies and realize their full potential. We are a family of volunteers, athletes, sponsors, campaign directors and donors that can be found in nearly every state and across the globe.

Name: Philanthropists of Bangladesh
Description:
As a platform of care and mutual respect "Philanthropists of Bangladesh" will do humanitarian works to achieve the sustainable development goals (SDG) as well as develop the skills of all the Bangladeshi to become a perfect match for the 4th industrial revolution.
So connected with us and be the part of it! <3

#1platform4allHumanitarians

Name: Georg & Agathe Foundation
Description: The Georg & Agathe Foundation is a non-profit that honors the multi-generational heritage of Captain Georg von Trapp and Agathe Gobertina Whitehead, parents to the seven von Trapp children portrayed in the musical and film, “The Sound of Music”.  Our mission is to champion the arts, promote peace, and archive & educate.

Name: The P.A.L Project
Description:
"Culture is the core of who we are and to be without it like lighting a candle with water!" 

The holistic development of an individual cannot happen without their culture as the nucleus and empathy can never truly be realized without exposure to the "why" of other communities. 

The P.A.L Projects believes in creating equity in Identity by providing a space for cultural exchange and education for; particularly, people of African descent through the use of edu-taining programing.

Name: Association des étudiants EPFL pour la Philosophie (EPɸL)
Description:
Nouvelle association pour la stimulation de la pensée critique, éthique et philosophique.
Nous organisons régulièrement des événements où des invités du milieu académique philosophique présentent un sujet qui est ensuite débattu.

Name: Patricia "Nurse Pat" Edwards Nursing Scholarship
Description:
The Nurse Pat Nursing Scholarship (NPNS) honors the legacy of Patricia "Nurse Pat" Edwards, a dedicated nurse who served the Greenville, SC community for over 35 years. NPNS aims to support junior and senior-level nursing students enrolled in accredited programs with financial aid and professional development resources. To sustain and expand our impact, we will host fundraising events and launch an e-commerce store offering nursing supplies and inspirational merchandise. Our goal is to generate revenue for scholarships and amplify Nurse Pat’s legacy by empowering future healthcare heroes.

Organization Description
Founded in memory of Nurse Pat, a DAISY Award recipient and ICU nurse, the NPNS is a nonprofit organization based in Greenville, South Carolina. The scholarship fund is designed to support Black nursing students and those who demonstrate resilience, compassion, and professionalism, similar to Nurse Pat. The organization also aims to create a pipeline of empowered nurses through scholarships, mentorships, and educational resources.

Name: Hobsons Bay Community Fund - Support the Hobsons Bay community
Kentucky Society of Washington
Description: The Kentucky Society of Washington, founded in 1912 is an organization that promotes goodwill, fellowship, and amicable relations among Kentuckians in the National Capital Region. The Society was formed to preserve and perpetuate the history and traditions of the Commonwealth of Kentucky. These ideals are promoted through numerous social, educational, and charitable functions each year.

Name: BuildingBlox Consulting
Description:
The world is full of challenges and opportunities. And many of the changes we want to see can only be achieved when people, motivated by a shared vision and values, work together.

But a lot of nonprofit organizations aren’t reaching their highest potential because they don’t have the resources they need.

BuildingBlox Consulting studies and teaches the best practices of nonprofit organizations. We coach leaders, help them clarify mission and strategy, and demystify fundraising.

 

When the organizations we work with succeed, they celebrate audacious generosity and are infused with a culture of philanthropy. And the impactful work they do is done with passion and purpose.

Name: Impact Las Vegas
Description: Impact Las Vegas is a Nevada nonprofit women’s giving circle. Members donate $1,000 per year and these donations are combined into one large grant awarded to a local nonprofit, chosen by our members, to fund a project or program benefiting Southern Nevada.

Name: Economic Opportunity Program of Chemung and Schuyler Counties
Description:
Since 1965, the Economic Opportunity Program of Chemung and Schuyler Counties (EOP) has been a multi-service organization for family success within the community. EOP is a designated nonprofit community action agency and is the largest private human services organization in Chemung and Schuyler Counties. At its core, EOP is a vehicle to provide an opportunity for individuals and families to earn a decent living wage, learn to protect and save the money they earn and provide a positive present and future for themselves and their children. Through a combination of family development services and energy services, EOP demonstrates its commitment to the community and promotes self-sufficiency. EOP’s programs include Head Start, Centers of Excellence, Ernie Davis Music Consortium, Literacy Volunteers, Family Support Services, the Ernie Davis Community Center, the Energy Services Bureau, and the Community Food for Jobs Program.

Vision | The Economic Opportunity Program Inc. of Chemung and Schuyler Counties (EOP) envisions a future where all people in our community live with dignity and have the opportunity and ability to live economically productive and satisfying lives.

Mission | To achieve this vision, EOP provides services that create positive change in individuals, families, and our community; and encourages and facilitates participation by all people who can help make our vision a reality.

Values | 
-People living with Dignity.
-Families and communities as the foundation for individual stability and growth.
-Diversity and the unique contributions of each individual.
-Active community participation and involvement by government, community leaders, donors, human service providers, and others in enabling people to enjoy economic stability and growth.
-A living wage for all individuals and families to promote financial stability.
-Excellence in services that create positive change in individuals and families.
-Effective and efficient use of all human, material, and financial resources.
-Integrity in allocating our resources and providing services.
-Collaborative partnerships in the community.
-Competent and caring staff to deliver quality services that honor people’s rights to confidentiality.
-Recognition of staff and volunteers as our most valuable resource in carrying out the mission of EOP.
-Human resource development and support as a primary means for ensuring personal and professional growth.

Name: InterAgir Sciences Po Lille
Description:
Interagir est une association étudiante dont le but est de sensibiliser à la précarité et de lutter contre toutes les formes de marginalisation et de discrimination par le biais de distributions, de rencontres, de conférences et d'actions diverses.

Apartisane, son objectif vise néanmoins à mettre en évidence des manquements politiques et des lacunes étatiques afin d'alerter sur des situations critiques et de réfléchir à des solutions plus humaines et responsables.

Name: 1 Million Cups Provo
Description:
About
1 Million Cups (1MC) is a simple way to engage entrepreneurs in communities around the world. Each week, the 1MC program offers two local entrepreneurs an opportunity to present their startups to a diverse audience of mentors, advisors, and entrepreneurs. Presenters prepare a 6 minute educational presentation and engage in 20 minutes of feedback and questioning after they present. Entrepreneurs gain insight into possible ways they can improve their businesses, gather realtime feedback, connect with a community that truly cares about their progress, and walk away feeling like they have advanced their business. The audience also learns a great deal from the presentations. Whether this is through passively listening or deeply engaging with the entrepreneurs, there is much to learn every week from these dynamic and diverse communities. This entire program is run by entrepreneurs or as we like to call them Community Organizers. Without the community leaders from each city, this program would not be sustainable. These Community Organizers are given this title because they are true entrepreneurs out in their communities that are volunteering to aid other startups and to further the entrepreneurial ecosystem within their community.

Name: Greater Charlotte SPCA
Description:
The Greater Charlotte Society for the Prevention of Cruelty to Animals (SPCA) serves the animals and people of Charlotte, NC and the surrounding counties. We are an autonomous organization not affiliated with or supported by any other SPCAs including the ASPCA. We are also not a government-run organization. The Greater Charlotte SPCA is a private 501(c)(3) non-profit. All donations are deductible to the fullest extent of the law.

The Greater Charlotte SPCA does not own or operate a shelter facility. Volunteers foster all animals in their homes until adopted.

OUR MISSION
To improve the quality of life of homeless animals through veterinary care, foster care, humane education, and spay/neuter programs.

“The greatness of a nation and its moral progress can be judged by the way its animals are treated.”
                      ~Mahatma Gandhi (1869-1948)

Name: Alpha Phi Omega Nu Rho
Description: Alpha Phi Omega is a gender inclusive collegiate community service fraternity that strives to develop three cardinal principles: Leadership, Friendship, and Service. The Nu Rho chapter at the College of William & Mary is a dedicated community committed to service to the campus, the community, the nation, and the fraternity.

Name: EVAM
Description: Ending Violence Against Migrants - A Global Initiative By deliberately restricting its scope, EVAM aims to address one of the disturbing aspect of migration: violence.

Name: Fundacja Rodziny Maciejko
Description: Fundacja Rodziny Maciejko powstała w 2009 r.  naszym celem jest szeroko zakrojona działalność organizowania i wspomagania inicjatyw społeczeństwa obywatelskiego. Kluczowym priorytetem jest praca oświatowa, a w szczególności podnoszenie poziomu wykładania matematyki i wiedzy matematycznej uczniów polskich  szkół.

Name: The Foundation for Maine's Community Colleges
Description: The Foundation for Maine’s Community Colleges was established in 2009 and is dedicated to ensuring that the state’s seven community colleges have the resources they need to provide a high quality and affordable college education for Maine people. The foundation seeks to enhance access to higher education and economic opportunity and to provide Maine employers with a highly trained and skilled workforce. To this end, the Foundation develops sustainable sources of philanthropic support for the Maine Community College System. Through this work, the Foundation helps the colleges achieve their mission of providing and increasing higher education opportunities for Maine citizens.

Name: Fundepalma
Description: La fundación para el desarrollo de las zonas palmeras de Colombia, Fundepalma fue creada en el año 2002 por empresarios  del sector palmero al ver la necesidad de brindarles una mejor calidad de vida a los trabajadores de las plantaciones y de las extractoras socias en las zonas del Magdalena y el Cesar.

Name: Grandads of the Atlantic
Description: Will 40 years of friendship and Parachute Regiment training help two Grandads to become the oldest pair to row across the Atlantic...?

Name: Altman Foundation
Description: The Jenifer Altman Foundation is a private foundation that supports creative work in health, education, the environment and justice.

Name: level
Description: A philanthropic consulting firm, specializing in innovative ways to help you do the most good. We work with businesses, brands, and individuals to find their cause, and maximize their impact, through compelling campaigns and critical industry insight.

Name: Science Serves
Description:
We offer you science deployment scenarios, compare world’s science actors, for more coordinated human development projects.

Research Areas:
-Science economics

Name: The Joseph Fund, Inc.
Description:
MISSION 

Passionate stewardship of resources supporting missions…transforming lives!

OUR DONOR VALUES

Shepherding resources to inspire self-determination, raise aspirations, and lift spirits of all.

Name: Colgate Global Health Coalition
Description: The Colgate Global Health Coalition (Colgate GHC) aims to create an inclusive environment and provide resources for students to learn about global public health issues through education, service, and career opportunities. The Coalition will build a strong alumni network to help current students explore the field of global public health, connect with mentors and peers who share similar interests, and be active global citizens and public health advocates. Another focus of the Coalition is to amplify opportunity and access for underrepresented students through partnerships with campus and community organizations. Moreover, as a chapter of the Foundation for International Medical Relief of Children (FIMRC), we will pursue creative volunteering, fundraising, and advocacy initiatives to support numerous project sites in 9 different countries.

Name: The Peter G. Dodge Foundation
Description:
With a particular focus on alcohol addiction, PGDF's key initiatives improve the systems that serve those who struggle with addiction and expand public knowledge about evidence-based care.

–> Through our innovative Technical Assistance Partnerships program, we work directly with national nonprofit partners focused on addiction and recovery, mental health, and wellness to support their missions.

–>Our guide, AlcoholUseDisorder.org, is a national web resource focused on alcohol addiction, with distinct paths for those seeking treatment, those in early recovery, and those wishing to change their relationship with alcohol.

–>PGDF started as a grantmaking organization, awarding $2.4M to exemplary nonprofit organizations from 2014-2023. Please note that we are not currently accepting Mission Grant applications.

Our Ethos
Addiction to alcohol or any substance is a complex, progressive health issue affecting the reward systems of the brain. Because its outward symptoms are behavioral, it is often misunderstood and stigmatized. Active addiction is painful for everyone involved— from the person struggling to change their behavior, often through conflicting impulses and fluctuating levels of motivation— to the family members and loved ones navigating the sometimes chaotic behavior that comes with that struggle. PGDF's work affirms the humanity of those affected and offers robust tools to help them take charge of their lives on their own terms.

Name: Bolsa de Valores Humanos
Description:
Diferentemente do mercado da Bolsa de Valores, nosso investimento não é em papéis e, sim, em crianças, adolescentes e jovens em situação de acolhimento, que residem em casas de passagem, abrigos, casas-lares, repúblicas e famílias acolhedoras, cuja realidade e desafios escapam ao conhecimento de grande parte da sociedade. Essa invisibilidade causa uma insuficiência de investimento nesse público e dificulta a construção de uma trajetória de oportunidades reais, gerando subaproveitamento da potência e protagonismo dessas crianças, adolescentes e jovens. A partir da premissa de que se trata de seres únicos e em desenvolvimento, a Bolsa de Valores Humanos, como articuladora social, atua na busca de recursos junto à sociedade e empresas nacionais e internacionais, com o objetivo de proporcionar projetos individuais e coletivos relacionados à assistência social, saúde, educação, esporte, capacitação profissional, lazer e qualquer outra atividade para o desenvolvimento integral e efetivo desse público.

NOSSA HISTÓRIA
A Bolsa de Valores Humanos surgiu em 2014, mas a vocação de fazer a diferença começou há mais tempo, motivada pelas observações de um grupo de amigos que atuava voluntariamente em um dos serviços de acolhimento de Campinas e vivenciava os desafios da instituição em assegurar a crianças, adolescentes e jovens acolhidos, atividades externas e complementares capazes de garantir a eles um olhar individualizado, tendo em vista a insuficiência de políticas públicas para atendê-los.
Desde o início, a intenção era ofertar a esse público possibilidades de vivências que os potencializasse para construção de uma nova perspectiva de vida, mas o aumento das demandas e da complexidade de cada história dava a certeza de que era preciso expandir a rede de investidores.
E foi assim, com a colaboração de mais pessoas e de investidores que acreditaram na missão da Bolsa de Valores Humanos que a associação foi formalizada em 2017.

Name: Phi Delta Epsilon: CA-Iota
Description:
We are the California Iota undergraduate chapter at UC Berkeley, founded in 2007 by Felix Wong and Tony Chen, with the sponsorship of Dr. Bernard Griego.

Through our passion and dedication towards excellence in the field of medicine, we have expanded tremendously since then, with hundreds of new members since our inception. Our alumni have been accepted and matriculated to prestigious programs at UCSF, Harvard, Columbia, USC, NYU, and more, and we pride ourselves on having an acceptance rate to medical schools much higher than the national and UC Berkeley averages.

Our active members are exceptionally hard working and academically focused, with an average GPA far above their major averages and the UC Berkeley average, many having multiple majors and minors. Our actives also balance challenging course loads with many professional activities such as work in research labs, consulting internships, doctor shadowing programs, and many more! 

We also have an extremely active alumni network whose work has spanned across the world, pursuing careers both in as well as outside of healthcare.

Name: Center for Migration and International Relations (CMIR)
Description:
Center for Migration and International Relations (CMIR) is a not-for-profit institute thriving to ensuring rights of migrant workers and their families are respected, guaranteed and fulfilled. We are a dedicated and dynamic team of returnee migrant workers, migrant rights’ activists and researchers having a long-time involvement history in the field of migration and development. In Nepal, CMIR is the first institute of involving all three groups: returnee migrant workers, migrant rights activists and researchers into a common platform. This enables CMIR to better understand the multi-faceted impacts of migration into different aspects and dynamics of the society and thus, help to come with concrete humanitarian actions, policy recommendations and enduring advocacy efforts in national, regional and international level.

CMIR is also the first and only institute in Nepal to involving international relations as a part of study and advocacy of migration dynamics. Though study and advocacy on bettering international relations makes an obvious impact on migration governance and patterns for any given country, CMIR explicitly focuses on including international relations as strong component to study and advocate for guaranteeing rights of migrant workers and their families.

Name: Affinity Impact
Description: Affinity Impact is the social initiative of a family office based in Asia that deploys both impact investments and philanthropic grants for social impact.

Name: Centro de Recuperação Nova Esperança - CERENE
Twigas'House
Description:
A Swiss non-profit organisation founded by two sisters to build a home for orphans in Gairo, Tanzania in collaboration with a local partner. 

What
We aim to provide a safe and stable environment as well as keys to personal development to 24 orphans in Gairo, Tanzania. In this way we hope to offer them prospects for their future and independence.

How
Together with you and our local partner we aim to have a direct positive impact on children’s livelihood at local level, therefore building and maintaining an orphanage to meet the children’s basic needs on the long term.

Go check www.twigashouse.ch for more information :)
And follow our adventure on social media: @twigashouse

Name: Association Le Cap
Description: Une opération " pas de sexisme dans mon entreprise " avec le soutien du secrétariat chargé de l'égalité entre les hommes et les femmes débute en 2019 à destination des entreprises de PACA et de Corse. Les entreprises et associations employeur.euse.s sont au coeur de l'action. Des actions de formation, sensibilisation sont offertes en contrepartie de la signature d'une charte.

Name: Tous en Selle
Description:
« Tous en Selle » est un centre équestre qui a été pensé et conçu autour du handicap.
Cette association a pour but de permettre au plus grand nombre un égal accès à la pratique de l'équitation et plus particulièrement, de développer l'insertion sociale et professionnelle du public en situation de handicap.
Tous en Selle est donc une association équestre ouverte à tous types de handicap.

Name: Obici Healthcare Foundation
Description:
The Obici Healthcare Foundation is a private, independent entity unrelated to any other health organization. Established in 2006 when the former Louise Obici Memorial Hospital was sold to Sentara Healthcare and coupled with assets of Mr. Obici’s first foundation, the assets of the Obici Healthcare Foundation are over $100 million.

The Foundation is governed by an independent board composed of citizens living in Suffolk and in surrounding communities in the previous service area of the Louise Obici Memorial Hospital.

It is the Foundation’s goal to continue on the philanthropic journey started by Amedeo Obici when he built a hospital to honor his wife’s memory and to serve the healthcare needs of the surrounding community.

The Foundation’s mission today reflects this same desire as we strive to address the unmet needs of the medically indigent and uninsured and to support programs which prevent illness and disease.

Name: Maximashelden
Description:
Kerstmannen op motoren met een missie! 

In 2017 werd onze stichting geboren uit een inspirerend initiatief in Turijn: ‘Babbo Natale in Forma’. Sindsdien organiseren wij elk jaar een bijzondere rit door Utrecht, waarbij motorrijders verkleed als Kerstman of -vrouw kinderen en zorghelden verrassen met een moment van licht en warmte.

Onze missie?
 Liefde, aandacht en donaties brengen aan de échte helden: de kinderen die vechten tegen ziekte.
 Elk jaar zoveel mogelijk geld ophalen voor het activiteitenfonds van het Prinses Máxima Centrum.

Maar we rijden niet alleen voor Máxima – ook andere Utrechtse zorginstellingen doen mee:
 De Hoogstraat Revalidatie
 Diakonessenhuis
 Wilhelmina Kinderziekenhuis (WKZ)
 UMC Utrecht – afdeling kinderpsychiatrie

Tijdens langdurige behandelingen zijn kinderen vaak weken van huis. Een moeilijke, emotionele tijd voor hen en hun families. Dankzij het activiteitenfonds kunnen we bijdragen aan:

 Creatieve workshops
 Evenementen en ontspanning
 Fysieke en emotionele ondersteuning
 Zingeving en afleiding tijdens zware dagen

Alle zorgprofessionals – van verpleging tot specialisten – doen hun uiterste best om het leven van deze kinderen zo draaglijk mogelijk te maken. Wij helpen hen daarbij met onze jaarlijkse kerstactie.

 Dankzij het grote succes van onze stichting zijn wij de inspiratiebron geworden voor andere groepen en fondaties die in navolging van ons soortgelijke initiatieven zijn gestart – in Utrecht en daarbuiten.

 Elk jaar maken wij Utrecht in december warmer, vrolijker en hoopvoller. Doe mee, steun ons of deel onze boodschap!

 Máxima’s Helden – omdat ieder kind recht heeft op kind-zijn, ook tijdens ziekte.

 www.maximashelden.nl
#maximashelden #kerstrit #prinsesmaximacentrum #dehoogstraat #diak #wkz #umcutrecht #motorrijdenvooreenmissie #doneer #vrijwilligerswerk #kinderkanker #inspiratie #samensterk

Name: CHS Legacy Foundation
Description:
Established in 2019 in honor of former Concord High School principal, Gene Connolly, CHS Legacy Foundation works to raise money for a scholarship in his memory for graduating seniors at Concord High School. 

CHS Legacy Foundation aims to create a community for past and present Concord High School students to provide support and resources to help them connect and thrive through the Foundation's new initiative "The Legacy Network".

Name: Michigan Gateway Community Foundation
Description: From our single community beginning in October of 1977, we have evolved into a full service, accredited, and standards compliant Foundation serving Southern Berrien County, focused on Niles and Buchanan, and all of Cass County. Our mission: fostering a community of philanthropy, for good, forever.

Name: Holton's Heroes Nonprofit
Description: Holton's Heroes, a 501(c)(3) not-for-profit charity, provides support, information, and therapy devices and tools for children and their families affected by a post-birth brain injury.

Name: TRIBE Inc.
Description:
TRIBE is a non-profit organization dedicated to the betterment of Black girls. 

We do this by advocating for change and increasing access to sustainable resources for Black girls to not only survive Blackgirlhood, but to thrive in it! 

While excelling and reaching great and new heights, statistics show that Black Women suffer from health disparities, lack of access to education, and political representation at a disproportionate rate - both nationally and globally.

Our mission is to change that narrative and advance the Black Girl Tribe by providing experience, education, and opportunity to live our best lives. 

We believe that as TRIBE succeeds in educating and empowering Black girls to uplift and advocate for one another,

those empowered Black girls can continue to be heroes for the global community.

Find out more about TRIBE
"The empowerment of Black women constitutes the empowerment of our entire community."
-Kimberlé Williams Crenshaw

Name: JDRF Triangle/Eastern NC
Description: Join us and help in the fight to find a cure, prevent, and treat Type 1 Diabetes! JDRF is the largest private funder of Type 1 Diabetes Research in the U.S.  Our chapter was founded in 1996 and since then has raised $26 million through events like our One Walk and Hope Gala. Our chapter also provides outreach support to our local families living with T1D. The chapter territory covers 51 counties in eastern North Carolina, from the Triangle region to the coast.

Name: Tix For Good
Description:
The easiest, most effective new fundraising tool available for nonprofits of any size!
Tix For Good can amplify your organization’s fundraising efforts with a powerful and compelling sweepstakes. We have worked with organizations of all sizes, ranging from local sports teams to chapters of nationally branded nonprofits.

Name: middle.io
Description: Have your ideas, thoughts or challenges heard by the right influencer, mentor or life coach, whilst helping charity.

Name: Adventureful
Description:
We help teens and young adults connect with the transformative power of nature and mindfulness to foster resilience, emotional balance, and a sense of hope for the journey ahead.

We aim to address the growing challenges of mental health while empowering youth through the combination of storytelling, immersive nature experiences and mindfulness practices.

Name: Campbell Foundation
Description: The Campbell Foundation is a private, nonprofit foundation that has been funding research into HIV/AIDS since 1995.  As of 2016, we have given over $10 million in grants to HIV research as well as direct support for other nonprofits that support the HIV+ community and their children on a consistent, daily basis.

Name: Fundación Cione Ruta de la Luz
Description:
La Fundación Cione Ruta de la Luz, tiene como misión mejorar la salud visual y auditiva de personas en riesgo de exclusión social en países en vías de desarrollo y en España.

La Ruta de la Luz surge de la inquietud solidaria de la cooperativa de ópticas Cione. Tras haberse instaurado de forma previa como proyecto, realizando tres expediciones a Mauritania en los años 1995, 1997 y 2000, y contando con un gran equipo de ópticos y oftalmólogos que practicaron más de 9.000 revisiones y entregaron 7.362 gafas graduadas, la Fundación Cione Ruta de la Luz se constituye legalmente el 29 de septiembre del año 2000.

Desde entonces, la Fundación ha viajado a 28 países de América Latina, África y Sudeste asiático y ha visitado 14 ciudades españolas, todo ello para llevar su labor cada vez más lejos.
Location: Las Rozas de Madrid, Comunidad de Madrid

Name: Leadership St. Tammany Alumni Foundation
Description:
It's who you get to know.

Graduates of Leadership St. Tammany reap a unique, invaluable benefit: access to membership in the Leadership St. Tammany Alumni Foundation (LSTAF). Established to help graduates implement and apply the fundamentals learned during the program, LSTAF also opens doors to hundreds of like-minded graduates from previous classes. Several annual Leadership Foundation events make it easy to continue growing long after graduation, like Leadership Revisited, the Membership Drive/Crawfish Boil and our end-of-the-year holiday party. And such networking programs are just a few of the many benefits of LSTAF membership.

Name: ROBERT W. WOODRUFF FOUNDATION, INC.
Description: Robert W. Woodruff Foundation is an independent private foundation.

Name: Numw Al-Khair For Marketing and Project Management
Description: شركة متخصصة في ادارة المشاريع الانسانية تعمل عبر اطلاق سلسلة من الحملات لدعم العطاء والخير حول العالم ايمانا منا بتوفير الرفاه والحياة الكريمة للناس .

Name: Rotaract Club Bern
Description: Rotaract ist die Jugendorganisation in der internationalen Rotary Bewegung, eine weltweite Gemeinschaft von über 176’000 jungen Frauen und Männern zwischen 18 und ca. 35 Jahren. Die einzelnen Rotaract Clubs setzen sich aus Schülern, Studenten, Auszubildenden und Berufstätigen aus allen Berufsgruppen zusammen. Die drei Pfeiler eines jeden Rotaract Club lauten LERNEN – HELFEN – FEIERN. Dies verbindet die einzelnen Rotaracter, verteilt in über 8'400 Clubs in 170 Ländern in allen 5 Kontinenten.

Name: HEROES, Inc.
Description: HEROES, Inc. supports the spouses and children of law enforcement officers, firefighters and federal agents who give their lives in the line of duty to the greater Washington DC community. Since 1964 HEROES has brought together caring members of our city to help support the families of fallen first responders. HEROES’ singular goal is to honor the supreme sacrifice made by law enforcement officers and firefighters and emergency personnel who give their lives in the line of duty while ensuring the safety and well-being of citizens in the greater Washington area. Our support begins within 24 hours of the tragic loss and continues indefinitely.

Name: H4H International
Description: Hearts for the Homeless (H4H) International contributes to the health and well-being of one of the most vulnerable populations in our nation with free heart health screenings, education, and by providing clinical outreach opportunities for students who wish to contribute to our effort.

Name: Pueblito Canada
Description: Pueblito Canada is a well respected international development agency dedicated to providing quality programs for young children in poor communities in Latin America. Working in partnership with local non-governmental organizations, Pueblito provides capacity building and financial assistance to develop culturally appropriate, innovative programs that benefit children and youth directly. Through partnership with teachers, parents and community leaders, the lives of each and every young person in our target communities are changed.

Name: 100+ Women Who Care San Francisco
Description:
Meet 100+ Women Who Care SF, an organization where powerful women come together to serve our community by raising awareness and funds for the Bay Area. We are building this group from the ground up with inspiring females who believe in giving back and helping others, and want you to be part of it!

Through 100+ Women Who Care SF, you will meet new friends, create new professional networks and, most importantly, you’ll make a tangible difference for people in need. Four times a year we come together to hear from nominated organizations, each with a $100 check to donate to a local nonprofit. At the end of our meet-up, we select a cause to receive our collective $10,000.

--- 

As individuals it is difficult to make a very large impact, but as a group we believe we have the ability to contribute to the growth of our community in ways that we never before thought possible.

Fill out our membership form to officially become a member.

Name: The Shepherd Center
Description: The Shepherd Center  is an ecumenical ministry of Rapides parish churches, synagogues and religious organizations.  It is a ministry of last resort whose purpose is to provide temporary aid to individuals and families experiencing a financial crisis.  This includes assistance with food, utilities, rent, shelter, transportation, furniture, clothing vouchers, referrals, advocacy, as well as other essential needs and services.  In addition to its ongoing emergency financial assistance, The Shepherd Center has two programs: Christmas Cheer for Children and the Case Management for Homeless Prevention.

Name: Homaira Rahman Foundation (HRF)
Description:
The Homaira Rahman Foundation (HRF) is a 501c3 non-profit charitable organization dedicated to empowering orphans in Afghanistan through education. 

HRF was established in April 2009 in honor of Ms. Homaira Rahman, who on July 4, 2008, had her very young life tragically cut short. While there is no way to explain this senseless tragedy, both friends and family agreed this foundation was a worthy tribute to honoring her legacy. It is a testament not only to Homaira’s giving nature, but more importantly to her ability of inspiring others to support worthy causes.

• Emphasizing orphanages in areas where distribution of, and access to resources is limited. 

• Securing the resources necessary for a quality education.

• Developing a viable program of assistance for long term sustainability of the educational infrastructure. 

• Facilitating the construction of schools and learning centers. 

• Removing any obstacles to the fulfillment of an orphan's basic physical and psychological needs.

Name: The Campership Fund
Description:
Since 1992, we have sent thousands of campers to the camp of their choice. We have been growing our reach and awareness of what we have to offer every year.

The Campership Fund for Christian Scientists’ mission is to identify, encourage, and financially aid Christian Science Sunday School students to attend the camp for Christian Scientists of their choice, when there is a financial need.

Name: Legacy Youth Organization
Description: Legacy Youth Organization, founded in 2002, is dedicated to providing performing arts, after- school and educational programs to low-income communities. Using our comprehensive understanding of parents needs for quality child care, and enrichment for children, Legacy helps New York City,  and Boston public schools families increase education standards in their communities. Our responsibility is to provide 4 to 18 year old young adults with the programs and activities that instill a sense of responsibility, a sense of belonging, and a positive path for their future.

Name: Meu Bom Amigo
Description:
The Meu Bom Amigo is a project that was created in the beginning of the pandemic in Brazil, with the purpose of helping people feeling lonely or anxious due to the quarantine, specially those in the group at risk, by connecting them on the phone with people who volunteer to talk on a daily basis. 
We're here to remind people who are feeling lonely or forgotten, that they're not alone, and we want to make people feel happier in spite of the challenging times that we are living.

Name: Rotary Club Paris Grenelle
Description:
Fondé en 2019, le RC Paris Grenelle réunit  dans l'amitié des hommes et des femmes de cultures et de professions différentes , partageant la même conception de la vie, du travail et le même idéal de tolérance et de paix dans le but d'agir ensemble pour le bien de la communauté.
Le Club se réunit les 1er et 3ème mercredis de chaque mois , pour les échanges entre membres, l'avancée de leurs actions, la participation à des actions du District 1660 ou du Rotary International, des rencontres avec d'autres Clubs, des conférences.

Name: American Friends of Neve Shalom/Wahat al-Salam
Description:
The American Friends of Neve Shalom/Wahat Al-Salam encourages, supports and publicizes the projects of Neve Shalom/Wahat al-Salam, the “Oasis of Peace.” For more than thirty years, Neve Shalom/Wahat al-Salam has been dedicated to dialogue, cooperation and a genuine and durable peace between Arabs and Jews, Palestinians and Israelis. 
Jewish and Palestinian citizens of Israel have chosen to live and work together as equals in this community to promote trust, understanding and mutual respect well beyond its own borders. At Neve Shalom/Wahat al-Salam, the bilingual, binational, multicultural Primary School, the School for Peace, the Pluralistic Spiritual Center, and other projects, serve the village and beyond reaching thousands of Jewish and Palestinian youth and adults.

Name: YLTrapped Organization Inc.
Description:
Mission Statement: Y.L.T.R.A.P.P.E.D.’s mission is to develop young girls into empowered productive women in society by building strength, knowledge and self-confidence while forming a strong unity within the community, thriving towards success while imparting these attributes to future members.

Vision Statement: Y.L.T.R.A.P.P.E.D.’s vision is to foster opportunities to outstanding young women in urban areas so that they can be extraordinary, independent, and have a sense of purpose, self-awareness and responsibility towards society.

Name: The Stella Society
Description: This Stella Society is a group of women who promote integrity, character, leadership, and sisterhood, through involvement in and service to the campus and community. Members dedicate themselves to the betterment of Trinity College and will support one another in life long achievement.

Name: Melbourne University Greek Association
Description:
A philanthropic club at the University of Melbourne, that aims to build a strong network of Hellenic and Philhellenic students. Furthemore, MUnGA fosters Hellenic traditions and linguistic innovations. Through the club, the committee organises social gatherings, networking events, academic seminars as well as volunteering at local Greek schools and making donations to the wider community. 

In addition to on-campus events, we hold the annual NUGAS Ball , organised by the National Union of Greek Australian Students (NUGAS). NUGAS is the representative body of all major Greek university clubs.

Name: Santa's Knights
Description: Santa's Knights' mission is to bring free martial arts and fitness to everyone, equitably, transcending socioeconomic, racial, and location boundaries, positively changing children's and adults' lives through exposure and lifestyle enhancement. Not only do we create and grow an environment and ecosystem of free martial arts and fitness, but also thousands of children receive thoughtful gifts from our Santa's letters program during Christmastime and throughout the year. For many years our main focus has been Harlem, NYC, but our classes and our mission have been expanding to national and, quite possibly soon, international reach and impact.

Name: ALBONIAN HUMANITARIAN CHARITY
Description:
نحن جمعية غير ربحية، وغير حكومية مستقلة لا تنتمي لأي حزب سياسي، بدأت عملها مع بدء الأزمة الإنسانية في سوريا، تسعى لتلبية حاجات المجتمع الأساسية للسوريين في الداخل السوري وفي بلاد اللجوء، من خلال المشاريع النوعية الإغاثية والتنموية، كما تعمل على رفع المستوى الثقافي والاجتماعي من خلال المشاريع التعليمية الهادفة والبناءة لتحقيق التنمية المستدامة، واستقطاب الكوادر العاملة المخلصة ذات السمعة الحسنة والخبرة في الأعمال الإغاثية إسهاماً في تخفيف المعاناة عن الشعب السوري المنكوب وفي رسم البسمة على شفاه أطفالنا وأهلنا.
والمنظمة مرخصة في الجمهورية التركية برقم: 139 / 198 – 34 ، تاريخ: 29/11/2013م.

We are a non-profit and non-governmental organization that does not belong to any political party, has started its work at the beginning of the humanitarian crisis in Syria. And seeks to meet the basic needs of the Syrian community inside Syria and in the countries of refuge through quality relief and development projects. And also works on raising the cultural and social level through educational projects aimed at achieving sustainable development. And to attract loyal good reputation well experience in relief works teams in order to mitigate the humanitarian suffering of afflicted Syrians and draw the smile on the lips of our children and our people.

Name: Shaking the Tree Interactive Productions
Description: Shaking the Tree Interactive Production is a unique content production company that utilizes professional theater, film, and facilitation to engage individuals and families of wealth to discuss issues such as family dynamics, psychology money, trust, decision-making, and responsibility.

Name: Blackstone Venture Fund
Description:
Blackstone Venture Fund (BVF) is a group of philanthropically-minded professionals directing the disposition of a charitable investment fund using a venture capital model to reward the entrepreneurial spirit of nonprofit organizations.  We make grants to organizations that are seeking to achieve a defined return on investment with requested funds.  In making grants, our focus is on enhancing the long-term sustainability of an organization and/or measurably increasing the impact the organization can have in achieving its mission.  Our fund held at the Omaha Community Foundation consists of donations from our members and local philanthropists who support our approach to charitable giving.

If you are interested in applying for a grant - please see our application for further information.

https://drive.google.com/file/d/1s_h1fXxvDsVQ7rYpFM0lM0nLYfffUkyU/view?usp=sharing

Name: ANE Pediatric Stroke Foundation
Description:
The ANE (Ayanna Noelani Emiko) Pediatric Stroke Foundation is a 501(c)(3) charity whose mission is to increase community awareness regarding pediatric strokes; in addition to providing support services to children and families who have been impacted by this life altering event.  
Support services offered to families and children on behalf of the ANE Pediatric Stroke Foundation shall include bill payment assistance, transportation assistance, meal assistance, sibling care assistance, as well as avenues for emotional and educational resource support.

Name: The Beaverbrook Foundation
Description:
The Beaverbrook Foundation is a UK charity, established in 1954 by the first Lord Beaverbrook. Beaverbrook was a giant of the British 20th Century political scene, who owned the Daily Express newspaper group, and among numerous achievements, sat the War Cabinet in both World Wars.

The Beaverbrook Foundation supports a variety of causes in the United Kingdom and Canada, including preserving heritage buildings and supporting charitable appeals. We have given three lifeboats the British RNLI, and supported over 400 separate charitable bodies in the last fifty years. The Foundation has also been entrusted with various important collections of art, political cartoons and political papers, which we look after for future generations.

Name: Chelsea Hicks Foundation
Description:
The Chelsea Hicks Foundation was created to inspire hope and healing to seriously and critically ill children and their families. 

Jason and Alison Hicks watched their youngest daughter Chelsea go through 22 months of cancer treatment. In doing so, they witnessed the joy and how her spirit and attitude were lifted when she knew her cousins were coming to the hospital to play dress up with her.

Chelsea was able to get other children at the hospital to play dress up with her also. Doctors and nurses would help with dress up/ tea parties in the hallway alcoves. Or even mini parades. Giving them special treats to eat and complimenting them on their creative costumes.

When Chelsea passed away from childhood cancer in February 2009 her family did not want that experience and those memories to be just for them. They thought other families needed those memories for their children as well. Those children battling childhood cancer or another serious illness need something special to do. We created the Chelsea Hicks Foundation to brighten the lives of seriously ill children with programs designed to add fun and create lasting memories for the whole family.

Please join our cause and help us to inspire hope and healing through dress up and play. See how you can donate or help on our donations page on our website.

Name: Scotch & Sharks
Description: Scotch & Sharks is a mens social club focused on building solid relationships while raising money for charity.  Business is not the focus but happens organically.

Name: Journal of Environmental Investing Scholarship Program
Description: Developing and deploying market-based solutions to our most pressing environmental problems requires rigorous interdisciplinary scholarship.  It is critical that we encourage and support young scholars to participate in and contribute to this process.  The Journal of Environmental Investing Scholarship Program,  a public charity under the United States Internal Revenue Code Section 501(c)3, is meant to reward graduate students who write an original, rigorous, and interdisciplinary work of scholarship in a critical area of environmental investing.

Name: Companion Animal Rescue Awards
Description:
The Companion Animal Rescue Awards is a national initiative created by Cathy Beer of Pets4Life. The mission of the awards is to celebrate and recognise inspiring examples of dedication to rehabilitating and rehoming of companion animals Australia wide.  Our Platinum Rescue Hero and Naming Partner is Pet Insurance Australia. 

Thank you to our partners and supporters for getting behind rescue and the inaugural awards.  This year, Jetpets is our Platinum Rescue Hero and naming Partner and the awards is known as the ‘Jetpets Companion Animal Rescue Awards 2018’ or ‘Jetpets Rescue Awards 2018’.

The Jetpets Rescue Awards 2018 was officially launched on Thursday, 14th June at a special event in Sydney.  At the launch, the awards categories and the magnificent seven judges from animal welfare and the pet industry were revealed!

Open for entries 1st July to 31st August 2018.  Enter via www.rescueawards.com.au

Don’t miss out, keep up to date with announcements and more via this page and sign up to the awards newsletter at www.rescueawards.com.au! 

#jetpetsrescueawards2018

Name: re:give
Description: re:give is a non-profit that collects unused or partially used gift cards, and consolidates like cards or reloads uncommon cards to redistribute to community outreach programs. As an environmentally conscious organization, re:give responsibly recycles all gift cards. Join re:give in bettering your local community by sending us your unused or partially used gift cards- visit our website to learn more!

Name: Johan Ferrier Fonds
Description:
Het Johan Ferrier Fonds is opgericht in naam en ter ere van Johan Ferrier, de laatste gouverneur en eerste president van Suriname.

Het fonds weerspiegelt het gedachtengoed van Johan Ferrier en geeft steun aan maatschappij opbouw en cultuur in Suriname, iets dat hij zijn hele leven in de praktijk heeft gebracht en dat het Johan Ferrier Fonds zal voortzetten. 

Doel: Het Johan Ferrier Fonds stelt zich tot doel reeds gestarte, kansrijke projecten en programma’s in Suriname financieel te ondersteunen. Het fonds legt de focus op onderwijs en cultuur en richt zich op zowel jongeren als ouderen, zowel in de steden als in de binnenlanden van Suriname.

U kunt het fonds steunen met een donatie (via iDeal op de website), maar ook al door onze website onder de aandacht te brengen van anderen: uw omgeving, uw netwerk of uw bedrijf, of door bijvoorbeeld te schrijven over het Johan Ferrier Fonds.

Name: Iowa Literacy Council
Description:
Did you know more than 36 million adults struggle with basic literacy, and more than 171,000 adults across Iowa lack high school diplomas. Iowans who lack education or training beyond high school are more likely to be unemployed, working in low-wage jobs, and living in poverty, than those with higher levels of education. 

That's why our goal at the Iowa Literacy Council is to help every Iowan achieve the literacy skills they need to reach their personal and professional goals.

To make that goal a reality, we are here to provide support, advocacy, resources and community connections that enable Iowans to find literacy opportunities near them. Whether you're looking for the nearest literacy programs to improve your reading or you want to join our family of volunteers, we're to make your journey a little easier.

Name: Del Laboratorio al Campo
Description: Somos una corporación sin animo de lucro comprometida con el desarrollo y transformación de comunidades vulnerables mediante la gestión de proyectos enfocados en ciencia, tecnología e innovación que permitan la aplicación, generación y apropiación del conocimiento a través de la pedagogía para fomentar el desarrollo sostenible y mejor calidad de vida.

Name: Flood Defenders
Description:
Flooding is by far the most costly and destructive natural disaster and climate change is making it much worse.  The most vulnerable are those that can least afford to protect themselves.   Governments at local, state, and federal levels are sweeping this problem under the rug - mainly because they aren’t hearing from their constituents.  

It’s time for our government to provide better flood protection. Flood Defenders is a non-profit, non-partisan organization giving a powerful voice to citizens affected by flooding.

We’re a disruptive start-up, built by entrepreneurs passionate about climate.

We have big aspirations and the financial backing to pursue them.

We’re building a world-class team with exceptional talent.

Name: Community Foundation of Dunn County
Description:
The Community Foundation of Dunn County was created in 1995 by a group of community members to promote charitable giving that would create a permanent source of capital to benefit and enrich the quality of life of everyone in the Dunn County area.

At the Community Foundation we help donors turn each and every dollar they give into projects that add energy and vitality to our community. The first dollars that were invested in the Community Foundation in 1995 are still hard at work in our community. That has continued to grow and along the way has supported a nonprofit, a special project, or an education.

Hundreds of individuals, families, businesses and organizations have created charitable legacies by establishing or donating to funds at the Community Foundation of Dunn County. Each fund has its own purpose and history, but all of them gain strength from being managed together. The Community Foundation is honored to serve as the steward of these funds, striving to make our community stronger today and in the future.

Name: Crowd Scholar
Description:
Crowd Scholar is a charity (registration number: 1174788) committed to creating a community-led scholarship programme to help high-achieving students from disadvantaged backgrounds attend university.
 
We strongly believe that exceptional students from disadvantaged backgrounds deserve to be supported, recognised, and encouraged.


http://www.crowdscholar.co.uk/

Name: Freemasonry (Provincial Grand Lodge Southern Division)
Description:
Who are we?

A Freemason is a man who has taken an obligation to uphold our timeless principles of Brotherly love, relief, and truth. Beyond these basics, being a Freemason means so much more. A Freemason is a man who is committed to bettering himself and his community, having taken a solemn vow to help and mentor his Masonic Brothers do the same. A Freemason is a man eager to be part of something bigger than himself, with a reverence for history, compassion in his heart and a desire to create a better future.

Join The Brotherhood Today!

Name: Wheelchairs for Nigeria
Description:
Nigeria has more polio than anywhere in the world. Tens of thousands of children and adults spend their lives crawling on the ground. Tragically, no one is doing anything in a significant way about it. Hundreds of millions of dollars have been given by the Gates Foundation, Rotary International, and others for polio eradication, which is absolutely wonderful. But very little is being done for the victims.

Dr. Ron Rice, a retired Presbyterian pastor, and his Nigerian partner Ayuba Gufwan, who walks on his hands from polio, have built and donated over 8,700 wheelchairs to Nigeria's polio survivors. They have their own shop in Jos, Nigeria, with 27 employees, where they build these 3-wheeled, self-pedaled "tricycles" out of bicycle parts for $150. Five of the employees are handicapped themselves. This is by far the largest wheelchair ministry in all of Nigeria, a country that is half the population of the U.S.

For $150, a child's life is absolutely transformed. Now they can go to school. Now they have hope and a future. Adults can now get vocational training or an apprenticeship, become self-supporting, and not have to resort to begging. Wheelchairs are given to both Christians and Muslims. In a country frequently torn by tribal and religious violence, Christians giving wheelchairs to Muslims is many times a witness for peace and reconciliation. Even employees in the shop are both Christian and Muslim.

Name: Parkinson Society Eastern Ontario
Description:
Parkinson Society Eastern Ontario is the voice of people living with Parkinson's in the region. Our purpose is to ease the burden and find a cure through education, support services, research and advocacy. Every day we work towards our vision: a better life with a brighter future for Canadians living with Parkinson's today. A world without Parkinson's tomorrow.  www.parkinsons.ca  

Parkinson Society Eastern Ontario, incorporated in 1978, is a registered Canadian charity and a non-profit organization dedicated to improving the quality of life of people with Parkinson's and their families in the Eastern Ontario area.  As a regional partner of Parkinson Society Canada, Parkinson Society Eastern Ontario is an integral, active member in this nation-wide federation committed to excellence. Our programs and are evidence-based, delivered by professionals and incorporate the best available knowledge and practice.  

Our office is located at 1 - 200 Colonnade Road South, Ottawa, ON  K2E 7M1and our hours are Monday to Friday 8:00 a.m. - 4:00 p.m. We are here to help so we invite you to drop by for information, browse our resource library, and connect with staff and others who are here to support you and/or your loved one on this journey.

Our legal name is Parkinson Society Eastern Ontario and our charitable registration number is 119080497RR0001.

Name: Amarilla
Description:
Llevamos más de 10 años creando espacios de formación humana, afectiva y emocional para jóvenes, adultos y familias. 

Si quieres llevar alguno de nuestros espacios formativos a tu empresa, colegio o grupo de amigos, estaremos felices de escucharte y construir a tu medida.

Name: Spay-Neuter Services of Indiana, Inc.
Description:
Spay-Neuter Services of Indiana (SNSI) is a statewide financial assistance organization whose mission is to eliminate the euthanasia of healthy and treatable cats and dogs. 

 SNSI provides free spay/neuter surgery certificates to nearly 250 animal welfare organizations in Indiana, and provides $20 surgeries to limited-income Hoosiers. Certificates can be redeemed at any of the 130 vets in our network. 

For every Pet Friendly license plate that is purchased, $25 goes back to SNSI to fund spay/neuter surgeries.  To date, SNSI has funded more than 130,000 surgeries. Many Indiana shelters are experiencing success, with several reporting live release rates above 90%. 

For more information about SNSI, please visit our web site or e-mail us at info@getthemfixed.org

Name: Butler University Dance Marathon
Description:
Butler University Dance Marathon (aka BUDM) is a 12-hour dance marathon that happens every February on Butler University's campus. BUDM is centered around raising money and spreading awareness for pediatric care. Founded in 2003, BUDM is held in honor of Sarah Michelle Cohen each year and has raised $2.7 million for Riley Hospital for Children since then.

BUDM is directed by a 39 member executive team. Participants of BUDM can join as an individual dancer or as a member of one of 17 committees. Members at all levels of the organization work throughout the school year putting on fundraising events, spreading awareness, and preparing for the day of marathon. BUDM 2022  raised $283,245.19 for Riley Hospital for Children!

Name: Monthly Dignity France
Description: Monthly Dignity France is the French branch of a Canadian NGO fighting against period poverty.

Name: Graceful Gears
Description:
Our mission at Graceful Gears is to bring joy and happiness to those individuals with a serious medical condition or special needs by riding in a car of their choice: Exotic, Super, Sports, Luxury or Classic Cars. 

Our motto: "Up-Shifting Your Spirits" 

With the thought of our riders being the ultimate motivation, Graceful Gears strives towards creating the best experience possible for all who attend an event. Events are held at a variety of locations with the goal of "up-shifting" the spirits of as many people as possible. Graceful Gears events consist of registered drivers who will give participants the opportunity to ride in some of the world's nicest brand name cars, all aided by volunteers. Graceful Gears is 501(c)(3) tax exempt organization.

Name: SENSEi India
Description:
A registered society under the name, Pyramid Meditation Center (PMC), SENSEi is a
voluntary, not for profit movement with a vision to enable all to live a Medicine free, Empowered and Joyous Life.
We do this through: 
- Introducing Mindfulness Programs at schools, colleges and corporates and co-create customised course curriculum to execute the program
- Mass Community Outreach initiatives through unplugged music performances at public spaces to spread awareness about Meditation 
- Large scale events under the flagship name “Swa-Anubhav” to enable meaningful dialogue for change and mass meditation along with using art to create awareness.

Name: nycTIES (Group Dissolved)
Description: nycTIES’ mission is to enable young professionals to strengthen their ties to New York City through partnerships with local charities. Members learn the tangible impact of philanthropic giving and develop invaluable leadership skills by serving charities through raising funds, awareness, and applying their personal skills and talents.

Name: Pet Rescue Pilots
Description: Pet Rescue Pilots is a 501(c)3 public charity dedicated to providing life-saving transportation to our pets. We focus on the immediate transportation of pets in overcrowded shelter areas at great risk of euthanasia and move them by air to rescue groups, foster homes and adoptive families who can take them in.

Name: Hope Sports
Description: Hope Sports is devoted to a two-pronged mission: 1) To engage professional and olympic athletes in community service, providing them with an opportunity to connect meaningfully to a world beyond their athletic career, and 2) To build homes for low-income families in third world countries. We join our two major themes by bringing together teams of active and recently retired professional athletes who provide the manual labor to build homes for the poor. Through their collective efforts, individual athletes and sports teams collaborate on projects that inspire them live with a greater purpose and heart of service.

Name: Enfants du Laos - Association Humanitaire
Description:
Notre Association Humanitaire au Laos

Enfants du Laos a pour but de développer l’accès à l’éducation dans les ethnies du pays pour permettre aux enfants d’avoir un meilleur avenir par leurs propres moyens.

Ces villages manquent de ressources et de fournitures scolaires, c’est pourquoi nous nous efforçons de développer des infrastructures et y instaurons des systèmes d'éducation en jumelage avec des écoles à l'international.

Nos objectifs sur le long terme sont de fournir une aide durable et une autonomie aux familles pour contribuer à l'éducation de leurs enfants et leur assurer un avenir professionnel plus serein.

Name: The Paul & Phyllis Fireman Charitable Foundation
Description:
The Paul & Phyllis Fireman Charitable Foundation brings entrepreneurial vision and resources to support transformational change in communities where the family lives and works.

The Foundation was created by Paul Fireman, former CEO of Reebok International, and his wife Phyllis, after Reebok became a public company in 1985.

Name: Grateful Nation Project
Description: Our mission is to serve those who served—to meet veterans wherever they are in life and show them the love of Jesus Christ through acts of service. We strive to show appreciation to our heroes by providing support, friendship, and love. Through every interaction, we focus on increasing their quality of life and having a positive impact on their future.

Name: Temabex Inc
Description: Notre entreprise, sans but lucratif, s'est donné la mission de favoriser l’intégration des personnes ayant des limitations dans un environnement de travail sain.

Name: Sakidan Charity Association
Description: Sakidan supports good causes and positive change all over the world by actively contributing and trying to make a difference. We believe in empowering the people we help to build sustainable results.

Name: Rotaract Club of Central Calcutta Youth
Association sportive solidaire et culturelle Cartigny Oran
Description:
Association roubaisienne divisée en trois pôles :
