- Le pôle SOLIDAIRE : aide aux plus démunis et aux SDF via des maraudes mensuelles dans la métropole lilloise

- Le pôle CULTUREL : intervention de sensibilisation et organisation d’évènements dans des structures sociales à destination des jeunes

- Le pôle SPORTIF : création (prochainement) d’une Kid's academy de futsal dans les quartiers nord de Roubaix

Name: Rollins College Bonner Leaders Program
Description:
Developed by the Bonner Foundation, a national philanthropic organization based in Princeton, New Jersey, the Bonner Leaders Program works to transform not only the students who are directly supported by the program, but also the campus and community in which they serve and learn.

The Rollins College Bonner Leaders Program is a cohort-based community service program that pairs undergraduate students in our Liberal Arts Program with local nonprofit organizations in an intensive and sustained manner.

Name: Refugee Strong
Description: Refugee Strong is a 501(c)(3) organization committed to empowering refugee communities by providing support and hope through education. We strive to help students living in refugee camps achieve their educational dreams by providing the resources needed to succeed and the inspiration to embolden.

Name: Nellie Philanthropy Foundation
Description:
Founded in 2001 as the “Points to a Dream” Foundation, the Nellie Philanthropy Foundation (NPF) has expanded and grown into one of the most responsive grassroots foundations in Canada.

The NPF is a Montreal-based Canadian charitable public foundation, committed to making a difference in Montreal, Quebec, Canada and around the world. Our main focus is to provide help to those who are less fortunate, whether they are underprivileged children, the homeless, seniors, or pregnant and lactating women, to name a few, although we have many programs that target the general public as well. Many donors would like to donate, but are unaware of the ways to do so. In light of this, the aim of the NPF is multi-faceted, with the overriding goal to facilitate and encourage charitable giving for donors and recipients alike.

We believe strongly in food security and summer camp programs for children in all communities regardless of race, religion, colour or sexual orientation.

The NPF is an incubator and aggregator of other charitable programs, call it the “Berkshire Hathaway” of philanthropic programs. We aim to democratize and remove the complexity of being involved in philanthropy. The NPF specializes in the concept of “Flash Philanthropy” or “Citizen Philanthropy”. Like the notion of “Citizen Science”, “Citizen Philanthropy” hopes to mobilize on the desire of donors to do good on their own, expeditiously, through their personal initiative, to be involved in charitable projects, whether they be “one-offs” or ongoing efforts without being bogged down by the constraints of larger charitable organizations. Have a charitable idea? We want to hear it (please email us), allow us to be your incubator for innovative philanthropic ventures.

Name: La Fâbrica
Description:
We contribute to the improvement of the status quo, supporting adolescents in search of better horizons and in need of moral support; young professionals who require coaching to advance their careers and their training and inclusion in the workplace; as well as animals that need means to maintain or improve their quality of life.

Born in early  2021, in the face of the challenge of demotivation and hopelessness observed in different countries of the world as a result of wars, pandemics and effects of climate change on society.
The growing gap between developed and developing countries, the war conflicts experienced by many nations, as well as environmental causes are favoring the flight of people to places with expectations of social and economic development are more favorable.  In societies with greater economic growth, we see how the lack of social and family support networks; in some cases, lack of knowledge of the language, difficulties in accessing a job, housing or health care; racism and xenophobia tend to despair young people and diminish their desire to contribute positively to their ecosystems.
Additionally, in times of crisis where it is difficult to maintain livelihoods in humans, domestic animals are abandoned, stray animals mistreated and, in many cases, wild animals are captivated and exploited.

So instead of just donating funds to mitigate these challenges, why not start now & help avoid them at an earlier stage?
La Fâbrica is a space where we look for ways to help and connect resources with needs, helping with small changes, to create different futures for hundreds of individuals across geographies. 

Our Values:

Solidarity:  respect and help towards the populations that suffer most from the problems of inequality.  Access to a "dignified life" for all/all people, especially those from impoverished countries 
Ethics: transparency & good work within the framework of a non-profit organization
Professionalism: commitment to the populations we serve

Name: Matan - United way Israel
Description: Matan–Investing in the Community (United Way Israel), the leading Israeli nonprofit organization forging corporate-community partnerships and effective community investments

Name: Creand Fundació
Description:
Creand Fundació va néixer el 15 de desembre de 1987 amb la creació d'un programa de beques pensades per oferir als joves del país la possibilitat de formar-se. Des de llavors, Creand Fundació ha anat creixent fins esdevenir una de les principals fundacions privades del país, tant en nombre de programes que desenvolupa com de recursos que hi destina.

Creand Fundació actua en tres àmbits: el social, el cultural i l'educatiu. En cadascun d'aquests camps es duen a terme diferents activitats que pretenen ajudar les persones perquè tinguin una millor qualitat de vida i un desenvolupament personal.

Coneix de primera mà què fa Creand Fundació per a les persones del país i descobreix què és el que pot fer per a tu.
Location: Andorra la Vella, Andorra la Vella

Name: The Josh Fund
Description:
Facilitate and support access and opportunity to the world of music for aspiring musicians and in doing so honor the memory of Josh Markowitz: musician, composer, producer, and entrepreneur. 

Josh believed that music is transformative, empowering the lives of youth, turning dreams into reality. To honor this belief, family & friends have established a fund to support and nourish aspiring young musicians. 

By appreciating music that came from all over the world, Josh understood that his own music could be sent out and shared all over the world. He believed aspiring, talented artists shouldn’t find their reach limited by their circumstances. That belief, and now that legacy, will live on forever in support of musical inspiration and the unhindered chance to apply and share it.

Financial support will be provided in the form of a scholarship or scholarships to qualified students pursuing a Bachelor of Music degree and entering the final years of study at the University of the Witwatersrand School.

Separately, additional financial support in the form of mini grants will be considered, empowering aspiring artists to share their music with the world.

Name: Citizen Diplomacy International
Description: Citizen Diplomacy International (CDI) is a nonprofit, nonpartisan organization dedicated to promoting local, sustainable, and positive change around the world through people to people empowerment.

Name: The Friese Foundation
Description:
The Friese Foundation is a nonprofit organization inspired by Donald Friese Sr.’s life-long commitment to improving the lives of those around him. Just as Mr. Friese has described himself as being “in the business of people,” the Foundation is committed to providing impactful and life-changing opportunities, as well as promoting healing and wellness for underserved individuals and communities. By partnering with charities that share these goals, we hope to bridge the gap from hopelessness to wholeness.

The Friese Foundation supports organizations that help low-income families, unhoused individuals, and other communities in need. These include programs serving veterans; youth programs and education assistance; animal rescue and welfare organizations; and organizations that provide physical and mental health services.

Name: People's Liberty
Description:
People’s Liberty is a philanthropic lab that brings together civic minded talent to address challenges and uncover
opportunities to accelerate the positive transformation of Greater Cincinnati.

Name: Pyro.Solutions
Description:
Founded by financial analysts and prospect researchers, Pyro.Solutions is dedicated to bringing new wealth analysis technology to philanthropy. They have created three new resources for the sector:

> Code-fi - Global Wealth Analytics Platform
> Orbit. - UK Wealth Intelligence Database *NEW*
> Nova - UK Wealth Screening

Name: Integral European Conference (IEC)
Description:
Held every two years on the European continent, IECs are not only a huge social catalyst where international integral collaborations begin, enabling our human capital to grow, they also have a mission based on integral principles.

IEC1 was in 2014, IEC2 in 2016, IEC3 in 2018, and IEC4 will be in 2022. In between these big IEC-s there were interval conferences called IEC Regatherings, in May 2017 and May 2019. Due to the global pandemic IEC 4 was postponed from 2020 to 2022 while the first Online Global IEC was held in May 2020 and the second in May 2021.  

Hopefully, the global integral community will gather in person in Hungary this year. Come and join!

Name: Palamedes Partners
Description: Palamedes Partners is a free, confidential, student-run startup advisement group for professors and students in the STEM field. If you have an idea, no matter the stage of development, we can provide complimentary actionable insight to help you take your idea to the next level. Please send us a quick note describing your idea, and our partners would love to talk with you more about how we could be of service to you and your team

Name: The Up and Under Foundation
Description:
The Up and Under Foundation was set up to ensure that wherever possible financial restraints did not prevent any willing young person or groups of young people from experiencing all that the great outdoors can offer to broaden and enrich their life experiences.

Each year we aim to support as many people as our funds allow. We rely on public donations to carry out our work.  All our team are volunteers.

Name: Charity Association Ajlan - الجمعية الخيرية عجلان
Description: مؤسسة خيرية إجتماعية غير ربحية تساعد في إغاثة الفقراء و المحتاجين وفق اهداف محددة و عبر مجموعة من المختصين و رؤيتها العمل على تحويل الاسر المحتاجة إلى اسر منتجة وفق برامج تأهيلية تحقق لهم الأكتفاء الذاتي.

Name: Foundation Participatory and Action Research Centre Uma Kiwe
Description: Participatory and Action Research Centre Uma Kiwe is a non-governmental, non-profit organization, regardless of social status, politics, race, or religion. Its objective is to generate, analyze, and share diagnoses and interventions of factors associated with the causes and effects of social behaviors with an interdisciplinary approach, based on the philosophy of community-based participatory research.

Name: SERGEANT AUDIE MURPHY ASSOCIATION
Description: The Sergeant Audie Murphy Association is a private 501(c)(3) dedicated to the enrichment of the communities we serve.

Name: Startup Weekend Lajeado
Description:
O Startup Weekend é um evento de imersão, uma experiência única onde empreendedores e aspirantes a empreendedores podem descobrir se suas ideias de startups são viáveis. Venha compartilhar ideias, formar equipes e lançar startups.

Todos os eventos do Startup Weekend seguem o mesmo modelo básico: qualquer pessoa é bem vinda para expor a sua ideia de startup e receber feedback de outros participantes. São formadas equipes em torno das melhores ideias (determinadas por votação) e a partir daí são 54 horas de criação de modelos de negócios, programação, design e validação de mercado. O fim de semana termina com a apresentação dos projetos a empreendedores de sucesso em uma nova oportunidade para receber feedback.

O evento contará com a participação de mentores, jurados, mídia, oradores, investidores e muito mais. Prepare-se para o melhor fim de semana de sua vida!

Name: Mulheres Emigrantes Unidas
Description:
Uma organização idealizada por mulheres imigrantes e para mulheres, a fim de atender a comunidade de mulheres e meninas migrantes que se encontram no Brasil, inicialmente em Curitiba. O objetivo é permitir que as migrantes gozem de seus direitos e se sintam empoderadas, apesar das dificuldades de estarem em um país estrangeiro. Queremos garantir direitos de proteção, vida, saúde física e mental, estudos, empregos, entre outros, contemplando a mulher migrante em todos os âmbitos.
Precisamos de sua ajuda, entre em contato conosco pelo telefone: 42985478200 para mais informações.

Name: The Llama Sanctuary
Description: The Llama Sanctuary aims to provide a long term home for any llama in need of assistance on the mainland of Britain where the current owners are no longer capable of providing adequate levels of care for their animal(s) due to health, finances, or any other reason.

Name: Aavanee
Description:
Aavanee is a non-profit  501(c)(3) charitable organization located in Clarksburg and Frederick, Maryland. We are focused on helping women, seniors and families, to make the world a better and happier place.
With help from the City of Frederick, Frederick County, Montgomery County, Community Foundation of Frederick County, Women’s Goving Circle and Collaborators (Minnodi LLC, FCC, ADG, PTA) and other generous donors we organize community-building events such as Women's Project (Basic IT training, HR Training), STEM for Kids and Senior Networking programs, fundraisers and in-depth training sessions for the community. If you are interested in participating in one of these events, please check our schedule and upcoming events.

Name: Minnesota Urology Foundation
Description: We provide opportunities and programming to increase conversation, knowledge, treatment, and prevention of urologic conditions.

Name: Mother & Child Education Center
Description:
Connecting, Educating, & Supporting Parents from Pregnancy through Toddler Stage with Services & Supplies.  

No cost services - with no income or demographic requirement - to best serve parents in need in greater Portland & surrounding areas, in the nurturing of their children.

Name: Burn Advocates Network Ltd.
Description:
A non-profit organization based in Teaneck, New Jersey dedicated to helping pediatric and veteran burn survivors all over the country and other parts of the world.  We provide volunteers and medical supplies to burn centers in the U.S., Haiti, and Israel, as well as fund and run burn camps internationally.

The mission of the Burn Advocates Network is to support burn survivors - whether they are children, young men and women who are wounded in combat, or the elderly - as they face the challenges of recovery, rehabilitation, and reintegration.

Our current work includes building the first burn center in Haiti, the Sababa Israel Burn Camp, support for US burn veterans, and burn camp music program.  Visit our website for more information and to find out how you can help. We are always in need of contributions and volunteers!

Name: Nurturing God's Treasures (NGT)
Description:
Mission:
to raise the genius in the African child by imbibing the right mentality, developing their individual talents and skills, and tasking their thought patterns.

Vision:
a future of well skilled African children who see possibilities when others see impossibilities and are first to be reckoned with round the world, highly incomparable to their peers.

Name: Forum Foundation
Description:
Forum Foundation is a non-profit organization supporting community development in rural Panama.

Since 2006, the Foundation has worked with community leaders in the mountains of Coclé Province to define the core needs of rural areas and to develop the most practical means of meeting those needs. Paramount among these are the expansion and improvement of secondary schools and public health centers in the region.

We began by providing uniforms and computers to schools as well as transportation for students who could not otherwise attend because of distant commutes. By the end of 2013, over 9,000 students had benefited from Forum Foundation programs. Aid had expanded from uniforms, computers, and buses to building new schools, remodeling classrooms, creating science labs, constructing dormitories, installing solar panels, renovating kitchens, and offering meal assistance scholarships.

In 2014, Forum Foundation opened its own Forum Academy (Academia Forum), a tuition-free private boarding school in Chiguiri Arriba. Forum Academy offers a three-year program devoted to fulfilling the Panamanian Ministry of Education (MEDUCA) requirements for high school graduation, and in 2015 became the country's first International Baccalaureate (IB) World School operating outside of Panama City. Forum Academy has since graduated two classes from its IB Diploma Program. The Academy’s mission is to ensure that these rural students have access to the education they need to succeed. Computer technology, sciences, and English are at the center of the school’s rigorous curriculum, designed to prepare its students for professional careers in Panama’s growing economy.

----

We believe in the importance of working together with the community leaders, educators, and parents where projects take root. Our goal is to provide the tools that every community can use to build a more prosperous future.

Name: Student2Scholar
Description: Formed in December 2020, Student2Scholar strives to be the one-stop shop for postsecondary-minded high school students in search of scholarship resources in the Greater Richmond Area. Our resources include individual and group scholarship coaching, a community scholarship online database and in-person fair, and our own community-funded scholarships.

Name: Part'âgé - Projet GATE
Description: Après le succès de ses fondateurs, Part'âgé repart cette année avec une nouvelle équipe encore plus motivée pour rapprocher nos aînés du numérique. Ce projet étudiant, maintenant fort d'une année d'expérience, vise à favoriser l'inclusion numérique des personnes âgées. La pièce maitresse de notre approche est la délivrance de formation au local de l'Union des Retraités d'Evry. En nous adaptant au niveau de chacun, nous permettons à ceux qui réclament notre aide de comprendre et de maîtriser les outils du numérique devenus aujourd'hui si indispensables. Plus que cela, nous cherchons cette année encore à équiper ceux qui en ont besoin de matériel numérique. Ainsi, si vous êtes au sein d'une entreprise ou d'un groupe qui souhaite participer à notre lutte pour le bien commun, n'hésitez pas à nous contacter sur cette page Linkedin ou par mail à gate.partage@gmail.com pour faire n'importe quelle donation ou bien à donner de l'argent sur notre cagnotte : https://www.helloasso.com/associations/gate-part-age.

Name: The Champions Club
Description: The Champions Club was founded in 2019 with one mission, to help people meet people. Our world has become dominated by technology both for the better and the worst regarding the welfare of humanity. We strive to create events and opportunities for people to meet and develop relationships which we believe will lead to strong communities and local economies.

Name: Anna Nabuh Foundation
Description: The ANNA NABUH Foundation VISION is; empowering vulnerable girl child into women of value. It's MISSION is via education, advocate against domestic violence, voice for the voiceless and self-development programs that put SMILE on the vulnerable.

Name: ALIMA - The Alliance for International Medical Action
Description: ALIMA - The Alliance for International Medical Action is a philanthropy company based out of Route de l'Aeroport, Rue NG 96, Dakar, Senegal.

Name: Samea Cambodge
Description:
Chaque enfant a le droit à une véritable égalité des chances dans la vie (UNICEF).
Samea Cambodge est une association humanitaire à but non lucratif visant à l'instauration d'une meilleure qualité de vie pour tous au Cambodge.  Les membres de l'association sont tous étudiants de l’ESME Sudria, école d'ingénieurs située à Lyon.
Cette année, notre mission porte sur l'accès à l'éducation. Elle se compose de deux parties et a été élaborée en collaboration avec Codeau Khmer, une ONG cambodgienne reconnue.
Dans un premier temps, nous avons décidé d'agir dans le village de Samrong situé au nord du Cambodge. Nous souhaitions y rénover deux salles de classe et installer des sanitaires dans l'école. 
Dans un deuxième temps, nous agirons pour l'université de Battambang. Nous souhaitons rendre cette université de province plus attractive aux yeux des étudiants cambodgiens. Notre action portera sur les cursus génie éléctrique et français. Nous allons participer à la construction d'un laboratoire et à l'élaboration du programme du cursus génie éléctrique. Pour le cursus français, nous souhaitons aménager un espace bibliothèque au sein de l'université et le fournir d'ouvrages, de films, de CD. 

Nous vous invitons à consulter notre site web pour en savoir plus sur notre philosophie et notre mission !

Name: Fondation Térrevent
Description:
La Fondation Terrévent a pour mission d’apporter aide et soutien en travaillant activement avec des entrepreneurs sociaux, ONG et associations dans les domaines suivants:

Handicaps
Insertion, intégration et retour dans la société

Racines
recherche et entretien de nos racines qu’elles soient culturelles, historiques, linguistiques ou autres, tout en préservant leur richesse et diversité

Musique
améliorer par la musique la qualité de vie, les échanges, l’éducation

Passion
soutien et promotion de jeunes talents dans les domaines les plus divers

Name: For Thuy and Thu
Description: For Thuy ans Thu est une association humanitaire créée en septembre 2017 et reconnue d'interêt général. Elle a pour but de subvenir aux besoins en termes d’éducation, de santé et d’hygiène de vie à d'enfants vietnamiens.

Name: Fundación SiFuturo
Description:
La fundación SiFuturo se constituyó en junio del 2002, por un grupo de profesionales de la salud, todos profesores universitarios, quienes motivados por la amenaza a la continuidad del programa de atención de la Universidad de Antioquia y el Hospital Infantil de Medellín -  por la carencia de verdaderos programas de atención integral para los niños con infección VIH / Sida en ese momento en la ciudad y las dificultades que surgían para ellos con la implementación de la ley 100 de 1993. Así se origina la necesidad de crear una Fundación que ayude a mitigar los problemas de estos niños y sus familias y que vele por sus necesidades y derechos.

La personería jurídica No 564 de la entidad fue otorgada en mayo del 2003 por el Instituto Colombiano de Bienestar Familiar.  

Estamos afiliados a la Federación Antioqueña de ONG, adherimos a la Red de ONG por la Transparencia y simpatizantes de Pacientes Colombia. Hacemos parte del Mecanismo Social de Apoyo y Control de VIH,  la Mesa Nacional de Niños y Jóvenes viviendo con VIH y del Comité Intersectorial de VIH en Antioquia.

Nuestro principal aliado es el Hospital La María de Medellín, entidad que nos acoge desde nuestros inicios.

Name: GduCoeur
Description:
GduCoeur est l’association humanitaire de la Paris School of Business, grâce à ses nombreux pôles, elle permet à chacun de trouver une cause à défendre.

Nos actions  sont diverses et variées, auprès des personnes handicapées, des enfants dans le besoin, des retraités, des animaux et de l’environnement.

GduCœur représente l’association à but caritatif, de la Paris School of Business. Grâce à ses nombreux pôles, elle propose et permet aux élèves de pouvoir s’investir dans des projets humanitaires à différentes échelles, aussi bien sur le campus, que sur le plan national ou international.

Les étudiants, peuvent donc grâce à cette association, s’investir dans de nombreuses actions tel que la sensibilisation des élèves, des collectes de fonds, l’organisation de journées d’animations au sein du campus ou bien encore des voyages humanitaires à travers le monde.

Name: The Giving Crowd
Description:
The Giving Crowd is a movement of people connected by the idea that, moving together, we create a more generous world. Working alongside churches, organizations and individual donors, we serve with a fresh and impartial set of eyes.

Joined by a common mission, people discover that they are capable of making a greater impact than they ever imagined during and beyond their lifetime. They become catalysts for tangible movements within the organizations they support….organizations just like yours!

Name: Foundation for KCH
Description:
Why leave a legacy?
At the Foundation for Knox Community Hospital, we want to ensure that future generations have quality healthcare close to home.

Legacy means building charitable partnerships with caring individuals and committed businesses. It means recruiting the best medical teams possible and purchasing state-of-art health technologies that facilitate quick and effective healing. There are many great communities and organizations, but only one Knox Community Hospital. Your partnership with the Foundation is part of a vital legacy that transforms lives.

If you would like to have your name added to those who support our growing hospital, please give us a call at 740.326.3366 or click here.

Name: Team Humanity
Description:
Team Humanity is a not-for-profit humanitarian organization that was started in 2015 on the Greek island of Lesvos in the Mediterranean Sea to provide aid to refugees. 

We are committed to providing humanitarian and educational outreach to vulnerable communities in the US and abroad by responding to major emergencies, fighting disease and poverty, as well as nurturing and promoting human welfare.

Team Humanity envisions a world where we seek to alleviate human suffering by advocating for basic human rights, promoting human welfare, and creating opportunities for communities in despair.

Name: Quadrans consultants
Description: Quadrans consultants specializes in applying for subsidies and funds with focus on the social domain, nature conservation projects and international development.

Name: Youth Colombian Leaders
Description:
Youth Colombian Leaders es el grupo de Juventud integrado por jóvenes embajadores y jóvenes líderes colombianos.

Buscamos fortalecer y promover el liderazgo y participación cívica en el país a través de proyectos realizados por líderes voluntarios de distintas organizaciones.

Name: Little Rascals Foundation
Description: The Little Rascals Foundation is supporting children with disabilities and their families. This is something that we are very passionate about and we will strive to make a difference for them. This is the story behind what led us to set up the foundation and what we hope to achieve.

Name: The Survivor Project
Description:
The Survivor Project is an organisation founded in January 2019, with a goal to tackle the issue of sexual assault with a specific focus on Lagos, Nigeria. As our organization has grown, we have held fundraisers in Lagos and London and taken on a number of new projects. These all aim to decrease the instances of sexual violence in Nigeria or support survivors of sexual violence. The establishment of this group was triggered by the realisation of the extent to which sexual violence has affected our society, as well as the growing rape culture in our communities. 

As a group we aim to challenge the aspects of Nigerian culture and the pillars in Nigerian society that enable sexual violence and perpetuate the silencing of victims. We also aim to work with sexual assault reference centers and individual organisations to ensure that survivors of sexual violence and people who find themselves at risk of sexual violence have access to the available aid resources. Additionally we want to make anti-violence education, specifically anti-sexual violence, a part of primary and secondary education across Niggeria. We aim to increase proactive initiatives against sexual violence as there are already several existing reactive initiatives.

Name: LE ROSEY FOUNDATION
Description: Another lighting on education. Le Rosey Foundation helps related teaching projects come to life.

Name: Impulsora de la Cultura y de las Artes
Description: Institución de Asistencia Privada con el propósito de desarrollar programas y obras de educación, arte y cultura en el municipio de Ahome para contribuir a mejorar y elevar su nivel y calidad de vida.

Name: Help for Defense Haitian Children
Description: HDHC is a non-profit organization in Haiti which aims to help the children.

Name: Polio Survivors Fund
Description: We are an association set up to support children who are newly affected by lifelong polio paralysis.

Name: NSU Public Health and Sciences Club
Description: NSU Public Health and Sciences Club (PHSC) is a graduate and undergraduate level student organization; an extra co-curricular club. PHSC of NSU works towards promoting the health and wellbeing of the NSU community and beyond. In addition, the club focuses on developing public health professionals with exposure to leadership qualities. Since its’ inception in 2006, Public Health and Sciences Club has been pursuing its’ motto to develop a healthy community.

Name: Reconnect
Description:
Reconnect is a new ‘pay it forward’ style volunteer scheme in London, modelled on the successful French project “La Carillon”. Businesses across the city can now provide services free of charge to homeless people with Reconnect providing free stickers, vouchers, support and advice.

It is entirely a volunteer-led initiative aiming at re-establishing social ties in the community. We're now building a network of local businesses that provide free or low-cost services to people experiencing homelessness.

Name: Leo Club Bordeaux International
Description:
Le Leo club Bordeaux international est un club service (filleul du Lions Club International) qui rassemble des jeunes de 18 à 32 ans de toutes origines, sans distinction sociale, professionnelle ou religieuse, qui s’engagent bénévolement pour le bien de la cité.

Le LIONS Club est le plus gros réseau mondial de professionnels (1,4 million de membres). Visées HUMANISTES au sens large : tout ce qui peut aider son prochain est une possibilité d'action à mener. 

Quelques exemples : 
- au niveau INTERNATIONAL: lutte contre la ROUGEOLE

- au niveau NATIONAL: récupération des LUNETTES usagées pour les reconditionner puis les redistribuer en Afrique pour munir ceux qui n'auraient jamais pu s'en procurer

- au niveau LOCAL : dîner dans le noir pour sensibiliser à la cécité. 

Nous apportons notre aide et notre soutien aux grandes causes (Téléthon, Banque Alimentaire…) mais nous contribuons également à des actions de proximité dans les domaines caritatifs et humanitaires.

Le Leo club de Bordeaux c’est:
- La participation à des événements caritatifs tout le long de l’année
- La possibilité de proposer son propre projet

Si les valeurs entraide, solidarité, amitié et partage ont un sens pour toi alors rejoins-nous !

Name: Dream Team Des Moines
Description:
We provide young adults with opportunities to build confidence, set goals, and create a healthy lifestyle through mentor-based interactions centered around bicycling.

In March, a team of 50-60 teenagers start their 5 month journey to RAGBRAI, a week-long bike ride across Iowa that occurs each July.  The riders work with caring volunteers to set goals, work hard, and DREAM BIG!

The bicycle is used as a tool to inspire and motivate young people, encourage lifelong wellness, and build confidence through accomplishment.  Completing a 400+ mile bike ride across the state gives the participant a tangible sense of achievement, as well as a framework for setting and achieving life goals.

The Dream Team makes the seemingly impossible, possible.  Learn more at dreamteamdesmoines.org!

Name: GAIA International
Description:
GAIA International is a 501c3 tax-exempt organization, founded in 2013, by students at Arizona State University.  Our focus is on creating long lasting partnerships with communities in developing countries to help rural farmers mitigate the effects of poverty, hunger and malnutrition through the use of biochar and other sustainable agricultural practices. 

Our goal is to improve the lives of those at the bottom of the pyramid, through agriculture, by providing them with the tools and knowledge to empower themselves. 

At GAIA International we understand that the success in agriculture depends largely on the fertility of the soil. Therefore, our methods are directed at helping farmers to improve soil quality in order to increase crop yields. We do this through teaching our farmers to make biochar.  Biochar is charcoal (carbon) produced when organic matter is burned at relatively low temperatures in the absence of oxygen. Biochar improves the soil by acting like a sponge, retaining plant nutrients and soil moisture. This improved soil quality results in higher crop yields. In fact, research has shown that when charcoal and fertilizers are combined, it can yield as much as 880 percent more than when fertilizers are used by themselves (University of Bayreuth, 2004).   This is life changing for people who barely exist on subsistence farming. 

Our process works because it is simple, sustainable, reliable, affordable, replicable, but most importantly…centered on using local resources. It provides long lasting fertility and durability due to its high levels of organic carbon, which can last in the soil for thousands of years.  

We envision moving these farmers from subsistence to surplus, where they move from merely feeding their families to becoming business owners, selling their produce to market.  Our hope is to one day see a world that is free of hunger, poverty and ecological degradation.

http://www.linkedin.com/in/gaiainternational

Located on 1255 acres of prime farmland, the property is ideally suited for extensive educational programming and agricultural research.

Name: My Dog Eats First
Description:
My Dog Eats First, Inc. is a grassroots non-profit based in Louisville, KY. Our mission is to provide free pet food, supplies, vaccinations and spay/neuter services for the pets of the homeless and underserved within our community. We provide this support in a judgement free environment for individuals and families in who are committed to the health and wellness of their pets and desire to keep them. In doing so, we believe we are:

1. Keeping the homeless and underserved united with what in most cases is the only living thing that provides with them with companionship, protection and unconditional love

2. Reducing the amount of healthy pets surrendered to shelters because of financial burden and ultimately

3. Saving healthy pets from unnecessary euthanasia by keeping them with their families.

We will always welcome all people, no matter what their station in life may be or what personal challenges they face. We believe in the love and companionship that these pets bring to their underserved and homeless owners. 

Everyone deserves someone that makes them look forward to tomorrow.

Description: The Sunshine Foundation was first launched in 1989 in California. Patricia Lapan wanted to help students who may not qualify for scholarships but had the drive and determination to succeed.

The goal of Arrive is to bring volunteers and awareness, action and advancement to impoverished regions in the world by providing shelter, promoting good health, offering access to education and giving at risk children tools and opportunities for a successful, independent, and fulfilling life.

free of charge.

Name: Niños en Alegria A.C.
Description:
Desde 2003, ha logrado construir 11 escuelas primarias en zonas marginadas de Acapulco Gro, beneficiando directamente a más de 3,200 niños y a sus familiares; asimismo, en colaboración con UNICEF ha operado el programa “Todos a la Escuela” en Guerrero, por medio del cual se combate el rezago educativo.

NEA es la única asociación de la sociedad civil que opera en Guerrero dedicada a la construcción de infraestructura educativa. El contexto socio-económico donde operamos, son las colonias, barrios y localidades de mayor marginación en los municipios de Acapulco de Juárez y Coyuca de Benítez.
Gracias a la focalización de nuestras intervenciones, el 81.18 % nuestros egresos llegan a nuestros beneficiarios de manera muy concreta.
Los resultados de nuestras intervenciones en escuelas son muy visibles y responden a las necesidades que impacten en el mejoramiento de las condiciones educativas de nuestros beneficiarios.

Name: onya.
Description:
We believe that charitable giving should be quick, easy and effective. Our platform takes the hassle out of philanthropy by automating your giving, and embedding it painlessly into your everyday life.

onya. started as an ambitious idea on a lazy Saturday arvo in our sharehouse. All three of us are uni students, but we didn’t want to wait until graduation to start making an impact. So we decided we would try to reshape Australian philanthropy. Unlikely? Perhaps.

We’ve launched round-ups as our first giving method but we’ve got big plans ahead. Carbon offsetting fuel purchases, income pledges, donating $2 for every goal your team kicks, 5 bucks for every time Bob Katter mentions crocodiles - whatever you are keen on, we’ll do our best to make it happen.

For now, join us as we try to do our bit to change the world.

Name: VASOS2021
Description:
A VASOS é uma Plataforma de Impacto, totalmente sem fins lucrativos, agregadora de vontades: a vontade de dar e a vontade de receber!

Movidos pela filantropia e pelas ações pro-bono, estabelecemos de forma rápida e à distância de um clique vasos comunicantes entre Entidades da Economia Social e Doadores (bens ou apoio especializado).
Somos brokers, isto é intermediários, que estabelecem contacto entre quem quer doar ou pretende ser voluntário pro bono e todas as entidades que constam da base de dados da Economia Social em Portugal.

A plataforma VASOS é para todos aqueles que, independentemente de ser uma pessoa particular ou uma empresa, acreditam que REUTILIZAR é um dos importantes mobilizadores de uma economia sustentável e que o VOLUNTARIADO baseado na Experiência Acumulada ao longo de uma vida são drivers importantes no desenvolvimento de uma sociedade mais solidária, inclusiva, resiliente e sustentável.

Name: The Putt Away Cancer Foundation
Description: Our foundation is a small family owned 501c3 nonprofit organization. Our mission  is simple: take a community approach to make an impact on the cancer community. As a family organization, we create and participate in events that allow the entire family to come together and FUNdraise to provide donations for cancer warriors. We inspire HOPE by provide Circle of HOPE Awards, which is a financial donation to cancer warriors to use at their discretion. We also grant Linda C. Bryant HOPE Scholarships to Texas high school seniors that are going off to college. All monies raised are through event fundraising and donations.

Description:
The CMH Foundation is the philanthropic arm of Columbia Memorial Health, an affiliate of Albany Med. 

The Foundation secures, manages, and distributes gifts to enhance the mission of CMH, which is to provide our communities with safe, high-quality, comprehensive health care services in a dignified and compassionate environment.

Name: Jim & Amy Lee Family Fund
Description: Donor Advised Fund administered through the Greater Houston Community Foundation.

Name: Fondation d'Alep
Description:
Fondation d’Alep (The Aleppo Foundation) is a charity organization based and registered in Quebec. Our charity was founded in March 2015 by the initiative of a group of young Montrealers of Aleppian origins who felt the need to establish a community organization to support the growing number of Syrians arriving to Montreal. Our current team is comprised of only volunteers of all ages and different areas of expertise who share the same values of humanity, community service and empathy.

Our mission is to serve the newly arrived Syrians in Montreal financially and through community and cultural actions. We seek to assist them in their transition and to provide them with the opportunities that will allow them to pursue their dreams and aspirations. Fondation d'Alep also aims to foster a sense of community where newly arrived Syrians can feel secure and supported in order to grow and succeed. Our goal is to empower them and to encourage them in becoming active citizens of Montreal’s community.

Name: The Community Partnership

We serve children, youth, and families through a variety of programs in the primary areas of foster care, pregnant and parenting youth, childhood disability, and early education.  We are governed by a board of directors and are funded through state contracts, foundation grants, and individual donations.

Description:
Educational Empowerment is dedicated to empowering women and children living in poverty in Myanmar through literacy and education. 

Through partnerships with local community-based organizations, Educational Empowerment provides new socio-economic opportunities through microfinance, publishes books, develops libraries, and empowers teachers with training and tools. Through a literacy exchange program between the Myanmar schools and Bainbridge Island schools, students in both countries develop an awareness of each other’s languages and lives.

Name: Tamerlaine Sanctuary & Preserve
Description:
Tamerlaine has been providing rehabilitation and exceptional care for abused, neglected, and discarded animals since being founded in 2013. Since then, we have opened our doors to thousands of visitors who want to learn about making a difference for a better future.  

Tamerlaine rests on a historic 336-acre property dating back to 1774 and is enveloped in beautiful nature, forests, wetlands, ponds, and vast fields. We are dedicated to exemplary care for our rescued farmed animals, meaningful humane education programs for all ages, imperative local wildlife initiatives, and diligent advocacy. We are currently the home to over 250 unique and beautiful rescued animals.

OUR THREE PART MISSION:
CARING-for abused and neglected farmed animals.	
EDUCATING-about animal protection and environmental ethics.
ADVOCATING-for a compassionate society

Name: Ajmal Samuel Foundation
Description:
After a traffic accident while serving in the Pakistan Armed Forces rendered him a paraplegic at the age of 21, Ajmal Samuel thought he was condemned to a cramped and restricted life, forever to be confined to a wheelchair and denied opportunity.

His road to recovery included dark days of struggle only lit by the love and support of people and institutions that helped him along this journey. That support inspired him to move ahead with his life and career and to transcend the limits of his accident to become an international businessman, a world class athlete in endurance sports including triathlon and rowing and a reputed adventurer. Now it is his time to give back.

Born of this experience, The Ajmal Samuel Foundation (ASF) has been established as a charitable foundation in order to encourage inclusive everyone, able bodied and disabled; serious and amateur; young and old to pursue physical and emotional wellbeing for  healthy living.

Name: SOLE BROS INC FOUNDATION
Description:
College Students and shoe enthusiasts Spencer Boyd, Curtis Harrison, Breiland Anderson, Kellen Newman and Jonah Karschnik are on a goodwill mission to outfit youth in need with a pair of new or nearly new quality sneakers through their 501C3 non-profit.  It's really no surprise to those who knew them, considering that they are all know to be compassionate young men who only excel in sports and academics but also in community service.

Sole Bros collect quality sneakers and donations to improve the lives of children across the globe.  The teens delivered 1200 pairs of quality sneakers to youth in Kingston and Montego Bay Jamaica, 900 pairs to youth in the Volta Region of Ghana and over a thousand pairs to victims of Hurricane Harvey in Houston and Beaumont Texas. In 2018 they opened a Sneaker Closet in the West End at Revelation Baptist Church stocked with sneakers and housing KPs Corner (in honor of friend and classmate Kyle Plush) which offers free tennis equipment for youth

The young men grow their organization by partnering with other non-profits that deliver services in areas with extremely limited resources.  They anticipate eventually sending shoes to other Countries in the Caribbean, Africa and depressed areas in the United States.

Sole Bros was created, in part, so that every kid could feel the same pride and sense of self-worth that it's founders do when they slip on a pair of quality athletic shoes for the first time.  



These young men are seeking to INSPIRE other youth to change the world! In order to further spread their message of INSPIRATION they wrote and published a children's book "Sole Bros Change the World" in 2018.

Name: The Sir Ron Foundation
Description:
Our Mission

Guide and support families based on our personal experience surrounding the demoralizing and traumatic effects associated with a diagnosis of a genetic, progressive, disease called Ataxia

Our Vision 

Through education and providing in-home services, to see families across the world; come together, support, and assist their loved ones through an under-researched, individualized, difficult transition

Our Values

Personalized experience

Name: Jeunes Leaders Africains pour accélérer l'atteinte des 17 Objectifs de Développement Durable.
Description:
Lettre de Motivation
La pauvreté sévit tout le continent africain, n’épargnant particulièrement pas les couches vulnérables, dont les jeunes. Ce fléau demeure aujourd’hui le plus grand défi pour la communauté internationale qui du reste continue à définir des stratégies intégrées de lutte contre la pauvreté faisant juste part à différentes priorités (1). Beaucoup de facteurs sont mis en relief et cités comme aggravant ce mal de vivre qui prive l’être humain de sa dignité. 
A date la réduction de la pauvreté par la croissance est le défi que la communauté mondiale doit relever, l’Afrique actrice et bénéficiaire. Il reste que la tendance mondiale à favoriser la participation constitue l’une des mutations fondamentales de notre époque rendue possible par la valorisation des ressources humaines, par les moyens de communication et d’échange. 
Les jeunes africains doivent engager toutes les énergies en vue de se placer au premier rang pour justement traduire sur le terrain du développement les discours prononcés à ce sujet. Le premier pas et non le moindre est de s’approprier le contenu des ODD, partant les essaimer dans des cadres de partenariat en vue de la réalisation des objectifs escomptés 
Nous tissons des liens de partenariat de développement de style nouveau. 
Nous voulons: 
	Développer l’esprit de leadership chez les jeunes africains
	Mettre en place une vision commune chez les jeunes africains et créer un esprit d’unité pour le Développement de l’Afrique 
	Initier des actions mettant en relief le Leadership des jeunes africains, de créer un lien d’échanges et un cadre de concertation entre les jeunes leaders africains eux-mêmes et avec les organisations s’évertuant pour le Développement de l’Afrique 

	Rechercher des financements pour propulser les jeunes leaders africains 
	Promouvoir des cadres de Partenariat dans l’atteinte des ODD 
Ethique 
 « Traites les autres comme tu voudrais être traité toi-même »

Name: Raise Community Group
Description:
Raise Community Group Now hopes to establish a sustainable model that is self-sufficient, replicable, and scalable in vulnerable communities. Recently expanded from our original Raise Uganda Now organization to maintain the same mission but for a broader audience.

For more information, visit www.raisecommunitygroup.org or email info@raisecommunitygroup.org

Name: SANAD Foundation
Description:
SANAD Foundation:
The Sudanese American National Affairs and Development Foundation (SANAD) is a non-profit 501(c)(3) broad-based community initiative dedicated to enhancing the quality of life of Sudanese American children and families.

Objectives:

1- To enable men, women, and families to overcome cultural and language barriers, and to motivate and inspire them to be educated, enlightened citizens.

2- To strengthen the family ties and community spirit and to introduce community programs that bridge the gap and enhance the relationships between parents and their children in a family, as well as among families within the community.

3- To sponsor, organize and carry out cultural and educational events in order to preserve Sudanese cultural values while exposing community members to core American values.

4- To create forums for exchange of ideas, experiences and information in order to address the needs of the community.

5- To assist new immigrants, refugees and recently displaced individuals with translation, counseling services that are crucial to the resettlement process.

6- To provide emotional as well as financial assistance to
families that experience the loss of the head of a household income due to illness, death, or other crisis or situations.

7- To advocate cultural exchange, to promote education at all levels, and to cooperate with charitable and other organizations involved in community services.

8- To establish a school for the children of immigrants that promotes the preservation of their Sudanese languages and cultures while preparing and assisting to be successful in the mainstream public school system.

9- To establish a community center that houses the school, and a central community information office in order to formalize and strengthen the operations necessary to service the community.

Name: Western Colorado Community Foundation
Description: The Western Colorado Community Foundation (WCCF) connects people who care with causes that matter. WCCF works with community-minded donors who share a connection with western Colorado, and want to impact and improve their community through their charitable giving. WCCF is a collection of many different charitable funds – over 250 – each separately accounted for and with its own purpose as described by the donors who establish the funds.  Through these funds, WCCF annually awards nearly $2 million in grants and scholarships throughout the seven counties we serve, including Eagle, Garfield, Rio Blanco, Mesa, Delta, Montrose and Ouray Counties.

Name: DEAR Foundation-Solidarité Suisse
Description: We support people in Switzerland who have experienced economic and social hardship as a result of Covid-19.

Name: Royal Astronomical Society of Canada - Ottawa Centre
Description: The Royal Astronomical Society of Canada is Canada's leading astronomy organization bringing together over 4,000 enthusiastic amateurs, educators and professionals in 28 Centres across Canada. In addition to many national services, the Ottawa Centre holds monthly meetings at the Canada Aviation and Space Museum, operates the Fred Lossing Observatory and SmartScope observatory, and has telescope and book libraries for members' use.  It holds free public star parties in the summer months and for special astronomical events.

Name: Starts With You (SWY) Foundation
Description: Founded in 2020, SWY Foundation was established to financially support African American students who are seeking higher education, as well as those pursing entrepreneurial goals. Every year as tuition increases, a dream is deferred. We offer two awards to help keep those dreams on track. Please visit our website for more information.

Name: Foundation for New American Art
Description: We create free afterschool and Saturday arts and music programs for underserved, vulnerable, at-risk children. We believe children everywhere deserve quality art and music education.

Name: Florida Diabetes Camp
Description:
At Florida Diabetes Camp, we create life-changing opportunities for children and families living with type 1 diabetes. Our mission is to provide a safe, fun, and diabetes-friendly environment where campers can gain the confidence, skills, and knowledge to reach their full potential. Through engaging activities, hands-on diabetes education, and the support of a caring community, we empower kids to thrive while building lifelong friendships and unforgettable memories.

With the support of experienced medical professionals, passionate staff, and dedicated volunteers, we ensure that every camper feels safe, supported, and celebrated. Together, we are creating a brighter future for children with type 1 diabetes by fostering independence, resilience, and a spirit of adventure. Whether you’re a camper, volunteer, donor, or advocate, we invite you to join us in making a lasting impact!

Name: St. Mary's Dining Room
Description:
St. Mary's Dining Room responds to poverty in San Joaquin County by feeding the hungry, caring for health issues and restoring human dignity to over 700 individuals each day.

Our vision is to see a community where all are empowered and able to be self-reliant, independent as well as able to meet their physical emotional and psychological well-being

OUR PROGRAMS

KITCHEN AND DINING ROOM
The dining room is open 7 days a week serving a hot, nutritious breakfast and lunch. A sack meal is distributed at 2 pm.

MEN’S SERVICES
The men’s services department is open Monday through Friday, enabling our clients to shower and receive an exchange of clothing. Haircuts are also provided several days each week.

WOMEN’S AND CHILDREN’S 
SERVICES
The women’s services department serves women and children Monday through Friday. Services include showers, hygiene products, clean clothes. Diapers and formula, when available, are also distributed to parents.

MEDICAL CLINIC
All doctors, nurses and pharmacists are volunteers serving the uninsured. Eye exams are also given and prescription eye glass provided free of charge to those who need them.

DENTAL CLINIC
All dentists and dental hygienists are volunteers serving the uninsured. Our clinic is the only one of its kind in San Joaquin County.

SOCIAL SERVICES
The social services department staff and volunteers provide case management for clients by assisting with applications and forms, securing emergency shelter, and referrals to other programs. Homeless Court is held once a month, allowing individuals to clear up old traffic tickets, minor criminal charges and fines.

HEAD START
The Child Development Council provides preschool, as well as infant and toddler programs.

Name: San Luis Obispo County Community Foundation
100 Men Who Care Southern Maine
Description:
We are 100 men who meet quarterly with $100 each to make a $10,000 impact to our communities.  

Our goal is to support charities and non-profit organizations dedicated to serving and making a difference in our communities. These organizations are almost universally under-resourced. Together, we dramatically multiply our individual impact.

100 Men Who Care members are able to nominate and share about charities and non-profit organizations they  want to support with $10,000 in one hour!

Members also learn more about the communities in which we live and what other organizations are doing throughout Southern Maine to meet needs, give hope and opportunity to the lives of others, or protect and sustain our environment and the welfare of the creatures within.

Name: Global Projects
Description: Global Projects is a faith based 501C-3 community service organization created for the purpose of empowering and assisting under served and impoverished people who experience economical, mental health and social challenges.Global Projects can be found on the web at www.globalprojects.org

Name: 2040 Foundation
Description: 2040 Foundation supports social entrepreneurs who are fully dedicated to accelerating Americas leadership in perhaps the most important cause of our time.

Name: Fifty-One International
Description:
WHO WE ARE ?

A lot of thinking over and manifold meetings with interested people led them to the decision to create a new movement, a completely independent service club.

The 8th of March 1966 took place the Constitutive meeting of the first club of the Fifty-One International, the club of Waterloo (Belgium).

The 21st of October 1966 the Fifty-One International was officially born,
the first service club founded on the European continent.

The founding members wanted that from Waterloo starts an appeal to tolerance and brotherhood as well as a message of peace and human solidarity. The Fifty-One wished to become international because men of good will are to be found everywhere, friendship knows no frontiers.

Without considering philosophical, racial or political issues the Fifty-One International works towards a better understanding between men by helping them to know and appreciate each other more while serving a common cause.

Joining in the clubs the enthusiastic eagerness of the youngest members to the wise experience of the eldest, the Fifty-One International achieves within a strong friendship an enriching human experience and the highest moral values.

Support for youth, disabled and students is one of our constant concerns, both at club, district or Fifty-One International.

Since 2016, associated with children's rights, we strive to provide specific support to this fragile and vulnerable group.

His Majesty King Philippe of Belgium is Honorary Governor of Fifty-One International.

Name: End No Sleep, Inc.
Description: End No Sleep is a California-based (501)(c)(3) nonprofit on a mission to empower high school students to optimize mental well-being through preventative care by prioritizing regular sleep habits for optimum brain function. Its holistic curriculum guides students through the science of sleep, it's impact on mental, emotional, and social health, and provides evidence-based tips and interactive, mindfulness exercises for improved sleep patterns.

Name: Haitian Timoun Foundation

Name: Hearing Charities of America
Description:
The purpose of Hearing Charities is to support those impacted by speech and hearing disorders by expanding awareness, volunteerism, and philanthropy in the communities Hearing Charities serves. Hearing Charities provides resources for hearing professionals, manufacturers and individuals with hearing issues. We do this through three related activities: awareness programs, scholarships and Adopt-An-Agency. These activities support Hearing Charitiesâ€ mission of commitment to communication in every community.

The activities of Hearing Charities and of those organizations it supports are limited to charitable, religious, educational and scientific purposes, including, the making of distributions to organizations that qualify as exempt organizations under Section 501(c)(3) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1954 (or the corresponding provision of any future United States Internal Revenue Law).

Name: Fondation Avec
Description:
Reconnue d'utilité publique, la Fondation Avec s'engage dans la lutte contre le cancer en assurant trois missions pour que l'espoir et la vie soient plus forts que cette maladie :
- l'amélioration de la qualité de vie des patients
- le recherche médicale
- la mobilisation internationale

Name: playforhelp
Description: Turning fun into giving!

Name: OurSTEMjourney
Description: OurSTEMjourney is a mentoring platform to promote STEM and tech among young Spanish women

Name: National Celiac Disease Society
Description:
Founded in 2011, the National Celiac Disease Society, (formerly the Kicking 4 Celiac Foundation) is an IRS recognized 501(c)(3) non-profit organization with a mission to raise Celiac Disease education and awareness. The National Celiac Disease Society raises funds through meaningful charity events which help develop our organization and its nationally recognized programs. We look forward to continuously work to help improve the lives of those who are affected by this auto-immune disorder.

The National Celiac Disease Society scholarship program is offered to graduating High School students affected with Celiac Disease, to help off-set costs and expenses of requiring a gluten-free diet while in college. This scholarship program began with the graduating class of 2011/2012 and will be continuously offered to well deserving, graduating high school seniors each year.

Our "Kicking 4 Celiac Cleats 4 Kids" program was developed to help educate youth in underserved areas about proper nutrition, healthy eating, and the importance of an active lifestyle. To date we have partnered with multiple organizations to work with hundreds of children and teens through this program!

Our Student Ambassador program is designed to help people in the Celiac Disease community feel empowered, and help educate others about this auto-immune disorder. Ambassadors reach out to their schools and communities to relay the information necessary to raise Celiac Disease awareness in their area.

Name: Fashion For Philanthropy
Description:
Fashion For Philanthropy is a student-run organization at Fordham University that supports the Make-A-Wish Foundation through annual fundraisers. Our members share an inclination to make the world a better place through their passion for fashion. 

Our most notable annual event is the student-run fashion show that we host every spring. For the show, we partner with several companies who are generous enough to donate anything from runway styles to gift bag giveaways, in order to aid us in fundraising for the Make-A-Wish Foundation. The fashion show is organized, DJ'ed, decorated, modeled, photographed, hair and make-up'ed, and operated by the dependable and driven and students who are involved!

Name: Global Philanthropy Alliance
Description: Global Philanthropy Alliance makes grants to non-profit organizations in Africa (Kenya, Nigeria, South Africa) that support youth to be leaders in effecting positive social/economic change in their communities. It also learns from this work to share with others.

Name: GVCFoundationMN
Description:
The Golden Valley Community Foundation uses philanthropy, community initiatives and funding support to address the changing needs and interests of Golden Valley neighbors and friends.

Donate online: https://givemn.org/organization/Golden-Valley-Community-Foundation

Golden Valley is a thriving community with many individuals and organizations actively seeking to improve our neighborhoods and our city. There are a number of organizations and initiatives that solicit and distribute funds for charitable purposes in the community. The Golden Valley Community Foundation (GVCF) is a nonprofit public charity created by and for the people of Golden Valley. Our mission is to enhance the quality of life for all the people of Golden Valley by encouraging philanthropy, providing services to the donors and those who serve to meet the needs of the community.

The Foundation grew from the Envision Connection Project (ECP), formed as a result of a citizen-led community initiative called Envision Golden Valley. GVCF is supported by Golden Valley’s elected leaders and other Golden Valley organizations. We are an independent, self-governed 501 (c) 3 community foundation.

GVCF is the community conduit for philanthropic giving that receives, manages, and distributes funds to benefit the Golden Valley community. GVCF provides a structured way to solicit and distribute funds to the maximum benefit of residents and community organizations. We consider our constituents to be people who live in Golden Valley, organizations and businesses located in and serving Golden Valley, and neighborhood or other community groups that support activities, developments and ideas that benefit our geographic area.

The GVCF Board of Directors, which is composed of individuals who live or work in Golden Valley.

Name: DCIS Foundation
Description:
The Denver Center for International Studies Foundation's mission is to raise money for scholarships and grants so that all students at the four DCIS schools can engage in educational travel. 

Established in 1997, our scholarships and grants have made a big impact: more than 5200 students (K-12) traveled; 7 languages practiced; 45 countries visited; countless inter-cultural and learning moments experienced; and student purpose and worth realized. 

Our donors have contributed $500K+, and our volunteers and board members have given over 83,000 hours of service. 

Together, we are student travel heroes. Visit our website to learn more about our impact.

Name: Gregorius Mättig - Foundation/Stiftung
Description: The future grows out of the past. The preservation of a transmitted historical legacy, its development and its study, form a strong pillar for offering a vision and an orientation for our local community.

Name: Music for the Growing Mind
Description: Music for the Growing Mind aims at establishing free Schools of Orchestra in China for children in difficult social and economic situations. It is inspired by the acclaimed experience of 'El Sistema' in Venezuela.

Name: DFW Humane Society
Description: Founded in 1967, DFW Humane Society provides shelter and care for homeless, neglected, and unwanted pets and gives them a second chance in life through adoption. We house approximately 80-100 pets at any given time, and they remain with us until they find their forever home.

Name: KCMO Angels
Description:
The KCMO Angels mission is to walk alongside children, youth, & families in the foster care community by offering consistent support through intentional giving, relationship building, and mentorship. Through our Love Box and Dare to Dream programs, we help build more sustainable foster homes in the Kansas City, Missouri area.  

Our Love Box program provides foster families with community and holistic support so that they can continue to do the important and meaningful work of being a foster parent. As a Love Box group, you are matched with a local foster family based on compatibility and scope of needs. When our families are matched with committed volunteers that show up monthly, caretakers feel supported and children gain a greater sense of belonging and self-confidence.  

Our Dare to Dream program offers an opportunity to mentor a youth in foster care or a youth who has aged out of foster care ranging from 12-21 years old. Many foster youth are victims of abuse and neglect, may have trouble forming healthy relationships, and are often ill-prepared for life after foster care. As a mentor, you commit to meeting with your mentee twice per month to set goals, reach our 10 milestones, and help them achieve their dreams.

KCMO Angels is part of a growing organization called National Angels, which has a long-term vision to open chapters in every major metropolitan city in then nation. We believe that every single foster child in the US can be supported and empowered to succeed.

Name: OWN IT Summit
Description: OWN IT IS BRIDGING THE GAP BETWEEN FEMALE LEADERS OF THE 21ST CENTURY AND THE MILLENNIALS WHO ADMIRE THEM, BY SHAKING UP THE WOMEN'S LEADERSHIP ARENA AND PRODUCING ACCESSIBLE EVENTS FOR COLLEGE-AGED WOMEN.

Name: Orientar-Associação de Intervenção para a Mudança
Description:
A ORIENTAR – Associação de Intervenção para a Mudança é uma associação de solidariedade social sem fins lucrativos, com o estatuto de IPSS/Pessoa Coletiva de Utilidade Pública, que visa promover a inclusão social e profissional de pessoas em situação de vulnerabilidade, através da criação de condições que suportem a sua autonomia, estamos no terreno desde 2001. 
No âmbito da nossa atividade desenvolvemos um apoio integrado com vista à reintegração socioprofissional dos nossos clientes, assente no desenvolvimento e treino de competências que estimulem a autonomia de vida, e na implementação de uma rede de suporte facilitadora do processo de inserção na vida ativa.
Os destinatários do Projeto Orientar são:
- Todas as pessoas em idade ativa;
- Sem consumo de substâncias psicoativas ou em estado final de tratamento.
- Pessoas que demonstrem motivação para redefinir o seu projeto de vida com vista à autonomização.

Name: LES AMIS DU LIBAN À MONACO
International Day of Pink-Journée Rose Internationale
Description:
International Day of Pink has one purpose, to create a more inclusive and diverse world. We do this by encouraging young people to challenge social norms, ask more of their educators, and stand up against bullying towards their 2SLGBTQIA+ peers. Over the years we have worked with countless educators, politicians and organizations around Canada to spread this message and create young activists for this mission. We hope to continue this work and reach more communities with every passing year. We can only do this with your support and involvement. So please consider sharing our work, wearing our merch, donating to our cause, and joining our movement in any way you can.

La journée Rose Internationale a un objectif : créer un monde où la diversité et l’inclusion sont valorisés. Les jeunes sont encouragés de confronter les normes sociétales, d’en demander plus de la part de leurs éducateurs de s’affirmer contre l’intimidation ciblant leurs pairs dans la communauté 2SLGBTQIA+. Au courant des dernières années, nous avons travaillé côte-à-côteavec des éducateurs, des politiciens et différents organismes canadiens afin de transmettre ce message et de former des jeunes militants pour cette cause. Nous espérons continuer ce travail en tissant des liens avec plusieurs autres communautés. Votre soutien et votre appui sont essentiels à notre mission. Svp songez à partager notre travail, à porter notre marchandise, à faire un don ou à vous joindre à notre mouvement.

Name: Collectif Jeunes Pousses
Description: The Collectif JEUNES POUSSES is a group of Cameroonians who wish to provide aspiring champions with the resources necessary for their progression and maturation on both a sporting and academic/professional level.

Name: One Need, Inc.
Description:
One Need is a benevolence system for the church. We are always looking for new partners so let me know if you would like to talk about your church’s benevolence system.

Here is what some of our partners are saying:

“One Need changed our benevolence ministry. They enable us to focus on the ministry side of the needs as they take care of the vetting and monetary side. Rodney has a heart for those in need and has excellent discernment in meeting the real need people have. I have been impressed by his ability to decipher between what is a physical need over a spiritual or lifestyle change. Working with One Need has been a huge blessing to our ministry here at His Hands Church.” – Sheila Keefe, Care and Outreach Pastor At His Hands Church – Woodstock, GA

“One Need has been a tremendous blessing to our church. We have been able to simplify our benevolence process and meet more needs in our community than would be possible without our partnership with One Need. We are grateful to God for our church to get to be a part of helping people in need, both through processing needs for their neighbors and through meeting needs that are published from other partner churches. If you’re looking to expand your benevolence reach while outsourcing some of the filtering process to help your people meet pressing needs, I recommend Rodney and One Need to you.” – Ben Wittinghill, Lead Pastor at Rivertown Church – Brattleboro, VT

“One Need has been a game changer for our Care Ministry here at Passion City Church. Not only are they able to partner + raise financial assistance for the people of our local church, but Rodney also reaches out to each and every person with love and encouragement, as well as shooting people straight about their current financial crisis. We believe that One Need sees the PERSON BEFORE THE NEED, and that means everything to us here at PCC and to our community!” - Matt Carter, Care Pastor at Passion City Church

Name: Eiva Arts Foundation
Description: Eiva Arts Foundation was established in 2012 in Armenia with a mission to value the role of art globally as well as contribute to the development of arts in Armenia and foster the global recognition of Armenian art. The projects implemented by the Eiva Arts Foundation are special due to their cultural and historic context as well as cognitive and educational content. The unique element and the important feature of  Eiva Arts Foundation lies in the implementation of socially engaged art project, which is used as a tool of advocacy contributing to the upheaval of social as well as individual stigmas and stereotypes also serving as a great educational format. Eiva enhances the role of art in various educational fields not only turning them into innovations but also amplifying the potential of the field to develop both scientifically and economically. Recognizing art’s undeniable power as a source of inspiration and invigoration, Eiva Arts Foundation designs and develops projects that revitalize art heritage and brings it closer people. Since its establishment Eiva Arts Foundation has shown works that highlight the value of historical and cultural heritage in contemporary format and how it is penetrated in the art context globally.

Name: Wish STL
Description:
The Make-A-Wish Missouri & Kansas St. Louis Young Professionals, or better known as Wish STL, are a diverse group of young leaders who are dedicated to supporting Make-A-Wish in St. Louis. Members help promote Make-A-Wish by hosting events geared towards young professionals in order to increase awareness, encourage volunteerism and raise funds for the Foundation.

It is our mission to provide a forum to develop and enhance professional growth, leadership skills and civic duty in a fun and social atmosphere.

Name: planetapontocom
Description:
O Planetapontocom é uma  instituição, cuja missão é desenvolver soluções inovadoras para a educação pública brasileira.
Trabalhamos orientados por pesquisas que identificam os principais problemas da educação pública investimos na busca de soluções para os ensinos Fundamental e Médio.

Name: Elena and Gennady Timchenko Charitable Foundation
Description:
The Timchenko Foundation is the abbreviated name of the Elena and Gennady Timchenko Charitable Foundation, which was established in Moscow in 2010 and known as the Ladoga Foundation until the end of August 2013. The Foundation’s work is directed towards solving Russia’s social problems systemically. Some of its key focus areas include: providing support to the older generation; developing sport  and culture; and helping  families and children. 
By establishing long-term partnerships with non-governmental organisations, government agencies and local authorities, we are addressing social issues and aiding the development of local charities. We think it is important that anything launched by the Foundation should be self-sustaining in the future. Our work is underpinned by the principles of transparency and personal responsibility undertaken by the founders, the Timchenkos, who have been involved in charitable causes for over twenty years. Employing and maintaining the best practices used by charities both in Russia and globally, we are shaping the future of family philanthropy in Russia, making it open, consistent and focused on long-term sustainability.

Name: Butterfly Foundation Cyprus
Description: The Butterfly Foundation is a charity organisation established by Imperio Group in 2015. Acknowledging the needs of today’s society and as part of its corporate social responsibility, Imperio has set up the charity wanting to contribute back to the society. Each year the Butterfly Foundation will dedicate its contribution to one cause ranging from education, health, nature, and wildlife.

Description:
The T.L.L Temple Foundation works alongside rural communities
to build a thriving East Texas and to alleviate poverty, creating
access and opportunity for all.

Name: Change a Life Foundation
Description:
Change a Life Foundation is a private family foundation that is funded by one individual donor family founded in 2001.  Change a Life Foundation has funded over $32,000,000 in grants and scholarships by changing  one life at a time --and impacted over 672,060 individual lives during that time period. 

The Foundation’s purpose is to significantly help individuals and families who, through no fault of their own, have experienced an injury, illness, disability, or catastrophic life event, and who are suffering financial hardship, by providing support for critically needed direct services that promote their self-sufficiency and improve their quality of life. Grants are made through a one-time or short-term (average twelve months) intervention.  Grant beneficiaries are required to be legal residents or US citizens.  The Foundation intends that funds will be used as a last resort.  Applications must discuss research into all available alternate funding sources.  All of these elements are required for applications to be eligible.

Applications are submitted online and are only available to only  pre-selected, board approved  non-profit charitable  organizations that apply to the Foundation on behalf of their clients in their case management. The Foundation does not accept unsolicited applications.
