Name: Family2Family
Description: Family2Family was established in 2018 as 'Baby2Baby'. This growing charity is based in Crowborough and operates across the county -  through the simple act of redistribution, we seek to alleviate poverty and associated adversity by gifting good quality baby and children's clothing and essential items to those in need of support in East Sussex and surrounding areas.

Name: Boston Young Professionals of the American Cancer Society
Description: YPACS Boston empowers and engages emerging leaders to network and build relationships while spreading awareness about early detection and prevention of cancer as well as the need to support cancer patients and their families through American Cancer Society programs like Hope Lodge, Road to Recovery and the National Cancer Information Center.

Name: Animal Investigation and Response
Description:
Animal Investigation and Response's (AIR) mission is to connect law enforcement, animal control and individuals with strategies and resources to assist agencies with large scale animal issues as well as provide assistance for  cruelty investigations.  AIR also assists agencies with resources for natural disaster situations that affects animals safety. 

We are an all-volunteer, 501(c)3 organization.

Name: Akademia Liderów Innowacji i Przedsiębiorczości Fundacja dr Bogusława Federa
Description:
Czy wiesz że gdyby nie mikro-firmy, polska gospodarka przestałaby istnieć? Aż 96% z nich wytwarza ponad połowę krajowego zysku i zapewnia 75% wszystkich wpływów z podatków. A czy wiesz, że możesz zainwestować 1,5% Twojego podatku i darowiznę w bezpłatną pomoc dla  poczatkujących mikro firm? Od 2008 roku Akademia liderów innowacji i Przedsiębiorczości Fundacja Dr Bogusława Federa poprzez serwisy mikroporady.pl/  jestemszefem.pl pomaga i  wspiera mikro firmy udzielając bezpłatnych, eksperckich porad w zakresie: pomysłu na działalność i zakładania firmy, polskich i unijnych regulacji prawnych, finansów, zatrudnienia pracowników, certyfikacji i innych. Możesz zainwestować 1,5% Twojego podatku i darowizny w bezpłatną pomoc dla  poczatkujących mikro firm. Wspierając tę inicjatywę, nie tylko pomagasz innym, ale także korzystasz z możliwości odliczenia darowizny od podatku

Na co pójdą Twoje pieniądze?

bezpłatny dostęp do eksperckiej wiedzy ( nowe pomysły na biznes, wzory umów, poradniki, porady, instrukcje, dostęp do nagrań z webinariów) i indywidualnych porad online przez 24 h/365 nieprzerwanie od 2008
co miesiąc bezpłatne webinaria rocznie  ponad 1000 uczestników
kwartalna aktualizacja prawna względem prawa polskiego i unijnego ponad 1000 dokumentów z interpretacjami.
 
Kto korzysta z przekazanych darowizn i 1,5% ?

2,9 mln użytkowników rocznie serwisów mikroporady.pl/ jestemszefem.pl (źródło: Google Analytics wrzesień 2023)
60 tys. zarejestrowanych użytkowników**
1000 serwisów urzędów miast i gmin**

Warto również pamiętać, że przekazując darowiznę lub 1,5 % podatku na rzecz Akademii Liderów Innowacji i Przedsiębiorczości Fundacji Dr Bogusława Federa, można skorzystać z możliwości odliczenia tej kwoty od podatku.

Name: UNH Board of Young Hospitality Professionals
Description: Hospitality Management and Recreation Management and Policy alumni of the University of New Hampshire supporting students in their transition from academics to the workforce through alumni involvement, mentorship, guest lectures, and scholarships. Board members provide the students with guidance, expertise, and financial contributions that help them navigate career paths and gain the skills and resources needed to succeed professionally.

Name: The Karibu Centre
Description:
We are a holistic community program that is educating children and empowering families in Kenya. 
Our programs not only engage children and youth, but also empower parents, driving change throughout the whole community.
We believe that the key to success is being completely self sustainable and eliminating the reliance on donors. In 2016 we founded our Social Enterprise, Organic Savanna, an all natural, african skincare range of which 100% of proceeds fund the programmes at the Karibu Centre.

Name: NEW DEFI
Description: NEW DEFI est une association de l'école d'ingénieurs Supméca. Cette association a pour but de récolter des fonds afin de mener à bien des projets de construction d'écoles au Bénin. Certains d'entre nous ont la chance de partir au Bénin pour aider à la construction de ces établissements scolaires.

Name: Escamel
Description:
Escamel est une association caritative à but non lucratif déclarée sous la loi du 1er juillet 1901. Celle-ci a  été créée dans l'objectif de récolter des dons pour participer à la 25ᵉ édition du 4L Trophy qui se déroulera en 2022. Pour ses deux fondateurs, Allan et Clément, Escamel va au-delà de la course, en apportant un soutien financier à des opérations solidaires - comme la construction d’école, puits, infrastructures et l’aide alimentaire, qui grâce à vos dons, achats et participations pourront  se réaliser. Cette aventure ne s'arrêtera pas à une course, Escamel continuera d'exister afin d'aider ceux qui en ont besoin par d'autres actions et d'autres voyages humanitaires. 

Vous pouvez nous contacter :
- par mail : contact@escamel.fr
- par téléphone : 09 72 58 48 40

Name: Task Force Innov COVID19
Description:
Sèmè City and the United Nations Population Fund (UNFPA Benin) have gathered 100 innovation members within an Innovation Task Force intended to bolster the fight against COVID-19 and its economic impact. 

This initiative complements the actions taken by the Government of Benin. It aims to find local and innovative solutions, adapted to the socio-economic and cultural environments of Benin and West Africa. Its purpose is also to increase the economic resilience of entrepreneurs and key players of Benin’s innovation ecosystem, whose roles will be crucial for the management of the crisis and its aftermaths.

Name: Yambi Rwanda
Description: Yambi Rwanda is a non-profit (501)(c)(3) dedicated to transcending genocide and overcoming poverty in Rwanda through the creation of opportunity and the improvement of the lives of Rwandans, one-by-one, with a focus on education, faith, the imparting of life skills, the celebration of Rwandan arts and culture, and sustained provision of the essentials of life, such as clean water, adequate nutrition, and improved health care.

Name: Boud'Choumins
Description:
Boud’Choumins est un projet de solidarité local qui a trait à l’enfance. Nous intervenons tous les dimanches à l’hôpital de la Timone pour égayer la journée des enfants des étages 5 (oncologie et hématologie pédiatrique) et 16 (gastroentérologie pédiatrique) en leur proposant des activités : activités manuelles, jeux de société, après-midis à thème, prestations d’intervenants du spectacle…
Notre mission est d’apporter un peu de bonheur aux enfants hospitalisés dont le quotidien est loin d’être facile, surtout en cette période.
Notre projet organise en parallèle des dons de sang sur le campus de Kedge BS et sensibilise les étudiants aux différents dons de soi.

Name: Project Voice Fund
Description:
Founded in 2021, the Project Voice Fund is a giving circle that supports early-stage AAPI-led and serving narrative change platforms. The projects we fund have an intersectional approach in the stories they share and uplift voices from historically marginalized communities.

Our application process is currently invitation-only. We release funds on an annual basis. Organizations are invited in the spring to submit an application and notified in the summer about final decisions.

Name: AMIRA - Associação Mafra Intervenção e Resgate Animal
Description:
Associação de intervenção e resgate animal. 
A nossa missão: Esterlizar, Resgatar, Cuidar , Proteger, Sensibilizar e encontrar adotantes para a vida.

Name: Microprojets.org
Description:
Plateforme de crowdfunding humanitaire, Microprojets.org finance des projets de solidarité internationale partout dans le monde grâce aux dons.

Ces projets visent à assurer l’autonomie des populations bénéficiaires tout en préservant les us et coutumes locaux. Ils couvrent des domaines divers : eau, éducation, santé, agriculture, environnement, etc.

Le fonctionnement de l'association garantit la bonne allocation des fonds ainsi que le sérieux et la pérennité des microprojets. Les projets sont sélectionnés par un jury d'experts. Ils doivent être portés par des associations françaises de plus de 3 ans. Des missions d’évaluation sont régulièrement menées sur le terrain. Et le troisième tiers du financement n’est versé qu’après la remise d’un bilan intermédiaire d’évaluation.

L’association Microprojets.org, soutenue par la Guilde, a été créée en partenariat avec l'Agence des Micro Projets, qui accompagne, sélectionne, contrôle et évalue les projets. Après avoir notamment décroché 3 millions d’euros de financement auprès de l’Agence Française de Développement, l’association a lancé son site de financement participatif en décembre 2014 pour permettre aux particuliers et aux entreprises de contribuer également aux projets.

Name: Behind My Mind
Description:
Behind My Mind is a campaign created to foster a safe space for individuals to speak openly about their mental health journeys, with the goal of raising awareness for mental health and illnesses, and inspiring others to do the same.

Behind My Mind also fundraises for the Canadian Mental Health Association to support the mental health programs and services that help Canadians take care of their mental health.

Name: r.o.o.f. Red Oak Opportunity Foundation
Description:
Red Oak Realty established R.O.O.F. in 1985 for the purpose of supporting local homeless, educational, and other organizations whose focus is helping residents of the communities Red Oak serves. 

The goal of R.O.O.F. is to assist smaller local charitable organizations which do not receive significant funding from other sources, in order to make a significant and observable contribution to the aims and objectives of that organization. 

Contributions to R.O.O.F. come from Red Oak Realty agents, matching grants from the company, staff, clients, business associates, and friends. R.O.O.F. is a 501c(3) tax-exempt non-profit organization. All donations to R.O.O.F. are tax deductible. All administrative costs for R.O.O.F. are paid by Red Oak Realty, so 100% of all donations goes to the grant recipients. 

Since 1985, thanks to the generosity of the R.O.O.F. community, R.O.O.F. has distributed grants totaling more than $550,000. In 2006 and 2007 alone, R.O.O.F. granted nearly $200,000 to worthy organizations, enabling them to make a real difference in the lives of many East Bay residents. Please see the list of current grant recipients. 

Grants are determined by a revolving committee of owners and agents of Red Oak Realty. The grants range from $500 to $5,000. 

If you would like more information, would like make a tax-deductible contribution to R.O.O.F., or if you know of a local non-profit group where a R.O.O.F. grant would make a difference, please follow the links on this site or contact: 

Jimmy Reina
Red Oak Opportunity Foundation
1891 Solano Avenue
Berkeley, CA 94707
510-280-2103 
info@redoak-roof.org

Name: Oficina Curumim Parents Society
Description: Oficina Curumim is a non-profit created in 2014 with the mission to support Brazilian immigrant families to ensure their children speak their heritage language and experience first-hand the Brazilian culture and customs.

Name: Rotary Club - Tampa Westchase
Description: We provide service to others, promote integrity, and advance world understanding, goodwill, and peace through our fellowship of business, professional, and community leaders.

Name: Ghana Association of Charlottesville
Description:
The Ghana Association of Charlottesville (G-Club) is a non-profit making organization formed by a group of  Ghanaians living in the Charlottesville and surrounding areas of Virginia in the United States of America (U. S. A). Our main aim is, seeking to help under privileged or needy communities back home in Ghana since all the members have ties connecting them to under privileged communities in Ghana. Recently, we received donations from the University of Virginia Health Systems in the form of medical supplies which we duly presented to our
 various under privileged communities.
 
We also identify and promote the interest and welfare of members and help members explore various opportunities in the U. S. A, as well as in Ghana. We form closer links and collaborate with other Ghanaians and various societies in the U. S. A. Our membership is opened to all Ghanaians and people with interest in Ghana or who have Ghana on their hearts, irrespective of ethnic background, religion, sex, age and belief.

Name: CJVAF, Mission Humanitaire
Description:
CJVAF est une association humanitaire qui oeuvre dans les domaines de l'éducation, la santé, le parrainage des enfants et l'environnement.

Nous organisons des cours de soutien scolaire, l'animation et des stages selon la demande du volontaire.

Name: NeedPlasma
Description:
NeedPlasma is a social initiative started to bridge the gap between Plasma seekers and Plasma Donors for convalescent plasma treatment. 

The initiative was started out of Delhi but now has reached Pan-India. The initiative has 200+ volunteers across different cities who are committed in promoting Plasma donation and finding relevant Plasma Donors. 

It is an entirely not for profit initiative which is run by volunteers efforts.

Name: In-Visible NGO
Description:
Mission
Our mission is to bring real and lasting change to children in poverty. We do this by creating partnerships with communities and implementing programs which will reduce their daily struggles, invest in their potential, and provide them with the opportunity to grow up healthy, educated, and prepared to succeed and contribute to society.

Vision
Our vision is a global community, free from poverty, where children are protected and have the opportunity to reach their full potential.

Our Projects

1. School Feeding Program
Malnutrition among children is a serious concern in the Philippines today. Hunger and malnutrition are a serious hindrance to improving student achievement and outcomes in the public schools. Present data indicates that there’s around 562,000 severely wasted and around 1.5M wasted elementary school children or a total of about 2M children in the Philippines.

In Quezon City alone, there are around 10,000 severely wasted children. In partnership with Ateneo de Manila University’s Center for Educational Development , invisible works towards the improvement of hunger and malnutrition by providing feeding programs to these severely wasted children. They receive free daily lunch every day. This meal is likely their only meal of the day.

Children in our feeding program are monitored regularly. BMI and school performance are measured every 3 months. Once a child is enrolled into our feeding program, he/she is entitled to receive free meals for the entire duration of his/her primary education.

2. Girls Education
In-Visible supports girls education by providing school bursaries for girls in the Philippines. We also provide uniforms, food, and transportation if and when needed. 

3. Children Community Centre
Launching March 2015

Invisible launched October 2013. Our registered UK charity number is 1155656. Our charity is registered as ‘Not Invisible’ but our working name is ‘Invisible’.

Name: Rotary Club of Accra-South
Description: Chartered on 8th August, 2000, Accra South is one of 43 Rotary Clubs in Ghana, and one of over 34,000 clubs that belong to Rotary International.

Name: The Marro Foundation
Description: Marc and Robin Pollack founded The Marro Foundation as a social impact fund to educate and advocate for arts, education and cultural organizations, particularly in the areas of food, attainable and affordable housing, homelessness, horticulture and conservation.

Name: Frost Family Foundation
Description: At the Frost Family Foundation, we believe that thriving equitable relationships are at the heart of impactful philanthropy. We are honored to partner with some of Hawaii’s most impactful changemakers. Without them, our impact wouldn’t be possible.

Name: Present Wageningen
Description:
Present Wageningen slaat een brug tussen mensen die iets te bieden hebben en mensen die daarmee geholpen kunnen worden. 

Wij doen dat door groepen vrijwilligers te werven en vanuit deze potentie aan een hulpvraag te koppelen via een maatschappelijke organisatie. 

En dit alles zeer professioneel met persoonlijke begeleiding van groepen en project.

Name: OSC SOS Mulheres
Description: ONG de apoio ao enfrentamento da violência doméstica, familiar e de gênero.

Name: Philanthropy By Design
Description:
What we do
Our mission is to transform nonprofit environments through design.
 PBD is a volunteer network of design industry professionals. We renovate the interior environments of community service organizations and save tons of furniture from the landfill through creative reuse. Our work benefits thousands of under served Bay Area residents; at-risk youth, abused women, seniors, AIDS patients and homeless people, bringing them hope, inspiration and comfort.

Learn more at www.pbd.org

Name: Amigos de los animales Cañete
Description: Amigos de los animales es un grupo de voluntarios que busca la concientización sobre el respeto en los animales, contando con un pequeño temporal ubicado en la provincia de Cañete.

Name: July6th
Description: July6th promotes , facilitate and recognizes your charity work Globally,Corporate Social Investment (CSI) Brand and individual recognition &  Endorsement on U.N criteria . Rewards Charity work Against SDGs priority ,Resource Mobilization for  CSI initiatives .

Name: Austin Conservation Project
Description: The Austin Conservation Project is a student organization that aims to bring together like-minded individuals to preserve, learn about and enjoy the city of Austin. This organization gets members actively involved in environmentalism in Austin by hosting lake cleanups and public advocacy events. The Austin Conservation Project also aims to help students explore more of the culturally unique aspects of Austin, outside of the University bubble. We have a big focus on local businesses and work closely to help promote and spread awareness by interviewing businesses for our social media, hosting events at local restaurants and doing profit shares. Ultimately, this organization's goal is to give students a platform to conserve both Austin's environment and culture. Recently, we were the winner of the 2020 Swing Out award.

Name: Princess Svanevit
Description:
Princess Svanevit byggdes 1930 på Neglingevarvet för att sätta Sverige på kartan som kappseglings- och hantverksnation. Hon är konstruerad som en kappseglingsbåt, men inredd som en konstsalong.

Båten ägs av Princess Svanevit AB. Majoritetsägare är den ideella Föreningen Svenska Träbåtar som förvaltar den donation från Familjen Burre Hellmans stiftelse som gör renoveringen möjlig. Syftet med det ideella ägandet är att Princess Svanevit ska utgöra en unik möjlighet att öka och sprida intresset för alla de fantastiska båtar som har ritats och byggts i Sverige. Princess Svanevit ska segla mer än på kappseglingsbanor – hon ska aktivt gästa hamnar längs hela Sveriges kuster och göra skäl för namnet ”Hela Sveriges Båt”. Istället för att segla under en specifik flagg kommer hon att segla under ett helt flaggspel av vimplar från de klubbar hon samarbetar med.

Princess Svanevit erbjuder en möjlighet att få bidra till den klassiska båtkulturens fortlevnad. Alla pengar som kommer in till Princess Svanevits ägare, ideella föreningen Svenska Träbåtar, går vidare till projektet och den dag då Princess Svanevit säljs går försäljningsintäkten vidare till "Princess Svanevits renoveringspris”, ett pris som nu är under utformning. Priset ska gynna branschen och hantverket, fundamentet i bevarandet av våra klassiska båtar. Varje krona till ideella föreningen Svenska träbåtar kommer således att stödja svensk träbåtskultur. 

FAKTA
Princess Svanevit 12 mR S-2
Konstruktör: Gustav Estlander
Byggd: 1930
Beställare: Erik Åkerlund
Löa: 21,96 m
Lvl: 13,95 m
Bredd: 3,64 m
Djup: 2,70 m
Segelarea: 182 kvm
Vikt: 27 ton
Renoveras nu: på Stockholms Båtsnickeri

Name: The Queen's Foundation
Description:
So she can be the generation of change.

The Queens Foundation's (TQF) mission is to develop and promote under-served young women to reach
their greatest potential to higher education and leadership through a society of women across North Carolina.
TQF specifically targets young women who will be first in their family to earn a four-year degree and who
live in poverty. TQF provides both a middle and high school program track lasting no less than 4-7 years,
completing a young woman's first completed year of college. TQF's programs are designed to address a local
and state-wide issue of poverty and decline in workforce readiness (development), by advancing a young
woman's access to higher education and exposure to opportunity. Participants are selected with the goal to
empower them to break the cycle of poverty for her family and lead her family's next generation to become
highly educated and economically stable.

94 % of the young women that have entered our program have matriculated to a four year university. With the statistics of a students living in poverty and first generation entering college being less than 40%, we are confident we have made a substantial impact in our states' longterm ability to develop and be a state with a thriving and growing economy.

As a volunteer-based organization, The Queen's Foundation has dedicated professionals in each region we serve raising funds, securing mentors and building awareness in the local communities across the state, that we serve. The opportunities to get involved with TQF range from just a few hours each year, to full stakeholder where you may invest several hours per week. This can include becoming a one-time volunteer, program volunteer, leadership team member, board member, mentor or investor. To learn more about these opportunities and apply, visit us at www.thequeensfoundation.org

Name: Capitol Hill Community Foundation (DC)
Description:
The Capitol Hill Community Foundation draws on the generosity and idealism of our neighborhood to help build a stronger and more caring community—a place where families can flourish and where every resident can share in a better future.
We support activities, projects and organizations that enrich the lives of residents and celebrate the history of our Capitol Hill community. Since its founding in 1989, the Foundation has directed more than $6.6 million to a broad range of projects.

The generosity of our community has enabled the Foundation to provide seed money for new efforts, support worthy community programs and organizations, and also undertake important initiatives for our neighborhood.

We have also launched some major initiatives, including a thriving neighborhood history project, an awards program honoring individuals for distinguished service to our community, the renovation of 8 school libraries, the Eastern Market Fund to help meet urgent needs after the devastating fire on April 30, 2007, Shakespeare at School: A Folger Shakespeare Library George Didden Memorial Project, and A Literary Feast, a series of literary themed dinners.

Please join us by contributing today, or by applying for a grant. Together we’re making Capitol Hill a better place to live, for all of us. We thank you for your continued generous support of the community we love. For more information download our brochure.

“
The work is tangible and immediate. Unlike other similar organizations, [the Foundation] is capable of responding to community needs in real time, and is able to provide targeted and cost-effective response to worthy requests.
— James Loots, Boardmember

Donations can be made at https://www.capitolhillcommunityfoundation.com/donate

Name: CENTENNIAL PARKSIDE CDC
Description: Our mission at Centennial Parkside Community Development Corporation is to preserve, promote and revitalize the East Parkside neighborhood in West Philadelphia through partnerships with businesses and institutions and programs that engage residents, increase opportunity, and grow a diverse, thriving community.

Name: Level Ground Mixed Martial Arts
Description:
Our mission is to unleash the strength, power, and ingenuity of urban youth.  We couple athletic training with mentorship, academic advancement, and employment opportunities.

At Level Ground, we are passionate about our students' growth on and off the mat.  We've gotten kids off the streets, hooked up with meaningful jobs, and into college.  Join us to empower young people to fight for - and seize - their dreams.

Accomplishments to date:
-In 20 months, grew from 3 to 110 students served weekly across 3 sites through holistic programming
-To date, 100% of seniors have entered college or meaningful employment
-Provided 16 youth with internship, employment, or workforce development opportunities this year

Name: The Howat Foundation
Description:
In 2005 Margaret Howat was diagnosed with Chronic Myeloid Leukaemia. The treatment is punishing and extremely experimental, and through the guidance of Professor Tessa Holyoake (Professor of Experimental Haematology at The University of Glasgow) and her team, she has had a Bone Marrow transplant and now leads a very normal life. 

The Howat Foundation was founded by Malcolm and Margaret Howat in 2009. We are proud to support cancer research at the University of Glasgow,  and we hope that you will join us.

Name: Fundación Mi Llave
Description:
Hola, Bienvenido a Fundación Mi Llave
Somos una entidad sin ánimo de lucro, nos anima el fortalecimiento de una sociedad justa que reduce la brecha social mediante la trasformación del concepto de empresa. Creemos en el redireccionamiento de recursos y esfuerzos al servicio de los grupos poblacionales más vulnerables como una de las metas más elevadas de las empresas y grupos económicos.

La principal fuente de ingresos de Fundación Mi llave provendrá de las empresas vinculadas las cuales destinaran sus utilidades para el desarrollo del objeto social de la Fundación, todo bajo un esquema de Economía colaborativa.

Name: (Stray) Animal Foundation Platform
FFLV USA
Description:
FFLV USA is a 501(C)(3) recognized Non Profit Organization dedicated to raising awareness, funds and coordinating support for Food For Life Vrindavan (FFLV).
Since 1990, FFLV is dedicated to serving the poorest of the poor residents of Vrindavan and the Braj region of Uttar Pradesh, India. Particularly girls and women by providing comprehensive, essential human services. Our work includes, providing education, food rations, medical support, prevention of child marriage, distribution of free meals, providing water wells for villages, environment projects, animal protection projects and organic farming.
Currently we have 1500 girls in our three schools. Learn more about our projects and get involved!

Name: For Kids Foundation
Description:
Mission: For Kids Foundation provides one-time funding for the urgent, unmet needs of children (from birth through age 20), who have fallen through the cracks of existing agencies and charitable organizations. 

Follow us on social media:

Name: The MacJannet Foundation
Description: Since 1968, The MacJannet Foundation has promoted international understanding and active citizenship by championing the hands-on educational philosophy of our founders, Donald and Charlotte MacJannet. As pioneers of “learning through doing,” the MacJannets created extraordinary learning environments that connected thousands of young people across national boundaries. Today the MacJannet Foundation continues this work by providing scholarships and grants to cultural exchange programs, supporting the Tufts University European Center in Talloires, France, and co-sponsoring the MacJannet Prize for Global Citizenship for exemplary university student service-learning programs throughout the globe.

Maria Skłodowska-Curie

Name: The Validation Project
Description: The Validation Project is an international organization founded by current college junior Valerie Weisler. Founded in 2013, The Validation Project provides mentorship and social justice support to more than 6,000 young people in 105 countries. The Validation Project's "kindness curriculum" is taught in 1,000 schools, teaching educators how to work with students to solve issues in the community while also fostering the self-worth of their students. The Validation Project's work has been featured by CNN, New York Times, The Meridith Viera Show, Inc., L'Oreal Paris, and other platforms.

Name: Ação Contra a Fome
Description:
In 1993, ordinary people sensitized to Betinho's calls in his Solidarity Campaign, to fight hunger and misery in our country, decided to unite and create the Action Committee Against Hunger in Vila Carrão.

The activities of this Committee, marked by distribution of basic food baskets, began on August 24, 1993 with its first distribution. Since then, the "Action Against Hunger", can be considered one of the oldest and most active solidarity committees that emerged at that time.

Our goal from the beginning was to alleviate the problems of hunger and misery that affect the residents of our neighborhood, especially women and the elderly over 70 years old.

The assisted population is formed by poor families at risk who live in collective housing in the regions of Vila Carrão, Baixo Aricanduva, Vila Antonieta, Vila Nhocuné, Itaquera, Jardim da Conquista and surroundings. They are victims of floods, unemployment, serious or chronic illnesses, people without retirement or pensions, with few or often no resources to guarantee their own livelihood, let alone their dependents.

Name: La Libertad Consulting
Description:
“Is my work in service of justice + liberation for all?”
 
This is the question we must ask ourselves every day as we seek to work collaboratively with others on the most imperative work in philanthropy: the decolonization of the sector through forging unwavering commitments and collaborations with other like-minded philanthropists to do explicitly anti-racist, anti-misogynistic, anti-xenophobic, anti-homophobic and anti-ableist work. 
 
Our work must be grounded in radically disrupting systemic oppression and the many manifestations of cis-gendered white male supremacy that create harm and trauma for all—both individually, organizationally and in our sector. 
 
We are all called to envision and enact liberated and just organizational cultures, truth-telling community advocacy and systems of equitable power and resource sharing.  

We have work to do. Not tomorrow. Not aspirationally. Not incrementally. 
 
Today. Together.

Name: Friends of Club 21
Description: Friends of Club 21 is a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization based in Colorado Springs, CO. Friends of Club 21 exists to celebrate Down syndrome locally and around the world. We exist to see supported communities, social awareness and global generosity for families affected by Down syndrome.

Name: Center for Shared Prosperity
Description: The Center for Shared Prosperity works to bring the vision of a more equitable future for our region into reality through compassion, curiosity and collaboration.  One mistake that institutions have consistently made in the journey towards this goal is the exclusion of their surrounding community within the problem solving process.  This Center will work to bring faculty, staff and students from Carnegie Mellon University together with community-based organizations and community leaders to build an infrastructure based in collaboration, whose goal is to  identify and address structural barriers to equity.

Name: Mission St-Michaels
Description:
For over 95 years, St-Michael’s Mission has been a warm and friendly refuge in the heart of Montreal, providing services to the most disadvantaged and vulnerable members of our community.
 
St. Michael's Mission was founded in 1927 by the Parish of St. John the Evangelist and later supported by the Diocese of Montreal as an outreach to the poor and homeless in Montreal’s downtown core. The Mission is open, inclusive, and respectful of the beliefs and integrity of all people.

Under its red roof, St. Michael’s Mission is an independent not for profit Corporation and today offers a safe and warm shelter, food, socialization and other basic hygiene, medical and clothing services to a multicultural clientele.  Frontline services have evolved to include crisis intervention, as a first step in the process of dealing with mental illness enabling clients to acquire self respect and confidence, find employment, and reintegrate into society. St. Michael's Mission is part of a supported, recognized and integrated network of services that delivers the basic needs to the poor and homeless of Montreal. While there are a number of night shelters located in Montreal, St Michael’s Mission, as a resource centre, provides an important link in the chain of services for the City’s disadvantaged and marginalized population.

Name: Hijaz Community, UK
Description: The Hijaz Community is a leading UK national Islamic umbrella network, and a key member of the International Muslim Organisation which has hubs in over 80 countries. The network is centred on enriching society through individual personal development and action. It benefits from the support of thousands of volunteers from diverse backgrounds e.g. doctors, lawyers and teachers, university students and stay at home parents. The network is active at a grass roots level throughout the UK, including London, Manchester, Birmingham, Bradford and Watford.

Name: Asociación Udutama para el apoyo de minorías étnicas o culturales
Description:
Asociación sin ánimo de lucro, laica e independiente, de cooperación internacional.  Los objetivos que figuran en los estatutos de la asociación ´son:

■promover, preservar, divulgar y fortalecer el legado cultural de las minorías étnicas o culturales que se vean amenazadas por la hegemonía de otras culturas 
■ayudar a mejorar las condiciones socioeconómicas y políticas de las comunidades de minorías étnicas o culturales amenazadas, según las necesidades definidas y acciones coordinadas para y desde las propias comunidades 
■en particular, apoyar la cultura y estructura socioeconómica de los Akha, minoría étnica que vive en el norte de Tailandia, Laos, Myanmar y sur de China.

Name: Gloriamundi
Description:
Gloriamundi Foundation is a private, state-approved, non-profit organisation with no religious or political affiliations.
It assists humanitarian organisations with limited finances around the world by donating and designing promotional material. These include brochures, logos, photo shoots, documentaries, films and websites.


Gloriamundi est une fondation privée, reconnue d’utilité publique, sans but lucratif, sans caractère religieux ou politique.
La Fondation conçoit et réalise gratuitement des brochures, logos, photographies, films, sites internet et tout autre matériel sur support papier ou informatique pour des œuvres humanitaires ou sociales disposant de peu de moyens.

Name: R4 Foundation
Description:
We R4 what is possible.

R4 Foundation will listen, learn and act with agility to help people in need adapt their substantive dreams into meaningful reality.

R4 Foundation is a private foundation created to help people find what is possible by making grants to service providers within four pillars of charitable purpose:

                                                       Education • Community • Wellness • Arts

Name: Deutsch-Japanische Gesellschaft Bielefeld e.V.
Description: Wir, die Deutsch-Japanische Gesellschaft (DJG) Bielefeld, verstehen uns als eine Plattform für den kulturellen Austausch zwischen Japan und Deutschland in Bielefeld und ganz OWL. Die DJG ist Anlaufpunkt für in Ostwestfalen lebende JapanerInnen sowie für an Japan interessierte Deutsche. Um Interesse am jeweils anderen zu wecken und das Kennenlernen zu ermöglichen, organisieren wir verschiedene Veranstaltungen wie Kulturtage, gemeinsame Feste oder Vorträge. Darüber hinaus unterhalten wir eine direkte Verbindung nach Japan über die Partnerschaft mit der Japanisch-Deutschen Gesellschaft Tochigi sowie über den Austausch von SchülerInnen, Studierenden und Reisegruppen. Der Japanische Garten Bielefeld (ein Trockenlandschaftsgarten) am Ausbildungshotel Lindenhof in Bethel wurde von der DJG Bielefeld in Erinnerung an den Besuch des Japanischen Kaiserehepaares im Jahr 1993 10 Jahre später errichtet und 2003 der Öffentlichkeit übergeben. Hier finden regelmäßig Japankulturtage statt.

Name: Found Aid
Description:
Found Aid is a purpose-drive company with the mission make it easy to do good.
We help foundations conquer the rising complexity of philanthropy and need for transparency through collaboration and digitalization to get the best possible outcome for those who need the help.

Name: Breastfeeding Family Friendly Communities
Description: The Breastfeeding Family Friendly Communities (BFFC) initiative is a community-wide program to impact Health Equity, as supported by the World Health Organization guidelines to work with communities to improve breastfeeding support services. The BFFC brings together stakeholders from diverse backgrounds, organizations, agencies, and institutions.    Change may take time; however, the goals of BFFC include advocating for lactation friendly and equitable health care policies and practices for families by supporting healthcare providers to be accountable for top quality and equitable care; helping all families find excellent prenatal and postpartum care; and teaching every family in our community why breast/chestfeeding matters. Every family that wants to nurse will be able to nurse for as long as that family chooses without shame or guilt and with educated and supported choices that are not undermined. By implementing BFFC practices, we will create a more breast/chestfeeding family friendly community to impact health equity for all families.

Name: Civic Leadership Development
Description:
Civic Leadership Development (CLD) was established at the Kelley School of Business at Indiana University in 1988 to promote civic responsibility and citizenship among undergraduate business students. As of the mid-2000s, CLD was staffed by a full-time staff member, full-time graduate student, and a CLD Board of Directors. CLD helps Kelley students understand and appreciate philanthropy, good citizenship, and social responsibility.

While members are encouraged to stay active and be involved, CLD allows them to contribute, even if they are able to commit only a few hours each semester. CLD's long and short-term events provide flexibility to match with your personal goals and availability.

Name: ScoreSide
Description: Scoreside is the best place to connect your passion for sports with action and impact.

Name: Disability in Wales and Africa
Description:
We are a group of individuals and organisations, working through a series of community and institutional Links, fostering solidarity and support between Wales and Africa. The specific role DWA is to ensure the activities of the Links incorporate needs and lives of disabled people, their families, and support agencies. Although some Links are “disability specific”, most of them are associated with Health, Education, Livelihood, Agriculture, and other “mainstream” agendas. The challenge for DWA is to assist Links to include disability issues within these agendas.

Both Wales and Africa are on a developmental road regarding the needs of disabled people, and the challenges being faced. Although Wales has support services, technology and trained personnel, Africa allows the opportunity to do things differently, particularly with its often greater community emphasis . Both Wales and Africa have things to learn from each other.

This website is not just to tell you what is happening within DWA it is also to be a sharing forum to allow readers within Wales, Africa, and beyond, to discuss ideas and demonstrate how they have overcome respective barriers. We want to hear from you, what you have achieved, and why it has worked. Please feel free to engage with us through our blogs. Send us articles and features for the DWA newsletter. Show how disabled people are becoming part of mainstream society.

Name: Equality Through Education Foundation
Description: We believe that the keys to alleviating poverty are universal education, gender equality, and community building. We also believe that regardless of who they are or what their economic status is, people have the capacity to lead useful lives and write their own future. Three principles guide our work.

Name: SisterFriend, Inc.
Description:
WE BELIEVE IT IS THE RIGHT FOR ALL WOMEN TO HYGIENICALLY MANAGE THEIR MENSTRUATION WHEREVER THEY ARE

STREETS | SHELTERS | SCHOOLS | CORRECTIONAL FACILITIES

Name: Leergeld Zuidwest Friesland
Description:
In de gemeentes Súdwest Fryslân en de Fryske Marren wonen momenteel 2000 kinderen die om financiële redenen niet mee kunnen doen aan activiteiten die voor hun leeftijdsgenootjes heel normaal zijn. Zij staan letterlijk aan de zijlijn. Leergeld wil deze kinderen laten meedoen.

In Nederland zijn bijna 100 lokale Stichtingen Leergeld. Stichting Leergeld Zuidwest Friesland (ZWF) is actief in de gemeentes; Sudwest Fryslan en de Fryske Marren. Onze mensen zetten zich in om alle kinderen mee te laten doen. waardoor zij weer opbloeien, kennis en vaardigheden ontwikkelen en trots op zichzelf kunnen zijn.

Meedoen op school en in hun vrije tijd plezier maken met andere kinderen; is voor iedereen. Niet meedoen kan leiden tot gezondheidsproblemen, eenzaamheid, lagere onderwijsprestaties. waardoor ze in een later stadium ook in een armoedesituatie terecht kunnen komen.

Leergeld zegt: Alle kinderen mogen meedoen!

Name: World U.P.
Description:
VISION

World U.P. is a collective of innovative and passionate people who dream of an Understanding and Peaceful global community, where everyone belongs, is celebrated, and is appreciated for our differences.

 

MISSION

We are building our World U.P. by improving access to resources that allow humans to connect, learn, and empower each other through kindness and love.

Name: Project sWish
Description:
We use basketball as a vehicle for reducing street violence and providing mental health resources to young people across Chicago. Our programs aim to create a comfortable environment for addressing the mental health challenges faced in communities affected by intense violence. 

Our flagship program is the Swish League, which is a free basketball league in Chicago's most violent neighborhoods at the highest crime times of the day. We supplement the league with speakers, workshops, and mentorship opportunities for the young people we work with. Our programs are segmented by age, gender, and education level, and we are data-driven when measuring their success. 

Our partners include Chicago Public Schools, the Chicago Park District, Nike, and the Steve Harvey Foundation.

Name: Clemmons Food Pantry
Description:
The Clemmons Food Pantry is a local non-profit organization providing supplemental groceries to residents of Forsyth County. Please learn more about our organization at www.clemmonsfoodpantry.org

Our Mission
We hold strong to our roots as a faith based organization with a mission to help those in need by filling the most basic of needs – nourishing the body. From the start it was determined that The Pantry would not turn away anyone in need, regardless of circumstance. This still holds true today and clients may receive groceries every 60 days no matter their income or situation.

Company Overview
Established in 2004, the Clemmons Food Pantry is a non-profit organization providing supplemental groceries to residents of Forsyth County. Started as a Sunday school project by a small group,

The Pantry has since grown to an award winning community organization that has extended its services to over 130,000 people.

Name: Myanmar Free Ambulance Foundation
Description: Myanmar Free Ambulance Foundation (MFA) is a not-for-profit charity organization registered in Myanmar in 2016. The goal of MFA is to provide emergency health care to remote regions, humanitarian assistance to refugees and IDPs, and support the people of Myanmar with all medical means as they strive to obtain freedom from oppression. That Others Might Live.

Name: Foundation for Health Advancement
Description: We support New Jersey researchers and students who are developing cures for diseases and solutions to health-related needs. Our programs grow new science-based businesses in collaboration with New Jersey-based research institutions and inspire students to pursue STEM majors and careers.

Name: Movos
Description: We aim at empowering entrepreneurs to foster the prosperity of their business while contributing to social and environmental challenges

Name: The Carole Stinson Literacy Foundation
Description:
The Carole Stinson Literacy Foundation (CSLF) was formed in June 2006. We are a charitable foundation dedicated to supporting organizations and activities that will enhance the literacy skills of children and adults. We also seek opportunities to provide public awareness about literacy issues.

The Carole Stinson Literacy Foundation is a tax-exempt 501(c)(3) public charity. Donations are tax deductible in accordance with IRS regulations concerning charitable contributions. Our donor information will never be shared, sold, or traded.

Name: Association of Influencers & Content Creators
Description:
We are influencers and content creators, united for the common goal of ensuring the advancement, growth, and longevity of our industry.

The Association of Influencers & Content Creators (AICC) is the official trade association for American digital creators recognized as Influencers and Content Creators — in order to advocate for rights and to secure protections into the 21st century and beyond. 
 
We create content within four major categories: blogs, video, photography, and audio — and are bloggers, YouTubers, podcasters, social media personalities, and other digital professionals. Our work is seen and heard on the internet, on our owned websites, across every social media platform, and most media distribution platforms. We influence, reach, and inform the masses in America and the world.
 
AICC is committed to organizing and establishing standards for our industry; including diversity and inclusion efforts, access to affordable benefits, preserving and expanding members’ work opportunities, industry-wide legislative protections; and introducing guidelines to preserve the integrity, authenticity, consistency and quality of work.

Name: Moraga Community Foundation
Description: The Moraga Community Foundation is a philanthropic organization that works with the community to build a vibrant future for Moraga.

Name: The Salamanca Foundation
Description:
We believe that all Jews should be able to live with dignity.
The Salamanca Foundation is assisting in rebuilding Jewish communities in Muslim countries, while also helping disenfranchised segments of the population in Israel.

Name: Wish Youth Network Society
Description:
Wish Youth Network Society (WYNS) is a student based, non- profit organization to reach out to children with serious medical conditions and their families. Our mandate is to strengthen and promote the power of hope in those who need it most. With a student body currently dissipated throughout schools from all across the province of BC, WYNS provides an opportunity for students to give back to the community and to connect with like minded youths through interactive volunteerism and fundraisers.

Our partnerships with local hospitals come with holiday surprises, arts/sports workshops, and other events for the children.  The money we raise go to support these events, and further to financially support operation costs of the patients.

Currently, WYNS is in its third year of running, with an expansion plan to other parts of North America.  To partner with us at our Vancouver HQ or for our expansion project, or to sponsor us, visit us at wishyouthnetwork.org, or email us at info@wishyouthnetwork.org!

Name: VNSA - Volunteer Nonprofit Service Association, Inc.
Description:
In 62 years of service to the Phoenix community, VNSA has become synonymous with the largest charity used book sale in the Southwest.  This all-volunteer organization collects, categorizes, and prepares donated books and media throughout the year for the annual VNSA Used Book Sale,  a two-day event held every February at the Arizona State Fairgrounds. All proceeds from the sale benefit local charities, including Arizona Friends of Foster Children Foundation and Literacy Volunteers of Maricopa County.

VNSA began in 1949 as the auxiliary and fundraising arm of the Visiting Nurse Service in Phoenix. When the Visiting Nurse Service became affiliated with a hospital in 1987, the members of VNSA chose to remain active supporting Valley nonprofit agencies. The members adopted a new name (Volunteer Nonprofit Service Association) and continued their signature book sale.

The VNSA Used Book Sale is the sole event of VNSA. Each year, more than 500,000 books, media, and book-related items are available at the sale, attracting thousands of shoppers from all parts of the United States. In 2023, the VNSA Used Book Sale raised more than $400,000 for local charities. Since the first sale in 1957, VNSA has returned more than $9.5 million to our community.

Name: Juneteenth of Palm Beach County, Inc.
Description: Our mission is to commemorate Black culture & history by raising awareness, connecting the community to comprehensive resources, and enrich lives.

Name: RayZo, Inc.
Description:
We are your dedicated team, combining deep industry experience with proven success in grant writing, fund development, and small business marketing. Our practical advice and coaching empower non-profit leaders, while our tailored digital design and marketing solutions help organizations achieve their fundraising goals. As industry experts, we leverage the latest trends to build awareness and expand reach effectively.

RayZo’s team is equipped with the expertise, staff, and corporate support to deliver exceptional service. We adhere to structured accounting practices and utilize an internal oversight system to manage assignments, budgets, and resource allocation efficiently. With a track record of meeting deadlines and delivering results, our team of seasoned communications professionals ensures success in every project we undertake.

Name: 100 Women Who Care Toronto
Description:
Bringing together 100+ Toronto women, who care about local community causes, for one hour, four times a year.  At our meetings, we jointly select a local charity or not-for-profit organization and each write a cheque for $100 to the chosen charity.  At just one meeting, watch how 100+ individual commitments turns into a $10,000 donation for a worthy cause.

If you are committed to creating positive change in our community, but are stretched for time; want to be part of a powerful group of Toronto women making an immediate, direct and powerful impact on charities in need; and want 100% of your donation to go directly to a charity in our community, then visit our website at www.100womentoronto.com to find out how you can be part of this amazing group.

Name: The Zoë Foundation
Description:
Some of the most difficult times for cancer patients are enduring chemotherapy, stem cell or bone marrow transplants.   We aim to provide an outlet for all cancer patients with something to look forward to, as they endure this grueling experience.

The Zoe Foundation funds art programs for cancer patients being treated at the following cancer centers:

-UCSF Benioff Children’s Hospital, California

-Van Elslander Cancer Center, Michigan

-Hartford Hospital, Connecticut 

-Karmanos Cancer Institute, Michigan

-Mercy Medical Center, Maryland

-Henry Ford Cancer Institute, Detroit and Macomb Campus, Michigan


Cancer Centers supported over the last 15 years:  

-University of Michigan Cancer Center, Michigan

-Mount Clemens Cancer Center, Michigan

-St. Joseph Mercy Ann Arbor, Michigan

-Oakwood Healthcare System, Michigan

-Loyola University Health System, Illinois

-Children’s Hospital of Michigan, Michigan


“The Zoe Foundation has been the anchor to our art therapy program for almost a decade. From daily smiles from kids elbow-deep in paint to deep emotional healing through one-on-one therapy, you have truly made a difference. On behalf of thousands of patients and their families, thank you.”

- Michael Towne, Child Life Manager, UCSF Benioff Children’s Hospital San Francisco

100% of all donations are provided to the cancer centers.  The Zoe Foundation is a 501(c)3 non-profit organization.

Description: At CUWLN, we create a network of resources to ensure female students can reach their fullest potential in academia and the workplace. By collaborating with female leaders and professionals, we strive to reveal the potential of all genders in the legal realm.

Description:

Name: Uncork For Hope Foundation
Description:
The Uncork for Hope Foundation's mission is to empower at-risk youth through community partnerships and innovative programming. We accomplish this mission by being the catalyst in building synergies between organizations to go beyond what they would do alone.

Our vision is to build a foundation with the ultimate goal of bringing people together to raise funds to support both existing programming and to encourage new programming for at risk youth. The Foundation will encourage new programming, strengthen existing charities and create greater awareness of the substantial and wide-ranging needs of at-risk youth. We want to encourage cooperation among charities and within the community.

Name: The Bexel Initiative
Description:
The Bexel Initiative is a community development platform designed to expand and develop opportunities within underserved communities, through an emphasis on investment management and entrepreneurship.

Bexel operates as a 501(c)(3), the Bexel Foundation, that sources and distributes capital to expand opportunities within underserved communities, and

Separate from the Foundation, Bexel is also organized as a "Community Development Company," for the establishment of strategic partnerships and community development centered around expanding access, exposure and equity.

Name: Dreams Take Flight Vancouver
Description:
Dreams Take Flight is a national non-profit organization made up entirely of  volunteers 
dedicated to providing the trip of a lifetime to medically, mentally, physically, socially or emotionally challenged children. With the aid of the Air Canada Foundation and local sponsors and businesses we make dreams a reality for special kids across British Columbia. We believe we can make a major difference in these children’s lives by opening a window of possibilities without limitations.

Dreams Take Flight took wing in 1989, when a group of Air Canada employees in Toronto organized a trip that took 70 special needs children on board a DC-9 aircraft to Walt Disney World in Florida.

Fast forward to 1997, when the charity expanded to service eight cities across this great country: Vancouver, Edmonton, Calgary, Winnipeg, Toronto, Ottawa, Montreal and Halifax. Each chapter’s dedicated group of volunteers take a planeload of deserving children to either Disneyland or Disney World for a once in a life time adventure.

Air Canada donates an aircraft to each chapter for the day and the money we raise throughout the year is used to pay for all other expenses incurred. This includes fuel for the aircraft, admission to the park, spending money, souvenirs, gifts and clothing to identify the children and their escorts. We are grateful to our wonderful sponsors who donate services and products to our charity.

By the end of this year, Dreams Take Flight will have taken more than 25,000 children to the happiest place on earth – Disneyland. There they experience the wonder of friendship and magic. Our goal is to help them forget their troubles for one incredible day and to provide them with a lifetime of memories.

Name: Global Partners: Running Waters
Description:
MISSION: to build relationships that will provide financial resources to rural communities in Latin America and meet needs for clean water, food, education, and improved health. We offer opportunities for North Americans to experience reality as it is experienced by people in the developing world. Our goal is to build compassion and solidarity.

Global Partners Running Waters, Inc. (GPRW) is a nonprofit organization, 501(c)(3), established to build relationships through collaboration on water, food, health & education projects in Latin America. Its roots lie in the mission experiences of Sister Jan Gregorcich (SSND), who lived and worked with the people of Guatemala & Honduras during a period of 12 years.

GPRW is building on Sister Jan’s mission work, by connecting friends in the US with those in Latin America. After initial relationships are established, working together on projects deepens a partnership. GPRW is a supported ministry of the School Sisters of Notre Dame, who are dedicated and passionate in their mission to serve the poor. Their mission greatly aligns with that of GPRW. 

GPRW believes that building relationships with the people of Latin America can offer opportunities that:

-Share cultural and spiritual resources.
-Improve health and living conditions.
-Enhance international dialogue.
-Experience the best of humanity.

Since 2003, more than 50,000 people have received clean water- their lives forever transformed.

Join the parrtnership! 
Visit our website at http://globalpartnersrunningwaters.org/index.html 
and follow us on social media (Facebook, YouTube, Instagram, Pinterest)

Name: Global Concern
Description:
Global Concern- Driven By Humans, Not People

Global Concern is related to and working on some of the Burning Concerns & Problems in the world.

At Global Concern we not only try to find the answers but the correct solutions to burning concern and problems of our world
They are:-

Agriculture- Problems in Agriculture, Use of Chemical Pesticides & Fertilizers, Lack of Agricultural Resources, Scarcity of Agriculture land and water etc.
Food Security
Conservation of Natural Resources- Conservation of water, soil, prevention of soil erosion, soil pollution, loss of soil fertility etc
Preservation and Care for Forest & Wildlife- prevention of endangered wild animals, prevention of forest and trees.
Food, Shelter, Clothing
Education and Literacy
Youth & Adolescence- Problems related to youth and adolescence, change in thoughts and mentality, generation gap etc
Women Empowerment- women abuse, women care and women up-liftment, role of women in the society, rape, sexual and physical harassment of women, dowry system.
Child & Child Issues- child labor, child abuse, sexual and physical harassment of young children
Hunger, Poverty & Unemployment
Energy Conservation and Regeneration- alternative and renewable sources of energy, conservation and preservation of energy. 
Human Rights, Social Justice & International Peace
Health & Diseases- Health, physical fitness, Drug prevention, Diseases, genetically transferred diseases, sexually transmitted disease.
Spirituality
We hope to make this world a better place to live and give every social, economical, and democratic right to each and every citizen of this earth.

Name: Instituto A Casa do jardim
Description:
O Instituto A Casa do Jardim começou em 2007. Começou com a nossa indignação ao ver crianças remexendo o lixo atrás de material reciclável. Crianças de todas as idades, tamanhos, cor e sexo, invisíveis para a sociedade! Todas têm sonhos.

No princípio os trabalhos assistenciais eram realizados nas ruas de Santo André. 

Hoje a Casa do Jardim tem sede em imóvel alugado em um espaço adequado para o atendimento de crianças e adolescentes de 4 a 16 anos no período de contraturno escolar.

Name: Streets Ahead
Description:
We are a UK registered Charity who work with schools in economically and socially challenged areas of London, delivering a practical and inspiring course to students aged 14-18. The course is designed to help individuals recognise and develop the skills they need to achieve their own version of success despite the lack of resources they may face.

We believe that outside of academic achievements, the core skills young people need to succeed are a strong sense of self-confidence, the ability for independent thought and a willingness to identify and address their own strengths and weaknesses. We aim to help students develop these, and other soft-skill characteristics, via a simple, accessible modular course.

We want to harness the most disruptive, difficult and challenging students and help society understand that these are the ones that are demonstrating the skills we require to influence change in the world. We celebrate such behaviour at entrepreneurial level but try to eradicate it so early on in youth. We want this to change.

Name: Mekuno Project
Description:
Our Vision: Mekuno Project envisions a gender-equitable world where every girl is empowered to create a hope-filled future of her own design free of gender-based violence. 

Our Mission: We support solutions that work. By connecting and funding projects that eliminate the root causes of FGM and child marriage in Kenya, the conditions are set for hope-filled futures to flourish.

Name: A Alternativa Associação de Assistência ao Excepcional
Description:
A Alternativa – Associação de Assistência ao Excepcional completa 35 anos de fundação em 2019. Nossa missão é proporcionar a oportunidade de um trabalho significativo e realizador para jovens, adultos e idosos com deficiência intelectual e deficiência múltipla. 
Os artesãos atendidos na oficina abrigada de trabalho, recebem orientação e de acordo com suas possibilidades e limitações, desenvolvem trabalhos variados com madeira, mosaico, pintura em tecido, vidro, porcelana e barro, tecelagem, entre outros.    
A Alternativa é uma entidade filantrópica, sem fins lucrativos que depende exclusivamente de doações para se manter. Ao longo do ano, realizamos alguns eventos beneficentes para arrecadação de fundos e buscamos parceiros para ações de longo prazo

Name: OKP Philanthropy & Marketing
Description:
35+ Years I  Experts  I  Advancing Giving I  Practical & Sustainable Solutions 

Working collaboratively, OKP provides customised philanthropic, fundraising, marketing and research solutions to global, national, regional, local and rural organisations.

A wide variety of organisations engage our services - not for profits, universities, schools, health and wellbeing organisations, arts & cultural organisation, the government, large comparisons and philanthropic foundations and families.

OKP have planned and executed hundreds of successful campaigns and raised over $500m through major gifts, capital campaign and endowment initiatives. We thrive on a challenge and are proud our expertise, experience and collective effort  contributes to our clients success.

By appointing OKP, your organisation would be assured of an agency with intimate knowledge of philanthropic and marketing principles and practices, as well as a sound understanding of the industry’s fundamentals as they relate to the people. Name: Education Equal Opportunity Group, Inc.
Description: The Education Equal Opportunity Group is a Non-Profit organization that is committed to decreasing the educational, social, and economic disparities among “at risk” high school students by providing them access to the resources and guidance necessary to excel. We’ve inspired over 14,000 students to change the trajectory of their life through leadership training and career exposure. Help us continue to impact these kid's lives!

Name: AVEC l'Ethiopie
Description:
AVEC L'Ethiopie 

L'association AVEC l'Ethiopie est une association de coopération avec deux collectivités de la zone sud-Gondar; Debré Tabor et le Farta Wereda.
Le travail de coopération se fait en priorité en matière d'eau  et d'assainissement mais également sur des thématiques annexes telles que l'Education, le Sport ou la Santé. 


The association AVEC l'Ethiopie is a cooperative association with two communities of the South Gondar zone; Debré Tabor and Farta Wereda.
The work of cooperation is done in priority in the field of water and sanitation but also on related themes such as Education, Sport or Health.

Name: AMOUR asbl
Description:
Activités Multiples des Orphelins Unis pour la Reconstruction de l'Espérance, AMOUR en sigle, est une organisation sans but lucratif de droit congolais. Elle contribue à l'épanouissement socio-économique des enfants orphelins dépourvus de tout encadrement communautaire et vivant avec des problèmes sérieux des maladies comme la malnutrition chronique et autres.
L'objectif principal de AMOUR asbl est de contribuer à l'épanouissement socio-économique des enfants orphelins.

Name: Kathleen Loehr  & Associates LLC
Description: Kathleen E. Loehr, Principal of Kathleen Loehr & Associates, has a three-decade career in the nonprofit sector that includes leadership within the fields of early childhood education, fundraising, disaster fundraising, women in philanthropy, international non-governmental organizations, and nonprofit effectiveness.  Her fundraising background includes leading the development for the American Red Cross, Save the Children, the International Crisis Group, and key departments at Cornell University. She is a member of the Advisory Council for the Women’s Philanthropy Institute.  Her women’s philanthropy expertise results from her work across twenty years with the President’s Council of Cornell Women, American Red Cross’ Tiffany Circle, and leading the strategies for Duke, Drexel, University of San Francisco, William & Mary and National Geographic to more deeply engage with women.   She is also a skilled leadership coach, giving her the ability to understand what is preventing individuals or teams from achieving their goals.  The combination of her consulting, strategy design and coaching can result in 85 percent more successful outcomes in relationships, dollars raised, processes, and communication to create breakthrough changes.

Name: Mayberry Memorial
Description: Mayberry Memorial is a Lynch syndrome non-profit with the mission of raising awareness and funds for this genetic condition. With a history of barbecue cook off fundraising events in memory of Steve Mayberry, we are continuing to evolve to spread the message about Lynch syndrome to a larger community.

Name: Casa Hogar Belén, A.C.
Description:
Somos una organización de carácter no lucrativo y no damos cabida a ninguna discriminación de tipo social, racial o religioso.
El objetivo que perseguimos es aplicar nuestros mayores esfuerzos y medios para encontrar los mecanismos más adecuados para proteger y brindar asistencia a los menores desamparados y en situaciones vulnerables, brindándoles servicios de alimentos, vestido, salud, educación y demás servicios que los aprovechen, todos estos servicios se brindan los 365 días del año.  Los servicios que proporcionamos a nuestros beneficiarios, son con el propósito de forjar ciudadanos con valores y futuros excelentes jefes de familia.
Para lograr nuestro objetivo, implementamos acciones a corto y largo plazo encaminadas a proporcionar los satisfactores y brindar protección necesaria para lograr el normal desarrollo y formación de los menores desamparados, con la finalidad de integrarlos adecuadamente a la sociedad.
Estudiamos la problemática de los menores desamparados y el planteamiento de posibles soluciones mediante la implementación de programas dirigidos a lograr su bienestar.
Buscamos promover la participación de la sociedad en forma organizada a fin de lograr la solución de los problemas que afectan a los menores desamparados.
Estamos en constante búsqueda de patrocinio financiero, para conseguir los servicios necesarios para la persecución de los fines expresados con anterioridad y para realizar construcciones y/o restauraciones a nuestras instalaciones; también buscamos donativos en especie para satisfacer las necesidades básicas de nuestros beneficiarios y donación de tiempo como trabajador voluntario.
Tus donativos son los que verdaderamente marcan la diferencia en la calidad de nuestros servicios!

Name: Cenit Ecuador
Description:
More than 25 years ago, Cenit was created by the Sisters of the Good Shepherd in response to the high numbers of working children in the city of Quito, especially among young girls. Fast forward to 2017, Cenit carries on with its quest to eradicate child labour, promote their rights, and improve their living conditions in the sectors of Camal and San Roque.

Cenit functions both as a NGO and as a community school, providing educational support, health prevention, and cheap meals to the children, while playing an active role in its neighbourghoods daily life through the organization of cultural events and social gatherings. Its full-time ecuadorian staff works in conjunction with a dedicated team of volunteers from all horizons in order to support their communities and safeguard the future of their children.

Name: Sherburne County Area United Way
Description:
At SCAUW we unite people and resources to impact lives in the Sherburne County area. As a result, we are able to help fund local human service agencies and nonprofits. We hold Community Connect in the fall at the Elk River High School as a way to offer education, resources and services to people in need.

Sherburne County Area United Way serves the communities of Big Lake, Elk River, Princeton, Otsego, Santiago and Zimmerman.

Our purpose is exclusively educational and charitable.

Name: Walker Family Events Foundation

Walker Family Events Foundation is a non-profit organization dedicated to serving veterans who are experiencing homelessness and hardship.

Description:
DC Angels provide a safe space for the children of our country. 

Besides our passion for activism and children's rights, we offer services that fulfil the circle of courage. With the help of trained counsellors, psycho-social programmes and life coaches, we can give our children independence to pursue their best future.

Name: Movember Auction
Description:
Movember Auction fokuserer på prostata- og testikelkræft gennem informationsdeling og slutteligt en uges internetauktion. Overskuddet går til Movember.
Beskrivelse
November står for døren – Dermed den årlige chance vi mænd har for at sætte fokus på prostata- og testikelkræft igennem ”Movember” konceptet (se http://dk.movember.com/). 

Vi afholder en internetauktion, hvor overskuddet går til Movembers officielle indsamling, så vi kan hjælpe til bekæmpelsen af disse kræfttyper og forbedre livskvaliteten hos de mænd der lever med prostata- eller testikelkræft.

Igennem hele november søger vi sponsorater, så vi slutteligt, når ”Movember”afslutningen nærmer sig, kan sende en ordentligt bunke penge afsted.

Kan du bidrage med noget?
Som virksomhed eller enkeltperson? 

Hvis ikke; gro et overskæg, spred det gode budskab og deltag i vores internetauktion, der starter i slutningen af november!

Name: De Madera
Description: De Madera, es un Colectivo Multidisciplinario de creación (Diseño, Audiovisual, Fotografía, Pintura, Música) cualquier otro tipo de manifestación artística o idea generadora de conciencia, cambio y cultura. A partir de la Colaboración, Gestión, Promoción y Producción. Creemos podemos crecer, si empezamos a creer. Estamos #EnConstrucciónSiempre.

Name: Joshua Kernohan Memorial Fund
Description:
To honor the life of Joshua Kernohan 1997-2015.

In 2016, the Joshua Kernohan Memorial Fund was established to raise funds and awareness for youth golf and suicide prevention in Joshua's memory.  Joshua loved golfing with his father, family and friends and at Carrollwood Day School as 5 year starter on the Varsity golf team. The Foundation allows us to remember and embrace Joshua’s love of family, friends, and community all the while supporting The First Tee of Tampa Bay and the American Foundation for Suicide Prevention.

With the overwhelming support of family and friends, we are going into our third year of the annual Joshua Kernohan Memorial Golf Tournament with the tournament scheduled for April 15, 2018 at the Cheval Golf Club. We thank everyone for their participation, sponsorships, donations, silent auctions donations, and attendance at dinner in loving support, remembrance and celebration of Joshua and hope to continue to grow the event year after year.

Name: The Empow(her) Network
Description:
Empow(her) Network provides young women in Ottawa opportunities for growth and networking while encouraging them to assume positions of leadership.

We are a network of young women for young women. 
Our mission is to empower young women to see themselves as the leaders of tomorrow by providing them with motivation, information, and leadership development opportunities today.

Name: Patriots Hockey Program
Description:
Patriots Hockey is an all-volunteer organization comprised of active duty service members and veterans from the every branch of the US Armed Forces. Patriots Hockey’s goals are to give back to the active/veteran military communities through Charity Ice Hockey Games, Adult League Teams, and Community Relations Projects.

Patriots Hockey Program is a program of Fighting For Home Sports, a 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization.

Name: Sea Mercy
Description:
SAILING WITH A GREATER PURPOSE

Often separated by hundreds of miles, one of the greatest challenges for the survival of the thousands of "at risk" remote islands across the South Pacific is staying connected to health care, disaster response, education and economic development services that are only available on the few primary islands. In search of proper health care, education and work opportunities to support their families, a growing exodus of talent and youth are relocating to the primary islands. Adding to that challenge, natural disasters (cyclones) are becoming more common and powerful each year, further depleting what little infrastructure (water, food production, shelter, income and health care) is in place. 

Sea Mercy’s mission has been, and will continue to be about bringing hope and a future to the forgotten and often neglected people living on the remote islands of the South Pacific. Since 2012, Sea Mercy and our partners have slowly been connecting the remote islands to these much-needed services by creating a local Sea Bridge of volunteer yachts, captains and experts to assist our island nation partners.

Free Health Care Bridge - Bringing reliable and comprehensive Health Care services (dental, medical and eye care) to the remote island communities

Disaster Response & Recovery Bridge - Delivering emergency food, water, shelter, and medical aid following natural disasters (cyclones, earthquakes, tsunamis) including the tools and ongoing supplies needed to rebuild devastated remote island communities

Education & Training Bridge – Providing local and international education, research, and training opportunities bringing balance and fairness to the remote island citizens 

Economic Development Bridge – Empowering those living on the remote islands to return to the healthy, self-sustaining and thriving communities they once were

To learn more about our programs, please visit our website at: www.seamercy.org.

Name: Fundacion Filantropia
Description:
The Universal Foundation for the Videogame users' protection and information (Filantropia) founded in Venezuela in 2009 in consequence of the promulgation of the Law for Banning of warlike videogames.
Filantropia is a non-profit non-government organization.

Name: HeartCoin
Description: HeartCoin es la moneda solidaria que incentiva y multiplica buenas acciones (donaciones y voluntariados), utilizando blockchain para automatizar y crear un innovador mercado del bien y de sostenibilidad, canalizando toda generosidad de personas, organizaciones y empresas que dispongan de voluntad, donaciones y causas sociales. Somos una mezcla entre un "Uber del bien", una "Teletón digital permanente" y un "sistema de fidelización de puntos sociales".

Name: CLIMBING FOR KIDS
Description:
Climbing for Kids on Friday Aug. 2, 2019

Climbing for Kids is one of Colorado’s most anticipated summer family events. The charity 14’er mountain climb benefits Children’s Hospital Colorado, supporting Colorado children with mental health challenges.

The Pediatric Mental Health Institute (PMHI) at Children's Colorado served more than 6,500 children last year, but there is still much work to do. The suicide rate in Colorado is the 7th highest in the nation and suicide is the second leading cause of death among adolescents in our state. One in five youth are affected by mental illness and only one of three of those affected receive mental health services. On average, youth struggle with symptoms of mental health issues for eight to 10 years before accessing care. We know that early access to quality mental health care significantly improves outcomes, so we need to let families know it is necessary to speak up and seek services for their children. 

Climbing for Kids aims to drive awareness and financial support for the care of children and families who require early prevention, ongoing therapy, and access to the medical professionals who can make a difference for kids who need it most…for the kids who have a much larger mountain to climb.

Whether you’ve climbed a 14’er before or not, there’s a way to help the cause. 

Join Us@http://www.childrenscoloradofoundation.org/events/climbing-for-kids/

Name: Gum Moon AWRC
Description:
Empowering immigrant women & families to build a better future.

Gum Moon Women’s Residence & the Asian Women’s Resource Center address the unmet needs of women & families in transition by providing a safe sanctuary to live in and enrichment programs that help develop life skills.

Gum Moon’s core services include: providing a sanctuary for survivors of domestic violence, human trafficking and sexual assault, where they can stabilize their lives and gain independence; affordable permanent housing and other underprivileged women; and through our Asian Women’s Resource Center, family support and parent support programs that set up children and families for success.

Name: Técnica Sem Fronteiras Brasil
Description:
Técnica sem Fronteiras Brasil é uma associação criada em Araranguá, no estado de Santa Catarina, com intuito de apoiar estudantes de engenharia na implementação de tecnologias de baixo custo em regiões em desenvolvimento, visando à melhoria das condições de vida de países em desenvolvimento. A ideia surgiu na Alemanha, na cidade de Nuremberg em 2010, no qual existe a associação fundadora do Técnica sem Fronteiras.
Para atingir nosso objetivo, trabalhamos com projetos em três áreas de atividades:
