1.	Coordenando grupos de desenvolvimento cooperativo para que façam uso eficaz de recursos disponíveis;
2.	Fornecendo treinamento e educação a populações locais para que elas mesmas promovam mudanças. 
3.	Estimulando o desenvolvimento sustentável, através, por exemplo, de iniciativas de microempresas.  

Através dessas ações, queremos usar nosso conhecimento técnico para ajudar significativamente outras pessoas. Se essas ideias inspiraram você, nós adoraríamos tê-lo trabalhando conosco!

Name: Hack the Crisis Sweden
Description: Hack the Crisis Sweden is an online hackathon with focus on the social challenges caused by the ongoing pandemic, Covid-19. The hackathon is a context for all parts of society to contribute to finding solutions which could help solve these challanges– public sector, private sector, academia, non-profit organizations and citizens all came toghether and colloborated to join in the fight.

Name: Alec Dickson Trust
Description:
The Alec Dickson Trust supports young people who, through volunteering or community service, aim to enhance the lives of others, particularly those most marginalised by society. 

Applications for grants, not normally in excess of £500, are invited from individuals aged 30 and under or from small groups of young people who believe their actions to be in pursuit of the aims of the Trust.

The Trust is run solely by the board of Trustees. In the spirit of Alec Dickson and of the Trust, all Trustees are young people aged under 30 with a commitment to volunteering or community service. Trustees meet quarterly to agree funding decisions and Trust priorities for future months.

Name: Association Internationale d'aide au Développement(AIAD)
Description: The International Association for Development Aid  founded in Brazzaville, Republic of Congo, is a non-governmental, humanitarian, independent, private and structured organization. Its services aim to improve the human development index in general, self-promotion and community empowerment, and specifically contribute to the achievement of the target countries of the sustainable development goals, as well as environmental protection.

Name: The Gratitude Project
Description:
An organization helping others to fall in love with the life they already have. The purpose of The Gratitude Project is to improve personal relationships, workplace environments and overall quality of life through the practice of intentional gratitude. 

Founded by Rachael Teare

Name: Lions Club Maastricht Mondial
Description:
Lions Club Maastricht Mondial (LCMM) is a non-profit organisation (under the Lions Club International umbrella). LCMM is one of the few English-speaking clubs in the Netherlands, and consists of 11 different nationalities and 72% of the club are non-Dutch.

The LCMM's main objective is to raise funds to support local and international charitable causes. We organize several key events during the year, one of which is the International MayFair, which brings expats and locals together to mix and mingle for a day at a unique location within Limburg. During the MayFair there is usually an auction or a raffle, from which the proceeds will be donated to a good cause. 

Another event that is becoming a trademark is the Lions Gala. The main beneficiary for the proceeds of the Gala is usually announced prior to the event and last year it has benefited the Children's department of the AZM hospital in Maastricht.

Name: The Wandering Voice
Description:
The Wandering Voice is a social enterprise based in Hong Kong, dedicated to facilitate inclusion of migrant domestic workers, refugees & ethnic minorities through media and immersive programs. It also publishes media content which humanizes the depiction of the these communities - the 10%.
Approximately 1 out of every 10 people in Hong Kong is either an ethnic minority, migrant worker or refugee. Due to various reasons including segregation and social misunderstandings, there is a lack of trust and opportunity for this 10%. We want to create a systematic change to this cycle - using media and immersive programs to bring people together. It is our goal to help bring about universal access to things like upward mobility and social inclusion by bringing about a change in social structures and maximizing social initiatives.

Name: Give Basic Needs
Description:
Why was Give Basic Needs created?

Many in our communities go without basic need items such as food, shelter and medical services. We created this project in order to reduce the suffering people endure, while recognizing the need for more happiness in the world. This understanding led us to form an organization where we randomly give essential resources to those in need.

Organization Mission

At Give Basic Needs, our mission is to embody “Small Acts. Big Happy.” as an international nonprofit. We partner with Private Operating Foundations, like the Colussi Family Foundation, to deliver critical charitable services—including shelter, food, and medical care—to economically disadvantaged individuals and families. Our random acts of giving are designed to cultivate community happiness and reduce suffering, inspiring those we touch to do the same.

Organization Vision

Give Basic Needs aims to reduce suffering and maximize happiness for all of humanity.

Motto
Small Acts. Big Happy!

Name: Compassionate Farming Education Initiative (CFEI)
Description:
The Compassionate Farming Education Initiative is a 501c3 vegan educational non-profit organization based out of southeast Florida. CFEI is committed to creating conscious, educated, empowered consumers; thereby shifting mass industry towards vegan, sustainable products. We help people to transition their diets and lifestyle into an ethical vegan model, while learning about animal sentience and the rich emotional and intellectual capacities of typically “farmed" animals. We do this through outreach and education, utilizing online and open-source materials.  

We seek to shift the old paradigm of farming. We believe that Compassionate Farming is Plant-Based Farming. It is an essential shift, for our world is in peril - and animal agriculture is the leading cause of greenhouse gas emissions, pollution, deforestation, water depletion, ocean acidification, species extinction and world hunger. The animals suffer, our planet suffers and our health suffers.

We aim to educate the public in the following areas:

Current factory farming practices and their impact on our health and the world
The meat industry’s marketing myth of ”humane” animal farming
Healthy, convenient and affordable plant-based food alternatives
The personal and global impact of our food choices
The psychological facets of animal ethics, speciesism and carnism
Targeted, effective advocacy techniques for animal rights advocates
Local access to Farm Animal Sanctuaries where people can get to know the animals face to face

Via farm animal rescue sanctuaries, people have the opportunity to deeply connect with rescued farm animals on location. Where sanctuaries are not readily accessible, we will offer open-source curriculum designed for digital download distribution to schools, community centers, and small outreach groups. These services will be at no cost to the consumer, through private funding. We are currently developing this curriculum and will unveil its content shortly.

Name: Science Partnership Fund
Description: The mission of the Science Partnership Fund is to facilitate foundation and corporate support for crowdfunding projects in physics and related areas.  We recognize that today’s potentially revolutionary scientific endeavors do not always lend themselves to traditional grant-based funding, and many promising technical innovations are not yet ripe for venture capital investment. Crowdfunding fills this funding gap and allows the public to participate in the scientific and entrepreneurial process.

Name: The LEV Foundation
Description:
About The LEV Foundation

The LEV Foundation has been created in honor of Daniel D. Levian to carry on the spirit of his life, the radiance of his energy, and the passion of his values, in order to foster productive, balanced lifestyles among our up-and-coming youth. Despite the remarkable advancements achieved by our community, specifically, the notable accomplishments of the contemporary generation, much of our youth is unquestionably entering a path of unbalance and even recklessness.

The LEV Mission

The LEV Foundation is committed to impacting youth to lead productive, balanced lifestyles. The youth are at the core of LEV’s novel approach, which strives to elevate awareness and empower individuals with a mindset to promote vigilance and responsibility. The LEV foundation will achieve its promise through innovative educational programs, special events, and community services.

Name: Cane Rosso Rescue
Description:
Cane Rosso Rescue is a 501(c)(3) dog rescue based in Dallas, Texas.

We focus on rescuing, fostering and finding furever homes for sporting breed and sporting breed mix dogs. We offer both a fostering and center based approach for dogs we are rescuing, allowing us to maximize the number we can save at any one time.

If you are interested in volunteering, fostering, adopting or donating please visit our website.

Name: European Us Girls
Description:
A double award-winning programme, Us Girls was designed to increase and sustain young women’s participation in sport & physical activity across some of the the most disadvantaged communities in the UK and Europe. Through successful networking and influencing, StreetGames aims to support and change the behaviour and practice of community sport institutions in order to make female sports participation a routine part of their core work in communities challenged by poverty and disadvantage.

The Us Girls key message is #funfitnessfriends and is about removing the barriers which affect girls accessing sport, fitness and exercise. Us Girls successful practises include: female-only sessions, lifestyle role models, reward schemes, wellbeing and confidence inspiring activities, empowerment and the development of women and girls as motivators, volunteers, and peer leaders.
The programme launched in England in 2011 as a two year initiative to get 30,000 young females (aged 16-25 years) more active. The initiative received £2.3 million of National Lottery investment from Sport England as part of their campaign to tackle the gender gap in sport. The project initially focused on 50 disadvantaged areas across England and, with over 34,000 young women taking part in the initial sessions, that target was soon exceeded.

In 2013, Us Girls won the public vote for the National Lottery Award for Best Sports Project.

In 2015, Us Girls was launched in Wales, focusing on females aged 13-19 living in deprived areas who were either inactive or semi-active. To date, the Us Girls programme in Wales has reached over 5,500 female participants and has involved 130 new Us Girls volunteers. The programme was a roaring success and was named the Sporting Experience of the Year for 2017. The prize, handed out as part of Wales Sport Awards was granted to StreetGames in recognition of their efforts to energise and empower disadvantaged young women through sport and physical activity.

Name: Rehema Home US
Description: At Rehema Home Nairobi and Rehema Home Bukura, missionaries and Kenyan staff work hard to rescue orphaned and abandoned children in their communities. Rehema Home US exists solely to support these teams by raising funds and awareness.

Name: CFUW Milton & District
Description:
who we are...

The Canadian Federation of University Women (CFUW) is an equality-seeking advocacy organization of women working to enhance women’s rights and the lives of girls and women in Canada and around the world.

The Milton and District club was formed in 1962, its members dedicated advocates for gender parity, raising awareness of women’s issues and promoting continuing education for women and girls.

The club has held an Annual Used Book Sale for 46 years to raise funds for scholarships for young women, organized election forums for National and Provincial elections and forged partnerships within the Town of Milton and Region of Halton.

Name: Bing Consulting Services, Inc.
Description:
Bing Consulting Services, Inc. is a full-service event production and consulting firm, serving nonprofit organizations, corporations and individuals.

Our success lies in the details. With an acute attention to the specific needs of each project, our ability to execute on both the grand and minute levels results in the unforgettable.

Our many years of experience include strong partnerships with the many people and players needed to create extraordinary moments. Together, as a team, we make magic happen.

With a strong background in management, fund development, and event production, BCS is poised to both bring these skills to corporations and nonprofits alike. Consulting services for nonprofits include donor strategies and interim director contracts.

Name: Association Solidarité Belgique Sénégal
Description: L'Association Solidarité Belgique Sénégal intervient au Sénégal dans différents domaines: accès à l'eau, volontariat, éducation, tournage...

Name: Alpha Omega Sorority
Description:
Alpha Omega Sorority focuses on philanthropy work, overall sisterhood support, academic success, and positive social integration. Alpha Omega is a local sorority meaning everything we do derives from our founding at Laurier. Our sisters in Waterloo have created our own by-laws, which includes a strict no hazing policy. The Alpha Omega motto is “Harmony through Individuality” and sisters genuinely appreciate each young woman’s uniqueness. Although we may have our own strengths and weaknesses, we come together to work hard for our education, raise money for the people and animals that need support, and strive to be the best we can be socially, physically, and emotionally. 

Our main charity is Anselma House, run by Women's Crisis Services of Waterloo Region. Anselma House is a shelter for women and their children who have dealt with domestic violence and abuse. Through the shelter, women are able to get the necessities and support they need to get back on their feet through rehabilitation and more. Alpha Omega raises money for Anselma House mainly through our annual "Breakfast in Bloom" charity brunch and our bi-annual "Guilty Pleasures" all-you-can-eat bake sale. 

Our second philanthropic endeavour is Black Women in Motion, an organization based in Toronto, ON that supports Black womxn and survivors of sexual violence. Alpha Omega Raises money for BWIM through our Greek Week event each year. 

In the past we have done other fundraisers for Save The Manatee Foundation; these have been "AΩ Game Show", "Singing Under the Sea", "AΩ Game Show", and more.

Name: The Rapid Foundation
Description: The Rapid Foundations goal is to provide easy access, low cost technologies to people with the aim of empowering them to design, create and invent.

Name: The Watson-Brown Foundation
Description: Watson-Brown Foundation, Inc. was established in 1970 by the late Walter J. Brown primarily as a college scholarship program. Watson-Brown is among Georgia’s largest private foundations, with annual giving totaling more than $6 million. Currently, the Foundation funds over 900 students studying at 140 colleges and universities across the country. In addition to its scholarship program, the Watson-Brown Foundation also operates three historic sites and house museums and maintains a grant program that funds historic preservation and programs in the humanities.

Name: Black Youth Network
Description:
Black Youth Network, Inc., is a nonprofit organization with a mission of connecting and empowering young African-American leaders and youth-led organizations. Black Youth Network seeks to provide funding, mentorship, networking and resource opportunities for young leaders and youth-led organizations.

Black Youth Network organizes digital and in-person convenings of some of America’s top young, Black and gifted minds to strengthen relationships and build opportunities for support, networking and collaborations. Black Youth Network is composed of over forty young leaders ranging from nonprofit founders, motivational speakers and business owners, to published authors, award-winning musicians and renown activists.

Name: Peque Innova
Description:
Peque Innova es un programa de educación gratuito basado en un trabajo de talleres especializados en áreas STEAM. Estos talleres están destinados a despertar la pasión en las ciencias en niños de 5-12 años, que en general no pueden acceder a este tipo de oportunidades y/o educación.
Nuestros principales beneficiarios son niñas y niños de escuelas públicas, áreas rurales, hospitales y orfanatos. De esta manera, somos un equipo predispuesto a toda voluntad en conocer la vida de estos niños, padres y/o tutores, con el fin de crear un vínculo de empatía que nos dirige a todos hacia una visión común.

Este Programa está financiado a través de la red de becarios Inspira de la Embajada de Estados Unidos. El equipo está liderado por jóvenes becarios de los programas: Fulbrigth, National Youth Science Camp, FISE, Campamento de innovación, Winter Institute, Youth Ambassadors, Access y MLK.

Name: The Overton Project
Description: The Overton Project is a social impact entity focused on citywide initiatives in Birmingham, Alabama

Name: Institut d'Optique Sans Frontières (IOSF)
Description:
L’Institut d’Optique Sans Frontières (IOSF) est l’association humanitaire de l’Institut d’Optique Graduate School (IOGS).
Les missions sont à la fois locales et internationales. 

Chaque année un groupe d'entre 5 et 10 élèves partent à l'étranger pour réaliser divers missions auprès des populations locales. Cette année, l'IOSF part un mois en Moldavie pour aider à rénover une école et être au contact des enfants locaux.


Deuxièmement, l'IOSF a pour mission de mettre en place des actions locales en régions parisiennes, comme des collectes alimentaires et de vêtements en partenariat avec le Secours Populaires Français de Palaiseau, l'organisation de maraudes, ou encore une aide à d’autres associations solidaires, afin d'apporter notre soutien aux personnes dans le besoin autour de nous.

Si vous êtes intéressés pas notre association et si vous souhaitez apporter une quelconque aide, n’hésitez pas à nous contacter par mail ou par les réseaux sociaux !

Contact :
Mail : humanitaire.iosf@gmail.com
Facebook : https://www.facebook.com/iosfhumanitaire
Instagram : https://www.instagram.com/iosf_supoptique/

Name: TheDutch4Kids
Description: TheDutch4Kids is een onafhankelijke stichting die activiteiten rondom golf organiseert met het doel geld in te zamelen voor kansarme en hulpbehoevende kinderen in de wereld. De opbrengsten van de projecten waar TheDutch4Kids zich voor inzet, worden in samenwerking met gerenommeerde (internationale) stichtingen, aan vooraf bepaalde “kinderprojecten” besteed.

Name: Eagles for Children
Description:
Eagles for Children is a network of private golf clubs whose members have pledged a dollar amount every time an eagle is scored on their course. At the end of every season, local nonprofits that serve children are invited to apply for grants. The members at the participating clubs decided which children’s charities 100% of their funds raised will be granted!

In 2023, 20 private golf clubs raised and donated $1,337,540 to 155 local children's charities. Since inception in 2012, Eagles for Children has donated over $6.1 million ... FOR THE KIDS!

Name: Hmong Impact Giving Network
Description: The Hmong Impact Giving Network is a national intermediary and grantmaking organization that strives to cultivate effective Hmong philanthropists, strengthening Hmong nonprofits and investing in collaborative solutions and relationships to tackle our community’s social challenges. The Hmong Impact Giving Network is fiscally sponsored by Social Good Fund.

Name: Stem Cells For A Cure
Description:
For A Cure Foundation - Advancing Scientific Stem Cell Research

 - CUTTING EDGE SCIENCE
 - REGENERATIVE MEDICINE
 - STEM CELL TECHNOLOGIES
 - FUNDS DIRECTLY INTO LABS

Our mission is to accelerate stem cell research and expedite breakthroughs to cure the major diseases of our time. This research promises to find cures and therapies for cancer, heart disease, Arthritis, Diabetes, Parkinson’s, spinal cord injury and other major diseases.

The evolving For A Cure Foundation will include humanitarians, environmentalists, scientists, celebrities and think tanks to unite people of the world as one.

Unite and fight the illnesses of the world – One love

Name: Idaho Housing Foundation
Description: Working to enhance safe, stable and affordable housing throughout Idaho.

Name: Croix-Rouge jurassienne
Description:
La Croix-Rouge jurassienne fait partie du plus vaste réseau humanitaire de volontaires au monde.

Association locale, elle soutient les personnes vulnérables du canton du Jura, sans discrimination aucune. 

Elle contribue ainsi à plus de paix et d’humanité pour les habitants du Canton du Jura.

Familles en situation précaire, personnes âgées souffrant de solitude : leur détresse n’est pas toujours visible ; elle est pourtant bien réelle. 


C’est au quotidien que la Croix-Rouge jurassienne leur apporte une aide concrète. Elle leur permet de passer une période difficile, de mieux s’intégrer dans la société, de ne pas rester seules.

Name: Playground of Hope
Description: Playground of Hope is a Tokyo-registered non-profit organization that works towards providing children in at-risk communities such as children’s homes and disaster zones with access to essential outdoor play equipment.

Name: Engineers Without Borders - Northeast Ohio Professional Chapter
Description:
In the world’s toughest places, Engineers Without Borders USA (EWB-USA) is partnering with communities to meet their basic human needs. We’re building footbridges to provide pathways to opportunities. We’re installing solar panels to bring light where it is dark. We’re digging for water so hope can spring from the ground. Each project builds the foundation for a community to thrive for years to come. 

But it takes more than materials to build a strong foundation.EWB-USA’s 16,800 members are the heartbeat of our organization. These members volunteer their time, their energy and their expertise in pursuit of our vision of a world where every community has the capacity to sustainably meet their basic human needs. 

Join the Northeast Ohio Professionals chapter of Engineers Without Borders USA and help build a better world through engineering projects that empower communities to meet their basic human needs and equip leaders to solve the world’s most pressing challenges.

Our Vision

EWB-USA’s vision is a world in which every community has the capacity to sustainably meet their basic human needs.

Name: Chi Omega Fraternity Pi Kappa Chapter
Description:
Chi Omega is an intergenerational women’s organization forever committed to our founding purposes:

Friendship
Personal Integrity
Service to Others
Academic Excellence and Intellectual Pursuits
Community and Campus Involvement
Personal and Career Development

The Pi Kappa Chapter strives to live by these purposes and better each woman who chooses to spend their four years with us. We hope to spread our experience in Pi Kappa beyond our college years through connecting and networking with alumni.

Name: Fundación Aljaraque
Description:
La Fundación Aljaraque se constituyó el día 6 de noviembre de 1997 y nace en 1997 con una clara vocación de ayuda a los sectores de población más desfavorecidos y en especial a los más desprotegidos

Se encuentra inscrita por Resolución de 12/05/1998 en el Registro de Fundaciones dependiente del Ministerio de Educación con el nº 387.
Desde su constitución ha rendido cuentas puntalmente ante el Protectorado del que depende y está al día en todas sus obligaciones contables, legales y fiscales.

A pesar de la no obligatoriedad y para mayor transparencia y seguridad, la Fundación ha auditado las cuentas anuales de algunos ejercicios anteriores, información que está a disposición de la entidad que así lo solicite.

Name: Les Femmes Invisibles
Description:
Les Femmes Invisibles est une association loi 1901 d'aide aux personnes sans-abris, et de sensibilisation aux situations des femmes et familles précaires. Reconnaissables à leur gilet bleu ciel sur le dos et leurs cabas remplis à ras bord, les bénévoles distribuent près de 700 kits d'hygiène chaque semestre lors des maraudes de l'association. Dans chacun des kits, des produits de première nécessité : dentifrice et brosses à dents, brosses à cheveux, serviettes hygiéniques, couches pour bébé, savons, préservatifs, crème hydratante, mouchoirs ou maquillage. L'ensemble coûte entre 15 et 20 euros.

Fondée en octobre 2018, l'association compte 70 membres bénévoles. Son siège se trouve à Montpellier. C'est l'histoire de Laura, jeune femme enceinte sans-abris, qui poussera les bénévoles à lancer un appel à l'aide sur les réseaux sociaux et ainsi s'aventurer dans la création de l'association.

La maraude de Noël devient une tradition au sein de l'association, où les bénévoles distribuent des cadeaux aux enfants des familles sans-abris le 24 décembre au soir. Les Femmes Invisible est présente chaque année lors de la marche organisée à Montpellier pour la journée internationale des droits des femmes. L'association organise des événements (soirée cinéma, vide-grenier, stand foires...) et des collectes de fonds. Certains événements, notamment celui organisé par Les Mixeuses solidaires, permettent de récolter beaucoup d'argent destiné à la création des kits. L'association développe depuis ses débuts des partenariats avec d'autres associations ainsi que des commerces, pour développer par exemple les dons, les cafés et pains suspendus, et les distributeurs gratuits de lingettes hygiéniques.

L'association s'engage à distribuer des kits d'hygiène dans les résidences universitaires et au CROUS de Montpellier depuis la crise sanitaire liée au COVID-19. Le confinement a rendu les étudiants en grande précarité beaucoup plus vulnérables.

Name: GDP Foundation
Description:
Covid-19 has raised a million questions related to our existence. But at the same time, it has triggered many bright minds to go back to the drawing board and sketch ideas that can make our world a better place.

The GDP Foundation gives these creative highbrows all the confidence they need to chase their dream project. That's because we see immense potential in every idea. And so do you. So let's collaborate.

Name: Banque Alimentaire de Moselle
Description: La Banque Alimentaire de Moselle est une association à but non lucratif, située au 2 rue Gaston Ramon à Metz, Grand-Est, France. Fondée en 1987, elle fait partie du plus important réseau de lutte contre la faim et la précarité, avec pour mot d'ordre la lutte contre le gaspillage alimentaire.

Name: GDP Foundation
Description:
Covid-19 has raised a million questions related to our existence. But at the same time, it has triggered many bright minds to go back to the drawing board and sketch ideas that can make our world a better place.

The GDP Foundation gives these creative highbrows all the confidence they need to chase their dream project. That's because we see immense potential in every idea. And so do you. So let's collaborate.

Name: Pallotyńska Fundacja Misyjna Salvatti.pl
Description:
Jesteśmy Fundacją innowacyjną. Pomagamy poprzez edukowanie do przedsiębiorczości. Dlatego właśnie relacje biznesowe z Afryką, organizowanie spotkań B2B i misji biznesowych oraz wyjazdów turystycznych do Afryki, a także afrykański sklep z kawiarnią i import kawy SPECIALITY to domena działalności  gospodarczej Fundacji Salvatti.pl. Nie dajemy Afrykańczykom ryby, ani wędki, ale kupujemy od nich rybę, którą potrafią sobie złowić sami. Prowadzimy też Dom Mediowy, by zdobyć środki na cele charytatywne. A wszystko po to, by każdego dnia zdobywać środki na pomoc wciąż kolejnym dzieciom w krajach Globalnego Południa, by móc je nakarmić, opłacić szkołę, kupić zeszyty.
Zapraszamy do współpracy!
Zamawiając kawę na www.sklep.salvatti.pl lub w AMAKURU przy al. Solidarności 101 w Warszawie, zmniejszasz rozmiar nędzy na świecie!

Name: CHIPS: play-based clubs, playschemes and events for SEND children aged 3 to 19
Description:
CHIPS stands for Children’s Integrated Playschemes. We are an East Herts charity providing safe, supportive and social clubs, events and playschemes for children aged 3 to 19 with additional needs

Play is vital in any child’s development. It is not only fun, play is a key component of healthy living by enabling good physical, emotional, mental and social development.

CHIPS is run by a group of parents and professionals who all live locally. We organise Saturday short play sessions during school term time and day long play sessions during the school holidays including 3 week sessions during the summer holidays. CHIPS supports over 300 local SEND children and more than 200 families.  Our playschemes run in Ware, Hertford, Bishop's Stortford, Hoddesdon and Cheshunt.

Name: Dhuro
Description: Ne kemi vetëm një qëllim dhe kjo është që të vendosim në mënyrë të duhur dhe të shpejtë vullnetarë të sigurt dhe të sjellshëm që mund të ndihmojnë në komunitetet e tyre lokale me njerëz që ndihma ju duhet më shumë!

Name: Sparrow Nonprofit Solutions
Description:
What sets Sparrow apart is our care for the nonprofit leader behind the impact. We invest in you personally, because genuine love for people can change the world for the better. It’s the only thing that ever has.

WHY SPARROW? 

We don’t accept there’s an either-or choice between excellence or heart.  Not only is it possible to have both–it’s how we serve our clients every day.

- Insight: In a world full of voices claiming to be experts, you deserve a discerning listener who can help you cut through the complexity and craft creative solutions that stand the test of time.

- Excellence: True workmanship is hard to find these days. Your nonprofit deserves a partner who will sweat the smallest detail, not because it will be seen, but because it's the right thing to do.

- Heart: You're not in this for the big bucks. You do this work to see real lives changed. Well, same goes for us. We believe in world-changing results without losing our heart for people.

- - -

WHAT WE DO

BOARD DEVELOPMENT: We take underperforming boards and turn them into engines of impact. Let’s talk about unleashing the strengths of each and every board member.

GRANT RESEARCH & WRITING: Need to expand your funding model? You’ll love our do-it-for-you approach to finding foundations and writing winning grant proposals.

FUNDRAISING: Learn how to tap into new funding sources and how to create stronger relationships with existing funders.

The world’s challenges are massive. But together we can solve them. Let’s discuss how Sparrow can help you and your nonprofit realize your full world-changing potential.

Name: Whitefish Community Foundation
Description: Whitefish Community Foundation is a philanthropy company based out of P.O. BOX 1060, Whitefish, Montana, United States.

Name: VoteTheOcean
Description:
Vote The Ocean is an innovative approach to addressing the issues facing our ocean. We’re pragmatic in our thinking and we're driven to inspire a movement for better solutions by:

+ Working in collaboration with businesses, nonprofits, and lawmakers. We’re all in this together. If we want healthy oceans and clean beaches, we’ll need to work together
+ Fostering innovation by bringing entrepreneurs and investors together to scale solutions
+ Inspiring people to take action through creative storytelling

We're all in this together and the only way to solve the issues facing our ocean is step by step and tale by tale. We hope you'll join us by supporting our programs and projects. These are all on-ramps for you to be part of the solution.

Vipe Desai | Executive Director

Name: Nonprofit Investor
Description:
In-depth, public research and analysis of nonprofits promotes more impactful giving. Nonprofit Investor volunteers from investing and due diligence backgrounds publish objective research in order to improve capital allocation an in philanthropy and drive operating improvements at nonprofits.

NPI's vision is that informed nonprofit capital allocation decisions based on access to in-depth research and analysis will support the growth of high-impact organizations while reducing the funding of inefficient and fraudulent ones. Such research and analysis will also allow all nonprofits to benchmark and improve.

Name: Texoma Giving Partners
Description:
Texoma Giving Partners strives to make charitable giving simple,
impactful and joyful. Our community needs passionate and
generous partners, like you, who are willing to make a difference.
When you join Texoma Giving Partners, we remove all guesswork
so you can focus on the joy of giving. Together, we can make a
difference in the lives of our neighbors.

Part of the Texoma Community Foundation

Name: Fundacja MARTEL
Description: Fundacja Martel z siedzibą w Kaliszu jest organizacją non profit. Działamy na rzecz hospitalizowanych dzieci. Dzięki wsparciu osób prywatnych i firm, budujemy biblioteczki z darmowymi książkami oraz kolorowankami na dziecięcych oddziałach szpitalnych. Chcesz zrobić coś dobrego i zostać patronem bibliotecznego serca w swoim mieście? Dołącz do nas!

Name: Cornerstone for Hope
Description:
Cornerstone for Hope is a non-profit organization that exists to support individuals to achieve and sustain sobriety in Chester, Montgomery and Delaware counties of PA.

Our program Recovery Worx provides vocational training for individuals in recovery. We help individuals build futures and rejoin society in a productive way as they recover from the disease of addiction. 
We provide:
> Vocational Training
> Job Placement
> Mentorship

Name: The Howat Foundation
Description:
In 2005 Margaret Howat was diagnosed with Chronic Myeloid Leukaemia. The treatment is punishing and extremely experimental, and through the guidance of Professor Tessa Holyoake (Professor of Experimental Haematology at The University of Glasgow) and her team, she has had a Bone Marrow transplant and now leads a very normal life. 

The Howat Foundation was founded by Malcolm and Margaret Howat in 2009. We are proud to support cancer research at the University of Glasgow,  and we hope that you will join us.

Name: Web for Purpose
Description: A two phase program that exposes at-promise youth to a career in web development. In partnership with Bridging Cultural Gaps, we aim to spur an interest in a high-demand and foundational skillset and offer students an opportunity to give back to their communities. Phase one includes a web development course, regular mentorship, and career readiness workshops. In phase 2, students will put these skills to the test by designing a website for a local business.

Name: Aidants scolaires
Description:
Le mouvement les Aidants scolaires s’est donné comme mission de développer la culture du bénévolat scolaire au Québec et, éventuellement, dans les pays francophones. Le mouvement offre un encadrement et des outils pour soutenir les écoles qui désirent se bâtir un réseau de bénévoles stable et sécuritaire.

Il contribue ainsi au progrès et aux succès des jeunes grâce à une structure de bénévolat qui facilite le recrutement, la formation et la reconnaissance des bénévoles.

Issus de la communauté environnante elle-même, les Aidants n’interviennent aucunement dans les fonctions du personnel de l’école. Ils jouent le rôle de support que leur délimite lui-même le personnel enseignant et non-enseignant.

Name: Crooms AoIT Alumni Association, Inc.
Description: The Crooms AoIT Alumni Association is dedicated to serving Crooms AoIT and communicating with alumni and friends to support our alma mater's educational and character building mission. The organization will provide services of value, promote the ongoing advancement of Crooms AoIT through committed volunteer leadership, and provide members with the possibility to connect with their classmates and their roots.

Name: The MAP foundation.
Description:
Teaching women Agroprpcessing and Encourage financial independence through skills development

Linking Corporates to Enterprises
Coaching and mentoring in entrepreneurship 
Consulting in Operational Management 
Digital Marketing 

Assist foster mothers with skills development in making school uniform and needlework projects

Name: WEngineers.org
Description:
WEngineers is a global community of engineers and technicians providing free online support to social initiatives facing unexpected circumstances.

In emergencies or due to unexpected conditions, professionals operating in social initiatives do not have access to technical information or specific competencies within an acceptable timeframe to effectively pursue their mission.

WEngineers is a worldwide community of engineers and technicians connected through a web platform. Together, our goal is to provide free remote, first-response, technical support to colleagues operating for NGOs, charitable organizations, and hospitals in developing countries or emergencies.

Name: ONG SOS Mulheres
Description: ONG de apoio ao enfrentamento da violência doméstica, familiar e de gênero.

Name: Infomigra
Description:
INFOMIGRA es una iniciativa en innovación social que busca ser el referente sobre información de migración en Chile. Actualmente nos desarrollamos como un Sistema Efectivo de Información GRATUITO para inmigrantes vulnerables en nuestro territorio.

Nuestro sistema está conformado por 4 componentes afines que interactúan de manera estratégica entre sí, permitiendo que la información esté disponible para satisfacer las necesidades de nuestros/as usuarios/as de manera gratuita, directa y fácil.

Name: Together Against Diabetes1
Description: T.A.D.1 är en ideell organisation som arbetar med att stötta, stärka och underlätta livet för personer med typ 1 diabetes. T.A.D.1 startades 2016 av då 17-åriga Elin Cederbrant.

Name: Effektiv Altruisme UiB
Description:
Vi gir studenter og andre i Bergen kunnskap, motivasjon og mulighet til å gjøre en positiv forskjell i verden.

Effektiv altruisme er en voksende sosial bevegelse basert på et ønske om å gjøre verden best mulig for alle, for alltid. Vi bruker evidens og kritisk tenkning for å finne ut hvordan vi kan gjøre dette, og til å faktisk gjennomføre det. Effektiv Altruisme UiB er et fellesskap for studenter og andre tilhengere av effektiv altruisme i Bergen.

Name: Desafio Brasil
Description: Podcast com grandes especialistas dos principais problemas do Brasil para entender: Quais são os maiores desafios do Brasil? Como definí-los? Quais são as suas causas, consequências e soluções?

Name: Joyce and Irving Goldman Family Foundation
Description:
The mission of the Joyce and Irving Goldman Family Foundation is to make a measurable difference, of lasting value, on important issues affecting the fundamental quality of human lives.

Before founding the Joyce and Irving Goldman Family Foundation, Irving Goldman was a successful business entrepreneur and philanthropist. A second-generation immigrant who left college during WWII to serve in the South Pacific, Irving worked closely with his brother Sol to build a real estate empire that at one point made them the largest property owners in the New York metro area, behind only the City of New York and the New York Archdiocese. Several years after the loss of his beloved wife Joyce to breast cancer, Irving founded the philanthropy that became the Goldman Family Foundation. 

Irving believed that every individual, through hard work and strength of character, deserved the opportunity to achieve success. He also believed that family could be a great source of learning and support. The Talmudic saying, “Whoever teaches his children teaches not only his children, but also his children’s children, and so on, to the end of generations,” especially resonated with him. It was Irving Goldman’s hope that the Foundation would be an institution that passed along the family’s charitable traditions, and the importance of giving back, from generation to generation.

Today, the Foundation is led by Irving and Joyce’s children - Dorian, Katja and Lloyd Goldman - who have grounded the Foundation’s work in the values they share with their parents. They believe that the Foundation and its grantees will serve as a lasting legacy for their family, and as a testament to the qualities their parents valued most - strength of character, courage, integrity, an independent and entrepreneurial spirit, and a deep commitment to giving others a chance in life.

Name: Kin Canada Bursaries
Description: Kin Canada Bursaries, the sole program of the Hal Rogers Endowment Fund, is a registered Canadian charity that assists post-secondary students across Canada to remove some of the financial barrier to their education by awarding $1000 bursaries. Recipients are selected based off financial need, their demonstration of overcoming adversity and their community involvement.

Name: August Ludvig Hartwall Foundation
Description:
Stiftelsens ändamål är att understöda familjer och personer som av sociala, medicinska, åldersmässiga eller andra oförutsedda orsaker är i behov av hjälp. Stiftelsens ändamål är också främjandet av en ren miljö. I andra hand är ändamålet stöd av verksamhet som stöder entreprenörskap. Stiftelsen understöder även forskning inom ovan nämnda områden. Ändamålen stöds främst i Finland, men kan även stödas globalt.

Säätiön tarkoituksena on tukea perheitä ja yksilöitä, jotka sosiaalisista, terveydellisistä, ikääntymiseen liittyvistä tai muista odottamattomista syistä ovat avun tarpeessa. Säätiön tarkoituksena on myös puhtaan ympäristön edistäminen. Toissijaisesti tavoitteena on tukea toimintaa, jolla tuetaan yrittäjyyttä. Säätiö tukee myös tutkimuksia edellä mainituilla aloilla. Säätiö harjoittaa tarkoituksenmukaista toimintaansa pääasiassa Suomessa, mutta voi tehdä sitä myös maailmanlaajuisesti.

Name: Youth Geographic Association
Description: The Youth Geographic Society (YGA) is an upcoming global, student-led organization dedicated to educating the youth and general public about global issues in the areas of natural science, exploration, and conservation.

Name: FORTALESSA
Description:
Antecedentes
Es indudable que cada vez mas ciudadanos buscamos participar en la transformación social de México. El creciente número de organizaciones que nacen con el fin de colaborar con el desarrollo de la comunidad es un reflejo de esto. Sin embargo para que ésta poderosa fuerza social trascienda e impacte realmente las comunidades, es necesario que provenga de acciones planeadas, estructuradas y eficientes.

Conscientes de esto, la Fundación del Empresariado Chihuahuense y el Tecnológico de Monterrey en el año 2003 establecen una alianza con el objetivo de impulsar un Centro de atención y servicio para las organizaciones de la sociedad civil de Ciudad Juárez.

Debido al proceso de adecuación y evolución natural de nuestro modelo y con el propósito de extender la cobertura local y a otras comunidades, desarrollamos FORTALESSA, Construyendo Capacidades en las Organizaciones de la Sociedad Civil, A.C.

A través de los años, hemos capacitado a más de 6,000 personas representantes de  más de 500 organizaciones de la sociedad civil que trabajan en Ciudad Juárez y del interior de la república. Asimismo, hemos desarrollado procesos de  Asistencia Técnica y Consultoría Individual a más de 200 organizaciones sociales.

Misión

Fortalecer  las estructuras y formas de operación de las organizaciones de la sociedad civil a través de servicios de asistencia técnica, consultoría  y capacitación, para generar un impacto efectivo en la comunidad.

Visión

Nuestra organización es un modelo exitoso, eficiente y sustentable cuyo principal propósito es contribuir al desarrollo ejemplar de las OSC de la localidad.

Name: GLOW
Description:
The world’s most impactful businesses are made up of dedicated and hardworking women whose talents, skills and commitments are critical to the company’s success. Although great strides have been made in recent times to uplift women, still, more can be done. Just 4% of Fortune 500 companies are led by a female CEO, and 25% of global businesses have no women in senior management roles. 

We are a network of women who empower each other in personal, professional and purposeful ways. By creating a community that connects, inspires and celebrates women, we can unlock unlimited possibilities for women’s growth and leadership in the business community.

Name: Minot Area Community Foundation
Description:
The Minot Area Community Foundation is a leading voice and catalyst for connecting donors, nonprofit organizations, professional advisors, community leaders and other partners to inspire charitable giving and improve the quality of life in communities in our area.

We work directly with donors, foundations, corporations and nonprofit organizations to educate them about issues affecting our communities and offer support for their charitable goals.

Due to the generosity and vision of all our donors, the Community Foundation is able to build stronger, healthier communities for today and for the future. We will continue working for our donors and the communities we serve with visionary leadership, effective grantmaking and endowment building services.

Name: Aly Richards, CEO at Let's Grow Kids
Description:
Founded in 2000 by two philanthropists who were seeking a proactive way to make a difference for children and families in Vermont, the Permanent Fund for Vermont’s Children is a non-profit 501(c)3 foundation and a supporting organization with the Vermont Community Foundation.

Since 2000, the Permanent Fund for Vermont’s Children has led a collaborative, focused approach to ensure that outstanding programs for all of Vermont’s youngest children are launched, funded and integrated fully into Vermont’s early care and education system.

We serve as a catalyst and seed-funder for the most innovative initiatives and provide support critical to Vermont’s efforts to attract further funding to implement these programs.

Mission

The mission of the Permanent Fund for Vermont’s Children is to “Ensure that every Vermont child has access to high quality and affordable early care and education by 2025.”

Name: Applewood Business Association
Description:
Bringing Business and Community Together.

The ABA strives to be the premier business organization in the greater Applewood marketplace and West Denver Metro region by connecting businesses in our community, while enhancing the lives of residents, through networking, events, collaboration and inclusion.

Name: Columbus Diaper Coalition
Description:
We are a diaper bank, established in November 2013, as a 501c3 non-profit organization.  Our mission is to provide free diapers to babies while supporting families facing financial hardship and experiencing diaper need. We believe access to clean diapers is a basic human need, essential to the health and well-being of children.   
Modeled after successful diaper banks around the country, we distribute diapers to local 501c3 partner agencies made up of food pantries or social service who support families through their comprehensive programs and services.  By supplying diapers to our Partners, it allows us to be more effective, extending a greater reach of diaper need in our community. To receive free diapers and other supportive services, find a partner agency near you by visiting our website @ https://www.columbusdiapercoalition.org/get-diapers.

Name: FairProgramming
Description: Fair Programming is a certification that a product or IT service has been realized with a strong social impact and support to demographics subject to discrimination. Consumers reward companies with this practice by purchasing their services.

Name: Red Bucket Equine Rescue
Description:
Our mission is to save and rehabilitate horses, restore their trust in humankind, and find them safe, loving and permanent adoptive homes.

Red Bucket was founded because we couldn’t turn our backs. We understand that “rescue is only the beginning,” and we have created a model of rescue that significantly increases the probability of success. We are committed to saving and serving slaughter-bound, abused, high risk, and desperate horses. Our dedicated team lovingly cares for the horses as they heal and are assessed, rehabilitated and trained in preparation for their forever homes. Once the horse is carefully matched with an adopter, our Field Support Team partners with the adopter to remove any barriers to success and facilitates a happy and safe transition. Our program removes obstacles and provides the tools, training and support that help keep the horses happy and safe in their new home.

The amazing transformations of hundreds of shattered horses and their second chances are only possible through the generosity of others who wish to help us help the horses. The Red Bucket Team and the horses that they serve are deeply grateful for the donations that make our meaningful work possible.

Name: Edu-GIRLS Inc.
Description:
Our mission is to enable girls living in extreme poverty  to achieve financial independence through customized quality education, vocational training and professional education. 

We start from age 3 and stay with our girls till they are in jobs applying our Ten Mantras model whihc delivers real results at about $1/per day per girl.

IIMHR University of Jaipur as part of its enthographic evaluation of our model at Vimukti Girls School attributed the success to the empathy of our teachers, the heavy emphasis on quality of learning, development of all round skills and active consoling, skill development and placement backed up by scholarships.i

Name: Machenstiftung
Description: Bildung, soziale Gerechtigkeit und Klima sind die Themen, für die die Machenstiftung steht. Aus diesen Bereichen suchen wir Projekte, bei denen wir spürbar Wirkung erzeugen können.

Name: Global Peace Builders
Description: Global Peace Builders es una ONG de voluntariado educativo para proyectos de bienestar y construcción de paz.

Name: Full Belly Foods
Description: Central MA Food Rescue was founded in 2020 with the singular goal of providing high quality food directly from farms to food banks. The coronavirus pandemic fundamentally broke down the food supply chain in the United States and highlighted the devastating food access inequities that already permeated our society. With your help, we are purchasing produce from farms and trucking it to those in need. We're doing our part to ensure that no child goes to sleep hungry. Get involved and join the cause. We need you.

Name: Saving Hope Haiti
Description: Association sans but lucratif intervenant dans les domaines du médico-social, psychosocial, socio-éducatif, socioculturel et tous objets connexes et autres moyens susceptibles de favoriser son développement

Name: Project Kenny
Description:
Project Kenny is a 501c3 non-profit that operates on generous contributions from amazing folks like you.

Our goal is to provide service dogs to veterans and services members who have served their country at no cost to the recipient.

A contribution is our way of saying thanks for the sacrifice that they have made to protect our freedoms.

We run fundraising campaign efforts to raise funds in order to unite a dog with a soldier so they can together be best friends and help each other in need.

Name: Initiative for the Child
Description:
“Initiative for the Child” was founded by a group of volunteers in Veria, in April 2008. The tragic case of disappearance of a young boy called Alex shocked the local community and became the main reason for the establishment of the group. Today, more than 200 active volunteers offer their services while the number of its members exceeds over 300.
Goal
Paramount to achieving the goals of “Initiative for the Child” is the provision of care and protection to children in need and at risk. Our main target group consists of children who come from dysfunctional or broken families and have been experiencing serious neglect and abuse of any type as well as children who have been abandoned or orphaned. These children most of the times are found at the fringes of the familial, school and social groups and very often are rejected by these environments. As a result, in many cases, these kids end up in as users of youth delinquency system services.
Some other wider target groups of “Initiative for the Child” consist of the members of the families of the children using our services, other socially vulnerable families with children and vulnerable sector groups in the local community facing poverty and social exclusion.
Some of our other main goals are the promotion of foster care and volunteerism as well as the creation and application of sensitization and informative programs in the population in general.

Name: Soul Box Project
Description:
Art revealing the gun violence epidemic.

The purpose of the Soul Box Project is to visually reveal the ever-growing number of gun-related deaths and injuries in the United States. The growing number of victims is nearly impossible to grasp – over 218,000 from 2014-2017. Suicide by guns add another 22,000 lives lost each year.*  The Soul Box Project is a creative and thought-provoking response to these horrific losses.

Our vision is to help our nation comprehend this epidemic with massive public displays of small origami Soul Boxes, each one representing one life touched by gunfire. In the tradition of other national art projects like the AIDS Memorial Quilt, which grew to cover the National Mall in 1996, our goal is to display tens of thousands of Soul Boxes together, revealing the staggering number of victims in a way that cannot be ignored. 

Imagine the impact of huge piles, long walls, or soaring columns of individual Soul Boxes, some with personal stories attached, each made by a man, woman, or child saying: No more. We will not forget. We will not look away. You are loved. 

The Soul Box Project begins with folding paper into a box to acknowledge one life. It comes together in a massive art statement. It continues with a cultural shift, as people are awakened to take actions that align with their concerns.

Name: Lovedesh - Ethical Luxury
Description:
Lovedesh is a new British heritage luxury social impact brand. On a mission to protect our people/planet. Without fear or favour. 

We are a London based. Heartbroken by global poverty and modern day slavery as well as climate change  

Create & deliver social impact and  sustainability solutions - without ‘greenwashing’ or ‘sweat-washing’ - a phrase I have coined that hides modern day slavery.

Lovedesh is working with artisans in other stigmatised nation’s and regions in or emerging from past conflict. Eg Rwanda, Iraq, Syria, South Sudan. 

It is the brainchild of British start up social  entrepreneur and designer Yasmin Choudhury, whose British immigrant parents originated from Bangladesh. 

She has been visiting since the age of seven and scouted out original and authentic experiences from Bangladesh, which are curated into fine fashion, food and cultural experiences.

Overview by Lovedesh founder. Yasmin Choudhury. 

"I’m on a mission to smash the unfairly stigmatised, maligned reputation attributed to colloquially named 'Third World' nations such as Bangladesh. Ethiopia, Rwanda, Haiti. 

"I got fed up about how we never hear about all the good stuff. How global influencers remain ignorant and in the dark about what is unjustly seemed the “poorest nations” in the world. 

“All we get to see and hear is bad news and images. Or charity appeals constantly feeding us images showing malnourished kids and adults. It is a skewed, unbalanced view often produced by Westernised foreigners. 

"And we want to show that these nations have incredible heritage skills that are at risk of being lost to the world, so we can protect, platform and promote.  

"That's where Lovedesh comes in. We showcase and share stunning fashion and design, and introduce boutique food and travel secrets to get the rest of the world chance to change how we see and experience developing countries.  

“Why? To put more pennies into the pockets of local talented people.“.

Name: Bless Mobile App
Description:
Bless is a mobile app designed to bring communities together around the world to help one another. It's where needs are shared, hearts are touched, help is given, lives are changed and people come together! 

You can use Bless to:

· Share a need of your own, or the need of someone you know and give others in your community a chance to help.
· Offer to Meet a need. It could be anything from a listening ear, someone to walk with, or items to give away. Use your time, talents and gently used goods as an opportunity to meet a need of another.
· View posts to share a need or meet a need in real time. Interact with posts that have impacted you by leaving comments and hearts.
· Experience the power of Bless. Send a secure private message to express your interest in any post.
· Create or join a group that supports your passion. Let your passion inspire others to be a part. Together, we can Bless the world around us!
· Invite others to join your group within the app or send an invitation to those who have yet to experience the power of Bless by text or email.
· Build trust and help keep the Bless Community safe through Vouch for me. Send Vouch for me requests by text, email or to other Bless users. Plus, you can vouch for someone you know before they even send a request.
· View profiles of Bless members to learn more about them. See which groups they belong to and who has vouched for them.
· Get to know more about a group by viewing the group profile. Discover the passion that drives them, check out current members and even view their posts.
· Share your experience with others so they can see all of the Blessings taking place in the community!

The Bless Mobile app is FREE to use, FREE to install and contains no ads. Bless is a GIFT!

Name: Lar do Idoso Servas do Senhor Bebedouro SP
Description: O Lar do Idoso tem por finalidade o amparo e assistência aos idosos em regime integral, assistência essa como se fosse continuidade do seu lar, visando a promoção humana com melhor qualidade de vida. Hoje tem como presidente: Irmã Lucimara da Silva. Temos a capacidade de atendimento para 40 Idosos.

Name: Hearts of Rescue
Description: The simplest things in life are sometimes the most complex ~ For those that are homeless, the simplest things in life are the hardest to obtain. If we can help and provide support to people in need, our mission is fulfilled. Our goal is to reach out and touch as many as possible. We are a non profit organization that thrive to service those that are often overlooked and neglected. We strive to provide basic essentials, spiritual support, and mentorship. Our aim is to restore self-worth and dignity back into those that we serve.

Name: Nourish Box
Description: Nourish Box is a nonprofit dedicated to fighting the devastating effects of hunger. Nourish Box taps into the popularity of meal kit subscriptions with a twist. Supported by donors, we send free meal kits to food-insecure families and individuals to ensure they have access to healthy and delicious meals.

Name: BRIDGE
Description:
Our mission at BRIDGE is to inspire and elevate ourselves and our communities to the highest of aspirations.

We believe that a world in which everyone is honored and inspired to address social gaps is achievable. Our goal is to develop and support a movement that holds itself accountable for positive change and the promotion of goodness.

Name: Angling for Relief, Corp
Description: Angling for Relief is a non profit that brings fishing to children who are going through cancer treatment, or are cancer survivors.  Through the programs we have developed, children will be able to learn to fish from their home/hospital using our “Dry Fishing Kit” and educational materials. We also offer them the opportunity to put their skills to the test at our monthly Lake Fishing Event once they are cleared by their medical team/parents . At these events, the entire family is welcome to join in the fun and find some fishing relief, too.

Name: ACDD
Description: A ACDD (Associação Cristã de Deficientes Físicos) é uma entidade sem fins lucrativos mantenedora da Escola Cristian Eduardo Hack Cardozo de Educação Infantil e Ensino Fundamental na modalidade Educação Especial, que há mais de 36 anos presta atendimento especializado nas áreas de Educação, Saúde e Assistência Social à crianças e adultos com deficiência física neuromotora, associada a Múltiplas Deficiências. Atualmente a ACDD atente 115 alunos e suas famílias e a maioria deles em situação de extrema vulnerabilidade social.

Name: Great Men
Description:
Great Men is a free school workshop programme for boys aged 14+ that explores gender issues.

The 3-hour workshop challenges teenage boys to think about the expectations we inherit of what boys and girls should or shouldn’t be. Using participant-led activities, we aim to give them the tools to be active ambassadors for gender equality in their daily lives.

We are currently looking for partner schools and interested teachers for the London pilot this academic year. Please do not hesitate to get in touch if you would be interested in hosting a workshop at your school. 

Great Men has been generously funded by the Big Lottery and is backed by The Department for Education. We hope that the learning and evidence from our pilot project will influence other organizations and education policy.

Great Men is a project of The GREAT Initiative, a gender equality charity founded by Mariella Frostrup, Jason McCue and Karen Ruimy. Take a look at our main charity website for more information about our international work in grassroots support and research.

Name: Three Women Foundation
Description:
"Three Women" is a non-profit foundation that emerged during the early days of the COVID-19 pandemic in Bulgaria. With an unwavering commitment to fostering positive societal transformations and aiding individuals and communities facing challenging or critical circumstances, the foundation has emerged as a driving force.

The Three Women Foundation transcends the limitations of its three founders, thanks to the invaluable support it receives from corporations, individuals who share its vision, friends, and the media. This collective effort has propelled the foundation to remarkable heights.

The name of the foundation, "Three Women," aptly encapsulates the profound impact that a small group of individuals can have. It serves as a testament to the infectious power of goodwill, capable of inspiring and mobilizing like-minded individuals to contribute their time and energy to devotedly serving communities in need.

Name: Projet Vi
Description:
Le projet Vi ("enfant" en Fongbe, langue majoritaire du Bénin) est un projet humanitaire au Bénin. Chaque année, l’équipe de projet Vi œuvre pour l’éducation, la santé et le développement économique du village de Taneka-Koko, composé de 18 000 habitants dont 16 000 enfants. 

Pour faire un don : https://www.helloasso.com/associations/unis-terre/collectes/projet-vi-3

Cette année, avec l'aide de l'association Un Partage, un Sourire, un Bonheur, nous souhaitons financer le forage d'un puits d'eau potable. En effet, Tanéka-Koko compte 18 000 habitants pour seulement un puits d'eau potable, rendant le village très vulnérable face aux sécheresses.

Name: Mothers' Bridge of Love
Description:
The Mothers’ Bridge of Love is a charity to set up a bridge between China and the world; primarily for those western families, who have adopted children from China, who want to learn more about the culture their children come from and share experiences with other families who’ve adopted children from China.

It was founded by Xinran who is a mother, of Panpan, an author, of books including ‘The Good Women of China’ ‘Sky Burial’ and ‘Message from an Unknown Chinese Mother’ (which focuses on stories of Chinese mothers who have lost their daughters, and includes details of how to donate to MBL). She is also a columnist of The Guardian newspaper (G2); and freelance adviser of TV & Publishing House. Her aim for this project was to build a “mothers’ bridge of love” to show Chinese orphans how much a mother’s love means to them, no matter where the love comes from, be it birth or adoptive mother, from China or rest of the world.

She spent eight months researching the possibility and looking for partners for the project and as of 19 August 2004, The Mothers’ bridge Bridge of Love has been entered into the Central Register of the Charity Commission for England and Wales in the United Kingdom.

Name: The GiveJoy Foundation
Description:
We create strategic partnerships to help champion and fulfill our mission. We have partnered  with organizations in sectors including sports nutrition, sports apparel, business, philanthropy, and food & beverage. Together we actively work to demonstrate the value of providing developing youth with proper nutrition, medical, movement, and mentoring resources.

In a few shorts years our organization which started as Fit360, an internal philanthropic initiative championed by Nutrabolt has contributed over a quarter of a million dollars into Bryan. These funds have been used to build/purchase fitness equipment, provide health and fitness education programming, and active learning furniture for schools.

Get in touch with us to become a partner, or use the contact form at the bottom of this page to engage wit us as we continue empowering and guiding students to a strong, healthy future.

Name: 林荫公益 FutureChina NGO
Description:
FutureChina(www.futurechina.org.cn) is an NGO dedicated to eliminate the unbalanced distribution of education resources in China. To solve the problem of unequal distribution of educational resources, we designed a series of activities and programs to help. We tend to promote the education resources sharing system from both the curricular and extracurricular perspectives. Our programs include: FutureChina Online Education “Welfare Class”, FuthreChina Public-Welfare Camp of Individual Development, Hand-in-hand Mentor Plan, “Going into the World” Plan, Public-Welfare Book Publication Plan, Online Letures, etc. FutureChina has already helped nearly one hundred students, and established a network covering 3,000 families and 80,000 students in Southwest China.

In the meantime, FutureChina is calling for an equal distribution of educational resourced on the international stage. Our delegates have attended Committee on Non-Governmental Organizations and Youth Assembly at UN, and participating in the drafting of APEC Youth Declaration. We were also invited to give a speech the Future China Education Forum at Columbia University. In this year’s Two Sessions in Sichuan Province, the resolution signed by FutureChina will promote poverty-supporting in education of this region.

Name: UK Somali Student
Description:
UK Somali Student (USS) is a UK based charity run entirely by university student volunteers. USS believes that education is key in healing a nation. Our express aim is to elevate the educational attainment of underprivileged Somali students both in the diaspora and back home in the Somali horn. We currently run two programmes: A tutoring service in the UK and a scholarship program in the Horn of Africa.

Tutoring

Our tutoring program provides guidance in helping our GCSE tutees successfully navigate key milestones along their educational journey. We draw upon the high social capital of our tutors to inspire aspiration and instil an aptitude for learning thus driving academic success. Our long-term aim is to build the capacity for social mobility of the UK Somali community.

Gift of Education

Our scholarship program - Gift of Education - is aimed at widening access to tertiary education for Somali students from orphanages or poor backgrounds in the Horn of Africa. Commencing September 2011 and every year thereafter we will provide scholarships for 200 students across 10 universities in the Horn of Africa including:

1)  Amoud University	
2)  Benadir University	
3)  East Africa University	
4)  Galkayo University	
5)  Kismayo University	
6)  Mogadishu University
7)  Nugaal University 
8)  Puntland State University
9)  Sanaag University	
10) University of Hargeisa

Name: All Out Africa
Description:
All Out Africa is a social enterprise comprising of a group of companies and a non-profit based in South Africa, Swaziland, Mozambique and Botswana. We cater to people looking for a wide variety of African experiences from volunteering, tours, research, internships and University field studies, whilst at the same time building sustainable long-term projects. Our goal is to partner with you so we can continue to share our passion for Southern Africa’s people, places and wildlife with the world and offer adventures that change lives.

VOLUNTEER
Find out why 100% of our volunteers rave about our projects! Volunteers work along side members of the community to build shelters, teach classes, coach sports or conduct important conservation research; this experience gives back as much as our volunteers gain from it. Our in-house Tour and Foundation division means we can offer packages that combine adventure tours with volunteer or intern experiences, as well as contribute a donation fee straight back to our projects. 

LEARN
All Out Africa is taking learning to new levels! By joining as an intern, researcher or as a group class, our programs encourage school or university students to step out of the classroom, and gain some exciting hands on experience, whilst living in Southern Africa. Our Savannah Research Centre and Marine Research Center are perfect facilities for university students and groups looking to gain field training in African Ecosystems. Our projects have received international recognition for their good work, including over 60 scientific publications!

TOUR
Take a tour with us! Whether you want to zip line through the mountains of northern of Swaziland or spot elephant from a open- air safari truck in the Kruger National Park, All Out Africa Tours offers a wide selection of tours sure to make your African holiday an adventure of a lifetime.

Name: De Verhalenweverij
Description: De Verhalenweverij is een organisatie die leesplezier bevordert bij kinderen uit kansengroepen. We vertellen verhalen met boeken of kamishibai, lezen samen of gaan creatief met verhalen aan de slag. Onze activiteiten hebben voornamelijk plaats in asielcentra en in de bijzondere jeugdzorg, of in samenwerking met de bibliotheek.

Name: CSCMP Talent Center
Description:
Our mission is to transform the world's supply chain professional through the discovery and cultivation of its future talent. 

The CSCMP Talent Center is the center of opportunity and growth for the “heartbeats” of the supply chain professional to be ready and able to face – and succeed in – the opportunities and challenges in the coming decades.

The Talent Center was borne of CSCMP, a professional association of members in the supply chain industry who seek to advance, educate, and connect with other professionals regarding advanced career opportunities.

Name: Awesome Glasgow
Description:
We are the Glasgow chapter of the Awesome Foundation.

Awesome Glasgow awards £500 grants every month to help your awesome project happen. No strings attached. 

Contact us glasgow@awesomefoundation.org for more information.

Name: Project Opioid Central Florida
Description:
Our Mission
Project Opioid CFL aligns leaders around 1 shared goal: to reduce overdose deaths caused or contributed by lethal opioids, like fentanyl.

About Us
Project Opioid was founded in 2018 to respond to one of the most devastating man-made public health crises of the 21st century: the modern overdose crisis, primarily fueled by opioids. Project Opioid was created with idea that building coalitions of top leaders, many from the business, philanthropic, & faith community, is the first step in creating real progress. While our public sector & other frontline advocates have long worked on this public health crisis, the reality is that without the support of influential leaders in all sectors, that are empowered to respond to this crisis and save lives, we will not find solutions & rescue those in need. We know that saving lives requires building coalitions of influential leaders & engaging those working on the frontlines of our communities. The urgency has reached a level of intensity that has escalated the imperative for leaders to get more involved in this deadly crisis. Our leaders, who hail from top companies, hospitals, law enforcement, congregations, foundations, as well as leaders from local governments are heroes.

The time for leaders to act is now and that is why Project Opioid is launching the Overdose Initiative, a community strategy that aims to reduce overdoses & related deaths across our community and in regions around Florida.
1. Saving lives by stopping overdoses when they happen – Harm Reduction Outreach
2. Outreach & engagement for those in our most Vulnerable Communities
3. Changing hearts & minds with cutting-edge advocacy and outreach - What’s The Fix? 4. Collaborating with leaders in Business, Faith, & Philanthropy to create a “New Frontline” - Take the Pledge
5. Medicine & tech as a solution for those in need – Support & promote the use of MAT & Telehealth
6. No “wrong door” & no one left without help – Innovations in Services Navigation

Name: Renaissance Knights Chess Foundation
Description:
Renaissance Knights is a nonprofit organization bridging the educational opportunity gap for Chicago Public School (CPS) students by providing them with chess centered programs that supports their academic, social, career, and leadership success.

We are partnered with Chicago Public Schools (CPS) to administer their CPS Academic chess program that provides high-quality competitive chess programming to approximately 2500 students each year. 

Our Cops & Kids Chess Initiative is partnered with CPS and the Chicago Police Department to promote non-violence and positive engagement between youth and police officers while utilizing the game of chess as the vehicle for engagement.

Name: Swiss Diabetes Kids
Description:
Unsere Mission ist, das Leben für Kinder und Jugendliche mit Diabetes Typ 1 zu verbessern. 
Wir vernetzen Familien, die Kinder und Jugendliche mit Diabetes Typ 1 haben, indem wir Events organisieren. 
Psychosoziale Betreuung, vor allem nach der Diagnose, ist sehr wichtig für die Eltern. Unser Vorstand steht gerne für einen telefonischen Austausch zur Verfügung. 
Indem wir Fachvorträge zu Spezialthemen organisieren, leisten wir Aufklärungsarbeit. Wir versuchen bei der Themensuche, diverse Entwicklungsstufen der Kinder zu berücksichtigen und darauf einzugehen.
Unsere Zusammenarbeit mit "diabetesschweiz" hilft uns, mehr Gewicht dem Thema Typ 1 Diabetes im Alltag zu geben, uns auch politisch zu engagieren.

Name: Imagine Zero
Description:
Help us turn the climate crisis into humanity's finest hour.

"If 100M people spend an hour week to protect the climate, what should they do?"

Together with our partners, we find out and make climate activism user-centric: accessible, easy, and rewarding.

Name: Foux Foundation
Description:
Foux Foundation is a private charitable foundation supporting charities working in the following areas:

The relief of sickness and promotion of good health
The advancement of education
The advancement of disadvantaged young people
The prevention or relief of poverty

For more information please see our website.

Name: JosiahKids Incorporated
Description: JosiahKids is a youth development 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization with a mission to educate, empower and equip young students and teens in the local community in the areas of faith, leadership, community service, and the arts.

Name: Un Autre Monde
Description: Un Autre Monde, est une association, créée en 1993 au sein d’Audencia Business School à Nantes. Elle organise tous les ans des missions humanitaires réalisées à l’étranger et proposées aux étudiants de première année. Madagascar, Pérou, Inde, Népal … les membres d’UAM ont voyagé sur tous les continents au fil des années. La principale préoccupation de l’association est de créer un impact concret et durable de façon à bénéficier aux populations locales sur le long terme.

Name: cherry
Description:
Imagine a platform where you can buy and sell clothing, with all proceeds going to your charities of choice. 
We are looking for assistance from people like you, who want to make a difference and be part of something beautiful. 

Please follow our GitHub here: https://github.com/Cherry-CIC
Our Insta and tikTok are both: cherrycic_

PSA: Our website is currently under maintenance. 
In the meantime, you can view our App design prototype here:
https://www.figma.com/design/FRnf2l7Kt3IH9kmlNXoB1p/Cherry-Prototype?node-id=0-1&node-type=canvas&t=F91cGka9xgbvrcAQ-0
Thanks!
Join us.

Name: DPL Foundation
Description:
For over 80 years, the DPL Foundation has been dedicated to the preservation and provision of educational resources and freedom of access that dares to make space for the dreamers of Detroit.

The DPL Foundation is a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization based in Detroit, MI.

Name: Backpack Buddies
Description: Backpack Buddies is a 501(c)3 nonprofit founded in the spring of 2010 by then nine-year-old Lucia Hoerr. Backpack Buddies's mission is to prepare underprivileged children for the start of school, as well as relieve their parents and teachers of a heavy financial burden, by providing backpacks filled with school supplies to hundreds of Virginia students at the start of each school year. Since our founding, Backpack Buddies has donated more than 3,500 packed backpacks to elementary, middle, and high school students in need.

Name: Youth Engagement Fund
Description: The Youth Engagement Fund (YEF) is the only donor collaborative in the US devoted to building power for youth of color. We work to build a more just and inclusive democracy and help youth of color flex their voting power – giving $3M a year to youth groups in AZ, FL, GA, LA, MS, NM, TX.

Name: Asociación de Amigos de la Universidad de Navarra
Description:
La Asociación de Amigos de la Universidad de Navarra nace en 1960 con el objetivo de promover la colaboración de la sociedad civil para financiar la educación y la investigación desarrollada en la Universidad de Navarra. Está formada por personas de diferentes países y profesiones, empresas e instituciones que desean ayudar al desarrollo de la Universidad de Navarra. 

Nuestra asociación, siguiendo el deseo de San Josemaría Escrivá de Balaguer, aporta los medios económicos necesarios para que la Universidad de Navarra pueda becar a alumnos e investigadores excelentes que impulsen a la Universidad como una de las "Research Universities" más relevantes del mundo.

Name: Clean Water Corps
Description:
