Headed by former US Army Officer, Rob McQueen, the Clean Water Corps (CWC) serves as a specialized clean water task force, sharply focused on combating the global water crisis. It is operated entirely by veterans—many of them, though they have transitioned from active duty, are still keen to be out there in the world, applying their knowledge and expertise, into something bigger than onself. It combines our, no-nonsense, guerrilla humanitarianism mindset, with the time-tested structural qualities of the military—organization, discipline, strategy, planning etc — ultimately using them to help solve a major global issue.

With a current roster of 40 veterans the Clean Water Corps has grown into two regions, West and East, each engaging its members across multiple fundraising efforts and projects globally. Since its inception the Clean Water Corps has launched 28 project, across 18 countries.

Name: Fairley Foundation
Description:
The Fairley Foundation is a place-based funder, focused on supporting programs in Greater Shepparton. We empower grassroots community programs that contribute to making Greater Shepparton more liveable, culturally rich, educationally successful and supportive of diversity.

The Fairley Foundation was established in 1965 with a bequest from respected Shepparton businessman, Sir Andrew Fairley, of Shepparton Preserving Company Ltd (SPC). In 1927 he became the first Mayor of Shepparton. According to his instructions, the Fairley Foundation is for the benefit of the people and communities of the Greater Shepparton area.

Name: Global Education and Research Alliance (GERA)
Description:
Global Education and Research Alliance (GERA), Inc. is a not-for-profit, non-governmental organization committed to supporting students and young professionals in their pursuit of excellence in academia, industry, and the arts by providing specialized life-mapping services. Through the use of mentor-based programs, GERA is committed to build a new generation of entrepreneurial and academic leaders – young men and women possessed of the training and confidence necessary for building a better tomorrow.

GERA’s six pillars are:
1.	Promotion of Literacy and Academics 
2.	Advancement of Research and Knowledge
3.	Encouragement of Entrepreneurship and Innovation  
4.	Talent Discovery and Development  
5.	Enhancing Multicultural Acceptance 
6.	General Student Support

The headquarters is located in Washington, DC with affiliate offices in NJ, NY, CA, and the Republic of Armenia. 

GERA currently has 24 unique and innovative programs to offer its members and has partnered with leading companies and organizations to help in its cause.  

GERA focuses its attention on a wide range of interconnected issues such as drop-out rate reduction and financial literacy, serving as liaison between international students and educational institutions. Members have a chance to dramatically increase their knowledge and skills through exposure to various resources and personalized programs, all of which are designed to maximize their potential in an ever changing, highly competitive, global society.

Name: Alpha Phi Omega - Iota Phi
Description:
Alpha Phi Omega is a co-ed collegiate community service fraternity that strives to develop three cardinal principles: Leadership, Friendship, and Service. The Iota Phi chapter at UC Davis is among the largest in the nation, with members serving more than 10,000 hours in the Davis community each year.

Leadership: Alpha Phi Omega develops the leadership skills of its members through shared leadership. Our members have the opportunity to serve as a chapter officer or to plan and execute any number of volunteer projects and social programs, as well as have the chance to participate in a variety of leadership development programs sponsored by Alpha Phi Omega. These programs consist of both online and in-person workshops and are presented by trained volunteer staff, to improve leadership skills for work within APO and in their professional lives.

Friendship: The surest way to have a friend, is to be a friend.

Service: The Iota Phi chapter of Alpha Phi Omega annually performs over 10,000 hours of service to Davis and the surrounding areas, working with schools, food banks, and community leaders.

Iota Phi Chapter - A History by Ashish A. Bhatt: http://www.iotaphi.org/wik/images/2/29/IotaPhiChapterAHistory.pdf

Contact Us:
President: president@iotaphi.org
Service Vice-Presidents: service@iotaphi.org
Membership Vice-Presidents: membership@iotaphi.org
Fellowship Vice-Presidents: fellowship@iotaphi.org
Finance Vice-Presidents: finance@iotaphi.org
Pledge Masters: pledgeparents@iotaphi.org
Recording Secretaries: recsecs@iotaphi.org
Corresponding Secretaries: corsecs@iotaphi.org
Outreach Secretaries: outreachsecs@iotaphi.org
Historians: historians@iotaphi.org
Sergeant At Arms: saas@iotaphi.org
Webmasters: webmaster@iotaphi.org
Advisors: advisors@iotaphi.org

Name: HobbleJog Foundation
Description:
The HobbleJog Foundation was created to help improve the quality of life for 
individuals with traumatic brain injury (TBI). Our primary focus is to support 
proven and innovative post-acute services that strengthen the independence, 
productivity, and well-being of TBI survivors. We provide grants on a national scale to qualified organizations that provide post-acute resources. Our grants are funded by donations derived from our fundraising efforts.

Name: Slipper Club of Dallas
Description:
Founded in 1934, Slipper Club was the first women's social club in Dallas and today boasts more than 500 active members. the club was founded by Mrs. Mary Blankeny Mallon, Mrs. Lavonne "Bonnie" Compton Crawford, Mrs. Lucy Jo Davis Burnett, and Mrs. Sally Dunlap Tetreau. the women were four idle-wide debutantes who designed the slipper club to be an organization that would promote friendships among Dallas' future female leaders. 

Slipper Club offers a unique network of friendship and opportunities for professional women throughout Dallas. while still committed to the founding vision of fostering lasting relationships, the mission has now been expanded to include community service and a charitable giving program. 

In 1995, Slipper Club became a 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization. to date, we have raised more than $815,000 for local nonprofit organizations and charitable agencies. every year the club researches and selects a different charity to support, helping to lift up the women and children of Dallas who are in need.

Name: Huis voor de Gezondheid
Description: In 2008 ontstond het gedachtegoed voor het Huis voor de Gezondheid. Alzheimer Nederland, Diabetes Fonds, Fonds Psychische Gezondheid, Longfonds (voorheen Astma Fonds) en Maag Lever Darm Stichting besloten in een pand te gaan samenwonen met als doel krachten te bundelen. Het pand kreeg als nieuwe bestemming “Huis voor de Gezondheid” waarbij de gezondheidsorganisaties meer kwaliteit realiseren door samenwerking. Voorzitter van het Huis voor de Gezondheid Michael Rutgers: ‘Het Huis voor de Gezondheid sluit goed aan bij de ontwikkelingen van dit kabinet, waarin de overheid in een razend tempo ‘ontzorgt’. Juist daarom is het belangrijk om als Huis voor de Gezondheid krachten te bundelen en samen een vuist te maken voor de chronisch zieken.’ Het Huis voor de Gezondheid is mede mogelijk gemaakt door de VriendenLoterij.

Name: CHEER For Life Foundation
Description:
The CHEER For Life Foundation is a California 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization dedicated to improving the lives of at-risk individuals in the communities we serve.

Since 1980 our programs have entertained, inspired and motivated individuals around the globe — powering our grant-making efforts that support organizations providing critical services to at-risk members of our communities.

Name: Enfants du Cambodge ONG
Description:
Enfants du Cambodge (EDC) est une association humanitaire basée en France pour l'éducation et la santé d'enfants au sud du Cambodge via le parrainage.  

EDC est reconnue d’intérêt général depuis 2003 et est habilitée à produire des avoirs fiscaux. Association à but non lucratif dont les membres du bureau sont tous bénévoles.

Name: LES CHAMANETTES DU DESERT
Description: Elena, Marion, Nina... 3 amies, 3 tempéraments, 3 personnalités et 1 amitié ! Ensemble, elles ont l'ambition de se dépasser pour la bonne cause, le cancer du sein. Le #TrekdesRoses c'est une aventure 100% féminine et 100% sportive. We need YOU !
Location: La Bernerie-en-Retz, Pays de la Loire

Name: National Charity League, Inc. - Stanford Hills Chapter
Description:
National Charity League, Inc. is a non-profit national organization of mother and daughter members in Chapters across the United States.

Through this mother-daughter relationship, our organization develops strong women leaders serving and impacting communities today and for generations to come. Our core values are:

• Honoring the mother-daughter bond by together learning, growing and modeling responsibility and graciousness
• Empowering women with the skills and confidence to lead
• Nurturing through mentorship
• Inspiring a legacy of social awareness and compassion
• Providing depth of support in local communities
• Integrity and excellence in everything we do

Stanford Hill Chapter members shall reside in Atherton, East Palo Alto, Los Altos, Los Altos Hills, Menlo Park, Mountain View, Palo Alto, Portola Valley, Redwood City, and Woodside.

For more information about our chapter, or to learn how to join, use our submission form: https://members.nationalcharityleague.org/?nd=vms_public_form&form_id=814

Name: International Institute of Peace and Development Studies (IIPDS)
Description:
The International Institute of Peace Studies and Development Studies (IIPDS) offers courses and runs workshops across Asia on Peace, Development Studies and Human Rights and targets the empowerment of vulnerable communities. It facilitates a collective learning process, the development of critical awareness and the strengthening of a global network.

We provide technical knowledge and skills on peace, conflict and sustainable development and also organize workshops intended to improve the performance and the management of outputs, outcomes and the social impact of organizations.

Each year, more than 250 activists, scholars, trainers, early-stage and mid-level professionals participate in the courses, workshops, forums, and meetings offered by IIPDS and our precious partners.
The training center is located in a rural setting in Nong-Chok, approximately 35km from the International Airport Suvarnabhumi and 40km from Bangkok city-center. The center can seat up to 40 participants and provides on-site accommodation.

Name: The International Flag of Planet Earth Organization (IFOPE-O)
Description:
The International Flag of Planet Earth Organization (IFOPE-O) is a registered nonprofit organization, a public charity, which represents the proposed The International Flag of Planet Earth. Our mission is make the flag recognized and utilized as the flag of planet Earth, and to share its message across the globe. In short, our job is to promote the flag proposal. We do this through extensive outreach work, investing in collaborations, campaigns and awareness creation. 

WHAT OUR ORGANIZATION SERVES TO PROMOTE
- What we have in common, instead of our differences
- Something that is far greater than ourselves; a flag and symbol for planet Earth
- The message that we need to take care of our planet, each other and all life
- The power of visual communication

“We are not here to create a movement bound by time, but to inspire systematic change; a new way of relating to the natural world and the life it supports.”

“When a flag like this is adopted, people are more frequently exposed to the message it represents; that we share this planet. We firmly believe that its exposure will create substantial impact with social, cultural, environmental and economic ripple effects.”

If our work is calling you to collaborate or support our mission, please don’t hesitate to reach out.

Name: Soutoura Fondation
Description:
Soutoura est une association humanitaire créée en 2005 afin d’aider les couches défavorisées de la population sénégalaise à faire face à leurs besoins primaires d’accès aux soins médicaux, de nutrition et d’éducation pour leurs enfants. Depuis plus de onze ans, Soutoura vole au secours des populations les plus vulnérables pour leur apporter des aides d’urgence, ponctuelles mais également pour leur assurer une prise en charge à long terme.

 Aujourd’hui présente au Sénégal, en France, aux États-Unis et au Canada, Soutoura s’est forgée autour de valeurs telles que la solidarité, la générosité, le respect et la dignité, avec en son cœur une équipe de bénévoles engagés à soulager le maximum de souffrance possible autour d’eux en s’appuyant sur la cotisation mensuelle et continue de généreux donateurs. Soutoura est en  pleine croissance, et vous lance un appel : répondez-y, car la souffrance est bien là devant nos portes, ouvrons nos yeux pour la voir et nos cœurs pour l'éradiquer.

Name: UnsilencedVoices
Description:
Unsilenced Voices' objective is to provide shelter and relief for victims of domestic abuse and sexual gender based violence worldwide.  The 501(c)3 non profit organization is currently operating in Ghana and Sierra Leone. Presently there are few, if any shelters or vocational centers protecting and educating women and girls about their rights. Unsilenced Voices is working with our partners to implement shelters, sensitization programs, legal assistance, vocational training, medical and counseling to women and girls in both countries.

In collaboration with Unsilenced Voices six partners, we encourage the women to speak up about what is happening to them, breaking the cultural belief to keep silent. By educating the public about abuse laws, we begin to shift the cultural mentality to keep silent. Those who are abused see the shelters as safe havens not only for their security and protection, but also as a source of empowerment for them in the face of fighting for their rights.

Name: Keepers
Description: Projet d'@Unis-Terre au sein de KEDGE BS visant à défendre la cause animale

Name: We Care, We Connect, We Share
Description: We Care, We Connect, We Share is a movement where we honor citizens who are leading the noble life of sacrifice and service. Stories shared are aimed to serve as inspiration for others and bring us together as people to make a difference in saving and building a better world.

Name: A Greater Good Foundation
Description: We Mentor Youth, Build Communities, and Model Wellness! With our mentees, we practice and model what it means to live in a healthy community—with ourselves and with others. We encourage them to love themselves, set boundaries, and explore new possibilities. We believe that how we treat others is often a reflection of how we treat ourselves. That’s why, to strengthen the communities we serve, our vision starts with helping each person cultivate the love they have for themselves.

Name: Chruch of Christ
Description: Predicación del evangelio de nuestro Señor Jesucristo. Porque no me averguenzo del evangelio porque es poder de Dios a todo aquel que cree , al judío primeramente y luego al griego. El plan de salvación para todos los hombres. Oir, Creer, Arrepentirse, Confesar la fe en el Hijo de Dios y Bautizarce para el perdón de los pecados. Perseverar hasta la muerte donde se le dará la corona de vida.

Name: Capital for Children
Description: Established in 2007, Capital for Children is a philanthropic and networking organization of leaders in private equity and related industries that invests in nonprofits serving children, youth, and young adults in the DC region. Through a combination of financial support and members’ participation on boards, Capital for Children forms relationships with its nonprofit partners and helps strengthen their capacity and impact. Capital for Children leverages the expertise of its members and provides guidance to nonprofits in areas such as organizational infrastructure, finance, audit, governance, and strategic planning.

Name: WHITTEN NEWMAN FOUNDATION
Description:
WNF believes in philanthropy and education through stigma work and documentary storytelling. In the last 21 years, the Whitten-Newman family has created numerous nonprofits, our umbrella Whitten-Newman Foundation, Pro’s For Africa, Sewing Hope, FATE, ExplorOlogy, and others in honor of our much-missed boys.

Our sons, Brandon Whitten and Dylan Cheatwood, both died at 25 years old and they died 15 years apart. Brandon passed away due to addiction in 2002 and Dylan died of Sudden Unexpected Death in Epilepsy in 2017.

We believe documentaries can change lives and are proud to have produced Killing Pain, which was awarded 3 Regional Emmy’s. Killing Pain was produced in honor of Brandon, who died of opioid addiction. We are also proud to have produced Sewing Hope, voiced over by the incredible Forest Whitaker. All funds raised from Sewing Hope have and continue to go to women and children in need in Uganda. These are two of our more notable projects and we are also launching another documentary about epilepsy in September of this year.

F.A.T.E. works to prevent stigma by producing the Killing Pain docu-series and Reggie Whitten has toured the state of Oklahoma for 21 years telling Brandon’s story and warning kids of the dangers of opioid addiction.

Sewing Hope sends money to victimized women and children in Uganda and South Sudan through the funds raised from the Sewing Hope documentary, Sewing Hope book, and selling pop tab purses made in Africa.

ExplorOlogy, in partnership with the Sam Noble Museum, sends kids from around the nation to dig up dinosaur bones at Black Mesa in the panhandle of Oklahoma. We believe in inspiring kids to get involved in science!

You can check out our website at https://wnff.org

Name: Elaine - SDOH Transportation
Description:
Elaine provides transportation to resources and services surrounding the Social Determinants of Health, which are the economic and social conditions that influence individual and group differences in health status.

The types of rides Elaine offers include:

Healthcare: Medical appointments and prescription refills
Food Access: Grocery store and food boxes
Employment: Interviews and day jobs
Housing: Interviews and lease signings
Criminal Justice: Court and case management appointments
Economic Opportunity: DES, bank visits
Educational opportunity: Financial literacy courses
Social and Cultural Cohesion: Community centers and support groups
Critical Errands: Laundry, mobile phone providers, etc

Name: Ecole d'été sur la pratique des droits humains
Description:
L'école d'été offre chaque année une formation résolument pratique, qui repose, dans une dimension internationale, sur des leçons, des ateliers, des mises en situation, et des cas pratiques.

Tout au long de la formation, les participants apprennent à maîtriser de manière concrète les outils du droit international des droits humains : au programme, la saisine de la Cour européenne et des organes interaméricain et africain des droits de l’homme, l'organisation d'une campagne de dénonciation de violations des droits humains, les entrainements à la plaidoirie devant un juge international, aux enquêtes sur les violations des droits humains, à la protection des victimes dans les situations d’urgence, à l'organisation d'une enquête de terrain mais aussi la constitution d'un projet de promotion ou de défense des droits humains.

Name: Cap Coopération
Description:
Cap Coopération est le centre de ressources aquitain pour la coopération internationale.

Son objectif est à la fois d'encourager les projets de coopération internationale et de favoriser la synergie entre les acteurs aquitains.

Créé en 2008 à l'initiative du réseau de documentation RAFID, du réseau des associations RADSI, du laboratoire Les Afriques dans le Monde (LAM) de Sciences Po Bordeaux, de l'institut de formation IFAID, de l'Université de Bordeaux et bénéficiant du soutien du Conseil régional d'Aquitaine et du Ministère des Affaires Etrangères et Européennes, Cap Coopération est le centre de ressources aquitain pour la coopération internationale.

Name: 4L Trophy - Equipage 4L4U
Description:
Le 4L Trophy est un rallye-raid humanitaire auquel participent chaque année plus de 4000 étudiants. Tous en Renault 4L, le but est de faire le tour du désert marocain pour apporter des fournitures scolaires, des affaires de sport et de la nourriture aux enfants dans le besoin.

En plus du défi sportif (conduite en 4L et orientation dans le désert), c’est un projet humanitaire ! Le 4L Trophy est donc en partenariat avec l’association Enfants du Désert.

Cette année, pour les 20 ans du rallye, l'aventure se déroulera du 16 au 26 février 2017. Au programme : départ de Biarritz, traversée de l’Espagne et du désert marocain, avec pour destination finale Marrakech !

Name: Grande Oriente de Minas Gerais
Description: Em Sessão Especial realizada em 12 de setembro de 1944, em Belo Horizonte, constituiu-se o Grande Oriente de Minas Gerais como entidade maçônica autônoma.

Name: Get Giving South Africa
Description: Get Giving was created by three, young, charismatic students who were helping out by donating meals to the underprivileged. It has since expanded, with the aim to: provide basic amenities, tutoring/educational services and uplift the community in any possible way. They now want you to get involved and help reach even more families that need your help. Get in touch and see how much of a difference you can make.

Name: Eden Prairie Community Foundation
Description:
The Eden Prairie Community Foundation is a tax-exempt, nonprofit institution committed to establishing permanent funds from donuts for the long-term, charitable benefit of the residents of the southwestern Twin Cities suburb of Eden Prairie.

Using our incredible network of donors, volunteers, and partners, we have raised over $2M for nonprofits to gain traction and make an impact in our community. We aim to help all nonprofits of EP, especially those especially in need, reach their full potential through philanthropy, collaboration, and leadership 

Contact Information:
EPCF office: Eden Prairie City Center, 8080 Mitchell Rd., Eden Prairie, MN. 
Website: www.epcommunityfoundation.org for more information

Name: ALQURAN EDUCATION FOUNDATION
Description: memorizing the Holy Quran, caring for its sciences and understanding, and developing its teaching methods in Pakistan. The Foundation's vision: To become the primary reference for serving the Noble Qur’an in Pakistan. The mission of the Foundation: Taking care of the Holy Qur’an in order to preserve, understand, and act in order to advance humanity through distinguished institutional work
Location: bahawalpur, البنجاب، باكستان, 4W3W+QM Mahand Sharif, باكستان

Name: SPCA Roussillon
Description:
Notre mission est de protéger et représenter l’intérêt des animaux avant tout.

Notre rôle est de venir en aide aux animaux qui n’ont pas ou plus la chance d’avoir un propriétaire pour voir à leurs besoins. Nos pensionnaires incluent des animaux de la faune ou de compagnie – perdus, blessés, errants, abandonnés, négligés, en détresse.

Nous optons pour la recherche de solutions qui favorisent le bien-être des animaux, qu’ils soient destinés à une vie sauvage ou domestique.

Organisme de bienfaisance #81503 0689 RR0001, à but non-lucratif, nous ne recevons aucune contribution gouvernementale pour nos opérations.

Name: Made In Care
Description:
Association caritative étudiante. 
- Maraudes : distribution de vêtements et repas à des sans-abris et échanges avec eux
- Soutien scolaire auprès de collégiens étrangers arrivant en France UPEAA
- 3 partenariats : 
Visicoeurs : Visites à la maison des aveugles
Panach'age : rencontres inter-générationelles pour permettre à toutes les générations de se rencontrer
Simon de Cyrène : Activités avec des personnes handicapées pour lutter contre l'isolement
- Récoltes de fonds monétaire et textiles avec réalisation d'événements caritatifs et de collectes

Name: Tsiky Zanaka
Description:
Tsiky Zanaka ("Sourire d'enfant" en malgache) est une association de solidarité internationale fondée en 2005 par des étudiants de l'école d'ingénieurs des Mines d'Alès.

Le but de cette association, reconnue d'utilité publique en 2009, est de participer à des projets de solidarité à travers le monde, de Madagascar aux Philippines, en passant par le Pérou. Parmi ces projets, on peut citer la construction d'écoles et de centres culturels, en collaboration avec des associations locales, comme le SPV Felana à Madagascar.

Chaque année, une vingtaine d'étudiants bénévoles se mobilise pour récolter des fonds en organisant divers événements (repas, tombolas, ...) et ainsi financer les projets.

Enfin, l'un des objectifs phares de l'association est aujourd'hui d'éviter la création de dépendance humanitaire. Ce dernier point passe par un échange régulier entre populations locales et étudiants bénévoles, même après leur départ, afin de pérenniser les projets initiés.

Name: VOLthon, Dance Marathon at the University of Tennessee
Description:
VOLthon is the University of Tennessee's Dance Marathon program raising funds and awareness for East Tennessee Children's Hospital. We host an annual 12-hour Dance Marathon event in the Spring after a year of engagement, education, advocacy, and fundraising for our local Children's Miracle Network Hospital. VOLthon was founded in 1995 and continues to help families all over East Tennessee. 

We are currently the top Miracle Network Dance Marathon program in the state of Tennessee!

Name: Living Peace Foundation
Description:
Living Peace Foundation is a non-profit organization founded by Kelly Thornton Hallman that creates opportunities for people to explore their purpose, discover their gifts, and share them with the world. 

In partnership with innovative thinkers and doers, we support evidence-based curricula, cutting-edge research, and transformative practicum focused on the embodied experience of inner peace and its impact on the world.

We believe that a culture of living peace is possible when each of us uphold the values of collaboration, compassion, courage, and creativity. We are inspired by leaders who make good happen by using their power to spark joyful, peaceful, and compassionate change.

Name: Acton-Boxborough United Way
Description:
Acton-Boxborough United Way envisions a community where every resident belongs and thrives. United, we fight for the health, education, and financial stability of every person in Acton and Boxborough. 

We identify and address the most pressing issues in our community and bring together the people to solve them. We fund and collaborate with local agencies and community partners to address 5 critical challenges: Mental Health, Early Childhood Success, Financial Stability, Crisis Preparedness, and Equity & Racial Justice. 

Acton-Boxborough Resource Center (ABRC) opened in September 2023 at the AB Schools Administration Building (15 Charter Rd, Acton). It is operated by AB United Way staff and volunteers. Open to all residents of Acton and Boxborough, we offer information about local resources, and our onsite AB Exchange offers free seasonal clothing and other essential items.

Name: UPAM - UNIDOS POR UMA ANGOLA MELHOR
Description:
A Associação “UPAM” é uma instituição de
solidariedade social que visa apoiar às pessoas
mais necessitadas, quer financeiramente como a
nível de formação, sem fins lucrativos, de interesse
geral e duração ilimitada.

Name: Royal Astronomical Society of Canada - Toronto Centre
Description: The Royal Astronomical Society of Canada is Canada's leading astronomy organization bringing together over 4,000 enthusiastic amateurs, educators and professionals. In addition to many national services, the Toronto Centre offers local programs and services.

Name: Florida State Firefighter Association
Description: Chartered in 1925, The Florida State Firefighters Association is the oldest fire service organization in Florida. The mission of the Association is to promote and protect the fire  service while providing continued education to members along with preserving the history and heritage of the Florida firefighter.

Name: Spirit of Harmony Foundation
Description:
Our Mission: Providing opportunities for personal development and self-expression through the support of music and music education.

The Spirit of Harmony Foundation, Todd Rundgren's philanthropic organization, supports the moral imperative of music education and music performance. The Foundation nurtures the cultural, academic, social, neurological, and personal benefits of music, through fundraising, special events, mentoring, advocacy, social entrepreneurship, and strategic partnerships.

Spirit of Harmony Foundation is based on the universally accepted concept that music is an integral element of the human experience, instrumental in the development of critical thinking, communication, collaboration, creativity, and the formation of lifeskills.

The Foundation functions to leverage Rundgren’s philanthropic interests, professorial activities, worldview, vision, talent, celebrity, experience, fan-base, and professional resources to direct financial and real-world resources to nurture music-based education programs and the individual performer.

Spirit of Harmony Foundation is unique in that its focus is to cultivate individual talent: from the classroom (or the artist creating an independent path), into the studio and on to a career under the direction of an artist with the significance, experience, and vision of Todd Rundgren.

Name: Fundación para la Protección de la Niñez, I.A.P.
Description: Facilitamos acciones filantrópicas de empresas, instituciones y personas que buscan transformar la realidad de la niñez mexicana vulnerable y canalizar sus aportaciones en efectivo y en especie a organizaciones de la sociedad civil que trabajan de manera profesional y efectiva.

Name: The Anti-Bullying Initiative
Description:
OUR MISSION

To reduce violence, emotional trauma and youth suicide by improving school faculty skill sets through innovative anti-bullying training programs.

WHAT WE DO

In an effort to reduce violence, emotional trauma and youth suicide, The Anti-Bullying Initiative (ABI) works with a team of psychologists and psychotherapists to develop onsite training programs that are taught to faculty and staff in schools across America. These innovative programs are designed to educate educators on how to identify signs of emotional distress, potential bullying situations, and how to appropriately respond.

HOW WE DO IT

By working with teams of professional psychologists and psychotherapists, ABI is able to develop proprietary training programs focused on our educators.  These innovative programs are taught to our network of professional trainers who in turn visit schools and conduct group training sessions among teachers and staff.  Currently, these programs are funded by corporations and individual donors like you, so even schools in poverty-stricken areas can take advantage of ABI’s services.

Name: Perle du Monde Association
Description:
Perle du Monde Association works to empower rural communities in Togo, particularly in the Plateaux region, through participatory and sustainable approaches. The organization focuses on key areas such as:

Access to clean water
Education
Ecological agriculture
Local employment

All while directly involving beneficiaries at every stage of projects

Name: It's a She Thing, Inc.
Description: We’re a nonprofit organization that empowers, enriches and edifies the lives of women and girls.

Name: Malaysian Humanitarian Foundation
Description: It is a not for profit community organisation that works with volunteers in Malaysia especially in the areas related to World Kindness Day, World Cleanup Day, World Animal Day and International Day of Charity. It also supports volunteers with tree planting initiatives and works with partners on climate exchange platforms.

Name: Hosea's House
Description: Hosea's House is committed to women & children in crisis by serving them with the love of Christ through housing, education, Bible study and advocacy.

Name: Fundacja Nie Warto
Description:
Organizujemy badania przesiewowe pod kątem nowotworów jąder, prostaty i piersi.
Przebadaliśmy już ponad 40.000 osób, wykrywając setki zmian nowotworowych u mężczyzn i kobiet. 

Jesień 2025 odbędzie się dwunasta edycja kampanii Mosznowładcy.
Wiosna 2025 to trzecia seria badań z Biustowniczkami.

Zostań naszym partnerem. Realizuj swoją politykę CSR i zdobywaj zasięgi dzięki zaangażowaniu Twojej firmy w badania przesiewowe dla tysięcy osób na terenie całego kraju!

Oprócz wydarzeń otwartych, odpowiadamy także na zaproszenia prywatne:  realizujemy badania w biurach i zakładach pracy. Organizujemy wydarzenia wewnętrzne, wspierające profilaktykę i edukację zdrowotną.
Wspólnie z nami zorganizujesz wartościowe spotkanie w zakresie profilaktyki i edukacji zdrowotnej dla wszystkich pracowników, jednocześnie wspierając ogólnopolskie kampanie Mosznowładcy i Biustowniczki. Nasi edukatorzy przygotują także spotkanie on-line na temat profilaktyki dla Twoich koleżanek i kolegów - po polsku i po angielsku.

Umów badania dla swoich kolegów wraz z Mosznowładcami i dla koleżanek dzięki Biustowniczkom.

raczej (at) niewarto.org zwlekać!

Name: Doctors for Doctors
Description: Doctors for Doctors provides full scholarships to aspiring medical students in rural Nicaragua. We create opportunities for rural youth where there were none, by enabling youth to become health care professionals and support rural communities. We work with partner organizations on the ground in Nicaragua to find high potential young people in areas where medical care is especially sparse, and build strong relationships with all stakeholders to ensure long term success in these communities. When students return, they will improve access to care and the health knowledge of local populations.

Name: Twelve Days of Christmas, Inc.
Description:
The Twelve Days of Christmas, Inc. celebrates more than 20 years as a volunteer service organization committed to keeping the spirit of the holidays for less fortunate families. Beyond the holiday season, the organization has expanded to serve our communities throughout the year by encouraging and ensuring a successful return to school to students around the country by providing uniforms, backpacks and grade appropriate school supplies. This outstanding service organization is composed entirely of volunteers currently consisting of six chapters located in Houston, TX, Oakland, CA, Washington, DC, New York, NY, Atlanta, GA and the newest chapter in Detroit, MI, which was established in 2014. The legacy of “parties with a purpose” was founded in 1999 by Gayla Gardner and Donya Gardner of the Houston Chapter. The organization continues to grow and fulfill its mission of sharing our blessings.

The members of the Twelve Days of Christmas, Inc. are diverse distinguished volunteers who are individual achievers. They are community, business and civic leaders, mentors, activists, and role models with significant backgrounds in legal, academia, healthcare, media / marketing and public relations, government, the arts and business. They are engaged within and outside the organization to maximize their effort to enrich the lives of others. Proceeds from our annual fundraiser and other donations directly serve the families in need. This organization has provided over 6,000 new toys under Christmas trees, over 1600 Christmas and Easter meals, and over 1,000 children with backpack, school supplies and uniforms. Twelve Days of Christmas, Inc. works closely with sponsors and supporters. We are committed to remembering the true spirit of Christmas throughout the year, and making a difference with the gift of giving. Our organization’s energy highly impacts lives in the communities served.

Name: Tender Loving Canines Assistance Dogs (TLCAD)
Description: Tender Loving Canines Assistance Dogs (TLCAD) is a non-profit 501(c)(3) organization with the mission of transforming lives with service dogs. We train and place service dogs with wounded warriors, individuals with autism, and as facility dogs. Our dogs are trained by prison inmates and incarcerated youth using positive reinforcement training. TLCAD is an Assistance Dogs International accredited organization. TLCAD partners with individuals, correctional facilities and dog rescue organizations to train service dogs.

Name: Count On Me Family Foundation
Description: We believe that every child, regardless of their circumstance, deserves to have a voice. We strive to empower all children through programs and community outreach aimed to nurture and inspire youth, instilling confidence to reach their potential and achieve their dreams. Formerly the Jim Mora Count On Me Family Foundation.

Name: La Gran Familia
Description: Nuestra misión es brindar a menores de edad privados de cuidados parentales, un acogimiento residencial inspirado por valores y habilidades familiares que encienda en ellos la esperanza y les prepare para un proyecto de vida en familia.

Name: Forte Charitable Foundation
Description: Trusthouse’s mission is to improve vulnerable lives in disadvantaged communities of the UK. Formed in 1997, the Trusthouse Charitable Foundation holds an endowment of circa £80 million. The return from these investments provides an annual grants budget of £2.5 million awarded to UK charitable organisations.

Name: SparkRebels
Description:
Stichting Mixclubs heeft een rebranding ondergaan en daar is SparkRebels uit ontstaan. Dezelfde missie, dezelfde doelgroep, dezelfde inhoud en dezelfde aanpak, maar dan net even sprankelender, krachtiger, authentieker, onderscheidender en aansluitender bij de doelgroep en stakeholders.

SparkRebels biedt jongeren een unieke kans om hun innerlijke vonk te ontdekken en tot actie over te gaan. Via ondernemende programma's leren zij een pop-upstore, maatschappelijke organisatie, politieke partij en kunsttentoonstelling opzetten.

Wij maken van jongeren actieve deelnemers aan de maatschappij.

Om kansengelijkheid en burgerschap te bevorderen.

Name: Dalit Foundation
Description: Dalit Foundation is a philanthropy company based out of C - 58 2nd Floor,, New Delhi, Delhi, India.

Name: جمعية فتاة الخليج الخيرية النسائية
Description: نحن جمعية فتاة الخليج الخيرية النسائية بالخبر نسعى إلى تأصيل مفهوم التنمية الاجتماعية و العمل الطوعي , بإدارة متميزة في بيئة جاذبة بتقنية عالية الجودة , تحقق مبدأ التكافل في الأسرة والمجتمع .

Name: Ordinary Women Doing Ordinary Things
Description: Everyone has a story, a story that ignites us to be creative, bold and forthcoming on why we do what we do. But no one incident or story can define my reasoning for being OWDOT. “Ordinary Women Doing Ordinary Things” are collections compiled over a lifetime of real life situations that I have faced throughout my time here on Earth. I use those collections to help introduce a positive impact on those seeking to better themselves in a way that drives leadership, service, diversity and inclusion.

Name: Obra Social y Cultural Sopeña
Description:
La Obra Social y Cultural Sopeña, OSCUS, es una institución dedicada básicamente a la capacitación en diferentes oficios, con la finalidad de mejorar la calidad de vida de las personas, entregándoles las herramientas necesarias para desenvolverse en nuevos contextos laborales.
.
OSCUS ES:

    Una Asociación Civil
    con personalidad jurídica.
    de ámbito internacional.
    reconocida oficialmente como entidad privada.
    sin fines de lucro.
    con finalidad social.
    de inspiración Cristiana.

Fundada por Dolores Sopeña en España el año 1902 y desde entonces se ha extendido por diversos países: Italia,  España, Argentina, Colombia, Cuba, Ecuador, México y República Dominicana, Chile.

Name: Artrium
Description:
Artrium champions the opportunities of Web3 technologies for the benefit of museums worldwide. We provide museums with expert insights and solutions to develop income streams, build audience engagement and diversify collections.

Over centuries, museums have built public trust through the conservation, study and dissemination of culture and heritage. The digital revolution is reshaping this role as museums begin to leverage technology to engage audiences and generate revenues by virtual means. 

We are committed to being the trusted space for museums in the Web3 environment, offering practical solutions for them to thrive and prosper in a fast-changing, innovation-driven world.

Name: Tracy Family Foundation
Description: Tracy Family Foundation is a philanthropy company based out of P.O. BOX 25, Mount Sterling, IL, United States.

Name: Fondation Secours Humanitaire
Description:
La Fondation Secours humanitaire agit pour faire renaître l’espoir et pour préserver la dignité de l’Homme.Elle veut être avant tout le lien solidaire entre, d’une part, les gens qui souffrent et, d’autre part, des femmes et des hommes épris de justice et sensible à la détresse humaine qui désirent agir concrètement.
NOS OBJECTIFS
Aider les pauvres, malades, orphelins et victimes de désastres et de famines.
Participer à la fourniture de matériaux nécessaires aux centres médicaux, aux centres éducatifs et aux projets de développement.
Coopérer et coordonner avec d’autres institutions et organismes ayant des projets similaires.
NOS VALEURS
Nos projets sont menés en accord avec nos valeurs qui sont:
Solidarité avec les plus démunis sans distinction d'origine, de religion ou de race .
Respect de la dignité humaine.
Valorisation des compétences et du savoir-faire des communautés bénéficiaires.
indépendance et professionnalisme dans l'exécution des nos projets
Rigueur, intégrité et transparence dans la gestion des dons

Name: ACTE 59
Description:
ACTE 59 est une association de centres solidaires située dans le département du Nord qui agit contre toute exclusion.
L'association permet de fournir à plus de 2000 familles en difficulté passagère différentes prestations telles que la vente à prix réduit de produits alimentaires, d'hygiène, d'entretien, de prêt à porter, d'aménagement et de décoration pour son foyer.
L'association propose également des services de coiffure et d'esthétisme qui s'inscrit dans un parcours de valorisation de la personne et d'insertion professionnelle.

Name: THE ONE LESS FOUNDATION
Description:
The One Less Foundation is a non-profit organization (and U.N. recognized civil society organization) that takes a two-generation approach to solve the systemic problems of poverty. We provide financial literacy and capability, supplemental education and personal mentoring programs; and advocate for policies that support financial inclusion and equity. 

We take a grassroots approach by creating programs with flexibility so they can be tailored to fit individual needs versus the use of generic blanket "solutions". We work with proven concepts and theories to create programmatic solutions for each individual, family, and community we work with. 

Our advocacy works to solve the root causes of poverty, and endeavors to create an equitable financial floor that no one falls below. Our goal is to affect policy that puts everyone on a solid financial foundation so they can thrive. 

With our programs and our advocacy work - we create paths out of poverty.

Name: Hulusi Efendi Vakfı
Description: Hulusi Efendi Vakfı 1986 yılında Şeyh Hamid-i Veli Somuncu Baba ahfâdından Osman Hulusi Efendi tarafından kurulmuştur. Eğitim, sağlık, sosyal yardımlar, tarihi eserlerin restorasyonu, çevre düzenleme ve kültürel alanlarda hizmet veren Vakfımızın genel merkezi Malatya'nın Darende ilçesinde olup, Türkiye genelinde temsilcilikleri bulunmaktadır.

Name: Friends of CITY DOGS Cleveland
Description:
Friends of CITY DOGS Cleveland is a 501.c.3, volunteer run organization established in April 2020 with the mission of providing the animals taken in by Cleveland Animal Care and Control (CACC) with medical care and enrichment during their time at the kennel, preparing them for adoption into loving homes, and creating opportunities for potential adopters to get to know them as the great family members they were meant to be. Friends of CITY DOGS Cleveland is CACC’s  primary fundraising partner. CACC is the exclusive beneficiary of the Friends’ fundraising efforts. 



OUR MISSION

To provide financial assistance for the animals at Cleveland Animal Care and Control, which includes daily enrichment opportunities, medical care and supporting the CITY DOGS adoption and volunteer programs. 



OUR VISION

Enriching, improving and advocating for the lives of Cleveland's homeless dogs as they navigate their journey to a better life.

Name: Cool Planet Food
Description: At Cool Planet Food, our mission is to rapidly reduce the impact of industrialized animal agriculture. We do this by advising individual philanthropists, foundations and impact investors on the most effective organizations and start-ups working towards a low-carbon, new protein future through farm industry innovation, food tech, policy initiatives, and consumer education.

Name: Martini Education & Opportunity Trust
Description: To put wind in the sails of changemakers ready to tackle environmental, social, and governance sustainability challenges.

Name: The Tess Foundation Africa (#TTFAFRICA)
Description:
The Tess Foundation is a 501(c) 3 grassroots non-profit humanitarian organization incorporated in the states of  New Jersey, USA by a group of professionals with the aim of enabling community development by utilizing state-of-the-art technology to improve access to quality healthcare and education across rural areas of Sub-Saharan Africa.

We pledge to work in partnership to help rural areas of  Sub-Saharan African countries achieve Sustainable Healthcare & Education Development Goals (SDGs) 3&4– which outlines several health and education -specific targets. This builds on the tremendous progress made by the Millennium Development Goals (MDGs) and will help address the growing needs for more equitable and sustainable developments to promote inclusive economic growth and social development, thereby benefiting rural communities across sub-Saharan Africa.

The Tess Foundation Africa Initiatives provide community based solutions to rural African communities in commitment to United Nations sustainable development goals 2030. We support the development of rural communities in a sustainable and socially responsible way that helps eradicate poverty by implementing programs that are effective, measurable and sustainable through strategic partnerships.

Name: 2 Nutriments Foundation
Description: Somos Fundación 2 Alimentos, una organización sin fines de lucro dedicada a alimentar a niños, jóvenes y adultos con alimento físico y espiritual
Location: Avda Jorge Basare 1066 San Isidro, Lima

Name: Onda Verde
Description:
NGO founded in 2014, with projects executed in São Paulo, São Paulo Coast Area and eventually in Lisbon Area. We work to strength sport as a tool for social inclusion and environmental awarness, with three main projects: #PRAIALIMPA, SOCIAL ATHLET and ONDA VERDE TOURNAMENT, besides developing  punctual programs and works in partness with other organizations.
Furthermore, Onda Verde has it owns veggie brand - ONDA HEALTHY - wich produces healthy and vegan food, looking to estimulate healthy diet and to provide financial support for the NGO.

Name: National Coalition of 100 Black Women Oakland/Bay Area Chapter
Description: The Oakland/Bay Area Chapter of the National Coalition of 100 Black Women is committed to providing programs that increase the participation of African American women in economic, civic, entrepreneurial, and human service arenas of their respective communities through education, scholarship, and collaboration.

Name: Casa Transitória Nossa Senhora Aparecida
Description: Somos uma entidade particular sem fins lucrativos, que acolhe crianças e adolescentes (de recém-nascidos a 17 anos e 11 meses), encaminhados pelo Conselho Tutelar ou pela Vara da Infância, os mesmos ficam sob nossa tutela até que possam voltar ao lar de forma segura ou para uma família adotiva. Nos mantemos por meio de doações, campanhas e eventos beneficentes e também por meio de convênio com a Prefeitura Municipal de Jundiaí.

Name: ThinkingNext
Description: Creating community intervention programs of support and for opportunity.

Name: Iota Phi Lambda Sorority, Inc. - Gamma Nu Chapter
Description:
Iota Phi Lambda Sorority Inc. (IΦΛ) is the first African American Greek-lettered business sorority established by African American business women. There are now more than 100 chapters with membership numbering more than 5,000 in 85 cities and the US Virgin Islands.

The Gamma Nu Chapter was organized in the City and County of San Francisco on June 1, 1963. The chapter’s programs are conducted within the framework of Iota’s National Programs and Projects.
National and Regional Structure

The Iota Phi Lambda Sorority, Inc., is organized into five (5) regions:
1. Central Region
2. Eastern Region
3. Far Western Region
4. Southern Region
5. Southwestern Region

Gamma Nu Chapter is a part of the Far Western Region.

Name: Schweizerische Interpretenstiftung
Description:
Die Schweizerische Interpretenstiftung SIS unterstützt ausübende Künstler*innen in den Bereichen Musik und Darstellende Künste in der Schweiz und Liechtenstein bei der künstlerischen Entfaltung, Verbreitung und in der sozialen Absicherung:

- Auftritte und Tourneen im In- und Ausland. 
- Projekte und Veranstaltungen zur Förderung und Verbreitung des schweizerischen Musik-, Schauspiel- und Tanzschaffens.
- Projekte und Veranstaltungen im Bereich der Prävention von Gesundheitsrisiken.
- Projekte und Massnahmen zur sozialen Absicherung und Aufklärung über soziale Sicherheit.
- Beratung und Unterstützung von in Not geratene Künstlerinnen und Künstler.

Name: Junior League of Roanoke Valley
Description:
The Junior League of Roanoke Valley is an organization of women whose mission is to advance women's leadership for meaningful community impact through volunteer action, collaboration, and training.

The Junior League of Roanoke Valley is an exclusively educational, charitable organization which reaches out to women of all races, religions, or national origins who demonstrate an interest in and commitment to voluntarism.  We are committed to inclusive environments of individuals, organizations, and communities.

The Junior League of Roanoke Valley is committed to strengthening families and empowering women by providing enriching opportunities to women, parents, and children.

We support the goals and dreams of families and women and offer opportunities for them to grow and succeed. By providing resources and support to build bridges and utilize community resources we strive to make a lasting impact in our community.

Name: Uganda Marathon
Description:
The Uganda Marathon

Want to do something truly remarkable? 

Join 5,000 athletes, fun runners and foolhardy adventurers from all over the world to challenge yourself against our stunning marathon course through rural Uganda. 

Not just a race, but a 7-day adventure and festival of volunteering on the Equator.

Name: LEO CLUB - Jeunes Humanitaire à Nancy
Description:
À Nancy et Metz, le LIONS c'est 9 clubs et 280 professionnels qui se sont mis en réseau pour mener des actions de développement économique, écologique et humanitaire.

Notre réseau comprend 1,4 million de membres dans le monde portant des actions pour ceux qui en ont le plus besoin.

Quelques exemples :

- au niveau international : lutte contre la rougeole (http://www.lcif.org/FR/our-work/humanitarian-efforts/measles-lions.php)

- au niveau national : récupération des lunettes usagées pour les reconditionner puis les redistribuer en Afrique pour munir ceux qui n'auraient jamais pu s'en procurer (http://www.lions-france.org/medico_lions_clubs_de_france-49.html)

- au niveau local : tenir deux stands tartines pendant le festival de musique Le Cabaret Vert 48000€ CA en 4 jours, dont 28000€ reversés à la lutte contre la maladie d'Alzheimer

Envie d’en savoir+ ou de nous rejoindre ?
Envoie-nous un e-mail à leoclub.nancy@gmail.com

Name: The Longview Foundation for Education in World Affairs and International Understanding
Description:
The Longview Foundation seeks to build a more peaceful, just, & sustainable world by equipping youth with a global perspective and understanding of political, social, and environmental issues worldwide. 

We accomplish this by supporting teacher preparation, state initiatives and coalitions, and innovative programs focused on internationalizing education in the United States.

Name: Erga Omnes Cooperativa Sociale
Description:
La Cooperativa Sociale Erga Omnes, basata su principi di mutualità, democraticità e solidarietà, senza fini di lucro, offre numerosi servizi alla persona con l’obiettivo di migliorare il livello di benessere individuale e sociale.
Tra i vari, offre servizi socio-sanitari (domiciliari e non per anziani, disabili e persone con particolari esigenze), servizi di ludoteca e di animazione con finalità educative e di socializzazione, organizzazione e promozione di eventi, organizzazione e gestione di corsi di formazione, qualificazione ed aggiornamento professionale, promozione di iniziative di carattere assistenziale, ricreativo, turistico e culturale ed organizzazione di corsi formativi, mostre ed eventi culturali, artistici e non.
Promuove, inoltre, l’inserimento nel mondo del lavoro di soggetti svantaggiati e persone con disabilità cognitive e fisiche.

Name: GPF Foundation
Description: The GPF Foundation was established through an outpouring of love by close friends and the Friedman family in honor of Gregory Paul Friedman. Although millions of young adults have used MDMA or ecstasy, many don’t realize that they may be ingesting other substances such as methamphetamine or cocaine. They are also unaware of its potential side effects such as panic attack, depression, suicidal ideation, and dehydration. Meanwhile, medical providers often do not have the information and tools they need to effectively treat people experiencing these MDMA-related conditions. GPFF makes a difference by supporting education, appropriate treatment and overall awareness of the dangers related to recreational drugs, particularly MDMA or ecstasy.

Name: Little Giraffe Foundation
Description:
The Little Giraffe Foundation was founded by Mike and Amanda Santoro in March 2011 after the loss of their preemie daughter, Cheyenne. Little Giraffe Foundation is dedicated to both funding neonatal research and supporting patients and families of the Neonatal Intensive Care Unit (NICU).

We have reached thousands of families by way of holiday gift bags filled with books and toys. When an infant is in an incubator and too sick for a parent to hold, reading a book to the child may be the only way for the parent to connect. A toy may  remind parents that there is hope for tomorrow and that they have not been forgotten.

In addition, since its inception, Little Giraffe Foundation has funded over $300,000 in Neonatal Research and NICU Support Grants and has donated over 29,000 holiday gift bags to NICU families in Chicagoland and throughout the country.

Little Giraffe Foundation is a registered 501(c)(3) organization dedicated to funding neonatal research and supporting families of the Neonatal Intensive Care Unit (NICU).

To learn more visit http://www.littlegiraffefoundation.org or follow the organization on Facebook at http://www.facebook.com/littlegiraffefoundation

Name: RADFLY Inc (Realise A Difference for Local Youth)
Description:
RADFLY (Est 2010) is a non-profit organisation developed by passionate volunteers who are committed to making a positive and significant contribution to young people in our community with a focus on fundraising for two local youth organisations: Ohana for Youth incorporating Arcadia College, and YHES House, Southport (Wesley Mission QLD). 
Our Mission: To raise awareness and funds for local youth focussed organisations.
OUR VISION: A community where youth are supported and guided to realise their potential and become engaged community members.

Name: Tchendukua Suisse
Description:
Association humanitaire, non-gouvernementale, à but non lucratif, Tchendukua Ici et Ailleurs – Suisse est neutre tant au point de vue politique que confessionnel et est basée sur le bénévolat.
Elle aide à défendre les territoires des indiens Kogis de Colombie.
Nos tâches sont :
la recherche de fonds attribués en priorité au rachat des terres ancestrales des Kogis
la facilitation et l’encouragement d’échanges et de rencontres entre les cultures indigènes (peuples premiers, autochtones) et d’autres cultures
le relaiement et le développement d’actions, produits et animations qui facilitent ou contribuent au travail d’accompagnement au travers de différentes manifestations, notamment auprès des jeunes
les engagements à but pédagogique
la collaboration avec l’association Tchendukua –Ici et Ailleurs (France), dont le but principal est d’accompagner les communautés indigènes dans la prise en charge de leur développement, notamment le peuple Kogi de la Sierra de Santa Marta en Colombie !
 
 
TCHENDUKUA Ici et Ailleurs – SUISSE
Avenue Trembley 14
1209 Genève
tchendukua-ch@gmail.com

Name: The Azraq Education and Community Fund
Description:
The Azraq Education and Community Fund (AECF) is a non-profit organization dedicated to helping Syrian refugees and other vulnerable families in the rural and underserved community of South Azraq, Jordan.

AECF operates The Azraq Education Center, a non-formal education program serving 300+ students daily in remedial education (Arabic, English, Math), enrichment programs, and more. We also provide regular food assistance and other humanitarian aid to ensure that our beneficiaries' basic needs are being met.

Name: The Princess Project UK
Description:
Transforming mums' lives through unconditional love, support and encouragement.

The Princess Project helps mums and their families to thrive by providing a holistic package of physical, emotional, social and spiritual support.
We provide the following services:

*Mum2Mum befriending service for vulnerable mums 16-30
*Parenting groups and courses
*Totcycle, a baby goods exchange
*drop-in support for parents through Mums' Hubs across Maidstone and Medway
*New mum gift bags
*One to one pastoral support for mums through our committed team of staff and volunteers

We are currently working with the Cinnamon Network as part of their project lab, mentoring us to be able to replicate our Mum2Mum befriending service through churches across the country.

Name: The Sheltering Arms Foundation
Description:
The Sheltering Arms Foundation's mission is to invest in the lives of Minnesota's children and help them reach their full potential.  We fund non-profit organizations and support policies that benefit children and their families who have the least access to resources.

Since 1883, Sheltering Arms has been embracing and caring for Minnesota children. What began as one Episcopal woman’s commitment to the well-being of children, has evolved into The Sheltering Arms Foundation, a grantmaking organization supporting high quality direct service programs for children ages 0-12 in the areas of early childhood and youth development as well as advocacy efforts that raise the level of public awareness and commitment to the well-being of children.

Name: MaxMods dba Santa's Little Hackers
Description:
MaxMods is a Colorado nonprofit corporation founded to assist people with physical limitations with modifications and adaptions of toys and devices. We conduct an event called Santa's Little Hackers that provides adapted toys for disabled individuals.

We are a registered public charity organization as described in Section 501(c)(3) of the United States Internal Revenue Code.

http://www.maxmods.org/

Name: Samerian Foundation, INC
Description:
Founded in 2003, the Samerian Foundation is dedicated to perpetuating the vision of Cindy and Paul Skjodt, the founders. They seek to enhance the quality of life by providing funds through a grant making process for education, economic development, health, youth sports, community organizations meeting the needs of the underserved, and humanitarian projects.
The Samerian Foundation is the philanthropic vehicle of the Skjodt’s generosity. To date, they have granted 2,105 requests of nearly $93.4 million.

Name: Raffi Foundation for Child Honouring
Description:
The Raffi Foundation for Child Honouring is a non-profit charitable organization dedicated to advancing Child Honouring as a universal ethic, an organizing principle for societal transformation.

Child Honouring is a unique social change revolution, one with the child at its heart. It is a positive vision that holds "the primacy of early years" as key to activating the powerful potential of our species.

Founded by Raffi, a renowned Canadian songwriter and children’s entertainer, Child Honouring is a holistic philosophy involving a children-first approach to healing communities and restoring ecosystems. It views how we regard and treat our young as the key to building a humane and sustainable world; organizing society around the priority needs of its youngest members.

Take the course: https://raffifoundation.org/take-the-course/
to embrace Child Honouring and make it part of your life. Developed by Raffi and educator Kristin Wiens and based on Raffi’s Covenant For Honouring Children and its 9 principles, this is an interdisciplinary course in conscious living.

Name: Books 4 Life
Description:
BOOKS 4 LIFE: Dé tweedehands boekwinkels voor het goede doel.

Tweedehands boeken kopen bij Books 4 Life betekent dat u het goede doel steunt. Wij stimuleren hergebruik door tweedehands boeken in te zamelen en tegen een eerlijke prijs te verkopen. Vrijwilligers vinden in de winkels en besturen een zinvolle tijdsbesteding. Met onze opbrengst steunen wij goede doelen, zowel lokaal als internationaal, die zich inzetten voor mensenrechten en armoedebestrijding. Maar Books 4 Life heeft met name gewoon hele leuke en goede boeken. Dus voor de kleine portemonnee, de verzamelaar en boekensnuffelaar, is het interessant om een kijkje te nemen in een van de winkels.

Books 4 Life bestaat sinds 2005 en heeft op dit moment zes winkels in Nederland. Dit zijn grassroots vestigingen, dus zelfstandige stichtingen. Zij worden volledig gerund en bemand door vrijwilligers. Wist u dat er zelfs twee winkels in Oostenrijk zijn? Het concept slaat aan!

Books 4 Life vestigingen in Nederland: 
Amsterdam, Eindhoven, Groningen, Nijmegen, Tilburg, Utrecht.

Books 4 Life vestigingen in het buitenland: 
Graz, Wenen.

Name: Fundación Emalaikat
Description:
La fundación Emalaikat es una organización privada no lucrativa fundada en 2008 e inscrita en el registro de Asociaciones y Fundaciones del Ministerio de Sanidad, Servicios Sociales e Igualdad. Clasificada como Fundación cuyos fines de interés general son cooperación al desarrollo y asistencia social

Nuestra misión: 
Promover la creación y desarrollo de recursos acuíferos sostenibles para el almacenamiento y aprovechamiento de los escasos recursos hídricos de las zonas áridas y semiáridas del planeta. Estos recursos facilitan el desarrollo social y económico de la población.

Promover proyectos de mejora en la nutrición, recursos agrícolas, salud y educación con el fin de potenciar el desarrollo integral de la persona humana y de los pueblos, especialmente en África.

Trabajar en colaboración con la población local y en su propio beneficio para promover el desarrollo social y económico de las áreas donde se desarrollan los proyectos.

Name: WE Unite Communities
Description:
Planting good deeds since 2010, WE Unite began as a way to connect in an increasingly detached society. Growing from one woman to hundreds, the organization has bloomed into a movement rooted in philanthropy. Today members support their community through hands-on volunteering, events to support charitable initiatives and gatherings that feed the souls of our members so they can feed the souls of others.
WE Unite weaves a community fabric that is strong and vibrant and provides its members the opportunity to connect, support, grow and make an impact on their small corner of the world.

Making A Difference Through Purpose

Annual Candle Light Hikes - Memorial walk for lost members of our community.

Annual Christmas Eve Candle delivery - For families who have lost a family member

Soup Off - Litres of soup made for local shelters

Christmas Love - Families in need receive gifts and food

Cooking For A Cause - Frozen meals for families in need

Homemade Hot Meal Deliveries - For families in Need

Funeral Support - All food made, delivered and set up 

sMiles for Amanda Annual Run - Remembering a young women lost in the community while raising funds for local needs

Pay It Forward Programs - Student participation in facilitating events

Housekeeping - For families in need

Local Advocacy - To promote memorial efforts i.e. memorial walkway buit

Food Bank Programs

Charity Bottle Drives

Shelter Resources - Youth Haven Christmas Gifts

Helping Hands for Transportation - For families in need

The list goes on..... What ever people need we try to help!

Name: Synergy Social Ventures
Description:
Synergy Social Ventures is a non-profit organization investing in entrepreneurial solutions to global problems.

We support startup social ventures in Asia by providing appropriate financing (in the form of grants and debt/equity investments) and capacity building resources, enabling entrepreneurs to build strong and sustainable organizations that will continue to create and grow their impact far into the future.

Name: Ghana Medical Relief (GMR)
Description:
Ghana Medical Relief (GMR) is a non-profit humanitarian organization registered in the United States of America and Ghana. The organization is aimed at improving healthcare in deprived villages in Ghana. GMR is made up of healthcare and non-healthcare volunteers with a common goal of delivering better healthcare to the people of Ghana in the form of general medicine, pediatric, dental, urology, obstetrics, gynecology, ophthalmology and health education.

In addition, Ghana Medical Relief has donated medical supplies in the form of hospital beds, ultrasound machines, examination tables, cardiac defibrillators, operating room equipment as well as medications to some hospitals and clinics in Ghana.

In July 2019 and July 2018, GMR undertook health missions in Anomabo in the Central Region and Asiakwa in the Eastern Region of Ghana, respectively. In Anomabo, more than 5,000 patients were treated from the community and its neighboring villages. Similarily in Asiakwa, over 3500 patients were treated from this village and its surrounding communities.

Name: Medi'N'Seec - INSEEC
Description:
Medi’N’seec est une association humanitaire créée en 2005 par des étudiants de l'INSEEC School of Business & Economics.

L’association œuvre au Burkina Faso au sein du village de Kiripalogo en partenariat avec une association locale, AKIM (Association Kiripalogo Ménégré).
L’objectif de Medi’N’seec est de rendre ce village indépendant. Pour cela, il met en place différentes structures depuis 2005 dans le but d’aider le village à se développer et intervient donc dans les domaines éducatif, agricole et dans l'accès à la scolarité. 
 
Bien plus qu’une association humanitaire, Medi’N’seec est aussi une grande famille d’étudiants motivés mettant en commun leurs compétences dans la réalisation de ces projets.

Name: Catholic Business Network of Greater Philadelphia
Description: The Catholic Business Network of Greater Philadelphia, is an independent, non-profit 501(c)3 organization serving businesses and individual professionals who strive to apply the principles of the Roman Catholic faith to daily life in the marketplace. We share professional experiences, promoting community service and create valuable networking opportunities. Catholic education is the focus of our charitable work. All the proceeds we raise benefit students attending Archdiocesan Catholic Schools in the form of scholarship essay contests, need-based scholarships, school grant programs and student mentoring programs.

Name: Instituição Padre Rubens
Description:
Há 50 anos realizamos o trabalho de educação infantil e ações sociais, contribuindo para o crescimento saudável de crianças e, consequentemente, suas famílias na região da Vila Palmares, em Santo André. Nos interessa contribuir para o desenvolvimento da nossa comunidade e impactar positivamente na vida dos cidadãos que residem nesse território. 

Tivemos uma longa e revolucionária jornada como Creche João XXIII, agora somos Instituição Padre Rubens Chasseraux em homenagem ao nosso fundador, Emílio Rubens Chasseraux. Nosso maior objetivo é expandir as ações e o acompanhamento social da instituição, desta forma, estar presente e atuante nas demandas da nossa comunidade.

Se somarmos solidariedade, empatia com o nosso histórico de trabalho temos uma força transformadora capaz de mudar vidas, de reconstruir famílias e garantir direitos que são negados constantemente a indivíduos que estão à margem da sociedade.

Name: Trusthouse Charitable Foundation
Description: Trusthouse’s mission is to improve vulnerable lives in disadvantaged communities of the UK. Formed in 1997, the Trusthouse Charitable Foundation holds an endowment of circa £80 million. The return from these investments provides an annual grants budget of £2.5 million awarded to UK charitable organisations.

Name: Rotary Club Leusden CA
Description:
Rotary is een internationaal netwerk met meer dan 1,2 miljoen leden. Je bent lid op basis van je beroep. Doel is je kennis en kunde beschikbaar te stellen aan non-profit projecten. Onze club telt ruim 30 actieve mannen en vrouwen die wonen in Leusden. We bestaan in 2022 50 jaar!

Wat doen wij?
Wij helpen door het starten en ondersteunen van projecten op allerlei terreinen in Leusden, Nederland of elders in de wereld. Dat kan in de vorm van geld of door onze kennis en ervaring in te zetten. Die kennis en ervaring doen we op in ons dagelijks werk, het geld halen we op met fondsenwervingsactiviteiten zoals Dorpsgolf Leusden.

Name: ShareFonds
Description: ShareFonds zet zich in voor een samenleving die verbindt en waarin iedereen mee kan doen. Dit doen we vanuit betrokkenheid. Hierbij activeren we ons netwerk. Niet alleen door geld, maar ook door aandacht en kennis. Delen in de breedste zin van het woord. Gericht op ontwikkeling en verbinding. Gericht op samen leven. We werken hierbij, waar mogelijk, samen met maatschappelijke organisaties die hun expertise inzetten om bij te dragen aan een samenleving waar iedereen onderdeel van is.

Name: The Moriah Fund
Description:
The Moriah Fund is a private foundation that was established in 1985 to perpetuate the lifelong philanthropic commitment of its founders, Robert and Clarence Efroymson. Their philosophy of giving was rooted in fundamental Jewish values: a concern for the disadvantaged and an emphasis on self-help; a commitment to equity and justice; and a desire to improve the quality of life for Jews and non-Jews alike.

Moriah seeks to promote human rights and social justice, help disadvantaged people gain self-sufficiency and control over their lives, foster sustainable development, and promote women’s rights and reproductive health.

Name: Volunteer Centre Sutton
Network Young Philanthropic Professionals (NYPP)
Description:
Stichting NYPP is het platform voor jong bestuurstalent dat zich wil inzetten voor filantropische vermogensfondsen. Vooral voor alumni van de PAO-opleiding Besturen van Filantropische Fondsen aan de Vrije Universiteit.

Jonge bestuurders kunnen bijdragen aan verscheidene doelstellingen van een dergelijk fonds waaronder:

- Het verbeteren van de deskundigheid en de professionaliteit
- Het waarborgen van de continuïteit
- Het zorgen voor de diversiteit

Stichting NYPP geeft bekendheid aan de PAO-opleiding "Besturen van Filantropische Fondsen" aan de Vrije Universiteit en verzorgt tevens stageplaatsen voor deze opleiding. Wij willen jonge mensen die zijn opgeleid tot bestuursleden van filantropische vermogensfondsen in contact te brengen met de besturen van deze fondsen. Daarnaast verzorgt Stichting NYPP ook cursussen en permanente educatie. Daarbij wordt getracht om de onderlinge band van afgestudeerden van de PAO-opleiding "Besturen van Filantropische Fondsen" aan de Vrije Universiteit te verstevigen door middel van netwerkactiviteiten, werkbezoeken, kennismaking en kennisuitwisseling met ervaren bestuurders van vermogensfondsen.

Name: The Battle Continues
Description:
**The Battle Continues Inc. is a 501c3 nonprofit aimed to help veterans who have returned from war.

**100% of your donation goes directly to veterans.

**All of your donated dollars go to cut a check to injured veterans to cover medical costs as directed by the Board of Directors. Your donations are not used to cover administrative fees.

**Please consider giving a donation to our veteran fund. Any amount helps greatly…even as little as $5.

**Veterans wrote a blank check on their lives when they went to war and risked not coming home to their families. Some never got to see their child be born because they were overseas protecting our freedom. The least we can do in return is help our fellow veteran with any financial burdens they may have upon return from combat by giving a monetary donation. Every dollar helps no matter how little it is.

Name: Club Social ANIR
Description: Le Club Social ANIR Encg-Kenitra fondé en 2009, l'a bien compris : Les enfants, les personnes âgées, les orphelins ... toute personne souffrant d'un quelconque manque a besoin de se sentir en harmonie avec le milieu qui l'entoure.
Location: Kenitra , Route Sidi Allal Al Bahraoui

Name: Milagro Foundation, Inc. (Milagro Center)
Description:
Milagro Center is a 501 (c)3 nonprofit organization whose mission is to ensure the social and academic success of underserved children and youth through Cultural Arts, Living Values, Academic Support, and Mentoring. 

We are a center of creative cultural collaboration, engaging education and inspiration that dissolves social barriers, forms lasting connections and sets the stage for future success.

Name: Projeto Alegria Virtual
Description: O Projeto Alegria Virtual foi desenvolvido pela agência Casa Mais e busca levar a Realidade Virtual, a Realidade Aumentada e o Metaverso como terapia para o ambiente hospitalar e asilo. Sem fins lucrativos, a experiência imersiva faz com que pacientes encontrem na ferramenta dessas tecnologias a possibilidade de realizar sonhos e amenizar o sofrimento.

Name: Gardermarch
Description: Foreningen Gardermarch står bag gennemførelsen af det årlige arrangement Gardermarch, der gennemføres til støtte for veteranarbejdet ved Den Kongelige Livgarde

Name: Limehouse Project Ltd
Description:
Established in 1984 by a former Head Teacher, a Councillor and a local Community Worker, the Limehouse Project continues to strive to meet the needs of local communities throughout Tower Hamlets. We work on a simple ethos established by our three founders: through the empowering of one, we can empower an entire community.

The Limehouse Project (LHP) has worked to respond to the needs of minority communities throughout the London Borough of Tower Hamlets for over 25 years. Our aim is to alleviate the difficulties and help realise the aspirations of the most disadvantaged members of local communities, with an especial focus on women, all peoples of ethnic minority origin and those for whom English is not a first language. Our growing successes and achievements are dependent upon our ability to provide:

- Information, advice and advocacy support in our clients’ own languages;

- Education, training, employment and volunteering opportunities geared towards helping individuals gain the confidence and skills they need to play a full role in their social and economic environments; and

- A range of educational and recreational activities / experiences for families, children, older people and others geared towards enhancing their life experiences but which would otherwise be beyond their reach.

We seek to empower marginalised individuals by researching, identifying, and establishing new pathways to help them overcome the personal and socio-economic barriers to self-empowerment, physical and mental well-being and the establishment of stable, fulfilled lives.  

We understand that by enabling and supporting an individual, we have the potential to strengthen a family from within, thereby creating stronger communities and a collectively peaceful society.

Name: Saskatchewan Roughrider Foundation
Description: The Foundation’s mission is to empower youth and communities through the passion of Rider Nation, teaching them skills like leadership, resilience and responsibility. We help to build on the strengths of our youth ensuring they have an opportunity to reach their full potential, as well as empower them to achieve their goals.

Name: Adera Foundation
Description: Adera’s primary objective is to strengthen the family unit in the nation of Ethiopia. Poverty, disease, poor education, high unemployment, and a variety of other socioeconomic factors are destroying the family unit in Ethiopia. Our desire is to facilitate programs to meet basic needs and to promote self-sustainability among the at-risk families. One key area of emphasis is assisting mothers with young children. By targeting this extremely vulnerable segment of the Ethiopian population, we hope to provide loving parents with the training and business opportunities needed to foster long-term success. Our feeding and preschool programs provide the children in these families with the assistance they need not only to survive but thrive even in difficult circumstances. Adera works closely with government entities and village councils in order to ensure that our work is locally supported and meets all national and regional regulatory standards.

Name: Psoríase Brasil Oficial
Description: A Associação Brasileira de Psoríase, Artrite Psoriásica e Outras Doenças Crônicas de Pele -  Psoríase Brasil é uma entidade civil sem fins lucrativos que busca o acesso universal pelo SUS e planos privados de saúde de tratamentos às doenças. Sua missão é lutar por políticas públicas a pacientes com psoríase e outras doenças crônicas de pele, como urticária crônica espontânea, dermatite atópica, vitiligo, entre outras. Criada em 2005, em Porto Alegre (RS), a Psoríase Brasil foi oficializada em 2010 e, desde então, passou a ter representatividade nacional e internacional. A Psoríase Brasil realiza trabalho de advocacy (busca por políticas públicas) em prol dos pacientes e pela implementação da Resolução da OMS (2014) em relação à psoríase. A entidade conta com um extenso grupo de médicos parceiros.

Name: Fundación Humaniza
Description:
La Fundación Humaniza nace con el sentido de ser “el alma” de Humaniza, como impulso integrador e innovador de sus socios fundadores y accionistas en construir espacios para otro mundo posible.
