El fin de interés general de la Fundación es contribuir al desarrollo y mejora de la realidad social mediante la cooperación en acciones solidarias, a nivel de las personas, de sus organizaciones y de sus países.

Nuestros ámbitos de actuación como actividad solidaria de desarrollo, son:

1) El desarrollo en la infancia, en la adolescencia y en la educación.
2) La intervención en colectivos de personas en riesgo de exclusión social.
3) La contribución al desarrollo de organizaciones de acción social.

Name: BrioEarth
Description:
The BrioEarth Foundation, now closed, was a humanitarian entity committed to serve the original philanthropic mission of Briotech’s corporate philosophy, and dedicated to raising joy by providing BrioHOCl formulations, and other donation means, to vulnerable and impoverished populations of the world with a primary focus on under-served children, at-risk women, communities suffering from racial and economic inequity, and individuals who are vulnerable to infectious disease.

Since the inception of Briotech, Inc. in 2010, work to establish a humanitarian division for the company was always a major focus of the Founders, and with the onset of the COVID-19 pandemic, BrioEarth was formally founded in May of 2020 to provide urgent response to individuals and communities in need.

BrioEarth has donated to hundreds of causes and non-profits across the United States and around the world. Though BrioEarth has primarily donated goods by way of personal care, cosmetic, and sanitizing products to those in need such as first responders, tribal nations, homeless endeavors, disaster relief efforts, and wildfire animal rescue associations, BrioEarth has also been honored to provide monetary donations to educational institutions, to organizations that support victims of trafficking, to charitable organizations of Briotech's employees’ choosing such as the Leukemia & Lymphoma Society, Pancreatic Cancer Action Network, Creutzfeldt-Jakob Disease Foundation, Hawaii Wildlife Fund, Seattle Humane Society, Juvenile Diabetes Research Foundation, Rainbow Center, Autism Speaks, Children’s Burn Foundation, Seattle Children’s Hospital, Trevor Project, Black Girls Code, National Alliance on Mental Illness, Wigs for Kids, Westside Food Brigade, Ovarian Cancer Research Alliance, the Mind & Life Institute, and many, many more.

Name: Trek Advancement
Description:
Trek Advancement is a firm focused on philanthropy.  We believe that sustainable and strategic philanthropy can change the world.  We see ourselves as stewards of philanthropy and as such we feel a strong commitment to helping individuals, companies and non-profits effectively leverage their philanthropic investments.

Trek Advancement brings creativity and passion to everything we do.  We love philanthropy and offer a holistic approach to our work helping our clients make informed decisions about their philanthropy and become more actively engaged with the social change they fund.

Name: ClubsHelp Foundation, Inc
Description: ClubsHELP MISSION is to connect golf and member clubs with local communities to activate a powerful network that provides critical resources in times of need. The Foundation was created to service hospitals and emergency service providers to aid frontline workers in the fight against COVID-19.

Name: Hanworth House
Description:
Hanworth House is an Award-Winning Heritage Listed Home offering Accommodation and Venue Space for Special Events. Our Mission is to raise $100,000 per year to benefit local charities whilst connecting like-minded women and men in the spirit of the Heart of Hanworth. With a rich history of bringing together entrepreneurial minds and supporting causes that span from women and children to cancer research, Hanworth is a Brisbane Icon that gives back to its community through its philanthropic endeavours. 

One of the oldest homes ever built in Brisbane, Queensland, Australia, the original owner was Captain George Poynter Heath, the first Portmaster of Brisbane who was responsible for charting parts of the Great Barrier Reef and established many of the lighthouses across the Queensland Coast. Captain Heath, his wife and nine children originated from Hanworth, Norfolk, England hence the namesake. The first brick was laid in 1864 and this majestic home has hosted numerous high society parties. Mary Wienholt purchased the home in 1912 in dedication of her mother Ann Powell who passed away and for the next 80+ years repurposed Hanworth as a Hospice for the Aged for impecunious gentlewomen. Hanworth was gifted to the Theosophical Society and then the Anglican Church.

Almost 100 years after Mary Wienholt, in similar fashion, Marisa Vecchio and her family purchased Hanworth in honour of Marisa's mother, Romana Preston. The home was found in a dilapidated state and after renovations were complete, mere weeks from opening as accommodation to the public, the house was tragically set on fire by an arsonist. Sadly, 80% of the home was damaged and extensive restoration took place once more. Hanworth is now lovingly restored and like a phoenix rising from the ashes, she shines brighter than ever. Some of you may have seen the Happy Tree light up the path for the commuters along Lytton Rd at nightfall. We hope to welcome you to Hanworth House soon!

Name: Delta Tau Delta - Zeta Sigma
Description: We are the men of Delta Tau Delta - Zeta Sigma, from Texas A&M. This page serves as an outlet for actives and alumni to connect, update, recruit, and advertise to fellow members.

Name: GiveRise
Description: GiveRise is an e-commerce fundraising marketplace supporting Canadian nonprofits while reducing waste. We are woman-owned, using our passions for art and sustainable living to support local communities.

Name: Actcept
Description: Act to Accept - A social initiative to raise awareness against the  discrimination faced by the  transgender community of Pakistan and empower them with Education and Employment opportunities.

Name: Fonds Social Tourangeau (Action Sociale)
Description:
Fonds Social Tourangeaux | Entraide pour un monde meilleur 
 Localisation : Tours, France
 À propos de nous :
Le Fonds Social Tourangeaux est une association à but non lucratif engagée dans la lutte contre les inégalités et la promotion du bien-être des populations vulnérables, en particulier en Afrique. Notre mission est de mobiliser des ressources financières pour soutenir des projets humanitaires et des organisations locales qui œuvrent pour l’amélioration des conditions de vie dans les pays africains.

 Notre engagement :
 Récolter des fonds pour des projets d’éducation, de santé, d’accès à l’eau potable et au logement dans les régions les plus nécessiteuses.
 Soutenir financièrement des associations locales en Afrique qui travaillent auprès des communautés pour répondre à leurs besoins essentiels.
 Promouvoir la solidarité et l’empathie en sensibilisant la société à l’importance de l’aide humanitaire.

 Rejoignez-nous dans cette noble cause ! Ensemble, nous pouvons faire une réelle différence dans la vie des personnes les plus vulnérables.

 Contactez-nous pour en savoir plus sur nos actions et découvrez comment vous pouvez contribuer à notre mission humanitaire. 

#FondsSocialTourangeaux #AideHumanitaire #Solidarité #Entraide #Afrique #ChangementSocial

Name: Fondazione Vittorio Polli ed Anna Maria Stoppani
Description:
La Fondazione nasce nel 2009 per volontà della signora Anna Maria Stoppani per realizzazzare i desideri del compianto marito avv. Vittorio Polli con la finalità di proseguire nel tempo, in loro memoria, le attività benefiche e assistenziali già attuate dagli stessi e garantendo nel contempo sostentamento all’amato Museo della Valle di Zogno (Fondazione Polli Stoppani ONLUS).


Le finalità della Fondazione, oltre al sostentamento del Museo, sono volte principalmente all’assistenza degli anziani, delle donne, dei bambini e delle persone in difficoltà con particolare riguardo per il territorio bergamasco, finanziando anche iniziative culturali di recupero del patrimonio artistico e storico locale.


La Fondazione ha sede nell’omonimo palazzo, residenza dei Fondatori, a Bergamo in Via San Giacomo 9, dove oltre ai locali istituzionali ed alla segreteria, trovano posto l’ufficio della presidenza e diversi spazi attrezzati per poter ospitare mostre temporanee e/o eventi, nel rispetto delle finalità costitutive della stessa.

Name: Florian Holsboer Foundation
Description:
Psychische Erkrankungen gehen uns alle an. Jeder hat irgendwas – und wenn man selbst nichts hat, kennt man jemanden, der was hat. 

Aber kaum jemand spricht darüber. Uns fehlen die Worte, vor allem die richtigen. Depression? Reiß dich zusammen. Angst? Nicht so schlimm. Diese Trivialisierung muss enden. 

Die Stigmatisierung psychischer Erkrankungen muss aufhören. Wir setzen uns für ein neues Bewusstsein für mentale Gesundheit ein. Denn wo Bewusstsein beginnt, hört das Stigma auf.

Wir wollen erklären, aufklären, informieren. Und einladen – zu einer Reise zum spannendsten aller Organe: unserem Gehirn. Genau dort entstehen psychische Erkrankungen. 

Wir müssen endlich verstehen: Psychische Erkrankungen haben eine körperliche Ursache und sind keine Einbildung, Simulation oder Charakterschwäche. Und wir haben eine Botschaft, die Trost und Zuversicht spendet: Depression ist heilbar – mit der individuell richtigen Therapie.

Und jeder, der offen weint, ist erst recht ein Held. Krankengeschichten werden zu Erfolgsgeschichten, die uns Mut und Hoffnung schenken. Wir wollen mit unseren Projekten Vorurteile gegenüber mentalen Problemen abbauen. Wir brauchen eine neue Sensibilität und neue Sprache für mentale Gesundheit. 

 Wir brauchen eine neue Erzählung psychischer Erkrankungen – darum verleihen wir der Seele in unserem Podcast ALLES NUR IM KOPF eine neue Stimme. https://spoti.fi/3VE4KMI

Name: Frederiksbergfonden
Description:
Frederiksbergfonden er en almennyttig, erhvervsdrivende fond, som har til formål at støtte aktiviteter på Frederiksberg, som engagerer, fornyer og understøtter byens udvikling.

Fonden støtter mange forskellige ansøgninger fra foreninger, netværk, arrangører, klubber, borgergrupper, kulturorganisationer og institutioner. Ud over løbende at støtte ansøgninger har Frederiksbergfonden flerårige partnerskaber, ligesom fonden kan igangsætte egne projekter.

Fælles for Fondens støtte til ansøgninger, partnerskaber eller egne projekter er, at Frederiksbergfonden arbejder inden for tre filantropiske retninger: Opvækst, Oplevelser og Medborgerskab.

Name: Pupkewitz Foundation
Description: Key to PUPKEWITZ Foundation’s Strategy are deliberate partnership with government and reputable non-profit organisations whose development goals are aimed at viable, innovative and sustainable solutions in response to national and global priorities. The Foundation’s projects are therefore aimed at addressing social and economic disparities and responding to government’s Education, Health, Community Care, Environment, Culture & Sport

Name: Dianui A.C.
Description:
Dianui AC es una organización sin fines de lucro que cumplirá 25 años de buscar activamente que las niñas y los niños tengan oportunidad de disfrutar de salud y nutrición plena en entornos familiares y sociales propicios para ese fin 

Se apega al cumplimiento de los Objetivos de Desarrollo Sustentable (ODS),  iniciativa promovida por las Naciones Unidas, especialmente en 5 de estos:
 
 ODS 2: Hambre cero
 ODS 3: Salud y Bienestar
 ODS 4: Educación de Calidad
 ODS 6: Agua Limpia y Saneamiento
 ODS 17: Alianzas para lograr los Objetivos

Dianui enfoca sus acciones a 5 momentos clave en la vida de las niñas y los niños:

1. Gestación
2. Lactancia
3. Desarrollo infantil
4. Escuelas Saludables
5. Adolescencia Saludable

Dianui cuenta con diversos programas de  "educación en nutrición" y "orientación alimentaria" dirigidos a los pequeños y sus mamas. Tales como :

"Nutribus tu transporte a la salud": programa dirigido a escuelas primarias, impartido de manera presencial, mixta o virtual.

"Buenas Nutricias : cultivando tu salud": consejos, talleres, educación continua para las mamas y la familia

Distintivo Código L: reconocimiento y educación a empresas con programa para apoyar a sus empleadas lactantes
Location: Naucalpan de Juarez, Estado de México

Name: <CodeAid>
Description:
<CodeAid> empowers charities through technology. 
We are a non-for-profit group aiming to help charities expand their online reach and potential by providing digital transformation services and agile coaching.

In 2021, we created a brand new experience for SecondTree, a charity supporting refugees' integration in the european society. We built fully re-usable campaign pages and donation journey for World Refugee Day and helped them beat their own fundraising record.

In 2022, we partnered with Project Access, a charity providing mentorship for students applying to top european universities. We supported them in the development of their matching software. 
Another partnership we had was with The Survivor Project, a charity supporting survivors of domestic abuse. We tranformed their digital experience with a brand new websites, catered specifically to the needs of survivors with plenty of exciting features.

In 2023, we are partnering with 5 charities: 
1. SecondTree, supporting refugees
2. Choice in Hackney, supporting independent living for disabled people
3. WILPF, supporting women's rights, peace and freedom all over the world
4. British Ukrainian Aid, supporting war victims in Ukraine
5. Elephant Care Relief, providing support for an animal charity

We went from 6 members in 2021 to more than 60 volunteers with 20 mentors in 2023, so we're extremely thrilled, humbled and exciting to see our teams grow and do tech for good.

Name: Worthy Village, Inc.
Description:
Worthy Village, Inc. is a non-profit organization dedicated to   providing impoverished women and children of Guatemala with a better quality of life.  To achieve our mission, we partnered with various groups of women who hand-craft beautiful products such as purses, ponchos and scarves.  Due to the lack of  economic opportunity surrounding the villages, these talented women are left in poverty.

Worthy Village acts as an intermediary between the artists and consumers.  Our goal is to create a viable marketplace for these women by importing their products to the U.S. for sale at www.worthyvillage.org.  We seek to justly compensate the Worthy Women of Guatemala to increase their income.

Worthy Village is also involved in other community projects.  One such project is the Worthy Village Healthcare Initiative, which seeks to educate families on basic healthcare, hygiene, and CPR.  The program also supplies toothbrushes and toothpaste for those in need. 

Many children of the families we work with do not progress in their education beyond 6th grade.  Our goal is to implement an after school program in 2015 in which students can come for tutoring, homework help and extracurricular activities.

With your help, Worthy Village can improve the lives of the impoverished people of Guatemala and make futures brighter.

Name: Impact 100 TC
Description:
Impact100 Traverse City collectively funds transformational grants in the areas of arts & culture, education, environment & recreation, family, and health & wellness in the five-county area including Grand Traverse, Antrim, Leelanau, Benzie, and Kalkaska.

The concept is simple and powerful: Gather a group of at least 100 dynamic women together to each give $1,000, pooling their money together to create grants of at least $100,000 each. Local nonprofits apply for funding by submitting their proposals for transformational initiatives that benefit our community.

By joining together, we're making a huge difference in the community. We want you to be a part of it.

Interested in making a larger impact, being around like-minded women, and meeting others that want to be a part of the solution? Check out the impact on our website!

Name: ASSOCIATION DELFINA
Description:
DELFINA is a non-profit association of general interest. Its aim is to help the most disadvantaged populations, particularly in developing African countries, as well as the homeless in France.
It implements long-term projects within the framework of integrated development taking into account the expressed needs of local populations such as
1. The supply of drinking water (wells, boreholes)
2. Rehabilitation of concrete schools, supplying schools with school supplies
3. Equip dispensaries with medical and first aid equipment.
4. The creation of vegetable gardens and chicken houses in schools and villages.
5. To facilitate access to schooling

To ensure:

● the design and development of the project
● the search for the necessary funding to carry out these projects
the implementation of these technical / or financial projects
● The control, the realization and the creation of a follow-up committee

L'association DELFINA est une association de loi 1901 à but non lucratif, créée en 2012. Elle a pour but de venir en aide aux populations défavorisées en Afrique, ainsi qu’auprès des sans-abris en France.
 
Elle met en œuvre des projets socio-éducatifs, sanitaires et agro-alimentaires, qui s’inscrivent au mieux dans le long terme. En réponse prioritaire aux besoins de la population locale, l'association intègre leur développement en proposant des initiatives appropriées et respectueuses.

Elle met en œuvre des projets à long terme dans le cadre d’un développement intégré prenant en compte les besoins exprimés des populations locales tels que :

- Adduction à l'eau potable (forage, puits)
- Réhabilitation des écoles en béton et approvisionner en fourniture scolaire
- Équiper les dispensaires en matériel médical et de premier soins
- Mise en place de jardin potager et de poulailler
- Faciliter l'accès à la scolarisation

Name: Greenbridge Family Foundation
Description:
The Greenbridge Family Foundation is a family-led philanthropic organization that prioritizes backing exceptional leaders and entrepreneurs driving durable, system-level change. We deploy philanthropic capital and impact-oriented investments to support innovative, scalable, and tech-enabled solutions that expand access, improve outcomes, and reshape the systems holding back progress.

Our decisions start with people — the founders, leaders, and teams with the talent, grit, strategic clarity, and learning mindset needed to tackle hard problems. We then look at the nature of the change they’re pursuing, the scalability and evidence behind their approach, and how their work aligns with our core portfolios and long-term vision. We aim not just to fund great ideas but to partner with the leaders turning those ideas into impact.

Our work spans several priority areas where we believe targeted capital and strong leadership can unlock outsized impact:
- education and opportunity: improving learning outcomes and expanding pathways to economic mobility
- mental health: advancing access to early, effective, and scalable mental health solutions
- environmental sustainability: supporting climate and nature-positive solutions that protect long-term ecological health
- women and children: strengthening social, educational, and economic opportunities for women and young people
- criminal justice innovation: supporting alternatives to incarceration and second-chance employment pathways
- entrepreneurship and innovation: backing underrepresented founders and emerging venture managers building inclusive solutions

Name: GivingFund
Description:
GivingFund is a digital donation and impact investing platform that allows every individual to be strategic with their philanthropy and put their money where their heart is. 

Using the Donor Advised Fund model, GivingFund activates strategic philanthropy and provides a single portal through which you can manage both your philanthropy and your impact investing. Automatically deduct payments into your personal GivingFund each month and then watch your capital grow through impact investing vehicles. 

When you're ready, deduct money from your GivingFund to make either strategic monthly donations, or in-the-moment gifts to any nonprofit of your choice. Large or small, local or international, we never limit which nonprofit organizations you can give to, because we know that each person's philanthropic choices are unique. GivingFund provides you with one-on-one support and data to help you understand your overall impact and develop a more personalized and strategic approach to achieving your impact goals.

Name: Good Karma Bikes
Description: Each year, thousands of bicycles come to us through donation and impound. Some are sold in our bike thrift shop, even more are refurbished by our volunteers and granted to those in- need. Our clients are primarily homeless in recovery, veterans, low-wage workers, and low- income families. However, we have never turned anyone away for financial reasons.

Name: Casa Ronald McDonald Belém
Description:
Em 26 de junho de 2012 foi inaugurada a Casa Ronald McDonald Belém por autoridades, parceiros e voluntários. A Casa Ronald Belém é destinada para crianças e adolescentes em tratamento de câncer no Hospital Ophir Loyola, provenientes do interior do Estado e Estados vizinhos e que não possuem onde se abrigar em Belém.

A Associação Colorindo a Vida viabiliza a sustentabilidade da Casa Ronald McDonald Belém com a coordenação do Instituto Ronald McDonald, principalmente através da Campanha McDia Feliz e de outras campanhas e doações de instituições públicas e privadas.

No Brasil existem apenas 06 (seis) Casas Ronald McDonald, sendo uma no Rio de Janeiro, quatro no Estado de São Paulo (São Paulo, Santo André, Campinas e Jahu) e a Casa Ronald McDonald em Belém, a única da Amazônia.

Continuem a ajudar a “colorir a vida” das crianças e adolescentes em tratamento de câncer no Hospital Ophir Loyola, pois ‘‘sempre é tempo de colorir a vida’’.

Name: Friends of Boston's Homeless
Description:
Friends of Boston’s Homeless supports innovative, solutions-oriented programs that help people experiencing homelessness transition from the streets and shelters into safe, permanent housing and lead hopeful, peaceful lives in our community. We focus on removing the final barriers of transition that city and state programs, and the people they help, can’t often address. The final steps out of homelessness are often the most crucial, and we help make them happen.

Friends supports a number of vital programs in the City of Boston that help end homelessness and our unique, nationally recognized Housing and Career Start-up Fund bridges the financial gaps to housing and opportunities individuals experiencing homelessness frequently face. Like a Parent/Teachers Organization or a “Friends of the Public Library” group, Friends connects private sector resources with public sector funding to address the needs of people experiencing homelessness. Friends therefore provides a unique vehicle for individuals, businesses, foundations and charitable organizations to be a part of the solution to homelessness.

Name: MAI Foundation
Description:
MAI Foundation is a venture philanthropic foundation committed to supporting the empowerment of young people through social enterprise.

Utilising extensive experience in partnerships and joint ventures, MAI Foundation is contributing to the development and expansion of the social enterprise ecosystem in Australia through support for innovative programs for young people.

MAI Foundation is the corporate charitable partner of ICD Property, a specialist property development firm based in Melbourne, and MAI Capital, a venture capital firm with a vision to encourage entrepreneurship in Australia.

Name: Grupo de Compras - FEHOSC
Description:
Solução em Gestão de Compras Conjuntas de Insumos Hospitalares para Entidades de Saúde

Há 12 anos o Grupo de Compras desenvolve processos de negociação de compras coletivas que possibilitem obter resultados diferenciados em pró de seus associados, esta gestão objetiva a obtenção dos melhores preços, condições financeiras, logística e qualidade. Oferecemos um serviço diferenciado, realizando as compras coletivas unificando os quantitativos dos associados, com isso, temos grandes volumes atrativos aos fornecedores.

(48) 3224.5866

Name: Sacramento Kings Foundation
Description:
The Sacramento Kings are about much more than just basketball.
By investing our time, our resources, our talent and our passion, the Sacramento Kings Foundation gives our region the support it needs to succeed, economically and emotionally. Because being a positive agent of change in our community is at the core of our ongoing efforts to do good, impact the world and make Sacramento Proud.

Name: I Read I Innovate
Description:
I Read I Innovate is an initiative aimed at teaching children how to love reading and be curious. 

This initiative aims to nurture love for reading, promote curiosity and encourage innovative mind-sets. Our mission is to reach out to different communities, start the conversation on reading, nurture abilities and publish their stories. The reading sessions are managed by volunteers.

This initiative was founded by Alice Namuli Blazevic, a Tech Lawyer, Partner at Katende Ssempebwa and Company Advocates and two-time Vice President of Uganda Law Society.

Name: Gallo Kriserådgivning & Værested
Description:
Gallo Kriserådgivning er en privat psykosocial forening. Vi arbejder helhedsorienteret og tværfagligt og tilbyder gratis samtaleterapi, socialrådgivning og værested - alt sammen samlet under ét tag. 

Vi arbejder hver dag indædt for, at alle skal have muligheden for, at leve et så meningsfuldt og berigende et liv som overhovedet muligt, uanfægtet sociale- og psykiske problematikker. 

Flere og flere danskere rammes af sociale- og psykiske udfordringer. Det er altafgørende, at der er mulighed for at få hurtig og gratis rådgivning, hjælp og social støtte. Alt for mange får ikke hjælp i tide, enten fordi de ikke har råd til at betale for en psykolog, eller fordi de er endt på lange ventelister til behandling i det offentlige. Det er dyrt for den enkelte, og det er dyrt for os som samfund. 

Vores løsning er helt simpel - vi møder mennesker der træder ind af vores dør med spørgsmålet om, hvad de har brug for. Herefter kan vi tilbyde den hjælp og støtte der er brug for, uden at skulle forholde os til kassetænkning, restriktioner eller begrænsninger. Den tilgang er grunden til, at vi i mange tilfælde er den instans folk kommer til, når der ikke er andre der kan hjælpe dem. 

Er du interesseret i at høre mere om vores arbejde eller overvejer du at støtte vores os, er du meget velkommen til at kontakte os på:

Gallo@kriseraadgivning.dk

Name: HOPE - Sophia-Antipolis SKEMA
Description:
Fondée en 2016 HOPE (Humanitarian Organisation Promoting Equity) est la plus grande association basée à SKEMA Business School et a été élue meilleure association humanitaire et sociale étudiante de France en 2018.
L'association est impliquée dans ses missions locales comme internationales, environnementales ou animalières.

Name: Danny Garcia Walking Man
Description: Danny and his wife Jackie Garcia (Lt Col, USAF, retired) work side by side to tell his story in his first book (estimated release Fall 2021).  Danny Garcia has walked on six continents, over 52,000,000 steps, for more than 24 years.  A calling, a movement to spread the love of God, to inspire, to share wisdom, and give hope. How? By walking and meeting people where they are physically and showing them how to walk in the spirit.

Name: Room To Heal
Description:
At the heart of our work lies the cultivation of a healing community through which people can overcome the legacy of trauma. This sits alongside an integrated programme of therapeutic and casework assistance. We work at depth and intensity over the long-term to support each individual in their journey towards healing and to help them to integrate into the UK.

We assist and we advocate. We do this because we believe in a world where human rights are respected, where those who are persecuted for their beliefs are supported, and their plight publicised. 

Our community, based in Islington, is made up of people from 30 countries, of all ages and religions, united by a common belief in humanity and respect for human rights. We provide people with a broad spectrum of support, from physical and psychological therapy to practical assistance with housing, legal protection and related challenges. 

Everything we do is intended to build and nurture the healing community that lies at the heart of our work. Our members see Room to Heal as their family in the UK, the place which they can rely on, the place where they belong. That means a lot when set against the backdrop of loss and trauma, broken trust, marginalisation and isolation people typically face in their daily lives.

Name: Stichting Urgente Noden Lekstroom (SUN Lekstroom)
Description: Ondanks alle goede voorzieningen in onze welvaartsmaatschappij zijn er veel mensen die in een urgente financiële noodsituatie zijn geraakt. Voor die mensen zet SUN Lekstroom (Stichting Urgente Noden Lekstroom) zich in. Aan mensen die tussen wal en schip dreigen te vallen, bieden wij hulp bij acute financiele nood. Wij helpen als andere loketten gesloten blijven; hulp in de vorm van een financiele bijdrage (gift) en/of een renteloze lening.

Name: TOP Jewish Foundation
Description: We make giving easy! The TOP Jewish Foundation can help you achieve your philanthropic goals through our consulting and investment management services.

Name: MeaningfulWorld
Description: The Association for Trauma Outreach and Prevention (ATOP) Meaningfulworld aims to raise consciousness, nurture resilience, and foster sustainability among local and global communities. Highlighting the perspectives of children, women, and refugees, ATOP assists in transforming emotional and psychological scars to promote healing, hope, reconciliation, and mindfulness. Dr. Kalayjian says, “The only healthy and permanent means of resolution for past traumas is through spiritual connections, through love, forgiveness, acceptance and release.”  ATOP addresses local and global epidemics, offering insight into the remedies that promote health, unity, and peace through mind, body, and spirit integration for collective and individual needs.

Name: The Daring Minds
Description: Daring is something that can do wonders in our lives, and in order to do that, we need to be daring first. The idea of "The Daring Minds" came into my mind while I was working out late at 1 am, and I asked myself, Why am I working out so late at night? What is my why? Is it just for being healthy, or is there something more than that? I am still trying to find my answer, though, and that gave me an idea to start The Daring Minds. There are so many people around us who have shown some sort of daring in their lives, but nobody knows about them. People have gone way beyond their comfort zones in order to achieve or fulfill their dreams, but at the same time, many of these people are not known to the world. With the help of The Daring Minds, I am trying to talk to those people who have shown some daring in their lives. What made them do so? What is their why? What are they trying to achieve? Is it money, fame, or something beyond that? Then I started to look around to find these individuals, and I was surprised to see that there are a plethora of people in all aspects of our lives who have shown a lot of courage, determination, and passion in achieving their life goals, which are not monetary but something that is core to their character. These individuals are real role models for our society, and people can learn a lot from them. The purpose of "The Daring Minds" is to showcase these daring individuals' stories to the world and how we can create a positive society around us by breaking out of our comfort zones and showing a little daring in our lives. If you have some daring stories to share or if you know any daring minds, then feel free to contact us through email at thedaringminds@gmail.com. It would be really helpful if you gave us your feedback or opinion about our series, The Daring Minds.

Name: Catherine Peachey Fund
Description: The mission of the Catherine Peachey Fund is to promote advances in breast cancer research and treatment.

Name: Landsforeningen Ung Uden Mobning
Description:
Velkommen til Ung Uden Mobning

MISSION
Udbrede viden om mobning til de voksne omkring børn og unge og samspilsproblematikker for dermed at sikre fælles afsæt, forståelse og sprog.

Være med til at skabe og vedligeholde rammerne for det tolerante og inkluderende fællesskab

Bidrage til at skabe et fælles vi, hvor fagpersoner, skole og hjem er samarbejdspartnere gennem kurser, oplæg og workshops

MÅL OG STRATEGIER

1)	At klæde de fagprofessionelle på til at kunne forebygge og håndtere mobbesituationer så de bedre kan:

            - Navigere i strukturerne i mobningsramte og mobningstruede grupper/klasser
            - Genkende og forstå problematikker og sociale mønstre
            - Arbejde langsigtet relationelt med fællesskabende didaktikker der skaber optimale forhold for 
              trivsel for alle i sunde fællesskabet. 

2)	At klæde forældre på til bedst muligt at kunne hjælpe deres mobningsramte barn

2)	At støtte, rådgive og vejledede mobberamte fællesskaber

Tlf 42 95 42 08
mail@uum.dk

Name: Boardified
Description: Boardified has joined Leading Edge! (This page is no longer active.) Follow us there for updates on volunteer leadership in the Jewish nonprofit sector. https://www.linkedin.com/company/leading-edge-hq/

Name: Crisi Come Opportunità ETS
Description:
Nel 2006 un editore indipendente, una produttrice multimediale, un artista, un musicista, una manager e un’esperta di cooperazione internazionale e comunicazione, hanno deciso di unire le proprie competenze e passioni per creare un progetto innovativo: un’organizzazione senza scopo di lucro impegnata nel settore della comunicazione sociale per  forzare il messaggio di ruolo sociale di impresa attraverso i principali motori della comunicazione pubblica: televisione, radio, stampa, web e riviste specializzate in terzo settore. 
Nasce con questo intento The CO2 Crisis Opportunity Onlus.
Il nome dell'organizzazione sottolinea le opportunità che nascono dalle crisi. L’anidride carbonica si trasforma in ossigeno nella fotosintesi, cosi le sfide nella crisi sono opportunità di emancipazione e riscatto individuale e collettivo. The CO2 Crisis Opportunity Onlus ha sede a Roma, lavora in Italia e all’estero, definendo di volta in volta le sedi operative, a livello locale, nell’ambito degli accordi di cooperazione, occupandosi del reperimento delle risorse economiche, tecnologiche, umane e ambientali.

Name: The Greater Give
Description:
The Greater Give, Inc. is a 501(c)(6) founded to compel more giving by cultivating a movement of shared responsibility in the workplace. Everyday, working Americans should feel inspired to give back, and we believe that their employers should have the tools and resources to help them. Our aim is to help employers easily implement workplace giving initiatives that empower the Everyday Philanthropist. We support businesses by educating employers on strategic philanthropy, advocating for changes in public policy, and connecting like-minded businesses. 

The Greater Give, Inc. is currently working to advance the Everyday Philanthropist Act – a bill that would establish the Flexible Giving Account (FGA), a pre-tax payroll deduction for employee giving that would make charitable giving easier for Americans and their finances.

Name: Zunft zu Safran
Description:
Geschichte
Zur Gründungszeit der Zunft bestand ein Zunftzwang. Nur wer Bürger der Stadt war und der Zunft beitrat, konnte einen Krämerladen eröffnen und an die Märkte gehen. Im 15. Jahrhundert schlossen sich andere Handwerker wie Bauhandwerker, Zimmerleute, Maurer und Steinmetze an und die Gesellschaft nannte sich fortan Gesellschaft zum Fritschi.

Nach Auflösung der alten Eidgenossenschaft um 1798, nach dem Einmarsch der Franzosen, wurden die Privilegien des Adels und der Zünfte aufgehoben. Als einzige überlebte die Zunft zu Safran diese Auflösungswelle der Zünfte in Luzern. Sie wurde als bürgerliche Vereinigung mit Interesse am luzernischen Brauchtum und an Traditionen weitergeführt.

Entsprechend der Devise des Zunftmeisters: «Freude der Jugend, Hilfe den Armen, Liebe dem Alter», hat die Zunft auch einen sozialen Charakter.

Heute schwankt der Mitgliederbestand zwischen 420 und 440 Zünftlern. Um in die Zunft aufgenommen zu werden, muss man männlichen Geschlechts sein, das Bürgerrecht der Stadt Luzern oder einer Agglomerationsgemeinde besitzen, mindestens 10 Jahre in der Stadt oder Agglomeration Wohnsitz haben und einen guten Ruf besitzen. Die Zunft zu Safran ist keine Fasnachtszunft.

Name: Casa Durval Paiva
Description:
m 1994, a partir de um diagnóstico de câncer infantil, uma família da cidade de Natal, no RN, começou uma história de lutas e superações. Eles apostaram no tratamento e viajaram com Fernando Campos para a Filadélfia, no estado americano da Pensilvânia, onde receberam apoio e solidariedade da primeira Casa Ronald McDonald do mundo.

Foi a partir desse drama pessoal e da ajuda recebida que surgiu a ideia de fundar na capital potiguar uma instituição semelhante à que foram acolhidos, onde crianças e adolescentes em situação de vulnerabilidade social são atendidas com dignidade e esperança de dias melhores.

O espaço físico foi doado pelo avô materno de Fernando e no dia 11 de julho de 1995 foi fundada a Casa Durval Paiva de Apoio à Criança com Câncer. Desde então, é realizado um trabalho psicossocial voltado à doenças oncológicas e hematológicas.

Hoje, a Casa Durval Paiva surge como instrumento que supre a deficiência do Estado, promovendo bem estar, qualidade de vida e inclusão social por meio dos demais projetos realizados com os pacientes assistidos pela Instituição e seus acompanhantes.

Description:

EWB-SFP has 5 active programs helping developing communities worldwide with their engineering needs as well as our ongoing R&D work with ATDT:

Vanua Levu Island, Fiji – water sanitation and distribution to stem typhoid outbreak

Arombe, Kenya – clean water supply – project assessment efforts

El Llanito, Nicaragua – water supply based on existing well

Weledi, Ethiopia – monsoon flood management and structural repairs for local clinic and school

Santa Rosa de Comunal, Ecuador - water supply and treatment 

Community Engineering Corps – local projects for under-served or displaced communities
Our members volunteer their time, energy, and expertise to projects that build the foundation for a community to thrive for years to come.

For Artists
Become part of WRAP portfolio of artists and position your art made from 100% recyclable materials for success within the art-buying community.

For Corporations
WRAP helps corporate philanthropy and finance teams to gain access to a wide range of artists working with 100% recyclable materials.

For Private Collectors
Today art is not only an aesthetic statement but rather a social position. Add an environmentally aware art piece to your private collection.

ART IS TIMELESS AND SO SHOULD BE OUR PLANET.
Help protect the environment by raising awareness about the recycled waste crisis through art.

ARTISTS – JOIN THE CAUSE BY ADDING YOUR ART TO WRAP’S DB.
By listing your art with WRAP you are protecting the environment, raising awareness about the waste crisis, and helping our planet.

Name: GO Humanity
Description:
*Mission*
GO Humanity is a 501c3 nonprofit that seeks to end poverty and hunger, promote good health and well-being, and foster employment opportunities and economic growth in ways that exemplify humanist values.

*Vision*
We envision a world in which all people have access to food, water, and life’s basic necessities.

*Our Humanist Values*
Humans Helping Humans. Small groups of people who roll up their sleeves to make a difference locally can create a thriving humanity globally. We support the simple idea that humans pitching in to support one another is how we make change.

Shared Power. Local Power. We work together rather than working for or over others. We recognize that people are experts in the needs of their own local communities. We center and support communities seeking their own liberation and empowerment.

Radical Inclusion. We need many diverse hands working together to build one beautiful world. We invite everyone who shares our values to join us — no exceptions. We aim for the uplift of the whole human family regardless of age, race, color, national origin or immigration status, body size, disability, relationship style or status, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, conviction history, or other culturally outlawed way of being.

Name: Orphanage Without Borders
Description:
Orphanage Without Borders harvests the power of technology to connect and engage individuals and resources with the needs of orphan children around the world.

 
Our approach

1. We gather and present knowledge about the needs of orphan children.
2. We provide a practical source of information for volunteers and organizations, increasing the flow of information.
3. We facilitate the creation of small tangible projects to meet the essential needs of these children.
4. We connect existing resources (organizations, individuals and corporations) to these projects.
5. We empower people to actively participate and make decisions that directly help orphans around the world.
6. We visually represent the projects and resources for anyone who wants to improve the situation.
7. We foster collaboration and crowdsourcing for more efficient results.
8. We promote the exchange of ideas and experiences, bringing donors, organizations and volunteers closer.
9. We provide transparency about the legitimacy of orphanages around the world.
10. We issue updates on the status of projects that are addressing the needs of orphan children.

Name: Working Classicists
Description:
There is a class issue with Classics and Classical Studies. Over the last hundred years, Classical Studies, Latin, and Greek have been gradually phased out of state schools in the United Kingdom. There are loads of reasons for this, and many of them are difficult to tackle, and deeply entwined with other issues.

University courses seem to overflow with alumni from independent schools, and this can be hugely alienating for those who went to the local comp. The experience of coming from the 93% turns from being the norm to them suddenly being the outsider. In such circumstances, outsiders need allies.

To be a Working Classicist is a way for a whole group of people - amateurs, academics, and professionals - to identify themselves with pride, and to make connections with others from a similar background.

It is a way of overcoming the imposter syndrome which so many Classicists from the 93% feel.

It is for those who consider themselves socialists (and those who aren't sure), who feel the world can be fairer, and who want to see changes in attitudes which have been entrenched over centuries around class-worth, academic opportunity, gender and sexual-orientation prejudice, racism and arbitrary exclusion created by our society and its institutions.

#HumansForHumans

Name: The Good Life Farm
Description:
The Good Life Farm has a simple purpose: to help children and teenagers who’ve fallen through the gaps and never been given a chance in life.

Since 2005, The Good Life Farm has provided animal-based therapy and educational programs to at-risk young people aged between 12 and 17. The Farm provides a safe, supportive and therapeutic environment where our young people can build their self-esteem, social skills, trust and confidence, while learning vocational skills that will help them with their employment prospects.

Our 'grassroots' healing program is the only one of its kind in Australia. We work with an internationally-recognised therapeutic model on a sustainable farm with a variety of animals.

We play an important role in the lives of the young people who come to us. As we watch them grow, develop and flourish, we see them discover their inner-child for the first time - and that is a truly wonderful thing.

Name: Communities for Communities
Description:
We are about bringing communities together.

With the support of our friends in Sydney, we've hosted more than 100 community events and raised more than AU$2m. This has built houses, schools, and community facilities in disadvantaged communities overseas in Thailand, the Philippines, South Vietnam, Indonesia and now a project in Fiji. Additionally, we have been able to fund paediatric physiotherapy hours, a play ground and now a hydrotherapy facility in Lucas Gardens School in Five Dock.

Our Four Pillars
•  We build, experience and celebrate our community. 
•  We reach out and help other communities to meet their needs. 
•  We raise awareness and understanding of the needs of others. 
•  We challenge and encourage other communities to set up their own Communities for Communities model. 

 "The proceeds of our work go beyond our community but the benefit stays here" - Lance Brooks, Founder.

Description:
Coral Gables Woman's Club is a volunteer organization that started in 1923 housed in the first Coral Gables City library.  We are a diverse group of Latino/Hispanic, African American, Caucasian and European women dedicated to service the national and international community by supporting the arts, conservation, education, public issues, health and financial programs, and especially our own free Children's dental clinic. 

Since 1939 the Club has operated our own children dental clinic and every year we provide over $200,000 in free dental services to underserved children in the Miami-Dade area.  We get these funds through grants and donations from individuals, and our Annual Casino Night fundraising event.

Some of our major donors are the John T. MacDonald Foundation, Bacardi, Delta Dental Community Care Foundation and the South Florida Health Foundation

Name: Feeding Nations Through Education (FNTE)
Description:
Our aim is simple:  HELPING PEOPLE HELP THEMSELVES. 
We empower the poor to become generous through a pay-it-forward economy. We do not give them fish; we teach them how to fish. It’s a hand-up not a handout charity. FNTE is a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization with a sustainable strategy that is taking thousands out of extreme poverty.

Name: THE BOUCHEDID FOUNDATION
Description:
The Bouchedid Foundation is a registered 501(c)3 non-profit charitable organization whose mission is to identify and evaluate thousands of charities and non-profit service organizations and rank them using our proprietary algorithm to determine which causes to fund directly.

We consider a multitude of variables including the focus of the charity, the urgency and direness of their cause, and the level of disenfranchisement and vulnerability of the group they service. We maintain a negligible operational overhead to ensure as close to 100% of all contributions go directly to the chosen charities. The ultimate goal is to fund the most immediate and worthwhile causes that are affecting the most underprivileged and marginalized groups.

Locally, nationally and internationally, The Bouchedid Foundation is an advocate for the most marginalized and disenfranchised. We scour every corner of the globe to help children and families in need. The Bouchedid Foundation (pronounced 'boosh-deed) takes a unique approach to helping donors large and small navigate through the hundreds of thousands of charities and non profit organizations that exist today (more than 664,000 to be exact). Many donors don't have the time, resources and expertise to figure out which charities are best aligned with the causes that mean the most to them.

We are currently focusing on Child Welfare and Family Hunger, Homelessness, Refugee Crisis, Human Trafficking, Domestic Violence, Food Insecurity, LGBTQ, Health Services, Suicide Prevention and Autism among other causes.

Name: Chris Bertish Foundation
Description: The Chris Bertish Foundation was developed out of Chris Bertish’s desire to continue to do good around the world, give back, and help others. We inspire positive change in the world through inspirational talks, films, books, and workshops, educational curriculums and guidance on Conservation and Sustainability.

Name: Studenteninitiative Weitblick
Description:
WHO WE ARE 

Weitblick (English: Far Sight, Vision) is a student initiative, founded in 2008. Weitblick promotes and supports equitable access to education worldwide. To achieve this goal, Weitblick works with dedicated and enthusiastic student volunteers in numerous locations throughout Germany. It offers students of all disciplines the opportunity to apply their skills, knowledge, and talents to a social project with a good cause while studying. Through commitment within our initiative, we hope to increase awareness of social responsibility. Rather than acting impulsively, Weitblick strives to achieve its goals in a well-organized and sustainable manner. The driving force of Weitblick is the idea that we can make a difference with our work.

WHAT WE DO

The guiding principles of our non-profit student organization are to mediate (exchanges), promote (projects) and educate (people). Each Weitblick location is responsible for, and provides support to, education projects in Germany and around the world. The projects are financed through donations, which are collected during numerous activities and events, such as parties and charity runs. In addition, we organize worldwide exchanges and excursions (for interested volunteers). The pillars of Weitblick’s existence are our members’ dedication and the financial support of our sponsors and friends.

Name: Santa Claus Incorporated
Description:
The mission of Santa Claus, Inc. is to provide critical assistance to youth in the Inland Empire by empowering children, thereby strengthening the family unit through a variety of desperately needed year round and holiday community based programs. 

Santa Claus, Inc. is a children’s charity, serving the California Inland Empire since 1952. Through the efforts of its many volunteers, including up to 4 generations, Santa Claus has been able to serve over 600,000 impoverished children. Since Santa Claus, Inc.'s inception, its goal has been to bring essential tangible items to children in low-income circumstances - from clothing, shoes, undergarments, blankets, and toiletries to backpacks, school supplies, books, and toys.

Youth Enrichment Services (Yes!) provides year round services under the umbrella of Santa Claus, Inc. Significantly touching the lives of over 100,000 children annually, Santa Claus, Inc.'s YES! programs are vital to children throughout the Inland Empire. YES! touches the lives of youth in our most impoverished communities in ways that help enrich and improve the quality of each child's life. Youth Enrichment Services says "YES we do, YES we will, YES we can" in times of greatest need in the lives of children.

Name: The Motivation Story
Description: The Motivation Story is a podcast where people can come and share their stories with the world!

Name: Associação dos Condomínios do Brasil
Description:
A ASCOB Associação de Condomínios do Brasil está há 27 anos no mercado e é destaque em seu ramo de atuação. Somos dotados de personalidade jurídica, constituída na forma de Associação, ou seja, uma reunião de pessoas com fins comuns, a mesma é sem fins econômicos constituída por prazo indeterminado, tendo por objetivo promover programas sociais aos condomínios e moradores de condomínios.

Devido ao seu historio de prestação de serviços desde 1991, houve a necessidade da criação de um registro definitivo para a ASCOB, este que foi oficialmente reconhecido no ano de 2015. 

Atualmente a ASCOB mantém seus endereços sede nas localizações em Contagem e Belo Horizonte. Espaços exclusivos para realização de suas palestras, cursos, consultorias entre outros, tais ações que vão ao encontro à nossa filosofia, missão e visão.

Name: Ferndale Cat Shelter
Description: The Ferndale Cat Shelter is a non-profit 501(c)3 dedicated to providing medical care, adoption services, foster homes and trap-neuter-release programs for felines in the Ferndale area and beyond. As a no-kill organization, our mission is to improve the lives of felines in the Metro Detroit community and elevate the standards of cat guardianship and adoption. We are also affiliated with Ferndale’s Catfé Lounge, a cat café that serves as our hub for cat adoption and fostering.

Name: CASIEF Canadian Anesthesiologists' Society International Education Foundation
Description:
Five billion people lack access to safe surgical care. Surgical disease kills more people annually than HIV, TB, and malaria combined. Unfortunately, surgical capacity is least where it is needed most. Surgical care in low resource settings is cost effective and an indispensable, indivisible part of basic health care. Our philosophy is to work with anesthesia providers in low and middle-income countries (LMIC) to develop self-sustaining programs appropriate to their own needs. Consultations and collaboration with ministries of health, anesthesia providers, anesthesia societies, and local training institutions are key to building successful partnerships.
For more information or to volunteer: admin@casief.ca

Name: The Bamboo Straw Company
Description:
In the US, 500 million straws are used per day, that is a staggering 175 BILLION per year. One can only imagine how much that is in Europe. Many of those plastic straws end up in our oceans, polluting the water and harming sea life. If we don’t act now, by 2050 plastics in the ocean will outweigh the fish. One small change can have a big impact: #stopsucking on plastic straws and use alternatives, such as bamboo.

Here at The Bamboo Straw Company we decided to act against this complete waste of nature. Our mission, to educate people and to ban plastic straws. There are so many good alternative, ranging from steel, paper and of course bamboo straws. The benefits of using a bamboo straw is that they are re-usable and it is a nature grown product, so less waste for the production cycle.

Name: Dev'Assos
Description: Fundraising consultancy

Name: Phillip and Christine Gore Family Foundation
Description:
Husband and wife team, Phillip and Christine Gore, formed The Phillip and Christine Gore Family Foundation (the "Gore Foundation") in an effort to ensure that as many Jamaicans as possible, especially children, are afforded equal opportunities to, amongst other things, a good, sound and solid education. With the foregoing in mind, the Foundation, via Phillip and Christine, has expended time, financial resources, ingenuity and considerable expertise geared towards improving the health and welfare of their fellow citizens. The Gore Foundation is the charitable arm of Gore Developments Limited.

Twitter: @1gorefoundation 
YouTube: Gore Foundation
Instagram: @gorefamilyfoundation

Name: HrlCentreUhai
Description: Le Healing Rebuilding and Living - Centre UHAI est une organisation de droit Congolais sans but lucratif établit au Nord Kivu à Goma. A travers ses objectifs, Il focalise sur l’amélioration des pratiques sociaux culturelles, les préoccupations citoyennes, l’initiation à l’éducation de la paix pour le développement et renforcement de la cohésion sociale des communautés.

Name: Business for Culture & the Arts (BCA)
Description:
BCA connects Oregon businesses with local arts organizations to champion leadership, funding and participation in the arts. The result? A community where innovation and ingenuity rule.

Programs & Initiatives:
Art Fair:Rediscover the diversity of our cultural community and learn about stimulating opportunities. 

Art of Leadership: Participants broaden their networks and ultimately play important roles in the arts. 

Arts Breakfast of Champions: BCA is proud to honor businesses and business leaders for their outstanding support of arts and culture. 

Behind-the-Scenes Events: BCA members are invited to network with other enlightened business leaders and explore special events hosted by arts creators.

Business Volunteers for the Arts: BCA matches skilled professionals with nonprofit arts organizations to accomplish important work. 

Cultural Concierge: Cultural Concierges are designated employees at member companies who distribute information about arts opportunities. 

Discounted Ticket Opportunities: BCA members can take advantage of discounted tickets to arts shows and performances.

Special Training Workshops: BCA offers a variety of special in-house training workshops, topics range from board and leadership training to seminars on current arts issues.

Coordinated Advocacy: On behalf of its members, BCA advocates state and local policy makers to improve Oregon cultural policy and public funding for arts and culture.

Name: Rotary Club of Salisbury Maryland
Description:
The more than 100 year old Rotary Club of Salisbury is a well-respected member organization whose product is service.  Fun, fellowship, and community service are hallmarks of the club.                                      
                                                                                                             
Interested in joining the Rotary Club of Salisbury and becoming one of our People of Action? Contact Ginny Malone at 410-251-6188 or gmalone@cbmove.com.

Name: DemoDairy Foundation
Description: To support existing dairy industry and training organisations and individuals improve the leadership and dairy farm business management capacity of dairy farm managers and owners making use of innovative learning approaches and evolving new technologies. These will contribute to a sustained improvement in farm profitability and farmer lifestyles.

Name: Yoga Aid Foundation
Description: Yoga Aid is a non-profit organisation that runs events to build awareness of yoga in the community and raise funds for charities in need.

Name: Coup de pouce
Description:
Coup de pouce partage divers projets humanitaires qui lui tienne à coeur. 
L'objectif ? Inviter au like pour les faire connaître.

Name: South African Schools Debating Board
Description: The SASDB is the official governing body of schools debating in South Africa. We oversee provincial debating and set policy at a national level. Our major purpose is to co-ordinate, develop and grow debating in the country. Every year, we (1) host the National Schools Debating Championships, (2) host the Academy and (3) select and train the South African Debating Team, who will represent us at international competitions. This year marks the start of two major SASDB projects: the new areas development project, and the new provinces development project. Both aim to develop and strengthen in areas without strong debating structures and resources.

Name: The Best of the World Network
Scholarship Foundation of St. Joseph County
Description: Whether you are a St. Joseph County high school student in search of scholarship assistance, or a potential donor wanting to make a difference by helping local students achieve their educational dreams, you’ve come to the right place. We are the oldest and largest St. Joseph County organization dedicated solely to raising and awarding funds for college scholarships.

Name: United Way Sault Ste. Marie & Algoma District
Description:
United Way Sault Ste. Marie & Algoma District is addressing the root causes of poverty and focusing on three of the priority community issues and their respective outcomes: Essential Services, Workforce Entry, and Food Security. 

Working collaboratively with the community is at the heart of our work. Because, when people come together, we can accomplish so much more than we ever could alone. United Way Sault Ste. Marie & Algoma District works with multiple community partners on strategies which reduce the impact of low income on the health and well-being of individuals and families in our community.

Name: All About Omaha
Description:
All About Omaha is a local, non-profit organization comprised of young professionals ages 21-40 who are looking to get involved both civically and socially and enjoy all Omaha has to offer.

Our mission is simple; we want you to get involved! Omaha is an amazing city with lots to do and so much more on the horizon. We work hard to find and create events and opportunities for you to network and make a difference. We focus primarily on Omaha awareness, community involvement and leadership development and we think you’ll find just about anything can fall into those categories.

Name: Global Action Through Fashion
Description: GLOBAL ACTION THROUGH FASHION (GATF) is a non-profit organization founded with the vision to create a more equitable and sustainable world through fashion. We provide education & informational resources for consumers, producers & fashion industry professionals.

Name: Kappa Alpha Theta, Alpha Lambda Chapter
Description: Theta teaches us the importance of aspiring to something higher and envelops us in a sisterhood that is loyal and loving. Theta gives us challenges, opportunities, and dear friends. It supports learning and service and encourages philanthropy. Theta greatly broadens our world.

Name: Saint Andrew's Freedom Forum
Description: The Saint Andrew’s Freedom Forum is working to raise the awareness of the denial of human rights and religious liberties of the Ecumenical Patriarchate while also celebrating and recognizing human rights and religious freedom defenders throughout the world. The Saint Andrew’s Freedom Forum is a secular initiative drawing inspiration from the Ecumenical Patriarchate of Constantinople, the religious center for the world’s 300-million Orthodox Christians, which is located in present day Istanbul, Turkey. The Ecumenical Patriarchate has acted with fortitude and dignity in the face of state sanctioned abuse and discrimination. Where the Ecumenical Patriarchate suffers the abridgment of human rights and religious freedoms, it has responded with calls for conciliation, tolerance and understanding among all peoples. To bring further attention to those whose human rights and religious freedoms are being denied, the Saint Andrew’s Freedom Forum organizes activities throughout the United States and internationally, particularly governmental forums, to echo the Ecumenical Patriarchate’s message that we must all co-exist in a harmonious and tolerant global community.

Name: Foundation for a Drug Free World HUNGARY
Description:
► A global problem, drug abuse demands a global solution.

“Research has demonstrated that the single most destructive element present in our current culture is drugs.”

Because young people presented with the unvarnished facts about what drugs can do are far more likely to reject them on their own, Truth About Drugs is in high demand as an educational tool in schools, law enforcement units and community-based settings. More than 62 million educational booklets have been distributed, and public service announcements have reached more than 260 million viewers in 123 countries.

► Education is best tool in fight against drug abuse

The Foundation for a Drug-Free World is a nonprofit organization headquartered in Los Angeles, California, and dedicated to the eradication of illicit drugs, their abuse and their attendant criminality. The Drug-Free World campaign is predicated on the statistically proven fact that wherever young people are presented with the unvarnished “truth about drugs,” illicit usage drops. Accordingly, the Drug-Free World information and prevention campaign features thirteen Truth About Drugs booklets—one for each of the most commonly abused substances. Campaign materials also include an Educator’s Kit to provide teachers, law enforcement and community groups effective tools to help young people make the right decision. Incisive public service announcements and documentary videos complement The Truth About Drugs series—ninety minutes on every drug of choice from those who have been there.

The Hungarian chapter of the Foundation is in Budapest.

Name: Rotary Club of Troy, MI
Description:
Rotary International is a global organization of more than 35,000 Rotary clubs. Since 1905 its mission has been to provide service to others, promote high ethical standards, and advance world understanding, goodwill and peace by connecting business, professional and community leaders.

Rotary’s charitable arm is The Rotary Foundation, whose grants enable Rotary club members to advance that mission. The Foundation is funded solely by voluntary contributions from members and friends who share the Foundation’s vision of a better world.

The Rotary Club of Troy, MI is one of the 35,000 located in southeast Michigan in the USA.  Our members are a group of people looking to assist people on a local as well as an international level. Thru social activities, fundraisers and fellowship we look to make the City of Troy as well as the rest of the world a better place.

Name: Edmonton Firefighter's Burn Treatment Society
Description:
Who are we?

The Edmonton Firefighter's Burn Treatment Society raises funds and awareness for research, treatment, and the recovery of thermal injuries since 1977. 
The Burn Treatment Unit is where you will find highly educated, strongly motivated and extremely caring and dedicated individuals who give of themselves to the burn survivors and their families when they need it.

Name: Blind Start of America
Description:
Blind Start of America is a non-profit, charitable 501(c)3 organization with the mission to help blind & visually impaired people learn & develop job skills to lead lives of independence. This will be achieved through education, training,counseling, communication & technology. One of Blind Start of America’s primary objectives is to provide job skills training & placement necessary to provide disabled persons with the personal & financial independence of purchasing or renting a home.

Brian C. Tom, Founder & Executive Director of Blind Start of America, became visually impaired in his early 20’s while he was starting his educational services company, California Educational Centers, Inc. Despite the onslaught of his disability, Brian overcame challenges faced with visual impairment & has operated a successful business since 1991. Today, Brian strives to help fellow blind & visually impaired persons enhance their job skills & other personal attributes to excel in the workplace & society.

Name: Tailored-by
Description: The call for transparency in the clothing industry has increased tremendously in the last decade. Tailored-by is an independent organisation that is committed to create transparency in the clothing industry in a unique way. Tailored-by believes that you can only make the right decision in life when all the necessary information is available. We therefore see it as our responsibility to provide this information to consumers through our labels. Tailored-by will show you the people behind the clothes and the Tailored-by foundation makes it possible to show your appreciation to your tailor(s).

Name: Community Works CDC
Description:
Three hundred sixty-five days a year, Community Works CDC works to mitigate barriers due to hunger, incarceration, father abseteeism, joblessness, affordable houstong shortages, unreliable or no transportation, exposure to crime, abuse and educational inequities. We tirelessley work to offer aid to wrap around services which include educatio, employment, housing, economic resources, and crisis management support. 

Our mission is simple: To restore Northeast Houston through faith-based programs promoting at-risk youth services, workforce development, and neighborhood revitalization.

Name: Foxbox
Description: We help support infants, and their families, through long hospital stays.

Name: Fundación Social Más Oporunidades
Description:
We want to build hope, tending with these actions to close existing gaps in the access and maintenance of people's life opportunities, through a comprehensive intervention strategy that is framed in 5 lines of action:
1. Health
2. Entrepreneurship
3. Education
4. Environment
5. Culture

For the benefit of the Family and all the people that comprise it: boys, girls, adolescents, young people, women, men and especially older adults.

Name: Printemps Solidaire (Association affiliée au Master II DHSD)
Description:

Printemps Solidaire is a Non-profit Association that aims to promote human rights through various actions in favor of the right of women, access to education, public awareness, poverty etc...
Created by the Master II Human Rights, Security and Development of the Catholic University of Lille, Printemps Solidaire operates at the local and national level in order to achieve its goals. 

If you share our values, feel free to contact us and join or create a mission that will help to promote human rights and make a difference in the world !

Printemps Solidaire est une association humanitaire à but non lucratif qui travaille pour la promotion des Droits de l'Homme au travers d'une grande diversité de missions au service du droit des femmes, de l'éducation pour tous, la reducation de la pauvreté etc.

L'association est affiliée au Master II Droits de l'Homme, Securité et Development (DHSD) de l'Université Catholique de Lille et opère à l'échelle locale et nationale.

Name: Kito International
Description: Kito International is a 501(c)3 registered social enterprise that partners with street youth in Kenya to help them work their way out of poverty through education, entrepreneurship training and employment. We believe Kito youth have the power to build a global movement of street youth helping their peers escape poverty.

Name: Fondation Groupe Santé Arbec
Description:
Organisme de bienfaisance enregistré depuis le 11 septembre 2018, la Fondation Groupe Santé Arbec a pour mission de recueillir des fonds afin de promouvoir, améliorer et contribuer à la qualité de vie et au mieux-être des résidents hébergés dans les Centres d’hébergement de soins et de longue durée (CHSLD) et les ressources intermédiaires (RI) du Groupe Santé Arbec. 

La Fondation vise à soulager les conditions des aînés en perte d’autonomie cognitive ou physique en leur offrant des services et des équipements visant à rétablir, maintenir ou améliorer leurs capacités mentales et physiques ainsi qu’en mettant sur pied des activités sociales et diversifiées à leur intention. 

Basée sur sa profonde conviction des valeurs familiales et du respect des résidents de même que de sa volonté à renforcer son rôle social et communautaire, la Fondation compte sur l’implication bénévole. 

C’est à travailler ensemble qu’il sera possible de réduire l’écart entre les besoins des personnes âgées en perte d’autonomie et le financement gouvernemental alloué aux établissements.

Name: Girls Of Destiny Canada
Description:
In the summer of 2003, Professor Pat Chacha embarked on her mission to build confidence in young women and empower them to accept and value their uniqueness. Professor Chacha created a one week summer camp called “Girls of Destiny”, the first girls camp of it's kind in Mississauga, that would change the lives of attendees by focusing on three pillars of Leadership, Etiquette and Personal Development.

15 years later, The Girls of Destiny summer day camp has impacted the lives of over 500 girls by helping them realize their dreams and creating their world. The activities in the day camp focus on fun and friendship while addressing issues that are specific to this group of girls aged 11-16 years old. At such a critical growing phase in a young woman’s life, Girls of Destiny has acknowledged the needs of these individuals such as self- confidence, healthy relationships with oneself and others, appreciating one’s inner & outer beauty and spirituality.

This powerful curriculum based on interactive workshops, physical activities and self expression has evidently resulted in confident, goal-oriented young women. In one week, these girls are now of their own destiny, aware of their beauty that radiates from within and equipped to make their own positive contribution to the world.

Name: Stanford University Dance Marathon
Description:
Stanford University Dance Marathon (SUDM) is one of the most well-established student-run philanthropies in the nation, as well as the largest student-run philanthropic event in the Bay Area. Every February, more than 2000 students dance for 24 consecutive hours to raise money for a charitable health-focused organization. 
	
SUDM is a collection of individuals with a common set of goals, working to champion the local fight for affordable, accessible healthcare. Since its founding in 2005, SUDM has raised over $1 million for 5 beneficiaries (Partners in Health Rwanda, Young Positives, Ellipse, FACE AIDS, and the Elizabeth Glaser Pediatric AIDS Foundation). 

In 2017, we are so excited to be benefitting the Uncompensated Care program within the Bass Center for Childhood Cancer and Blood Diseases at Lucile Packard Children’s Hospital (LPCH). The Bass Center is a treatment center relentlessly committed to providing care for every child. The Center’s mission is threefold: extraordinary care, continual learning, and breakthrough discoveries. Lucile Salter Packard, the founder of LPCH, aimed to ‘heal humanity through science and compassion, one child and family at a time.’ LPCH’s values of collaboration, advancement, respect, education, and service ensure that Lucile Salter Packard’s vision is realized by nurturing both the body and the soul of every child.

SUDM works to create a formative and defining experience for so many loving, empathetic, passionate DMers in the Stanford community. Everyone involved, from dancers to performers, musicians, technicians, professors, and speakers, joins together in the fight against pediatric cancer! We are committed to spreading awareness about childhood cancer and blood diseases and helping the Bass Center in its fight to improve the lives of children and their families, because they deserve the best care possible!

Name: The Doonie Fund
Description:
Kathryn Finney started The Doonie Fund on her birthday, April 5, 2020,with a $10,000 personal donation to encourage other black women entrepreneurs to continue to build their businesses in the middle of the COVID-19 crisis.  One Medium post, a few emails and 6 weeks later, the fund grew to over $150,000, providing micro investments to over 1500 Black women-owned businesses.  


The Doonie Fund is named after Finney’s grandmother, Kathryn “Doonie” Hale, a black woman entrepreneur who inspired Finney to become an entrepreneur.  The fund takes a holistic approach to economic impact by investing in a wide variety of businesses .

The Doonie Fund investment criteria are straightforward.  Potential investments have to: a. self-identify as a black woman, b. work on their business full-time, and c. have an established web presence (ex: an active website, shopify, instagram, etc). The fund is purposely frictionless as we firmly believes that black women know what's best for their businesses. Investments are distributed directly into the entrepreneurs’ accounts, within days.

Name: Herofund USA, Inc.
Description:
Today’s First-Responders face unprecedented challenges with every emergency call they answer. Emerging threats and new technologies create safety risks every day. Many departments, large or small, face the need for high-technology safety precautions while some departments struggle to fund even the most basic
safety needs for their personnel.

HeroFundUSA, Inc. was founded to meet these challenges head-on. Keeping our local First-Responders SAFE while they work to keep us safe is our primary mission. We also have funding available to assist families of First-Responders during times of tragic line-of-duty death and critical injury.

HeroFundUSA, Inc. is the First-Responder’s FIRST RESPONDER! We are there to support them BEFORE tragedy strikes to keep them safe!

Name: The Philadelphia Award
Description: The Philadelphia Award is among the most cherished, meaningful and prestigious awards conferred in, by and for the Philadelphia community. The award is given each year to a citizen of the Philadelphia region who, during the preceding year, acted and served on behalf of the best interests of the community.

Name: BOOK BANK USA
Description: BOOK BANK USA is a 501c(3) non-profit based in San Rafael, CA, USA since 1966.

Name: Kicks 4 The City
Description:
Kicks 4 the City is a charitable program, powered by the 501(c)(3) non-profit organization Legacy Leavers, that is committed to providing shoes for homeless communities within select cities across the U.S. and worldwide.

Kicks 4 the City connects like-minded individuals who share this philanthropic spirit and provides a platform for giving back through shoe and monetary donations, volunteering and service as a K4tC Ambassador.

Name: Tipsy Monk Foundation
Description:
Tipsy Monk Foundation is on a mission to help cancer patients by providing various services during and after chemotherapy. Medical Cannabis Consultation including FREE products, Grocery & Meal Deals, Meditation, Yoga, Cancer Support Group, Motivational Videos, Exercise via Virtual programs or digital content. Patients can access the content digitally 24 X 7. 

Donors get access to "Yogis Crib" virtual and physical community center. Donors get access to Weekly "Ganja Chai" & "Ganja Chanting" including tons of other cool perks.

Name: HoldTalks
Description: O HoldTalks é um podcast que surgiu durante a pandemia com um propósito inspirador e solidário. Seu principal objetivo é arrecadar fundos para o Hospital Fabiano de Cristo, uma instituição sem fins lucrativos que oferece suporte a pessoas carentes com câncer e suas famílias.
