Name: American Wheelchair Mission
Description: The American Wheelchair Mission is a non-profit organization with a goal to deliver brand new, free wheelchairs and mobility aids to physically disabled children, teens and adults throughout the world who are without mobility or the means to acquire a wheelchair. The American Wheelchair Mission will continue to change the lives of entire families with the gifts of hope, dignity, freedom and independence.

Name: Solon E Summerfield Foundation
Description: The Solon E. Summerfield Foundation engages in grant partnerships in the NYC-Metro area that seek to transform systems and pathways of opportunity so that young people most impacted by social, racial, and economic injustice can live choice-filled lives.

Name: Connect & Care
Description: Connect & Care creates events that help companies to live their values while connecting with young people.

Name: Sail for Water
Description:
Sail for Water, ONG reconnue d'intérêt général, est une grande aventure solidaire et collaborative qui vise à promouvoir un accès universel à l'eau potable en réalisant un tour du monde à la voile pour distribuer gratuitement des filtres à eau aux populations les plus démunies.

L’ambition de Sail for Water est triple : apporter de l’eau potable à tous ceux qui en ont besoin grâce à un filtre ultra performant et économique ; permettre à chacun de s’investir personnellement et de participer au projet ; sensibiliser le plus grand nombre, en particulier les plus jeunes.

Notre mission : Distribuer 1 000 filtres en 1 000 jours, pour venir en aide à 100 000 personnes !

En partenariat avec l’ONG Waves for Water, engagée dans la lutte contre l’eau insalubre depuis plus de 5 ans et ayant déjà distribué plus de 100 000 filtres dans une douzaine de pays, la mission principale de Sail for Water sera de poursuivre la distribution de filtres dans les zones qui, d’après l’OMS et l’UNICEF, sont les plus touchées par les maladies liées à l’eau insalubre.

Sail for Water c’est aussi une grande aventure collaborative qui a pour objectif d’investir pleinement sa communauté de membres, de lui faire prendre conscience de l’importance de la cause qu’elle défend et de l’utilité de son investissement.
Ainsi, les donateurs auront la possibilité de cibler leurs engagements, en choisissant le pays de distribution des filtres qu’ils auront financés, ou encore en rejoignant l’équipage le temps d’une action terrain.

Ensemble, avec Sail for Water, agissons pour que l'accès à l'eau potable devienne une réalité pour tous !

Name: Westland Familiefonds
Description: Het Westland Familiefonds brengt haar maatschappelijke verantwoordelijkheid op diverse manieren in praktijk. Lokaal en regionaal steunen we kleinschalige culturele en maatschappelijke doelen met het vrijmaken van tijd en financiële middelen. Een aantal keren per jaar besteden we daarom met alle werknemers en familieleden van Westland tijd aan maatschappelijke instellingen, door tastbare activiteiten met of voor deze groepen mensen te organiseren.

Name: Brockville and Area Community Foundation
Description: The Brockville and Area Community Foundation is dedicated to encouraging the establishment of endowment funds, supporting charitable initiatives and strengthening our community for the benefit of all by providing leadership on key local issues.

Name: No Rival Industries
Description:
Philanthropic impact project started with various friends across creative industries.
100% of our profits are used to solve generational issues.
We have a bias towards action--- Impact Over Apathy.
Location: Seattle (Production based in Miami, FL), WA

Name: C the difference: Cory Cares
Description:
C the difference: Cory Cares is dedicated to easing the hunger issues on Long Island by shopping for and stocking the shelves of food pantries, raising awareness of hunger issues and encouraging young people to make a difference in their communities by giving  of themselves to better the lives of people who will never be able to thank them personally. 

C the difference: Cory Cares  was started in 2012, by 12 year old Cory Nichols, in Oceanside, New York. 
 
Inspired by a documentary about the hunger issues on Long Island, Cory set out to raise funds, asking  for donations from family and friends and using social media, to provide monthly deliveries to the food pantry in his town, with the hopes of expanding to other communities in the years ahead. 
 
Today we have young people shopping and stocking the shelves of at least 4 food pantries each month. We have engaged hundreds of teens in the fight against hunger and eased the burden of hunger and food insecurity for thousands of our neighbors in need across Long Island, NY.

Name: Luna Peak Foundation
Description: We are a family run non-profit foundation dedicated to inspiring others.  We create books and products that celebrate life.  We are passionate about elevating the stories of survivors in the cancer and grief communities and strive to inspire others to embrace life with the same vigor.

Name: Monastery Vatopedi
Description: The Holy and Great Monastery of Vatopedi (Greek: Βατοπέδι, pronounced [vatoˈpeði]) is an Eastern Orthodox monastery on Mount Athos, Greece. Vatopedi was founded in the second half of the 10th century by three monks, Athanasius, Nicholas, and Antonius, from Adrianople, who were disciples of Athanasius the Athonite. The monastery was expanded several times during its history, particularly during the Byzantine period and in the 18th and 19th centuries. More than 120 monks live in the monastery.

Name: SEPO
Description:
SEPO is a non-profit organization dedicated to sustainability, education, and progress in western Zambia and northeastern Wisconsin.

Guided by anti-colonial and anti-racist principles and strategies, SEPO identifies local needs, harnesses community strengths, develops global partnerships, and builds more sustainable futures.

Name: Chicago Says No More
Description:
ABOUT US
Chicago Says No More is a collaboration of civic and business leaders, philanthropists and the leaders of not-for-profits dedicated to addressing the challenges of domestic violence and sexual assault in the Chicago metro area. 

GET INVOLVED – TAKE THE PLEDGE
Visit chicagosaysnomore.org to take the pledge to educate yourself and others about domestic violence and sexual assault. Be part of a movement to change the culture and the conversation surrounding these issues, to recognize their indicators and their impact. Awareness, education and engagement concerning domestic violence and sexual assault make a difference.   

WHEN YOU TAKE THE PLEDGE, YOU’RE MAKING A DIFFERENCE
Chicago Says No More is focused on breaking the silence and shame, and changing the way that America talks about—and doesn’t talk about—domestic violence and sexual assault. It’s about ending the excuses and misinformation that allow this American epidemic to continue to grow.

Name: Minnesota Planned Giving Council
Description: Our mission: To maximize philanthropy throughout Minnesota for the public good by providing education and programming that advances the knowledge and value of gift planning.

Name: LoveSpring - Water for Water
Description:
LoveSpring has a simple purpose – to provide clean drinking water to the world's poorest.

Since 2008, we have provided clean drinking water to more than 100,000 people in Africa, South America and Asia, through the sale of water bottles, organizing events, school runs, as well as partnerships with companies and donations from foundations. We work closely with local NGOs to strengthen local communities and to ensure the quality and maintenance of the projects. 

Read more on our website or contact us directly at info@lovespring.com

Name: FoodFlow ZA
Description:
With many South Africans already living below the breadline, the sudden lack of income caused by the COVID-19 national lockdown has caused a staggering number of people to no longer be able to afford to feed themselves and their families. At the same time our local farms are still growing food, but many restaurants, hotels and markets they deliver to are closed. We wanted to collaborate in shifting the food flow and get the produce to those who need it most during lockdown. Donors sponsor harvest bags which Food Flow purchases directly from small-scale farmers who deliver to targeted feeding sites in communities facing food insecurity. This model supports food livelihoods, enabling farmers to sustainably feed their communities in this time of increased need.
Food Flow acts as a stopgap, stepping in to temporarily replace clients lost due to COVID-19 and financially sustain the food livelihoods of small-scale farmers and producers until their original clients are back and business can return to usual. In the first month since launching, we raised more than a million rand through individual sponsorship of harvest bags. To date, six weeks since launch, 6,796 harvest bags have been distributed to more than 25 communities and R 809,537 has been paid out to small scale food producers benefiting more than 250 individual farmers. 
We work primarily in the Cape Metro and Winelands areas but have expanded into the Eastern Cape, KwaZulu-Natal and Limpopo.

Name: Cancer Support Community South Central Indiana
Description: Cancer Support Community South Central Indiana (CSCSCI) is a satellite office of Cancer Support Community Indiana (CSCI). CSCSCI office is located in Bloomington, IN and provides support services to anyone impacted by cancer (patients, survivors, family members, friends and caregivers). All programs and services are FREE to anyone who needs them. Programs are offered in several main "Pillar" areas: Support & Counseling, Nutrition & Wellness, Education, Social Connections, Information, Assistance, & Referrals. Check out our website for program listings and schedules, or stop by our office at 1719 W 3rd St, Bloomington, IN 47404.

Name: Union of Charitable Organizations of Russia
Description:
Союз благотворительных организаций России создан для упрочения и развития благотворительности в Российской Федерации, создание эффективной системы благотворительной деятельности.

Union of Charitable Organizations of Russia has its mission in consolidation, strengthening and development of philanthropy in Russian federation, in establishment of an efficient system of charity.

Name: Kastrup Rotary Klub
Description:
Kastrup Rotary Klub støtter en del projekter bla LIFE-projektet på Rigshospitalet. Midlerne skaffer vi ved at arrangere et socialt maratonløb på året sidste dag. Der er altid stor tilslutning til løbet der ofte indbringer 50.000 kr som vi donerer til formålet.
Derudover støtter vi lokalt dårlige stillede familier eller enkeltpersoner.

Hvert andet år udsender og modtager Kastrup Rotary Klub udvekslingsstudenter, der alle får en oplevelse for livet og er med til at skabe kulturforståelse og globale relationer.
"Long term" udvekslingen retter sig mod de 16-18 årige, der oplever, hvordan det er at bo i et fremmed land, gå i skole, være en del af en ny familie og få den lokale kultur helt ind under kroppen.
Hvert år sender Rotary globalt omkring 8.000 unge på et udvekslingsophold i et andet land, heraf op mod 200 danske unge til 19 forskellige lande.

Name: Capital Arts Patrons' Organisation
Description: The Capital Arts Patrons' Organisation is a volunteer non-profit organisation that has supported the ACT region's artists for over 35 years.

Name: Southern Sun Farm Sanctuary, Inc.
Description:
Southern Sun Farm Sanctuary is nestled in the Blue Ridge Mountains of the North Carolina High Country.  The property is adjacent to the Blue Ridge Parkway and located at 3500 feet.  The purpose of Southern Sun Farm Sanctuary is to provide a safe, secure place for abandoned, abused, neglected or retired animals.  Although the emphasis is on equines, the "herd" also includes various dogs and cats (as well as the occasional raccoon, 'possum or fawn).  
     Some animals are adopted into loving homes, some can have useful lives here as riding horses and some are simply "pasture potatoes".   No matter their outcome, all are treasured.
Southern Sun Farm Sanctuary is a 501(c)(3) public charity.

Name: LUND DEVELOPMENT SOLUTIONS INC.
Description: LUND DEVELOPMENT SOLUTIONS INC. is a company based in Eugene, Oregon, United States.

Name: Ametz Oved / אמץ עובד
Description:
Due to the COVID-19 outbreak, and just about 1M unemployed individuals in Israel as a result, "Ametz Oved" was launched.
"Ametz Oved" is a completely voluntary initiative that matches newly unemployed, highly qualified Israelis with individual coaches, who can encourage and assist them in their job search, through advice, connections or just providing a sounding board, confidence and accountability.

Name: Holky holkám
Description:
Holky, které vědí, že se mají dobře, a tak myslí na holky, co se dobře nemají.

Name: Caribbean Art Initiative
Description:
The Caribbean Art Initiative is an independent, non-commercial program and network that is working to foster the development of arts and culture across the entire Caribbean region, raising awareness and promoting dialogue worldwide.



The focus of the Caribbean Art Initiative is on the arts and culture of the entire region, including artists working internationally in the Caribbean diaspora. We seek to place Caribbean art and artists in open and active dialogue with artists and art institutions around the world.

Each year, the Caribbean Art Initiative provides opportunities for artists, writers, and curators from the Caribbean to engage with the world, and for non-Caribbean artists, writers, and curators to engage with the rich and diverse cultural context of the Caribbean. Our multi-tiered program encompasses residencies, support for research trips, and platforms for cultural dialogue.

The Caribbean Art Initiative collaborates actively and on the long-term with cultural institutions and not-for-profit organizations that exhibit and support artists inside the Caribbean region, as well as with organizations outside the region that provide public interest and exposure for Caribbean arts. The Initiative is frequently present at major international events that foster an interplay between regional and global art scenes.

Through the sum of these activities, the Caribbean Art Initiative is working to nurture the next generation of artists, curators, and writers interested in the region and in contemporary issues in global development. These efforts advance future intercultural dialogue in diverse environments and recognize cross-border movements. As such, the Initiative is committed to the belief that artistic expression can shape social, economic, political, and cultural conditions – that a healthy cultural life is indispensable to a well-functioning, prosperous, equitable society.

Name: SUARA FOUNDATION
Description:
Suara Foundation is a non-profit organization whose main aim is to offer better living conditions to street cats in Barcelona.
The Foundation carries out the control of feline colonies through the program C.E.S., we give veterinary assistance to sick cats, we take care and socialize them before giving them up for adoption. We also stand out the cat figure as a companion animal through different activities.

Description: La ruée vers l'eau est une association humanitaire qui vient en aide aux populations les plus démunies du Niger à travers l'élaboration de forages et l'aide aux orphelins. #développementdurable #eautonomie

Name: Team Holyfield
Description:
Evander Holyfield is the former five time heavyweight champion of the world; Evander was recently named the “Greatest Cruiserweight of All-Time”.

Much more importantly, Evander is a faith-based individual, unambiguously devoted to the principles and guidelines espoused by the Bible.

Evander’s dedication to his faith is reflected in his passionate investment of time and energy in his family and philanthropic activities.

Team Holyfield embodies and manifests Evander’s vision on faith, family and foundation.

Team Holyfield’s mission and prime directive is focused on creating and sustaining relationships with individuals, companies and organizations that support altruism, community relations and humanitarian efforts.

Team Holyfield’s unrelenting energy is directed towards one objective: to surface and evangelize faith, core family values and selfless contributions to those in need.

Together, with the help of our best-in-class partners, the future will become one with The Glory.

Name: Felix E. Martin Jr. Foundation
Description: The Felix E. Martin Jr. Foundation is committed to empowering the residents of Muhlenberg County as they build on the strengths of the community, shaping the best possible place to live, work & raise a family.  We support transformative ideas and projects that build a more vibrant community and expand opportunities for all who live in Muhlenberg County.

Name: جمعية غياث
Description: جمعية متخصصة في العون العاجل واللاحق للمنكوبين من المواطنين والوافدين في منطقة جازان وتأهيل المتطوعين وتثقيف المجتمع فيما يتعلق بحالات الطوارئ.

Name: Roarthon at Florida International University
Description: Roarthon, FIU’s Dance Marathon, is a year-long fundraising campaign ending in a 17-hour dance party. All the funds raised benefit the Children's Miracle Network Hospitals program at Miami Children's Hospital Foundation, the fundraising arm of Nicklaus Children's Hospital. Children's Miracle Network Hospitals is a non-profit organization dedicated to saving and improving the lives of children by raising funds and awareness for 170 children's hospitals throughout North America. Since 1985, Nicklaus has been the only Children's Miracle Network Hospital in South Florida. Over 300 students participate in the 17-hour fundraiser that takes an entire year and a committee of 50 plus students to plan and activate. As of 2017, the FIU community has raised over 1 million dollars!

Name: Interact Club de Nova Prata
Description: O Interact une jovens de 12 a 18 anos para desenvolverem suas habilidades de liderança e descobrirem a força do lema “Dar de Si Antes de Pensar em Si”.

Name: ONG Sourires d'Enfants
Description: Association de solidarité internationale œuvrant en faveur d’un accès durable à l’éducation de base, la formation professionnelle, l’hygiène et la nutrition dans les zones rurales isolées du Laos, du Vietnam et du Bénin.

Name: Hamptons Community Outreach
Description: Hamptons Community Outreach works to bridge the opportunity gap experienced by underserved youth, families, and others across our community. Our goal is to elevate the quality of life of local children, families, and seniors—addressing their basic physical and emotional needs while nurturing young people creatively and academically. We bring people of diverse backgrounds together in a spirit of service, generosity, and kindness.

Name: Kiwanis Club of Kalamazoo
Description:
Welcome to the Kiwanis Club of Downtown Kalamazoo!

Kiwanis is a global organization of volunteers dedicated to changing the world one child and one community at a time.

Every Wednesday we meet at noon at the Radisson Plaza Hotel in downtown Kalamazoo for lunch. We enjoy socializing, laughter, and a fun and informative program about current events, local businesses, interesting people, historical topics, or other items of interest. We are never bored or boring.

We fund raise and work for local nonprofit charities. Children are the primary focus of Kiwanis clubs internationally and they are our primary focus as well. We spend a lot of time helping the folks at Pretty Lake Vacation Camp provide an outstanding, week-long summer resident camp to underprivileged children. We provide dictionaries to every third grader in Kalamazoo Public Schools, and we support Constance Brown Hearing Centers provide new born hearing screenings. We are busy and active, but always having fun.

We invite you to come join us for lunch and see what we are all about. You will not regret it, and the children you help will be forever changed because of it.

Name: LiveGive
Description: We are philanthropy consultants.  We advise families, foundations and corporations on their charitable giving in order to facilitate the greatest impact.  We work with you to accomplish three goals:  gain clarity, invest strategically and maximize value.

Name: Effective Altruism Melbourne
Description:
Most of us want to make a difference. We see suffering, injustice and death, and are moved to do something about them. But working out what that ‘something’ is, let alone actually doing it, is a difficult problem. It would be easy to be disheartened by the challenge.

 Effective altruism is a response to this challenge. Effective altruism is a research field which uses high-quality evidence and careful reasoning to work out how to help others as much as possible. It is also a community of people taking these answers seriously, focusing their efforts on the most promising solutions to the world's most pressing problems.

Name: The Giving Lens
Description:
The Giving Lens was founded in 2009 by Colby Brown in an attempt to give something back to the world and the vary places that we all love to capture. 

Today, The Giving Lens is fueled by a vision. Our vision is to see the three things we love most come together for a greater good: our love of travel, our passion for photography, and our deep desire to give back to those in need. Our vision comes is to partner with amazing organizations in developing nations, who are at the ground-level of a community, reaching out with the utmost respect and admiration for the people, and attempting in their seemingly small, but actually enormous way, to help.

 Our vision is to see photographers moved to action out of compassion, humbleness, and the understanding that seeing the world through a lens and capturing all it’s moments of beauty and heartache, of victory and agony, of joy and struggle must be one of the most blessed roles on Earth – whether you’ve shot half a million frames, or just a few; whether it is your hobby, your job, or your entire life. Our vision is to have willing hands and hearts meet us around the world to do what they can to make the world just that much better. To meet us on a the ground, somewhere far away, and give our time, energy, resources, and love and to humbly hold out our cameras as an offering to anyone who may need it.

Name: Age Concern Bermuda (ACB)
Description:
Our programs consist of information, advice and referral services, financial hardship fund support for groceries, prescription drugs, food and minor home repairs for qualifying members. 

In addition, Age Concern members have access to free information seminars, discounts and concessions from local businesses including but not limited to: legal assistance, successful ageing planning and home care support.

Name: The LENS - Lived Experience Network
Description:
The LENS is a network of people who believe in the benefits of creative and cultural engagement to individual and collective wellbeing.

By working in partnership and acting as a critical friend, The LENS will champion access to creative and cultural opportunities for all. 

The LENS will work to ensure that the voices of those with lived experience remain at the heart of the arts, health and wellbeing movement.

Name: Major and Planned Giving Development
Description:
Many charities never maximize their true fundraising capacity because they ignore or minimize the importance of offering gift planning to all their donors. The fundraising landscape is changing. Donors want options and they want the charities they support to change the way they fundraise. Donors want to make meaningful gifts on their terms. This requires a shift in the way organizations think about gift planning. 

We help to develop and execute a plan to raise more planned gifts with an approach that integrates gift planning into every aspect of fundraising. Throw away the 3 legged stool. They are not annual fund donors, major gift donors or planned giving donors. They are donors period. 

If your organization is ready to launch or expand your planned giving effort or take a mediocre program to new heights we can help. We have worked with many non-profits who knew they could do so much more in planned giving but never figured out how. This is often due to a lack of process and plan, an apathetic board. We work closely with our clients to develop and implement a plan that gets results.

Name: Next Level Social Impact
Description: Next Level’s mission focused on addressing persistent inequities in Greater Boston by connecting high impact nonprofits with the resources they needed to thrive. From 2019-2022 Next Level supported its portfolio with a total investment of over $1.5 million through a combination of direct multi-year unrestricted cash grants, co-investments, and grantee-centered strategic counsel from a network of local partners.

Name: Plataforma Mexicana de Cooperación Internacional
Description: Somos una  plataforma dedicada a incentivar el  intercambio comercial, cultural, científico y académico entre México, Los Países Bajos  y la Unión Europea.

Name: Le PolyCrew
Description: Clement Massit (pilote) et Dorian Caullireau (co-pilote), deux étudiants de 20 ans de Polytech Annecy-Chambéry, s’associent pour embarquer dans l’aventure 4L Trophy, lors de la 25ème édition du raid, en février 2022 !

Name: Children do Amanhã
Description:
Children do Amanhã est une association humanitaire et caritative qui a pour objet d’aider au développement des populations défavorisées au niveau international.

Elle intervient principalement auprès des enfants afin d'améliorer leur quotidien scolaire et pouvoir les aider à construire un avenir.

Notre objectif :
Faciliter et permettre l’accès au savoir et à la connaissance. 
Car sans instruction, sans sensibilisation, sans éducation, aucun développement humain, social, sanitaire ou économique n’est possible.

                           *******************************************

Children do Amanhã is a humanitarian and charitable association which aims to help the development of disadvantaged populations at the international level.

It works mainly with children in order to improve their daily school life and be able to help them build a future.

Our objective :
Facilitate and enable access to knowledge.
Because without instruction, without awareness, without education, no human, social, health or economic development is possible.

Name: Albanian Canadian Association of Calgary
Description: ACAC is a non-profit organization that provides a voice to the Albanian community in Calgary.  ACAC operates without religious, political or regional divisions to maintain the national Albanian spirit in Canada. It does this through the cultivation of values, culture, national traditions and Albanian language as well as the provision of diverse services for different age groups in the Albanian community. ACAC strives with dedication for the honored involvement of the Albanian community in Canadian society, the enhancement of the values and the name of the Albanians in Canada, their full representation in different state and social structures as equal among all the other nationalities in the composition of Canadian multi-cultural society.

Name: Hanne Howard Fund
Description: Hanne Howard Fund is a hands-on, family-oriented charity that works directly with 115 vulnerable children and youth in Kenya to help transform their lives and achieve self-reliance through education, compassionate care and skills training. HHF was founded in 2006 by Hanne Howard and Ted Horton.

Name: Save Ukraine Today
Description: Stop this war. We need to act now! Glory to Ukraine!

Name: 黑土麦田公益
Description:
黑土麦田（Serve For China）是在中华人民共和国民政部备案的人才培养与精准扶贫公益项目，由耶鲁大学的优秀中国毕业生联合发起。

黑土麦田“乡村创客”计划（Serve For China Fellowship）每年选拔、培训、资助、支持一批国内外顶尖学府的中国籍优秀毕业生到国家级贫困县的乡村开展产业扶贫和社会服务。旨在让中国的优秀青年易于投身公共事业，用实干与创新为脱贫攻坚和农村发展作出贡献，通过磨砺成长为中国和世界的建设者。

Name: Women's Fund of Long Island
Description:
The Women’s Fund of Long Island consists of dedicated women and men who have worked together for over twenty years to empower women and girls on Long Island through our three program areas:

Impactful Grantmaking
Young Women's Leadership Program
Education and Awareness

Our vision is a Long Island where all women and girls reach their full potential. Our mission is to ignite networks that transform the lives of women and girls on Long Island through grant-making, leadership development, education and philanthropy.

Women's Fund of Long Island: Investing in the Power of Women and the Dreams of Girls

Name: Règles d'Or
Description:
Saviez-vous qu'en France, 1,7 million de femmes et de jeunes filles réglées n'ont pas accès aux protections périodiques par manque d'argent ? Certaines s'interdisent même d'aller en cours ou d'aller travailler des jours-là.

Etudiants à l'emlyon, l'iaelyon et l'INSEEC, c'est à partir de ce constat que nous avons décidé de nous associer pour lutter contre la précarité menstruelle.

L'initiative étudiante Règles d'Or organise des collectes de protections périodiques dans la région lyonnaise et libère la parole autour du cycle menstruel.

Name: Elays Network
Description:
Elays is youth based organisation, our aim is to create a platform for youth where they can share their talent and ideas. Living in a western society is a challenging task and it's time to bridge the gap and reach out to something big. Be part of our notion and make something of yourself.

Our aims to inspire the youth through skill enhancing projects and encourages the young to become role models, volunteers and productive members of society. Elays builds a platform for kids and young adult to interact with the professional world. It liaises with government and community groups to ensure that their voices are heard. Although Elays welcomes every ethnic background, 
 
CHANGING THE PROSPECTIVE OF THE NEGLECTED.
