Sports (swimming, Football and Gym)
International Relations & Politics (working with other NGOs, American Embassy and Foreign Office)
Networking with Professionals in the field 
Project Vulnerable - working as a prevent worker to help young people before expulsion. Preventing NEET, Gang violence and extremism

Name: CSWCSS ALL-ROUND EDUCATION FOUNDATION
Description:
CSWCSS ALL-ROUND EDUCATION FOUNDATION

Cheung Sha Wan Catholic English Secondary School (CSWCSS) was founded in 1970, and has entered its 50th anniversary in a blink of an eye. CSWCSS has always adhered to the belief in quality teaching. The development and achievements in various fields such as art are obvious to all. Graduated alumni still adhere to the spirit of the school motto of "unstoppable self-improvement".

Quality education enables students to improve their competitiveness. Schools need the full support of various stakeholders to help improve teaching effectiveness and promote diversified education, so that more students can develop their strengths. In addition, whole-person education is the general trend, which requires a lot of additional and stable resources.

Whole-person education not only cultivates students' development in many aspects, including morality, intelligence, physique, social group, and beauty; CSWCSS is also determined to develop youth leadership training, hoping that they can pass on this spirit to other students in the school, and use their lives to influence lives. Let them give back to the society and their alma mater after graduating in the future, so as to achieve the effect of passing on fire from generation to generation, and sow excellent seeds for the long-term future.

CSWCSS is located in Sham Shui Po District. Many students come from grassroots families. We must not let them lose their studies and opportunities for upstream because of their financial ability. In view of this, under the promotion of successive principals, the "CSWCSS All-Round Education Foundation" was officially established on October 27, 2018 to assist CSWCSS in fully implementing whole-person education. After a long application process and complicated procedures, "CSWCSS All-Round Education Foundation" was finally officially notified by the Hong Kong Inland Revenue Department on July 15, 2020 as a Hong Kong charity organization, and an official

Name: Player's Provisions
Description: Our mission is to provide in and out of classroom resources for underserved K-12 students in Alabama through student athlete advocation. By partnering with local school systems we provide students the resources they need to get the best output from their education.

Name: The Black Fathers Foundation
Description: The mission of the Black Fathers Foundation is to give black men and organizations working with them the resources needed to help fathers overcome any and all obstacles to being the best fathers they can possibly be.

Name: Fundación Criteria
Description:
Fundación Criteria es una entidad políticamente apartidaria, sin fines de lucro, cuyo fin primordial es propender al bien común y trabajar por la Seguridad y el Desarrollo Humanos teniendo a la Sociedad Civil como actor protagónico complementario a la acción del Estado.

En su alcance, atiende una problemática amplia y variada: Seguridad económica, educativa, sanitaria y medioambiental, entre otros ámbitos de interés.

 Nuestro principal desafío es incrementar, fortalecer el sentido de responsabilidad y el compromiso del conjunto de la sociedad, en particular sus jóvenes líderes, con principios y valores que aseguren el derecho a la libertad y dignidad de la persona humana.


Criteria Foundation is a non-profit, without political trend organization, whose primary purpose is to foster the common good and work for Human Development & Human Security having Civil Society as a complementary lead actor to state action.

In scope, serving wide and varied problems: economics, educational, health and environmental safety, among other areas of interests...
Our main challenge is to increase and strengthen the sense of responsibility and commitment of the whole society, but in particular young leaders with principles and values that ensure the right for freedom and dignity of the human person.

Name: Minds Matter Detroit
Description:
The youth is the future and Minds Matter Detroit is working to make the investment in their dreams and passions. Minds Matter Detroit is a non-profit organization dedicated to transforming the lives of accomplished high school students from low-income families by broadening their dreams and preparing them for college success. We bring academically talented and motivated high school students together with dedicated mentors to help them achieve academic excellence and expand their educational horizons, with the goal of ultimately gaining acceptance to college. This is accomplished by students committing to a 3-year program that includes academic instruction, SAT test prep, individualized mentoring support, and summer pre-college programs. 

Our Minds Matter Detroit students experience our program at no cost to them with the help of our devoted volunteers, generous donors, and consistent fundraising efforts.

For more information on how to get involved visit our website at http://mindsmatterdetroit.org/ or contact us at Detroit@MindsMatter.org!

Name: Rei Foundation Limited
Description:
Rei Foundation Limited was established in 2012 with the goal of contributing to social change through sustainable development. Rei Foundation collaborates with diverse partners in New Zealand and internationally to facilitate positive development for communities and individuals. Rei Foundation also supports research projects that aim to provide new knowledge, valuable in the fields of community development and peacebuilding.

Our mission: To contribute to creating a world in which every person lives with dignity and has their life celebrated.

Our values: Advancing human dignity, celebrating human diversity.

Rei Foundation insists upon a human rights-based inclusive approach, promotes creativity to express the uniqueness and identities of individuals and communities, and builds peace and promotes wellbeing within and among individuals, communities and the wider global community.

Name: Premio Eugenio Garza Sada
Description:
El Premio Eugenio Garza Sada se instituyó para destacar y reconocer las actividades realizadas por personas e instituciones nacionales o extranjeras que coadyuven al bienestar de la comunidad mexicana a través del desarrollo, mejoramiento y promoción de la capacidad productiva de sus recursos humanos y físicos.

FEMSA y el Tecnológico de Monterrey, invitan a las instituciones de investigación, agrupaciones profesionales, asociaciones civiles, organismos intermedios, empresas, instituciones educativas y entidades gubernamentales del país o extranjeras a presentar candidatos para el Premio Eugenio Garza Sada 2016.

Name: Harmony Revolution
Description:
Purpose: Restore the World for Future Generations

Mission: We are a community-based, diversity-rooted organization focused on helping make the world around us a better, happier place. With the help of our tireless staff, we organize fundraisers, exciting community-building events, and offer in-depth training sessions for our special volunteers.

Vision: Global Restoration

Where we recognize, embrace and embody the Oneness of All.

We Welcome ALL to Join Harmony Revolution & Make an Impact!

Name: UNAH (Unissons Nous pour l'Avenir de l'Humanité)
Description: UNAH s’intéresse aux actions de solidarité, de développement, de formation, d’éducation, d’intégration sociale en faveur des enfants en situation de vulnérabilité dans le monde en général et en Afrique en particulier. Depuis 2016, le territoire sur lequel l’association concentre son action correspond aux territoires situés à l’est de la République Démocratique du Congo. Il s’agit du Sud et du Nord Kivu.

Name: Rule Foundation
Description: The Rule Foundation seeks to advance the health, wellbeing and visibility of lesbian, gay, bisexual, transgender and intersex (LGBTI) New Zealanders. We give funds for projects and activities that support LGBTI people, and for education and research on LGBTI issues. Donate today to help us help LGBTI Kiwis.

Name: Soap Aid North America
Description:
Soap Aid is a not-for-profit organization committed to saving children’s lives through improved hygiene while positively impacting the environment. Soap Aid has been operating in Australia since 2011 and now expanded into the United States.

Our humanitarian and environmental mission is made possible by our partnership with the hotel industry. We reprocess and deliver recycled hotel soap to disadvantaged communities around the globe.

Our impact partners work closely with local communities to provide vital hygiene education on the importance of handwashing with soap.

Together we aim to achieve lifesaving and sustainable improvements in global hygiene practices.

Please contact us for more information on how you can be involved in this critical mission.

Name: RobynGoods
Description: We are a Solidarity Community in favour of the defence of Social Causes. We rely on our online platform where we allocate 75% of the profit from our products to the Non-Profit Entity that our users choose.
Location: Santa Cruz de Tenerife, Santa Cruz de Tenerife

Name: Dutch American Police Department Foundation
Description:
The Dutch American Police Department Foundation is a group of enthusiastic volunteers, whose goal is to let children (and sometimes adults) who are obstructed in their life because they suffer from a chronic physical, mental and / or terminal illness, experience a positive break from their every day battle. We do this by supporting and lustering rides and events for this target group with replicas of original U.S. police vehicles and other special vehicles. To make the experience as realistic as possible we use vehicles that resemble police vehicles in the U.S. and during these events we dress in the original clothing that is used by the policeservice of the particular vehicle.

What exactly does the Dutch American Police Department ?

Our main objective is, as stated before, providing the most beautiful and best possible positive experience for children (and sometimes adults) who have a very difficult and stressful time because of medical or psychological reasons. We have various ways to establish this, where multiple options can be combined. Here are some of these options:
- Escorting for example Truck Runs and motorcycle sidecar rides.
- Attendance at events for the target group, where the vehicles can be viewed and the children can sit in them.
- Re-enactment of police actions by the U.S. police at events for the target group (this is explicitly not taking place on public roads!).
- Transportation of persons from the target group as part of a special wish. This is demanded by Wish foundations and only if the medical condition allows it. 
In addition we do our utmost best to fullfill special requests where possible. We expressly state that we do not cooperate with demands or requests which can compromise public order and security . It is our aim to have a good relationship with the Dutch Police and other rescue agencies, based on the great respect we have for their tasks and duties.

Name: GO InterNational
Description: We empower leaders around the world who are sharing the gospel by providing outreach, medical and translation resources.

Name: Just Human Foundation
Description: The mission of Just Human Foundation is to build a community that collects, stores, and redistributes curated home goods donations to families transitioning out of homelessness and escaping domestic violence. By creating this community, we are able to support the families’ emotional well-being and housing stability and, ultimately, disrupt the cycle of family violence.

Name: Alumnos IEEM
Description: AlumnosIEEM.com es una iniciativa de Florencia Sarasola para unir a los alumnos del IEEM tras causas nobles que requieran el tipo de apoyo que este colectivo puede ofrecer.

Name: Integrity Children's Fund
Description:
Integrity Children's Fund (Integrity), founded in 2002 and headquartered in Georgia, is a direct response to the problem of relatively high illiteracy rate among Jamaican teenagers. The response has been extended to address the challenges of literacy among immigrant children who have settled in the metro Atlanta area. Illiteracy limits the ability of youth to reason rationally and is the primary factor in many youth making bad "life choices".

Integrity, a 501 (c) 3 organization, is committed to reducing the illiteracy rate among at-risk teenagers. Since inception, Integrity has provided approximately US $1,000,000.00 to educate nearly 600 students annually from inner-city communities in Jamaica. Recently, Integrity launched a support program for students at an Atlanta based school, a program focused on reading comprehension and math skills.

Name: DICKINSON AREA COMMUNITY FOUNDATION
Description: The Dickinson Area Community Foundation is a permanent, charitable endowment created by the community, for the community.

Name: Product Of Our Environment
Description: Product of Our Environment is a charitable, tax-exempt, 501(c) (3) dedicated to building stronger communities by uplifting the youth to become model citizens that reinvest in their communities. We aspire to encourage young individuals with ideas concerning solving social issues, financial literacy, exposure to various arts, the respect of cultural diversity, and education.

Name: BBB Center for Ethics
Description: BBB Center for Ethics is a 501(c)(3) affiliate of BBB Serving Southern Ohio, Northern Kentucky, and Southeastern Indiana. Our Mission is to provide ethics education, direction, and recognition to employers and employees of today and tomorrow.

Name: A-prendre en main (NGO)
Description:
A-prendre en main is a non-profit organization - law 1901 - promoting and providing support to local population worldwide to guid them to their autonomy. This comes through projects aiming to their social, educational or economic development with a sustainable approach.

http://a-prendre-en-main.org/

Name: WIRE Foundation
Description: We promote the importance for an interactive real estate mentorship program in order to prepare the future , female leaders in real estate. Through our program, girls build the skills and network needed to build their future.

Name: Thai Young Philanthropist Network
Description:
Founded in 2008, Thai Young Philanthropist Network (TYPN) is a network of young social-minded professionals with a common goal to make a positive difference to Thailand.

Vision
-------
Citizen sector as the key driver of sustainable development in Thailand

Mission
---------
Create a vibrant citizen movement at the top of the pyramid in order to become a capacity builder of the citizen sector in Thailand

Objectives
------------
• To cultivate a culture of social responsibility and community leadership and establish a network of concerned citizens among young professionals in Thailand
• To utilize and/or mobilize our resources for social good
• To bridge the knowledge gap between the top and bottom of the pyramid
• To incubate the grass-root business and social entrepreneurs
• To design innovative social solutions in order to empower the citizen sector in Thailand

Name: The Anthony Quinn Foundation
Description:
The Anthony Quinn Foundation was established to advocate for the important role arts education plays in personal development and in the overall improvement of social, economic and cultural systems.
Central to the pursuit of the Foundation’s mission is the life story of Anthony Quinn. His artistic legacy inspires young adults; learning about the challenges of his youth and the ways in which he overcame them helps inspire confidence in their own creative potential. AQF provides scholarship support to gifted young adults seeking to further their artistic talents, sponsors exhibitions and retrospectives, and 
provides access to the creative world of Anthony Quinn.

Name: Lionnes'aise en 4L
Description:
C'est surtout le côté humanitaire du 4L Trophy qui nous a encouragé à nous lancer dans cette aventure. 

Notre objectif: Recueillir un maximum de dons par le biais de notre association.
Une fois nos frais de fonctionnement couverts, nous reverserons intégralement les dons en faveur de l'association "Enfants du desert".

Name: PitCare
Description:
Everything we do is to improve lives in our community...

PitCare, Inc. grew out of a vision that two couples had for becoming good neighbors in the economically challenged town of Pitcairn, PA. By moving into Pitcairn in 2010, this vision became a reality. Pitcare, Inc. was formed and attained its 501c3 Non-Profit status in June of 2014. By March of 2015, PitCare, Inc. hired its first Executive Director, who also took up residence in Pitcairn. By 2020, PitCare expanded its Board of Directors and hired a Director of Operations, along with a few support staff.

From the onset PitCare, Inc. has focused its resources on empowering families, building community, and promoting economic stability in the town in Pitcairn, PA.

Please check out our website to see how we are seeking to fulfill our mission.

Name: Semper Gratus.
Description: Founded in 2016, Semper Gratus., Latin for Always Grateful., is a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization whose intent is to recognize and support other local nonprofits and charities.  It provides a forum for volunteers to join together and express gratitude through the opportunity to give back to those who work tirelessly for the betterment of others.

Name: Amplifier Giving
Description:
Give Better. Give Together.
Amplifier builds and sustains giving circles inspired by Jewish values. Giving circles are groups that join together, pool their charitable donations, and decide how to give them away. Our global network is close to 100 giving circles strong, and includes more than 2,000 people who grant millions of dollars every year. Amplifier’s offerings include:
				
Pop-up Giving Circle: Get a taste for the giving circle experience in 90 minutes through an interactive, fun, social event. In a pop-up giving circle, participants discuss their values, learn about new organizations, pitch potential grantees and make a grant.

Incubator: Interested in starting a new giving circle? The Incubator trains you in every step of the process from recruitment and planning your first meeting through making and celebrating your grants. 

Institute: The Institute empowers giving circle leaders to strengthen and sustain their circles by deepening their impact in four key areas: ethical philanthropy, engaging in civil society, Jewish expression and community building. Circle leaders train with a top-notch faculty, and receive coaching to design new initiatives for their circles in their area of choice. 	

Coaching: Amplifier’s staff and growing network of coaches help support you as you launch and sustain your giving circle. Get connected to an expert in the eld for one-to-one coaching, help getting unstuck and best practices from seasoned circle leaders.

Web Platform: 

Resource Library: Contains over 85 resources for every step of the giving circle process, covering topics including group dynamics, facilitation, grantmaking and Jewish texts.

Giving Circle Dashboard: Your giving circle’s home on the internet! Launch grant opportunities, review applications and interact with your giving circle members through communications features like polls, events and messages. Edit your giving circle profile to spread the word about the great work you’re doing.

Name: Starlight Global
Description:
Starlight Global, Inc. is the Corporate Social Responsibility (CSR) initiative of Capitor and is a United States registered, nonprofit 501(c)(3) exempt private foundation with Federal Tax ID Number 81-2060813. Contributions to Starlight Global, Inc. are tax-deductible to the extent permitted by law. 

We aim to empower people by investing in their education, culture and economic autonomy. Our impact would not be possible without the generous support of our volunteers and donors.

Starlight Global does not compensate any of its Officers, Directors, Trustees or Foundation Managers. As part of our commitment to transparancy, our annual 990-PF returns may be obtained by request.

A COPY OF THE OFFICIAL REGISTRATION AND FINANCIAL INFORMATION MAY BE OBTAINED FROM THE DIVISION OF CONSUMER SERVICES BY CALLING TOLL-FREE 1-800-HELP-FLA OR ONLINE AT www.FloridaConsumerHelp.com, REGISTRATION DOES NOT IMPLY ENDORSEMENT, APPROVAL, OR RECOMMENDATION BY THE STATE. REGISTRATION #: CH53491

Name: AFRICAN DEVELOPMENT FOUNDATION
Description:
AFRICAN DEVELOPMENT FOUNDATION
"Better life for all"

The importance of financial literacy skills cannot be overemphasized for the effective management of resources and the development of an economy. Rather, this very important skill is under looked.
African development foundation (ADF) is a nonprofit organization with a mission to fight poverty by developing financial literacy skills in kids, women, and men. 
Our believe is that financial literacy skills will foster better resource management because everything in life can be measured in financial terms.

ADF has an objective to;
1.	Encourage a savings for rainy days habit
2.	Serve as a forum for beneficial and mutual exchange of ideas, information, and experience
3.	Encourage rural and urban grass root women to be self-reliant

We seek to attract donor funding and corporate sponsorships to train Africans and guide them on their journey to financial wellness.

Name: University of Arizona Alpha Phi
Description: Beta Epsilon | University of Arizona

Name: Pink the Basin
Description:
Pink the Basin, Inc. Mission Statement:
"Support women in taking charge of their health through community awareness, education and service initiatives."
Pink the basin is a non-profit organization that works to raise funds in order to provide grants which pay for women in need to receive free or low cost mammograms.

Name: Vila Betânia
Description:
A Vila Betânia tem como objetivo acolher meninas de 0 a 17 anos em situação de risco e vulnerabilidade social no município de Jaboatão dos Guararapes.

Somos uma instituição e ministério debaixo da Shores of Grace Ministries, norteado pelo Estatuto da Criança e do Adolescente. Trabalhamos focados na excepcionalidade do acolhimento como medida de proteção, tendo como base a reintegração familiar e o princípio do melhor interesse da criança, através de trabalho multidisciplinar.

Vila Betânia é um lar para crianças e adolescentes, do sexo feminino, na faixa etária de 0 (zero) a 18 (dezoito) anos incompletos e meninos até 2 (dois) anos de idade no Município de Jaboatão dos Guararapes/PE. A casa é um lugar de segurança e recuperação onde nossa equipe, treinada consistentemente, cuida das meninas em um ambiente de amor e encorajamento. Temos uma equipe de profissionais, incluindo psicólogos, assistentes sociais e cuidadores que irão assegurar que as necessidades espirituais, emocionais e físicas de cada menina sejam atendidas.

Nossa equipe trabalha junto com a vara infantil regional e com trabalhadores de casos para determinar se existe a possibilidade da reintegração com a família biológica. Essa é a nossa esperança para cada menina que vem para a Vila Betânia; que sua família possa ser restaurada e que possam se reencontrar de forma segura. Nos casos em que a reintegração não é possível, nós trabalhamos com a vara da infância local para buscar outras opções, incluindo adoção. O nosso desejo é que cada menina receba amor e cuidado com uma excelência profissional, independente de quanto tempo ela passe conosco. Nós acreditamos firmemente em uma combinação de desenvolvimento espiritual e excelência em psicologia, terapia e educação. Enquanto essas meninas encontram o amor do Pai na Vila Betânia, elas também terão uma equipe de profissionais apoiando-as e trabalhando para restaurar e proteger seus direitos e futuros.

Name: Centro de Orientação e Serviços a Comunidade (COSC Tatuí)
Description: Atuamos facilitando o acesso a direitos desde 1963, através do fortalecimento de vínculos e convivência!

Name: ONG GBEWA
Description: Soutenir et améliorer les conditions de vie des populations défavorisées au Bénin

Name: Indigenous Giving Circle
Description:
A community of like-minded individual givers who are dedicated to reparations in Australia and the Pacific. Indigenous Giving Circle uses a reparations model to support and resource Indigenously led, owned, and operated grass-roots initiatives. The fund works to highlight the ongoing impact of colonisation on our First Nations people, while using the power of giving to offer collective healing and equity. IGC re imagines a shift from benevolence to a more just-approach to philanthropy. 

To receive future communications from us: click link below.
https://lnkd.in/ghEtBxxh

Name: Taly Foundation
Description: The Taly Foundation's mission is to provide all preschool-age children with access to early childhood education.  Research demonstrates that the years from 2-5 are critical to a child's development, yet 17,000 preschool-aged children in Massachusetts are not attending preschool.  Every child has a contribution to make to society.  It's our goal to help them discover it.

Name: Lar Da Redenção
Vietnamese Student Association at the University of Washington
Description:
The Vietnamese Student Association at the University of Washington (VSAUW) is a Registered Student Organization.  VSAUW is a cultural, social, and civically engaged organization. Since the 1980s, we've continue to cultivate relationships between students at the University of Washington and the greater Seattle Vietnamese community.  We aim to do this by developing the next generation of leaders strive to help the Vietnamese community in Seattle move in a forward direction. VSAUW host events that we believe showcases the Vietnamese heritage and culture, as well as supporting our sister VSAs at other college campuses.  We strive to make an impact to our campus and city community by learning, educating, and taking action. 

Throughout the year, VSAUW will host cultural, social, and educational events, including: Hoa Khôi Liên Trường, Phở King, professional development workshops, Phở Nights, a family (Anh Chị Em) program, and more.

We encourage you to participate in our events and meetings to learn more about VSA!

Questions or comments? 
Email us at vsauw@uw.edu	

Follow us for updates!
Instagram: @VSAUW
Snapchat: @VSAUW
FaceBook: /VSAUWSeattle
Website: www.vsauw.com

Name: Alice's Kids
Description:
Alice’s Kids is a non-profit organization that provides short term financial assistance to children with an immediate need. Relying on referrals from school staff, charitable organizations, government social workers and churches, Alice’s Kids will provide a child with a gift card worth between $25-50 to purchase the needed item. We do not ask the child’s name, the process is totally anonymous. We simply review the request from the referring agent and provide the agent with the gift card, who then passes it on to the child and/or responsible party. We ask the referring agent to inform Alice’s Kids when the item is purchased, if that is possible.

Our goal is to do what we can to raise the self esteem of a child in need. Children with healthy self esteem feel good about themselves. They do better in school. They are more successful.

We believe a little help can go a long way.

Name: Društvo AED
Description:
Društvo AED je neprofitna organizacija, ki že vrsto let skrbi za širjenje mreže javno dostopnih AED naprav po Sloveniji. Poslanstvo Društva AED je zagotoviti, da vsaka oseba, ki doživi srčni zastoj, pravočasno dobi oskrbo z avtomatskim eksternim defibrilatorjem (AED). 

Društvo AED organizacijam zagotavlja sredstva za nakup in vzdrževanje defibrilatorjev, izvaja brezplačne delavnice temeljnih postopkov oživljanja (TPO) in z različnimi aktivnostmi ozavešča o problematiki srčnih zastojev. 

Društvo povezuje medicinske sodelavce, strokovnjake iz različnih strok, promotorje in mnoge druge. Srečate nas lahko v vseh krajih v Sloveniji na različnih lokacijah - od trgovskih centrov, preko fakultet, do izletniških točk.

Name: Volunteer Illini Projects, Inc.
Description:
Volunteer Illini Projects, Inc. (VIP) is a student-run, student-staffed volunteer organization committed to peace, unity, and change. By working with and within the community, VIP makes a positive impact through service, thereby enriching students’ educational experiences and committing students to a lifetime of volunteerism. 

VIP strives to foster unity and enhance interpersonal connections through mutual understanding and empowerment. The dynamic tradition of past student initiatives and vision for positive social action guides the future of Volunteer Illini Projects, Inc.

Name: Pincelazos
Description:
Colectivo juvenil en Medellín que busca la inclusión de grupos sociales a través del arte y la cultura, con el fin de promover la apropiación del territorio, eliminar barreras sociales y paradigmas, y fomentar la sana convivencia. 
---
Youth-lead non-profit organization in Medellín that seeks the inclusion of different social groups through arts and culture to promote appropriation for the territory, eliminate social barriers and paradigms, and encourage local peacemaking processes.

Name: Kazakhstan Society in Canada
Description:
Kazakhstan Society in Canada is a first official (non-profit) organization for Kazakhstani who lives in Canada.
- To connect students together by organizing cultural, social and educational activities
- To provide information related to education, visas, and accommodation in Canada
- To raise awareness about Kazakhstan, Kazakh culture and current affairs
- To organize charity activities to assist Canadian and Kazakhstan people in need

Name: UKRaide
Description: UKRaide collecte et achemine des dons en Ukraine, pour soutenir les établissements de santé, les écoles, les orphelinats...

Name: Sociedade Rural De Montes Claros
Description: Sociedade Rural De Montes Claros is a company based out of Praca Lindolfo Laughton 1373, Montes Claros, Mg, Brazil.

Name: The Morgan Family Foundation
Description:
We are the family of Joanne and Gareth Morgan of Wellington and have established this charitable trust primarily for the purpose of reducing the wealth disparities between people. In particular the focus of the Foundation is on improving the lives of the poorest folk in the world and enabling people to participate in their society and workplaces to the full extent of their potential. Our work extends in New Zealand to public interest research and education as well as to investment in conservation and natural environmental enhancement.

The trustees are Joanne, Gareth and independent Charles Purcell. The advisory board for the trust comprises our four children – Ruby, Floyd, Jessi and Sam. The Manager of the Trust is Margaret Mather, and her responsibilities include liaising with applicants for funding, administering our grants programme, and ensuring the Foundation participates in philanthropic community activities to the extent the trustees desire. The trust has no religious orientation although willingly finances religious-based organisations doing work that satisfies the criteria outlined on our homepage.

Name: Asif Naeem Shaheed Foundation
Description: ANS (Asif Naeem Shaheed) Foundation is a student run body working to improve the situation of poor and underprivileged classes within Pakistan by helping in implementing the Sustainable Development Goals on Quality Education, Mental Health, No Poverty, and Climate Change.

Name: Veteranforeningen Operators Hockey Club
Description:
OPHC er en landsdækkende veteranforening, der gennem ishockeysporten giver veteraner og pårørende mulighed for at samles i og dyrke et fællesskab. Foreningens motto er ”No one gets left behind”.

Historie:
OPHC er startet som et firma ishockeyhold af medarbejdere på Flyvestation Aalborg. Formålet var at skabe et frirum og mulighed for fællesskab og sammenhold udenfor den daglige tjeneste. Firmaholdet oplevede fra starten en stor opbakning og engagement hos deltagere, pårørende og Flyvestation Aalborg.  Sidenhen er OPHC blevet dannet som en veteranforening som favner Danske veteraner fra hele landet.

Mål:
Foreningens mål er at skabe et frirum og glæde hos de danske kvinder og mænd, der har været med til at yde en indsats for Danmark.  Dette gælder uanset om man er rask eller har ar på krop og sjæl som f.eks. PTSD. 

Historier fra vores OPHC medlemmer:  
Ishockey er for mig friheden til at komme væk fra de negative tanker. Det sammenhold og kammeratskab vi har i OPHC giver mig glæde og har været med til at højne min selvtillid. I OPHC er der plads til at være sig selv, også på de dårlige dage. OPHC og ishockeysporten har lært mig, at udfordringer er okay, især på grund af sammenholdet og det har lært mig at se positiv på fremtiden.  (Jonathan Ø, Har PTSD)

Jeg har aldrig prøvet at spille før og manglede noget socialt og motion i min hverdag efter min PTSD diagnose. Jeg havde ikke regnet med at det kunne gøre så meget, men det har hjulpet mig helt af med min medicin, jeg har fået mere overskud i hverdagen og er blevet mere social. Jeg har også tabt 25 af de kg medicinen har været medvirkende til jeg har taget på. Sidst men bestemt ikke mindst har jeg fået et støre netværk, super kammerater og en glæde tilbage i livet jeg ikke har haft i mange år. Stor tak til alle i OPHC. (Jesper M)

Name: Suburban Chicago Planned Giving Council
Description: Suburban Chicago Planned Giving Council facilitates major and planned charitable giving throughout suburban Chicago. Additionally our members and their guests enjoy the opportunity of attending meetings featuring nationally recognized speakers on diverse gift planning vehicles and topics; the opportunity to network regularly with their peers and most importantly to upgrade their professional skills.

Name: Community Trust South
Description:
Community Trust South is the guardian of a community endowment of over $224 million. We help a wide range of groups and people in the south of New Zealand every year, by distributing millions of dollars in grants, scholarships and community loans.

We are proud to be associated with so many people and organisations as we continue to work together to make the south a wonderful place to live.

Name: HYFONARU asbl
Description: L'Association HYFONARU asbl agit pour fournir de l'eau potable aux populations qui n'en ont pas. Elle a été créée certains natif de la colline Rukere ,Zone Jene,Commune KABARORE et Province Kayanza.Pourtant ,ses activités ne sont pas limitées sur la localité mais peuvent s'étendre dur le plan National. Jeune denviron 8 mois,l’Association Hyfonaru a déjà effectue le premier travail en fournissant de l’eau potable à la population de la colline Rukere.Pour ainsi dire qu’une partie de cette population  a été servie et que l’association continu à se battre en vue de trouver des moyens financiers pour  continuer le travail entrepris. Elle a déjà construite quelques fontaines. Pourtant, le pas à franchir reste considérable.

Name: Alianza Mujeres Líderes de las Américas (AMLA)
Description: Una plataforma cultural que nos une en la diversidad, en la red promocionamos nuestros eventos, hacemos networking y apoyamos las iniciativas locales, regionales y entre países de otras mujeres líderes de proyectos en Centroamérica.

Name: Bracelets4Buildings.com
Description:
Bracelets4Buildings (B4B) is a registered charity supporting the less fortunate in Cambodia and Thailand. We strive to:
 
* Raise funds and awareness with bracelets made by Cambodian artisans.

* Build homes for those most in need.

* Give people opportunities to be self-sufficient.

Name: Josephine's Place
Description: Since we opened our door in 2003, women have come to Josephine’s Place to find a safe haven - a welcoming and supportive environment where they can connect with one another and develop the skills they need to succeed, such as English as a Second Language (ESL) classes, computer and job readiness training. We also provide guidance and support through the citizenship process.  At Josephine’s Place, women develop and grow in confidence to realize their full potential and be better equipped in day-to-day life. In doing so, they uplift themselves, their families, communities and future generations.

Name: umalqura .org
Description: أول جمعية نسائية يقع تحت مسؤوليتها خمسة مراكز مختلفة الخدمات، وتقوم على رعاية كافة إحتياجات المجتمع المكي، إذ أنها ترعى من خلال هذه المراكز الأسر ذوي الدخل المحدود، الأسر المنتجة، فئات الحالات الخاصة، فئات ذوي الظروف الخاصة، والمعنفات، وهي كذلك تحتوي أكبر وأول روضة طفل نموذجية تحت مظلة وزارة التعليم وتعتمد نظام نور في تعاملاتها، كما أن أحدث مركزها يقوم على رعاية الفئات الشابة من الفتيات ذوي المشاريع الصغيرة، فتساهم الجمعية بتنمية وتطوير هذه المشاريع وفتح باب التعاون معهن لإبرازها وتُحقق الهدف الأول من أهدافها بتمكين المرأة المكية.

Name: Zonta Club of Toronto
Description:
The Zonta Club of Toronto is committed to advancing the status of women through service projects in our local community, and to supporting the efforts of Zonta International to improve the status of women worldwide. Zonta International is a worldwide service organization of executives in business and the professions, with over 30,000 members in more than 1,200 clubs in 63 countries. Zonta takes its name from the Lakota Sioux Indian word meaning, "honest and trustworthy".

2012 will mark our 85th year of service to the Toronto community. Since 2011 we have worked with Sistering, a women's agency serving homeless, marginalized and low-income women in Toronto. We sponsor scholarships at The Michener Institute, Woodsworth College of the University of Toronto and Ryerson University. We are also proud to have recently assembled in excess of 1000 birthing kits for distribution internationally in co-operation with the Mennonite Central Committee.

Join the Zonta Club of Toronto to:
- make a difference in the lives of women and girls everywhere
- participate in and support quality service, information & scholarship programs
- meet a diverse range of business executives and professionals
- build leadership and mentoring skills, and
- broaden international understanding through fellowship and fun.

Send an email to president@zontatoronto.org for more information.

Name: BWA
Description:
Badan Wakaf Al Qur'an adalah organisasi nirlaba (non-profit organization).

Visi:
Menjadikan Wakaf Sebagai Gaya Hidup Muslim
Menjadi lembaga filantropi wakaf profesional, yang terdepan dan syar'ie

Misi:
Menyalurkan Al Qur'an kedaerah-daerah rawan pendidikan dan rawan aqidah.
Mendukung Para Da'i di Pelosok Negeri Melakukan Pembinaan dan Pengajaran Al Qur'an dengan metode yang membekas.
Mengembangkan Program Pendukung yang inovatif menyentuh problem asasi masyarakat sehingga memberikan nilai tambah bagi kehidupan.
Menyalurkan manfaat kepada umat melalui program wakaf dan Kemanusiaan. 
 

Kilas Balik dan Legalitas Lembaga:

Al-Quran adalah petunjuk kepada jalan yang lurus dan memberi kabar gembira bagi setiap mukmin yang mengerjakan amal shaleh.

Al Quran juga merupakan pedoman praktis (amaliy) untuk manusia ketika menjalani berbagai aspek kehidupannya. Oleh karena itu mengajarkan Al Quran di tengah masyarakat, dalam bentuk yang implementatif adalah suatu hal yang dharuri (penting).

Atas kesadaran ini, maka pada tahun 2005 sejumlah Ulama dan Profesional Muslim menggagas sebuah lembaga yang diberi nama Badan Wakaf Al Qur'an (BWA) dan tercatat dalam Akte Notaris H. Rizul Sudarmadi No. 119 Tanggal 28 April 2005.

Pada 1 Juni 2006, BWA mendapat sambutan baik dan dukungan dari MUI sesuai dengan Surat Rekomendasi MUI Nomor U-217/MUI/VI/2006.

Akta Pendirian Perkumpulan Badan Wakaf Al Qur'an di Notaris H. Rizul Sudarmadi, SH. Mkn tanggal: 12 Desember 2014 Nomor. 88

Keputusan Menteri Hukum dan Hak Asasi Manusia nomor AHU-00851.60.10.2014 sebagai Badan Hukum Perkumpulan pada 16 Desember 2014

Tanda daftar Perkumpulan/Organisasi Sosial nomor: 01/10.1.0/31/74.01.1001/-1.848/2017 tanggal 13 September 2017

NPWP : 80.905.824.1-015.000

Name: Soundwrx
Description: Soundwrx is a 501(c)(3) whose purpose is to create more inclusive opportunities for people with hearing loss. Our mission is to educate, inspire, and demonstrate the unlimited abilities of people with hearing loss and allow them the opportunity to live inclusively and to their full potential. We aim to make the world more accepting and understanding for those with hearing loss. Because everyone is made to matter.

Name: Stichting Goede Doelen Nh1816
Description: Stichting Goede Doelen Nh1816 (SGD Nh1816) ondersteunt landelijke, regionale en lokale projecten op diverse gebieden. Zodat die voor iedereen behouden en toegankelijk blijven. Nu en in de toekomst. SGD Nh1816 levert daarmee een bijdrage aan het leefbaar houden van wat we samen hebben opgebouwd en graag in stand willen houden.

Name: GOPIO France Metropole
MacHub
Description:
At MacHub, our mission is to empower our community by efficiently sorting and processing donations and transforming them into valuable resources. Through the profits generated, we aim to create a sustainable cycle of giving and receiving. We are dedicated to making a tangible difference, ensuring that every donation not only finds a new purpose but also contributes to the betterment of our community. 

MacHub's heartbeat is our community. We receive and process donations from nonprofits and individuals in the Yamhill County area. Those donations are then redistributed throughout the area to meet specific needs, or are sold through our multiple marketplaces. The proceeds generated from these sales fund MacHub’s operations in strengthening our local community by: creating internships for students, diverting materials from landfills, redistributing resources throughout our community, performing donation pick-ups for those in need, providing volunteer opportunities, and providing job placement programs and training.

Name: Fraterno20
Description: Somos um grupo que se formou durante a crise de Brumadinho, que se reencontra em momentos de crise, para criar soluções tecnológicas que venham atender a sociedade.

Name: 4LTrophy équipage Entrée-Plat-Désert
Description: Théo Gloanec et Lucas Moreau, une équipe SupMeca en course pour l'édition 2021

Name: Water Well-ness Project
Description:
Water Well-ness Project is a Canadian organization that provides water and sanitation facilities to people in need through Canada's first of its kind H2O Investments program. Through H2O Investments, we help international communities gain access to WASH (Water, Sanitation and Hygiene) facilities. We collaborate with local NGOs, micro finance institutions, and the communities themselves to implement the infrastructures. Unique to our program is that investors for these micro-credit projects (YOU!) receive a financial return on the humanitarian investment.

In all our endeavours we adhere to our four principles (SEAS) to ensure we are maximizing our impact: Sustainability, Environmental Consciousness, Accountability and Social Consciousness.

Want to be part of a revolutionary model for philanthropy? 
Subscribe to our communications at www.waterwellnessproject.ca.

You have the opportunity to:
Invest in our projects
Become a WWP Ambassador
Learn how to create a water-conscious lifestyle for a sustainable world

Want to learn about important world water, environment, and health issues? Subscribe to our blog here: http://www.waterwellnessproject.ca/blog/

Connect with us today!

Name: Kinsmen Club of Calgary
Description: The Kinsmen are a Dynamic Volunteer Organization Enriching our Communities through Service while Embracing National Pride, Positive Values, Personal Development and Lasting Friendships.

Name: The Mary Duke Biddle Foundation
Description: The Mary Duke Biddle Foundation supports arts organizations, artists, and K-12 education in the Triangle region of North Carolina. The foundation focuses on projects in which our modest grants can make a significant difference. In addition, MDBF supports Duke University, the University of North Carolina School of the Arts, and three churches, all institutions important to Mrs. Biddle and her family.

Name: NEON Foundation
Description: The NEON Foundation is the philanthropic arm of NEON Adventures.

Name: Fundacio Privada Daniel Bravo Andreu
Description:
Daniel Bravo-Andreu Private Foundation  is patient-focused, contributing to building Patient-Centred Healthcare and aimed at developing Biomedical Research.


The Daniel Bravo-Andreu Private Foundation is engaged in encouraging partnerships and collaborative work between Patient-organizations and Hospitals in an aim to alleviate the suffering of patients and their families.

Name: B4T.org
Description: We empower indigenous believers and churches for the work of ministry (Eph. 4:12) by multiplying Kingdom businesses in poor and unreached communities in order to see Kingdom Business playing its vital role in fulfilling the Great Commission.

Name: Lesyeuxquibrillent
Description: Ce fonds non lucratif accompagne les femmes et les hommes qui osent porter des projets audacieux pour inclure le handicap dans le monde de l entreprise . Le fonds de dotation soutient techniquement et financièrement les porteurs de projets dans leur concrétisation. En outre, nous finançons également des actions menant à l'insertion par l'emploi

Name: Operation Uganda
Description:
Operation Uganda LTD is an Australian, registered charitable organisation; ABN 27 134 735 292, and with your help we rescue and care for orphaned and vulnerable children and create sustainable community development projects in Uganda, one of the poorest countries in the world.
Operation Uganda focuses primarily on the most vulnerable and destitute children. In our sponsorship program children must meet a strict criteria of being either orphaned or vulnerable. The women in our empowerment projects are either widowed or solo mothers in destitute situations. Your support makes a real difference to the world’s most needy.  

Together we can bring hope and long lasting change to some of the poorest communities in Uganda.

Operation Uganda is based on 3 pillars – Rescue a child, empower a community, transform a nation.

All donations made in Australia and the United States are tax deductible.Operation Uganda is committed to integrity and financial accountability. We are governed by a board of directors, submit annual financial reports to Australian Investment Securities Commission, and submit of six monthly reports to Global Development Group who are a member of ACFID the Australian Council for International Development. Operation Uganda is also an accredited Non-Government Organisation in Uganda and submits annual financial and operational reports to the Ugandan Government.

Name: Chesapeake Service Systems (CSS)
Description: Chesapeake Service Systems is a non-profit organization that provides job opportunities to individuals with mental and physical disabilities.  The CSS mission is to provide work opportunities in an environment that will stimulate development and improve the health and quality of life of people with developmental disabilities.

Name: Afreek'Ed France
Description: Nous œuvrons pour la promotion de l'éducation des enfants en Afrique. Faites un don aujourd'hui sur notre site internet pour scolariser un enfant défavorisé.

Name: Elixirs Health Foundation
Description:
Who we are:
We are a group of medical students and professionals who work on creating a healthcare system which provides free of cost and accessible healthcare for every individual regardless of race, religion, cast or social status.
The nature of our foundation shall be non-profitable and charitable. The donations derived shall be applied solely towards the welfare cases and no portion thereof shall be paid to or divided amongst any of its members by way of profits.

What we do :

Bringing free healthcare to the most needy and advanced medical solutions to the entire region, all under one roof. 
We personally identify, verify, and raise funds for welfare cases, we also provide free- of- cost medical consultations by licensed professionals. 

Our objective :
-Health for all! 
Mental fitness is as important as physical fitness, that's why we provide online consultation for both free of cost. 

Our Aim :
Our aim is to provide free of cost medical consultations and advanced healthcare to every non- affording individual without any discrimination.
Every life is precious and no one deserves to die at the hands of poverty or suffocate under social hierarchies.

Our Collaborations :
Partnered with several Non-profit/non-governmental organisations and other private fund- raisers we are working towards our shared dream of free and accessible healthcare for all.

What our volunteers do :

Our volunteers are the backbone of our organization. They help us in increasing the reach of our donation appeals. 
Most of our volunteers are medical students so they identify and verify welfare cases for us in their respective hospitals.

About our funds :

We raise our funds by appealing the public for donations online through our social media pages.

Web portal coming soon..

Name: Fundación López Quintás
Description:
FUNDACIÓN LÓPEZ QUINTÁS PARA EL FOMENTO DE LA CREATIVIDAD Y LOS VALORES
Actualmente la FLQ desarrolla en colaboración con la Cátedra de Ética Empresarial de la Universidad de Comillas el Seminario Permanente de Investigación sobre Liderazgo Ético basado en el Encuentro.
El objetivo de este seminario es analizar cómo la fundamentación ética que ha desarrollado el profesor Alfonso López Quintás a lo largo de su obra puede contribuir de modo eficaz en la elaboración de  procedimientos prácticos, aplicables a la gestión de la empresa, que permitan devolver a la persona a la posición de centralidad que su propia dignidad requiere, posibilitando de esta manera la creación de riqueza económica, personal  y social, objetivos éstos que deberían ser inherentes  toda empresa.

Name: AMREF MONACO - FLYING DOCTORS
Description: Première ONG de santé publique en Afrique, L’AMREF FLYING DOCTORS s'engage à améliorer de façon durable la santé des communautés africaines.

Name: FoodBank Aalborg
Description:
FoodBank Aalborg er en frivillig, non-profit organisation, der bekæmper madspild og samtidig hjælper trængte borgere økonomisk.

Vi indsamler overskudsfødevarer fra lokale butikker og uddeler dem gratis til udsatte familier og enkeltpersoner. Ved at samarbejde med os får virksomheder ikke blot reduceret deres omkostninger til bortskaffelse, men bidrager også til miljøansvar og social værdi. Alle fødevarer afhentes uden beregning – en gevinst for både miljøet og samfundet. Dette kunne vi ikke have gjort uden alle vores fantastiske frivillige.

At samarbejde med FoodBank Aalborg er et partnerskab mod madspild samt en hjælpende hånd til individer, der har allermest brug for det.

FoodBank Aalborgs 3 hovedformål:
1. At hjælpe økonomisk trængende ved at give adgang til overskudsfødevarer
2. At skabe et netværk for ensomme og marginaliserede enkeltpersoner og familier
3. At værne om vores miljø og tage et medansvar for at minimere butikkers madspild

Kort sagt er vi et lokalt fællesskab med fokus på madspild, social ansvarlighed og bæredygtighed 

Find os på Facebook: www.facebook.com/FoodBankAalborg
Læs mere om vores projekter på vores hjemmeside: www.foodbank.nu

Name: Legal Hackers Sete Lagoas
Description: Capítulo de Sete Lagoas/MG do movimento mundial de Legal Hackers, que estuda os temas de interseção em Direito e Tecnologia

Name: Salvation Army Silicon Valley
Description:
The Salvation Army Silicon Valley provides Santa Clara County residents with emergency assistance to pay rent + deposit, and utility bills; and other basic needs like food, clothing, and public transportation.

We operate the Emmanuel House, a 78 bed sober living environment & homeless shelter with programming for men to find and maintain employment. Emmanuel House serves lunch and dinner to the public every day.

Our Senior Community Center provides a clean, safe place for seniors to relax and enjoy the camaraderie of friendship, join in exercises, bingo, field trips, have coffee/tea and snacks in the morning, and a great lunch every day. 

A wide range of fun, educational, confidence building activities including music classes, after school homework help, troop style activities, teen groups, summer day camps, and outdoor nature camps are available for children throughout the year.

Name: EMCOM Francophone
Description: Nous oeuvrons pour la promotion de l'utilisation de la radio en moyen de gestion de crise.

Name: Beyond The Basics, Inc.
Description:
Our mission is to empower underserved communities to realize their full potential by providing enriching experiences and opportunities. Our vision is a world where every person has access to the support and resources necessary to be the best version of themselves.

Beyond the Basics Inc. has distributed more than $800,000 through our initiatives and have impacted over 8,500 lives. With Maryland and New Jersey serving as our flagship states for impact, Beyond The Basics has served people across the country, with active markets in 21 states – and we’re just getting started.

Founded by Brandon Copeland and Taylor Copeland in 2016, Beyond The Basics Inc. is registered a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization.

Name: Seva Charities
Description:
Seva Charities is a student-run, 501c3 non-profit benefiting Akshaya Patra, and the largest South Asian organization on campus!
Seva is partnered with Akshaya Patra, India’s largest NGO, who provides mid-day meals to underprivileged children in India so that they can spend more time working to attain a quality education. Seva supports the effort of Akshaya Patra by hosting a multitude of events and fundraisers throughout the year. Connect with us to find out how you can volunteer! Our email is sevacharities@gmail.com.

The 3 Pillars of Seva
Philanthropy - Members of Seva actively volunteer with local charities and schools in the Austin area to fulfill the philanthropy pillar. In the past members have worked to help clean local parks and assist nearby animal shelters.

Global - Our global pillar is driven by our work with Akshaya Patra. We raise money to provide meals to children across the world so they that can focus on school and worry about working towards their dreams instead of working for their next meal. 

Local - Seva holds large-scale events and fundraisers throughout the year such as our annual banquet, petting zoo's and bake sales that are open to the entire University of Austin at Texas community.

Name: Malibu Foundation
Description: Malibu Foundation is a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization that is working to help Southern California rebuild and recover after the Woolsey Fire. Our internal programs and partner organizations are doing everything they can to serve the full scope of the community’s needs: housing, job placement, rebuilding, wellness, legal assistance, advocacy, and finance. We are also working to serve our environment and the beautiful flora and fauna that were also affected by the fires to create sustaining, resilient communities.

Name: Rajchman Foundation
Description: We aim to contribute to a more frequent and friendly dialogue between future leaders in their respective fields from all around Poland. We want to help build a community that will provide a framework for future cooperation between these talented individuals when they will be in positions of importance to our community’s well-being and development. This goal is a rather ambitious one and we do not suppose that we alone will able to resolve it completely, if anything can, but through our effort we hope to make a significant enough change to make our country and region more stable, prosperous and responsible internationally. We understand that in order to achieve such levels of civic responsibility and engagement as in countries with long and established democratic traditions, we need to take grassroots action and commit ourselves to it with patience and hard work, so that we can make up for nearly 50 years of foreign-imposed totalitarian regime that desecrated our civic life. We see our commitment as an investment in a more stable and prosperous country, society and region.

Name: Waislitz Foundation
Description: The Waislitz Foundation exists to create a positive social impact, locally and globally, through innovative projects that empower individuals to meet their full potential.

Name: European Foundation for Cambodian Children
The Spaatz Association, Inc.
Description:
The Spaatz Association, Inc., is the fraternal organization for recipients of the Civil Air Patrol General Carl A. Spaatz Award.  Established in 1994, the Association's goals are simple, yet visionary:

 To promote and maintain the prestige of the General Carl A. Spaatz Award.
 To support and preserve the high standards of achievement required to attain the General Carl A. Spaatz Award.
 To provide an entity to harness the collective energy, enthusiasm, experience, and talent of General Carl A. Spaatz Award recipients to assist in making the CAP Cadet Program the finest youth development program in America.
 To promote the development of positive personal characteristics, including leadership, professionalism, integrity, and perseverance, in America’s youth.

Since 1997, The Spaatz Association has awarded more than $100,000 to developing young leaders through its Aerospace Leadership Flight Scholarship and Leadership Grant programs.

Name: Global Order of Middle Eastern Christians
Description:
The Global Order of Middle Eastern Christians is a not-for-profit organization with the goal of bringing Middle Eastern Christians together for business, humanitarian, and networking opportunities.

We are a Middle Eastern Christian business organization, not a religious sect affiliated with any archdiocese, or fraternity.

Established in 2017, the vision of the Global Order of Middle Eastern Christians is to engage in sustainable initiatives that are meaningful and measurable, so that every deliverable achieved will be like solid stone, steel and concrete in the infrastructure of the community.

Name: Helpology
Description: Helpology is a start-up NGO that empowers vulnerable populations through the provision of information, advice and assistance to facilitate access to justice and enable them to receive equal treatment before the law.

Name: Stockholm Philanthropy Symposium
Description: As Sweden’s only international philanthropy symposium, this Annual event brings together leading voices from several countries to boost philanthropic engagement in the country. The universal consensus is that Business Entrepreneurs and Philanthropy have a great deal in common.

Name: ASRADD-CAMEROUN
Go Do Good App
Description: The Go Do Good mobile application is a tool designed to help our community understand the importance of serving others and encourage everyone to "go do good" through daily kindness challenges and outreach opportunities.

Name: Gear Up Foundation
Description: GEAR UP FOUNDATION IS A LIVING TRIBUTE TO THOSE WHO PERISHED ON 911 AND THOSE WHO MAKE THE ULTIMATE SACRIFICE EACH AND EVERY DAY. Uniting our world in response to tragedy. TOGETHER WE CAN MAKE A DIFFERENCE >>> LETS GEAR UP WORLD!

Description:
This page is under construction and more information will follow in the next days and weeks. 

powered by Friends of Education Africa gGmbH

We support Education Africa NPO in South Africa to fight poverty through education, equipping and empowerment. Our partner on the ground in South Africa is Education Africa, successful for over thirty years in building good practice in addressing South Africa's societal challenges in reducing educational inequities and social inequality.

Name: Eric's Journey Foundation
Description: The mission of Eric's Journey Foundation is to lead, inspire and support individuals around the world impacted by rare diseases.

Name: Fundacja Go'n'Act
Description: Misją naszego zespołu jest praca na rzecz zapewnienia każdemu dziecku i młodemu człowiekowi pełnego poszanowania jego praw, bezpiecznego dzieciństwa i okresu młodości, poczucia przynależności do lokalnej społeczności (również w sytuacji, gdy dziecko lub młody człowiek należy do grupy mniejszościowej). Poprzez edukację, działania na rzecz praw dziecka, projekty integracyjne wspieramy też wychowanie obecnego pokolenia dzieci i młodzieży na odpowiedzialnych i moralnych obywateli, którzy w przyszłości zadbają o przyjazny, bezpieczny i sprawiedliwy świat. W celu realizacji naszej misji skupiamy się na działaniach w trzech obszarach: Prawa dziecka, Edukacja dla pokoju, Integracja społeczna dzieci i młodzież.

Name: Jessica Impact
Description: Uma iniciativa para gerar impacto social positivo através da mobilização e colaboração.

Name: The Library Foundation Topeka
Description:
The Library Foundation is an independent not-for-profit 501(c)(3) organization that secures private funding for the Topeka and Shawnee County Public Library. All charitable gifts to the foundation are 100% tax deductible. Each year, The Library Foundation has an independent audit conducted.

The Library Foundation was formed in 1982 by the Friends of the Library. The purpose of The Library Foundation is to ensure the future of the library as an indispensable community resource. We do this by helping secure financial resources to support collections, programs, services, technology and physical facilities of the library.

The Library Foundation pursues this purpose by seeking philanthropic support, providing conscientious stewardship of the assets in its care, encouraging appropriate community partnerships and advocating on behalf of the library’s mission and continue helping our community be the best place to live, work, learn and play.

The Topeka and Shawnee County Public Library is the sole beneficiary of funds raised by The Library Foundation.

Every gift is important, no matter the size.

Name: Association des Nations Unies du Tchad /United Nations Association of Chad
Description:
Association de Nations Unies du Tchad et une association Membre de Federation Mondiale des Associations de Nations Unies (FMANU).
The United Nations Association of Chad,
member of the World Federation of United Nations Association (WFUNA

Name: Fundación Desarrollo Sostenible ADF
Description:
La Fundación Desarrollo Sostenible ADF es una entidad sin ánimo de lucro en la que trabajamos con la ilusión de emplear nuestras habilidades y nuestro equipo para conseguir un mundo más justo y más sano. Creemos que el Desarrollo es imprescindible, pero ha de ser Sostenible.

Nuestro fin fundacional está 100% alineado con los Objetivos de Desarrollo Sostenible de Naciones Unidas.

Nuestra Misión es promover  la transformación de la sociedad y la economía, hacia modelos de desarrollo más sostenibles.

Nuestra Visión es trabajar por un mundo más equitativo y sostenible, en el que las personas puedan desarrollar su potencial en armonía con su entorno.

Nos centramos en tres ejes de actuación: 

- Desarrollo Rural
- Población Vulnerable
- Medioambiente y Cambio Climático

El foco es de máxima importancia para nosotros. Creemos que desarrollando muy pocos proyectos, que tienen gran potencial de transformación, es la mejor forma de maximizar nuestro impacto y cumplir con nuestra misión: trabajar en pro de un desarrollo sostenible.

Los proyectos deben formar parte de intervenciones más amplias, con vocación de permanencia en el muy largo plazo.

Nuestros proyectos se centran en:

Población Rural:
- Apoyar a comunidades rurales en Latinoamérica y España.
- Impulsar iniciativas económicas que apuesten por la innovación y el empleo.
- Apoyar la adaptación de pequeños productores a modos de producción sostenibles.
- Estimular alianzas entre productores, sector público y privado, en pro del desarrollo.

Población Vulnerable:
- Acciones para la protección de los colectivos/poblaciones en situación de mayor riesgo o precariedad, especialmente en España pero también a nivel internacional (Latinoamérica), colaborando con otras organizaciones.

Medioambiente y Cambio Climático
- Incrementar la concienciación sobre la conservación de la naturaleza y el cambio climático con especial foco en el uso de la fotografía como vehículo de concienciación.
- Modelos de consumo más sostenibles.

Name: Rotaract Club FSTM Marina
Description:
Rotaract est un club pour adultes de 18 à 30 ans qui se réunit deux fois par mois pour échanger des idées, planifier des activités et des projets et pour socialiser. Alors que les clubs Rotary agissent en tant que sponsors, les clubs Rotaract décident comment organiser et gérer leur club, ainsi que des projets et activités à mener.

Les membres de Rotaract forment une communauté mondiale de jeunes adultes qui agissent pour un changement positif, échangent des idées et envisagent les défis du monde d’une manière nouvelle. Les clubs s'engagent dans des projets de services pratiques, des activités sociales et des opportunités de développement professionnel.

Pour vous inscrire au club Rotaract FSTM, adressez-vous au représentants du Club Rotaract à la Faculté des Sciences et Techniques-Mohammedia, ou bien contactez  nous pour en savoir plus sur notre réunions, nos projet de service ou nos événements sociaux.

Name: LA 4L TALENCAISE
Description: Le 4L trophy est une course sportif et humanitaire se déroulant dans trois pays (France, Espagne, Maroc). Nous parcourons plus de 6000 Km en 4L pour acheminer des fournitures scolaires et sportives aux "Enfants du désert"

Name: Associacao Beneficente Amar
Description: Somos uma organização da sociedade civil sem fins lucrativos, criadas em 2000, situada no município do Rio de Janeiro. Desenvolvemos nosso trabalho junto a crianças, adolescentes e jovens em situação de vulnerabilidade social. Nossas atividades têm como eixo inspirador os princípios do Estatuto da Crianças e do Adolescente (ECA) e visam, através de ações socioeducativas e profissionalizantes, o resgate da autoestima, a formação para a vida cidadã e a promoção humana e transcendental de todos os atendidos em nossos projetos.
Location: Rio de Janeiro, Rio de Janeiro

Name: EMAS Foundation
Description: The EMAS Foundation works with the Edinburgh Montessori Arts School to bring the benefits of the Montessori method to young people and communities located in Scotland. “It is not true,” said Dr. Montessori, “that I invented what is called the Montessori Method. I have studied the child, I have taken what the child has given me and expressed it, and that is what is called the Montessori Method.”

Name: San Leandro Education Foundation
Description:
We believe that when a student succeeds, we all succeed; that it is incumbent upon each of us to provide the support students need to grow into confident, compassionate and successful adults.

We are a community-based organization dedicated to strengthening and enriching the educational experience of San Leandro’s children and youth; We believe our efforts to support public schools ultimately strengthens and enriches our community as a whole, and prepares so many young minds for tomorrow’s challenges. 

Won’t you join us? We are looking for TIME, TALENT and TREASURE. If you are a passionate about your job or your hobby and are willing to share that passion and experience with students working on a related project,  contact us! If you would like to support our work financially, please visit our website or contact us directly. If you would like to keep up on our activities, follow us on Facebook and subscribe to our e-newsletter.

www.sledfund.org • 510-618-4483 • info@sledfund.org

SLED’s Mission

Our mission is to identify and mobilize local, regional and national resources in order to bring increased funding, innovative programming and strong partnerships to San Leandro Unified School District’s public schools.

Our focus areas are:

          o Positive Youth Development; supporting programs that help students grow into confident, compassionate adults.
          o S.T.E.A.M Enrichment; providing hands-on experiences that inspire students to explore science, technology, engineering, art-design, and mathematics.
          o Community Engagement; linking caring adults to students.

Name: Community Life Centers Of Haiti
Description: Community Life Centers of Haiti is a place for the Gospel to be shared through culturally integrated and age-relevant preaching and teaching. The church can become a source of HOPE in Haiti by also meeting the physical and social needs of this community, particularly the nearly 2.5 million Haitians between the ages of 15 and 24.

Name: The Philanthropy Club
Description: The Philanthropy Club is a private members' club for a new generation of philanthropists. We inspire, engage and bring together like-minded individuals to drive strategy, effectiveness, peer learning, collaboration and innovation in philanthropy, to create bigger impact.

Name: Saat Saath Arts
Description:
THE SAAT SAATH ARTS FOUNDATION supports international exchange between India and the rest of the world through the visual arts and education initiatives. Founded in 2010 by Director Aparajita Jain, it is a non-proﬁt initiative, with headquarters in New Delhi and working with museums and galleries across the world.

The main mandate of Saat Saath Arts is the Saat Saath Curatorial Research Grant, provide support and raise funds for exhibitions which include Indian artists, in international institutions and promote Contemporary Art through adopting initiatives within India and abroad.

THE FOUNDATION has already received international acclaim for its Curatorial Research Grant program, conceived by Aparajita and Diana Cambpell Betancourt, artistic director of the Dhaka Art Summit.

The Grant essentially endows international curators with resources to extend their research on Indian art practitioners, thus nurturing a vital exchange of knowledge between India and the rest of the world.

The foundation as per its mandate has also supported the mounting of Jitish Kallat’s poetic piece, “Covering Letter”, at the CSMVS (formerly the Prince of Wales Museum) in Mumbai, and “Matter” featuring the work of artist Bharti Kher at Vancouver Art Gallery, which was the result of a research trip funded by the organization.

MOST RECENTLY, Saat Saath Arts has joined hands with the Government of Rajasthan to produce The Sculpture Park at Madhavendra Palace, Nahargarh Fort, Jaipur.

It will be a ﬁrst of its kind focussing on promoting and highlighting Contemporary Art. This initiative is something that aligns with the society`s mandate and hence will be backed with the corporate support raised by the foundation.

Name: The Haiti Project
Description: The Haiti Project, founded in 2001, is a collaborative, all-volunteer, non-profit organization that partners with the Vassar Haiti Project, a student organizaton on Vassar College's campus to promote Haitian art, foster sustainable development in Haiti, and provide student and community volunteers a life-changing experiential education in global citizenship. By selling the Haitian artworks that we purchase directly from their Haitian creators in Haiti through art auctions and sales, we directly support the welfare of hundreds of Haitian artists and artisans. These art sales in turn provide our primary means of support for funding education, health care, water access and purification, women's empowerment, and reforestation initiatives in Chermaitre, a mountain village in the Northwest department of Haiti, and neighboring communities in the L'Acul region. VHP also has a strong educational component, dedicated both to raising awareness of Haitian culture and history, and to providing a hands-on, practical education in global citizenship to students, alumnae/i, and other participants within VHP. It is incorporated in New York as Haiti Project, Inc., and is a registered U.S. 501(c)3 non-profit organization.

Name: Boys & Girls Club of McAllen
Description:
Our Mission:

"The Boys & Girls Club of McAllen is dedicated to helping our community by mentoring youth to become productive and caring citizens, by providing nationally recognized programs in a safe and positive environment, through the support of caring and dedicated youth development professionals."

WHO WE ARE

BGCM's is dedicated to helping our community by mentoring youth so that they will become productive and caring citizens. This begins by engaging our youth through nationally recognized programs in a safe and positive environment after school, during vacation periods and throughout the summer months. BGCM continuously strives to inspire community partners and alumni to positively impact future generations of BGCM youth.

Name: Stichting Mixclubs
Description:
De missie
De missie van Stichting Mixclubs is om mensen vanuit interne motivatie (passie) te laten deelnemen aan de maatschappij. Wij denken dat je daar niet vroeg genoeg mee kan beginnen en daarom richten we ons op kinderen. 

De projecten
Alle projecten beginnen met het door de kinderen laten vaststellen van een leuk doel. Een voorbeeld van een doel is een pretpark. Vervolgens bedenken ze met behulp van sturende vragen hoe ze dat doel kunnen bereiken. Voor een pretpark heb je geld nodig en ze bedenken dan dat ze op een markt willen staan om dit geld te verdienen. Ze bellen zelf professionals en ondernemers op om langs te gaan voor advies en hulp. Bij de markt waren bijvoorbeeld een bakker, een grafisch ontwerper, een marktmeester, marktkoopmannen en de gemeente betrokken. 

Het ontstaan
Via een vriendin kwam Elles terecht op een jongerencentrum in de Slaaghwijk in Leiden. Er is een sterke connectie tussen haar en kinderen en vooral deze kinderen grepen haar meteen. De kinderen vroegen haar of ze een kinderclub voor hen wilde opzetten, maar ze zat in een moeilijke periode van haar leven. Een aantal maanden hierna besloot ze om het te doen. Het heeft haar geholpen.

Name: The George Shinn Foundation
Description:
The George Shinn Foundation is dedicated to helping people in need.  It is committed to building God's kingdom and reflecting its founder's deep Christian faith by furthering the work of churches, ministries, missionaries, and non-profit organizations.

After his diagnosis and successful treatment of prostate cancer, business and leadership entrepreneur George Shinn created the Foundation as a way to extend his resources to others.  In 2011, George and his wife, Denise, moved their family and The Foundation to Nashville, Tennessee.  Today, Denise Shinn spearheads The George Shinn Foundation's community initiatives and was instrumental in The Foundation's re-launch in 2009.

Recent Foundation support has been provided to: 

- The George Shinn Foundation Clinic: a multi-story urgent care medical clinic near Port-au-Prince, Haiti in partnership with the Amer-Haitian Bon Zami House of Hope school, orphanage, and church complex

- Hoops for Homes: a charity to rebuild homes and communities destroyed by Hurricane Katrina in New Orleans, Louisiana

- The George Shinn Leadership Retreat Center:  a 117-acre Tennessee ranch donated by the Shinns to serve as a peaceful retreat  for missionaries, ministers, and ministry leaders from around the world to find rest and renewal

- Other non-profit initiatives supporting vocational training, homeless care, and youth opportunity

Formerly The TruLight Foundation, it was officially renamed in 2009.

Name: Rotary Club of Gaithersburg, Maryland
Description:
Gaithersburg club of Rotary International, a large and wonderful service organization with more than 1.2 million members in some 33,000 clubs, worldwide. The Gaithersburg Club meets for lunch on Tuesdays to meet and greet, have fun, and plan local and international service activities as well as fundraisers and occasional service events. Clubs are nonpolitical, nonreligious, and open to all cultures, races, and creeds. As signified by the motto Service Above Self, Rotary’s main objective is service — in the community, in the workplace, and throughout the world.


In Rotary you can join leaders, exchange ideas, and take action.

Rotary Four Way Test:

First, is it the truth?
Second, Is it fair to all concerned?
Third, Will be it build goodwill and better friendship?
Fourth, Will it be beneficial to all concerned?

Name: 1 Ohana Charities
Description:
1 Ohana Charities is a non-profit organization founded in 2021.  Our mission is for every young adult that ages out of the Foster Care System to have the support and resources to live a healthy and thriving life.

Every year, over 23,000 teenagers age out of the Foster Care system in the United States.  At 18, they have no home, no job, and often no adult support.  The statistics for these young adults are dire, and they often fall prey to their socioeconomic circumstances.  

1 Ohana Charities believes that these individuals should not be forgotten or left without hope, services, or support.  We have the ability to reverse the current trends and help these young adults develop into healthy and thriving individuals.  

Through support services, mentorship, job placement, and financial assistance, we can provide a stable foundation that will enable this forgotten population to feel cared for, achieve their goals, and attain a positive future.

Name: Global DIRT
Description:
The mission of Global Disaster Immediate Response Team (DIRT) is to fill the void that exists between the time of a disaster occurrence and that of the traditional response effort. The international non-governmental organization has operated based on this premise since its creation in 2010. Global DIRT utilizes the Special Force’s small unit leadership model, combining its all-volunteer force of first responders, subject matter experts, and prior service military personnel to respond within the first 24-48 hours of a disaster. Volunteers provide medical assistance, communications access, search and rescue capabilities, and coordination support. Global DIRT is able to insert into a disaster or post-disaster situation with key equipment and knowledge, thus limiting the loss of life, property, and resources, all while operating efficiently to ensure that every donor dollar contributes to mission success.

Global DIRT responds to short term emergencies and the long term ramifications of disasters. Since 2010, the organization has responded to a variety of emergencies around the world, ranging from flooding to Emergency Medical Services development to radiation monitoring in countries such as Haiti, Pakistan, New Zealand, Japan, and the United States. The team has utilized the latest technologies such as GATR satellite terminals, Life Giving Force mobile filters, Noah Water Systems UV potable water treatment system, FLIR thermal imaging cameras, and Immersive Media equipment in its missions. The positioning of this equipment has providing critical life-saving support to survivors.
