Name: Power Of A Voice with Lori Corken
Description:
The Power of a Voice Series, with Lori Corken, dives into the lives of people who are willing to share their stories in the hopes to help others. Lori’s life changed forever on October 11, 2019 when she had a stroke. From this, Lori learned that everyone has a story and it’s now time to share. 

On this page, you can expect impactful videos that will help you find your path through others' stories.

Name: PIERIS Fund
Description:
We foster economic and social development for those most in need through supporting scientific research, accessible education, and investments into sustainable technology driven businesses. 

Here are some of our engagements and activities:

> We identify and fund promising research programs, establishment of laboratories. 

> We support students who need funding to continue their education or to present their research. 

> We organize cross disciplinary education and sports camps, conferences.

Name: The Elevate Community, Inc
Description: The Elevate Community, was started in 2012 to bring the community together through feel-good events that benefit charitable causes.  Over the years, we have raised over $500,000 through and for events and we have supported over 60 charities.  After being greatly impacted by the loss of a neighbor, as our own ED battled postpartum we started to really focus on the importance of community, mental health support and dreamt up our women's wellness event.  After our directors cross country move, our board expanded nationally to continue focusing on these pillars to elevate and support so many more through these specific lenses.  Join us as we continue our annual wellness event, but also look to bring elevate community groups to women nationally, and build out a mental health app and podcast to make an even greater impact!

Name: Fundación de Intervenciones en Salud Asistidas por Animales - FISAA
Description:
¿Quienes somos?
Somos un grupo de profesionales del área de la salud trabajando en promover la mejora y el desarrollo en las funciones físicas, sociales, emocionales y/o cognitivas de las personas, a través de la intervención asistida por animales.

Misión 
Contribuir a mejorar y desarrollar el bienestar, la calidad de vida y la salud de las personas, aplicando metodologías terapéuticas paralelas en salud, a través de las intervenciones asistidas con animales.

Visión
Posicionar las intervenciones asistidas con animales como metodología de trabajo en materias de salud, aplicando este recurso natural como factor de cambio.
Ser un referente significativo a nivel nacional en el desarrollo de estrategias de salud, a través de las intervenciones asistidas con animales.

Name: Project 5K
Description:
Project 5K is a non-profit organization created by youths for youths. The founders of Project 5K noticed that many youths want to volunteer, but they don't know where to volunteer. At the same time, there are many events who don't have enough volunteers.

That's the reason why Project 5K started; we aim to make volunteering opportunities more easily accessible for youths and connect them to various enjoyable volunteer opportunities to make their community a better place. By signing up for Project 5K, you get volunteering opportunities directly to your email and we make the sign-up process both simple and efficient for both our volunteers and our event partners.

Our Mission
We are trying to get youths to become inspired, get connected, and volunteer! We're on a mission to help over 5000 participants into our youth empowerment program. Since our initial launch in March 2014, we have established a network of over 1800 participants from over 150 different schools across the Ontario, and our network is still growing at a rapid pace. We keep a private and confidential volunteer database that allows our participants to sign up for volunteering opportunities through our system safely.

Description: Guys with Ties Charlotte is a non-profit organization founded in Charlotte, North Carolina in 2006 by 5 men seeking to enrich the Charlotte community through social events targeted at the young professional community that raise money and awareness for deserving charities in the Charlotte area. The goal of Guys with Ties is to connect Charlotte’s growing population of young professionals to organizations in our community that make Charlotte a better place.

Name: Dutch Down Support Foundation
Description:
Dutch Down Support laat wereldwijd kinderen en volwassenen met Downsyndroom en hun omgeving delen in de kennis, ervaring en middelen die in Nederland inmiddels gewoon zijn.

Veel mensen die buiten Nederland actief zijn voor jongeren en volwassenen met Downsyndroom hebben wensen en dromen. Maar het ontbreekt hen aan een netwerk om ze te realiseren. Wij verbinden doelen en mensen.

Mensen met Downsyndroom leiden in veel landen vaak een teruggetrokken leven. Sommigen zijn zelfs weggestopt; deze mensen hebben geen bezigheden en hebben geen contact met de samenleving. Wij stimuleren acceptatie en integratie.

Name: Rebuild Haiti
Description:
Rebuild Haiti is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit founded in the aftermath of the 2010 earthquake, with the mission of assisting Haitians in rebuilding their nation. We regularly travel to Haiti, working shoulder to shoulder with our Haitian brothers and sisters, so far investing over a million dollars in improving access to food, education and housing.

Our goal is to build ethical, healthy, and sustainable communities that support individuals, families, and the collective. We believe all people have the right to realize their full potential and be treated with dignity. As we work alongside them, our Haitian partners have invested thousands of hours of sweat equity, learning new skills and becoming community leaders. We would value your support in building a brighter future for Haiti.

Name: Hebrew Free Loan of Metropolitan Detroit
Description:
Since 1895, Hebrew Free Loan has served the Jewish community of Metropolitan Detroit (and later all of Michigan) by making short-term loans without interest to individuals who have no other means of help.

Through donations from generous individuals, Hebrew Free Loan helps people achieve financial independence, small ventures to launch, and much more. Our loans enable members of the Jewish community to go to college, adopt children, establish or expand their businesses, enjoy a summer camp experience, celebrate weddings, Bar and Bat Mitzvahs or help with an emergency that arises. 

Hebrew Free Loan is a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization participating in community outreach and fundraising; including direct mail, in-person contact, advertising, special events and membership campaigns. There are many ways to support our important service to people in need through our annual giving campaign, the Major Loan Capital Campaign, Tributes, general donations, endowments and named funds.

Name: 26.2 with Donna Foundation
Description:
In April 2016, The 26.2 with DONNA Foundation and The DONNA Foundation merged.

The DONNA Foundation, a 501(C)3 nonprofit organization established in June of 2003, provides financial assistance for the critical needs of those living with breast cancer. Our mission is to provide economic assistance and emotional support to individuals living with breast cancer and fund ground breaking breast cancer research.

The 26.2 with DONNA, or DONNA Marathon Weekend is an official event of The DONNA  Foundation.  Held annually the second weekend of February, DONNA Marathon Weekend (5k, 10k, Relay, Half Marathon, Marathon, Ultramarathon and Event Challenges) fills the City of Jacksonville and its Beaches in Northeast Florida with over 10,000 runners from all 50 states and 20 countries. Participants enjoy a flat, fast Boston-Qualifying marathon course that takes them through four beautiful beach communities with scenic views and an outpouring of crowd support. Expect water stations nearly every mile, race day temps averaging 55° F, and streets lined with a 26-mile beach block party. And it’s pink.  Very pink.  There’s a reason this event has been recognized by CNN Travel as one of the “Seven Marathons Worth the Trip” and “Favorite Charity Race” from the readers of Women’s Running Magazine. We are the National Marathon to Finish Breast Cancer.  Start training.

Our work will be always driven by a passionate belief that we all have a shared responsibility towards the communities we live in. Globalie

Globalie draws from academics, policy specialists, philanthropists, business executives, and financiers in a holistic approach to actualizing this change.

Name: Jagathon: IUPUI's Dance Marathon
Description:
IUPUI's Dance Marathon, commonly known as Jagathon, is a 15-hour dance marathon that takes place every first weekend of March at IUPUI with the purpose of raising both funds and awareness for pediatric research. We listen to music, play games, spend time with the kids and families we support, and even do a bit of dancing. Dance Marathon is For The Kids; that’s always our focus. Jagathon is a 365-day commitment to raising money for Riley Hospital for Children. With the goal of raising money for Riley comes the yearly celebration of our hard work during Dance Marathon Weekend. In 2020, we raised over $607,000 for the kids. Over the last nineteen years, Jagathon has raised nearly 1.5 million dollars for the kids.

Jagathon supports all kids treated at Riley Hospital for Children. We serve. We fundraise. We inspire. We hope. Dance Marathon is neither a dance nor a marathon; it is a celebration of our movement. Jagathon has been helping children with life-threatening illnesses for nearly two decades, giving financial and emotional support for hundreds of families across Indiana through the Herman B Wells Center for Pediatric Research.

Name: Ten Gav
Description:
Ten Gav is an online crowd-funding platform that matches donors with individuals in Israel who have specific, relatively modest needs that have been submitted for backing by social service professionals.

The giving experience on tengav.org provides personal and verified giving, enabling donors to be a part of the Israeli story by providing concrete support to Israeli families in need.


IT'S PERSONAL - Read a personal story on tengav.org and choose the individual or family you wish to help.

IT'S POWERFUL - All needs are small enough that your donation will make a real difference. No single need is greater than $1500 so your donation won't be lost in a sea of monies. Your money really matters

IT'S REAL - Every personal story comes directly from professional social workers. You can be confident that your donation will fund a real person meeting a real challenge.

IT'S THE 100% MODEL - All administrative costs are covered by private funding so 100% of your donation goes toward funding the personal story you choose.

Name: Relay For Life at The University of South Carolina
Description:
We are a group of students who are passionate about bringing an end to cancer. Working alongside the local American Cancer Society office, students plan, organize, and host several events, including USC's annual Relay for Life event. This organization is aimed at remembering loved ones, celebrating survivors, and fighting back against cancer. 

Our organization has several different committees, all with different focus areas, but we all work together to accomplish our main goal: end cancer. Sign up for Relay at uscrelay.org. Follow us on instagram @ uscrelay4life

Name: Collectif HAMEB
Description:
Le Collectif HAMEB (Halte Au Massacre En Birmanie) est une ONG humanitaire internationale dédiée à la cause des minorités persécutées en Birmanie. L'ONG s’est formée afin d’informer et de sensibiliser les concitoyens sur les conditions de vie des minorités ethniques de ce pays et notamment sur la communauté Rohingya, aujourd'hui considérée par l'ONU comme « l'une des minorités les plus persécutées du monde ». L'association est surtout la pionnière en France à dénoncer les atrocités commises par des régimes et dictatures à l’encontre des minorités vulnérables telles que les Rohingyas, Chins, Kamans...etc en Birmanie.

Le Collectif HAMEB effectue régulièrement des opérations humanitaires à haut risque en Birmanie, mais également en faveur des réfugiées birmans au Bangladesh, en Malaisie, en Thaïlande et en Indonésie.

Le Collectif HAMEB compte aujourd’hui une cinquantaine de membres actifs bénévoles, répartie sur plusieurs antennes en France, en Belgique, en Suisse, au Canada et en Malaisie ; parmi lesquels des militants associatifs, des travailleurs et des étudiants.

Le Collectif HAMEB est une association à but non lucratif fondée en 2012.

Name: ReHuNa - Rede pela Humanização do Parto e Nascimento
Description:
A ReHuNa - Rede pela Humanização do Parto e Nascimento - é uma organização da sociedade civil que vem atuando desde 1993 em forma de rede de associadas(os) em todo o Brasil. Nosso objetivo principal é a divulgação de assistência e cuidados perinatais com base em evidências científicas. Essa rede tem um papel fundamental na estruturação de um movimento que hoje é denominado “humanização do parto/nascimento”. Esse movimento pretende diminuir as intervenções desnecessárias e promover um cuidado ao processo de gravidez/parto/nascimento/amamentação baseado na compreensão do processo natural e fisiológico.

A ReHuNa apóia, promove e reivindica a prática do atendimento humanizado ao parto/ nascimento em todas as suas etapas, a partir do protagonismo da mulher, da unidade MãeBebê e da medicina baseada em evidências científicas. Essa missão vem sendo buscada na prática diária de pessoas, profissionais, grupos e entidades filiados à rede e preocupadas(os) com a melhoria da qualidade de vida, o bem estar e bem nascer.

Name: James M. and Cathleen D. Stone Foundation
Geneva Alliance for Human Rights and Development
Description:
Geneva Alliance for Human Rights and Development is a non-governmental organization dedicated to protecting human rights and promoting development projects in the MENA region at national and international levels. It is governed by the Swiss Civil Code and its statutes and has 2 offices; in Geneva and New York.

Our mission is to defend and promote the values of human rights, as established in relevant international instruments and covenants and promote development in the MENA region with the ultimate aim of building societies in which citizens exercise their basic rights and live in peace, dignity and prosperity.

Name: Josefa Foundation
Description:
Reconnue d’Utilité Publique depuis le 15 juin 2012, la Fondation Josefa a pour intuition fondamentale que, par nature, en tant qu’êtres humains, nous sommes « tous migrants » et qu’une hospitalité en réciprocité ouvre une voie nouvelle pour les Etats, pour leurs régions et leurs cités qui s’enrichissent de nos migrations, opportunes réalités tant pour nos sociétés, aujourd’hui, que pour les générations à venir. 
Nos migrations sont au cœur de notre condition humaine, au cœur de notre Histoire humaine. Elles éclairent nos sociétés et disent le SENS de notre humanité en marche. 
Forte de son itinéraire et de l’expertise de son équipe, en rupture avec les schémas classiques, la Fondation Josefa ose s’engager, questionner et proposer une voie renouvelée face aux défis du fait migratoire, selon sa vision sociétale : « Tous Migrants », et selon son expérience au quotidien, au seuil et au sein de la Maison Josefa.
Vision : « Tous migrants » selon une approche voulue globale (physique, psycho-intellectuelle et spirituelle) pour chacune des personnes que nous sommes, pour chacun en son unicité, en privilégiant les dimensions culturelles et convictionnelles attachées à nos migrations.
Mission : Depuis mi 2015, la Maison Josefa est au service d’une conversion de regard sur nos migrations. En son sein, à Bruxelles, vivent une trentaine de co-résidents, migrants du monde, et des propositions multiples : sociales, culturelles et convictionnelles, ouvertes à tous.
Voici donc l’invitation de la Maison Josefa, visage de la Fondation, qui entrouvre ses portes, en s’affranchissant d’itinéraires migratoires prédéfinis, mais sans renier la mémoire de nos origines ou de nos traditions, aussi diverses fussent-elles. 
Pour Josefa, le moment est venu de convertir notre regard sur nos migrations : il y va de notre responsabilité aujourd’hui et à l’égard des générations futures.

Name: Partners Asia
Description:
Partners Asia forges relationships with local leaders who work to serve the needs of “invisible” populations—refugees, migrants, ethnic minorities, LGBTQI - living in South East Asia and beyond.

We elevate the work of these local groups and support them as they grow. We do not “help” our partners. Instead, we listen to what they say they need, and follow their lead. We partner.

And we bridge the gap between local organizations and global funding.

Name: National Parks Conservation Trust
Description:
The National Parks Conservation Trust (Parks Trust) is committed to supporting conservation in and around Australia’s iconic national parks. Our work is possible thanks to visionary funding partners and donors who share our commitment to the conservation of Australia’s natural environment and cultural heritage.

The Parks Trust was launched in 2019 and is a registered charity. We connect people with in-the-field conservation projects that have lasting positive impact in and around Australia’s extraordinary Commonwealth reserves. These unique parks include Uluru-Kata Tjuta, Kakadu and Booderee, the parks on Christmas Island and Norfolk Island, 58 Marine parks surrounding Australia and the Australian National Botanic Gardens in Canberra.

With the help of environmentally responsible individuals, foundations, corporations and government agencies, the Parks Trust works together to support scientists, traditional owners and park staff on site-specific projects that increase knowledge and protect the environmental health and ancient cultures of Australia.

 Visit parkstrust.org.au to find out more about our work and how you can get involved or email us at hello@parkstrust.org.au

 Act now and change the future for Australia’s unique places for generations to come.

Name: iCubed
Description:
iCubed's mission-driven team possesses a depth of experience in finance, tax, law, deal structuring, and philanthropy. iCubed brings capital markets expertise to help activate philanthropic investment. iCubed provides efficiency in implementing impact investments with a tailored approach, based on each individual organization’s capacity, mission, and risk appetite for impact investing.

Deploying your philanthropic capital more efficiently, effectively, and purposefully with impact expertise.

Name: Amigos del Museo de Bellas Artes de Asturias
Description: La Asociación de Amigos del Museo de Bellas Artes es la entidad que permite vehicular su compromiso personal o corporativo con el patrimonio artístico de Asturias.

Name: Empulsa
Description:
Impulsamos proyectos de vida de los niños, jóvenes, adultos y las comunidades más vulnerables de Colombia a través de la educación. 

Nuestros pilares educativos son:
- Emprendimiento
- Educación Ambiental 
- Autoconocimiento 

Únete a nuestra causa!

Name: Christopher J. Aubrie Foundation
Description: Providing scholarships and promoting suicide awareness and prevention.

Name: Madagascoeur
Description: Association humanitaire née en 2016 à la suite d'un voyage en famille à Madagascar. Depuis, différentes actions sont organisées en Suisse pour permettre la réalisation de plusieurs projets distincts à Madagascar.

Name: Robert Owen Communities
Description:
ROC offers forward looking services for the benefit of adults with a learning disability. These include
- Specialist services (including residential)
- Transition services
- Independent living
- Work
- Wellbeing
- Skills and learning
- Personalisation

Name: Operation:  Heal Our Heroes
Description:
The mission of Operation: Heal Our Heroes is to help stop the widespread suicide endemic that has plagued our nation’s veterans.

Concept of the Operation: Many Americans are unaware of the fact that every single day nearly 22 military veterans are lost to suicide. There are two fundamental reasons for this tragic issue. The first is because the transition back to “normal life” can often be a difficult journey, particularly without a strong support system to lean on. The second is because our government is unfortunately under resourced to effectively provide the attention and care required for the thousands of soldiers, sailors, airmen, and Marines who have endured and continue to bear the psychological burdens associated with our country’s time at war.

Operation HOH was launched to adroitly tackle both of these issues simultaneously. Throughout the past year, we have held various functions that have served as a forum to help educate hundreds of Americans, both civilian and military alike, on why we continue to lose so many of our nation’s Heroes despite being back on our home soil. Through this knowledge comes empowerment, as we’ve enabled numerous Americans to be better equipped to recognize the signals of a struggling friend or family member.

Additionally, our events have generated thousands of dollars that have gone toward facilitating easier access to private mental healthcare to ensure that our Veterans can expeditiously get the help and support they need and deserve. Operation HOH has partnered with both Give an Hour, (http://www.giveanhour.org/) and The HeadStrong Project (http://getheadstrong.org/), both organizations that have had an immense impact in the mental health space, to help promulgate this effort.

Our quest to improve mental health among our Veterans is a fight that all of us as Americans must shoulder together.

Name: Prabina Foundation Switzerland
Description:
Vision
We provide vulnerable children and youths in Nepal a safe, familial home and good education for them to build a self-reliant and dignified life.

Mission
We focus our efforts in Nepal on vulnerable children and youths by creating a local, loving and enabling home environment. By supporting capability-based education, children and youths are enabled to build a self-reliant life

Activities
Prabina Foundation currently provides a safe home, access to quality education, and health and well-being to over 80 Nepali children and youths in order to enable them to build a self-reliant and dignified life. The Prabina Foundation is able to carry out the following core activities through the support of sponsor parents, individual donations and grants: 

A safe home
Access to quality education
Transition support to self-reliance
Health and well-being

We are a loving, large family made up of Nepali children and youths and sponsor parents and donors from Europe. Our sponsor parents are in regular, personal contact and exchange with their respective child or youth as we believe that this form of direct sponsorship and care is integral to building trusting, long-term relationships. We enable while respecting the local context and culture, and nurture the entire education and well-being of vulnerable children and youths to help ensure they receive 360 degree care. This holistic approach translates into support for the individual, capability-based needs of each Prabina student. Our enabling approach aims to ensure that each student is accompanied through their entire education until they are self-reliant and able to live on their own with dignity. After completing their sponsorship they usually lead a self-reliant life, have built something for themselves and keep telling the story of how being a member of our big Prabina family has changed their life for good in a sustainable way.

Name: The Anna Plowden Trust
Description:
The Anna Plowden Trust was established in 1998 to contribute to the development of skills in the conservation of the movable heritage and to help raise awareness to the profession of conservation.  It is the only grant giving trust in the UK whose primary focus is supporting student and practising conservators.

The Trust was established in memory of Anna Plowden CBE, one of the foremost object conservators of her generation, who died in 1997.   

It is vital to maintain a highly qualified cadre of conservators for the sake of the extraordinary heritage held in the UK; since its foundation, the Trust has awarded some one hundred and fifty Conservation Bursaries towards course fees on (usually postgraduate) courses.  In addition more than one hundred CPD Grants have been awarded to practising conservators to enable them to attend courses and conferences in the UK and overseas.  In total the Trust has given grants of over £350,000 – a substantial investment in conservation skills and in the profession.

The Trustees include family members and leading members of the conservation profession.

Name: Grad Mentors
Description:
GRAD Mentors is a volunteer-run organization that is committed to supporting those that are unable to access traditional GMAT & GRE study resources due to financial or situational limitations, and those from communities that are underrepresented in business. We accomplish this by matching our volunteer mentors with mentees, fostering a relationship that will allow mentees to access individualized advice and support throughout their studies.

All of our services are free. Our mentors are volunteers, so mentees can get GRAD support at no cost!

Everyone has different studying habits and life situations that warrant personalized GRAD strategies. Our mentors want to share their experiences and tailor their advice to each individual through one-on-one sessions.

GRAD Mentors has helped mentees across 25+ different countries!

Name: Syrian Kids Foundation
Description: The Syrian Kids Foundation (SKF) is a Canadian charity (registration number: 805889631RR0001) that aims to offer humanitarian relief and social services to Syrian refugees from all different backgrounds, sects and religions. The services include free education, psychological counselling, social relief, recreational activities, and essential subsidies. Al Salam School in Reyhanli-Turkey, a project of SKF, blends the best of Syrian and Canadian values — to rebuild trust, self-worth, compassion, and open-mindedness in Syrian refugee children. We aim to empower a new generation with a worldview that would help create the necessary conditions to establish a democratic society.

Name: Fullsoul Canada
Description:
~Soulful People making a difference in the world~

| Maternal Health | Medical Kits | Social Enterprise |

FullSoul is a not-for-profit equipping hospitals in Uganda with medical supplies.

Our Mission:

We are working to equip hospitals in Uganda with medical kits outfitted with the proper tools needed for a safer, more hygienic birth. These medical kits will contain items such as scissors, needle holders, kidney dishes, forceps and more. Access to these kits will increase the safety of the delivery process and decrease the risk of spreading infections such as HIV.

Through our networks of supporters and volunteers, FullSoul Canada supplies medical delivery kits in Uganda and other societies that could benefit from safer and more sanitary delivery processes. Through our 'Sindica' (push) for change, we are working to weave better maternal health into the lives of mothers, new born babies and medical practitioners in Uganda, and similar communities all over the word.



The medical kits will help establish a more soulful Planet. The FullSoul team's effort to become our best, while also changing the world will make us more soulful People.

Name: Fundacion Yo Soy Autismo
Description:
Yo Soy Autismo es una Fundación que busca difundir, informar a la comunidad sobre Trastorno de Espectro Autista, generar espacios, contención y alianzas con otras instituciones públicas y privadas. 
Las actividades de la Fundación están relacionadas en mantener como beneficiarios a los integrantes de la agrupación ciudadana TEApaño Autismo

Name: J. Reuben Clark Law Society - São Paulo Chapter
Description:
Os trabalhos que são executados pelos associados da J. Reuben Clark Law Society são todos voluntários e objetivam auxiliar juridicamente aos cidadãos, sejam em palestras ou em prática legal, A IMPORTÂNCIA DE SE TER UMA CRENÇA RELIGIOSA PARA VERDADEIRAMENTE ENRAIZAR OS VALORES ÉTICOS DE UM PROFISSIONAL EM DIREITO. Portanto, como se tratam de atividades pro bono, nossa expectativa é de fazer eventos para alicerçar estes princípios, haja vista que deveremos continuar desempenhando normalmente nossos labores diários.

Os membros são Advogados ou graduados em Faculdade de Direito que apoiam a missão desta Sociedade, que é: “Nós afirmamos a força trazida com a lei por um advogado que tenha uma convicção religiosa pessoal. Nós nos esforçamos por meio de prestar serviços públicos e com excelência profissional para promover a justiça e a virtude fundada no Estado de Direito”.

NÃO HÁ taxa de adesão para ser um associado ou pagamento de anuidade, e podem incluir estudantes de Direito e outros membros associados. A maioria dos membros são advogados mórmons, mas os de todos os credos são bem-vindos. NÃO HÁ nenhuma exigência quanto à preferência religiosa, ter uma inscrição ativa na OAB ou ter frequentado a Faculdade de Direito J. Reuben Clark. Ou seja, a graduação em uma Faculdade de Direito e o apoio da missão acima transcrita são os únicos requisitos para a adesão. A associação continuará indefinidamente até ser denunciada por quaisquer das partes.

Name: Cia dos ClownAticos
Ukrainian Women in Business NFP
Description:
UWB is a nonprofit organization dedicated to furthering female Ukrainian entrepreneurs through networking,  member promotion, monthly meetings, educational seminars, and philanthropic events. 

The cornerstone of our mission is to promote and support our community of social entrepreneurs, while always remembering to give back in kind. Membership in Ukrainian Women in Business comes with a social justice component and members will have the ability to get as involved as they choose in the philanthropic work of the organization. Additionally, Ukrainian Women in Business offers intermediary services for families looking to adopt from Ukraine.

Our mission
To support, encourage and provide opportunities for female Ukrainian entrepreneurs to grow and flourish professionally while also giving back to the community through charitable volunteer work.
 
Objectives
UWB seeks to be an advocate for professional women, offering resources for business development, marketing and PR, while also acting as an open community that fosters entrepreneurial creativity and ingenuity.
 
UWB prides itself on being an organization that supports and promotes social entrepreneurship and philanthropic endeavors. It is in this spirit of social justice that UWB is dedicated to raising awareness and funds for the many desolate orphanages in Ukraine which house and educate children in need. Additionally, we also act as a much needed resource for families looking to adopt from Ukraine--a process which can be quite daunting.

Membership
If you are a woman of Ukrainian descent interested in membership in our organization, please contact us and join our fast growing community of industry professionals. If you are not Ukrainian, but share in our social justice mission, we would still like for you to get involved however you are able. Please visit our Membership Page for information on joining our organization.

Name: Bainbridge Community Foundation
Description: Residents on Bainbridge Island deeply care about the community, and came together in 2001 to officially create the Bainbridge Community Foundation. BCF aims to encourage, inspire, and provide meaningful ways for people to enhance and sustain all sectors of society in the area. We fulfill that role by analyzing community needs, providing grants to local nonprofits, serving as a leading agent for philanthropy, providing expert guidance to donors and advisors, and improving capacity of nonprofits.

Name: 100 Men Who Care Knoxville
Description:
We are 100 men who meet quarterly with $100 each to make a $10,000 impact to our community.  

Our goal is to support charities and non-profit organizations dedicated to serving and making a difference in our community. These organizations are almost universally under-resourced. Together, we dramatically multiply our individual impact.

100 Men Who Care members are able to nominate and share about charities and non-profit organizations they  want to support with $10,000 in one hour!

Members also learn more about the community in which we live and what other organizations are doing throughout Greater Knoxville to meet needs, give hope and opportunity to the lives of others, or protect and sustain our environment and the welfare of the creatures within.

Name: Loden Initiatives
Description:
EDUCATION

Access to knowledge for all is the conviction that drives these initiatives. Lack of financial resources should not be a barrier between a child and their education or an obstacle between young person and their dreams.
Since 2012, the Loden Foundation has supported students engaged in higher education. It was also a pioneer in the establishment and operation of early learning centers for young children in rural areas.

ENTREPRENEURSHIP

Today, agricultural and trading activities cannot provide employment for the growing number of educated young people entering the labor market. The Loden Foundation by developing a corporate culture can meet these needs.  
 
The Loden Entrepreneurship program was created in 2008 when the Foundation began supporting entrepreneurs through loans without interest or collateral, in order to promote and encourage entrepreneurial activity, while maintaining social and ethical responsibility. Since then, more than 220 entrepreneurs have been supported by the Loden Foundation.  
 
In 2014, efforts went beyond financial support to explore the entrepreneurial potential of students in educational institutions, now operating as the Loden SEED program (Student Empowerment through Entrepreneurship Development).

CULTURE

The Loden cultural program aims to preserve and pass on Bhutan's rich cultural heritage to future generations. The program focuses on the documentation and study of written and oral traditions.
 
For more than 20 years, Loden Foundation has been at the forefront in terms of documentation and study of cultural heritage. The program currently has three parts : written heritage, oral traditions, arts and architecture.

Name: Kyle Palmieri Foundation
Description:
The Kyle Palmieri Foundation (Federal Tax ID 82-3386658) is a nonprofit corporation with federal tax-exempt status as a public charity under Section 501(c)(3) founded by New York Islanders forward Kyle Palmieri. The mission of the Kyle Palmieri Foundation is to give back to the patriots who put their lives on the line to preserve our freedoms as Americans.

While there is no way to adequately thank active and veteran service members and their families for the sacrifices they have made, we do our best to show our sincerest appreciation by raising funds for programs that benefit the military community.

The issue is personal to Kyle, as he has several family ties to the military. Currently, his sister and brother-in-law are honorably serving our country in the National Guard and U.S. Army. This close connection to the military inspired Kyle to do more to help those who sacrifice the most.

What began as a military recognition program called Squad21 in 2016, the Kyle Palmieri Foundation has since evolved into a full-fledged charity organization that supports three military nonprofits. The original Squad21 program also still lives on under the Foundation.

Name: Bengal Lancer Alumni Foundation
Description: The Bengal Lancer Alumni Foundation is a non-profit 501(c)(3) charitable organization incorporated by alumni of the Bengal Lancer fraternity at Trinity University in San Antonio, Texas for the purpose of providing scholarships to active members  of the Bengal Lancer fraternity enrolled at Trinity University and to support the endeavors of Trinity University. In keeping with the purpose and ideals of the Bengal Lancer fraternity, the Foundation shall foster fellowship and brotherhood among ourselves and all Trinity students, by upholding the name of our school, our club, and Almighty God, by being of service to our university, and by stimulating and creating school spirit while at the same time strengthening and tempering the bonds of our fellowship.

Name: Double Plus
Description:
POWERING UP THROUGH CHARITABLE OUTREACH

We align brands with initiatives that drive conversation, create connections, and impact lives.

With modern media’s ability to communicate with audiences in real time.   (YouTube/Twitch/Mixer/etc.), companies now have the ability to engage 1:1 with their target demographic. The downside is, there is a torrential firehose of communications being blasted out every nano-second making discoverability one of the greatest challenges of the online era.

Great casues, experiences, and people are all going under the radar because they simply aren’t getting the visibility they need.

Double Plus is working on changing that game…and the world.

Name: Washington AIDS Partnership
Description: As an initiative of the Washington Regional Association of Grantmakers, the Partnership invests in local organizations, focusing programming and resources on the communities most affected by the epidemic. The Partnership also provides technical assistance to local nonprofits to increase capacity and expertise; initiates public policy initiatives to address systemic issues; convenes local government, providers, funders, and other stakeholders to implement innovative strategies to reduce HIV risk and improve the lives of people living with HIV; and each year, recruits and mentors a team of young people who provide direct volunteer service in the community.

Name: Omoana
Description: Omoana is a Swiss non-profit organization founded in 2003. Its mission is to support Ugandan and Iraqi youth in developing prospects for the future. The organization provides strategic, technical and financial support to local organizations or initiatives in the fields of education, mental health and psychosocial support.

Name: Foundation for Edmonds School District
Description:
We develop community partnerships and bridge sustainable funding in support of the Edmonds School District’s children, families, and educators in their pursuit of educational excellence.

Foundation for Edmonds School District is a local 501 (c)(3) local non-profit, charity that serves students, families and teachers in the Edmonds School District which serves the communities of Brier, Edmonds, Lynnwood, Mountlake Terrace, Woodway and portions of unincorporated Snohomish County.

Name: Reino da Garotada de Poá
Description:
O Reino da Garotada de Poá é uma associação civil, sem fins lucrativos, fundada há 77 anos, pelo padre holandês Simon Switzar.
Tem como missão, promover a inclusão social, proporcionando assistência e formação para crianças, adolescentes, jovens e famílias de baixa renda, conforme tipificação nacional dos serviços socioassistenciais.
Atualmente a instituição atende gratuitamente, em média, 1000 crianças, adolescentes, jovens e famílias nos seguintes Programas:
- Creche - Educação Infantil (0 a 3 anos e 11 meses de idade);
- Centro de Juventude – Serviço de Convivência e Fortalecimento de Vínculos (6 a 15 anos de idade);
- Qualificação Profissional (a partir de 14 anos) nas áreas de: Informática, Auxiliar Administrativo, Corte e Costura, Tapeçaria de Móveis, Panificação e Confeitaria, Instalações Elétricas, Marcenaria e Mecânica Geral, em parceria técnica com o Senai. Oferece também cursos de Tear Artesanal e Fotografia.
- Jovem Aprendiz em serviços bancários, varejo e almoxarifado.
A instituição está inscrita e participa ativamente, entre outros, no Conselho Municipal de Assistência Social, Conselho Municipal da Criança e do Adolescente, Conselho Municipal da Educação, com certificação em todos.
A entidade conta com auditoria externa, hoje atendida pela empresa PP&C – Auditores Independentes. Mantemos apresentação pública regular de todas as nossas atividades e desembolsos, tudo no sentido de oferecer transparência aos nossos colaboradores.

Name: No Right Brain Left Behind
Description:
We are creatives, educators, doers, thinkers, and provocateurs who share a conviction that the creative industries can solve some of the most pressing issues the world is facing today. Creativity in education is our focus. 

The problems we'll face in the next hundred years will be radically different from the last 100. The reality is schools are preparing children for the 21st century with a 19th century model. Creativity and innovation in classrooms is directly related to the ability to cope with issues never before seen or encountered. The progress of the global marketplace and our culture's ability to endure is also directly related to our ability to break through conventional thinking. Creativity lies at the core of all that. It is also missing where needed most - in classrooms.

Name: Fortitude Fund
Description: A micro-grant fund for Northeast Indiana's bold, inventive entrepreneurs. Formerly the "Farnsworth Fund"

Name: Fundación PGP
Description:
Fundación PGP es un proyecto generador de proyectos dedicado a crear y compartir soluciones que contribuyan con la mejora en la vida de las personas.

Muy pronto te estaremos convocando para formar parte de una red que cambiará la forma de ver la felicidad y el éxito. Espéranos!

Name: St. Paul Central High School Foundation
Description: A vibrant network of alumni, parents, and friends who work toward providing post-secondary scholarships to deserving Central seniors and who help to strengthen connections across alumni, current students, and other members of the Central Family.

Name: Bridgeport Animal Rescue Crew of Connecticut
Description:
MISSION:
BARC-CT is a 501(c)3 nonprofit animal advocacy + rescue organization that exists to rescue, rehabilitate, and rehome unwanted dogs from within our community and throughout Connecticut.

FOCUS:
BARC-CT focuses on rescuing animals and providing them with medical care and access to trainers and behaviorists in order to help them make a full recovery regardless of what they have endured.

We rely solely on dedicated volunteers, including fosters who take rescued animals into their home and provide them with the care and nurturing they need. The generosity of donors provides funding for the many needs of the animals such as vetting, boarding, training, and general care.

All of our foster and adoptive homes are fully screened; this includes checking personal and vet references, obtaining landlord permission to have a pet, and meeting with the applicant while conducting a home visit to ensure the home is the right fit for the animal. All of our animals are vaccinated, microchipped and altered prior to adoption.

GOALS:
Our goal is to restore dignity, trust, and quality of life to each of the dogs in our care while working diligently to place them into the appropriate, loving “forever” home they deserve. We would love to have you join us on our mission in whatever way you choose: 
 
1) Adopt
          When you adopt a dog from BARC-CT you are actually saving the lives of two dogs—by adopting one, space opens for us to save the life of another. So adopt, don't shop! Please see the Adopt page on www.barc-ct.org for more information.

2) Foster
          Our organization relies on volunteers to foster dogs until a "forever" home is found. Fostering saves lives! Please see the Foster page on www.barc-ct.org for more information.

3) Donate
          The greatest burden on an animal rescue organization is never having enough money. All of our dog adoptions include basic vetting, but many of our animals require additional care. These costs add up. Whatever your donation amount, 100% of it is tax deductible and will go directly towards the care of our animals. Please see the Donate page on www.barc-ct.org for more information.

Name: Mountain T.O.P. (Tennessee Outreach Project)
Description:
Mountain T.O.P. (Tennessee Outreach Project) is a Christian nonprofit, partnership ministry seeking to meet the physical, social, emotional and spiritual needs of the Cumberland Mountain people. Mountain T.O.P. believes in elevating local assets to address the challenges of persistent poverty. We see value in the people and this place and trust that building meaningful relationships will result in more sustainable change – an internal empowerment to make a difference.

For over 40 years, Mountain T.O.P. has thrived as a strategic community partner in Grundy County, TN located in the Appalachian region. Mountain T.O.P. focuses on activities such as reducing substandard housing, supporting lifelong learning and leadership, and promoting overall wellness. Through the direct services of home repair and summer day camps for local children, Mountain T.O.P. provides an opportunity for participants nationwide to work alongside local families, crossing cultural barriers to build community, finding sustainable solutions for essential needs, and ultimately reversing the effects of fatalism.

Name: Association SPE
Description:
L'Association SPE (Solidarité Partage et Education) est une association à but non lucratif qui a été créée en 2020 par 8 étudiants de master à l'IAE Clermont Auvergne, et qui a pour but d'être transmise d'année en année pour fédérer étudiants, particuliers et acteurs économiques autour d'une cause humanitaire :
l'accès à l'éducation.

Nos œuvrons au bénéfice de l'école du village d'Oulad'mtaa et/ou l'école de Tighdouine.

Name: Topo Sans Frontière Madagascar
Description:
Nous sommes un groupe d'étudiants de l’École Supérieure des Géomètres et Topographes (ESGT) et membre de l'association Topo Sans Frontière (TSF) de cette école.

Le projet de notre équipe concerne Madagascar, et a pour objectif cette année 3 missions humanitaires principales: la formation d'étudiants à l’École Supérieure Polytechnique d'Antananarivo, l'implantation de réseaux potables à Alakamisy-Ambohimaha (en partenariat avec l'association IDEE MADAGASCAR), et le relèvement de rizière à Mahasoa pour un hôpital d'enfants tenu par des religieuses.

En parallèle, nous souhaitons cette année lancer un nouveau projet  appelé "Open Topo", qui a pour objectif de concevoir une suite de relèvement topographique open source, c'est à dire gratuit et accessible par n'importe qui dans le monde. Cette suite sera composée d'appareils de mesure (antenne GPS, tachéomètre), dont la particularité sera d'être constructible à partir de pièces du marché et à un coût réduit pour permettre son accessibilité par tous les pays en développement et en reconstruction après une guerre ou une catastrophe naturelle. 

Afin de pouvoir réaliser ces projets, nous recherchons des financements et des dons auprès d'entreprises telles que la vôtre. En échange d'une aide financière, nous proposons diverses actions de communication: vous associer à notre projet de mission d'innovation et humanitaire, en vous inscrivant dans notre liste de partenaires, promouvoir votre participation sur les réseaux sociaux (Facebook, LinkedIn...) et sur notre site internet www.tsf-madagascar-2017.fr. Lors de notre mission sur place, nous vous proposons également  de prendre des photos que vous pourrez librement utiliser dans vos campagnes de communication.

Name: Chamadia Foundation
Description: Chamadia Foundation is a Non Profit organization started to serve social and educational needs of the society.

Name: The Leadership Club
Description:
For the last 15 years Mark Ashton has been using his disruptive influence and passion for “The Arts” to change the lives of thousands of young people through his multi award winning social business. During this time Mark has collaborated with many inspirational people who share his passion for seeing other people succeed. 

As part of a new website and podcast series, Mark invites some of those friends along to share their stories and leadership insights in the hope that it will help to inspire some of the young leaders of tomorrow. 

"As leaders we understand the benefit of collaboration and working together. I am looking for people to join me in showcasing the best leadership examples to our impressionable young people. This is not about me or anyone else, this is about a stronger, more connected, happier future for us all" - Mark Ashton

Name: Key Changes Therapy Services, Inc
Description: Key Changes Therapy Services, Inc is a non-profit therapy organization with a mission of making creative arts therapies accessible to all. Speech therapy, occupational, and music therapy are provided by qualified and credentialed therapists. We are always seeking to expand access to music, art, drama, and other creative arts therapies in the midlands.

Name: Horses Help Kids
Description: After founding the originating project Voice For The Horse in 2007, we branched off in 2014 to create our new company, Horses Help Kids. We are a business based on philanthropy which tackles chronic societal problems with sustainable solutions. Through the universal language of music and our new signature program, Leading The Change Music Project For Kids, we have built a strong foundation where we promote four important causes: respect and advocacy for horses, anti-bullying, mental health and world kindness.

Name: Manabi Camella
Description:
El proyecto es un aporte de empresarios, micro empresarios y ciudadanos emprendedores, que de forma privada e independiente hemos pensado y analizado alternativas viables para que comunidades paralizadas en su economía, cuyo fin más importante es la reestructuración económica y el fortalecimiento de las comunidades a las que llegue el proyecto. 
La propuesta general se basa en fomentar el desarrollo de proyectos de emprendimiento, en donde sea la comunidad la generadora de productos, que elaborados bajo parámetros de calidad supervisados por empresarios y emprendedores con experiencia, puedan dar a conocer su trabajo, posicionar marcas a nivel local, nacional y a futuro hacerlo también internacionalmente.

Name: We Give a Boot
Description: We Give a Boot is a charity started by 3 primary school aged sisters that collects used soccer and footy boots, based in Melbourne, Australia. This initiative started in 2016 to collect used boots in good condition to then pass on to organised charities who then distribute the boots to those most in need. We have partnerships with three established charities who distribute the boots across Australia, Africa, Asia and the South Pacific Islands. Most of our collections are organised through local soccer and footy clubs as well as schools. If you are based in Australia and interested in arranging a collection please get in contact with the girls father Paul Tucker - pmtucker10@gmail.com

Name: DuPont - Moore Family Trust Foundation
Description: The DuPont - Moore Family Trust Foundation is a family-funded foundation that continues to invest in Philanthropic Endeavors with a history of over 200 Years.

Name: Bridget’s Cradles
Description: Bridget's Cradles is a 501c3 nonprofit organization based in Kechi, Kansas.

Name: Lilian Bildungsintiative gGmbH
Description: Jugendlichen Mädchen und jungen Frauen in Afrika Bildungschancen ermöglichen; Ausbildungsnetzwerk aufbauen; Lilian Education

Name: Kupi Culture
Description:
KUPI CULTURE is an independent global organisation working towards positive social change and economic sustainability for the youth of Banda Aceh.

We work collectively and collaboratively across a range of platforms combining architecture, design thinking, urbanism, entrepreneurship and social innovation.

We bring together individuals and organisations from diverse creative, business and research fields to participate in design based workshops for idea generation and change implementation.

Our aim is to actively engage young people in the making of their city and to equip them with the skills and support they need to build sustainable futures within a global context. 

By working with us you are supporting young innovators to craft their futures using their own talents and passions as seeds for change. Together we can develop creative economies in a post-disaster area, laying groundwork for future development in banda aceh and providing precedence for the economic and cultural recovery of other post-disaster areas.

For corporates this provides a co-venturing opportunity for your product
development within an innovative design thinking and lean startup environment with passionate local youth.

Become part of a global multidisciplinary collaboration to create sustainable futures in local communities. connect with youth in indonesia, australia and japan to strengthen global cross-cultural social and economic ties. Enable the youth of Banda Aceh to become change makers of the future while having fun at the same time!

Name: K9 Mission Inc.
Description:
These service dogs don’t just change lives, they save lives. With the help of a service dog, those with disabilities, can feel empowered to complete tasks that before may have felt traumatizing or impossible to complete alone. Simply walking outside or the sound of a plane can bring a veteran back to the thoughts of war, this fear can be paralyzing. 

K9 Mission is driven by helping people with disabilities gain confidence and to help them feel that they are no longer lost. K9 Mission’s service dogs provide purpose to those who felt like they no longer had one. Our K9s have been proven to combat anxiety, stress, depression, and suicide. 

The mission is to provide these dogs to those suffering from PTSD in lieu of medication. In some cases, service dogs are a veterans last hope. 

These K9s bring light and comfort to those suffering and to the families of those suffering. They brighten spirits and change dynamics of homes for the better.

Our goal is to train and place as many K9s as possible, we just need your help.

Name: 100 Women of Lake Highlands
Description: 100 Women of Lake Highlands is a group of 100+ women who live in Lake Highlands and are passionate about the community. The women will gather three times in 2017 for a one-hour meeting, where members will hear from fellow members about three local nonprofits and their projects and/or immediate needs within the Lake Highlands community. Present members then vote to select one charity, then each member (present or non-present) will write a $100 check to that selected charity. This combined donation is intended to make a positive and meaningful impact in the community we live.

Name: Grow A Row
Description: In conjunction with Burlington Together and the Burlington Food Bank, Grow-A-Row is a volunteer-run charitable program that encourages home gardeners to grow an extra row with the intent to donate the harvest to the food bank.

Name: OneUmbrella Foundation
Description:
Mission: OneUmbrella Foundation focuses on organizing and executing philanthropic events and campaigns on a Regional, National and Global scale. These include engaging in partnerships with other charitable organizations, in an effort to unite cultures and provide assistant to those in need.
 

Vision: We operate with one vision to aid and empower those in need through educational programs designed to create an effective path for today’s society. We assist in individual growth, active community programs and job placement.
 

Strategic Plan: Create an effective platform for any and all Individuals to develop realistic goals aligned with their career path. Bringing fourth a process and procedure that allows individuals to gain knowledge, be involved in the community and become one with society.

Name: Make Giving Happen
Description:
What is Make Giving Happen?

Make Giving Happen offers a better approach to crowdfunding—by connecting organizations to a unique, cloud-based fundraising model that unifies new crowdfunding initiatives and traditional giving programs.

The Cascade Funding tool—a single, simple-to-integrate online giving platform—can give organizations a steady stream of funds from a range of sources: both new crowds through crowdfunding and current communities through traditional online fundraising.


Make Giving Happen presents a powerful way of working with any funds that can flow an organization’s way—unrestricted funds, focused funds, directed funds—all on the same platform, at the same time. 

Learn more at makegivinghappen.com 

Also, follow us on: 
facebook.com/makegivinghappen/ 
twitter.com/givinghappen 
instagram.com/givinghappen/

Name: Uniting Global Communities through Aid (UGCA)
Description:
UGCA’s Mission: Improving access to education, livelihoods, and empowerment

UGCA was created in July 2008, with the belief that every person has the right and the desire to lead a better life. It is our ambition to create an enabling framework that supports people in their struggle to combat poverty within their communities. Through education, empowerment, and access to livelihoods, UGCA aims to take a holistic approach to combating poverty - recognizing that there is no silver bullet and that each location requires a unique approach.

In 2011 we partnered with the Women’s Resource Centre for Slums (RCS) in Kibera, Kenya. We want to make this Centre sustainable. Their mission and purpose is to provide a holistic support to women living with and affected by HIV/AIDS in the sprawling Kibera Slum. 

Check out our Products Page to order your jewelry today and help support this wonderful program!

Name: YOU ARE CO.
Description:
YOU ARE CO. is a 501c(3) nonprofit organization that provides safe housing and education to women and girls living in extreme poverty because educated and empowered women transform communities. 

For more information, go to youareco.org!

Name: Towncreek Vision Corporation
Description:
Our Mission
Towncreek Vision Corporation works with rural communities to empower people to enhance health, end hunger and improve the financial well being of its citizens.

Our Vision 
Our vision is to promote thriving, sustainable rural communities that will allow its citizens to thrive within the communities they live in.

Name: The ESTEEM Program
Description: The ESTEEM Program is a highly interactive 10 week program based upon biblical principles of self- worth. It caters to middle school and high school girls guiding them through a detailed self -worth curriculum including lessons, journal assignments, engaging activities and homework. The goal is to get every girl to see her value, worth and uniqueness so she can be the leader and person she was called to be.

Name: Bike the UK for MS
Description:
Bike the UK for MS runs cross country bicycle trips to fund multiple sclerosis research and raise awareness. Our cyclists raise at least £1 per mile to impact MS research, treatment, and help MS support groups across the UK.
Join us for spectacular trips across the country - NC500, Sea to Sea or the full Land's End to John O'Groats.

Name: Connect to Community
Description: Connect to Community is a not-for-profit organization dedicated to finding and facilitating purposeful employment and enjoyment for adults with developmental disabilities. Further, in an effort to help these adults live the lives they desire and deserve within the community, we will provide person-centered services across all aspects of community life.

Name: Center for Advancing Health
Description:
The Center for Advancing Health works to increase people’s engagement in their health care. We listen to patient perspectives. We translate what we learn into resources that help all of us participate fully in our health care and that enable policy makers and clinicians to support our efforts.

Our activities include: 

 - Be a Prepared Patient (http://www.cfah.org/prepared-patient/): To benefit fully from heath care for ourselves or our loved ones, we must engage in our care. CFAH designed these resources to help you not only find good health care but also make the most of it. 

- Engagement (http://www.cfah.org/engagement/): We listen to patient perspectives and translate what we learn into resources that help all of us participate fully in our health care and that enable policy makers and clinicians to support us in doing so.

 - the Health Behavior News Service (http://www.cfah.org/hbns/), which writes original news stories about the latest findings from peer-reviewed research journals and disseminates them world-wide to subscribed journalists; 

 - the William Ziff Fellows (http://www.cfah.org/about/william-a-ziff-fellows), a group of leading patient engagement experts who exchange ideas about how to raise visibility and credibility of new approaches to increase people’s participation in their health care; 

 - and a wealth of other research and publications ( www.cfah.org).

Name: Effective Altruism Auckland
Description:
Effective altruism is a philosophy and growing social movement that applies evidence and analysis to determine the most effective ways to improve the world.

Many people want to make the world a better place, but not all good-intentioned acts have the same impact. Some have no discernible impact, some actually make things worse, but the very best actions can make an incredibly large difference. By seeking and acting on evidence and using critical thinking we can all make a significant positive impact on the world.

Name: Carpinteria Freemasons
Description:
This is the official LinkedIn page for the Carpinteria Masons and Carpinteria Masonic Lodge #444.  

Carpinteria Freemasons' mission is to foster Masonic values in our community. We seek to meet our members’ needs, to promote fraternal ties, and to make the world a better place, one Mason at a time. Our Lodge welcomes good men and provides them with opportunities for personal growth and development in a stable, safe, caring, fraternal atmosphere. We offer our members and their families the opportunity to meet each other socially and to build and renew lifelong friendships. We are a light in our community, reaching out in ways that will improve people and enhance their lives.

If you are interested in becoming a Freemason, you may complete the request for information form at our website www.carpinteriafreemasons.org and someone will contact you regarding membership. You may also call the lodge directly at (805) 684-4433.

Name: Save The World
Description: Launching 2020, Founded by Jean de Hoog

Name: The Covitech Challenge
Description: As India grapples with the new COVID-19 surge, reports of oxygen and medical supply shortages continue to pour in, perhaps cruelly summed up by social media posts of hospitals pleading with authorities for oxygen supplies, helpless friends and family desperately looking for oxygen cans for their loved ones, drugs or hospital care for their family members. The aim of the "Covitech Challenge 2021" is to raise money for Hoomans Of Calcutta's fundraiser to provide oxygen to senior citizens and low-income families in the region. Participants will be asked to create a short video explaining a specific and unique solution to a current problem. No coding experience is required, participants can submit researched solutions too. Registrations are now open: https://bit.ly/covitech-registration!

Name: The-Light Foundation
Description:
Guided by the belief that every life has equal value, the Light Foundation works to help all people lead healthy, productive lives. In developing countries, it focuses on improving people’s health and giving them the chance to lift themselves out of hunger and extreme poverty. It seeks to ensure that all people—especially those with the fewest resources—have access to the opportunities they need to succeed in school and life. Based in Lagos, the foundation is led by Lekan Adejumo, Clement Olalekan and Kayode Balogun under the direction of God.

Our Program Teams research and make grant & investment proposals that are aligned with a program strategy. They are responsible for ongoing management of grants and investments.   

Our Operating Teams composed of Legal, Finance, and Grants & Contracts Management support program teams to design, create & execute grant & investment agreements. Our Human Resources, Global Digital Technology (IT), and Global Facilities, Events, and Travel teams provide the staff, technology and logistics that enable program teams to run effectively and work smartly around the world.

We strive to create a peak career experience. We know that by creating an environment for our employees to personally and professionally thrive and contribute at their highest potential, we will have maximum impact towards our missions.

Our missions are the outcomes that help us achieve our vision of seeing a world where every person has the opportunity to live a healthy, productive life:
* Ensure more children and young people survive and thrive
* Empower the poorest, especially women and girls, to transform their lives
* Combat infectious diseases that particularly affect the poorest 
* Inspire people to take action to change the world

Name: FUNDAÇÃO ORLANDO PANTERA
Description:
Missão:
Contribuir para o desenvolvimento sócio-cultural cabo-verdiano, através de ações de recolha, proteção, estudo e divulgação do património de Orlando Pantera.

Visão: 
A Fundação ambiciona contribuir para a divulgação, promoção e valorização da cultura e identidade do povo cabo-verdiano, contribuindo igualmente para a inclusão social de jovens e crianças mais desfavorecidos. Queremos contribuir para uma sociedade mais participativa, solidária, inclusiva e humana.

Valores:
- Solidariedade e Inclusão 
- Ética, Transparência e rigor 
- Fraternidade 
- Inovação

Name: Acts of Greatness
Description:
We empower LGBTQ youth to be who they were born to be.

To learn more about how we empower our next generation of LGBTQ leaders, visit http://www.actsofgreatness.org/.

Name: Widow's Mite Inc.
Description: Widow’s Mite’s mission is to help and empower grief stricken surviving spouses and families cope when faced with the loss of a significant life partner, or in preparing to face the death of a husband or wife as a result of serious or life-threatening trauma or illness. To take control of their destiny and function productively by providing them with the necessary skills to realize their dreams, through counseling, education, social skills training, motivation, and participating in the treatment of abnormal behaviors to become successful.

Name: Hopping 4 A Cure for Multiple Sclerosis
Description:
Hopping 4 A Cure for Multiple Sclerosis is a Trademarked, Incorporated, Full 501(c)3 non-profit organization that hosts Recess Olympics and Hopscotch Tournaments raising money & awareness for Multiple Sclerosis. Proceeds go toward College Scholarships, Caregiver Support, and Alternative Medical Research.

With your help, we can all HOP in the HOPE of a world free of Multiple Sclerosis.

Hop onboard at http://www.mshopscotch.org/

Name: Upside Nepal
Description: Upside is an Australian based not-for-profit working in partnership with villages to develop community-owned agricultural businesses across Nepal.The best part is that the profits from these businesses are used to fund local schools and health posts now and long, long into the future

Name: Quench and Connect
Description:
Quench and Connect is a non-profit organization dedicated to supporting schools in Uganda. Recognizing that health and good education are primary tools to lift a population out of poverty, the mission of Quench and Connect builds on two pillars: 
   • to provide clean safe water to students
   • to provide educational tools that will 
      significantly increase the academic
      achievement of the students

Name: CURA For The World Foundation
Description:
CURA for the world was founded on a vision to heal and nourish the neglected, to break the chains of the illiterate, to reach the unreachable and to have limitless love in a world where love is often forgotten.

Our Vision: Partnering with non-medical organizations to expand their reach to help in humanitarian aid nationally and internationally.

The Story behind our Logo:
It all started with a doctor’s dream, a vision, like minded friends, lots of hard work and the will to make the world a better place by lending a helping hand.

Genesis: Cūra  (genitive cūrae); a healing hand, a helping hand in Latin.

"Our logo tells the story of a helping hand -CURA- gently holding the globe, represented by the letter “C”, a simple circle open and ready to help the neglected. The “U” which makes a turn around the globe and comes reaching out to the rest of the letters, creates one strong entity. Cura’s logo clearly shows the mission to travel the world to help those in need, this hand-drawn shape brings to life a dream, colored in black to reflect the seriousness of the work. After all, black is the combination of all primary colors."

- Jihan Fadel

Name: Harmony Hill Farm Sanctuary
Description: Harmony Hill Farm Sanctuary provides loving care and a forever home for neglected, abandoned or homeless farm animals.

Name: SOLIDARIEDADE NA MOKILI
Description:
SOLIDARIEDADE NA MOKILI, a Global nonprofit 501c3 public charity dedicated to addressing human development. We work around the world to save lives ,defeat pervasive poverty and also we provide tools for sustainable change to the people most vulnerable to hunger, violence and disease.

Our organization places women and children in the forefront because we believe women are the heart of the family, as well as cultural, health and social balances within every community. Women play a central role in health development and education. As such, their empowerment is an indispensable factor for peace, social, economic, and environmental progress. We are convinced giving women the right to choose their lives independently, anywhere in the world, is one of the keys to meeting the challenges of this century. Furthermore, we believe if women earn a competitive income, have equal rights and opportunities; this will result in a better future for children and accelerate the global goals and create a better world for all.

Our Mission :

SOLIDARIEDADE NA MOKILI works around the planet  to save lives, end poverty and achieve the UN Sustainable Development Goals.

Our Vision :

We seek  a world of where every human deserves to live in peace , security and dignity

We  create social jusitce in the  world by ending poverty, protect the planet and guarantee well-being for women and children .

Why we do this ?

We put women and children  in the center because Women are at the heart of family, cultural, health and social balances. They play a central role in health, development and education

Name: The Jewish Women's Foundation of the Greater Palm Beaches (JWF)
Description: Guided by Jewish values, the Jewish Women's Foundation of the Greater Palm Beaches (JWF) is committed to work for opportunities to empower women and girls to achieve social and economic equality. JWF uses social change philanthropy, advocacy and education to ensure that women and girls can reach their fullest potential. JWF offers its donors a vehicle to develop an informed Jewish philanthropic response to women's issues.

Name: INSTITUTO RANGEL PESTANA
Description:
O Instituto Rangel Pestana foi criado em Setembro de 1922 para ser um braço social da Loja Maçônica Rangel Pestana. Atualmente, oferecemos atendimento Educacional, Social, Psicológico, Pedagógico á quase 1000 (mil) familias, quase 2.200 (duas mil e duzentas) pessoas, com o objetivo de auxiliar no reatamento e fortalecimento dos vínculos familiares á uma sociedade mais justa e igualitária.



As comunidades que atendemos, chegam até nossas unidades por encaminhamento de equipamentos de saúde (hospitais, UBSs, CAPS), fórum, delegacias, defensoria pública, escolas, abrigos, equipamentos e serviços da assistência social (Centros da Criança e Adolescente, Centros de Juventude, Serviço de Medida Sócio Educativa em Meio Aberto, Centro de Referência Especializado da Assistência Social), além de diálogos realizados em creches que auxiliamos mensalmente em atividades e projetos.



Ressalta-se também que muitas das pessoas que atendemos, nos procuram espontaneamente, pedindo ajuda e recebendo de forma fraterna ajuda.



A Diretoria do Instituto Rangel Pestana é formada pelos membros da Loja Maçônica Rangel Pestana, Loja filiada ao Grande Oriente do Brasil São Paulo.

Name: Instituto AMA
Description: O AMA tem em sua missão promover e articular ações de defesa de direitos, prevenção, orientações e a prestação de serviços para a melhoria da qualidade de vida das crianças em situação de vulnerabilidade e risco social e o apoio a família, buscando a construção de uma sociedade mais justa e solidária.

Name: OutdoorProject.org
Description: Join our mission to be active, engaged adventure stewards. OutdoorProject.org is a 501(c)(3) that supports and promotes non-profit groups doing critical work in the areas of outdoor conservation and environmental education and creates inspired and enriching outdoor educational content. #AdventureLikeYouGiveADamn

Name: CalvinCare
Description: CalvinCare is an organization that provides financial assistance for owners in need of day-to-day expenses like veterinary visits, grooming, food, and approved supplies, for their Service Animals or Emotional Support Animals.

Name: Da Terra Brasil Foundation
Description:
The Da Terra Brasil Foundation is dedicated to supporting and improving the lives of marginalized communities in Brazil which are most affected by poverty and social injustice.

The Da Terra Brasil Foundation believes that every human being deserves love, respect, and a chance to live a dignified life. Through fundraising initiatives, community events, and the support of a network of friends, and volunteers, the Da Terra Brasil Foundation encourages individuals to join in our efforts to provide better opportunities for those who are struggling to survive in the most impoverished communities in Brazil.

Name: Angel Producers
Description:
We produce films events & social media buzz for select causes, celebrities and brands for raising awareness, eyebrows, funds, buzz & the bar. We have produced dozens of short films, events and presentations for celebrities and causes including: [Elton John, George Clooney, Barrack Obama, Matt Damon] [United Nations, IRC, WFP, UNICEF, Save The Children, buildOn, Lalela Project]

See www.Crossingborders.tv for the next next human project for change....

Name: Rota Talks Foundation
Description: Rota Talks is a youth-oriented talk show organisation founded by the Youth Of Kolkata in the year 2018. Our organisation hosts talk shows to spread our motto, “Listen, Learn and Evolve".

Name: Angels For Allison
Description: Angels for Allison helps with the financial needs of families who are suffering with the loss of a child. We have created a community-based art program to paint and sell brightly colored metal angels to support the mission of the Foundation. Angels for Allison believes that through a community painting project like ours, we can pool our resources and provide help. While partnering with 15 Jacksonville area schools and hundreds of volunteers with our painting project, we pool our community resources of time and talent to come to the aid of families in need who have lost a child.

Name: Pakistan Stammering Foundation
Description:
Our Vision: is to make world a better place by helping people towards speech fluency and create equal opportunity for everyone. 

Our Mission: Work with people having speech disorder (stammering/stuttering) and aid them and guide them to achieve better position in personal and professional life.

Name: The Foundation Trust
Description: The Foundation Trust is a private philanthropic foundation.  Our regional focus is New England with special focus on Greater Boston.  Our mission is to support the ongoing work of small to medium-sized non-profit organizations that provide vital services to under-resourced populations that larger charitable foundations may overlook.

Name: Austin Habitat Young Professionals (HYP)
Description:
Keep up with Austin Habitat Young Professionals:
