Name: Asociación Erasmus Pamplona
Description:
Hello!!

We are a group of young people who live in Pamplona and who have had an erasmus experience before. We are willing to help new erasmus incoming people in anything they need. We are students and ex-students from "Universidad Publica de Navarra" so we can also help you somehow with academic stuff.

But this is not only about helping you guys in the early days of your erasmus journey. We will try to solve the doubts which people post in this wall, and also we will try to organize for you some activities, trips, and of course parties.

Nowadays this is a modest proposal but we will try to do our best. We will try that this tool becomes a useful one for everyone.

Name: Abrigo dos Velhinhos Frederico Ozanam
Description:
O Abrigo criado em maio de 1966, a ideia de construí-lo partiu do saudoso Padre Bruno Carra, pároco da Igreja São Pedro de Tremembé, naquela época. O vicentino José Almeida Silva e sua esposa Alice doaram o terreno. Os pioneiros foram os Vicentinos da Conferência São Pedro, instalada na Paróquia. O Abrigo é mais uma Obra Unida da Sociedade de São Vicente de Paulo, à qual é vinculada, obedecendo a sua Regra e normas.

O Abrigo começou pequeno, apoiados pelos católicos conscientes da comunidade do Tremembé, os vicentinos construíram algumas casinhas em condições muito modestas. Eram poucos e de ambos os sexos os idosos ali abrigados. Mais tarde outros terrenos foram adquiridos, onde foi construído um prédio maior, especialmente destinado ao acolhimento de pessoas idosas e pobres. A mudança das casinhas para o Abrigo novo foi feita gradativamente.

Em 19 de abril de l970, o Abrigo foi inaugurado com a realização de uma missa celebrada por Dom Paulo Evaristo Arns. Em determinado momento houve a necessidade de optar pelo atendimento somente de um sexo, foram contempladas as vovós. Os vovôs foram encaminhados para outras Obras Unidas.

Dentro do espírito vicentino de levar a espiritualidade a seus assistidos, a capela foi construída. Hoje as missas são diárias. Atualmente o Abrigo conta com um quadro de 29 empregados, dentre eles: médico, enfermeira, fisioterapeuta, nutricionista, assistente social, auxiliar de Enfermagem,  Auxiliar de cozinha, faxineira, auxiliar de manutenção. Com 67 voluntários(as), contamos madrinhas, pedagoga, podologa, manicure cabeleira, enfermeiro, etc..

Name: Success Solutions Ltd
Description:
Success Solutions works in partnership with One Team Global (OTG), an independently funded and not-for-profit entrepreneur support and training organisation, to empower and support individuals on their journey to entrepreneurial success.

With access to a wide range of skill sets, experience, and other valuable resources, Success Solutions provides a proven pathway to success for those who have not yet achieved the results or successes that they feel they are truly capable or deserving of.

In many cases, through our regular free live or online introductory business briefing events, our one-day workshops, or our 3-day international summits people who have been stuck in a rut (whether in a job or their own business) find the strategies, ideas, techniques, business models, product sectors, inspiration or mentor required to make the changes needed and move forward with enthusiasm to develop and achieve their goals and dreams.

Find out when there is an event coming up near you or online, or just contact us to find out more, and take the first steps to changing your results today.

info@success-solutions.co.uk

Name: Haiti Water Partners
Description:
The purpose of the Haiti Water Partners (formerly Dlo Geri) is to collaborate with the people of Haiti to ensure safe drinking water and improved sanitation for the projects and communities where we work. We intend to use sound scientific and engineering principles to implement water and wastewater infrastructure that address the public health issues facing the Haitian people. Using proven approaches developed elsewhere, we will work with the Haitian people to implement sustainable and repeatable long-term solutions. Overall we want to communicate an approach to water that takes the best public health practices and employs them in Haiti. We aim improve the quality of life for the people of Haiti so they can thrive and enjoy life to its fullest.

As required by 501(c)3 requirements, “the organization is organized for charitable and scientific purposes under 501(c)3 of the Internal Revenue Code or corresponding section of any future federal tax code.”

Name: BKFA
Description:
It all begins with the birth of a child - maternal health is a catalyst for empowerment, education and development in marginalised communities.

Our Mission
The Birthing Kit Foundation (Australia) works with organisations and communities to provide a clean birthing environment for women in developing countries in order to reduce the incidence of infant and maternal mortality.

We respect peoples' dignity and values and work according to principles of basic human rights. We raise awareness, provide support and resources and act as a catalyst for the creation of birth attendant training programmes and community development projects.

About BKFA
Every woman has the right to a clean and safe childbirth.

The Birthing Kit Foundation (Australia) is an organisation dedicated to improving the conditions for women who give birth at home in developing countries. 

"Every minute, a woman dies of complications related to pregnancy and childbirth"

With an estimated 385,000 women dying annually in childbirth, many from infections acquired during childbirth, there is a great need for our clean birthing kits and maternal health programs.

We work with women in the most marginalised parts of the developing world with the highest rates of maternal mortality and morbidity. Many mothers and babies die from preventable infections. Birthing kits are essential to reduce the likelihood of infections and transmission of HIV.

Our programs also work to empower women, families and communities with health promotion, education and community development for long term improved health, social change and development. 

The Foundation is a not-for-profit non-government organisation (NGO). We have no religious or political affiliations.

Name: Les Graoulliennes
Description:
- Les Graoulliennes - Team 57 Trek'in Gazelles
Du 10 au 15 novembre 2022, nous allons participer au Trek'in Gazelles au Maroc! Un trek 100% féminin, 100% solidaire et 100% responsable pour 4 étapes de 20km dans le désert marocain. Sans GPS, uniquement guidées par nos cartes et boussoles...

Name: The Victors
Description: The Victors is a 501 (c)(3) public charity. We are hopeful for our efforts at creating City Center Outreach Programs for Norfolk, VA, and surrounding areas, initially targeting Sports Programs for City Center Youth. Our hope is to build stronger people, stronger families, and stronger communities through a myriad of cooperating individuals and entities. Please help us if you can - Your prayers are accepted!

Name: Corpo Cidadão
Description:
Fundado no ano de 2000, o Corpo Cidadão é uma organização da sociedade civil, sem fins lucrativos, que promove o desenvolvimento de crianças, adolescentes e jovens através de oficinas de Arte Educação.

O Corpo Cidadão tem como objetivo democratizar o acesso à arte de qualidade, oferecendo oportunidades para o desenvolvimento de talentos em áreas desde as artes visuais, dança, música, audiovisual e outras carreiras diversas.

Atualmente, o Corpo Cidadão é uma associação reconhecida como de utilidade pública municipal e estadual. Atua com oficinas de arte educação, direcionadas por uma Proposta Artística Educativa (PAE) que é a força motriz e a base a partir das quais o trabalho se desenvolve.

Por meio de leis de incentivo à cultura municipais, estaduais e federais, o Corpo Cidadão sustenta seus projetos, que incluem as atividades do Grupo Experimental de Dança (GED), voltado para jovens da região de Belo Horizonte e com aulas de Balé e Dança Contemporânea, além das atividades dos Núcleos de Arte Educação (NAEs), que funcionam em Lagoa Santa e Ouro Branco, promovendo oficinas de Artes Visuais e de Dança para crianças e adolescentes.

Atualmente, os patrocinadores do Corpo Cidadão são:
CEMIG
GERDAU
Instituto Marina & Flávio Guimarães (Grupo BMG)
CODEME
Mascarenhas Barbosa Roscoe Construções

O Corpo Cidadão está sempre em busca de novos parceiros e patrocinadores que possibilitem a expansão e melhoria dos projetos. Entre em contato pelo site para mais informações ou pelo email: contato@corpocidadao.org.br

Name: From Syria With Love
Description:
UK based charity raises awareness and funds through different activities: 
•Art Exhibition: for drawings done by children living in refugee camps in Lebanon, to reflect the trauma these children have gone through and to show that art and childhood are universal language can be understood by anyone, anywhere.

•Informative talk: about Syria in general and the recent crisis and refugee crisis in particular to inform people and spread awareness as well as challenging stereotypes and misconceptions.

•Book: features different stories by different writers and stories from the children in the camp. 

•Music CD: done by Devon based artists, all money coming from selling the CD goes directly to help those in most need.

we use the raised funds from the previous activities to help people in need working on four main pillars: 
•Education,
•Empowerment, 
•Emergencies, 
•Relief.

and we do that through:
•Zero management fees/administration costs.
•Highest possible level of transparency.
•Not harming beneficiaries’ dignity.

Our activities was well received in the UK, where we raised more than £50,000 and spoke to more than 5000 people over 50 venues. And we are rapidly expanding.
We are opened to any kind of collaboration, discussions about any ideas that can help people.

Name: Effective Altruism Sydney
Description:
Most of us want to make the world a better place – but it's not always clear how.

Which charities use my donation most effectively?
How can I help the most people with my career?
How do I know that my efforts aren't going to waste?

In order to provide answers to these questions, we take a scientific approach to doing good, one that doesn't rely on gut instincts or blind faith. It is this evidence-based approach, combined with our motive to do good, that distinguishes us from other social movements and charity groups.

Our community exists to provide support and advice to anyone who wants to make a difference.

•  •  •

Join the Facebook group and page:
https://www.facebook.com/groups/effectivealtruismsydney/
https://www.facebook.com/easydney/

Learn more about effective altruism and the causes areas it supports:
https://effectivealtruism.org

Receive advice on how to lead a more interesting and rewarding career:
https://80000hours.org

Make tax-deductible donations to top-performing charities in global health and development:
https://effectivealtruism.org.au

Buy tickets to our annual Australia-wide conference, September 27–29 in Sydney:
https://eagxaustralia.com

Name: Fundacion Amen Comunicaciones-Padre Carlos Yepes
Description:
Fundación Amén Comunicaciones (Apostolado de Medios para una Evangelización Nueva) es una productora católica conformada por sacerdotes y laicos profesionales formados en el área de radio, producción audiovisual y comunicación digital.
Nace como una respuesta al llamado del magisterio de la iglesia, para anunciar el evangelio en los “areópagos modernos”, como son las Tecnologías de la Información y la Comunicación (TIC).

Name: Badges Supporting Fallen Officers’ Families (Fallen)
Description:
Fallen’s Board of Directors, all current and former law enforcement officers, recognized long ago the need for immediate financial support for the survivors of law enforcement officers killed in the line of duty throughout our nation. Our mission is to provide direct financial support within 72 hours to the surviving family member. This assistance will bridge the delay between the immediate needs of the survivors and when federal, state, local and life insurance resources are made available.

Fallen strives to involve the 900,000 law enforcement officers in the US in this effort through a $25 annual contribution as well as corporate sponsors and citizens that recognize the sacrifice law enforcement officers and their families make protecting us and our families. Donations made to Fallen are tax deductible under the provisions of the IRS Code 501(c)(3). (Tax ID 45-3752411)

Name: The Canadian Social Entrepreneurship Foundation
Description:
Founded in 2003, The Canadian Social Entrepreneurship Foundation, is a Non Profit, in Toronto, Ontario.  It is one of Canada's first organizations to educate, invest and celebrate Social Entrepreneurs in Canada.  

The Canadian Social Entrepreneurship Vision
To be a hotbed for Canadian Social Entrepreneurs. The CSEF will be a recognized Canadian leader in creating online innovative programs, and systems to develop and recognize budding Social Entrepreneurs.


The Canadian Social Entrepreneurship Mission
To develop the profession of social entrepreneurship in Canada. The CSEF’s goal is to develop Canadian social entrepreneurs so that they can contribute to the economic and social development of Canada and the world. The key components of our model provide Education, Resources, and a Network for Social Entrepreneurs.

Name: NAASC Washington DC Metro Chapter
Description:
About the Washington DC Metro Chapter
Founded in 1943 as the Washington, DC Spelman Club, the Washington DC Metro Chapter pre-dates the national alumnae association. We are a non-profit organization of the NAASC serving Washington, DC and several of the surrounding DC, Maryland and Virginia cities. Our organization seeks to:

*Support the college and its students financially through annual scholarship awards and an annual gift to the college.

*Uplift the Washington DC Metro community through service projects and activities.

*Engage in recruitment on behalf of the college while sharing our experiences with potential students. Chapter members participate in college recruitment fairs to share their Spelman experience with potential students. 

*Promote leadership, growth and development opportunities for our membership.

*Provide networking and social engagement for members locally, regionally and nationally within the Association and with other Historically Black Colleges and Universities.

Our signature event is The Loving Me Girls Conference (LMGC) established in March of 2010. Additional activities include the DC HBCU Alliance 5k Run/Walk, DMV Founders Day Celebration, New Student Send-Off and Holiday Social. Each year an alumna who has demonstrated distinguished service to the chapter is awarded the Mae Clowney Wilson Alumna of the Year Award.

Name: Party4aCause NYC
Description:
In 2006, Party4aCause was founded to serve a wide range of charities through fundraising events. Party4aCause is an organization led by a group of young, active New York City professionals dedicated to supporting the local community through social fundraising events and bringing others closer to the cause.

Join us as we lend a hand to our beloved New York City community, one party at a time.

Name: Adelaide City Rotaract Club
Description:
"Never doubt that a small group of thoughtful, committed citizens can change the world. Indeed that is the only thing that ever has" - Margaret Mead.

Rotaract is a global organisation that empowers students and young professionals to create positive change in their local communities and around the world. Rotaract provides unique opportunities that assist its members in becoming the business and professional leaders of tomorrow. We are an international network of 163,000 students and young professionals that span 163 countries worldwide.

Adelaide City Rotaract Club meets on the 2nd and 4th Monday of each month. Check our Facebook events for details or write to us for more information.

Name: 500 Women Scientists Cambridge
Description: Welcome to the 500 Women Scientists Cambridge pod! This page has been created to share information and initiatives carried out by us.

Name: Opportuneety
Description: Opportuneety helps civil society organizations, social enterprises, foundations, and impact investors design, implement, and evaluate education-to-employment pathways that provide youth who are Not in Education, Employment or Training (NEET) with career-launching work opportunities that would otherwise be inaccessible.

Name: Network of Empowered Women
Description: Non-profit organzation that focuses on issues related to domestic abuse

Name: Mindful Tribe
Description:
“It is our mission to inspire hope and contribute to long term sustained change for communities in need, so that they can rise out of poverty”

CEO & FOUNDER - Neil Parr

Whether you’re seeking awareness, enlightenment, clarity, health, or a community, you’ve found your tribe right here.

Mindful Tribe is a For Profit Organization.

Committed to give a minimum of 15% of all profit to organizations for projects that are focused on making sustained changes in some the most impoverished communities around the world. 

To help change the trajectory of generations to come. 

The idea for Mindful Tribe was born in 2016 when founder, Neil Parr, found himself at a crossroads in life. 

Initially devastated by events, and perceiving them as a negative, after some time Neil was able to rethink circumstances as an opportunity to re-direct energies, and realign with his natural desire to make the world a better place.

A TRIBE IS BORN...

Mindful Tribe is a community of passionate people focused on making a difference. 

We inspire, entertain, and empower people to use the power of mindfulness, gratitude, and yoga to change their own life and the lives of others.

Live Your Yoga | Join the Tribe | Change a Life

Name: Room To Dream Foundation
Description:
The mission of the Room to Dream Foundation is to create healing environments in hospitals, communities, and homes for children and adolescents who are facing chronic illnesses.


At a time when children are supposed to be full of energy, exploring the world, and exhausting their potential, many are coping with sickness in sterile hospital rooms or confined to their homes for weeks, months, even years.   Our focus is to make chronically-ill, inner-city or suburban children feel special and improve their quality of life by transforming their environments. It could be a hospital room, a bedroom at home or a facility for sick children and their families. Each and every situation provides a unique opportunity for us to work our magic. And to promote strength, imagination, and self-worth in every child.  


Please join us in our mission to provide chronically ill children with the best healing environment imaginable while strengthening their spirits. If you're a design professional, a general contractor, or just someone who would like to get involved and volunteer, please email us at info@roomtodreamfoundation.org.

Name: Project SCIFI
Description:
The Scientific Coat Initiative for Future Innovators promotes students’ scientific passions for higher education, provides teachers the resources necessary for hands-on scientific instruction, encourages the growth of mentor-mentee relationships, and fosters a visible community supporting young scientists’ endeavors.

We achieve these goals by partnering with corporate and academic sponsors who endorse the purchase of laboratory coats embroidered with the sponsors’ logos. We then distribute these coats to qualifying under-resourced schools, along with other materials intended to aid educators in taking on a hands-on approach to scientific instruction.

Our motivation as a non-profit organization stems from our early exposure to science, which ultimately inspired us to pursue scientific educations and careers. Thus, our goal is to empower and inspire future generations of scientists by providing K–12 students the necessary tools to develop fundamental scientific reasoning, address societal problems, and move on to further scientific instruction.

Name: Rotaract Cholet 1510
Description: Association à but non lucratif loi 1901.

Name: Bella's Buddies
Description: Bella’s Buddies will join canines trained to alert and respond to specific medical and emotional needs with organizations and individuals to increase social and behavioral proficiencies, promote physical wellness, and encourage independence in solitary and social environments. The Bella’s Buddies K9U training program will work to promote public school student interest in contributing to society and appreciating the service of veterans by utilizing the classroom setting to train future service, facility and emotional support dogs for individuals whose lives may be enriched by a canine helper.

Name: Associação Dar a Mão
Description: Associação Dar a Mão é uma rede de apoio de pais, crianças/pessoas com Agenesia de Membros, malformação congênitas ou outras deficiências, afetados por síndromes ou doenças raras, com amputações ou mobilidade física limitada. Realiza acolhimento da PcD e projetos como: palestras, pesquisa em tecnologia assistiva, produção de dispositivos protéticos feitos em impressão 3D, elaboração de cartilhas informativas, Livro de literatura infantil, campanhas de conscientização, capacitação profissional do uso de tecnologia e conscientização social e educacional.

Name: VAMO
Description:
Vetor Afro-Indígena na Moda

Diante da conscientização da real história do Brasil é inegável a dívida histórica com os povos pretos e indígenas, responsáveis diretos pela construção de boa parte do solo, da cultura e das riqueza nacionais. Junto com diversos setores da sociedade, do mercado e da indústria, a Moda perpetuou comportamento opressor e negligente por muito tempo com esses povos.

O movimento VAMO é formado por um time de profissionais interraciais e nasce para iniciar um processo reparatório na moda nacional, onde pretos e indígenas possam ter ferramentas e oportunidades anteriormente negadas pela estrutura racista do mercado.

VAMO é um chamado para o exercício de uma educação através da descolonização do pensamento eurocentrista e tem como objetivo criar espaços, métodos e ações equitativas que viabilizem, valorizem, empoderem e transformem a realidade e o pertencimento da população afro-indígena multi potencial na indústria e na sociedade.

A partir dos três pilares da sustentabilidade (ambiental, social e econômico) o movimento  estimula a criação de um novo ambiente, para além da inclusão, verdadeiramente plural e inovador, potencializando uma outra maneira de projetar o futuro.

E aí? VAMO

Name: Le Spa de la Rue
Description:
Chaque semaine, notre équipe de thérapeutes bénévoles transforment des organismes communautaires en «Spa d'un jour» pour les personnes démunies!

Massages suédois, californien, shiatsu, taï, ostéopathie, soins énergétiques et même des conseils santé sont au programme de nos cliniques solidaires.

Nos clientèles souffrent généralement de maux de dos, de douleurs articulaires, de stress et d’insomnies. Dans le cadre d’une recherche sur l’impact des massages aromatiques sur l’évolution des maladies dégénératives, certains clients font l'objet d'un suivi particulier dans le temps.

Name: NUOVA NAUTICA ITALIANA • NNI
Description:
NUOVA NAUTICA ITALIANA • NNI

NNI è l'acronimo che identifica l'organizzazione non lucrativa denominata NUOVA NAUTICA ITALIANA. 

Associazione per lo sviluppo sostenibile e la tutela delle alte professionalità della nautica, dell'industria navale e del mare.

Serbatoio di idee e movimento culturale avviato a Roma con finalità scientifiche e industriali volte particolarmente ai temi del lavoro, dell'impresa e della neo-sostenibilità per il mare.

INFO@NUOVANAUTICAITALIANA.COM

Description: Help Us Help

Name: PARIS TENU
Description: Paris Tenu est une équipe composée de huit étudiants, réunis et motivés autour d'un projet solidaire. Ils interviennent pour la cause des adolescentes défavorisées du foyer Léopold Bellan à Asnières-sur-Seine . L'équipe Paris Tenu intervient directement auprès des jeunes filles du foyer dans le but de leur faire découvrir les pays du monde à travers diverses activités.

Name: Tunisian Red Crescent, Local Committee of M'saken
Time to Save
Description: Time to Save is a foundation with a vision and a passion - a world where people of all ages are wise in the ways of money and gain value from their use of it. We provide our members with personal finance information, education, and cash back on purchases they make with our partners. The cash back is invested by our members to help them prepare and save for their future. Or, it can be donated to the charity / individual of their choice. We are actively sharing our vision of a world of financially responsible individuals.

Name: Good Mob
Description: An online platform Good Mob making it fun and easy to come together with your friends or colleagues to support the causes you care about.

Name: Care2Share Non-Profit
Description:
Our mission is to bring poverty relief to the poorest Vietnamese people in ethnic and rural villages of Vietnam & Cambodia, particularly areas that are not visited by Vietnamese expatriates, foreigners, or supported by the government.

A 100% volunteer-run 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization founded in 1999 with headquarters in Cincinnati, Ohio, we fund projects that drive economic development and build self-sufficient communities. We use your support to provide childcare support and facilities, clean water filtration systems, scholarships, medical care, economic opportunities, shelter, and the human touch.

Care2Share envisions that the poorest and most neglected people will have the power to lift themselves out of poverty and to create vital, healthy lives for themselves and their families.

Name: Bone Health Foundation
Description:
The Bone Health Foundation (formerly known as the Bone Growth Foundation) is an Australian not-for-profit organisation that raises money for education and research into bone health and musculoskeletal conditions.

Since it was founded in 1991, the Foundation has invested over $2.5 million in Australian-based research projects to improve the health and wellbeing of all those affected by musculoskeletal conditions.

Name: Safeplace, Inc.
Description: Safeplace, Inc. is one of seventeen domestic violence shelters serving victims of domestic violence throughout the state of Alabama. As the only comprehensive domestic violence program in Northwest Alabama, Safeplace serves Colbert, Franklin, Lauderdale, Lawrence, Marion, and Winston Counties.

Name: ATHENA Rapid Response Innovation Lab
Description:
Originally founded in 2016 as “TechGarden” in Alexandria, VA, ATHENA Rapid Response Innovation Lab is a 501(c)3 organization which has grown to encompass a broad range of services. We have rebranded to reflect this expanded mission. We are proud to serve both the local community and communities in need around the planet. 

EIN: 81-1653441

Our mission is to provide "tools and training at the point of need". This has included numerous projects from disaster response, to new technology development, to providing free desks to students during virtual learning. 

But… why “Athena”?
Athena is the name of the Greek goddess associated with wisdom, courage (especially in warfare), and crafting. These are also traits we value as we work to harness collective wisdom during turbulent times and direct that energy toward crafting solutions! In looking for a symbol of the work we do, Athena stood out both as a representation of our intent and as a reminder of the ability of communities to create lasting international impact.

Name: Go Girl Caruaru
Description: Somos uma ONG focada em empoderar mulheres através da educação.

Name: Indian Anti Cancer Trust
Description:
Indian Anti Cancer Trust is non proft organization that has been established by Mr. Shri Bhagwan Goel, a businessman and humanitarian.
Dr. Arun Kumar Goel (Surgical Oncology), Dr. Dinesh Singh (Radiation Oncology) and Dr. Malay Nandy (Medical Oncology) are its trustees. Dr. Arun Kumar Goel is the executive trustee.
The trust has been established with the objective of fighting the menace of cancer through all means possible within the resources of the trust.

Name: Changemakers Australia
Description:
Changemakers Australia is an independent, non-profit organisation. 

Our goal is to raise the profile of social change philanthropy in Australia, and find ways to make grant making more effective and equitable.

Take a look at our website to find out how you can be a trail blazer and make a real difference.

Name: Business For Good Foundation
Description: The Business For Good Family Foundation is the probono philanthropic arm of Good Business which is a real estate investment firm that specialized in making passive real estate investments more accessible to women and their families.

Name: PLeace Awaken
Description: PLeace Awaken is about bringing the world together through sport and solidarity with each other. We find solutions for humanitarian challenges to take a stand against human rights violations and to uplift those who can't speak for themselves. We use #golf4change. Together we can make a difference.

Name: Entraide Missionnaire Internationale - E.M.I.
Description:
Association d'entraide solidaire pour couvrir la santé des religieuses, religieux et prêtres Chrétiens, quelle que soit leur situation dans le monde lorsque ceux ci ne disposent pas d'un système de santé.
Son fonctionnement est conçu sur des valeurs chrétiennes et de solidarité.

Name: Big Brothers Big Sisters of Toronto Young Leaders
Description:
BBBST’s Young Leaders (YL) is a group of resourceful, community minded, and motivated young professionals who have an interest in engaging more with Big Brothers Big Sisters of Toronto. BBBST looks to the Young Leaders as volunteers who will use their established skill sets to take a leadership role in the development and execution of special event based fundraisers as well as creating awareness in the community about BBBST’s mission and the need for mentors and positive role models for children and youth.
 
Mission: To expand BBBST’s community presence through each member’s professional and social contacts as ambassadors for BBBST and to support the organization’s mission, focusing on three specific pillars; outreach, recruitment and fundraising.

Name: Catcalls of LDN
Description:
Founded by Farah Benis, Catcalls of LDN began as a grassroots campaign against public sexual harassment and has since grown into a vibrant Instagram community of over 25,000 followers. The mission: to educate and raise awareness about male violence against women and girls, while fostering meaningful conversations around these critical issues.

Offering workshops focused on consent, harassment, community, and online safety, particularly tailored for young people. Through this work, the aim is to empower individuals, create safer communities, and drive systemic change. Join the mission to challenge harmful behaviours and build a future where everyone feels safe and respected in public spaces.

Name: IDARF - International Drug Awareness Research Foundation
Description:
Headquartered and incorporated as a not for profit in 1999 in the state of Florida, the International Drug Awareness Research Foundation (IDARF), is a world-renowned organization on the vanguard of drug education and prevention. Committed to the research, development and distribution of highly effective health care information products, IDARF has spearheaded educational initiatives to teach children and adults about the devastating effects of drug abuse.

Forming partnerships with business leaders and activists worldwide, IDARF creates learning tools and programs to educate communities about the dangers of substance abuse. Our world famous, life-saving poster, “The Self-Destructive Use of Drugs” uses innovative and intellectual technology to promote drug awareness. 

Vetted by government experts on substance abuse, the poster is now in demand in over 120 countries. It has also received endorsements from the United Nations, American, Canadian and foreign government agencies, activists, businesses and corporations. With exclusive international copyrights, “The Self-Destructive Use of Drugs” has no viable competition and remains in high demand worldwide. 

Over the last 18 years, IDARF has continued to gain international recognition and now has a membership at the United Nations. 

Developing International Distributorships

IDARF is now issuing master of license distributorships in Canada as well as Europe and the United States. 

Many years of working around the globe, have led to contracts and establishment of numerous international governments, distributors, NGO's, dissemination programs and affiliations in Europe, Asia, Australia, South America, Africa, and of course across North America.

Total Marketing Capability

Through our legal, technical, marketing, and distribution affiliations both at home and abroad, IDARF is able to assume a full range of marketing responsibilities, such as:

-Dealing with foreign government regulations
-Modifications and adjustments, if necessary, of products or services, to meet various national requirements
-Translation services
-Establishment of proper and effective channels of distribution
-Guidance on pricing, packaging and promotion
-Product research and development
-Organizing and implementing joint ventures, licensing agreements and exclusive sales arrangements
-Establishment of foreign and domestic distributorships with exclusivity and other protective mechanisms to ensure maximum market penetration
-Full support from HQ Office to local distributors
-Full marketing sponsorship, co-logo, branding opportunities, support, and guidelines
-Full Digital Marketing and Social Media management
-Addition and support and placement of online resources, e-learning programs

Name: Michele Baratta atHome
Description:
Michele Baratta atHome Opportunity
Michele Baratta atHome is a California Jewelry company created to empower women. Our Independent Consultants own their own boutique jewelry business and sell through home parties and online.

Michele Baratta atHome is unique in the home party world, but not new to merchandising and designing. Celebrities such as Felicity Huffman, Kathie Lee Gifford, Taylor Swift, Rascal Flatts, Kelly Pickler and fashion magazines love Michele’s jewelry. She has sold wholesale to Nordstrom, Jjill, Sundance Catalog, Marshall Fields, Land of Nod, Macy’s and thousands of boutiques around the country for 15 years prior to opening the Michele Baratta atHome direct selling company.

Unlike other jewelry companies we don’t purchase our jewelry from different vendors in other countries and then resell it. Our jewelry is "MADE IN AMERICA". Michele actually designs every piece.

“If you wear it, you’ve created an opportunity.” You will be stopped on the street by people asking, “Where did you get that Photo Jewelry?” Guaranteed! But, that's only 20% of our line. We have over 240 beautiful pieces to choose from in our 84-page catalog.

Earn even more by building a team, creating a residual income and being an entrepreneur! Isn’t it time to be your own CEO?

Ground Floor Opportunity
Come see what it really means to be a "Ground Floor Opportunity!" As a young company, not saturated in the market, we are “wide open” for growth for anyone. “No Experience needed!” We have an extensive back office- knowledge center and weekly training. Our philosophy is “if you succeed, we succeed.” 

Seasoned Direct Sellers
We are currently looking for those that really know how to build a business and want to do it with a passion. We need leaders for all of our inquiries! We value those that have experience, and we offer incentives for just that reason! Ask us about our “Business Builder” positions.

Name: Photo Patch Foundation
Description:
About
PhotoPatch Foundation helps kids communicate with their incarcerated parent. Kids upload photos to our site:We Print, Package and Ship the photos for them.

Mission
The Photo Patch Foundation strives to help build-up a solid social support system for children who have an incarcerated parent. This support system will help the child flourish in school, build leadership skills, de-stigmatize having an incarcerated parent, help the child communicate with their incarcerated parent and much more so that the child has a bright childhood and a brighter future.

Name: The Thomas M. Brennan Memorial Foundation
Description: We are a non-profit established in memory of Tom "Beezo" Brennan, a victim of the 9/11/01 terrorist attacks.  The TMBMF's primary mission is to fund scholarships at Tom's alma mater, Boston College.

Name: Fundación Animalia
Description:
Fundación chilena sin fines de lucro dedicada al rescate, rehabilitación y adopción de animales en riesgo.

Mediante una red integrada de voluntarios, socios y amigos, poder rescatar, rehabilitar física y psicológicamente a animales en riesgo para posteriormente, darles la oportunidad de tener un hogar.

Name: Red Barnet Middelfart
Description:
Red Barnets lokalforening i Middelfart er en aktiv forening med en skøn blanding af vidt forskellige frivillige. Vi har mange planer og arbejder på at realisere dem alle. Vi brænder for at hjælpe udsatte børn både i Middelfart kommune og globalt. 

Vi skaber gode oplevelser og fællesskaber sammen med børn og deres forældre. Og børnene får gode fortællinger, som de kan dele med deres kammerater i børnehaven og i skolen. 

Hvis du har lyst til at være med til at skabe bedre trivsel for børn, så bliv en del af Red Barnet – Middelfart lokalafdeling.  

Kontakt middelfart@redbarnet.dk for mere information. 

Vi har altid plads til flere frivillige!

Alle donationer af pengegaver og naturalier går til projekter, der på den ene eller anden måde er med til at give børn et bedre og mere indholdsrigt liv.

Name: Robert Schalkenbach Foundation
Description:
ROBERT SCHALKENBACH FOUNDATION (RSF) was organized in 1925 as an operating foundation to promote public awareness of the social philosophy and economic reforms advocated by Henry George (1839-1897), including the “single tax on land values”. To this end, RSF publishes and distributes books and scholarly articles, particularly those of Henry George, including his best-selling original classic work Progress and Poverty.


HENRY GEORGE began with the ethical premise that all people have an equal right to the use of the earth. From that he concluded that exclusive private ownership of land (natural resources) creates unwarranted special privileges. Furthermore, he observed that holding land out of production drives down real wages and the returns to capital equipment. This process is further exacerbated by taxes on production and income that increase unemployment, discourage productive investment, and encourage unproductive land speculation and rent-seeking.


To counteract this self-destructive system, George advocated shifting taxes from labor and capital onto the value of land and natural resources. Today, as a growing population confronts the need to fairly, peacefully, and efficiently allocate access to the gifts of nature, the ideas of Henry George have gained a new relevance.

Name: Swedish Rotary Service
Description:
SRS is a MDAG (Multi District Administration Group) of the ten Rotary International Districts 2320-2410.
It's organized as an association and is governed by the 10 current District Governors (DG's).

The Board consists of the DG's. The IT Board consists of the District IT Coordinators. All representing their respective district.

The foremost duties of SRS is to enable cooperation between the districts and to facilitate common IT, and PR-services for all districts and clubs.

Rotary International is the worlds first Service Organization founded by Paul Harris in Chicago back in 1905.
Our motto is "Service Above Self" and we strive to improve lives of those in need.
The largest project that we have done world wide so far is what we call Polio Plus or the Polio Eradication Initiative started by Rotary in the eighties. Until today (April 2013) we have together with other organizations, like WHO and CDC (of USA) eradicated polio in 99% of the world! It's now just three Countries left; Afghanistan, Pakistan and Nigeria.

To learn more about Rotary Clubs and Rotary International, please see www.rotary.org and www.rotary.se.

Name: HearAid Foundation
Description:
Imagine your world without hearing. Millions of Americans suffer from hearing loss. Most cases can be treated with easy-to-access solutions like hearing aids, but unfortunately, not every American can afford the devices and services they need to hear their world

HearAid Foundation started out with a mission to provide early access to hearing aids and devices for infants diagnosed with hearing loss. When parents of these children cannot themselves afford hearing aids, public assistance can take up to six months to provide them. Six months of hearing loss during critical developmental years can dramatically affect a child’s ability to acquire speech and language skills, which can have a lifelong impact. 

But hearing is critical for every stage of life, and our mission quickly expanded to help people of all ages get the help they need to hear. We believe that in the technologically advanced world in which we live, no one who can be helped to hear should go without that help.

Name: AJX Foundation - A 501c3 Charity
Description:
The Mission of the AJX Foundation is to help young people learn, succeed and lead through opportunities forged by an alliance of people passionate about the next generation.

The AJX FOUNDATION is a community foundation supported by local business professionals throughout the North Texas region. The Foundation support comes from local business leaders who have donated their time and services to assist in developing tomorrow’s future.

“Leadership and guidance supporting our next generation.”

Name: Texas Architectural Foundation
Description:
TAF MISSION
The purpose of the Texas Architectural Foundation is to inspire and build leadership to enhance and protect Texas’ unique culture and environment for the benefit of future generations.

TAF CORE VALUES
-The way we plan, design and build our communities determines how we interact as a society.  Quality design positively influences our behavior, perceptions, values and emotional well-being. 
-Education is essential to protect and enhance the vitality of the communities, open spaces and natural resources of Texas.
-Enduring architecture and civic places are critical to the traditions and culture of our Texas community.
-Development patterns and building design affect the consumption of resources.  Design and planning are the critical keys to effective stewardship of the environment and natural resources.

TAF VISION
Through education, community initiatives and environmental advocacy programs, TAF will create an understanding of how architecture and planning impact our daily lives, natural environment and cultural legacy. As a result, more Texans will have the knowledge, skill and desire to create a livable future.

Name: Caring Hearts USA
Description:
About

Caring Hearts USA is committed to helping families under financial distress due to devastating costs of reconstructive joints.

Mission

Our initial mission is to sell 600,000 shirts before November 1, 2013. We will use these funds to help save our veteran’s home from foreclosure and pay high-cost medical expenses as we help other patients in similar situations. Caring Hearts USA will expand its merchandise line in the near future and work to become a nonprofit ministry. 

“Look! The wages you failed to pay the workmen who mowed your fields are crying out against you. The cries of the harvesters have reached the ears of the Lord Almighty.” James 5:4

Description

We will raise funds for families under financial distress due to catastrophic medical expenses from failed metal-on-metal implants. Please sign our petition for a National Joint Replacement Registry located on our blog: www.caringheartsusa.com

General Information

Caring Hearts USA started in Waupaca, Wisconsin in 2010. Our company began in efforts to help raise funds for a disabled veteran’s high cost medical expenses and to save the family home from foreclosure. Our veteran became disabled at 47 years of age and yet we continue to fight for benefits from an alleged service-connected disability. Now after nine surgeries, our veteran faces home foreclosure and cannot afford to pay for another needed major reconstructive surgery.

Name: Better Communication Forum
Description:
The Better Communication Forum (BCF) is a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization whose objective is to teach non-profit marketing strategies and communication tips. The mission of the BCF is to reduce the economic and human cost of poor, inaccurate and misdirected communication.The BCF works with non-profits to develop communications strategies and outreach programs to disseminate tools and resources through webinars, newsletters, website postings, and case studies.  

The future success of non-profits depends on how effectively they communicate electronically through social media, email, online advertising, blog articles, and more. The BCF teaches non-profits how to effectively use these mediums to reach donors, supporters, event attendees, and volunteers.

The BCF is a voice for non-profit communication best practices and works to help non-profits reach more people through better outreach techniques.

Name: The Giving Fund
Description:
The Giving Fund (“TGF”) was established in 2014 by Lindsay and Alex Davis. The Davis’ wanted to be able to effect individuals and see the results of their contributions in real-time.  The problem with so many organizations in the United States are that you have administrative costs, salaries, expenses, all that may make up of over 90% of the actual gift, making the amount of the donation nearly insignificant.  The Davis’ feel TGF will help raise awareness for more local, centralized problems that others can contribute to in addition to the funds received by TGF in a more grassroots type effort. 

The mission of TGF is to simply change lives, this doesn’t mean money will be the route cause of that change, but if a significant amount of money is helpful for an individual, for example, having to face a life-changing surgery that they wouldn’t otherwise be able to afford, then TGF has accomplished its goal for the week.

At the end of a full year, www.thegivingfund.org will have posted (on a weekly basis), the stories that moved us and made us want to make 52 $10,000 weekly contributions.  The “Success Stories” page will feature the videos we choose, and will hopefully help in spreading the awareness for causes that touched us the most.

Finally, TGF is a unique way of helping.  It is not meant to be taken advantage or thought of for anything other than incredibly life changing circumstances.  We thank you all ahead of time for your curiosity and appreciation for what we are doing.  TGF will make incredibly challenging decisions as there are so many problems in the world that could use a financial boost, and only one chosen cause per week.  We would love nothing more than to change the way others think about giving, we certainly have, and we are incredibly grateful to be able to help in this fashion.
