Name: AMURTEL Haiti
Description:
The mission of AMURTEL Haiti is to provide immediate and long-term support to Haiti’s most vulnerable women and children. Grounded in the philosophy and practice of yoga, AMURTEL strives to empower communities in the pursuit of economic sustainability, as well as social and spiritual fulfillment.

For over 20 years, AMURTEL has been a leader in the grassroots movement for equality and empowerment in Haiti. In partnership with local leaders, AMURTEL has provided a home for children in need, education and health services, literacy programs, reforestation initiatives, micro finance and communications programs, as well as women’s leadership, yoga, and human rights trainings. In its two decades of collaboration in Haiti, AMURTEL has become deeply connected to the communities it serves, holding respect for local language, religion and custom at the heart of its work.   

After the Earthquake of 2010, AMURTEL managed the provision of supplies to a major area of Port au Prince called Vallee Bourdon. Since 2010, the organization’s focus has shifted from disaster relief to efforts towards long-term partnership, training, and empowerment.

Name: Cuba Foundation
Description:
The Cuba Foundation inspires, promotes and facilitates philanthropic engagement that directly benefits the Cuban people.  We do this by mobilizing financial, intellectual and human capital to advance purposeful and impactful development in Cuba.  We are neither political nor ideological; we are philanthropic with a strong bias in favor of private sector solutions to help address social and economic challenges and opportunities.  The Cuba Foundation is focused on the following four financing and operating clusters: 
o -- Entrepreneurship and Innovation
o -- Sports and Development
o -- Educational Exchanges
o -- Natural and Cultural Heritage

Name: History in Your Hands Foundation
Description:
The History in Your Hands Foundation (HIYHF) is a not-for-profit organization that provides educators with historical objects to support and enhance the study of U.S. and World History. Our goal is to provide students the opportunity for hands-on interaction with historical objects thereby promoting interest and enjoyment in history via an enriched learning experience. HIYHF will work in cooperation with educational institutions to provide programs that bring history into student's hands to help promote a deeper understanding of the world.  

We believe teachers have one of the hardest jobs anyone can do and each teacher knows their students best.  Therefore HIYHF is here to help add to the learning experience and will not take over the classroom but will merely work with each teacher to bring exciting activitities using historical objects into their classrooms. Bring Egypt, Rome or Greece to life or study coins from 7th century B.C. to modern age as well as historical maps from centuries past.  Every one of the pieces we can bring have a story to tell.  Contact us today to find out how we can bring history to your classroom.

Name: RAISE LIFE FOUNDATION
Description:
RAISE LIFE Foundation is an indigenous Ghanaian Non-governmental organisation to empower deprived children in Ghana. We believe strongly in sustainable development goals and strive to help and raise the dreams of deprived children in various communities across the country. Raise life foundation takes a holistic approach to sustainable development, consulting and collaborating with communities and schools to meet their needs and goals; ensure that projects will bring the most opportunity and success to children and empower the communities across the nation.
The mission of raise life foundation is to transform and shape the lives of deprived children by giving special importance to their social-emotional and psychological framework. The foundation seeks to promote the core values of discipline, respect, compassion, love and integrity. The vision of this foundation is to become an all-encompassing stronghold for the deprived and ensure child development and education.

Name: Creative Treaty
Description:
Creative Treaty is a charitable organisation that raises public understanding of human rights through creative engagement and skill building.

We deliver imaginative and innovative programmes that enhance individual empowerment and encourage community understanding of Human Rights framework. We also provide material, resources, training sessions and mediation services to people, charities and private organisations - with our range of programmes developed under guidance from the United Nations Office of the High Commissioner of Human Rights.

We humanise Human Rights and seek to make the laws and legislation surrounding them easier to understand.

Name: Laguna Beach Community Foundation
Description: The Laguna Beach Community Foundation strengthens our community by encouraging philanthropy. We provide expertise and resources to assist local charities, connect donor passions with nonprofit needs, and work with local professional advisors in assisting their clients in giving now and beyond their lifetimes with a legacy gift.

Name: International Guild of Visual Peacemakers
Description:
The International Guild of Visual Peacemakers (IGVP) are visual communicators devoted to peacemaking and breaking down stereotypes by displaying the beauty and dignity of various cultures around the world. We are not interested in glossing over conflict, tragedy, or injustice. However, we believe that displaying the beauty and dignity of people is a more creative and redemptive pursuit than “shock value.”

As humans we often fall into the trap of making enemies with, stereotyping, and fighting the “other.” More recently, this has been fueled by the growing availability of technology, especially photography and videography. There is a void regarding the ethics of images due to their subjective nature and this opens the door for photographers to exploit people’s desire to confirm their thoughts about the “other.”

Since 9/11, conflicts between Muslim cultures and western cultures have been growing in intensity, deepening misunderstandings and stereotypes that are producing widespread fear and anger.

IGVP is a membership-based network of visual communicators and staff united around one purpose: Visual Peacemaking. The Guild aims to build bridges of peace across ethnic, cultural, and religious boundaries through visual communication that is both accountable to an ethical standard and created by those who authentically care about people and who value our common humanity.

Name: Duck Team 6 Street Dog Rescue
Description: The mission of Duck Team 6 is to reduce the street dog population through Capture, Placement, and Outreach. We are a 501(c) (3) non- profit organization that rescues dogs from the streets of Dallas. We are an all-volunteer group that captures hard-to-catch dogs that have lived their lives as strays. We are not a shelter so we rely on fosters and other rescue organizations to assist us until we can find permanent homes for the dogs we capture.  We also provide full veterinary care including spay/neuter, microchips and vaccinations for our “Duck Dogs.”

Name: AMP Foundation India
Description: At AMP Group, we aspire to be at the forefront of innovation and growth in a fast developing India. Central to our philosophy is our commitment to enhance the quality of life of those around us, by empowering them with the education they need to be a part of India's growth trajectory and succeed in the competitive world we live in today. Grounded in these principles, the AMP Foundation aims to uphold and skillfully execute our responsibility to add value to our community at large.

Name: BWIE
Description: We at Brazilian Women in Entertainment – BWIE are always partnering and co-hosting events throughout the year with organizations that support women’s initiatives in the entertainment business.

Name: Meals on Wheels in Greater New Brunswick
Description: Meals on Wheels in Greater New Brunswick enriches the lives of seniors and assists them in maintaining independence by providing nutritious food, human connections and social services support.

Name: Kynder App
Description: Pledge a % of your net monthly spend to causes you care most about. Kynder is an innovate new fintech fundraising platform for charities and good causes. It works by donating a chosen percentage of the users monthly expenditure, not a nominated fixed sum. This way the donation flexes to your means and is never more than you can afford. Available on Apple or Android.

Name: 心路基金會
Description: 心路以智能障礙、自閉症、唐氏症、腦性麻痺或其他伴隨有智能障礙之多重障礙者為服務對象。以讓「智能及發展障礙者擁有美好的希望、尊嚴的生活與豐富的生命」做為持續向前邁進的目標，心路期待能透過全方位的服務，為智能及發展障礙者的生命創造最大可能。
Location: 臺北市, 中山區(Zhongshan District)

Name: La Dauphine
Description:
Depuis plus de 29 ans, La Dauphine a pour mission de contribuer à prévenir une détérioration de la situation des jeunes en difficulté, garçons et filles, âgés de 12 à 35 ans, et à les accompagner dans leur cheminement vers leur pleine autonomie.

Elle offre aux jeunes la possibilité de reconstituer un milieu de vie proche de leur vécu et teinté de leurs aspirations, mais surtout qui les accepte pour ce qu’ils sont. Elle leur offre un environnement sécurisant et apaisant, afin qu’ils puissent se questionner, réfléchir et prendre le recul nécessaire par rapport à ce qu’ils vivent, le but étant de susciter leur engagement dans la résolution de leurs problèmes.

Des services essentiels et d’appoint sont offerts aux jeunes tout en leur assurant une relation d’aide personnalisée et un accompagnement dans la réhabilitation à l’emploi ou le retour aux études. En plus d’offrir des services de première nécessité : nourriture, hygiène, soins de santé, soutien juridique et autres, La Dauphine permet aux jeunes qui le désirent de faire une remise en action par l’entremise de différents programmes dont le Service spécialisé Jeunes et l’École de la Rue. Elle accompagne également une trentaine de jeunes familles.

Le jeune qui se présente à La Dauphine peut recevoir une panoplie de services, selon ses besoins, dans une formule « Clé en main ».

Au fil des années, elle a su gagner le cœur de la communauté en tissant des liens autant avec ses partenaires qu’avec les jeunes de la rue et la population en général qui lui témoignent en retour reconnaissance et confiance.

Facebook : https://www.facebook.com/ladauphine.org

Name: DIMSON FOUNDATION
Description: Dimson FOUNDATION is a non-profit, that funds and empowers women in the sub-saharian african countries. We provide general operating support grants, coaching and training to enable women and girls to reach their potential and live free of discrimination and violence.

Name: Pembroke Rotary Club Bermuda
Description:
Pembroke Rotary Club is one of 4 Rotary Clubs here on the beautiful island of Bermuda. Bermuda is part of District 7230 which also covers New York City, Bronx, Staten Island, and Westchester County. 

Together we provide service to others, promote integrity, and advance world understanding, goodwill, and peace through our fellowship of business, professional, and community leaders.

We meet virtually every other Thursday Morning via zoom at 8. Pre-COVID, we host breakfast meetings at the Royal Bermuda Yacht Club on Thursdays at 7:30.

Name: REA - Résilience Enfants d'Asie
Description:
REA - Résilience Enfants d’Asie est une association française à but non lucratif, créée en 2006, dont la vocation est d’apporter aux ONG implantées en Asie un complément de compétences dans l’accompagnement psychologique et psychosomatique des enfants ayant subi des traumatismes graves.
Sa structure comprend 25 bénévoles parmi lesquels pédopsychiatres et psychocliniciens. 

Vous souhaitez soutenir Résilience Enfants d'Asie en devenant bénévole ? Contactez benevolat@resilienceenfantsdasie.org 

Pour en savoir plus : http://www.resilienceenfantsdasie.org

Name: Doa Brasil
Description:
O Doa Brasil é uma organização social sem fins lucrativos ou econômicos que tem como propósito tornar a prática da doação um ato simples e comum na vida dos brasileiros e brasileiras.  

Nossa missão é fazer com que o maior número de organizações sociais no Brasil sejam capazes de receber e gerenciar doações financeiras com facilidade, agilidade, segurança e sem custos. 

Temos toda tecnologia e inteligência necessária para ferramentar e capacitar gestores sociais e captadores de recursos de forma remota. 

Criamos um programa de aceleração on-line e uma sistema digital de campanhas atrelados a um modelo de benefícios para impulsionar organizações sociais que atuam com diferentes tipos de causas.

Name: Lions Club Marne-la-Vallée - Noisy-le-Grand
Description:
« On ne peut aller bien loin dans la vie si l'on ne commence pas par faire quelque chose pour quelqu'un d'autre », devise de Melvin Jones, créateur du lions club international devenue 1re ONG mondiale et dont le nom – LIONS - résume son engagement : Liberty Intelligence Our Nation’s Safety (Liberté et compréhension sont la sauvegarde de nos nations).

Le Lions Clubs International est une fédération d’associations, dits clubs services. Sa force tient en un seul mot : l’action et en une seule devise : le service, sa devis : « Nous servons ! ».

Les Lions sont des hommes et des femmes libres qui ont décidé d’ajouter à leur vie une dimension humaniste et humanitaire. Leur soutien vers les plus démunis est à l’origine même du projet du Lions Clubs International. Si les Lions œuvrent au quotidien au service des autres sur le plan humanitaire, ils développent également de nombreuses opérations dans le domaine du savoir, de l’éducation et de la culture.

Name: Luv Mrk
Description:
Luv Mrk (love mark) is a socially conscious movement that provides self-awareness programs and kindness campaigns to promote treating ourselves and others with respect, kindness, peace and Luv. This movement is about encouraging each of us to make a positive impact with our words and actions so that we leave our mark with Luv in our personal lives, in our cites, this nation, and around the world. 

Interactive Workshops: Impacting Perceptions is a transformative workshop consisting of brain teaser exercises, illusions, videos and activities used to explore our personal views and how these interpretations affect self and relationships with others. Discover how outside influences from our experiences and environment to the media shapes our perceptions which in turn inspire our choices and actions. When we identify the beneficial and/or destructive impact our beliefs have on our lives we can then consciously choose to evolve our approach to the way we treat ourselves and those around us in order to cultivate the peace and Luv we seek in the world.

Kindness Campaigns: We ask you and fellow kindhearted individuals to participate in activities uplifting others. This is another opportunity to leave your Luv Mrk in the world! 

Merchandise: T-Shirts represent an outer expression of being the Luv you seek in the world in order to leave your Luv Mrk. Giving the shirts as a gift is best!  Let people know you see them as making a positive difference in the world!

Name: Friends of Angels Germany e.V.
Description: With social projects in the field of health care and therapy, we improve access to and the quality of professional medical care and support for children and young people with disabilities and serious illnesses in Latin America, thus increasing their quality of life and chances of living in a society based on solidarity.

Name: Red Shoe Society of the Triangle
Description:
The mission of the Red Shoe Society of the Triangle is to support the Ronald McDonald Houses of Chapel Hill and Durham & Wake, through philanthropic and leadership endeavors, to support their work of providing a “home-away-from-home for the families of seriously ill or injured children.

Our vision is to be a leading community organization to engage, empower and inspire young professionals and the young at heart, to support and build awareness of the Ronald McDonald Houses in Durham, Raleigh, and Chapel Hill.

To accomplish our mission and advance our vision:

We SERVE as ambassadors for the Ronald McDonald Houses of Chapel Hill and Durham & Wake in the statewide community. 

We PROVIDE social and professional networking opportunities for members. 

We SUPPORT the House in fundraising endeavors. 

We CREATE meaningful volunteer opportunities

Name: J.Allen Lamb & Edward S. Pocock III Foundation
Description: 501(c)(3) Foundation dedicated to police marksmanship, especially in the area of rifle marksmanship; accredited higher education police command training, as exampled by the Southern Police Institute; accredited higher education justice administration/criminal justice scholarships, as exampled by the University of Louisville; vocational training scholarships; and other educational scholarships as determined by the Foundation. The Foundation adheres to a strict Code of Ethics.  EIN: 46-3644133  (Determination letter received 8.8.2014)

Name: Cuatro Por Venezuela
Description: We're a 501(C)(3) nonprofit organization based in Houston, Texas. This initiative was born on October of 2016 from the effort of four Venezuelan women living in the United States compelled to action to help their country. The Foundation was formally established by Gloria Mattiuzzi, Gabriela Rondón, Maria Elena Texeira, and Carolina Febres on December 14th of 2016, and it is dedicated to creating programs and partnerships to deliver health, nutritional, and educational relief to any corner of the national territory of Venezuela.

Name: FreeWriters
Description:
FreeWriters provides inmates in the county jail system with creative writing opportunities that improve mental health and reduce recidivism.

FreeWriters provides our fellow citizens behind bars a much-needed chance for creative expression and emotional release — and inspiration for a future free of incarceration.

Sign up for the FreeWriters monthly newsletter at https://freewriters.org/.

Name: TU MISMO ERES
Description:
TU MISMO ERES es una organización que busca gestionar el talento especial de nuestra comunidad (personas con discapacidad) y generar nuevas alternativas para ellos mediante los siguientes tres pilares:

EMPLEABILIDAD – Diseñando intervenciones y generando cercanías que permitan a nuestra comunidad participar de procesos de selección y capacitación de manera exitosa buscando el desarrollo de todo su potencial y así ser contratados en empresas privadas, organismos públicos y diversos interesados. 

EMPRENDIMIENTO – Optimizando y asesorando a nuestra comunidad en el desarrollo de ideas de negocio sostenible que puedan ser apoyadas por las empresas privadas, organismos públicos y diversos interesados buscando generarles una fuente de ingresos importantes para ellos y sus familias.

TECNOLOGÍA – Identificando, analizando e implementando nuevas formas de hacer las cosas en beneficio de nuestra comunidad buscando que la tecnología además de un mecanismo de desarrollo se convierta en un puente para inclusión.

Nuestro sueño es convertir al Perú en el país reconocido a nivel mundial como el lugar donde mejor se atienda, gestiona y desarrolla al talento especial, y para lograrlo necesitamos tu ayuda.

Porque para mejorar las oportunidades y el futuro de nuestro comunidad, TU MISMO ERES.

Name: FieldsOfAmber.Org (on Facebook)
Description:
FieldsOfAmber.Org promotes safety education for children 
& develops support for law enforcement "Cold Case" units
nationwide.

A Wild Flowers Living Memorial
for our Taken, Missing and Lost Loved Ones

On Facebook: "FieldsOfAmber.org"
Email: BarbaraBizInc@aol.com
PO Box 1242
Fallbrook, CA U.S.A. 92088

Name: The Norman Foundation
Description:
The Norman Foundation is a family foundation headquartered in New York City. We support efforts that strengthen the ability of communities to determine their own economic, environmental, and social well-being, and that help people control those forces that affect their lives.  

The Norman Foundation funds organizations that promote economic justice and development through community organizing, coalition building and policy reform efforts. In addition, we support organizations that work to prevent the disposal of toxics in communities as well as support efforts that link community-based economic and environmental justice organizing to national and international reform efforts.

Name: الباحث التنموي
Description:
الباحث التنموي، صفحة مهتم بالمؤسسات التنموية والمسؤولية الاجتماعية للشركات وريادة الأعمال الاجتماعية.
وسوف يتم تقديم محتوي تدريبي ومحتوى مرئي ومحتوى مقروء من أجل خدمة هذه الأهداف.

Name: Club Rotary Québec - La Cité
Description:
Le Club Québec La Cité supporte de nombreux organismes des quartiers Vanier, St-Roch, St-Sauveur et Limoilou ainsi que des causes internationales comme l’éradication de la poliomyélite, l’éducation, l’accès à l’eau et plusieurs autres.
Le Rotary International est une association qui rassemble au-delà de 35.000 clubs présents dans près de 200 pays et régions géographiques.

Name: Hill Cities
Description:
Our Vision
Our Vision is to be a community of spirit-filled leaders compassionately engaged with God's mission in the workplace.

Our President
Jeff Hagen has served in marketplace ministry since 1984. Before that, he was a Special Representative to the President of Campus Crusade for 8 years. Jeff was a member of the University of Minnesota Baseball team that won a number of Big Ten Conference Championships and was a teammate of Hall of Famers Dave Winfield and Paul Molitor. He earned a degree in education from the University of Minnesota and an M.Div. from Bethel Seminary.

Our Board of Directors
Ric Escobar is a former Division 1 athlete who is now the director of Latin American Missions for One Challenge, overseeing missionaries and training pastors to plant churches. One Challenge International

Name: Open Hands Food Pantry
Description:
Hunger is real in Oakland County.  The Open Hands Food Pantry was started in a small closet to provide food for neighbors in need.  Today it serves 14,000 annually with food and toiletries.  Open Hands distributes food twice a week to Oakland County residents.  

Open Hands is a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization and has expanded to include the Open Hands Garden to provide fresh produce to food pantry clients.

Name: Good Samaritans of Silicon Valley
Description: Good Samaritans of Silicon Valley is a nonprofit organization run by Bay Area natives to support their neighbors who don't have healthy food to eat or essential items needed to live a healthy life. To bring its mission to life, GSSV asks for the support of Silicon Valley and the world. With food, monetary, cloth, and hygiene product donations, the GSSV team packages and distributes a wide range of nutritious food and everyday items to marginalized members of the San Jose community. If you are able, please join in the effort. To support the mission financially, please visit the GoFundMe linked to the website. Silicon Valley appreciates what you can do.

Name: Togantine
Description:
Togantine est un projet né en 2020 visant à mettre en place une cantine autonome et durable pour l’école primaire publique d’Avévé au Togo. Les enjeux de ce projet sont de lutter contre la malnutrition et la déscolarisation des enfants sur place. La cantine proposera des repas gratuits et équilibrés aux élèves de l’école, établis en collaboration avec une nutritionniste.  

Avec l’aide de nos partenaires, le Comité Villageois de Développement d’Avévé et l’ONG française Ecoles et Amitié, nous sommes en train d’aménager un potager pour permettre à la cantine de produire en grande partie ses propres aliments. A cela viendra s’ajouter la construction d’un poulailler et d’un local de stockage ainsi que la formation du comité de gestion de la cantine permettant d’assurer la pérennité du projet.

Name: Care & Share
Description: CLB Thiện nguyện C&S - We Care We Share (Care&Share)

Name: РОО "Семейные Ценности"
Description:
Консолидируя усилия государственных учреждений, предпринимательского сообщества, общественных и образовательных организаций – мы создаем условия для оказания адресной финансовой помощи семьям, пострадавшим от неблагоприятных обстоятельств.

Создавая социально ориентированные программы, проекты и благотворительные акции, нацеленные на финансовую поддержку семьи – реализуем из внебюджетных источников.

Name: Golden & District Community Foundation
Description:
Mission
Our mission is to attract and effectively grow permanent funds; provide leadership and administration that helps in addressing significant community needs; and help donors fulfill their philanthropic interests.

History
The Golden and District Community Foundation began as a steering committee in March, 2001, was incorporated in BC as a non-profit Society in June, 2001, and then became a registered charity in December, 2001. 

Purpose
To accumulate charitable donations in a permanent endowment fund to provide residents with an enduring means to help shape our community’s future. 
To allocate income from these donations to support a wide range of initiatives which build and strengthen our community for years to come.

Name: UniraidUnilasalle
Description:
L’association UNIRAID est une association étudiante de solidarité internationale fondée en 1997 qui s’engage à lutter contre la pauvreté et pour la protection de l’enfance en s’associant à différents partenaires dans les pays en développement. Elle est formée d’une vingtaine d’élèves ingénieurs issus des spécialités Sciences de la Terre et Environnement, Agronomie et Agro-industrie et Alimentation et Santé à UniLaSalle, campus de Beauvais. 

L’association est non lucrative et d’intérêt général. Elle a pour but la levée de fond pour le soutien de projet de solidarité internationale. 

Site internet : https://uniraidasso.wixsite.com/monsite?fbclid=IwAR2XV0oYZoX1Yw6rHhknsJJ-EFcN9dWUUqVds55v9PehkAGChD10hQWGkbI

Lien de notre page Facebook : https://www.facebook.com/pg/Uniraid-326750197414979/about/?ref=page_internal

Lien de notre page instagram : https://www.instagram.com/uniraid_unilasalle/


Lien de notre Cagnotte: Cagnotte :
https://www.leetchi.com/c/voyage-humanitaire-au-togo-rvaabob5

Name: Investors Philanthropic
Description: Future donor advised fund coming soon! Follow us on Instagram & Twitter @joygivingseries

Name: Vidyaa-O-Samman Kendra
Description:
At VOS, we support the Government School Kids with enough teaching material, extra curricular exposure and pedagogy to try and bring them at par with the public school education system. The belief is simply that money should never define how much education you can get.
And the samman part- well, we also help the families of these kids to be abreast with the Government Schemes and also help enroll them with the relevant ones.

Name: Day for Change San Diego
Description:
There are many wonderful organizations in the County that serve abused and neglected children, but the vast majority of them are underfunded.
Funds to support crisis intervention, preparation for emancipation, foster-care placement, mentoring programs, and access to mental health services are desperately needed to make a difference in the lives of these children.

Day for Change is a group of Financial Executives dedicating their efforts to help fund organizations that make a difference in the lives of abused & neglected children in San Diego County.

By unifying our efforts, we can all make a difference 

100% of all funds raised will be awarded to local charities that focus on making a difference in the lives of abused & neglected children.
Day for Change is a 501(c)(3) public charity, nonprofit organization founded by leading financial management professionals across San Diego County.

Name: United Donors Foundation
Description:
The United Donors Foundation is a non-profit society which is incorporated under BCRegistries. 

Our mission is to promote organ donation in racially and ethnically diverse British Columbian communities. We believe that through raising awareness about the disproportion of donors to recipients with matching blood types, will increase the amount of available organs for these communities.

A critical requirement for a successful organ transplant is that both the donor and recipient share the same blood type; however, blood types are not equally distributed among all ethnic groups. Such as, approximately 10% of Caucasians have B blood type compared to 25% of Asians or about 33% in South East Asians. Therefore, if you are Asian or South East Asian you will be on the transplant waiting list for longer simply because of unequal distribution. 

Caucasians made up 89% of donors and 69% of recipients, while East Asians (Chinese, Japanese, Korean) made up 4% of donors and 12% of recipients, South Asians (Indians, Pakistanis) made up 1% of donors and 8% of recipients, we aim to increase the amount of registered donors in order to optimize the process of receiving an organ when in need.

Name: The Nawien Foundation
Description:
Woorden schieten tekort als we het hebben over het verdriet en leed van het Afghaanse volk. Decennia lang kent Afghanistan oorlog, armoede en onderdrukking. Er zijn duizenden mensen vermoord, miljoenen voor het leven getekend en nog eens miljoenen op de vlucht gejaagd. Nog steeds leven veel Afghanen in zware, vaak onmenselijke omstandigheden en zonder enig perspectief op een betere en veiligere omgeving. Angst regeert in een land dat nauwelijks veiligheid voor haar inwoners kent. In bijna het hele land ontbreken basisbehoeften.

Stichting Nawien streeft ernaar om deze barre omstandigheden te verbeteren en mensen een beter perspectief te bieden in Afghanistan. De stichting ondersteunt projecten op het gebied van onderwijs, gezondheidszorg, huisvesting en werkgelegenheid. Wij stimuleren, geven impulsen en leggen een basis, zodat de hulpbehoevenden vanuit daar een eigen toekomst bouwen. Collectief kunnen wij meer bereiken en meer betekenen voor elkaar. Daarom hebben wij uw hulp nodig. Met slechts 4 euro per maand kunt u bijdragen aan een beter leven voor een kleine groep mensen in Afghanistan.

Name: Nourishing Networks
Description:
We work with local communities to solve local problems; leveraging the assets of existing government agencies, other nonprofits, and individuals within the community. 

Currently we have networks in western Washington and are expanding throughout the rest of Washington and Oregon.

Name: NeED FOUNDATION
Description:
a concrete support to fight neglected diseases, particularly tuberculosis and malaria. 
these diseases kill every year millions of people, concentrated in the poorest regions of the World, where malnutrition, low sanitary measures, wars, contribute to their diffusion.
limited drug availability, counterfeit and poor information are the main problems that need immediate solutions.
NeED Objectives are:
1-facilitate the distribution of drugs and vaccines
2-contribute to fight counterfeit
3-promote and develop scientific research

Founders are internationally known scientists who have been involved in the discovery and development of antiinfectives for the treatment of neglected diseases. 

WE NEED YOUR HELP

WWW.NEED-FOUNDATION.ORG

Name: Vietnamese Student Association at Florida State University
Description:
The Vietnamese Student Association will voice our message by tackling any social barriers that may hinder us from displaying our culture and heritage with pride, at the same time, show appreciation of the "American" in Vietnamese "American" through community service, social activities, programs, and vast networking of friends, family, and peers.

This organization was created in order to: create and stimulate awareness of the transforming Vietnamese-American cultural identity to its members and community, educate and empower our community through various programs and activities to create conscious awareness, aid in community service alongside the other affiliations of the Asian American Student Union in any way this organization is able to.

Name: Loyola University Chicago Dance Marathon
Description:
Loyola University Chicago Dance Marathon is an entirely student-run philanthropy that benefits Ann & Robert H. Lurie Children's Hospital.  We devote our entire year to planning fundraising events in an effort to support the patients and families at the hospital.  

Our work culminates in an 8-hour Dance Marathon that brings together hundreds of students willing to dance and fundraise to honor the sick children who inspire us everyday.  We strive to surpass our previous accomplishments and to continue expanding Loyola University Chicago Dance Marathon every day.

Name: Chicken Nugget and Gang, Inc.
Description: Chicken Nugget and Gang is a non-profit animal rescue and sanctuary. Our goal is not only to help and save the animals at our rescue, but we also want to educate the community on animal behavior, fostering and adoption, and overall wellness for animals.

Name: Pike Township Educational Foundation
Description: Pike Township Educational Foundation is a philanthropy company based out of 6901 Zionsville Rd, Indianapolis, Indiana, United States.

Name: Voices Against Violence
Description: Voices Against Violence (formerly Vaginas Against Violence) is a club at Carleton University in Ottawa that aims to raise awareness for gender-based violence and promote gender equality. Every year, VAV performs The Vagina Monologues to raise money for the Ottawa Rape Crisis Centre.

Name: ReDawn
Description: ReDawn is an iOS application dedicated to putting power back into the hands of survivors of sexual violence.

Name: Morrinho U.S.A.
Description:
Morrinho USA gives U.S.-based support to favela-based youth organizations in Rio de Janeiro.  We are an all-volunteer team that focuses our efforts on fundraising, marketing, and project management, specifically for not-for-profit organizations. 

Taking advantage of the resources that can be shared via web applications and the larger donor network of the United States, we use a simple and effective approach to support.  Below is a description of what we can do.

Marketing.  We have had amazing success with event and product marketing using social networking sites including Facebook, Orkut, Twitter, Flickr, and Jumo.   Through the consolidation of email addresses, we can also facilitate an informative and low-cost email newsletter.

Project Management.  Key to running a successful organization is a professional-looking public image. We can help to design, launch, and manage a website that reflects what a project is about. By placing on the website that the project is interested in working with international volunteers that would like to spend some time in Rio de Janeiro, we then initiate and facilitate this process.

Fundraising.  With our 501(c)3 tax-deductable status, we attract an increased number of U.S.-based donations.  We work with our Rio project to accept donations both from the U.S. and abroad via PayPal and Guide Star.

Name: The Asheville Soap Company
Description: As a 501c3 non-profit company, we generate funds for charitable purposes, such as micro-loans to honorably released veterans in need of financial assistance. During their service, those in the armed forces earn money to their GI bill. When activated is designed to keep them financially secure in the community in which they live while going to school during their transition to the private sector. Barbara our co-founder, was a USMC Sergeant Aircraft Rescue Firefighter. After her 4 years of service it took over 9 months to activate something she had earned, designed to keep her protected during her transition. She was fortunate to have friends & family to help her, what about those who dont? Your tax deductible contribution through the purchase of our soap can be used to pay a veteran utility bill or other such critical need. In return, the veteran serves in the community providing services equal to a living wage until the micro-loan is paid off using their donated time.

Name: Anthony F. Cordeiro Charitable Foundation Inc.
Description: The Anthony F. Cordeiro Charitable Foundation provides funding to nonprofit organizations serving communities in Southeastern Massachusetts and Rhode Island whose mission supports and enriches the lives of families and children. The Foundation's goal is to assist those burdened by life's challenges by providing access to vital resources including, but not limited to, health and mental health services, food and shelter.

Name: Asssociation Pépite d'espoir
Description:
L’association est née du désir de pouvoir offrir une chance aux enfants dans des pays émergents, afin qu’ils puissent étudier et grandir dans des conditions décentes.
Face au constat de l’état de sous équipement des pays en voie de développement en matière d’éducation, l’association souhaite par ses actions offrir un environnement sain et propice aux enfants.

Name: Fall Formal
Description: The Fall Formal is an annual event for Boston area young professionals to get together, have fun, and support Dana-Farber Cancer Institute. In the Fall Formal's inaugural year (2014), it was recognized as the Rookie Event of the Year by Dana-Farber, for being the most successful new event in its' first year. In just the first two years the event has raised over $650,000 for Dana-Farber Cancer Institute.

Name: Teach Ghana Trust
Description:
The Teach Ghana Trust is a charity, registered in England & Wales (registration number 1099409) on 12 September 2003. Its objects are as follows: 

To advance the education of children and young people in Ghana by the provision of books, equipment, clothing and such other means as the trustees may from time to time determine.

Name: Sidewalk Salon: Haircuts for the Homeless
Description: Sidewalk Salon is a non-profit organization that provides haircuts to the homeless. We partner with volunteers from local hair salons and cosmetology schools who offer their professional skills to help those less fortunate.  We bring our services to homeless shelters throughout the San Francisco bay area.

Name: Vida Na Rua
Description:
Nossa ONG é comprometida em fazer o bem na Zona Leste de São Paulo. O projeto Vida na Rua não tem vínculo com instituições religiosas ou governamentais, por isso, precisa de você. 

Venha conhecer mais de perto o nosso trabalho e faça parte!

Name: Elas Também Sangram
Description: Arrecadação de absorventes para mulheres e meninas em situação de vulnerabilidade. Estejam elas onde estiverem. Promovemos acesso à higiene básica em um período que por si só já é incômodo para a mulher.

Name: Keeping Memories
Description: Keeping Memories is a 501(c)3 non-profit dedicated to funding the discovery of advanced treatment methods for Alzheimer's Disease and other neurodegenerative disorders. The organization is promoting awareness of the benefits of brain fitness, and finding new solutions to improve memory, prevent dementia and improve overall cognitive function.

Name: Human Capital Project (HCP)
Description:
HCP provides personal equity finance to poor Cambodian students to attend university. We are linked with the University of Management and Economics (UME) with two of their campuses (Battbambang & Kampong Cham). HCP is a non-government and non-profit organisation started in 2007 and entirely funded by voluntary donations. 

In the medium term, HCP is designed to be a self-financing operation, with former graduates contributing to the costs of educating the next generation of poor students. At that point, it may be possible to create a "personal equity market" which will substantially increase the amount of money available.

Name: NAVIKA
Description:
Welcome to NAVIKA (ನಾವು ವಿಶ್ವ ಕನ್ನಡಿಗರು ) 

NAVIKA (North America Vishwa Kannada Association) is a nonprofit grassroots organization created to promote and enrich the very rich cultural heritage of Karnataka. The ancient history of Kannadigas dates back to 3000BC.  NAVIKA aims to assimilates Kannadigas from all walks of life under one canopy, creating lasting partnerships in areas of education, culture, business, government and philanthropy. It is estimated that nearly 60 thousand people from Karnataka are living in the US. Regardless of their socioeconomic status, NAVIKA welcomes all these Kannadigas and people of Karnataka origin residing outside of India. This diverse community is eminently successful and is determined to contribute and benefit from Karnataka’s progress and development in addition to the purposes mentioned above.

NAVIKA Boston 2013

NAVIKA Boston 2013 is the grand 2nd World Kannada Summit which is being held in the world renowned DCU Convention and Exhibition Center, on August 30th to September 1st, 2013. The Summit is a canvass for bringing together the highest quality art, literature, education, business, healthcare and technology under one roof. The grand event will showcase varied talents with energetic participation from all spectrums of global community along with local talents in the US.  The cultural events will include traditional dance, drama, music, comedy, talent competition, game show, fashion show and beauty pageant.

Name: C. Charles Jackson Foundation
Description: The C. Charles Jackson Foundation's mission is to advance leadership, character, and life-skills education for students of all ages, with an emphasis in K-22 educational institutions and not for profit organizations that seek to advance student development and leadership potential.

Name: TEAM HYDRO FOUNDATION
Description: Team Hydro is a group dedicated to raising awareness about and finding a cure for the life-threatening medical condition known as hydrocephalus.

Name: ONG Ajuda
Description: Unidades de Tratamento para usuários de Álcool e outras Drogas!

Name: Fundación Eguizabal-Marruffo
Description: Nuestra misión es ayudar a personas y animales vulnerables, mejorar el medio ambiente, promover los valores, el respeto y mejorar la educación.

Name: Jumpstart-up
Description:
Jumpstart-up provides seed level support and funding for high impact, financially sustainable, scalable, replicable entrepreneurial ventures.

High impact entrepreneurs pioneer ventures whose innovations shift or create whole industries addressing some of the toughest challenges facing humanity: food, health, environment, education, clean water, housing, and energy.

Support and funding for early stage high impact ventures is frequently missing. Risks are poorly understood. Stakeholders tend to enter the market at a later, safer, stage e.g. venture capital firms. Ideas often come from poor parts of the world with few role models and no informal seed funding from friends and family.

We search the world for such people and provide a catalyst to turn their ideas into companies that change the world. 

Above all, they get validation and access to our international network of high impact entrepreneurs, mentors, and seed level investors. 

In short, we provide an ecosystem for seed level high impact ventures.

Name: Blooms for Benefit
Description: Our mission is to bring love and hope to those experiencing tough situations by delivering repurposed flowers donated by organizations and events. We aim to comfort others and improve their well-being and emotional health through the vitality of flowers.

Name: Give a Damn Vancouver
Description:
100+ friends are asked to give $100 directly to one of three nominated charities, four times per year. Each quarter members attend a one-hour meeting at which three charities present their causes for 5 minutes each. At the end of the brief presentations, members vote to determine by majority which charity will be the recipient of the $10,000+.

We're are a non-organization. We are not registered; we have no treasurer; we have no bank account because we don't collect, spend or donate anything - it’s our members who make the donations. The group and its events are run on a budget of zero by a core team of big-hearted volunteers and with the assistance of some very generous sponsors, who coincidentally also give a damn.

Name: Empowering Spirits Foundation, Inc.
Description: The Empowering Spirits Foundation, Inc. (ESF) is a national nonprofit civil rights organization working to achieve lesbian, gay, bisexual and transgender (LGBT) equality through community service activities. Instead of attempting to change laws ESF strives to change the hearts and minds of individuals and institutions that may have not previously favored gay rights or supported LGBT populations, through events designed to allow both sides to work together in a positive environment to foster non-confrontational dialogue and dispel negative stereotypes. Volunteer today and help ESF promote equality!

Name: Soleils en Inde
Description:
Soleil en Inde est une association étudiante de l'Université de technologie de Compiègne.

Notre objectif principal est de faire de la sensibilisation sur les problématiques humanitaires en Inde, ainsi que de récolter des fonds grâce à diverses activités et évènements sur Compiègne et/ou à l’UTC même. 

Ces fonds nous permettront ensuite de poursuivre notre collaboration avec une ONG française (Calcutta : de la rue à l’école) ainsi qu’une ONG locale, en Inde (Tomorrow’s Foundation), afin de soutenir des actions pour venir en aide aux enfants et enseignants sur place. 

Tous les ans, un groupe d’étudiants se rend en Inde pendant un mois pour aider les enfants en réalisant une mission donnée par l’ONG (numérisation de cours, sensibilisation sur les droits dans les bidonvilles, dispenser des cours en anglais…).

Name: Motorrad Angels
Description:
We are Motorrad Angels! Adventure motorcycle riders bringing clean water and other life saving assistance to the world's most vulnerable communities.

We provide Sawyer Water Filtration kits to those who do not have access to clean drinking water. Each filter supplies 100 people with clean water for 5-7 years = 1,000,000 gallons. 

Motorrad Angels has 29 chapters in 29 countries and is continuing to grow rapidly aiding individuals in acquiring life's most basic needs.

Name: THE ORIOLE ADVOCATES CHARITABLE FOUNDATION INC
Description: THE ORIOLE ADVOCATE CHARITABLE FOUNDATION INC is a company based out of PO BOX 2295, BALTIMORE, Maryland, United States.

Name: Miscarriage of Justice Support Fund Inc.
Description: The Miscarriage of Justice Support Fund Inc. is a public benevolent association with deductible gift recipient status, that aims to help victims of miscarriage of justice.

Name: Sunny Side for Equality
Description: Sunny Side for Equality is a movement to facilitate the intercommunication of racial equity and equality organizations. This is acheived through branding our symbol and message through merchandise and media. Profits from merchandise are donated to various equity organizations.

Name: Your Human Rights
Description:
Your Human Rights Charitable Trust strive to make human rights work for you. 

Our goal is to become a catalyst for the strengthening of human rights in 
the policies and actions of governments, companies and aid agencies.

Human rights belong to all of us, regardless of how old or how young we may be, 
or where we live in the world.

Name: Wie Uel
Description: Organização internacional com o intuito de motivar e incentivar mulheres a entrar e permanecer nas áreas de STEM

Name: Kris Foundation
Description:
Kris Foundation is a non-profit initiative set up in 2009 by Singaporean philanthropist Kris Tan. The Foundation is dedicated to empowering young classical musicians in Singapore and the region. Over the past 12 years, the Foundation has expanded to supporting the wider arts community. 

Through its signature recital series, Kris Foundation has established a professional concert platform in Singapore, presenting over 30 concerts with more than 58 top classical musicians from Southeast Asia. With a desire to develop a uniquely Singaporean sound and encourage new music, the Foundation has commissioned and presented works by Singaporean composers. 

The Foundation supports organisations with a strong ethos and innovative initiatives with a transformative impact. These include the Singapore Symphony Orchestra, Singapore International Violin Competition, Asian Civilisations Museum, National Gallery Singapore, KKheART, Metropolitan Festival Orchestra, Ding Yi Music Company, More Than Music concert series and New Opera Singapore. 

The Foundation has previously awarded scholarships to music students at the Nanyang Academy of Fine Arts (NAFA) and sponsored the annual NAFA— Kris Foundation Fund Concerto Competition from 2017 to 2020. In 2020, the Foundation established the Kris Foundation Viola Masterclass Fund at the Yong Siew Toh Conservatory of Music, National University of Singapore. In 2021, Kris Foundation supported ACMtalks - a monthly webinar series by the Asian Civilisations Museum in Singapore, that brings leading scholars in conversation with the ACM’s curators. Our additional philanthropic initiatives include sponsoring tertiary students with special needs, specialising in the performing arts.

In recognition of the Foundation’s contributions towards the development of the arts in Singapore, the Foundation has received the National Arts Council’s Patron of the Arts Award annually since 2012, including the Distinguished Patron of the Arts Award from 2019 to 2022.

Name: Fundación Submarino Amarillo
Description:
Organización no gubernamental (ONG)
Sumérgete en la aventura de crear y promocionar proyectos de atención integral a la población a lo largo de su ciclo de vida.
Manta, Ecuador

Name: PRINZ & CO
Description:
Prinz & Co is a hybrid agency. We work with brands and organisations for a greater purpose as well as developing our own products. Our main goal is to make a real difference for people, business and society.

Petter Prinz and Kaspar Prinz are two award winning tech savvy multidisciplinary makers and brothers. In 2011 they got headhunted to be part of starting up Google Creative Lab in London. After 4+ years at Google they decided to start their own creative co-operative.

Name: Casa de Santa Ana
Location: Rio de Janeiro, Rio de Janeiro

Name: Helping the Homeless Together
Description: Using the power of social media to find like-minded people who want to do something to help those who are homeless and living on our streets.

Name: American Center for Philanthropy
Description:
The American Center for Philanthropy (ACP) is an independent, non-profit organization established to increase charitable giving by providing donors with useful information, unbiased guidance, and advantageous ways to give.

Our mission at ACP is to support individuals, gatekeepers and non-profit organizations in the transfer of social capital to a meaningful and effective purpose. With a unique focus on donor education, the American Center for Philanthropy strives to help donors Experience the Power and Joy of Giving.

Name: It Can Be Done!
Description:
It Can Be Done! is a non-profit educational and charitable organization creating awareness and sustainable solutions for safe water access, health, education and economic opportunities in Tanzania, Africa. We are dedicated to respectful partnership, linking people and resources for shared wisdom, health and well being.

Founded in 2006, our all volunteer, grass roots organization is made up of a diverse and dedicated group of experienced American business people, healthcare workers, engineers, community leaders, friends, neighbors and students… who share their skills and open hearts… to turn their compassion into action.

The work of It Can Be Done! is made possible by the generosity of these private donors, volunteers and friends; joined together in the spirit of service, with our friends and partners around the world.

Name: Effective Altruism Exeter
Description:
The effective altruism movement is all about doing the most good we can do, by using evidence and rationality to discover the best ways to help the world. We focus on many different cause areas, including (but not limited to) global poverty, animal welfare, and existential risk.

The Effective Altruism (EA) Society is an Exeter-based community of people who believe in the core EA values of helping the world, rationality, and optimisation. The society acts as a platform for EA-focused discussion. We aspire to learn how we can use our resources, careers, and free time to help the world as much as possible, and put this knowledge into practice. We hold weekly discussion sessions, which are open to anyone and everyone, and are cultivating a rich community of people who are passionate about doing good.

Interested in learning more? Visit our page, and join our Facebook group to hear about our weekly events!

Name: Happy Planet Children
Description:
We have a grand vision at Happy Planet Children, to make the world a better place by helping vulnerable - yet deserving - children on their path to self-reliance and self-sufficiency. But we also understand our constraints and have a lot to learn. So we’ll dream big and strive to make a difference every step of the way.

By contributing to our fundraising campaign, you are making the statement that despite being abandoned by their family or orphaned, these children deserve a decent childhood, an education, and a chance at a better life. Thank you for your consideration and generosity.

Our Programs

Financial Relief - Providing financial aid to destitute children in Vietnam. $1 per day will pay for a child's living costs. An additional $1 per day will pay for their education.

Made with Love - Partnering with The Giving Plant to create and sell beautifully designed succulent arrangements. 100% of the profits benefit underprivileged children in Vietnam.

Skills Builder - Helping children and young people in the United States and Vietnam with ESL skills, the Google CS First curriculum, and engineering leadership training programs.

Business Outreach - Working with businesses to promote fundraising activities, sponsor programs, and offer employment opportunities to qualified disadvantaged young adults.

Get Involved

Want to help us make the world a better place, one child at a time? That's great! We're looking forward to connecting with you and joining forces.

Name: Kondanani Zambia
The Hatch Foundation
Description: Our Mission: The Hatch Foundation partners with individuals, organizations, and communities to enhance fund-raising efforts. The Foundation seeks to provide opportunities to families and children in need, community enrichment through the arts and improve the standard of living for people locally and internationally.

Name: Community Systems Group, LLC.
Description:
Community Systems Group was founded in 1999 with a passion for solving community problems. For the past decade, CSG has supported foundations, government organizations, universities, and local partnerships as they tried to create better lives for everyone in the community. CSG has worked to reduce teen pregnancy, improve nutrition, increase physical activity, promote early childhood development and education, eliminate poverty, increase literacy, prevent crime, and fight drug abuse.
 
While CSG has expertise in each of these specific areas, our specialty and passion is the process of solving community problems. The technology that supports it. The skills required by those who lead it. The systems that outsiders such as funders, consultants, and trainers need to nurture it. The evaluation that feeds it. The research that proves it works.
 
To support community problem solving CSG has helped create strong local initiatives through our local community support business. CSG has created complete community surveillance, grantee monitoring, and foundation focused evaluation   systems for our institutional clients. CSG has designed, developed, and deployed exemplary national training efforts designed to increase the skills of local community leaders and support their important work. A decade of intensive work at all three levels—community, institution, and large scale initiative, has resulted in consulting, technology, and training for community problem solving that works.

Name: Tickets For Troops Inc
Description:
Tickets For Troops Inc is a 501c3 Charity based in Knoxville, TN. We work to provide reuniting families with a well deserved Welcome Home Celebration. These events have included great outings like a fine dining date night to a day at Autozone park to watch a Redbirds baseball game. 

With our expansion into East Tennessee we are excited to partner with local companies to offer seats for the 2018-2019 University of Tennessee football under the leadership of our new head coach Jeremy Pruitt.  

Aside from the excellent offerings for together time for our veterans' there is a great sense of pride in returning to work here on the homefront. We are offering resume reviews, job sharing and much more to empower our military families the opportunity to succeed in corporate America as well.

Name: LoVE USA (Lily of the Valley Endeavor)
Description: LoVE is a non-profit organization created to help AIDS infected and affected children and their communities, beginning at Lily of the Valley, iKhethelo, LIV Children's Villages in KwaZulu Natal, South Africa - the heart of the AIDS pandemic - and to create awareness in the USA.

Name: Equal Opportunity Network
Description:
At the Equal Opportunity Network, we believe society and humanity as a whole benefits when everyone gets an opportunity to pursue their dreams. We believe in making minor personal sacrifices that can be life changing to our fellow members of society.

We are currently sponsoring disadvantaged, high potential students in rural Zimbabwean schools by paying for their school fees, school uniforms and other basic requirements to make this happen. This is funded entirely through contributions from our members. If you think this is a meaningful cause and you can make a contribution, please join us so we can help more young people.

Success in life depends on hard work and talent. It also depends on chance. The fortune of having guardians who can afford to take care of basic needs can be the difference between a hard working youth who earns a good life for themselves and one being stuck at the bottom rung of society.

Name: TheAlpacaTimes
Description:
Creating awareness on sustainability is a fragile topic, as it is ever so often used to deceive and misguide; The Alpaca Times focuses on true, reliable content based on evidence presented in an understandable form and manner - For everyone.

Only a community can secure long-term knowledge in the complex and omnipresent world of sustainability.

Name: Sandy Springs Rotary Club
Description:
The Rotary Club of Sandy Springs meets most Mondays beginning at 12:15 p.m. for lunch at Hilton Perimeter Suites
6120 Peachtree Dunwoody Rd., Atlanta, Georgia, 30328-4513
Check sandyspringsrotary.org for upcoming meeting dates.

Name: IMIDI Xalapa
Description:
IMIDI cree en el desarrollo de México y sus habitantes,  mostrando de manera contundente que la sociedad civil esta lista para hacer su parte.
Coadyuvamos, fomentamos y creamos herramientas para consolidar los mecanismos en los procesos de inclusión de personas en situación de discapacidad en las distintas áreas de desarrollo social. Ofrecemos consultoría educativa y empresarial, diplomados, talleres, cursos, terapia de lenguaje, estimulación temprana y programas de formación integral para personas con síndrome de Down en diversas modalidades (presencial, foráneo, online).

Name: Australian Standfirst Foundation Inc.
Description: This incorporated association provides pro bono philanthropic guidance and facilities, broader community philanthropic education where and when appropriate and always on a gratis basis. All members participate on a voluntary and unremunerated basis.

Name: Chicks on Sticks VT
Description:
Once upon a time, way back in 2016, some strong ladies* sat in the audience of a ski and snowboard film festival and noticed that there was not a single female-identifying person in any of the videos on the screen in front of them. Determined not to let it happen again, they set out to create their own edit. When they talked to all of the other rad lady shredders in their lives, they realized they were all tired of this exhausting, male-dominated culture that prevailed in the sports they loved. The idea to create an edit then grew into a club of incredible female-identifying skiers and riders at the University of Vermont (UVM). 

Within a few years, Chicks on Sticks went from a small group of women meeting in the comfy chairs on the first floor of the Davis Center to a Student Government Association (SGA) recognized club with over seventy members attending weekly meetings. Our focus shifted from creating an edit to promoting access and equity in the outdoors. From coordinating rides to mountains, backcountry skiing and raising money for nonprofits or running mentorship programs, Chicks on Sticks VT continues to bring people together to do what they love most - spending time outside, sliding on snow. 

Chicks on Sticks is a group of female-identifying skiers and riders advocating for accessibility and inclusivity in the outdoors, specifically snow sports. We fulfill our mission through on campus outdoor programming, youth mentorship programming, community partnerships and fundraising. Our core values of adventure, community, leadership, gender equity and sustainability guide our work.

Name: Foundation "Niesiemy Nadzieję"
Description:
Our foundation provides care for children who suffer from life-threatening diseases such as mucoviscidosis, muscular dystrophy and children on renal dialysis who are waiting for kidney transplants. We support several dozens of families in need mostly by subsidizing the purchase of medicaments or by helping to buy other things essential for treatment and rehabilitation. Our help is mostly directed to children from Greater Poland and Lubusz Voivodeships.
At Christmas and Easter we prepare packages with food and cleaning products which are distributed among nearly one hundred families from Greater Poland Voivodeship.
Apart from helping the ill children’s families we try to make our little friends’ lives as joyful as possible. In this way they can forget about their diseases and everyday suffering. Going to the cinema or on a picnic together brings a smile to their faces as well as helps them to make friends and create a little big family.

Name: Fans Against Violence
Description: Fans Against Violence (FAV) is a fan based organization. We represent and include all fan bases and promote friendly rivalries. Stereotypes and misconceptions have played an adverse role within rivalries, increasing and encouraging hostility between opposing fans. Through our mission, we hope to provide fans a platform where this hostility can be diffused. Quite simply, we are all far more alike than we realize. If we don't work together to improve the climate of our sporting events, the leagues, venues, and government will impose further limits and regulations upon us. Let's work together to take a stand in"Taking Back The Stands".

Name: Action pour le Développement Durable en Afrique
Description: <<Action pour le Développement Durable en Afrique >>  est une ONG qui se donne pour mission de promouvoir le développement durable au travers de multiples actions ciblées principalement vers les couches les plus vulnérables. Elle intervient ainsi dans le renforcement des capacités des acteurs de développement à la base, œuvrant pour l'insertion socioprofessionnelle, l'autonomisation et la lutte acharnée contre la pauvreté et l'insécurité alimentaire.

Name: Leaders Ignite
Description:
We facilitate workshops that inspire empathy, prioritize balance and empower growth.

Leaders Ignite is comprised of volunteers from the San Francisco Bay Area who met through a transformational leadership program. Inspired by the program and knowing that simple yet powerful awareness tools can positively affect people’s lives and in turn, their communities, Leaders Ignite was formed. Our goal is to pass the torch and share what we have learned, and more importantly, to take it to the next level by facilitating workshops that are more inclusive, more accessible, more relevant to contemporary challenges, and more impactful than ever!

Name: Salsita
Description:
Salsita is a non-profit supporting artists who are working to make a difference in the lives of children through creative development.

We support artists through the donation of material goods, promotion of their art in the United States, and contribution of revenue from the sale of phone cases.

Name: Beauties of 5 Continents
Description: Beauties of 5 Continents is an international pageant with a wide philanthropic focus of bettering the lives of children worldwide and creating empowered, talented ambassadors on a world stage to promote pluralism and the eradication of poverty.

Name: Paper Bag Fund
Description:
Paper Bag Fund is a network of people advocating for fresh college and grad school graduates with innovative ideas needing a little nudge.

Paper Bag Fund offers two programs: Micro Grants and Opportunity Grants. 

The Micro Grants are distributed quarterly to fresh graduates in support of their innovative projects in their professional and/or local communities. The Paper Bag Fund’s amazing Giving Team contribute every quarter making each grant possible and a Review Team of young movers and shakers determine the grantee each quarter. This program will launch Summer/Fall 2011.

The Opportunity Grants feature different opportunities that will make the grantee’s professional and personal goals attainable. Each Opportunity Grant will be linked to our partnerships with other organizations and individuals. Opportunities will vary from studio space, internships, to exclusive informational interviews with high-level executives in the fields of art, humanities, and the sciences. This program will launch Fall/Winter 2012.

Official Website will launch August 2011.

Name: Global Family Reunion
Description:
The Global Family Reunion – coming June 6, 2015 – is the biggest, most extraordinary and most inclusive family reunion in history.

Come meet fascinating cousins you never knew you had — and learn about how we are building a Family Tree of the entire Human Race.

What: An event like no other. Presentations by entertainers, authors and scientists! Music! Food! Exhibits! Comedy! Games for kids!

Who’s Invited: You! All seven billion members of the human family. Those with a proven connection to the world’s biggest family tree (currently at 77 million people) get a bracelet and take part of the largest family photo in history.

Presentations: The GFR will feature talks and presentations by a wide range of fascinating experts and entertainers. Among those who have already agreed to participate: Filmmaker Morgan Spurlock, President George H.W. Bush and Barbara Bush (via video), Comedy Central star Nick Kroll, NPR host Ophira Eisenberg and former Obama administration member Cass Sunstein. 

How: Thanks to amazing advances in genealogy and DNA, we are building a Family Tree of the entire world. Currently it’s at 77 million people. It’s easy to join! Websites such as Geni and WikiTree allow you to collaborate with hundreds of others and figure out your place on the World Family tree. Hundreds of people are working to make the Global Family Reunion a reality.

Partners: Among those helping to create the Global Family Reunion are MyHeritage, Geni.com, FamilySearch 23andMe.com, FamilyTreeDNA, Federation of Genealogical Societies and WikiTree.

The Cause: Fighting Alzheimer’s. The Global Family Reunion has partnered with two remarkable organizations devoted to finding a cure for Alzheimer’s Disease. Namely, the Cure Alzheimer’s Fund and the Alzheimer’s Association New York Chapter. Alzheimer’s is a devastating disease that affects more than 40 million members of the Human Family, a number that will climb exponentially unless we find a cure.

Name: Au Service Du Social

Name: Ironbark Housing
Description:
Objective: To assist in the provision of affordable housing solutions to eligible clients including indigenous, retirement, social and accessible housing types

Goal: To increase the availability of affordable rental and owner-occupied properties in the Australian market.

Strategy: The Affordable Housing Charity develops affordable housing for both owner-occupiers and built to rent.
The built to rent properties are offered as rentals to registered Community Housing Providers (CHPs) at a discount to market rent, secured by leases of a minimum of 10 years.
This achieves the Charity’s purpose.

Name: CappSci
Description:
CappSci is a private foundation engaged in applying science and engineering to real world problems. We identify the most pressing challenges in sustainability, ecosystem restoration, and human health, and then we create public prize competitions to address them. We collaborate with the winners to develop novel and cost-effective technology. CappSci creates the leverage needed for positive change by following three equally important steps:
1. Define a problem accurately
2. Find the people, knowledge, and capital to solve it
3. Measure the results.

Name: Effective Altruism Nepal
Description:
Effective Altruism Nepal (EA Nepal) is part of a large network of EA chapters across the world. Effective altruism (EA) about being generous with your time and your money to do the most good you can. 
We use evidence and careful analysis to find the very best causes to work on.

Name: International Diplomatic Council of Tamil Eelam
Alberta Turtle and Tortoise Society
Description:
Our non-profit organization is dedicated towards the proper conservation, rescue, care and fostering of any injured and unwanted turtle or tortoise in need.

The main objective of our society is to provide a space for donated, unwanted and abused turtles and tortoises, so they can eventually find a proper home. The second objective is to educate the public about the dangers of improper handling and the negative environmental impact from discarding a turtle into the wild.

These objectives come from two situations that pose the majority of the problems. First, many people that take in a turtle or tortoise don’t realize the dedication and space that is needed to care for them. Second, a great majority of similar non-profit societies do not have the facilities and manpower to sufficiently care for turtles and tortoises. These situations eventually lead to euthanasia, the owners releasing turtles into the wild, or killing them because owners no longer want them.

We are currently working with existing societies across Canada to strengthen our network and knowledge in order to provide the best possible outcome for the society as a whole.

Name: WALK a MILE in MY SHOES~~~on the Path to PEACE!!!
Description:
WALK a MILE in MY SHOES~~~on the Path to PEACE!!!...a 501c3 Non-Profit Organization based in Mesa, AZ. created in 2008.

Now...in Our 8th year of SERVICE, WAMMS has shared 'gently-loved' SHOES with more that 40,000PEOPLE...'along the Path.  With a mission which states that We 'Provide a Sturdy Foundation for a PEACEFUL Day' to Folks having NO SHOES, at All...WAMMS welcomes YOUR participation.  

How to participate with WAMMS...It''s as EASY as 1~2~3!!!
1. Host WAMMS as You conduct a WAMMS Community SHOEDrive  (Ask for details.)
2. Share 'gently-loved' SHOES with WAMMS during a WAMMS Community SHOEDrive.
3. Support WAMMS with Your generous donation to WAMMSNow.  (www.paypal.com  WAMMS)

Do STEP FORward to visit:  www.facebook.com/WAMMSNow to learn of the diverse ways in which You may SERVE.  We welcome Your participation...Your support...Your sharing.  

Blessings of PEACE to You...ALWAYS!!!

WALK a MILE in MY SHOES~~~on the Path to PEACE!!!
480-373-5930
WHSPER0851@HOTMAIL.COM

Name: IMPACT LEGACY
Description: IMPACT LEGACY is a philanthropy advisory and fundraising firm. We work with high net-worth individuals and families to help them engage in impactful philanthropic giving, as part of building their overall personal legacies. We identify nonprofit organizations with successful programs and the ability, with strategic donations, to scale their impact and achieve measurable results. We connect our clients to great causes and high-quality, high-potential nonprofit organizations.

Name: Awesome Foundation Lusaka
Description:
We've come together to help you propel your awesome idea forward. Whether your idea is about bringing light to dark matter, generating higher yielding farmland in Sub-Saharan Africa or putting on a music festival in Lusaka, we're keen to support your ideas.

Our trusteeship is made up from people across three continents including trustees with first-hand knowledge of start-ups, marketing, finance, investment, philanthropy, sales, urban design and engineering.

Things we love:

Moonshot ideas - projects that think big and if they succeed, could change the world.

Local ideas - projects that will make things better or brighter for people right here and now.

Purposeful ideas - projects that creatively and responsibly solve some of the many challenges that surround us everyday.

Empowering ideas - projects that help others achieve something great, or that grow as more and more people get involved.

Achievable ideas - projects that not only sound exciting but are feasible and will get off the ground.

Top tip: Have a succinct plan detailing exactly how you plan to invest the grant to make your idea a reality (although we do like your Great Uncle Jim, paying for his rent or yours isn't a winning idea).

Name: Pesantren Quran Taqwa
Description:
Lembaga filantropi pengelola sedekah, wakaf, dana pendidikan & kemanusiaan.

Kami berharap setiap Ikhtiar yang kami lakukan dapat menjadi estafet kebaikan bagi banyak orang sehingga semakin banyak pula manfaat yang dibagikan.
