Name: St. Helena Public Schools Foundation
Description:
The St. Helena Public Schools Foundation is a community-based non-profit organization whose mission is to promote academic excellence, champion innovation, and provide sustained financial support to St. Helena’s public schools.  

The board of the St. Helena Public Schools Foundation recognizes that children thrive in an educational environment that promotes engaged learning and provides diverse opportunities to develop skills for self-expression and for navigating the 21st century world.  We believe that vibrant educational programs are a vital part of each child’s educational experience.  Our vision is to secure the necessary funding for programs that provide all children access to a complete education, including meaningful and sustained exposure to not only core curriculum, but also art, music, literature, science, language, and history.

Name: Climate Rocks
Description: Rocking climate change!

Name: BIGHUG
Description: Sorrir e gerar sorrisos, viver e realizar sonhos, dando um novo sentido ao poder do compartilhamento e seu alcance através de uma rede social de doações e entretenimento.

Name: Association Mikou
Description:
Active depuis 2014, l’Association Mikou est animée par le désir d’aider les personnes dans le besoin.

A chaque rentrées scolaires nous distribuons des cartables remplis de fournitures à des orphelins et à des enfants dans le besoin dans différentes villes du Maroc.

Notre objectif est d’aider les enfants à s’épanouir dans leur éducation en leur fournissant un accès à du matériel scolaire.

Pour l’association Mikou l’éducation a une grande importance puisqu’elle a pour finalité d'améliorer la qualité de vie d'une personne. Elle offre aux adultes et aux enfants défavorisés une chance de sortir de la pauvreté, de faire entendre leur voix dans leur communauté et de bénéficier de meilleures opportunités pour leur avenir.

Name: The Write Source, LLC
Description:
At the Write Source, we are professional grant writers that do more than just design winning grant proposals! That is why we identify ourselves as 'proposal designers' and not just 'grant writers.' 

We provide total grant seeking management services. Simply put, we navigate the way throughout the grant seeking process from identifying potential funding opportunities to preparing the grant proposal to coaching your nonprofit team to use our successful methods to win grants.

We provide only grant seeking and development writing services for nonprofit organizations. This sets us apart from other consulting firms that work in all areas of fundraising and nonprofit management including the highly specialized field of grant seeking. While we concentrate on just one aspect of fundraising, we have years of experience in all forms of fund development to help your organization incorporate grants into your fundraising mix.

While we have many long-term clients that depend on us for all their grant seeking management efforts, we also empower organizations to seek grants on their own.  Our proposal review and critique service provides a great deal of coaching to ensure organizations learn all they can about the grants eeking process.

The Write Source also provides half-, full-, and two-day workshops in the areas of grant proposal and development writing, researching grantmakers, and advanced grant seeking. We know how to effectively train board members, staff, and other groups in the grant seeking process and give them the tools to be successful.

When it comes to total grant seeking management, we are the experts to call. Let us help your organization obtain the funds it needs to succeed in fulfilling its mission.

Name: Tell Her Stories
Description: Tell Her Stories (THS) is a Belgium-based intersectional feminist platform which provides a safe space to share women’s experiences. We believe in the power of storytelling to promote connection, understanding and ultimately, to create positive change which empowers the entire community. We organise events on a variety of topics around gender, diversity, culture, and other issues that affect women in their private and professional lives.

Name: LongLive.Vendee
Description:
Bienvenue sur la page Linkedin de l’association Longlive !

Ici, vous retrouverez toutes les informations et l'actualité de cette association créée en août 2022 par deux jeunes vendéens.

« Pour nous, Long Live c’est l’occasion de pouvoir apporter notre participation pour améliorer, à notre échelle, une partie du monde.
En réalisant nos futures actions, nous souhaitons réaliser des aventures qui changent drastiquement de notre quotidien. L’objectif est donc de pouvoir s’ouvrir culturellement et de découvrir le monde en apportant notre aide au niveau social et écologique. Afin que chacun, puisse avoir une longue vie. »

Lien de notre dossier de sponsoring :
https://drive.google.com/file/d/1fa8Rt05NaIakYPE0GQEkmLzyhzDNFK6h/view?usp=sharing

Name: African Soul, American Heart
Description:
African Soul, American Heart's Mission is to PROTECT. EDUCATE. and EMPOWER. orphaned girls from South Sudan. In 2014, we were displaced from the village of Duk Payuel, Jonglei State, South Sudan by the ongoing civil war. Our boarding school was badly damaged and some buildings destroyed. We located our students in 11 refugee camps in three countries and moved them to Moyo, Uganda where we continue to operate a boarding facility, feeding, clothing, providing medical care, and loving staff, our students attend primary schools and secondary boarding schools.

The ASAH program Protects these vulnerable orphaned girls from forced marriage, Educates them in school subjects and practical life skills, and Empowers them to be leaders and to give back to their communities.

Name: LAZ Al Bunyan
Description: Laz Al Bunyan is an Amil Zakat institution in West Java, focusing on community empowerment and humanitarian efforts.

Name: Chai Project
Description: Chai project was formed to enable an open platform for social and innovation initiatives to take place. The purpose is to bring together a collective of technical, commercial, social and innovation individuals, and facilitate the the co-creation of communitised industry initiatives.

Name: جمعية الصحة الخيرية في القطيفة
Description: جمعية خيرية متخصصة في تقديم الخدمات الصحية بمفهومها الشامل وخدمات إدارة الحالة وحماية الأطفال وتعزيز الحماية الاجتماعية المتكاملة والتمكين.

Name: Phi Beta Psi Sorority
Description:
Phi Beta Psi Sorority is a national women’s philanthropic organization which was founded in Columbus, OH, in 1904. It is not affiliated with a college or university, and a college degree is not required to join.

The purpose of Phi Beta Psi is to participate in charitable work, to promote fraternal relationship among its members, and to establish and assist other chapters of the sorority.

Today, grants are presented to six doctors who conduct cancer research. One hundred percent of all memorials and donations are made to the National Project, Cancer Research. Since 1941, over $11,000,000 in grant funding has been given to outstanding clinical researchers.

Name: Roof Angels
Description:
Founded in 2013 by Aspenmark Roofing & Solar CEO Chris Zazo, Roof Angels is a Texas-based non-profit providing roof replacement and repair assistance to homeowners in the DFW Metroplex. The organization seeks to aid families who cannot afford to manage the cost of replacement of repairs on their own, or who have no insurance to cover the costs.

Visit us at RoofAngels.org

Name: The Emiliani Project
Description:
To provide comprehensive care and education to impoverished orphans and abandoned children so that they are able to live healthy and fulfilling lives, from childhood through adulthood.

Objective: Build, improve or lease an existing facility to clothe, feed, house, educate, and medically/psychologically treat impoverished abandoned and orphan children.  Provide for intermediate, higher education and job placement while sustaining their physical and emotional needs.  

Means: Fund initial start-up costs and operational costs through private U.S. donations, providing donors with the opportunity to become directly involved with the project.  Purchase local materials and goods.  Hire local laborers and staff for the facility.  Solicit assistance from local and non-local volunteers.  Eventually solicit private donation and long-term corporate commitments (e.g., % of profits, regular donation, scholarships, etc.)

Name: Toos Foundation, a UK registered charity established by Jamil Kharrazi Foundation in 2006
Description:
The Toos Foundation is devoted to the promotion and preservation of Iranian art and culture. The Foundation is guided by those values and principles which uphold understanding, tolerance and respect within the community and among people from diverse backgrounds.

The Foundation supports cultural and educational activities that bring to light the richness and diversity of Iranian culture in order to foster a closer sense of community among Iranians and those interested in Iranian cultural heritage. The Foundation encourages inter-cultural communication and interaction between people of all cultures.

The Foundation initiates a wide range of activities intended to facilitate the study and understanding of Iran’s history and culture, supported by multi-disciplinary events that present different aspects of Iranian culture, both ancient and modern.

The supporters and patrons of the Foundation include, Baroness Caroline Cox of Queensbury, Professor Colm A. O’Muircheartaigh, Professor Susan E. Mayer, Dr Raja Kamal, Mahmoud Khoshnam, Shiva shafighian, Jane Lewisohn and Dr Leonard Lewisohn.

Name: Ending NICU Obstacles
Description: Ending NICU Obstacles is a non-profit organization committed to financially supporting families with babies in greater Sacramento area Neonatal Intensive Care Units (NICU).  Most commonly these babies are born premature and are frequently called preemies. We strive to help these families get to the hospital so they can be with their baby without worrying about the financial strains associated with the NICU. The mission will be carried out by providing grants to families in need for transportation, lodging, and child care costs

Name: Bangalore Social Entrepreneurs
Description:
The Bangalore Social Entrepreneurs community is a local hub for social entrepreneurs with events that share knowledge from sector leaders, create networking opportunities, and connect you to a large community of support.

The motto is to showcase the opportunities available in the social enterprise sector and inspire more people to take social entrepreneurship to impact India's poor.

This platform is open to all social impact events happening in Bangalore, please share details of events taking place you are aware of.

Name: Projeto Buddy
Description: O Projeto Buddy, inspirado em experiências internacionais como o Programa Erasmus, tem como objetivo apoiar os estudantes internacionais da Universidade Federal de Juiz de Fora (UFJF) a se estabelecerem em uma nova cultura e auxiliá-los nos processos burocráticos da instituição. Os Buddies que acompanham os alunos internacionais são estudantes da própria universidade que passaram por uma seleção rigorosa para fazer parte da equipe. As funções pontuais de um Buddy são muito variadas e vão desde ser o primeiro amigo do intercambista - uma pessoa de confiança para ele - até ajudá-lo a fazer seu Registro de Estrangeiro, CPF, carteirinha de estudantes, apresentar pontos turísticos da região, entre outros. Também são promovidos eventos mensalmente para interagir e mostrar a cultura do Brasil e da cidade para os intercambistas. A meta do projeto é dar aos intercambistas uma experiência de intercâmbio mais proveitosa. A Coordenação, por sua vez, é responsável pela manutenção interna do Projeto: pareando intercambistas com buddies, promovendo eventos de integração entre alunos e intercambistas, elaborando eventos que visem apresentar a cultura brasileira a eles e também pareá-los com pessoas com vínculos com a UFJF para oferecer residência temporária aos alunos internacionais.

Name: Kelly Family Foundation
Description:
KELLY FAMILY FOUNDATION 
We are a private, charitable foundation based on the guiding principle that through the support of well run, not-for-profit organizations we can help make society a little better.

Name: Center for Child and Family Achievement
Description: The Center for Child and Family Achievement, believing that parents and children can learn together, seeks to inspire and to develop stronger academic achievement in children living in poverty from Kindergarten through High School graduation, by addressing the total needs of the family.

Name: Grace Foundation
Description:
Grace Foundation is the leadership and support organization for the network of nearly 1,800 communities based in 45 countries and territories. We envision a world where all individuals and families achieve their human potential through education, income stability and healthy lives.

To advance the common good and mobilize the caring power of communities around the world, we will:

Ignite a worldwide social movement, and thereby mobilize millions to action — to give, advocate and volunteer to improve the conditions in which they live.

Galvanize and connect all sectors of society — individuals, businesses, non-profit organizations and governments — to create long-term social change that produces healthy, well-educated and financially-stable individuals and families.

Raise, invest and leverage billions of dollars annually in philanthropic contributions to create and support innovative programs and approaches to generate sustained impact in local communities.

Hold ourselves accountable to this cause through our steadfast commitment to continually measure — in real terms — improvements in education, income and health

Name: Nonenonenone42642
Description: 

Name: Sculptura
Description:
Sculptura is a brand new innovative concept combining the Arts with Social Impact at the 'glocal' level.
We are registered VO (voluntary organisation) based in Malta and with global outreach and impact.

Mission: 
To create art to directly support cultural, social & learning impact producing an inclusive cultural system of exchange & respect, that helps foster other long-term legacy-building, sustainable and socially impactful initiatives globally.
To become the collaborative ‘cultural nexus of change’ to directly support the disadvantaged and disenfranchised - especially where children are affected most.

Legacy Services:
We provide project management services in the core areas of art creation, installation, & delivery where all profits and proceeds are transparently supporting social impact, plus sustainable global initiatives and causes.
We start our work with a discovery exercise together with our clients, where we discuss ideas and purpose to create a legacy, which can be expressed through various projects:
Creation
Remembrance
Restoration
Reactivation
Recreation
Our other art-based projects include:
Auctioning
Education
Funding 
E-Books

Contact us: info@sculptura.world

Name: Les Dames d'Escoffier-Dallas Chapter
Description:
Les Dames d’Escoffier International is a philanthropic organization of women leaders in the fields of food, fine beverage and hospitality. The by-invitation membership, composed of over 2,200 members in 38 chapters in the United States, Canada, the United Kingdom and Mexico, is highly diversified and reflects the multifaceted fields of contemporary gastronomy and hospitality.

Our Vision

LDEI is an international organization of women leaders who create a supportive culture in their communities to achieve excellence in the food, fine beverage and hospitality fields.

To make this vision a reality, LDEI members:
Share knowledge
Support members
Provide leadership, educational opportunities and philanthropic events for the community at large

Name: Foreningen Gate To Nature
Description:
Vi vil gøre en forskel. Men modsat mange andre, vil vi skabe oplevelser og feste imens vi gør det.

Det er nemlig dér vi tror på, at den virkelig forskel ligger. Hvor oplevelser og minder skabes - for livet og for naturen.

Sammen med Randers Regnskov & Ree Park Safari vil Gate to Nature ændre VERDEN.

Så simpelt er det!

Name: FUNDACION INICIATIVA ALMA
Description: INICIATIVA ALMA nace en el año 2014 cuando cinco amigos nos unimos con el fin de apoyar y regalar momentos de felicidad y solidaridad a niños y niñas de comunidades vulnerables de la ciudad de Medellín.  A nosotros se han ido uniendo maravillosos voluntarios y empresas dispuestos a regalar sonrisas y compartir momentos inolvidables sin esperar nada a cambio.

Name: Fondazione di Comunità Fano Flaminia Cesano
Description:
La Fondazione di Comunità Fano Flaminia Cesano è un’organizzazione Non profit che s’impegna a migliorare la qualità di vita delle persone che abitano il territorio, ponendosi come intermediario di rete, nella raccolta e ridistribuzione mirata di fondi dedicati a progetti di varia natura. Fa proprie e valorizza le idee della comunità, mettendo in relazione tutti i soggetti che desiderano “investire” nel benessere comune con chi s’impegna per la realizzazione dei progetti. La Fondazione di Comunità vuole essere il punto d’incontro tra i bisogni espressi dal suo territorio e le potenzialità in grado di soddisfarli.

L’obiettivo primario della Fondazione di Comunità Fano Flaminia Cesano è quello di essere il punto d’incontro tra le esigenze della comunità e i potenziali donatori, rappresentando di fatto l’anello di congiunzione tra i bisogni/problemi e le svariate risorse in grado di soddisfarli. Si identifica quindi come un intermediario sociale e culturale capace di rendere disponibili beni relazionali e finanziari, trasformandoli in ricchezze a disposizione della collettività. Tra i suoi obiettivi infatti la Fondazione annovera anche la volontà di lavorare sulle qualità del territorio nella prospettiva di una comunità più coesa, solidale e partecipativa a garanzia del benessere comune.

Name: One for the Kids
Description:
One for the Kids is a nonprofit organization 501(c)(3) that was established in 1993 by a group of concerned individuals who wanted to make a difference. 

Our first event was held at Old Oaks Golf course and raised a few thousand dollars. Since that time we have raised over $1,500,000 for several different charities. Our two partner charities are Special Olympics Chicago and Advocate Children's Heart Institute. We also chose a host family for The Curve Ball as a beneficiary as well.

Name: The Friendly Almshouses
Description: The Friendly Almshouses is a company based out of 167 Stockwell Park Road  , LONDON, United Kingdom.

Name: Sigma Pi Educational Foundation
Description: Non-Profit Educational Foundation for Sigma Pi Fraternity, International.  We are a 501(c)(3) entity that solicits and manages tax deductible funds for the educational benefit of the Fraternity as a whole, and for its members.  Educational programs such as the Mid-Year Conference and Sigma Pi University for undergraduate members are supported, and scholarships are awarded to both undergraduate and graduate students.

Name: Integración por el CIP C.D Ica
Description:
Ing. Ángel Huanca Borda:
Como candidato a Decano Departamental, he reunido un equipo comprometido para asegurar un futuro sólido y transformador para nuestro Consejo Departamental. 
Por ello presento con mucho orgullo a Nuestros Candidatos; el liderazgo, sus principios y la transparencia  de cada uno de ellos apresuraran  el camino hacia un futuro mejor para nuestro consejo departamental.
Unidos, impulsaremos una ética profesional intachable, honesta, alejando cualquier sombra de malas prácticas, garantizando y generando nuevos servicios de apoyo académico, social y de salud que no solo representen el Bienestar de nuestros colegiados, sino que también establezcan un nuevo estándar de calidad y excelencia.
"TIEMPOS DE TRANSFORMACIÓN CON HONESTIDAD"

Name: Stichting Pan de Vida
Description: Stichting Pan de Vida is op 27 december 2006 opgericht met het doel om de allerarmste kinderen in Peru te helpen. Pan de Vida werkt onder de armste kinderen van Peru. Inmiddels zijn er 37 kinderrestaurants, waar ruim 2.700 kinderen op schooldagen een goede maaltijd krijgen en waar het Evangelie met ze gedeeld wordt. Daarnaast zijn er twee kinderdagverblijven en een kindertehuis waarin 18 kinderen worden opgevangen.

Name: The Ramlan Foundation(R)
Description: Building a better community.

Name: Anzafrika
Description:
Anzafrika’s mission is to support African women entrepreneurs and their communities in fragile contexts to build and scale up sustainable, innovative socio-economic systems.

'Anza' means 'Start' or 'First' in Swahili.

Name: Pasadena Independent Schools Foundation
Description:
The Pasadena Independent Schools Foundation Program (PISFP) is an innovative new youth philanthropy initiative.  As part of this program, middle to high school-aged students from six San Gabriel Valley area schools will gain hands-on experience on how to establish and operate a private foundation, ultimately making grants to charitable organizations in their local and surrounding areas. 

The goal of the program is to give the students a comprehensive and thorough understanding of philanthropy and nonprofit operation, learn skills in critical thinking and decision making, and develop and build leadership skills and learn the benefits of voluntarism.  By giving the participants of this program the opportunity to experience the fulfillment of contributing to the betterment of their community it is ultimately our hope that these students will acquire a life-long passion for giving.

Name: Rotaract District 9100
Description: An organisation of community service for young adults between the ages of 18 and 30 in fourteen West African countries, namely,  Benin, Burkina Faso, Cape Verde, Cote Divoire, Gambia, Ghana, Guinea, Guinea Bissau, Liberia, Mali, Niger, Senegal, Sierra Leone and Togo.

Name: 100 Women Lexington
Description: 100 Women is a grassroots, all-volunteer women's advocacy and philanthropic group serving the Bluegrass region of Kentucky.  We proudly support nonprofits that serve women and family needs.

Name: Action Développement Le Morne
Description:
LE PROJET ACCELERATEUR DE DEVELOPPEMENT DU VILLAGE

Pour impliquer la population locale dans le développement du territoire où elle vit, lui permettre de meilleures conditions de vie et un avenir partagé.

Name: ACG Cares
Description:
Our Mission:
ACG Cares assists college and graduate students from a range of social backgrounds who are interested in a career in business find their first job.

Background:
ACG Cares - NY Chapter, Inc., a 501 (c) 3 non-profit corporation, was created by the New York Chapter of the Association for Corporate Growth to serve as a way to nurture the next generation of business leaders in New York. ACG Cares does this by providing access to established executives who can assist college and graduate students in their job-search. In addition, ACG Cares will support nonprofit organizations that facilitate career placement in business.

Our Strategy:
ACG Cares will host 3-4 events per year where we will connect educated young adult candidates with middle market executives and their firms. ACG Cares plans to partner with the New York chapter of the Association for Corporate Growth (ACG New York) and other non profit organizations.

ACG New York is the premier association in New York for middle market deal-making professionals: private capital providers (both equity and debt), M&A advisors, corporate executives, accountants, attorneys and other related service providers: www.acgnyc.org

Benefits to Candidates:
* Meet Leading Middle Market Professionals and Firms
* Receive Constructive Feedback, Guidance and Advice
* Establish Long Lasting Relationships

Benefits to Middle Market Professionals and Firms:
* Source Exceptional and Diverse Candidates
* Demonstrate Leadership in World’s Financial Capital
* Engage Future Leaders of Middle Market Industry

Benefits to Our Community:
* Create Jobs in New York Metro Area
* Support Local Higher Education
* Drive Middle Market Growth

Name: Asylo Espírita João Evangelista
Description:
O Asylo Espírita João Evangelista é uma associação beneficente localizada no Humaitá, no Rio de Janeiro, que cuida de crianças carentes de 3 a 15 anos, do sexo feminino. É lá que 80 meninas encontram apoio extra familiar.

Sem qualquer ajuda financeira do Poder Público, o Asylo Espírita João Evangelista é uma associação beneficente mantida por associados e colaboradores eventuais que fazem com que o dia-a-dia na instituição permaneça em pleno funcionamento durante todo o ano.

A instituição dá suporte a famílias que não têm com quem deixar suas crianças durante o dia, enquanto estão trabalhando para garantir o salário que as sustenta.

As meninas ingressam no Asylo com a idade de 3 a 6 anos, após entrevistas sociais com seus responsáveis e avaliação psicológica por nossos profissionais. Desde o ingresso até completarem 15 anos de idade, nossa casa se encarrega de prover-lhes educação moral e intelectual visando o desenvolvimento das meninas como cidadãs.

Fundado em 1923 pela médium brasileira Adelaide Augusta Câmara, o AEJE realiza regularmente Reuniões Espíritas Públicas e, mensalmente, reuniões de estudo da Doutrina Espírita.

We bring doctors, researchers and advocates together in these communities to centralize the discussion, educate the average person, and create a demand for the latest most effective treatments. Name: HopeSprings
Description:
1 in 41 people, ages 13 and over, are HIV+ in Baltimore City.  In 4 zip codes, that rate is closer to 1 in 20 people.  In 2006, Central Presbyterian and Grace Fellowship in Timonium were awakened to the HIV/AIDS epidemic and joined hands to respond, founding HopeSprings. Since then, HopeSprings has been equipping and engaging volunteers from 60+ churches to respond to the HIV epidemic in Baltimore serving over 16,000 hours. Through our network of churches, we train volunteers to serve our HIV infected brothers and sisters and partner with local HIV service providers such as AIRS, Moveable Feast and the JACQUES Initiative, an HIV clinic at the University of Maryland’s Institute of Human Virology. Volunteers have the opportunity to serve in many different ways including assisting in testing and linkage to care efforts, meal delivery and assistance, providing tables of people around our brothers and sisters struggling to remain in care or with medication adherence, and providing life skills and mentoring training.

Even if you are not infected, we are all affected; either way, you are loved.

Name: New African
Description:
ABOUT  
 
Who we are : 
 
New African is a grassroots nongovernmental organization committed to exposing and mitigating corruption in central Africa.  
 
Much of our work is focused on the Democratic Republic of Congo and its northern neighbor Central African Republic, two nations which continue to be mired in corruption and bad governance. We are the watchdogs who keep a vigilant eye on politicians and their cronies who illicitly benefit from their nation’s wealth at the expense of the citizenry.  
 
We venture into the dark underbelly of central African politics and capture primary source evidence on corruption, bribery, embezzlement, tenderpreneurship, and other insidious kleptocratic practices that rob African nations of their inherent abilities to reach their fullest potential. 
 
Due to threats posed by the political classes and their close circle of beneficiaries, all our administrative work is handled from our de facto headquarters in Paris, where we can expose information about corruption beyond the scope of local-level censorship. 
 
Our team on the ground is forced to operate covertly and with aliases when collecting, transferring, and exposing important information pertaining to high-level corruption as they have a need to protect their identities and avoid punishment- death of which is just one example. 
 
Regardless of risk, we remain dutifully committed to stamping out corruption in Central Africa and improving the conditions for the people who live through it on a daily basis. 
 
 
What we do: 
 
We are comprised of locals from the DRC and CAR, and our efforts are bolstered by a network of connections that we maintain in France.  
 
Our core mission is to (1) explain corruption to the citizenry, (2) expose acts of corruption committed by politicians at all levels, and (3) mitigate the effects of corruption while encouraging a New African way forward in which nations transform together and adopt better practices of governance. To this end, we regularly host lectures that comprehensively cover the basics of corruption while advocating for democracy, liberty and freedom. We also partner with 
other social movements who work alongside us and help get our message across to the people.   
 
We are committed to unveiling bad practices and calling the ruling elite to account in the interests of forging a better, more prosperous Africa for all its people.

Name: Dr. Theodore A. Atlas Foundation
Description:
The Dr. Theodore Atlas Foundation was founded by boxing trainer and commentator Teddy Atlas, with the purpose to honor the memory of his father, Dr. Theodore Atlas
The main mission of the Foundation is the awarding of scholarships and grants to worthy individuals and organizations based upon scholastic potential, community service, athletic achievement and economic need. The Foundation is a tax-exempt, not-for-profit 501 (c) (3) organization.
Since it's inception in 1997, the Foundation, thanks to the generosity of it's supporters, has been able to give in excess of $1,000,000.00 to worthy causes. These have ranged from scholastic athletics to scholarship funds; from community service groups to after-school programs; and from families and individuals in financial need to those simply needing to be told someone cares.
The Foundation is a 100% volunteer organization. It has no administrative costs. There are no professional fund-raisers, no paid employees, and no fashionable office space that would drain away any funds from the Foundation's only purpose; to give help to those in need when they need it.

Name: Feeding Our Frontlines
Description:
Together, we are stimulating our economy, helping local restaurants emerge triumphant from the COVID-19 pandemic, and supporting our healthcare heroes who are putting their lives on the line for us all. 

On our accessible platform, donors can buy a meal for our frontline workers from their favourite local restaurants. That’s what makes us different from other initiatives out there: the increased transparency of knowing exactly where your money is going. On top of that, 100% of profits go directly to the restaurants themselves and meals are provided to both hospital and long-term care home workers — the latter is commonly overlooked when people think of “healthcare heroes”.

Within less than 3 days of outreach, we’ve already partnered with over 40 restaurants in Vancouver, Calgary, Toronto, and Montreal in addition to numerous healthcare partners across the country.

Powered by 3 Vancouver-based students who are vehement supporters of our local economy and immensely grateful for our healthcare heroes.

Name: Oduneye Foundation
Description:
Mission
The Oduneye Foundation is committed to fostering knowledge and innovation to enrich and sustain the lives and livelihoods throughout Africa.

The Oduneye Foundation supports initiatives that improve access to health and educational opportunities. Based in Lagos and London, the Foundation works with a distinguished Board of Trustees.

Vision
Building an Africa where all citizens have access to quality health care, education and equal opportunities to realize their potentials.

Main Goals
– Enhance community health care by providing free medical services to rural communities which lack adequate medical facilities
_Promoting Entrepreneurship
– Improve the quality of education of children and young people 
– Alleviate the extreme poverty in communities across the country by providing clean drinking water, and encouraging the education/empowerment of women.

Values
The Foundation will operate with the following core values:

Informed and responsive grant making
Community involvement
Government participation
Accountability to beneficiaries
Encourages innovation
Promote philanthropy 

Focus Areas
Community Health Initiatives
Enhancing Quality of Education
Income Generation/Entrepreneurship

How the Foundation works
The Oduneye Foundation works mainly through Non-Governmental Organisations and Community Based groups. Our strategy of working through partnerships is intended to build institutional capacity of groups, supporting innovation and ensuring that the funding provided meets the needs of the target beneficiaries and is culturally relevant. In doing so, the Foundation encourages its grantees to forge collaborations and partnerships with local, state and national government agencies to enhance sustainability of the projects.
The Foundation also seeks collaboration with other grant makers and international agencies working in its target areas. Where necessary, the Foundation will leverage additional funding from other sources to support partner’s initiatives.

Name: Direct Sales Business Coach/ Bhip Global

Description: The foundation was launched on June 1, 2013, under their original name of Sisters by GraceTM, after the National Board of Trustees formed a committee to establish the Foundation. In February of 2021, the foundation had a name change to The Sigma Alpha Omega Foundation. The Sigma Alpha Omega Foundation functions as an alumnae-run, non-profit organization in direct support of the sisters of Sigma Alpha Omega. It strives to provide funds and opportunities for sister development in academics, leadership, spiritual growth, and mission work. All of these activities are designed to grow not only the relationships with our sisters, family, and friends, but also, and most importantly, our relationships with Christ. Name: Eastview Community Foundation
Description:
Since 2000, Eastview Community Foundation has been supporting the students and schools of the Eastview learning community. It’s not just about the high school! We support Eastview High School, School of Environmental Studies, ALC, Dakota Ridge and the three middle schools and five elementary schools that feed into Eastview. Since we have no paid staff, all funds raised are directed to the students, schools and the academic community. Thanks to the generous financial support of individual donors, families, local communities and businesses, The Eastview Community Foundation has been able to award: Over $158,250 in Random Acts of Kindness to directly support students in need. Over $260,000 in Grants to the teachers at our schools. Over $1,400,000 in Scholarships to graduating seniors in our community.

The academic community served includes Highland, Diamond Path, Greenleaf, Parkview and Thomas Lake and Oak Ridge Elementary Schools; Black Hawk, Falcon Ridge, and Scott Highlands Middle Schools; the School of Environmental Studies, Eastview High School; the Area Learning Center and
Dakota Ridge.

VISION
The Eastview Community Foundation provides resources that open doors to educational and personal growth opportunities for all K-12 students in the Eastview attendance area.

MISSION
We empower every student to achieve educational and personal growth by…

-Mobilizing generous hearts in the community
-Fostering equitable access to opportunities and resources children need to excel
-Igniting meaningful connections within our schools and community

Name: The Free Store Project
Description:
Our mission at The Free Store project is to provide for, unite, and empower our neighbors relieving the immediate impact COVID-19 has had on individuals, households, and small businesses. We do this by building beautiful accessible pop-up markets – where there is no catch and EVERYTHING IS FREE. 
Free Stores are an extension of the care and kindness New Yorkers have for one another. They give us opportunities to rebuild, restore, and nourish the futures of our communities. 
The Free Store Project launched in October 2020. The founder, Myles, was inspired to create this project after noticing the increasing number of storefront vacancies amid the pandemic. After seeing a similar project popup in her native Chicago, she decided to take action. “There are people who want to do something in this time…They want to take care of each other. 
A Free Store is exactly what it sounds like: a store, but EVERYTHING IN IT IS FREE! Open, and accessible to the public 24/7 – no one is turned away. All are welcome, and encouraged to take whatever they need and contribute if they can.

Name: JERNIGAN FOUNDATION
Description:
The Jernigan Foundation was started in 2006 out of a desire to serve together as a family and use the gifts and resources we’ve been given to bless our communities and the world.  We have supported many things we are passionate about through the years. 

VISION
We have big goals. We have wild dreams. Our vision is a world full of empowered, flourishing people, families and communities.

MISSION
To achieve our vision, we invest in solutions that empower the vulnerable, at-risk, and defenseless toward a flourishing future.

FOCUS AREAS

YOUTH
We believe young people are amazing and they deserve hope, life and a future.
We invest in organizations that are helping youth thrive – especially youth who have had a rough go in life.

EDUCATION
We believe education is one of the keys to success in life.
We provide access to education for those who may not have the opportunity or resources to obtain it.

ENDING HUMAN TRAFFICKING
We believe every human has the right to be free from slavery and harmful uses of their body. We invest in organizations that lose the chains of injustice and bring freedom to the captives.

Description:
We are an animal protection charity striving to reduce suffering of all animals and enrich animals' lives in inner city locales through advocacy and action. We:
• Advocate and collaborate with spay and neuter programs to reduce overpopulation
• Rescue and aid abused and abandoned animals
• Transport domestic pets from Camden City for spay and neuter for owners who have no transportation
• Advocate for animals and fight against animal cruelty, dog-fighting, neglect and abandonment
• Educate owners regarding responsible pet ownership
• Bring to justice and help to prosecute animal abusers through the court system

Name: The Futures Fund
Description:
Through The Futures Fund, a STEAM education and workforce development program, we train middle and high school students for the jobs of the future. The ten-week program is administered every spring and fall by highly trained industry professionals to build a sustainable ecosystem of economic empowerment and development with youth.

In the Digital Arts program, students learn current computer coding languages such as CSS, HTML, Java, Ruby, and Python. In the Visual Arts program, students train with photographers gaining skills and insight unavailable elsewhere. In addition to hard skills, students learn 21st century soft job skills, such as critical and creative thinking, professional communication, effective collaboration, problem solving, analysis with evidential support, project management, and public speaking. 

By developing these skills early, students are set on a path to secondary education, or a career in growing industries with higher earning potential. To date, our graduates have generated $25,000 in revenue with paid internships and  have launched their own start-up businesses in web design, photography, clothing, and social media marketing. 

These programs are committed to building sustained economic development and a reduction of poverty.

Name: Graybridge

Graybridge offers a platform for individuals, schools, businesses, and organizations against racism.


Graybridge was founded in 2020 and was headquartered in Washington, DC.

Name: Choose to Succeed
Description:
Choose to Succeed is a nonprofit organization working to attract the nation’s best public charter schools to San Antonio. Our mission is to ensure that every family has access to exceptional, tuition-free public education options. The Choose to Succeed movement was born in 2011 when local foundations and community members undertook to improve educational results in San Antonio by building out a high-performing charter sector. The goal is to serve upward of 150,000 of San Antonio public school students by 2030. To accomplish this, we are recruiting the nation’s top charter networks to San Antonio to join in a multi-network collaborative effort. 

Our current charter partners include BASIS Schools, Great Hearts Academies, IDEA Public Schools, KIPP San Antonio, Compass Rose Public Schools, Promesa Academy Charter School, School of Science and Technology, The Gathering Place, San Antonio Preparatory Community School, and Royal Public Schools.

Name: Secours populaire français en Côtes d'Armor
Description: Jour après jour, ici comme ailleurs, le Secours populaire, association reconnue d’utilité publique, agit pour un monde plus juste et plus solidaire, en permettant à chacun de s’émanciper et trouver sa place de citoyen, là où il vit, travaille ou étudie. Issu du peuple, animé par lui, le Secours populaire promeut une relation d’égal à égal véritablement unique et un accueil inconditionnel. Présent partout, au bout de la rue comme au bout du monde avec son réseau de partenaires, il valorise systématiquement l’initiative comme mode d’action. L’organisation de l’association est profondément décentralisée et chaque animateur-collecteur bénévole peut ainsi agir : actions d’urgence, actions dans la durée, accompagnement global, sur les plans matériel, alimentaire, médical, moral, juridique, de formation, d’insertion sociale et professionnelle, d’accès au droit, à la culture et aux loisirs, au numérique, etc. Animateurs-collecteurs bénévoles comme partenaires, entreprises et donateurs, adultes comme enfants dès le plus jeune âge... tout le monde est invité à passer à l’action, à s'émanciper, à vivre la solidarité jour après jour. Enfant de la Résistance, le Secours populaire est lucide sur les obstacles, il est clairement désireux de peser sur les conséquences, sa façon d’y contribuer est d’inviter chacune et chacun à y prendre part en s’abstenant de tout clivage : il rassemble et crée du lien autour des valeurs partagées. Son engagement est un combat. Un mode d’action. Il est sur le pont. Là où ça bouge. Là où ça compte. Il a, tout naturellement, un rôle de vigie vis-à-vis des pouvoirs publics.

Name: South Haven Area Community Foundation
Description:
The Greater South Haven Area Community Foundation is a 501(3)(c) non-profit organization whose mission is to create growth and positive change within our community. To accomplish this, we provide financial support to initiatives that support, cultivate and encourage education, economic growth and arts and culture in our community, enriching the lives of area residents.

Through generous contributions from supporters, General and NGO-specific endowments  are created and managed to become large enough to sustain the many worthy non-profit initiatives in the greater South Haven area.  Prudent investment of donor dollars and extraordinarily low investment fees allow our endowments to provide economic stability for area non-profits and to support the introduction, growth and maturation of new, exciting and much needed programs.

Name: Walnut Creek Sunrise Rotary Club
Description:
Rotary is an organization of business and professional leaders united worldwide who provide humanitarian service, encourage high ethical standards in all vocations, and help build goodwill and peace in the world. In more than 160 countries worldwide, approximately 1.2 million Rotarians belong to more than 31,000 Rotary clubs.

Rotary club membership represents a cross-section of the community's business and professional men and women. The world's Rotary clubs meet weekly and are nonpolitical, nonreligious, and open to all cultures, races, and creeds.

Name: Mason's Toy Box Foundation, Inc.
Description:
Mason’s Toy Box is nonprofit organization which lends support to families engaged in pediatric medical life-altering struggles by offering gifts to hospitalized children (and their family members). We provide relief to families facing overwhelming emotional and financial burdens. We raise awareness for childhood cancer and we fund pediatric medical research.

Our signature event is an annual holiday toy drive in memory of Mason Clark Thomas who was taken from us by Neuroblastoma at 11 yrs. old. Mason loved to give his toys and games to kids he thought could use some cheering up and, perhaps, just needed them more.

Name: Beramaljariyah
Description:
Yayasan Sahabat Beramal Jariyah (beramaljariyah.org) mempermudah orang Indonesia untuk berzakat, infaq, sedekah dan wakaf (ZISWAF) secara online. Sebagai lembaga resmi dengan akta pendirian No. 79 Tanggal 31-01-2020, platform beramaljariyah.org ingin membuat dampak sosial (social impact) kepada seluruh orang Indonesia dengan membantu menggalang dan menyalurkan donasi secara online dengan program-program penggalangan zakat mal dan zakat penghasilan, bantuan kesehatan, santunan yatim dan jompo, bencana alam, pembangunan fasilitas-fasilitas masyarakat dan perlindungan hewan. Bersama BeramalJariyah kita permudah untuk menyalurkan harapan dan memperoleh berkah untuk Indonesia.

Didirikan pada tahun 2019, BeramalJariyah telah membantu lebih dari 50,000+ yatim dan jompo, 200+ individu berpenyakit kritis dan 80+ pembangunan fasilitas umum dan masjid. Kedepannya, kita juga membutuhkan bantuan Kamu untuk bersama-sama membangun platform yang membuat Indonesia jadi lebih baik, bergabunglah bersama Kami.

BeramalJariyah didukung oleh Evermos, platform social commerce terbesar di Indonesia yang memberikan dampak sosial untuk individu untuk mendapatkan penghasilan sendiri dengan berjualan dan memajukan UKM UMKM. Evermos bertujuan untuk menciptakan ekonomi inklusif dengan memberikan akses, pelatihan dan kesempatan kepada semua orang Indonesia, termasuk melalui BeramalJariyah.

Name: BoaCausa.net
Description:
É muito comum pensarmos que nossos problemas são extremamente complicados, os mais difíceis de resolver e que só acontecem com a gente. Mas a verdade é que a maioria das coisas que vivemos em nossas vidas, boas ou ruins, são resultados da nossa história individual, que abarca todas as nossas experiências, por isso o BoaCausa veio para ajudar a transformar sua vida em busca de seus objetivo mais difíceis, através das campanhas criadas por nossos usuários.

Não será fácil manter o foco na meta quando os obstáculos começarem a surgir. Durante este processo, você precisará tomar decisões, fazer escolhas, e muitas vezes pensará em mudar o caminho. Por isso, é importante saber o que você está disposto a fazer para realizar o que deseja.

Name: The Little Hands - Big Hearts Foundation
Description: We are a USA based 501(c)(3) supporting the Manos Chicas Corazones Grandes  (MCCG, in English LHBH) ministry in Trujillo, Honduras.  Our mission is to build a sustainable, Christian community of support for children with disabilities and their families through the provision of medical, educational, physical, and spiritual support.

Name: Miss Vietnam DC (MVNDC)
Description:
OUR MISSION

Miss Vietnam DC empowers women through education, collaboration, leadership and community service.

EDUCATION

Miss Vietnam DC provides educational programs and scholarships for women to pursue higher education or in fields of their dreams.

COLLABORATION

Miss Vietnam DC bridges the cultural gap between mainstream, ethnic groups and among ethnic groups through social and economic development collaborations.

LEADERSHIP

Miss Vietnam DC inspires leadership among women by guiding them to serve as role models for future generations as they start their very own leadership journey. Miss Vietnam DC fosters “big sister little sister” mentoring programs. We provide access and connections to successful people so young women can be inspired.

COMMUNITY SERVICE

Miss Vietnam DC promotes community service through the concept of giving back. We connect young women with different types of community projects. We provide an annual community service award for a distinguished individual each year.

We are a non-profit organization pending 501(c)3 status.


Empowerment through Community – Join the Legacy.

Name: The Nameless Good
Description:
The Nameless Good is a non-profit, non-governmental organisation made up of like-minded students striving to make a difference, no matter how small.

TNG+  was founded with the vision of aiding the less fortunate and differently abled by supporting the education of children in developing nations, education centres for the differently abled, and the labour sector of the U.A.E. Unlike conventional student run social groups, We strive to host a range of various activities and events to raise funds legally; in order to make sure that the funds aren't just donations by individuals, but rather wholehearted support.

Name: 4L Pas Comme Les Autres
Description:
Nous sommes 6 amis, 3 équipages et nous avons 1 objectif : 
Atteindre la ligne d’arrivée du 4L TROPHY ensemble !

Pour officialiser notre union, nous avons créé l'association 4L PAS COMME LES AUTRES.

Notre association à but non lucratif a été créé durant le mois de juillet 2020 dans le cadre de la loi 1901. Elle nous permettra de valoriser nos actions auprès des particuliers tout en apportant une garantie légale auprès des entreprises.

Elle nous permettra également de collecter les fonds financiers nécessaires à la réalisation de notre projet d'aventure et humanitaire.

Ayant le goût du challenge, notre fratrie est prête à s’aventurer dans le désert à bord de la mythique Renault 4L lors de la 25ème édition du « 4L Trophy » en Février 2022, afin de vivre une aventure humanitaire entre amis et relever un défi hors du commun !

Name: DES UNES AUX AUTRES
Description:
L’association de Solidarité Internationale Des Unes Aux Autres a été créée en avril 2014 à l’initiative de Catherine Bidet Emeriau, titulaire d’un DEES (Diplôme Européen en Coopération Internationale – gestion et coordination de projet – 2012).
Le Conseil d’Administration de l’association est composé de six membres.

L’association a pour objectif de contribuer à l’amélioration des conditions de vie des femmes, des jeunes filles et de façon plus générale, des familles en situation de vulnérabilité. La finalité est de favoriser l’accès à l’autonomie des bénéficiaires dans le respect des cultures locales, la pérennité des projets et dans le cadre d’ un enrichissement mutuel.

Les domaines d’activité concernent la vie quotidienne, économique et culturelle, l’éducation, la formation, la santé.

Nos actions participent au soutien et au développement d’activités génératrices de revenus et l’accès aux crédits pour les femmes des groupements accompagnés par l’ONG ADEF. Elles visent également la promotion et la valorisation d’échanges interculturels et des actions en faveur des populations.

Nous développons plusieurs projets au Bénin sur la commune de Tchaourou, en partenariat avec l’ONG locale ADEF (Actions pour le Développement et l’Épanouissement de la Famille), ainsi qu’avec les autres acteurs locaux (groupements de femmes, Centre de Promotion Sociale, Mairie, Collèges, syndicat des artisans…).

Name: Klasse im Garten
Description:
Brigitte Rieser, owner of Klasse im Garten, retires with end of September 2020. No services are offered anymore. Brigitte stays an enthusiastic natural gardener interested in all aspects of gardening with and for life, and an enthusiastic photographer.

She will gladly answer any question which can be ansered with an email.

Name: Meditators Without Borders / Meditadores Sin Fronteras
Description:
Meditators Without Borders is a global Decentralized Autonomous Organization (DAO) building the world's first "First Responders for the Mind." We connect individuals in acute emotional distress with verified, compassionate practitioners—anytime, anywhere, within 90 seconds.

Our Mission: To move beyond traditional crisis management by offering skill transfer, not just support. Our goal isn't just to help users survive a difficult moment, but to equip them with the physiological tools and natural philosophy required to build long-term resilience.

What Makes Us Different:

Human-Verified Trust: Unlike standard automated apps, every "Meditator" on our platform is rigorously vetted via our "Proof of Stillness" protocol—verified by senior practitioners through a decentralized web of trust.

Decentralized Governance: We are owned and operated by our community. Through our reputation-based token system ($MERIT), practitioners earn governance rights based on service and integrity, not capital.

Privacy-First Technology: Built on a secure Web3 infrastructure, we ensure total anonym.

Name: Latim Foundation
Description: Latim Foundation is a non-profit organization founded in 2017  and aimed at inspiring generations and changing the future by finding solutions to Africas biggest problems  which include poverty, disease, ingnorance.

Name: Ventura Farms Animal Rescue
Description: Ventura Farms Animals Rescue is a 501(c)(3) animal rescue and sanctuary set on an incredible 2,000 acres of open fields and grass pastures in Westlake Village, California. Our picturesque location is the perfect place for abused and neglected animals to heal and be rehabilitated while they wait for their forever homes. Along with medical care, our team provides all the love, attention and training that our animals need to recover from their difficult pasts and flourish in their new homes.

Name: Rotary Club of West Chester/Liberty, OH
Description:
The Four-Way Test is a nonpartisan and nonsectarian ethical guide for Rotarians to use for their personal and professional relationships. The test has been translated into more than 100 languages, and Rotarians recite it at club meetings:
Of the things we think, say or do

Is it the TRUTH?
Is it FAIR to all concerned?
Will it build GOODWILL and BETTER FRIENDSHIPS?
Will it be BENEFICIAL to all concerned?

Name: Army of Saints
Description: Lids of Encouragement DBA Army of Saints has, and always will be, an organization that strives to uniquely encourage those in need. The past few years have caused not only us here at home to fall back, but the entire world to grow silent. Although it may seem like we have been quieter than most, I am thankful and happy to reassure you that we have not lost our sights on our goal. We began with a giant cast iron pot and some homemade jambalaya when we first sought out the kind hearts of our community. They helped us decorate the lids of the containers we gave to those in need with words of hope and humanity. Today, after the world has been through so much we have realized we must do more. Lids of Encouragement has been silently working on ways to help improve our city's homeless epidemic on a much larger scale. Unfortunately, the number of those we strive to encourage has grown tremendously after the affects of Covid-19. So we've decided to get back to our roots and ask for those kind hearted individuals to help us. Ask us about helping to feed those in need, plz donate to get a roof over everyone's head, help us help them get care and the peace of mind they deserved. We all need some Encouragement. You don't know who gets it, but remember, who gets it matters.  Be the light that shines in people's life!

Name: Caleb & Calder Sloan Awesome Foundation
Description:
The mission of Caleb and Calder Sloan’s Awesome Foundation is to help disadvantaged children in need as well as children who are dealing with loss.  The Foundation repairs schools and playgrounds, creates colorful murals and identifies tangible items schools and educational facilities need but can not afford.   

After our son Calder was killed in a tragic accident we felt a call to action.  His self-portrait from first grade became an international social media sensation with the words: Adventure, Laughter, Kindness added to it as a catch phrase.   Incorporating art and fun into our give back events quickly became important to us.    

As the Foundation continues to grow we plan to create programs continuing to help children cope with loss.

Program 1: Children who are disadvantaged and/or their schools that are in need.

Program 2: Specifically toward siblings dealing with loss.  

This can include things like one week free summer camp which includes, physical activity, group activities, therapy/art therapy.    Two camps that fall into this category are: Experience Camps and Pediatric Psychology Associates Surf and Paddle Board Camp.

Program 3: Geared toward children who were close friends and extended family.

Program 4: Children in Puerto Rico or other communities like Key West who continue to suffer loss having lost their homes and still unable to attend school on a regular basis.

Name: San Diego Makers Guild, Inc.
Description:
Our Mission: The San Diego Makers Guild is committed to fostering the maker community and to promoting, showcasing, and encouraging adoption of making by individuals, public and private institutions with the goal of advancing education, innovation, commerce, and lifelong learning.

Our Vision:  The San Diego Makers Guild will help develop San Diego into a nationally known maker city.

Name: The Little Light Project, Inc
Description:
Welcome to The Little Light Project, a 501c3 non-profit organization!  We believe in being a voice for the kids:  kids who are struggling today due to heightened sensitivity/intuitive gifts, kids dealing with recent sexual abuse/incest, those who have experienced sexual abuse as kids and are now grown, and kids in spirit who so desperately want their parents to know they are still with them, loving them and guiding them.  
So what makes us different than other non-profits with similar causes?  We have teamed with mental health professionals, intuitive therapists, alternative healing specialists, spiritual leaders, and private detectives in order to offer FREE holistic services and support for families and individuals.  We honor both scientific and intuitive treatment/counseling methodologies, blending the two for a balanced approach to helping those struggling with child loss, memories of child sexual abuse, and heightened intuition/sensitivity as well as parents struggling with diagnosis of mental disorders such as ADD, ADHD, Autism, and Sensory Processing Disorder in their little ones.

Name: Manusaya
Description:
Manusaya is born out of our team anxiety about the 'new normal' that occurred in the world, where bad things we once considered uncommon, now feel normal. We used to be quiet about this until we saw people start to position differences as a weapon to break the nation apart instead of a way to unite us all. 

We always believe that diversity is part of our DNA as an Indonesian, but at that moment we almost questioned that belief. We started to see people forgetting that despite all of our differences, we are all human and therefore we should treat each other like our own. 

One thing that stops us from giving up is the thought of Giving Up itself. How would you feel if someone gives up on you because you made the wrong turn in life? We know we would be very heartbroken because as a human being, we make mistakes every day, and therefore we shouldn't give up on people. At least not now when we haven't done anything." We believe there are more good people in the world than the bad ones. And we have faith that love can change one's heart. 

With that belief in mind, we started a movement that shows love in the things that we do, so we could remind people what it means to be human. We created Manusaya project, a youth organization that aims to put back humanity values in human. We focus on one cause at a time and help both the marginalized and privileged at the same time.

We created a program to empower those who are often forgotten or ignored. At the same time, we also do a social campaign to raise awareness towards the issues in social media, to burst the bubble of the more privileged ones, and to inspire more actions. We believe understanding each other's point of view make the society less harming and more acceptance towards diversity. Through our contents, we are hoping for people to say "People are good and things are gonna get better."

For more info about the project check out our Instagram account @manusaya.project.

Name: Champaran Foundation
Description:
The purpose of Champaran Foundation is to serve of humanity by any way .
Weather it is helping the affected people from natural disasters , whether it is raising funds , whether it is arranging for blood for needy persons , whether it is helping poor children in their education,whatever the case may be .
so if u have any pain in your heart join Us and also tell others to join it.

Name: St Helens Economic Development Corporation (SHEDCo)
Description:
Welcome to SHEDCO, our mission is

“To provide the leadership and coordination necessary to enhance the economic viability of St. Helens.”

SHEDCO, formed in 2008, is a volunteer driven, non-profit organization. These volunteers serve on committees addressing the four main components of downtown revitalization: Design, Promotion, Business Development and Organization.  Their only goal is to find a way to improve the economic vitality of St Helens, Oregon.  SHEDCO volunteers include business owners, property owners, individual citizens of St Helens, and members of other civic institutions in St. Helens.

Following The Main Street Approach, SHEDCO has chosen to focus on the traditional business districts of St Helens; the Houlton Business District (Up Town) and the nationally registered St Helens Downtown Historic District (downtown or Olde Towne) and the area of Columbia Boulevard that connects  those two areas.

Name: Humane Touch Trust
Description: We are an NGO based in Bengaluru, registered as a Trust since 1999. Conducted mass marriages for more than 1000+ couples, provide educational scholarships to students, disburse micro-credit to women entrepreneurs and a lot more!

Name: Be H.O.P.E.
Description: Red de personas y empresas que unidas a través de una plataforma, buscan participar y donar pequeños montos de dinero, para apoyar de forma colectiva, historias publicadas que requieren un incentivo monetario para alcanzar metas. Esta plataforma abarca las necesidades de la comunidad y entrega herramientas para que los potenciales donantes no solo entreguen su aporte monetario, si no que también tengan poder de decisión sobre el mismo y sean partícipe del desarrollo de cada una de las historias.

Name: Colors of Life
Description: Colors of Life is a Section 501(c)(3) non-profit corporation based in Washington, DC, with a mission of combining the evocative power of art with philanthropic efforts, dedicated principally to improving the quality of life and conditions of less-fortunate children.

Name: Namaste Vim Services
Description: Namaste Vim Services is a Sole Proprietorship business founded by Lawrence Tawiah, specializing in providing consultancy services and creating partnerships with existing industrial, corporate, and retail businesses alike to sustainably operate at scale.

Name: FFH Foundation for Youth & Families
Description: FFH Foundation for Youth & Families (FFHFYF) is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization and has been making a difference in the Michigan community of  Farmington and Farmington Hills since 1995. FFHFYF was originally founded to support the after school program for middle school students, but has since expanded its focus to grant funds to many worthwhile programs serving youth and families in the Farmington and Farmington Hills area.  Strategic grants are made twice a year to programs and organizations in the community who benefit youth and families in the areas of critical need, community support and education, or arts and culture.

Name: Figueira Fundo Patrimonial
Description:
O Fundo Figueira tem o objetivo de apoiar causas na área de saúde, educação e assistência social e, para isso, conta com profissionais que buscam transformações positivas na sociedade.
Nosso foco social é formar cidadãos inteligentes capazes de fortalecer as organizações da sociedade civil, fomentar o impacto na sociedade com pessoas de bem e estimular a participação cidadã nos assuntos de interesse público.

Name: Common Ground Canberra
Description: Common Ground Canberra is the national capital's first low-cost supportive housing initiative focused on providing quality tenant outcomes for people who have experienced chronic homelessness.

Name: Instituto 20 mil amigos
Description:
O 20 mil amigos nasceu do profundo desejo de modificar a realidade de crianças que vivem em um hospital ou vivem em home care desde que nasceram ou a maior parte de suas vidas. Essas crianças precisam de tratamento especial e não possuem acesso. Elas muitas vezes tem passado por situações que adultos não conseguiriam imaginar e possuem desafios diários pela sobrevivência.

Queremos que essas crianças tenham oportunidade de serem crianças, fora de um hospital ou tendo acesso ao tratamento adequado.

Desenvolvemos ações de apoio, incentivo e arrecadação de fundos que são destinados diretamente para essas crianças, visando solucionar os problemas e garantir sua qualidade de vida.

A doação de valores é repassada para as crianças ou, na maioria das vezes, diretamente do doador para as contas das crianças. Por isso temos muito cuidado ao iniciar uma nova campanha sem, contudo, deixar de ter um olhar humano sobre os pedidos dirigidos a nós.

Muitas vezes podemos ser a primeira porta para a solução e o primeiro vislumbre de esperança. Nosso trabalho é pensado por um grupo de pessoas, unido e em constante contato. Temos por princípio manter um número de crianças sendo atendidas compatível com o número de profissionais envolvidos e de administradores livres para assumir novas campanhas. Em cada campanha há sempre um dos integrantes do grupo.

Se você se identificou com nosso perfil de atuação e se sente apto emocionalmente a participar da vida de crianças que lutam pela vida, convidamos para que faça parte dessa história.

Name: PROOF Positive Fund
Description:
PROOF Positive Fund is PROOF Coffee Roasters' philanthropic arm.

By way of PROOF Positive Fund, PROOF Coffee Roasters donates 5% profit across the board to charities focused on creating a massive positive impact in the areas of mental health (resources, outreach, and awareness), keeping children from going hungry right here in our communities, ending human rights atrocities at home and abroad, and fighting for the rights and freedoms of all genders, races, ethnicities, and creeds.

Current charities include:
American Foundation for Suicide Prevention
Feed the Children
Amnesty International
ACLU

When you buy PROOF Coffee Roasters' coffee, you're helping directly create a better future (and present) for humankind.

Name: Fondation Je marche vers mon avenir
Description:
Mission et objectifs de la Fondation

Conformément à ses règlements généraux, la Fondation « Je marche vers mon avenir » est un organisme de bienfaisance oeuvrant auprès de la petite enfance autochtone et ce, plus particulièrement auprès de la Nation atikamekw.

Sa mission est de soutenir les parents et leurs enfants âgés de 0 à 5 ans, issus des communautés atikamekw de la province de Québec. Ce soutien est constitué essentiellement de mesures éducatives, de matériel ainsi que de services professionnels. La Fondation veut ainsi permettre à chaque enfant d’atteindre ses rêves et de s’épanouir, physiquement et psychologiquement, malgré les problèmes socioéconomiques et l’isolement des communautés atikamekw.

Pour réaliser cette mission, la Fondation poursuit les objectifs suivants:
Financer la formation et l’information des parents et des éducateurs concernant les habiletés parentales de façon à permettre à chaque enfant d’être encadré et stimulé d’une manière structurée à l’aide d’outils adaptés à la langue et à la culture atikamekw.

Financer du matériel et des services adaptés aux enfants dont ceux ayant des besoins particuliers découlant principalement de la consommation d’alcool et de drogue pendant la grossesse, des handicaps physiques, des retards développementaux dont les troubles du spectre de l’autisme.

Exécuter ou faire exécuter des projets. Travailler d’une manière coordonnée et complémentaire avec les conseils de bande, les services de la santé, de l’éducation, les services sociaux, la sécurité du revenu et la sécurité publique des trois communautés atikamekw. Rechercher du financement dans le secteur public et privé.

Name: Jordan's Way Foundation
Description: Jordan’s Way is a family-led nonprofit organization that fights to end gun violence among youth in the Washington, DC Metropolitan area. The goal of the organization is to foster a commitment to the community and educate the public about the impact of gun violence. Partnerships will be formed with area schools and youth programs to promote confidence, self-worth, and community stewardship. Jordan’s Way feels that through these three pillars, the youth will be empowered with qualities to progress in life.

Name: Krisi
Description: Discern. Be Wise.

Name: Surrendered Artist
Description:
Surrendered Artist is a certified 501c3 non-profit, and publisher of The BIG JOY Journal for kids, who is dedicated to promoting the use of art as a healing tool for coping with physical and emotional stress, and breaking the cycles of trauma and dysfunction. 

We proudly provide educational content, programs, workshops and live events, as well as award resources to equip individuals with the skills needed for self-expression and healing through art to ensure that the next generation of leaders knows how to handle any difficulties that life may throw their way.

We believe that authenticity is today’s currency and through the intentional use of art, we can create a more loving world.  We envision a society where emotional intelligence is valued and cultivated, and where every person has the tools to express themselves, connect with others, and thrive.

Name: Columbia University South Asian Feminism(s) Alliance
Incenter Cares
Description: We are the philanthropic arm to Incenter!

Name: Focused Intensity Foundation
Description:
Focused Intensity Foundation was born out of a desire to pay it forward. Addiction and mental health disorders are issues that affect all social, political, and economic demographics. Founder, Ryan Leaf, was only able to get the treatment he needed through the philanthropy and assistance of others. Focused Intensity Foundation serves to address the gap between the desire for help and the ability to pay for treatment. 
Through partnerships with leading treatment centers across the US, all donations received go directly to scholarships for applicants willing to take an active role in their own transformation. 

Ryan, and the entire team at Focused Intensity Foundation, are committed to help those motivated and dedicated to change, access the resources and guidance they need to build a life of long-lasting recovery.

Name: EmpowerHub - Empowering Women through Education
Description:
The EmpowerHub is a non-profit organization with the mission of empowering women to lead by giving them the tools and strategies to recognize their own innate abilities.

Our vision of strengthening the courage, confidence, and leadership qualities of women will be realized through creating a massive community wide movement, supported by people, non-profit groups, the media and individuals, to transform the quality of life for women around the world.

Name: Fondation Sinkam Charles (FOSIC)
Description: The objective of the Foundation is to promote the Social, Cultural and Economic Development of the Bangou village and its surroundings, for the well-being of its people, the Cameroonian people, humanity in general; this, in accordance with the philosophy and vision of His Majesty Sinkam Charles, Chief Superior of the Bangou village, which gave Bangou village its modern structure and its noted economic, social and cultural development framework, which made Bangou one of the flagship villages of the Bamileke Region. In this context, FOSIC intends to settle in other regions of Cameroon, even in other countries, in order to extend the impact of this philosophy of humanitarian assistance beyond the borders of Cameroon.

Name: The Unlimited Learning Foundation
Description:
The Unlimited Learning Foundation (ULF) creates purpose and freedom through education!

The ULF provides a vehicle for philanthropists from both the private and public sector to make a meaningful and sustainable contribution at the individual, family, business and community level by offering them an opportunity to fund educational training programs focused on personal growth, leadership and business.  

The ULF mission is to change the world one life at a time!

The Unlimited Learning Foundation is in the business of recruiting, developing, and supporting grant-writers. By building teams of independent grant writers, the Unlimited Learning Foundation will be able to secure necessary funding that will enable them to deliver training programs in the areas of Entrepreneurship, Micro-Lending, Financial Literacy, Women’s Empowerment, Personal Development, Schools and Sustainable Communities.

ULF has partnered with Serving Others Worldwide (www.servingothers.org) in an effort to combine programs and resources to bring powerful training, projects and funding opportunities to those in need.

Name: Umama
Description: Women in Uganda play a crucial role in their families and communities but often face barriers in accessing financial resources, education, and skills. Umama is dedicated to empowering women by providing microloans, financial literacy training, and job skills development. These tools enable women to gain financial independence, improve their businesses, and create lasting change in their communities.

Name: منظمة القلوب الرحيمة لكفالة اليتيم والاعمال الخيرية
Description:
1.	حصر الايتام وتبويبهم حسب الحالة الاجتماعية.
2.	الرعاية الصحية والتعليمية والتربوية للأيتام.
3.	منحة مالية شهرية التكفل بالاحتياجات المدرسية حسب الاستطاعة.
4.	مساعدات عينية في الأعياد.
5.	دعم دوي الاحتياجات الخاصة

Name: The Ozzie Foundation
Description: Making the world a better place by nurturing and providing resources to the underserved and underprivileged.

Name: Paul Nikolai Ehlers - Stiftung
Description:
Wer sind wir?

Ins Leben gerufen von der Familie Ehlers mit Wurzeln in Russland und Deutschland, will die Paul Nikolai Ehlers-Stiftung Projekte in Russland und Deutschland unterstützen, die sich dort um die Bildung und Gesundheit bedürftiger Kinder bis zur Volljährigkeit kümmern.

Unsere Mission ist es, so vielen Kindern wie möglich Halt, Orientierung und ein kleines bisschen Hoffnung auf eine bessere Zukunft zu geben. Die zentralen Stiftungsinhalte sind daher die Themen Bildung und Gesundheit.

Um diese hochgesteckten Ziele auch erreichen zu können, sind wir auf die Unterstützung von Menschen wie Ihnen angewiesen. Helfen Sie uns dabei, anderen zu helfen und leisten Sie bereits mit einem kleinen Beitrag etwas ganz Großes! Da die Personal- und Sachkosten ein Privatunternehmen  übernimmt, kommt der gesamte Betrag Ihrer Spende direkt einem oder sogar mehreren sorgfältig ausgewählten Projekten zugute.

Name: Many-Me EU Project
Description:
The project “Social Interactive Care System to support the wellbeing of people living with dementia” (acronym: Many-Me) is a European research project within the Active and Assisted Living Programme. Many-Me is realized under Call 2016 “Living well with dementia”.

The Many-Me project aims to improve wellbeing and help to overcome physical, psychological and financial problems related to progressive intellectual deterioration which is a characteristic symptom for dementia.

Many-Me builds a social interactive care system using information and communication technology (ICT) and user-centered services to help people with dementia, their relatives, informal and formal carers.

Placing older people in long care homes is not a good option, both in terms of financial burdens and, particularly, negative influence of new environment on older people (especially those with dementia) and their dislike for this option. Therefore we believe that seeking for holistic approaches concerning not only people with dementia, but also their relatives, informal carers, is of a great importance. The latter face high pressure, lack of time and capacity for giving care as well as changed behavior of the diseased people. In result, they often fall in depression.

A novelty brought by Many-Me is a personalization of content of an ICT support. People with moderate and mild dementia are generally able to use simple electronic devices which enable to improve their self-confidence. However, appropriate design and meeting the real needs of people with dementia, are among main challenges that providers of an ICT care systems for older adults must try to overcome.
