Name: Avenir pour l'enfant des rizières - ONG
Description:
L'Association Française A.E.R. de Solidarité Internationale, Loi 1901, fondée en 1979, s'est donné pour vocation, au Cambodge, d'accompagner des enfants vivant en situation de précarité de la maternelle à l'enseignement supérieur afin qu'ils puissent se donner les moyens de se construire un avenir meilleur et de participer au mieux au développement de leur pays.

 Au Nord-Ouest du pays, dans la province de Banteay Meanchey, de nombreuses familles rurales ne parviennent pas à assumer totalement le coût de la scolarité de leurs enfants. 
 Alors, dans le cadre d'une coopération avec les familles, dans le respect de la culture et des particularismes locaux, et en liaison avec les autorités du pays, l'A.E.R. œuvre à l'accompagnement des familles pour l’éducation et la formation des enfants (notamment grâce au parrainage individuel), à la réalisation, puis à la maintenance d'infrastructures (écoles, postes de santé, ouvrages hydrauliques, centres d'accueil, logements pour les élèves du secondaire,...), mais également à des programmes de développement rural et agricole.

L'organisation c'est : 
• Au Cambodge : un coordinateur des programmes, des surveillants et une cuisinière, tous cambodgiens et salariés de l'AER.
• En France : 12 administrateurs , des comités régionaux, des traducteurs, tous bénévoles, des adhérents, des parrains et des donateurs.
Un siège à Toulouse, 15 chemin du Chèvrefeuille, 31300.
Une équipe dirigée par le Président, Bernard Artero, organisée en commissions :
 parrainage •  éducation •  communication •  recherche de fonds •  trésorerie.

Pour nous soutenir :
 parrainage d'un enfant
 parrainage de programmes
 faire un don

n'hésitez pas à aller visiter notre site internet ou à contacter l'association pour davantage de renseignements. 
 Adresse mail de contact : aer.contactasso@gmail.com
Instagram : aer_cambodge

Name: Global Sun Foundation
Description:
Our inspiration, the Sun, is by far the most magnificent source of energy and life on Earth. 
It is an unstoppable force of nature, constantly reminding us that we are all stardust. 
We are actually... all the same.

Global Sun foundation is a blockchain supported foundation, intended to provide a better future for the children that struggle with diseases.

Global Sun foundation is based in Puerto Rico, with its first subsidiary in Belgrade, Serbia.

The first goal of the foundation: Helping sick children to have a better quality of life.

Find out how can you get involved on: https://www.globalsunfoundation.com/

Name: Jonn P.B.R. DuClau Memorial Foundation
Description: The Jonn P.B.R. DuClau Memorial Foundation, a Rhode Island Non-Profit organization, whose mission is to provide charitable assistance to Veterans who served during Operation Iraqi Freedom (OIF) and Operation Enduring Freedom (OEF) and their families suffering from disabling conditions, related to their service.

Name: Kenya Orphans Foundation
Description:
The Kenya Orphans Foundation (Stichting Weeskinderen Kenia) lets the Kenyan orphans share the wealth of the Dutch, so that they can have a better future. The Kenya Orphans Foundation consists entirely of volunteers. The foundation was founded on March 21st 1995 by a number of KLM-crew members and others who provided help on an individual basis to an orphanage in Nairobi. Today the foundation provides support to a number of small projects in three orphanages in Nairobi. The foundation organizes and coordinates projects in the Netherlands all aimed at raising funds and it receives donations.
At least 95 % of all donations go to the orphans.

Name: Sunderland LGBT+ Network
Description:
Sunderland LGBT+ Network is community-based, not for profit voluntary organisation.   

Our Aims:

To promote equality and diversity and advances in education. 
To help eliminate discrimination against the LGBT+ community in Sunderland and the North East

We also organise Sunderland's annual Pride event.

Name: Fundación Aliatar
Description: Fundación Solidaria

Name: P&K Pühringer Gemeinnützige Stiftung
Description: P&K Pühringer charitable foundation is focused on improving research and education in Austria, Germany and Switzerland. At P&K, we are building longstanding successful partnerships with leading institutions to foster change.

Name: Ozark STEM Foundation
Description: Connecting K-16 students to STEM events and funding in NWA since 2014

Name: Nadace Naděje dětem
Description:
Nadace Naděje dětem začala na území České republiky působit v roce 2005 a je podporována společností Swiss Life Select. Finanční prostředky plynou do nadace zcela unikátním způsobem, a to tak, že každý privátní finanční poradce společnosti Swiss Life Select v České republice měsíčně přispívá do nadace Naděje dětem jedním procentem ze svého výdělku.

Nadace Naděje dětem si klade za cíl nezištně pomáhat dětem, které se ocitly v ohrožení, potřebují pomoc pro zlepšení kvality života a zmírnění utrpení, jež jim způsobují často nevyléčitelné nemoci.

V České republice žije mnoho nemocných dětí v nouzi, pro mnohé je naše pomoc životně důležitá a ulehčuje jejich nepříznivý osud.

Name: CFUW K-W
Description:
The Canadian Federation of University Women Kitchener-Waterloo (CFUW K-W) offers a forum for women to meet for camaraderie, intellectual curiosity, involvement and service to the community.

We are a vibrant, welcoming group of around 200 women of all disciplines and ages, meeting to hear monthly guest speakers, and in 40+ special interest groups to share activities, friendship and fun.

Membership is open to all women who share our vision.

Name: TETEA Inc
Description:
TETEA is dedicated to expanding educational opportunities in Tanzania through providing scholarships to needy students and working to develop learning centers in the country.

TETEA was founded in November 2008 and is a registered 501(c)(3) non-profit organization. In Tanzania, TETEA works with a partner organization of the same name, which is a registered NGO.

Name: Elevation Society
Description:
We are a nonprofit with a mission to ultimately live in a world where there are no more suicides. 

Through focusing on protective factors, we aim to reach people before suicide becomes their only option. Our current goals include the following: 

1. Normalize the conversation, and make sure all people regardless of income and location have access to standard mental health resources. 

2. Collect data to find common barriers that prevent people from seeking help.

3. Curate unique events focused on bringing people together and promoting healthy mindset practices

From its very founding, Elevation Society has always sought to combat suicide through leveraging the power of community.
 
Within a few years, Elevation Society has been fortunate enough to,
host 30+ community events in 5 different states. Through awareness campaigns, online content, and events Elevation has reached over 13,500+ lives.

Name: LBSA Fund
Description: We strive to engage and grow alumni relationships to assist Latinos continued professional growth. In our efforts we have given out over $91,000 in  scholarships used for educational and living expenses and continue to strengthen community bonds through networking events and mentorship both to students and alumni.

Name: Nottingham Park Garden Trail
Description: The Park Garden Trail is held every two years, where residents of Nottingham's historic Park Estate open their gardens to the public. Around 5000 visitors attend - each receiving a glossy A5 programme. As well as gardens, there is a central space containing food stalls, a mini farmers' market, entertainment and children's activities. All proceeds from the event are donated to 10 small local charities. PGT has been running since 1986, and the last event in 2019 raised a record £31500 for our charities. Sponsorship and advertising opportunities are available.

Name: Cupertino Rotary Club
Description: We are the Cupertino Rotary Club, part of the 1.3 Million member of Rotary International. Our motto of Service above Self defines our mission- to do good in our community, our country and the world.

Name: Friends Foundation
Description:
We created Friends Foundation to help make the non-profit sector more professional.

Friends Foundation was founded by three companions: Yan Yanovskiy,  Gor Nahapetyan and Dmitriy Yampolsky in 2015 to help the Russian charity industry become more professional.
Having supported various non-for-profit organizations and individuals for years, the three friends have come to a realization that the non-commercial sector requires systematic changes.
Structurally, Friends Foundation is a “foundation of foundations”, a combination of opportunities, knowledge, resources and experience. We do not partake in direct support and assistance, we are not mediators between the donators and charity foundations, and we do not support the foundations during their entire life term, but instead we guide them to become more stable and self-reliable.

Name: CKFC Fonds Solidaire
Description: CKFC est le premier fonds de dotation chinois en France. Basé à Paris, il a été autorisé et certifié par le gouvernement français en juillet 2014. D’une vision dépassant les limites des territoires et des peuples, CKFC se concentre sur l’humanisme. CKFC unit l’ensemble des acteurs solidaires, afin de favoriser l’intégration et l’insertion professionnelle des jeunes et des étudiants chinois dans la société française, et ainsi de diffuser le plus largement possible le concept « rendre service aux autres». De cette manière, la philanthropie chinoise se joint aux grands thèmes des actions solidaires françaises.

Name: Awesome Foundation Portland
Description:
We fund awesome projects in the Portland area in arts, culture, and community through $1,000 micro-grants.

Our criteria for funding projects are:
1. Is it awesome?
2. Does it benefit Portand or the surrounding areas?
3. Will $1,000 make an actual impact?
4. Is it immediately actionable?

Awesome Portland is a chapter of the Awesome Foundation for the Arts and Sciences, an international network of autonomous chapters of micro-philanthropists. Chapter members (called "trustees") each give $100 to fund the grants. We are always on the hunt for project submissions and for trustees, so please reach out!

Name: Baton Rouge High School Foundation
Description: The Baton Rouge High School Foundation’s mission is to encourage philanthropy and fellowship of alumni, families of students, faculty and the community so that Baton Rouge High School will continue to advance in educational excellence, student achievement and national prominence.

Name: Helmets to Heal LLC.
Description:
Helmets to Heal (H2H) is a not for profit organization created in August of 2016. Our mission at Helmets to Heal is to decrease the risk of traumatic brain injuries in sports, while increasing youth sports participation. Through our 3 step assessment platform, we look to donate, mitigate, and participate in every program we partner with. Our belief is simple, if we can protect every youth athlete while creating a bond of trust with the parents together with our business partners we can #savethebrain and #savethegame. 

H2H is the official community outreach program for Xenith. H2H has also partnered with the finest companies in athletic safety. Our proud partners are Athlete Intelligence, Team Safe, Hobert- Mayfield, Tackle Tubes USA, and 2nd Skull. You can learn more about us on Facebook, Twitter, and our website.
FB @helmetstoheal, Twitter @helmetsheal, website www.helmetstoheal.com.

Name: Online Giving Foundation Ltd
Description: We are passionate about inspiring others to get involved in events to raise money for charity in Australia and making it as easy as possible for people to donate. We are committed to professional and ethical fundraising and are working to transform the face of charitable giving in Australia. We love what we do and we hope it shows.

Name: Adrian Schools Educational Foundation
Description: Supporting the Educational Excellence of Adrian Public Schools

Name: Cure MECP2 Bucks County
Description:
The Rett Syndrome Research Trust’s Duplication Fund spurs and supports research on MECP2 Duplication Syndrome, a devastating neurological disorder. With support from the Duplication Fund, in 2015, Dr. Huda Zoghbi of Baylor College of Medicine demonstrated for the first time that a dramatic reversal of Duplication Syndrome is possible. Her research also showed preliminary data for a potential treatment.

CURE MECP2 BUCKS COUNTY is raising funds to continue this exciting scientific momentum. We are confident that this research will have an impact on our son, Bo Spangler, and the lives of others struggling with this disorder.

Name: Ignite a Dream
Description:
Austin based non profit organization created in order to provide after school athletic programs to the at risk youth of Austin, Texas. Founded by Carrie Arsenault, Executive Director is Dinka Hazdic (dinka@igniteadream.com.)


MISSION 
 
Our mission is to ignite the desire of at risk youth communities in Austin to become lifelong leaders by actively engaging them in safe and structured sport activities through tennis, soccer and other athletic programs. Ignite a Dream will achieve its mission by providing all underprivileged children with athletic opportunities that will teach them valuable life skills as well as provide them with tools, resources and the passion necessary to excel.
 
 
 GOALS 
To work with schools that lack financial resources to help create tennis and soccer programs 
To raise awareness within communities for long term sustainability 
To educate children through sports to lead healthy and constructive lives 
To provide character building as well as life enhancing programs


 
  
 WHAT CAN IGNITE A DREAM ACCOMPLISH?

 

• Higher levels of fitness are associated with better academic performance 

 

• Higher levels of fitness were associated with better school attendance. 

 

• Higher levels of fitness were also associated with fewer disciplinary incidents involving drugs, alcohol, violence and truancy.

WITH YOUR HELP...

Ignite A Dream can touch the lives of many at risk children in the Austin area.

Name: Lions Club Paris Montaigne
Description: Le Lions Club Paris Montaigne est un club d'excellence, réunissant des personnes de tout horizon, de tout âge, et de tout genre, pour se dépasser au travers de causes humanitaires : diabète, cécité, jeunesse, solidarité, environnement, culture

Name: Sapphire Corporation Pvt. Ltd.
Description:
Sapphire was formed in the year 2007 as a corporate entity to implement the vision of the directors who had been researching and planning viable business opportunities in J&K since year 2002. Harnessing Natural resources was the central idea in the ecosystem of Sapphire Corp. Post the unrest of 2008, the sapphire core team did a revision of the roadmap viz a viz the conditions on the ground and the plan was revisited keeping the conditions, resources and needs in mind.  It was thus decided to take the Social-Entrepreneurship route with a focus on thrust areas viz Health, Education, Environment, Infrastructure & Agriculture. The plan was mobilized by allocating resources towards HealthCARE under CSR (Corporate Social Responsibility). Subsequently, other sectors were rolled out in a phased manner.

Under SHI centers equipped for GIS mapping and socio-ecological data collection were set-up in identified areas to understand the resources, needs & pain areas. Based on the collected data indigenous location specific plans for socio-ecological & economic development plans were prepared.

The Sapphire vision envisions these citizens as potential partners and clients that would see Sapphire towards its cherished goal.

Name: Generational Wealth and Planning (GWAP)
Description:
Generational Wealth and Planning (GWAP) focuses on providing real-world personal finance education to Philadelphia's youth in low-income neighborhoods.

Every two years, the Council for Economic Education (CEE) conducts a comprehensive look into the state of K-12 economic and financial education in the United States. The 2020 survey of the states show 25 states require a high school course to be offered. Pennsylvania is not included on that list. 

Through classroom-based as well as direct to student interaction, GWAP looks to fill that void in underrepresented areas of Philadelphia.

Name: Camp Kesem at Cornell University
Description:
Kesem is a nationwide community, driven by passionate college student leaders, that supports children through and beyond their parent's cancer.

A program of Kesem, Camp Kesem operates free summer camps for children who have been impacted by a parent's cancer.

Founded at Stanford University in 2000, Camp Kesem has since expanded to over 100 chapters in 40 states across the country.

The Cornell chapter was founded in 2014 and rapidly growing in University members and campers.

Name: Northern Marianas Coalition Against Domestic & Sexual Violence
Description: The Northern Marianas Coalition Against Domestic & Sexual Violence is a 501(c)3 nonprofit organization that officially began on March 21, 2007 by members in the community with the mission "to promote, nourish, and sustain a collective movement to end violence in the CNMI." NMCADSV works to fulfill this mission by addressing the issues of domestic violence and sexual assault through community outreach efforts, providing training to service providers and responders, nurturing meaningful partnerships with groups or individuals, as well as working to create social change.

Name: South African Muslim Charitable Trust
Description: The South African Muslim Charitable Trust (SAMCT) was originally established in 2008 to act as a conduit for the provision of funding assets, services and other resources to approved public benefit organisations. Since its creation, the Trust has made significant donations towards poverty alleviation, empowerment, community upliftment and sustainability programmes.

Name: Camino de Santiago  |  Jakobsweg  |  St. James  |  | San Giacomo |  St. Jacques
Description:
The Camino de Santiago (Latin: Peregrinatio Compostellana, "Pilgrimage of Compostela"; Galician: O Camiño de Santiago),[1] known in English as the Way of St. James, is a network of pilgrims' ways or pilgrimages leading to the shrine of the apostle Saint James the Great in the cathedral of Santiago de Compostela in Galicia in northwestern Spain, where tradition has it that the remains of the saints are buried there. Many follow its routes as a form of spiritual path or retreat for their spiritual growth. It is also popular with hiking and cycling enthusiasts and organized tour groups.

Source: Wikipedia

Name: 100X Development Foundation
Description:
We work to generate and support creative solutions to eradicate poverty and its negative effects by operating on the principle of Mark 4:8, "Other seeds fell into the good soil, and as they grew up and increased, they yielded a crop and produced thirty, sixty, and a hundredfold.” 

Through the innovative use of technology and strategic public private partnerships, we come alongside and empower individuals and organizations that are implementing best practice programs that transform the lives of impoverished communities throughout the world.

Name: Arab African Solidarity Association
Description: The Arab African Solidarity Association is a Nigerien charity with a global strategic vision for social development.

Name: Rivera Memorial Foundation, Inc.
Description:
The Rivera Memorial Foundation, Inc. provides families of all backgrounds the opportunity to enroll their children into quality, affordable programs. 

The Rivera Memorial Foundation, Inc. exists first and foremost to develop the full human potential of its participants by fostering their intellectual, social, economical, emotional, moral and spiritual growth.

On May 10, 1990 Firefighter Heriberto “Eddie” Rivera and fellow firefighter Howard Hughes, were killed when their engine lost its brakes while heading to a false alarm and slammed into a tree on West Main Street. Shortly afterward, family and friends began gathering at what was then West Dover Street Park for picnics and other events held in the men’s memory.

Thus, for the over twenty years Maritza Rivera (widow), family, and friends of Eddie, have continued in Heriberto’s memory, a tradition of selfless giving to the youths, Latinos, and general community of Waterbury. Therefore, in an effort to address the growing needs of the youths, Latinos, and other residents of the city of Waterbury, several grassroots Associations have come together to form a non-profit corporation for the purpose of maintaining and continuing the memory and spirit of one of its dear friends and neighbors who was tragically taken from the community, in honor and memory of Mr. Rivera.

Name: Grain de sable - FGES
Description:
Grain de Sable est une association humanitaire de la FGES et soutient le village Ifri au Maroc.
Nous avons besoin de votre aide pour développer des projets pour le village et récolter le maximum de fournitures scolaires et fonds pour les enfants.

Name: Adopta una Familia, Inc.
Description:
AUF is a United States based, non-profit, 501(c)3 organization partnering with community leaders in Guayaquil, Ecuador in three key development areas. Our goal is to use local and global resources in the most efficient and effective way to promote opportunities for quality

1.	Housing
2.	Education, and
3.	Health

Created as a grass-roots community project in the barrio of Guasmo Sur, Ecuador in 1999, AUF began with building bathrooms in families’ homes. It has since grown into a multi-tiered program that has enriched the lives of hundreds of people in Ecuador and North America. As a by-product of the program’s success, AUF is proud of its history of inspiring service in those that have participated over the years.

Name: Operation Embrace
Description: Operation Embrace (OE) offers assistance to injured survivors of terror in Israel. With the expert guidance from social workers throughout Israel and Bituach Leumi (Israel’s National Insurance Agency), Operation Embrace helps all people who have been violated by random acts of terror and does not discriminate by race or religion.Operation Embrace provides direct assistance as well as group support through a variety of PTSD and trauma support programs. OE funds medical, therapeutic, and rehabilitative needs to help people move forward. The goal is to facilitate a brighter future and to help individuals who suffer with physical/emotional trauma to rebuild their lives.

Name: Youai Scotland CIC 有爱苏格兰
Description:
Youai Scotland CIC is a a Chinese Volunteers Non-profitable organization that exists to help Chinese students integrate into local communities through participating volunteering work. 

It aims to improve Chinese students’ life experience during their study in Scotland, help them improve their English skill, understand more about local culture, promote Chines culture to local society and become a knowledgable and experienced culture ambassador in the end of their study time. 

It will also conduct fundraising activities to support school-aged children in poorer area of China to continue their study and aim to provide volunteering teaching opportunities in future. 

We hope to build up a standard of Chinese Overseas Students, set up good examples to motivate the whole community to learn more, participate more and return more during their year of study. 

1. Voluntary activities

- Thrift Shop: 
Collect second hand goods from mainly Chinese overseas students and resell them for fund. 

- Happy Chinese Class: 
Teach Chinese language, promote the culture and discuss the differences. 

- Food Bank:
Alliance with local Chinese restaurants to donate Chinese hot meals to shelters and charity organisations. 

2. Other activities: 

Educational activities: organise workshops about western social skills, English skills, work skills, business skills, etc. 
Social activities: visit local families, make internationals friends and hang out with people from various backgrounds. 
Physical activities: play sports games with local sports club. 
Adventurous activities: organise trips and expeditions to explore Scotland

3. Chinese Overseas Student Award: 

Based on how much they learned from Voluntary, Educational, Social, Physical and Adventurous activities, we will grant them COS Awards in Gold, Silver and Bronze. They can also be awarded Duke of Edinburgh Awards if they meet the standard.

Name: FutureNow Youth Mentor's Association
Description: By exposing our young to high quality mentoring networks, we empower them young holistically to become healthy and well-adjusted adults who will be exemplary parents, mentors, leaders & entrepreneurs in their community

Name: Dance Marathon at UW-Madison
Description:
In our inaugural year, Dance Marathon at UW-Madison will be supporting Children’s Wisconsin, the local CMN Hospital in Milwaukee, WI. 

Miracle Network Dance Marathonis an international movement, involving over 400 colleges, universities and K-12 schools across North America that fundraise for their localChildren’s Miracle Network Hospitals.Since its inception in 1991, Miracle Network Dance Marathon has raised more than $300 million–ensuring that no child or family fights pediatric illness or injury alone.

Name: Barbera Foundation
Description:
Barbera Foundation is a tax-exempt 501(c)(3) non-profit organization whose mission is to promote positive change by inspiring others to engage in the community and help those less fortunate.

In order to accomplish this mission, Barbera Foundation organizes a wide variety of events that unite people from all walks of life. From delivering care packages of food and supplies to the homeless and preparing Thanksgiving dinner for those less fortunate during the holiday season, to supporting underprivileged kids with books and school supplies, Barbera Foundation strives to improve the lives of those in need to make the community as a whole, a better place for everyone.

The success of the Barbera Foundation relies 100% on volunteer and donor contributions. As a result, we need YOUR help!

Want to get involved? Every contribution, big or small, goes a long way in helping others and yourself to live a more positive and fulfilling life.

As Mahatma Gandhi once said: “The best way to find yourself is to lose yourself in the service of others.” 

Make your mark on our community by doing something positive for someone else. You have the potential to make a lasting impact and to be a part of something bigger than yourself. Be the change you wish to see in the world.

www.barberafoundation.org


Subscribe to our newsletter: http://eepurl.com/c-Q1jL

Name: Food and Environment
Description: "Food and Environment" is a science communication website run by a few professionals and activists dedicated to contribute to make the world better place to live. The website shares the knowledge, experiences, news and views in the broad area of food security, climate change, sustainability, and related field. We also advocate to enhance food security, to protect the environment and simply for anything to make the world better.

Name: Mommy and Me Cancer Foundation
Description:
Mommy and Me Cancer Foundation (MAMCF) is a 501(c)3 non-profit organization whose mission is to support mothers battling cancer, who also raise minor children. Our work is dedicated to funding and delivering charitable services through the help of dedicated members and volunteers.  We strive to inspire and improve the lives of those who need assistance.

Jennifer Boyd, CEO and Co-Founder of Mommy and Me Cancer Foundation (MAMCF) started the organization at a point in her life where she, herself, was ill and caring for a young one at the same time.  As a single mom, she experienced first hand how difficult it was to do things alone.  When one of her dearest friends got diagnosed with cancer, she was inspired to create this non-profit so that she could help other ill moms.  

In 2013, MAMCF started with the help of a small team and volunteers, supporting a young mom battling cancer while simultaneously raising a toddler. Over the years, MAMCF has been able to help many moms and their families thanks to our dedicated team, donors/sponsors, and volunteers. We help families with financial assistance, peer to peer support, and provide their children with education in regards to what is happening to mommy. Our goal is to ensure that these moms and their families that they are not fighting their battle alone.

Name: Project Silence No More
Description:
Our mission is to end the stigma surrounding mental health, depression, and suicide within our community.

Many organizations exist with the mission of ending the stigma around mental health. However, our community is unique compared to most which are served by these organizations. Just as the members of our community are unique, so are the issues we face. We’ve partnered with school district administrators and other community leaders to develop a plan to address these issues and make our community a better place.

Name: Swiftwater Search & Rescue
Description:
Saving lives when disaster strikes; of both people and pets alike. We are a pet-centric search and rescue organization that recognizes the incredible connection that people and pets share, and the unique challenges pets face during disaster recovery.

Whether it be in the form of search and rescue extraction, transporting supplies or cleaning and bandaging injured pets – wherever we can have the most impact is where you will find us.

Our M.O. is maximum efficacy with full transparency. Being a nonprofit we want to ensure that all those who support our cause know precisely where their donations are going. Therefore, all fundraising and subsequent expenses will be posted directly to our site so that anyone can see where every dollar that comes in is being applied. 100% of all donations will be spent on search and rescue efforts/equipment or disaster relief supplies. All of our volunteers’ time and personal resources which are utilized are paid for out of our own pockets.

If you are interested in donating, becoming a partner or learning more about our organization please visit: swiftwatersearchandrescue.org

Name: Main dans la Main
Description: Main dans la Main est une ONG créée en 1988 par Marie Nomo Messina. L'association humanitaire a pour vocation de protéger les enfants les plus vulnérables de Douala : orphelins, enfants abandonnés, enfants victimes de violence ou victimes de trafic, de prostitution entre autres.

Name: Lions Club Vélizy Concorde
Description:
Les Lions sont des hommes et des femmes libres qui ont décidé d’ajouter à leur vie une dimension humaniste et humanitaire. Leur devise est “ Nous Servons ”.
Les Lions définissent leurs actions au cours de réunions toutes les trois semaines environ, pendant lesquelles ils partagent des valeurs d’amitié et de solidarité, leur diversité socioculturelle permettant à chacun d’élargir sa vision de la société.
Les Lions visent à l’épanouissement de la personne humaine. Ils développent de multiples opérations locales, nationales et internationales dans le domaine du savoir, de l’éducation et de la culture. Ils favorisent le rapprochement entre les peuples par les échanges de jeunes du monde entier.
Les Lions apportent leur soutien aux plus démunis. Leurs actions peuvent être mondiales, comme lors de catastrophes naturelles majeures ou pour des programmes en faveur de grandes causes telle la lutte contre la cécité évitable. Au quotidien, elles sont nationales et locales, reflétant l’implication profonde des Lions dans la vie de la cité.
Les Lions financent leurs multiples actions en organisant des opérations menées par eux-mêmes. La totalité des résultats financiers de ces manifestations est affectée à l’œuvre définie au préalable. Les frais de fonctionnement du Lions Clubs sont entièrement couverts par les cotisations des Lions.
Le Lions Clubs International est le plus grand Club Service du monde, avec ses 1 400 000 membres dans 46 000 clubs implantés dans 210 pays et zones géographiques, dont 27 000 en France comptant 1 207 clubs. Reconnu en tant qu’ONG, Le Lions Club International dispose d’un siège consultatif dans les grandes institutions internationales (ONU, UNESCO, UNICEF, OMS et Conseil de l’Europe).

Name: Vrijmetselaarsloge Concordia Vincit Animos
Description:
Loge Concordia Vincit Animos (CVA) behoort met haar rangnummer 5 tot de oudste vrijmetselaarsloges van Nederland. Vanaf haar oprichting, meer dan 250 jaar geleden, heeft CVA zich doen kennen door vernieuwende initiatieven, zowel binnen de vrijmetselarij als in de maatschappij.

Vrijmetselarij Loge Concordia Vincit Animos is één van de twaalf loges die in Amsterdam actief zijn. Zij beoefent de symbolieke graden van leerling-, gezel- en meestervrijmetselaar. Onze loge telt 60 leden en komt iedere donderdagavond bijeen in het logegebouw van de Amsterdamse Loges aan de Vondelstraat.

Name: Urgence-Telecom
Description:
L'association :
Urgence-Telecom a été créé en 2009. C'est une association humanitaire et de sécurité civile à destination des populations sinistrées et des collectivités, touchées par une catastrophe naturelle.

Pour répondre à un besoin vital :
L'association Urgence-Telecom a été créé pour répondre à une demande importante des sinistrés au moment d'une situation d'urgence créée par une catastrophe naturelle ou un évènement majeur. Alors que les moyens de communication publiques sont hors d'usage, ou que l'accès à l'énergie est devenu impossible, nous intervenons au plus près des personnes touchées pour leur permettre de joindre leurs proches.

Utiliser des moyens adaptables :
Les réseaux sociaux sont devenus incontournables pour acheminer les messages en masse des populations sinistrés ou réduire les risques par la prévention. Nous intervenons avec les réseaux sociaux pour les pays industrialisés, mais avec des moyens adaptés pour les pays ou régions avec peu d'infrastructure. Il s'agit aussi de s'adapter aux situations complexes sur le terrain : Il peux s'agir simplement de fournir un point d'énergie pour recharger les téléphones portables, ou un "call center" pour permettre à tous de joindre un proche.

Des volontaires engagés :
Les volontaires ont de multiples compétences. C'est aussi leur engagement qui nous permet d'intervenir au plus vite et avec les moyens adaptés.

Name: Antipode Inde
Description:
L'association Antipode Inde, fondée en 1998 par un groupe d'étudiants ingénieurs de l'Icam (Institut Catholique  des Arts et Métiers) site de Nantes, a pour but d'apporter un soutien économique et humain aux Intouchables du  village de Mangalapuram en Inde.
Ainsi, l’association, composée de huit étudiants en deuxième année à l'Icam, récolte des fonds tout au long de l’année pour permettre la construction d'infrastructures en Inde que nous réalisons conjointement avec une main-d’œuvre locale. Depuis 1998, une soixantaine de maisons, une citerne d’eau, l'annexe d'une maternité et des satinaires pour une école  ont été construits dans le sud-est de l’Inde, à Mangalapuram, un village situé à 50 km de Pondichéry.

Name: Rotaract Club of Vancouver
Description:
The Rotaract Club of Vancouver is a group of globally connected youth who cultivate relationships and leadership skills through community service. We engage in a wide range of hands-on service projects, fun networking and social activities, and professional development opportunities. As a member of the Rotaract organization, we are a part of an international network of 175,000 young leaders belonging to over 7,500 clubs in 156 countries.

We are part of Rotaract District 5040, a dynamic group of 8 clubs around British Columbia full of youth passionate about leadership development, community service, and bringing peace and international understanding to the world.

We are sponsored by the Rotary Club of Vancouver.

Name: Bettie Brand Mothers' Empowerment Fund
Description: The Bettie Brand fund gives stipends to “provide women in need with a hand up, not a hand out, so they may live with dignity and independence.” Women chosen to receive stipends from the Bettie Brand Fund must agree to “pay it forward” in whatever way they can and whenever they are ready.

Name: Robert G. Wilmers Integrity Prize
Description: An annual award for people making a difference in their community.

Name: Offthestreets
Description: Off the Streets are a group of concerned residents who felt more needed to be done to help the rough sleepers and to come alongside to give them a better chance to move along their journey. We opened up a night shelter in Beedell avenue in September 2018 with the motto `a stepping stone and not a stopping point`. Ever since then we have worked to help our guests get housed and lead successful lives, after all, any of us could find ourselves homeless at any point. In the past year we have been awarded the Housing Justice Kitemark at Excellence Standard and become a registered charity (1184331).

Name: Inventshift.com
Description:
With an idea born to contribute to solving global issues in August 2016 while taking a road trip in Melbourne, Inventshift was found in January 2017 and launched in April 2017 by founders Iranthi Gomes and Jarkko Oksanen.

Inventshift.com is the largest community to raise awareness for social enterprise, conscious buying and ethical businesses

Inventshift is the largest online community raising awareness for social enterprise, ethical businesses and conscious buying. 

We work with ServiceHired.com that enables to book any local service around the world from caterers to plumbers to accountants. Through every booking ServiceHired.com contributes 10% of their revenue to the Inventshift fund with a goal to help eradicate poverty through education.

Our vision is to make it easy for existing businesses to turn social, and for new entrepreneurs to start off social. The goal is simply  to educate and grow interest on social enterprise.

At Inventshift.com you can;
- Apply to access the Inventshift fund if you are a charity working around education
- Browse the largest social enterprise directory in the world
- Browse the social enterprise job board
- Read all social enterprise related content on the blog

Are you social enterprise or an ethical business and would like to be listed on the social enterprise directory for free? Please let us know by sending an email to iranthi@inventshift.com

Name: Hamstrings Club, UCH
Description: A student-run Sociophilanthropic organisation established in 1985 with the aim of improving the lives of the less-privileged in the society.

Name: Zeta Kappa Rho
Description: This international sorority was founded in Stirling, Scotland in August, 2015. Founded at the University of Stirling by Annika Hamann and Victoria Martin, this sorority aimed to unite girls from different backgrounds and provide a safe haven for them as they adjusted to University life.

Name: Associação Empresarial de São Tomé e Príncipe - AESTP
Misty Crabtree Eastham Endowment
Description:
In 2011, after being diagnosed with cancer at the age of 36, Misty asked that I donate a portion of her life insurance to the Girl Scouts-North Carolina Coastal Pines Council (GS-NCCP) as her way of giving back to the organization that rescued her from homelessness and helped her attend college. This endowment is the product of that wish and will be an enduring legacy in her honor. Currently it provides $5,000 in scholarships to two deserving Girl Scouts annually.

Despite coming from a broken home, enduring abuse, and a mother that suffered from severe alcoholism, she found the courage and compassion to serve others her entire adult life. As a military wife of 15 years, she stood by me despite us often being separated due to countless schools, field exercises, and three combat deployments. We moved often, but she was always upbeat about the next duty station and would volunteer her time and money in every community in which we lived. She started or co-led Girl Scout troops, provided support for other military spouses, was active in her churches, and even became a volunteer Vehicle Extrication Technician. She completed an Associate's Degree in Food Service Management, a Bachelor's Degree in Public Management, a Business Administrative Technology Certificate, and had been accepted into a Pharmacy Tech program.

To date, this Endowment has awarded $36,600 in scholarships since its founding in 2013.

Visit www.caringbridge.org/visit/MistyEastham, www.nccoastalpines.org, and https://www.facebook.com/MistyCrabtreeEasthamEndowment to learn more about her, her struggle with cancer, and her Endowment.

Please make a tax deductible donation by clicking the "Visit Website" button or go to https://gsnccp.wufoo.com/forms/q1bs58n91041bpu/.

Name: HackCancerSF
Description: Founded in 2013, HackCancer is a group of philanthropic, young professionals in the San Francisco Bay Area who create fun events that bring together great people to raise awareness and funds for good causes. Over the past 2 years, they have raised almost $500,000 for the Leukemia & Lymphoma Society and other cancer related non-profits.

Name: p.oven
Description: On the cold shoulder of the windy Galway prom, a great man rose from the sands and the sea to create a business like no other. His intention was simple: to eat a pizza. But from where? Dominos, Mizzonis, Apache? All fine options, but their mismatched ingredients could not satisfy the flavour deep within him. After days and weeks and months of searching, only a single viable option presented itself to Philip Murphy. He must create his own pizza. In a crusade that lasted nigh on four months, Murphy labored to make a beautiful creation of brick and stone and cement, crafted solely by his able, artistic hands. The building of the Great Stone Oven, as it has been since named, suffered innumerable delays, caused by severe weather, injury and complications with the busy schedule of an already busy man. Murphy invited friends and family to accompany him on his odyssey through Instagram, under the handle @p.oven. He tells The University Times about the name: “The obvious answers lie in both my name and the product. But the ‘P’ is not just for ‘Phil’ or for ‘pizza’. The ‘P’ is something greater”. What that “something greater” is is almost beyond words, according to Murphy. Yet while the task of trying to understand the “P” remains unfinished, the oven has successfully been completed. on September 27th, 2017, P.Oven opened her sublime doors. A flame was lit, ingredients were piled on top of one another in precise order, and the first P.Oven pizza was created. Always the family man, Murphy gave the first products to his immediate family, and then to the people of Galway. The initial reactions to the pizzas were beyond Murphy’s wildest dreams.

Name: Pure Water Joy
Description:
Pure Water Joy is a registered Non-Governmental Organization (NGO/000946) in Tanzania that aims to purchase a borehole drill which the founder, Keneth will use throughout the western region of Tanzania (an area of the country currently devoid of international water aid efforts) to drill and install wells, at cost, for rural communities that lack access to a clean water source. To ensure the longevity of each well installed, Keneth will train the community on proper well care and maintenance, empowering them to take care of their new fresh water source long term.

As Pure Water Joy installs wells in rural areas, a long-term study will be initiated using data from the villages serviced to determine the impact on the health, safety and educational opportunities of each community. Data will be taken before wells are installed, and every 6 months after installation to monitor the effects of providing clean water to rural villages in Tanzania. This data will be used to gauge Pure Water Joy’s success in meeting its goals and results will be published on the organization's website.

Pure Water Joy also aims to provide constructive opportunities for the residents in each community once a clean water supply has been established in their location.  We will achieve this goal by partnering with local organizations to provide entrepreneurship training and workshops to women and children in the community.

In addition to drilling wells, we also have a goal to increase access to environmental education in rural communities which we will facilitate by creating and leading satellite  Roots and Shoots environmental clubs as well as leading tree planting initiatives in an effort to mitigate the impact of deforestation in rural areas.

Name: Dance The Bay
Description:
Dance the Bay seeks to uplift individuals and community through the medium of dance.

We currently have three branches: Youth Program, Performance Outreach, and Community Workshops.

Youth Program’s mission is to make dance accessible to youth by providing dance at preschool and after-school programs, emphasizing dance as an outlet for creativity and self-expression, and a means of learning self-discipline, collaboration, and confidence. 

Performance Outreach seeks to draw from the vibrant dance community at UC Berkeley and in the Bay Area to bring dance to communities who otherwise would have limited access to the arts. These monthly workshops and/or performances take place in senior homes, homeless shelters, and children’s hospitals and will provide the opportunity for students to engage in performance opportunity, teaching experience, and service learning.

Community Workshops’ mission is to reach out to budding dancers and dance educators at Berkeley to motivate them to learn basics in a safe learning space so they can, in turn, teach others through frequent, free workshops that happen on the UC Berkeley campus.

Name: Yes, Ma'am! Mammograms Are A Must(R)
Description:
Early detection of breast cancer is ESSENTIAL!  Yes, Ma'am! Mammograms Are A Must(R) is a philanthropic social enterprise to raise awareness of the importance of annual screenings for all women ages 40+.

The women's pink T-shirt is sold individually online (www.YesMammTee.com) and as a fundraising vehicle for businesses and groups. The $20 tee donates 25% to help fund mammograms for uninsured women. 

* Breast imaging centers
* OB-GYN practices
* Hospital gift shops
* Women-owned businesses
* Small- and medium-size companies
* Churches / Women's ministry groups
* Book clubs, neighborhood organizations, etc.

30 tees sold = 1 mammogram funded 

Businesses and groups choose what women's healthcare/mammogram fund in their community the donation goes to.

Yes, Ma'am! Mammograms Are A Must(R) tees can be purchased for breast cancer awareness events/teams across the country.

Name: Adenike Adeyemi Foundation
Description: The Adenike Adeyemi Foundation is a non-profit organization working to amplify and inspire the next generation of Nigeria’s youth. We are led by the mindset that everyone deserves to be happy. Our primary focus is on improving people’s quality of life by providing them with opportunities that will help empower them to create lasting change.

Name: SmarterGive
Description:
SmarterGive provides philanthropic advice to individuals, family foundations, and businesses. Resources in the social sector are limited and only by optimizing the dollars spent will real change being to occur. With the right strategies and approaches to social issues, philanthropists have the opportunity to transform our world. However, often, philanthropists do not have the background knowledge or experience to identify the path forward alone.  SmarterGive has extensive consulting, strategy, and non-profit experience to help donors to become better equipped to navigate the social sector and ultimately to have a greater impact. It is the hope and mission of the organization to arm donors with social sector knowledge and tools and ultimately to drive change for good. Like the philanthropists it supports, SmarterGive, is also truly motivated by a sense of wanting to do good. 

In order to assist donors, Smarter Give provides a range of services including:

Strategy Devleopment--The social sector is complex and rapidly changing; many potential grantmakers donât know how to get going or where to start.

Program Design--For philanthropists who have a sense of the impact they want to make as a donor, but need support designing a program that fits their needs.

On Going Admin Support--Administration should not be a barrier to good grant-making. Smarter Give can help manage the on-going details.

Evaluation--Smarter Give helps donors think through simple solutions to ensure that their gifts are being used effectively and that they are having the impact they intended.

Name: Golden Age Village for the Elderly
Description:
We are a nonprofit entity established with the fundamental objective to build and maintain a Senior Residence catering to Vietnamese in the GTA. We do this by working actively within the Vietnamese community and the broader Ontario community.

What we do: 
- Organize free classes for seniors
- Hold events to bridge the generational gap and celebrate Vietnamese-Canadian culture and identity
- Run programs to respond to the immediate needs in our community due to COVID-19
- Provide support and guidance to youth and student associations
- Strive to create a stage for not only Vietnamese but also other communities to celebrate cultural diversity in Canada

Name: West Bank Business Association
Description:
The West Bank Business Association engages and supports the neighborhood business community in the responsible economic development of the West Bank District while preserving the unique character and heritage of the Cedar-Riverside area of Minneapolis.

We support our thriving district by nurturing businesses, working as a community connector, creating opportunities, and facilitating events and activities on the West Bank.

Name: WishList SEO
Description: WishList SEO (Southeast Oakville) is a group of individuals that dedicate volunteering hours to build community, support local businesses, and ultimately raise funds for local charities.  In 2017, our first event, "Moms night of shopping", raised $1,400 for two charities that are breaking the poverty cycle:  Food4Kids Halton and Kerr Street Missions. In 2018, the second event surpassed our goal and raised $2,700 for SafetyNet, Home Suite Home, and Food4Kids Halton.  When the covid pandemic hit in 2020, we shifted to a 100% Digital/Virtual holiday event and raised $16,000 (combined $s and value-in-kind) for Home Suite Hope and Food4Kids Halton.  By Spring 2022 we returned to in-person with our first Easter Egg Hunt, which raised $2,700 for Red Cross Ukraine.  WishList is currently planning a Black Tie Gala event in November 2022 benefiting ROCK (Reach out Center for kids) and LightHouse for Grieving Children and Families.  Thank you to all of our donors, sponsors, followers, and attendees for making WishList the success that it is!

Name: AAUW NYC
Description: AAUW Empire State NYC supports women's education & pay equity in NYC through social outreach, professional networking & salary negotiation workshops. We are a branch of AAUW National (est. 1881), which promotes women’s education, economic security, and pay equity. AAUW provides roughly $4 million annually in scholarships, grants, and awards for women and community action projects.

Name: RCI Inc
Description:
Welcome to RCI. Since 1958, RCI a non-profit community-based organization has responded to the increasing and changing needs of children and adults with physical, developmental or behavioral challenges. Our innovative programs and services, led by certified, experienced and professional staff, assist people of all ages - from infants to senior citizens - to reach their personal goals. Today, RCI's programs touch more than 2,000 lives each year.

At RCI we focus on the individual and their specific needs, it is a cornerstone of our programs and services. Our clients have access to quality education, training, medical care and other support necessary to advance toward his/her personal goals. Services are designed to assure opportunities to participate in community life and assume civic responsibilities, and to have opportunities for relationships with family and friends.

We are committed to providing a caring and stimulating environment where disabled developmentally delayed or behaviorally challenged children and adults will recognize and achieve their highest potential. RCI provides safe, sanitary, and humane facilities and services to the individuals it serves. No person is excluded or denied any service or benefit from RCI due to race, creed, color, gender, sexual preference, or national origin, and all persons are treated with respect and dignity.

Whether you're hiring a person with a disability, seeking employment or community-oriented services for persons with disabilities, or looking for an early interventional or therapeutic children's program, contact RCI.

Name: Futureproof Bama
Description: Futureproof Bama is a 501(c)(3) pending nonprofit founded to help the state of Alabama prepare for the transition period for when robotics, artificial intelligence, and other autonomous innovations will make most traditional work obsolete.

Name: Spartan Fair Chance
Description:
Spartan Fair Chance (SFC) supports clients with a criminal record at Northwest Initiative in Lansing, MI.  SFC began in 2014 at Michigan State University’s School of Human Resources and Labor Relation (SHRLR). Masters students volunteered at Advocacy, Resources, Reentry, Outreach (ARRO), part of Northwest Initiative in Lansing, MI (http://nwlansing.org/programs/a-r-r-o/), to assist ex-offenders with résumé and interview preparation.  Clients include people on probation and participants in diversion programs and Ingham County’s mental health court. SHRLR volunteers assist 50 to 60 ARRO clients each year.  In the summer of 2015, SFC began offering job readiness training for ARRO clients, modeled on an empirically-based curriculum developed by Dr. Connie Sung. The curriculum, known as EPASS (Employment Preparation And Social Skills) Program was originally developed for MSU students on the autism spectrum. It focuses on skills necessary to enter the job market, such as job search strategies, résumé development, and interview preparation. Preliminary results indicate that participation in this program shifted participants’ attitudes about work and their own employability. It also improved employment outcomes.  

Beginning in June 2016, SFC began offering a second curriculum known as ASSET (Assistive Social Skills and Employment Training) Program, which focuses on developing personal attributes necessary for success in the workplace. The ASSET program is followed by the EPASS curriculum.  The  overall goal of the program is to increase the likelihood of long-term, sustainable employment and decrease the likelihood of recidivism for clients.

Name: Deborah Rise Movement
Description:
DEBORAH RISE MOVEMENT

DRM is a group of godly women who are committed to serving the Lord through their gifts and talents, living in joyful fellowship and being truly present in heart and spirit for one another, and enriching each other’s lives by the power of the Holy Spirit, as the Lord designed for His daughters, the body of Christ. 


VISION

To bring about a holistic transformation in the lives of  
the abused, violated, oppressed, neglected, and victimized women, children, and girls, through prayer, counseling, mentoring, and connecting them to such organizations where they are equipped to lead a meaningful life, with dignity and purpose. The DRM movement envisions every member of this group to be the voice of the unheard and a backdrop for those in distress and despair.

MISSION

The Deborah Rise Movement encourages and equips its members to be world changers by bringing healing to the broken in body, soul, and spirit through intercession, counseling, mentoring, and social transformation.

Name: Business Club
Description: Business club is the place for the best networking for investors in real estate fields. We gather here to help you to find best deals and partners.

Name: DOC NC
Description:
DOC NC is a community of students who want to, little by little, make a difference in people’s lives. We work towards our vision, a network of inspired individuals around the world making a difference, by holding various volunteering events. We plan shows, complete with dancing, singing, and magic tricks, for the police department and retirement homes to show our appreciation.
Outside of that, we hold fundraisers such as car washes, volunteer at local events, collaborate with local businesses, and much more. Each event has its own merits and teaches members new skills to carry with them throughout their lives. For example, they allow students to teach themselves leadership skills and how to help people around them. The numerous events also give our members the chance to be creative to bring about change and effects they wish to see. Lastly, there are the amazing bonds formed; kids of all ages work together as a unified team, collectively helping make our society a better place.

Name: Laura Vicuna Pro Education Foundation
Description:
As  Laura Vicuna Pro Education Foundation, we are an apolitical entity,  non profit and unreligious of the Daughters of Maria Auxiliadora (FMA) of the Santísimo Salvador Province North Central America, which comprises El Salvador, Honduras and Guatemala.

We develop projects in the health, nutrition, values, school aid, sponsorships and microcredits.

Name: Ovarian Cancer Connection
Description:
Since our establishment in 2005, our organization has consistently demonstrated a profound commitment to advancing awareness surrounding ovarian cancer throughout the regions of Ohio and Michigan. We have established ourselves as a pivotal local advocate for patients and families contending with this disease. Through the dedicated efforts of our committed volunteers, we have forged a robust platform for ovarian cancer awareness.

Recognizing the imperative to support ovarian cancer survivors and channel resources directly to our local community, we have ensured that 100% of funds raised remain allocated to this cause. Presently, we are privileged to collaborate with esteemed local gynecologic oncologists, including our recent partnership with Mercy Health. Together, we are steadfastly working towards the eradication of ovarian and other gynecological cancers across NW Ohio & SE Michigan.

Name: GiveGetGo Inc
Description:
Giv GetGo's expertise lies in its knowledgeable and creative thinking directors. Their backgrounds, experience and ability to seek out compelling projects, as well as engaging enthusiastic and dedicated build people, for those developing countries in need of a team of volunteers looking to make a difference.
Objectives
GiveGetGo is committed to creating a positive, lasting difference in the lives of its build recipients, the communities that they serve, and the volunteers that work hard and give so much to others. While giving to others we also know our volunteers have a desire to gain a firsthand understanding of the people, culture and geography of the area in which they build.
Structure
GiveGetGo designs its trips to ensure safe, rewarding, well organized, and ultimately unforgettable, build~travel experiences.
The skills, experience and interests of our volunteers are incorporated into all aspects of this adventure while also being completely responsive and sensitive to the communities within which we work and travel.

Name: South Central Community Foundation
Description:
"Assuring a Bright Future for South Central Kansas".

The beginning of South Central Community Foundation can best be described by the words of Margaret Mead, “Never doubt that a small group of committed citizens can change the world; indeed, it’s the only thing that ever has.”

 In 1994 a group of committed, future-focused citizens met to discuss how they could retain resources in the area for the benefit of charitable work. Through their vision, South Central Community Foundation was incorporated September 26, 1994 as a not for profit corporation for charitable purposes and began to work toward the mission of “Assuring a Bright Future for South Central Kansas.”

This mission could be reached by:
~Providing a vehicle and service for donors with varied interests and levels of giving;
~Serving as a leader in assessing and responding to emerging and changing community needs;
~Acting as a resource and catalyst for charitable activities and
~Soliciting and managing funds to build permanent and flexible endowments for charitable purposes.

Name: McMaster EngiQueers
Description: A social connection and advocacy group for the LGBTQ and ally community within McMaster Engineering.

Name: Brown Philanthropy Advisors
Description: Brown Philanthropy Advisors provides philanthropic advice and consulting to individuals, families, foundations, and corporations to help them achieve greater impact with their philanthropic dollars.

Name: Water In Nepal
Description: Water in Nepal (WIN) invests in water filtration and infrastructure projects to bring easier access to clean water to Nepalese government schools, rural villages, and local institutions. We have funded over 20 clean-water projects so far, and we are determined to reach more Nepalese in the upcoming year. An estimated 85% of Nepal's population is not served with safe drinking water, and Nepal is ranked five with the poorest developed water system in the world. We recognize that there is still much more work that needs to be done. Visit our website to learn more!

Name: Clay Soper Memorial Fund
Description:
The Clay Soper Memorial Fund seeks to educate, engage and empower our youth with the documentary program "If They Had Known." The documentary, combined with the Program Toolkit, catalyzes peer-to-peer discussions about the risk of misusing and abusing prescription drugs and provides teens with potentially life-saving information. Getting the program into schools and communities nationwide is vital to our young people. 

The documentary program "If They Had Known" is an honest account of a death that shouldn't have happened. The 35-minute film shares the night of Clay Soper's tragic death from mixing prescription drugs with alcohol. On July 18, 2015, 19-year-old Clay and his teenage friends gathered for a party. They were ready for a typical night of fun. During the party, Clay and some other partygoers mixed Xanax with alcohol. For Clay, it was a lethal mixture that caused his respiratory system and heart to stop. No one at the party knew that combining prescription drugs with alcohol could be fatal. The life-saving message comes from Clay's friends and peers, not actors. The key message is genuine and impactful: Knowing These 5 Things Could Save Your Life or the Life of a Friend.

Since its debut, the documentary has aired in 32 states, 53 colleges, and 280+ high schools and communities. Our viewing audience is 800,000+. For parents and families interested in "If They Had Known," individual rental is available on Amazon and Google Play. 

The documentary "If They Had Known" is a Clay Soper Memorial Fund program. Our mission is to educate, engage and empower today's youth with life-saving information about the risks of misusing and abusing prescription drugs and to help motivate cultural change among young people.

Name: Sharing Kindness
Description:
Our mission is to empower kids for a kinder tomorrow and give parents the tools to feel confident in themselves to create a positive mindset for children. 

We want to help kids approach their problems with compassion, treat themselves and others with kindness, feel a strong sense of self-worth, and become resilient in a world that doesn’t always serve their best interests. 

Kindness starts within, and what we say to ourselves is what we become, so choose to be kind.

Name: The Compass Experience
Description:
A charitable 501C3 nonprofit organization dedicated to teaching leadership, business competency, financial literacy, and interpersonal skills to set them up for success in the workplace, in relationships and in life in their communities.
Young men and women will not succeed in business or any arena of life they enter unless they are taught common fundamentals. Cash & Career is a starting place before they are placed in businesses. We have local banks come and teach on financial literacy pieces, we have professionals and educators teach on leadership, goal setting, responsibility. We emphasize interactive lessons and open dialogue regarding job readiness topics and social skills, to apply to their jobs we give them in local businesses for the duration of the program.
Impact Shops continue their experience and education throughout the school year helping teach emotional intelligence, conflict resolution, critical thinking skills and self awareness to help them manage the thing they always have control over...themselves. 
Journey is the high school program where we build on the foundation our middle school program laid down and we begin to open up the possibilities each student has ahead of them and help foster the skills they need to pursue these things including employment and deeper understanding in financial literacy. We walk through the stages leading up to the "adulthood realm" by 'informing to form' the person they have the potential to be when equipped properly.

Name: Ouragan Casanier
Description:
Nous sommes Alexandre et Maxime et nous participons au 4L Trophy 2023 sous le nom d'Ouragan Casanier !

Qu'est ce que le 4L Trophy ? Une aventure humaine, sportive et solidaire. Le but, traverser le désert Marocain et rejoindre Marrakech pour remettre des fourniture scolaires et sportives aux enfants les plus démunis du Maroc et tout cela à bord de la mythique Renault 4L ! 

Vous retrouverez ici toutes nos dernières actualités jusqu'au départ de l'aventure qui se passera du 16 au 26 février 2023 !

Nous cherchons activement des sponsors pour financer cet évènement alors n'hésitez pas à nous contacter !

r4antholincktrophy@gmail.com

Name: En 4L avec elles
Description:
4L Trophy
 Raid humanitaire
Des montagnes Lozériennes aux dunes du Sahara
 Chloé  Eva

Name: Mark W. Gura | Author | Director  | President
Description: Mark W. Gura is a published author, speaker, and experienced director and manager in the nonprofit and for-profit sectors. His career started with Fortune 500 fintech SaaS companies like Intuit and Paychex. Mark later expanded into the nonprofit sector, promoting causes that drive positive change and create a meaningful global impact. With over 15 years of strategic team management and operational leadership expertise, he remains dedicated to making a significant difference.

Name: Sephardic Heritage Foundation
Description: Sephardic Heritage Foundation is a Non-Profit Organization focused on facilitating the religious and cultural observance of the Jewish Syrian-Sephardic Communities. We have been producing prayer books for over 50 years, catering to the Syrian Sephardic Communities around the world including the U.S, Mexico, Central, and South America, Europe, and Asia. By distributing publications, Sephardic Heritage Foundation strives to perpetuate the venerated prayer, sacred traditions, and valued customs of one of the oldest uninterrupted Jewish Communities of the world, the Community of Aram Soba (Aleppo, Syria). Our work is influenced and inspired by our late founder, Mr. Sam Catton, may he rest in peace.

Name: Herencia Chocolate
Description:
Looking to increase their earnings and have a better life, over 800 cacao farmer families from Esmeraldas province have come together to craft Herencia Chocolate. Meet Herencia, a farmer-owned social enterprise.

By supporting Herencia Chocolate products, you are not only helping preserve the ancestral tradition of cacao growing, but you are also directly supporting small farmer communities, empowering them to pursue a greater future for themselves and their children.

In order to craft Herencia Esmeraldas, we were kindly supported by the “Cacao Justo” (Fair Cacao) project lead by our friends from the ACRA Foundation, alongside with CEFODI and the provincial government of Esmeraldas (GADPE) in the years of 2015 to 2019. The goal of the project was to strengthen the production chain of organic, Fair Trade, cacao Fino de Aroma; focusing on the farmers of Esmeraldas, with an environmentally sustainable approach, and gender equality. The project was made possible thanks to the kind funding of the Italian Agency for Cooperation and Development and funding by other implementation partners organizations.

Name: Help Humanity Organization
Description: We are non-profit organization that offer pro-bono services and consultancy to those in need, focusing on refugees from around the world settled in Canada.

Name: Breathe Love Movement
Description: “Breathe Love” can mean different things for different people, but at its heart, this movement is about awakening and empowering individuals to protect and heal themselves, each other, and this planet.

Name: Awake and Alive, Inc.
Description:
Awake and Alive’s mission is to move individuals and communities from complacency to vibrancy, replacing a routine existence for a passionate and meaningful one in which people look beyond themselves to assist the poor, distressed, and underprivileged in the world around them.

We accomplish this through three unique components: 1) to advocate for Africans locally by building relationships and easing the burden that often comes with a new culture 2) to assist existing organizations in Africa that are already making a difference in the lives of the poor and marginalized 3) to rouse Christians through education and experience, inspiring them to go beyond themselves.

Our first big project is Bright Future Academy, a kindergarten for 30 of the most vulnerable children in the slum of Kechene and is beginning in September 2012.

Name: Friends & Company | GLBT Friendly Networking
Description:
We are Vancouver’s leading network for GLBT-friendly business people, professionals, students, and allies. Our events are designed to enable us to cultivate business opportunities, make new contacts and friends, and develop existing friendships. All net proceeds of Friends & Company go to the anti-bullying and teen suicide prevention initiatives of Out in Schools. Friends & Company is a not-for-profit, non-partisan initiative.

Our mission is to facilitate networking and social connections between GLBT-friendly business people of all ages and backgrounds, while giving back to vulnerable GLBT youth in our community.

Name: Equi'Sables
Description:
Equi’Sables est une association de loi 1901, participant au raid du 4L Trophy 2020. Une aventure humanitaire consistant à apporter des fournitures scolaires et des affaires de sport aux enfants du Sud marocain, lors d’une course effrénée de 6000kms. 

A bord de nos vieilles Renault 4L, nous sommes plus de 3000 étudiants à participer à cette aventure, dont le maître mot est la philanthropie.

En dehors des prérequis obligatoires pour y participer : une voiture et des dons physiques, Equi’Sables à la volonté d’en faire davantage. Nous avons deux souhaits qui nous motivent :
-	Apporter des équipements high tech aux enfants
-	Défendre les valeurs des égalités hommes femmes

Nous allons faire notre possible pour réunir des dons physiques allant dans le sens de nos objectifs.

Sous le numéro d’équipage 235 nous souhaitons relever ce merveilleux défi, qui débute dès aujourd’hui.

Name: ONG Les Ballons Rouges
Description:
Fondée en 2008, l'ONG Les Ballons Rouges accompagne les populations du Burkina Faso vers l'autonomie. Elle intervient pour le développement de projets économiques, sociaux, culturels et sanitaires au sein du village de Zam à 80 kms à l'Est de Ouagadougou. Elle intervient en collaboration avec son association soeur MoringaWays. Parmi les nombreux projets réalisés on peut noter:
- une plantation de moringa
- des forages d'eau potable
- un centre culturel
- une maison d'accueil
- des halls d'études

Name: Galila - For A Better Future  Foundation
Description:
The Galila Foundation was established in 2005  as a grass-roots organization, founded and supported by  a group of  local residents and volunteers .
Our mission is to  promote , support and develop the quality of life in the peripheral northern region of Israel, for the primary benefit of the most challenged populations.
We are a small and motivated non-profit organization, performing our mission in partnership with those that believe in the same goals and values .
Galila  supports all communities in the northern Galilee and we respect the different ethnic and cultural characteristics of all populations. 
We believe that by strengthening the Galilee and the northern region we strengthen the State of Israel.
We recognize that each program that we support or initiate will have high-impact and a positive follow-on effect on that community. 
In order to be effective change-agents, we need to empower and support  the people that can make it all happened.

Name: Surf dog Ricochet, the SURFice dog!
Description:
Surf Dog Ricochet is an initiative of Puppy Prodigies, a 501(c)3 organization founded to raise and train service dogs for people with disabilities. 

In 2009, one of our dogs named Ricochet made an independent decision to jump on a surfboard with a boy who is quadriplegic. She clearly showed the world what her life purpose is. As a result, she's the only SURFice dog on the planet who provides therapeutic assistance while creating unique surfing experiences and other activities that reduce social anxiety and empower kids with special needs, people with disabilities, wounded warriors & veterans with PTSD.

Ricochet's pioneering efforts have organically grown to her newest therapeutic initiative, Waves of Empowerment. It's a collaborative mentorship & comradeship program that focuses on the enrichment and well-being of veterans and civilians of all ages, abilities & backgrounds as they connect through the challenges they face. Veterans & kids with special needs are paired for a day of mentoring through canine-assisted surfing℠, paddling, swimming, playing and therapy... taking them from the battleground to the playground!

Ricochet is a registered therapy dog with Alliance of Therapy Dogs, a certified goal directed therapy dog with Paws'itive Teams and holds the highest AKC therapy dog title of Distinguished. She is also an American Human Association hero dog, ASPCA dog of the year, holds the AKC award for Canine Excellence, is an official Team USA Adaptive Surfing member and sits in two surf dog hall of fames. She lives a lifestyle of helping others by 'pawing it forward' while raising awareness & over 1/2 million dollars for human/animal causes.

But what makes Ricochet extraordinary is her powerful ability to make immediate, deep, heart-to-heart connections with the individuals she interacts with. She intuitively & empathically alerts to their emotional & physical changes which allows for profound paws-on healing interventions.

She balances boards... and lives!

Name: BNicer
Description: BNicer er en innovativ indsamlingsplatform, hvor man kan samle ind til velgørende formål ved hjælp af e-handel. Vi har et samarbejde med førende danske såvel som udenlandske webshops, som udbetaler en provision til os, hver gang vi henviser en kunde til et køb på de pågældende webshops. Denne provision omdanner vi så til en donation til dit foretrukne velgørende formål. Dermed kan du støtte et godt formål blot ved at få de bedste priser og tilbud fra din yndlings webshop.

Name: Hotel Without Borders
Description:
The challenge to become a successful hotel is no longer only in how well you handle your hotel. The new challenge in the hotel industry is how you become visible online first ! The overwhelming amount of knowledge of online marketing & digital business required is leaving lots of small family businesses far behind.

In June 2018, Rebeca Mora and Louis-Alban Batard-Dupre decided to launch a solidarity initiative to empower local families running home-stays around APAC, struggling with the online world. The objective is to teach them how attract more customers online, improve digital guest experiences and increase revenues.

So far, we traveled to 12 countries and helped 14 families.

Name: Hope for Veterans
Description: Hope For Veterans provides financial support as well as pet fostering support for veterans and active troops. We aim to exceed the needs of our veterans and active troops through our endless commitment and through the continued support of our amazing volunteers, sponsors, donations, grants, and events held throughout the United States.

Name: BrightFuturesCambodia
Description:
We are a non-profit organization with the mission is to empower the lives of people in Cambodia to experience a bright future. Our vision is to eliminate extreme poverty in Cambodia through education, job creation, and healthcare. We are unique in that we have both a Khmer Team and board in Sousaphon and a board in the United States. We love connecting our donors to the people that they are directly impacting in Cambodia.  
Education:  We have partnered with New Hope School, K-5, that serve students living in extreme poverty. New Hope School’s needs are monthly salary for two teachers, classroom supplies, students’ school supplies and uniforms. In addition, we support students in completing a high school education. Monthly support helps provide a high school student with not only an education, but also safe shelter, a bike, three meals a day, and all of their school materials.
Job Creation: We empower families with earning a sustainable income through shrimp farming. We work closely with the shrimp specialist in making sure that the shrimp farmer is successful. These are Australian shrimp that will be able to be sold back to the specialist or to families that want to start a shrimp farm. Bright Futures Cambodia’s goal is to have started 8 shrimp farms in 2021.
Healthcare: We currently have a healthcare clinic for the students at New Hope School. This clinic will be able to provide basic healthcare needs that include medicines for headaches, stomachaches, and colds. The clinic will be overseen by one of our Khmer team members who is a certified government nurse. 
Covid Relief Fund: Covid has had a huge impact on the economy in Cambodia. People that are living in extreme poverty and depend on selling their produce and fish at markets are not able to. For every $25, a family will receive a food package that includes rice, noodles, soy sauce, sugar, salt and sesame oil.
Co-Founders: Michele Nishioka & Chhunly Bin
