Description:
"Your partners in training, accreditation,    risk management and occupational healthcare solutions."

                   Heavy Duty Holdings
                      "Kings of SHEQ"

       "Leaving our fingerprint on the world."

Name: Strategic Renewal
Description: Strategic Renewal International, Inc. is a non-profit Christian organization, chartered in the State of California and tax exempt by the Internal Revenue Service under code 501(c)(3).  The mission of Strategic Renewal is to help leaders and churches experience spiritual renewal.  Strategic Renewal offers a wide array of products, seminars, and support services to assist leaders and their churches as they grow in their quest to know God.

Name: Teen Challenge SA
Description:
Teen Challenge is a not-for-profit community organisation whose primary aim is to assist young people to overcome life-controlling problems through our residential rehabilitation program. 

At Teen Challenge South Australia, our mission is to powerfully influence individuals to reach their full potential.

We create a framework that challenges people to reach their fullest potential –spiritually, socially, educationally and occupationally – through our men's Living Free program.

Teen Challenge is the oldest, largest and most successful program of its kind in the world. The success of the Teen Challenge approach to helping men and women trapped by addictions has been seen in many cultures around the world.
Established in 1958 by David Wilkerson, Teen Challenge has grown to more than 1400 affiliate centers in over 129 nations around the globe. If you would like to know how Teen Challenge started, it's told in the book 'The Cross and the Switchblade'.

Teen Challenge has a special ministry of standing between the street and the church. It is our goal to be effective in ministry. We want to have answers for the people who come. It is our desire to do more than simply pray for people, important as that is.

Teen Challenge can only continue with it's mission with outside financial support.  We would welcome a conversation about how your Company, Organisation or Business can help us change lives right here in South Australia.

Name: Kappa Alpha Theta - Zeta Upsilon Chapter
Description:
In establishing Kappa Alpha Theta, our founders created a women’s fraternity in which they could learn, grow, support one another, and be part of something larger than themselves. We, the members of Zeta Upsilon Chapter, are proud to follow in their footsteps.
Our members are athletes and artists, musicians and mathematicians, dreamers and doers; we play an active role in campus life.

Name: Neighborhood United
Description: Neighborhood United is changing the way charity is done. We don't ask for donations. Where does all that money go anyway? Instead, we've created a fresh new approach to charity. Through concerts, festivals, competitions, and community events, we bring people together naturally. We focus on local talent, help people get discovered, and support the community within. But we don't stop there. By using our social platform, YOU are empowered to decide how profits are reinvested into YOUR community. We want you involved! You raised the money and you should decide how it is used! Join Neighborhood United and make a difference in your community today!

Name: Tolong Foundation
Description: Tolong Foundation is a non-governmental organization engaged in crowdfunding. We are here as a solution for all levels of society who want to learn more about what currently happen in our society and channel aid funds to problems and disasters that occur.

Name: Royal Orders of the Golden Fire Dog & Obeng II
Description:
The Aduana people are believed to have been led out of the ground by a dog which had fire in his mouth. Hence, they are referred to as Ogyaasefuo. It is also believed that the first Aduana had a dog that once was out to search for food and on seeing fire the dog thought there was something to eat for him and in doing so got his mouth seriously burnt. When the owner saw it coming with the fire he then said, “m'atwea woabre me adie” meaning “my dog you have brought me something worthy.”

 

The Royal Order of the Golden Fire Dog (ROGFD) is constituted and inaugurated under the auspices of the Royal House and Traditional Council of Sefwi Obeng-Mim and the Chieftaincy of His Majesty Oheneba Nana Kwame Obeng II, in order to promote the ancient code of the military protectors, Asafo, of the ancient Akan Kingdom. This tradition dates back to time immemorial and the Asafo being in service to the Royal House of Sefwi Obeng-Mim. Keeping the spirit of the traditional Asafo of the Akan, this Order respectfully emulates a more known system of codifying the chivalric rankings carried with our noble and sacred Order.

 

The international members of this Royal Order are dedicated to the development of the Stool Lands under the care of the Royal House. Acceptance into the Order is based on the assessment of the human values and quality of character of the applicant, without distinction or discrimination based on religious, social class, race, political tendencies, physical disability, or sexual orientation. Advancement in rank is based on merit.

 

Each Knight or Dame admitted should be aware that he/she is part of a chivalric institution representing the Royal House of Sefwi Obeng-Mim, are recognized as noble members under traditional customs of Sefwi Obeng-Mim Traditional Council and the Royal House, and consequently should be prepared to accept and to practice a Chivalric Code of Honor, such as provided in this Constitution.

Name: Elevate, Canadian Progress Club
Description: Elevate is a women's  Canadian Progress Club committed to serving the community of Calgary.

Name: Buy To Cure Foundation
Description: The Buy To Cure Foundation is a social start-up that works closely with NGOs, Orphanages, School etc. We believe that every child should have exceptional skills from the earliest childhood because they’re the future. We digitalize the artworks made by children and sell them on market places etc. the profit generated by those artworks is then donated to various NGOs, Orphanages, Small schools for conducting skill enhancement workshops.

Name: WAY TO THE NATIONS
Description: Way to the Nations is a non-profit, non-denominational humanitarian association, which works to help children stigmatized as witches by religious leaders and subsequently abandoned by their families in Nigeria. Founded in 2009, it promotes condition for the freedom and equality of individuals, contributing to social inclusion, while fighting situations of marginalisation and social discrimination. We rescue the stigmatised children, who many times are very hurt, ill and traumatised and offer them a safe place, love, food, health treatment and education. As of today, 41 children are fully cared by the project and several more assisted in their homes by the nine native staff members based in the city of Eket, south of Nigeria.

Name: VCU Transform

Name: Cann-I-Dream Foundation
Description:
Mission Statement:

The mission of the Cann-I-Dream Foundation is to make a better life for our children,  thus creating a world full of abundance, love, and hope for our future. 

Vision Statement:

At the Cann-I-Dream Foundation, our vision of a better and brighter tomorrow, is achieved through dedicated ethical fundraising, as well as unconditional giving, inspiring, and educating those in need.

The Cann-I-Dream Foundation is a non-profit 501(c)(3) charity corporation, designed to ethically fund-raise, in order to distribute those funds, to local and global children's programs, and charities. This can include, but is not limited to, community outreach programs, food banks, stop urban blight campaigns, providing school supplies, and other living essentials, as well as education, and donations to global programs, i.e. St Jude's Children's hospital,Elizabeth Glaser Pediatric Aids Foundation, etc. .

CANN I DREAM? YES I CANN!!!

We are an informal organization connecting amateur athletes who seek challenges.

By overcoming sport difficulties we want to demonstrate that there is a solution even when conditions are unfavourable.

We are using resources raised on our sports events to create equal opportunities for young people who refused to give up.

Name: St. Andrew's Society of Athens (Charitable Scottish Dancing Club)
Description:
www.standrewssociety.gr 

The Saint Andrew's society of Athens represents the Greek community of Scots in Athens and promotes Scottish culture and traditions. There is a large community of Scottish people in Greece and our society organizes social events with plenty of cheerful Scottish country dancing.
Apart from promoting Scottish traditions and culture, profits from our events are donated to local charities to help those in most need.

Name: Lucky Dawg Animal Rescue
Description:
We are a no-kill, foster based, all volunteer animal rescue. Lucky Dawg is dedicated to rescuing homeless, abused or unwanted animals in our local communities, as well as educating the public. 

We place animals in responsible, committed and loving homes. Considerable care is taken to find good matches for both the animal and the adopter. Lucky Dawg will remain committed to the animal’s welfare beyond the adoption process.

We are located in Chester County, PA. We adopt out to families anywhere within a 2-hour radius.

Name: Pontifical John Paul Ii Institute For Studies On Marriage and Family
Description: The D.C. session of the Pontifical John Paul II Institute for Studies on Marriage and Family, located on the campus of Catholic University of America, awards theological, ecclesiastical degrees: MTS, STL, STD; and also a PhD, with a specialization in Person, Marriage, and Family. As a canonically recognized ecclesiastical faculty, the Institute is one of seven institutions in the United States that grant degrees by the authority of the Holy See.

Name: Fanø Sommercup
Description:
I 1980 var der en samling gæve mænd og kvinder der gerne ville hjælpe Fanø Boldsklubs ungdomsafdeling med økonomien. De fik sig en god snak over en kop kaffe, og snart var idéen formet. Den ene fik fat i et par store grill, en anden fik lidt øl og vand, og en tredje fik stablet et par dommere på benene. Det var den spæde start på Fanø Sommercup tilbage i 1980.

Siden da er Fanø Sommercup vokset og vokset.

Name: Association Hors Norme
Description:
Hors Norme est une association à but non lucratif, créée en 2012 par 8 étudiants de l'IESEG School of Management. 

L'objectif de l'association est de sensibiliser les jeunes au handicap par le biais d’événements ou rencontres sportives. Régulièrement, l'équipe Hors Norme intervient auprès d'écoles primaires de région parisienne pour changer le regard des jeunes sur le handicap.

Name: The Maryam Parman Foundation for Injured Children, Inc.
Description:
The Maryam Parman Foundation for Injured Children provides healthcare and other related services to children who suffer from a catastrophic injury. Each child and their family is assessed for their individual need, whether it be healthcare services, handicap accommodations, or assistance navigating the legal system.

With your help, we can be sure no child is left without the best medical treatment and families are able to receive support navigating these life challenges. Visit our website to learn more or make a donation https://mpfic.org.

Name: A. Lindsay & Olive B. O'Connor Foundation
Description: Founded in 1965, our mission is to strengthen and uplift the rural communities of Delaware County, NY, by providing meaningful grants that enhance the well-being of all residents. Guided by a commitment to equity, collaboration, and sustainability, we aim to support initiatives that foster opportunity, resilience, and a vibrant future for our region.

Name: Moliba Makasi
Description:
French NGO

L'association Moliba Makasi est une association "loi 1901", à vocation humanitaire dont les principales activités sont
- l'organisation d'expéditions lointaines en véhicule ancien (Citroën 2cv, 203 Peugeot, Jeep Willys…) à destination de l'Afrique du Nord. C'est bien évidemment plus la rencontre que la performance qui est alors recherchée.
- la proposition de séjours de type 'voyage solidaire' dans différents pays de l'Afrique de l'Ouest. Afin que ceux qui sont désireux de mieux partager puissent s'investir ponctuellement mais intensément, sans recherche fastidieuse d'une structure d'accueil : Moliba Makasi met en relation une demande qu'elle peut recueillir au Nord avec un besoin qu'elle aura recensé au Sud dans une association.

Pour bien marquer son ancrage africain et sa volonté d'action partagée, l'association s'est choisi un nom à sa mesure : MOLIBA MAKASI. Ces mots sont issus d'une chanson congolaise et signifient 'Le courant est trop fort'. Moliba Makasi entretient des contacts avec diverses associations implantées localement en Afrique. Son but est essentiellement de favoriser les contacts Nord-Sud. Par les voyages à destination de l'Afrique, effectués par ses membres plusieurs fois par an, Moliba Makasi entend pérenniser et faire fructifier ses contacts en Afrique de l'Ouest.

Name: Shri Mahajan Anath Balashram, Nazar Building, Katargam-Surat
Description: Founded by Shri Hardevram Vakil, son of Justice Nanabhai Haridas in the year 1900 A.D. during the worst possible period of plague and famine in the region around Surat.

Name: Jake’s Wish Dog Rescue
Description:
Jake's Wish Dog Rescue in San Jose, CA is a non profit (501c3), volunteer-driven organization dedicated to rescuing abandoned dogs who would otherwise be euthanized in shelters due to lack of space.

We strive to do this with kindness, by providing a feeling of security through in-home fostering until a match is found that suits both the adopter and dog.

We serve our community by providing information and education on responsible dog ownership.

Name: Men of Color Global
Description:
About Men of Color Global:
Men of Color Global, a 501 (c) (3) not-for-profit corporation, was founded by Pauline E. Higgins, LLM, JD, CPA, in 2010 to create a lasting pipeline of men of color through eliminating unconscious bias from Pre-K through high school, during college and in the workplace.  Men of Color Global helps individuals and organizations through diversity and inclusion efforts that concentrate on the following:

EDUCATION – RECRUITMENT – RETENTION – PROFESSIONAL DEVELOPMENT & LEADERSHIP THROUGH MENTORING AND SPONSORING

Men of Color Global has four areas of focus:
•	Men of Color in School
•	Men of Color in College
•	Men of Color in the Workplace
•	Men of Color in Leadership

Men of Color will assist students, employees, teams and organizations to interact and progress through recognizing each other’s strengths and by actively seeking to eliminate unconscious bias in our communities, schools, colleges, workplaces and in leadership.

Name: NCMA-نسمة
Description: NCMA - the North Carolina Moroccan-American Association - is a 501c3 based in North Carolina. NCMA builds a strong Moroccan-American community by connecting to networks and resources; hosting social and educational events; and programming that encourages financial and emotional well-being to Moroccan-American men, women, youth, and children living in North Carolina.

Name: Varico Charitable Foundation
Description: A Registered Canadian Charity, working along-side leaders and organizations to help them meet the needs in their community.

Name: FORTIFY
Description: Fortify’s mission is to reduce iron deficiency anemia (IDA) — the leading underlying cause of maternal mortality during childbirth — by driving innovation in food fortification. Fortify is an advocacy organization exclusively focused on finding the best ways to add iron to the family meal in developing countries where IDA is insidious and pervasive. Fortify works with food companies that have existing distribution channels to encourage them to help solve micronutrient malnutrition by adding iron to their tomato products. In much of Africa, tomato paste is part of the everyday meal, which makes it the ideal food vehicle for iron because tomatoes contain high levels of Vitamin C, a critical nutrient to boost iron absorption. To accomplish the mission and build awareness, Fortify collaborates with leading organizations, academic institutions and foundations dedicated to food, nutrition, health, and international development, and with government agencies. Fortify was founded in 2012 and incorporated after incubating at the prestigious University of Chicago Booth School of Business and winning seed funding at their Social New Venture Competition.

Name: JCI Sundsvall Alumner

(AJLI) is an organization of women committed to promoting voluntarism, developing the potential of women and improving communities through the effective action and leadership of trained volunteers. Its purpose is exclusively educational and charitable.

Name: BWF Insight
Description:
BWF Insight is the prospect development and analytics division of Bentz Whaley Flessner. Prospect development describes the underlying infrastructure necessary to operate a best-in-class fundraising program with a major-gift oriented business model. The components of this infrastructure include analytics to identify prospects, segment the base, and evaluate portfolios; prospect research to feed the pipeline, discover emerging constituent sectors, and profile prospects; and relationship management to manage portfolio composition, gift officer activity, and provide contextual, actionable reporting.
 
As the leader in consulting for prospect development, BWF Insight uniquely provides The Total Pipeline Solution. Using the latest innovations in data science, BWF Insight meets the critical needs of feeding the pipeline, clearing the pipeline of clogs, and keeping the pipeline moving. Additionally, our experienced team of analysts and consultants will guide the development and excellence of in-house programs. BWF has established several hundred prospect development programs and formed nearly 100 in-house analytics departments.
 
Whether you need a provider to find you more prospects, a guide to develop a cutting-edge program, or outsourcing of interim analytics and prospect development efforts, BWF Insight is your ideal partner.  Contact us today to bring data science to your fundraising business.

Name: Atlanta Boxer Rescue
Description: Atlanta Boxer Rescue is a non-profit organization that strives to rescue, medically rehabilitate, and re-home unwanted and abandoned Boxers within the greater Atlanta area as well as providing education to pet owners. We work to inform people about the realities of pet guardianship, promote spaying/neutering, and encourage responsible care of the Boxer to enhance the lives of pet owners and their companion animals. Atlanta Boxer Rescue, Inc., is 501(c)(3) non-profit organization. All contributions are tax deductible to the fullest extent allowed by IRS law.

Name: Life Debt/Life Renewal
Description:
OUR MISSION

LifeDebt’s mission was born from a necessity for First Responders to be recognized as an at-risk segment of society, who have spent their lives helping, protecting, saving, and being strong and stoic for citizens.

These First Responders, like the Military, have seen things with their eyes that their soul cannot reconcile.
First Responders and Military have a higher risk of suicide, divorce, alcoholism, and undiagnosed and untreated PTSD.
LD/LR is here to be the safety net that is needed after leaving a career that has hurt their hearts and scarred their souls.
We will be offering many energy healing methods of stress and anxiety relief so life after leaving these high risk, high anxiety careers, can go on and be happy and fulfilled.

Our mission is to offer alternative and traditional remedies, along with a social program of like minded, like experienced men and women who have seen what they have seen, felt what they have felt, left the comradery of their units, and felt the desperate, unexplained anxiety of going out in crowds, and trying to walk a path of balance.

We have plans for, and will be offering, Yoga, Meditation, Reflexology, Reiki, aroma therapy, nature trails, weekly gatherings of music, dance, conversation and fun around the fire pit.

CPR for Basic Life Saving will be offered to every attendee of our grounds.

After a life of saving, and being that line between chaos and peace, this life saving skill fits perfectly for the First Responder and Military retirees. The instructor has 15 years of training law enforcement, and it will include CPR for adults, children, infants, Foreign Body Obstruction training, for adults, children and infants, Rescue breathing, use of the defibrillator , and basic life saving to keep organs inflated until advanced life saving arrives.
We will rescue K9’s who were once a part of a loving family but have found themselves in a shelter after the death of their beloved human …alone, confused, and mourning, and at a very high risk to be euthanized.

These K9’s will feel love once again, and they will be, integrated, and retrained, so they can be rehomed and trained as Emotional Support Dogs, Therapy Dogs, and in the rare instance when one feels the need, Service dogs. Failing that, they will be given a forever home on our grounds, and spend their remaining lives with other dogs, cats, and go to a petting room ,when any attendee who feels the need for comfort and peace from a K9, or the steady ,calming purring of a cat, can share some heartwarming moments.

We are a start-up charity, and will be offering raffles, auctions, and fundraising activities to make our vision, a reality.
Our advisors, who have been First Responders, Rescuers, and Military, will give their opinions to the Board of Directors, who will meet monthly, and make adjustments and plan effective ways to raise funds for these adjustments. these proposed adjustments and the costs thereof every Charity needs to be periodically fine tuned to meet the needs of those they serve, and this is the purpose of our existence.

Of course, donations are always a staple of sustainability, and much appreciated

Each and every dollar that comes in, will be posted on our website, along with expenses for each month, as well as projected needs and related costs ,to help serve and save those, who have dedicated their lives to helping and saving citizens. We pledge to provide fiscal transparency, We will never be complacent and go with “as is”.Our Board of Directors and our skilled Advisory Board are independent thinkers. This will keep us moving forward and expanding.

ImaginN Law enforcement, Fire, EMS, Military and Aviation…a fusion…we are all the same beneath the uniform.

We are in this together.

Name: Kazuri Properties Ltd
Description:
In the words of Oscar Wilde, 

"Every saint has a past and every sinner has a future." 

Mission Statement: Our mission is to accept responsibility for repairing our broken society and helping those who have been disenfranchised and are vulnerable. We acknowledge that women  who have suffered trauma or violence  are more than the sum of their criminal records and the things that have happened to them  and we believe they can be fully re-socialised and reintegrated to become participating stakeholders in society.

Name: Westerville Scholarship Foundation
Description: The Westerville Scholarship Foundation is led by a small group of local volunteers and board members. The Foundation provides scholarships to high school and adult continuing education students.  The Foundation is funded through private donations from members of the community with most donations resulting from the Westerville Community Bowl-A-Thon each February.  Email Chairperson, Tara Trigg with questions regarding sponsorship or scholarships (tara.l.trigg@gmail.com).

Name: ANTHEM FOUNDATION INC
Description:
Anthem Foundation is a private, nonprofit organization that provides grants for improving health and wellness.

Anthem Foundation is a philanthropy company wholly funded by Anthem, Inc.

Name: Rotary Club of Edmonton Whyte Avenue
Description:
Whyte Avenue Rotary is a non-profit service organization that brings together community members, businesses, and professional leaders to provide humanitarian service, support community efforts, and to advance goodwill and peace in Edmonton and around the world.

We meet on Tuesdays,, from 6PM- 7PM at Rooster Cafe & Kitchen (10732 82 Ave NW). Come join us!

Name: Poghosyan Foundation
Description:
The Poghosyan brothers found their way from Esfahan (Persia) to India, Singapore and Indonesia in the 19th century. At every step to more success they also gave back more to the local Armenian communities. The new Poghosyan Foundation continues the centuries old tradition laid by the original founders. 

Founded by one of the brothers’ direct descendants and two friends, the Poghosyan Foundation aims to further the well-being of the Armenian Diaspora and the Republic of Armenia, to conserve Armenian culture and to provide (financial) support for the further development of the Republic of Armenia in its broadest sense.

Name: Buscamos Casita
Description:
Nuestra Misión
Promover el bienestar animal y la tenencia responsable, a través de la implementación de actividades de rescate, rehabilitación, adopción y educación en temáticas relacionadas con el cuidado y mantención de mascotas.

Nuestra Visión
Aportar en convertirnos en una sociedad que garantiza el bienestar animal a través de la tenencia responsable de mascotas.

Name: Ranskalaisen koulun ystävät ry
Description: Ranskalaisen koulun ystävät ry tarjoaa rahallista tukea Helsingin Ranskalais-Suomalaisen Koulun oppilaille myöntämällä stipendejä, matka-avustustuksia ja muita tukia. Yhdistys myös ylläpitää koulun tiloissa ranskankielistä leikkikoulua ja iltapäiväkerhoa.

Name: JADEAIR
Description: L'assemblée générale a décidé la dissolution de l'ASBL, constaté que celle-ci n'a pas de bien à affecter, prononcé la clôture de la liquidation, et constaté que l'association a cessé d'exister.

Name: MedsUA
Description: Vital medicines for the people of Ukraine.

Name: Freeman Foundation
Description: The Carl M. Freeman Foundation, established in 1960, has a long history of supporting nonprofit organizations - both large and small.

Name: Grants Management Services
Description:
Grants Management Services conducts and manages proposal review, monitoring/evaluation and full reporting services for grantors, foundations, trusts and other grantors in the philanthropic and non-profit sectors.

GMS provides desktop studies and physical investigations to help grantors determine whether a proposal is accurate, complete and in-line with their philanthropic portfolio. 

GMS is available to conduct pre-, mid- and post- project on-site visits to ensure the grantor is fully satisfied the use of gifted funds.

Grants Management Services staff have been working in the non-profit sector for over 20 years.

Name: Au-delà des regards
Description:
Association humanitaire engagée à soutenir les personnes en situation de précarité notamment via l'accès à la culture.

 Notre approche comprend la promotion de l'accès à la culture via des dons de livres, visant à restaurer l'estime de soi et à créer des opportunités de changement.

 Sensibles à la vulnérabilité des femmes en situation précaire, nous avons mis en place une initiative de solidarité fournissant des produits d'hygiène essentiels. 

 Au-Delà des Regards vous offre l'opportunité de contribuer à des initiatives humanitaires impactantes. Rejoignez-nous dans notre engagement à faire une différence significative dans la vie de ceux qui ont besoin de soutien. #AuDelàDesRegards #Humanitaire #Solidarité

Name: The Union of Marash Armenians Student Fund
Description: The Union of Marash Armenian Student Fund exists to promote the education of Armenian students by granting scholarships to any Armenian or any descendant of an Armenian, and to encourage the educational, cultural, and athletic endeavors of Armenian Youth.

Name: Abre Tu Corazón
Description:
Abre tu Corazón es una institución sin fines de lucro con de 25 años de actividad trabajando a beneficio de la educación de niños y jóvenes con falta de oportunidades en el Estado de Puebla. La Asociación ejecuta su apoyo a través de 3 proyectos:
**Becando con el Corazón: Programa en el cual la Asociación apoya a niños y jóvenes a terminar su educación básica escolar de kínder, primaria, secundaria, técnico superior y universitarios con el objetivo de mejorar su entorno social y empoderarlos a través de proyectos alternativos en su seguridad, confianza, formación para el trabajo, emprendimiento, entre otros.
**Infraestructura Digna: Programa en el cual se crean y dignifican espacios de usos múltiples para brindar calidad educativa a niños y jóvenes de zonas desfavorables para contribuir con su enseñanza.
**Apoyo Comunitario: la asociación ha ido extendiendo el apoyo a las familias más necesitadas a través de campañas en épocas de frío, colecta de útiles escolares, entrega de despensas, entre otros.

Name: Christ for the Americas
Description:
Somos una iglesia que busca llevarte a vivir y  experimentar la adoración inspirada por Dios de una  manera dinámica. Creemos que cada día Jesús manifiesta su amor, poder y gloria en tu vida y la de los tuyos.
Tenemos Pasión por su palabra, entendemos las escrituras como un pilar fundamental de sabiduría y gozo.
Amamos tu matrimonio y tu familia, por medio de discipulados y reuniones en casas de oración buscamos que desde el corazón de los hogares nos extendamos hacia la plenitud en Dios.

Name: Keeping Hope Alive IVF
Description: Keeping Hope Alive, Inc. is a not-for-profit organization incorporated in June of 2017 whose mission is to provide couples support while achieving parenthood through In Vitro Fertilization (IVF). The founders of KHA have all been touched personally with the wonders of IVF, and we can not wait to help others! The organization will be conducting various fundraising functions throughout the calendar year including a golf outing, 5k Run/Walk, benefits, and more! We want to provide both monetary and emotional support to couples throughout the Philadelphia region. If you would like to get involved in this great organization, please send an email to keepinghopeivf@gmail.com. Please browse our site and thank you from the bottom of our hearts for your continued support!

Name: Grounded Greencastle
Description: Through the work of Grounded, Co. we hope to build a community that welcomes and loves every individual that comes into the space, allows them to freely explore their faith, and experience spiritual growth. Grounded, Co. will serve the greater community as a space a multi-functional gathering place an arts venue, a study space, a meeting location, or simply a place to share a cup of coffee.

Name: Comité Jeunes Leaders Fondation CRDM
Description: Nous sommes de jeunes professionnels ambitieux engagés à briser les tabous de la dépendance en organisant des événements de collecte de fonds innovants et audacieux au profit de la Fondation du CRDM.

Name: Blooming S'épanouir à l'école
Description:
Blooming est une association laïque de Loi 1901 créée en 2013 par Nadia Haton, sa Présidente.
Notre mission est de donner aux enfants qui vivent dans la pauvreté, un accès à l'éducation et à toute activité qui contribue à leur développement intellectuel, physique ou artistique. Blooming a vocation à intervenir dans le monde entier ; ses premiers projets sont lancés en Inde. Après de nombreux voyages en Asie et en Afrique, un séjour de 2 ans en Inde, Nadia Haton a décidé de créer, avec l’aide de plusieurs amis proches, l’association Blooming – S’épanouir à l’Ecole.

Name: Alternative Development and Research Center
Description:
The Alternative Development and Research Centre (ADRC) is a registered nonprofit trust in Jaipur, Rajasthan province of India.

ADRC aims to promote education, human development and well-being of the deprived particularly those being excluded from the present development model . Community based education and development of slum and street children along with empowerment and skills training of slum women is a priority. ADRC tries to promote inter-cultural understanding, peace and build global solidarity in the spirit of the Sanskrit saying ‘vasudhaiva kutumbakam’ or world is a family.

Research, book writing projects and writing articles on subjects like alternative development and redefining development and social progress, on children’s rights and intercultural understanding and peace, independent media will be undertaken to clarify the issues and suggest alternatives and disseminate knowledge.




WHAT WE DOING ? (PROJECTS)
– Two schools for education/nutrition for slum children under 22 Godam bridge and Valmiki Basti Jyoti Nagar
– Vocational training for women and girls
– Schools for Slum-Street Children
– Children’s Rights Forum
– Womens Empowerment and Training
– Intercultural Understanding Centre and proposed Intercultural Circle Cafe
– Research, Documentation related to alternative development and children’s rights etc

Name: Monolith
Description:
We are Monolith, a society founded on the belief that help should reach those that need it and anyone, no matter in what capacity, can be the one to provide that help. This is, after all, what it means to be part of the united entity we know as the human race. 

At Monolith, we open doors to partnership opportunities between benefactors and beneficiaries and aim to establish, facilitate and reinforce such relationships among all our partners. We believe that our efforts will create a sustainable framework of providing help and differentiate ourselves from the model of transient assistance that often has limited effectiveness in the long run.

Find out more about Monolith - https://www.monolithsg.org

Name: Human Elephant Foundation
Description:
The Human Elephant Foundation’s mandate is to raise funds for suitable projects and implementing organisations, specifically for elephant conservation in Africa. Our projects and interventions include corridors, game reserves, elephant art, and helping resolve human elephant conflict. 

The Human Elephant Foundation(HEF) acts as a reputable fund-raising organization, ensuring all funding connected with any project we support are properly administered and distributed. The organization has Section 18 A status and can therefore make tax relief available to corporate contributions.

We appeal to you to contribute towards this noble cause.

The Human Elephant Foundation is an Audited Tax Exempt Trust

DONATE
If you would like to make a donation, large or small, please go to our website, www.humanelephant.org to make your pledge. Your contribution will be used towards the work of the HEF and will be administered by Deloittes.

Name: Stillwater Foundation
Description:
Stillwater Foundation (SF) is dedicated to supporting the work of Christian ministries and missionary groups throughout the world, who are focused on presenting the saving life of Christ by meeting the needs of the communities in which they serve. We are committed to not only utilize “next step technologies” in these endeavors, but also developing such technologies. This is based on our CAST model (Creating Appropriate and Sustainable Technology).
Thus, SF directs much of its resources toward Kingdom focused research and development (R&D), specifically for products or projects to increase - physically, emotionally and spiritually -  the health, wellness and prosperity of families across the world. 

For information on our current projects, please contact us. If you have a concept or idea that would help meet a need in your outreach endeavors, or might help others - and would like additional help our input - please let us know how we can help.

In His service!

Name: School of Hope Foundation
Description: School of Hope Foundation seeks to provide a wholistic education that frees vulnerable youth of Kibera to realize their dreams and to live for others. The Foundation was formed in 2017 to provide fundraising and operational support in the US for St. Aloysius Gonzaga Secondary School for AIDS orphans and other vulnerable children in Nairobi, Kenya. The Foundation seeks to make donations impactful by providing funds for Jesuit college preparatory education and opportunities for college to young men and women while instilling in them the values of service to their community.

Lather
Description: Lather strives to provide a Fresh Start Community by bridging the divide between hotels with shelters. Hotel contributions of amenities and linen reduce shelters' operational costs while supporting those in need.

Name: SisterStory
Description: SisterStory is a national campaign aimed at broadening awareness of Catholic sisters. Who are Catholic sisters? What are their lives like? What impact have they had over the course of American history? What difference do they make today? Our intention is to demystify religious life – the vows of poverty, celibacy and obedience, the experience of living in community, the desire to belong totally to God – by sharing the stories of Catholic sisters. We aim to connect sisters with young women and to share their impressions in an authentic, first-person 20-something voice.

Name: Griffith Family Foundation
Description: Our mission is to inspire, encourage, and empower the citizens of Macon-Bibb by cultivating education and the arts, creating economic opportunity, and developing compassionate leaders.

Name: Impact Now App
Description: We provide impact through socially responsible next generation solutions that empower People and Cultures for forces of good

Name: Donation Medium
Description: Donation Medium, with offices in New York and Delaware, is a Donor Advised Fund and Non-Profit Funding Source dedicated to matching elite donors with qualified non-profits and assisting organizations with donation acquisition, crowdfunding and crowdsourcing.

Name: Alabama STEM Volunteers
Description:
Our goal is to instill a love of the STEAM disciplines in elementary students by providing an exceptional educational opportunity, helping them to achieve academic success in the short term, and inspiring them to develop personal goals for the future.

"STEAM’s foundations lie in inquiry, critical thinking, and process-based learning. That is extremely important. The entire idea surrounding STEAM lessons and the STEAM approach is that it’s based around questioning and really deep questioning.  We want to start asking non-Googleable questions.
Inquiry, curiosity, being able to find solutions to a problem, and being creative in the finding of the solutions is at the heart of this approach. This means that the humanities are woven into STEAM just like everything else.”
~Institute for Art Integration and STEAM


President: Sidney Radford
Vice President: Lisa Bonatz
Director of Public Relations: Cassidy Beamon
Treasurer:

Name: Effective Altruism IIITD
Description: EA IIITD in affiliation with PhiloSoc aims to help students figure out how they can use their careers and resources to help solve the world's most pressing problems. We aim to help students pursue high impact careers, by spreading EA awareness through our flagship fellowships and other events.

Name: Tifny.me | Data & CRM Community for Women
DesignLab Give
Description:
Our team carefully crafts learning programs with a focus on a dedicated one-on-one approach, ultimately providing the necessary tools for girls & women to succeed. 

With these learned skills, communities and families all benefit.

Our aim is simple; to change the cycle of poverty through creativity, for as many women as possible, one person at a time.

Name: Americas Association for the Care of Children
Description:
Our projects in the United States, Nicaragua, Haiti, Nepal, and Kenya are based on the CEPET model : Community | Education | Participation | Empowerment | Transformation.
We would be happy to work with you, if you are interested in committing in actions in favor of empowering communities, through compassionate and holistic education, with the purpose of reducing the impact of poverty and marginalization in the world’s underserved populations.

Name: Paint Your Heart Out (PYHO)
Description:
Paint Your Heart Out, Inc. (PYHO) is a volunteer driven nonprofit organization that helps low income, senior or disabled homeowners maintain their dignity, safety and connections to the community.

With our teams of volunteers, Paint Your Heart Out paints and refurbishes homes for low income seniors or disabled residents of Anaheim, Brea and Santa Ana for free. Additionally, we do yard clean-up, gate/fence repairs, driveway repaving, window repairs, stucco/wood patching depending on the needs of the homeowners. All the work is done by our wonderful volunteers and materials are donated to us by local businesses within the communities we serve.

Name: Sirius Enterprise Agency
Description:
Founded in 1999 by BP, Sirius Enterprise Agency provides free, confidential support for pre-start and existing businesses. Whether you are an individual looking to become self-employed for the first time, or an existing business looking to expand or seeking help with specific issues.
In addition to business development support, Sirius provides access to funding from a range of organisations local, regional and national.
All support is free and confidential. Contact the office for an informal chat for free impartial advice about how we can support the development of your ideas. 
Sirius Advisors operate from an office in Hedon, East Riding of Yorkshire covering Hull and the East Riding of Yorkshire.

Name: Parohia Sf. Ier. Nicolae si Sf. Ier. Nectarie - Pitesti
Description:
Bisericuţa din lemn în care slujim în prezent a fost ridica la inițiativa pr. Soveja Ionuț Codruț și sfinţită de Înaltpreasfinţitul Părinte Calinic, Arhiepiscop al Argeșului și Muscelului, în luna decembrie a anului 2006. La scurt timp, ocrotitor alături de Sf. Ier. Nicolae, este numit şi Sf. Ier. Nectarie, mare făcător de minuni și tămăduitorul bolnavilor.
Aceşti doi sfinţi sunt rugători în ceruri înaintea lui Dumnezeu pentru enoriaşii acestei parohii nou înfiinţată. 
Întrucât este un spaţiu mic dedicat serviciului divin, în viitor ne dorim construirea unei biserici de dimensiuni mai mari. În momentul de faţă se lucrează la documentaţia tehnică şi obținerea avizelor pentru autorizaţia de construcţie.

Name: MSU Alumni Association of Idaho
Description:
We promote Michigan State University, offer programs and experiences to support our community within Idaho, and connect Spartan alumni and friends of the university!

To learn more or get involved, email us at MSUIdaho@gmail.com.  To join, visit www.alumni.msu.edu.

#SpartansWill  #GoGreen  #IdahoSpartans


MSUAA Membership

There is no fee for joining our local alumni club, and effective July 1, 2016, membership in the MSUAA will no longer be fee-based, signaling a new model that welcomes each and every Spartan. As part of this transition, the university is also acknowledging alumni and friends who contribute at least $100 annually between July 1 and June 30 to the MSU college or program of their choice. In appreciation of this investment, MSU will consider all of these donors as Spartan Loyal and they will receive enhanced services and recognition. For more information, refer to the FAQ.

Have you recently moved to Idaho? MSU and our club may not know how to get in touch with you! Please go to alumni.msu.edu and follow the link to “Update your address” on the top right of the main page in order to update your contact information in the centralized MSU alumni database.

Name: United Way of the Desert
Description: Our mission is to break the cycle of poverty in the Coachella Valley by fighting for the health, education, and financial stability of every resident.

Name: Home Partnership Foundation
Description: Working to enhance safe, stable and affordable housing throughout Idaho.

Name: Women in Evolution
Description: Mexican organization seeking to empower young mexican women leaders. Helding an annual forum for 60 mexican women that are previously selected via an online application.

Name: One in an ARMY
Description:
OUR MISSION

 

One In An ARMY is a fan collective comprised of volunteers across the globe. Driven by the idea of using our collective power for global good, we seek out non-profit organizations worldwide and harness the power of ARMY into giving micro-donations over a one month period. Our motto, “I am ONE in an ARMY”, plays with the idea that many people giving small amounts can create a substantial impact when we work together.


OUR GOAL



One In An ARMY's goal is to act as a guide in global fundraising initiatives by researching and partnering with non-profit organizations and establishing a way for ARMY all around the world to gift micro-donations over one month periods.

Name: Sonoma Young Professionals
Description: The Sonoma Valley Young Professionals is a networking group of Sonoma locals under 40 committed to sharing ideas and making the community a better place.

Name: Camp Kesem at University of Illinois
Description:
Kesem is a nationwide community, driven by passionate college student leaders, that supports children through and beyond a parent’s cancer. Kesem’s flagship program, Camp Kesem, operates over 125 free summer camps across the country for children ages 6 to 18 who have been impacted by a parent’s cancer. Kesem programs have a lasting impact on children by providing them a peer-support network that understands their unique needs, builds confidence and strengthens their communication skills. In 2019, Kesem served more than 10,200 children in 44 states – all funded by generous donations from individuals and corporate support. For more information on Kesem, please visit kesem.org, Facebook.com/CampKesem, and @CampKesem on Twitter & Instagram.
 
Camp Kesem at University of Illinois was founded in 2007 and supports children in the central Illinois community by providing a free week-long summer camp experience and year-round peer support. Camp Kesem at University of Illinois is operated by 70 student volunteers and serves 120 campers ages 6-18 per year. For more information about Camp Kesem at University of Illinois please visit campkesem.org/illinois or facebook.com/campkesemillinois.

Name: EM'donésie
Description: Association humanitaire visant à améliorer les conditions de vie des familles balinaises en situation de précarité extrême.

Name: Silverlines Philanthropy Corporation
Description:
The Silverlines Philanthropy Corporation is dedicated to improving quality of life and happiness of the National Capital Region. Silverlines accomplishes this goal by serving three main groups:

- People with mood disorders. Silverlines runs an initiative called Sonrisa, which will organize, promote, and provide support for mental health groups in Ottawa-Gatineau. Additionally, Silverlines will facilitate free events in public spaces with the simple goal of brightening someone’s day.

- Seniors in need of companionship. Through a program named Horiza, Silverlines coordinates volunteers at various hospitals and retirement centres around the city, helping ensure that no one feels lonely. Silverlines also donates to other charities in Ottawa-Gatineau that make a positive difference with our elderly residents.

- Children suffering from a serious illness. Silverlines supports Child’s Play, a global charity that provides toys, books, movies, and videogames to children’s hospitals. Donations made through Silverlines go directly toward cheering up patients at CHEO. Through Kind Sol, Silverlines also coordinates social events for non-hospitalized sick children, at home and in care facilities like Rogers House.

Silverlines’s initiatives are year-round, but we prioritize a third of the year for each campaign, to ensure that each receives adequate assistance. Sonrisa runs February-May, Horiza runs June-September, and Kind Sol runs October-January.

Charitable tax id: #85013 7449 RR0001

Name: THRIVE (Executive Servant Leadership Network)
Description: THRIVE exists to promote the practices of Servant Leadership among current and future leaders in our communities and around the world. Servant Leadership works for the well-being of all stakeholders by the pursuit of excellence in both people and process within organizations. Servant Leadership is the best path to long term business success and results in human sustainability. Our organization is a nationwide nonprofit comprised of C-Level leaders in #100 million + organizations who work individually and  together for the good of all people by promoting the healthy practice of Servant Leadership.

Name: Komunitná nadácia Zdravé mesto
Description: Our mission is to strengthen the social capital, to improve the quality of life in the region of Banská Bystrica, Sliač, Zvolen (200.000 inhabitants) and to support the development of volunteering and philanthropy. We are supporting small community projects, running the YouthBank program, developing programs for donors.

Name: Julia Anne Kirsch Foundation
Description:
The Julia Anne Kirsch Foundation seeks to improve the lives of disabled individuals and their families.  The Foundation aims to do this through outright grants and also through the support of organizations who support the Foundation’s goals.  

Some examples of how we have helped include:

•	Equipment Grant - Provided specialized equipment to disabled individuals. 

•	Education - Supported a program through The Arc of Bergen & Passaic designed to help families navigate the system to obtain services and to protect their financial interests. 

•	Cooking Lessons - Provided a series of cooking lessons to disabled adults designed to teach them how to prepare healthy meals. 

•	Dance Program - Provided support to a ballet program at a New Jersey school that serves disabled children.

•      Scholarship - Established a scholarship at a New Jersey high school for a students that is interested in pursuing college studies that will lead to a career in serving the disabled.

Name: Empreende Menina
Description: O Empreende Menina é uma associação privada sem fins lucrativos. Nosso propósito é dar voz a meninas em vulnerabilidade social, formar uma nova geração de mulheres empreendedoras capazes de mudar o seu destino e depois o mundo.

Name: Educadev
Description:
Fondée en 1991, Educadev est une association d'intérêt général intervenant pour aider des enfants défavorisés à poursuivre leur scolarité dans de bonnes conditions.

L'association a été créée par un groupe d'amis ayant vécu en Colombie et participé sur place à des actions solidaires dans les quartiers pauvres de Bogotá. Au fil des années, Educadev a monté et géré des programmes dans 10 pays, en s'appuyant sur des correspondants locaux. Ces correspondants sont des personnes de confiance, que nous connaissons personnellement, qui animent les programmes sur le terrain et rendent compte qualitativement et financièrement.

Name: Hygeia Corp. Preventative Mobile Dentistry
Description: Hygiea Corp: Preventative Mobile Dentistry provides preventative dental services to the elder population with mobile and tele-dentistry.  We bring oral care to the elders home.  We provide dental prophylaxis's, digital x-rays, periodontal screenings, oral cancer screenings, tele-dentistry, fluoride and silver diamine treatments, and necessary referrals to dentists and specialists.  We also provide training to nursing staff and the community about the importance of oral health throughout our entire lifetime.

Name: Ethos Advisory Services
Description: Ethos Advisory is a consulting firm that works with nonprofit organizations, executives, private and corporate foundations to develop the strategies that center community voice in social impact solutions. Ethos shortens the space between good and great social impact solutions through strategic advising, facilitation, and coaching.

Name: Stray Haven Humane Society & SPCA
Description: Stray Haven Humane Society & SPCA is a small, not-for-profit animal shelter organization serving all of Tioga County, NY and our neighboring counties. We receive no operational funding from the state or county level or other charitable agencies and rely totally on adoptions and our very generous donors to keep our doors open. Stray haven was founded over 40 years ago by a woman named Elaine Forkan, who wanted to provide a shelter for lost and unwanted animals to be taken care of until they found new homes. Our facility began with a donated building in 1967 and with generous donations, we now have an office, small animal clinic and the original kennels which are undergoing updates. Recent donations have allowed us to install a fence around much of the property giving our shelter animals a safe place to exercise. We continue to raise funds to keep our buildings safe and secure for years to come. Today we offer many services, including pet adoptions, spay/neuter programs, dog obedience classes, pet therapy, cruelty investigations and humane education.

Name: A Heart For China
Description:
On 25 April 2015, an earthquake in Nepal, east of Gorkha District, killed nearly 9,000 people and injured nearly 22,000. Hundreds of thousands of people were left homeless and whole villages were leveled across many districts of the country. In the folowing hours and days worldwide aid was offered to the country and its population. Rescue and medical teams were send to the afflicted area with medical supplies, food, water and other life-saving items. Donations were gathered in many countries of the world to send to Nepal. The humanitarion response was tremendous!

It was on the evening of 29 April 2015 that I asked myself why we are often roused to help others only in the wake of a disaster. At that moment, it struck me to do something good and simple for the people around me in the country and city that I was living in: Shenzhen, China. I quickly created a group on WeChat, the largest Chinese social media platform, to gather more like-minded people looking to give back to their community. And so, A Heart for China (AHFC) was born.

AHFC has grown into an international charity volunteer group that helps the poor, the elderly, autistic and disabled children, orphans and the helpless in China who need aid and affection. The idea was to donate our time by doing volunteer work and and providing warmth, love and appreciation to those in need.

The organization A Heart for China was officially founded on May 5th, 2015 by  Ronny Verdoodt, Belgium, and has since organized over 110 events. Nearly every weekend, our volunteers visit rehabilitation centers for special-needs children and nursing homes for the elderly to accompany them, chat, play games and organize fun performances. When possible, we offer ideas for improvements to the centers and provide essential daily items or donations.

Our group has been featured by several media outlets in China, and many partner organizations have helped us spread our message of love and positivity to inspire more people.

Name: We Will Survive Cancer
Description: We Will Survive Cancer (WWSC) is a 501(c)3 not for profit, tax exempt organization whose purpose is to provide support to families of cancer victims who lack the time, financial, or other resources to adequately address their needs.  WWSC will raise money to serve as a conduit to organizations and individuals to assist the families of cancer victims address the myriad needs of managing a family, to include, but not limited to child care, family counseling, managing household finances, arranging family transportation to visit the patient, and to provide general assistance to the families of the cancer victim.

Name: Urban Roots - Philadelphia
Description:
Urban Roots is a philanthropic organization dedicated to revitalizing communities by building and enhancing urban redevelopment projects, with an emphasis on youth mentoring, healthy living and fine art.

Through a Partnership with ACE Mentoring, Youth Workshops are developed to Engage, Excite, and Enlighten high school students to pursue careers in urban redevelopment. Using actual urban redevelopment projects as case studies, students learn about Design, Construction, Fine Art; and the importance of community engagement to the success of this process. 

In addition to conducting youth workshops throughout the year, Urban Roots also hosts a series of tours with students from Youth Build Charter School and the Workshop School in West Philadelphia. Our objective is to expose youth to a wide variety of different vocational opportunities throughout the city of Philadelphia that will best align with their skills and interest. Site locations include: Lincoln Financial Field, the Murano Building, the Naval Yard, and coming up soon Citizens Bank Park!

Description:

Name: Westside Dance Marathon
Description: Westside Dance Marathon is a year-long fundraising effort to benefit Omaha Children's Hospital. We host monthly events with our miracle kids, culminating in a 6-hour high-energy celebration of the kids' lives. Our DM chapter, the first ever in Nebraska, was founded in 2021. Over our first year, we raised $26,697.35 and won the Best New High School Dance Marathon at the 2022 Dance Marathon Leadership Conference. Description:

The financial and emotional tolls are high and Joshua’s Gift aims to provide relief through funding and social experiences that might otherwise be inaccessible. 

By equipping families with the support and tools to navigate an autism diagnosis, we are fostering an environment of awareness and inclusion, not just for those affected by autism but communities far and wide.

Name: University of Oregon Dance Marathon
Description: University of Oregon Dance Marathon (UODM) is a student-led non-profit organization focused on raising funds and awareness for their local Children's Miracle Network Hospital, Peace Health Sacred Heart Medical Center at Riverbend, in Springfield, OR. All money raised goes directly to the hospital in helping with various funding throughout as well as within the hospital where it is most needed. Since first being founded in 2007, UODM has raised over $200,000 for the hospital. The organization hosts many events throughout the academic year to help gain awareness over the cause, as well as a large event in the Winter.

Name: Association Narin
Description:
The association's objective is to promote and protect the rights of individuals to equal access of possibilities irrespective of their nationality, gender, religion, sexual orientation or physical abilities.

In order to achieve this, we organise punctiform activities that will contribute to a more inclusive society. Everyone is welcome to help us with that effort!

Name: Fonds de dotation Habitats et Familles Solidaires
Description: La Fondation Habitats et Familles Solidaires se veut un outil financier efficace et pérenne pour soutenir les organismes d’intérêt général œuvrant dans l’accueil de publics vulnérables, au travers de projets et de réalisations d’habitats.

Name: Sign For Malaysia
Description: Sign For Malaysia is a Deaf empowerment project started by Serena Lee in 2018. We aim to initiate dialogue on Deaf issues, foster empathy for the Deaf community, and increase awareness about the Malaysian national sign language, Bahasa Isyarat Malaysia (BIM).

Name: Creative Minds Talks
Description: Creative Minds Talks is a platform that features the ‘art of storytelling’ and sits with the brightest minds in art, architecture, fashion, music, film, sports and philanthropy for captivating, on-stage dialogue. Passion meets purpose here as we aim to engage, educate, entertain, and inspire through conversation. Creative Minds Talks also believes in giving back to the community and does so by making financial contributions to causes championed by our guest speakers, as well as investing in the next generation of leaders, by inviting local youth to share in these enriching experiences.

Name: Soroptimist International of the TriCities
Description:
Soroptimist International of the TriCities serves the communities of Port Moody, Coquitlam and Port Coquitlam.  We are part of Soroptimist International; a global volunteer organization of 76,000 working to improve the lives of women and girls through programs leading to social and economic empowerment.
We have a membership of 50 dedicated volunteers, and our community impact has grown enormously since our inception 22 years ago.  Our club is solely volunteer-driven, and the Tri Cities Chamber of Commerce honoured us with the prestigious award of Not-for-Profit of the Year in February 2016.

Our mandate is to educate, enable, and empower girls and women.  Our broad community impact includes such programs as: 
A Warm Place for Women - a monthly event providing meals, friendship, education, clothing and household items to 900+ vulnerable women and their children annually
Bea's Kloset - a ‘free store’ for women and young girls transitioning to life on their own
CABE Lunch and Learn - a monthly mentoring and educational program for at-risk teen girls attending this alternate school. 
Suwa'lkh– a monthly education program for at-risk aboriginal girls, with similar curriculum as the CABE project
Dream it Be It Career Education- an educational conference for teen girls, exploring goals and careers, followed up by monthly workshops
Live Your Dream Awards - for post secondary education awarded to vulnerable women returning to school or outstanding teen girls who volunteer in the community.
Club Diginista – 8 week workshops teaching young girls the basics of coding skills
Walk a Mile  - an educational “walk” to encourage societal discussion on issues facing women today
We are change agents. We are business owners, nurses, realtors, managers, instructors, IT experts, mortgage brokers, politicians, consultants, interior designers and more. We are mothers, neighbours, sisters, daughters, caregivers and friends.
Are You One of Us? 
Ready to Make a Difference?

Name: FARMER HELP!
Description:
FARMER HELP!!Empowering Farmers 

Growing Climate Change, The Rising Cost of Cultivation, High Rate of Interest on Loans and Scanty rainfall have all contributed to making life a living hell for most important community in our Country, Our Farmers.The need to help farmers in India is now greater than ever and you can help through small donation.

We at FARMER HELP intend to provide Seeds, Saplings, Pesticides, Free Pumps for irrigation, Pay Electricity Bills of farmers to assist in the farming process.By doing so FARMER HELP want to ensure farmers have a viable source of income and livelihood to support their families.
FARMER HELP's aim is to improve livelihood of farmers in this country and make this Country Green.We want to help to put an end to FARMER SUICIDE and CHRONIC POVERTY in community that depend on Agriculture for their livelihoods.
WE WANT TO HAVE ENVIRONMENTAL AND SOCIAL IMPACT .WE ALSO WANT TO PROMOTE IRRIGATION WITHOUT EMITTING POLLUTING GREENHOUSE GASES

Name: Canterbury Partners
Description:
Canterbury Partners invests in entrepreneurs who have a clear vision of how to effect positive change in people’s lives, combined with the experience, hard work and commitment to deliver on this. When relevant, we also provide advice and mentoring to help these visionary social entrepreneurs succeed.

We understand the importance of diversity in the field of social impact, and the barriers that many women-led enterprises, in particular, face when accessing capital. We believe this market has huge potential for growth, but is currently underserved by institutional investors. For this reason, we also focus on companies where women own at least 50 per cent of the business.

Our investments range in size from £25,000 to £3,000,000. For larger investments, our preference is to take a majority shareholding in the business.

Name: Buddy2Buddy
Description:
Dear Linkedin Family,

No one is as powerful as everyone. In Buddy2Buddy initiative, we are inviting people to share untold stories of founders/start-ups, who are making our world a better place with their innovative ideas, hard work, sacrifices and struggles. Participation rules are simple:

(1)	Share a founder-startup story/incident, which is inspirational (maximum 100 words).
(2)	Tag the founder/startup, your close friends along with Buddy2Buddy page (@Buddy2Buddy) to allow them to share/comment their feelings.
(3)	Founder may comment about his challenges and need any help.
(4)	Buddy2Buddy Community will share this story with the entire world to bring recognition and support to the founder. 

Note:
The story must start as "I know Mr. ....... of ....... for last ... years, when he/she  ........................... I remember when ........... .........“ One thing, I truly admire about him is ....... Preferably, you can add a picture (or video) of the founder to make it more lively.

Let we all undiscover the true stories of our time and build a community to recognize & help many such founders to serve our society. Thank you.

https://www.linkedin.com/company/buddy2buddy

Name: Council on Fundraising - CONFR
Description:
CONFR’s mission is to educate and empower the nonprofit community to raise more money for a stronger New Hampshire. CONFR is the sole provider of ongoing, statewide, high-quality professional fundraising training in New Hampshire. CONFR builds the capacity of nonprofit organizations by improving the skills and expanding the knowledge of professional and volunteer fundraisers - including executive directors, development officers, and board members - that focuses on fundraising applications and best practices in fundraising management. We believe this training will help organizations fulfill their missions in their respective communities while increasing charitable giving throughout the state. More than 5000 fundraisers have attended CONFR fund development programs since 1982. Connecting - Educating - Empowering: Advancing fundraising to make missions happen.
www.confr.org.

Conferences - Coffee with CONFR - Workshops - CONFR to Go for training for YOUR board and staff at YOUR place at YOUR time.

Name: Zonta Club of Auckland North Shore
Description:
Zonta International is a global organisation of women executives and professionals working together to advance the status of women worldwide through service and advocacy. Formed in 1919, Zonta has over 31,000 members and is active in 65 countries of the world. Learn more about Zonta International on www.zonta.org   
  
Members of Zonta Clubs support women and girls in their local communities, run projects in many parts of the world that address some of the most pressing needs facing women, and advocate on women issues at all levels of society. Zonta International has a long and well respected history of partnership in conjunction with the United Nations agencies, and maintains consultative status on women issues with the UN, and Council of Europe.

In New Zealand, Zonta has been active for more than 45 years, and has 28 clubs from Northland, to Otago. We combine service and advocacy, with fun and fellowship. We welcome membership enquiries from women wishing to join us in this important work.

Name: Unforeseen Clothing
Description: Bringing high quality products and community-driven culture, Unforeseen is a movement that aims to recalibrate the use and love of streetwear. Name: Global Philanthropy Advisors
Description:
We combine creative, strategic thinking with best practices and networks cultivated through decades of hands-on experience working with leading organizations and philanthropists around the globe.

STRATEGIC PHILANTHROPY: We partner with individuals and family foundations at every stage of the philanthropic journey. Together, we will help chart a mission and strategy that aligns with your beliefs, create and manage effective programs and foundations, conduct due diligence on non-profits, manage grant portfolios, and monitor impact against your overarching goals.

FUNDRAISING: Your organization’s ability to create change tomorrow depends on your fundraising success today. From crafting holistic development plans and diversifying Boards, to creating innovative fundraising solutions across individual, foundation, and corporate funding streams, we help leading non-profits turn their mission into impact. 

DONOR COMMUNICATIONS: Powerful stories alone aren’t enough to drive action; readers need to see themselves as a critical part of the solution. We create donor-centered issue briefs, impact and annual reports, online and direct mail appeals, and more that inspire increased engagement and generate vital resources to help advance your mission.  

ADVOCACY: Whether bringing attention to the plight of sexual violence survivors, or introducing child safety experts, social justice activists, and first responders to the most influential foundations and policy shapers in the country, we help raise the visibility and funding needed to ensure your movement succeeds.

EVENT PRODUCTION: Every detail — sponsor and marketing materials, speakers, seating, decor, follow-up — is an opportunity to endear attendees to your cause. We have created field visits and cultivation and fundraising events for 10-1,000+ guests in private homes, palaces, movie studios, minefields, and more, and maintain an extensive network of vendors around the world to help maximize your investment.

Name: African Spirit by EMN
Description:
African Spirit est une association humanitaire à but non lucratif composée d'étudiants issus de l’Ecole de Management de Normandie. Elle intervient dans tout le continent africain. Le pays est sélectionné en fonction du projet de l’année qu’African Spirit choisis. 

Ses principaux objectifs sont :  
-	L’apport d’aide physique et financière dans le but de soutenir les personnes défavorisées
-	Aider au développement et à l’amélioration des conditions de vie des populations défavorisés 
-	Développer les régions reculées 

Le projet 2018 se situe à Saint-Louis au Sénégal et à pour but de continuer la construction d'une toiture de salle de soin et d'une aire de jeux pour les enfants démunis. Nous agirons également sur le plan éducatif avec des soutiens scolaires en mathématiques et en français. 

Les projets déjà réalisés sont : 
2013 : Reconstruction d’une école à Antanambao – Madagascar
2014 : construction d’une école et peinture de pirogues à Atapolo – Madagascar
2015 : construction d’un centre de soin à Antanambao – Madagascar
2016 : rénovation du toit d’une école à Abobo Baoulé– Côte d’Ivoire
2017 : cours et rénovation d’une partie d’une école à Kigamboni – Tanzanie

Name: Central Pennsylvania Operation Warm Hearts
Description: We are an organization committed to letting local populations know that someone cares. We plan to reach out to the community through a variety of programs, such as providing knitted essentials to the less fortunate.

Name: LUZEMI
Description: Luzemi busca difundir los horrores y atender los horrores dejados por terrorismo en el Perú entre 1980-2000. Nuestro objetivo es ayudar a las víctimas del terrorismo, brindarles la información, orientación y ayudarlos en la obtención de sus derechos.

Name: Projeto Social - Viver para Servir Suzano
Description:
O projeto Viver para Servir nasceu em nossos corações com o propósito de transformar vidas. A transformação que acreditamos não se resume à doações de cestas básicas, embora esta ação faça parte do nosso trabalho e esteja tão bem indicada em Provérbios 3:27 e 28- “Quando lhe for possível, não deixe de fazer o bem a quem dele precisa. Não diga ao seu próximo: ‘Volte amanhã, e eu darei algo a você’, se pode ajudá-lo hoje”. Entendemos que se o próprio Senhor Jesus veio ao mundo para servir, assim nós também faremos. Nesse sentido, nossa equipe conta com a participação de diversos voluntários que compartilham dos ideais de doação, entrega e amor de Cristo. Estamos engajados em diferentes frentes de trabalho, como: 

Departamento de Psicologia: quando pensamos em saúde não devemos estar restritos à saúde do nosso corpo (ausência de doenças, desconfortos físicos), mas contemplar também o cuidado com nossos sentimentos, emoções, e até mesmo com a nossa própria história.  Os profissionais deste departamento, portanto, nos auxiliam com esse trabalho. 

Departamento de Serviço Social: porta de entrada, coordenação e administração de recursos e doações do projeto Servir para Viver. 

Departamento jurídico: ampliar e assegurar o acesso de pessoas vulneráveis para resolução de conflitos judiciais. 

Departamento Odontológico: cuidado com a saúde bucal da nossa comunidade. Bem aventurado seja o profissional que tem a melhor recompensa que poderia ter: o sorriso de alguém. 

Departamento Médico: assistência médica à saúde. 

Departamento de LIBRAS: a palavra de Deus deve ser acessível a todos. Buscando a inclusão de pessoas surdas, o grupo “Mãos Consagradas” oferece curso de LIBRAS. 

Curso preparatório para o ENEM- SUZAD: acreditamos na força transformadora da educação, e, junto com competentes professores lutamos com jovens para que estes alcancem um espaço universitário e  se realizem profissionalmente.

Name: The Canadian Arctic Project
Description: The Canadian Arctic Project raises funds and advocacy for Food insecurity in Iqaluit and provides free health coaching services through alumni of the largest nutrition school in the world, The Institute for Integrative Nutrition.

Name: AMREX - Asociación de Músicos en Riesgo de Exclusión
Description: AMREX es la Asociación de Músicos en Riesgo de Exclusión, una organización sin ánimo de lucro perteneciente a la Fundación Altavista. Esta iniciativa busca reivindicar el papel sociocultural de la música y, especialmente, asistir, representar y atender los intereses de los profesionales autónomos de la música, dando preferencia a las personas en riesgo de exclusión social.

Name: Real Life. My Music.
Description:
Founded in 2009, Real Life. My Music. exists to create a safer and healthier Detroit for our school children by providing them access to quality afterschool programming that emphasizes structured training in fine arts; specifically music and dance. By offering students an artistic outlet in which to express themselves and by supporting their interest in pursuing fine arts programs if they choose to do so, it is our belief that they will not only learn an art form, but also develop positive characteristics and skills that are proven to be correlated with the improvement of academic and personal outcomes.

Real Life. My Music.  holds hour and a half long music, dance and art classes three times per week for a period of sixteen weeks. Our classes cover technique as well as an appreciation and history of the mediums.  This sixteen-week period culminates with a showcase of the children’s newly learned craft.

We also offer a program called Field Trip Saturdays. One Saturday a month RLMM provides the students enrolled in our program the opportunity to experience art outside of their daily environment through attending informative and hands-on field trips meant to entice and stimulate their mind. In the metro Detroit area there are a lot of spectacular things for kids to experience such as the Detroit Institute of Art, Center for Creative Studies, Henry Ford Museum and a host of others.

Name: Aashraya
Description:
Aashraya is an accountable, self-sufficient and consistent social entrepreneurial group of enthusiastic and fiercely driven individuals who seek a presence both in the business world and the social sector.

Aashraya emerges from a well-networked group of business professionals, who work self-sufficiently in the Aashraya realm, supported by their existing organizations and businesses.  These professionals work together with existing businesses, combining existing skill and innovative ideas to develop products that are beneficial both to the existing businesses that provide materials for them, and for the social entrepreneurial side of the coin – providing profit that is funneled directly into existing, transparently effective, and overseen social programs.  Because existing business networks are tapped to develop Aashraya products, and because professionals are supported by existing business ventures, Aashraya products have no operating costs, eliminating the often-crippling need for outside support from grants and supporters.  Aashraya is thus not at the mercy of supporters’ whims and desires when developing projects for social investment.

Name: Change Your Change
Description:
We've found that many people want to give & help others, but they have so little themselves, & when you're living in a place like the US, giving someone $20 will pretty much just get them a meal or two at most.

So we created Change Your Change, an organization that connects donors with people in the Philippines, where the small donation of $20 is the equivalent of 2 days' wages.

We do not accept solicitations, but we find the people who aren't asking for help, but who could obviously use it.

We want people to know that they are not alone, that they are noticed and appreciated and loved. So we seek these people out and do things for them that will improve their lives.

Sometimes what we do is simply fun, like taking a group of kids & their grandmother, Zima, to the movies for the 1st time (none of them had seen a movie in the theater before, and two of the kids were as old as 12!).

Often, what we do has a lasting, meaningful impact on our recipients' lives, like finding Lolo Inkong, an 84 year-old fisherman who fishes in a broken-down boat to help support his widowed daughter and her children & then buying him a brand new boat (so now he has 2 boats!).

The ways that we can help are endless, which means that the ways that YOU can help are endless, too.  We want you to be a part of things and we want you to know that even though you can only afford to give a little, you can really change a lot because of it.

We love you & we hope that you will join our cause.

Love,
Joshua & Resha
