Name: MOND'ACTION
Description:
Créée le 19 août 1996 par Joelle et Denis Bordeneuve, l'association MOND’ACTION qui va bientôt fêter ses 20 ans d’existence, a pour but principal de porter secours à l'enfance et aux familles en détresse à travers 3 axes : Santé, Education et Développement local.

MOND'ACTION intervient aujourd'hui principalement en Afrique: au Niger et au Togo, mais aussi sur le Mali et le Burkina Faso. Grâce à des partenariats avec des ONG françaises et internationales, ainsi que des associations autochtones locales, MOND'ACTION a pu mener à bien plusieurs projets d’envergure qui sont aujourd'hui reconnus et soutenus par les autorités des pays concernés. Nous travaillons toujours de concert avec les villageois que ce soit dans l’évaluation des besoins locaux, l’écriture des projets ou leurs mise en oeuvre.

Nous privilégions la concertation, la formation et l’implication des équipes locales, via des partenariats et des échanges multiples, dans le but d’assurer une gouvernance, une autonomie et une durabilité des projets optimales. Aujourd’hui nous sommes heureux de compter plusieurs milliers de bénéficiaires annuels, que ce soit par la dispense de soins de qualité, l’accès à l’éducation,le placement en centre d’accueil, ou encore l’embauche d’hommes et de femmes sans activité sur tous ces projets.

Name: 100MARIN
Description:
100+ People Who Care: Marin County (100MARIN) is a members-only circle of like-minded, philanthropic individuals dedicated to enhancing the support and awareness of Marin County nonprofits. 

Founded in January 2015, the group has over 400 members. 

100MARIN was created by a group of local, community-minded business professionals, with the following complementary objectives:

1. To increase each circle member’s overall giving impact 

2. To develop and strengthen the network of individuals who are passionate about the local non-profit community

We invite you to explore this page and become part of Marin County's premier giving circle: #100MARIN

Name: That Spark 悦享新知
Description:
That Spark is a social enterprise that joyfully creates and shares content and learning experiences to inspire, connect, and engage China's community of changemakers.

We believe that everyone deserves the opportunity to develop into the person and professional they want to be, including and especially those that work in service of others.

Making a generous contribution to a better world isn't easy. We want to provide that spark to provide fuel for the journey. We do this by offering a unique mix of user-driven, scalable content and small-group learning experiences.

Name: Enrichment Fund for Guilford County Schools
Description: The Enrichment Fund for the Guilford County Schools makes it possible for all students to benefit from the same enrichment opportunities and for teachers to be as creative as they’re inspired to be. Each year, we award hundreds of small gifts that support high-impact learning experiences for as many people as possible. Our vision: that teachers dedicated to going above and beyond have the support to carry out transformative experiences for their classes — and every student can benefit from them.

Name: Fundación Paréntesis 20.11
Description:
Brindamos acompañamiento integral a individuos y familias en condiciones de vulnerabilidad en la República Dominicana.

Las acciones de la Fundación Parentesis 20.11 han sido reconocidas con el Premio al Voluntariado Solidario de Vicepresidencia de la República en el año 2014, y con el Premio Nacional de la Juventud en el año 2016.

El trabajo de la organización se concentra en:

1- Brindar asistencia integral a familias en condiciones especiales.

2- Acompañar estudiantes meritorios de escasos recursos en el retorno a clases.

3- Organizar jornadas de capacitación técnica - empresarial para grupos vulnerables.

4- Desarrollar programas de charlas de prevención de violencia de género, medidas preventivas para las adicciones, entre otros.

Name: Rotary Club of Bakersfield Twilight
Description: Rotary Club of Bakersfield Twilight brings like-minded people together for fellowship and service.

Name: I Love Schools, Inc. | iLoveSchools.com
Description: iLoveSchools.com is a unique national non-profit helping teachers and schools by linking them to donors of new, used and in-kind classroom resources. Our unique Internet-based solution enables donors to place learning resources inside the classroom regardless of economic, demographic or geographic circumstances.

Name: The Young Professionals' Group - Singapore
Description:
YPG Singapore is a registered society that provides character-based scholarship and mentorship to young people to help them achieve their professional dreams. 

YPG Singapore would be delighted to explore partnership opportunities with potential sponsors and donors. Feel free to reach out to us. Thank you!

Name: VETERANS IN CRISIS
Description: Veterans in Crisis (VIC) is the sole community-based nonprofit serving Northern Michigan military veterans facing acute crises. As a “go-to” resource, VIC takes rapid, targeted action to deliver immediate and direct relief, providing for the urgent needs of those who are too often the last to ask for help. Established in 2015, VIC serves by leveraging local relationships and building the necessary capital to effect straightforward, timely solutions that preserve individual dignity. Find out how you can help by visiting www.veteransincrisis.org.

Name: UppFutures
Description:
UppFutures was founded in 2020 to help children in economically poor areas of Ghana to receive an education through the provision of school uniforms and other essential equipment. School uniforms are compulsory for a child to attend school, however this expense is too much for some low income families. 

If a child doesn't attend school, they are at a greater risk of being exploited through child labour, often of which is physically dangerous. Additionally, for each year spent out of school, the future prospects for a child diminish.

We don't want the lack of school uniforms to be a reason why any child has to miss out on an education, so UppFutures is committed to providing school uniforms in regions where there is a high proportion of school absenteeism.

We work with local seamstresses to make the school uniforms, ensuring we support the local economies we operate within. 

We'd be delighted if you follow our journey and help with our projects.

Name: FUNDANOVA
Description: FUNDANOVA es una organización privada sin fines de lucro, que canaliza la experiencia y responsabilidad social de jóvenes salvadoreños comprometidos con el desarrollo humano y la educación. Se solidariza con los jóvenes menos favorecidos y se pone al servicio de ellos a través de acciones que promueven proyectos para el desarrollo integral de las familias.

Name: Stone of Help - Haiti
Description:
Stone of Help Haiti is an international 501(c)(3) non-profit organization. Our mission is to promote spiritual and social transformation of the Haitian people, primarily through Christian Discipleship and actions in Education, Health and Nutrition within various communities. Our vision is to witness the life-changing power of the Gospel of Jesus Christ as it is seen and spoken in lives and relationships.

We are proactive in offering aid and assistance to the life challenges of Haiti’s most vulnerable communities.

Christian Discipleship: We provide pastoral oversight and ministry training to community churches and church leaders in Haiti. Our aim is to teach and unify this network of churches in sound Biblical doctrine. It is our desire to raise up healthy and well-balanced churches through prayer, praise, and proclamation of God’s Word, the Bible.

Education: We endeavor to provide an atmosphere where children and youth may realize and develop their God-given gifts and abilities. Our vision is to see God’s children reach their full potential and become a positive asset in the communities where they reside.

Health: We are dedicated to confronting ongoing healthcare issues while seeking to impact communities via the gospel of Jesus Christ. We purpose to serve healthcare needs of local communities and remote villages by mobilizing volunteers and healthcare professionals who operate primarily through local churches and schools.

Nutrition: We are active in confronting the harsh reality of widespread hunger. We resolve to provide children with a school-time meal on a daily basis. When bodily needs are satisfied, a person is also much more receptive to learning. Stone of Help Haiti also serves to encourage the local production of crops from which we purchase most extensively.

http://www.stoneofhelphaiti.org

Name: California Medical Billing Advocates
Description: California Medical Billing Advocates is a non-profit organization dedicated to helping consumers with unjust medical costs. Medical debt is a leading cause of US bankruptcies. We believe that it is important to intervene at the medical billing stage, before unpaid bills become medical debt.

Name: JIBIKA Foundation
Description: JIBIKA’ is a Bengali term meaning ‘Livelihood’ that refers to earning bread for survival. The objective of establishing the project is to create opportunities for those who are unemployed, underemployed or not in a position to recover from financial adversity afflicting their scope of survival.

Name: Croeni Foundation
Description:
HAPPIER, HEALTHIER HUMANS AND ANIMALS

Croeni Charity is a community of positive change makers and social entrepreneurs.

We are united by our philanthropic actions for a truly harmonic, compassionate and liveable world.

Since 2015, we unlocked USD10M+ in funding for 25+ partners and grantees, 
protected endangered animals and 2,500ha rain forest, supported the training of 500+ social entrepreneurs, and much more...

Name: THOMAS MORE Kultur.Club e.V.
Description:
On a 6,500 sqm plot of land leased for 25 years, we are building a large cultural and community garden open to all interested parties. It is a generational project in Hochheim in the location "Langgewann" in the immediate vicinity of allotments and vineyards. A meadow orchard, insect biosphere including bee boxes, kitchen garden, fireplace and a large open space for play, sports and cultural events form the heart of this sustainable project. Of particular note is the continuation of our diverse cultural festival and the creation of a natural ecology education program.

The Kultur.Garten will also be available for non-profit and church associations as well as schools and kindergartens as a place for activities such as large group games, nature experience days, tent overnights, outdoor lessons and many more.

Name: Pritchard Francis Perth to Surf
Description:
Pritchard Francis Perth to Surf is a charity group aiming to raise money for Telethon through the annual event of a continuous walk between Perth and Margaret River, Western Australia (330km walk, <70 hours).

Founded in 2012, and having raised more than $45,000 for Telethon from the first two events, the 2014 event will be even bigger than before, aiming to raise $40,000.

Please consult the website (www.perthtosurf.com) for more details.

Name: Farmington/Farmington Hills Education Foundation
Description:
The Farmington/Farmington Hills Education Foundation is a non-profit 501(c)(3) charitable oganization.  Our mission is to foster investment in innovative educational opportunities to benefit the students of Farmington Public Schools.  We are governed by a volunteer Board of Trustees comprised of community leaders, parents and educators.  Our targeted areas of investment are Technology, Scholastic Achievement, The Arts and Extracurricular Enrichment.

We seek individual, civic and corporate donations to assist us in achieving our goal of providing additional learning opportunities for the students of Farmington Public Schools.  We seek mentoring opportunities, project development and internship experiences with our local businesses and civic organizations.

Name: Fundación Viae
Description:
Somos una entidad sin fines de lucro que acompaña a niñas, niños, adolescentes de 0 a 16 años y su entorno familiar en situación de vulnerabilidad. 

Viae se inició  con un grupo de profesionales quienes – por inquietudes e intereses personales y trayectoria laboral tanto en el ámbito público como privado – creímos que debíamos hacer algo que nos permitiera brindar apoyo y acompañamiento a aquellos niñas, niños, adolescentes y sus familias que se encuentren en situación de vulnerabilidad.   

También para tender una mano a aquellas organizaciones que ya vienen trabajando en este sentido, y muchas veces necesitan colaboración o ampliar sus horizontes.

Nuestro compromiso es acompañar el camino y generar espacios de encuentro como una manera de construir un mundo más equitativo.

Nuestro foco está en prevenir y brindar apoyo para hacer frente a desafíos complejos, como enfermedades crónicas, desnutrición, discapacidad, acceso a la educación o situaciones de violencia.

Name: KCL Global Health Society
Description: KCL Global Health Society is a society that celebrates the interdisciplinary nature of health from social science perspectives, by providing a platform to students with a keen interest in health at a global level and the socioeconomic, political and cultural determinants that highlight how health is more than a medical matter!

Name: Generaction Project
Description: We are a non-profit organization which gathers youth volunteers from different backgrounds to teach English to underprivileged children or in remote areas.

Name: BEAUNE Générations
Description:
Organisation à but non-lucratif, créée en janvier 2020, à disposition des actions et assocations beaunoises.
Nous comptons aujourd'hui plus de 230 membres actifs.
Tous réunis, nous avons offert plus de 250 heures de bénévolat à des actions caritatives à Beaune.
Nos maîtres-mots : Union et Action
www.beaunegenerations.fr

Name: SoftPower
Description:
Established by trust, SoftPower are objectively targeted Pilot Communities that create
 new environments maximised for women to flourish.

Community Empowerment and Hunger Projects
Quantum Leaps to Make the Impossible, Possible

Each Project:
Enables customised environments to be seeded, supported, grown, fertilised and cross pollinated for a Quantum Leap in global women empowered communities.   

Will be a collaboration of Qi Women’s Token, community, education and the realisation of possibility employing technology and blockchain

SoftPower was created in July 2018, as the Winning Team in the UN Decade of Women #HackQuantum Worldwide Series in London, at London Finch Week 2018 in the UK.

The Decade of Women HackQuantum Worldwide Series advances quantum leap solutions to meet the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals by uniting the exponential power of women with blockchain, smart contracts and digital assets: to make the impossible possible. 

This hackathon is focused on utilising Qi Women Currency to support SDG No:5 “Gender Equity” in achieving solutions to SDG No:2 No Hunger: Women and Ending World Hunger. 

Now the journey to execution of the Pilot Projects commences.

It starts with a presentation at the Sustainable Development Goals Finance Summit on the International Day of Peace at the United Nations headquarters in New York.

The entire team is thrilled! Watch this space.

Lisa Short
Vince Ming
Tania Zayets
Freya Sheer Hardwick
Toby Sheer Hardwick
Sophia Wang
James Awe

Name: The Quinn Foundation
Description: The Quinn Foundation

Name: La p'tite Escapade
Description:
Bonjour à tous et bienvenue sur la page Linkedin de la P'tite escapade.

En partenariat avec le Secours Populaire, notre projet est de faire découvrir la mer à des enfants n'ayant jamais eu l'occasion de partir en vacances. 

Pour la réalisation de ce projet nous avons besoin de récolter au moins 5000€ et pour le moment nous en avons déjà récolté 3800 grâce à plusieurs événements que nous avons organisé mais il reste encore un long chemin à parcourir avant d'atteindre notre budget estimé. 
Nous comptons donc sur votre générosité pour y parvenir.
                                Chaque don a son importance ! 

Lien de notre cagnotte en ligne : 
https://bit.ly/2TDf7A2

Merci d'avance pour votre aide,

L'équipe de la P'tite Escapade

Name: SIPH•LAB
Description:
SIPH•LAB è un’azienda che opera nel settore della comunicazione mediante le nuove tecnologie.

Ma prima ancora e più ancora di questo, SIPH•LAB è una squadra di giovani avanguardisti che provano a immaginare cosa e come sarà il futuro, per non subirlo e provare con coraggio e fantasia a coglierne invece tutte le opportunità per possederlo.

Ci siamo guardati intorno, abbiamo messo insieme le nostre esperienze accademiche, tecniche, professionali (ancora brevi ma non senza significato) per rispondere a una domanda, che ci appassiona e ci intriga: “Come sarà il futuro di tanti servizi, prodotti e prestazioni che sono già oggi sul mercato?”.

E da questa domanda è iniziata una sfida, fatta d’idee, prove, tentativi, aggiustamenti, adattamenti e affinamenti, caratterizzata da studio, ricerca, sviluppo e test, poi ancora studio, ricerca e test, per rendere sempre più efficace la nostra nuova proposta di comunicazione, proiettata al futuro, che guarda alla volontà e capacità d’imprese e aziende, di essere sempre competitive nel tempo.

Name: Community health and informative solutions initiative
Description:
Community health and informative solutions (CHAIS) is a registered non-profit initiative centered on community development which broadly cuts into these four (4) thematic areas; health, education, advocacy and developmental programs.
Her vision is to improve health, community development and productivity for all especially for the under-served and hard-to-reach communities.
We work with selfless individuals, communities, government institutions, private sector companies, and civil society groups to design, execute and deliver sustainable solutions aimed at creating lasting impact for our beneficiaries.
Our programs are designed to bring healthcare services to the under-served and far-reached communities in Nigeria, through various community mobilization interventions.
CHAIS Initiative's business model improves access to healthcare by providing a logistics platform(mobile clinic) via bi-monthly sensitization exercise to facilitate delivery of medical supplies, life-saving medicines, training and empowering local health workers with appropriate tools for effective delivery.
We are committed to championing solutions and build strong alliances from grassroots to highest policy level to play a pivotal role in achieving universal health coverage and health inclusion in Nigeria and Africa at large. 

Theory of change & solution:
1) Everyone has a role in helping build the right system for development and productivity for all.
2) By promoting open participation, collaboration and tri-sector partnership.
3) By creating connecting platforms for focused groups, change-makers, youth leaders and communities to dialogue and exchange ideas and solutions for impact.

Name: The Sequoia Awards
Description:
It all started in 1990.

A small group of civic leaders led by Pete and Paula Uccelli established The Sequoia Awards in 1990 as a 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization dedicated to honoring volunteerism in Redwood City.

The first Sequoia Awards scholarship was for $500. Since then, The Sequoia Awards has grown significantly thanks to support from local businesses, other nonprofits, and individuals in our community who understand the importance of an active and engaged citizenry. To date, we have distributed more than $2.4 million in scholarships.

Our scholarships are based solely on the volunteerism of our honorees. Academics or participation in school-sponsored activities and athletics are not part of the criteria.

In 2022, The Sequoia Awards distributed $210,000 to 21 students representing 6 local high schools and the middle college program. We named two Outstanding Students: Kenzy Mashraki from Woodside High School and Daniel Torres Aguilar from Everest Public High School. Each received a $30,000 scholarship. Additional honorees received scholarships ranging from $7,500 to $10,000.

Name: Zoorefugio Tarqui
Description:
ZOOREFUGIO TARQUI
The Wildlife Assessment and Reception center - We give you a warm welcome to our Amazonian Animal Refuge Center in Tarqui, located at parish of Tarqui, the city of Puyo, Pastaza Province.

Mission:
Our mission is to preserve wildlife in the rainforest of Ecuador, which is threatened today by anthropogenic activities. For this purpose, we provide specialized care for the welfare of the animal, by promoting scientific research and offering a message of environmental awareness to general public.

Vision:
Our vision is to gather information on behavor, nutrition, and reproduction when necessary, with that object of compiling such information in a database. This information could be accessed by people or organizations to help wild animal care or reserch.

Values:
Because this is the center where we all work carefully with wild species, we must take into account the responsibility, identity, communication, honesty, solidarity, compassion and respect, in order to continue with the daily struggles we face for animal's welfare and conservation of biodiversity in Ecuador.

Our Goals:
To receive, Rehabilitate and Release wild animals when they need it, in case they are rescued from the illegal Wildlife smuggling or in some adverse situations.
To conserve wildlife in the Ecuator's Amazon
To safeguard wildlife and reintroduce them into their natural habitat when we can.
To conduct scientifically viable research.
To educate and sensitize the general public, doing tours and talks about environmental education.
To increase awareness of Ecuador's threatened biodiversity and its importance and value in the field of wildlife conservation.

We handle a variety of species distributed in:
Mammals : Primates, Felinos, Prociónidos, Artiodáctilos, Perisodáctilos, Roedores, Mustélidos
Birds : Psitácidas, Rapaces
Reptiles : Alligators, Tortugas, Serpientes

Name: Friends of Arlington Animal Services
Description: Friends of Arlington Animal Services is a 501(c)(3) non-profit acting independently of the Arlington shelter, whose mission is to help increase the Live Release Rate at Arlington Animal Services and to see to the well being of homeless animals in our city. FAAS is committed to the homeless pets in Arlington and keeping them safe through promoting adoptions, marketing, encouraging volunteerism, supporting rescues, decreasing birth rates, and engaging the community.

Name: The Urban Soccer Initiative
Description:
The Urban Soccer Initiative improves soccer pitch accessibility in cities by bringing high-traffic, smaller-sized pitches to urban neighborhoods where space for full-sized fields is limited or non-existent, and leading in the restoration of high-traffic urban soccer pitches that are in a state of disrepair. 

We build and rehabilitate urban soccer pitches through conducting targeted demographic analyses to identify neighborhoods, sites and prospective site owners/operators that would benefit from a new pitch or pitch restoration; acting as a bridge between financing and/or funding sources and site owners/operators, and; designing, delivering, and building/restoring each pitch.

Name: Caring for Canines
Description:
We are a 501(c)3 non-profit organization dedicated to rescuing homeless dogs and ensuring that they are placed in loving, caring homes. Dogs are rescued both from the euthanasia list at animal shelters and from people who are no longer able to care for their animals. Caring for Canines takes in dogs of all breeds and ages. 

We provide them with medical care, spay and neuter services, vaccinations, referrals and behavioral training. After careful screening, we attempt to adopt the dogs to the best home possible. 

As pet overpopulation is a major problem in Maricopa County and thousands of animals are put to sleep each year, we feel fortunate to have the opportunity to help in this area and are greatly appreciative of any and all community assistance.

We welcome help in areas such as: dog walking, playing and socializing with puppies and adult dogs, general cleanup and maintenance, carpentry (dog houses and shelters), washing and drying dogs, providing nursing care for sick puppies and dogs, transporting dogs, fundraising activities, marketing, veterinary care and assistance.

Name: The Eyeglasses Project
Description: The Eyeglasses Project is a Vancouver-based, volunteer-led initiative whose mission is to: Make Eyesight Accessible. We bring together skilled and resourceful people to help the vulnerable get access to eye examinations and prescription eyeglasses. The skilled include optometrists and other eye care professionals. The resourceful includes community service organizations and sponsors. The Eyeglasses Project was initially founded with the goal to provide the marginalized who reside in Vancouver’s Downtown Eastside with a basic tool, eyeglasses, to help them live more productive lives. Our renewed goal is to serve 1,000,000 disenfranchised people across Canada.

Name: Indo-Africa Charitable Society
Description: The Indo-Africa Charitable Trust (UK) has been established to provide free medical services in under service areas in India and Africa to villagers, suffering from poor vision and eye infections and dental problems.

Name: 3QualiTy
Description:
3QualiTy is a fintech company that offers an easy solution to charitable
giving. Through our digital token, 3QT, and our philosophy ‘Every Human is
Equal,’ charity and cryptocurrency go hand in hand.

Name: Samuel Morris Foundation
Description:
The Samuel Morris Foundation, established in March 2007, is Australia’s only charity supporting children disabled by near drowning (or other hypoxic brain injuries) and preventing future drowning deaths and disabilities through education and awareness.

In Australia and other developed nations drowning is one of the three leading causes of accidental death in 0-4 year olds. In some jurisdictions drowning is in fact the leading cause of injury related death in this age group. Sadly the death toll from drowning is only part of the picture……….

Every year hundreds of Australian children aged 0-4 are admitted to hospital following a near drowning. Tragically around 35 children in this age group will lose their lives and almost one quarter (22.5%) of those admitted to hospital following a near drowning will suffer a brain injury due to lack of oxygen that will result in disabilities for life.  Many of the children who survive with brain injuries will need a wide range of equipment, support and services and the Samuel Morris Foundation is here to help provide it.

Name: Schmidt Legacy Foundation
Description: The Schmidt Legacy Foundation seeks to make a difference in the lives of those who are challenged by diseases, illnesses or other hardships. We aspire to make a difference in our community by tackling challenging medical and social issues of today and tomorrow. Our current focus is to fund research that improves the quality and length of life for lung cancer patients; to raise hope and find a cure.

Name: Flintridge Bryant
Description: Flintridge Bryant supports ideas that build new financing instruments for global development and environmental challenges, put purpose back into business, tackle global development with the private sector and use philanthropic funds to mobilise new flows of capital to development.

Name: Lyndhurst Foundation Inc
Description: The Lyndhurst Foundation identifies and invests in initiatives, institutions, people and programs that contribute to the longterm livability and resilience of the greater Chattanooga region.

Name: Harvard College European Business Group
Description: The Harvard College European Business Group (EBG) is dedicated to providing the Harvard Student body with knowledge about the European business environment, economic climate, investment opportunities and politics affecting the business sector of Europe. Furthermore, the EBG seeks to give students the opportunity to explore different career paths in the region and to connect with representatives of leading companies from the continent in sectors such as finance, consulting, manufacturing and more. The EBG will also create a space where students with the same interests can meet and learn from each other. We will achieve our goals through organizing guest speaker events, discussions, seminars, conferences, social activities and through other channels.

Name: Sigma Nu Fraternity - Delta Kappa Chapter
Description: Page for UD Sigma Nu

Name: The Outdoor Collaborative
Description:
The Outdoor Collaborative (TOC), is a registered Charitable Incorporated Organisation (CIO) in England and Wales that delivers free, guided walks around the British countryside for disadvantaged young adults. The charity also provides free training to improve digital media skills whilst on the guided walks. A location is then provided, to give further training to edit the captured digital media.
By providing these services, it gives the users an opportunity to improve digital media skills and develop life experiences. The lives of the disadvantaged young adults will be improved by providing an inspiring opportunity to travel out of the inner city and see the British countryside, in turn improving and promoting mental and physical health. 

Aims
To provide free training to help develop digital media and editing skills.
To inspire and improve the lives of young adults by providing an opportunity to visit the British countryside, whilst providing a structured learning environment. 
To develop and ameliorate the mental health of young adults.

Objectives
•	Guided walks operating in the British countryside by a qualified competent guide.
•	Walking equipment made available for the walks.
•	Training provided to capture photography and video of the guided walks. 
•	Media equipment made available for use.
•	A location to edit the media made available.
•	Computing equipment, software and training provided to edit the captured media. 
•	The edited media made available for the users to keep.

Name: Section Juan
Description: Section Juan is the pioneering Filipino youth association in Hong Kong.

Name: Women Leadership Club Costa Rica
Description:
Women Leadership Club Costa Rica was founded on the Lean In principles - to gather inspirational female professionals with a power to lead and inspire. We want to nurture a community of aspiring female leaders with versatile backgrounds and interests, empower them and help them succeed. 

Our focus is specifically on women leadership and empowerment - via regular meet-ups, panel discussions, movie screenings, workshops, partnering with corporations and like-minded groups in Costa Rica.

Name: M3akoum.ma
Description:
M3akoum.ma est une plateforme mise en place par l'association SANABEL active depuis 2013 dans le domaine des projets solidaires.
Cette plateforme a pour objectif de permettre à des contacts référents (Association, Employeur, Personnes) d’inscrire des familles qui ont perdu leurs revenus à cause de l'Etat d'Urgence Sanitaire et que ne peuvent pas bénéficier des mécanismes de subsistance mis en place par l’Etat.

Les dons seront versés au chef de famille qui a perdu sa source de revenu, en fonction du nombre de personnes à charges, par l’intermédiaire d’une mise à disposition auprès d’une agence Cash Plus partenaire de l’initiative.

La plateforme permet à des donateurs de choisir la ou les familles (anonymes) qui pourront bénéficier de leurs dons. Ils pourront choisir les familles en fonction de leur priorité (territoriale, orphelins, handicap) et pourvoir s'assurer que le don est bien arrivé à destination.
Les familles qui ont bénéficié d'un don correspondant à un budget d'une quinzaine de jours n’apparaîtront pas sur la plateforme pendant 2 semaines pour donner la chance à d'autres familles de bénéficier des dons des donateurs.
Ainsi, avec m3akoum.ma chacun de nous peut aider ceux dont le revenu exige de sortir à rester chez eux.
#m3akoum

Name: The Purnima Foundation
Description:
The Purnima Foundation is a Registered Non-Profit Charitable Trust that serves as a resource for people, organizations, and institutions in India. Our goals and objectives are to

* Reduce poverty and injustice
* Promote equality in educational opportunities across different economic strata
* Provide access to clean drinking water in rural communities
* Reduce human suffering through access to quality medical care
* Provide opportunities for empowering women in both rural and urban centres
* Enhance opportunities for social contribution by elderly people
* Advance human achievement

The Purnima Foundation supports attempts at meeting these challenges by working closely with people and institutions where such problems are located.

The Foundation actively promotes collaboration and participation by women and men from all levels of society.

Name: Team Ox Cycling
Description: Team Ox Cycling is a recreational cycling club committed to raising money for various charities, most notably The National Multiple Sclerosis Society via The Bike MS City to Shore Bike Tour. We also lead group training rides most weekends throughout the year.

Name: Foothills Gateway Rehabilitation Center Charitable Foundation Trust
Description:
The Foothills Gateway Rehabilitation Center Charitable Foundation Trust, established in 1990, has a mission to provide and direct resources in support of enhancing the quality of life of individuals with intellectual and developmental disabilities through the following actions:
- Promoting community involvement and awareness
- Providing community education
- Raising funds and resources to support these efforts

Name: The Give Black Foundation
Description:
The mission of The Give Black Foundation is to leverage technology and new media toward facilitating, invigorating, and telling the story of black philanthropy.  We are one of few organizations focused on promoting, researching and collaborating around black philanthropy at the national level. 

We engage individual and institutional donors, non-profits, researchers and activists to advance the African-American philanthropic tradition.  We want to make it easier and more compelling for people to "give black”.

Name: Social Supper Club London
Description: Find out more about our supper clubs and how you can support the work that we do.

Name: Love Builds Hope Foundation
Description: The Love Builds Hope Foundation is home to gifts contributed to campaigns and development initiatives of the Catholic Diocese of Brownsville. Name: Insaniyat
Description:
Insaniyat is a non-profit organization aimed towards social development. We believe it is possible to live as a community based on mutual respect, to work together and deal with existing problems of inequality and illiteracy with radical steps.

Look out of your window! Turn on your TV! See which values are being promoted, which aspects of humanity are being celebrated.

India is a country where wealth and poverty share a disturbing proximity. We seem to be stuck on the idea that an individual is separate from society. You can hear that it’s crazy; you can see that it’s wrong; you feel it is beyond disturbing. Too many times are we told, “there’s nothing we can do about it, this is just the way things are.” Naturally, you begin to believe it.

Equality and respect are essential to our humanity. Educating the young, empowering women, providing for the needy is our top priority. In an attempt to stand for what is right and a will to achieve these goals we set for ourselves, a group of like-minded friends established ‘Insaniyat.’

Don't forget to follow us!

Instagram: insaniyatofficial
YouTube: Insaniyat Co

Name: MATI USA
Description:
MATI USA is a U.S.-based not-for-profit corporation founded to support the work of MATI Jerusalem in stimulating the economy of Jerusalem by boosting job and new business creation, eradicating poverty and joblessness through programs that encourage and facilitate entrepreneurship.

MATI-USA is lead by a dedicated Board of Directors both Americans and Israelis and guided in the US by a committed Advisory Committee.

MATI recognizes the vital impact that building new businesses has on the local economy and its residents. Each new business MATI helps, in turn creates an average of three new jobs.

Name: Armenian Culture, Science & Education Development Foundation
Description:
Aims and Purpose of the Armenian Culture, Science and Education Development Foundation:

1. Fostering dialogue, strategic partnerships and collaboration within the Armenian communities worldwide. Aiding in the realization of projects associated to Armenian Culture, Science and Education.

2. Participating in the strengthening and preservation of Armenian diaspora and homeland relations involving both individuals and organisations. Linking Armenian cultural change-makers and communities across the world.

3. Encouraging and galvanising active youth involvement and engagement in Armenian Culture, Arts, Science, History and Heritage. Culturally connecting one generation to the next through special programs and projects.

4. Providing support and fostering creativity amongst Armenians through grant making, network building, and programming; convening regularly to set a course forward in driving positive social-cultural change in communities across the nation and globally.

5. Providing a platform for exclusive analysis, research and policy comment on Armenian affairs covering Armenian Culture, Science and Education preservation and development issues worldwide.

6. Publishing analytical briefs and commentaries with the unique perspective on matters related to Armenian Culture, Science and Education.

7. Diversifying expert discussion by focusing on aspects of Armenian international (or Pan-Armenian) cultural policy and its future strategy. Benefiting from the expanding power of the Internet, will work to spread reliable information, critical thought and progressive ideas.

8. Coordinating youth talent promotion program

Name: IBLC - Instituto Brasileiro de Letramento Científico
Description:
O Instituto Brasileiro de Letramento Científico é uma instituição de caráter filantrópico, cultural e científico, constituída em 2017 com a missão de disseminar a cultura científica no Brasil. 

O IBLC atua em 3 eixos: Exposições, Publicações e Projetos Socioeducacionais, sempre com o conhecimento científico como tema central.

O IBLC abrange as esferas pública e privada, estimulando a discussão sobre políticas públicas no ensino de ciências e viabilizando o engajamento da iniciativa privada.

Em 2017, assumiu parte dos ativos do antigo Instituto Abramundo (OSCIP com mais de 13 anos de experiência, muito bem conceituada em Projetos Socioeducativos e Exposições realizadas em diversas cidades do território nacional). O IBLC conta hoje com obras, publicações, acervos, direitos autorais, material e know-how de 9 exposições culturais de temática científica, sendo 5 inéditas e 4 mostras internacionais adaptadas para o Brasil, em parceria com o Museu de História Natural de Nova York. Além de uma linha editorial que já publicou 16 títulos: 4 sobre divulgação científica, 8 sobre educação, cultura e sociedade e 4 catálogos das grandes mostras internacionais. Pioneirismo na realização de uma pesquisa inédita sobre o Índice de Letramento Científico no Brasil (ILC). 10 projetos socioeducacionais (oficinas, programas de ensino em ciências e intercâmbio entre educadores e cientistas).

Contato

E-mail: iblc@iblc.org.br

Name: Building Hope Foundation, Inc.
Description:
The Building Hope Foundation, Inc. was originally founded as the Vision Fund, a non-profit charitable organization.  In 2011, the name of the 501(c)3 organization was changed to reflect the ongoing efforts to assist the families, veterans, emergency responders and other charitable organizations of Fairfield County, CT with structural improvements - access ramps, elevators, safety doors etc.

Our relationship with the Real Estate Development and the Building community throughout the tri-state area  uniquely positions our organization with the proper industry knowledge, resources and skills to execute code compliant handicap assistance projects and to provide the end user with a more accessible and comfortable living environment specific to that individuals needs. 

The Building Hope Foundation is active in supporting charitable activities in Fairfield county communities. Past involvement has included participation in building handicap accessible ramps for clients of St. Vincent’s Special Needs Center and The Needs Clearing House, donations to the EJ’s House construction project in Ridgefield, the Extreme Makeover: Home Edition project in Bridgeport, Habitat for Humanity, the Marine Corps League and the Girls Scouts Cookies for the Troops program. The Building Hope Foundation has also improved the accessibility to the food storage area for the food pantry at Blessed Sacrament and recently provided oversight for the new industrial kitchen at Blessed Sacrament Church. 

For more information about Building Hope or donating, go to www.buildinghopefoundation.org

Name: Chernobyl Children's Lifeline (Bristol Link)
Description:
The Bristol Link of CCLL is part of a national charity founded in 1992. There are currently over 60 Links established throughout the United Kingdom, raising money to support the children of Belarus and Ukraine, whose lives will be forever affected by the 1986 Chernobyl nuclear disaster.

We are a team of enthusiastic volunteers who continually raise funds to bring 16 Belarussian children to Bristol each summer- for 4 weeks of fresh air, uncontaminated food and fun, as well as dental and eye check ups. They are from the town of Osipovichi its nearby village of Protasevichi, where deprivation levels are high. 

Imagine: Living the rest of your life in a radioactive polluted environment. There is no escape. You are eating contaminated food, but what else can you do?

Imagine: Coming to the UK and eating fresh food and breathing fresh air and going home with your immune system recharged.

Imagine: Knowing that people DO care.

But our efforts don't stop there! We also support the village school in Protasevichi, including modernising the heating system; equipping the school with it's first ever indoor toilets; providing audio-visual equipment to establish an Assembly Room; and supplying sports equipment. In Summer 2017 we also replaced the rotting and broken windows in the village Kindergarten. We even fund respite camps, with specialist medical resources and nursing, for children (and their families) who are too ill to travel. 

So, what do we need? PEOPLE, MONEY and IDEAS!

The World Health Organisation states that 500,000 children in Belarus are at risk- only 6% are considered to be healthy. Nationally almost 50,000 children have stayed with host families since 1992. We can only continue to do this work with the assistance of local people, charities and businesses. The search for individuals and businesses to support our work is a constant one.

For more information, please see:
https://www.facebook.com/ccllbristol or http://www.ccll.org.uk/bristol

Name: Saratoga Education Foundation

SEF believes that the whole child is nurtured and developed with programs such as music, art, computer instruction, library, PE and science programs. Our annual goal is to raise funds from our parents and community to enable the Saratoga Union School District to provide these types of programs to Saratoga children.

Saratoga Education Foundation is a non-profit registered charity (EIN No. 94-2853969). SEF is 100% volunteer run – there are no paid employees.  More information on what programs are funded by the SEF grant can be found on our website, www.saratoga-sef.org.

Name: Asia center foundation
Description:
Asia Center Foundation (ACF) is a Christian charity registered as a Non-Government Organization (NGO) on Phuket, Thailand in operation since 2002. Our mission is to help disadvantaged children and children-at-risk by providing care, training, and education so that they will fulfill their God-given potential.

The ACF’s vision is to restore children to God, transform and develop them into leaders influencing their world.

ACF employs local Thai staff and also makes use of the help of local and international volunteers to engage and work with underprivileged, impoverished children and children-at-risk on Phuket.

Out of the 57 million primary-school-age children who are out of school around the world, 611,000 live in Thailand. The Asia Center Foundation started as a Christian charity, by responding to a need in the community for basic English teaching in Patong, one of the main tourist areas. Since then, ACF rented facilities to host its childcare centers in different parts of the island. By creating both educational and sports programs, ACF helps children to focus on their education and overall development. Despite the difficult and impoverished situations of the students, ACF aims to give them hope to believe in themselves for a brighter future. Read more about our history.

With the help of a dedicated team of staff, board members, volunteers, and donors, ACF has managed to make an impact on many children's’ lives since 2002. Read stories from children to learn how ACF has helped them.

Name: Pregnancy Resource Center of the Poconos
Description: The Pregnancy Resource Center of the Poconos has stood as a beacon of hope in our Community for 34 years. In those 34 years, we’ve helped more than 4,500 women dealing with unplanned pregnancies sort through their feelings, and find answers. In 2013 the Center became a medical facility under the leadership of our Medical Director. We have a vibrant part-time staff, including 3 RN/trained sonographers to ensure we provide excellent medical services to our clients. Our licensed Medical Director oversees our medical clinic operations. PRC is open to walk-in clients 3 days a week for a total of 24 hours, plus additional hours for evening programming and appointments. More than 45 dedicated volunteers donate more than 2,500 hours of their time a year to help us provide compassion, care and service to each woman that walks through our doors.

Name: The Barnett-Searing National Cancer Foundation
Description:
The Barnett-Searing National Cancer Foundation was created in 2001 to honor two courageous women and to continue their legacy through philanthropy.  Jenny Searing lost her battle to ovarian sarcoma, and Mary Barnett lost her fight to uterine sarcoma.  Their families and friends accepted the challenge to make a difference in the lives of those currently battling cancer and BSNCF was founded.  The goal was to create a charity where the founders, friends, and family could give back and and improve the quality of life for cancer patients and their loved ones while keeping the light of these two women shining brightly.

Through the years, the Foundation has made a difference in countless lives through a variety of projects which offer comfort to cancer patients and their families.  The Foundation's mission is to support women of all ages affected by cancer and the loved ones who care for them by providing unexpected acts of kindness.  This was inspired by a poem Mary Barnett left for her family where she asked that her spirit live on through acts of kindness.  We are committed to making a positive impact on their emotional and physical health, lifting them up and offering hope.

Our key programs include “Smile Kits” for cancer patients.   "Smile Kits" include comforting and rehabilitative items such as:  a soothing blanket, fuzzy socks, soothing lip balm, adult coloring books and colored pencils for therapy.   Each kit costs approximately $20 and a $5,500 donation would mean that 250 patients could receive a kit.  The “Smile Kits” are branded with sayings about smiles to uplift the patient such as “When life gives you SMILES, rearrange the letters to say SMILE.”   

Corporations can partner with us by holding a recycled shoe drive, a "Smile Kit" making teambuiding event or making a donation.

Name: Cantata Best Life Foundation
Description: Gaze 20 years into a future in which you are living your best possible life. What does it look like? While each person’s image of that life will be unique, it’s likely you picture a life filled with purpose, vitality, outlook and balance. We’re convinced the Best Life you envision is possible and accessible regardless of age. In that spirit, the Cantata Best Life Foundation was formed to investigate, redefine and accelerate Best Life in later life. We invite you to become part of this aspirational, inspirational, transformational, and, yes, profoundly radical, movement.

Name: Tet Festival San Jose
Description:
Tet Festival is the largest and most important celebration in Vietnamese culture. Tet is an all-in-one festival that celebrates the union of nature, human, culture, the living and the dead. There are many rituals and customs being carried out during this special occasion which include family reunions, giving lucky money in red envelopes to children and seniors, ancestor worship, and clearing out debts and misdeeds. The Vietnamese people celebrate this holiday with up to 30 days of festivities.

We encourage everyone to come out to support and learn about the Vietnamese community, culture, neighbors, and local businesses. Let’s ring in the new year of prosperity, good health, and luck with family, new friends, and neighbors.

ACTIVITIES: Firecracker, Lion Dance, Ao Dai Contest, Traditional Dances, Martial Arts, Cultural Ceremony, Cultural Village, Banh Mi eating contest, Pho Challenge, Watermelon Eating contest, Talent Show, Chess Competition, Commercial Vendors, Community Booths, Vietnamese Food, Cooking Contest, Smelling Contest, Carnival Games, VIP Section and many more..

Tet Festival 2016 is a team project of Vietnamese Voluntary Foundation (VIVO). VIVO is a registered 501c3 non-profit organization serving the Vietnamese community since 1979. VIVO EIN is 94-2764504. All donations are 100% tax-deductible to the extent allowed by law.

Please visit http://vivousa.org/causes/tetfestival/ for more information.

Proceeds of the ticket sales will goes to VIVO projects and programs that directly impact the local communities and part of it will be use to reserved to fund next year Tet Festival.

Name: citypakproject
Description:
The CITYPAK Project is a nonprofit organization dedicated to providing custom-designed, high-quality backpacks to individuals experiencing homelessness or housing transition due to natural disasters. Founded by music industry executive Ron Kaplan and supported by exclusive manufacturing partners Samsonite and High Sierra, CITYPAK collaborates with nonprofit and community organizations around the world to distribute durable, thoughtfully crafted packs that offer not only practical support but also dignity, safety, and connection. Now in its 12th year, CITYPAK has distributed more than 120,000 PAKs across the U.S. and globally—each one designed to meet the unique needs of life on the move. Through meaningful partnerships and a commitment to compassion, CITYPAK continues to create impact—one backpack, and one human connection, at a time.

Learn more at www.citypak.org or follow us on social media @TheCITYPAKProject.

Together with international adventure travel gear manufacturer High Sierra, and in partnership with organizations serving un-housed neighbors, organizations working with refugees, and institutions providing disaster relief solutions, CITYPAK strives for dignity in homelessness.

Name: Athens Jaycees
M100 Foundation
Description:
The M100 Foundation is all about secure funding & maximizing sadaqah, or charity.

M = Micro-philanthropy is a small acts of charity over a wide base that add up to immense change.

100 represents 100% of a donors contribution goes directly to the grant. M100 covers all admin costs.

M100 equals consistency – maximized and spread out to a diverse audience. M100 serves donors, who in turn, contribute to the bigger picture over a longer period of time.

The M100 Foundation gives grants to DC Metro Area organizations. The funding is collected only from US citizens or US legal residents who commit at least $100 a month for 6 months.

Each month, m100 grants that money to a specific project or 501c3 organization that has undergone background checks and approvals. Grant money is monitored and donors receive progress reports.

Description:

Instead of complaining about these issues, a group of young men from all walks of life came together to be the individuals who rectify them.
