Name: A Voice Discovered
Description:
A Voice Discovered is a non-profit organization with 501(c)3 status.   We believe that the ability to communicate is a fundamental human right.  Therefore, it is the mission of this foundation to provide  FUNDING ASSISTANCE, FAMILY AND COMMUNITY EDUCATION, SUPPORT AND ADVOCACY to enhance the quality of life for individuals with communication impairments and thus benefit families and their extended community through access to appropriate technology resulting in improved communication means.

A Voice Discovered provides funding through our grant program to assist people in our community with complex communication needs in gaining access to services and equipment.  The services can include augmentative and alternative communication (AAC) assessments, therapy and/or training that is provided for their families as well as other community members who play important roles in their lives.  The equipment can include communication devices (e.g., iPad, communication apps) and/or the necessary accessories to access those devices (e.g., wheelchair mounts, accessibility switches, etc.).  

A Voice Discovered also provides quarterly workshops in the Ventura County area to families, caregivers and professionals of people with complex communication needs who currently use or would benefit from AAC.  These workshops include information about AAC, AAC assessments, AAC therapy, AAC implementation, funding and the funding process, as well as client rights information.

A Voice Discovered has a small but growing lending library that includes iPads with communication apps, switches, switch toys and iPad/switch mounts for families and caregivers to trial before purchasing or requesting a grant.  

A Voice Discovered also has a growing parent/caregiver group that meets monthly to bimonthly providing coffee meet-ups for parents and caregivers and social meet-ups for the entire family.

Name: Skagit Animals In Need (S.A.I.N.)
Description: To prevent and respond to animal cruelty and neglect primarily in support of law enforcement.

Name: I AM 4 Kids
Description:
The I AM 4 Kids Foundation is committed to helping every elementary school aged child develop an empowering identity. I AM 4 Kids program shows children how to decide for themselves who are… starting right now! Our mission is to do for empowerment and development what Sesame Street did for Math and Reading. 

We create interactive learning programs taught in schools that teach kids how to create an empowering identity for themselves.  In short, we show them how to be their own role model.

OUR MISSION: To have the I AM 4 Kids program implemented in every public elementary and charter school within the next 10 years at NO COST to the schools.

Name: Ireland's Hope Foundation
Description:
The mission of Ireland’s Hope is to increase pediatric organ donation while providing financial assistance to families of children who are undergoing a heart transplant. We are dedicated to sending the message of HOPE.

Providing Financial Support
Families who have a child who is undergoing a pediatric heart transplant are frequently left both emotionally and financially devastated. Many parents are needing to stop working outside the home in order to care for their ill child. Ireland’s Hope helps families with their non-medical day-to-day living expenses. It is our desire to help restore hope during this difficult time.

Ireland’s Hope provides financial support to families who have a child currently awaiting a heart transplant or recovering from a heart transplant in Region 7 of the United States (Minnesota, Wisconsin, Illinois, North Dakota and South Dakota).

Organ Donor Registration
We are committed to increasing organ donor awareness in the United States. There is currently more than 122,000 people on the National Transplant List waiting to receive an organ. Over 2,000 of the individuals waiting are children. It is paramount that this issue exists in the mainstream. We are committed to bringing national attention to this cause through education, public forums, and social media.

Equally importantly, we are dedicated to increasing organ donor registration within the United States. With a new name being added to the National Transplant List every 10 minutes, it is of utmost importance that those who are able to register for organ donation actually do register. We want to ensure that as many people in need of transplants can find the care and help they deserve.

Name: The Paid Family and Medical Leave Fund
Şəhid və Qazi Ailələrinə Könüllü Yardım Fondu
Description: Şəhid və qazi ailələrinə könüllü yardım” Fondu maddi vəziyyətinin aşağı olması ilə əlaqədar ehtiyacı olan şəxslərin, xüsusilə də şəhid ailələrinin üzvlərinin, vətənin müstəqilliyi və ərazi bütövlüyü uğrunda döyüşlərdə iştirak etmiş, yaralanmış və ya əlil olmuş şəxslərin və onların ailə üzvlərinin  maddi və sosial təminatlarının yaxşılaşdırılmasına, sağlamlıqlarının bərpa edilməsinə və zəruri təlabatlarının ödənilməsinə yardım edilməsi və maarifləndirilməsi, eləcə də onların ehtiyaclarına uyğun olaraq zəruri dövlət orqanları ilə əlaqələndirilməsi məqsədilə daimi fəaliyyət üçün təsis edilən qeyri-hökumət təşkilatdır.

Name: Echelon Chicago
Description:
Echelon is a community of young professionals engaged in networking, events, fundraising and service projects to benefit The Salvation Army. Whether it’s serving a meal, ringing bells, or hosting a fundraiser to support a life-changing program, Echelon members are serving together and making a real difference.

At The Salvation Army, we #FightforGood. 
We fight this difficult fight because the needs in Chicago are staggering. Twenty-seven percent of our population lives in poverty, with another 20 percent considered low-income, and rates in Chicago’s closest neighborhoods growing at an alarming rate. One in seven Chicago residents faces food insecurity. The city’s homicide total in 2018 topped L.A. and New York City combined.  
Because of these rising needs, we have sounded the alarm in Chicago. We have told our friends and supporters that not only do we Fight for Good. With these realities, #TheFightIsNow.
We’ve launched a $7 million campaign to raise the resources and manpower to continue the battle against #poverty, #substanceuse, #hunger, #homelessness, and #humantrafficking in our city. Our weapons in this fight are Love, Hope and Compassion. 
Our staff and volunteers are meeting the needs of the most vulnerable. That includes hard working but struggling families who are fighting to pay rent, put food on the table, keep the lights on, and give their children a safe place to spend time after school. It also includes survivors of trafficking, victims of natural disasters, people battling substance use disorders, and incarcerated individuals who are re-entering society. 
We need your help every day in this battle. Our neighbors in need depend on us.
 
Join our #FightforGood at TheFightisNow.org.

Name: Misión Cárcel
Description:
Misión Cárcel es un proyecto de voluntariado social católico que tiene como objetivo motivar el desarrollo personal de los jóvenes infractores de ley privados de libertad. Esto, generando relaciones de amistad reales con los jóvenes con los que trabajamos a través del trato horizontal simétrico basado en la confianza, el respeto, la honestidad y la continuidad de las relaciones “uno a uno”.

Realizamos visitas  los sábados en la mañana, durante todo el año y misiones durante 10 días (aprox.) en los períodos de vacaciones de invierno y de verano, en los que se visitan las dependencias todos los días.

Lo que se realice durante nuestros ingresos va a depender del enfoque que se le esté dando al trabajo y  metas del semestre, siempre apuntando a nuestro objetivo principal. Esto va desde actividades más lúdicas, como concursos, dinámicas de juego y de equipo, pasando por talleres -que pueden ser teóricos, como Photoshop y comunicación escrita, y más prácticos, como música, pintura de yesos y murales, baile, etc.- hasta llegar a instancias más reflexivas.

Todo esto, en el marco del acompañamiento que buscamos proveer para ellos durante esta instancia semanal, siempre complementando el trabajo dentro de la cárcel con la misión de difusión fuera de ella, esto es, generando cultura y conciencia social en el país respecto de lo que el tema de la desvaloración de la persona en situación carcelaria significa, y de las implicancias que esto tiene, tanto a nivel personal como colectivo.

Name: Heisenberg Institute
Description:
Founded by a grant thanks to an anonymous donor in the name of Werner Heisenberg, a noted theoretical physicist, commonly referred to as the father of quantum mechanics.

The vision of  Heisenberg Institute is to promote the global good and to create the next generation of transformational change agents to achieve unprecedented levels of societal progress. The Heisenberg Institute leverages this vision by accelerating the development of high potential young leaders through financial investment and by harnessing executive insights via a customized leadership development program known as the Heisenberg Fellowship.

The prestigious Heisenberg Fellowship anoints our scholars or fellows embuing each fellow with the resources required to empower them to meet their full potential.

Name: Citizen Philanthropy
Description: Journey toward purpose.

Name: Post 21 Club, A NJ Nonprofit Corporation
Description:
The Post 21 Club is a group of parents and other concerned community members who provide support to adults with autism who have aged out of New Jersey’s social service system for children.

Post 21 Club sees its game-changing, highly positive philosophies, as well as its proven collaborative model for public/private partnerships, as helping to enable New Jersey to soon lead all other states in the areas of adult autism supports, services, and community interaction.

Name: Prestige Invitational
Description: The Annual Prestige Invitational works in partnership with charitable organizations, bringing together philanthropists from all walks of life to bond over a game of golf while helping raise funding for partner organizations' initiatives.

Name: Jaca contra o Corona
Description: O projeto visa incidir em segurança alimentar e comunicação quanto aos riscos de contágio do Coronavírus na favela do Jacarezinho.

Name: Book Bank
Description: Book Bank is calling on students who purchase their textbooks for their classes to give back. It's hard to sell back textbooks, especially to bookstores or on Amazon, and if a place will buy them, it is usually for very little. Students who do not have adequate funds to purchase textbooks can use the donations for one semester, then they will be returned to Book Bank for future use. This will benefit all students, especially those who need help affording expensive textbooks. The entire program is free; donations will not be compensated and textbook loans will not be taxed. This is an organization for students.

Name: Impact Giving Advisors
Description:
Impact Giving Advisors is a philanthropic advisory firm that believes in a new wave of philanthropy: building co-creative solutions for long-lasting societal and systems change. 

We have a proven Impact Giving process that takes you from ideation to implementation — to optimize and leverage your philanthropic impact.

Name: Our Common Thread
Description: Our Common Thread is an organization dedicated to celebrating and empowering young minority men by providing executive leadership training and tools for career readiness. Each year we work with Charlotte business executives, entrepreneurs, and innovators to host a multi-day experience of mentorship, workshops, and networking. Our annual cohort of Associates are fitted with their very own personalized tailored suits, from Abbeydale, along with receiving career coaching, etiquette training, and professional development sessions with our corporate sponsors.

Name: National Bell Festival
Description: The National Bell Festival is the annual New Year’s Day celebration in Washington, D.C. On the first day of the year, bells in towers across the nation’s capital ring out together and unite the community around listening parties, eclectic events, and special programming.

Name: ASA Social Fund for Hidden Peoples
Description:
The mission of the ASA Social Fund for Hidden Peoples is to provide financial support for vulnerable and fragile children and adults who are impacted by extreme poverty, civil strife, abandonment, gender violence, disfigurement and physical disabilities.  These peoples are not served by the Uganda social support system. The ASA focus is on the basic human, health and educational needs of these hidden peoples  including, but not limited to: 

Parents of children who are disabled and have special needs
Women and children who are victims of gender violence
Children who are homeless, victims of conflict, HIV/AIDS and/or impacted by extreme poverty

Name: Mankind Is My Business
Description: Mankind is My Business was founded in 2015 in order to do good worldwide. We are an all-female board, who have lived in North America, South America, Europe, and Asia. We have education and professional training in nursing, health policy, history, philosophy, statistics, film, business management, and suicide prevention. In the past year alone, we partnered with 15 separate organizations in 11 different countries. We are particularly interested in gathering data to analyze the impact of our efforts, in order to do good better.

Name: Ajvir Singh Sandhu Leadership Foundation
Description:
The Ajvir Singh Sandhu Leadership Foundation works to recognise passion, commitment and an outstanding dedication to various fields amongst young people aged 11-21. 

Resolving to help individuals to progress in their respective disciplines, the foundation will reward, assist and encourage young people who display skills which demonstrate the seeds for growth of successful future leaders. The foundation is dedicated to supporting young people achieve their dreams through a combination of financial aids, accolades and access to a support network . 

The organisation welcomes individuals who excel in sport, music , academics and military disciplines with the primary aim to develop and create more opportunities for promising individuals.

Name: Rollvo AHBF (Burkina Faso).
Description: Éducation et formation.

Name: Education et Santé sans Frontière
Description:
Créée en 2006 à Paris, ESAFRO est une association loi 1901 sans but lucratif qui vise à favoriser l’accès à l’éducation et améliorer les conditions de vie des populations défavorisées dans les pays en développement.
Le programme d’actions d’ESAFRO s’articule autour de 3 principaux volets
- L’éducation
- La santé
- Le développement économique

Name: Innovative Finance Foundation
Description: Our mission is to raise innovative funding for social and environmental goals.

Name: Bronx Letters Foundation
Description: Our foundation supports the UA Bronx Academy of Letters, which is located in the Mott Haven neighborhood of the South Bronx, our country's poorest congressional district — three miles from Manhattan's Upper East Side. When the school opened its doors as a high school in September 2003, our founders – a dedicated team of students, parents, teachers, and community leaders – were united by a single idea: Students who can express themselves clearly in writing can do better in any path they choose in life. Today, our rigorous, college-preparatory academic program spans grades six through twelve. Our core curriculum is standards-based, with an intense focus on developing literacy and driving social change across the disciplines. It includes honors and AP courses, master-level arts electives, externships, and independent study courses.

Name: Casa do Passarinho
Description:
A Casa do Passarinho , desde sua fundação, tem sido um excelente espaço de oportunidades para o desenvolvimento intelectual de crianças, jovens e idosos.

Fundada em 24 de Maio de 2013 para ser um espaço de recreação para crianças, jovens e idosos pertencentes a famílias de baixa, os moradores são envolvidos em diversas atividades educacionais, tais como: Educação de Jovens e Adultos (EJA) - Apoio Pedagógico a Crianças, Jovens e Adultos (Reforço) - Fisioterapia - Informática - Musicoterapia - Odontologia - Serviços Sociais - Leve Leite - Aulas de Teatro - Danças - Música - Percussão - Ballet - Inglês - Francês - Espanhol - Gastronomia - Filosofia - Consultas Médicas - Time de Futebol - Time de Rugby dentre outras.

Name: Utah Diaper Bank
Description:
Did you know our safety-net programs do not cover the cost of diapers? That's despite a monthly cost of over $100 per month per family per child. The Utah Diaper Bank distributes over 70,000 diapers every month to community partners for families in need. 

The Utah Diaper Bank was founded in 2012 by a few concerned individuals who saw a community need for an organized approach to collecting and distributing Diapers.  The first awareness of this need surfaced in 2010 when a call went out from local television and radio stations to fill an urgent need of the local shelters for diapers. 


Over the next two years donations to the shelters continued on a personal basis, but in May of 2012 the local television stations once again broadcast an urgent need for diapers.  It was from this second call for aid that the resolve was formed to “Do something about this ongoing problem”  Initial research found that there was already a loosely organized group of diaper banks in thirty- two states but Utah was not one of those states.  It was with this resolve and information that the decision was reached that Utah should join the other thirty-two states and have its own organized diaper bank

Name: Junior League of Akron
Description:
CATALYSTS FOR CHANGE
The Junior League of Akron (JLA) is the local chapter of the Association of Junior Leagues International, Inc.  JLA is an organization of women committed to promoting voluntarism, developing the potential of women, and improving communities through the effective action and leadership of trained volunteers. Its purpose is exclusively educational and charitable.

Since our founding in 1926, the Junior League of Akron has grown to represent nearly 30 communities throughout the Greater Akron area. Our members include business leaders, entrepreneurs, professional women and stay-at-home moms who are committed to serving our community. In fact, Junior League of Akron members have become catalysts for change— raising funds, chairing events, founding organizations and delivering critical assistance to local people in need.

Name: Kids Boost
Description:
Kids Boost is a non-profit organization designed to empower kids to give back to the world.  

Kids Boost helps children use their gifts, talents and passions to raise money for a charity of their choice. We provide kids with $100 in start up money, a one-on-one coach and resources to turn their money into more for a cause that is important to the child.

Name: un partage, un sourire, un bonheur
Description:
L'association « Un Partage, Un sourire, un Bonheur » est née en Australie, un soir d'été à Port Hedland dans la région du Pilbara, où règne fer, zinc, or, gaz… toutes les plus grandes sources minières au monde qui « alimentent » le concept fondamental de nos sociétés de production; où commence notre esprit de profit et de consommation de masse.

Le but de l’association « Un Partage, un Sourire, un Bonheur » est d’allier partage et passions, pour des sourires inscrits dans le temps, que ce bonheur remplisse le cœur des enfants.

Name: Coalition Internationale des Entités  pour le Développement de l'Afrique et du Congo (Ciedac)
Description:
La Coalition Internationale des Entités pour le Développement de l'Afrique et du Congo (Ciedac) asbl est une association sans but lucratif qui combat pour  
 le développent socioéconomique de l'Afrique et du Congo .
la ciedac est une association qui a comme membres ; Des personnes morales adhérant dans notre Combat , des personnes physiques , membres d'honneur qui contribuent dans la vision en apportant leurs cotisations mensuelles , trimestrielles, ou annuelle pour l'avancement de la vision .

Name: 'WEEN DREAM
Description:
‘WEEN DREAM is a 501(c)(3) national nonprofit organization that provides free Halloween costumes for children in need.  When you make a donation to 'WEEN DREAM, you help us give the gift of Halloween!  

For more information, please visit our website at www.weendream.org or email info@weendream.org.

Name: BarnRaisers of Indiana
Description: We are a volunteer-based 501c3 committed to raising and distributing funds to support local and global projects that improve the health and quality of life for orphaned and/or under-privileged children and youth.  Thanks to the generosity of donors and sponsors, we have contributed nearly $400,000 to date to help kids in need.

Name: Justice International
Description:
Justice International (JI) is an independent, non-partisan, non-governmental organization (NGO), established in 2016, with an aim to support International Criminal Court (ICC) investigations and civil society engagement in them. 

Since the beginning of ICC investigation into Georgia’s situation, JI has been acting as Georgian civil society focal point at the Court. JI is a member of Georgian and international Coalitions for the ICC and is actively engaged in their activities.

JI aims to promote the administration of justice and to fight impunity of perpetrators of serious human rights violations, including crimes of international law. Our efforts mainly focus on coordination of activities of Georgian Coalition for the ICC, as well as on facilitation of communication between different sections of the Court and victims’ groups, and on leading international advocacy efforts to support victims of armed conflicts and effective implementation of the ICC’s mandate.

Main areas of work include: international armed conflicts, transitional justice, legal aid, International Criminal Court (ICC) investigations, independent investigation of grave crimes, victim representation, reparations.

Main activities: documentation of human rights conditions, international advocacy, monitoring socio-economic developments and security conditions in conflict areas, risk assessment, facilitation of communication between different sections of the ICC and local civil society, awareness raising and trainings.

Name: Association Abinanien
Description:
Abinanien est une Association humanitaire de loi 1901 reconnue d'intérêt général, qui oeuvre pour venir en aide aux nourrissons orphelins ou abandonnés de Bobo-Dioulasso.

Au Burkina Faso, les centres d'accueil pour les bébés en situation précaire sont peu nombreux et surchargés. Face à cette urgence, nous travaillons à l'ouverture d'un orphelinat pour nourrissons à Bobo-Dioulasso où nous louons une maison qui sera adaptée pour accueillir une dizaine de bébés. 

Notre projet est de faire construire une structure nous permettant d'accueillir les nourrissons ainsi que les enfants de plus de 3 ans.

Name: JVSI
Description:
JVSI est une association de droit togolais proposant des programmes de missions de volontariat international. Depuis 3 ans, nous avons, grâce à nos expériences, nous avons réalisé plus d'une centaine de chantiers solidaires/missions de volontariat et accueilli plusieurs étudiants pour des stage optionnels & conventionné dans différents domaines tout ceci en collaboration avec plusieurs partenaires Africains et occidentaux.
Nouveaux partenaires et partenaires de toujours, faites participer vos jeunes à nos Missions de volontariat, Camps chantiers solidaires spéciaux 2015...

Name: Children's Policy Council of Jefferson County
Description:
We are a nonprofit council dedicated to building partnerships among all those in Jefferson County, Alabama who work for the well-being of children.

There is no other area organization dedicated to bringing people together to learn about children's issues, to find resources, and to build projects and programs through partnerships. When people work with the Children's Policy Council of Jefferson County, they learn more about area programs and resources.


We're Building Partnerships to Benefit Jefferson County Children.

Name: FUNDERÉTICA - Fundación Europea para el Estudio y Reflexión Ética
Description:
Nuestro fin fundacional es la realización de actividades que tengan por objeto la educación, formación e investigación en la Filosofía Moral.

¿Cuáles son nuestros fines?

-La educación, formación e investigación en la Filosofía Moral.
-La defensa y promoción de la Ética.
-La difusión de valores éticos a través de la educación y de la cultura.
-La contribución a una buena formación de la ciudadanía española y europea fomentando un comportamiento ético y deontológico integral.

Nuestros Valores

-Respeto: sustentamos todas las acciones y relaciones en el respeto hacia la persona y la sociedad.
-Profesionalidad: contamos con la colaboración de voluntarios y profesionales cualificados para cumplir nuestros fines.
-Servicio: fomentamos la educación, investigación y formación en el campo de la ética y la filosofía moral.
-Eficacia y eficiencia: diseñamos una estructura de crecimiento sostenido, consolidación económica y transparencia financiera.
-Comunicación: incentivamos el trabajo en equipo, promovemos y coordinamos una comunicación abierta y positiva. Procuramos apoyarnos en los innovación tecnológica y sistema de información permanente que refuerce nuestra prestigio en la sociedad.

Name: The George E. Richmond Foundation
Description:
Established in 2006, the George E Richmond Foundation is the culmination of decades long interest and focus in the oral systemic field. The Foundation is organized for charitable purposes to expand the understanding and the importance of oral health to an individual's overall health.  

Believing that access to quality and affordable oral health care has the potential to improve the overall health of individuals and reduce healthcare costs, the Foundation uses its resources to fund projects and programs consistent with this mission. 

Activities and initiatives of the Foundation include research funding, supporting other aligned non-profit organizations and providing direct health care.

Name: Estuary Masonic Lodge #43
Description:
https://www.facebook.com/estuarylodge43
Welcome to Estuary Lodge No. 43's LinkedIn page. As Connecticut Freemasons we embrace the tenets of Friendship, Morality and Brotherly Love. We do this by using the teachings of Freemasonry to better ourselves, our community and each other.
Freemasonry is a charitable, benevolent, educational and religious society. Its principles are proclaimed as widely as men will hear. Its only secrets are in methods of recognition and of symbolic instruction.
It is charitable in that it is not organized for profit and none of its income inures to the benefit of any individual, but all is devoted to the promotion of the welfare and happiness of mankind.
It is benevolent in that it teaches and exemplifies altruism as a duty.
It is educational in that it teaches by prescribed ceremonials a system of morality and brotherhood based upon the Sacred Law.
It is religious in that it teaches monotheism, the Holy Bible is open upon its Altars whenever a Lodge is in session, reverence for God is ever present in its ceremonials, and to its Brethren are constantly addressed lessons of morality: yet it is not sectarian or theological.
It is a social organization only so far as it furnishes additional inducement that men may foregather in numbers, thereby providing more material for its primary work of education, of worship, and of charity.
Through the improvement and strengthening of the character of individual man, Freemasonry seeks to improve the community. Thus it impresses upon its members the principles of personal righteousness and personal responsibility, enlightens them as to those things which make for human welfare, and inspires them with that feeling of charity, or goodwill, toward all mankind which will move them to translate principle and conviction into action.

Name: LA TURDINE MOBILE
Description:
Nous sommes fiers de vous présenter notre association LA TURDINE MOBILE qui participera au 4L Trophy 2023 !!!

Lucas LEDUCQ et Quentin NUTI

Name: CK7 FAMILY
Description:
CK7 Family is an Organization Committed to Charitable Investments aimed at impacting the nation, one community at a time. All our programs seek to add value and improve the quality of life of the communities we serve.

 The Organization was founded by the current President, 'Clarence Kaguora (CK7)' in September 2022. We are Officially Incorporated by the Registrar of Companies in Kenya.

 For enquiries or feedback kindly email us at: ck7family.ke.global@gmail.com

Name: Pearce Family Foundation
Description: The Pearce Family Foundation is a public 501 C3 non-profit charity that exists to provide needed financial assistance, mobilize resources and engage the community to support families with a child facing chronic or life-threatening illness. All proceeds raised benefit the Pearce Family Foundation’s main initiative, Angels Around Us of AZ, to help ease the burdens of daily life so these courageous families can focus solely on their child’s journey to get well. Pearce Family Foundation is a proud Nonprofit Organization founded in Phoenix, Arizona.

Name: Minds Matter of Washington D.C.
Description:
Minds Matter DC is a strong, passionate group of individuals dedicated to providing extra support and resources needed to help highly motivated, low-income DC students reach their highest potential without additional costs to the school system or students. Our team members have a wide range of experience and skills in the non-profit and private sectors, including a number of DC residents, three members who previously volunteered as mentors at Minds Matter NYC, and one member who has two young children in DC Public Schools.

Minds Matter DC is a chapter of the national Minds Matter organization. Since its founding in New York City in 1991, Minds Matter has grown into a national organization with eleven chapters across the United States in Boston, Chicago, Cleveland, Denver, Los Angeles, New York City, Philadelphia, Portland, San Francisco, Seattle, the Twin Cities and now Washington D.C. For more than 22 years, Minds Matter has been making competitive college admission a reality for underserved students nationwide. Minds Matter transforms the lives of highly-motivated, low-income students by providing the individualized mentoring, standardized test preparation, and writing and critical thinking instruction that has been proven necessary to achieve college success.

As a result of those efforts, on a nationwide basis, 100% of our graduates have been accepted into a four-year college or university, and more than 95% of our tracked alumni have graduated from or are currently enrolled in college. 89% of Minds Matter students accepted to college are first-generation college students.

Name: Africa Outreach USA Foundation
Description:
Africa Outreach USA is a 501(c)-3 non-profit volunteer organization dedicated to bring the dream of sports opportunities closer to reality for millions of under served children in Africa. We deploy sport, basketball in particular as a tool to accelerate self-esteem development needed to drive gender balance, education advancement, healthy livelihoods, faith based lifestyles, and permanently disrupt the poverty cycle for children in resource poor constituents. 

This is achieved through the Community 80/20 Sweat Equity Model, a ground breaking burden sharing concept that empowers rural schools to take ownership of sports infrastructure development beneficial to their children. The concept mandates schools to make an 80 % non-monetary contribution towards the construction process in the form of digging trenches, molding 4000 bricks, rubble filling & pouring concrete mix under our strategic guidance. We make up for the remaining 20 % in the form of resources that schools cannot afford including cement, paint, & backboards. 

Once completed, the 4-In-1 structures also cater to volleyball, tennis, and netball. We follow up with donating gently used sporting gear collected from the USA including uniforms, balls, sneakers, mini-pumps and basketball training resources. We partner with volunteer professional coaches from the USA that travel to Africa to train teachers on sound fundamentals of the game and help set up organized youth sports programs within 
the communities.

In order to effectively deliver on our No Child Plays Without 2030 bold vision, to all children in Africa, we have unleashed a set of additional layers of support that are essential to complete the youth empowerment process and help increase program adoption & success rate. These include: the Tuition Assist Scholarship Program, (TASP), Virtual Coach Connect, (VCC), Parent Involve Club, (PIC), Sports Gear Collection Drive, (SGCD),  and Train-U-Train, (TUT). Learn more at www.africaoutreachusa.org

Name: Zharfa Student Society
Description: Zharfa is a multi-major scientific society of students of Physics, Mathematics and Philosophy of Science resided in the Physics faculty of Sharif University of Technology.

Name: Eyes Front
Description: Eyes Front is a forward-focused organisation. We believe in the power of community and humanity. Our commitment to remaining focused on the future, empowering communities through knowledge, learning, and support, in collective impact communities, will enable us to make a difference, especially for the next generation, as well as provide a better life for all now.

Name: Positivity Bomb
Description:
A Positivity Bomb is an explosion of kindness detonated by people everywhere that is felt immediately. 

Positivity Bomb is a social good brand that fosters positivity through events and collaboration. Positivity Bomb exists to increase empathy, gratitude, and kindness amongst people and to bring funding and awareness to causes creating a positive impact on society. The intent is to raise funds and awareness for a new charity at every PB event. Positivity Bomb has a pending non-profit status.

Mission #1
Indiana is the #2 most depresses state behind Rhode Island. Positivity Bomb will change that. We are focused on spreading kindness in Indianapolis by creating kind interactions and opportunities for community collaboration.

Name: Toby's Dream Foundation
Description:
Mission

Toby’s Dream Foundation ensures that children living with life-threatening illnesses in Greater Hampton Roads & the Eastern Shore of Virginia have the opportunity and resources to imagine and experience their most fantastic dream.

Vision

Toby’s Dream Foundation creates dream experiences for children facing an uncertain tomorrow, which allows them to continue believing in the possibility of a moment unencumbered and a life full of hope.

Not all children live the care-free lives they deserve and, as a result, their capacity to dream becomes hindered by the news of an uncertain tomorrow. For some, their days become consumed with treatment and therapy, doctor’s offices and hospitals. These children face challenges that many adults would cower from, and are able to do so with courage that inspires so many.

Name: VOICES4FREEDOM
Description:
VOICES4FREEDOM is helping people to freedom. We are creating awareness, raising money, and giving back to individuals and organizations addressing the urgent issue of modern-day slavery through the following programs:

SCHOOLS4FREEDOM: an innovative transitional school model educating child slaves and in the process freeing entire villages from generations of slavery.

ENSLAVED: an interactive, immersive, multi-media art exhibition documenting the lives of slaves and celebrating the freedom they never dreamed possible.

THE FREEDOM PRIZES: an international award ceremony honoring heroes of the anti-slavery movement.

We have built 24 transitional schools, helping more than 2,700 people break the bonds of modern-day slavery and move towards freedom. 

Learn more about us: http://voices4freedom.org/

Name: Touch Foundation
Description: Touch Foundation’s core competence lies in helping people as well as teach practical, sustainable ways of good health that benefits both the individual and their communities, enabling them to reach their full potential. We encourage awareness about community ownership, local resources and self-sufficiency, as well as teaching skills to bring physical, mental, moral and economic transformation to the people in India.

Name: META (Making Education the Answer) Foundation
Description: META (Making Education The Answer) is a nonprofit organization that provides opportunities to the underserved Latinx student community in Southern California through college scholarships and educational mentorship.

Name: IAS Engage / International Aid Services Denmark
Description:
International Aid Services Denmark (IAS) er en udviklings- og nødhjælpsorganisation, som er grundlagt på kristne værdier. IAS DK har siden begyndelsen i 2000 været i vækst og har i dag et omfattende arbejde i Afrika - primært Østafrika og Afrikas Horn. IAS DK samarbejder i IAS' internationale alliance og gennem partnere i det globale syd.

IAS Engage er vores platform for engagement med enkeltpersoner, virksomheder, netværk, skoler m.fl. Som partner kan I være med til at skabe forandring og bidrage til FN's verdensmål gennem:

• Socialt ansvarlige projekter
• Jobskabende aktiviteter
• Uddannelse
• Nødhjælp

IAS DK's tematiske fokusområder er:
•social retfærdighed
•marginaliserede børn
•uddannelse for alle

Vi arbejder for at forbedre vilkårene for nogle af de mest marginaliserede børn, som lever i fattige områder og skrøbelige situationer:
•børn med handicap og særlige behov
•forældreløse børn
•børn der lever i ekstrem fattigdom
•uledsagede mindreårige flygtninge
•piger
•børn fra nomadiske kulturer

Vi prioriterer at styrke vores partnere, så de selv kan lede arbejdet, danne netværk, øge deres indflydelse og skabe resultater. Målet er at vores partnere kan fortsætte det arbejde, vi har påbegyndt sammen. Det er med til at gøre projekterne bæredygtige.

Vi arbejder i det østlige Afrika i Somaliland (Somalia), Etiopien, Uganda, Sydsudan, Sudan, Kenya og Tanzania. Vi er også involveret i projekter i andre dele af verden, hvor vores danske medlemskirker har partnere, og hvor vi kan bidrage indenfor vores tematiske fokusområder.

IAS DK har hovedkontor i København og et afdelingskontor i Brande.

Kontakt os gerne hvis I har interesse for at høre mere om at indgå et samarbejde med IAS DK.

Name: Make a Wave
Description: We are a non-profit committed to promoting unity and teamwork for the common good.

Name: Hoops for Haiti
Description:
Hoops for Haiti brings a message of hope and love through basketball.  

We use basketball as a bridge to go beyond hoops with the objective of encouraging economic and educational sustainability.

Since November of 2009, we have been working with basketball programs throughout the United States and Canada to collect used basketball gear. It is our goal to collect enough gear to equip the youth attending the camps with the things they need to play basketball—basketball shoes, basketballs and uniforms.  If you are willing, or if you know of an organization that is willing, to donate equipment, please contact me or send it directly to Hoops for Haiti at the address below. 

Stay tuned to the website for photos, blog posts about our progress and preparation, and later, a re-cap of our trip, with pictures and videos. Thank you, in advance, for your generous support.

As part of our trip to Haiti in May, Hoops for Haiti will be shooting video for a documentary film.  With a working title of “Beyond Hoops for Haiti,” we will be looking to show the impact that “a message of hope and love through basketball” can have on the lives of the young people of Haiti.

Name: Ethos Education Group
Description: Ethos Education Group is a non profit organization

Name: The Tandon Foundation
Description:
"Philanthropy is the confluence of love and compassion; it is a sanctuary for all those people for whom there is no one coming." - SL Tandon.

Philanthropy and Social services has always been the cornerstone of our ethos and we're at the forefront of eco-conservation, public health, and education in the communities since the 1950s.

The Tandon Foundation (TTF) continue to be guided by the principles of its founder, SL Tandon. His vision of proactive philanthropy and societal development while ensuring that initiatives and interventions have a contemporary relevance to the nation; TTF has played a pivotal role in transforming traditional ideas of charity to make a real difference to the communities.

TTF's establishment is the culmination of these social endeavors and help transform the lives of the under-served and underprivileged people through education and financial assistance across regions, age groups and income strata.

All educational programs initiated by TTF entail close partnerships with the government, corporations, local communities and the general public. These programs aim at holistic development of children making them employable citizens with a deep sense of commitment to society and the nation.

One of our key criterion for support is the ability to track and trace the investment and see tangible results. The on-ground teams guarantee full transparency, no administration costs and provides detailed insights into the projects.

The Tandon Foundation believes that considerable positive change can occur when we collaborate with like-minded partners and nurture projects that are scalable, ensuring a long-term impact.

There is a lot to be done, and we are proud to be working with some of the greatest talents in the field of philanthropy and social service, thus helping us making a real difference.

Description:

Who are we? A team of like-minded people who are enthusiastic to prove their commitment in providing social support for the benefit of our compatriots.
What do we strive for? Our organization is designed to contribute to the improvement in the quality of life of our compatriots through adopting a systematic approach to support vulnerable groups in the framework of providing various services.
We encourage you to become part of our patriotic mission, since we are deeply convinced that joint humanitarian assistance operations will become a unique platform to support our fellow citizens and each of you will be proud of his direct involvement.

Name: Eastland Community Trust
Description:
ECT is Tairawhiti's local energy trust - with a goal of assisting in the creation of a more positive, prosperous and attractive community. 

ECT commits resources to those investments that make the greatest contribution towards achieving this vision and transforming our region.

Our mission...
To contribute towards increasing the quantity and quality of choices people and groups can make, to build meaningful and fulfilled lives. Our focus is on fostering the Gisborne district's economic prosperity through acquiring, owning and prudently governing investments and utilising resources to support and encourage selected commercial and community initiatives.

Name: Alcuin Amal
Description: Alcuin Amal is een orgaan van K.S.V. Sanctus Virgilius dat zich inzet voor vluchtelingkinderen in Griekenland. Het is opgericht in 2019 en na jaren bijgedragen te hebben gedaan aan de organisatie van Happy Caravan, is het sinds 2024 gekoppeld aan Lighthouse Relief.

Our core focus is on food, hygiene and environment. All from a point of departure in the communities of the island.

We get things done. Also in emergencies. Action speak louder.

Description: Singai Tamil Sangam is an organisation in Singapore aiming to provide a platform for the Tamils in Singapore to bond, network and share their knowledge with one another.

Description: Soñar Despierto es una organizaciòn creada con el objetivo de comprometer a jóvenes líderes con el desarrollo de su comunidad, formando en ellos una verdadera conciencia social, a través de acciones positivas a favor dela niñez mas necesitada.

Name: Woman's Club of Wilmette
Description: For over 130 years, the Woman's Club of Wilmette has provided a variety of programs and activities bringing North Shore women together for civic and service opportunities. The WCW is a vibrant, inclusive resource for social progress and community outreach through inspiring education and engagement. We are open to all women who are interested in making a difference in their community. You'll have opportunities to give back to your community, connect with friendly and dedicated women in social and service settings, and share your talents and energy in making WCW a vibrant community centet.

Name: Pancretan Youth Association of America
Description:
PANCRETAN YOUTH OF AMERICA

The PYA is the oldest federation of Greek American youth organizations in the nation. Founded in 1948, tens of thousands of Greek American youth and young adults have passed through its ranks into adulthood, keeping alive the sacred traditions, history and heritage of their ancestral homeland--the island of Crete.

The PYA is comprised of twenty organized youth chapters and members at large from throughout the United States and Canada and is governed by a national Board of Directors, which is democratically elected at biennial national conventions.

Membership benefits include the opportunity to participate in an active, democratic federation of young adults sharing the same ideals and heritage, scholarships for university level students, summer study and travel programs in Crete, the building of cultural ties to Crete and so much more.

The PYA's purpose is to promote and develop social and cultural relationships and mutuality among all of the Cretans and their descendants residing in America. To promote and develop education through scholarships. To raise voluntary contributions and funds to aid philanthropic institutions in the United States, Canada, on the island of Crete, and to other philanthropic purposes. And, to work in cooperation with and as a part of the PAA, promoting its activities and programs in addition to the activities and programs of the PYA.

Name: YouCareWeCare France
Description:
L’idée présentée est simple.

Mettre gratuitement à la disposition du personnel soignant une maison de campagne, un appartement à la mer, un gîte dans le Gers, une résidence secondaire à l'étranger, un bateau, un chalet, un mobil-home, une chambre d'hôtel, un séjour en village de vacances, pour qu'il puisse se ressourcer une fois la crise passée .

Alors, si vous pouvez mettre une ou plusieurs semaines d’un bien à leur disposition, remplissez le formulaire ci-dessous.

Nous mettrons en contact les généreux prêteurs avec les responsables des hôpitaux.

Cette action n'est valable que pour le personnel hospitalier se trouvant en première ligne face au virus dans les hôpitaux en France.

La répartition des semaines se fera sous le contrôle d'un huissier.

Name: American Benevolent Society
Description:
The American Benevolent Society was founded in 1868, one of Mexico’s first philanthropic organizations, celebrating 146 years of community service. We are an independent, non-religious, non-partisan, charity organization. We are Americans, helping our own and others with medical, financial, legal, psychological and other needs; and in addition bringing together the English speaking communities in Mexico City.

The efforts of the ABS have always reflected the support of volunteers and philanthropists working together to combine resources and experiences. The ABS founded the American Hospital (which later became the ABC Medical Center) in 1886 and the American Cemetery in 1898. Explore our website to learn more about what we do, our purpose, our history and how you can help.

Name: Reconnect
Description:
Nós somos a Reconnect, um projeto que surgiu a partir do requerimento de um trabalho final do curso de porgramação no instituto Proa, em que nossa equipe faz parte.
Nosso foco é a resolução da ODS 1 da ONU - A Erradicação da Pobreza - E vimos as pessoas em situação de rua como os mais vuneráveis na escala da pobreza, por isto nossa missão é conhecer suas histórias e buscar trazer para os mesmos um recomeço. Vamos por meio de nossa plataforma on-line fazer o cadastro de pessoas em situação de rua que desejam retornar para suas familias, e as familias em busca de seu familiar que esta na rua também fara o cadastro para encontra-lo. Acreditamos que desta forma poderemos cruzar informações para unir familias novamentes e formar estes reecontros, a partir de seu retorno para casa, estarão incluidos novamente na sociedade. Já para os que não quiserem retornar para casa, temos como projeto futuro os dar a oportunidade de emprego, para assim essa pessoa conquistar sua independência e ser incluso na sociedade por meio disto.

- Para tudo isso ocorrer contaremos com ONG'S parceiras e voluntários que serão responsáveis por realizar os cadastros do morador de rua.
- já no projeto de empregabilidade, contaremos com empresas e empresarios  do mercado de trabalho que estejam abertos a dar uma nova chance de recomeço para estas pessoas.

Name: The Rotary Club of Columbia
Description:
We embrace the original spirit of James Rouse's Columbia as a growing, diverse and welcoming group committed to making our community and the world a better place through our efforts and our donations.

You'll find members volunteering in communities at home and abroad to support education and job training, provide clean water, combat hunger, improve health and sanitation and eradicate polio.

General Information ~
We have weekly meetings on Tuesday nights at 6:30 pm at Eggspectation, at 6010 University Boulevard, Ellicott City, MD 21043. Visitors are Welcome! Your First Dinner is on us!!

Name: Moore for Kids
Description:
Our Mission

Our mission is to collaborate and work with businesses and not-for-profit organizations to assist individuals and families during difficult times.  It is our passion to develop sustainable programs that will bring attention to organizations that we support.  Moore for Kids is dedicated to raising awareness for the goodness of giving, as the Lord our God expects us to do, coloring the world with kindness.

Our Beliefs

We believe God is the alpha and omega. He expects us as Christians to help those in need with the resources he has provided us. It is our responsibility to create programs and generate awareness for those that are in need.

We believe that God has blessed us in order for us to help others. With that blessing, comes great responsibility. We will work hard to ensure that funds donated to any of our programs go to organizations that will utilize those funds as stated in their by-laws. We work closely with local organizations to ensure that those we say we are helping indeed receive that help.

Moore for Kids was created to help children and their families in need. Families that are dealing with sickness, poverty, and other family issues affects children greatly and our community as well. We believe that it is our responsibility to help those that are truly in need in our community and abroad where we can make an impact.

We are passionate about helping others. We look for ways to work with other organizations without being concerned about who gets the donation. We believe that people will donate to causes that are local and where they have a history with the cause of an organization. We also believe that people are concerned about how much of their donation goes to the cause. My wife and I currently fund Moore for Kids and we do not have a staff. We do not draw salaries and never will.

EDUCATE TO LEAD

Name: Pink Week
Description:
Pink Week was founded in Clare College, Cambridge, by Nina Rauch to celebrate the life and legacy of Dina Rabinovitch.
The aims of Pink Week are two-fold; we want to engage and educate students, altering the common rhetoric surrounding breast cancer and encouraging self-awareness of the body.  
Held over a week in February the third annual Pink Week in 2017 saw 62 events spread across Cambridge University campi and colleges, with 55 incredible committee members working hard to ensure engagement and attendance was high across the multiplicity of events organised; of which there was 'something for everyone'.
A great privilege and result of the events held by Pink Week is the opportunity to raise funds for breast cancer care and research charities.  2017 saw over £34,000 raised, making us the University's largest and most successful student-led charitable organisation. 

Pink Week is now at over 12 campus's both in the UK and the US. If you'd like to get involved with Pink Week, or are interesting in sponsoring the initiative - please get in touch - we'd love to chat!

Name: Save A Rupee Spread A Smile Society
Description:
“Helping the needy or unprivileged doesn’t cost much in terms of our time or money “was the thread of thought that led to the formation of a charitable society called ‘Save a Rupee, Spread a Smile’.
What is SARSAS

“Helping the less fortunate doesn’t cost us much in terms of our time or money” From this tiny thread of thought was formed ‘Save a Rupee, Spread a Smile’ , a charitable society. We believe that service is not what we want to do but what we have to do. What started with monthly visits to old age homes and orphanages has now grown into manifold domains of community service, education, health and hygiene, food and nutrition, animal care etc. On the big canvas of life, these petite strokes go a long way in creating a magnum opus.

Why SARSAS
Every day and every smile is taking us closer to that cherished dream of a healthier, happier and wealthier society. Come hold our hands, let’s build it up together. After all it’s about spreading those precious little smiles!



~ Our Vision ~
Forming a charitable society is by no means a child’s play. But carrying it on in a sustainable manner is more challenging. We at SARSAS give much importance to sustainable development in all areas of education, health and hygiene awareness, environmental awareness etc. Our vision is to help create a positive change in the society through motivating youngsters to be more empathetic and service minded and at the same time empowering the unprivileged.

What We DO

Community Service
*	Developmental projects in rural areas.
*	Disaster relief in affected areas.
*	Old age home visits.
*	Palliative care.
*	Distribute useful materials (new/used) to the needy.

Educational aid
*	Sponsoring a child programme
*	Developmental projects in rural schools.
*	Setting up of libraries in orphanages, rural schools etc.
*	Teaching at orphanages.
*	Vocational and life skills development programmes.

Food and Nutrition
*	Mid-day meal programme at Medical College, Trivandrum.
*	Nutritional level / health check-ups are held at orphanages.
*	Special life events are celebrated by sponsoring breakfast/lunch/dinner
at orphanages/old age homes/ rehabilitation centres etc.


Health and Hygiene
*	FInancial aid for Cancer treatment.
*	Blood donation camps .
*	Blood and organ donation awarness campaigns.
*	Distribution of medicines/sanitary items etc to the needy.

Animal Care :
We co-ordinate with animal shelters and people for animal groups to help
care for stray animals.

Name: Sewing New Futures, Inc
Description:
Sewing New Futures (SNF) is a 501 (c) 3 non-profit organization based in Florida that supports women and girls who have survived or evaded sex trafficking.

SNF’s aim is to create sustainable alternatives for women and girls who are victims or at risk of human trafficking.

Name: Inland Northwest Planned Giving Council
Description: The Inland Northwest Planned Giving Council provides education and networking opportunities for planned giving practitioners, including development officers and fundraisers as well as financial, legal and estate planning professionals; encourages professionalism in the planned giving arena; and promotes planned giving for charitable purposes.  Membership is open to development officers, financial planners, estate planning attorneys, CPAs and other allied professionals.

Name: Resources, Love and Faith Inc.
Description:
Resources, Love and Faith Inc. (or RLAF) is a 501(c)(3) organization established to promote the welfare of others. RLAF aims to provide monetary donations to those who may not be able to help themselves; animals, children, and senior citizens. 

We believe a little help can make a big difference to those in need.

Just as you did it to one of the least of these who are members of my family, you did it to me. - Matthew 25:40

Name: Beating Hearts Malta
Description: Beating Hearts Malta (BHM) was founded in December 2011 with its main objective being to provide support and encouragement for all adults and children with CHD, their partners and families in Malta through sharing personal experience and knowledge as well as, to promote awareness and educate social care professionals, employers, insurers and the general public about the existence and the needs of people with CHD.

Name: NSW Hen Rescue
Description: NSW Hen Rescue's mission is simple: Liberate. Educate. Inspire. Since 2010, NSW Hen Rescue has liberated thousands of hens and other factory farmed animals from places of abuse and exploitation. Whilst we rescue all animals, our focus in on caged hens.  We are a small, volunteer run team who rehabilitates and rehomes, or offers sanctuary to all animals that come into our care.

Name: Marin Charitable
Description:
OUR MISSION: We believe that that every child deserves the opportunity to have a happy and fulfilling childhood. Marin Charitable provides grants to organizations that offer critical support, education and enrichment for school-aged children in Marin County who have limited opportunities.

Marin Charitable is an all-volunteer, nonprofit organization celebrating over 50 years of service. Marin Charitable grants are provided to nonprofit organizations with programs serving school-aged children within Marin County. Marin Charitable is a registered 501(c)(3) charity.

For more information about grants, please go to our website.

Name: ALL IN.foundation
Description:
We are a social impact tech start-up and digital non-profit foundation determined to help end extreme poverty. 

Being part of the richest generation in the history of humankind, we have the knowledge, skills and financial means necessary to eradicate poverty. Yet, 736 million worldwide still live in extreme poverty. 800 million people suffer from hunger. Over 600 million people lack access to clean water. 2 billion people lack vital nutrients. 1 billion people are infected with worms. And half a million people die every year from malaria. This is not the world we want to live in so let’s enable change.

Our mission is to enable everyone to have a real impact in fighting extreme poverty, no matter their budget.

All In was founded to significantly increase private donations and engagement to fight extreme poverty. We believe that donations should be simple, effective, engaging and transparent. Most people want to give but current fundraising practices are not adapted to an increasingly mobile-only lifestyle. Given the sheer multitude of non-profit organizations around the world, it’s also difficult to find the most effective ones where your donation makes a real difference. And if you donate, there often is little transparency into where your money goes and what the impact is. Not to talk about the high costs of many of the big organizations. This is why we built the All In app - To provide you with a simple, effective, engaging and transparent way to help fight extreme poverty, no matter the size of your budget.

Name: United Against Poverty and Disease (UAPAD)
Description:
Together, we work towards raising awareness on issues relating to global poverty and disease. UAPAD's mission is to educate, encourage and inspire individuals and organizations to join in the fight to alleviate these global burdens in the developing world. By providing prevention and sustainable solutions, the basic needs of communities will be met by empowering people worldwide to stand united with us in taking action against poverty and disease.

For recruiting information, please go here: http://www.idealist.org/view/internship/36DmJ5cmPfsP/

Description:
At Through Our Eyes Travel, we believe that travel is not just about seeing new places, but about experiencing them in a meaningful way. 

We are a full-service travel company catering to professional athletes and travelers seeking itineraries that include charitable projects, thrilling excursions, and authentic cultural exchange experiences. Our team is dedicated to creating personalized trips that help our clients gain a deeper understanding of the world and make a positive impact on the communities they visit.

Name: Assistance League of Indianapolis

Philanthropic programs include: Operation School Bell where qualifying children receive new school clothes, coats and athletic shoes; Assault Survivor Kits which provides garments for victims of assault; ALI Bears which gives teddy bears to hospitals, trauma centers and hospice facilities; ALI Friends whose mission is offering activities and companionship to older and disabled adults; and OSB Reading Buddies where members share the love of reading to early elementary school children.
