Description:
Started in 2015, Simpligiiv is a 501(c)(3) charitable nonprofit technology start-up focused on bringing high-tech efficiency to the charitable giving space. We help donors give to causes & categories containing hundreds of well-run charities, rather than donors having to research & donate to charities one-at-a-time. 

Many charities all over the country do great work every day in relative obscurity.  Our causes & categories help donors easily share their generosity with these well-run local organizations, as well as the “big name” charities we all know. And as new charities are added to our lists, they're automatically included, too! 

"We handle the research and logistics...you simply give."

Name: TGI Ghana
Description:
TGI Ghana is a team of selfless individuals who have dedicated their lives to helping others. Impact is usually made in an increasing scale from communal, regional, national, continental and to the biggest level – global. The only impact that will be greater than a global one will be that which will be sustained across generations (trans-generational). We at TGI believe in transforming this world by empowering people’s minds and challenging them to make an impact in the lives of others.

Every individual deserves equal opportunity to quality livelihood. We see a lot of human potential go waste in many communities in Ghana and abroad due to barriers of extreme poverty, deprivation, exclusion, lack of quality education and limited opportunities. TGI Ghana is determined to make great strides in reaching many communities in Ghana with various developmental projects, bringing hope to many individuals and families by providing skills and empowering the most vulnerable to make the most of the little they have.

Our vision 

Caring for the underprivileged and empowering the minds of others to impact this generation and beyond.

Our Objectives

1. Identifying target groups in the deprived parts of the country where people live in poverty.
2. Providing for the basic needs of needy individuals; both children and adults in rural and urban centers
3. Developing strategies to empower the younger generation with the aim of producing influential, responsible   and patriotic citizens for this country and the world at large.
4. Unearthing leadership potential in people with the aim of producing efficient leaders across the globe.

Name: I Am Character Youth Foundation
Description:
Our mission is to establish a scalable model for providing young boys with recurrent exposure to fun and interactive learning experiences that include computer programming, website design, 3D game animation, app design, coding with robots & drones, and a variety of other STEM activities. In addition, there is an explicit and simultaneous focus on strengthening students' innate leadership, determination, and problem-solving skills while building their self-confidence.

We provide a strong core mentoring program for young men of all cultures and backgrounds, including at-risk children experiencing academic and behavior challenges due to family-related and social-economic issues.

Our Foundation believes the greatest impact for growth and success starts when boys are young. Our principles are built upon a firm foundation of education, leadership, and character. Our focus is to help develop the gifts within each child we work with, build their self-confidence, and provide consistent support so that our mentees are positioned to lead successful and productive lives.

Our program adheres to a four-step approach: 
STEP 1 - Increase the student's general awareness of STEM education and careers.
STEP 2 - Motivate students to pursue the education necessary to participate in those careers.
STEP 3 - Provide students with technology-rich experiences that develop the skills needed for pursuing advanced STEM education and entering the STEM workforce.
STEP 4 - Partner with individuals, corporations, and organizations who will support and advance our mission of exposing young males to technology and STEM opportunities.

We are continually looking to connect with community partners, donors, and prospective mentors. To learn more about our program and ways you can support our mission, please visit IACYF.org.

Name: Dual Mentoring
Description:
Dual Mentoring Conversations with Impact is an initiative of Rotary of Elizabeth Quay and is the brainchild of a team of conversation innovators. We are a collective of psychologists, HR specialists, business professionals, and philanthropists, building discussions to change the world. 
We will pair you with a mentoring partner, and over the course of thirty weeks, we will send you material created specifically to produced stimulating conversations between exceptional people.

Name: Grand Citizens
Description: Grand Citizens Is A Public Service And Charity Organization Formed By The Grand Organization. It Originally Came Into Hammaad Salman's Mind As An Idea When He Was Writing An Essay On Fixing The City's Problems. He Thought Why Not Turn This Idea Into Reality; Thus, Grand Citizens Came Into Existence. It Operates in Karachi Currently, And Has Had Successful Projects  Like Serving Iftaar To The General Public, And Serving Sherbet  During A Heatstroke.

Name: IThrive Network
Description:
IThrive Network empowers women who have faced a variety of trauma in their lives to take back control. We are committed to changing and reshaping the quality of broken lives through relationships, friendships, and mentorship. We provide therapeutic and holistic approaches that teach women to soar through life's hardships and challenges. Our mission is rooted in healing discomforts through accountability and positivity. 

Currently, we’re focused on wrapping loving arms around military women with PTSD.

Name: Donera
Description: Donera is the first nonprofit designed for Gen Z donors to give to climate crisis-related causes. We select the best nonprofits by measuring their ability to remove CO2/dollar from the atmosphere, help donors give directly to such causes and report progress via social media. We are mobile-first and cut to the point, helping Gen Z be as impactful as possible with their donation.

Name: Service Dogs at Syracuse
Description: SDS is an organization that allows students to train service dogs and become involved both as a puppy sitter and through various events throughout the school year. Our goal is to raise awareness of service animals and the community they assist.

Name: EMadagas'Care
Description:
EMadagas’Care, c’est quoi ?
Nous sommes une jeune équipe composée d’étudiants de l’EM Strasbourg. Dans le cadre de nos études en deuxième année, nous avons choisi d’effectuer une mission humanitaire à Madagascar. Pour ce faire, nous avons fondé une association : EMadagas’Care !  

Nous souhaitons dans notre mission agir sur place dans un lycée, le lycée Justin Manambelona, situé près de Mananjary. Nos objectifs à réaliser pendant notre mission sont les suivants : 
Financer la construction d’un puits
Construire un château d’eau
Apporter des fournitures scolaires aux enfants du lycée
Donner des cours et organiser des activités pour les étudiants
Rénover le lycée sur place


Ce projet, nous ne pouvons pas le réaliser sans VOTRE aide ! C’est pourquoi nous comptons sur votre soutien afin de de faire évoluer notre projet et récolter assez de fonds pour mener à bien ce grand projet ! 
Nous comptons sur VOUS ! 
Vous pouvez nous soutenir à travers notre plateforme de dons : https://www.gofundme.com/emadagascare
Pour en apprendre plus sur notre équipe et notre projet, nous vous invitons à visiter notre site internet : https://emadagascare.org !


Notre équipe : EMadagas'Care
Nous vous remercions pour votre intérêt, et vous remercions pour le soutient que vous nous apportez !

« EMadagas’Care, Show That You Care ! »

Name: The Science Line
Description:
The wearable technology market  is set to surpass $30 billion by 2020. While science and technology jobs continue to grow in this area, women still make up less than 28% of the science and engineering workforce. Women continue to avoid Science, Technology, Engineering and Math (S.T.E.M.)  fields for many reasons including lack of role models for young girls to look up to, negative stereotypes about their capabilities as a girl, lack of skills being taught at an early age and stereotypes of the S.T.E.M. fields that are different than how girls see themselves. Like many problems, in order to really change the future, the solution needs to confront the  source.  

The Science Line seeks to change these statistics by  combining science and technology with fashion to break down barriers. We enable young girls to be introduced to S.T.E.M. fields, have positive female role models in these fields and learn useful skills to empower them. Founded in 2016, The Science Line is a fiscally sponsored non-profit, working to bridge the gap between science, technology and fashion by inspiring youth through S.T.E.M. fashion shows, competitions, educational kits and hands-on workshops.

The Science Line is a Fiscally Sponsored Project of Inquiring Systems,Inc. (ISI). ISI is a California Nonprofit 501(c) (3) Corporation Founded in 1978

Name: Solma (Stichting Ontwikkelikngshulp Lesotho Malealea)
Description: SOLMA raises funds in The Netherlands for the Malealea Development Trust, Malealea, Lesotho. SOLMA has completed drinking water systems for two villages uin the Malealea valley, four classrooms for the Litsokeleng School and is presently building a new building for the Botsoela Preschool.

Name: Gate Breaker Ministries
Description: We are committed to providing education which will propel people into greater levels of freedom. Whether through  primary and trade schools in Pakistan, training for pastors in Kenya, yearly tours through Israel, or informational courses Christianity or other relevant topics, we are passionately invested in breaking down the gates of oppression through education.

Name: HoHoTO, supporting workplace diversity initiatives across Toronto
Description:
HoHoTO began in 2008 with a call to arms on Twitter: Toronto’s digital mavens can do more for our community.

From that first event and every year that’s followed, we've continued to work towards making a significant impact on the digital community in Toronto, now with over $400,000 raised for local charities.

Now impact and opportunity are our focus, as we bring the digital community together in support of organizations that directly affect beneficial change in our technology ecosystem with a comprehensive approach to improving workplace diversity.

We understand the complexity of workplace diversity in the digital industry. It’s our belief that this challenge is exacerbated by a lack of access and engagement in digital pursuits by youth, particularly girls and young women. For example, when you have a university computer science class with only a handful of women, you end up having dozens more men ready to be hired into the workforce.

By initiating programs for young women and minorities to learn more about technology-driven businesses and entrepreneurship, while giving them access to technology—from computers to programming to connected devices—we’re setting the stage for more women and minorities to pursue digital careers.

• HoHoTO’s holiday party is a chance for professionals to get together to support these initiatives and celebrate our successes throughout the year.

• HoHoTO’s Girls’ Tech Tour is a chance for young women to be exposed to leading companies, products, and digital pursuits.

• HoHoTO’s mentorship program is an opportunity for both professionals and young women to inspire one another.

Name: Code Uplift
Description: Code Uplift is a volunteer organization co-founded by siblings Navleen and Raj Boparai to help empower underprivileged students through technology. We offer free coding lessons for low-income students between grades 3-8, speaker events, workshops, and mentorship.

Name: HANDA Rehabilitation & Welfare Association
Description:
HANDA  Rehabilitation & Welfare Association (HANDA) was established in 1996, as a grassroot NGO of the people affected by leprosy in China. Since its establishment, HANDA has carried out comprehensive leprosy rehabilitation projects mainly on empowerment and participation of people affected by leprosy which enabled people to recognize their self value. 

Mission: To promote the dignity and respect of people affected by leprosy

Vision: For a world without stigma, but equality.

Main Projects: 

Social-psychological rehabilitation
Improve the social understanding towards leprosy, promote dignity and respect of people affected by leprosy so that they can reintegrate back into the community.

Mobile Prosthesis Workshop
HANDA owns a Mobile Prosthesis Workshop.  This mobile workshop provides complete facilities for the production of prosthesis. Help people to stand up again and to be able to live a dignified life.


Mobile Eye Sight Saving Project
In cooperation with the Guangzhou Aier ophthalmic hospital, HANDA sends its operating vehicle to perform cataract surgery in remote mountainous areas.  This not only helps to restore sight to people who are visually impaired but also trains people affected by leprosy in self-care.

Community Development Project
HANDA’s Community Development Project encourages people affected by leprosy to earn their own living through self-support projects in order to improve the village’s economic conditions.  This also helps to create a harmonious and happy village for people affected by leprosy.

Scholarship Project
HANDA’s Scholarship Project strives to financially support people affected by leprosy and their children so that they can have access to education and enhance their ability to support themselves as well as integrate into society in the future.

Name: Just Wanna Live
Description: Just Wanna Live est une association composé d’étudiants qui a pour objectif de venir en aide aux personnes dans le besoin et aux étudiants en situation de précarité.

Name: COVID Response Network
Description: Not everyone can afford to social distance. Millions of Americans have no choice but to gather in nursing homes, hospitals, homeless shelters, food pantries, and jails. These are the people most at risk of contracting COVID-19, and these are the people we are fighting to protect.

Name: Andron Philanthropy
Description:
Andron Philanthropy is an initiative of Andron family based out of Zurich, Switzerland to help those who have become a victim of natural or man-made disaster.

Andron family has been undertaking philanthropic work in various countries to solve different issues for decades. From India to Indonesia, their support for charitable causes goes beyond boundaries and cultures. Whether it’s a natural calamity in Nepal or human inflicted terror in Syria, their sole motto is to help businesses and individuals get back to their lives by supporting them in every possible way. From monetary to advisory, rehabilitation to relocation, the length and breadth of their services encompasses everything that ignites a hope in sinking heart and reaffirms their faith in humanity.



Philanthropy requires a disruptive mindset, innovative thinking and an entrepreneurial insight. With a strong team of like-minded professionals with years of project management experience behind them, Andron Philanthropy has been successfully carrying out its relief programs in various countries. Hailing from different backgrounds, all our professionals share the same vision of helping those in need and bringing in their life a new ray of hope.

Name: Influence the World
Description: Complete the First ITW Journey Delivering Resources to a Targeted Charitable Cause. Build Teamwork Amongst The ITW Ambassadors on The Trip. Raise Awareness of the Charitable Cause and ITW Mission in Our Influencer Communities.

Name: Bright Eyes Uganda, Inc.
Description: Bright Eyes Uganda's mission is to care for the indigent and underserved populations of Uganda, specifically the children. Our goal is to improve the infrastructure of their rural villages and provide clean water, nutritional food, accessible health care and quality education. Bright Eyes Uganda has been operating in the East African country of Uganda since 2005. We are a private, nonprofit and nondenominational organization as well as a 501(c)(3) tax-exempt organization recognized by the IRS. All members of Bright Eyes Uganda team are volunteers. There are no paid employees. Labor, supplies and funds used in our projects are acquired by private donation or through grants from private funders.

Name: Astro Kasih
Description: Astro Kasih believes in serving the community in a balanced and sustainable manner. Our work anchors in four focus areas; Lifelong Learning, Community Development, Sports & Wellness and Environment. We do this with our staff volunteers as well as strategic partners. Astro believes in working together and making a difference in our community and ultimately the world we live in.

Name: LICHTEVOLUTION
Description: Wir übermitteln Wissen über finanzielle, philosophische, psychologische und spirituelle Themen.

Name: La Olla Común
Description: Fundación que busca unir a las personas que desean ayudar junto a las que lo necesitan. Recaudamos recursos y aportamos con organización a las ollas comunes de Chile. Aportamos con beneficios a nuestros usuarios para motivar su colaboración.

Name: #WeCodeBusiness
Description:
The goal of #WeCodeBusiness is to spread the spirit of entrepreneurship among students. We believe that entrepreneurship is the way to make great impacts on the world we are living in and unleash students’ innovation and potential.

Objective.
• To promote hacking culture among students in Hong Kong. Drawing students from a pool of diverse
disciplines who would like to build and make things to shape a better future for themselves and the society.
• To bridge the mindsets of diverse disciplines. Give business students a chance to work with makers,
introduce a technical engineer to a dreamer, and even partner with the most creative artist with a student
honoring salesmanship.

Name: AD&D Foundation
Description: Foundation with a purely philanthropic profile.

Name: Student Voice Foundation
Description: The Student Voice Foundation strives to help every student and educator believe in the power of his or her own voice to effect positive change. The Global Student Voice Film Festival is the Foundation’s first effort in pursuit of this goal. In addition to the primary goal of building student voice, it is also designed to help students build deeper empathy and explore new possibilities for their future.

Name: Jihomoravská komunitní nadace
Description: Jihomoravská komunitní nadace působí od roku 2008 na Břeclavsku a Hodonínsku. Jejím posláním je podpora rozvoje filantropie a občanské společnosti na tomto území. Nadace podporuje kulturní, výchovné, ekologické, sportovní a další projekty, které přinášejí prospěch místním obyvatelům a přispívají k rozvoji regionu.

Name: Save U.S. Pets Foundation, Inc.
Description: Save U.S. Pets Foundation, Inc. is a 501(c)(3) tax-exempt organization based in New Jersey, managed by veterinary, legal and financial professionals, which raises funds and makes grants to help responsible pet owners pay for extraordinary veterinary expenses.

Name: CCPI - Conselho Comunitario Ponte do Imaruim
Matchme Philanthropy
Description:
Matchme helps businesses convey their commitment to brand-aligned social impact, collaborate with millennials and convert those millennials into customers through crowdfunded gift matching campaigns.

U.S. businesses spend heavily on conventional CSR initiatives, such as employee matching and direct donations, but these initiatives fail to engage consumers.  As a result, while consumers are increasingly concerned about the positive impact of their goods and services providers, they are unsure which businesses are socially responsible.

Matchme solves this problem by providing a platform for businesses to launch matching campaigns that consumers can support.  The flow is simple: the business selects a charity, offers to match all donations to the campaign for that charity up to a limit, promotes the matching campaign through the matchme site and social media, and then matches all donations when the campaign ends.

By donating to charity via a matchme campaign, instead of directly, businesses leverage their donations to enhance their brand, boost ROI and potentially double the amount that the charitable recipient receives.  It's a win-win-win for businesses, consumers and the causes they love.

matchme - give more by giving together

Name: La valigia delle idee
Description: La Valigia delle Idee nasce dal desiderio di affiliazione di un gruppo di persone dalle diverse età, professionalità e storie di vita. Il loro intento è dare vita ad una realtà territoriale che coinvolga tutti quei cittadini interessati a portare il loro contributo personale alla crescita della comunità a cui appartengono.

Name: End Youth Poverty in Africa
Description:
EYPA is a grassroots non profit organisation that works to empower youth in Africa through community driven initiatives focused on education.

Mission : 
---------
EYPA key objectives include the reduction of poverty and inequality in youth, the realization of rights, the social justice, the protection of the environment, the strengthening of civil society and democratic governance

Vision :
--------
Shape the future through entrepreneurship. Help create and share opportunities that improve youth standards. We are committed to play a vital role in addressing transitional problems, such as climate change, global poverty, urbanization, complex humanitarian crises and security threats in a globalized world.

Name: Progeny, Inc.
Description:
THE PROGENY FOUNDATION rescues and provides safety, treatment, and rehabilitation for exploited and endangered children. Our organization functions at the epicenter of the growing problem of child trafficking, enslavement, and sexual exploitation with a “boots-on-the-ground” approach to doing somethingabout this insidious crime against humanity. We work tirelessly around the world with non-governmental organizations (NGOs): child welfare organizations, and homes or hospices for disavowed children or sick children, as well as law enforcement agencies tasked with bringing criminals who exploit children to justice.

Our methods are unique and extremely effective and revolve around a process that begins with the detailed documentation of each child for whom we advocate, accomplished through PROGENY, the name we gave to our highly-secure and sophisticated data system.

The PROGENY System is monitored by some of the preeminent medical specialists and physicians in the world, all of whom act as consultants at absolutely no charge. Access to the medical records of the sick children recorded in the PROGENY System enables our specialists to share the most up-to-date knowledge and information with attending doctors or other health care professionals worldwide about new drugs and treatments being developed or in use that will benefit the sick children in their care, and to thereby facilitate all possible means for their successful survival, treatment and rehabilitation.

Name: The Association of Stylists and Barbers
Description: TASB is an elite group of beauticians with big hearts and a passion for their craft. Our volunteers give their valuable time and professional skills to causes that are important to all of us. Make a change for the better and bring TASB to your community.

Name: GIVE (Global Initiative for Vision and Eyecare)
Description:
The Global Initiative for Vision and Eyecare (GIVE), is a non-profit organization based out of Mississauga, Canada aimed to eliminate preventible blindness worldwide. We work to establish locally managed eye-care programs through missions, clinical screenings, and educational programs, that will continue to serve people and communities that lack the proper resources.

Giving someone the gift of sight empowers them to make changes in their life to better themselves. According to the World Health Organization (WHO), 80% of all blindness is preventable. This means that with proper medical care and basic eye hygiene, the majority of blindness can be eradicated. 

GIVE was founded by Dr. Yasser Khan with the mission in mind to make the world a better place through the gift of sight. With GIVE's mission in mind, Dr. Khan has trained local doctors and provided eye care in over 15 countries worldwide over the past 10 years. Through GIVE, Dr. Khan and his team aim to give the world a better life through better sight.

Name: Sigma Delta Nu
Description:
The honorable sorority Sigma Delta Nu is a non-profit organization founded in 2007 from an alliance of 7 very different and unique women. They saw an opportunity to join interpersonal relations, academic development, and a civic and social compromise to create an institution that would break the known 'sorority' stereotype; maintaining one common and clear purpose of helping and strengthening their community.
Our mission has, is, and will always be to create a sisterhood of firm, strong and empowered women where our members feel completely safe and at home, and where our main goal would be to enrich the community by bringing back the values of family, mutual respect and selflessness. This goal has lead us to success and renown in both, UPR-Mayagüez and UIPR-San Germán, by helping the community through volunteering, organizing benefits, promoting social awareness and the value of unity.

La honorable sororidad Sigma Delta Nu es una organización sin fines de lucro fundada en el 2007 nacida de la alianza entre 7 mujeres diferentes y únicas. Observaron la oportunidad de unir las relaciones interpersonales, el crecimiento académico y el compromiso social y cívico para crear una institución que rompiera con los estereotipos de lo que se conoce como 'sororidad' manteniendo un fin claro de ayudar y fortalecer a su comunidad.
Nuestra misión fue, es y será crear una hermandad de damas firmes, fuertes y líderes en la cuál nuestros integrantes se sientan en familia y completa seguridad; donde nuestro propósito principal es enriquecer la comunidad re-estableciendo los valores familiares, respeto mutuo y altruismo. Este propósito nos ha llevado a ser una organización exitosa y reconocida, tanto en UPR-Mayagüez como en UIPR-San Germán, ayudando a la comunidad mediante voluntariado, organización de benéficas, promoviendo conciencia social y el valor de la unidad.

Name: funddeed
Description: funddeed is a crowdfunding platform where creative individuals share their talents and raise funds for their favorite non-profits. At funddeed, you volunteer the way you want. Just do your thing, and together we'll make it an engaging fundraiser for good. We bring all these loving projects together to attract large audiences and amplify the collective impact. Go to funddeed.com to learn more about starting your own creative fundraising project today. Happy creating!

Name: Life Momentum
Description: Life Momentum is the philanthropic arm of Brand Momentum Inc. with the support of clients, partners and employees, Life Momentum strives to aid underprivileged youth in Canada and around the world through the funding of educational livelihoods programs, providing opportunities to acquire the skills and training needed to pursue their own dreams. Life Momentum has recently collaborated with international development and relief foundation (IDRF) to develop and implement international livelihood programs for youth in Africa. In 2014, Brand Momentum’s CEO Hesham Shafie was part of a group that made it to the summit of Mount Kilimanjaro to help raise funds for the cause.

Name: CRO Foundation
Description: Everyone has the right to a dignified, fulfilling, and independent life. Every child has the right to education, care, playtime, and happy childhood. We collaborate with our Ugandan partner organisation, Child Restoration Outreach, to make these rights a reality for children who live on the street. This serves our common goal to provide these children with an independent future.

Name: Startland
Description: The Vancouver Startup Community is helping Syrians get connected to the local startup scene by providing technical training, equipment & access to startups

Name: Keen Sight Fund
Description: humanitarian work

Name: Luminate Consulting
Description:
Luminate Consulting partners with nonprofit organizations seeking to articulate their work, convey their impact, and inspire support.

Luminate Consulting provides end-to-end support in program design and fundraising with specialties spanning writing, research, strategy, and activation. 

Bring your impact to light with Luminate.

Name: Amigos de Tara
Description:
La Asociación Amigos de Tara, es una entidad sin ánimo de lucro fundada en 2005, constituida para el co-desarrollo humanitario.

Amigos de Tara desarrolla su Misión en Calcuta, India y en Madanpur I, Nepal.

Nuestro principal objetivo es cubrir las necesidades educativas, alimenticias y sanitarias de los más desfavorecidos, fomentando una cultura de solidaridad entre los pueblos.

Name: Autumn Acres Farm Sanctuary
Description: At Autumn Acres, we believe that every life is precious and that all beings want to live. We are dedicated to rescuing farm and domestic animals, so they can live out their lives in a loving home.

Name: Auxilium Advisers
Description:
Auxilium Advisers is an advisory and consulting service that helps achieve more impactful giving.

Whether individuals or companies looking to develop and put into practice a philanthropic giving strategy, or charities and other not-for-profit organisations that translate those funds or other resources into positive outcomes, we help our clients to achieve their objectives with maximum impact.  We believe that we are better able to advise one if we understand the other.  

Auxilium Advisers was founded by qualified lawyers with years of experience in advising high net worth individuals and their businesses, charities and other not-for-profit organisations. Our directors are also charity trustees with a deep understanding of the unique challenges faced by the charitable sector.

Our services are based on a simple philosophy that everyone, especially those you are trying to help, benefits more from giving that is well thought out, strategic and conscious of what is needed to achieve the most impact.

Name: Project Team
Description:
Deora Bodley's Project Team teaches the value of unity and teamwork to youth through creative and interactive group activities.

Deora Bodley's Project Team (DBPT) is a nonprofit organization under section 501(c)(3) of the IRS that brings young adults together to mentor local youth and to work on creating bonds in the communities through service outreach events. Our work is dedicated to the life of Deora Bodley, who tragically died aboard United Flight 93 on September 11, 2001.

As mentors of local youth, our volunteers serve as positive role models and teach the importance of working together using fun, hands on group activities. In addition, DBPT creates a network between the volunteers and members of the local communities by joining them together on numerous short term projects, such as park cleanups, feeding the homeless, and safety awareness. 

The program relies heavily on the enthusiasm and creativity of its volunteers. We are interested in being directly involved in our communities so that we know how to assess the needs of the community and how to inspire positive change.

In the long term, DBPT hopes to open and run its own after-school community center for elementary-aged youth and spread the Project Team program nationwide.

Name: Santa Claus Anonymous
Description:
In December of 1987, a ballroom in downtown Boston stood vacant due to a canceled corporate party.  A group of 15 friends decided to make use of the space by holding a benefit to raise funds to aid inner-city children.  With 270 people in attendance, the event raised $3,968 and so began the SnowBall. The success of the first SnowBall led to the group to establish Santa Claus Anonymous (SCA), a 501(c)(3) non-profit fundraising organization.

For over two decades, the mission of SCA has been to have a positive impact on the development of youth in the Greater Boston area by supporting programs for educational, social and character development.

SCA’s annual signature event, the SnowBall, has raised over $1 million for local youth programs in its 26-year history and has become Boston’s largest all-volunteer-run black-tie event with hundreds of volunteers and thousands of guests.

Name: CamPower For All
Description: CamPower For All is a nonprofit corporation, operated exclusively for charitable purposes, specifically to provide support and aid to healthcare givers. We raise monetary donations and collect supplies for hospital's child life departments.

Name: American Conservation Coalition - Southern California Branch
Description: The American Conservation Coalition (ACC) is a 501(c)(4) nonprofit organization dedicated to mobilizing young people around environmental action through common-sense, market-based, and limited-government ideals. ACC believes economic and environmental success go hand-in-hand, and that everyone should feel empowered to take a seat at the table in discussions concerning conservation, clean energy, sportsmen’s rights, agriculture, climate, and much more This is the Southern California Branch of ACC.

Name: Burkin'Ardenn'Avenir
Description:
Burkin'Ardenn'Avenir est née le 19 décembre 2006 de la rencontre et de la volonté de deux personnes:

• Yacouba BARRY, vice-président, originaire de la province du Poni au Burkina Faso.
• Claude ROQUIER, président, décidé à agir dans le domaine humanitaire, très sensibilisé par les peuples d'Afrique Noire et plus particulièrement du Burkina Faso.
 
L'association Burkin'Ardenn'Avenir est gérée par un conseil d'administration de 15 membres d'horizon et de sensibilité différente.

Elle oeuvre dans la province du PONI, plus précisement au village de GALGOULI (région d'origine de Y.BARRY, vice-président).

L'association a des contacts étroits avec cette région et les autorités, ainsi qu'avec l'association BURKIN ARDENN GALGOULI AVENIR.
Localement, BURKIN ARDENN GALGOULI AVENIR a été créée pour:
• conseiller et orienter sur les priorités de soutien
• mettre en oeuvre les projets et en suivre la réalisation sur place
 
Compte-tenu des attentes recensées, nous intervenons dans les domaines suivants:
• de l'eau (Puits & forage...)
• de la santé (fourniture de lit, et table de soin, éclairage solaire...)
• de l'éducation (construction de la bibliothèque et de la cantine, fournitures scolaires, denrées pour la cantine, équipement pour le sport...)
• de l'agriculture (materiel agricole, aide pour la culture du niebé, projet maraîchage...)
• de l'économie (micro-crédit)
 
Siège et téléphone :
4, allée des fougères
08090 Montcy-Notre-Dame
03.24.59.45.39 / 06.84.20.24.18

Name: Honorvol Community Service Organization
Description: We facilitate involvement in the Bloomington community for IU students via coordinated volunteer events and mini service projects. Our goal is to make volunteering easy and accessible for everyone and introduce opportunities for service to more people. While we are sponsored by Hutton Honors College, anyone at IU can be a member.

Name: Ten Eighteen Uganda
Description:
Our Mission:

Today’s Ten Eighteen is focused on three critical areas in Uganda:

* Food for at-risk children in Mbarara, Bukaleba, and Rwakobo Village.

* Vocational training, intervention, and assistance for young women and mothers in Uganda’s largest slum, Namuwongo.

*  Assistance to schools who have a significant population of non-paying  students: Arise Africa Primary and Secondary Schools (Bukaleba),  Hopeland School (Mbarara), and Wells of Hope School (Rwakobo Village).

Name: Slime Mom
Description:
Worker owned and governed cooperative, supplying freelance contracts, benefits, and resume-enhancing opportunities for experiencers of transmisogyny and some of their allies.

Founded by Lillian Gardenia Seabreeze. For more information about us please refer to the Slime Mom Handbook ().

To apply for membership contact an existing member of Slime Mom or simply email contact@slimemom.com.

Additional Resources:

@slimemom on Twitter
contact@slimemom.com

Name: Humble Hues
Description: Founded by visual artist Mitch Weiss, and management consultant Michael Winston, Humble Hues is a team of dedicated creative professionals who leverage art, brand strategy, and elegant design to create distinctive philanthropic projects that further awareness of our clients' causes.

Name: Drifter's Hearts of Hope
Description:
Drifter's Hearts of Hope is a 501(C)3 non-profit organization committed to circumventing the horse-slaughter pipeline.

OUR HISTORY
In 2014, Drifter's Hearts of Hope was founded to give at-risk horses a second chance at a forever home. The organization's namesake is a papered Quarter Horse: a champion roping horse who ended up as a stray after an injury. He went from being found on the side of the road to the heart horse for the organization that would become Drifter's Hearts of Hope.

HOW TO HELP
With the dedication and ongoing support of the community around us, DHOH continues to increase it's reach and impact in the horse community. We've seen many of our rescues go to wonderful loving, forever homes, all through the efforts of the members and supporters of  DHOH. Our efforts are supported solely by donations, adoption fees, sponsorships, and A LOT of volunteer sweat (we are completely volunteer run). Want to get involved and help us save horses?

Name: Sylvia's Students Foundation, Inc.
Description: Sylvia's Student Foundation was formed for the charitable purpose of assisting impoverished students in Primary Schools and Early Learning Development (“ELD”) programs, in Africa, beginning with the Nkoile Primary School in Kenya. We provide learning materials such as books, supplies, uniforms and help to fund ELD teacher salaries in order to provide a more conducive learning environment for students and to reduce school fees to lessen the burden on families and increase attendance.

Name: Hear See Hope
Description: HEAR SEE HOPE is a philanthropy company based out of 826 SW 152nd St, Burien, Washington, United States.

Name: Cuisines de l'Amitié
Description:
Cuisines de l’Amitié est une ressource en sécurité alimentaire dont la mission est de briser l’isolement et de favoriser une sécurité alimentaire, entre-autre par des ateliers de cuisines collectives.

Nos services :
Cuisines collectives
Plats des aînés (préparation de repas gratuits, distribués par les intervenants du CLSC Samuel-de-Champlain)
Plats à vendre

Name: Fundación Sabios, Expertos y Genios
Description:
Somos una organización sin fines de lucro, dedicada a realizar acciones en distintos ámbitos sociales; generando ideas y proyectos para sensibilizar, movilizar, innovar e incentivar un cambio colectivo, impulsando el Diseño Universal a través de proyectos que impacten en todas las esferas sociales, centros de formación, medios de comunicación, espacios culturales, de entretenimiento, ocio y turismo. Nuestra plataforma de comunicación y oportunidades multidisciplinar promueve las capacidades de las personas sin importar su edad, condición intelectual, sensorial, física, raza, sexo, género, cultura…

Estimulamos una labor orientada por los Siete Principios del Diseño Universal, de acuerdo con las premisas del Centro de Diseño Universal “Center for Universal Design”, de la Universidad de Carolina del Norte en Estados Unidos.
Location: Santo Domingo, Santo Domingo D. N.

Name: The Giving Goose
Description: We are a husband and wife team trying to bring more awareness to childhood cancers and raise funds for research to help find cures for these diseases. Our daughter was diagnosed in September of 2015 with AT/RT (a type of brain cancer and at the time she was only 15 months old), and after fighting for a year she is currently two years into remission. We had such an out pouring of support and help during our journey that we only felt it right in our hearts to find a way to give back to the childhood cancer community. We created The Giving Goose to provide you with must have products that when purchased 50% of all precedes go to non-profit childhood cancer organizations. Outside of trying to help with awareness and funding for childhood cancer research, we hope to connect with other families and individuals to give them the emotional support they need. No one should ever walk this journey alone, and we are here for you to lean on. We hope that our organization is able to have an impact in your lives, and you join us in this movement of giving back to others fighting this unfortunate battle. We want to thank you in advance for taking the time to visit our website. Please take one more minute to share our link, you never know who will see it and need the support. Thank you for helping us make a difference.

Name: FONDATION EBARI
Description: « EBAR-RIR », signifie entraide en Adioukrou, une langue du sud de la Cote d’Ivoire. C’est la principale valeur de la fondation dont les objectifs sont la distribution de donations en Cote d’Ivoire à travers des œuvres de charité, de bienfaisance, d'éducation, et d'assistance sociale.

Name: Baobab Strong
Description:
BAOBAB STRONG is a nonprofit organization whose mission is to strengthen people living in poverty by supporting quality education and fostering healthy communities.

We help improve lives by enabling access to Education, Healthcare, Improved Infrastructure, and Income Opportunities. We also perform Charitable Outreach to especially underprivileged people. Visit our website at www.BaobabStrong.org.

Name: Het Bunschoten Fonds
Description: Het is de overtuiging van het Bunschoten Fonds dat het voortleven van de klassieke muziek geen vanzelfsprekendheid is, maar ook afhangt van wat wij er met z’n allen aan aandacht, tijd en geld voor over hebben. Het is aan ons allemaal om ervoor zorgen dat de beroemde en geliefde, maar ook de minder bekende werken blijven klinken op de podia. Dat gerijpt vakmanschap wordt overgedragen op nieuwe generaties en op de ensembles en orkesten van de toekomst. Het is aan u en ons om de unieke, kwetsbare klassieke-muziekcultuur waar mogelijk te beschermen tegen de huidige bezuinigingen door de overheid. Daarmee brengen we de laatste wens van onze oprichter op passende wijze ten uitvoer.

Name: Paws 4 A Cause Inc
Description:
Paws 4 A Cause is a Non-Profit organization, based out of Columbia, Missouri. The corporation was established in July, 2017. 

Paws 4 A Cause promotes an active lifestyle, while raising funds for pet-related charities and organizations. All events will be fitness, or pet related. 
 
The organization will strive to help prevent pet euthanization by hosting adoption events. In addition, a portion of all funds collected for event tickets will be donated to local pet-related charities and organizations.  
 
People of all ages are welcome to participate in events. Fitness lovers and animal lovers will be the target audience of the organization. Anyone who supports pet related causes, or enjoys fitness related events will be welcome to participate. 

Paws 4 A Cause Inc was created to help animals in need. Events will bring the community together by inspiring participants to give back while promoting an active lifestyle.

Name: BOX - timetobox.org
Description:
BOX is a not for profit group, founded by women, dedicated to building the connections that support the women and girls of our communities. We are focused on three critical areas: supplying safe and easy access to menstrual supplies for our local women and girls who cannot afford them; creating awareness around the political and social issues that continue to surround women's menstrual health; while destigmatizing the conversation around periods and driving lasting change by tackling the big issues at the legislative level.

We believe that all women and girls should have access to the menstrual supplies they need.

We believe that we should be embarrassed about the pink tax and inequality - not embarrassed to talk about periods.

And we believe your period should not be taxed as a luxury.

Name: Math in Motion... Girls in Gear
Description: Math in Motion … Girls in Gear! (mimgig) is an annual event that engages 100 Grade 9 girls from across the GTA, to participate in a full-day of hands-on activities and interactive lectures lead by female role models in Math, Science and Engineering. mimgig is an outreach event of the department of Computer Science & Mathematics at the University of Toronto Scarborough.

Name: ongekobebrasil
Description:
A Ekobé Brasil é uma organização nacional conservacionista dos recursos naturais, de iniciativa particular, de caráter científico, cultural e filantrópico. Tem sede e foro na cidade de São Mateus, Espírito Santo.
A Ekobé tem como objetivo social o apoio ao desenvolvimento sustentável e a conservação do meio ambiente. Para tal, a ONG desenvolve projetos, estudos e pesquisas que visem a conservação e preservação do patrimônio natural, incentiva atividades que promovam a preservação do ecossistema além de firmar acordos com empresas e organizações para realização de ações com iniciativas condizentes aos princípios da ONG.

Name: Financial Services Coalition of Puget Sound Charitable Foundation
CREATIVATORY
Description: CREATIVATORY is a public charity under section 501 (c) (3) of the internal revenue code, involved in donations of creative tools to children in economically underserved areas.

Name: Cultural Passage
Description:
Our Mission
To promote global understanding, inspire minds, and bridge gaps of inequality through cultural exchange and by fostering sustainable solutions for development goals.

Our Goals
To provide safe experiences that alter perspectives and lead to personal growth.
To influence and shift the conceptual foundation that guides our view of humanity.

What We're About
Cultural Passage is committed to sustainable development. Our volunteer and internship partner projects are selected based on their potential to further Sustainable Development Goals as outlined in the United Nations 2030 agenda. We employ best practices that ensure our volunteer contributions and interactions are conducted in such a way as to empower communities rather than creating dependency.

Name: DUO Magazine
Description:
DUO Magazine is more than just a magazine; it's a meeting place for men and women to come together to share their similarities and embrace their differences. It's a heartfelt journey through the endeavors of the charitable organizations, foundations and the Unsung Heroes who are changing lives throughout South Florida. DUO celebrates the achievements of those wh give back without looking back, the people who love to live and live to love!!

We are looking for Unsung Heroes: tell us their stories.
Come advertise with a magazine that truly wishes to make a difference - do your part as well!!

Name: Aileen S Andrew Foundation
Description: Aileen S Andrew Foundation is a philanthropy company based out of 10701 Winterset Dr, Orland Park, Illinois, United States.

Name: Stichting Sarfath
Description:
Sarfath helps people and churches in Eastern Europe, especially in Romany. Sarfath is focussed on the Hungarian Reformed Church and helps on different ways:
- a preacher-adoption programme to make it possible to keep preachers in small villages that are too small to pay a preacher
- food packages for churches in smaller villages
- material help, f.e. computers, furniture, for churches, schools and theology students
- new garment, used to distribute to poorer people in the villages where Sarfath is active, and partly used in small stores of which the revenues are used for the poor people in those villages
- used clothes, used the same way as new garment

Name: Regional Youth Roundtable
Description:
The Regional Youth Roundtable (RYR) is a non-profit organization funded by the Ontario Trillium Foundation, and supported by ACCESS.

The goal of the Roundtable is to alleviate pressures facing minority youth groups (i.e. newcomer youth, special needs youth, racialized youth) by creating a collaborative impact through prominent youth-led organizations, currently beginning with the Peel region.

The Roundtable also aims to act as a platform for the sharing of ideas and initiatives between youth-led organizations, ensuring said organizations are reaching their full potential and mandate.

Name: Tukikauppa.fi
Description: Tukikauppa.fi tarjoaa helpon tavan tukea valittuja Meripelastusseuran yhdistyksiä. Ostamalla kaupasta tuotteita voit valita mitä yhdistystä haluat tukea.

Name: ChariCare
Description:
Non-Profits and Charities most valuable asset is its Volunteers and Staff. Their goal is to generate and support movement behind projects in society that carry great social impact. 

The hardest part about driving social impact is having a solid and cohesive foundation behind you - prepared for every win, and every loss. We help non-profits and charities grow their organisations to support their goals through organisational structure, strategy, marketing and advertising, and conflict resolution and mediation.

With access to partners, grants, and technology all over the world, we help put organisations in the right place to make bigger impacts in our communities.

Name: Help One Saskatoon Charity
Description:
Help One has been operating quietly in Saskatoon since 2010, mostly through word of mouth.

Founded by Bruce Rempel of Rempel Brothers Construction, the charity was inspired by Care and Share Saskatoon, a similar program for elementary school students.

“Kids going into high school face quite a few financial challenges most families don't think about. A lot of the programs that provide support for kids either aren't available or are insufficient.” Rempel said.

In short, Help One fills the gap. Whether it be basic human needs such as hygiene products and clothing, or fees associated with athletics, arts and other school-related activities, including graduation.

Help One has assisted more than 3,000 students, gifting them over $500,000 and counting.

In the news: 
https://thestarphoenix.com/life/bridges/bridges-cover-bruce-rempels-underground-charity-hits-the-mainstream
https://thestarphoenix.com/news/local-news/9999-news-help-one

Name: Global Initiative to Advance Entrepreneurship
Description:
Global Initiative to Advance Entrepreneurship (GIVE) is a nonprofit 501(c)(3) corporation founded by Steven Van Yoder of Get Slightly Famous that is dedicated to addressing social challenges in the US and abroad. 

GIVE works to reduce global poverty by using a unique multi-faceted approach to advance entrepreneurship among individuals and NGOs in struggling countries.

GIVE's members and project partners are drawn from the small business, corporate and nonprofit sectors. We forge global alliances with individuals, businesses and organizations that have a stake in targeted communities.

Our overarching mission is to produce job creators rather than job seekers through entrepreneurial education and small business development projects. 

GIVE achieves its mission by forging complementary relationships between the for-profit and nonprofit sectors, and by linking proven models and resources to alleviate global poverty.

Name: International Piano Competition of Lyon
Description:
In 10 years, the International Piano Competition of Lyon has attracted about 600 competitors from 35 countries. Every year, 50 talented young pianists compete over a seven day tournament in front of a Jury made of renowned international music professionals. Some 800 people attended the 2017 Final. This non-profit organization is almost entirely financed by private sponsorship and philanthropy.

On the banks of the Rhône River, the City of Lyon is one of the largest and oldest cities of France, with a rich cultural and economic life.

President: Vincent Grimaldi de Puget
Founder & Artistic Director: Svetlana Eganian

Name: the nature network
Description: the nature network is a philanthropy company based out of 5 Dutendorfer Stra??e, Vestenbergsgreuth, Bavaria, Germany.

Name: Sacred Valley Initiative
Description: Sacred Valley Initiative will support and strengthen the grass roots operations of non-governmental organizations (NGO) in the Sacred Valley region of Peru in the Andes Mountains. This will enable the NGO’s to help the communities flourish while preserving one of the largest indigenous communities and cultures of the world.

Name: Fortress Foundation
Description:
A Refuge in Time of Need. 
The Fortress Foundation is founded on Biblical principles in Matthew 25, that calls each of us to help those in need. We are a non-profit 501c(3) Private Foundation that supports other charities, and organizations that are aligned with our mission, and have a track record of doing so. To learn more contact us, or go to our website.

Name: Crafting For Change - SONU
Description:
Crafting for Change est un projet porté par le Pôle Développement de l'association Sorbonne Pour l'Organisation des Nations Unies ( SONU ) visant la promotion de l'artisanat à travers l'adoption du design en tant que vecteur de développement d'activités productives.  

Pour ce faire, trois ateliers coordonnés liant étudiants en design et artisans en difficulté seront organisés en France et en Argentine, dans un esprit de partage mutuel de connaissances techniques, et de mise en avant de l’importance de l’intégration sociale des artisans en question. 

Les résultats et la documentation de ces trois ateliers, feront ensuite l’objet d’une exposition qui sera organisée à Paris puis à Buenos Aires, permettant ainsi l’augmentation de la visibilité des problèmes auxquels sont confrontés ces communautés d’artisans, et la création d’éventuels débouchés à leurs créations.

Name: Luce in LA
Description: Offer a wide range of services as a nonprofit consultant. Serves include board strategic planning, capacity building, communications, branding, development, fundraising, digital communications and social media.

Name: ELYPSIUM Foundation News
Description: Noticias sobre asuntos sociales y proyectos humanitarios en el Principado de Andorra, Europa y Centroamérica.

Name: Today's Verse
Description: Daily shot of inspiration

Name: HumanumRaid
Description: HumanumRaid est une association solidaire, humanitaire et environnementale baséé au 38 rue Jean-Louis Barrault, Angers, Pays de la Loire, France.

Name: Continental Societies, Inc. Houston Metro Chapter
Description:
Spring 2004 a core group of women, Cheryl Nelson Butler, Rhonda Arnold, Asheligh Rosette, 
Dana Hollie and Keryl Morgan met and discussed the possibility of organizing a group of women to apply 
for chapter affiliation with Continental Societies, Inc.  By mid-summer, an interest group was fully 
organized and performing service projects.   On October 4, 2004, the Houston Metropolitan Chapter was 
chartered and set forth to execute the mission of the organization in the greater Houston area.  Since it’s 
chartering, the chapter has made great strides in serving children.

Name: Global Relief Products, Inc.
Description:
Global Relief Products is in the business of providing comfort to people in need because of being homeless, displaced from disasters, and third world poverty.
GRP provides unique portable beds with the partnership of the donors, sponsors and our distribution partners.
We have sent beds around the world with an ongoing project with World Vision sending beds to Africa.

Name: Save The Uglies
Description: Our Mission: Save the Uglies is a youth-led student nonprofit that is committed to raising awareness for lesser-known species and environmental issues, championing education and advocacy to address issues of biodiversity, sustainability, and environmental justice. Environmental degradation and anthropogenic climate change are the greatest threats to our survival, as the ecosystems and food webs that support us are falling apart. In response to this, we take a collaborative approach, working together with other environmental organizations statewide to fundraise and educate for conservation efforts across California.

Name: ImpactGiv
Description:
Impactgiv.com is a platform dedicated to democratizing philanthropy by connecting micro-philanthropists with purpose-driven projects. Our mission is to make philanthropy accessible to individuals from all walks of life and empower them to make a positive impact in the world.

Through Impactgiv.com, micro-philanthropists have the opportunity to support projects that align with their values and passions. By pairing them with purpose-driven initiatives, we aim to foster a sense of solidarity and encourage collective action towards creating a better world for all.

We sincerely believe in the power of individuals coming together to drive positive change. With your participation and support, we can make a meaningful difference in addressing various social and environmental challenges. Together, let's make a lasting impact and promote a more equitable and sustainable future for our planet.

Name: Damar Bakti Nusantara
Description: Empowering Youth, Empowering People

Name: Speak Up Book Club
Description:
Our mission is to encourage dialogue and action through educating parents and children on tough topics such as racism and white privilege.  We understand these issues are tough to tackle, and we want to help.  Each month we choose an engaging book your child will love,  provide new resources, actions plans, activities and crafts that help parents facilitate discussions of these hard topics.

Each subscription = 1 book donation. We aim to create in home libraries where each child can see themselves being celebrated in the books they read.

Name: CHARITABLE ORGANIZATION "Charity Fund "UHELPIS"
Description:
Благодійний фонд “UHELPIS” - це сильна професійна команда волонтерів. Це, в першу чергу, люди, які у мирному житті займаються досить популярними професіями, такі як сільське господарство, маркетинг, юриспруденція, сфера IT та багато інших. Це більше сорока героїв, які роблять усе можливе, щоб задовільнити потреби армії та прискорити нашу перемогу.

Благодійний фонд “UHELPIS”має досить широкий сектор завдань та зберiгає ключові принципи: чiткий фокус, прозорiсть та, в першу чергу, розумнi витрати.
Що до розумних витрат: ми намагаємося враховувати усi потреби бійців на передовій, поглиблюємося у військові стандарти України і НАТО та купуємо перевірене спорядження за прийнятними цінами.

Name: iFoodshare.org
Description:
I food share è cultura della solidarietà che si fonda sullo scambio solidale del bene primario, il cibo, fondamento dello sviluppo umano. Il diritto all’alimentazione è un diritto di ogni essere umano come diritto inviolabile.

I food share è condivisione, è partecipazione solidale nel settore dell’alimentazione umana.

Name: Provide Now
Description:
We create gifts-in-kind donation registries; we list items that charities need & donors can purchase listed items for those charities.

Our mission is to promote transparent donations of material items to equip nonprofits and communities with the resources necessary to thrive. 

Our vision to do more good by positively reinforcing donorship in order to help new generations build a strong and consistent relationship with philanthropy.

Name: Center For African Rights Advocacy
Description:
CARA develops a checklist that can help children, engage qualified personnel as well as experts on human rights, indigenous peoples issues, psychology, and leadership and management programs to help children and young people alike. CARA engages in collecting and distributing donated decent clothes, discreetly but in free atmospheres, to help young people get going with good careers and actually to address the issue of poverty that drives young people into prostitution and other vices. CARA encourages donation of working gadgets for qualified children, young adults and women.

CARA provides support to African immigrants in the US by providing Welcome Packages to last for the 1st 2 weeks, provides support to obtain required documentations, assists with understanding rights and helps with eliminating prejudice and discrimination etc.; advocates and promotes programs in support of children, young people and women in Africa as well as promotes and supports the recognition and funding of the work of the African Commission. CARA is devoted to amicable and peaceful resolution of all issues, peaceful existence/co-existence of migrants and host peoples and provides travel funds to eligible parties (private/public) to the Commission.

Name: TradeOrDonate.com
Description:
TradeOrDonate.com is a way to live socially, a simple way to swap an item or a service with something you need, or donate it to a club of your choice. No matter where you live or how much you have, there will always be something that you can exchange with others FOR FREE.

We are:
Achtida Magrioti, Co-Founder
Doros Antoniadis, Co-Founder
Marios Maltezis, Developer
Vagelis Vazas, Web Designer

Name: Canadian Association of Gift Planners Waterloo-Wellington
Description:
CAGP is a national association that inspires and educates the people involved in strategic charitable gift planning. We advocate for a beneficial tax and legislative environment that strengthens philanthropic giving, create a networking environment with like-minded professionals and experts, and provide access to outstanding learning opportunities and professional development.

A local chapter of the national, non-profit organization established in 1993, we are the only Canadian professional association that brings together charitable fundraisers and professional advisors. Through a commitment to a stringent Code of Ethics, continual learning and networking, and a willingness to share, our members help each other grow and develop as professionals in their respective fields.

As a local, community-based chapter, we are an essential partner in CAGP’s mission and play a vital role in building relationships and engaging members to strengthen philanthropy and strategic charitable gift planning.

Name: Alpina Charity Foundation
Description:
VISION >
We aim to serve as a platform to empower young healthcare leaders in Hong Kong to contribute to the betterment of the world’s medical development.

MISSION >
To nurture future leaders of medical humanitarian assistance and medical innovation in Hong Kong.

To broaden the international horizon of young people in Hong Kong through local and overseas healthcare volunteer services, and allow
them to further apply the skills they have acquired to philanthropic projects in Hong Kong and along the belt-and-road.

Name: Kimse Kimsesiz Olmasın !
Description: İnsanı depresyona sürükleyen en önemli olgulardan biri kendini yalnız hissetmesi değil mi? Salgın nedeniyle kendimizi daha da depresif hissetmiyor muyuz? Bizim belki sadece bir dönem hissettiğimiz bu duyguyu, bir de kimsesiz çocuklar adına bir anlık düşünmenizi rica ediyorum. Çocuk Esirgeme Yurdunda yaşayan çocukları, çocukken orada olduğunuzu düşünün neyi en çok hissederdiniz? Kimsesizliği..yüreğinize ağırlık çöker..ve bu duygu sizi hayata katılaştırır, sevgi, onaylanmama, paylaşım olmadan yaşar gidersiniz. Dünyada her şey çift olarak yaratılmıştır: kuzey-güney kutbu, yaz-kış, sıcak-soğuk gibi hatta insan bedeninde dahi her şey çifttir, iki göz, iki el, iki kulak ama bir tek kalp tektir. Bu kalbin çifti ise sadece başka bir kalp ile buluştuğu zaman tamamlanıyor. İşte tam da bu nedenle kimse kimsesiz olmasın diyorum, tek kalan kalpleri birleştirmek için. Bu hayalime ortak olmaya var mısınız ?

Name: Fund of Development of Ukraine
Description: Charitable Fund of Development of Ukraine provides professional assistance directly at the front. This is the equipment of field hospitals, also first-aid kits for soldiers and other tactical equipment.

Name: Fundación Aronowsky
Description: Fundación creada para ir en apoyo del desarrollo humano, social y medio ambiental, enmarcado en los Objetivos de Desarrollo Sostenible de la ONU

Name: Rootworks
Description: Rootworks is a non-profit organization aiming to provide access to clean drinking water in rural Somali and Ethiopian communities. Our mission is to successfully construct sustainable rainwater-harvesting technologies in East Africa through donations and sponsorships.

Name: DoMore4:Good
Description: In order to promote our mission, we work with high schools and local nonprofits to set up a program that matches students with volunteer opportunities in a fun, rewards based competition. As a result of this initiative, the participating high schools and their communities will see increased and ongoing acts of kindness and service among all people – many of whom will be inspired by the acts of the younger population. We anticipate a streamlined integration among nonprofit organizations, businesses and individuals who serve the region.

Name: Het Nieuwe Collectief
Description: Het Nieuwe Collectief realiseert woningen vóór en dóór starters in de vier grote steden. Alle (vrienden)groepen binnen HNC hebben hun eigen identiteit, die tot uiting komt in de woonomgeving die we met elkaar maken. Volwaardige woningen, je vrienden als buur, op jullie zelf gemaakte plek.
Location: Amsterdam, Den Haag, Rotterdam, Utrecht, Randstad

Name: Fondo de Ayuda de Buena Fe
Erhvervsvækstforum
Description:
Erhvervsvækstforum har 4,5 million kroner om året til at støtte projekter der skaber vækst i Ringkøbing-Skjern kommune. 

Har du en virksomhed eller er medarbejder i en virksomhed i Ringkøbing-Skjern kommune og har du nogle ideer til hvordan vi kan skabe vækst i kommunen, så kantakt os. 

Vi kan støtte projekter der er til gavn for en større åben gruppe af virksomheder. Vi kan ikke give direkte støtte til virksomheder eller personer MEN vi vil gerne i dialog med dig omkring hvilke udfordringer i har i virksomhederne. Måske kan vi finde en løsning der kan være til gavn for flere virksomheder.

Skriv en besked, så svare vi så hurtig vi kan.

Her på siden vil du kunne læse om de projekter vi giver støtte til.

Name: JAMAKIN ME SMART, Incorporated
Description:
The purpose of JAMAKIN ME SMART is to raise monetary funds and in-kind school supply donations for Jamaican students and support programs geared toward the positive self-development of youth. Although Jamaica is known to many as a luxurious tourist location, the cost of living is very high and after the primary school level of education there are no forms of public high schooling. All students are required to pay their school fees each term, which can be quite costly. The school supplies available in Jamaica are very limited compared to the United States and are very expensive. Growing up, our Founder and CEO, Adrianna Boulin was blessed with the opportunity to spend summers in Jamaica visiting her family and growing with her cousins and peers within the community. The older that she became, the more cognizant she was of the disparity that exists in Jamaica. School shopping with her cousins was not like school shopping in the states. School shopping was a much bigger task and only supplies that were absolutely necessary could be purchased, as her aunts and uncles had spent months saving for it. Compared to her school shopping where every year she would purchase in excess; getting notebooks in every color, back packs to match outfits, markers, colored pencils and crayons. She realized around age 17 that there was a big difference and that it was a difference that I wanted to change. 

With this in mind came the creation of “JAMAKIN ME SMART”, starting as a project, now a 501(c)(3) non profit organization organization, with the purpose of collecting school supplies for the ambitious students of Jamaica and encouraging the positive self- development of youth. JAMAKIN ME SMART was founded on September 14, 2008, and incorporated on August 19, 2015.

Description: The Sodgerel Foundation is a program dedicated to delivering financial aid and incentives for community growth in the Chapel Hill/Durham area of North Carolina. Founded by East Chapel Hill High School alumni Temuulen Sodgerel many of Sodgerel Foundation's programs are primarily funded by his cadet salary. The program has reinvested thousands of dollars back into the Chapel Hill-Carrboro City School System in it's pilot year alone and looks to make financial status a non-factor in childhood education.

We make other realize their dreams, this we do by making them use their God given inner potentials to the fullest. We encourage creativity and the ''Can Do'' spirit in all that come our way. Come talk to us today and watch your life make a rebound.

Description: Happy Day is a student-run non-profit offering teenage entrepreneurs the capital in the form of a micro loan to pursue their business aspirations. Happy Day entrepreneurs get involved hands-on with entrepreneurship to learn skills for entrepreneurship and life.

UWQ works with corporate, government and community businesses to address the critical issues facing QLD communities. Funds are raised through work place giving and action is taken through corporate volunteering.

Name: Lundon Enterprises LLC
Description:
Our mission statement at Lundon Enterprises is to provide a social networking community which raises awareness, financial support, and network support for multiple charitable causes.

Lundon Enterprises social network, CurePals.com launched February 9, 2011. CurePals.com offers an on-line community that provides hope and inspiration where children and families can connect, share and heal. Originally designed for children in hospitals, it has now expanded to include all children and families, as well as other non-profits, educators, and hospitals across the country and the world. 

CurePals.com provides users a platform where communication, education and entertainment coexist. It is a social network that uses the web to anonymously connect children and families who are sharing similar health challenges, and provide a voice of hope and shared inspiration for one another.

Name: Samaria Smith Foundation
Description:
The Samaria Smith Foundation was founded in 2011 by Samaria K. Smith. We are currently working to become a full-scale, non-profit organization dedicated to ensuring the quality education of Atlanta's children. Specifically, we target areas of poverty and low-income in the Atlanta Public School district.

The Samaria Smith Foundation believes that a lack of quality education for all individuals in our country is at the heart of dramatically improving some of our society’s most devastating afflictions. Poverty, violence, income/racial disparities, substance abuse, unemployment, physical/mental health challenges, and several other problems affecting our communities are all directly linked or associated with a lack of education in some form. Therefore, it is our single most important goal to offer and support strategies devoted to improving education and increasing awareness of its essential value to human life.

 The long term efforts of the foundation will be to operate as a public charity pooling the majority of its resources from a diverse base of public support and then utilizing those resources to improve and advance its mission for the community. Eventually, we hope to reach a position of financial security that enable us to fulfill our mission of providing grants for internal and external programs and initiatives dedicated to the enrichment of educational elevation for children.

Description: We are an NGO based in Luxembourg dedicated to financing projects in Haiti; fundraising events across Luxembourg are held on an annual bais. Working towards a more sustainable future for Haitian people by providing funds for educational projects, school meals and scholarships. We are focusing on children’s education, disaster relief, environmental projects and entrepreneurship in Haiti.

Name: P.A.W.S. Forward Foundation (Pets And Wounded Soldiers)
Description:
Veterans and Support Animals

Veteran homelessness and at-risk homelessness is an unacceptable reality that we face.  Many of these veterans survive because of the service animals and pets that give them comfort without judgment.  At times, a veteran can barely take care of themselves, much less their animals.  The reality is that a veteran will take care of their pets before themselves.

Pets and Wounded Soldiers (P.A.W.S.) Forward Foundation has the mission of ending veteran suicide by assisting veterans of the U.S. military to provide for themselves and the working or emotional support animals assigned to them in an effort to reduce and eliminate the financial, environmental, and mental stressors that lead to homelessness and suicide.

This is accomplished through 4 major programs:

Veterans Food Assistance
Provides groceries for the veteran with a referral for additional food support.  This program depends on monetary donations.

K9/Feline Food Assistance
Provides food service or support animal of the veteran for one month.  This program is provided through generous public donations.

Veterinary Service Grants
A VSG provides the veteran with the ability to provide basic medical care for their pet for a year.  This grant provides the following:

1.  Comprehensive Physical Examination
2.  Comprehensive Blood Panel
3.  Comprehensive Spay/Neuter Procedure
4.  Complete annual dental procedures to include extractions, cleaning, and pain management, as needed.
5  Complete set of core and non-core vaccines, as needed.
6.  Free veterinary exams during the grant period
7.  Discounted pricing for services outside of the grant scope.
* Assistance may be available to pay for additional services depending on current funding.

Veteran Referral Services
Connects veteran with wrap-around services to include benefits, housing, food, financial planning and mental health services.

Name: wunda
Description: Wunda is a social enterprise designed to deliver a modern and interactive ‘TED Talk’-style seminar to a) educate young people to the dangers of social media and the effects it can have on mental and physical health, and b) inspire them towards reducing their use of it and instead focus that time gained towards activities that will better enrich and fulfil their lives; such as realising a talent, developing skills, or growing knowledge and expertise through traditional offline pursuits such as sports clubs, shared interest groups, reading, cultural experiences, and so, so much more.

My family is from Sierra Leone and I was raised as someone that always looks out to help the next person, instead of helping myself. We have royal blood in our family and have ran the country twice. These attributes remain true in myself today, and I honestly wouldn’t want to do anything else on this Earth than to change the world on a large scale. I feel like I was put on this planet to do what I’m intending to do. The three areas we will tackle are Social Justice, Educational Outreach, and International Outreach. We have a solid team of High-Level Politicians, CEO’s, Athletes, and Actors to help us spread our message through Social Media and to support our tangible efforts on the ground.

Name: The Grant Lab, Inc.
Description:
ABOUT US:

We are passionate about grant writing to create social change. Our specialty is creating award-winning grant proposals and elevating grant development strategies for organizations that improve our world. 

We are humbled to serve all types of organizations, but we specialize in community colleges, rural development, environmental education and sustainability initiatives, and visual and performing arts.  

WHAT WE DO:

An investment in grant development strategy makes a direct impact on those you serve. Our #1 goal is to make your life easier so you can focus on your mission. 

We create custom grant development strategies and grant writing services that can significantly increase your ROI so you can achieve your goals.

Name: The George E. Archer Foundation
Description:
The George E. Archer Foundation strives to fulfill its mission to “help boys and girls learn about gardening” in our community in South Central Indiana.

There are many reasons to teach children to garden, and the list continues to grow as new research emerges from around the country. Whether it’s fighting the growing trend of childhood obesity, teaching children to make better food choices, creating a more sustainable environment or reconnecting today’s tech-obsessed youth with the nature that surrounds them, it’s time to get kids back in the dirt.

That’s where you come in, and we’re here to help.

The George E. Archer Foundation and its partners provide grants that support gardening education initiatives for K-8 youth. This might include supplies for a new school garden, curriculum to support a Junior Master Gardener program or startup funds for a children’s garden to support a food bank. The possibilities are endless.

In addition to providing grants, the Foundation will further its impact by:

-publishing program results and learnings via our website
-continuing to build partnerships that provide support and resources for anyone interested in teaching kids to garden
-growing our strong community network of gardening enthusiasts and advocates
-evaluating grantee and partner feedback to improve the services we provide and best meet the needs of the communities we serve.

Name: International Friendship Project
Description: At the International Friendship Project (IFP), we put hope on international cooperation to extend a hand during times of crisis. Currently, IFP supports countries heavily affected by Covid19 in donating medical supplies for affected countries.

In 4-year colleges, Amentorous focuses on building the professional network with the goal of improving students’ chances of entering careers without apprehensions. In community colleges, Amentorous focuses on building the student network with the goal of connecting 4-year and 2-year college students, giving the community college students a chance to intimately learn about their target academic institutions.
