We believe that being "For Profit For Others" can provide significant impact by utilizing revenue-generating assets to replace the need for donors, allowing scalable solutions and recurring revenue for social and environmental needs.

#ThePhilanthropreneurs #ForProfitForOthers #impact #socialventure #philanthropy #philanthropreneur #charitablegiving #nonprofit #for-profit

Name: Return the Salute
Forgotten Wishes Foundation
Description:
Over half of all people with disabilities report feeling lonely every day. I founded the Forgotten Wishes Foundation to change that. Inspired by my adult daughter, Lindsey, who has Developmental Disabilities and Autism, I wanted to ensure people with disabilities are remembered and celebrated, not overlooked, on birthdays, holidays, and other special occasions.
This simple act of mailing cards had a big impact and inspired our first program, the Forget-Me-Not Friends Club. In addition to birthday cards and holiday cards, we deliver handcrafted cards and letters of friendship year-round to club members in all 50 states, reminding them that they are seen, valued, and never forgotten. To meet the growing demand, we launched the Card Party Initiative, which brings together corporations, small businesses, clubs, churches, and community groups to create cards that spread joy and foster a sense of belonging across the nation.
We are seeking thousands of volunteers and partners to help us spark joy and build a more inclusive world, one card at a time. If your company or team is looking for a meaningful way to connect, I’d love to tell you more about how a simple  Card Party can be an incredible volunteer service or team-building activity.

Description:
THE SYDNEY D. HOLLAND FOUNDATION IS PRINCIPALLY COMMITTED TO RAISING AWARENESS OF AT-RISK YOUTH AND TO FUNDING SUBSTANCE ABUSE PREVENTION PROGRAMS SERVING WOMEN AND THE YOUNG.

Our core strategy is to identify, promote and share best practices among leading treatment and intervention
programs that have a measurable record of effectively harboring and helping teens and adult women as they
break habits of addiction to substance and destructive lifestyles.

The foundation was created by entrepreneur Sydney D. Holland and is led by her mother, Dr. Louise Stanger,

Name: Agir Pour Yorro - APY France
Description:
Association Française de Solidarité Internationale régie par la loi 1901, « Agir pour Yorro » finance des actions de développement du village de Yorro, au Cameroun.
L’association a pour but d’apporter une aide à des populations rurales isolées et vulnérables, en leur donnant l’accès aux soins médicaux de proximité afin d’assurer leur suivi sanitaire par la création d’un centre médico-social, en améliorant les conditions d’enseignement des enfants, en permettant un approvisionnement fiable en eau potable et en électricité, en encourageant l’essor du commerce, en organisant des ateliers partagés de confection et de bricolage. 
La finalité de ces actions est de donner à ces populations les moyens d’un développement autonome de leur village, et d’encourager les jeunes générations à rester au pays, afin de limiter les tentatives souvent tragiques de rejoindre l’Europe, l’exode rural vers les villes, la fuite des forces vives du Cameroun.

Name: Women Impact Now, Inc.
Description:
Women Impact Now (WIN) is a 501 (c)(3) non-profit consortium established to empower disadvantaged women to create dynamic change socially, physically, educationally, spiritually and financially. The word "disadvantaged" is defined as lacking the normal or usual necessities and comforts of life, as proper housing, educational opportunities, job security, adequate medical care, etc.   According to the U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics, American women remain disadvantaged in terms of pay, promotion prospects and work-life balance.  Other areas of concern include educational attainment and labor force participation, legal maternity rights and family-friendly workplaces, and presence of women in positions of power.  Women make as little as 77 cents for every dollar that men earn for equivalent work. 

WIN believes that education is the process of human enlightenment and empowerment for a better and higher quality of life.  It is the cornerstone of woman's empowerment as well, because it enables them to respond to opportunities, to challenge their traditional roles and to change their lives for the betterment of themselves, their families and the society at large.  Therefore, WIN will provide education by giving quality resources/programs which will enable women to gain the knowledge, status, positive self esteem, self confidence, necessary courage and inner strength to face every challenge in their lives.  Our programs will include identifying professional clothing, professional development opportunities, financial education, mentoring support and networking opportunities as a need for women entering or re-entering the workforce.

Name: Ignite Hampton Roads
Description:
Fast-paced, Fun, Thought-provoking, social, local, global—Ignite is all of these and more. It's a high-energy evening of 5-minute talks by people who have an idea—and the guts to get onstage and share it with their hometown crowd. Run by local volunteers who are connected through the global Ignite network, Ignite is a force for raising the collective IQ and building connections in each city. And, via streaming and archived videos of local talks, local Ignites share all that knowledge and passion with the world.

Ignite is an international grassroots effort to rejuvenate creative thought and entrepreneurial passions for local economies around the world. The organizers of Ignite Hampton Roads invite the public enjoy this unique forum as an opportunity to hear geeks, designers, technologists, thinkers, poets and artists showcase their ideas for innovation, entrepreneurship, “life-hacks” and community interest.

Name: St. Grip op de Knip (SchuldHulpMaatje) Heerde
Description:
In Nederland heeft 1 op de 5 huishoudens te maken met schulden. Uit schaamte zoeken veel mensen (te) laat hulp. Stichting Grip op de Knip Heerde, handelend onder de naam SchuldHulpMaatje, zet zich vanuit naastenliefde in om mensen met financiële problemen zo vroeg mogelijk te bereiken en zonder oordeel te helpen. Begeleiding in gedragsverandering en financiële zelfredzaamheid staat daarbij centraal. Het doel is om de toenemende schuldenproblematiek in de gemeente Heerde terug te dringen.

Grip op de Knip is in 2016 op initiatief van een aantal Heerder kerken opgericht. Er wordt nauw samen gewerkt met de landelijke stichting Schuldhulpmaatje, de Gemeente Heerde en maatschappelijke organisaties in Heerde. 

Een SchuldHulpMaatje: 
•	Zet samen met jou al je inkomsten, uitgaven, betalingsachterstanden en eventuele schulden op een rij. Daarna ga je samen aan de slag voor een goede oplossing.
•	Leert je hoe je uitgaven kunt plannen en je administratie bijhoudt.
•	Blijft je ondersteunen als je schuldhulpverlening nodig hebt.

Een SchuldHulpMaatje verhoogt de slagingskans bij schuldhulpverlening van 30% naar 70%. De hulp is gratis voor hulpvragers. 

Heeft uw bedrijf te maken met medewerkers met schulden? SchuldHulpMaatje kan helpen en zo voorkomen dat uw medewerker in de schuldsanering komt met de nodige stress en mogelijke uitval van dien. 

Heeft u zelf hulp nodig? Of wilt u meer informatie over het werk van een Maatje? Kijk dan op onze website: www.gripopdeknip.nl of stuur een e-mail aan coordinator@gripopdeknip.nl. 

Wij zoeken ook Maatjes. Soms zijn dit mensen met een financiële achtergrond, maar dit hoeft zeker niet. Je krijgt via ons een opleiding om onze cliënten te begeleiden. Het is belangrijker dat je initiatiefrijk, daadkrachtig, stabiel en in staat bent om snel en handelend op te treden. Ben jij betrouwbaar, zorgvuldig en heb je inlevingsvermogen? Dan maken we graag kennis met je. Stuur een e-mail aan coordinator@gripopdeknip.nl om een afspraak te maken.

Name: Pregnancy and Postpartum Resource Center (Coalition)
Description:
The Pregnancy and Postpartum Resource Coalition is a 501(c)3 nonprofit corporation, originally founded in 2003 by Meeka Centimano.  Our Mission is “to improve the emotional well-being of families before and after the birth of a child.”  

PPRC offers the following resources:
1. A warmline for parents who are struggling and who need non-urgent support.  Individuals can call (913.677.1300) or email and a trained volunteer will get back to you.  
2. Presentations to increase awareness with the community and healthcare professionals about Perinatal Mood and Anxiety Disorders (PMADs) and the importance of Screening.  
3. Pregnancy and postpartum support group facilitator training for individuals who want to lead a support group in their area.  The training utilizes a nine-week curriculum.  
4. Volunteer training for those who would like to be on the front lines of support via the warmline.
5. Resources for mothers and fathers via the webpage www.pprc.support (e.g. links to articles, literature, local support groups).

Name: Phumuza - Share the Load
Description:
Phumuza is a Zimbabwean based Registered Charity, set up to provide:
1. Vulnerable children with schooling assistance and other basic needs.
2. To provide psychological support to vulnerable children at Insiza District in Matabeleland South / Zimbabwe.
‘Phumuza’ meaning ‘to ease the burden’ or ‘to share the load’.
Together we give priority to health, education and reconstruction projects based on the principle of self-help. We try not to run projects but give support to local initiatives, small and large. People all over the world can now can now play role in Zimbabwe by supporting these vulnerable children to gain the skills they need to build their country.

Phumza was founded in 2003 during Suku and Peter Baileys visit to Suku’s hometown of Filabusi, Insiza district. Phumuza which translates to ‘ease the burden’ or ‘to share the load’ in English is a Private Voluntary Organization (PVO 1/2008) registered in Zimbabwe. Phumuza is based in Bulawayo however concentrates its charitable work in Filabusi in Insiza district.

Name: CVHS Small Change for Big Change
Description:
This is the official LinkedIn company page for Small Change for Big Change.

Small Change for Big Change (SCBC) is a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization run by alumni of Crescenta Valley High School in La Crescenta, California.
SCBC is dedicated to recognizing educators and education at CVHS.  We aim to do this through fundraising from alumni and the community in order to give back to the school.  CVHS, like many schools today, strives to maintain excellence in education while navigating education budget cuts.

If you are an alumnus of CVHS, please join us in giving back to a great cause.

We also invite you to recognize any educator at CV, past or present, who inspired you and made an impact on your life and successes.  

There are over 12,000 CVHS alumni.  Please pass this on to any alums you many know and help us build this network.

Go Falcons!

Name: Flawed Mom
Description:
Flawed Mom encourages moms to choose better and break through. Flawed Moms are women (mothers) who deserve a second chance for any reason.

Our mission is to raise awareness about day-to-day issues that moms face during any transformation through education, and hosting workshops where we share our personal paths and experiences of motherhood.

Name: ARCA SP
Description:
Fundado em Junho de 2019, o A.R.C.A. São Paulo promove o controle de natalidade de cães e gatos na cidade de São Paulo. Resgatamos, castramos e promovemos adoções responsáveis para cada animal resgatado pelo projeto.

No últimos 2 anos, resgatamos e doamos mais de 150 animais, vacinamos mais de 1000 cães e gatos e arrecadamos cerca de 5 toneladas de ração para ajudar na alimentação de abrigos e protetores independentes. Tudo feito de maneira bem clara, afinal não visamos NENHUM lucro financeiro.

Name: Bald Beauties Project
Description:
The Bald Beauties Project was conceived and created by Kelsey Taylor Luria during her treatment for Acute Myeloid Leukemia. Although Kelsey lost her battle with cancer, her spirit, legacy and desire to positively impact the lives of children with cancer lives on through the Bald Beauties Project.

Our Mission is to empowering & impacting the lives of children & young adults with cancer.  Currently the Bald Beauties Project primarily focuses on three areas:

Beauty & Self Confidence · The Bald Beauties Photo Project will empower children and teen cancer patients who lose their hair through chemotherapy by providing them a professional photo shoot to help them gain courage, strength and confidence while also enabling them to feel beautiful even without their hair.

Kindness · Teenagers who have been diagnosed with cancer have multiple needs and many are quite different that those of non-teenage patients. While young children are often offered a variety of toys, stuffed animals and gifts during their hospitalization, there are few age appropriate items for teens. With this in mind, the Bald Beauties Project will provide teens with a Welcome Kit that provides comforting and useful items to ease the burden of being hospitalized.

Research · In partnership with the Children’s Oncology Group Foundation, the Bald Beauties Project will support ongoing research of Acute Myeloid Leukemia to help “kick cancer’s ass” altogether, so no one else has to fight this insidious disease.

Name: Steve Glidden Foundation
Description:
All-volunteer charitable foundation dedicated to providing summer camp scholarships for children who have experienced violence in their homes or communities, are homeless or at risk for homelessness, or are refugees.

Created in memory of Steve Glidden, a wonderful 12-year old boy who was killed in a bus crash while participating in a school event on April 27, 2001.  May his memory be a blessing forever.

Name: HESICH AFRICA SOCIETY
Description: We advocate for and implement sustainable education interventions for the underprivieged African child

Name: Better Future Foundation
Description:
Better Future Foundation is a registered non-profit organization committed to the cause of positive social change in Pakistan. Established in 2017, the organization aims to empower Pakistani Youth through sports in order to improve the conditions and image of Pakistani society.  

It is an organization formed by a group of individuals who have the desire and required experience to achieve sustainable development through football in Pakistan. The members at management and board positions have experience of playing, coaching, managing and officiating at national and international level. Members also contain experience in development sector in areas of community development, advocacy and research. 

This  Foundation has been founded on Public Participation Approach. We believe that without active involvement of community, an organization can’t achieve its vision of social development. Therefore the board members include individuals with years of service in their respective communities in Pakistan and for Pakistanis based in Europe. As Better Future is an organization for youth, former players of Street Child Football Team who represented Pakistan in several international tournaments have been given positions at Board and Managerial levels.

Despite being a newly formed organization, Better Future Foundation has strong ties with community based organizations, professional football clubs, media and international youth tournaments and academies. In future, organization aims to expand its horizons but we firmly believe that true success for any organization can only come with the support of locals. And the status of football and young footballers in Pakistan can only be uplifted by Pakistanis themselves.

Name: Namaste Networking LLC
Description: Offering consulting & coaching to individuals and businesses.

Name: All Occasions Consulting, LLC
Description:
All Occasions Consulting, LLC (AOC) was founded in 2005 specializing in special events raising funds to meet organizational goals or creating memorable donor appreciation events. Jannett Wiens has added fundraising for charitable purposes, volunteer management consulting and philanthropic mentoring to AOC's list of services.  Jannett Wiens has 12 years of successful fundraising experience in Northeast Kansas.
    
Services include:
          Registration (set up displays, check-in attendees, hand out meeting materials)
          Audio-Visual (ensure delivery and set up are as directed)
          Ensure food and beverage are ordered and presented as directed by client 
          Ensure meeting room is ordered and prepared per client instruction
          Arrange ground transportation for speakers
          Make program announcements
          Ensure entertainment accommodations and set up meet client instructions
          Collect evaluation forms and provide summaries
          Distribute accreditations, if applicable
          Re-cap summary

Description: Spare Change is a not-for-profit that works with New York restaurants, asking them to offer their guests the opportunity to round up their bills to the next dollar to benefit Food Bank For New York City. By raising approximately $4/hour, a restaurant can raise $20,000.00 in one year.

Name: Madhu Mansion Charitable Trust
Description:
Home For Children With No Home.
Madhu mansion is a trust which runs an orphanage for children in Marathahalli area of Bangalore. 
Madhu Mansion cares for orphans, nurturing their childhood with high-quality education at a recognized school and  providing a safe and secure homely environment to them.
It is run by Bro Samson.

Bro Samson provides for food, lodging and education of 30+ under privileged kids mostly on his own, supplementing with charitable donations.

You are all welcome to visit and celebrate your blessings with these kids and make some joy-filled memories.

The first unit of this orphanage is located close to Marathahalli, Whitefield, Kundalahalli, HAL or K R Puram.

Description: Our mission is to create and empower philanthropists and non-profit organizations in the age of digital philanthropy.

Name: TURN: The Ultimate Resource Network
Description:
We envision a Far-Eastside community that is transformed, God-directed and thriving spiritually, socially, and economically. In order to do this, we spend our efforts uniting in the following areas:

Social Engagement: We will participate with positive influence over this region and will bring awareness in our serving efforts to support local events; promote social media posts; and connect resources to those in need.

Economic Development: We will create wealth from which community benefits will be realized. This is more than a jobs program, it is an investment in growing the Far-Eastside of Columbus’ economy and enhancing the prosperity and quality of life of all residents.

Name: MajorGiving
Description: MajorGiving is a full service consulting firm, specializing in major giving, capital campaigns, planned giving, and strategic planning. With clients throughout the United States, MajorGiving.com works to provide highly customized strategies for all of its clients, following the best practices and proven techniques of the fundraising profession and adhering to the Code of Professional Ethics of the Association of Fundraising Professionals.

Name: Latino Business League, Inc.
Description: The Latino Business League, Inc. is a 501 (c) (3) nonprofit organization, based in Canton, Ohio, committed to promoting the advancement and development of the Latino Community of Northeast Ohio, by identifying, establishing and supporting small business opportunities as well as facilitating cultural and educational exchanges with the community at large in Summit, Stark, and Tuscarawas counties.

Name: The Doctors' Goodwill Foundation
Description:
The Doctor’s Goodwill Foundation (DGF), was established in 2003 as a result of the efforts and vision of a group of physicians at Parrish Medical Center. These dedicated doctors and medical technicians made frequent donations of their services to medical missions across the globe. Those who established DGF recognized the need to provide compassionate medical assistance for those with dire needs worldwide. Through the support and expertise of Parrish Medical Center; its medical and professional staff, the vision was formed. A local organization was established here in Central Brevard to provide emergency medical and health services for those in need, across the world or around the corner. The Doctor’s Goodwill Foundation was established as a non-profit, charitable, (pending) tax-exempt organization in December, 2003. The first medical mission of compassionate care was to Tibet.

The Doctors’ Goodwill Foundation is a 501 (c)(3) tax exempt charitable organization in the United States, which means that all donations are tax deductible.

Name: Help Change A Life Trust
Description: The objective of Help Change A Life is to help increase the income of extremely poor families, making less than a dollar a day per person, on a sustainable basis.

Name: givr
Description: Givr is revolutionizing philanthropy by streamlining the connection between donors, organizations, and businesses through an innovative, user-friendly platform. By harnessing the power of technology, alongside the influence of celebrities, athletes, and social media influencers, Givr brings unparalleled visibility to meaningful causes. We also partner with businesses to enhance their Corporate Social Responsibility (CSR) initiatives, making it easier to support impactful programs and engage employees in giving back. Our mission is to make giving more accessible, impactful, and engaging—empowering communities and organizations to drive meaningful change.

Name: DeliverAid
Description: Our team is helping feed the heroes on the NHS frontline – donate towards healthy meals delivered directly to NHS staff.

Description: Texas Tech Alpha Phi - Gamma Iota Chapter

Name: Pelos&Apelos
Description:
Formada por alunos da Administração Empresarial da ESAG da Universidade do Estado de Santa Catarina.  É um projeto supervisionado pelo professor e especialista em gerenciamento de projetos, Leandro Schmitz.
Trata-se da continuação de uma metodologia de ensino que já realizou outros 37 projetos. Nosso objetivo é ajudar a ONG Organização Bem-Animal (OBA!), responsável por tratar  os animais abandonados e vítimas de maus tratos e consequentemente à saúde pública da população.

Name: JH Walker Foundation
Description:
The JH Walker Foundation is a private family foundation and is a registered nonprofit organization in the state of Michigan under IRS code 501(c)(3).



The Foundation was formed to honour the memory of James Henry (Jimmy) Walker, who was taken from us in 2015 in a tragic automobile collision at the age of 4 months. The foundation’s goals are to support our community by promoting child safety and enrichment through 3 programs.



The first program is centered around child travel safety. The foundation provides targeted donations of high-quality safety certified infant and child car seats to hospitals and EMS services to help ensure that children involved in traumas can go home as soon as possible. Lindsay Walker, the foundation’s president, is a certified car seat safety technician and can help to install car seats or perform safety checks on your car seats to ensure they are installed properly.



The second program is our safe sleep initiative. The US National Institute of Child Health and Human Development (NICHD) at the US National Institutes of Health encourages parents to have their infants sleep on their backs (supine position) with no loose bedding to reduce the risk of Sudden Infant Death Syndrome (SIDS). The foundation helps spread this message through outreach, education, and by targeted donations of Sleep Sacks or other swaddle-style bedding to new babies via the NICU or maternity wards of local hospitals.



Our 3rd program is centered on child enrichment. The foundation believes in a well-rounded education that includes music and arts. As such, the foundation provides grants for children and young adults to further their arts education by attending music camps, purchasing instruments or supplies, and other such activities.



Your generous donations are gladly accepted and may be tax-deductible under IRS 501(c)(3).

Name: Amoziar
Description: Amoziar is a charity platform that serves those who want to donate money or equipment to students in poor and deprived regions. Amoziar adheres to the transparency principle and based on this principle the platform is designed in a way that donors can follow and trace the payments and the donated items up to the donees and get in touch to check if those are delivered indeed. Also, the payments to the Amoziar would appear on the website immediately for all crwodfunding projects.

Name: Dodo Dare
Description:
How do you make sure that today's spontaneous acts of solidarity flourish aujourd'hui and beyond the crisis ?

With DoDo Dare, we dare you to act for the good of your community !

DoDo Dare is a fun app incentivising YOU to act for the good of your community by taking on challenges with a positive impact on our society.

DoDo Dare is a fun app incentivising YOU to act for the good of your community by taking on challenges with a positive impact on our society !

Name: Ridgewood Arts Foundation
Description: The Ridgewood Arts Foundation is a 501(c)3 public charitable foundation and non-profit corporation whose mission is to provide funding to student and adult visual and performing artists and cultural organizations in northern New Jersey.

Name: TAAG (Transylvania Animal Alliance Group, Inc)
Description:
TAAG is Transylvania Animal Alliance Group, Inc, a non-profit animal rescue group.

We are a small group of dedicated individuals hoping to turn the tide on the flood of unwanted animals in Western North Carolina. 

We are a 501c3 non-profit organization and donations are tax-deductible.

Name: Tapestry of Community Offerings
Description:
TOCO operates a shop and textile pantry which has served thousands of families over the past six years out of our headquarters at 825 West Main Street in Belleville, Illinois. Through referrals from community members, schools, churches, and service agencies, TOCO Shop and Textile Pantry provide subsidized and free clothing, shoes, and housewares to children and families.  Last year alone we served over 650 families.

In December 2015, we opened a second location at 2714 Cherokee Street to aid our St. Louis neighbors. With the added location, we hope to triple our outreach in 2016 to 1500 families.   And we have plans to launch a third location in Edwardsville by Spring of 2016.

Description: Parkinson's Disease South Africa NPC (Parkinson's ZA) aims to promote a better overall quality of life for People impacted by Parkinson's Disease in South Africa through education and lifestyle management tools.

Name: Greens Ledge Light Preservation Society
Description:
Greens Ledge Light Preservation Society is a 501(c)3 non-profit organization that acquired Greens Ledge Light with the mission of saving and preserving this historic icon of maritime Connecticut.

Our mission is to save Greens Ledge Light and protect this historic landmark. 

Contact us at SaveGreensLedge@gmail.com.

Description: Our mission is to promote healthy, sustainable communities by investing in innovative educational programs; protecting and enhancing our natural environment; and providing housing and supportive programs for disadvantaged families.

Name: Asociación de la Felicidad Romy Monzón
Description:
ASOCIACIÓN DE LA FELICIDAD ROMY MONZÓN es una asociación creada para atender y apoyar a poblaciones vulnerables, que nace en honor a mi Madre Romy Monzón y su profundo amor por atender a las necesidades de los demás sin pedir nada a cambio. Ella esta en el cielo y desde allí guié mis pasos en el buen obrar de esta Asociación.

Asociación Romy Monzón recibe donaciones de las Empresas de MULTIVERSO SIG S.A.C.

Name: MacDonell Consulting
Description: Since 2001, I've helped nonprofits raise $100 million and improve their governance structures – building effective teams along the way. I also help individual and family donors find purpose and realize their visions in support of great nonprofits and their critical work.

Name: Maynor Biggers Artist Fund
Description:
The Maynor Biggers Artist Fund (MBAF), founded in 2015 by Nefertari Kirkman-Bey and Alison McNeil, MBAF is a giving circle made up of Hampton University alumni that pool resources together to invest in artists and artist companies who use their art as a vehicle for social change. MBAF recipients exemplify a true commitment to shifting the narrative, raising awareness, enhancing consciousness around societal ills in communities.

The fund was named in honor of Dorothy Maynor and John Biggers. Both were artists that attended Hampton University. Dorothy Maynor was an African-American soprano and music educator that founded the Harlem School of the Arts. Maynor was also the first African-American on the Metropolitan Opera’s Board of Directors. John Biggers was a muralist and the founding chairman of Texas Southern University’s art department. Biggers was one of the first African-Americans to receive a UNESCO fellowship to travel to West Africa to study Western African cultural traditions.

While both Maynor and Biggers have passed away, their legacy continues to impact the arts and culture field worldwide.

To date, MBAF has provided EIGHT unrestricted financial gifts to amazing  artists across the country. To donate to MBAF, or to learn more about becoming a member, please DM or connect @ www.hamptonmbaf.org.

Name: FriendlyFriday
Description:
A movement to get the world talking and a not for profit that helps provide grants and investments for young people.

Since starting Student Beans over 9 years ago, I'm often asked to speak at events and run sessions in schools, universities and businesses. The more we grow, the more I appreciate the connections and network of people behind every success story. Let me share a short story...

18 months ago I was on the tube and sitting next to me was a guy with a CV in his hand. I struck up a conversation and asked if he was looking for a job. It turned out he was, we were recruiting and to cut a slightly longer story short, Nick (yes, the guy on the tube!) came and joined us at The Beans Group. 

This got me thinking - how can I help other people connect and create more of these positive collisions? Every day there are missed opportunities with people who pass us by, during our commute, on the street, at events and even at our at our own workplaces. Often we never even know about the opportunities we’ve missed. I've had an idea that I think is going to change that. It's one of those big ideas that's got to start somewhere. FriendlyFriday was born.

Name: Three Talents
Description: We assist organizations to expand their inclusion of people with disabilities.  We help develop, and launch innovative “Bridge Academies” which develop skills and enable employment for people with disabilities in high paying knowledge workers jobs.

Name: Fight The Good Fight Foundation
Description:
Human Trafficking is a local, national and worldwide issue victims can be any age, race, gender or nationality. Modern day slavery may involve, force, fraud or use coercion in exchange for labor or commercial sex acts. Human Trafficking, especially sex trafficking, is running rampant in the United States.

Our mission is to build safe houses locally, nationally and worldwide. For rescued victims of human trafficking, we will
partner with other organizations to provide rehabilitation, medical benefits, counseling, education, and life skills training so they can transition from victim to survivor and integrate successfully back into society.

Name: Art Collision, Inc.
Description: Art Collision’s Mission is to use the Arts to Raise Funds & Awareness for Justice. Within the last seven years, we have held benefit concerts for organizations such as EMPOWER (Formerly Known As Stop Child Trafficking Now  www.endhumantrafficking.com), an organization dedicated to stopping the demand for victims who are being trafficked; NOMI NETWORK www.nominetwork.org, an organization dedicated to creating job opportunities for survivors of human trafficking; and HOPE INITIATIVES (www.hopeinitiatives.org), an international organization that works with governments to free victims of human trafficking, return them home to their loved ones, and provide the psychological counseling and training necessary for legitimate and stable income. At our benefit concerts, artists of all genres in Music & Dance come together in hopes of raising awareness to Stop sexual slavery. Proceeds from the events are given to related organizations to further support the cause to end the pandemic. Art Collision is an official 501(c)3 organization. Your donations are tax deductible to the fullest extent allowed by law.

Name: Foundation 64
Description:
Foundation 64’s primary aim is to create visibility for NGOs and charities that demonstrate an outstanding commitment in their support of human rights causes and project sustainability. This is done through the visual portfolios that Foundation 64 creates in collaboration with humanitarian photographer Aydin Matlabi which explore the diverse range of these organizations achievements in the field. 
 
The second aim of Foundation 64 is to connect NGOs with for profit organizations whose social values are similarly aligned. This twofold approach aims to increase visibility for both parties and the work they do, to ensure that their stories of hope and innovation no longer go untold. 
 
Our mandate is to provide a platform to demonstrate the power of hope with a specific focus on women and children.

Name: Ripple Theory
Description: Ripple Theory is an interactive, digital community that uses web and mobile technology to inspire positive and progressive social change. Our unique, centralized, multi-tiered system focuses on community organization and involvement, philanthropy, local business and volunteer activity to provide a simple and direct connection between the organization or business and the community member. We provide the user with a deep amount of information and feedback needed to help him or her immediately realize the effects of their actions. Whether you are a volunteer, an organization, a guerilla philanthropist, a small business owner or any of the other millions of people who want to make a difference, you belong at Ripple Theory. We Are The Action Class.

Name: Operation Project HOPE
Description:
Operation Project HOPE, Inc. is an organization committed to:
•	Creating safe, inspiring, learning environments
•	Providing leadership tools and coaching to empower children and families to manifest their dreams and build community 

Founded in 2011, our vision is that all youth have a safe and supportive learning environment where children can achieve their highest potential. With the addition of positive affirmations and playground murals to the learning environment, Operation Project HOPE is changing the world one school, one child at a time.

Name: TrePadges Inc.
Description: TrePadges is a charitable foundation dedicated to making a difference in the lives of children at risk in the heart of Virginia. Our foundation focuses on raising money and awareness for those charities in Central Virginia that help children in need. Since 2001, we have raised over $250,000 to help children fighting cancer, children who have been abused, children from low-income families, children living with diabetes and many other children who are facing challenges on a daily basis.

Name: Rodeheaver Foundation
Description:
Since our incorporation in 1995 Rodeheaver Foundation has been advocating for future generations of boys who need a caring and nurturing environment to become successful adults. Our priority is to provide financial support for Rodeheaver Boys Ranch to continue their legacy of high quality childcare in a loving and stable family setting, with open arms and necessary resources. Rodeheaver Boys Ranch, located in Palatka, FL, has been providing complete care for up to 50 disadvantaged boys since 1950. 

Children make their way to the Rodeheaver Boys Ranch because they have no home of their own due to parental death, desertion, divorce, disability or dysfunction. The Ranch, a full 'working ranch', teaches the boys responsibility and respect, and provides these young men with structure and an opportunity to build a strong foundation for their future. Our mission is to nurture children in a safe environment, providing roots for them to become thriving adults. In the words of Nelson Mandela "Safety and security don't just happen, they are the result of collective consensus and public investment. We owe our children, the most vulnerable citizens in our society, a life free of violence and fear." We know this is no longer only true in South Africa, but the story of many children here at home. 

Rodeheaver Boys Ranch focuses on at-risk and underprivileged youth. The Ranch accepts children from all over the state of Florida and neighboring states. After their stay at the Ranch, many boys grow to be accomplished members of society. There are many programs and resources available to the boys at Rodeheaver Boys Ranch, including their own Boy Scouts troop located on the Ranch, 4H and their equestrian program- the Rockin R' Horse Program. Our mission is to nurture children in a safe environment, providing roots for them to become thriving adults. It is our job to provide all the necessary resources needed at Rodeheaver Boys Ranch.

Name: Biscuits and Breadsticks, Inc.
Description:
Biscuits and Breadsticks is a 501c3 non-profit organization (EIN 85-3977674) dedicated to making the world a little safer for the most vulnerable populations in our community. While our focus is primarily on neonatal, mobility challenged and incontinent kittens, we know that the impact of education in this space will reach far beyond just fragile felines. 

We believe it is possible to take both a compassionate and strategic approach to raising kittens - which means we will always look for new ways to provide the best care possible. We understand that many veterinarians are not going to have a ton of experience with these kittens, but we are dedicated to building a network of open-minded care givers who are committed to learning along with us.

We will work not just to find these babies loving forever homes, but to help educate veterinarians, shelter staff, volunteers, and pet parents about how to provide stellar preventative care for pets with seemingly complex needs, and hopefully reduce instances of unnecessary euthanasia of mobility challenged and incontinent animals.

Name: Water and Education Int'l (WEI)
Description: The mission of Water & Education International, Inc. is to combat poverty and disease while promoting education, sustainability, wellness and nutrition by providing safe water and educational opportunities to the underprivileged of the global community.

Name: Vegas Strong
Description:
The word strong has several meanings: "possessing skills and qualities that create a likelihood of success." "powerfully affecting the mind, senses, or emotions" At Vegas Strong Inc, we understand that emotional, physical and psychological trauma is a normal part a many peoples' lives and that if untreated or addressed, can have many negative side affects. We are here to unveil the strong person inside all of us, and to support you as you address the trauma that you have experienced.

Our mission is to help people heal from the psychological effects of experiencing traumatic events through physical, nutritional, educational and emotional support programs.

Name: Astre Naissant
Description:
Les poussières cosmiques se rassemblent pour former astéroïdes, satellites, planètes, étoiles, galaxies... Tant de choses différentes et pourtant toutes fascinantes. Nos jeunes sont pareils à ses poussières qui, perdus dans l'immensité de l'espace, ont le potentiel d'éblouir le monde. Ils sont un astre naissant.

Notre rôle: Leur montrer le chemin, combattre la démotivation et le fatalisme, inspirer des passions.
Comment: En organisant des conférences dynamiques dans les établissements en présentant des profils épanouis ayant connu l'échec sous différentes formes et pour différentes raison.

Name: Pro-CEMA

Description:

 He is involved in Adhyatma and Philanthropy for more than 25years and also more than 40 years in sports.
 He is an investor for more than 25 years and an angel investor and venture partner with Venture catalysts and 9 Unicorn. He is the managing director of multiple private limited companies like SARC & ASR Venture And My Place Coworking etc. 
He is also associated with many leading National/ International org
and here through this platform, he is sharing his experience of life. 
He strongly believes in " सर्वे भवंतु सुख़िना (All be Happy)" and  वसुधेव कूटुम्बकम ( This world is family)"

Name: Blokes United
Description: We are a confidential, non-judgemental, social inclusion support group helping men in the blackburn area. We offer support through experience and football therapy. www.instagram.com/blokes_united/ Twitter.com/united_blokes

Name: Los Angeles Philanthropic Foundation
Description:
Since 1951, the Los Angeles Philanthropic Foundation has given exceptional students the opportunity to benefit from a private college education regardless of their financial circumstances.
          Today, that commitment continues as we award scholarships to students who have demonstrated consistent academic achievement, personal initiative, and a heart for community service: qualities embodied in the American character and reflective of LAPF’s founding principles.

Name: Secretly Giving Back

Secretly Giving Backs is dedicated to:
Children & Youth Development
Women & Minority Empowerment

Housing & Community Improvement

Name: Native Youth Foundation, Inc.
Description: Promoting the physical, mental and spiritual health of Native and Indigenous children through sports and education.

Name: Jay-B Jallah Live For Love Foundation
Description: Jay-B Jallah was an aspiring psychologist. He had a vision to help impoverished youths within his community, by becoming a school guidance counselor at the very high school he attended, Penn Wood High School. Jay-B spent all of his time bringing joy and laughter to all that he came in contact with, and never turned down an opportunity to help someone through any complications they were facing. He loved playing tennis, traveling to explore the world, hosting events that brought people together, and spending time with family. Unfortunately, Jay-B was born and battled with Lupus, a terminal illness that attacks the immune system. The disease progressively overcame him and kept him in the hospital most months throughout a single year. Those extended periods in the hospital amounted to a staggering amount of personal and medical debt that put a heavy strain on him and his family. Ultimately, he succumbed to his illness and passed on August 10th, 2020. Moved by his endearing strong fighting spirit to live every moment for the things and people that he loved, a group of his loved ones impacted by his loving soul, created the Jay-B Jallah Live for Love Foundation.

Name: BE-COME
Description:
‘BE-COME’ is een stichting die zich richt op het zelfredzaam maken van lokale initiatieven in ontwikkelingslanden, gericht op de mensontwikkeling van wezen, straatkinderen en tienermoeders.

Betekenis hebben, daar draait het voor ons om. We zijn altijd bezig met het samenbrengen van betrokken mensen en organisaties om samen betekenis te hebben voor elkaar en voor degene die het zoveel meer nodig hebben.

Name: Sikh Exercise and Welfare Association
Description: The primary goal of SEWA is to provide access to resources for disadvantaged and impoverished persons whilst continually building stronger communities. One of SEWA’s anti-poverty projects aims to provide homeless persons in Toronto with clothes and food. SEWA also runs an after-school basketball program to provide underprivileged youth in the GTA with access to indoor basketball courts for recreational use. The goal of SEWA’s after-school basketball program is to promote healthy lifestyles for children in families with limited financial resources.

Name: Projeto Raiz
Description:
Desde sua fundação, em 22 de novembro de 1998 se constrói sobre seis princípios que acredita serem essenciais a um movimento de educação contra a desigualdade:

Da voluntariedade (100% voluntário e 100% gratuito)
Da horizontalidade (constante redução dos poderes e hierarquias internas)
Da democracia consensual (mais consenso, menos voto e menos representação)
Da autogestão (todos trabalhando por todos com o máximo de rotatividade)
Da pedagogia libertária (essencialmente de esquerda, crítica e combativa)
Da Zona Autônoma Temporal (sempre um levante ocupando o espaço público)

Portanto, não é ONG, não é instituição, não é partido. É um movimento social, e como tal, apoia integralmente e participa parcialmente de todos os movimentos sociais, coletivos e grupos quais considere legítimos na luta contra as desigualdades.

Name: Gweneviere Mann Foundation
Description:
The Gweneviere Mann Foundation continues her legacy by helping more people get scanned for brain tumor and lung cancer early so that they can thrive in life like she did.

Gweneviere was a true star born to the earth who captivated everyone she met with her beautiful voice, contagious laughter, contemplative wisdom and generous loving heart. 

In 2008, Gwen was diagnosed with a meningioma brain tumor. With the loving care of her husband, Yasir, she was able to overcome it in time and thrive. She became an accomplished writer, marathon runner and continued to blossom as a singer as chronicled by StoryCorps, PBS, NPR and more. 

10 years later, on July 22, 2018, a rare form of lung cancer (exon 20) that would take her life.  77% of people with this type of lung cancer are non-smokers.

Name: Dr. Adekunle & Mrs. Adefunke Onabadejo Foundation
Description:
Dr. Adekunle and Mrs. Adefunke Onabadejo, in their lifetime, believed that every child deserved the opportunity to be educated to reach his or her potential and become a responsible contributor to the society.

We provide scholarships based on a demonstrated financial need and academic merit to enable those who cannot afford it to get to their next level of education.

By the grace of God, this foundation will translate into reality the belief of Dr. Adekunle and Mrs. Adefunke Onabadejo.

Name: 12HELP Foundation
Description:
12HELP Foundation helpt arme kinderen in India.
Onze stichting biedt kinderen onderwijs, onderdak, eten, kleding en gezondheidszorg.

Wij geloven dat elk kind, arm of rijk, verschillende talenten heeft.
We geloven ook dat niet elk kind evenveel kans krijgt om deze talenten te ontplooien. Simpelweg , omdat de mogelijkheden en middelen daarvoor niet aanwezig zijn. Wij willen kinderen een kans geven hun talenten te ontwikkelen.

Ons doel is kinderen gebruik te laten maken van hun talenten en later als ze ouder zijn dit weer over te brengen om andere kinderen. Zo wordt de cirkel rond.  
Onze stichting wil het fundament zijn van die cirkel. 

Om onze doelen te verwezelijken werft 12HELP sponsors en donateurs.

Name: The Social Impact Strategist
Description: The Social Impact Strategist aims to provide support to and promote businesses that contribute to social good while addressing social problems of the underserved through partnerships with grassroots non-profit organizations.

Name: The Mosaic Miracle
Description:
The Mosaic Miracle was founded to empower Christian females and raise up distinguished young women in the marketplace.

Since the initial launch, God has expanded the vision and the target audience of the Mosaic Miracle which now stands as a virtual rehabilitation hub acting as the bridge between healing and discipleship into purpose for Christians in the UK.  

We aim to support stand-alone ministries, events, and fellowships by providing in-depth aftercare and discipleship programmes to the newly saved, newly healed, newly delivered and those who may be struggling with mental health issues. 

For more information about who we are, and the work God is doing through us, please visit https://www.themosaicmiracle.com.

Name: Czech-American Alumni Network
Description: Czech American Alumni Network (CAAN) is a non-profit organization created to provide increased effectiveness in establishing meaningful connections between Czech and American organizations, professionals, students, and alumni of US colleges/universities. Through its activities, CAAN seeks to support the long-term development and improvement of Czech society.

Name: Color de Cambio
Description:
MISSION: Color de Cambio is a global group of Afro-descendants who seek to improve the quality of life in our communities so that they achieve sustainable autonomy; focusing its actions on five pillars: Ethno-Education, Economic Empowerment, Community Service, Communications, and Culture and Events. 

VISION: AfrosUnidos will be a leading organization of advocacy, promotion of development and autonomy of Afro-descendant communities. The work of AfrosUnidos will allow the formation of an international network of collaborators who will jointly carry out actions that will lead to the growth, strengthening and empowerment of the Afro people.

Name: Generation To Generation Seattle
Description:
The Generation To Generation Seattle movement mobilizes Adults age 50+ to improve the lives of young people in vulnerable situations, reinforcing the interdependence that exists between older and younger generations. Nationally, through Encore.org, millions of people are using their passions, skills and decades of experience to make a difference in their communities. 

Because the Greater Seattle community responded enthusiastically to this new intergenerational movement, Seattle was chosen as one of four Showcase Cities to pilot this five-year national campaign. Click here to learn more and stay in touch with Gen2Gen Seattle.

Name: A Little Something
Description: A Little Something is a 501(c)3 nonprofit organization on a mission to support children all around the world. Through our care package program, we strive to provide essential resources to special needs organizations, clinics, educational facilities, and more. Care packages are also a reminder that no matter how far away you may be, you're always going to be there for your loved ones. Every person deserves to feel special, so we aim to deliver smiles and fun through our care packages. After all, it's the little things that count!

Name: Paula Ford-Martin Memorial Fund
FAST - Female Athlete Support Team
Description: A team of female athletes at Dartmouth College who have come together to empower a younger generation.

Name: Loker Foundation
Description:
The Donald P. and Katherine B. Loker Foundation supports educational, medical, and environmental initiatives impacting current and future generations.

About Us

The Donald P. and Katherine B. Loker Foundation is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization (EIN 33-0166134) focused on advancing philanthropic causes benefiting education, medical care, and the environment. The Foundation primarily serves the communities reflecting its benefactors’ heritage, California and New England, with the goal of positively impacting current and future generations.

Our History

The Donald P. and Katherine B. Loker Foundation honors and continues the legacy of the Loker-Bogdanovich family. For over eight decades, the family’s philanthropy has supported programs designed to open new doors and serve a wide range of communities across the socioeconomic spectrum. Inspired by Donald and Katherine’s generosity, the Foundation today extends its commitment to bettering the lives of California and New England residents by supporting impactful educational, medical, and environmental causes.

How We Work

The Donald P. and Katherine B. Loker Foundation supports organizations that primarily benefit educational, medical, and environmental causes located in California and New England. The Foundation achieves its goals by allocating resources to initiatives that align with this mission. If your organization is interested in submitting a proposal, please direct your request to contact@lokerfoundation.org.

Name: Syracuse Animal Rights Organization
Description: Syracuse Animal Rights Organization (SARO) is a registered student organization at Syracuse University that works to educate the public on issues related to animals that fall victim to the farming, fashion, research, and entertainment industries. We voice our opinions in political and social forums, and do so with dignity and compassion. We support and utilize non-violent tactics in the pursuit of our goals, and embrace educational outreach.

Name: Project Brigid
Description:
We believe tourism has a lot more to offer our community than economic gain.

At our Community Cultural Center, travelers have an opportunity to learn to absorb the local culture by engaging local people, AND our neighbors have a chance to connect with people, who come from different customs & cultures from around the world!

Project Brigid offers an open environment where anyone can make food, play music, create art and more!

We host workshops focused on alternative & ecological living, gardening and home building. We also host free monthly picnics for our neighbors in Las Herrerias to unite with the travelers. 

We are the birthplace of MIX Movement, a social enterprise using our MIX Model to power an ambitious community to connect cultures in cities around the world!

Name: Instituto SOPA
Description: O Instituto Sopa (Sociedade Organizada Pão que Alimenta) Dona Odette é uma entidade sem fins lucrativos que presta auxílio a moradores de rua e famílias carentes de Mogi das Cruzes há mais de 40 anos. A instituição conta com 100 voluntários e atende cerca de três mil pessoas por mês.

Name: Miles of Scarves
Description: Miles of Scarves is a youth-led 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization that exists to improve the lives of those living with multiple sclerosis (MS). Dedicated volunteers knit scarves and other items to sell online, at craft fairs, at MS Society events, and outside local shops. Every year, Miles of Scarves donates to MS research at the Virginia Commonwealth University, sponsors a rest stop on Bike MS: Colonial Crossroads, sponsors a scholarship for a graduating high school senior who has MS or a parent with MS, and buys holiday gifts for a family affected by the disease. Miles of Scarves hosts an annual silent auction and cocktail party called the Yarn Ball to raise money for research. Since 2009, Miles of Scarves has raised over $75,000.

Name: Eyad Karkoutly Lymphoma Leukemia Research Foundation
Description: The Eyad Karkoutly Lymphoma Leukemia Research Foundation supports blood cancer research through fundraising efforts and donation contributions to Texas-based institutes that work to advance treatment options for chronic lymphocytic leukemia (CLL). In support of our mission, we host annual community fundraising events, including our West Texas kite-flying community event, Fly For a Cure.

Name: Godparents.in
Description:
Godparents.in (http://www.godparents.in) is an online platform with a mission to bring together NGOs and potential donors assisting in the nationwide effort to create opportunities for the millions of underprivileged children who otherwise do not have access to even the most basic necessities of life. 
Individuals can browse through various profiles of children and choose to financially support the education, healthcare of one or more children.

Name: Adams 14 Education Foundation
Description: Is to enhance the quality of education and provide expanded learning opportunities to foster the lifelong success of Adams 14 students.

Name: STRONG FOR VETERANS
Description:
STRONG FOR VETERANS is an all-volunteer non-profit focused on Leadership, Strength, and Community. We support Veterans, Law Enforcement, and their Families by creating a platform that brings different resources together, enabling collaboration to better serve the Veteran and LEO community. 

Received the 2020 Public Service Recognition Week Award: Excellence in Community Engagement Award issued by the Oregon Federal Executive Board, October 2020.

1/1/2023 UPDATE: we have adapted following COVID and are doing things differently. We are deeply committed to our community and remain self-funded. Therefore, it may not make sense to renew the 501(c)3 going forward. Website will be updated in early 2023. www.strongforveterans.org

Name: Share Your Share
Description: Share Your Share is a nonprofit organization with a simple mission: helping people donate to charity. We provide an online platform to make donations to multiple charities from a single location. By allowing microdonations, we enable you to support your favorite nonprofit in a way that is right for you.

Name: KINDS - Kindness Initiative for a New and Different Society
Description: K.I.N.D.S. is a voluntary youth organization founded by a 16-year-old, Siya Kohli. The aim is to spread kindness through volunteerism to create a happy and sustainable society. It is operated through social media to maximize the impact of spreading kindness towards human beings, animals, the environment, and even ourselves. In a nutshell, it is an umbrella initiative for various activities involving spreading awareness via creating (visual and written) digital content as well as volunteering programs, tie-ups with local NGOs, fundraising campaigns, etc.

Name: Battle Brotherhood. International March of Veterans
The Lakota Cultural Exchange Program
Description:
Purpose

The educational and enrichment opportunities taken for granted in communities across the country are dangerously lacking for teen girls on the Pine Ridge Reservation. The Lakota Cultural Exchange is an earnest attempt to offer one girl at a time the chance to participate in the empowering Girls Rock! Chicago camp. This camp and the volunteers who tirelessly work it are entirely devoted to helping teen girls find their voice. One hundred percent of any donation to the exchange program will go directly toward the transportation, camp and hosting expenses of a Lakota girl for one week in Chicago.

For summer 2015, thanks to an incredibly generous donation from an anonymous philanthropist who covered all flight and camp expenses for a girl coming from Pine Ridge to Chicago, my focus was on building on the previous summer’s extended pilot by tweaking the exchange experience, particularly in enhancing the guest one through host support for basics like transportation to and from O’Hare for pick-up and drop-off. The plan for summer 2016, with the exchange here on a one-year hiatus, is to take the camp directly to the girls in South Dakota for a weekend modeled on the women’s camp run by Girls Rock! Chicago. To cover instrument purchase, I’m revisiting my original fundraising effort, and to facilitate some instrument donation, I’m currently working to partner with a Chicago-based NFP focused on Pine Ridge.

Name: Project Nicaragua OSU
Description: We are a 501(c)3 nonprofit that cultivates and advocates for sustainable projects in the fields of nutrition, socioeconomic development, water quality, and education. We are the Ohio State chapter of a national organization that partners with institutions and communities in both Nicaragua and The United States. Through dialogue and collaboration, the organization works to understand local challenges and create realistic solutions, challenging both undergraduate and graduate students to emerge as empowered, passionate leaders who make meaningful differences.

Name: Sangue Corinthiano Jundiaí
Description:
“Sangue Corinthiano Jundiaí” faz parte do projeto nacional idealizado e promovido pela torcida corinthiana, que usa a força e união da Fiel para conscientizar a população sobre a importância da doação de sangue. A doação voluntária é a melhor maneira de mantermos abastecidos os estoques dos hemocentros.

A campanha foi idealizada pelo torcedor Milton de Oliveira em setembro de 2008, na cidade de São Paulo. Em 30 novembro de 2009, realizamos a primeira edição em Jundiaí, sempre com a colaboração da Colsan.

Name: Twelve Days of Christmas, Inc., NY Chapter
Description:
The Twelve Days of Christmas, Inc. New York City Chapter was founded in 2010 by Kendahl Lauren Lucas, daughter of founder Gayla Gardner. Kendahl, along with six dynamic women hosted a holiday party, which sponsored three local families. Their successful “Party with a Purpose” fundraiser provided Christmas cheer to children across all five boroughs. Our holiday event has grown to host over 200 people in New York City’s most famed venues and service families from all over Manhattan. 

The Twelve Days of Christmas, Inc. celebrates twenty years as a volunteer service organization committed to keeping the spirit of the holidays for less fortunate families. Beyond the holiday season, the organization has expanded to serve our communities throughout the year by encouraging and ensuring a successful return to school to students around the country by providing uniforms, backpacks and grade appropriate school supplies. This outstanding service organization is composed entirely of volunteers currently consisting of six chapters located in Houston, TX, Oakland, CA, Washington, DC, Dallas, TX, New York, NY and Atlanta, GA. The legacy of “parties with a purpose” was founded in 1999 by Gayla Gardner and Donya Gardner of the Houston Chapter. The organization continues to grow and fulfill its mission of sharing our blessings.

The members of the Twelve Days of Christmas, Inc. are diverse distinguished volunteers who are individual achievers. They are community, business and civic leaders, mentors, activists, and role models with significant backgrounds in legal, academia, healthcare, media / marketing and public relations, government, the arts and business. They are engaged within and outside the organization to maximize their effort to enrich the lives of others. All donations, directly serve the families in need.

Name: Queen's EngiQueers
Description: EngiQueers is dedicated to cultivating a positive, accepting, inclusive, and accessible environment for all students by running events and initiatives for LGBTQ+ students and their Allies in Engineering at Queen's University.

Name: Y Service Clubs International (HQ in Geneva)
Description:
The International Association of Y's Men's Clubs (South Pacific Area) is a group of vibrant, community-based organisations that successfully: 
 - Support the programs and aims of Y's Men International and the YMCA 
 - Encourage youth activities 
 - Interact with other local service clubs and charities 
 - Promote international goodwill through support of international projects 
 - Promote personal development 
 - Encourage their members to have FUN

Name: Sister Girl Presents Inc.
Description:
Sister Girl Presents Inc. is a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization dedicated to meeting the needs of the orphaned and needy in Liberia.  Sister Girl assists viable projects to help orphans thrive and develop to become successful citizens, taking their place in society as productive and contributing members.

Sister Girl Presents awards grants to qualifying organizations/projects that provide food, clothing, education, medical assistance and church mission activities to orphans.
 
Sister Girl promotes successful and viable programs to help orphans thrive and develop to be successful citizens, taking their place in society as productive and contributing members.

Name: Pretty Gyrlz Pray Worldwide
Description: We are here to equip young girls to live fulfilled and productive lives after trauma. We believe there is purpose after trauma we assist young girls from the age of 7-17 offering services such as counseling, mentorship, life skills, traveling, and career readiness.

Name: Carers of Africa Inc.
Description:
Carers of Africa (CoA) is a Not-for-Profit organisation committed to support disadvantaged people of culturally and linguistically diverse (CALD) backgrounds both locally and internationally.  We strive to empower individuals in achieving optimal health outcomes.
Carers of Africa works to improve access to health services for disadvantaged persons from diverse backgrounds experiencing health issues, family and domestic violence and unemployment. Services offered include counselling, art therapy, mentoring, legal and other referrals, and individualised personal and professional development programs.

Name: Angel Mamas Inc.
Description: angel mamas is a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization created for moms. We host and support a variety of charitable events across the Valley to utilize the natural energy and giving spirit of moms. We are fundraising, volunteering, and raising awareness in the Valley.

Name: Projeto Ajuda Pantanal
Description: O bioma pantaneiro, maior planície alagada do mundo, enfrentou a pior seca dos últimos 47 anos em 2020. Mais de 26% do bioma foi consumido pelas chamas, milhões de animais morreram, vítimas do despreparo e das mudanças climáticas. Por isso, o movimento Fridays For Future em conjunto com o SOS Pantanal criou a campanha Ajuda Pantanal.
Location: Campo Grande, Mato Grosso do Sul

Name: Abuntoo
Description: Abuntoo is a booster rocket for the non-profit community's online fundraising efforts.  Our Philanthropy-as-a-Service platform serves both the private and non-profit sectors in creating localized networks of abundance.  Abuntoo is a public benefit corporation and a social enterprise with a big heart!

Name: VA-YA
Description:
Mission: To bring others' ideas for service to light.

With VA-YA, I provide thoughtful, collaborative and compassionate guidance to everyone with whom I work. Whether it's an individual or an organization, my focus is on your success -- and on the message you want shared being delivered your way.

Name: Bright Future Foundation
Description:
Bright Future Foundation 501c3 is dedicated to educate, uplift and inspire humanity through wellness solutions and healing to make the world a better place for all. Children are the Future is one of our initiatives dedicated to solving the children's mental health crisis and teacher burnout crisis in schools in America by 2030. 

We provide higher education, CEUs and certification programs to school teachers to teach them Harvard research backed skills based from yoga, meditation, emotional intelligence and healthy life skills to school teachers so they may teach these fundamental life skills to the children in their classrooms. 

WIth 3.6 million teachers and over 50 million school children in America, and statistically over 53% of them reporting being so burned out they want to quit, we are dedicated to solve this crisis in alignment with the United Nations UNSDG goals, by 2030 with our priority on education and health.

Your support is gratefully welcomed, as we aim to raise the funds necessary to provide scholarships to teachers and schools in lower socio economic school districts where they need it the most. We will also be building healing retreat centers in America and world wide, and your donations will provide scholarships for teachers and children to come to healing retreats to get the deeper healing necessary to restore their health and well being, over come PTSD, depression, anxiety, and / or suicidal considerations. This is an urgent matter and deserves the full priority focus. If you have a heart for children, this is an urgent cause you can feel 100% confident you are making a difference and leaving a positive legacy for the future generations to benefit from your contribution. 

Find out how you can make a 100% tax deductible donation or legacy contribution to the Children are the Future Endowment fund to provide scholarships to help those who need it most, to create a brighter future for humanity. 

https://www.childfuture.org

Name: Dream Big Baby, Inc.
Description:
DREAM BIG BABY, INC. (DBB) is a full-service nonprofit operations firm specializing in fundraising, program development & events management. Dream Big Baby Inc. provides personalized philanthropic advisory services to professional athletes and entertainers, high net worth individuals, existing nonprofit organizations, corporations and others who are truly passionate about serving as catalysts for social change.

Our mission is to convert possibility into reality and understanding into results through collaboration, innovation and action. Whether your goal is to create a nonprofit organization, support your favorite nonprofit organization grow its mission, or become a seasoned philanthropist, we help you strategize the approach and execute the vision.

Name: ACHEMA
Description:
ACHEMA soutien plusieurs actions caritatives en Mauritanie listés brièvement ci-dessous

L’ACTION CHEVRES 
Selon le principe du microcrédit, des femmes seules avec enfants reçoivent en prêt une chèvre et son chevreau, et une certaine quantité de fourrage.

LE CENTRE DE SOUTIEN ALIMENTAIRE 
Une cantine offre à 100 enfants deux repas par jour, six jours par semaine, toute l’année. Budget annuel d’une cantine : environ 15.000 CHF.

COUSCOUS ARTISANAL
Grâce à une aide au démarrage et à la gestion, des femmes gagnent leur autonomie financière en montant un petit commerce.

L’ALPHABETISATION 
Cette action vise à apporter un soutien scolaire aux enfants de château d’eau pour leur permettre d’atteindre le certificat d’étude à l’école public.

L’ASSOCIATION AMSAE
Créée par nos partenaires mauritaniens dans le but de prendre en charge progressivement des actions et devenir autonome. ACHEMA soutient AMSAE dans ses activités de gestions.

Name: The Family Village
Description: THE FOSTER VILLAGE is a Non-Profit focused on providing support for our local foster children. We currently operate as a donation center located in Walker, Louisiana. TFV is stocked with items used to equip children that have been through traumatic events of loss. All services offered to these children are free of charge. The children we assist fall under multiple categories which include foster care, kinship, family services, etc. It is our practice to verify placements &/or receive referrals from a staff member on the case. By providing clothing, hygiene items, bedding, etc. we hope to make their transition phase as smooth as possible. Our mission is to make sure every child in need is clothed with dignity & given something of their own since most have lost everything. We want to help pave the road to a better life by providing gently used/new items, as well as, host events where they can find support & encouragement.

Name: US STEM Foundation
Description: The US STEM Foundation was established to orchestrate a partnership connecting Science, Technology, Engineering, and Mathematics businesses and professionals with students to make a real difference in the lives of our youth and the future of our community. Together, our current mentors and volunteers are striving to make a difference and inspire the next generation of STEM professionals.

Name: Zazasoa
Description: Association humanitaire basée sur Le Havre visant à favoriser l'éducation des enfants malgaches
