Name: Foundation for Catholic Values
Description: To raise the awareness, educate, and promote all aspects of living in accord with Catholic values through various forms of communication, lectures, online webinars, seminars, events, exhibits, and any and all other appropriate means.

Name: New Dream of Cambodian Children Organisation
Description:
New Dream of Cambodian Children Organisation (NDCCO) is non-profit organisation located in Phneachey Village, in the outskirts of Siem Reap, Cambodia.
We provide free education, school meals and community support to underprivileged children and families. 
NDCCO was founded in 2014 and was registered as an official NGO in 2018 with the government of Cambodia under number 2197.

Name: More Than An Addict
Description:
Who are we? We are your: father, mother, brother, sister, coworker, friend. We are bankers, doctors, lawyers, salesmen, saleswomen, waiters, waitresses, entrepreneurs, stylists, athletes, scientists, tech gurus, chefs, musicians, and much more. We are ending addiction stigma, because addicts recover, and when they do, they become powerful, vital, and meaningful forces in society. 

We are ending addiction stigma by promoting education and entrepreneurship. By providing financial support of grants, scholarships, microloans, and venture capital, we are helping individuals in long-term recovery from addiction step into their new identity as more than an addict...as a valued member of society with much to offer. We are starting a movement. 

What is stigma?
Stigma is the mark of disgrace associated with a particular circumstance, quality, or person. Stigma impacts millions of individuals in long-term recovery from addiction. This stigma can serve as either a negative deterrent to asking for help for addiction treatment, or can serve as an immense barrier once an addict has reached recovery. 

Our commitment
More Than An Addict is committed to eradicating addiction stigma in a unique way. We want to educate and empower individuals in long-term recovery by providing financial support through scholarships, grants, microloans, and venture capital funding. Through promoting education and entrepreneurship, we are giving individuals meaningful ways to enhance recovery, achieve their goals, and spread the message of "More Than An Addict." 

We are currently raising funds so we can open applications to begin providing financial support in 2016.
To support our work financially, please contact info@morethananaddict.org

Name: Noon Philanthropy
Description:
Noon Philanthropy is an Israel-based mulit-family office representing a select group of donors from around the world, managing their philanthropic endeavours in Israel and taking pride in our personal, committed and discreet relationships with our clients.

Our mission is to help our clients streamline their philanthropic affairs in Israel. We provide data and do due diligence on the nonprofits, and we create insight so philanthropic funders can better define, assess, and improve their effectiveness – and, as a result, their intended impact.

Name: Maine East Rotary Interact
Description: Rotary Interact is the largest, most prestigious, philanthropic club at Maine Township High School East

Name: Clothes4ALL
Description:
Clothes4All 

My dear friends in the business community, my name is Karam Ouadahi a French Expat, and as I am relatively new to Malaysia, I wanted to do, something to really help the local community, so they benefit from me being here as much as I enjoy the country myself. 

We appreciate from looking in our own wardrobes that there must be a massive amount of clothes in the World that people just don’t wear anymore. It may because the clothes are too big or too small, or even just not in style anymore. What we want to do is to redistribute these unused clothes to people throughout Malaysia to those who we know would really appreciate a helping hand. 
Working in co-operation with some very special NGO’S we have come up with a great way for you and all your colleagues to get involved with our project and support this worthwhile cause. 

The way Clothes4All works is very simple:

*	You agree to support our cause by agreeing for us to place a clothes collection unit in your office at least once a year. 
*	We deliver the unit to you, ( Rough Size: 1.5m x 1.5m) 
*	The collection competition runs for 1 month, and then we come back and collect it from you and total up all the clothes collected from your workplace. 
*	We will be starting the collection from 1st September 2017. 
*	We will try to engage with all your employee’s via our social media page  and look to encourage positive action and 
*	We will arrange an end of year celebration event for all the companies who take part, in order to meet each other and for us to give our some special prizes to those taking part. 

We will send our more detailed information in the following weeks, but at this stage we appreciate you confirming you participation.



Keep calm & happy hunting!

-- 
Clothes4ALL TEAM
Let's get naked for the good cause !
Mobile: +60 173882556 - @: clothes4ALL.my@gmail.com

[https://www.facebook.com/Clothes4ALL-1217654495013059/]

Name: 中国志愿服务网
Description:
中国志愿服务网作为共青团信息化支持平台、志愿者活动信息发布和展示平台、注册志愿者 开展互动、自我展示的网络平台在中国志愿者工作及宣传上发挥了重要作用。经过网站专业运营团队的共同努力，目前已成长为中国最大的志愿者公益门户。
中国志愿服务网曾在北京2008年奥运会中承担“志愿中国 人文奥运”主题活动与京外省（区、市）赛会志愿者招募工作宣传、信息发布的重要任务。网站全程报道了奥运志愿者的服务风采，获得了北京奥组委授予的北京奥运会、残奥会志愿者工作突出贡献奖。为网站迈向公益新闻门户的战略目标打定了坚实基础。
中国志愿服务网信息管理系统已经开发完毕并在全国各地逐步授权使用，注册系统功能包括志愿者在线注册、志愿服务信息发布平台、需求机构信息接收、志愿者机构人员管理，实现多角色一站式的现代管理体系。消息中心实现站内短消息、邮件群发、定向手机短信等手段的交流方式。依托共青团中央志愿者工作部及中国青年志愿者协会的政府资源，网站在公益慈善事业上将逐步涉入公益活动策划，指导中国行业龙头企业开展有影响力、有品牌价值的公益慈善活动。联合全国官方的慈善基金会、志愿者协会、NGO组织以及行业媒体一道为中国志愿者事业发展做好排头兵。

Name: Leading Through Reading
Description:
We believe education is a human right. Yet, many individuals are hindered by illiteracy and inaccessible education. The mission of Leading Through Reading is to join in hand and hope with communities in developing countries and impoverished areas to combat these issues by building and supporting libraries and educational centers. A community library is more than a house of books. It can be a center built to serve the needs of the community in education, health, and family life. It can be a partner for teachers and a support network for students, both young and old. It can be a common ground for a diverse population, and it can be a safe space where dreams are nurtured and goals are realized.


Throughout our work, we seek to achieve the following goals:

1. Combat illiteracy and inaccessible education
2. Provide supportive learning materials for teachers and students
3. Create a resource-rich network for learners of all subjects and ages
4. Build a safe space that encourages individuals to come together to build skills for their future

Name: Philanthropy AI
Description:
We are leveraging machine learning to build a fully automated platform for more effective investments in people. Our micro-scholarships are cash awards or vouchers designed to help students meet the everyday challenges associated with attending school. 

By reducing the everyday economic challenges associated with attending college, our micro-scholarships enable students to focus on their education and growth.

Name: Sportula Microgrants Tasmania
Description:
Sportula Microgrants' mission is to see Medical Students at the University of Tasmania thrive and graduate as healthy Junior Doctors who can in turn support the Medical Students of the future. We provide microgrants for students who need it, and we make it easy.

We hope you’ll support us in making our local medicine faculty one where everyone feels supported, healthy and welcome, whether by spreading the word or by making a donation.

Name: Tirésias
Description: Tirésias est une application collaborative solidaire qui a pour but d’améliorer le quotidien des personnes en situation de handicap, notamment les personnes non voyantes et mal voyantes. L'un de nos membres ayant été bénévole au sein de l'association Auxiliaires des Aveugles, c'est son expérience qui nous a donné envie d'approfondir notre intérêt pour ce sujet. L’objectif de l'application est de mobiliser chacun d’entre nous afin de recenser tous les dangers potentiels présents sur la chaussée (encombrants, travaux, verglas ...), mais aussi tous les lieux propices à l’accueil des personnes en situation de handicap. L’application permet aussi de déclencher un appel d'urgence qui mobilisera un bénévole à proximité afin qu'il puisse intervenir au plus vite. L’application a pour but de créer une réelle communauté. Des vidéos et tutoriels y seront disponibles afin de sensibiliser tout un chacun et les bénévoles pourront se porter candidat afin de remplir des missions solidaires.

Name: The Lab Foundation
Description:
The Lab Foundation is a fresh thinking, Community Interest Company (CIC) that gives space back. We help communities become conscious of their surroundings and collaborate to empower change. Creating projects that are sustainable we enhance people's lives based on their needs.

The best relationships begin with a hello!

Whether you have a brilliant idea, want to collaborate with us or just have a chat, we're always interested in meeting new people.

Name: Benefit Arts
Description:
Benefit Arts is your primary source for information about the important progressive causes that artists and musicians are working to support.

Benefit Arts gathers the latest news in this unique space so that you can stay up to date on the issues that your favorite artists want you to know about and support. We also regularly feature up-and-coming activists so that you can learn about new artists and the grassroots causes they support.

Description: NORTH ST. LOUIS COUNTY HABITAT FOR HUMANITY is a philanthropy company based out of PO BOX 24, VIRGINIA, Minnesota, United States.

Name: Maddie's Mark Foundation Inc.
Bellflower Masonic Lodge No. 320
Description:
Masonry is the world’s first and largest fraternal organization, and is based on the belief that each man has a responsibility to help make the world a better place. The primary focus of Bellflower No. 320 is on the community of Bellflower, CA and is a nonprofit organization with more than 200 members and is chartered by the Grand Lodge of California which has 60,000 members statewide. 

Similar to the mission of the Masons of California, ours also is to foster personal growth and improve the lives of others. We are a values-driven organization, committed to a culture of leadership, philanthropy, and service. We provide an engaging and relevant membership experience through a wide variety of member services, including educational programming, leadership development opportunities, charitable initiatives, and supportive services. We are dedicated to disciplined innovation and enjoy an organizational culture of continuous quality improvement and learning.

Name: L'équipe à Brigitte
Description: L'association 1901 L'équipe à Brigitte a pour objectif d'organiser et de financer la participation de Jules Hardy, étudiant à l'ISEP, et de Pierre Cleyet, étudiant à McGill University, au rallye-raid humanitaire du 4L Trophy. Cet événement a pour but de fournir du matériel scolaire et sportif à des enfants démunis vivant dans le désert du Sahara. Suivez les dans leur aventure, et n'hésitez pas à les contacter pour leur apporter votre soutien ! A bientot !

Name: VNoW - Virtual Network of Women
Description:
Mission Statement
Women Empowerment by educating them and making them financially independent Creating Women management leaders and entrepreneurs.
Description
VNoW (Virtual Network of women) for Women Empowerment mainly (Global Project currently started in Africa and Asia). When it comes to VNoW I am looking for volunteers who can make our organization a global success by impacting life of as many women over the globe as possible. women needs financial help such as scholarship for attending school, college or university. Also, provide books, stationary, uniform and teaching assistance. VNoW is planning to start workshops or training for professional skill development. Once these women will develop a professional acumen, they should do apprenticeship with professional organizations or businesses so that they can learn and hone their skills further by working on shop floor. For which we will like to develop internship partnership with local businesses or organizations. Once they will be proficient in their field. We will assist them by providing HR services by assisting them in Resume Writing, Cover Letter Writing, Interviewing Technique and overall help to get employment in the market. Those who will like to start their own setup we will try to get them loan either by micro financing or connecting with incubation center in their area or country.

Name: HopeScope Foundation
Description:
HopeScope is a charitable endeavor which is solely focused on providing community assistance, through personal donations of contributors and well-wishers who do not seek any personal gain. All donations are made in a transparent manner.

HopeScope brings together individuals from all walks of life who are determined to make a difference by lending a helping hand to society. Through its community inspiring endeavors, the HopeScope team touches the lives of children across the country, with special focus on assisting children to continue their education for the betterment of their lives and the future of the country.

Over the years, the small-scale projects have grown, benefiting more children every year. Together with a few generous contributors, HopeScope steered a book donation project last year, targeting 3000 school children from under-privileged families in the Hambantota, Ampara and Colombo districts, including those under the care of an all-girl orphanage. Among the donated items were notebooks, shoes, school utensils sufficient for a full year, clothing items and food.

Name: The West Indies Foundation
Description:
The West Indies Foundation (hereafter referred to as 'the Foundation') was created in September of 2010, in response to the evident lack of basic health services and the destitute situation of the poor in the developing countries of the Caribbean. While there are many local charities in our current operating country of Trinidad and Tobago, they often lack the specialized knowledge or resources required to create lasting change in the lives of the disadvantaged people that they serve.

The Foundation seeks to work with established charities, churches, and governments to improve the lives of the people that they are responsible for. Where many charities try to create new, expensive, often redundant frameworks of support and program implementation,  the Foundation works within established networks to avoid these high costs and to make sure that more of the donated dollars get to those who need them the most. We support health education programs, free health clinics, homes for abandoned children, and are committed to establishing a permanent, free health clinic in Trinidad and Tobago in the future.

Funding comes mainly from individuals and corporations, along with money given by Christian organizations. We strive to maintain true transparency and will publish all financial information which will include how much we have received and how much of that was used on overhead costs and program expenses.

Name: GPSIX - Global Philanthropic Solutions
Description:
GPSIX was formed by a group of individuals dedicated to philanthropy.
 
Our mission is to help you make a difference.

With a transparent and easy to use service, GPSIX leverages 15 years of expertise in bringing you the swiftest determinations,  and impartial 3rd party SaaS product advice.

Here, we at GPSIX have a better way...

GPSIX services fall into two main areas: Equivalency Determinations, and Philanthropic program implementation for corporations.

GPSIX website is a global online store allowing multinational corporations, as well as every day individuals to:

1 - Search and select currently eligible organization
2 - Request new listings to be reviewed, and
3 - Issue Grants to organizations around the world 

Via our unique and user friendly interface, anyone can purchase a review certificate, confirming the current eligibility status of international charities. GPSIX site members can submit a request to have an entity reviewed.

Name: Nalla-Africa
Description:
Nalla-Africa is a non-profit project striving to empower African women in leadership roles, through specialized studies and creation of projects in the IT industry so that they can contribute and become innovative globally. 

 

Currently, we are working on laying the groundwork in Kenya in various rural schools. We are aiming to reach up to 500+ students, to develop their digital literacy through computer provision, and adapted tutorials. Eventually, our goal is to stretch to other African countries.

Nalla-Africa tackles a complex issue, considering youth unemployment continues to be a key challenge in Kenya, especially among women and rural youth. IT is an emerging growth sector with potential to create jobs, and it is imperative that youth be prepared, with necessary skills for the future digital opportunities.

Name: Společný cíl
Description:
Jednoho dne, když jsme byli na cestě z dětského domova, maximálně nabití pozitivní energií, jsme se zamysleli, že by bylo fajn, kdyby nás na to bylo víc a mohli jsme tak dělat mnohem více radosti. A tak vznikl Společný cíl.

A jaký společný cíl vlastně máme? Prostý, naučit lidi si navzájem pomáhat, jen tak, bez očekávání odměny. 

Budeme zveřejňovat některé naše společné akce, při kterých společně pomůžeme někomu dalšímu. Budeme vám také nabízet možnost se do těchto akcí s námi zapojit a udělat druhým radost. 

Naší hlavní myšlenkou je ukázat, že pomáhat se dá často i bez vynaložení finančních prostředků. Kolikrát stačí věnovat vlastní energii a svůj čas.

Chceme vybudovat komunitu, založenou na správných hodnotách, komunitu která bude spojovat ty kteří to potřebují s těmi, kteří pomoc mohou poskytnout.

Name: PeppedUp.org (PeppedUp, Inc)
Description: PeppedUp, Inc. is a nonprofit organization dedicated to providing portable entertainment to child patients suffering from life threatening or terminal diseases such as Lymphoma, Leukemia, Myeloma, and other forms of cancer in an effort to help them and their families cope with the pain, fear, and isolation of their illness.

Name: Three Strand Cord
Description:
Three Strand Cord is a 501(c)3, tax-exempt organization with programs designed to create and promote lasting change in international settings. Non-denominational and cross-cultural, Three Strand Cord partners with many organizations all over the globe. Although based on Biblical values, Three Strand Cord is not a religious organization.
Three Strand Cord relies almost entirely on the hard work and commitment of a great number of volunteers. Volunteers raise money, find and donate supplies and coordinate the distribution of all that Three Strand Cord has collected.

Name: Operation American Dream
Description: We raise scholarships for the children of Florida's fallen law enforcement, fire rescue, and military personnel.

Name: Rainbow Children Foundation
Description:
The Rainbow Children Foundation exists to build and safeguard a free, fair and open society, in which every child enjoys the fundamental rights of liberty, equality and community, and in which no child is enslaved by poverty, ignorance or conformity. The Foundation champions the freedom, dignity and well-being of every Nigerian child and respects their right to develop their talents to the full.

Our mission is to improve the lives of less privileged children and to advance a culture that promotes a good and healthy life for every Nigerian child.

To achieve this, the Rainbow Children Foundation provides and facilitates the provision of basic education and vocational training for disadvantaged children. It provides material relief for children in need, especially those in conflict or disaster areas and produces educational radio and television programs that promote the physical, mental and emotional development of children as well as strengthen family life.

In addition, the Foundation provides professional training, management and technical support for child welfare providers and volunteers, as well as participates in child-related researches and projects.

Name: Bill W Club
Description:
The Bill W Club: promotes the personal development of all people in recovery from any addiction and the development of social skills in a safe environment,

This is done in a safe relaxed coffee shop environment.

The club encourages the families to understand the malady of addiction and directs these people to support facilities for themselves such as al anon , gam anon or co dependency anonymous.

We also hold events that cost very little, fishing , bar be que's, mountain climbing, guitar lessons, swimming and leisure complex membership etc in an effort to demonstrate that a person does not need mood altering chemicals to enjoy themselves.

The club organises events such as paint balling, horse riding and trips to recovery event like the blue bonnetts in Scotland.

The club has sports channel so the members can enjoy sporting events in a club like atmosphere.

We have a buddy system in the club where newcomers are assigned a more experienced person to help them through difficult times.

Group counseling is spontaneous can be availed of at short notice.

If one to one counseling is desired or required that will be provided or arranged if necessary.

The club encourages members to engage in activities and responsibilities within the club in an effort to reinforce their recovery and commitment to the club to ensure regular attendance and contact.

Any club member with a specific problem can approach the management and efforts will be made to assist the member either by referral to experts or agencies or through our own resources or network of partners, such as training or housing needs.

Name: Vineyard Community Services (VCS)
Description:
Our Mission:  Is to serve individuals and families (our "Neighbors") in crisis to meet their basic needs and provide opportunities for self sufficiency.  We welcome all who enter our doors as equals, respecting their dignity and accepting without judgment.  We encourage those we serve to develop their capacity for independence.  We are thrifty with our resources, generous in our hospitality, and responsible to the communities that we support. 

One of our principle activities is the Fruit of the Vine (FOTV)  food shelf.  As one of Minnesota’s largest single-location food shelves, FOTV provides emergency hunger relief and basic needs services to thousands of neighbors in the nine county Twin Cities area ... with a focus on Dakota County. 

While our core is food and basic needs relief, our efforts extend beyond hunger by engaging, educating and empowering our neighbors to achieve economic self sufficiency and their potential to thrive in life.  Included in our service activities are: Social Services, Employment Services, Senior Services, Emergency Assistance, and Literacy (ESL courses).

Our Vision is to assist our beneficiaries (we call them "Neighbors") from crisis, to rebuilding, to wholeness, stability and self-sufficiency.

Name: Refu-Aid
Description:
Refugees in various European countries live in difficult conditions and are treated badly.
They sleep on the ground, with no places to wash. Or out in the open fields waiting for borders to be opened.
Starving children receive food once a day and pregnant women fear for the health of their unborn children.
Help us be their voice!


In order to show the world a different perspective on the refugees, we would like to highlight their suffering so the world will appreciate the hardships they have been through. We are looking for creative people to help us shine a new light on this issue, to tell the stories of this refugees from a new angle.

Refu-Aid is an organization that supports refugees in Europe, we help them with everyday things they need, so when they thrive they will be able to contribute back to the society instead of being a burden.

Name: Fundacja Porta
Description:
Fundacja Porta założona przez Lidię i Arinze Nwolisa, wprowadza tolerancję do polskich szkół, firm i serc oraz pomaga dzieciom ulicy w Nigerii.

W ramach swojej działalności w Polsce fundacja prowadzi:
- edukację społeczną w mediach
- warsztaty antydyskryminacyjne w szkołach 
- szkolenia z zakresu DE&I w organizacjach
- mediacje w sytuacjach konfliktu
- konsultacje i coaching. 

W Nigerii zaś fundacja dostarcza wsparcie oraz fundusze na powrót do szkoły dla dzieci ulicy. 

Założycielami Fundacji Porta jest polsko-nigeryjska rodzina z 20-letnią historią życia w Polsce, gdzie inny kolor skóry potrafi być nadal piętnowany. Istotą działalności stało się przekucie osobistej traumy w aktywizm na rzecz całego społeczeństwa. 

W realizację misji fundacji angażuje się cała rodzina razem z czwórką dzieci, jednocześnie modelując, że wielokulturowe społeczności mogą żyć w pokoju i harmonii.

Name: Mutt Society
Description:
A group of passionate riders using bicycles to energize and elevate the youth in communities around the world.

With the focus on youth and local communities, our goal is to use bicycles as a catalyst to promote real and lasting change; pledging 90% of our fundraising/revenue to this mission.

WHY BIKES?

FREEDOM: For many of us, our bicycle was our first glimpse at freedom. It allowed us to expand our accessible world from our backyard, to our friends and local businesses. This freedom can engage new opportunities for work, play, and happiness.

PHYSICAL and MENTAL HEALTH: There have been many studies proving the benefits of exercise. Bicycling engages the body while freeing the mind. Just riding on familiar streets with the wind in the hair is enough to ease the mind and transport you to another place, even for a short time.

ENERGIZING COMMUNITY: With a sense of belonging comes a reactive sense of pride. Where happiness thrives, so does an energy among the community. Whether it is attracting more tourism, watching a supported team race, or just helping bring the community together for an event, bicycles give a push towards promoting economy.

Name: March For Science Chicago
Description:
A MORE HUMAN SCIENCE

We support the scientific community.
We safeguard the value and funding of the scientific process.
We celebrate the role of science in society.
We encourage scientific curiosity and exploration.

A BETTER SCIENCE

Recent events in public policy have put a spotlight on the scientific community, science, and their role within government, media, and the public.

We march to bridge the distance between science and society.
We march to showcase Chicago as The Science City.
We march toward an ethical science.
Now is the time to take a stand for science and the communities it affects.

WHO WE ARE

We are scientists.
We are enthusiasts.
We are supporters.
We are Chicagoans.
Science is meaningful to all of us whether we are scientists ourselves, enthusiasts of science, or simply Chicagoans that value a method to uncover truth. We were motivated to become a guardian of science despite our differences. Our diversity strengthens us.

Name: ESCP for Others
Description:
Our purpose is to encourage fellow students to think about those less fortunate than themselves, and to show that a small amount of effort can really make a big impact to the homeless community of London. Whether it be providing a good meal or some friendly conversation, human warmth and consideration can go a long way.

The society has concentrated so far on collecting surplus food from local cafes and restaurants and distributing it to those in need every Friday. They also work alongside Back 2 Basics Foundation at King's Cross station each Friday, helping to serve warm meals from a pitch. They have also given out clothes and raise money to pay for basic necessities.

Name: Anchor House Foundation
Description:
Anchor House Foundation was founded to serve our combat veterans.  Though I did not serve in the military, I was raised by an Air Force brat. My grandfather served as a Master Sergeant and in the Vietnam War.  H.T.’s career spanned 22 years.  He was born and raised in Cullman, Alabama.  After being stationed in numerous states, he retired in Alabama.  His son, my dad, settled in Cullman and it became my hometown, as well.  Of all the places our family could have ended up, we landed right back in the location of our roots.  There’s a reason I was raised here.  I feel that reason is to serve the community that so many of my family members have called “home.”  Therefore, our organization's purpose is to serve, first, our local combat veterans and their families.  I believe that prioritizing the care of our own allows us and those we assist to participate in further-reaching service efforts.
As a developing organization, we will begin our support by partnering with our local VFW post in Cullman, Alabama to provide additional resources and manpower for projects.

Name: Serving Others Forward Together
Description:
Serving Others Forward is a resource and process to help us Live Our Faith.  Unlike Pay It Forward or Charity, SOFT encourages personal involvement in serving others AND collaborating with friends for emotional support and accountability.  Serving others changes us.  It make us better mothers and fathers; son and daughters; friends and co-workers. 
1) SOFT empowers you to start and sustain a serving habit
2) It empowers you to serve others more effectively, have greater impact on their lives
3) You can inspire and invite others to serve
4) You can communicate easily with friends and share stories
5) You can change the world - change the way we ALL treat each other

We love Dallas, Texas. Plain and simple.

Our mission is to support nonprofits in the Dallas area by helping them raise money and connect with the local community through fun and engaging events. Our goal is to host 1 event every month, benefitting a different nonprofit organization every time. All proceeds go towards 501(c)(3) nonprofits and are tax-deductible to the extent allowed by law.

We hope to see you at one of our upcoming events! Can’t make it? Consider donating to our nonprofit partners.

Name: Downtown Books & Cookies
Description:
Downtown Books & Cookies’s mission is to give children of West Michigan a memorable downtown experience with elements of education and tons of fun! Grand Rapids strongly promotes the growing urbanite community and city center activities. It is in our best interest to teach our youth that our downtown is safe, fun and vibrant.

Every month, from a downtown venue, Downtown Books & Cookies will feature a new panel of community leaders reading their favorite childhood stories to the families of West Michigan. Cookies and treats are provided by local organic cookie makers and confectioners.

We partner with local orphanages to develop programs to nurture and empower orphans to become productive members of their society. We will be building an orphanage in Guadalajara Mexico (construction expected to being Q1 of 2012) in the first of many steps to aggressively engage the volatile state of the orphans' welfare there.

Name: Art Beyond the Glass
Description:
Art Beyond the Glass (ABTG) was founded in 2011 by Daniel Djang and Zahra Bates as a special event for bartenders to showcase their artistic talents when they're not behind the bar. From cocktails to paintings, photography, live music, DJs, spoken word and more, ABTG is a celebration of bartender artistry inside and beyond the glass. 

Proceeds from every ABTG event are donated to a local arts-based nonprofit - to date, ABTG has donated more than $210,000 to these vital community organizations.

In response to the devastating impact of COVID-19 on the hospitality industry, ABTG is pivoting its fundraising mission in order to support bartenders suffering financial hardship due to loss of employment or reduced work hours.

To accomplish its new focus on COVID-19 relief, ABTG is being supported by Community Partners, a 501(c)3 nonprofit organization, as part of its Fiscal Sponsorship program.

Communities have always been about the people, not just the buildings and land that surround them. The Collins Management Foundation is eager to give back to our communities in a relevant and meaningful way.

Description:
The ZACK ROSS FOUNDATION is a global non-profit charitable organization with a focus on Poverty, Education and Mental Health development. The reality of limited and restricted resources to a vast majority of the world leads to the HUMAN PERSON not being able to live out their OPTIMAL PERPETUAL POTENTIAL.

Access to Education should be a fundamental HUMAN RIGHT, whereas information is the basis for HUMAN DECISION-MAKING, and enables one to BETTER CHOICES & OPPORTUNITIES.

Mental Health facilitates the betterment of LIVED EXPERIENCES, and promotes stability, productivity, and progress within a civilized social framework.

Please support our MISSION to make our WORLD full of HUMANITARIANS.

Name: Hope In Art
Description: We aspire to spread hope in the difficult world of artists. There is incredible talent in our country, but still be it lack of exposure or lack of marketing skills, many of these talents live an obscure life and fight a battle of survival. On the other hand, the low income group patients and their families struggle to get the required medical treatment at the right time. With a two-fold objective of helping artists get a convenient platform and patients get the required medical need from the proceeds we generate, we have started our journey of “Hope In Art”. We sincerely hope that you will join us in our journey and build a strong army ready to protect the happiness of others. We want to transact in hope.

Name: The Brantley Foundation
Description:
The Brantley Foundation was formed in November 2017 by Booker "Terry" Brantley III in honor of his father's legacy. As a young man, Terry remembers his father using the phrase "Son, Listen" before he would share his knowledge and personal opinions in their discussions. An Atlanta icon but also a very wise business and family man, Mr. Booker T. Brantley Jr. has influenced the lives and careers of many and today his son known by most as "Terry" is walking in his father's steps and creating his own legacy.

The Brantley Foundation's programs are designed to give men, women, and youth, a supportive network that encourages their personal growth, teaches them technical skills, shares insider knowledge, and experiences, and promotes creativity and individuality.

Our Mission is to support the needs of men, women, and youth with aspirations of improving their quality of life. We want to advocate knowledge instead of myth about careers and the capabilities of self-sustaining economies and the ability of our clients to impact growth and prosperity independent of the systemic misidentification of successful career paths.

Name: Donor Motivation Program Georgia
Description: Increase the number of qualified planned giving donors with an easy-to-understand, donor-centric message that motivates donors to act and doubles the results of planned giving efforts.

Name: Bexley Women's Club
Description:
We are an engaged women's network actively participating in and
supporting our community through scholastic and social services.

Name: Worthington Education Foundation
Description: The Worthington Education Foundation funds grants for educators in our district to test emerging trends, conduct innovative research, and provide experiential learning for students, parents, and the community.

Name: The EPIC Kids Foundation
Description:
The EPIC Kids Foundation is a 501(c)3 approved children’s non-profit organization.  Our mission is to develop healthy communities through technology, renewable energy, training, education and health related projects and initiatives.

As a privately funded organization, EPIC Kids is empowered by individuals and corporations with a passion for the development of healthy communities. We receive our financial and volunteering support from businesses, grants, private citizens, civic and faith-based organizations.

Through organizational initiatives, EPIC Kids encourages power in community by creating volunteer opportunities for youth of all ages to make a difference in the life of others.  We serve children and families of all ages from all economic, racial, ethnic and religious backgrounds.

Name: Giftuu
Description: Based on the idea of gift economies, Giftuu makes it easy to give skills, help or things to people in our local or online community. The idea comes from a simple realisation - that things get done much better in groups when people freely give to each other.

Name: Diretório Central dos Estudantes - DCE Uninorte
Description: O Diretório Central dos Estudantes do Centro Universitário do Norte, doravante chamado apenas DCE Uninorte, é uma entidade representativa de todos os discentes matriculados nos cursos de graduação e pós-graduação da Uninorte Laureate, com sede organizacional e jurídica na própria Instituição, constituído como associação civil autônoma, sem filiação político partidário, livre e independente dos órgãos públicos e governamentais, definida nos termos da Lei Federal n° 7.395, de 31 de outubro de 1985, sendo ainda a duração de seu funcionamento ilimitada.

Name: Miles for Moms
Description:
Miles For Moms is a student-led (and developed) initiative that was founded by members of Circle K International. A prominent community service organization both on the UA campus and around the world, Circle K is the collegiate equivalent of Kiwanis. Kiwanis's ELIMINATE Project was designed to combat Maternal/Neonatal Tetanus (MNT), a vicious disease that takes a life every 11 seconds, and to provide aid for those afflicted.

The fundraiser will consist of a 754 mile-long charity hike beginning in Lexington, Virginia and concluding in Toronto, Canada, the site of the 2016 Kiwanis/Circle K International Convention. Each mile hiked will raise money for vaccines, and after our team has completed the hike we will carry a banner into the convention that displays our sponsors and their contributions in front of a crowd of thousands as well as multiple news outlets. Our goal is to raise $100,000 from individual and corporate donations to provide ELIMINATE with the financial resources it requires to vaccinate those affected by MNT around the world.

Name: Claremont Challah
Description:
Challah for Hunger was founded in response to the humanitarian crisis in Darfur. Through the simple act of baking, students at Scripps began to raise awareness and proceeds for the victims of genocide over four years ago. 

Claremont Challah continues to combine our passion for baking and social justice, focusing on a specific social justice issue in the Los Angeles Area each semester. We believe there is much Tikkun Olam—“repairing the world”—to be done in our own back yard. Structural inequality, discrimination, racism and poverty still plague out society, impeding out vision of social, racial and economic justice.

Every Thursday evening and Friday morning we sell freshly baked challah to students at the Claremont Colleges, donating 100% of our profits to the charities of our choice. For example, in the 2018-2019 school year, we donated over 5,000 dollars to the Inland Empire Food Bank. If you are a student looking to enhance both the Claremont Colleges and greater LA community, don't hesitate to reach out.

Name: Good Movement
Description: Good Movement is an unincorporated non-profit group that devotes its time and effort to alleviating the pressures that homeless youth face throughout Peel Region. By facilitating youth programming around the issues of advocacy, awareness and social action to solve the root problems of homelessness, we ultimately produce both preventative and supportive solutions.

Name: Sarah Chowdhury Limited
Description:
Sarah Chowdhury Limited provides bespoke advisory services to the charity and corporate social responsibility sector. Director Sarah Chowdhury’s diverse professional background spans across several sectors and professional landscapes enabling clients and to capitalise on a vast foundation of knowledge and experience.

Sarah Chowdhury Ltd’s values are that of Sarah as an individual:
Committed – always delivering what is agreed
Transparent	– always honest and open
Passionate – always delivering earnestly
Loyal – always discreet and striving to provide a positive impact	

Sarah Chowdhury Ltd is currently providing retained services to Graham Layton Trust and Lloyds Banking Group. Sarah Chowdhury Ltd is happy to be approached for support on a retainer or project specific basis.

Name: Meet For Impact
Description:
Meet For Impact is an online fundraising platform. 
Our mission is to transform fundraising and engage more donors by creating a win-win solution that benefits both charities and donors. 

Meet For Impact headline product is an online auction where donors bid for exclusive experiences and meetings with prominent businessmen, media personalities, cultural opinion leaders, politicians, and top athletes. Meet For Impact has raised over $3M to date for 120+ charity foundations across Eastern Europe.

Bid, meet and make a difference as #everymeetingmatters!

Name: Ubuntu Peace Project
Description:
Let's connect with one another, and put an END to violence, crime, corruption and bitterness by:

Bringing awareness to consciousness, and what it is. 
 
Embracing the spirit of uBuntu - unity, kindness, respect, care and cooperation towards each other and towards our environment.  
 
Being mindful of our thoughts, our words and our behaviour; and remembering that "Peace begins with ME!"      
 
Focusing on the peace and harmony we want as opposed to "fighting against" what we don't want.

 Helping to address the root CAUSES of violence and crime.  Read More about Ending Crime & Violence >>

Name: 2040 Vision
Description:
Our mission is to help create a brighter future by identifying ways corporations can make doing good a profitable part of their core business. 

Our first step toward achieving this goal has been to publish Tales from 2040, which you can get free-of-charge at 2040.net. This book contains three short stories that show how prominent leaders of today can use capitalism to help solve some of the world's toughest social problems... and make a fortune in the process.

Name: Le Rêve de Putu
Description:
L’association « Le Rêve de Putu » est née d’une rencontre à l’été 2015 lors de vacances passées à Bali. Deux des fondateurs, Laurent et Dominique et leur famille, séjournent dans une villa louée dans le nord de l’île, à Dencarik. A cette occasion, ils font la connaissance de Ketut GAJAH, le chauffeur de la villa.

Ketut organise toutes les excursions et visites et conduit les familles à la découverte de l’île des Dieux. Ils passent donc beaucoup de temps ensemble et apprennent à se connaitre. Une relation se noue, la confiance s’installe et des échanges riches et sincères s’installent entre eux.

Ils répondent aux questions de Ketut sur leur mode de vie en France et en profitent pour s’informer de la réalité du quotidien des balinais en général et de la famille de Ketut en particulier.

Ketut leur parle des siens, de sa femme (Putu Kusmiasih),de sa fille de 16 ans (Putu Krisna) et de son fils de 7 ans (Komang Arisutha). Il décrit le système scolaire indonésien, avec en particulier le port obligatoire de l’uniforme. Il indique également que sa fille aurait aimé aller à l’université faire des études supérieures, mais que cela sera impossible.

En effet, Ketut précise qu’une année d’université coûte environ 2.000 Euro, et qu’il ne peut pas financer d’études universitaires car son salaire moyen est d’environ 150 Euro par mois.

Laurent et Dominique décident alors de lancer une collecte de dons afin de tenter de financer les futures études de Putu Krisna. Devant le succès incroyable de cette initiative, ils décident de créer l’association.

Name: The STEAM Centre
Description: The STEAM Centre  is a web-based platform that increases accessibility of local educational services to grades K-12 community (students, teachers, and parents). The STEAM Centre includes resources covering Science, Technology, Engineering, Arts, and Mathematics in the form of engaging and interactive events, workshops, summer programs, etc.

Name: AFRICA'SOUT!
Description:
AFRICA’SOUT!s mission is to honor, support and defend artists who radically change the narrative around Africa and its Diaspora, first and foremost, for themselves.

Founded in 2014 by artist and activist Wangechi Mutu, AFRICA'SOUT! harnesses the power of artists and the creative community, especially from within Africa and its diaspora, to highlight the urgency of pressing social and political issues through unique and dynamic platforms. 

A grant-making, NYS 501c3 organization, AFRICA'SOUT! advances radical change through the power of art and activism, particularly supporting artists, initiatives and institutions from Africa and its Diaspora that celebrate freedom of creative expression.

Name: Vatelia Foundation
Description: The Vatelia foundation empowers and uplifts the broken spirits of those in need. We provide a source of hope for those who have lost all faith in themselves and their future. By attending the immediate need of those who have faced devastation and poverty we hope to be a source of light for those who live in a darkened world. The Vatelia foundation primarily feeds the hungry.

Name: The Outlier Foundation
Description:
In the 5th century, a greek playwright coined the term philanthropy. The definition? "Love of humanity." 

Today,  philanthropy means generosity in all its forms. It is often defined as giving gifts of time, talent and treasure to help make life better for others + the world around us. This is the very essence of our mission. To be fearless lovers of humanity + relentless givers of our gifts.

Name: The Makeshift Movement
Description:
At The Makeshift Movement, we strive to broaden and improve our collection of resources and education related services surrounding addiction and mental health. This is something we will all be adding to as time goes on—our “makeshift” resource—Our Makeshift Movement.

The Mission of The Makeshift Movement is to create a safe place where our community can talk openly about mental health, suicide, and substance abuse. We aim to:

- Give a voice to those struggling with mental illness and addiction and encourage them to speak up before it's too late
- Equip individuals with the tools to support their family members and friends
- Empower communities to identify and approach these issues head on

Name: Ecofuego Guatemala
Description:
Una empresa con responsabilidad social que busca mejorar la calidad de vida de las personas por medio de un proceso de cocina más eficiente, limpio y económico.

Hoy en día una de las mayores causas de muertes en Guatemala se relaciona a problemas respiratorios, siendo el aire contaminado de los hogares una de las principales causas. 
Más del 80% de hogares del área rural en el país cocinan con leña, siendo expuestos a altos niveles de humo. 

Con Ecofuego, ayudamos a diminuir la cantidad de humo generado durante la cocina, disminuyendo el impacto en la salud de la familia. Al mismo tiempo, disminuye el uso de leña en un 30%, reduciendo el gasto del hogar y ayudando a disminuir la deforestación del país.

Name: Beautiful Minds Project Academy for Trauma Studies and Disaster Response
Description:
Beautiful Minds Project is a non-profit organization that brings practical and culturally relevant healing programs, education and training to populations suffering from psychological and emotional trauma caused by war conflict, natural disasters, poverty, sexual abuse, and injustice.

Through teams of clinical trauma specialists and professionals within the areas of international community development, international business and marketing, and other specialties, we create strategic resiliency programs that target the needs of populations served.

We serve refugees, orphans, child soldiers, victims of human trafficking, and communities who desire to be equipped in growing emotional and psychological resilience in the face of human and natural disasters

Our volunteers mostly come from our partnership with Family Dental Health, Carolina Family Orthodontics, and Apple Seeds Pediatric Dentistry.

Description: OASIS advocates for our local seniors by helping them stay healthy, engage in meaningful activities, have fun, build new friendships and learn new things. We are an independent nonprofit that has been enriching lives and serving Northern Santa Barbara County since 1984.

Description:
Connect is a privately held charitable initiative working with philanthropist, private citizens and non profit organizations. 

Significant past projects include the establishing of infrastructure for women who lost their source of income during  the post tsunami recovery efforts in South Asia. Coordinating efforts and connecting IT professionals with nutrition centers to ensure the ongoing supply of resources for malnourished infants. Yearly, Connect undertakes small to medium scale initiatives and operates independently or with partnerships.

Name: Business For Hope
Description:
As the official philanthropy of World Advisory, Business For Hope provides small business consulting services to help disadvantaged individuals start and grow businesses free of charge.

There are many bright and ambitious entrepreneurs who, due to circumstances beyond their control, are disadvantaged and need our help the most.

This includes people who are low-income, immigrants, refugees, prisoners, students, and more.

Business For Hope connects these passionate entrepreneurs with World Advisory's professional Business Consultants and covers their service fees.

Clients are able to receive ongoing guidance and assistance in a variety of ways to start grow and their businesses.

Through our operations and with the support of generous individuals and organizations, we're able to help people succeed and reach their full potential.

Our goal is to make entrepreneurship more accessible to all individuals and make it easier for anyone to start and grow a business.

You can learn more and get involved at BusinessForHope.org

Name: Live for the Cause Foundation
Description: Live for the Cause (LFTC) is a Montreal-based community foundation, whose is mission is to engage, empower, and educate people of all ages on the important and rewarding aspects of becoming involved in philanthropy, with the ultimate goal of enhancing the community at large. Our team is comprised of a group of young philanthropists, who volunteer their time and expertise to educate youth on the importance of philanthropy and develop creative initiatives to solicit community engagement and support important causes in the local community.

Name: Candor Community Services Corporation
Description: Candor Community Services has a broad mission to improve life in Candor. This includes recognizing community volunteers as 'citizens of the year', recognizing student volunteers with scholarships, planting trees, supporting music and the arts, assisting the food pantry, and many other projects. There's always room for new ideas and leadership here.

Name: Wings of Karen
Description:
Wings of Karen is a non-profit breast cancer foundation supporting research locally, benefiting women everywhere.

The mission of this community based organization is to fund promising breast cancer research in the Pacific NW and Texas.  We are committed to finding a cure, while empowering and educating women with a positive, helping hand. In the spirit of Karen Denmark, Wings of Karen is a resource that shows the strength and courage of those fighting back at cancer.
 
It is our goal to fund the study of all aspects of breast cancer, including but not limited to, prevention, detection, and treatment.

Name: Robert and Frances Chaney Family Foundation
Description:
The Robert & Frances Chaney Family Foundation's mission is to benefit the welfare of children and families and to support public safety. We support communities in three areas:

1. Children and families receive the help they need to overcome abuse, addiction and poverty so they can have a brighter future.
2. Public safety providers have the skills and equipment needed to protect and save lives.
3. Families are provided a solid foundation through faith based programs.

Our geographic focus is Barry County, Missouri, Cabell County, West Virginia, and Jackson & Josephine Counties, Oregon.

Name: TIM PEDULI
Description: Indonesian Volunteer Team distributing aid for Palestine and Syria refugees in Jordan.

Description:
What do you do when you learn your son has a life-threatening disease? 

If you are Shellie Nichol Chandar, you start a non-profit organization and call it Hope's Not a Crime. That was 2014.  

Hope's Not a Crime has evolved into Project Foster Care -- an initiative focused on the hopes and dreams of children in foster care who have been freed for adoption and on the people who love and care for them.

Name: Queen's Coalition Against Racial and Ethnic Discrimination (QCRED)
Description: Queen’s Coalition against Racial and Ethnic Discrimination strives to be an anti-oppressive non-hierarchical coalition of students, staff, and faculty that works to create an anti-racist climate on the Queen’s University campus. QCRED works towards community wellness for historically marginalized racial and ethnic groups. Through education, community organizing, and commitment to struggle across differences, we seek to responsibly reflect, represent and serve our various communities.

Name: Udruženje "Dobro delo"
Description:
Mi smo udruženje “Dobro delo” i bavimo se humanitarnim radom od 2012-te godine.

Kroz humanitarne projekte podižemo svest o činjenju dobrih dela.

Smatramo da nivo svesti stanovništva direktno utiče na kvalitet života u sredini u kojoj živimo. Zbog toga intenzivno radimo na projektima koji omogućavaju bolje uslove za odrastanje mališanima iz ugroženih grupa (deca obolela od raka, deca bez roditeljskog staranja i socijalno ugrožena deca).

Projekat koji je pokazao najviše uspeha je “Humanitarna kafa”.

Šta je "Humanitarna kafa"?

„Humanitarna kafa” je projekat preko kojeg mreža ugostiteljskih objekata donira iznos svih popijenih kafa određenog dana kada organizujemo događaj. Svako ko tog dana dođe i popije kafu, kompletan iznos cene kafe odlazi u humanitarne svrhe.

Novac preusmeravamo deci iz gore pomenutih grupa. Projekat se pokazao kao jedan od najefikasnijih načina za prikupljanje sredstava i za podizanje svesti o činjenju dobrih dela, što je ujedno i misija udruženja “Dobro delo”. Do sada je popijeno preko 10.000 humanitarnih kafa, a 2018. godine osvojena je Virtus nagrada za filantropiju.

Kako je pandemija uticala na “Humanitarnu kafu”?

Od kad je počela pandemija, zbog bezbednosti stanovništva nismo organizovali ni jedan događaj ali smo zato “Humanitarnu kafu” prilagodili novonastaloj situaciji i sada će biti dostupnija svima – u planu je “Humanitarna kafa” u instant kesicama koju će svi moći da poručuju putem interneta.

Name: Neighbors
Description: Neighbors est un moyen de mettre en lien les personnes voulant agir (mais non bénévole) avec les institutions correspondantes afin de pouvoir aider simplement et selon ses disponibilités

Name: Co-Hort
Description: We're a non-profit organization consulting, designing and constructing recirculating soilless culture systems and developing entrepreneurs in vulnerable communities

Name: The Pan-African Family Empowerment & Land Preservation Network, Inc.
Description:
Among other things, the Pan-African Family Empowerment & Land Preservation Network, Inc. is a non-profit organization dedicated to helping families of African descent preserve and maintain long-term ownership of their ancestral homes and land. 

Although founded in the federally recognized Gullah-Geechee Cultural Corridor of the United States, the mission and vision of the PAFEN is global in scope. We actively promote the Pan-African goals of creating strong, healthy, safe, educated, and economically stable Black families globally, while creating unity among African peoples both in Africa and the far-flung African Diaspora. 

Currently, we're best known for our popular "Help Save Gullah-Geechee Land Campaign," which has helped to save black-owned land in Beaufort, Colleton, Georgetown, and Horry counties in South Carolina--with an assessed value of more than $5.5-million.  The "Help Save Gullah-Geechee Land Campaign" was launched by our Founder & CEO Theresa White in March 2015. It includes technical and referral assistance, as well as financial resources to prevent land-rich, but cash-poor Gullah-Geechee families from losing their beloved homes and land through delinquent tax sales and other methods.

Name: The Sampson Foundation
Description:
The mission of the Sampson Foundation is to create a culture of wellness by encouraging healthy lifestyles as well as promoting whole-person patient care.

The Foundation strives to advance its mission through two primary initiatives:

Healthy Lifestyles (Prevention)

Education of healthy eating as preventative care
Improving access to healthy foods
Promoting programs that support active living and exercise
Whole-Patient Care (Integrative Medicine)

Providing access to educational programs on integrative medicine
Educating health care providers around the practice of integrative medicine
Investing in research to help build the evidence base for complementary and alternative medicine

Name: GlocaLand
