Name: Collective Medicine
Description:
Collective Medicine is an indigenous organization committed to restoring quality of life to tribal nations in our region by addressing the essential needs of people across the Navajo Nation and beyond through our cultural value of community service. Our main project, Water Warriors United, focuses on improving water security for families by preventing exposures to harmful environments and reducing burdens associated with inequity to access of clean water.

We are an indigenous-led, reservation-born team, and work directly in the communities we grew up in. We believe that community-driven systems are the core of a community’s power and can create alternative ecosystems that value equity. We have learned that people are the most powerful pipeline for increasing water access. Our team organizes grassroots efforts to collectively act as a vital network for helping community members access and advocate for essential resources. Collectively, we are good medicine.

Name: AFTER THE MUSIC FOUNDATION
Description:
As Idaho's cultural and economic footprint grows, fostering a supportive environment for musicians will be crucial.
 
Local initiatives and nonprofits such as After the Music Foundation can work together to address gaps in funding, access, and professional development development. After the Music Foundation is uniquely positioned to provide not only financial aid but also advocacy , helping Idaho's music community thrive even in times of crisis. 

By investing in these efforts, Idaho can ensure that its musical heritage continues to grow, enriching the lives of its residents while supporting the livelihoods of its artists. Independent musicians in Idaho often operate without financial stability. Unforeseen medical emergencies or lost/damaged instruments can derail careers entirely. 

After The Music Foundation is a 501(c)3 Corporation will provide vital needs for musicians in crisis, providing assistance for medical expenses, stolen or damaged instruments, and even funeral costs. This work is essential in Idaho, where the tight labor market and rural disparities can leave musicians without safety net.

After the Music Foundation will provide, for example, a funeral cremation grant/voucher that they could use at any funeral home.

Through Concerts, Fundraisers, Corporate/Private Donations and Grants, our purpose is to help those musicians in their time of need and lighten the costly burden associated during their time of need. 

Musicians may apply via the www.afterthemusicfoundation.org website. After a review by the board, a voucher would be granted for funds, for example, with the closest participating funeral home for a simple cremation (Including Removal/Cremation Container/Remains Container/Cremation Process). 
***Other services provided by the funeral home would be the responsibility of the family depending on the type of extra services that are offered (Memorial Event At Funeral Home/Internment/Special Containers/Urns).

Name: Word of Restoration Ministries
Description: Global Christian missions, church planting, leadership development, and church-centered community development.

Name: Samuel Olutuyi Foundation
Description:
Samuel Olutuyi Foundation’s work focuses on four key areas of Education, Health, Civic engagement and Entrepreneurial support and development that create opportunities for everyone in our communities to live a better life.

All our programs and initiatives are designed to make life easier and bring stability to the lives of people in our community. Our programs and projects are grassroots focused, thus enabling us to reach the most vulnerable.

Our strategic partnership ensures that we find lasting solutions to the problems that bedevil our communities, inequality in education, inadequate support for apprentice and entrepreneurs especially petty traders, poor health and wellness information and the huge gap in civic engagement.

Our work is carried out with focus on solutions. This is also done with a flexible approach that allows us to apply what we learn from every program or strategy. We also carry out our work through collaborations with other organisations. Our impacts are measured by the actual growth and development of the lives and communities touched.

The Foundation is not a fully grant making organization, rather an operating foundation that designs and initiate its own programs to support its mission. Most of the foundation’s grants are also part of its self-initiated programs.

The solutions we create go beyond short-term charity. Our solutions are carefully executed to provide lasting and long-term solutions to the challenges that face our communities. Our solutions and support for education, health and entrepreneurship are creating vibrant communities that ensures people are able to exit the cycle of poverty. 

Over the past years, our solutions have reduced poverty through the provision of micro credit to petty traders and provision of grant to apprentice through our apprenticeship grant. Our educational grants as well as our Water for Education Project (WAFEP) ensures access to quality education especially for the girl child.

Name: Rumah Pangan Bangsa
Description: Organisasi non profit di bidang filantropi untuk mencapai kesetaraan pangan di Indonesia

Name: Friends of the Pico Union Library
Description: The Friends of the Pico Union Library enhances and extends library resources, services, and facilities for the Pico Union Los Angeles Public Library through advocacy and community building.

Name: One for the World University of Florida
Description:
Founded at Wharton in 2014, and thriving at 15 other schools across the nation, One for the World UF is the 16th chapter. We believe that there is a smart way for students to engage in effective giving, and asks UF students and alumni to donate 1% of their income to fight extreme poverty. 

The mission of One for the World is to help change the culture of giving in affluent countries and to generate donations to highly impactful nonprofits that reduce suffering and premature death, and improve life and livelihoods, for people living in extreme poverty. Everything we do aims to expand awareness of the concept of “effective giving” and grow revenue for our recommended nonprofits. 

For more information, please visit our national website www.1fortheworld.org

Name: White Light Foundation
Description:
White Light Foundation is a non-profit organization built on a common bond of music and founded on the principles of community, love, generosity and passion. Inspired by the music of Vermont quartet Twiddle, the main mission of White Light Foundation is to spread the love, healing and kindness. Through fundraising and community initiatives, White Light Foundation works to assist various charitable organizations and community causes.

Founded in 2015, White Light Foundation evolved naturally from a community focused on self-improvement through service and community contributions. These ideals led White Light Foundation to the belief that assisting and helping others not only strengthens our community but reinforces our best internal values. We strive to represent the best of a musically inspired community as we encourage others to make a difference and love relentlessly.

White Light Foundation is administered completely by volunteers. No "employee" of the organization is paid for their efforts; rather, we are remunerated by the knowledge that we are contributing to something bigger than ourselves. If you'd like to learn more about upcoming events, volunteer or donate, please visit whitelightfoundation.org

Name: The Rising Stars
Description:
Rising Stars - Non Profit Organization in India focusing on Education, Social Service, Poverty, Economic Development, Health & Environment related issues.

Mission:
•To improve learning and enrichment opportunities for children.
•To improve the quality of life and advance the rights of children.
•To bring about change for good on behalf of children and families, and encourage them to participate in processes which enhance their equality, self-reliance and long term sustainable development.

Beginning with a small group of Committed People having passion to serve the society Rising Stars was formed in the year 1997 attempting to transform its beliefs into reality.

We want to empower the vulnerable sections of the society by building their capacities through Education, Health and Skill–based Development.
Rising Stars team comprises of Educationists, Development Professionals, Media Personnel, Corporates, Activists, Civil Servants, Bankers, Professionals and Consultants who all bring their experiences and perspectives to the organization and are unified by the common vision of improving our society. All of them have a dream to eradicate problems from society, for a better tomorrow.

As a philanthropic society, we intend to work with people that have intellectual traits & abilities to contribute to society. When numerous hearts & minds come together, every task & mission becomes easy, comprehensive & accomplish-able.

Name: Murphy's Community Centre
Description: Murphy’s Community Centre is PEI’s home for community, cultural, educational and recreational events in the heart of downtown Charlottetown. The building was built in 1963 and has a rich and colourful history as a place for people – especially youth - to gather to enjoy sports, social clubs, summer camps, and arts & drama in a safe and supportive environment. Murphy's Community Centre is also well-known as the home of The Alley, Prince Edward Island's premier bowling facility. Formally the Basilica Recreational Centre, the building was purchased in 2006, by Ray and Carolyn Murphy of Murphy's Pharmacies, renovated and upgraded, and currently operates as a not-for-profit under the direction of the Murphy Family and with the support of Murphy's Pharmacies. Murphy's Community Centre gives individuals and non-profit groups the opportunity to raise hundreds of thousands of dollars for various causes through benefit dances & fundraisers. Thousands of Islanders also benefit from subsidized rates on bowling and other recreational activities.

Name: Charism Enterprise
Description:
Charism Enterprise is a conglomerative umbrella committed to impacting the World through the holistic covering, nurturing and cultivation of its people.

Charism Enterprise is committed to identifying needs, securing resources and delivering life enhancing results, serving as a foundational vessel from the People to the People. The efforts of Charism Enterprise facilitates the collision of resources and need; impacting and changing Lives.

Name: Richmond Youth Foundation (RYF)
Description:
The Richmond Youth Foundation (RYF) exists to serve as a voice for youth in Richmond. Our role is to fund community projects that benefit youth, either as the project organizers or beneficiaries.

We are the youth branch of the Richmond Community Foundation which has been serving the city for more than 25 years, providing over $500,000 in grants. The money we grant comes from our youth forever fund – an account we’ve built through our own fundraising efforts and donated capital. We encourage youth leaders to submit proposals for positive community initiatives they would like to carry out in Richmond. 

When we come across an idea that aligns with our mission, vision & values, we provide grant money to bring the idea to life! We’re all about clear and simple granting. Our application process makes it easy for youth to tell us about their ideas, and get funding sooner than they would by seeking corporate sponsorship. When provided with the appropriate resources, we believe that youth are fully capable of addressing the issues they care about. Insufficient funding should never be a reason why a great initiative fails to prosper. 

We welcome creative thinking, thrive on youth leadership, and fund bright ideas. RYF also acts as a hub for youth philanthropy in Richmond. We do this by connecting youth with a variety of local non-profit organizations who may have common philanthropic interests.

Name: Nos Eco'Liées
Description:
“Nos Eco’Liées”, c’est deux jeunes qui partent en janvier 2019 pour un voyage participatif et solidaire en Amérique du Sud et en Asie. Le but ? Créer un projet pédagogique avec une école primaire à Mornant, dans la région Lyonnaise (69), afin d’échanger sur les différentes cultures et environnements rencontrés. 

Et si on en profitait pour venir en aide? Aller à la rencontre des autres, c’est aussi faire face à leur mode de vie et par conséquent à leurs difficultés... C’est pourquoi  “Nos Eco’Liées” s’engage à récolter des fonds en amont du voyage et à les mettre à profit dans un projet spécifique à chaque école visitée (achat et distribution de fourniture, participation à un programme de développement de l’école, financement d’interventions sociales, pédagogiques ... ou tout autre projet mis en place avec les écoles).

Pour mener à bien ce projet, et permettre d’offrir un maximum à chaque école, 90% des récoltes seront directement redistribuées à celles-ci. Les 10% restant assureront une part des frais de logistique du projet.
Pourquoi ce projet ?

Sans éducation, les enfants des pays défavorisés ne pourront pas développer leur situation. En leur apportant une aide judicieuse, nous leur permettons d’avoir un regard affuté sur le monde qui les entoure. 

A l’heure de la mondialisation, il nous semble important que chaque enfant connaisse les cultures du monde. Nous sommes convaincus que connaitre l’autre permet d’éviter les conflits et de favoriser les échanges.

Fanny & Clément
Location: Loire sur Rhône, Rhone Alpes Auvergne

Name: Silvana S. Foundation Ltd
Description:
Silvana Sutanto was full of life – this is the one phrase to describe her the best, and her photography works reveals who she is. Her photography captured the beauty of life which most people often miss out. She and her camera travelled hundreds of miles away for photography journey, and her kind heart never stopped looking for less privileged children and endangered wildlife. 

To continue her legacy, we formed the “Silvana S. Foundation”. On behalf of her, we would like to support and raise hope for children and wildlife. Please stay connected for the latest news and information. 


May God bless you all.

Name: The Willie Ng Family Foundation
Description:
Our mission is to:
Aid cancer patients and the elderly or immunocompromised in need of assistance when bills associated with treatment become unaffordable.

Ensure the continued quality education of students who do not have the means to pursue higher learning opportunities. 

Support animal rescues and hospitals that focus on stray intake and adoption.

Always reach across the divide to help ANYONE in need.

Name: Lauren's Hope Foundation, Inc.
Description: A non-profit 501c3 charity that benefits children with brain injuries.  We offer networking opportunities to our families, connect them with doctors, dentists, specialists and clinics for their child. We offer funding for therapeutic services and equipment that insurance doesn't cover. We host fun, family events and help our children get adapted toys to provide learning opportunities.  Our mission is to reach out and give hope to children with brain injuries for an enhanced quality of life and support for their families.

Name: The Friends Help Friends Foundation
Description:
A life changing Moment of compassion, generosity, and stewardship along with lasting memories has created The Friends Help Friends Foundation. The Friends Help Friends Foundation has one vision; friends help friends in a time of need, and treat others the way we want to be treated. With a mindset of helping others, being kind and compassionate, and treating others equally, The Friends Help Friends Foundation will provide more than material supplies. It will provide a foundation of a multigenerational mindset of providing opportunities to do good for all people.

The mission of The Friends Help Friends Foundation is to provide individuals, families, and communities specific assistance in a time of catastrophic loss and need.

Name: The Giver
Description:
Engaging corporate outings, supporting local causes
Powerfully designed volunteer experiences
Meaningfully curated local connections

Name: Fundacja dla AGH w Krakowie
Description:
FUNDACJA DLA AGH

Wyjątkowość AGH prowokuje nie tylko studentów, ale także pracowników i absolwentów do integracji w służbie rozwoju, zdobywania wiedzy oraz tworzenia odkryć. To właśnie nasza Fundacja chce obserwować i determinować tę współpracę!

Wierzymy, że pasja i zaangażowanie mogą przyczynić się do realizacji hasła „Tam sięgaj gdzie wzrok nie sięga, łam czego rozum nie złamie”.

Więc łamcie i sięgajcie razem z nami aby móc krzyknąć „Eureka!”.


Fundacja została zarejestrowana w maju 2007 roku z inicjatywy dr inż. Jerzego Kickiego, przy olbrzymim wsparciu na każdym etapie jej organizowania założyciela Fundacji JM Rektora Akademii Górniczo–Hutniczej prof. dr hab. inż. Antoniego Tajdusia.

Do jej powstania przyczyniło się liczne grono osób, które wniosły wiele cennych uwag do pierwotnego statutu przygotowanego w czerwcu 2006 roku. Ważną rolę na drodze do rejestracji Fundacji odegrała dr inż. Agnieszka Stopkowicz, która zajęła się działaniami związanymi z procedurą jej rejestracji.


Akademia Górniczo–Hutnicza do momentu rejestracji Fundacji była jedną z nielicznych uczelni nie współpracujących z żadną fundacją działającą na rzecz oczekiwań społecznych własnego środowiska.


Początków założenia Fundacji, która stanowiłaby wsparcie dla wielu inicjatyw Uczelni, należy upatrywać w końcu lat osiemdziesiątych, kiedy to w warunkach zmieniającego się systemu gospodarczego coraz trudniej było realizować niekonwencjonalne działania ją promujące i wspierające. W tym czasie powstały Fundacje wspomagające niektóre z wydziałów uczelni. Kolejne próby założenia podejmowane w latach dziewięćdziesiątych zakończyły się niepowodzeniem. Pomysł najprostszy z możliwych, iż założycielem jest Jego Magnificencja Rektor AGH okazał się skuteczny.

Mamy świadomość, iż nasz wizerunek jest świadectwem, które wystawiamy społeczności naszej Uczelni, świadectwem z umiejętności współpracy i budowania własnej tożsamości, ale przede wszystkim z rezultatów naszej pracy.

Name: WIase Foundation
Description: Wiase Foundation is the philanthropic arm of the Wiase Ecosystem. Wiase Foundation offers low or no-cost programs to underserved communities that are in need of economic development. Wiase Foundation is focusing on sustainable impact through philanthropic support to meet measurable initiatives.

Name: Sawil Pax Upendo Children's Academy
Description:
Sawil Pax Upendo Children’s Academy is a non-profit educational institution founded in October 2014 with a specific objective: to create a promising future for impoverished rural children through education, nutrition and care in Mwanza Region, Tanzania.

Facebook: www.facebook.com/upendochildrentz/
Instagram: @sawilpaxforchildren

Name: JMSSPD Guinée
Description: Journée Mondiale de la science au service de la Paix et du développement est une  journée, consacrée au science par UNESCO, elle est célébrée le 10 Novembre de  chaque année

Name: AhoyRunners
Description:
De AhoyRunners zijn sinds 2004 deelnemer aan de Roparun.

Op zaterdag 30 mei 2020 gaat de 29e Roparun van start. De AhoyRunners komen op maandag 2e Pinkerstdag 1 juni 2020 over de finish!

Ook in 2020 doet ons team, de AhoyRunners van Rotterdam Ahoy voor de 17e keer mee aan de Roparun. 

De Roparun is de langste non-stop estafette loopt vanuit Parijs of vanuit Bremen naar Rotterdam, die in 2020 voor de 29e keer gelopen word. Ondanks het sportieve karakter van de Roparun ligt de nadruk natuurlijk op het inzamelen van donaties. 

In 2019 hebben wij als Ahoyrunners een bedrag van € 29.010,28 over mogen maken aan de Stichting Roparun Palliatieve Zorg, in de afgelopen 17 jaar hebben wij als team een bedrag van ongeveer € 350.000,- mogen doneren aan de Stichting Roparun. Een bedrag dat wij dankzij heel veel sponsoren en inzet van teamleden bij elkaar opgehaald hebben. Nogmaals heel veel dank hiervoor. Graag willen wij u nog een keer mee laten genieten van onze run in 2020. 

Ons doel is om in 2020 minimaal € 25.000,- op te halen en over te gaan maken ten behoeve van Stichting Roparun. Dit bedrag willen wij bijeen brengen door te collecteren bij evenementen in Ahoy, het organiseren van een spinningmarathon (dit jaar met medewerking van oud-wielrenner Michael Boogerd & Leontien van Moorsel), het organiseren van bingo’s, de verkoop van loten en natuurlijk het samenwerken met sponsoren en andere activiteiten.

Name: Swipes for a Cause
Description:
Founded in 2020, Swipes for a Cause is a Vanderbilt-based, student-run organization dedicated to alleviating food insecurity in Middle Tennessee by donating Vanderbilt students' excess meals to food banks. Given Swipes for a Cause's storied history and service leadership, we have successfully leveraged Vanderbilt University's GET app platform and structured dining plans to enable donations worth > 400,000 meals. 

Join and support us today to further our efforts to end hunger.

Name: U-ee Foundation
Description:
bGiv. buy. Giv. simultaneously. 

Taking donated surplus inventory & monetizing it for charity by selling it at retail value.

100% of the purchase going to the charity of the buyer’s choice.

The U-ee Foundation takes giving to a new level  – as it uniquely integrates individuals, philanthropy and consumer brands with a new level of clarity, accountability and efficiency.

The U-ee mission is to unify, edify and educate — bringing brands and individuals together with charitable causes — to create new value in giving with a focus on finance and entertainment through technology.
bGiv.org is pioneering a new approach to the power of giving.  It is designed to be the most innovative brand image accountability platform, a charity navigator for brands.  bGiv combines the collective buying momentum of Groupon with a charitable Ebay auction environment, paired with a PayPal platform that tracks the efficiency of giving among brands, individuals and charities.

People, especially children, are the passion, drive and reason for bGiv

Name: Awesome Eh
Description: Awesome Eh? is a new social venture that is mapping the AWESOME around the globe!  We are building a directory of awesome and we want your awesome to be on the map.

Name: CEWS - Children Education & Welfare Society in Pakistan
Description:
Our mission is to usher a brighter future to Pakistan by supporting the most vulnerable in society. We aim to give impoverished and neglected children and women a fresh start towards their rights to education and healthcare. By providing aid to children and women, we believe we can help change the direction of an entire nation.

We visualize a Pakistan where each individual is able to reach their full potential, nurture higher ambitions, and realize their dreams.

Our ultimate motto is peace for the peace and by the peace with the prosperity and progress, including religious and civil harmony among the people of this world.

Name: Abaana Community Outreach Africa (ACO Africa)
Description:
Abaana Community Outreach Africa (ACO-Africa) is a local grassroots organisation based in Uganda, working to lift vulnerable children and women out of poverty, empowering them to achieve self-sufficiency through a holistic family-centered model that is both cost-effective and replicable. We provide health support, educational support, child rights/advocacy, nutritional support and financial literacy training giving families living in poverty the tools and resources they need to become more self- reliant. 
Currently ACO Africa operates in Wakiso, Rakai, Lwengo and Mityana Districts of Central Uganda

Name: Josephine S. Gumbiner Foundation
Description: The Josephine S. Gumbiner Foundation preserves the spirit of community involvement and service through charitable giving. Established by Josephine S. Gumbiner in 1989, the Foundation is dedicated to supporting programs that benefit women and children in the Long Beach area. This includes programs focusing on day care, education, housing, recreation, the arts, and health care, with a special emphasis on intervention, prevention and direct service. Previously funded projects have ranged from prenatal care to women's shelters to programs for at-risk youth and participatory cultural programs for children and teens.

Name: Instituto da Próstata
Description:
O INPRÓS é um serviço voluntário com foco na Saúde do Homem, buscando suprir o que o poder público não atende. É uma Instituição formada exclusivamente por voluntários, preparados pela OSC Parceiros Voluntários. Possui parcerias com outras Entidades públicas e privadas, para atendimento as questões relacionadas a Saúde do Homem, especialmente o Câncer de Próstata e sua prevenção. 

NOSSA VISÃO 
Ser um centro de referência de alta qualidade em Saúde do Homem.
NOSSA MISSÃO
Promover efetivamente a Saúde do Homem, em todos os seus aspectos, oferecendo amplo acesso a informação, através de ações qualificadas sobre educação, assistência e pesquisa.
NOSSOS VALORES
. Melhoria da qualidade de vida.
. Aumento da expectativa de vida.
. Prevenção das doenças masculinas.
Location: Porto Alegre, Rio Grande do Sul

Name: Logan Circle Community Association
Description:
The Logan Circle Community Association (LCCA) is one of the most effective and successful community associations in the District of Columbia. Established in 1972, it has achieved a remarkable record of steadily improving the environment and the quality of life in the Logan Circle area.

LCCA works to promote the civic and social interests of residents by:

•Enhancing the quality of life and promoting economic development.
•Preserving the historic, architectural and aesthetic value of property.
•Presenting the views of its membership to government and other organizations.
•Participating in charitable and educational activities.

The Association represents the Greater Logan Circle area, bounded by S Street to the north, K Street to the south, 9th Street to the east and 16th Street to the west. Membership is open to anyone who resides or owns a business or property within its boundaries.

Our 50 square blocks are rich in history, architecture and a vibrant mixture of residents and businesses. Our community takes pride in its diversity and activism, which combine to reflect the best of inner city living.

LCCA is a nonprofit community organization with a Board of Directors elected by the membership. LCCA invites you to join us in making a difference in your community.

Name: Weaver Philanthropic Strategies
Description: The cornerstone to Weaver Philanthropic Strategies is a long-term personal and significant commitment to civic engagement. We bring a unique perspective to our work because we serve this community deeply; volunteer roles include board of directors leadership, major gift donors, interim executive leadership, search committee and event committee leadership, as well as financial and management consulting experts for corporations and nonprofit organizations.

Name: Syrian Nour Associaition
Description:
من نحن : 
   جمعية خيرية غير حكومية غير حزبية غير ربحية تأسست عام 2011 وحصلت على الترخيص في تركيا عام 2012 تعمل على ايصال الخدمات الانسانية والتعليمية الصحية و التنموية و الخدمية لكافة شرائح المجتمع السوري في الداخل و الخارج دون التميز بينهم على أساس العرق أو الجنس أو الدين.
الرؤية  :
نبني الانسان لنرتقي بالمجتمع .
الرسالة  :
مساعدة الفئات الاجتماعية الاكثر احتياجا وفقا لمبادئ الكرامة الانسانية .
القيم   :
الشفافية: نزاهتنا وانفتاحنا في إجراءاتنا.
الاستقلالية: الالتزام بمعايير مهنية بعيداً عن الميول الشخصي أو الانتماء السياسي.
الإنسانية: الحرص على تحقيق الخير للإنسان.
المهنية: الخبرة والموضوعية إضافة إلى الاختصاص والكفاءة الفنية.
المساءلة: تمكين المستفيدين من إيضاح آلية استخدام صلاحياتهم وواجباتهم في إدارة مواردهم إضافة الى تعزيز قدرتهم على تحمل المسؤولية بما يخص سير العمل.
عدم التمييز: مساعدة الناس بغض النظر عن الجنس أو الدين أو العرق أو المعتقدات السياسية.
التشاركية: دور واضح تشاركي تكاملي لتعظيم الأثر مع المؤسسات السورية وممثلي المجتمع المحلي والمنظمات غير الحكومية المانحة.

الخطط المستقبلية :
1.	إعداد مشاريع تنموية وإنتاجية للمساهمة في توفير فرص عمل لقطاعي الشباب والمرأة .
2.	إعداد مشاريع عمل تنموية وبرامج خاصة بالطفولة .
3.	العمل على استمرارية الدورات التدريبية مثل دورات التدريب المهني ودورات ثقافية وعلمية لكافة شرائح المجتمع .
4.	العمل والتنسيق مع كافة المؤسسات الداخلية والخارجية وتقديم برامج ومشاريع عمل مشتركة تصب في خدمة أبناء شعبنا .
5.	تقديم الخدمات المميزة والتخصصية وكذلك للفئات ذو الاحتياجات الخاصة للارتقاء بالمستوى المعيشي للمواطن وتنمية التعاون والتكافل الاجتماعي .
6.	العمل بالتعاون مع المؤسسات الحكومية والدولية ذات الطابع الإنساني بهدف تقديم العون والمساعدة المادية والتنموية

Name: Bull&Bear Foundation
Description: Tomamos conciencia de las áreas de oportunidad que existen en México, por lo que desempeñamos acciones con la finalidad de retribuir en nuestra sociedad, a través de la coordinación de proyectos de inclusión social, trabajando de forma independiente, y apartada de grupos ideológicos.

Name: Cory's Crusaders, Inc.
Description:
Our Mission is to provide financial Assistance to families of children fighting Brain Cancer and to fund much needed research. By raising funds and awareness, one day the news no one wants to hear could come with a glimmer of hope, longer life expectancy or a more positive outcome. Our goal is to help families focus on what matters most, their child.

Cory's Crusaders, Inc. is a 501(c)3 Non- Profit Organization, Public Charity and all donations are tax deductible. Tax ID# 46-1849031 
The organization is overseen by an all-volunteer board who accept no compensation.

Name: Grand-Cru Solutions
Description:
A Grand-Cru Solutions promove uma maior e melhor divisão da prosperidade, aumentando a renda e o bem-estar de pessoas e famílias.

Somos um time de executivos com mais de 25 anos de experiência em empresas lideres de diversas industrias. Não somos consultores. Somos mentores de gestores e de empresas, e temos como missão transferir o conhecimento e a experiência que acumulamos ao longo de anos de trabalho para o beneficio de outros.

Nenhuma empresa é pequena demais para nós. Desenvolvemos planos sobre medida para cada situação comprometendo-nos com o retorno positivo do investimento em nossos serviços.

Nossos mentorados, além de fazer seus negócios prosperarem com a nossa ajuda, fazem um importante aporte ao bem-estar social. A maior parte do lucro gerado pela Grand-Cru Solutions, é encaminhado à Fundação Grand-Cru, que ajuda jovens a completar estudos universitários e facilita sua inclusão na sociedade.

Seja parte de este modelo que busca promover prosperidade e o bem-estar das pessoas. E beneficie-se da experiência e o conhecimento pratico de centenas de executivos sênior dispostos a ajudá-lo a fazer seu negocio crescer.

Entre em contato para um diagnostico preliminar sem custo algum.
Location: Rio de Janeiro, Rio de Janeiro

Name: RISE - Charity For Victims Of Fraud Ltd
Description:
Helping Victims of Fraud.
Consultation service on investment fraud and crypto related crime and web 3 exploitation. 
Fund raising activities. 
NFTs (coming soon)
RISE Fraud accreditation.
DAOs advice.
Crypto exchange public safety advice.

Name: Arntz Family Foundation
Description: The Arntz Family Foundation’s primary mission is to support environmental organizations, with an emphasis on those organizations that work toward systemic change and sustainability particularly where the areas of the environment and economic development come together.

Name: Wenk Family Charitable Foundation
Description: The Wenk Family Charitable Foundation is an independent family foundation dedicated to providing support and/or leadership for programs, initiatives and communities that encourage and promote the emotional, physical and mental health of youth and animals.

Name: Don Scott Memorial Foundation
Description: The Don Scott Memorial Foundation is a 501 (c) 3 non-profit foundation dedicated towards colon cancer fighting initiatives including community awareness, prevention, outreach, and research. In addition the foundation supports Manhasset High School student athletes surrounding the sports of cross –country and track and field.”

Name: Community for the Animals of Southeast Asia (CASA)
Description: We are a charity based in Barcelona and focused on animal welfare and environmental sustainability across Southeast Asia. Our primary focus is currently on the stray animals of Bali, Indonesia. Therefore this page will be a place to communicate more closely with a community of people dedicated to supporting our mission to rescue animals and empower others to get involved in animal welfare projects creating a long-term systemic change.

Name: Labakcare
Description:
The mission of Labakcare is to improve the health of underserved communities by providing no cost preventive health care services and health education. 

Through partnerships with local nonprofits, shelters, and religious institutions, Labakcare is able to offer health screenings, flu shots, and referrals to uninsured individuals at our free, local health fairs. These health fairs take place mostly in the Philadelphia, South Jersey, and Delaware areas, while Labakcare's international activities currently take place in Nigeria and Haiti.

The efforts of Labakcare have increased the knowledge of screened participants in understanding their diagnosed health issues.  Participants are taught to recognize signs and symptoms of their health problems as well as what to look for if their conditions deteriorate. 
 
Participants are also educated on the importance of diet modification, exercise, and medication compliance as a means to reduce the threat of a health crisis.  

Over 600 local and nearly 200 international volunteers have helped to make health fairs and international missions a success. 

With the help of involved and committed volunteers, Labakcare can continue to make positive change with a lasting impact.

Name: Epsilon Sigma Alpha - Kutztown University
Description: Epsilon Sigma Alpha at Kutztown University is a coed service organization whose mission is to give back and make a difference in the community. We inspire leadership and service by bringing students together to pursue programs that make a positive lasting difference. We support and help organizations such as St. Judes, Toys for Tots, Easter Seals, Hope for Heroes, supporting the fight against domestic violence, Warm Welcomes, promoting literacy, and other local and national organizations.

Name: The Game Changer Foundation
Description:
The Game Changer Foundation seeks to make the world a better place through the empowerment of individuals and organizations that struggle to fulfill their purposes and make the impact they desire.

The relevancy of The Game Changer Foundation is rooted the frustration that both individuals and corporations experience as they fail to optimize their resources, thereby diminishing their impact and impressions.  It is the losses to our communities; to the world, that has made the resources of The Game Changer Foundation relevant and necessary.

Name: Society for Viipuri School of Economics
Description: The Society for Viipuri School of Economics was established in January 1919 in the city of Vyborg (Finnish: Viipuri).

Name: Phi Gamma Delta - Sigma Kappa Chapter
Description: The Sigma Kappa chapter of Phi Gamma Delta at the University of Connecicut stands out as one of the most prominent members of the UConn greek community. Since the reception of our charter in March of 2013, UConn FIJI has been committed to brotherhood, academic excellence, and serving the community.

Name: Viaduct Foundation
Description: Viaduct Foundation is a family trust established for the advancement of education in the world's poorest countries.  We believe that education is a significant contributor to meaningful, long term improvement in not just the standard of living, but also the standard of life.

Name: Americans for Quality Arts Learning
Description: Americans for Quality Arts Learning is a non-profit 501(c)(3) organization. We believe every K-12 student deserves a quality arts education. Students who participate in the arts are more likely to stay in school, succeed in school, succeed in life, and succeed at work. To this end, we mobilize philanthropic support to provide art teachers the resources necessary to serve students most in need.

Name: The Star Story Project
Description:
The Star Story Project is a nonprofit philanthropic organization who's mission is to inspire reading as the foundation of today’s youth and to support the growth of resources in community schools. By gathering university students to come together we help support local community schools through donation and volunteer work. We are currently located at Texas A&M University.

If you would like to learn more or would like to inquire about getting involved at your university reach out to us!

jacobbmoore24@tamu.edu
(971) 263-7832

Name: CWEF Tanzania
Description: CWEF Tanzania is a chapter of the Canadian World Education Foundation (CWEF), a Windsor-based non-profit tax-exempt charitable organization committed to the education of children throughout the world. CWEF Tanzania's mission is to empower vulnerable children and communities in Tanzania.

Name: Turning Wheels Foundation, NFP
Description: The Turning Wheels Foundation is on a mission to ensure that all children have access to the therapy care they need.  Every day in the Chicagoland area thousands of children go without critical therapy needs due to financial constraints.  Each day that goes by leaves these children further behind and impacts their lives, both now and in the future.  By providing families and children with the right resources, we can change this outcome.

Name: Athletic TIPS
Description: Athletic TIPS (Toward Injury Prevention in Sports) is a national, not-for-profit organization that serves amateur athletes, driving expanded awareness and education around the role of multi-disciplinary care in the recognition, management and prevention of sports-related injuries.

Name: Shanghai Soup
Description:
Shanghai Soup is a project is based off the hugely successful Detroit Soup model and was founded with the aim of creating a fun and entertaining networking event which positively impacts the wider community through microfunding. The dinners are held on a bi-monthly basis at various locations in central Shanghai. 

The soup served at each event is sourced from local suppliers and restaurants, utilising food that would otherwise be thrown away due to overstocking or cosmetic damage. Roughly one third of the food produced in the world for human consumption every year — approximately 1.3 billion tonnes — gets lost or wasted (source: FAO).  In China, Liu, J. et al. (2013) suggest that 26 million hectares of land are used to grow crops which end up wasted every year, ‘the equivalent to the total arable land of Mexico’.  We feel such waste is unacceptable and hope to promote awareness and further details of what we as consumers can do to reduce our food waste at the events.

Fund your Shanghai community project with support from your neighbours – anything from creating an urban garden, to funding a class field trip, to starting a new business. Attendees pay 50RMB for soup and vote on which project they think benefits the community the most. The winner goes home with all of the money raised at the door to carry out their project. At the dinner, Shanghai residents and supporters connect, share ideas and community resources.

Name: Face-Off for the Forces (Indiana University)
Description:
Face-Off for the Forces is a lacrosse-based philanthropy located in Bloomington, Indiana. The event started in 2014 with just two fraternities, playing on a grass field with no lines 20 minutes off of campus. However, those two teams were able to raise an incredible $11,000 dollars for the Wounded Warrior Foundation. Since 2014, the philanthropy has grown in many different ways. There are now 10 fraternities involved and, most importantly, we are now benefiting the Hoosier Veterans Assistance Foundation (HVAF) based out of Indianapolis.
 
HVAF’s mission is to provide housing and reintegration services to veterans that are experiencing homelessness. By administering specific programs and services to at-risk veterans, they are able to help prevent many others from ever becoming homeless. Last year, 2,259 veterans had a safe place to sleep thanks to the efforts of HVAF. Additionally, 1,205 family members, including 554 children, received the counseling and guidance they needed to help become self-sufficient. This charity is unequivocally the most beneficial program for veterans within our state. Last year, with the help of Face-Off’s donation of $35,000,  HVAF began to implement a new and improved DART program, helping veterans overcome drug and alcohol addictions.

Name: Bear Care LLC
Description: Bear is a 2.5  teacup Pomeranian  service dog who has been surrounded by people  with philanthropic beliefs his whole life. The souls who have touched his life all believe in “pawing” it forward so Bear Care LLC was born.  As our CFO ( Chief Fur Officer) Bear works diligently to make people smile and provide some light in what sometimes can be a dark world.  Whether it’s caring for people  who have been through a major disaster or visiting children in a hospital,Bear and his humans want to make the world a little better one smile at a time. The purpose of Bear Care LLC is to give back so the proceeds from items sold will be donated to those in the most vulnerable communities- Veterans,  Sick or Homeless Children and Dog Rescues. Through a variety of carefully curated products and Print On Demand items, we hope to raise funds to help  make a difference to these communities.

With the kindness and love that God showed us, we will strive to give that same hope to families from Third World countries.  Just as God has no boundaries of compassion and caring, H.E.L.P. Missions will endeavor to emulate what God has taught us.

Our desire is to invest in children through the provision of academics, hygiene and life skills that will prepare them to have an opportunity to become productive members of their societies. With offices operating in 9 cities throughout Canada and the United States our passion for growth and innovation has allowed Engage to be a leader in our field. Description:
For over thirty years Smuggler’s Enterprises, Inc. has played a significant part in our community. We have grown from one food and beverage operation to five, including catering services, and an event management component.  In 2008, we formed The Smuggler’s Enterprises, Inc. Foundation. Our purpose is simple, “Establish partnerships, which carry out local community relations initiatives that align with our corporate strategies and address the needs of the community where we do business.” The Foundation enables us to streamline our philanthropic efforts through one organization. Administrated by the Charlotte Community Foundation, they lend their professional experience in the philanthropic world and provide the framework to achieve our common goals. We believe it is our corporate role to work with organizations and supporting programs through corporate philanthropy and community outreach.

Some of our efforts include, over $100,000 worth of food to local food pantries, providing Christmas and Thanksgiving to over 2 dozen local families, recycling efforts throughout our company, charity based guest bartending, youth programs, drug free programs, Habitat for Humanity, American Red Cross, American Cancer Society, Virginia B. Andes Clinic, and many more.

Name: One Blue Village

Despite this region being one of the nation’s most affluent areas, there are a number of individuals and families that do not fit in this category.

The center was established to service individuals in this demographic. Often, these individuals and families are marginalized by the greater community, and are in need of supplemental programs.

Name: Southside Health Education Foundation
Description:
It takes commitment to ensure the Southside community benefits from top-notch healthcare, cutting-edge research and caring, qualified health professionals. SHEF believes this vision can easily be reality - if we support the talented students who live and work here.

By providing scholarships to deserving students from our region who are studying to enter healthcare fields, we can help make their career dreams reality - enabling them to pursue careers here, in their own backyard.  SHEF supports the students who live and work in Southside.  Successful students access better jobs and pursue careers in the community.  Newly-trained health professionals attain economic stability for their families.  The region's healthcare employers meet their personnel requirements with a staff committed to the area.  With a strong healthcare workforce and economic vitality, everyone wins.

The statistics paint a picture of a community in need.  More than a quarter of the residents of Southside Virginia do not have a high school degree.  Just 10 percent of residents hold a bachelor's degree.  In Petersburg alone, annual per capita income is only $15,989.  That is where SHEF comes in.  Because many well paid healthcare jobs only require a high school degree and post-secondary training, we can make a large impact by offering scholarships.

One-by-one, we match area students who have the drive and talent for a career in health with the scholarships they need to pursue the education they deserve.  

One out of every ten emerging jobs in Virginia is in the health sector, according to 2010 findings of the U.S. Bureau of Statistics.  And with predictions from the U.S. Census Bureau that Virginia's population will increase by 12.6 percent by 2020, having a stable, well-staffed health system will be more important than ever.  With the right training and education, residents may be able to more than double their earning potential, thus increasing the economic vitality of the entire region.

SHEF is building a workforce.  In Southside Virginia, careers in healthcare may be just the ticket for many residents who wish to have more rewarding jobs and higher wages.  The growth is there - over the last 15 years, employment in the Southside health sector has increased by 41.3 percent.  The wages are there, too.  Per capita income for Petersburg is $15,989 annually, while the average area wage for a position in the health sector is $37,965.

There's a lot at stake for the Southside Virginia region.  Current health indicators for the region demonstrate the great need we have for quality healthcare.  Increasing the pool of qualified healthcare workers in our region will ultimately boost residents' access to the high-quality care they desperately need.

In its first five years, SHEF provided over $400,000 in scholarships to 162 area students with the ambition to better their lives.  In addition to supporting nursing students, we've launched scholarships for students pursuing coursework in allied health professions including radiology technology and sonography.

Name: Ariana Mae Hatami Foundation
Description:
Relationship abuse and domestic violence are a fateful everyday occurrence. The Ariana Mae Hatami Foundation (AMHF) is a place where we work through and in partnership with local agencies that are aligned with our purpose. We provide added resources so that victims of domestic violence will receive an extra layer of help. Our purpose is to help connect people, be a liaison to someone who is lost or does not know where to turn because they have become victimized.

Under the Ariana Mae Hatami Foundation (AMHF), there are no elite boundaries or social classes in order to receive the aid (financial or emotional) that is available to meet the needs of women who truly need it. AMHF operates on the “big heart” philosophy that was conceived from the young woman herself. Ariana told the people around her that she has a big heart and from that fundamental principle, we will strive to remove barriers that would otherwise cause additional hardship.

Name: Families of the Missing

The organization’s main activities include providing family support networks, introduce reforms and develop standards and procedures that address missing individuals from a family perspective, heighten public awareness, and assist families in resolving cases.

Our organizational mission is to build and inspire a new generation of youth by initiating constructive engagement and linking communities to discuss sustainable community development – we want to eradicate the existing sense of helplessness, exclusion, and desperation in our communities. Name: FaceShield - ABINFER
Description:
No início de março decidimos lançar o projeto KiCo – Kill the Covid, por conta da profunda preocupação com a pandemia que ameaçava assolar nosso País. Aderimos assim à algumas das ações estratégicas emergenciais dos Governos Federal, Estaduais e Municipais para combate ao Covid-19. 

Desde 24 de março, primeiro dia de atividade operacional, até hoje, decorreram 129 dias. Neste período foi gigantesca, determinante e emocionante a adesão de membros da ABINFER e de várias pessoas e empresas que sequer tínhamos contato ou sabíamos da existência. Todos, sem exceção, trabalhando e mergulhando na causa de forma espontânea e voluntária. Para nós, verdadeiros ANJOS que não mediram esforços para ajudar a prevenir o avanço da doença e minimizar as dores e sofrimento de muitas famílias. 

Foram mais de 2.000 entidades agraciadas e 500 munícipios atendidos. Produzimos, de forma totalmente graciosa, mais de 780.000 escudos faciais (Face Shields). Foram mais de 3.250 horas de máquinas injetoras, 19.500 kg de material, 260 km de elásticos, 24.375 placas de acetato, 14 moldes de injeção, 13.000 horas/homem e, adicionalmente, embalagens e logística. Números impressionantes e importantes no combate à pandemia.

Name: Baby Dakota Foundation
Description:
Baby Dakota Foundation is a Not-For-Profit specialising on equipping hospitals in regional/rural and remote areas with the required Neonatal Intensive Care equipment to keep pre-term and critically ill babies alive, with a strong emphaisis on remote Indigenous communities.
We believe that no more precious little lives should be lost simply due to a lack of equipment, facilities and funding. 
We aim to ensure that babies that are born prematurely or critically ill receive the best care that Australia has to offer.

Name: Live to Inspire Foundation
Description: Live to Inspire Foundation‘s mission is to provide awareness, educate, and fund research for rare medical conditions, focusing on the Carey Fineman Ziter syndrome (CFZ). CFZ is a rare syndrome that affects less than 15 people in the entire world. Until 2015 there where only 2 confirmed cases in the world. With very limited resources, an additional 9 have been confirmed with more on the way.

Name: Worldeka
Description: Collaborative network for social change.

Name: Lehigh Local Business Development Club
Description:
Lehigh's Local Business Development Club

The purpose and aim of The Local Business Development Club is to approach local businesses in Bethlehem and offer strategic advice in order to not only grow the businesses, but to also strengthen the connection between Lehigh students and the residents of Bethlehem. Members of the club will be able to participate in real life experiences which introduces them to the professional world.

Name: Heavenly Care
Description: Heavenly Care is a philanthropy company based out of 4839 Crazy Horse Ln, Westerville, Ohio, United States.

Name: Sugga's Industries International
Description: Sugga's Industries International, Inc.'s mission is to maintain a long-standing commitment to programs developed to enhance the quality of life for men, women, and children by exposing them to opportunities and helping them reach their fullest potential including support for education, health, literacy, and cultural arts. Name: Cute Transport Network
Description:
Network of more than 600 volunteer drivers dedicated to helping drive cats to wonderful homes and rescues all over the country. 

Since 2012, we have successfully rescued and transported more than 300 cats, most saved from kill shelters where they stood very little chance of making it out alive.

Our network is a growing, passionate, caring, and amazing group of people all dedicated to making a difference and saving lives.

To sign up as a volunteer driver, please fill out our volunteer driver form: http://bit.ly/cutetransport

Name: Caring Coalition AZ
Description:
Caring Coalition AZ was formed in 2014 when our founder saw that students from low income families in the community did not have enough to eat on the weekends–when these families could not utilize the school food program. 

Each week, volunteers help pack up non-perishable foods for 70+ students  as well as helping in food drives for summer and holiday breaks. 

Children have found it rewarding to volunteer their time each week to help other children.

Name: The R.A.D.A.R. Project
Description: We work with children and people that have suffered the consequences of sexual abuse and domestic violence. We provide various resources for the prevention, intervention and education related to these topics and other social problems. Our organization works to create a positive impact in diverse communities with the intention of creating a brighter tomorrow.

Name: JÄGER IT GmbH
Description: IT Service, Support, Consulting and Websites for Construction workers, aswell as Transports and Driver services are written big in our Portfolio.

Name: Fuerza MX
Description:
Fuerza MX es una iniciativa que nace de una idea sencilla: si cada mexicano ayuda a otro mexicano que necesita una mano, tanto y como pueda, de dos en dos podemos levantar a México de la crisis económica provocada por la pandemia de coronavirus.
Por eso, creamos fuerzamx.org, una plataforma que será el punto de encuentro entre cada mexicano que necesite ayuda y cada mexicano que pueda darla.

Fuerzamx.org es un directorio de Mexicanos para Mexicanos. En la plataforma encontrarás pequeños negocios que ofrecen productos y servicios alrededor de tu casa para que puedas considerarlos y consumir de ellos parte de tus necesidades cotidianas. Sin nuestro consumo miles de pequeños negocios se irán a la quiebra y cientos de miles de familias se quedarán sin ingresos básicos. #consumelocal 

Encontrarás un directorio de muchas otras iniciativas que han ido naciendo en México para apoyarnos entre todos durante esta crisis, y también podrás encontrar especialistas en diversos temas que pueden ayudarte con una asesoría corta gratuita que tal vez sea lo que necesites para re-orientar tu negocio, ajustar tus finanzas personales, asesoría de clases para tus hijos, o recibir ayuda para superar la ansiedad del encierro.

Name: Dogs And Pals
Description:
Dogs And Pals is a non-profit lifestyle brand inspired by our love for all animals. What started as a small Instagram page quickly turned into a large community with hundreds of thousands of followers and over 2.5 million tags. We truly LOVE all dogs, in all sizes, breeds, and colors. We wanted to create a platform that will tighten the bond between humans and their furry family members. We are inspired by the love people share with their dogs, the unbroken bonds, dedication, trust, and commitment. We are motivated by stories of hero animals and people who go out of their way in order to save lives and make a change.
We believe Animal Welfare is fundamental to any brand’s social responsibility and it is an equally critical part of operating a responsible and ethical business. Our brand is anti-leather and fur. Making a leash out of faux leather, and not using fur, may not stop animal cruelty, but it is a small step in the right direction. 100% of ALL profits will be directed to charity organizations, no-kill shelters, and life-saving emergency operations. We are committed to using resources wisely by collaborate and funding programs with proven results. We strongly believe in the power of giving, sharing and caring.

We empower Haiti’s youth to develop and implement programs that alleviate the suffering of families living poverty.
 
Through our programs, youth and families are equipped to reach their full potential and break cycle of poverty.

Free Food New Zealand is dedicated to the promotion of the idea that food should be grown freely in and around all human settlements, and to the promotion of methods, concepts, projects and means of doing so.
Food grows on trees for free, so it makes sense to plant the trees that support our lives as close to where we live as possible.

Name: Faith Church UCC New Braunfels
Description:
Faith UCC is a progressive, open-minded church in New Braunfels, Texas, and a part of the group of churches in the UCC who have declared themselves open and affirming. No matter who you are or where you are on life's journey, Faith UCC welcomes you.

At Faith UCC we believe in, and worship, a still-speaking God who calls us to meet the needs of all generations, celebrate diversity, and commit to social action and justice for all of God’s children.
We believe in extravagant welcome. We advocate justice for all. Our congregation extends hospitality as a sign of God's inclusive love. We teach that evangelism, offering bread to those in search of it, is God's mission. Our perspective is global, not provincial. We work with, not against, people of other faiths.
Why? Because God is still speaking

Name: TRI-MONY
Description:
TRI-MONY = Triple Harmony Economics

TRI-MONY is the successful eco-social concept for a radically new approach to monetary profit for the most successful companies and company owners worldwide, and for other ultra-rich stakeholders.

TRI-MONY's aim is to achieve an acute ethical and actual reorientation of the global economy. Until now, maximizing monetary profit has been an end in itself. As a radical yet more harmonious alternative, TRI-MONY is calling for the generation of maximum reasonable eco-social impact according to a predefined logic of action.

"Humankind, and therefore every single one of us, must make a decision: either we radically change the way we deal with monetary profit for the better, or we continue to exert our overall impact and remain the monster that we are." Frank Martin Püschel

Description:

To better educate not only our youth, but our older generations as well. Life is about so much more than war and death and I want the people of Afghanistan to experience that!

Name: Euxinus Foundation
Description:
EUXINUS Foundation is a cultural, scientific, social, non-governmental, non-political and non-profit establishment.

The name of the establishment, inspired by one of the ancient names of the Black Sea, Pontus Euxinus, proposes a symbolic projection of a cultural and spiritual area, determinative for the configuration of the European civilization.

The statutes and current form of organization of the Foundation have been settled on June 19, 2003.

The main activities of the EUXINUS Foundation:
- activities for education and research covering a wide range of disciplines (anthropology and ethnology, social and political sciences, historical and philological sciences, theology and philosophy, as well as their interference areas with other disciplines).
- philanthropic and charitable activities, based on the principle of mutual aid and social harmony.
- collaboration with other cultural, scientific, educational and charity institutions, from our country and from all over the world.

Description: Giving back is in our blood and our Culture. We love seeing the positive impacts our sponsorships have on our local communities and we believe that giving back is extremely important. Name: Homo Hubilis APS
Description:
Homo Hubilis APS è un’associazione di promozione sociale costituitasi  formalmente il 30 luglio 2019 e  nasce da un’idea di Roberto Salvato  nel 2017.
Dopo l’homo habilis, l’homo erectus e l’homo sapiens sapiens siamo entrati nell’era dell’ homo hubilis.
Il nome deriva dall’unione delle parola habilis (abile)  e la parola “hub”.
Hub  è un termine inglese che in italiano si può tradurre come  “dispositivo per connettere più elaboratori a una rete e più reti fra  loro”.

Oggi siamo più che mai connessi e connettori a nostra volta, siamo  parte di una rete connessa ad altre reti di persone, di relazioni, di  saperi, di prassi.
Diventa così di vitale importanza avere i “codici di accesso” a  questo mondo. In un epoca dove abbiamo a disposizione tutte le  informazioni che cerchiamo cresce paradossalmente l’ignoranza. 
Questo  perchè mancano le competenze per accedere in modo libero e  con spirito  critico a tali informazioni.
L’obiettivo di questa associazione è promuovere la cultura, le  conoscenze, le buon pratiche  per poter diventare “homini hubilis”.
Linea guida e faro ispiratore della nostra associazione è la risoluzione  adottata dall’Onu il 25 Settembre 2015 
Trasformare il nostro mondo:  l’Agenda 2030 per lo Sviluppo Sostenibile.
Gli obiettivi principali perseguiti dalla nostra associazione sono

    ISTRUZIONE DI QUALITA’

Assicurare un’istruzione di qualità, equa ed inclusiva, e promuovere opportunità di apprendimento permanente per tutti.

    LAVORO DIGNITOSO E CRESCITA ECONOMICA

Incentivare una crescita economica duratura, inclusiva e sostenibile,  un’occupazione piena e produttiva ed un lavoro dignitoso per tutti

    IMPRESE, INNOVAZIONE E INFRASTRUTTURE
Costruire una infrastruttura resiliente e promuovere l’innovazione ed una industrializzazione equa, responsabile e sostenibile
Rendere le città e gli insediamenti umani inclusivi, sicuri, duraturi e sostenibili
PARITÀ DI GENERE
Raggiungere l’uguaglianza di genere e l’empowerment

Name: Raika NGO
Description:
RAIKA est une ONG créée en 2018 qui a pour objectif de participer à sensibiliser et  d’éduquer que la préservation de l’environnement et le développement humain peut aller ensemble, main dans la main, 

Voulant œuvrer en faveur du développement durable, l’ONG intervient dans les zones où la biodiversité peut être préserver à travers l’implication et la collaboration avec la population locales, C’est pourquoi nous il est important d’apporter une collaboration dans la réhabilitation et la rénovation de certains biens communs qui contribue au développement locale.

Le projet d’éducation environnemental  est aussi né de cette optique voulant responsabiliser  et sensibiliser les plus jeunes dans la protection de l’environnement,

Le reboisement fait partie des solutions à apporter pour lutter contre cette déforestation massive que Madagascar fait face aujourd’hui, reboiser également pour lutter contre le changement climatique.

Name: Words To The Wise, Inc.
Description:
Words To The Wise has a mission to end homelessness in Southern California within the next 10 years. The organization is working on long-term solutions to the crisis through round-table discussions and legislation. 

So far, the organization has worked with several student organizations, Union Rescue Mission in Skid Row - Los Angeles, Central City Health, Insaan For Humanity, and Greater West Hollywood Food Coalition to provide food, water,  clothing, access to shelter, haircuts, advice, and counseling. 

With over 100 volunteers, Words To The Wise has been able to provide food for over 6,000 people. 

501(c)(3) Non-Profit: 81-2587226

We are an independent, not-for-profit charity founded by Roy Chen Zhang in Calgary, Alberta, Canada. Our mission is to improve the lives of orphans in Africa, and we are striving to achieve this in several ways:
- We gather donations from people, smaller entities, and corporations. Click here to go to our donations page.
- We operate clubs in Canadian schools, led and supported by students within those schools. Currently, we have clubs in Sir Winston Churchill High School, Western Canada High School, Forest Lawn High School, and Westmount Charter School. The clubs raise awareness and funds for our mission. Click here to see how to become an Area Director, that is, a representative leader of United For Africa Foundation at your school.
- We utilize the funds we gather towards building and sustaining orphanages so that the children can find shelter. Click here to find out about our goal of building our first orphanage.
- We organize volunteering trips to the orphanage building sites. Click here to find out more about how you can join.

O projeto CHOICES do LuzEspalhai.org, visa oferecer conteúdo de qualidade para pessoas que buscam saber um pouco mais sobre empreendedorismo, independente se aplicarão os conhecimentos nas organizações onde são contratados ou em suas próprias empresas.

Description: Since 1997, the Greater Bucky Open strives to bring comfort, support and welcome distraction to patients and families at the University of Wisconsin Health's American Family Children's Hospital along with showing appreciation for Bucky Badger student mascots. The GBO's main annual event surrounds a festive golf outing with Wisconsin notables on the Friday before a home Badgers football game. The GBO continually sells out and the staff is comprised of 100% volunteers. Huge thank you to our generous sponsors and participants for their continued generous support!

Name: DonateAnyBook
Description:
Through our platform anyone can create an account on our website and put their books up for donation.One can also get the donated book for free from anywhere in the United States and we will ship it to them.

We also support our local community by actively conducting book-drives and donating books to nearby schools.

Recently, we have also expanded our services to India.

Every day, companies produce tons of archives, use tons of papers and cartons.
In India, families cannot afford buying notepads for their kids, and this makes their education more difficult.

We collect papers, recycle them, and produce notepads for these kids.

Side effect: we also give a job to some workers.

Description: All children have the right to the essentials in life including access to clean water, food and medical assistance on a regular basis. The Pack the Essentials Project aims to provide much needed assistance to communities in Siem Reap through our food programs, the installation of bio-sand water filtration systems and education.

Name: The Key Class
Description:
The purpose of The Key Class is to teach the tools of successful business and social skills for anyone, especially youth, who wants to succeed in life. In both personal and professional situations every society has traditions in place that determine the way people are judged by the people they meet. Understanding these guide lines in society can make or break a person in social and business circles. Because these social rules are constantly changing, it is necessary to be aware of the latest etiquette techniques.

Our Mission Statement:
The Key Class strives to deliver a comprehensive yet simple guide to social and business etiquette to give participants the competitive edge in obtaining jobs, advancing in their current job or gaining entrance to college. The training is designed and beneficial for people of any age from teens to adults.

Name: The Tree House Project
Description: The Tree House Project was started to lend a helping hand by donating articles of clothing in the US. For every item purchased from our site, we donate an item to help a child, teen or adult in need. Our mission is simple, to help others meet their basic needs.

Name: Stichting Welzijnswijzer
Description:
De stichting heeft als doel om jongeren in en rondom de Stedendriehoek (Deventer, Apeldoorn en Zutphen) met een afstand tot de samenleving meer welzijn in hun leven te laten ervaren.
Meedoen in de maatschappij, voor jezelf en elkaar zorgen is belangrijk en
betekenisvol en voor de mensen die dit (net) niet zelfstandig redden biedt de
stichting een steuntje in de rug, door middel van een arbeids- en/of
dagbesteding, en het verrichten van al wat hiermee verband houdt of daartoe bevorderlijk kan zijn.
Tot dit doel behoort niet het doen van uitkeringen aan de oprichter of aan hen
die deel uitmaken van organen van de stichting. De stichting beoogt het algemeen nut en heeft geen winstoogmerk.

Name: Sister Connection
Description:
Sister Connection invests in the widows of Burundi, creating opportunities for financial independence.

Sister Connection was founded in 2006 to help widows rebuild their lives after decades of war. Civil conflict left a generation of widows and orphans without status and without support. We connected them to donors who provided the means to rebuild homes and support them financially.

Today, Sister Connection serves a new generation of widows. While the memory of war is fading, the economic prospects for widows and their children remain bleak. Our goal has shifted from the immediate needs of charity to a push for self-sufficiency and sustainable development.

Name: Propinquity LLC
Description: Propinquity LLC matches local businesses and like-minded individuals with nonprofits that share a high level of propinquity. With similar customers and constituents, these associations are more likely to fundamentally and financially benefit all participants.

Name: BILWA Foundation
Description:
BILWA Foundation is a Philanthropic Foundation set up in July 2014 to promote and uphold nuances of humanity, by supporting social causes of Women Empowerment, New Age Education, Music, Art and Literature.

The guiding light for the Foundation are the values lived and upheld by Smt. M LakshmiDevi and Sri. Nanjunda Sastry throughout their lives - Prudence, Humanity, Dedication, Self Reliance, “Vyakthi Swathanthrya”

Name: Estrada Wempen Foundation
Description:
The Estrada Wempen Foundation supports kids and adults facing life challenges related to addiction and other difficult situations—a charitable organization working to become an official 501(c)(3) organization.

Bryan and Michella Wempen founded the Wempen Estrada Foundation as a charity organization in 2018; it's not currently a 501(c)(3), but they are committed to achieving that status by 2026. Please get in touch with us with any questions or suggestions at info@cerrillosroad.com

Name: ROARforHope.org
Description:
The ROAR for Hope Project mission is to create an open source, web-based platform that collects and shares knowledge cultivated by the charitable endeavors of the project and a community of followers.

The ROAR for Hope Project is built on the following principles.  While they are fundamental to what we are solving for, we will remain open to amending them as we grow and learn more about how our work benefits worthy causes.

Reach
Create a resource library of content accessible anytime, anywhere assisting those seeking to develop their own worthy cause using the collective experiences of project creators, contributors and collaborators.

Opportunity
Leverage social media to raise project awareness, increase the success rate of fund-raising efforts, and actively engage a community of followers and fans.

Advancement
Assert our commitment by consistently adding meaningful content that empowers the charitable interests of all ages and backgrounds.

Responsibility
Lead by example to motivate and inspire people to take action to get involved in a cause that makes a difference in the lives of others.

Name: Positive Shift Alliance
Description:
Positive Shift Alliance exists for the study of personal conciousness and for the foundational recovery, realization and integration of the human mind through our connection to collective awareness.  

We deliver these tools through access to meditation retreats, lectures, seminars and workshops that will help shift your personal perspective and make more holistic choices.

Name: YouBetCha Trading Company
Description: YouBetCha Trading Company was founded through Minnesota State University, Mankato’s Integrated Business Experience. We are a team of highly motivated business students  who  have  a  passion  to  deliver  Minnesota  themed  products  to  our  customers, and  our  product  line  is  full  of  clever  and  useful  products  that  will  appeal  to  our  target market. We feel that through our business acumen, our mission, and our group unity, that YouBetCha Trading Company will be a resounding success in the greater Mankato area.

Name: Give Technology
Description: Headquartered in New Jersey and founded by technology and education leaders, Give Technology has created a model of giving which is essentially free to the donors, provides valuable skills for the recipients, utilizes no paid staff, and gets incredibly useful technology into the hands of those who need it most. 100% of equipment, monetary, and in-kind donations go directly to fund programs which help our recipients. We have no paid staff, so our only overhead costs are associated with the upgrades needed to repurpose our donated inventory. We want every penny to go toward helping those in need.

Name: Association Tié
Philedon Foundation
Description: The Philedon Foundation renews philanthropy. Its goal is to become the individuals and companies tool's so that they can, at their own pace, be and act as an innovative philanthrope. It carries the vision of a philanthropy for all, simple, agile and pioneering.

Name: Carter Brown Foundation
Description:
Carter Brown Foundation is a 501(c)(3) non-profit charity organization whose mission is to provide supplies, services, and financial support benefiting at-risk or in-need animals. Through projects and ongoing support of local shelters and rescues, the foundation is changing lives four paws at a time.

The Carter Brown Foundation was established in 2019 in memory of Carter, a beloved family rescue dog. She spent her 5 short but joyous years as a devoted companion to her humans and playmate to her dog siblings. She spread smiles to everyone she met. She was playful, goofy, gregarious, and terrified of thunderstorms and fireworks. In 2017 Carter was diagnosed with advanced melanoma and passed away. While she left a hole in her family's hearts and home, her passing inspired this foundation to support animals in need so that other pets and humans may have the cherished memories like those she left behind.

Name: Motirõ Saude
Description:
Somos o Motiro, um grupo de profissionais que se uniu para desenvolver soluções tecnológicas de alto impacto, simples, fáceis de serem implantadas e de baixo custo para o tratamento da COVID-19.

Nosso trabalho é totalmente voluntário. E tudo que fazemos é disponibilizado gratuitamente ao SUS - Sistema Único de Saude.

Contamos com a parceria de organizações e empresas privadas para viabilizar nossos projetos e assim garantir que profissionais da saúde e pacientes espalhados pelo Brasil tenham os melhores equipamentos para vencerem a COVID 19.

Name: V2RF (Vision to Reality Foundation)
Description:
V2RF (Vision to Reality Foundation) is fostering an ecosystem of technology projects that will collaborate on developing solutions that will assist us all in realising our highest visions for ourselves, our communities and our world.

V2RF is seeking to raise 100M GBP in funding to start a 3 year project working with hundreds of cutting edge projects to build this ecosystem. V2RF is approaching philanthropic funding individuals and organisations and projects that are interested in the evolution of open source technologies.

Name: Academic Women's Success and Mentorship (AWSM)
Description:
The Academic Women’s Success and Mentorship committee (AWSM) is committed to stimulating dialogue on ways to integrate and sustain a culture of leadership, mentorship, and success among female academics across McMaster University. 

LEADERSHIP - McMaster AWSM aspires to provide capacity building opportunities for female faculty members, graduate, and undergraduate students mentorship.

MENTORSHIP - McMaster AWSM aspires to provide a space for mentoring and networking activities between female faculty, community members and students at every level. 

SUCCESS - McMaster AWSM is committed to fostering an inclusive culture of empowerment in retaining young women in academia and facilitating their success.

Name: 100+ Guys Who Give Tucson
Description:
100 Guys Who Give Tucson was inspired by our sister organization, 100+ Women Who Care Tucson, and similar chapters around the country. We are a local philanthropic group of men from many backgrounds who are committed to making a positive impact in the Tucson and Southern Arizona communities.

We are men who want to give to our community and see this program as a way to combine resources, drastically increasing the impact of our giving. We have a leadership team who plans and organizes events and a group of sponsors that help cover expenses, specifically at our BIG GIVES. We strive to financially support local charities, raise exposure, and increase awareness in a simple and quick format.

We strive to have at least 100 men at each Give, which takes place quarterly. The fixed donation is $100, so $100 x 100 men = $10,000 per give and $40,000 per year, but hopefully we will raise much more through more participation. Our members come from all over the Tucson metro area to participate and we limit the charities to local 501(c)(3) organizations.

Name: Old Mission San Luis Rey Historic Foundation
Description:
The Old Mission San Luis Rey Historic Foundation is a non-profit organization that is committed to the restoration of the historic Mission.

The mission of our foundation is to:

•Support the maintenance, preservation, restoration and historic enhancement of the physical structures of Old Mission San Luis Rey de Francia located in Oceanside, California.
   
• Provide educational programs that promote the greater understanding of the California Mission system and the role that the individual missions play in the history of California and the local community.

Name: KidzFix Foundation
Description:
KidzFix Rally is about giving back to others in need. As part of our journey around Australia we aim to change the lives of sick and disadvantaged children.
We are all motivated by different things. You might have or know a child who has experienced a serious illness/incident or for others it might be about ensuring there are support services and appropriate medical facilities available should there ever be a need in their family. And for some, its the simple fact that children deserve the right to be kids, less worry and more play so whatever we can provide through our fundraising effort to make their lives more enjoyable is worth contributing to.

Whatever your motivation, you can be guaranteed that raising funds through KidzFix will make a difference in the lives of children around Australia as well as your own.

What makes raising money through KidzFix Rally so special?
We are about making a real and measurable difference to childrens lives. As such, you will be able to see and experience the benefits of all your hard fundraising activities.
