Tuljai Foundation aims to help underprivileged families to have a better life while at the same time giving the youth of today an opportunity to learn, grow, and feel like what they do will make a difference.

This foundation was started up by Tuljai Parkar during her final year of engineering after she had raised $12,000 during the Miss Universe New Zealand competition and sponsored 3 underprivileged kids. 

"When you give money to charity, you want to know where it's actually going to, who it's actually helping, and how it is helping" - Tuljai Parkar

"My goal for 'Tuljai Foundation' is to create community projects such as an irrigation system for a rural Indian village to bring fresh water to villages so that the farmers can grow crop, and so that we can stop all those farmers from committing suicide due to water related problems and leaving their young children to look after themselves, or a sweater making workshop at a library that will provide sweaters to keep underprivileged children warm in the cold New Zealand winter"

Anyone and everyone can help, by donating time to help run these projects, or money to provide the financial backing to these projects
To know more about how you can help, please contact the director, Tuljai Parkar on LinkedIn

Name: Split the Bill NY
Description: Split The Bill NY is an initiative created by Sidgmore Family Foundation designed to support local restaurants. We will reimburse 50% of your receipt total, up to $50, when you order takeout, delivery or drive-through from any of our Participating Restaurants.

Name: Pirates Vs. Cancer
Description:
Pirates Vs. Cancer unites diverse groups behind one common goal: empowering children in our communities and around the world to beat cancer. To date, we have raised over $30,000 in just two short years.

Our fundraising model is unique and focuses on maximizing impact for those who truly need it. In past years, 50% of money raised has supported national research while the remaining 50% went to children fighting cancer in our local community. Moving forward, we have made changes to allow 100% of all funds raised to go straight to the kids in our local community hospital who are battling cancer on a daily basis. Not a single dollar comes off the top for administrative expenses, as those funds are raised separately, a fact that we are extremely proud of. 

Our vision is to be the premier philanthropic event on the ECU Health Sciences Campus by providing a supportive and collaborative environment for interdisciplinary teams to push each other to new heights. By bringing us all together and fostering new relationships, Pirates Vs. Cancer will exponentially increase the number of children we can stand beside in their fights with cancer. 

Many kids battling cancer lose their hair involuntarily, but the rest of us have a choice. We are choosing to go bald to stand beside them in this fight.

Name: Beyond Borders
Description:
Fondé en 2015, à l'initiative d'un projet universitaire, Beyond the Border maintenant Beyond Borders cible la construction de maisons éco-responsables pour des personnes en difficulté économique dont l'un des enfants est victime d'un handicap.
Entourés d'une équipe soudée, nous levons des fonds chaque année afin de pouvoir assurer un habitat répondant aux besoins d'une famille. La construction d'une maison nécessite un budget de minimum 5000 dollars.
Pour ce faire, nous ne sommes pas seuls. Nous avons rencontré Tom Gimbert dès notre premier projet et travaillons depuis en partenariat avec lui. En tant qu'architecte mais aussi en tant qu'habitant de Mancora. En plus de Tom, chaque année l'équipe s'agrandit de différents volontaires, partenaires, ouvriers.

Name: SPICY 3
Description:
Un triathlon en équipe de 3 personnes
Un membre de l'équipe est porteur d’un handicap physique, sensoriel ou mental léger.
Les coéquipiers enchaînent ensemble les trois épreuves : 500 m natation, 24 km vélo, 6 km course à pied. 

Les équipes se font parrainer pour récolter des fonds destinés à la fondation L’hymne Aux Enfants. 
Plus d’informations sur la Fondation : www.hymne-aux-enfants.org

Engagement, dynamisme, sport pour tous dans le respect des différences… 
Votre entreprise partage aussi ces valeurs ? Alors, lancez-vous. Constituez une ou plusieurs équipes qui porteront haut vos couleurs. 

En pratique : 
3ème édition : 16/09/2018
Où : Lacs de l’Eau d’Heure - Falemprise
Montant du parrainage : minimum 1.500 €
Nous contacter : www.spicy3.be ou welcome@spicy3.be

Name: Children's Hope Fund
Description: Children’s Hope Fund is dedicated to working with others to promote the education and well-being of impoverished children by providing school supplies, nutritious meals, clothing, and more. By doing this, we can break the cycle of poverty that plagues the poorest children in the Philippines.

Name: Støt Din Sag
Description: Støt Din Sag gør det muligt for idrætsklubber at lave events, hvor de gennem aktiviteter samler penge ind til deres lokale klub/hold og støtte velgørenhed.

Name: PuneCollab
Description: Pune Collab is an online platform aiming to facilitate successful partnerships among NGOs and individuals in Pune to effectively utilize time (volunteering), existing resources, and information. We believe in facilitating collaboration amongst resources in the sector to increase impact.

Name: Effective Altruism USYD
Description: Effective altruism is all about combining empathy, reason and evidence.

Name: Arlo Rose
Description: Arlo Rose

Name: Christmas Morning
Description: Christmas Morning aims to spread the joy of the holiday season throughout our local community by pairing families in need with angel supporters.

Name: The WCP Foundation
Description: The WCP Foundation was born after a long-time dream to contribute to our community through not only the work that we do but also with the profits from our efforts. We want to ensure that through our work we can reach initiatives that create systemic changes while addressing social and economic inequalities such as health disparities, immigration reform, racial inequality, economic empowerment, housing sustainability, and environmental causes.

Name: Dixie Rear Detachment
Description: To support our military service members during their deployment and boosting morale with monthly care packages. Dixie Rear Detachment is run and operated by volunteers only. 100% of the organization's overhead is paid by our board of directors and every cent received through our supporters donation is used toward the making and shipping of those care packages. In the past 12 months we have supported in excess of 1.500 men and women through out all branches of our military, all around the world.

Name: Marlène Sans Frontière
Description: Marlène Sans Frontière assure aux enfants une assistance médicale dans les communautés les plus isolées et vulnérables.

Name: ReSight
Description: ReSight is a 501c3 organization that drives the positive cycle of change through vision.  It addresses a marginalized population: young adults with treatable blindness throughout the developing world.  ReSight helps these people, the majority of whom are unemployed or self-employed, get access to treatment as well as vocational training.

Name: Fondation des écoles catholique du Centre-Est
Description: La Fondation des écoles catholiques du Centre-Est (FECCE) est un organisme de bienfaisance enregistré. Son objectif est de lutter contre les effets de la pauvreté chez les élèves qui fréquentent les écoles catholiques du Centre-Est afin qu’ils puissent surmonter les difficultés auxquelles ils font face quotidiennement. En allégeant leur fardeau, ces élèves pourront se concentrer sur leur apprentissage et leur réussite.

Name: Clever Communities in Action
Description: Clever Communities in Action focuses on the promotion and placement of culturally affirming literature in order to improve literacy rates, build community, and heal traumas inflicted by systemic racism within the African-American community.

Name: Ladies of Virtue and Edification (L.O.V.E.) Ministries
Tibetan Education & Relief Association including Himalayan Communities
Description:
The Tibetan Education & Relief Association including Himalayan Communities – or ‘T.E.R.A.’ - was founded in 1986, and since then we’ve been creating better lives for the very poorest people of impoverished multi-ethnic communities in India, Nepal & Tibet. 

Throughout our history, we’ve helped to support these people with what the western world would consider as ‘basic needs’ - education, health and welfare. Thanks to direct personal connections with our representatives on the ground in Nepal and India, we’re well-placed to quickly respond to requests for help. Through the dedication and commitment of a small international team, our help has reached thousands of people by transforming your donations into emergency food parcels for Covid-19 relief, medications and equipment for lepers, school fees for children, outside toilets for a rural community, winter uniforms for schoolchildren, and so many more essentials that they would otherwise struggle to find, or simply not have at all.
Our Executive Members accept no salary and voluntarily pay for their own expenses so that 100% of your donation goes directly to those in most need. T.E.R.A. is registered for Gift Aid, so that UK taxpayers can make their donation worth 25% more at no extra cost to them.
For information on our latest project work, please visit our website www.tera-charity.ngo, where you can also see our archives, newsletters, Blog, and project histories. You can also find us on Facebook (/tibetaneducationandreliefassociation) and Twitter (TERACharity).

We are an unincorporated charity registered with the Charity Commission of England & Wales, and our registration number is 802974. 

Thank you for your interest in our work! 

PLEASE NOTE: T.E.R.A. is actively seeking funds to support its established and ongoing projects, which are its priorities for the foreseeable future. It is not able to grant funds to other organisations or individuals, so please do not send unsolicited requests for funding. Thank you.

Name: Lets Sow Together
Description:
The aim of Let’s Sow Together (LeST) corporation is to invest in people’s education and careers through interest-free microloans. fellowships and grants for equal opportunity.
LeST commenced in 2014 and already has empowered men and women in their careers and education. To date, LeST has elevated more than 18 individuals with total funds of over $20,000 and counting.
Every day loan and grant requests keep pouring in at LeST.  Due to limited resources available at the moment, the requests are carefully considered and evaluated to determine who can be supported through LeST immediately and efficiently.

Name: WorkingPhilanthropy.com
Description: In 2009 David and Lori Lawson founded the company with the goal of helping organizations, and the companies that serve them, do good great. Today we are working with leading nonprofits, educational institutions, and companies on a variety of projects including identification and development of prospect portfolios; forecasting of charitable giving; social media; rich-content intranets; and cloud-based donor management and engagement platforms.

Name: Asia Water Foundation
Description: Asia Water Foundation and Planet Water Foundation were established to bring clean, safe drinking water to Asia's poorest communities.

Name: Identity Organization
Description: Founded in 2020, Identity is an organization whose mission is to educate communities about multicultural diversity by promoting dialogue between cultures and people through local engagement activities.

Name: The Morgan Johnson Gruenebaum Legacy Fund to Cure Brain Cancer
Description: The Morgan Johnson Gruenebaum Legacy Fund to Cure Brain Cancer was founded to honor Seal Beach native Morgan Gruenebaum (Johnson) - who passed away on September 24, 2019 at 33 years old. All donations raised will advance progress in the diagnosis, prognosis and treatment of brain cancer, specifically glioblastoma multiforme (GBM) at UCLA.

Name: HIMA Foundation
Description: The Hima Foundation is a non-profit organization dedicated to helping the people and ecology of the lost city state of Loulan in the Xinjiang province of China. Loulan was once a thriving and essential trading post on the Silk Road. It is currently threatened by the rapid growth of the Taklimakan Desert. Loulan is losing its battle with nature. The Hima Foundation is committed to improving the odds for Loulan’s natives and its land.

Name: WiFi 4 Life
Description:
WiFi Changes Lives of Homeless People!

We introduce to you the charity project WiFi 4 Life which aims to help homeless people return to a full life.

Our people will share Wi-Fi using borrowed pocket Wi-Fi hotspots around the center of Prague. They will receive meals, clothing, allowances and decent accommodation in return for their services.

The project will not be funded from the taxpayers’ money but exclusively from private donations and grants. However, in order to get it going, the project needs capital for the first two months of its operation. And so we ask you, the people of planet Internet, for a contribution to the achievement of that amount.



THE DAY OF A HOTSPOT OFFICER
8:00 - he sets out from the shelter to the city centre by public transport
8:30 - he receives a breakfast and the Wi-fi hotspot device
9:00 - he sets out to his area where he will share the Wi-fi and a smile
12:00 - he receives a lunch coupon
17:00 - end of the working hours, the hotspot officer receives a dinner and the allowance

The working week runs from Wednesday to Sunday.
Public transport tickets are provided by our organisation.
The hotspot officer gets the meals to go or it is provided to him in a partner restaurant.
Every hotspot officer will be provided with a toilet facility.


AFTER SEVERAL MONTHS
Wifi 4 Life will enable re-integration of these people back into normal life, allow them to adopt healthy habits and to create valuable relationships. Reliable workers will be given a recommendation for future employers.

Name: STEM4fem
Description:
STEM4fem is a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization in Memphis, Tennessee working to help girls pursue interests in STEM related fields. In a society where innovation and change are becoming increasingly important, we hope to show young girls the potential for STEM to change the world.

STEM4fem was founded in the summer of 2017 because the founders noticed two major issues in STEM fields:  there is a major gender gap, and resources and opportunities are not always available to everyone. By providing a platform and network for girls to carry out their ideas and interact with professionals, we hope to help them maximize their potential and help achieve their goals.

Name: Friends of Noah-WI
Description:
As a pet rescue organization. we are committed to providing new opportunities for stray and abandoned animals through placement into permanent homes, educating the public, and offering assistance to pet owners struggling to provide for their pets.

Where we differ is what we ultimately hope for. Many pet rescue organizations hope to continue to adopt pets for years to come. Our goal is that we never have to adopt another pet because they are all in happy, loving homes. That is why our efforts include much more than simply rescue and adoption. 

Visit our PetFinder Page to see who's available for adoption today! https://www.petfinder.com/pet-search?shelterid=WI379

Name: Planet Earth Artist
Description:
Planet Earth Artist strives to help those who are in need of musical instruments. Musicians who don’t have the same opportunities as you and I. This in turn will bring the exposure these bands/musicians need and showcase the talent coming through all parts of Australasia.

Planet Earth Artist embraces any ideas or suggestions you may have. We feel the more people know about this and the more that are directly involved, the greater the chance of us achieving our number one objective. There are many chapters left in this story and I know this idea can unite people regardless of religion, creed or colour. We all deserve music.

Name: Waterday Foundation
Description:
Waterday Foundation offers commodity-based products that you already use, such as commercial bathroom products. Pricing is cost competitive in all markets we serve and 100% of all profits go directly to local charities. 
THERE IS NO CATCH!

Name: Beau Belle
Description: Voor de mooie dingen in het leven. Freelance fondsenwerving en meer.

Name: Sight Ihsan
Description:
“Who is it that would loan Allah a goodly loan so He may multiply it for him many times over? And it is Allah who withholds and grants abundance, and to Him you will be returned.” [II: 245]

The Messenger of God, Sallallahu Alaihi Wasallam said:
"If a slave(of Allah) gives a good charity, Allah accepts it from him, and he takes it in His Right Hand and breeds it as one of you breeds his colt  or young camel. And if a slave(of Allah) gives a mouthful(even as small as a date), it grows in Allah's hand until it becomes as big as a mountain. Therefore you you should give alms."

Name: Willie Lopez Emergency Assistance Fund
Description: The Willie Lopez Emergency Assistance Fund is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization supported by Tailored Brands, Inc. employees who donate money to help their fellow coworkers who are in financial emergencies caused by unforeseen and catastrophic situations. The Fund was started in honor of Willie Lopez, a highly regarded District Manager, who died unexpectedly leaving his wife and four young children in a desperate financial situation. Since supporting the Lopez family in 1997 the Willie Lopez Fund has provided nearly $7 million worth of assistance to thousands of grant recipients. We have no way of knowing what difficulties our employees covered by the Fund will encounter from year to year. Despite the uncertainties of life you can make a difference today by providing comfort, hope, and support to those who need it most.

Name: Sustainable Development Forum
Description:
Explroing ways to build susitainable cities, societies, and relationships. 
Sustainable economy. Sustainable governance. Sustainable humans.

Name: ENSO Paris
Description: Enso Paris is an association based in France for the advancement of artists and their careers.  Enso gives an opportunity for artists to find collaborators and synergies.  It works as a networking and collaboration tool, available to artists to encourage growth, learning and the development of their craft.

Name: Project Safehouse (Uganda)
Description: Project Safehouse is constructing safe homes and structures for the Pamuca clan and their entire village, all by employing the villagers as the preferred labor source.  As well, the project seeks to establish a proper school for the young children, employ an appropriate educator, and fund the ongoing education of the village into the future.  Additional bathroom facilities, water irrigation, improved farming techniques, and access to additional food sources are future  envisioned efforts for Project Safehouse.

Name: TRIFECTA Inc.
Description:
We are a non-profit, non-governmental organization located in the Roanoke and New River Valley focused on empowerment of communities and youth through various educational seminars, economic development, and curated events.

Founded in the Fall of 2017, Trifecta is a non-profit, non-governmental organization focused on the empowerment of communities and youth while facilitating economic development through collective impact initiatives throughout the Roanoke and New River Valleys. 

The future of underrepresented communities within the City of Roanoke depends on enterprises and organizations identifying the very issues that plague our community by creating simplified, collaborative remedial solutions. Trifecta, Inc. is an enterprise and social movement that empowers youth, individuals and communities through economic and community development. Simply stated, our mission is to "Guard it, Nurture it, and Reveal" it as we aim to strengthen community partnerships and collaborative efforts to create cultural paradigm shifts and relieve socioeconomic disparities within the STAR City Community.

Name: europ'raid ensiame
Description:
Nous nous présentons : PELLET Alexandre, COLAS Aurélien, LESAGE César, trois étudiants en école d'ingénieur à l'ENSIAME prêts à participer à l'Europ'raid. Un raid humanitaire a travers l'Europe pour livrer des fournitures dans des écoles défavorisées de pays d'Europe de l'Est à bord d'une Peugeot 205. 
Nous recherchons des sponsors pour participer à cet événement médiatisé.

Name: Authentical
Description:
We leven in een boeiende en complexe samenleving met grote uitdagingen. Wij willen mensen, bedrijven en organisaties helpen om een maximale positieve en blijvende impact creëren op de samen-leving van vandaag en morgen.

We doen dit door het begeleiden van start-ups en het omkaderen van projecten in bedrijven en organisaties. Daarbij vertrekken we vanuit de talenten en passies van de mens achter de doelstelling.

Naast deze projecten, brengen we onze inzichten ook over via onze eigen Academy.

Name: Brice Bomo Education Foundation
Description:
Charitable organization based and legally registered in Tbilisi, Georgia.
The Foundation’s primary focus areas are:
• Construction and maintenance of facilities in schools located in rural areas;
• provision of teaching and learning resources to schools located in rural areas; and
• provision of limited financial assistance to teachers of schools located in rural areas.

Name: Most High Ministries
Description: Most High Ministries is a faith-based addiction recovery and community outreach mission. We connect individuals with resources throughout the community to help them achieve success in recovery.

Name: PhysioTraveler
Clean Water Movement
Description:
Clean Water Movement, a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization, was founded in 2010 with the vision to work towards solving the global water crisis through sustainable, long-term solutions that eliminate dependency local populations have on foreign aid. 

CWM serves as a voice and a face for educational programs in rural populations of Uganda and Bolivia who are considered the poorest of the poor. These programs are centered around teaching these communities how to drill their own wells for clean water. With that foundation, they then teach appropriate farming methods, crop management, small business leadership, and much more. We are helping fund a large training center in Uganda in partnership with Water for All Int'l for missionaries and leaders throughout Africa to come learn these life-changing programs.

Name: Projeto PALMA
Description: Somos uma organização sem fins lucrativos que beneficia crianças carentes que tenham atraso no desenvolvimento neuromotor com atendimentos individuais de fisioterapia, fonoaudiologia e terapia ocupacional.

Name: Stephen & Camilla Forte Foundation
Description: The Stephen & Camilla Forte Foundation is a non-profit foundation established to support law enforcement officers and their families in Southern California through education, scholarships, community and business relationships

Name: Cystic Fibrosis Educational Services Corp.
Description:
Cystic Fibrosis Educational Services Corp. (CFEDSC.org) is a federally recognized 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization formed exclusively for charitable and educational purposes.

The purpose of CFEDSC is to provide educational material to the public about emerging medical and legal issues associated with Cystic Fibrosis.  In particular, CFEDSC reviews, summarizes and presents emerging scientific and medical developments towards the treatment and cure of Cystic Fibrosis.  This information is presented to the public; primarily through research reports, abstracts, full length academic style papers and clinical trial reports.

CFEDSC also raises funds through various fundraising events and makes financial contributions to academic research labs performing high quality translational research.

Name: TENNIS FOR ALL - AFRICA
Description:
A nossa MISSÃO é DESPERTAR o PODER INFINITO que tem DENTRO cada SER HUMANO.
FOCO na FELICIDADE PLENA e MAIS RESULTADOS como CONSEQUÊNCIA!

Name: JW Patterson Foundation
Description:
The J.W. Patterson Foundation is a non-profit 501c3 organization dedicated to providing opportunities in the fields of speech, debate, and leadership to talented high school and college students who would otherwise be excluded due to financial need. It is an avenue for those who acquired a world view of problem-solving through forensics, debate, and leadership to provide equal opportunity for future leaders. 

Our mission is to promote the academic study of persuasion, debate, and public speaking, the major instruments for making decisions resolving differences in a free society.

To this end, the Foundation's primary goal is the enhancement of academic training in the practice of debate on both the intercollegiate and interschool levels. In addition, the Foundation seeks to promote leadership training for both college and high school student leaders. 

Our founder, Dr. J.W. Patterson, strongly believes in the contribution that training in public speaking plays in leadership roles and has devoted his entire career to the instruction of students from all across the country in the field of speech and debate.

He has received hundreds of testimonials over the years from former students who attest that the training they received in debate and public speaking both strengthened their leadership skills and helped better prepare them for their chosen professions. Many of these individuals have cited their speech and debate experience as played a critical role in their success. 

Unfortunately, many institutions do not provide adequate resources, if any, for these opportunities. It is our goal, therefore, to promote these skills and do our best to make them available to all students, regardless of income accessibility.

Name: The Up Foundation
Description:
What began as a late evening conversation between a father and son, led to start of the UP Foundation. A family foundation based on the principle of helping children in need through family services, education, and awareness. 

"It's a Great Day for Up", the Dr. Suess children's classic, was the inspiration behind the Up Foundation. Its message is simple, look up to a better day. "Everyone's getting up" and that's what we want to inspire. Stand up for a better future for our children.

Our Mission

To provide support to children and their families affected by a disabilitating disease or disorder. 
Join us in our grassroots efforts to make a difference in our community and to touch the life of a child.

With your support, the future of our foundation is bright as we broaden the scope of charitable organizations in need. 

We live in a world where the need is great and if we can make a difference, then we intend to do just that.

Join Us!

Name: Chapter 702, California Scholarship Federation
Description: The California Scholarship Federation aims to recognize and encourage service and academic success, and Chapter 702 of CSF supports and furthers those aims at Leigh High School. As the largest student-led organization on campus, we offer chapter scholarships and service opportunities for academically achieving students.

Name: Spencer Environmental Consulting
Description:
Spencer Environmental Consulting's services include:

ISSUE CAMPAIGNS - Working with clients on campaigns around single issues and policies, such as challenging a natural gas pipeline, as well as multifaceted initiatives like building support for regional emissions reduction policies and programs.

➤Campaign Assessments
➤Campaign Planning
➤Campaign Implementation
➤Building Coalitions
➤Media Strategy & Communications
➤Opinion Leader / Influencer Outreach & Cultivation

GRANTMAKING: Consulting with donors, foundations and grantmaking entities to identify and better achieve goals.

➤ Research
➤ Program Analysis 
➤ Impact Strategy
➤ Strategic Planning

Name: Reach Out Morongo Basin
Description: Established in 1998, Reach Out Morongo Basin (ROMB) is an Interfaith Volunteer Caregiving Program. Our mission is to provide support, help, education, and referrals to our senior and disabled neighbors in the Morongo Basin. We are able to use volunteers to provide informal caregiving to our neighbors. This includes: friendly visits (both in person and by phone), shopping and errands, personal business help, minor home repairs, yard work, and transportation to medical, dental, and social service appointments. Reach Out fills in the gaps of traditional paid providers. The nature of the tasks assigned to our volunteers appear ordinary. Yet, these tasks often fall through the cracks as our neighbors age. Having before only catered to Twentynine Palms, Reach Out chose to expand. Our neighbors in the entire Morongo Basin were seeking our services. Calls came in from private citizens, caregivers, and other agencies. Our local churches are on the front line of these calls and help when possible. Unlike other agencies, we have a large amount of volunteers that come wanting to help out.

Name: LunchBag Social
Description: Lunch Bag Social is a community organization that began as a grassroots initiative turned local movement. Providing meals, services, essentials and educational materials to the under served and homeless populations is our root cause. The purpose of the organization is to ignite social change in urban areas by encouraging young professionals and millennials to be excited about philanthropy, giving back and community service.   To date, over 50,000 meals, toiletries and school supply bags have been hand delivered by over 1500 unique volunteers  since June of 2016.

Name: Dignity Toilets for Togo
Description: Ceci est un projet de la Rotary Nor'Wester Foundation à Port Angeles, Washington. Chaque famille participe en fournissant une partie du matériel, en fournissant de la main-d'œuvre ou en fournissant de la nourriture à l'équipage. Nous embauchons des travailleurs locaux et achetons des matériaux locaux, ce qui ajoute un stimulant économique à la communauté locale tout en améliorant ses réserves en matière de sécurité, d’assainissement et d’eaux souterraines.

Name: Wright Torres Foundation
Description: The Wright Torres Foundation supports participants in, and those affected by, the areas of animal wellness, mental health, and music. We accomplish this mission through scholarships, donations, and other supportive endeavors.

Name: Rotary klub Nova Gradiska
Description: Rotary je međunarodno udruženje osoba iz stručnog i poslovnog života koje se posvećuju humanim ciljevima, cijene i promiču visoka etička načela u poslovnom i privatnom životu, te šire dobru volju i razumijevanje u svijetu. Rotary je skup lidera koji razmjenjuju ideje i nastoje humanitarnim djelovanjem poboljšati situaciju u lokalnoj zajednici.

Name: Causa Clothing Ltd.
Description:
Creating awareness and demonstrating solidarity is the main objective of us. We design and sell apparel to a target group of mainly young adults around the world. Each piece of clothing contains an integrated QR code, which leads to our website providing information to the nature of a certain conflict, health or environmental issue.

Causa is a charitable fashion brand with a clear vision. Its aim is to educate consumers on current events and direct donations to incredible social projects.

Name: The Ikoku Charitable Trusts and Foundations
Description: The Ikoku Charitable Trusts and Foundations are non-profit organizations that promote and serve the public interest in Africa and its global diasporas.

Name: The Commons
Description:
The Commons believes in the democratization of photography. We believe that photography should not be limited to a wealthy, monochromatic few. Our world is boundlessly eclectic and diverse; talent and vision hail from all walks of life. 

Our mission is to provide a program for students to learn photography as a means of self-expression and skilled work. This program includes instruction, mentorship, exposure to opportunities in student’s cities, and continued education.

Name: High Tides International
Description: High Tides International is a Canadian non-profit organization whose mission is to provide an outlet for education, creativity and self-exploration in communities where resources are needed. Our current area of focus is Northern Peru. We have opened two community centers in the town of Lobitos through which we run our programs: Girls Club, yoga classes, and a library.  We have goals of being able to offer post secondary scholarships to local youth, and to complete a pool restoration project to be able to offer community swimming lessons for people of all ages.

Name: Climb for a Cure Inc.
Description:
MISSION
Climb for a Cure Inc. is a domestic nonprofit organization founded in 2009 by dedicated friends at DePauw University in Greencastle, Indiana. Our mission is to improve to the treatment, quality of life, and long-term outlook for children with brain cancer, through increased awareness and fundraising.

AWARENESS
By providing the public with clear information about the disease, and knowledge of the struggle these children and their families must endure when confronted by it, we hope to increase support for more intensive research programs, and hopefully, a better fighting chance.

RESEARCH
Only scientific research will identify the causes and lead to effective treatments for brain tumours in children. When you donate, every dollar goes directly to one of our three partner charities - all of which fund cutting-edge research in this field.

VOLUNTEERS
Climb for a Cure is entirely operated by student-volunteers. This strong backbone of selflessness allows us to keep our operational costs separate from your donations, and give you the peace of mind that you deserve.

Name: Make a Mark
Description:
Make a Mark was established in 1993 as a non-profit foundation and with your help is focused its efforts on the looming orphan crisis. 

As a result of the devastating AIDS pandemic, millions of orphans and vulnerable children are being left to fend for themselves. Make A Mark is working with a coalition of partners to support lasting and sustainable business solutions to the growing orphan crisis.

Name: Impact On The Ground Foundation
Description:
We are a youth-driven not-for-profit that addresses institutional inefficiencies and enhances the learning experience in rural India

By employing a hands-on, community-sourced approach, we ensure that we engage all relevant stakeholders as part of the solution-- in order to create interventions that are needs-based. Through our signature programs, The Naya Rang Initiative and Global Saathi Initiative, we have touched the lives of thousands of children in rural India since our founding in October 2018.

Name: Meow or Never
Description: Meow or Never is a Georgia 501(c)(3) non-profit organization that works to help the stray and feral cats and kittens of Atlanta through humane TNR (trap-neuter-return), or adoption into loving homes when returning or relocating is not possible. As an all-volunteer group, we are also dedicated to community outreach and education, promoting low-cost spay/neuter programs readily available for domestic and community cats. We also sponsor spay/neuters for owned pets (both cats and dogs).

Name: «People's Project» center of volunteers
Description: Over the years, we have collected more than 100 million hryvnias in charitable donations and implemented more than 100 projects about military assistance, medical care and social support. And all of it – by a limited forces of a small team.

Name: AMEDICAusa, Inc.
Description: A non-profit corporation formed specifically for providing medical, educational and disaster relief aid in Guatemala and Central America.

Name: Rotaract Club of South East Bay
Description: Rotaract is a service and business club for young professionals ranging from ages 18 to 30. As part of the Rotary International family, Rotaractors (members of Rotaract) are part of an international family of over 1.2 million members in over 160 different countries. The Rotaract Club of South East Bay is based in the San Francisco Bay Area cities of Fremont, Newark, Milpitas, and Union City in Rotary District 5170.

Name: Yayasan Pemuda Batak Bangkit
Description:
Siapa Kami? (Who are We?)
Pemuda Batak Bangkit didirikan oleh para pemuda batak yang memiliki visi untuk membawa perubahan dan menjadi perekat sosial untuk masyarakat Batak yang berlandaskan nilai-nilai, filsafat, dan teologi Kristen. Institusi ini adalah institusi yang bersifat nirlaba (non-profit) dan memanfaatkan prinsip gotong royong dari kita untuk kita. Melalui institusi ini, secara bersama-sama menjaring koneksi (network) baik dengan masyarakat di bonapasogit maupun mereka yang di perantauan (diaspora) serta multi-stakeholder lainnya untuk marsitukkol-tukkolan memberikan kontribusi untuk membangun masyarakat dan generasi muda di tanah Batak. 

Pendiri
Pendiri (Founder)
Derwin Tambunan, M.Sc

Co–Founder
Candra Haris Lubis, S.E, M.A, CSA\
dr. RD Tiopan Napitupulu

Pembina
Ir. Tujuh Martogi Siahaan, M.M

Pengawas
Dr. Tumbur Tobing, S.E, MBA

Name: Ames Pride
Description:
Ames Pride was established out of a need to build community for LGBTQIA+ people in Ames, Iowa State University and the surrounding area. We are committed to building relationships within the community and creating bridges with other groups in the Ames area.

We are a community of volunteers who identify as lesbian, gay, bisexual, transgender, queer, asexual, aromantic, or otherwise non-heterosexual, non-cisgender, as well as heterosexual and cisgender allies.  We strive to do work that promotes community building among LGBTQA+ people, to educate the community on issues affecting LGBTQA+ people, and to facilitate an environment that values the intersectional identities of all people living in and around the City of Ames, IA.

We are committed to maintaining a community that recognizes and values the inherent worth and dignity of every person; that fosters mutual respect among its directors and volunteers; that encourages each individual to reach their own potential; and that builds momentum for the equitable treatment of people.

Name: The WEL Foundation
Description: The Walter & Edward Luedke Memorial Foundation, established in 1993 and incorporated in 1999, honors the legacies of Walter and Edward Luedke by transforming personal loss into community strength. Founded by John F. Luedke in memory of his brothers, our foundation channels their stories into action—addressing mental health awareness, medical support, and community wellness initiatives.  WEL Foundation envisions a community where mental and physical wellness are accessible to all, where resilience is celebrated, and where every individual has the support they need to thrive. We create lasting impact through direct assistance to individuals, families, and community-wide initiatives.

Name: Association Prometheus Nemesis
Description:
Association à but non lucratif et humanitaire. 
Objectif principal : Aide alimentaire d'urgence aux personnes sans domicile fixe. 
L'association, forte d'une cinquantaine de bénévoles a pour but de lever des fonds, organiser des collectes de denrées alimentaires afin de les redistribuer aux personnes en situation de grande précarité. Par la suite l'association à comme objectif d'organiser des événements de type soupe populaire et collectes de vêtements, toujours en faveur des personnes sans domicile fixe. 
En partenariat avec l'école supérieure de commerce internationale ISG Lyon, dont la plupart des bénévoles font aujourd'hui partie, l'association aura pour perspective d'évolution d'ouvrir l'admission aux personnes extérieures, voire de créer des antennes dans différentes grandes villes. 
L'objectif secondaire de l'association sera, dès l'année 2019 d'envoyer plusieurs équipages participer au Raid Humanitaire 4L Trophy. Raid visant cette fois, par le biais de Renault 4L, à acheminer des fournitures scolaires, sportives et alimentaires au enfants vivant dans le désert du Sahara.

Name: Housing One Student at a Time, Inc
Description: Housing One Student At a Time (HOST) is an organization with an attempt to decrease the number of homeless 4-year college students. It is our goal to help these students relieve the stress of finding and keeping housing so that they may put their focus elsewhere-- graduating and finding a career.

Name: TodoVien
Description:
Hacemos Marketing Social para el posicionamiento de marcas comprometidas en generar un impacto positivo en la sociedad, contribuyendo a que logren sus objetivos de mercadotecnia y con ello obtengan una sustentabilidad a largo plazo.

Conectamos a las empresas, medios de comunicación y líderes de opinión con personas y acciones que mejoren nuestro entorno para poder cambiar el mundo.

Name: DreamS Foundation
Description: DreamS Foundation promotes social impact by supporting the fundraising process of non-profit organizations and social businesses. Honouring those who aspire to a brighter future and asserting that social and financial conditions should not limit the size of a dream, DreamS supports and advises its proteges in developing and launching their funding strategy, bridging the gap between social entrepreneurs and donors.

Name: Dr.-Max-Walter-Gerster-Stiftung
Description:
Stiftungszweck:
Finanzierung der Ausbildung (Studium, Lehre etc.) von talentierten Jugendlichen, deren Eltern bei der Lanz Oensingen AG oder bei einer andern KMU mit Sitz im Bezirk Gäu arbeiten oder gearbeitet haben und Jugendlichen, die in den Seegemeinden ansässig sind. Sie kann zu diesem Zweck die Jugendlichen direkt unterstützen oder die Kosten für deren Ausbildungen ganz oder teilweise übernehmen. Der Anwendungsbereich ist nicht beschränkt, mithin können die Jugendlichen im kulturellen, sportlichen oder berufsspezifischen Bereich tätig sein. Die Tätigkeit der Stiftung beschränkt sich auf das Gebiet der Schweiz.

Name: Start Up Guatemala
Description: Startup Guatemala es el primer lugar en donde las startup, los fondos de inversión, los servicios empresariales, los corporativos, los mentores, la academia y el gobierno uniran esfuerzos para crear casos de éxito desde Guatemala hacia el mundo.

Name: Illinois No. 3 Foundation
Description: The Illinois No. 3 Foundation supports organizations and projects that conserve and enhance land and water resources through sustainable practices, provide access to healthy food, shelter and education for those in need, advance medical research and promote the arts.

Name: UnlockFood
Description:
UnlockFood is an app that is focused on fighting world hunger.

The app places a small banner on the user's lock screen which generates a small amount of advertising revenue every time the user unlock their phone. This money is then used to feed hungry people all over the world.

Name: Make Compassion Cool Again
Description: MCCA is a nonprofit organization focused on a 360 giving model: Giving back to yourself through fitness, giving back to others through compassion, and giving back to the environment through sustainable choices. Organizing runs from city to city during which random acts of kindness are implemented and sustainable choices are made.

Name: Youth Beyond Enterprises
Description:
Youth Beyond Enterprises is an online resource for young entrepreneurs and young leaders in rural Ontario. I am primarily focused on fueling the inherent creativity of youth by showing them how they can take their ideas and turn them into viable businesses that are sustainable within rural Ontario.

All blog topics and information come from my own personal research and experiences as both an entrepreneur and as someone who grew up in rural Ontario. 

The information here can be used to either help teens launch a business venture or can help them to identify community problems/issues that they believe need resolving and the solutions needed. There are blogs filled with inspirational quotes, ideas for first time businesses, the benefits of being a young(er) entrepreneur and even how to figure out if entrepreneurship is right for you. This is designed to complement and enhance the already existing organizations within our community. After all, aren’t we here for the same reasons? You know, to help out our young people and the betterment of our community?

My Goals: Be the organization that brings all of the entrepreneurship-focused organizations together to collaborate for the sake of the community and our youth, guide you to where you need to/want to go next (be it finding a business counsellor, grant dollars, a place to talk to someone when you’re feeling depressed, etc.), provide extra information that you need access to for your business (i.e. organizational tips, how to register your business in different areas, why you need a mentor, etc.), and provide my insights as a young professional trying to make a go of it myself.

Name: Grise for Aphasia
Description:
Upon discovering the lack of awareness for aphasia and in an effort to address the particular difficulty that they face with communication barriers, we launched our organization named Grise for Aphasia, or Grise for short.

Our main initiative is currently a buddy system that pairs volunteers, such as high school and college students, with someone who has aphasia and their families. We are collaborating with organizations and different founders in order to bridge our buddy system with their existing events, such as their support groups.
 
The buddy system provides an opportunity for people who have aphasia to connect with others in a friendly setting during a time of large isolation for them (currently remote, but can be in-person once appropriate), and volunteers can also assist them with their conversations and speech. It gives them a unique chance to interact with those from younger generations. These sessions are on a regular basis depending on the desires of a specific group, such as bi-weekly, and it can also bring together different families who have aphasia.
 
In preparation, we are creating webinars and intuitive guides for volunteers so that they have the proper etiquette and have a plan for activities to do during their sessions.

(Fun fact: “Grise” is pronounced as “Greez”. Grise is French for gray, which is the same color as the awareness ribbon for aphasia.)
 

Interested in joining us? Check out our Linktree for some opportunities: https://www.linktr.ee/griseforaphasia/
Email us: grise.for.aphasia@gmail.com

Name: Just Love Collaborative Inc.
Description: Our mission is to support the development and overall advancement of women and girls of color through our Just Love Immersion School. We focus on the whole person while fortifying self-love, simultaneously building skills to create and sustain a greater quality of life.

Name: Duende Consultants, LLC
Description:
Over the past 15 years Duende Consultants has lead large-scale data systems transformation projects for some of the most respected foundations in the world. 

As CEO and Founder, Sheila Kappeler-Finn serves as the key advisor and strategic analyst for organizations before, during and after significant technology modernization efforts.  

As one of the first contributors to the Fluxx grants management platform, Sheila has worked with this technology since software development began at the Energy Foundation (EF) in 2009. Equipped with an in-depth knowledge of the philanthropic space as well as the Fluxx platform, Sheila 
* delivers in-depth technology audits for mature Fluxx clients; 
* leads clients through Fluxx modernization and upgrade endeavors; and
* advises and coaches clients on important technology decisions and their downstream impacts.

A passionate systems thinker and expert facilitator, Sheila is recognized for leading effective discovery sessions with diplomacy, clarity and respect. Sheila is committed to simplifying processes and risk management procedures for her grant maker clients so that they can focus on substance, strategy and the evolving needs of the populations they serve.

Name: regroupement des personens handicapés du haut richelieu
Description: fondé en 1989 le regroupement compte environ 275 membres à son actif. la société gère le transport adapté sur le teritoire de 14 municipalité, en plus de la gestion d'immeubles conçu en fonction des personnes handicapés, à mobilité réduite, à faible revenu et dans son nouveau projet Sieur de Normandie des logements pour des gens lourdement hadicapé. la société via ses filiales Vie Autonome Montérégie et L'Envol prodigue du supprot et de l'aide aux personnes handicapés et pour l'Envol du répit de jour pour des jeunes souffranr d'un handicap.

Name: MMCC Netherlands
Description:
Mmcc netherlands is de nederlandse vestiging van het Afghan Mobile Mini Circus for Children (mmcc).
Deze stichting zet zich in voor kinderen in het door oorlog geteisterde Afghanistan en biedt ze kansen om op te groeien zoals ieder kind zou moeten kunnen opgroeien, zelfvertrouwen op te bouwen, trauma’s te helen en weer te lachen doormiddel van het zogenoemde ‘social circus’.

Name: Impact Ayiti
Description:
ENGLISH: IMPACT’s mission is to help young Haitians view themselves as potential entrepreneurs who can create economic prosperity, rather than destined to a life of poverty. IMPACT empowers high school students by providing entrepreneurship education and tools to help them become financially responsible and conscious of their ability to impact Haiti’s economy.

HAITIAN CREOLE: Misyon IMPACT se ede jenn ayisyen yo wè tèt yo kòm potansyèl antreprenè ki ka kreye pwosperite ekonomik, olye moun ki destine pou viv nan povrete. IMPACT ap ranfòse elèv lekòl segondè nan ba yo edikasyon Antreprenarya ak zouti kap ede yo vin responsab ak lajan yo e konsyan de kapasite yo genyen pou fe enpak sou ekonomi Ayiti.

Name: SyrianStars Foundation
Description:
- Financial support towards 30 families in Ajman

- 1,400 USD per family raised: 42,000 USD total.

- When distributing relief funds, careful provision is often overlooked; we therefore believe in the importance of thorough research such that funds are allocated equitably.

- This organization therefore places great emphasis on the conducting of physical surveys in order to assess the economic needs and legitimacy of family situations. 

- Digitalizing key demographics & circumstances to prioritize families in order of needs.

Name: Sunshine Charitable Foundation
Description: Sunshine Charitable Foundation (“Sunshine”) is a private foundation that supports a variety of causes in the U.S., and has been especially active in children’s health, with a special emphasis on food allergy and eosinophilic esophagitis research.

Name: The Benefactors
Description:
We enable people to support noble causes by creating awareness as well as raising funds through our social media campaigns! The Benefactors ensures once-in-a-lifetime experiences & rewards through its gratification process by famous influencers & celebrities for supporting the causes they love.

Everyone can participate by making donations – large or small. They all add up to make a massive impact!

Name: PRN Alumni Foundation
Description:
PRN Alumni Foundation is a 501c3 non-profit formed by former employees of global superstar entertainer and musician, Prince. 
Our mission is to continue the generous philanthropy that Prince quietly facilitated for decades. 

We support youth education in music, art and technology; community gardening and food sustainability issues; and alumni members in times of crisis.

Name: Turrismo
Description:
TURRISMO l'Associazione dell'Amicizia!

L'associazione è dedicata alla memoria della nonnetta Antonietta e di tutte le nostre nonne e dei nostri nonni perchè vuole recuperare i loro principi e valori, che ahimè con la televisione ed l'uso sbagliato del Denaro abbiamo completamente perso. Dedica speciale anche a Cesare Dalfreddo scomparso a soli 37 anni per averci insegnato ad apprezzare le piccole, belle e abbondanti cose che ci circondano, la Natura!

L'amicizia è il valore fondamentale su cui si basa tutto il Turrismo! 
Senza gli amici non ci sarebbe Turrismo!

Name: Brasileiras in Biz
Description:
We build together the future of Brazilian Women in Calgary by providing opportunities for development, creating and fostering valuable connections, and facilitating member success through professional and personal growth.

We believe in the power of community and relationships to thrive in Calgary’s local market within our areas of expertise. Brasileiras in Biz isn’t about collecting as many business cards as possible; it is about surrounding yourself with a support network for your life.

Members share more than referrals, as they become ambassadors for each other in the business world. Expand your knowledge by taking advantage of the viewpoints and prior experience of others, and get help to identify opportunities for partnerships, joint ventures, or new areas of expansion for your career.

Our meetings occur monthly at the Central Library. Be a member and enjoy a fun group that embraces friendship, education, advice, referrals and new ideas for growing each other’s careers. 

If you are seeking a new way to contribute your knowledge and energy and join a group of like-minded people, contact us now!

Name: CEP Awards
Description: CEP Philanthrotainment Carnival presents a free, fun and rewarding opportunity for everyone to do good via social media, entertaining and informative content, audience voting for awards, prizes and more.

Name: Broodfonds De Stuute
Description: Een solidair vangnet voor ondernemers in Zeeland

Name: ARTS - A Reason To Smile
Description: The mission of ARTS is to empower communities without access to dental professionals to achieve and maintain a higher level of oral health by providing hygiene education, direct treatment, and dental supplies.

Name: Fondation Trône d'Enfants
Description: La Fondation Trône d'Enfants est un organisme sans but lucratif qui supporte les enfants en matière de santé, sécurité et éducation. L'organisme est une initiative étudiante qui a à coeur la philanthropie à Montréal.

Name: Veteran group G5
Description:
The Mainstay is military experience - The Foundation is a strong community - Interest is military history.

The veteran group G5, consists of veterans, former- and still serving personnel of the Army, the Air Force, the Navy, the Home Guard and other related friends. G5 is developing rapidly, and since its formal formation in 2014, it has supported several worthy purposes, such as veteran work and relevant military history projects. G5 feels a natural commitment to making a difference to what our members believe is our shared responsibility and legacy.

The G5 annually organizes a military history journey for its members. The purpose is to cultivate the community in the context of unique military-historical experiences.

Name: LEO Clube Tucumã Três Passos
Description:
O objetivo do Programa LEO Clubes é oferecer aos jovens do mundo uma oportunidade de desenvolvimento e contribuição, individual e coletiva, como membros responsáveis da comunidade local, nacional e internacional.

A sigla LEO, significa Liderança, Experiência e Oportunidade, mais especificadamente:

LIDERANÇA - Os companheiros LEO desenvolvem suas habilidades como organizadores, gerenciadores de tempo e motivadores de seus pares.

EXPERIÊNCIA - Os companheiros LEO entendem a importância da cooperação através do desempenho de serviços comunitários.

OPORTUNIDADE - A associação a um LEO Clube oferece aos jovens uma oportunidade de se distinguirem, desenvolverem traços de personalidade e de serem reconhecidos por suas contribuições à comunidade.

Com estes objetivos em mente, em meados de setembro do ano 1987, o governador José Henrique de Lima, em visita a esta cidade, procurou o Companheiro Leão Nissidio Maldaner propondo-lhe a fundação de um LEO Clube em Três Passos , uma vez que este estava incentivando a criação de novos LEO Clubes.

A partir desta data, incentivado pelo governador e muito motivado com esta idéia, o Companheiro Leão Maldaner procurou contatar os jovens de nossa comunidade, colhendo opiniões e procurando uni-los neste ideal com o apoio de sua filha, Isabel Maldaner, que entrou em contato com seus colegas de aula e amigos para fazerem parte deste clube.

Vários contatos foram mantidos e muitas reuniões marcadas, havendo o comparecimento, no início, somente de três pessoas, Isabel, Mara e Sandra. Estas então, ficaram encarregadas de convidar mais pessoas para participarem do LEO Clube.

Depois de várias tentativas, convidaram o C.LEO Newton Pereira, do LEO Clube de Tenente Portela, para que fizesse uma explanação sobre o que era um LEO Clube para um grupo de jovens que havia mostrado interesse em ingressar neste movimento.
Location: Trës Passos, Rio Grande do Sul

Name: Inspiira
Description: This project's mission is to help leverage people's personal and professional growth through the development and deployment of internet-based, freely accessible tools based on Personality Type theory.

Name: C.U.R.E. Ride
Description:
The C.U.R.E. (Cycling Under Reduced Eyesight) Ride is a 4-day bicycling event from Santa Barbara to San Diego with the purpose of raising money and awareness for Leber’s Hereditary Optic Neuropathy (LHON); a genetically inherited disease that, once triggered, leads to rapid loss of central vision. The disease usually affects people aged in their teens or twenties, and there is currently no treatment or cure.

Donations to the C.U.R.E Ride will support LHON research at Doheny Eye Institute, a top vision research center proudly affiliated with UCLA Stein Eye Institute. You can learn more about their efforts to find new cures and treatments for LHON at www.doheny.org/LHON.

Name: Garage Sale Queens
Description:
During the Christmas season in 1991, my seven-year old daughter and I began taking blankets to people living on the streets in Austin, Texas.  We have grown to two "queens" (Kate Murphy and I), the little princess (my now 25-year old daughter, Breezy) and a dozen volunteers of family members and friends.  

Throughout the year Kate and I (and several family members) shop the garage sales every Saturday for blankets, backpacks, winter clothes, shoes, knit hats and travel-size toiletries.  Then on the Saturday before Christmas Eve, we all gather with the truck-loads of items we have amassed (including the purchase of new socks and food) and head to various parks throughout Austin where we hand visit with the homeless and give them the things they need to make their lives a little more comfortable.  

In December of 2007, Texas Country Reporter filmed us for an episode that aired in December of 2008 and again in December of 2009.

Name: Jacob's Color Link Initiative
Description:
Our mission is simple: to INSPIRE and ADVOCATE for persons with disabilities in Haiti.
Our vision is to live in a world where persons with disabilities are thriving, contributing and realizing their full potential... free of charity. Jacob’s Color Link Initiative is a 501(c)(3) charitable nonprofit corporation and all contributions are deductible to the full extent allowed by law.

Name: Friends of Kasam Inc.
Description:
Friends of Kasam is a non-profit dedicated to support the education of underprivileged children in Kasam, Tamil Nadu, India. We are a registered, tax-exempt public charity as defined under section 501(c)(3) of the US Internal Revenue Code. 

For more, please visit us at www.friendsofkasam.org or email us at friendsofkasam@gmail.com

Name: Fourth Ward CLT
Description:
Fourth Ward CLT, is comprised of Fourth Ward Foundation, Inc., a 501(c)3, and Friends of Fourth Ward Neighborhood Association. 

As all-volunteer organizations, both are dedicated to the development and preservation of Charlotte’s Uptown’s historic Fourth Ward. We have helped to create residential interest, investment, and increased property values.

Fourth Ward CLT is located in Charlotte, North Carolina. It is approximately 30 city blocks, this stately neighborhood is bordered by the Tryon Street business and entertainment district, the historical Elmwood Cemetery, and lively Trade Street. It is anchored by the beautiful Fourth Ward Park. Wandering through narrow tree-lined streets, visitors delight in seeing the grand old Victorians with gingerbread millwork, charming front porches, and courtyard gardens. See Less

Name: Cancerfly Networks
Description:
Cancerfly Network is a social innovation established with an aim to help cancer survivors to obtain better quality of life. Our mission is to help cancer survivors to obtain employment and business opportunities. 

Cancerfly and its portal, www.cancerfly.com was officially launched on 20th February 2017 by the then Health Minister of Malaysia, Datuk Seri Dr. S. Subramaniam in Hospital Kuala Lumpur. 

There are 3 products offered:

- CanBazaar - An avenue for business advertisement. 
Many cancer survivors opt to run their own business after their treatment. This is a platform where cancer survivors promotes their products online as well as in physical bazaars. 

- CanEmploy - An avenue for employment opportunities.
Employers will pledge position(s) from their Organization to Cancerfly. Cancer Survivors may apply for these positions through www.cancerfly.com.

- CanSihat - Mobile Health Education and Medical Screening.
Cancerfly will partner with Organizations to host Mobile Health Education and Medical Screening at Malls, Hotels and Offices for early detection of Non-Communicable Diseases.

Cancerfly won 2 awards at The 2016 International Invention Innovation Competition in Canada (iCAN).
iCAN 2016 is the first ever international competition of creative ideas and innovation projects.

- The Romanian Inventors Forum (FIR) Honorary Special Award
- Best Invention of Malaysia

Cancerfly Networks was awarded as the Winners of 2018 The Star Golden Hearts Awards. This Award was inaugurated in 2015 to celebrate Malaysians from all walks of life who have acted selflessly or heroically, built bridges between communities, and promoted racial harmony and unity. 

For more information, kindly visit www.cancerfly.com or follow us on Facebook - Cancerfly Networks.

Name: Words Working 4 Women
Description:
Words Working 4 Women (WW4W) is a social venture established by Anne Summers and CobaltBlu in which words – books, articles, speeches and so on – are available for purchase with the proceeds going to build a loan portfolio to fund women entrepreneurs in developing countries.  

The first 'words' will be available for purchase at the formal launch for International Womens Day 2011, and will include a Timeline of Achievements, followed shortly after by Anne Summers's classic Damned Whores and God's Police (1975, 1994, 2002)

Name: NANO Lending 501c3
Description:
Nano Lending is a registered 501c3 nonprofit organization that lends microloans at 0% interest along with no terms and conditions to the impoverished in order to empower them to become entrepreneurs, start their own businesses, and gain a sustainable source of income. 

• Nano Lending has raised over $30,000 and given 102 non-repayment loans to 102 persons (81 awarded to homeless entrepreneurs). Entrepreneurship opportunities were given to 10% of the unhoused population in St. Louis City. Loans have also been given in multiple states and countries.
• Nano Lending's COVID-19 Relief for the Unhoused donated over 1,300 masks to shelters and healthcare workers - https://nanolending.wixsite.com/loans/donate
• Nano Lending conducts and starts entrepreneurship workshops and business incubators at homeless shelters by partnering with community, religious, industry and government leaders in St. Louis, MO.

Name: Community Foundation of Charles County
Description:
A Community Foundation is a tax exempt public charity, created by and for the people of geographical that helps individuals, groups, families, NPOs, corporations and more reach their philanthropic goals. By providing folks a variety of charitable funds, community foundations enable people to easily and effectively support the causes they care about most.  Each fund is professionally managed and endowed assets continue to grow over time. 

In addition, community foundations work closely with concerned citizens, nonprofits and governmental agencies to achieve community goals and maximize grant making effectiveness.

Name: Haven Animal Rescue
Description:
At Haven Animal Rescue, we believe that animals deserve a better life. Through our special programs, we hope to enhance the bond between animals and people.
Cats and dogs have a special place in our hearts here in the US. However, many other animals are often overlooked. Our mission is to show kindness to all animals by rescuing those in need, big and small, by providing sanctuary, permanent homes, and advocating for the humane treatment of animals.

Name: KomşuKitaplık
Description:
KomşuKitaplık, ikinci el akademik kitapların ihtiyacı olanlara ulaştırılmasını hedeflediğimiz bir sosyal sorumluluk projesidir.

Komşu Kitaplık is a social responsibility project where we offer an online platform acting as an intermediary for second-hand academic books’ circulation between people who don’t need them anymore and others in need.

Name: Youniti
Description:
Youniti's mission is to unite citizens and organizations around the world, with the goal of helping others and sharing wealth, to create a more egalitarian, fulfilling world while helping the environment and people, all accomplished in love, peace and respect for all.

For this, the Youniti Foundation which means "Unity" has been set up. If everyone and everyone unite to accomplish targeted actions, a new and more egalitarian world.  

To accomplish this mission, Youniti offers help on two levels : on the web and on the field.


ON THE WEB : YOUNITI.ORG
The new Youniti web platform allows citizens find, give or sell items for less than $40. The platform also offers businesses and organizations the opportunity to register and publish goods for less than $ 40, as well as promotions and liquidations. The goal is to create an affordable market where you can find everything while helping each other.


ON THE FIELD : QUEBEC
In Quebec, a portion of the revenues of the www.youniti.org platform will be donated to charities like Moisson Montréal, La Maison du père, La Guignolée des médias, La Rue des Femmes. 

In addition to numerous donation campaigns will take place across Quebec. For example, Youniti organizes a clothing donation campaign to help homeless people in Greater Montreal.


ON THE FIELD: INTERNATIONAL
Youniti will also hel on an international level. Indeed, we will start with Benin in Africa with the creation of the first Youniti Center. The center will help on many levels, including the donation of school equipment, the renovation of schools, the organization of gardening activities such as permaculture and much more.


Visit the Foundation's website: fondationyouniti.org.

Visit the web platform: youniti.org.

Name: Athletes Forward Foundation
Description: Our mission is to provide impactful services and resources that increase the professional growth and development of student athletes beyond their athletic endeavors.

Name: IDEEST
Description:
À propos de notre association :

Fondé par Chirel ETOUMBAKOUNDOU et Orland ETOUMBAKOUNDOU, L'IDEEST est né(e) en 2019 au Congo, Brazzaville. L'association Initiative du Développement Économique Environnemental et Social pour Tous à pour caractère socioéconomique, éducatif et environnemental, nous œuvrant pour améliorer les conditions de vie des personnes vivant en Répulique du Congo.

Ici, vous pouvez en apprendre plus sur nous et nos actions.

Name: The Sequoia Awards - Redwood City
Description:
It all started in 1990.

A small group of civic leaders led by Pete and Paula Uccelli established The Sequoia Awards in 1990 as a 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization dedicated to honoring volunteerism in Redwood City.

The first Sequoia Awards scholarship was for $500. Since then, The Sequoia Awards has grown significantly thanks to support from local businesses, other nonprofits, and individuals in our community who understand the importance of an active and engaged citizenry. To date, we have distributed more than $2.2 million in scholarships.

Our scholarships are based solely on the volunteerism of our honorees. Academics or participation in school-sponsored activities and athletics are not part of the criteria.

In 2020, The Sequoia Awards distributed more than $175,000 to 25 students representing 8 local high schools. Our Outstanding Student Award winner, Maria Casique, received the top award of $25,000. Maria overcame numerous personal obstacles and founded the Connect Tutoring Center, which provides one-on-one STEM tutoring to younger students. Additional honorees received scholarships ranging from $5,000 to $11,000. 

We also recognize one outstanding adult volunteer from our community. This year’s Outstanding Individual Award Winner, Annette Soby, was honored for her 3 decades of community service, including extensive work with the Kainos Home & Training Center and the Peninsula Covenant Church.

Name: Haven's Foundation
Description: Helping children that are sick or in need and to support the families and facilities that care for them.  Mission is to raise money, volunteer and directly help children, families, and partnering with children's hospitals, other charities to further our cause and reach.

Name: Tutors for a Cure
Description:
Tutors For A Cure is a peer tutoring organization that guarantees quality help for students in grades kindergarten to 12th. Our organization’s mission is twofold: we offer peer tutoring at competitive and affordable rates and contribute a portion of our profits to the American Cancer Society and other philanthropies that support the fight against cancer. Tutors For A Cure is run by extremely intelligent and ambitious students who are looking to raise other peers’ grades while supporting a cause that is close to many of their hearts. We chose the most accomplished students who take a range of challenging classes within the Honors and Advanced Placement programs at our school. Through our application process, we are devoted to be comprised of experienced and dedicated tutors. At $25 per hour, we charge a price that is significantly lower than the average tutoring agency. Twenty percent of all of our profits go straight to charity.
 
Our primary objectives are to enrich our students in their academic abilities while giving back to those in need. We aspire to enhance our students’ grades while donating to a cancer organization. Our group tutors in English, the sciences, mathematics, social students, foreign languages and computer skills. Tutors For A Cure instructors bring their own notes and dittos along with numerous practice materials and review notes. We know that cancer affects millions of Americans and would love to help find the cure. With your help, we promise better grades and the power to cure.

Name: KamerunGO!
Description: KamerunGO! ist eine humanitäre Non-Profit-Organisation mit Sitz in der Schweiz. Wir betreiben in Bekoko, Kamerun das erste medizinische Gesundheitszentrum.

Name: Favela em Rede
Description: A Favela em Rede atua de forma colaborativa com ativistas que representam as favelas de todo país. Nosso foco é qualificar e conectar lideranças através da criação de projetos de agenda social positiva em diversos estados do Brasil. Acreditamos que o fortalecimento do ativismo social só é possível com direitos civis democráticos atuantes dentro e fora das favelas.

Name: PostScript Project
Description:
The PostScript Project is a donation based, open-source, non-profit initiative, created to give investigative journalists a safer, more secure place to store their information and publish their work, while helping to prevent attacks against them.

Public access to an uncontaminated supply of information is paramount to the proper functioning of a democracy. Investigative journalists dedicate their lives to ensure that we, the people, are informed on issues of public interest.

The PostScipt system will allow journalists to store their data on an impenetrable, decentralized database. They won't have to trust an organization, central server or person with their data. The PostScript can't be hacked, tampered with or deleted, and the immutable nature of data on the blockchain means there is no point in even attempting to. 

The system will also make use of a smart-contract which can be set to publish a journalists work at a given date, whatever happens - and it can't be stopped. While this system can’t actively stop threats or attacks on journalists, the immutable nature of information stored on the blockchain, paired with the power of a self-publishing Postscript, will serve to at least limit incentive to do so.

Name: Aboli Foundation
Description: Aboli Foundation is a non-profit founded in 2016 by Saniya and Saloni More. The organization is based in India, and works to improve the level of education and well-being of students in government-run primary schools. Aboli Foundation currently operates at the district level by implementing healthcare services within the Palghar school district. We also work to implement entertainment, enrichment and exposure programs at schools in the district.

Name: Institute for the Advancement of Humanity
Description:
Our goal and aim is to bring love and care to women and children all over the world.

A small act of kindness can change someone’s life.

Name: The Giving Table
Description:
Doing good with food

The mission of The Giving Table is to connect food and philanthropy in an approachable way by serving as a resource that encourages conscious eating, educates the public about key issues affecting the food industry and promotes change in our own kitchens, communities and around the world. 

Wendell Berry said "to care about food but not the food industry is clearly absurd." More than ever, consumers are becoming part of the solution to our broken food system by recognizing that their choices make a difference. The Giving Table makes it easy to be part of the movement and stay informed about all things food including hunger, sustainable farming, healthy eating and policy.

Name: Lycoming County Rotary Clubs Festival
Description:
Lycoming County Rotary Clubs Festival

The Balloonfest/Air Show & So Much More is planned and hosted by a dedicated group of volunteers who are members of the various Rotary Clubs of Lycoming County. These volunteers truly exemplify Rotary’s motto, “Service Above Self”.

Each year we strive to improve your experience by adding fun and exciting things to do and see. We will again be at the Lycoming County Fairgrounds in Hughesville, PA to offer attractions both in the air and on the ground. 

The vision and original idea in bringing this event to our community was to promote Rotary awareness and service to our community, and to donate proceeds to local charities. It is no different today. Profits from the event will support local charities in Lycoming County.

We sincerely thank our sponsors. Several of them have supported our efforts since the time this event was just an idea. Others will be first-year sponsors and each one is important to the success of this event. Please support their businesses and thank them when you see them.

Name: HEAT THE STREET
Description:
We're not a company. We're not an organization. We're not a committee.
We're sort of a movement.

HEAT THE STREET - Streetwear your jacket!

Put the tag on a jacket you own and make it a new one to someone in need of the heat.
Get the heat out there!

Name: Credenda Empowerment Foundation
Description:
Credenda Empowerment Foundation champions the broad-based socio economic empowerment strategy of the South African government. 

Credenda is structured as a trust. Its objective is to enhance the prospects for Previously Disadvantaged Individuals to work in Management Consulting and Financial Services. Credenda has identified these 2 industries as significantly lagging in terms of employment equity, as set out in the Broad-Based Black Economic Empowerment codes of good-practice.

The beneficiaries of the trust participate in the Credenda Chrysalis Programme. This programme aims at promoting the development of skills that will allow the beneficiairies to find employment and develop successful careers in management consulting or financial services.

The Chrysalis programme has 2 main tenets:
- Education: the trust will assist each participant in gaining a relevant professional qualification by funding a formal study course
- Experience: the trust will enter into an agreement with a suitable consulting or financial company to offer a 2-year work contract to the participant. 

Candidates who wish to apply to become a beneficiary of the trust and participate in the Chrysalis programme must match the profile of a Previously Disadvantaged Individual and have tertiary education. For further information, please send an email to trustees@credenda.co.za

The funding of the programme is supported by investments into private companies. Credenda controls an investment company, Credo Business Group, who has majority interests in several private companies. Credo qualifies as a B-BEEE Level 2 organisation.

Description: March for Science St Louis is a diverse nonpartisan group supporting science-based education, innovation, and economic development for the betterment of society and our region. We connect with the value of Science, Technology, Engineering, Art, and Math (STEAM) and want to support and celebrate our bi-state region's leading role in Plant & Life Science, Healthcare, Advanced Manufacturing, and more!

Name: The Open Road Foundation, Inc.
Description:
The Open Road Foundation is a nonprofit charitable organization that provides disabled U.S. veterans and their families with handicapped-accessible, classic American vehicles.

Founded by Ryan Hemphill, the mission of the Open Road Foundation is to help wounded veterans and their families recapture the uniquely American spirits of Freedom, Independence and Pride that these patriots bravely fought and sacrificed to preserve for us all. The organization accomplishes this mission by providing our veterans with handicapped-accessible, classic, American, sports, vintage or other vehicles that uniquely inure the new owners and their families with a sense of freedom, independence and pride that may have diminished for them since becoming wounded or injured.  Restoring these uniquely American tenets within our wounded heroes is essential to their health, happiness and healing along the Open Road.

Name: Young Aces e.V.
