Name: WOEIGO
Description:
Woeigo is about doing good. Simply.


The problem Woeigo solves:

Woeigo removes all barriers in the process of doing good or starting good. It creates a hub for everyone who wants to do good to get together; while also making it completely transparent and more efficient. We make it easy, simple, and fun to do good and allow people to capitalize on "dead time", to make a difference in the world.


How Woeigo works:

Woeigo is a community and environment built around interactive projects. People can connect to and follow multiple projects and support them in multiple ways all from one screen and with a few clicks. Multiple benefits are offered to those who participate: Recognition, rewards, networking, experience, involvement, self fulfillment, and self discovery.

We are a marketing or engagement tool for Not for Profits and Businesses that are doing good because we provide transparency to their efforts. 

We will provide businesses better ROI than other alternatives because we understand that people care about doing good. We don't throw money into campaigns to win you over, we throw money into the right projects to make the most difference and build meaningful emotional connections.

Description:
We know from experience that cancer patients are stronger in their fight, cope with treatments better, and recover faster when they have family, friends and others in their support system around them. We also know that people want to help when someone is going through a difficult time. Cancer creates significant financial burden even with insurance.

There are a lot funds raised and donated for cancer research which will help patients of the future. We wanted to help current patients have the support they need to gain strength, create memories and…Live LIFE!!

Yay 4 Life! is a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization that was established in 2012 to provide cancer patients and their families with assistance for expenses such as parking, meals, travel costs and other costs incurred while patients are receiving medical care either as an inpatient or outpatient. Yay 4 Life! also provides scholarships and other educational support at Friendswood Independent School District.

Description:
Originally conceived in 2010, Veritas promotes Mental and Emotional Well-Being as a key part of the American lifestyle.

The percentage of Americans suffering and affected by mental health issues has skyrocketed in the last half-century; while funding for treatment and development of new programs has sunk to critical levels.

This is where we want to make a difference.

Veritas is a funding agency, one of the rarest and most integral parts of the charity food chain. As an organization, we focus on the support of groups working to better the mental and emotional well-being of their communities.

From research groups creating a new suicide-prevention program, to a local charity working to train police officers for mental health scenarios, Veritas works to provide the vital (and in most cases, otherwise unavailable) funding needed to make these projects happen, and with a company focus on minimizing overhead while maximizing distribution of funds to our partner organizations, Veritas can do the most possible good with every dollar and create the social action needed to make a difference, For Goodness’ Sake.

PLEASE CONTACT US IF YOU KNOW OF A LIPIZZAN OR LIPIZZAN CROSS IN NEED.

Our Mission Statement: The Lipizzan Rescue Foundation (LRF), as the only non-profit Lipizzan Rescue in the United States, joins others who have dedicated themselves to the preservation and safety of the Lipizzan horse breed. The first priority of the LRF is the rescue of Lipizzan/ Lipizzan Cross horses, who suffer from malnutrition or abuse, or are in physically dangerous or neglectful situations.

When the Coronavirus hit Luxembourg, we, a group of young professionals and students, spontaneously founded NALA - Now Act Local Assocation to support local shops during the Covid-19 crisis. 

We gathered our experiences in web design, economics, law and public relations and spontaneously started a platform where all businesses open during the confinement could register and clients could nationwide find the local shops, which offered a pick-up or delivery service. 

The platform gathered more than 700 shops, was available in 3 languages and was supported, amongst others, by the Luxembourg Chamber of Commerce. 

NALA was our way of supporting the Luxembourg community during the Covid-19 crisis.

Name: Oxford Mission Behala
Description:
The Oxford Mission in India has always been based in Kolkata (Calcutta), in Eastern India. It has been operating in the region since 1880, initially through the brotherhood of the Epiphany, and since 1902 by the sisterhood as well.
Today the mission has a compound at Behala, to the south of the city, and provides a variety of much needed assistance to the community in which it is implanted. Much of the work is carried out by lay people, but it remains under the constant eye and support of both the Bishop of Kolkata and his team, and supporters from around the world, notably the UK.
At Behala there is a boys’ orphanage, St. Joseph’s Primary School, an English-medium Primary School, Hostels, training schemes for less academic boys, and an ENT/Eye clinic. The new Mathieson Memorial Music Centre, set up in memory of Father Theodore, is now fully in use as a much grander replacement to the old band room. The OM boys use it for music practice and concerts and as an assembly room and it is available to other organisations and groups who wish to hire it.

Our summer instructors receive ongoing training in classroom management while teaching a group of wonderful kids.
Our part-time teaching assistants work under a professional teacher in the classroom during the fall and spring.
There's so much you can do to help our kids learn. Volunteer with us today and we will work with you to find the best fit for your interests and schedule.

Name: We Are We
Description:
We Are We stand to break down the structures and biases that negatively affect and hold women back in today’s society.

We seek to uncover and dismantle the gender taboos that are hiding in plain sight. We do this through research and organising activities with the purpose of encouraging and facilitating conversation on the subject relating to societal pressures, gender taboos and discrimination faced by women.

By doing this we seek to shed light on such areas and bring awareness with the aim to work on ways to tackle the root cause of these female-sensitive issues through collaboration with decision makers, organisations, volunteers and charities to name a few.

Our Process 

We focus on addressing each issue/topic through research and then based on what is revealed, launch a campaign on that particular area to bring awareness to the findings with the focus to:

• Educate
• Advocate
• Donate – to women who are impacted by the campaign topic at hand

We appreciate that not all women face certain issues that arise and are not just one homogenous group, therefore through research we will be able to pinpoint the right group and support them at the right time.

We welcome and value all genders as allies in bringing women issues to light and finding solutions to resolve them.

Name: Corona Solidarity Campaign
Description:
Corona Solidarity Campaign
The Corona Solidarity Campaign is a citizen-led initiative which aims to support those most vulnerable to the impacts of Covid-19. With a firm belief in extending community-based solidarity instead of charity,
COMMUNITY SOLIDARITY
The Corona Solidarity Campaign is founded on the principles of mutual aid, rather than charity. The campaign is citizen-led and organised around helping fellow citizens in need, filling in the gaps left by the state structure. The aim of this campaign is to extend solidarity by linking those with resources to the people who have built our cities but find themselves without adequate social safety nets.

EMERGENCY FOOD RATIONS
The Corona Solidarity Campaign was formed in response to the spread of the Covid-19 pandemic and the subsequent lockdown imposed on cities in Pakistan. A number of progressive groups and individuals in Islamabad and Rawalpindi formed the CSC in order to provide emergency food supplies to the communities worst affected by the lockdown, especially katchi abadis. Households are provided with a ration pack consisting of essential food supplies for a month, hygiene products and cash.

PRIORITISING SAFETY
We have a unique distribution mechanism that minimises physical interaction. First, our volunteers coordinate with local utility stores to assemble ration packs. Beneficiary families are issued ration chits which they can use at the utility store to claim their ration pack. We’ve also included an awareness pamphlet with each ration pack highlighting personal practices to help protect oneself against the coronavirus.

PPE KITS FOR HEALTH WORKERS
We also arrange personal protective equipment (PPE) for frontline health workers who need them, such as doctors, nurses, janitorial staff and relief volunteers. The protective gear is distributed in collaboration with relevant government departments such as the National Disaster Management Authority (NDMA), and includes masks, face shields and hazmat suits.

Name: Kind+
Description:
Kind+ is a smart budgeting app that promotes philanthropy via micro-donations. First of its kind in the Australian market, Kind+ offer our users an interactive tool to manage their budget, whilst making a continuous impact to cause that matter to them.

Partnering with charities from various sectors, our vision is to nurture a philanthropic generation that is financially conscious. We seek to revolutionise the way charities engage with their donors and reform the way ongoing donations are pledged.

As an emerging social enterprise, Kind+ is currently at an investment-ready stage and actively seeking for introduction to charities, investors, and everyday philanthropists like yourselves to make a difference.

Description:

We believe that a small donation makes a tremendous difference. Learn more about our impact and current initiative by visiting our website.

Name: Afro Girls Lead
Description:
We inspire Black girls around the world to be leaders, innovators and changemakers through education initiatives.

Our Work in Africa:

52 million primary to secondary age girls are still out of school across Africa. Inclusivity is a major challenge across the continent despite progress in several countries. Societal gender norms discouraging girls from attending school, political agendas and religious ideologies are major factors causing their absence from school, ultimately contribute to the cycle of poverty.

Afro Girls is committed to ending the cycle of poverty in Africa by empowering African girls through education. We inspire our girls to be responsible leaders, innovators and changemakers who use their influence to make a difference for their communities.

Our Work in America:

Our Work in America:
Despite the high achievements and major accomplishments of Black women around the world, Black girls in America still face significant barriers to quality education. Barriers like stereotypes, systemic racism and adultification, adversely impact the experiences that Black girls unfairly endure simply because of the color of their skin.

We're committed to positively impacting the narrative of Black girls in America through education initiatives. We focus on creating enriching programming, safe spaces for girls to speak up for themsleves, and positive representations in the media and everyday life.

In both regions, we also explore building skills in topics such as leadership, service, teamwork, positive and realistic representation, literacy, financial literacy, social entrepreneurship, media, healthy living and hygiene, WASH and STEAM.

We envision a world where Black girls from all socio-economic backgrounds and cultures believe in themselves, have access to quality education and fiercely pursue opportunities that allow them to fulfill their limitless potential. Our work, though customized to cater to the girls in the regions that we serve, reflect our vision.

Name: Lily Kelly Ministries
Description:
Singer songwriter, Lily Kelly was killed at 15 years old when the car she was riding in was hit by a drunk driver. She lived a life fully surrendered to Jesus Christ, shining brightly with love and kindness for everyone! 

Lily Kelly Ministries is sharing the message of God's love, hope & forgiveness through the telling of her precious life, lived in full surrender to her Lord & Savior. 

Lily Kelly's song,"I AM" written, sung  & recorded only 6 months before she was called home to Heaven, is the telling of her deep love and understanding of her Creator. Her life has quickly become an international movement of all who hear her story, to #LiveLikeLilyKelly #FullySurrendered and to experience the wholeness, peace, comfort, love, hope and forgiveness that having a close, personal relationship with the Lord Jesus Christ provides!
[ I AM by Lily Kelly available for download on iTunes, Google Play, Spotify and more]

Mark 12:30 "Love the Lord your God with all your heart and with all your soul and with all your mind and with all your strength."

Lily Kelly 
Gifted to Earth | April 2004
Returned to Jesus | June 1, 2019

Name: The Clarion Development Agency
Description:
We empower children, the youth and women in our communities in partnership with all stakeholders to help create a better today and brighter tomorrow using the core principles of Ubuntu.

The Clarion Development Agency intervenes to reduces pain and suffering in the world at three levels: the community, business and government leaderships. These three spheres contribute to the conditions in which people find themselves today. The way in which the community reacts to government interventions and business involvements influences the atmosphere in which they live and consequently the conditions of life. On the other hand, a business that balances the need for profits with those of the community contribute more towards sustainable living and creates conditions where government is better positioned to serve its people. For this reason, we have carefully and purposely selected programs that ensure balanced support as follows:

- Education and Training for a better expression in the country’s economy.
- Life Skills Development for a better understanding of the experience of life.
- HIV and AIDS Care and Counselling to help reduce the spread of HIV and AIDS in our society.
- Substance Abuse Care and Support to deal with issues of substance abuse in our communities and among the youth in particular.
- Arts and Sports to foster teamwork, discipline and focus and to develop creative thinking and imagination skills.
- African Heritage Training programs to strengthen the sense of identity necessary for purposeful living.
- Ubuntu Advocacy to rekindle the spirit of Ubuntu in our society.

Name: The One and for All Foundation
Description:
The One and for All Foundation
One World, One Humanity!

The One and for All Foundation is an institution that promotes personal development, human rights, inclusion and diversity.

Foundation that promotes the integral realization of individuals, taking life as their main value and focusing on finding the center, mission and life goals of each human being, strengthening their relationship with themselves, with others, with the Planet and with God; Generating equality in differences, and achieving a change in the World through respect for the values and beliefs of the other.

The One and for All Foundation, is a global institution with presence in several countries in 7 continents dedicated to:

Recognize, accept and impel that each individual at birth receives individual rights, obligations and guarantees that must be respected; In order to obtain all the opportunities within their reach to live a full and happy life, in harmony and with a promising future. Every human being must be respected in all areas of his life.

Recognize, accept and impel that each individual is unique and unrepeatable, and that through different programs he/she is provided with all the necessary tools to be himself / herself finding their mission and their reason to be in this life, in order to achieve its highest potential, and the highest degree of excellence as a human being.

Recognize, accept and impel that through the sum of the strengths and virtues of each human being generates the greatest universal wealth: humanity. Generating awareness that our similarities do not unite us, but our differences.

Universal Union: We generate a change in the World, as long as we respect the values, ideas and beliefs of the other.

Society as a whole must create ways so that all human beings, without exception, have the same rights and opportunities that allow them to obtain fullness and universal life.

Name: AatmSamman
Description:
AatmSamman is a social initiative based in New Delhi, started by 2 high-school students. We wish to give back to society in the most effective and dignified manner. We organise camps providing basic relief materials to the underprivileged, in order to help them sustain a higher quality of life. These materials are sourced through collection centres set-up across the city. Aiming emancipation of as many people as possible from impoverished lifestyle, we organise little camps providing basic relief materials to the abundant underprivileged people to help them sustain life. We provide them with such materials at subsidised rates to instil in them a sense of self esteem (AatmSamman) since they are not getting it for free.

We intend to save the humanity from the stigma associated with poverty created by humanity itself, prevalent across the globe, nucleated in India. This is social initiative initiated to help as many individuals as possible to enjoy the sense of self esteem and further protect their identity in the society.

Name: PACC - Persian Arts and Cultural Communities
Description:
MISSION STATEMENT
The Persian Arts & Cultural Communities (PACC) exists to strengthen and sustain the legacy of Iranian-American Arts and Culture through community-building, education and art therapy.


Mission description:
* PACC is a community-based, nonprofit organization that offers arts and cultural programming by and for the Iranian-American diaspora in order to preserve, sustain and continue the legacy of Persian arts and culture. 

Community Building:
* PACC, is a community-based organization that seeks to motivate, engage and enrich the lives of ordinary people through arts & culture. Since its formation, PACC has developed a rich and vibrant community of Iranian-Americans through teaching, leading, and performing traditional Iranian music, poetry and visual arts. 
* PACC brings together individuals and community groups, strengthen bonds, build cultural identity & unity.
*  PACC also facilitates seasonal celebrations, events and performances to showcase the work of the community.

Education:
* PACC educate the public about Persian arts and culture by programs and workshops. PACC offer weekly workshops in music theory and practice of traditional vocal and instruments, and supplemental workshops in visual art and poetry and more. PACC’s learning strategies are mainly based on understanding the language of each and every art form such as music, poetry and visual arts.

Art therapy:
* PACC, is a community-based organization that seeks to motivate, engage and enrich the lives of ordinary people through arts & culture. 
* Persian traditional arts( music, poetry, calligraphy and painting) are potentially therapeutic by nature. Our weekly group vocal music gives energy and release tension. PACC is planning to offer Art Therapy Programs specially for immigrants in the Persian communities.

The platform gives you the tools to turn overlapping lists and an array of visual assets into simple, graceful storylines, tours, and directories. The versatile tools are especially useful for volunteer, donor, sponsor and partner recognition and cataloging large volumes of content. 

The  platform allows administrators to layout stories that are responsive across devices. The content is branded, searchable and organized in client-specific categories. Media may include copy, images and video. Mapping capabilities allow for virtual touring of campuses, exhibits, or collections. Clients may opt for password-protected presentations as well as non-indexed web pages. 

Philanthrosphere is designed for presentation via a branded website or subdomain or your choosing. Presentations can be displayed on a kiosk or large screen, personal computers, and handheld devices. 

Explore the Philanthrosphere Demo or contact us to learn more.

Name: The SIblings Project
Description:
The Siblings Project was created in the light of Pali High's mission to inspire students of all backgrounds to create a positive difference in the community.  We look to inspire friendships and memories, while working to unite people from all walks of life under one common goal: supporting each other. Our program pairs participating students with Pali High Students who would serve as their “Older Sibling”.  Partners will plan weekly or bi-weekly virtual meetings at the time of their convenience and have the opportunity to pursue any bonding activity. They can play games, talk, educate each other, and share ideas, all while creating friendships and memories. 
Every “Older Sibling” is a current student at Palisades Charter High School who has undergone an extensive training and application process. Their journey begins with a written application that analyzes personality, maturity, ability, and interests. Furthermore, each written application is personally reviewed by our Cabinet. Students who make it past the written application are then given a solo interview in order to further gauge if they would be a good fit for our program. After the interviews, our final pool is selected and moved into training. 

We at the Siblings Project value a certain skill set within our program and therefore take the time to train our Siblings in the necessary topics in order to ensure a quality experience. These topics include proper communication, appropriate conduct, how to work with children, etc. We understand that parents are placing a certain level of trust within our program and therefore go the extra mile to provide a quality experience.

Name: Younite.Business
Description:
Younite.Business is committed to bringing together business owners and decision-makers to support and encourage engagement of products, services, resources, partnerships and opportunities between each other through a business community platform. 

Collectively we know by Youniting together and working with each other we have a greater chance of protecting businesses, jobs, and livelihoods.

How can you help, register at Younite.Business and if you believe in what we stand for then help us share our message.

Name: Holiday with a Hero
Description:

Participating students are enrolled in the Carson City School District McKinney-Vento Students-in-Transition program. For more information about eligibility, contact your school social worker or child's teacher.
To find out how you can volunteer or donate, please visit our website.

Name: Susie's Knee Corporation
Description:
Susie's Knee Corporation is dedicated to helping medical providers serve underprivileged patients by providing materials, equipment, and supplies.

Inspiration

Three years ago, our mother (Susie) fell and shattered her kneecap while my family was on vacation in Jamaica. We took her to the best local hospital there, and our experience there showed us how much we took our way of life for granted. Patients awaiting treatment lined the floors and flustered nurses rushed around trying in vain to find simple medical equipment that the hospital did not have. When the nurse that treated our mother went to cut the bandage off of her knee, she had to use office scissors because the hospital lacked medical shears. With the knowledge that this was one of the best hospitals in Jamaica, we could only imagine the conditions in the others. Still, even with the extremely limited supplies, the staff there worked hard to make sure that our mother got the best care possible. With the new realization of just how lucky we are to live comfortable lives every day, we became determined to make a difference in the lives of others.


With the help of generous donations from family and friends, a "test run" shipment was sent to St. Ann's Bay Regional Hospital in Jamaica to learn how to navigate the red tape of shipping to another country. The preliminary shipment included over $2,000 worth of medical supplies like hydrogen peroxide, disposible razors, alcohol swabs, examination gloves, assorted bandages, utility scissors, sponges, and even 100 pounds of gungo peas (a staple food on the island). 

We have been fundraising in order to send future shipments to St. Ann's Bay Regional Hospital and other hospitals that are in need of supplies.

Operamos negocios relacionados con el sector minorista y consumo masivo, diseñando e implementando estrategias para el crecimiento y sostenibilidad. Name: Solukhumbu Social Welfare Family
Description:
Solukhumbu Social Welfare Family is a community based totally non-profitable social organization registered in Solukhumbu district under the social welfare Act 2034 (Nepali Date) of Nepal Government and also affiliated with Social Welfare Council Kathmandu. It is established to develop, promote and empower the local people as well as to provide child education especially orphans and for those who are socially backward and who are under privilege groups. The organization focuses on health and sanitation, natural conservation and cultural preservation with full participation and effort of local people with coordination of the government of Nepal. 
Our Vision is to create knowledge based social organization. We facilitate education for children, conservation of cultural and traditional values, women empowerment, health and social awareness, supply drinking water and construct infrastructure that can bring change to large number of people.

Name: 10 Pieds sur Terre!
Description:
10 Pieds Sur Terre, ce sont 5 aventuriers animés par un rêve!

Celui de partir à l’aventure pendant 3 ans, à vélo, à la rencontre des hommes, des femmes et des enfants qui seront sur notre route, mais aussi et avant tout à la rencontre de soi. Passionnés de voyage et de plein-air, nous aspirons à vivre au rythme des enfants et de la nature.

Qui n’a jamais rêvé de tout quitter et de partir à l’aventure? Plusieurs y rêvent, mais rares sont ceux qui décident de le faire, par peur de l’inconnu. Nous avons décidé que la peur ne serait plus le moteur de notre vie. Au contraire, nous voulons la surpasser et ainsi prouver à nos enfants que tout est possible. Absolument tout!

Partir autour du monde est un projet hors du commun. Partir trois ans, ce l’est encore plus. Prendre la décision de vivre cette aventure avec nos trois enfants, c’est un peu fou! Le faire en vélo-camping, voilà un projet assez unique et nous sommes prêts à le relever. Bien entendu, partir à l’aventure pour une durée de trois ans est en fait un prétexte pour vivre des expériences, qui nous croyons, seront des plus enrichissantes pour tous les membres de la famille. Nous sommes déjà une famille avec des liens tissés serrés. Cependant, nous souhaitons les resserrer davantage lors de ce périple.  Nous voulons apprendre à être authentique envers nous-mêmes, connaître et dépasser nos limites personnelles et physiques et ainsi prendre confiance en nos capacités. Notre souhait le plus cher est de démontrer à nos enfants qu’il est possible de réaliser nos rêves.

Name: Sharing Seats Org.
Description: Sharing Seats provides children facing adversity the opportunity to attend a live event. Attending a sporting event, musical, or concert provides a distraction from life's everyday hardships and can create a forever moment for that child. . Description:
SeeeMe is a growing nonprofit seeking to better the lives of the people of Uganda. We think you'll agree when we say that all parts of life are interconnected; one's home, health, education, and income are linked and each play a key role in the extent that life can truly be lived. So, in order to make sustainable improvements, we're thinking about all aspects of life. And we're going to need everyone.

Sure, we're pretty new at this, but it quickly became obvious that everyone has something to give. We are certain of that. Maybe it's your time, your knowledge, your talents, your money. No matter what form it comes in, it's your ability to care that will make all the difference.

El objetivo es mejorar las condiciones de vida dentro y fuera de la comunidad LGBT+, por medio de contenido de valor que ayude a disminuir la discriminación y la ignorancia que se genera alrededor de las personas que son parte de la comunidad. 

Las acciones que se realizan tienen un enfoque en marketing social, generando productos distintos para poder llegar a la mayor cantidad de personas:

Description:
Amidst the public health crises and economic fallout caused by COVID-19, along with the ongoing civil rights movement onset by police brutality, our founding partners knew that we had to come together and leverage our experience in the entertainment industry to create a platform that both celebrates our multicultural coexistence and shared struggle for survival, while also raising money and awareness to combat systemic inequalities in our society. That’s how we founded the #SocialSolidarity Foundation.

We believe that music, dance, performance, and entertainment is a manifestation of cultural celebration – forms of storytelling. And the best way to build community among diverse populations is by acknowledging the experiential differences of each individual and community. By spectating or participating in our intersectional celebrations, we dare you to know each other.

Description:
My Beloved Child Ministries is a nonprofit 501c3 organization that was formed from the heart of an abuse victim.  From her heart and experience of inner healing, she has devoted herself to help other children who have no voice, and to help women that are victims of abuse and extreme poverty.

MBC's focus has been to offer Broadway based style productions to offer faith based productions while raising funds to help target abused and extreme poverty women and children.  Now targeting human trafficking victims, MBC is now working towards offering training programs to empower abused victims to experience healing and forgiveness in order to live a more fulfilled life with purpose.

Hand in Hand is passionate about giving children born in poverty a hope for the future.  Out ultimate aim is for each child's life to be transformed and the cycle of poverty to be broken.  

Inspired by the difference that one community project was making for destitute children and motivated by God's love, Hand in Hand began in 1998.

We realised our passion was to see children's lives transformed.  Children born into a life with no hope through no choice of their own.  Our desire is to see these lives turned around giving them hope and a future.

From the beginning our work has been a team effort, with partnership being at the heart of all we do. We stand with our project partners offering support, feedback and access to funds relying on their experience, perseverance and local knowledge.  Today, working in 4 countries, we need to raise at least £250,000 a year to make a difference.

Name: The Vanished Library
Description:
A space for donated books to find new homes; a dynamic library for book donations, barter and retail.

Many people today have books they do not have space for. Our aim is to provide a mutually beneficial way to assure that donated books remain in libraries; private, communal, or institutional. Through your book donation (from one book up to entire libraries) we create a dynamic library that keeps books accessible, in use and in circulation.

By building an interactive community library we promote a "free-to-read" environment where a wide scope of books, ranging in all disciplines and languages, are within reach. In the space we have provided at Bardo Clubhouse anyone can come read a book, and anyone can leave with one.

The dynamic library operates on three fronts:

- Exchange any book for a book.

- Books for sale. These are selected books of particular interest, rare and valuable editions, and cool finds. Not exchangeable. 

- We provide books as donations to orphanages, schools and institutes.

The Vanished Library is a place where all books find homes.

Name: A New Sunday
Description:
Weekly letters to my girls on the life well lived.

Becoming a parent changed my perspective in an instant. Decisions took on a larger significance.  Life stopped being about the next career move or dinner out in the city. For the first time I considered the world I was leaving my kids. The world they would inherit.

More personally, I wondered about the types of people my two girls would become and considered my role in the process. I quickly realized that I wasn't equipped to be a parent. One might say that caring and loving is enough, and while it's a great start it's insufficient.  

How would I ready them for the journey they were about to embark upon? How could I prepare them for the road ahead? Could they stand up to the challenges of bullying, drugs, and mounting social pressures? I wanted them to have the best chance to live a fulfilling life, be a caring friend, and contribute to society.  As a relative once told me, "kids don't just grow up, they need to be brought up."  

As I reflected on these questions, I realized I didn't really know what mattered to me.   How could I possibly expect my daughter's to know what mattered to our family?   I still had work to do.

So began A New Sunday.   A pause button on the whirlwind of everyday activity to remind myself and my family about how to orient our lives.  On the first day of the week, I highlight a small piece of universal wisdom providing inspiration and practical guidance on the life well lived.  New week, new idea.

I've made my share of mistakes, and I won't always get it right, but my sincere hope is that you and your family find these short life lessons useful as well.  We are all, quite literally, in this together.

Charlotte's Clinic
Description:
Charlotte’s Clinic has the primary aim of improving Menstrual Health Management. Our research has shown that this is a crisis area, particularly in Africa, and it is deepening poverty, negatively affecting the health of women, and destructive to the education of young girls in school.

We are combating this crisis by providing information, advice, and raising awareness about issues relating to women, particularly, but not limited to, menstrual hygiene and also making provisions for needed materials like Sanitary towels.

Charlotte’s clinic is committed to furthering research in this area in order to influence policy in Nigeria and ensure that there is a permanent change. We have identified three (3) key areas that are integral to our mission: Education, Poverty Relief and Sickness Prevention.

 

Education
1 in 10 girls miss school during their menstrual cycle (Sub-Saharan Africa).
Some estimate that 20% of the academic year is missed.
Many end up missing exams and dropping out.
Because of the stigma attached to the menstrual cycle, and inadequate materials available to girls, their rights to education are being violated. Because of the attitude to menstrual cycles, many girls start their periods with a lack of information and preparation. We plan to educate communities on the nature of menstrual cycles and remove the stigma attached to periods. We  are also working towards ensuring that all girls have access to sanitary pads so that they do not have to miss school because of not having “leak-proof” materials, and educating them on how these items are used, changed and disposed of.

 

Poverty Relief
Many of those that we are reaching out to live below the poverty line, and buying sanitary towels is just not an option. With the average packet of sanitary towels costing around 400 Naira, for many, buying what is now seen as a luxury is just out of the question.

 

Sickness Prevention
Rags
Paper
Socks
Tissue
Leaves/Plants
These are some of the materials that are being used by women in place of sanitary pads. These bad practices are dirty, unhygienic and often harsh on the skin. Bad odours are very common, and infections are rampant. This means that there is a lot of irritation and discomfort suffered, and often times, infections can migrate, affecting fallopian tubes and even ovaries. This can be prevented if we provide sanitary towels and educate women on how these are used.

 
Not only does this initiative better the poverty crisis, but also aids in the hygiene and sanitation crisis. It will help socially as this issue needs to stop being a secret and a taboo. Lets help educate and free our women of this “shame”.

Name: Fundación Escuela Nacional Preparatoria. A.C
Description:
Antecedentes:

La Fundación Escuela Nacional Preparatoria. A.C tiene sus antecedentes en la antiquísima agrupación de egresados de la generación 1920-1924 de la Escuela Nacional Preparatoria 1 del Antiguo Colegio de San Ildefonso esta agrupación cedió la estafeta y tradición filantrópica a la generación 1956-1957 del mismo recinto y que todavía prevalece y es presidida por la C.P. Rosa María Guerra Pérez misma que el día 1 de Marzo del 2019 cedió la estafeta y tradición a Juan Carlos Chávez Velasco actual Presidente de la Fundación Escuela Nacional Preparatoria. A.C.

Fundación ENP es un lazo de unión, absolutamente incluyente, de quienes integran la comunidad universitaria y preparatoriana: alumnos, académicos, trabajadores, funcionarios de la administración y ex alumnos; y de quienes se consideran amigos de nuestra Máxima Casa de Estudios y la Escuela Nacional Preparatoria.

Misión:

Apoyar a la UNAM y a la ENP en la consecución de sus tres objetivos básicos: la docencia, la investigación y la difusión de la cultura, y fortalecer su imagen mediante aportaciones de carácter económico, social o moral, así como coadyuvar con ella en sus tareas de enlace con los sectores público, privado y social.

Visión:

Constituirse como un referente a nivel nacional e internacional en las tareas de posibilitar a muchos jóvenes mexicanos la realización de sus estudios en educación media superior y superior, y contribuir de esta forma a alcanzar objetivos más justos de inclusión social, brindando mejores oportunidades de educación media y superior.

Principios:

La Fundación es una organización establecida en favor de las causas y objetivos de la UNAM y ENP para fortalecer su imagen, tanto en México como en el extranjero. Es ajena a toda actividad política y religiosa; no tiene propósitos de lucro, y se caracteriza por transparentar el uso de los recursos que se le encomiendan para el cumplimiento de sus fines.
Location: Ciudad de México, Ciudad de México

Name: Fundación Decido Sanar
Description:
Creamos la "Fundación Decido Sanar", una organización sin fines de lucro, por medio de la cual esperamos ser un agente de cambio en Chile para ayudar a revertir la obesidad que afecta a más de un 74% de la población, incluido niños, (Chile es #1 en obesidad en el mundo de acuerdo a la última publicación de la OCDE) y la diabetes tipo 2, condiciones que han crecido en los últimos 10 años a pesar de las políticas públicas "Etiquetas Alto En", "Elije Vivir Sano 1", "Contrapeso", "Elije Vivir Sano 2" y "CerObesidad".

Pensamos que las políticas anteriores no han dado resultados porque el diagnostico está mal hecho y, por lo tanto, las medidas también. 

Se estima que las "Enfermedades no Transmisibles" (ENT) tendrán un impacto de US$3.300 millones al año y que el "Síndrome Metabólico" impactará en un 3,8% al PIB, en un país que no crece a más de un 2%, esto generará una nueva crisis tanto social como en la economía del país. 

Queremos INSPIRAR a las personas que es posible eliminar exceso de grasa corporal, mejorar los indicadores de salud, revertir resistencia a la insulina, o prediabetes, y poner la diabetes tipo 2 en remisión a través de la alimentación. 

Queremos EDUCAR a las personas con la ciencia que hay detrás de las recomendaciones.

Queremos EMPODERAR a las personas para que escuchen a su cuerpo y tomen decisiones de acuerdo a ello: la talla y la sangre no mienten".

Name: Help Orphan Puppies, Inc.
Description:
Help Orphan Puppies, Inc. is a not-for-profit organization dedicated to the rescue of dogs in need of loving homes. We specialize in orphaned puppies, pregnant dogs, moms with nursing babies, puppies and puppies with special needs.  

Help Orphan Puppies believes that all good dogs deserve forever homes.   All of our dogs are fostered in volunteer homes in order to ensure their health and well being. During the foster process, we evaluate each dog’s temperament and personality in order to select the best possible forever home. All dogs are treated by a vet and receive full medical care before being placed with an adoptive family. 

Help Orphan Puppies strives to stay in contact with adoptive families to develop and expand a local community of dog lovers whose synergy and involvement will continue to benefit the community, our organization and the animals we serve.

Help Orphan Puppies also provides community education programs to schools, groups, and colleges regarding animal cruelty prevention and the correct and humane treatment of companion animals.

Name: Camptys Foundation
Description:

Children with special needs, complex medical conditions or developmental disabilities are challenged with an array of conditions from premature birth, chronic illnesses, or severe injuries. These conditions often require
a continuum of medical, educational, and rehabilitative long-term care and specialized services.  We support organisations that currently care for and educate children with disability.  

The Foundation appeals to the Caribbean community, to those in the diaspora and to friends of the Caribbean to join us in our endeavour to raise aspirations and make a difference in the lives of children living with disability.  This dynamic organisation uses varying methods to raise funds for the cause.  This includes marketing and selling items in our shop; please contact us if you would like further information:  info@camptys.org

Please go to our website: http://www.camptys.org/ and make a donation to this worthy cause.

Help a child, make a difference!
Help a child to make a difference!

Name: iKindness
Description:
Our mission is to make dreams come true for the less fortunate. I'm excited and grateful for the opportunity to turn a single asset - my original, one of a kind, 20-year old original iPhone collection - into $1M worth of food, shelter, clothing and resources for organizations and people in need. This isn't about charity; its about people helping people for $1.29.

How can you help? 
1. Read about us and join this historical event at ikindness.org

2. Share the ikindness.org link and a personal message to friends, and ask them to do the same. This isn't some chain letter scheme; it's a social experiment of gratitude and giving.  

3. Take part in the process through videos,streamed events, and blogs as we auction the collection to the community, or highest highest bidder (whichever comes first); then watch as we light up people's lives. 

A $1.29 and 5 minutes of time per person, magnified a million times, can make changes some consider impossible. Everyone who takes part in the event will have their name placed with the iPhone collection in an artistic digital rendering as a part of the original iPhone history. 

Kindness is all we can leave behind.  

~Nimo Patel

Name: World Digital Impact Foundation(WDIF)
Description:

It accepts and distributes digital assets through decentralized blockchain technology, firstly initiated by Chinese. It was officially announced at the Davos Forum in Switzerland on January 23, 2018. The headquarters of the WDCF is located in Stockholm, Sweden. The members come from over 20 countries and regions in the world and have long been engaged in public welfare activities. Currently, we have offices in over 20 cities, such as Stockholm, Beijing, Shanghai, Hong Kong, Vancouver, Taipei, etc.

Adhering to the concept of "Digital Commonweal Builds a Community of Human Destinies", WDCF promotes the prosperous development of digital technology and commonweal through blockchain technology. WDCF will build a community of common destiny for all human kind in many fields such as culture and education, technological innovation, disaster relief, environmental protection, energy saving and emission reduction, and health care.

Pelos que já venceram,
pelos que partiram...
e pelos que ainda lutam contra esta doença.

'NÃO VAMOS PARAR ATÉ ENCONTRARMOS A CURA!'

Name: Partnerships in Education & Service
Description:
In 2007, a group of educators got together to support a school attempting to open in New Orleans after Hurricane Katrina.  The educators worked alongside the school staff to insure students would enter a building that was inviting and encouraged students to learn and not to remember the pain of disaster.  Since then, the group has expanded to include people from all walks of life united with the purpose of “rebuilding communities, one school at a time.” 

Service team leaders met with school administration to design a plan for the time volunteers will be on site.  Collectively leaders set priorities that meet the needs of the school and talent of the service team members.  Frequently, that plan is adapted during the trip as the school’s needs and priorities change.

Teams have helped with a multitude of responsibilities.  Our teams are most well-known for our extensive work establishing leveled libraries for classrooms and/or school-wide use.  In addition, teams have helped prepare orientation and first day packets for students and families.  Others have designed bulletin boards and room layouts that are appealing to students. 

Beyond the tasks that use the talents of in-service and pre-service educators, teams have taken out garbage, painted walls and assembled furniture.  PIEs believe that the best way to support a school is not to enter and tell the school what we will do, rather, engage in a conversation to meet the needs of the school with the breadth of talent each team uniquely possess. Therefore, each service trip is unique to the school we partner with and the individuals who commit to serve.

As we move forward, we remain committed to serving populations in need, in particular those affected by disaster.  We look to expand to other parts of the United States and of world partnering with schools to strengthen communities--rebuilding communities, one school at a time.

Name: Noble Indonesia

Name: School2Home
Description:
School2Home is a comprehensive program to assist low-performing California middle schools in implementing cost-effective, technology-based strategies to improve student educational outcomes and digital literacy. These strategies center on providing each student with a computing device to use in the classroom and at home with his or her family, and to leverage the power of broadband to deliver educational resources and strengthen school-to-home connections.
The goals of School2Home are to: Increase digital literacy and educational performance among targeted middle school students. Improve the 21st Century skills of students so they can contribute to a more competitive workforce upon graduation. Strengthen the involvement of parents in the education of their child through the use of home-based digital technologies and, in turn, expand the adoption of broadband technology.
The approach of School2Home is distinct from other school technology programs. School2Home places equal importance on the home and school environments as places for learning and works to enrich student opportunity by making stronger connections between them. The program builds on evidence and lessons learned from 1-to-1 laptop initiatives across California and the country and also advances the growing body of research underscoring the educational significance of a home computer and Internet access. While technology-rich classrooms connect students to updated information, engaging multimedia and distance learning, home computers lengthen the school day, facilitate individualized homework assignments and provide opportunities to engage parents as learning partners.
School2Home aims to support both the school and home learning environments in a way never undertaken by a school technology program and on a scale never achieved in California.

Name: Palestinian Welfare House PWH
Description:
Palestinian Welfare House (PWH) is a well-known Palestinian NGO, non-governmental, humanitarian association (licensure No. 7622) that was established in 2001 to and registered in 2006 to meet the needs of the Palestinian community from social, educational, and health care perspectives through implementing humanitarian developmental projects and programs that could help the Palestinians, mainly who are poor and marginalized. 
PWH believes that humanitarian work is one of the best communication ways between the Palestinians and the other nations in order to help in the building process of the Palestinian community through implementing many comprehensive developmental and relief projects.
It is well-known that the Palestinian people suffer from many humanitarian problems (poverty, high unemployment rate) as a result of the exceptional situation that we live in, and this is what put a heavy gracious and ethical responsibility on the PWH to take its humanitarian role in the Palestinian community.

Name: Stacks Youth Project
Description:
Stacks Youth Project is a 501c3 non-profit organization that was founded in 2009. Our mission is to empower teens to become world changers through eliminating the barriers stacked up against them by providing a positive family environment with mentors that encourage all to discover their purpose and who help teens stack up tools to achieve excellence. Stacks long term desire is to be a large 24 hour youth center that offers a wide variety of activities for youth. Currently Stacks holds regular events and activities at our Youth Center located at the 305 North Scott, Belton Mo.

Our Vision is to Empower Teens to Excellence. We plan to do this through our mentors working one on one with students and taking them through a program that we our developing called Stacks. Our Goal is that every student that comes through our doors would have the opportunity to be mentored and walked through the levels. Every student would start at level 1 then the goal would be they would leave at level six.

Stack 1    Eliminating barriers (On Going)
                  -eating disorders, Drugs, self-harm, grades, homelessness, food, driver licenses, abuse, etc. 
Stack 2    Developing Good Character necessary for achieving excellence (On going)
Stack 3    Exploring interest and gifts
Stack 4    Finding their purpose and working one on one with a mentor to help them develop their vision and a plan of action to achieve it.
Stack 5    Mentor a younger student
Stack 6    Mentors support teens beyond Stacks as they work towards achieving their dreams.
Stack 7    Teens living the dream and coming back to Stacks to share.

Name: Bervann One-Kid-At-A-Time
Description:
“It is every man’s obligation to put back into the world at least the equivalent of what he takes out of it”. Albert Einstein

For centuries, the world has missed out on the brilliance of its youth due to wealth inequality. If given the opportunity, these young women and men can make the most intergenerational impact in history.

The Bervann One-Kid-At-A-Time is a division of Bervann Foundation, a US non-profit and tax-exempt organization under section 501(c)(3) organization of the Internal Revenue Code. We promote a community mindset of caring and charitable giving, from the public as well as the business community, to give back and support communities and organizations in their greatest need, with a primary focus on students and families from underprivileged and minority backgrounds. 

One-Kid-At-A-Time receives a load of applications from students and families of minority and underprivileged backgrounds. We review their credentials, hold several interviews with them and their parents; We then select a handful of them who have shown great determination, strong work ethic and have been able to secure at least a portion of the funding through their own efforts.

Our February Spotlight Student is Mr. Kai Kawashima, a talented young man who overcame the challenges of moving across 3 countries during school, adapting to studies in 2 languages and 3 cultures. A low-income student of visible minority, he dedicated himself to his academics, arts, and sports, excelling in all three throughout his high school career.

He gained acceptance in the prestigious Digipen Institute of Technology, the 3rd most difficult school to get into in Washington State with an acceptance rate of just 34%, ranked among the top 5 schools for Game Design in 2020 by The Princeton Review. Now in his second year of studies, he is maintaining a 3.85 GPA and has made the Dean’s list for 2 semesters.

Description:
As an Affiliate Chapter of The 100 Black Men of America, Inc., we are dedicated to building stronger young men, loving families and better communities by working together in a network of Chapters around the world.

The chapter serves as a beacon of hope, commitment, and leadership for an area that is one of the bedrocks for human rights movements in this country. The members, advisors, and the leadership are intricately involved in every aspect of life in the Greater Montgomery area: judges, lawyers, doctors, nurses, technicians, educators, administrators, active duty and retired retired military members, aviators, engineers, financial managers, businessmen, and other professions.

Name: International Arts Foundation For Kids
Description:
Our mission is to bring art to life for the less fortunate children in our communities.

To make this happen, the International Art Foundation for Kids collects new art supplies, new and gently used music and dance equipment, and tickets to museums, shows and concerts. We then distribute these items to children of need within our communities for whom artistic pursuit can be difficult.

While we at the IAFK organize, coordinate and manage outreach campaigns, our ultimate success depends on the support of local communities and the generosity of the people who donate art supplies, as well as those who make monetary donations for the purchase of supplies.

Social welfare agencies, churches, local community agencies, local business leaders and other members of the community make our outreach possible. They help us to identify needy children in our communities, and they play an important role assisting us in collecting and distributing art supplies.

By working together, we can all introduce the beauty of art into the lives of those less fortunate.

Name: Le Blaireau CC
Description:
We believe that riding bikes will save the world. We exist to find and fund inspiring bike projects that help people. 

As a social enterprise, we give 100% of profit to good cycling projects around the world. One hundred percent. Every penny. As a family start-up, we are small but powerful. We don't have shareholders or loads of staff or big budgets. Instead we have boundless creativity, a kind heart and relentless drive.

Oh, and we sell t-shirts, run a clubhouse in the French Pyrenees and are always - ALWAYS - looking for collaborators.

Name: Pilotlight Australia
Description:
Pilotlight Australia is an agent for social change, a tiny flame sparking big ideas, projects, and alliances to tackle issues of injustice in creative and unexpected ways. We connect unlikely combinations of people to generate fresh approaches to philanthropy.

We are deliberately small and lean with an increasing network of partners and friends spanning the corporate, philanthropic, government and community sectors.

We start from the belief that nothing is impossible and that everyone – through their passion, skills, imagination and ideals – has the right and responsibility to become an agent for change.

Name: Heart 2 Heart uOttawa
Description: We strive to help provide access to high quality healthcare for everyone. Our vision involves supporting and empowering individuals, hospitals, and healthcare networks globally to reduce financial barriers that patients may encounter in achieving the care and services they need.

Name: Fundacja Przypominajka
Description:
Fundacja” Przypominajka” to projekt, który powstał z chęci wspierania i promowania odpowiedzialności społecznej w biznesie. Swoją działalnością chcemy wzmacniać i przyczyniać się do rozwoju innych - firm i jednostek oferując przedmioty i produkty rozwojowe.

Jesteśmy szczególnie uważni na potrzeby ludzi i wyrównywanie szans, dlatego zatrudniamy cudownie zaangażowane osoby z niepełnosprawnościami i razem tworzymy zespół, który pracuje z przyjemnością i pasją.

Naszym celem jest słuchać, zrozumieć potrzeby, zaproponować, ale nie narzucać rozwiązań. Wiemy, że  dobrze zadane pytania rodzą przyjazne odpowiedzi. 

Pytamy,  ale nie oceniamy - z nami możesz czuć się bezpiecznie- naszym priorytetem jest pomoc w Twoim osobistym rozwoju.

 Ekologia jest nam bliska dlatego promujemy zasadę wielorazowości  Nasze produkty reklamowe tworzymy przede wszystkim mając na uwadze jakość. Nie opieramy się na gotowych wzorach szukamy ich nieszablonowo. Kryterium zawsze jest jednak poszanowanie dla środowiska, wyjątkowy pomysł i design. 

Szanujemy Klienta i szanujemy pracę jako proces twórczy. Dlatego każdy przedmiot pakujemy ręcznie i oddzielnie. Większy wysiłek rekompensowany jest dopracowanym, dopieszczonym finalnie produktem.

Pomagając Klientom, dzielimy się swoja wiedzą bez zazdrości. Naszą zasadą jest dawać więcej. Nie strzeżemy swojego know how zazdrośnie, bo wiemy, że nasi Klienci potrafią to docenić. Dzielimy bowiem wspólną wizję świata i biznesu w którym nasze wspólne najwyższe dobro to ludzie i środowisko.
